title: A Cheap and Delicate Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of butter
- a cup of sugar
- a cup of milk
- two eggs
- a pint of sifted flour
- a small teaspoonful of soda
- two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- two tablespoonfuls of water
- lemon sauce or rich wine sauce

## Directions

1. Put into the flour a small teaspoonful of soda and two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar; sift this in carefully, and set the flour aside
2. Beat the eggs, yolks and whites together, briskly until they foam
3. Add to the eggs two tablespoonfuls of water; beat them sharply again until the tissues of the eggs thoroughly blend with the water
4. Mix the sugar and butter together; add the eggs, beat again
5. Pour in the flour which will make a stiff batter
6. Thin this with the small cup of milk (sweet milk is the best)
7. Bake in shallow pans and serve with lemon sauce, or a rich wine sauce if that is preferred

title: A Cold Dish For Lunch
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled egg
- raw veal
- pepper
- salt
- herbs (such as parsley, thyme and sage)
- bacon
- mace
- floured cloth

## Directions

1. Cut a hard boiled egg in thin slices, and place it in the middle of a bowl
2. Put a layer of raw veal cut in thin slices, and sprinkled with a mixture of pepper, salt and herbs (such as parsley, thyme and sage)
3. Place next very thin slices of bacon, and continue to put alternate layers of veal, seasoned with a little mace and of bacon, until the bowl is filled
4. Mash it down and tie a floured cloth over it very tight
5. Turn it down in a pot of hot water, and let it boil 2 hours
6. It is eaten cold

title: A Crawfish Bush
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Fine Crawfish
- Celery Tips
- Asparagus Tips
- Parsley Tips

## Directions

1. Boil the Crawfish according to recipe
2. Take a glass fruit or cake stand and place in the center of the table
3. Set a goblet upon it
4. Fit the goblet with celery tips and parsley tips, and hang a number of Crawfish gracefully around the goblet from the rim or outer edges
5. Continue hanging the dish with celery, asparagus and parsley tips, and hanging the Crawfish around the edges of the fruit stand, and in and out amid the greenery
6. Serve the Crawfish from the bush

title: A Cream Sauce for Fillet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of mushrooms
- 1 small box of truffles
- 1/2 small lemon
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 large tablespoon of flour
- 1 small teaspoon of eschallot or onion, minced extremely fine
- 3/5 of a teacup of rich sweet milk
- cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour a can of mushrooms through a cullender, to drain them well
2. put them in a clean wooden bowl, and with a vegetable mincer, mince them as fine as you can
3. add a small box of truffles, well drained and minced
4. drain the juice of 1/2 of a small lemon over them
5. put 2 large tablespoonfuls of butter in a saucepan
6. when quite hot, stir in 1 large tablespoon of flour
7. have a small teaspoon of eschallot or onion, minced extremely fine, which stir in the butter and flour
8. stir it all the time to keep from burning, and let it remain long enough on the fire to take a golden color
9. then pour slowly in 3/5 of a teacup of rich sweet milk
10. let this mix thoroughly, stirring all the time, for about 5 minutes, to prevent lumping
11. add to the sauce the minced mushrooms and truffles, and let all simmer together for a few minutes
12. season this highly with cayenne pepper and salt
13. the sauce should be quite pasty before the mushrooms are added
14. This sauce should be made quite thick, and can be made early in the day
15. when wanted, place the vessel in one of boiling water, to get well heated
16. Last of all, add the juice of the other 1/2 of the lemon

title: A Creole Summer Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Lemon Jelly
- 1 Cup of Stoned Cherries
- 1 Cup of Malaga Grapes
- 2 Oranges
- 2 Bananas
- 1/2 a Pineapple
- 1 Pint of Snow Cream

## Directions

1. Make a Lemon Jelly, add stoned cherries, a few Malaga grapes, and the meat of two oranges, two bananas and half a pineapple, sliced.
2. Pour a little jelly into each custard glass.
3. Put a layer first of one fruit, then of another, then a layer of snow cream, and continue alternating till the glass is filled with the snow on top.
4. Serve ice cold.

title: A Delicious Flavor from Thyme, Etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- thyme
- mint
- sweet marjoram
- rosemary
- strong vinegar or brandy

## Directions

1. pick thyme, mint, sweet marjoram and rosemary from the stalks when gathered in full perfection
2. put into a large jar
3. pour strong vinegar or brandy over them
4. let them stay in this twenty-four hours
5. take the herbs out, and throw in fresh bunches
6. do this three times
7. strain the liquor or vinegar
8. Cork and seal the bottles tight
9. Do not let the herbs stay more than twenty or twenty-four hours in the liquid before straining

title: A Delicious Omelet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- a tablespoonful of chopped green onion
- a tablespoonful of parsley, chopped fine
- two tablespoonfuls of water
- salt
- a teaspoonful of nice sweet butter

## Directions

1. Beat separately, and lightly, six eggs
2. add to them a tablespoonful of chopped green onion, and the same of parsley, chopped fine
3. beat them into the eggs with two tablespoonfuls of water, and at the last moment a little salt
4. Have a thick-bottomed skillet or pan on the fire, put in a teaspoonful of nice sweet butter
5. when this is hot put in the eggs
6. Take a broad-bladed knife and keep rolling the omelet as it sets
7. do not let it get too brown, but roll it in an oblong shape
8. never turn an omelet over, but push and roll it as described
9. slide it on a hot dish
10. pour a spoonful of melted butter over it
11. send it to table hot

title: A Delicious Pudding, Very Easily Made
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- thin slices of rolls
- currants
- citron cut up fine
- a quart of milk
- four eggs
- half a pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Butter some thin slices of rolls.
2. Lay them in a pudding-dish with currants and citron cut up fine, and strewed between the slices.
3. Pour over the rolls a custard made of a quart of milk, four eggs and half a pound of sugar.
4. Flavor this and bake lightly.

title: A Dessert for a Delicate Person
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- a teacup of rich sweet cream
- half a teacup of any acid jelly—currant is the best but plum, strawberry or lemon will do
- sweetened cream

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of rice until perfectly soft, then add a teacup of rich sweet cream, and half a teacup of any acid jelly.
2. Put it over the fire a few minutes, turn it into a mould.
3. Eat with sweetened cream.

title: A Fine Temperance Beverage
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of a dozen lemons
- one pound and a half of double refined sugar
- a picked quart of raspberries or strawberries
- a ripe pineapple
- ice water

## Directions

1. To the juice of a dozen lemons put one pound and a half of double refined sugar, and a picked quart of raspberries or strawberries
2. Pare a ripe pineapple and slice it, put over it half a pound of sugar
3. Stir the lemon juice with the sugar
4. Crush in the berries slightly bruise the pineapple and chop it up in small pieces
5. Put the lemon juice in a large punch bowl
6. Add to it three quarts of ice water, then put in the strawberry and pineapple juice
7. Stir it until all the sugar is dissolved, and then set it on ice
8. Serve in punch glasses

title: A French Fried Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- a quart of milk
- sugar
- flavoring
- baker's bread
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs to a quart of milk, sweeten and flavor to taste
2. Cut slices of baker's bread and steep them until thoroughly saturated
3. Fry in hot butter and serve

title: A French Pilou
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Chickens
- 0.75 Cup of Rice
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the fowls according to recipe.
2. When done, take out about a pint of the liquor in which it was boiled, and put the rice, which you will have washed well, into the remaining boiling broth.
3. Let it cook well for twenty minutes, and then add two tablespoonfuls of butter to the rice.
4. Butter the bottom of a dish, and put upon it one-half of the rice, spreading out nicely.
5. Lay upon it the chickens, which have been disjointed and buttered.
6. Add the remaining chicken broth, pouring over the chicken.
7. Then cover the fowls with the other half of the rice.
8. Make the top perfectly smooth.
9. Spread over it the yolks of two eggs, which have been well beaten.
10. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, and dot with little bits of butter here and there.
11. Set in the oven, let it brown, and serve hot.

title: A Garfish of Parsley and Radish
body:
## Ingredients

- Spare ribs
- Salt and pepper
- Butter
- Parsley
- Radishes

## Directions

1. Dredge the spare ribs lightly with salt and pepper, after having washed well and wiped dry with a coarse towel.
2. Place them in the baking pan and dredge with butter.
3. Place them in the oven and cover with a piece of buttered paper.
4. Allow twenty minutes to every pound in cooking.
5. About twenty minutes before serving take off the buttered paper, dredge again with melted butter, and let it brown nicely.
6. Serve with a garnish of parsley and radishes.

title: A General Rule for Making Sponge Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar equal in weight to the eggs used
- flour equal to half the weight of eggs used
- eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and sugar together
2. Add the flour
3. Beat the whites to a high froth
4. Add the whites last
5. Stir them thoroughly together
6. Put into a paper-lined basin or pan
7. Bake in a quick oven
8. Optional: flavor with lemon, rose, or vanilla extracts, nutmeg, or brandy
9. Optional: add pounded almonds or grated cocoanut

title: A Good Every-Day Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Chopped Soup Meat
- 1 Onion
- 2 Potatoes
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- ½ Pint of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Chop the left-over bouilli fine in dice shapes, and to every quart of the meat allow one onion, a tablespoonful of butter, two hard-boiled eggs, two cold left-over potatoes, a half pint of water, and salt and pepper to taste
2. Chop the potatoes, onions and eggs fine and put them into the stewing pan with the meat, adding by degrees the butter, salt and pepper with a little dash of Cayenne
3. Stew very slowly for about fifteen or twenty minutes and serve hot

title: A Nice Chicken Salad
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- white parts of four or five heads of celery
- green celery leaves
- a fine baked chicken
- yolks of six hard-boiled eggs
- two spoonfuls of mixed mustard
- a teaspoonful of white sugar
- oil
- half a cup of vinegar
- white heads of lettuce
- four hard-boiled eggs sliced

## Directions

1. Cut up the white parts of four or five heads of celery, reserving the green leaves
2. Pick all the meat from a fine baked chicken, chop this up, and mix it with the cut-up celery
3. Lay it in a glass or china dish, where it will be cool
4. To make the dressing, rub the yolks of six hard-boiled eggs to a paste, with two spoonfuls of mixed mustard, a teaspoonful of white sugar, and enough oil to make it perfectly smooth
5. Put this in slowly, a little at a time
6. Finish the dressing by pouring in half a cup of vinegar
7. Pour this over the celery and chicken, and garnish with white heads of split lettuce, also the reserved celery leaves, and four sliced hard-boiled eggs

title: A Nice Cup-Cake Rich Enough For Any Company
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- three cups of sugar
- five eggs
- five cups of sifted flour
- one cup of sour cream
- one teaspoonful of soda
- a glass of wine
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Work butter and sugar to a cream
2. beat eggs separately
3. stir in flour
4. add sour cream and soda
5. flavor with wine and nutmeg
6. bake in quick oven in round tins
7. ice while warm

title: A Nice Ice Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of nice fresh milk
- the yolks of eight eggs
- a pound of fine white sugar
- extract, either of lemon, orange, peach or vanilla
- one quart of pure cream
- ice, pounded fine
- coarse dairy salt

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a stew-pan containing a quart of nice fresh milk.
2. While it is coming to the boil beat the yolks of eight eggs and a pound of fine white sugar.
3. When these are well beaten, take off the boiling milk, let it stand to cool five minutes, and pour it very hot over the eggs and sugar.
4. Strain this mixture, and add for flavoring any favorite extract, either of lemon, orange, peach or vanilla.
5. Let it stand to get cool, and pour it into the freezer and surround it with layers of ice, pounded fine, and coarse dairy salt, well beaten down.
6. Fill up till within a few inches of the top of the freezer.
7. Pour in one quart of pure cream, and beat it with a wooden spoon into the mixture in the freezer.
8. Turn the crank of your freezer briskly if you have a five minute freezer; if not, turn the can with your hand for fifteen minutes.
9. Pack round again with ice and salt.
10. Draw off the melted ice and salt water, and fill up again and set away to harden before serving.

title: A Nice Molasses Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- one and a half cups of sugar
- one cup of butter
- four eggs
- a cup of sour milk
- one heaping-spoonful of soda
- flour

## Directions

1. grease the pan carefully as molasses cake is liable to stick
2. Make the batter stiff with flour
3. bake in a pan

title: A Nice Omelet with Green Onion
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- green onion, chopped
- parsley, fine-cut
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks of six eggs separately
2. put in a tablespoonful of butter, a spoonful of chopped green onion and one of fine-cut parsley
3. mix with the eggs
4. put it into a thick-bottomed pan, in which you have placed a half cup of butter
5. Roll it up as it cooks
6. tilt the pan on one side, that the omelet may cook on the other side
7. roll up again as it cooks
8. Do not let it get hard and brown, but keep it soft
9. Keep on rolling as well as you can
10. When the eggs cook, butter, pepper and salt them
11. turn on a dish

title: A Nice Supper Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of cream
- one ounce of isinglass
- water
- vanilla bean
- four ounces of sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take one pint of cream, whip it until stiff
2. Boil one ounce of isinglass in about a pint of water
3. Boil it until reduced to half a pint
4. Boil in this water and isinglass, a vanilla bean
5. When nearly cold, take out the bean, add four ounces of sugar
6. When this is blood warm, stir in the cream
7. Eat with whipped cream

title: A Nice Way to Cook Okra or Gombo
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of young tender okra
- half as much skinned, ripe tomatoes
- an onion
- a tablespoonful of butter
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Take a pint of young tender okra, chop it up fine
2. Add to it half as much skinned, ripe tomatoes
3. Add an onion cut up in slices
4. Add a tablespoonful of butter, a little salt and pepper, and a spoonful of water
5. Stew all together till tender
6. Serve with meat or poultry

title: A Nice Way to Make Yeast Powder Biscuit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of flour
- two heaping spoonfuls of yeast or baking powder
- a tablespoonful of lard
- a little salt
- water

## Directions

1. Take a quart of flour and sift it
2. Sift one pint of the flour in a tin basin
3. Sift into it two heaping spoonfuls of yeast or baking powder
4. Add to the flour a tablespoonful of lard and put this also in the basin
5. Make a nice batter with a little salt
6. Beat the flour, lard and water very briskly until it is light
7. Take down your biscuit board and sift on it the other pint of flour
8. Make a hole in the flour and pour in your batter
9. Gently stir it until it is a soft dough
10. Keep it as soft as possible
11. Roll it out and cut it with the biscuit cutter
12. Bake quickly

title: A Nice Way to Serve Crabs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen crabs
- one pound bread crumbs
- two tablespoons butter
- one teaspoon salt
- half teaspoon black pepper
- slice of onion
- half tablespoon chopped parsley
- thyme
- one egg
- three tablespoons lard

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs five minutes
2. Pick and chop them, adding all the ingredients
3. Shape in forms of mutton chops, and roll in the beaten egg
4. Stick in a claw
5. Put in a frying pan three tablespoons of lard
6. When boiling, put in the crabs to brown, which they will do in three minutes
7. Serve in a round dish with claws in the center

title: A Plain Molasses Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Louisiana Molasses
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Boiling Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Ginger

## Directions

1. Dissolve the soda in boiling water, and add it to the molasses.
2. Then melt the butter, and add, and pour in gradually the boiling water, mixing well.
3. Now add gradually the flour, beating well, and add the ginger.
4. Beat until all is very smooth, and then bake for a half hour in a moderate oven.

title: A Plain Way to Cook a Turkey by Roasting
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- dressing made from bread or forcemeat
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- gravy
- bread sauce
- chestnuts

## Directions

1. Make a dressing to suit you; there are several to choose from in this book, made from bread, or forcemeat.
2. Stuff the turkey, season it with salt, pepper, and a little butter.
3. Dredge it with flour and put it in the oven; let the fire be slow at first, and hotter as it begins to cook.
4. Baste frequently with butter; when the turkey is well plumped up, and the steam draws toward the fire, it is nearly done.
5. Then dredge again with flour, and baste with more butter until it is a nice brown.
6. Serve with gravy and bread sauce; some like chestnuts stewed in the turkey gravy, and served with it.
7. A very large turkey will take three hours to roast, one of eight pounds will take two hours.

title: A Pretty Dish of Oranges Croquante
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or a dozen oranges
- one pound of loaf sugar
- half a glass of water
- whipped cream
- one glass of maraschino

## Directions

1. Take ten or a dozen oranges, remove the peel, all the white part and the seeds
2. Do this carefully by quartering them, retaining the transparent pulp and juice
3. Do not break the skins of the sections
4. Boil a pound of loaf sugar in half a glass of water until the syrup strings when lifted on a fork
5. Take it from the fire and dip each section of orange in this candy while it is hot
6. You can do this by placing each one on a little stick cut for the purpose
7. As the pieces are dipped, arrange them in some pretty form on a dish or bowl
8. Fill up the hollow with whipped cream, sweetened and seasoned with a glass of maraschino

title: A Quick Way of Pickling Cabbage
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 nice white cabbages
- salt
- water
- weak vinegar
- turmeric
- good cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Take 2 nice white cabbages and quarter them
2. If very large more than quarter them
3. Put them in salt and water to remain 24 hours
4. Boil them till tender in weak vinegar with a little turmeric in it
5. Put them in jars and pour over them good cold vinegar
6. Tie them up

title: A Quickly-Made Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- crackers
- raisins
- butter
- wine
- sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Split a few crackers, lay the surface over with raisins, and place the halves together again
2. Tie them closely in a cloth
3. Boil them fifteen minutes
4. Serve with a rich sauce of butter, wine, sugar and nutmeg

title: A Richer Molasses Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- one cup of sugar
- four eggs
- four tablespoonfuls of butter
- rich crust

## Directions

1. Mix together the sugar, butter and eggs
2. Stir in the molasses
3. Bake in a rich crust

title: A Shrimp Bush
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Lake Shrimp
- Celery Tips
- Asparagus Tips

## Directions

1. Boil the shrimp according to recipe.
2. Take a glass fruit or cake stand.
3. Pile the shrimp first into a small deep dish, and put a close cover on to press them down.
4. Turn them over and they will cling together in one solid mass.
5. If a cake stand is used, set a glass bowl or goblet on it.
6. Place the shrimp on top of this glass bowl or goblet.
7. Take dainty bits of celery tips and asparagus tips, and heap around as a border.
8. Form another row a little lower, intermingled with asparagus tips and celery tops, between which the pink shrimp are gracefully placed.
9. Serve the shrimp from the bush as an hors d'oeuvre.

title: A Simple Bread Stuffing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Stale Bread, Wet and Squeezed Thoroughly
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Chopped Parsley and Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wet the bread and squeeze
2. Add the minced herbs and season well with salt and pepper
3. Mix all thoroughly and fry in butter

title: A Simple Way of Baking Redfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Redfish or a Fish Weighing 4 Pounds
- 2 Large Onions
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Hollandaise Sauce

## Directions

1. When purchased in slices, clean the outer edges well of the fins, wash and then rub the fish well with salt and pepper
2. Prepare some chopped onion and parsley and lay in a deep baking pan
3. Place a layer of fish over this and then a layer of small lumps of butter and chopped parsley and onion
4. Place over this another layer of fish
5. Cover with another layer of chopped parsley, onions and butter, and place in the oven to bake
6. Bake about an hour, basting frequently, and serve with a Hollandaise Sauce

title: A Spring Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- A Spring Chicken
- Melted Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select spring chickens for broiling. For a family of six several will be required.
2. Clean the chickens, nicely, singe, and then split down the middle of the back, laying the chicken open.
3. Break the breastbone with a mallet and flatten out the chicken.
4. Season well with salt and pepper, and brush with melted butter.
5. Have the broiler ready over a moderate fire, and place the chicken between the double broiler and let the fowl broil slowly for about a half hour, if the chicken is very tender, otherwise three-quarters of an hour.
6. Keep a plate over it all the time, as it will retain its flavor better.
7. Turn the chicken frequently, so that it may be broiled through and through.
8. It should be slightly browned on the skin side.
9. When done, place in a heated dish, pour over melted butter and garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
10. A garnish of cresses is very pretty. The dish is then called 'Poulet Grille aux Cressons.'

title: A Summer Fast-Day Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- The Hearts of 6 Lettuce Cut in Pieces
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Cucumbers, Pared and Sliced
- 4 Pints of Young Green Peas
- Chopped Parsley
- 3 Lumps of Crushed Sugar
- 1/4 Pound of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Sprig of Mint

## Directions

1. Chop the vegetables fine and stew all together, except the young peas
2. After one hour add the young peas
3. Press them through a sieve and put all into the water in which they have been boiled
4. Add to this the vegetables that have been stewed in the butter and simmer about an hour and a half
5. A sprig of mint is added just before the soup is taken off the fire

title: A Superior Blackberry Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- boiling water
- refined sugar

## Directions

1. Bruise your berries and measure them
2. To every gallon, add a quart of boiling water
3. Let this stand twenty-four hours, stirring it three or four times during this time
4. The third day strain off the juice
5. To every gallon of this strained liquor, put two pounds of refined sugar
6. Cork it tight and let it stand until cool weather

title: A Superior Lemon Tart
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six lemons
- one pound of sugar
- one-fourth of a pound of butter
- yolks and whites of five eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from six lemons
2. Wash the rinds and boil them
3. If too strong of the lemon oil, it is better to change the water
4. Grate or pound the rinds, and when tender and cold, add to them one pound of sugar, one-fourth of a pound of butter, and the yolks and whites of five eggs
5. Stir in the juice of the lemons
6. Cook the batter gently until it is thick as honey
7. Bake it in puff paste without tops
8. Ornament

title: A Superior Lemon Tart
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six lemons
- one pound of sugar
- one-fourth of a pound of butter
- five eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from six lemons, wash the rinds and boil them; if too strong of the lemon oil, it is better to change the water.
2. Grate or pound the rinds, and when tender and cold, add to them one pound of sugar, one-fourth of a pound of butter, and the yolks and whites of five eggs.
3. Stir in the juice of the lemons, and cook the batter gently until it is thick as honey.
4. Bake it in puff paste without tops.
5. Ornament with fancy strips of paste.

title: A Very Good Cheap Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- one and a half cups of brown sugar
- one cup of milk
- sweet or sour
- yeast powder or soda
- one cup of molasses
- four eggs
- one nutmeg
- one paning of raisins
- five cups of flour

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients, using two teaspoonfuls yeast powder if using yeast powder, or one heaping teaspoonful soda if using soda

title: A Very Nice Potato Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds or pints of flour
- one pound or pint of warm, mashed mealy Irish potatoes
- milk and water
- a cup of yeast
- a little salt
- a little lard or butter

## Directions

1. To two pounds or pints of flour, add one pound or pint of warm, mashed mealy Irish potatoes
2. Add to this milk and water, a cup of yeast and a little salt
3. Make it after kneading it very well, into loaves, and place them in a pan to rise
4. If you desire a rich, short bread you may add a little lard or butter, but it is nice without

title: A Winter Fast-Day Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Dried Peas
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Lettuce, Sliced
- 1 Head of Celery
- 1 Carrot
- 2 Turnips
- 2 Large Onions
- Handful of Spinach
- Sprig Each of Mint, Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- A Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Stew all the vegetables, except the lettuce, together, after having chopped fine, until they are perfectly soft
2. Then return to the fire with the chopped lettuce, butter and sugar
3. Boil quickly about twenty minutes, and serve with Croutons

title: Aberdeen Crulla Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 lb. of pounded and sifted loaf sugar
- 4 well beaten eggs
- flour
- boiling lard or clarified suet
- pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Beat to a cream 1 lb. of fresh butter
2. Mix it with the same quantity of pounded and sifted loaf sugar and 4 well beaten eggs
3. Add flour till thick enough to roll out
4. Cut the paste into oblong pieces, 4 or 5 inches in length, with a paste cutter
5. Divide the centre into 3 or 4 strips
6. Wet the edges and plait one bar over the other, so as to meet in the centre
7. Throw them into boiling lard or clarified suet
8. When fried a light brown, drain them before the fire
9. Cover them with pounded sugar

title: Absinthe and Anisette
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a wineglass of absinthe
- two or three dashes of anisette

## Directions

1. Mix same as above

title: Absinthe and Sugar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a wineglass of absinthe
- a teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. mix same as above

title: Absinthe Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pints of brandy
- 1/4 pound of wormwood heads
- zest of 2 lemons
- 3 oranges, thinly sliced
- 6 pounds of sugar
- 3 pints of water

## Directions

1. Pound the wormwood and infuse it for two days in the brandy with the zest of the lemons or the sliced oranges
2. Distill the liquor to one-half the quantity
3. Dissolve six pounds of the finest white sugar in three pints of water, let it cool, and mix thoroughly with the brandy and wormwood
4. Strain it and put in tightly corked bottles

title: Absinthe Frappée
body:
## Ingredients

- A small glass filled with finely-crushed ice and water
- 1 tablespoonful of absinthe
- A dash of anisette

## Directions

1. Mix the absinthe and anisette together, strain into small, thin glasses, with crushed ice and water.
2. Let the mixture get very cold and serve immediately.

title: Absinthe or Wormwood Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of Wormwood
- 8 Ounces of Juniper Berries
- 2 Ounces of Ground Cinnamon
- 4 Drams of Angelica Rum
- 4 Pints of Good French Brandy
- 2 and a half Pounds of fine white powdered sugar
- 2 Pints of water, filtered
- 8 Ounces of doubly-distilled orange flower water

## Directions

1. Bruise and pound the wormwood and juniper berries, and steep them with four pints of brandy and the Angelica rum for twenty days.
2. Distill the mixture till it is reduced to three pounds, and re-distill till this is reduced to two pounds or two pints.
3. Add two and a half pounds of fine white powdered sugar and two pints of water, filtered, and eight ounces of doubly-distilled orange flower water.
4. Filter and bottle.

title: Adelaide's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- 1 egg
- flour
- 1 teaspoonfal of salt
- wine and sugar sauce

## Directions

1. Pare and core 6 apples
2. Fill up the cores with sugar and cinnamon
3. Make a batter of 1 egg, a little flour, 1 teaspoonfal of salt
4. Pour around it and bake
5. Serve hot with wine and sugar sauce

title: All The Year Round Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- three ounces of jam (strawberry or raspberry)
- three ounces of bread crumbs
- three ounces butter
- three ounces sugar
- the rind and juice of a large lemon
- a tablespoonful of water

## Directions

1. Line a pie dish with paste
2. Spread on this three ounces of jam
3. Beat well in a basin three ounces of bread crumbs, three ounces butter, and the same of sugar, and the rind and juice of a large lemon
4. Add this to the pastry and jam
5. If the lemon is not very juicy, add a tablespoonful of water to it
6. Bake half an hour

title: Almond and Macaroon Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint rich milk
- handful bitter almonds
- 1 quart milk
- 8 eggs
- 1 pint powdered sugar
- macaroon

## Directions

1. Boil in 1/2 pint of rich milk a handful of bitter almonds, blanched and broken up
2. When highly flavored, strain that milk and set aside
3. Boil 1 quart of milk by itself, and when cold, stir in slowly 8 well beaten eggs, adding the flavored milk, and 1 pint of powdered sugar
4. Stir the whole very hard at the last
5. Bake it in cups, and when done and quite cold, put on the top of each a macaroon with 4 others placed around it

title: Almond and Peanut Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 4 Ounces of Pistachio Kernels or Peanuts
- 4 Eggs
- 8 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- Four tablespoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Blanch, peel and pound the almonds and pistachio kernels or peanuts in a mortar till finely pulverized, adding the white of an egg, to keep the almonds from turning oily.
2. Beat the whites of the eggs to a froth, and then add the yolks, beaten separately to a cream with the sugar.
3. Beat well, and incorporate thoroughly.
4. Add four tablespoonfuls of flour, sifted well, and mix well.
5. Place in small cakes on wafer sheets of paper, and bake in a moderate oven till a very light color.

title: Almond Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound of almonds blanched and pounded very fine
- rose or orange flower water
- yolks of 8 eggs
- whites of 4 eggs
- ½ lb. of loaf sugar
- 3 ounces of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat almonds with rose or orange flower water
2. Beat the yolks and whites until very light
3. Add to the almonds
4. Beat in ½ lb. of loaf sugar
5. Continue to beat while the oven is heating
6. When ready, add 3 ounces of wheat flour, which must be stirred in and not beaten
7. Butter the pans and fill them half full
8. Sprinkle a little sugar over them as you put them in the oven
9. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Almond Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of almonds, blanched and pounded very fine
- rose or orange flower water
- yolks of 8 eggs
- whites of 4 eggs
- 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar
- 3 ounces of flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound almonds very fine with as much rose or orange flower water as will prevent their oiling
2. Beat yolks of 8 and whites of 4 eggs until very light
3. Add to the almonds
4. Beat in 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar
5. Continue to beat while the oven is heating
6. When ready, add 3 ounces of flour, which must be stirred in and not beaten
7. Butter the pan and fill 3/4 full
8. Dust a little sugar over as you put them in the oven
9. Bake

title: Almond Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Cups of Flour
- The Whites of Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful Lemon Extract
- 2 Pounds of Almonds
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. Add the milk, and gradually add the flour and the whites of the eggs alternately, and beat till light
3. Add the essence, and bake in jelly tins fifteen minutes
4. Take two pounds of almonds, blanch them, peel and pound to a flour
5. Beat the whites of two eggs to a froth with one-half cup of sugar, and spread on top of the bottom layer of cake as a frosting
6. Place over this a layer of the almonds, and then another layer of frosting
7. Add a layer of cake, and then go over the same process again till the top layer of cake is reached
8. Sprinkle with white sugar or ice neatly, according to taste

title: Almond Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 pound of sifted loaf sugar
- 3/4 pound of sifted flour
- 6 ounces sweet almonds
- zest of 2 citrons
- zest of 1 orange
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the whites separately, beating the latter to a stiff froth.
2. Blanch the almonds, and peel them and pound them to a fine flour in a mortar.
3. Grate the zest of the oranges and lemons or citrons, and mix well.
4. Beat the sugar and the yolks of the eggs to a cream, and gradually add one-half of the flour, beating well.
5. Then add the almonds, mixing thoroughly, and, after beating vigorously, add one-half of the whites of the eggs.
6. Beat well, add the remainder of the flour, and then add the remainder of the eggs.
7. Finally add the orange flower water, and mix lightly.

title: Almond Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of blanched bitter almonds
- 7 ounces of finely sifted flour, well dried
- 10 eggs
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar, pounded and sifted
- 2 tablespoonfuls of rose water
- whites of eggs and 24 teaspoonfuls of loaf sugar for icing
- lemon, rose water, or vanilla for seasoning

## Directions

1. Beat the almonds very fine
2. Mix with the flour, eggs, sugar, and rose water
3. Bake
4. Ice with the whites of eggs and sugar
5. Season with lemon, rose water, or vanilla

title: Almond Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 3/4 lb. of finely pounded white sugar
- 1/2 lb. dried and sifted flour
- 1/2 lb. of blanched sweet almonds cut into small thin pieces
- 1 well beaten egg
- a little rose water

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Mix in the sugar, flour, almonds, egg, and rose water
3. Mix all well together
4. With a spoon drop the batter upon wafer paper or tins
5. Bake

title: Almond Cheese Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces of almonds
- 1 spoonful of rose water
- 4 ounces of sugar
- 1 spoonful of cream
- whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound 4 ounces of almonds with a spoonful of rose water
2. add 4 ounces of sugar pounded, a spoonful of cream, the whites of 2 eggs well beaten
3. mix all as quick as possible, and bake it in a warm oven 20 minutes

title: Almond Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart new cream
- 1/2 nutmeg grated
- 1 blade of mace
- a bit of lemon peel
- 1 lb. almonds
- a spoonful of rose or orange flower water
- whites of 9 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the cream with nutmeg grated, a blade of mace, a bit of lemon peel, and sweeten to the taste
2. Blanch 1 lb. of almonds
3. Beat them very fine with a spoonful of rose or orange flower water
4. Take the whites of 9 eggs well beaten and strain them to the almonds
5. Beat them together
6. Rub them very well through a coarse hair sieve
7. Mix all together with the cream and set on the fire
8. Stir it one way all the time till it boils
9. Pour it into cups or dishes, and when cold serve it up

title: Almond Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of almonds
- one spoonful of water
- one pint of cream
- two spoonfuls of rose water
- yolks of four eggs
- sugar to make it pretty sweet

## Directions

1. Blanch and beat four ounces of almonds fine with a spoonful of water
2. Beat a pint of cream with two spoonfuls of rose water
3. Add them to the yolks of four eggs and as much sugar as will make it pretty sweet
4. Stir it over a slow fire till it is of a proper thickness, but do not boil
5. Pour it into custard glasses

title: Almond Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints cream
- 15 eggs
- 1/2 lb. blanched almonds
- sugar
- salt
- mace or rose water

## Directions

1. Mix 3 pints of cream, 15 eggs, 1/2 lb. of blanched almonds, sugar, salt and mace, or rose water, to the taste

title: Almond Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of almonds, blanched and pounded
- a large spoonful of orange flower water or rose water
- 1 pint of new milk
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of crushed sugar
- 1 quart of cream
- 1 lb. of fine sugar
- slices of sponge cake
- apple or quince jelly

## Directions

1. Pound 2 ounces of almonds, blanched, to a paste with a large spoonful of orange flower water or rose water, to prevent oiling
2. Put on a pint of new milk to boil
3. Beat the yolks of 6 eggs with 1 lb. of crushed sugar
4. When the milk boils, stir in the eggs and sugar, and then add the almonds
5. Mix all together, and set it aside to cool
6. Whip 1 quart of cream to a stiff froth, with 1 lb. of fine sugar
7. Drain the froth
8. Lay some slices of sponge cake in a dish
9. Spread apple or quince jelly on it
10. Pour the cold custard over it
11. Then add the whipped cream

title: Almond Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of New Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Blanched Almonds (scalded)
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- Two tablespoonfuls of white powdered sugar
- The Whites of the Eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds after shelling, and chop very fine.
2. Blend the eggs and sugar, beating thoroughly, and add to the boiling milk.
3. Then add the almonds.
4. Stir well, set into cups to cool.
5. Place on top a meringue made of two tablespoonfuls of white powdered sugar and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Set in the oven for a few seconds to brown, and serve cold.

title: Almond Custards
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cream
- 1/2 lb. almonds blanched and pounded fine
- 2 tablespoonfuls rose water
- yolks of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound almonds fine with 2 tablespoonfuls of rose water
2. Sweeten to the taste
3. Beat up the yolks of 4 eggs
4. Strain all the ingredients together over the fire until the mixture is thick
5. Pour it into cups

title: Almond Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- five ounces of sweet almonds
- three ounces of bitter almonds or peach kernels
- a little white of egg
- half a pound of sifted flour
- a pound of sugar
- four yolks of eggs
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound five ounces of sweet, and three ounces of bitter almonds (or peach kernels), with a little white of egg
2. Put half a pound of sifted flour on your dough board, make a hole in the middle of the flour
3. in which put the almonds, with a pound of sugar, four yolks of eggs, and a little salt
4. Make into a paste
5. Cut in pieces the size of a nut
6. lay them half an inch apart, on sheets of paper, in a baking-pan
7. bake in a moderate oven for fifteen or twenty minutes

title: Almond Florendine
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of blanched sweet almonds
- rose water
- 8 eggs
- a gill of cream
- sugar
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Beat 1/2 lb. of blanched sweet almonds with rose water
2. Beat the yolks of 8 eggs and add them, with a gill of cream, and sugar to the taste
3. Lay a sheet of puff paste in a plate or dish
4. Put the mixture on it, cover it with another sheet of paste, and bake 1 1/2 hours in a slow oven

title: Almond Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints of milk
- 8 ounces of cream
- 2 ounces of orange flower water
- 8 ounces of sweet almonds
- 4 ounces of bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Pound all in a marble mortar, pouring in from time to time a few drops of water
2. When thoroughly pounded, add the orange flower water and half the milk
3. Pass this, tightly squeezed, through a cloth
4. Boil the rest of the milk with the cream and keep stirring it with a wooden spoon
5. As soon as it is thick enough, pour in the almond milk
6. Give it 1 boiling, take it off and let it cool in a bowl or pitcher
7. Pour it into the mould for freezing

title: Almond Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Pound of Almonds, blanched and Pounded to a Paste
- The Whites of 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs
2. Stir in the almonds and sugar
3. Proceed to ice according to general directions

title: Almond Macaroons
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of almonds
- a little rose-water
- half a pound of sifted sugar
- the whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound with a little rose-water half a pound of almonds
2. add half a pound of sifted sugar
3. add the whites of two eggs (not beaten), form into a paste
4. Dip your hand in water, and roll the preparation into balls the size of a nutmeg
5. lay them an inch apart, on buttered paper, in a baking tin
6. Bake in a slow oven until a light brown

title: Almond Macaroons (Second Recipe)
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of the best white sugar
- a pound of blanched almonds
- a few drops of rose-water
- the well-beaten whites of six eggs
- a little flour
- white sugar

## Directions

1. To a pound of the best white sugar, sifted, add a pound of blanched almonds
2. put in a few drops of rose-water as you beat them together in a mortar
3. Add to them the well-beaten whites of six eggs
4. form the paste into shapes in the palm of the hand by using a little flour
5. butter some sheets of white paper, and drop the macaroons on it, leaving a space between them
6. Strew a little white sugar on them, and put in the oven to bake a light brown

title: Almond Meringue
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of two eggs
- quarter of a pound of powdered sugar
- quarter of a pound of blanched and cut almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of two eggs with a quarter of a pound of powdered sugar and a quarter of a pound of blanched and cut almonds
2. Form them into rings on letter paper
3. Put the paper on tin and place them in the stove oven to harden and brown lightly

title: Almond Pralines
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Beautiful New Almonds
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- ¼ Glass of Water
- A Pinch of Carmine

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds whole, and then rub them well with a linen cloth, to take off any dust.
2. Put them into a skillet with a pound of the finest white sugar, and a dash of Carmine, if you wish to tinge them to a beautiful rose.
3. Place the skillet on the fire, stirring all the time until the almonds crackle hard.
4. Then take off the fire and work until the sugar becomes sandy and well-detached from the almonds.
5. Separate one part of the sugar, and again put the almonds on the fire, stirring them lightly with a spoon as they again pick up the sugar, paying strict attention to the fire, that it be not too quick.
6. When the almonds have taken up this part of the sugar, put in that which you have reserved, and continue to parch until they have taken up all the sugar.
7. Take a piece of paper and put it in a sieve, and throw the almonds upon it, shaking around so as to separate those which still cling together.
8. Each almond must be separate and incrusted with sugar.

title: Almond Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of almonds
- 1 spoonful of rose water
- 1 spoonful of cream or milk
- 1 large spoonful of pounded biscuit
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 7 eggs
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 lb. of almonds, beat them smooth in a mortar
2. add 1 spoonful of rose water, 1 spoonful of cream or milk thickened with 1 large spoonful of pounded biscuit, 1/2 lb. of sugar, 7 eggs, and 1 nutmeg
3. bake it carefully in a moderate oven

title: Almond Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces of rice, well washed
- 1½ pints of rich milk or cream
- 40 sweet almonds
- 6 bitter almonds
- grated rind of a lemon or a small piece of vanilla bean
- new milk
- 2 ounces of butter
- sugar to taste
- yolks of 8 eggs beaten very light
- 5 whites of eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of sifted sugar per egg white

## Directions

1. Put rice into a milk or rice boiler containing milk or cream and let them boil gently until the rice is swollen and quite soft
2. Blanch almonds and pound them into a smooth paste
3. Grate the rind of a lemon or vanilla bean into a small quantity of new milk and boil until soft
4. Put cooked rice into a dish or pan and when cooled throw into it butter and sugar, mixing well
5. Add the almonds, beating well
6. Add the yolks of 8 eggs beaten very light
7. Butter the dish it is to be baked in
8. Beat the 5 whites of the eggs in a very light whip and add to each a tablespoonful of sifted sugar
9. The moment the oven is ready, quickly lay over the pudding the icing of eggs and bake 15 minutes
10. Serve immediately

title: Almond Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces of crumbs of bread, sliced
- 1 1/2 pints of cream
- 1 lb. of blanched almonds
- 2 teaspoonfuls of orange flower water
- yolks of 8 eggs and whites of 4
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 3 or 4 ounces of melted butter

## Directions

1. Steep 4 ounces of crumbs of bread, sliced, in 1 1/2 pints of cream, or grate the bread
2. Beat 1 lb. of blanched almonds very fine, till they become a paste, with 2 teaspoonfuls of orange flower water
3. Beat up the yolks of 8 eggs and the whites of 4
4. Mix all well together
5. Put in 1 lb. of loaf sugar, and stir in 3 or 4 ounces of melted butter
6. Put it over the fire, stirring it until it is thick
7. Lay a sheet of paper at the bottom of a dish, and pour in the ingredients
8. Bake 1/2 hour

title: Almonds Soufflés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sweet Almonds
- The White of 1 Egg
- 12 Ounces of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch, skin, wash and dry one pound of sweet almonds, and then cut them into very small pieces.
2. Add the white of one egg, beaten to a thick froth, and twelve ounces of powdered sugar.
3. When well-mixed drop upon sheets of white paper, and bake to a very light color in a moderate oven.

title: Ambrosia
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Sweet Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Cup of White Sugar
- 1 Cocoanut

## Directions

1. Grate the cocoanut after paring.
2. Peel and slice the oranges, taking out the seeds.
3. Have a nice large glass dish.
4. Put first a layer of oranges in the dish, sprinkle with sugar, and then a layer of cocoanut, till all the oranges and cocoanut are used, the layer of cocoanut being the last.
5. Sprinkle with powdered white sugar, and let it stand about two hours before serving.

title: Ambrosia of Orange or Pineapple
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cocoanut
- six oranges or one pineapple
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the cocoanut, and slice the oranges or pineapple
2. In a glass dish lay a layer of fruit, and a layer of the grated cocoanut, until your bowl is full
3. Strew powdered sugar over each layer of fruit, and on the top

title: American Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of new milk
- 3 large spoonfuls of rice flour
- 1 cup of milk
- salt
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- rind of 1 lemon
- nutmeg
- 1 wineglass of brandy
- 3 large spoonfuls of good brown sugar
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Put on a pint of new milk to scald
2. While this is heating, stir 3 large spoonfuls of rice flour into a cup of milk
3. When quite smooth, stir it into the boiling milk
4. Add a little salt and a large spoonful of butter, the rind of 1 lemon, a little nutmeg, and 1 wineglass of brandy
5. Beat all well together
6. Add 3 large spoonfuls of good brown sugar
7. Beat 5 eggs very light and stir in
8. Butter a dish and pour it in
9. Bake it as long as custard

title: Anchovies in Oil
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint Bottle of Boned Anchovies
- 1 Hard-Boiled Egg
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Take the contents of one pint bottle of boned Anchovies; drain them of all oil on a cloth and then arrange nicely in a flat glass or china celery or radish dish.
2. Take a hard-boiled egg, hash or slice daintily and decorate the dish with this.
3. Sprinkle over some chopped parsley and serve.

title: Anchovy Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Dry Toast
- 1 Ounce of Anchovy Butter
- 2 Dozen Anchovies

## Directions

1. Prepare the Anchovy Butter by adding to one ounce of good regular butter one teaspoonful of Anchovy Essence and mix well and set on ice till ready to use
2. Prepare six slices of bread, slicing them about one-half an inch thick and toasting to a golden brown
3. Trim the edges nicely and spread over each a little Anchovy Butter
4. Cover each with four Anchovies cut in halves, or pounded to a paste, according to taste
5. Place the toasts on a tin baking sheet in an oven for one minute
6. Arrange neatly on a folded napkin on a dish and serve

title: Anchovy Canapes With Gruyere Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of French Toast
- 2 Ounces of Gruyere Cheese
- 16 Anchovies
- 6 Minced Gherkins
- 1 Ounce of Anchovy Butter
- Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Prepare the French toast
2. Pound the Anchovies to a paste with the Gruyere cheese
3. Line very shallow gem pans with pie paste
4. Bake and set to cool
5. Fill in with the Anchovy preparation
6. Invert this on a circle of nicely buttered Anchovy toast
7. Garnish with the minced gherkins and serve

title: Anchovy Canapes With Hard-Boiled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of French Toast
- 1 Dozen Anchovies
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Anchovy Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare six slices of French toast
2. Spread over each a little Anchovy Butter
3. Spread over this buttered toast the Anchovies and hard-boiled eggs, which have been finely minced and mixed together
4. Place on a folded napkin in a dish and serve

title: Anchovy Canapes, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of French Toast
- 1 Dozen Anchovies
- 1 Ounce of Grated Ham
- 6 Gherkins
- 1/2 a Truffle
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salad Oil
- 1 Teaspoonful of Caper Vinegar
- 1/2 Ounce of Aspic Jelly

## Directions

1. Prepare the French toasts and trim the edges neatly
2. Chop the Anchovies very fine and mix with the ounce of grated boiled ham, and the truffle and gherkins, all minced very fine
3. Moisten this with the salad oil and vinegar, which have been well mixed
4. Spread over the toast and garnish nicely with the aspic jelly
5. Place on a folded napkin and serve

title: Anchovy Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- crisp lettuce
- chopped watercress
- chopped whites of eggs
- sour French Dressing

## Directions

1. Have an oval dish and arrange the anchovies drained of oil crosswise on a bed of crisp lettuce or of chopped watercress
2. Surround with a border of chopped whites of eggs and a similar border of chopped watercresses
3. Pour over all a sour French Dressing

title: Anchovy Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box of Anchovies
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Cut the sardines into pieces of about an inch in length
2. Season nicely with a French Dressing and serve

title: Anchovy Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Anchovy Butter
- 1.5 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- 1.5 Tablespoonfuls Butter

## Directions

1. Make a White Sauce
2. Add to this a tablespoonful of Anchovy butter
3. Let it melt and season to taste in the sauce
4. Serve with boiled fish

title: Andouilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Fat Pork
- 2 Pounds of Lean Pork
- 1 Pound of Inner Lining of Stomach of Hog
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 2 Large Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Salt and Pepper
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cayenne
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chili Pepper
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Mace, Cloves and Allspice, ground fine
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Minced Thyme, Sweet Marjoram and Parsley

## Directions

1. Select the largest intestines of the hog, wash clean, disgorge and thoroughly cleanse
2. Let soak for twenty four hours in fresh water, changing the water frequently
3. Drain and dry well
4. Cut them into threadlike pieces of about one inch in length
5. Hash the pork, lean and fat, together
6. Mix thoroughly with the threads of intestines or inner stomach of the hog
7. Season highly with the salt, pepper and Cayenne and Chili pepper
8. Mince the onion and garlic and herbs as fine as possible and add to the meat
9. Add the ground spices and mix and hash all together very fine
10. Take six or eight of the large intestines that have been thoroughly soaked and disgorged
11. Fill these casings with the preparation, after scalding and drying the casings thoroughly
12. Tie into the desired lengths
13. Boil in an aromatic water with an herb bouquet, or in milk
14. Broil, or bake in the oven for eight or ten minutes

title: Angel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Granulated Sugar, Sifted
- 1 Cup Sifted Flour
- The Whites of 11 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cream of Tartar
- 1 Teaspoonful Vanilla
- ½ Teaspoonful Salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour four times, and then put in the cream of tartar, mix well, and sift a fifth time.
2. Sift the sugar four times.
3. Have the whites of the eggs beaten to a very stiff froth, so stiff that they may be cut with a knife clearly.
4. Then add the flour by degrees, beating all the time.
5. Lastly, add the flavoring extract.
6. Do not butter the pan for this cake, but turn the mixture quickly into an ungreased Turk's head pan (a pan with a tube in the middle).
7. Bake for three-quarters of an hour in a moderate oven.
8. Test with a broom wisp.
9. When done, turn the cake upside down, resting on the tube of the pan, and let it cool.
10. The cake will fall out of the pan.

title: Angel's Food
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 11 eggs
- 1.5 tumblers sifted powdered sugar
- 1 tumbler sifted flour
- 1 teaspoonful cream of tartar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Sift the flour four times, leaving the tumbler even full
2. Then add the cream of tartar, and sift again
3. Beat the eggs to a stiff froth on a dish, and on same dish add sugar lightly, then flour gently, then vanilla
4. Do not stop beating until you put it in the oven to bake
5. Bake 40 minutes in a moderately heated oven, and if too soft let it remain a few minutes longer
6. Do not open the oven until it has been in 15 minutes
7. Turn the pan upside down to cool, and when cold take out by loosening cake with a knife
8. Then ice

title: Angel's Food
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box gelatine
- 1 quart of milk
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 cup of white sugar
- 1 cup sherry wine
- 1 teaspoon of vanilla
- whites of 3 eggs
- 2 1/2 cups of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak 1 box gelatine in 1 quart of milk 1 hour
2. Let it come to a boil
3. Beat together yolks of 3 eggs, 1 cup of white sugar, 1 cup sherry wine, 1 teaspoon of vanilla
4. Add to milk and gelatine
5. Let it boil
6. Add whites of the eggs beaten with 2 1/2 cups of sugar
7. Let it simmer

title: Angel's Food
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Sifted, Powdered White Sugar
- 1 Cup Sifted Flour
- Whites of 11 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful Vanilla
- ½ Teaspoonful Salt

## Directions

1. Put the baking powder and the salt (or use cream of tartar instead of baking powder) into the flour, and sift it seven times.
2. If you use cream of tartar, add it in the last sifting.
3. Sift the sugar seven times.
4. Beat the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth, and add gradually the sugar, mixing very carefully.
5. Then add the flour, gradually beating all the while, and lastly add the flavoring extract.
6. Pour the mixture into a Turk's head baking pan (ungreased).
7. Bake for forty-five minutes in a moderate oven.
8. Test with the broom wisp.
9. When done, turn the cake upside down, resting the pan on the tube till the cake falls off.

title: Angel's Repast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- white sugar
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Stew nice apples
2. Strain them
3. Sweeten with white sugar
4. Mix 4 whites of eggs
5. Add the stewed apples and eggs beaten to a stiff froth
6. Make a rich boiled custard
7. Pour it into a glass dish and drop on the custard the beaten apples and eggs

title: Angelica Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Angelica Stalk or Leaves
- 1 Ounce of Angelica Seed
- 1 Drachm of Nutmeg
- ⅜ Drachm of Ceylon Cinnamon
- 1 Drachm of Coriander Seed
- ½ Drachm of Cloves
- 4 and a quarter Pounds of sugar
- 2 and a half Pints of water

## Directions

1. Bruise the seeds and leaves well by pounding, and steep for two months in an earthen pitcher, well-covered, having added all the spices.
2. Take out and strain and add a syrup made of four and a quarter pounds of sugar and two and a half pints of water.
3. Filter and bottle, and keep in a cool place.

title: Anise Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Sugar
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Quart of Flour
- 0.25 Teaspoonful of Essence of Anise

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and eggs for about half an hour
2. Add the flour gradually, and the essence
3. Beat well
4. Have ready buttered sheets of tin, and drop the mixture from the spoon, and bake to a delicate brown

title: Aniseed Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Aniseed
- 2 Pints of Good French Cognac
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1½ Pounds of Sugar

## Directions

1. Bruise the aniseed and steep them for one month in the brandy.
2. Pass them through a sieve.
3. Add a syrup made of one pint of water and one pound and a half of sugar.
4. Filter and bottle.

title: Anisette
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 drops of oil of aniseed
- 3 pints of alcohol
- 2 pounds of the finest white clarified loaf sugar
- 1½ pints of water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup with the water and sugar, and clarify.
2. When cold, add the aniseed oil, which has been well-dissolved in the alcohol.
3. Mix all well, filter and bottle tightly.

title: Anisette de Bordeaux
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Green Aniseed
- 4 Ounces of Star Aniseed
- 1 Ounce of Coriander
- 2 Ounces of Hyson Tea
- 1 Ounce of Fennel
- 3½ Gallons of Alcohol
- 10 Pounds of Sugar
- 7 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Take the aniseed, coriander, fennel, and Hyson tea, and pound well.
2. Macerate or steep for fifteen days in three and a half gallons of the finest rectified alcohol.
3. Distill in a "bain-marie," or water bath, according to directions already given.
4. Make a syrup with the ten pounds of sugar and seven pints of water.
5. Mix well with the aniseed liquor and filter.
6. Bottle and keep in a cool, shady place.

title: Another Christmas Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of raisins
- One pound of currants
- One pound of suet
- Three-quarters of a pound of bread-crumbs
- One pint of milk
- Ten eggs
- Three-quarters of a pound of citron and orange-peel mixed
- One small nutmeg
- One glass of brandy
- Flour

## Directions

1. Stone the raisins and divide them
2. Wash and dry the currants
3. Cut the peel into slices
4. Mix all these with the bread-crumbs, flour and suet chopped very fine
5. Add the grated nutmeg
6. Stir in the eggs well-beaten, the brandy, and the milk
7. When the ingredients are well blended, put it into a mould
8. Tie a floured cloth over it
9. Boil it six hours
10. When done turn it out
11. Serve with brandy and arrowroot sauce

title: Another Cream Cake with Crust and Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarter pint of water
- half a pint of butter
- three-quarters of a pound of flour
- eight eggs
- a teaspoonful of dry soda
- two pints of milk
- one cup of flour
- two cups of sugar
- four eggs
- rose water

## Directions

1. For crust: boil the water and butter together
2. while boiling stir in the flour
3. take it off and let it cool
4. then add your eggs (beaten separately), and a teaspoonful of dry soda
5. Use about a spoonful of the crust for each puff
6. bake on tins for about twenty minutes
7. For cream: while the milk is boiling add your flour, sugar and eggs (previously well beaten together)
8. let it cook until it begins to thicken
9. take it off, and flavor with rose water
10. When crust is done cut it open and put in the cream

title: Another Cream Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Three cups of sugar
- half a cup of vinegar
- one-third of a cup of water
- butter
- flavoring essence

## Directions

1. Boil sugar, vinegar, and water together until it is thick and will harden when dropped into a cup of water
2. Butter some dishes
3. Just before filling them, add to the candy some flavoring essence
4. Pour the candy on the buttered dishes
5. When a little cool prepare to pull it until it is white and light

title: Another Cream Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of sugar
- half a cup of vinegar
- one-third of a cup of water
- flavoring essence

## Directions

1. Boil three cups of sugar, half a cup of vinegar, and one-third of a cup of water together until it is thick and will harden when dropped into a cup of water
2. Butter some dishes
3. Just before filling them, add to the candy some flavoring essence
4. Pour the candy on the buttered dishes
5. When a little cool prepare to pull it until it is white and light

title: Another Ice Cream Without Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk
- sugar
- yolks of eight eggs
- vanilla
- lemon or almond

## Directions

1. Add sugar to a quart of milk until it is very sweet
2. Let this boil on the fire
3. When it boils gently, take it off and pour it scalding hot to the beaten yolks of eight eggs
4. Stir it constantly, but never boil it as the scalding milk will cook the eggs sufficiently
5. It should also be stirred while cooking
6. Flavor with vanilla, or lemon or almond
7. If with a vanilla bean it is better to boil it in the milk before putting in the sugar
8. When the custard is cold, put it in the form or freezer
9. If you have no freezer you can make one, by using a tin kettle with a tight cover, set this in the centre of a tub that is large enough to leave a space of four or five inches around it
10. Fill the space with layers of cracked ice and coarse salt, a layer of ice last
11. Cover the whole with a woolen cover for half an hour
12. Then shake the kettle constantly, after that, until frozen
13. Cover up till wanted

title: Another Oyster Soup. Very Strengthening
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal or a piece of lamb
- a quart of water and a teaspoonful of salt to each pound
- half a pound of rolled crackers
- a quart of nice oysters
- a large tablespoonful of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- macaroni or vermicelli (if preferred)

## Directions

1. Allow a quart of water and a teaspoonful of salt to each pound
2. Set it over the fire, let it come to a boil, skim it well and then set it back on the stove
3. Let it simmer for two hours
4. This will form a fine, strong, nourishing stock for the soup
5. Take out the meat, and skim the stock clear
6. Put in half a pound of rolled crackers and a quart of nice oysters
7. Let it boil up, and finish by putting in a large tablespoonful of butter, and pepper and salt to taste
8. Macaroni or vermicelli can be substituted for the crackers, if preferred

title: Another Potato Yeast Without Hops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- flour
- yeast cake or good yeast

## Directions

1. Boil and mash sufficient potatoes to fill a pint cup
2. Add to them a pint of water and boil them together
3. Stir in flour enough to form a thick batter
4. When cool, add a yeast cake or a cup of good yeast
5. Bottle and put away in a cool place

title: Another Potato Yeast Without Hops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sufficient potatoes to fill a pint cup
- a pint of water
- flour
- a yeast cake, or a cup of good yeast

## Directions

1. Boil and mash sufficient potatoes to fill a pint cup
2. add to them a pint of water, boil them together
3. stir in flour enough to form a thick batter
4. when cool, add a yeast cake, or a cup of good yeast
5. Bottle and put away in a cool place

title: Another Quickly-Made Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a light, square loaf of bread
- raisins or currants
- rich sauce

## Directions

1. Get a light, square loaf of bread, split it in three or four horizontal slices
2. Strew in between the slices cut-up raisins or currants
3. Tie it up again
4. Boil half an hour
5. Serve it with a rich sauce

title: Another Recipe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cupfuls of butter
- two cupfuls of molasses
- one cupful of sugar
- two tablespoonfuls of ginger
- four tablespoonfuls of cream
- one teaspoonful of soda
- one-half an ounce of cinnamon
- about one and a half pounds of flour

## Directions

1. Roll
2. cut
3. bake in a moderate oven

title: Another Tomato Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- berries or small tomatoes
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Bruise your berries, or small tomatoes
2. Measure the juice, and add two pounds of sugar to each gallon
3. Put it in a cask, adding two gallons of water to each four gallons of juice
4. Let it ferment like blackberry wine

title: Another Watermelon Rind Preserve
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon rind
- lime
- water
- alum
- grape leaves
- ginger
- sugar

## Directions

1. In a bucket of cold water, put a handful of lime, stir it in, and when it settles clear, pour it over the watermelon rind you intend preserving.
2. Let it stay in the weak lime-water one day.
3. Soak it a few hours, and get the taste of the lime from the rind.
4. Put it in alum water and scald for ten minutes.
5. Put grape-leaves in with the alum water while scalding; they will make the rind green.
6. Take the rind from the alum, and put it in cold water for a few hours.
7. When cold, boil it in strong ginger tea until it is soft, and tastes of the ginger.
8. Make the syrup of one and a half pounds of sugar to each pound of rind, and a half pint of water to each pound of sugar.
9. Let it cook slowly.

title: Another Way - Spoon Cocktail
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One lump of sugar
- two dashes Angostura bitters
- one piece of lemon peel
- one lump of ice

## Directions

1. Serve plain in small bar glass with spoon

title: Another Way to Cook Egg Plant
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- boiling lard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Parboil egg plant
2. Slice them
3. Drop them into boiling lard without rolling them in anything
4. Season with salt and pepper

title: Another Way to Make Vinegar; No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of water
- two pounds of coarse brown sugar
- one slice of raised wheat-bread
- yeast

## Directions

1. To a gallon of water put two pounds of coarse brown sugar
2. Boil and skim it for half an hour
3. Put it in a tub or jar to ferment
4. Add to it in the tub a slice of raised wheat-bread soaked in yeast
5. It can be bottled off or put in a cask in a week or two, but must be left unstopped, and the bung covered with muslin to keep out insects

title: Another Way to Preserve Peaches
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six pounds of peaches
- six pounds of fine white sugar
- three pints of water

## Directions

1. Peel, cut and weigh six pounds of peaches
2. take six pounds of fine white sugar, throw the sugar on the peaches until they are well covered
3. let them stay all night
4. Early in the morning add three pints of water
5. boil all together for one hour
6. Skim carefully
7. take the peaches out on a large dish, still keeping the syrup gently boiling, and skimming it as it boils
8. Lay the peaches in the sun on dishes for at least two hours, to harden
9. Taking the fruit out of the syrup a few times improves it, giving it firmness and transparency
10. replace the peaches in the syrup
11. boil gently until they are clear

title: Appetizer
body:
## Ingredients

- A dash of absinthe
- 1 teaspoonful of vermouth
- 1 dash of pepsin
- 3 tablespoonfuls of seltzer water
- Finely crushed ice

## Directions

1. Fill a glass with finely-crushed ice, and add the above ingredients.
2. Shake well together, pour into a small, delicately thin tumbler, and serve cold.

title: Apple Batter Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Six eggs
- 8 tablespoonfuls of sifted flour
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 saltspoon of salt
- Pippins, pared, cored and sweetened
- Yolk of 1 egg
- Butter
- Sugar
- A little wine

## Directions

1. Stir the flour gradually into the milk, carefully dissolving all the lumps
2. Beat the eggs very light and add them by degrees to the milk and flour
3. Pour the batter over a dish of pippins, pared, cored and sweetened, mixing all well together
4. Bake it
5. Eat it with a rich sauce, made of the yolk of 1 egg, butter, sugar and a little wine

title: Apple Charlotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Large Apples
- 6 Apricots
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Sugar, or Sugar to Taste
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- ground cinnamon
- allspice to taste
- a quarter of a grated nutmeg
- 6 slices of bread

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and then cut them into slices, taking out the seeds and core.
2. Put them in a saucepan with three large tablespoonfuls of butter.
3. Add a little water to stew gently, and add the apricots.
4. Use four tablespoonfuls of butter and ground cinnamon, and allspice to taste.
5. Let all cook to a nice marmalade.
6. Take off and add a quarter of a grated nutmeg.
7. Cut six slices of bread, and butter well.
8. Butter a pan and garnish it around the bottom and around with the slices of bread, which must be very thin.
9. Fill with the apples, and cover on top with the bread.
10. Dot with butter and sprinkle with sugar, and put in an oven and bake to a golden brown.
11. When done, carefully loosen the edges and turn into a dish.
12. Serve it hot, with sugar and cream, or with a Custard Sauce, flavored with good brandy.

title: Apple Compote
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- one cup of water
- six nice sour apples

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of three-quarters of a pound of sugar and a cup of water and let it boil
2. While you are paring and taking out the cores of six nice sour apples, keep the syrup boiling
3. Throw the apples into the syrup and let them boil for half an hour, or until transparent
4. Pour into a glass or china dish and serve for a lunch or tea
5. They are nice when served warm

title: Apple Cornmeal Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pippin apples
- 1 quart of new milk
- 1 quart of sifted cornmeal
- a little salt
- 4 spoonfuls of chopped suet
- a teacup of good molasses

## Directions

1. Pare and core 12 pippin apples
2. Slice them very thin
3. Stir into 1 quart of new milk, 1 quart of sifted cornmeal
4. Add a little salt, then the apples, 4 spoonfuls of chopped suet and a teacup of good molasses
5. Mix these well together
6. Pour into a buttered dish, and bake 4 hours
7. Serve hot, with sugar and wine sauce

title: Apple Custard. A Nice Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen apples
- a large cupful of brown sugar
- a teacupful of water
- the grated rind of a lemon
- one pint of milk
- four eggs
- two ounces of loaf sugar
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel, cut and core the apples
2. Put them in a sauce-pan with the water
3. As they heat, add the brown sugar and lemon-peel
4. When mashed and well cooked, take it off
5. Put the fruit in the bottom of a deep dish, and pour a custard of the milk, sugar and eggs, over it
6. Grate over it before baking, a little nutmeg
7. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Apple Float
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve large apples
- water
- 2 whites of eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Quarter and core twelve large apples
2. Boil them in as little water as possible
3. Pass through a sieve
4. When cold, add 2 whites of eggs beaten
5. Sweeten to the taste
6. Beat all up with a spoon till quite stiff
7. Have made previously a soft custard, with the 2 yolks
8. Flavor and sweeten slightly

title: Apple Float
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One dozen large green apples
- water
- whites of 2 eggs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil one dozen large green apples in as little water as possible
2. Pass them through a fine hair sieve
3. When cold, sweeten to the taste
4. Add the whites of 2 eggs well beaten
5. Beat the whole with a spoon until it is quite stiff
6. When ready for table, grate nutmeg over it
7. Serve with cream

title: Apple Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fresh Apples
- 1 Gill of Brandy or Rum (if desired)
- Grated Peel of 1/2 Lemon
- Powdered Sugar
- Fritter Batter a la Creole

## Directions

1. Peel and core the apples, which will be all the nicer if they are a little tart.
2. Cut them into slices, more or less thick or thin, according to taste.
3. Soak them in brandy or good whisky, or rum, for the space of two hours, sprinkling with the grated zest of a lemon and sugar, according to judgment.
4. Make a batter a la Creole and have ready a deep saucepan of boiling lard.
5. Drain the apples.
6. Dip the slices, one at a time, into the batter, lift out with a large kitchen spoon, drop into the boiling lard, and fry to a golden brown.
7. Lift out with a skimmer, and set on brown paper, in the mouth of the oven, and drain.
8. Sift powdered white sugar over them, and serve hot, piling high in pyramidal shape, and sprinkling again with powdered white sugar.

title: Apple Fritters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 3 eggs
- wheat flour
- whites of 3 eggs
- apples
- brandy
- sugar
- lard

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of 3 eggs with wheat flour to a batter
2. Beat whites separately and add to it
3. Pare apples, core, and cut them in slices
4. Lay them in a bowl in brandy and sugar about 3 hours before dressing
5. Dip each piece in batter and fry in lard
6. Sprinkle white sugar over them

title: Apple Island
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 6 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of nicely stewed, seasoned apples
- sugar
- rose water or lemon or nutmeg
- milk

## Directions

1. To the whites of 6 eggs put 4 tablespoonfuls of nicely stewed, seasoned apples, rich with sugar, seasoned with rose water, lemon, or nutmeg
2. Beat till quite light
3. Season with milk, which is prepared like boiled custard, with the apple
4. Pour it in a dish, and put the island on it

title: Apple Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Pippins or other tart apples
- Water
- Grated rind of a lemon
- 1 pound of sugar to every pint of juice

## Directions

1. Pare apples and cut into quarters
2. Put into a preserving kettle and cover with water
3. Add the grated rind of a lemon
4. Let them boil to a marmalade
5. Strain the juice without squeezing through a clean jelly bag made of flannel
6. For every pint of juice add one pound of fine sugar
7. If the apples are not very tart, add the juice also

title: Apple Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of ripe apples, pared and cut thin
- lemon peel
- 1/4 lb. of white sugar

## Directions

1. Stew apples until quite soft with lemon peel
2. Add 1/4 lb. of white sugar
3. Stew it 1/4 hour

title: Apple Jelly, Without Water
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Sugar
- White of an egg

## Directions

1. Pare and core the fruit, which should be juicy and tart
2. Lay the apples in a vessel to cook without putting in any water
3. Cover them closely, and cook until properly soft
4. Strain the juice, and add three-fourths of a pound of sugar to a pint of apple juice
5. Beat in the white of an egg to clarify the jelly, and skim it as it boils
6. Try it and, as soon as it jellies, take it from the fire and put it in glasses

title: Apple Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3½ Pounds of Fruit
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- Grated rinds of several lemons

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and cut them into quarters, extracting all the seeds.
2. Then throw them into fresh water, sufficient to half cover, and set them on the fire.
3. When they begin to grow soft, mash them with a wooden spoon.
4. Then add the sugar and the grated rinds of several lemons; mix well.
5. Set all back upon the fire.
6. Stir the mixture while boiling until it is of the proper consistency.
7. Pour it into jars or glasses.

title: Apple Marmalade Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Pie Paste
- 3 Ounces of Apple Marmalade
- 3 Apples
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Prepare the Pie Paste and line six tart molds with it
2. Peel and core the apples and cut into quarters, and put into a saucepan with a pint of cold water
3. Let them cook on a hot stove for ten minutes
4. Remove and drain, and let cool for half an hour
5. Cut the quarters into three slices each
6. Line the bottom of the molds with the marmalade divided evenly
