7. Arrange the sliced apples nicely over the marmalade, and dredge nicely with two ounces of powdered sugar
8. Set in a baking dish and bake for twenty minutes, or a half hour if necessary, in a moderate oven
9. Take out and spread nicely with apple marmalade and serve

title: Apple Meringue
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- handsome pippin apples
- water
- jelly
- whites of eggs
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Select handsome pippin apples if you can get them, pare and core them whole
2. Put them in the oven with a little water in a deep dish, and let them cook a little but not enough to break
3. When plumped, take them out and let them get cold
4. Fill the centre of each apple with jelly
5. Make an icing of the whites of eggs, beaten with sifted sugar
6. Carefully cover each apple with it, wetting the knife while smoothing the icing
7. Sift a little sugar over them and put them in the oven to harden, but not to brown

title: Apple Meringue
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- apple compote
- whites of 8 eggs
- 1 teacup sugar
- white of eggs

## Directions

1. Fill a dish with apple compote
2. Whip the whites of 8 eggs to a stiff froth
3. Sprinkle in the eggs 1 teacup of sugar, working them well together
4. Spread this over the apple
5. Beat some white of eggs without sugar, and pile it high in pyramidical form, and brown it of a light fawn color

title: Apple Meringue Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Apples
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sweet Spices, Blended Equally
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla or Lemon Essence

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and stew ripe, juicy apples, and mash them through a sieve
2. Season with a little grated nutmeg
3. Fill the pie plates with the crust, bake and fill with the stewed apples
4. Let them bake for about twenty minutes
5. Spread over the top a thick meringue, made by beating the whites of three eggs with three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, or allow the white of one egg and one tablespoonful of sugar
6. Flavor with vanilla or lemon, and continue beating till it will stand alone
7. Cover the pies with this meringue three-quarters of an inch or one-quarter of an inch thick, according to quantity used
8. Set in the stove and let brown, and eat cold

title: Apple Meringue Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Apples
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of White Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Each of Nutmeg, Mace, Allspice and Cinnamon, Grated
- 1 Spoonful of Vanilla Essence
- Eggs
- White Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Stew the apples according to recipe, and while the apples are boiling stir in the butter and the spices.
2. Then take from the fire, and add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
3. Beat all very light, and put into a buttered dish, and bake for fifteen minutes in the oven.
4. Then cover with a meringue made with the whites of the eggs, the essence, and the white powdered sugar, beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Bake to a light brown, and serve cold with fresh milk or cream.

title: Apple Pie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of apples, well boiled and pounded
- ½ lb. of butter beaten to a cream
- 6 eggs with whites well beaten
- ½ lb. of powdered sugar
- peel of 2 lemons, well boiled and pounded
- thin crust

## Directions

1. To boiled and pounded apples add butter beaten to a cream while warm
2. Add eggs with whites well beaten
3. Add powdered sugar and lemon peel
4. Put thin crust at the bottom and round the sides of the dish
5. Bake 1 hour

title: Apple Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Apples (tart)
- 1 Cup Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful Sweet Spices, Blended Equally
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter

## Directions

1. Pare and slice the apples nicely.
2. Then put them in a porcelain-lined saucepan, and let them stew gently, adding the water and the butter.
3. Season well with a little ground cinnamon, mace and allspice.
4. When done, take off and allow to cool a little.
5. Make a flaky pie crust (see recipe), and line the tin plates, after greasing slightly with butter, and bake the bottom crust slightly brown.
6. Take out and fill in with the apples, and then cover the top with a thin layer of crust.
7. Decorate the edges, and set in the oven to bake.
8. When a nice, delicate brown, take out and let them cool.
9. Remove from the tin plate by slightly loosening the outer edge with a knife.
10. Turn over into a china plate, sprinkle with white powdered sugar, and serve cold or hot.

title: Apple Pork
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of pork
- salt
- pepper
- stewed apples
- nutmeg
- sugar
- rind of a lemon
- 2 dozen apples
- lemon juice
- water

## Directions

1. Rub the fillet of pork with a little salt and pepper
2. Score the outside skin
3. Take out the bone and fill it with nice stewed apples, free of paring and cores, nicely seasoned with nutmeg and sugar, with bits of the rind of a lemon
4. Have ready 2 dozen apples, pared, cored and cut in slices or quarters, sweetened well with sugar, and flavored with lemon
5. Add a little of the lemon juice for improvement
6. Put the pork into a large pot or iron bake oven
7. Fill up with the cut apples the space all around, adding just sufficient water to keep it from burning
8. Stew or bake it for 3 hours
9. When done, serve it on a clean dish and send it very hot to the table

title: Apple Pot Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of flour
- a heaping spoonful of lard
- a little salt
- cold water
- one dozen apples
- one large cup of sugar
- one tablespoonful of spices
- two of molasses
- one of butter
- water
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Rub into a pint of flour a heaping spoonful of lard
2. Strew in a little salt, and work it until the mass becomes numberless little globules and balls
3. Moisten with cold water, and press them together until they adhere
4. Pare, core and quarter one dozen apples
5. Put them in a baking pan, with one large cup of sugar, one tablespoonful of spices, two of molasses and one of butter
6. add water until the fruit is nearly covered
7. put it in the oven to bake and stew, and brown
8. When the apples begin to soften, dredge in a little flour, for the juice, though plentiful, must not be watery
9. Roll out the pastry
10. Cut the cover to suit the pan, and make the trimmings into dumplings, which must be dropped at intervals among the fruit
11. Fold the pie cover in half, make several oblique incisions for openings, lay it on and brown it lightly
12. Serve on a dish like peach cobbler

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Apples
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of White Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Each of Nutmeg, Mace, Allspice and Cinnamon, Grated
- 1 Spoonful of Vanilla Essence
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Stew the apples according to recipe, and while the apples are boiling stir in the butter and the spices.
2. Then take from the fire, and add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
3. Beat all very light.
4. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and beat well into the pudding.
5. Put into a buttered dish, and bake for fifteen minutes in the oven.
6. When nicely browned, grate nutmeg on top, and serve cold, with cream or milk.

title: Apple Sago
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sago
- 8 Large Apples
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- Sweetened Cream

## Directions

1. Soak the Sago for half an hour in cold water, and then put it in a farina boiler and add one quart of boiling water.
2. Pare the apples, and add to the Sago, and let all boil together till very tender.
3. Sweeten to taste.
4. Then bake in a moderate oven for twenty minutes, and serve with cream.

title: Apple Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Large Apples
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 4 Cloves
- 1 Stick Cinnamon
- 1 Cup Water
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the apples into pieces and peel
2. Let them boil till mashed into a jelly, stirring frequently to prevent burning
3. Add the ground cloves and the stick of cinnamon, ground fine
4. Let them boil at least three-quarters of an hour, mashing as they become tender
5. Take off the fire and press them through a coarse sieve
6. Add sugar to taste and add the butter
7. Set all back on the fire and let it simmer gently for five minutes longer
8. Set to cool in a dish and serve with Roast Pork or Roast Goose

title: Apple Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Apples
- 1 Pint of Cold Water
- 1/4 of a Lemon
- 1/2 Pound of Powdered White Sugar
- A Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- 1 Gill of Brandy

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter and seed the apples, and put them into a saucepan, with a pint of cold water and a quarter of a lemon.
2. Let them boil for half an hour.
3. Then press the cooked apples through a sieve into a bowl, and add the powdered sugar and the cinnamon and brandy.
4. Set back on the stove and let the juice boil for five minutes, and serve with apple puddings, baked apples, dumplings, etc.

title: Apple Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- rich, tart apples
- a very little water

## Directions

1. Peel, quarter, and core some rich, tart apples
2. Add a very little water, cover and set them over the fire
3. When tender, mash them smooth, and serve with roasted pork, goose, or any other gross meat

title: Apple Sauce for Roasted Goose
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fine apples
- water
- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and slice some fine apples
2. Put them into a saucepan with enough water to keep them from burning
3. Stew them till quite soft and tender
4. Mash them to a paste
5. Make them very sweet, adding butter and nutmeg
6. Be careful not to have the sauce thin and watery

title: Apple Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 1/2 Pints of Apple Juice
- 1 Quart of Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- about a dozen apples

## Directions

1. Pare, core and quarter the apples, and cook till very tender
2. Put the sugar and water to boil
3. Add the apples, and when very tender strain all through a sieve
4. Add the juice of the lemon and set to freeze as in Ice Cream
5. Serve as needed

title: Apple Snow
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 or 9 good sized apples
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 quart of milk
- 4 whites of eggs
- 3 large spoonfuls of pulverized sugar
- colored nonpareils

## Directions

1. Select 8 or 9 good sized apples
2. Core them
3. Put them into a pan and cover them with cold water
4. Whilst they are cooking slowly, make a nice boiled custard of the yolks of 4 eggs and 1 quart of milk
5. Set it aside to cool
6. When the apples are quite soft, lay them carefully on a sieve and remove the skin
7. Put the pulp into a bowl and whip to a stiff froth 4 whites of eggs and 3 large spoonfuls of pulverized sugar
8. Beat the apple pulp to a stiff froth and add to it the whipped eggs
9. Beat them until they resemble snow
10. Pour the cold custard into a glass bowl and heap on the whipped snow
11. Finish by sprinkling on colored nonpareils

title: Apple Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Apples
- 1 Cup Fine White Sugar
- The Whites of Six Fresh Creole Eggs
- The Juice of One Lemon
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pare and core and cut up the apples, and steam them in two tablespoonfuls of water, with a little grated lemon peel, till quite soft.
2. Then press them through a sieve, and add the sugar and the lemon juice.
3. Beat the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth, and add two-thirds to the apples, stirring all the time.
4. When the eggs and apples are very light beat this with a little powdered white sugar to form a meringue.
5. Place the apples in custard glasses, heap on the meringue, and serve immediately.

title: Apple Sponge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Apples
- 1 Pound Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 1/4 Box of Gelatine
- The Grated Zest of a Lemon
- 1/2 Pint of Boiling Water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water, and when very clear skim the surface.
2. Have the apples pared and cored and sliced nicely, and place them in the syrup.
3. Let them stew until very tender.
4. Then add the gelatine, which has been soaked in cold water, to the mixture.
5. When well done press all through a sieve.
6. Then add the rind and juice of the lemons, using only the zest of the rind in grating the lemon.
7. Stir until it begins to cool and thicken.
8. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, stirring and beating until cool and thick.
9. Turn the Apple Sponge in a dish and serve with Vanilla Sauce poured over it.

title: Apple Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Pie Paste
- 3 Ounces of Apple Marmalade
- 3 Apples
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Prepare the Pie Paste (see recipe), and line six tart molds with it.
2. Peel and core the apples and cut into quarters, and put into a saucepan with a pint of cold water.
3. Let them cook on a hot stove for ten minutes.
4. Then remove and drain, and let cool for half an hour.
5. Then cut the quarters into three slices each; line the bottom of the molds with the marmalade divided evenly; arrange the sliced apples nicely over the marmalade, and dredge nicely with two ounces of powdered sugar.
6. Set in a baking dish and bake for twenty minutes, or a half hour if necessary.

title: Apple Tea, or Water, for Invalids
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe apples
- peel of a fresh lemon
- boiling water
- loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Cut some ripe apples into thin pieces
2. add the peel of a fresh lemon
3. pour boiling water over them
4. let it stand till cold
5. then sweeten with loaf sugar

title: Apple Trifle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- rind of 1/2 a lemon grated
- sugar
- 1 pint milk
- 1 pint sweet cream
- yolk of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Scald as many apples as will make, when pulped, a thick layer at the bottom of a dish
2. Mix the rind of 1/2 a lemon, grated fine, and as much sugar as will sweeten to the taste
3. Mix 1 pint of milk, the same of sweet cream, and the yolk of 1 egg
4. Scald it over the fire, and stir it all the time
5. Do not let it boil
6. Add a little sugar, if required, and let it stand to cool
7. Lay over it the apples with a spoon, and then put on a whip made the day before

title: Apple Trifle—A Supper Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tart apples
- sugar
- lemon
- two eggs
- half a pint of milk
- half a cup of sugar
- one pint of rich cream
- strips of citron
- apple jelly

## Directions

1. Make a marmalade by stewing tart apples in sugar, seasoned with lemon
2. Lay it when cold in a deep glass dish
3. Pour over it a boiled custard made of two eggs, half a pint of milk, sweetened with half a cup of sugar
4. Whip a pint of rich cream to a froth
5. Pile it high on the custard
6. Ornament with strips of citron and apple jelly laid on the whipped cream

title: Apricot and Peach Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots or peaches
- Water
- Loaf sugar
- Peach kernels (optional)

## Directions

1. Mash the apricots or peaches in a mortar, remove the stones
2. To eight pounds of the pulp, add one quart of water
3. Let this stand twenty-four hours
4. Strain, and to each gallon of the juice, add two pounds of loaf sugar
5. Let it ferment, and when perfectly clear, bottle it
6. A few of the kernels may be added for flavoring, if wished

title: Apricot Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh Apricots or 1 Pint Can
- 1/2 Box of Gelatine, or 1/2 Pint
- 1 Pint of Cold Water
- 1 Pint of Cream

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine and whip the cream
2. If the apricots are fresh, stew them and sweeten to taste
3. If canned, press them through a colander or sieve
4. Add the gelatine, which has been dissolved in a little boiling water, and then turn into a bowl
5. Stand upon cracked ice, and stir constantly till it begins to thicken, and then add the whipped cream stirring constantly till mixed well
6. Turn into a mold to harden and serve with whipped cream

title: Apricot Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Apricots
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1/4 Pound of Apple Jelly

## Directions

1. Cut the apricots in two, and peel
2. Rinse them in cold water
3. Cook them in syrup made of sugar and a little water
4. Add a little Apple Jelly to the sugar after you have taken out the apricots
5. Mix the jelly well with the syrup, pour over the apricots and serve

title: Apricot Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh or a Half Can of Apricots
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- Grated Lemon Peel
- Fruit Fritter Batter a la Creole

## Directions

1. If the apricots are fresh, peel and stone them, and cut into halves.
2. Sprinkle them with the grated zest of a lemon and sugar, and pour over them sufficient Madeira Wine to thoroughly saturate.
3. Cover and set aside for two hours.
4. Drain off the liquor.
5. Make a Fruit Fritter Batter a la Creole, and have ready a saucepan filled deep with boiling lard.
6. Dip the apricots, one by one, into the batter, and drop from the spoon into the lard, and let them fry to a golden brown.
7. Serve in the same manner as Apple Fritters.

title: Apricot Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Apricots or 1 Pint Can

## Directions

1. Prepare the cream as in recipe Ice Cream and let cool for half an hour
2. Pare and mash the apricots, being careful to remove the stones
3. Mash them into the cream, mixing thoroughly for five minutes till well dissolved in the cream
4. Strain through a fine sieve into a freezer, pressing the fruit down with a wooden spoon
5. Proceed to freeze as in the directions for Plain Ice Cream

title: Apricot Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots that are just ripe
- Water
- 1 pound of sugar to every pint of fruit juice
- Grated rind of an orange

## Directions

1. Choose apricots that are just ripe and no more
2. Pare and cut in two and take out the stones
3. Boil them in water sufficient to cover well
4. Strain through a hair sieve and pour the decoction into earthen dishes
5. To every pint of liquid add a pound of sugar
6. Set on the fire and let it boil twenty minutes, skimming while on the fire
7. Pour immediately into cups, glasses or preserve jars and proceed to seal

title: Apricot Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Apricot Marmalade
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Gill of Water
- 1 Gill of Brandy

## Directions

1. Take four ounces of Apricot Marmalade and put into a saucepan.
2. Add the butter and water, and set on a brisk fire.
3. Stir constantly until it comes to a boil, and then add a gill of good brandy.
4. Let the mixture cook for two minutes more, and serve with baked apple dumplings, boiled dumplings, or apple pudding, as desired.

title: Apricot Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pint of Apricot Juice
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- about one dozen apricots

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and water to boil
2. Pare the apricots, press them through a sieve, and add to the boiling syrup
3. Let all boil for five or ten minutes
4. Press through a sieve again
5. Add the lemon juice, mix well and set to freeze as in general directions for freezing Ice Cream

title: Apricot Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Pints of Apricot Syrup
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1 Ounce of Essence of Apricots

## Directions

1. Pare and stone the apricots, then cut them into pieces, pound and mash well and set them aside for thirty-six hours in a cool place.
2. Then strain through a bag, pressing out all the juice.
3. Cook the sugar and water till it forms the syrup called "Le Petit Casse."
4. Then add the apricot juice and mix thoroughly; let it boil for five minutes, take off the fire, let it get almost cold, add the essence, and then bottle for use.

title: Apricots in Brandy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh apricots
- Half the weight of the fruit in sugar
- Water
- Brandy
- Bladder skin

## Directions

1. Gather apricots as fresh as possible, taken from the tree as soon as ripe
2. Rub each one with a coarse towel, but do not peel it
3. Make a syrup of half the weight of the fruit in sugar, and just water enough to dissolve it
4. When the syrup is prepared and hot, put in the apricots, let them simmer until tender
5. Take the fruit out, and place it on dishes, then expose them to the sun, or in a warm oven to dry and harden
6. Boil the syrup again, after the fruit is out, until it is quite rich and thick
7. Skim it carefully
8. When the apricots are cold and firm, put them in white earthen preserve-jars and fill up with syrup and brandy, half and half
9. Tie up with bladder skin

title: Archangel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sifted Flour
- 1 Cup of White Pulverized Sugar
- The Whites of 8 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth.
2. Add the baking powder to the flour, and sift it three times.
3. Sift the sugar three times.
4. Beat the butter, which has been well-washed, to a cream with the sugar.
5. Then gradually add the flour, beating all the while, and the flavoring extract.
6. Lastly, add the whites, which must be very stiff.
7. Mix all thoroughly, and bake for about half an hour in a moderately quick oven.
8. Use a Turk's head tin.

title: Aromatic Spices For Seasoning Meat Pies, Etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mace
- nutmeg
- cloves
- pepper corns
- marjoram
- thyme
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Take an ounce each of mace and nutmeg, two ounces of cloves, two of pepper corns (whole pepper will do), marjoram and thyme, each one ounce, bay leaves half an ounce.
2. Dry the herbs well first; put the spices and herbs in a paper closely folded, to keep in the aroma, and place them in a slow oven to dry for an hour, or two;
3. then pound and sift them through a sieve. Cork tightly.

title: Aromatic Water
body:
## Ingredients

- The Zest of 3 Limes
- The Zest of 4 Lemons
- 4 Ounces of Orange Flowers
- 1 Ounce of Sweet Basil
- 6 Ounces of Hoarhound
- 7 Pints of Brandy or Alcohol
- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Distilled Water

## Directions

1. Take the grated, macerated zests of the limes and lemons and the bruised fresh orange blossoms, Basil and White Hoarhound, and steep it in seven pints of fine French Brandy or alcohol for ten days
2. Distill the liquor by heating in a water bath and covering the saucepan with a piece of kid in which you have made small holes
3. Add a syrup made of three pounds of sugar and one quart of distilled water
4. Strain after mixing well, and bottle for use

title: Arrow-Root Blanc Mange
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of arrow-root
- cold water
- a pint of milk
- white sugar
- colored jelly

## Directions

1. Mix in a little cold water, two tablespoonfuls of arrow-root
2. sweeten a pint of milk with white sugar and put the arrow-root in the milk
3. Let it boil a few minutes, stirring it constantly
4. take it off, and if desired, you can let it cool and mould it in a bowl or jelly form
5. or it is nice to be eaten warm
6. Colored jelly over it is an improvement when moulded

title: Arrow-Root Blanc Mange for the Sick
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of new milk
- an ounce of Bermuda arrow-root
- cold milk
- a little salt
- fine white sugar
- lemon or orange water
- vanilla bean

## Directions

1. Put a pint of new milk to boil
2. make a smooth batter with an ounce of Bermuda arrow-root and cold milk
3. add a little salt
4. when the milk is boiling stir in the batter
5. let the fire be gentle or it will scorch
6. sweeten this with fine white sugar
7. let it boil a few minutes
8. flavor with lemon, or orange water, or if lemon is objected to, boil a vanilla bean in the milk before the arrow-root is put in
9. Take it off the fire, pour it in a mould and set it on ice
10. serve jelly or jam with the blanc mange, or eat it with cream

title: Arrow-Root Gruel
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of arrow-root
- cold water
- half a pint of boiling water
- salt
- sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix a tablespoonful of arrow-root, or for an infant, half as much
2. when mixed with cold water, stir in it half a pint of boiling water
3. Season with salt, sugar or nutmeg

title: Arrowroot Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dessertspoonful of arrowroot
- cold water
- 1 pint of boiling water
- sugar
- port or sherry wine

## Directions

1. To 1 dessertspoonful of arrowroot add as much cold water as will make it into a soft paste
2. Pour on 1 pint of boiling water
3. Stir it briskly for a few minutes until it becomes a clean, smooth jelly
4. Sweeten to the taste
5. Season with port or sherry wine

title: Arrowroot Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- arrowroot
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1 nutmeg
- grated lemon peel
- paste
- citron

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart of milk and make it into a thin batter with arrowroot
2. add 6 eggs, 1/2 lb. of butter, the same of sugar, 1 nutmeg and a little grated lemon peel
3. put a nice paste in the dish, and bake it nicely
4. when done, sift sugar over it, and stick slips of citron all over the top

title: Artichoke Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cold Boiled Artichokes
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vinegar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- French Dressing

## Directions

1. Boil the Artichokes (See recipe)
2. When cold, peel them and cut into quarters
3. Add chopped parsley and the French dressing; mix, and serve very cold

title: Artichokes a la Vinaigrette
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine, Tender Young Artichokes
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 1 Shallot
- The Yolk of a Hard-Boiled Egg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare and boil the artichokes as in recipe for French Artichokes Boiled.
2. Take the yolk of a hard-boiled egg, dilute it with two teaspoonfuls of vinegar, blend well, season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Chop the shallot very, very fine, add to the mixture, and then add gradually three tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil.
4. Mix all together well.
5. Place the artichokes on a folded napkin on a dish, and send to the table with the sauce in a separate dish.

title: Artichokes Sautes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Tender Artichokes
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- Any Sauce Desired

## Directions

1. Take six fine artichokes and cut into quarters.
2. Remove the choke entirely.
3. Trim the leaves neatly and parboil in hot water for five minutes, being careful to add a teaspoonful of salt and a tablespoonful of vinegar to the water.
4. After five minutes remove the artichokes and drain thoroughly.
5. Place in a saucepan or sautoire, with two good tablespoonfuls of butter.
6. Cover the pan tight and set to cook in a moderate oven for twenty-five minutes.
7. Then take the artichokes from the pan and place in a deep serving dish and serve with a Sauce a la Maltre d'Hotel, a Drawn Butter Sauce, a Hollandaise Sauce, or any sauce desired.

title: Ash Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Indian Meal
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Make the dough as for Corn Dodgers.
2. Shape the pone of bread like the Corn Dodgers.
3. Sweep a clean place on the hearth and draw out a pile of hot ashes.
4. Place the pone of bread on top of the ashes.
5. Cover with hot ashes and let bake to a nice brown.
6. Draw the cake out of the ashes and wipe clean with a cloth till every particle of ashes that adhered in the crevices disappeared.
7. Serve hot with butter or molasses.
8. Alternatively, wrap the cake in a layer of fig leaves before baking.

title: Asparagus on Toast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- water
- butter
- toast

## Directions

1. Pick and wash the asparagus; throw aside all stalks not crisp and tender
2. Cut off nearly all the horny white parts
3. Tie the rest in neat bunches
4. Boil in salted water for twenty minutes or half an hour
5. Take it out, let it drain a minute and lay on buttered toast, the heads all one way
6. Cover with rich drawn butter sauce

title: Asparagus Tips With Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Asparagus or 2 Bunches of Fresh Asparagus
- 1 Can of Green Peas or 1 Pint of Fresh Green Peas
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 6 Tablespoonfuls of Cream Sauce
- 6 or 8 Buttered Croutons
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the tender parts of the asparagus into pieces of about one inch in length.
2. Take the asparagus water in which they were put up, and set on the stove to heat.
3. Add the canned green peas to the fresh peas that have already been boiled.
4. Throw in the asparagus tips, and add water sufficient to cover.
5. Boil rapidly for ten minutes; then drain very thoroughly and return to the fire.
6. Add one tablespoonful of butter, salt, pepper and six tablespoonfuls of Cream Sauce.
7. Stir carefully, so that you may not break the tips.
8. Serve on neat Croutons of buttered toast, or place in a dish and bring piping hot to the table as an entree.

title: Asparagus Vinaigrette Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Asparagus
- 1/2 Pint of Vinaigrette Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the asparagus as in the Boiled Asparagus recipe.
2. Drain and set to cool.
3. Serve with a half pint of Vinaigrette Sauce.

title: Asparagus with Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- cream
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus in inch pieces
2. Boil
3. Throw it into rich hot cream
4. Season with pepper and salt

title: Asparagus— Italian Fashion
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- sweet oil
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take some asparagus, break them in pieces
2. Boil them soft and drain the water off
3. Take a little sweet oil, water and vinegar
4. Let it boil
5. Season it with pepper and salt
6. Pour it over the asparagus

title: Augustine Mangoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of horse radish
- 1 lb. of white mustard seed
- 1 lb. of white pepper
- 1 lb. of celery seed
- 2 ounces of mace
- 2 ounces of cloves
- 2 ounces of nutmeg
- 1 lb. of garlic
- 1 lb. of turmeric
- 1 bottle of pure sweet oil
- 1 bottle of mustard
- mangoes
- boiling vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together
2. After you have greened your mangoes for three days, wipe them dry
3. Stuff them with the prepared ingredients
4. Pour boiling vinegar over them
5. Cork them closely and put away for use
6. All the ingredients must be well beaten

title: Aunt Mary's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of stoned raisins
- currants nicely washed
- suet finely minced
- bread and apples grated
- brown sugar, 1/4 lb. of each
- 4 well beaten eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded ginger
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/4 nutmeg grated
- 1 wineglass of brandy

## Directions

1. Mix 1 lb. of stoned raisins, currants nicely washed, suet finely minced, bread and apples grated, and brown sugar, 1/4 lb. of each
2. Add 4 well beaten eggs
3. Add 1 teaspoonful of pounded ginger, 1/2 teaspoonful of salt, 1/4 nutmeg grated, and 1 wineglass of brandy
4. Mix all the ingredients well, and boil it in a cloth for 2 hours
5. Serve with a sauce of melted butter, a glass of wine, and sugar to the taste

title: Baba
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of the Best Flour
- ⅛ Ounce of Yeast
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- ⅛ Ounce of Salt
- 1 Pound of the Best Butter
- 12 or 14 Eggs
- ½ Pound of Seeded Raisins
- ¼ Pound of Currants
- ¼ Glass of Sweetened Water
- A Tinge of Saffron

## Directions

1. The dough for the Baba is exactly the same as that for the Brioche.
2. Proceed in exactly the same manner, only at the moment of adding the reserved flour to the leavened dough, add a half pound of raisins seeded, and a half pound of currants, washed, picked and dried.
3. Add also a half glass of sweetened, tepid water, in which you will have put a little saffron.
4. Keep this dough much softer than that for the Brioche, adding more eggs, if necessary, to insure the proper degree of consistency.
5. The dough must rise at least six or seven hours.
6. When it has increased to twice its volume, after the third rising, cook as you would the Brioche, in an oven a few degrees cooler than that used in baking Bread.
7. The Baba is formed into a round shape with the hands.
8. After baking let cool and sprinkle with powdered white sugar.

title: Bachelor's Loaf
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of sifted cornmeal
- 1 pint of boiling hot new milk
- 8 eggs (whites and yolks separated)
- a pinch of salt
- 1 spoonful of lard

## Directions

1. Pour boiling hot new milk on the sifted cornmeal
2. Stir well together
3. Beat the whites and yolks of eggs separately
4. Reserve the whites for the last ingredient added
5. Add salt and lard
6. Beat the whole quite light
7. Grease the pan and pour in the mixture
8. Bake 1 hour

title: Bacon and Beans a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Dried Red Beans
- 1 Pound Bacon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Soak the beans over night.
2. Drain off all water.
3. Place in a pot and cover well with cold water.
4. Add the bacon, leaving it in a single square piece.
5. When both have boiled about two hours, season well with pepper and a little salt, if necessary.
6. Place the bacon in the center of a baking dish.
7. Drain the beans and put them around the bacon.
8. Fill the pan to the top with liquor in which the beans have been boiled, and bake one hour and a half, or until the liquor is nearly all absorbed.
9. Serve hot.

title: Baked Apple Dumpling
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large sized cooking apples
- pie paste
- nutmeg
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pare and core apples carefully without making holes through
2. Enclose each in the usual pie paste
3. Place in a pan and fill apples with nutmeg, butter and sugar
4. Leave the tops of apples open
5. Flute with the fingers the top of crust
6. Bake until golden
7. Eat with hard sauce

title: Baked Apple Dumplings
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- pie crust
- yeast
- six good-sized tart apples
- sugar
- butter
- cinnamon
- spices
- nutmegs
- mace
- water
- wine or sugar and butter sauce

## Directions

1. Make a nice pie crust, raised with yeast, or not, as you desire
2. Divide it into six parts, and roll each part thin
3. Have ready six good-sized tart apples, pared and cored
4. Fill up the cores with sugar and butter
5. Close the dough neatly around the apples, and turn that side down in a deep dish
6. If they are made with raised dough they should stand one hour
7. If with unleavened paste, sprinkle some sugar over them, also a little grounded cinnamon or other spice
8. Spread a little batter over each of the dumplings as they go to the oven
9. Put plenty of spices, nutmegs, cinnamon and mace
10. Throw a little water in the dish, and bake three-quarters of an hour

title: Baked Apple Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of stewed apples
- 1 pint of cream or 2 ounces of butter
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 tablespoonful of rose water
- 1 teaspoonfal of grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Stew apples in as little water as possible, and not long enough for the pieces to break and lose their shape
2. When cold, mix them with 1 pint of cream or 2 ounces of butter, 1 lb. of powdered sugar, 1 grated nutmeg, 1 tablespoonful of rose water, 1 teaspoonfal of grated lemon peel

title: Baked Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Apples
- 6 Spoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Cupful of Water

## Directions

1. Cut the blossom end of the apple, and wash, but do not peel.
2. Set them in a baking pan, heap a spoonful of sugar over each in the little place scooped out at the blossom end, and pour a cupful of water in the bottom of the pan.
3. Set in the oven, and bake till very tender.
4. Place in a dish, pour over the syrup, and serve either hot or cold.

title: Baked Apples
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- molasses

## Directions

1. Bake them in a tin roaster, as iron discolors them
2. pour molasses over them and bake until soft

title: Baked Apples
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen large juicy apples
- white sugar
- lemon juice
- lemon peel
- water

## Directions

1. Take 1 dozen large juicy apples, pare and core them, but do not cut them in pieces; put them side by side into a large baking pan, and fill up with white sugar the holes from whence you have extracted the cores
2. Pour into each a little lemon juice, and stick in every one a long piece of lemon peel evenly cut; into the bottom of the pan put a very little water, just enough to prevent the apples from burning; bake them about 1 hour, or till they are tender through, but not till they break
3. When done, set them away to get cold; if closely covered they will keep 2 days; they are very nice with cream, eaten at tea, or at dinner, with boiled custard poured over them

title: Baked Arrowroot Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of rose water
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1 stick of cinnamon
- arrowroot

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and cinnamon together
2. Take the saucepan from the fire, and stir in quickly the arrowroot, which must be previously rubbed smooth in a little water or cold milk
3. Pour it into the dish in which it is to be baked, and while warm stir in the butter
4. The stick of cinnamon must be taken out of the pap as soon as it is turned into the dish, before the butter is mixed with it
5. Beat the eggs and sugar together until very light, and add them to the other ingredients
6. Bake for 15 or 20 minutes in rather a quick oven

title: Baked Batter Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 6 eggs
- 9 tablespoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Beat eggs separately and very light
2. Stir flour in gradually while the milk is boiling hot
3. Bake 1/2 hour in a quick oven
4. Serve with wine and sugar sauce
5. This pudding must be sent to the table as soon as it is done

title: Baked Batter Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 6 eggs beaten separately and very light
- 9 tablespoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Stir flour in gradually while the milk is boiling hot
2. Bake 1 hour in a quick oven
3. Serve with wine and sugar sauce
4. Send to the table as soon as it is done

title: Baked Beans
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- salt pork
- water

## Directions

1. Pick the beans, wash them, and put them to soak over night in plenty of water.
2. In the morning pour this water off and put the beans in a kettle of cold water; place them on the fire and let them simmer till quite tender.
3. Take them up and drain them; when thoroughly drained, put them in a baking pan with a large piece of salt pork; score the pork and lay it deep in among the beans, not upon them.
4. Pour boiling water over them and bake till brown.
5. If in a range, leave them in all night.

title: Baked Clams
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- 1 ounce of powdered mace and nutmeg
- butter
- grated bread crumbs
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the clams fine, and mix with them 1 ounce of powdered mace and nutmeg
2. Butter the sides and bottom of a large, deep dish
3. Cover the bottom with a layer of grated bread crumbs
4. Scatter some very small bits of the best fresh butter over this
5. Add a thick layer of the chopped clams
6. Add another layer of grated bread crumbs and small bits of butter
7. Add another layer of clams, and so on till the dish is full
8. Finish at the top with a layer of crumbs, butter and salt

title: Baked Corn à la Crème
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Ears of Young Corn
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Black Pepper
- 1 Dessertspoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Score the ears of corn down each row with a knife, and then cut from the cob.
2. With the knife press out all the pulp and corn juice, leaving the hulls in the cob.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs well, and then add the corn.
4. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
5. Add melted butter, and then add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Stir in carefully, and place the whole in a dish, which you will have buttered.
7. Set in the oven and bake for an hour, slowly at first, more rapidly towards the end of the last fifteen minutes.
8. Serve with roast beef, veal or lamb.

title: Baked Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints rich milk
- vanilla
- yolks of 14 eggs
- whites of 4 eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 3 pints of rich milk with vanilla, and let it cool
2. Beat the yolks of 14 eggs and the whites of 4
3. Sweeten the milk and eggs
4. Fill the custard cups and set them in a Dutch oven, with as much water as will reach nearly to the tops of the cups
5. Put over the fire and make the water boil, until you perceive a scum forming on the top of the custards
6. Put them in the stove, brown them quickly, and take them out of the water

title: Baked Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 8 eggs
- 5 or 6 spoonfuls of beaten sugar
- salt
- small piece of gelatine
- nutmeg or lemon rind

## Directions

1. Mix a quart of milk with 8 well beaten eggs
2. Strain the mixture
3. Add to it 5 or 6 spoonfuls of beaten sugar, a pinch of salt, with a small piece of gelatine
4. Pour the custard into a deep dish
5. Grate nutmeg or lemon rind over the top
6. Bake in a moderate oven
7. Serve it cold

title: Baked Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Large, Fine, Rosy Apples
- A Plain Paste
- Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a Plain Paste and cut into squares.
2. Proceed to prepare the dumplings in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for Boiled Dumplings.
3. Instead of boiling the dumplings, set in the oven in a baking dish and bake to a delicate brown.
4. Serve with a Hard, Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Baked Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large fish weighing 5 pounds or more
- sweet lard
- 24 or 30 cloves
- mace
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 small teaspoonful of cayenne
- 2 teaspoonfuls of black pepper
- 12 balls of butter about the size of a walnut
- 2 handfuls of chopped onion
- 1 handful of parsley
- 1 handful of bread crumbs
- egg
- pork
- 1 pint of water
- 1/2 pint red wine

## Directions

1. Take a large fish, weighing 5 pounds, or more
2. Scue the sides, and brown in a frying pan with a little sweet lard
3. Pound fine 24 or 30 cloves, a little mace, a tablespoonful of salt, a small teaspoonful of cayenne, 2 teaspoonfuls of black pepper, 12 balls of butter about the size of a walnut, each rolled in flour, 2 handfuls of chopped onion, 1 handful of parsley, 1 handful of bread crumbs
4. Fill the fish and sew up
5. Rub over the outside with egg, and cover with remainder of dressing
6. Put some slices of pork in the pan
7. Put the fish on, and set in the oven till brown
8. Add 1 pint of water, and bake 2 hours, basting it
9. 15 minutes before taking up, add 1/2 pint red wine

title: Baked Fish à la Crème
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- water
- 1 quart of cream or milk
- yolks of 4 eggs
- bunch of parsley
- 1 onion
- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 pound of butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the fish with salt in the water
2. flake off from the bones
3. boil 1 quart of cream (or milk), and pour hot on the yolks of 4 well beaten eggs
4. add a bunch of parsley, 1 onion, and salt
5. let it boil up and then strain
6. put it on the fire again, and when boiling, throw in 3 tablespoonfuls of flour, perfectly smooth
7. add 1/2 pound of butter
8. butter a deep dish, and put a layer of fish, then a layer of sauce
9. put in fish and sauce, until the dish is full
10. finish off with bread crumbs
11. Bake 1/2 hour

title: Baked Flounder
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Flounder
- 1 Large Onion
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1/2 Bottle of White Wine
- 6 Fresh Tomatoes
- 2 Dozen Lake Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Crackers
- Dry Toast Cut in Dice Shape
- Salt and Black Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the fish; place on a platter.
2. Chop one large onion fine.
3. Rub the fish first with salt and black pepper.
4. Take a large and deep kitchen pan, place within a tablespoonful of butter, the chopped onion, bay leaf and thyme.
5. Place the fish on top of this and pour over a half bottle of white wine.
6. Cover with another close pan and put the whole on top of the oven (not on the inside).
7. Bake from the bottom.
8. When it begins to boil from below, turn the fish over carefully without breaking, and let it bake on the other side.
9. Take a saucepan and put within a tablespoonful of butter and mix thoroughly with two tablespoonfuls of flour.
10. Let it brown without burning, and then add six fresh tomatoes, skinned and chopped fine, or a half can.
11. Add two dozen Lake Shrimp that have been cleaned well and scalded, a half can of mushrooms, salt and pepper to taste.
12. Let all cook for about five minutes and then water with the gravy in which the fish is cooking.
13. Mix well and cover the fish with it.
14. Parboil one dozen oysters, and when the fish is cooked, set it in the dish in which it is to be served and place the oysters all around it on small slices of dry toast.
15. Cover the fish alternately with the shrimp and oysters, as a garnish over and around it.
16. Mash two crackers into crumbs and sprinkle over it; also some finely-chopped parsley.
17. Place small bits of butter alternately over the fish.
18. Set the dish in the oven, and bake with a quick fire until brown.
19. Serve immediately.

title: Baked Grits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Grits
- 3/4 Quart of Water
- 3/4 Quart of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- Salt to Taste
- a gill of cream

## Directions

1. Boil the grits in the water and milk, mixed
2. Season, and when quite dry, take off the stove and let it cool a little
3. Beat the whites and yolks separately
4. When the grits is cool beat in the yolks, and blend thoroughly
5. Then add the whites, and beat till very light
6. Add a gill of cream
7. Set in an oven and bake to a beautiful brown, and serve hot

title: Baked Grouper
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Grouper (weight 3 pounds)
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 2 Dozen Boiled Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Boiled Crawfish
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread
- 2 Large Tomatoes
- 3/4 Can of Mushrooms
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 6 Allspice
- 3 Cloves
- 1 Bottle of White Wine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the Grouper thoroughly
2. Make a cut in the shape of the letter S on the back and stuff this with spice, thyme, clove and bay leaf, chopped fine
3. Rub thoroughly inside and out with salt and pepper
4. Make a good stuffing by taking one dozen oysters, one cup of stale bread crumbs, wet and squeezed of all water, one large onion chopped fine, a half teaspoonful of salt and a little black pepper
5. Mix well and fry in a pan with a tablespoonful of butter
6. Stuff the body of the fish and sew up with soft thread
7. Lard well, that is, rub thoroughly with lard and place in the oven
8. Pour over immediately a bottle of white wine, and let the fish bake well in the wine
9. In the meantime, prepare the following sauce a la Chambord: Take one large tablespoonful of butter, one large chopped onion, one sprig of thyme, one of bay leaf
10. Brown the onions and butter, being careful not to burn, and put in three large tomatoes
11. Add the chopped herbs and brown and add a pint of oyster water, which has been heated by blanching the oysters
12. Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste
13. Have ready in another dish one dozen parboiled or blanched oysters and two dozen boiled shrimps
14. Put the fish in the dish in which it is to be served, garnish with the oysters and shrimps placing them over the fish, and mingle between about a half can of mushrooms
15. Have also ready a dozen nicely boiled crawfish and garnish the fish with these, placing them all around it in the dish
16. Pour the sauce over all and set in the oven and bake a few minutes longer and serve hot

title: Baked Ham
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- onions
- cloves
- parsley
- sweet herbs
- sugar
- bread crumbs
- vegetables
- savory jelly

## Directions

1. Soak and clean your ham, boil it with onions, cloves, parsley and sweet herbs until it is nearly done, then let it cool in its own liquor.
2. When cold, pull off the skin and place the ham in the oven gate, with a little sugar and bread crumbs over it till it is brown.
3. If it is to be eaten hot, serve with vegetables and some acid or piquant sauce; if cold, send up savory jelly, No. 21.

title: Baked Irish Potatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 good sized Irish potatoes
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 pint of milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil soft 8 good sized Irish potatoes
2. Mash them and add 2 tablespoonfuls of butter while hot
3. Mix with it 1 pint of milk, add salt
4. Put in a dish and bake 1 hour

title: Baked Lemon Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pound of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Gill of Sherry and Brandy, Mixed
- 1 Small Stale Sponge Cake
- 6 Eggs
- oranges

## Directions

1. Grate the rind of the oranges, and squeeze out the juice after removing the seeds
2. Grate the inner meat
3. Beat the butter and sugar to a thick cream, and then add the wine
4. Add these to the oranges, grated with the rind
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and the yolks separately
6. Add them in order to the mixture
7. Place in a moderately quick oven, and let it bake for about a half hour
8. When done, let it cool slightly
9. Place a meringue on top, as in Apple Meringue Pudding, and set back to bake two minutes more
10. Serve cold

title: Baked Macaroni
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- milk
- water
- salt
- grated cheese
- butter
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Boil as much macaroni as will fill the dish, in milk and water, till quite tender
2. Drain it on a sieve
3. Sprinkle a little salt over it
4. Put a layer in the dish, and one of grated cheese, with butter, pepper and mustard, until you fill the dish
5. Bake it in a quick oven, some 20 or 30 minutes

title: Baked Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Spanish Onions
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Drawn Butter Sauce

## Directions

1. Trim the onions nicely, but do not peel them.
2. Put them into a kettle of boiling water, and let them boil rapidly for about an hour.
3. Drain in a colander.
4. Put them in a baking pan, and let them bake slowly for about an hour.
5. Take out and remove the skins, and place in a vegetable dish.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, and serve with Drawn Butter Sauce.

title: Baked Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Spanish Onions
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Trim the onions nicely, but do not peel them.
2. Then put them into a kettle of boiling water, and let them boil rapidly for about an hour.
3. Drain in a colander.
4. Then put them in a baking pan, and let them bake slowly for about an hour.
5. Take out and remove the skins, and place in a vegetable dish, and sprinkle with salt and pepper, and serve with Drawn Butter Sauce.

title: Baked Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Fine, Large Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Sauce Piquante
- 3 Shallots
- 1 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Gill White Wine
- Parsley, Thyme and Bay Leaf, chopped fine

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters about two minutes in their own liquor, dropping them in the liquor as it comes to the boiling point.
2. Pass them through a Sauce Piquante, rolling nicely.
3. Mix the melted butter and the chopped parsley, thyme, etc., the shallots chopped very fine, and moisten well with a little oyster juice; chop the mushrooms fine and add, pouring in the gill of wine.
4. After it is reduced, being careful to stir constantly, select the finest and largest shells of the oysters, which have been cleaned well, and place in each four or six oysters; pour over each shell the sauce, filling nicely, in pyramidal shape; place on each a bit of butter, and set in the stove for about five minutes, or over a gridiron on a slow fire for about ten minutes.
5. Serve in the shells, with garnish of parsley and lemons, sliced.

title: Baked Pears
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large pound pears
- lemon peel
- sugar
- cloves or pounded cinnamon
- port wine

## Directions

1. The best for baking are the large pound pears; remove the skins; cut them in half, and take out the cores; lay them in a deep dish, with a thin slip of lemon peel in the place from which each core was taken
2. Sprinkle them with sugar, and strew some cloves or pounded cinnamon among them; pour into the dish some port wine; to 1 dozen large pears allow 1 lb. of sugar, and 1 pint of wine
3. Cover the dish with a large sheet of brown paper, tied on; set it in a moderate oven and let them bake till tender all through, which you may ascertain by sticking a straw through them; you must not let them remain long enough in the oven to break to pieces

title: Baked Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 potatoes
- a drawn butter sauce, or sauce à la Hollandaise

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well, set them in the oven in their skins, and in about an hour open the oven slightly and take the potatoes out, one by one, in a cloth, and press lightly to see if they are quite soft to the touch.
2. Then either bring to the table in their jackets, as many prefer, or pare and serve with a drawn butter sauce, or with daubes, meats, sautés, etc., or with any fish or meats with gravies.

title: Baked Potatoes with Egg Whites
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Butter
- Milk
- Salt and pepper
- Whites of 2 eggs
- Egg yolks
- Gruyere cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Bake the potatoes, and when they are done, cut off the tops, and scoop out the meat into a hot bowl
2. Mash very fine, and add the butter, the milk and salt and pepper
3. Add a little grated Gruyere cheese if desired
4. Beat all till very light, and add the beaten whites of two eggs, and stir lightly
5. Fill the potato skins with this preparation, heaping the flaky potatoes nicely on top
6. Brush over lightly with the yolks of the eggs
7. Set in the oven to brown and serve hot

title: Baked Pumpkin
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Pumpkin or Cashaw
- Butter or Gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin in halves and then into quarters. Two quarters are enough for a family of six, if the pumpkin is large.
2. Remove the seeds, but do not peel the rind.
3. Place in a baking pan with the rind downwards, and bake until so tender that it may be pierced easily with a fork.
4. Serve in the rind at the table, helping it by spoonfuls.
5. It is eaten with butter or gravy.

title: Baked Pumpkin
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints of pumpkin or cashaw
- A dressing of butter or gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin in halves and then into quarters.
2. Remove the seeds, but do not peel the rind.
3. Place in a baking pan with the rind downwards.
4. Bake until so tender that it may be pierced easily with a fork.
5. Serve in the rind at the table.

title: Baked Quinces
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces (ripe)
- butter
- sugar
- nutmeg (a little)

## Directions

1. Gather the quinces when ripe and wash them nicely
2. Put them in an oven to bake
3. When quite soft, take them out and cut them open
4. Serve with butter, sugar, and a little nutmeg, while hot

title: Baked Red Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- butter
- parsley
- onions
- allspice
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. After the fish is well cleaned, take off the fins and tail
2. Cut it into 4 or 5 parts
3. Lay in a deep dish some lumps of butter, parsley and onions
4. Chop all very fine with a little allspice
5. Make a layer of fish, well seasoned with pepper and salt, flour it
6. Continue this layering until the dish is full
7. Bake 1 hour

title: Baked Red Snapper
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Red Snapper, 3 Pounds in Weight
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 2 Dozen Boiled Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Boiled Crawfish
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 3 Large Tomatoes
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 6 Allspice
- 3 Cloves
- 1 Bottle of White Wine
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the Red Snapper thoroughly.
2. Make a cut in the shape of the letter S on the back and stuff this with spice, thyme, clove and bay leaf, chopped fine.
3. Rub thoroughly inside and out with salt and pepper.
4. Make a good stuffing by taking one dozen oysters, one cup of stale bread crumbs, wet and squeezed of all water, one large onion chopped fine, a half teaspoonful of salt and a little black pepper.
5. Mix well and fry in a pan with a tablespoonful of butter.
6. Stuff the body of the fish and sew up with soft thread.
7. Lard well, that is, rub thoroughly with lard and place in the oven.
8. Pour over immediately a bottle of white wine, and let the fish bake well in the wine.
9. Prepare the following sauce: Take one large tablespoonful of butter, one large chopped onion, one sprig of thyme, one of bay leaf. Brown the onions and butter, being careful not to burn, and put in three tomatoes; add the chopped herbs; brown and add a pint of oyster water which has been heated by blanching the oysters.
10. Have ready in another dish one dozen parboiled or blanched oysters and two dozen boiled shrimp.
11. Put the fish in the dish in which it is to be served and garnish with the oysters and shrimp, placing them over the fish, and mingle about a half can of mushrooms.
12. Have also ready a dozen nicely boiled crawfish. Garnish the fish with these, placing them all around it in the dish in which it is to be served.
13. Pour the sauce over all and set in the oven and bake a few minutes longer and serve hot.

title: Baked Sheepshead
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Sheepshead
- 1 Large Onion
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1/2 Bottle of White Wine
- 6 Fresh Tomatoes
- 2 Dozen Lake Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Oysters
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Crackers
- Dry Toast Cut in Dice Shape
- 3 Sprigs Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and Black Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the fish and place on a platter
2. Chop one large onion fine and rub the fish first with salt and black pepper
3. Take a large and deep kitchen pan, place within a tablespoonful of butter, the chopped onion, bay leaf and thyme
4. Place the fish on top of this and pour over a half bottle of white wine
5. Cover with another close pan and put the whole on top of the oven
6. Bake from the bottom
7. When it begins to boil from below, turn the fish over carefully without breaking, and let it bake on the other side
8. Take a saucepan and put within a tablespoonful of butter and mix thoroughly with two tablespoonfuls of flour
9. Let it brown without burning, and then add six fresh tomatoes, skinned and chopped fine, or a half can
10. Add two dozen Lake Shrimp that have been cleaned well and scalded, a half can of mushrooms, salt and pepper to taste
11. Let all cook for about five minutes and then water with the gravy in which the fish is cooking
12. Mix well and cover the fish with it
13. Parboil one dozen oysters, and when the fish is cooked, set it in the dish in which it is to be served and place the oysters all around it on small slices of dry toast
14. Cover the fish alternately with the shrimp and oysters

title: Baked Sheepshead a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-Pound Sheepshead
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Pint of White Wine or Boiling Water
- Salt and Pepper and Flour to Dredge
- Hollandaise, Bechamel or Tomato Sauce
- Lemon slices
- Fried potato balls
- Optional: One dozen oysters or shrimp or the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Clean the fish thoroughly, inside and out; wash well and dry thoroughly with a clean towel.
2. Rub it well with salt and pepper.
3. Make a dressing by taking one cup of stale bread crumbs, wet and squeezed thoroughly of all water, one tablespoonful of melted butter, one tablespoonful of chopped parsley, a half teaspoonful of salt and a little black pepper.
4. Mix well and fry in a little lard, optionally adding one dozen oysters or shrimp or the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs.
5. Stuff the body of the fish and sew up with soft thread or soft yarn.
6. Score or slit the fish on either side, making the scores about an inch apart and lard it either by putting a strip of salt fat in each gash or filling with lard.
7. Grease the bottom of a baking pan and put the fish in it.
8. Dredge thickly with salt and flour and a little Cayenne.
9. Pour over a half bottle of white wine, or simply cover the bottom of the pan with the grease and add a little boiling water.
10. Put the fish in a hot oven.
11. Baste every ten minutes or so by taking a spoon and pouring the gravy over the fish.
12. Allow about fifteen minutes of baking to every pound of fish.
13. When brown on one side, turn on the other.
14. When done carefully, slide the fish into the center of the flat dish in which it is to be served.
15. Garnish nicely with slices of lemon, fried potato balls and chopped parsley, placing sprigs of parsley between the fried potato balls and laying on the slices of lemon.
16. Serve with Hollandaise Sauce or Bechamel Sauce.

title: Baked Shrimps
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- pounded biscuits
- shrimps
- stewed tomatoes
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Butter well a deep dish
2. Place a thick layer of pounded biscuits in the dish, having picked and boiled your shrimps
3. Put a layer of stewed tomatoes, with a little butter, pepper and salt
4. Add a thinner layer of beat biscuits and another of shrimps
5. Continue with layers until 3 or 4 layers of both are put in the dish
6. The last layer must be of biscuits
7. Bake quite brown

title: Baked Shrimps
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Lake Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Tomatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs or Crackers

## Directions

1. Boil the shrimps according to recipe
2. Butter a deep dish well and place within a layer of grated bread crumbs or powdered crackers
3. Pick and clean the shrimp and season well
4. Stew about a dozen tomatoes in a little butter and season with pepper and salt
5. Place a layer of the tomatoes in the dish and then a thin layer of crackers or grated bread and over this a layer of shrimp
6. Continue till you have four or five layers, the last being of the grated bread crumbs
7. Put little dots of butter here and there
8. Place in the oven and bake till quite brown

title: Baked Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or mutton
- vegetables
- salt
- pepper
- a pint of peas
- Patna rice
- four quarts of water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef or mutton and the vegetables in pieces, season them with salt and pepper, and put them into a jar with a pint of peas and the Patna rice. Pour in four quarts of water, cover the jar very closely, and set it in the oven to bake. When done, strain it through a sieve, and serve it very hot.

title: Baked Suet Pudding. Economical and Wholesome
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A pound of flour
- Six ounces of finely chopped suet
- Four eggs
- Milk to make a firm batter

## Directions

1. Add by degrees six ounces of finely chopped suet to a pound of flour
2. Add four eggs, together with as much milk as will make a firm batter
3. Beat all together hard, until the last moment before placing it in the oven
4. Pour it into a buttered dish
5. Bake
6. Serve as soon as done, with plain syrup, or butter and sugar sauce

title: Baked Sweet Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Potatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes, and scrub the skins, but do not peel them
2. Rub the skins of the potatoes lightly all over with a little lard, butter, or fat bacon to render the skins soft and pliable
3. Put the potatoes in a baking pan in the oven, and let them cook until their centers are mellow to the touch
4. Serve in their jackets immediately

title: Baked Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen or more fine, ripe tomatoes
- butter
- small pieces of bread
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald and peel 1 dozen or more fine, ripe tomatoes
2. Butter a shallow baking dish, and put in the finest without breaking them
3. Fill up the space between with small pieces of bread well buttered
4. Mash the rest of the tomatoes, and strain out all the hard parts
5. Mix with a spoonful of butter, pepper and salt
6. Pour it over the dish, and strew bread crumbs on the top
7. Bake about ¼ an hour

title: Baker's Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces or more of hops
- 3 pints of water
- 2 lbs. of malt
- 1/2 pint of good yeast

## Directions

1. Boil hops into a vessel with water
2. As it decreases, carefully stir all the time
3. Strain the liquor and mix it well with malt
4. Cover and let it stand for 8 hours, or until it is milkwarm
5. Stir in 1/2 pint of good yeast
6. When mixed well together, let it work for 10 hours
7. Strain it through a sieve

title: Bakers' Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Compressed Yeast
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- 2 Quarts of Water
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough

## Directions

1. Allow one ounce of Compressed Yeast to one quart of lukewarm water, and mix well in a wooden bread trough.
2. Then add flour enough to make a nice smooth dough of medium degree, not too stiff, nor yet too soft.
3. Work it well, and then let it stand for about five hours in a warm place so as to rise well.
4. When it drops, or begins to fall (you can tell this by watching the sides of the dough), add the same amount of water that was used in making the dough, putting two teaspoonfuls of salt into the water before adding it.
5. Work this well, and then throw down on the table, cut and mold the dough into loaves of whatever length is desired, and take a smooth stick and press lightly down across the loaf about two inches from the edge.
6. Put the loaves into the oven without setting in a pan.
7. Watch carefully, and see that the oven is of the proper temperature.
8. By following these directions, you will have nice, fresh, sweet New Orleans Bakers' Bread.

title: Baking Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- one large chopped onion
- wineglassful of white wine
- cup of oyster water
- juice of one lemon
- two tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms
- one tablespoonful of minced parsley
- thyme
- sweet marjoram
- ten allspice
- one clove of garlic
- Cayenne
- whole mushrooms
- slices of lemon
- crotons or dried toast

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, cutting off the fins.
2. Make the letter 'S' on the sides.
3. Rub well inside and out with pepper and salt.
4. Butter a stewpan and put in one large chopped onion and a wineglassful of white wine.
5. Place the fish in the pan, put in the oven and let it bake about twenty minutes, having been careful to place lumps of butter over it and basting frequently.
6. When done carefully, lift the fish out of the pan and put it into the dish in which it is to be served.
7. Take the gravy in which the fish has been cooked and add about a cup of oyster water, the juice of one lemon, two tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms, one tablespoonful of minced parsley, thyme and sweet marjoram, ten allspice, one clove of garlic, a little Cayenne, and salt and pepper to taste.
8. Mix all thoroughly over the stove, adding a little butter if the gravy adheres too much to the pan.
9. Pour over the fish, and garnish with whole mushrooms and slices of lemon laid alternately upon crotons or dried toast, cut diamond shape.

title: Balls for Mrs. Ames' Calf's Head Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound beef or veal
- 2 slices of salt pork
- lights and liver
- 6 small onions
- 1.5 large tablespoonfuls of sweet marjoram
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 1 desertspoon powdered mace
- 1 desertspoon cloves
- 8 eggs
- 3 sifted crackers
- 1/4 gill of wine
- butter the size of an egg
- 2 tablespoons salt
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Put beef or veal in chopping tray with 2 slices of salt pork an inch thick
2. Add some of the lights and liver, 6 small onions, 1.5 large tablespoonfuls of sweet marjoram, 1 nutmeg, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 1 desertspoon powdered mace, same of cloves, 8 eggs, 3 sifted crackers, 1/4 gill of wine, butter the size of an egg, and 2 tablespoons salt
3. Mix all well together
4. Roll the balls in flour
5. Fry slowly in lard

title: Banana Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Bananas
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of Half a Lemon
- 1/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the Bananas and cut them into halves
2. Put the sugar and the water on to boil, and after ten minutes add the lemon juice
3. Put the bananas, a few at a time, into the hot syrup and let them stew gently
4. After a half hour take them out lay in a dish, pour over the hot liquid syrup, and serve either hot or cold

title: Banana Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Bananas
- Fritter Batter a la Creole

## Directions

1. Make a Fritter Batter a la Creole.
2. Peel the bananas and then cut them in halves.
3. Slice them nicely, according to length.
4. Dip in the fritter batter, and proceed to cook and serve as in the recipe for Apple Fritters.

title: Banana Fritters with Cognac or Rum
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Bananas
- Fritter Batter a la Creole
- Sugar
- Sufficient Cognac or Rum to saturate

## Directions

1. Slice the banana and sprinkle with sugar, and cover with sufficient Cognac or Rum to saturate well.
2. Set them aside for half an hour, then drain, and proceed as in Apple Fritters.

title: Banana Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Large Bananas
- 1 Quart of Cream
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the bananas and slice and mash them
2. Boil the milk, and add the sugar, stirring till well dissolved
3. Add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light, and let cool for an hour
4. Stir in the bananas, which you will have previously beaten to a smooth paste
5. Proceed as directed above

title: Banana Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 1 1/2 Cups of Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon or Vanilla Extract
- 3 Bananas
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare four layers of Sponge Cake
2. Slice the bananas lengthwise very nicely and lay on the bottom layer
3. Sprinkle with a little powdered sugar, and then spread a little Cream Sauce which has been made to the consistency of Cream Puff Filling
4. Over this put on the second layer of cake; cover nicely with the bananas and the Cream Sauce, and proceed thus till the top layer is placed over the bananas
5. Then sprinkle with white sugar or Ice, according to taste

title: Banana Meringue Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- ½ Pound Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 3 Bananas
- 1 Cup of Cream Sauce
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- three tablespoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Make a Sponge Cake; slice in two across the cake.
2. Slice bananas lengthwise very nicely and lay on the bottom layer of cake.
3. Sprinkle with a little powdered sugar, and then spread over a little Cream Sauce, which you will have made to the consistency of Cream Puff filling.
4. Put over this the second layer of cake, and then repeat the process of spreading the bananas and cream on the layer.
5. Make a meringue with the whites of three eggs and three tablespoonfuls of sugar, and spread this over the whole.
6. Set in a quick oven and bake to a delicate brown.
7. Serve, either hot or cold, with a Wine Sauce, preferably Sauce au Vin de Madere.

title: Banana Meringue Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Bananas
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sweet Spices
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence
- 1 Teaspoonful Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and stew the bananas and mash through a sieve
2. Proceed as in directions for Apple Meringue Pie

title: Banana Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Bananas
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sweet Spices, Blended Equally
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and stew the bananas and mash through a sieve
2. Prepare a Pie Paste and proceed as in Apple Pie

title: Banana Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Bananas
- Stale Cake
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 3 Eggs
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the stale cake very thin.
2. Peel and slice the bananas.
3. Put a layer of cake at the bottom of a buttered baking dish.
4. Place over this a layer of the sliced bananas.
5. Pour over this a layer of milk custard, made from the above ingredients, and boiled very thin.
6. Add another layer of cake, then one of banana and custard, and so continue till you have used up all.
7. Make the top layer of the custard.
8. Put in an oven, cover with a piece of paper, bake for half an hour, and let the top brown slightly, and serve cold.

title: Banana Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Bananas
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water
- The Juice of 2 Oranges

## Directions

1. Peel and mash the bananas
2. Boil the sugar and water together for five minutes
3. Take from the fire, and let cool
4. Add the bananas and the orange juice, and press all through a sieve
5. Set to freeze as directed

title: Banana Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Banana Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1/4 Ounce of Banana Essence
- The Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Peel the bananas, then cut into small pieces and pound and mash well and set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours.
2. Then strain through a bag and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apricot Syrup, and add the essence and lemon juice when about to bottle.

title: Barley or Sage Cream for Invalids
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sage or barley
- water
- a stick of cinnamon
- rich boiled custard
- sugar
- a glass of wine

## Directions

1. Wash the sage or barley clean; take a cup of either
2. Put it on the fire with water to cover it
3. Boil it gently until it is soft
4. While boiling, put in a stick of cinnamon, or any seasoning that is agreeable
5. When the barley has boiled soft and thick, take it off and strain it
6. Add to it a rich boiled custard, sweeten it to taste
7. Add a glass of wine, if liked
8. Serve it frozen, or not, as is liked best by the sick

title: Barley Water
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four large tablespoonfuls of picked and washed pearl barley
- two quarts of boiling water
- salt
- lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take four large tablespoonfuls of picked and washed pearl barley, and put it into a porcelain-lined kettle with two quarts of boiling water
2. Let it boil slowly until the water is reduced one half
3. Strain it and season with salt, lemon, or sugar as may be agreeable to the sick

title: Batter Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 spoonfuls of flour
- 3 spoonfuls of cornmeal
- a little salt
- eggs
- a sufficient quantity of rich milk

## Directions

1. Sift together the flour, cornmeal, and salt
2. Make a thin batter with flour, eggs, and a sufficient quantity of rich milk
3. Beat the yolks and whites of the eggs separately
4. Bake it in little tin moulds in a quick oven

title: Batter Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Flour, Finely Sifted
- 2 Cupfuls of Sweet Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and the yolks of the eggs separately, the former to a stiff froth.
2. Add the flour to the yolks, and beat till very light.
3. Add the yeast powder and beat again.
4. Then add the sweet milk and beat well.
5. Now add the whites of the eggs and mix all to a very smooth, light batter, as thick as a batter that will run in a stream from the mouth of a spoon.
6. Have ready the hot griddles, which you will grease with lard lightly, or better still, with a piece of fat bacon.
7. Drop the cakes on the griddle from a large spoon, and bake about two minutes to a nice brown, turning first on one side and then quickly on the other.
8. Pile the cakes on a plate in the mouth of the oven, buttering each generously as soon as baked, and send to the table almost as fast as you bake.

title: Batter Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- six eggs
- seven tablespoonfuls of flour
- salt
- wine or lemon sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the milk
2. Stir in the eggs and flour, while the milk is nearly hot enough to boil
3. Do not let it boil when you stir in the flour, but take it off the fire, or you will curdle the eggs
4. Salt the milk slightly before boiling
5. Bake this batter half an hour
6. Eat it with wine or lemon sauce

title: Battered Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- small tablespoonful of butter
- wheat flour
- salt
- chicken

## Directions

1. Make a light batter with 3 eggs, a small tablespoonful of butter, a little wheat flour, and salt to the taste
2. Joint the chicken
3. Put them into the batter
4. Grease the frying pan
5. Throw the mixture of chicken and batter into it
6. Fry a good brown

title: Bavarian Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint milk
- 6 eggs
- 6 ounces sugar
- vanilla or other essence
- 1 pint whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and sugar till very light, with milk and flavor with vanilla or any other essence
2. Let it simmer on the fire without boiling for 16 minutes
3. When it becomes tepid, mix in 1 pint of whipped cream
4. Pour it into a mould, and surround it with ice
5. Whip up some cream and pour it into the dish after the form is turned out

title: Bavarian Creams
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1/2 Pint of Gelatine, or 1/2 Box
- 1 Gill of Cold Water
- 1/4 Cup of Sugar
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla or Rum

## Directions

1. Follow the general directions given for making Cream Desserts, viz: Soaking the gelatine, and, before whipping the cream, cool it for at least an hour on the ice
2. Put one pint of fresh cream or milk in a farina boiler, and add the sugar, and bring it to a boil
3. As it boils, pour in the gelatine, stir constantly till the latter is well dissolved and blended, and set aside to cool
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs to a cream, and stir them in the cold cream, blending and beating vigorously
5. Add the Vanilla or Rum, and set it away on the ice
