6. When the boiled cream is quite cold, and has begun to thicken, stir it till perfectly smooth, and then stir in the whipped cream with a few light strokes
7. Have ready the mold or freezer, well rinsed, and fill with the cream
8. Set it away to cool and when quite firm turn it out, and serve with plain cream or whipped cream

title: Bay Leaf Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 ounces of laurel leaf
- 2 1/2 ounces of myrtle flowers
- 1 gallon of brandy
- 6 pounds of white sugar
- 3 1/4 pints of water
- 12 cloves
- 1/2 of a nutmeg

## Directions

1. Bruise or pound the bay leaves, the myrtle flowers, the coarsely-grated nutmeg and the cloves
2. Pour over them one gallon of brandy
3. Reduce over a gentle fire the sugar and water till well-dissolved
4. Let it cool
5. Mix the whole with the bay leaves and brandy, mix well, filter, put into tightly corked bottles, and preserve

title: Bay Leaf, Very Fine
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh kidneys
- Salt and pepper
- One and a half tablespoonfuls of butter
- Chopped herbs
- One half glass of white wine
- One cup of water or consomme

## Directions

1. Select perfectly fresh kidneys; wash them well, and then slice very thin.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Put one and a half tablespoonfuls of butter into the saucepan; when melted and very hot, add the kidneys and chopped herbs, being very careful to stir constantly and very fast, to prevent burning.
4. Add a half glass of white wine, if possible, and one cup of water or consomme.
5. Let it boil up once, and the kidneys are ready to be served.
6. Five minutes should be sufficient to cook them well, and at no time should they be allowed to boil.

title: Bean Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Beans
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- raw egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mash the beans well by pressing through a colander.
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, a teaspoonful of vinegar, and a tablespoonful of melted butter.
3. Form into small boulettes, or balls, and dip in a raw egg, well beaten, and then roll in the bread crumbs.
4. Fry in boiling fat, and serve hot.

title: Bean Polenta
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Dried Beans
- 1 Tablespoonful of Molasses
- 1 Tablespoonful of Mustard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash two cupfuls of dried beans, having previously soaked them over night.
2. Pour off the water.
3. Put the beans in the stewpan and cover with fresh cold water, and cook the beans until tender.
4. Pour into a colander, and press the beans through.
5. Put this pulp into the stewpan, and add one tablespoonful of ready-made mustard, one tablespoonful of molasses, one of butter, one of vinegar, the juice of a lemon, and salt and pepper to taste.
6. Serve very hot, as a vegetable.

title: Bean Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cold Beans
- Vinaigrette Sauce

## Directions

1. Serve with a Vinaigrette Sauce (See recipe)

title: Bearnaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Shallots
- 0.5 Clove of Garlic
- 0.5 Gill of French Vinegar
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Flour and Butter
- Yolks of Four Eggs
- A Grated Nutmeg
- 0.5 Lemon's Juice
- Glace
- White Wine
- White Consomme
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Chop the shallots and mince the garlic very fine
2. Blend the butter and flour, or take a good tablespoonful of Glace, and moisten with a tablespoonful of White Wine and good white consomme, till you have about a pint
3. Set on the stove in a porcelain-lined saucepan
4. Add the pepper and salt and butter, and a quarter of a teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
5. Add half a gill of vinegar and the juice of a lemon, according to taste and acidity
6. When of the consistency approaching starch, take from the fire and add the yolks of four eggs, beaten well, stirring all the time, till you have the consistency of a thick starch
7. Serve immediately with broiled steak, broiled chops, or broiled fish

title: Beaten Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Heaping Teaspoonful of Lard
- 1 Pint of Milk or Water, or Half and Half

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and add the salt, mixing thoroughly.
2. Then add the lard, and blend by rubbing through the hands till not a lump remains in the flour.
3. Now add gradually the water or milk, or the milk and water combined, using half and half of each, and knead all the whole till the dough, which must not be too soft, but rather stiff, is formed.
4. Then lay the dough on a biscuit board on a block, and beat for a half hour with a rolling pin.
5. Knead lightly, and beat again for a full ten minutes, till from every portion of the surface and sides the air bubbles or blisters form.
6. Then roll out and cut into round biscuits with the cutter, or square ones with a knife, and stick here and there with a fork.
7. Bake in a moderate oven for about ten or fifteen minutes, till a delicate brown above and below.

title: Beauregard Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3/4 pint of fresh milk
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of cornstarch
- salt and pepper to taste
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs for five minutes, till hard.
2. Take out of the water and cool, take off the shells, and separate the whites from the yolks.
3. Rub the yolks through a sieve, and chop the whites very fine.
4. Do not mix them.
5. Have the milk ready to boil, and rub the butter and cornstarch together, and add to the boiling milk.
6. Add the whites of the eggs, and salt and pepper to taste.
7. Prepare some buttered toast, and cover it with a layer of this white sauce.
8. Add a layer of the yolks of the eggs.
9. Add another layer of the sauce, and another layer of the yolks, and then the remainder of the sauce.
10. Sprinkle the top with a little salt and pepper.

title: Bechamel Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Raw Ham
- 2 Fresh Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Pint of Veloute Sauce
- 2 Gills Rich Cream
- 1 Stick of Celery, Cut Very Fine
- 1/4 Carrot, Cut Very Fine
- 1/4 Onion, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Bunch Sweet Herbs
- 2 Cloves
- 4 Allspice
- Blade of Mace

## Directions

1. Put the butter in a saucepan, and as it melts add the chopped onion, and let it stew until very tender, but do not let it brown.
2. Mince the ham and cut the vegetables very fine, and add first the ham, letting it brown a minute, and then the vegetables, herbs and spices.
3. Let all simmer gently for ten minutes, without browning.
4. Add the Veloute Sauce (see recipe), stir in well, and bring all to a boil.
5. Let it boil ten minutes, and be sure to stir constantly.
6. Then add, by gentle degrees, the cream, which should not be heated, but which must be very rich and sweet (if not perfectly sweet it will spoil the sauce).
7. When all this is blended, the sauce is of a velvet smoothness, and very delicious.
8. Strain and set on the fire a minute longer to heat, and serve hot.
9. It is served with fish, chicken and sweetbreads.

title: Beef Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tender beef (fat and lean)
- onion
- boiled parsley
- grated crumbs of bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- beaten egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Mince very fine a piece of tender beef, fat and lean
2. Mince also an onion, with some boiled parsley
3. Add grated crumbs of bread, and season with pepper, salt, and nutmeg
4. Mix the whole together
5. Moisten with a beaten egg, roll it into balls, flour, and fry them

title: Beef Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 1 Pound of Pork or Sausage Meat
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Onions
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Mince the beef with sausage meat and add garlic, parsley, pepper, salt and onions, and bread crumbs soaked in water
2. Add the whites of two eggs beaten to a froth
3. Make into balls and roll in the beaten white of an egg, and fry, being careful not to cook too rapidly
4. When sufficiently browned, pile in a pyramid shape on a dish, garnish with parsley sprigs and serve

title: Beef Marined
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of beef, from the round or shoulder
- Four tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- Three bay leaves
- One onion
- One lemon
- Tablespoonful of vinegar
- One-eighth teaspoonful each of ground cloves, mace and allspice
- Salt and cayenne

## Directions

1. Mix the spices, salt and pepper thoroughly and rub well into both sides of the beef
2. Chop the onions fine, and cover the meat with them
3. Mix the oil and vinegar and the juice of one lemon, and pour this over the meat
4. Set it in the ice box, or a cool place, and let it stand overnight
5. Put it into the stewpan, and be careful to retain all the juices of the spices
6. Set on the fire and let it simmer ten minutes, adding the bay leaves, chopped very fine
7. Add a tablespoonful of flour, rolled smoothly in a half teaspoonful of butter or lard, melted
8. Let this brown, and then half cover the meat with boiling water
9. Cover closely and set on the oven, letting the beef cook two hours, and turning once, so that both sides may be well penetrated by the heat
10. Serve on a hot dish, pouring the gravy over

title: Beef Patties
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- underdone roast beef
- cayenne
- powdered nutmeg or mace or chopped sweet herbs
- mushroom gravy
- paste

## Directions

1. Mince all the lean and a very little of the fat of underdone roast beef
2. Season it with cayenne and powdered nutmeg, or mace, or else chopped sweet herbs
3. If you have a fancy for mushroom gravy, moisten the meat with that
4. Make a nice paste and cut it into small circular sheets, rolled out not very thin
5. Cover 1/2 of each sheet of paste with the minced beef (not too near the edge)
6. Fold over the other 1/2 so as to form a half-moon
7. Crimp them with a sharp knife
8. Lay the patties in square baking pans
9. Prick them with a fork and bake them brown

title: Beef Tea For Invalids
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound best beef
- 1 pint cold water

## Directions

1. Take off all the fat and cut the lean into small pieces
2. Pour over it 1 pint of cold water and let it stand 3 or 4 hours
3. Put it over a brisk fire and let it boil 20 minutes

title: Beefsteak And Oyster Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of lean beefsteak
- salt
- pepper
- beef broth
- mushroom catsup
- Harvey’s or Worcestershire sauce
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Cut three pounds of lean beefsteak
2. Salt, pepper and fry quickly so as to brown without cooking through
3. Then place in a deep dish
4. Get four dozen oysters, beard them, and lay them in the pan over the beef
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Take the gravy in which the steaks were fried
7. Pour out some of the grease
8. Dredge in a tablespoonful of flour
9. Let it brown and add to it a pint of good beef broth
10. Then put in a wine-glassful of mushroom catsup
11. Some of Harvey’s or Worcestershire sauce
12. Heat it, and let it boil up a few times
13. Then pour it over the oysters and steak
14. When the gravy has become cool
15. Cover the pie with a good puff paste
16. And bake it for an hour and a half

title: Beefsteak Pie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- thin paste
- beef
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- allspice
- catsup
- onion
- boiled Irish potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Butter a deep dish, spread a thin paste over the bottom, sides and edges
2. Cut away from the beef all the bone, fat and gristle
3. Cut the beef in thin pieces the size of the palm of your hand
4. Beat it well with a rolling pin
5. Put a layer of the beef, seasoned with pepper, salt, nutmeg, allspice, a little catsup and onion
6. Slice boiled Irish potatoes, and put a layer on the meat until the dish is filled
7. Pour in a little water and butter
8. Bake 1 hour

title: Beefsteak Pie (Vol-du-Vent de Boeuf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cold Cooked Meat
- 2 Slices of Breakfast Bacon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3/4 Dozen Potatoes
- Thyme, Bay Leaf and Parsley
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Pie Crust

## Directions

1. Make a nice pie crust.
2. Line a baking pan with this, and bake in the oven.
3. Cut the meat very fine, into dice, and season well, rubbing with the minced thyme, parsley, bay leaf, and salt and pepper.
4. Stew the meat as in Ragout de Veau a la Bourgeoise.
5. Place in the pan.
6. Dot the top with bits of butter, and place over all a layer of pie crust, decorating the edges nicely.
7. Bake to a nice brown.
8. Serve in the dish in which it was cooked, with any left-over sauce spread over the slices.

title: Beefsteak Smothered in Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Round Steak
- 6 Onions, sliced fine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 2 Sprigs each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Pint of Water
- Pepper and Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Beat the round steak well with the rolling pin or steak hammer; cut off the outer skin and press the meat back into shape
2. Place the tablespoonful of lard in the deep frying pan and let it melt
3. Lay in the sliced onions, and over these the beefsteak, which has been well seasoned with salt and pepper and dredged with the flour
4. Cover closely and let it simmer over a hot fire for a few minutes
5. Turn the steak on the other side and after three minutes, add two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, chopped parsley, thyme and bay leaf and a clove of garlic
6. Turn the steak, letting the flour brown well, and keep the pot closely covered
7. When brown pour over one cup of water, or a pint, which will be sufficient to cover the meat
8. Bring this to a brisk boil and set the pot back where it can simmer gently for about two hours

title: Beet Roots Sauté à la Béchamel or à la Crème
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Beet Roots
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- ½ Pint of Béchamel or Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the beets exactly as in the preceding recipe
2. About five minutes before serving add half a pint of hot Béchamel or Cream Sauce
3. Serve as a vegetable

title: Beet Roots Sautéd In Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Beets
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- A Pinch of Black Pepper
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the beet roots as in recipe for Boiled Beets
2. Peel neatly and cut up into dice-shaped pieces
3. Put them in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter
4. Season with a little pinch of black pepper and salt to taste
5. Sprinkle the powdered sugar over them
6. Let them cook for five minutes, tossing them lightly and almost constantly
7. Send to the table hot as a vegetable

title: Beet Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Red Beets
- French Dressing

## Directions

1. Boil the beets till done, and then peel and slice nicely
2. Set them to cool and pour over them a French dressing or a plain dressing of vinegar, salt and pepper

title: Beets Boiled
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the beets clean, but do not trim the roots
2. If the beets are young and tender, cook them whole
3. Stew them in a little butter, with sugar, salt and vinegar added
4. Let them simmer in this batter for twenty minutes
5. Serve

title: Belle Creole Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1/2 Cup Bread Crumbs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 3/4 Cup of Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1/2 cup of consommé or broth
- onion juice
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put the butter into the saucepan, and add the flour, letting it blend well, without burning or browning, for this is a white sauce
2. When it becomes a delicate yellow, add the bread crumbs, stir for one minute, and add the half cup of consommé or broth
3. Stir well, and add a half cup of cream, and salt and pepper to taste
4. Add the chopped parsley as a garnish, and a little onion juice
5. Take off the fire and add the well-chopped yolks of two eggs, and a tablespoonful of lemon juice
6. Serve with boiled fish or boiled meats of any kind

title: Benton Tea Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 6 ounces of butter
- 3 spoonfuls of yeast
- new milk

## Directions

1. Rub the butter and yeast into the flour
2. Make into a paste with new milk
3. Make into biscuits
4. Prick them with a clean fork
5. Bake

title: Bermuda Johnny Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- a little salt
- milk

## Directions

1. To each pint of flour allow 2 ounces of butter
2. Work in a small part of the butter with flour
3. Mix it up with a little salt and milk to consistency of paste
4. Roll it out and spread on the butter as for paste, twice dividing the butter
5. Put the paste into as many pieces as you wish cakes and roll them out round
6. 1 pint of flour will make 2 cakes the size of large plate
7. Bake them on a griddle
8. Let each cake remain on the griddle a short time off the fire after being done to make them crisp
9. Tear open each and butter quickly

title: Berryman Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of best white sugar
- 8 eggs
- 8 ounces of butter
- A soup plate of cornmeal mush
- Juice and rind of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix all well together till very light
2. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Berwick Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- ⅛ cups of sugar
- 2 cups of flour
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 cup of cold water
- 1 teaspoon of soda
- salt
- 3 more cups of flour

## Directions

1. Beat 6 eggs, yolks and whites together, 2 minutes
2. Add ⅛ cups of sugar and beat 6 minutes
3. Add 2 cups of flour with 2 teaspoons cream of tartar and beat 2 minutes
4. Add 1 cup of cold water with 1 teaspoon of soda dissolved in it, and beat 1 minute
5. Flavor, add a little salt
6. Add 3 more cups of flour (without cream of tartar) and beat 1 minute

title: Bewitched Beef or Veal
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of lean beef or veal
- 1/2 lb. of salt pork
- 1/2 teaspoon of cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoon of black pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon of cloves
- allspice
- mace
- teacupful of bread crumbs
- tablespoonful of butter
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Chop the meat very fine
2. Mix all of the ingredients thoroughly
3. Steam in a mould for 3 hours

title: Bird's Nest Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a package of gelatine
- water
- eggshells
- corn starch
- vanilla
- sugar
- lemon rind

## Directions

1. Using a little more than half the quantity of water given in the recipe for making jelly, prepare the gelatine; in all other respects use the same proportions.
2. When ready to strain put it into a large oval dish (a meat dish is nice); fill it nearly to the edge.
3. Set it away to harden.
4. Take some eggshells that you have broken just the end off in getting out the egg.
5. Make a blanc-mange of corn starch; flavor it with vanilla, and sweeten.
6. Put this into the shells before it cools and hardens at all.
7. Set the eggs on end in a vegetable-dish so that they will stand top up, being careful not to let the blanc-mange run out.
8. Cut some very thin yellow parings off the lemon rind, stew them in a little sugar and water.
9. When cold lay each piece separately in a circle on the jelly, making two or three nests.
10. Break open the eggshells, take out the blanc-mange, and lay it in groups like eggs inside the nest.
11. Ivy sprays or myrtle wound around the edge of the dish improves the appearance.

title: Bird's Nest Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six mellow apples
- pastry
- long narrow strips of citron
- one pint of powdered sugar
- half a pint of butter
- yolks of eight eggs
- whites of eight eggs
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel and core six mellow apples.
2. Line a pudding dish with pastry.
3. Lay the apples in the bottom of the dish, and stick long narrow strips of citron around them.
4. Stir to a cream a pint of powdered sugar, and half a pint of butter.
5. Beat separately the yolks and whites of eight eggs.
6. Mix them with the butter and sugar, season with nutmeg.
7. Place it on the fire, and stir until it is hot.
8. Pour it over the apples, and bake immediately.
9. It can be eaten warm or cold.
10. It should be covered with a pan when first put into the oven, to prevent the top from browning too soon.

title: Bird's Nest Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 or 10 apples
- 2 eggs
- 1 J cups of white sugar
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 4 cups of milk
- 2 cups of flour
- 1 teaspoon yeast powder
- I cup of sugar for scalding

## Directions

1. Pare and core 8 or 10 apples
2. Place in deep flat dish
3. Mix 2 eggs, 1 J cups of white sugar, 1 tablespoon of butter, 4 cups of milk, 2 cups of flour well
4. Add 1 teaspoon yeast powder last
5. Scald apples with I cup of sugar
6. Pour over the apples the mixture and bake

title: Bird's Nest Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Apples
- 1 Pint of Rich Cream
- 2 Cups of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Pare and core the apples, and then put them in the bottom of a buttered dish.
2. Mix the cream with the flour until smooth, and add a teaspoonful of baking powder, which you will have dissolved well in a tablespoonful of boiling water.
3. Mix again thoroughly, and pour over the apples.
4. Set in a moderate oven, and bake for one hour.
5. Serve with Hard Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Heaping Teaspoonful of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Yeast Powder
- 1 Pint of Water or Sweet Milk

## Directions

1. Sift the flour well, and add the salt and yeast powder, rubbing in thoroughly with your hands.
2. Then rub in the lard, rubbing the flour between your hands till every portion is permeated by the lard, and there are no lumps.
3. Gradually add water or milk, using about a pint, more or less, mixing in with your hands.
4. Then knead the dough until smooth and elastic.
5. Form into molds, and lay on the biscuit board, and roll out till about a quarter of an inch in thickness.
6. Cut into round cakes with the biscuit cutter.
7. Stick lightly with a fork here and there over the surface, and bake in a quick oven for about five or six minutes.

title: Biscuit Cream in Moulds
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of firm clabber
- one quart of sweet cream
- white sugar
- vanilla bean
- half a cup of sweet milk

## Directions

1. Make it very sweet with white sugar
2. Flavor with vanilla bean boiled in half a cup of sweet milk
3. Churn all together ten minutes
4. Freeze in moulds, or in any ordinary freezer

title: Biscuit Glace For Twenty
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- pulverized sugar
- cream
- vanilla extract
- egg whites
- strawberry sherbet
- ice
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix ten yolks of eggs with one and a half pounds pulverized sugar.
2. Add half a gallon of cream and vanilla extract.
3. Beat white of eggs well if the cream is too light.
4. Place inside box within the outer box, and stuff with ice and salt.
5. Let it freeze.
6. When frozen, place red sherbet on top of biscuit to give pretty appearance.
7. Freeze in a square box and cut in small pieces.

title: Biscuit Glacés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Gill of Maraschino or 1 Gill of Kirsch
- 1 Teaspoonful of Strawberry Extract
- a few drops of Cochineal

## Directions

1. Set one-half the cream to boil
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar till they are exceedingly light
3. Take the boiling milk from the fire, and stir in while still very hot the sugar and eggs
4. Set to cool
5. When cool add the Maraschino or Kirsch, and set to freeze as Ice Cream
6. Whip the remaining cream to a very thick froth, adding the Vanilla, and stir it into the freezing cream
7. Cut paper cases or use the specially prepared molds that come for Biscuit Glacés, and fill with the mixture
8. Fill the top one inch high with a little of the cream which you will have colored with a teaspoonful of Strawberry Extract and a few drops of Cochineal
9. Pack these molds or cases in salt, and freeze for two hours longer, until they can be turned out whole

title: Biscuit Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 8 ounces of granulated sugar
- 1 pint of sweet milk
- 3 pints of double sweet cream
- vanilla bean or 1 tablespoonful of Burnett's extract

## Directions

1. Break in a bowl the yolks of the eggs
2. Stir in them the 8 ounces of sugar until they bubble, do not beat
3. Put the milk to boil in a bain Marie
4. When it comes to a boil, stir in the eggs and sugar
5. Do not let it boil after you put the eggs in, only simmer to let it thicken well
6. Strain this custard through a piece of Swiss muslin and set aside to get cold
7. Have 3 pints of sweet cream packed in ice and salt to get very cold
8. Whip it well until it is quite thick
9. Gradually whip the cream into the cold custard
10. If vanilla bean is used, boil in the milk; if the extract, stir in the custard when cold
11. Freeze this until it is quite stiff and then put in moulds and pack in ice
12. If desired, add crystallized fruit, cut into quarters, and after the cream is partly frozen, stir the fruit in and pack in mould

title: Biscuit Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of boiling milk
- 3 Naples biscuits
- 4 eggs
- nutmeg
- a little brandy
- a spoonful of sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Pour a pint of boiling milk over 3 Naples biscuits grated
2. Cover it close
3. When cold, add the yolks of 4 eggs and the whites of 2, nutmeg, a little brandy, a spoonful of sugar and flour
4. Boil it 1 hour in a bowl

title: Biscuits Glacés aux Confitures
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Cup of Jam of Any Kind Desired

## Directions

1. Make a sponge cake or cup cake and bake on a baking sheet about three-quarters of an inch in thickness.
2. When cold, cut up into fanciful shapes, trim the edges neatly with a very sharp knife, and brush off the least particle of crumbs that may adhere.
3. Carefully spread the upper part and sides of the biscuit with apricot, peach, raspberry or any jam.
4. Hold one at a time on a silver fork, very carefully, and with a spoon pour over Transparent Chocolate Icing, around the sides and over the surface of the biscuit.
5. Carefully lay it upon a wire tray, resting upon a baking sheet.
6. Dry the biscuit in a very moderate oven for about five minutes.

title: Biscuits Glacés à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- 1/2 Pound of Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla or Lemon Extract
- 1 Pint of Cream

## Directions

1. Set one pint of milk to boil
2. Beat the yolks of six eggs with a half pound of white pulverized sugar until light
3. Stir in the milk
4. Take from the fire, and let cool
5. Flavor with vanilla and freeze
6. Whip one pint of cream to a stiff froth, and stir in
7. Fill molds or fancy paper cases with the mixture
8. Pack in salt and ice for two hours, and serve

title: Biscuits Panachés aux Confitures
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- ½ Pound of Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Cup of Mixed Preserved Fruits

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake as directed in the preceding recipe
2. Place on the baking sheet
3. Drop different-sized small bits of fine preserves, orange, apricot, damson, red currant, etc., on the biscuit, and spread smoothly, so as to form a beautifully marbled pattern
4. Cut out the cakes in any fanciful design
5. Cover with Transparent Icing
6. Dry in a moderate oven for five minutes

title: Bisque Potage—Madame Eugène
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 or more shrimp
- salt
- big black pepper
- a little nutmeg (grated)
- a bit of butter
- rice
- bouillon
- cream
- shrimp shells
- crust of bread
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Wash shrimp in 5 or 6 waters
2. Put them in a saucepan, adding salt and big black pepper, a little nutmeg (grated) and a bit of butter, on a brisk fire
3. Stir with a spoon for ½ hour
4. When cooked let the shrimp drain
5. Remove the meats and mash in mortar
6. Boil rice in bouillon ½ hour
7. Strain and put it in mortar with the shrimp
8. When well pounded, put in saucepan and thin with bouillon and pass through a sieve
9. Thin this purée (cream) so that it be neither too thick nor too thin
10. Mash the shrimp shells and add juice or butter in which the shrimps have been cooked
11. Strain the purée in a cullender, it should then be of reddish color
12. Put this in a saucepan on a moderate fire
13. Do not let come to a boil, but should be quite hot
14. Put crust of bread in soup dish and pour on a little broth, hot
15. When ready to serve add a little Madeira

title: Black Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of crushed sugar
- 12 eggs whipped to a froth
- 1 lb. of flour
- 3 lbs. of stoned raisins
- 3 lbs. of cleaned currants
- 5 grated nutmegs
- 1 ounce of pounded cinnamon
- 1 teaspoonful of ground cloves sifted
- 1 lb. of citron cut into thin slices

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Stir in the whipped eggs
3. Sift in the flour
4. Add the raisins, currants, nutmegs, cinnamon, cloves, and citron
5. Mix all well together
6. Bake in a buttered pan in rather a moderate oven

title: Black Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Brown Sugar
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Pound of Flour
- 2 Pounds Currants
- 2 Pounds of Seeded Raisins
- ¾ Pound of Citron, Cut very Thin
- ¾ Pound of Chipped Figs
- 1 Glass of Jelly
- 1 Wineglass of Brandy
- 1 Pound Almonds, Blanched and Chopped
- 12 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Extract of Cinnamon
- 1 Teaspoonful Cloves
- 2 Teaspoonfuls Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash and cleanse the currants thoroughly.
2. Seed the raisins.
3. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream.
4. Then add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten.
5. Add one-half of the flour and the spices.
6. Mix all the fruit in the remaining flour, having first sliced the citron very thin, and add to the mixture.
7. Then add the jelly, which should be homemade and of the best quality.
8. Otherwise, add one cup of the best Louisiana molasses.
9. Mix thoroughly.
10. Add the whites of the eggs and the brandy last.
11. Divide into two tins, which must be lined with well-greased paper.
12. Bake for four hours in a slow oven.
13. Test with the broom wisp.
14. If not done through cook longer, watching carefully, and using the test till the straw is dry.

title: Black Cake Plain
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 cups of flour
- 10 eggs
- 4 cups of sugar
- 4 cups of molasses
- 4 cups of butter
- 3 cups of lard
- 2 cups of seasoning such as ginger, cinnamon and mace
- 1 spoonful of soda to a tablespoonful of milk

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Bake in a regular oven

title: Blackberry Cordial
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe blackberries
- water
- loaf sugar
- mace
- cloves
- cinnamon
- French brandy

## Directions

1. Simmer nice ripe blackberries in water enough to cover them
2. When they are tender take them out, mash them and strain them through a strong cloth
3. Get all the juice out you can by squeezing, but do not let the pulp and seed come through the bag
4. Add a little of the water they were boiled in, however not more than two tablespoonfuls to each pint of strained juice
5. To every pint of this liquor, add one pound of loaf sugar, one teaspoonful of mace, same of cloves and cinnamon
6. Boil all these together a few minutes, and strain it again to free it from the spice
7. When this syrup is cool, add to each pint a wineglass of good French brandy
8. If you cannot get brandy, substitute rum or whiskey, remembering to use twice as much as you would brandy

title: Blackberry Cordial
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, ripe fruit
- loaf sugar
- brandy
- cloves
- cinnamon
- hot water

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe fruit
2. Squeeze the berries without boiling
3. To a quart of the strained juice, put a pound of loaf sugar
4. Boil it for half an hour, and add a quart of brandy, some cloves and cinnamon, when on the fire
5. If the fruit thickens too rapidly while boiling, throw in a cup of hot water

title: Blackberry Cordial
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Blackberry Juice
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- ½ Ounce of Ground Cloves
- ¼ Ounce of Allspice
- 1 Pint of the Best French Brandy

## Directions

1. Boil eight quarts of blackberries in a porcelain-lined pot till the juice is all extracted.
2. Take off the fire and press out all the juice through a flannel bag.
3. Add the sugar, allowing one glass of sugar always to two glasses of blackberries, if you wish to make larger quantities than the above measurements.
4. Add the cloves and the allspice, and set on the fire, and let it come to a good boil.
5. Let it boil up several times.
6. Take off, strain and add the brandy.
7. Bottle while hot.
8. Cork well and seal, and set away in a cool place, to be used after two months.

title: Blackberry Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Blackberries
- Water
- White sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the fruit till tender in a little water
2. Throw off the water, bruise and strain the fruit
3. To each pint of the juice add one pound of white sugar
4. Put it now in a preserving-pan, and boil it rapidly, but do not stir it while boiling, as that breaks the jelly
5. Skim it carefully, and when it jellies, pour it into tumblers or small jars

title: Blackberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Blackberries
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sweet Spices
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Stew the blackberries and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apple Pie, using the same proportions for two pies

title: Blackberry Roll
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Blackberries
- A Plain Paste, Not Too Flaky

## Directions

1. Make a Plain Paste, which should be rather firm, to prevent the berries from escaping.
2. Roll this out to about one and a half feet in length, or one foot, according to the size of the family, and the number of guests.
3. Spread the blackberries, which you will have carefully picked, over this paste.
4. Carefully roll over and over until the entire paste is taken up.
5. Sew in a clean piece of white muslin and put into a kettle of boiling water.
6. Let it boil continuously for at least two hours and a half.
7. Take out, let it cool slightly, rip the cloth which binds it, and serve immediately with a Hard Sauce or Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Blackberry Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pint of Blackberry Juice
- 1 Gallon of Syrup

## Directions

1. Pick, stem and wash the blackberries, then pound and mash them well set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours.
2. Then strain through a bag, pressing out all the juice, and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apricot Syrup.

title: Blackberry Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Blackberries
- 1 Quart of Good French Vinegar
- 1 Pound of white sugar per pint of liquor

## Directions

1. Put one quart of blackberries into a jar or deep vessel, and pour over the vinegar
2. Let them stand for twenty-four hours
3. Strain them, and again add the fruit
4. Repeat this operation and let stand twenty-four hours three times total
5. Strain through a muslin bag
6. Add one pound of white sugar to every pint of the liquor
7. Boil the whole in a porcelain kettle for half an hour
8. When cold, bottle it and keep it in a cool place

title: Blackberry Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mash the berries without boiling them
2. Strain the juice
3. To six pints of juice, add two pints of water and three pounds of sugar
4. Mix thoroughly and put it in a wide-mouthed stone jar to ferment
5. Cover it carefully with a cloth, to keep out all insects
6. Open it and skim it every morning; then cover it up again carefully
7. When it ceases to ferment, strain it and put it in a demijohn
8. Do not cork it tightly, as it must have a little air, but cover the loose stopper with a piece of muslin or tarlatan, to keep out the insects
9. It will be ready to bottle in two months

title: Blackberry Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Quarts of Currant Juice
- 1.33 Quarts of Cold Water
- 3 Pounds of Granulated Sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all the above ingredients well together
2. Let them stand for twenty-four hours
3. Let it stand for two days
4. Skim well, and set in a cool place to ferment slowly for at least six days
5. At the end of this time remove any froth that has collected, and add a half teacupful of the best French Brandy
6. Close tightly
7. In two days, when it is well-settled, draw off, without disturbing the sediment
8. Bottle tightly and seal

title: Blanc Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces isinglass
- 1 pint boiling water
- bitter almonds blanched
- sweet almonds blanched
- rose water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Whip 2 ounces of isinglass, pour a pint of boiling water over it to dissolve it
2. Next day, break it up, add to it some bitter and sweet almonds blanched, beat them with rose water and sugar
3. Put it on the fire and let it boil up 5 or 6 times
4. Take it off and strain it through a muslin bag

title: Blanc Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of French isinglass
- 3 pints of water
- 1 pint of good cream
- 1 glass of pure peach water

## Directions

1. Boil 2 ounces of French isinglass in 3 pints of water, 20 minutes
2. Strain this into 1 pint of good cream
3. Let it simmer 5 minutes
4. Add 1 glass of pure peach water
5. Sweeten, and strain through a sieve into a mould

title: Blanc Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of isinglass
- 1 pint boiling water
- bitter and sweet almonds blanched
- rose water
- sugar
- 2| quarts of cream, or a mixture of cream and milk

## Directions

1. Whip 2 ounces of isinglass
2. Pour a pint of boiling water over it to dissolve it
3. Next day, break it up
4. Add to it some bitter and sweet almonds blanched
5. Beat them with rose water and sugar
6. Put it on the fire and let it boil up 5 or 6 times
7. Take it off and strain it through a muslin bag
8. Put 2| quarts of cream, or a mixture of cream and milk, on the fire to simmer
9. Stir in the isinglass which has been prepared
10. Let them simmer together for a short time
11. Take it off and pour into a pitcher
12. When sufficiently cool, fill the moulds with it

title: Blanc Mange of Moss
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 ounce of Carrigean Moss
- 1 quart of new milk
- essence of bitter almonds
- 1 glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Take 1/2 ounce of Carrigean Moss (the white)
2. Put it into 1 quart of new milk
3. Reduce it, by simmering, to 1 pint
4. Flavor it with a few drops of essence of bitter almonds
5. Add 1 glass of white wine
6. Sweeten it to the taste
7. Pour it into a mould, until cold and firm
8. Serve in a glass dish

title: Blanc Mangier
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- % Cup of Sugar
- % Ounce of Gelatine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in water
2. Mix with the milk and cream
3. Let it come to a boil
4. Add the Vanilla
5. Pour into a mold
6. Let it harden
7. Serve cold

title: Blanquette of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Veal Brisket
- 2 Onions
- 1 Carrot
- 3/4 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 Gallon of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take a brisket of veal and cut into pieces of two square inches
2. Put in a stew pot and cover with a half gallon of water, and add salt and pepper and two onions and one carrot, chopped fine
3. Let it boil till very tender
4. Take the meat out of the saucepan, and keep the water in which it was boiled
5. Take another saucepan and put a tablespoonful of butter in it, and as it melts add a tablespoonful of flour
6. Mix well, continuing to dissolve till it becomes a smooth cream; do not let it brown
7. Add one pint of the water in which the veal was boiled
8. Stir well, making it very light, and not thick
9. Add one-half can of mushrooms, and let the whole boil about fifteen minutes, so as to be very light
10. Put in the veal, which is already cooked
11. Let it simmer for about fifteen minutes longer, and take off the fire
12. Add the yolks of two eggs, well beaten, with two tablespoonfuls of the gravy and the juice of one lemon
13. Serve hot

title: Blue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Ounce of Indigo
- 1 Tablespoonful of Alcohol

## Directions

1. Take one pound of sugar, one pint of water and boil to a syrup
2. Add one ounce of Indigo, powdered very fine
3. Let all cool
4. add a little alcohol, and strain and bottle

title: Blue Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Bluefish, or Fish in Quantity to Equal 3 Pounds
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Parsley to Garnish
- Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Split the fish in the middle of the back if the fish is large; if small, broil whole
2. Season well by rubbing with salt and pepper, mixed with a little sweet oil
3. Put the fish on the broiler and see that it is browned well on both sides, using always the double wire broiler
4. When done, place in a heated dish (heat by placing in a bain-marie or hot-water bath and dry thoroughly)
5. Butter the fish nicely and squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
6. Garnish with parsley and sliced lemon, and serve with a Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel

title: Boil Dumpling Crust Without Lard or Butter—For Dyspeptics
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of flour
- salt
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Sift a pint of flour in a basin, salt it as usual
2. pour on it a fine stream of boiling water from the spout of a kettle, pour it slowly
3. mix the flour and hot water with a spoon until it is a nice soft dough that you can handle
4. pour it on the biscuit board, which should be well floured
5. give it two or three turns, and it is ready for the fruit

title: Boiled Asparagus
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Asparagus or 2 Bunches of Fresh Asparagus
- 1/2 Pint of Hollandaise or 1 Gill of Drawn Butter Sauce
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. When it is possible to get fresh asparagus, carefully wash it in cold water, and cut off the tough white ends.
2. Scrape the white part well, and throw it into cold water, to soak for half an hour.
3. Then tie it in small bundles, and put it in a saucepan lined with porcelain.
4. Pour over boiling water, and let it cook for twenty minutes.
5. Add a teaspoonful of salt and cook ten minutes longer.
6. Take the asparagus up nicely.
7. Drain off all water.
8. Lay on a folded napkin, and serve with a Drawn Butter Sauce.

title: Boiled Asparagus
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Asparagus or 2 Bunches of Fresh Asparagus
- 1/2 Pint of Hollandaise or 1 Gill of Drawn Butter Sauce
- Salt

## Directions

1. When it is possible to get fresh asparagus, carefully wash it in cold water, and cut off the tough white ends.
2. Scrape the white part well, and throw it into cold water, to soak for half an hour.
3. Then tie it in small bundles, and put it in a saucepan lined with porcelain.
4. Pour over boiling water, and let it cook for twenty minutes.
5. Add a teaspoonful of salt and cook ten minutes longer.
6. Take the asparagus up nicely and drain off all water.
7. Lay on a folded napkin, and serve with a Drawn Butter Sauce.
8. If using canned asparagus, set on the stove and allow to heat, as it is already cooked.
9. Take out of the can by turning it downward in a dish, letting the asparagus slide gently out.
10. Drain off all water, and place on a folded napkin, and serve with a Drawn Butter Sauce.

title: Boiled Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- water

## Directions

1. To have it very tender it should be boiled slowly, and the pot well skimmed
2. The meat should be well covered with water, so that the skim may be removed easily
3. When beef is very salt it should boil 1/2 of an hour; then take it up, throw away the water it has boiled in
4. Fill up the pot with fresh water, replace the beef and let it boil gently for 3 hours
5. Observe to take off all the scum as it rises
6. The round is the most choice piece to boil, next, the H bone

title: Boiled Beef en Papillottes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 6 Sausages (Chaurice)
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup Bread Crumbs
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take the left-over bouilli cut in slices and parboil slightly in butter.
2. Make a forcemeat or quenelle of pork sausage, garlic, parsley and moistened bread crumbs, add two eggs, salt and pepper.
3. Put a layer of this farci between each layer of sliced beef, and then add the bread crumbs, mixed with chopped parsley.
4. Put the beef in oiled paper, folded as tightly as possible, cook a quarter of an hour in the oven and serve in the papillottes (paper).

title: Boiled Beef Gros Sel
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 1 Bunch of Parsley
- 1 Head of Lettuce
- A French Dressing or salt

## Directions

1. Take the bouilli from the bouillon, and serve on a platter, laying the whole on a bed of parsley and lettuce
2. Serve with salt or French dressing

title: Boiled Beef Sausage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Pork
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 6 Allspice
- 3 Truffles
- A Dash of Cayenne
- 1 Wineglass of Madeira
- ½ Cup of Bouillon
- Beef Extract
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Take the bouilli of the day before, mince and add chopped parsley, a few spices, salt and Cayenne pepper, and a little beef extract saved from the bouillon.
2. Take a pound of pork and add, mixing thoroughly.
3. When the whole is well mixed, add a few truffles and a little Madeira.
4. Fill some entrails that have been thoroughly cleansed with this meat and shape the sausage as one desires.
5. Boil in butter and serve alone.

title: Boiled Beef Sauté With Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Large Onions
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Cayenne Pepper

## Directions

1. Take three fine onions and parboil them in butter over a slow fire.
2. When a rich, creamy brown, add clove garlic and Cayenne pepper.
3. Cut the bouilli in thin slices and add, shaking the pan until browned.
4. Place in the platter and serve with chopped parsley dusted over, and the juice of a lemon squeezed over it.

title: Boiled Beef Sauté à la Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 3 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chili Vinegar
- The Peel of One Lemon

## Directions

1. Slice the onions and brown them in lard, using about one tablespoonful.
2. Skim the lard off the onions and put the beef in the pan.
3. Stir up and smother.
4. Add the oil, the peel of a lemon, cut fine, and the Chili vinegar.
5. Serve hot.

title: Boiled Beef With Carrot Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Carrots
- 2 Onions
- 1 Gill of Cream
- 2 Sticks of Celery
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Make a good puree of fine, red carrots and then strain in butter.
2. Add a gill of rich cream and salt and pepper to the taste.
3. Put the bouilli in the platter and pour the sauce around it, serving hot just after the soup.

title: Boiled Beef With Egg Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Bouilli
- 6 Slices of Stale Bread
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Parsley and Lettuce Leaves to Garnish

## Directions

1. Take left-over or stale bread, slice it thickly and dip in cream or milk.
2. Then dip it in the beaten whites and yolks of egg and fry in butter.
3. Cut the bouilli into slices to match the bread, dip it in the egg and fry also.
4. Serve on a dish with chopped parsley dashed over it and a garnish of parsley or lettuce leaves.

title: Boiled Beef With Lettuce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Head of Lettuce
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 12 Sausages (Chaurice)
- 1½ Cups Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Garlic
- Lard

## Directions

1. Take six fine, firm heads of lettuce, strip off all the green leaves, wash thoroughly and soak and blanch in boiling water.
2. Then throw them into cold water.
3. When very cold squeeze in a towel till they are thoroughly dry and cut off the stalks from below without injuring the heart.
4. Fill this open place with forcemeat balls, made from the bouilli, fried in lard with fresh bread crumbs soaked in bouillon and worked into the meat, chopped up with pepper, salt and garlic, and add one or two hard-boiled eggs.
5. Tie the balls up and cook without adding water and fill the heart of the lettuce.
6. This may be served around the body of the bouilli and makes a beautiful garnish.

title: Boiled Beef With Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Dozen Tomatoes
- 2 Cups of Bouillon
- ¼ Teaspoonful of Flour
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Butter
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Take a half dozen fine, ripe tomatoes, and parboil them in butter, being careful not to let them burn.
2. Add a pinch of flour and two good cups of bouillon, a little salt and pepper, a clove of garlic, a sprig of parsley, thyme and bay leaf.
3. After two hours, take out the tomatoes and allow the beef to cook a few minutes in the sauce.
4. Then serve on a flat dish, arranging the tomatoes around the beef and under each tomato put a nice piece of buttered toast.

title: Boiled Beef with Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- ¾ Dozen Tomatoes
- 2 Cups of Bouillon
- ¼ Teaspoonful of Flour
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take a half dozen fine, ripe tomatoes, and parboil them in butter, being careful not to let them burn.
2. Add a pinch of flour and two good cups of bouillon, a little salt

title: Boiled Beef à l'Indienne
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 1 Teaspoonful of Powdered Saffron
- 2 Cayenne Pepper Pods
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- ¾ Cup of Bouillon
- Lettuce Leaves
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Crush the pods of two Cayenne peppers and a teaspoonful of powdered saffron and heat and brown in butter.
2. Moisten with a little bouillon.
3. Boil the sauce down, and when nearly ready to serve, thicken with a little butter.
4. Serve in a gravy dish with the Bouilli, which has been nicely and tastefully garnished with lettuce leaves on a parsley bed.

title: Boiled Beef à la Bordelaise
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 1 Dozen Shallots
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Sauce Espagnole
- Beef Marrow from the Bone
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the left-over beef.
2. Hash the shallots into very fine pieces; add a glass of white wine, pepper and salt to taste, and boil to half the quantity over a brisk fire.
3. Add the mashed beef marrow from the bone and two teaspoonfuls of Sauce Espagnole, first melting the marrow in a little bouillon.
4. Stir rapidly over the fire, and as soon as it begins to bubble, withdraw it and set it back on the stove, letting it simmer gently for a quarter of an hour.
5. Add the sliced beef for about ten minutes and then serve with Croûtons or fried crusts.

title: Boiled Beef à la Bruxelloise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Brussels Sprouts
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take about a dozen Brussels sprouts and blanch them in boiling water.
2. Drain thoroughly and stew in butter with chopped parsley.
3. After they have cooked ten minutes, take them out of the pan and parboil them in fresh butter, which has been melted before the stove.
4. Salt and pepper to taste and garnish nicely around the bouilli and serve.

title: Boiled Beef à la Dyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Sausages (Chaurice)
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Eggs
- Bread Crumbs
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Water
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Make sausage meat of the bouilli, adding the pork sausage, garlic, parsley and thyme.
2. Moisten some bread crumbs in water and dissolve over them two eggs, salt and pepper.
3. Chop the whole and tie it tightly in a cabbage leaf.
4. An hour before serving take out the remaining bouilli and the farci or stuffed cabbage leaf.
5. Let them cool and cut them into slices and roll these in beaten eggs, and then in bread crumbs, and fry in butter.
6. Throw over them a dash of powdered parsley and squeeze over all the juice of a lemon.

title: Boiled Beef à la Marseillaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Small Onions
- ¾ Cup Claret
- ¾ Cup Meat Gravy
- ½ Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- ½ of a Grated Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Slice the bouilli into thin, fine slices.
2. Take a dozen onions, the smallest kind, and dust over with sugar, and bake in the oven.
3. When a good color, put a little of the bouillon in the stewing pan and boil down one half.
4. Moisten with a cup of red wine and thick meat sauce, allowing half and half in proportion.
5. Then add the beef, the mushrooms, the bouquet garni, salt, pepper and a little nutmeg, and serve very hot.

title: Boiled Beef à la Paysanne
body:
## Ingredients

- The Bouilli
- 5 Large Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Flour
- 1 Wineglassful of Claret
- Mustard and Vinegar

## Directions

1. Hash the left-over beef.
2. Chop five large onions very fine and cook them to a golden brown in butter.
3. When nearly done, dust over them a teaspoonful of flour and moisten with a little red wine.
4. Cook the onions till done and then put in the cold hashed beef, adding a dash of French vinegar and a little mustard and serve.

title: Boiled Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Beet Roots
- 2 Quarts of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut off the beet tops and save for boiling or purée or gumbo.
2. Soak the beets in cold fresh water, and wash well, taking off every particle of earth that may adhere.
3. Wash them carefully, without scraping them.
4. Put the beets into a pot of cold water, covering well, and boil until tender.
5. Set them to cool.
6. When cold, slice nicely and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste, and add vinegar, and set aside for an hour, for the vinegar to penetrate thoroughly.
7. Serve as a salad.

title: Boiled Brisket of Beef, Stuffed
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- butter
- salt
- sweet herbs
- mace
- onion
- egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Before boiling the beef make a dressing of bread crumbs, pepper, butter, salt, sweet herbs, mace, and an onion, all chopped fine and mixed with a beaten egg.
2. Put the dressing between the fat and the lean of the beef; sew it up to keep the dressing in.
3. Flour a cloth, tie the beef up tight in it, and let it boil five or six hours.

title: Boiled Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Head of Green and White Cabbage
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Red Pepper Pod
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chili Pepper

## Directions

1. Select a fine white head in which the green and white are prettily mingled
2. Remove all the outside leaves and reject them
3. Cut the cabbage head into quarters and let it soak in cold water for about an hour
4. Drain well and pull off each leaf separately to discover any lurking insects
5. Throw each leaf into a pan of fresh water and drain thoroughly
6. Put into a large pot of boiling water with a pound of ham or salt pork
7. After it has been in the water ten minutes, add a teaspoonful of salt and a red pepper pod cut in two
8. Add a teaspoonful of Chili pepper
9. Cover the cabbage and boil for one hour or more until tender

title: Boiled Cake Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of grated sponge cake
- whites of 8 eggs
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of butter

## Directions

1. To 1 lb. of grated sponge cake, add the whites of 8 eggs and the yolks of 2, 1/2 lb. of butter
2. beat very light and boil it for 2 hours in a mould

title: Boiled Cauliflower, Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Medium-Sized Cauliflowers
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- A Cream Sauce
- 2 Lemons Cut in Quarters

## Directions

1. Pick off the outer leaves of the cauliflower, leaving only the one delicate row near the bottom of the flowerets.
2. Cut the stem close to the flowerets.
3. Wash the cauliflower well in cold fresh water, and then soak, with the head downwards, about twenty minutes, to drain off all possible insects.
4. Have ready a pot of boiling water.
5. Take a nice, clean piece of cheesecloth, and tie the cauliflower in it, to prevent breaking while boiling.
6. Put the cauliflower in the kettle of boiling water, with the stem downwards.
7. Add a teaspoonful of salt, and cover the kettle.
8. Let the cauliflower boil from thirty to forty minutes, according to size, or until the vegetable is tender.
9. When cooked, lift it gently out by the cheesecloth, untie and set it in a dish, stem downward.
10. Pour over it a Cream Sauce and serve hot.
11. Place on each plate, when serving, a quarter of a lemon, nicely cut.

title: Boiled Chestnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chestnuts
- 1 Pint of Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. Prepare and boil the chestnuts according to above directions
2. When done, drain and press through a colander
3. The chestnuts must be cooked very soft for this dish
4. Add a Drawn Butter Sauce and sprinkle with salt and pepper or a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel
5. Serve as an entrée, like mashed potatoes

title: Boiled Chicken
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- white cloth
- boiling water
- celery sauce, oyster sauce, or egg sauce
- boiled tongue or ham

## Directions

1. After the chickens are cleaned and trussed fold them in a nice white cloth, put them in a large stew-pan and cover them with boiling water
2. Boil them gently, and skim carefully as long as any scum rises; let them simmer slowly as that will make them plump and white
3. When done lay them on a hot dish, and pour celery, oyster, or egg sauce over them
4. Serve some sauce also in a boat, as it keeps hot longer than when poured over the fowls
5. Boiled tongue or ham should be served with boiled chicken
6. If the chicken is not very tough, an hour or an hour and a quarter is sufficient to boil it

title: Boiled Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- A Spring Chicken
- Drawn Butter Sauce
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select a nice spring chicken, clean and singe and split down the middle of the back.
2. Season with salt and pepper, rubbing well on the inside of the chicken.
3. Place in a saucepan and cover well with water and let it simmer well for one hour if the chicken is young.
4. If the chicken is a year old and over, let it simmer for two hours, according to age.
5. When done take out of the water and place in a heated dish.
6. Pour over a Drawn Butter Sauce and garnish with chopped parsley.

title: Boiled Chickens with Stuffing
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- stuffing
- wheat flour
- boiling water
- hard-boiled egg sauce, parsley sauce, or oyster sauce

## Directions

1. Truss and stuff the chicken as for roasting, dredge it all over with wheat flour, and put it in a pot of boiling water
2. Take the pot off the fire for five minutes after the chicken is put in, or the skin will crack
3. Let it boil gently according to its age and weight, an old fowl requiring twice as long to boil as a young one; allow fifteen minutes to the pound
4. Take off all the scum as it rises, and when done serve with hard-boiled egg sauce, or parsley, or oyster sauce

title: Boiled Codfish
body:
## Ingredients

- Codfish
- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Gills of Fresh Cream or Milk
- The Oyster Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Soak the Codfish overnight, then boil about thirty or forty minutes and drain.
2. Scald the oysters in their own water about three minutes.
3. Make a Cream Sauce using the strained juice of the oyster to blend the flour and butter, and add the rich cream or milk to make up the desired quantity.
4. Add the oysters to the sauce, mixing thoroughly, seasoning with salt, pepper and Cayenne, preferably celery salt.
5. Let it boil up once and serve with the boiled Codfish.

title: Boiled Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Sprigs of Sweet Basil
- 4 Sprigs of Sweet Marjoram
- 3 Blades of Mace
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 4 Sprigs of Chives
- A Red Pepper Pod
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Black Pepper and Salt Sufficient to Make a Brine
- Bunches of Celery Tops
- Stalks of Celery Chopped Fine
- Four or Five Large Sprigs Each of Thyme
- Live Crabs

## Directions

1. Buy fine, large crabs. The livelier they are the better. The crabs must be alive when put into the pot.
2. Have ready a large pot of water.
3. Throw in bunches of celery tops, stalks of celery chopped fine, four or five large sprigs each of thyme, chopped sweet basil, marjoram, chives, two dozen allspice, three blades of mace, three bay leaves chopped fine; a pod of red pepper, a dash of Cayenne, black pepper and salt enough to make the water briny.
4. When this has boiled long enough to have extracted all the flavor of the herbs, throw in the live crabs and let them boil rapidly for about ten minutes, or until the shells are a bright red, but do not let them boil one minute longer than this, as they will become watery.
5. Let them cool a little in their own water and then take out, strip off the "dead man's fingers," crack the claws, without breaking open, and pile high in a broad platter and serve with salt and pepper.

title: Boiled Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Crabs
- 1 Bunch of Celery and Celery Tops
- 2 Dozen Allspice
- 4 Sprigs of Thyme
- 4 Sprigs of Sweet Basil
- 4 Sprigs of Sweet Marjoram
- 3 Blades of Mace
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 4 Sprigs of Chives
- A Red Pepper Pod
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Black Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Buy fine, large crabs. The livelier they are the better.
2. Have ready a large pot of water.
3. Throw in bunches of celery tops, stalks of celery chopped fine, four or five large sprigs each of thyme, chopped sweet basil, marjoram, chives, two dozen allspice, three blades of mace, three bay leaves chopped fine; a pod of red pepper, a dash of Cayenne, black pepper and salt enough to make a brine.
4. Proceed in the same manner as for boiling shrimp.

title: Boiled Crawfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Crawfish
- 1 Gallon of Water
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Half Gallon of French Vinegar
- chopped garlic
- one dozen allspice
- six cloves
- white wine or vinegar
- salt
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Put the water on to boil, adding the chopped herb bouquet, one clove of garlic chopped fine, one dozen allspice and six cloves
2. When the water has boiled long enough to have extracted all the juices of the herb bouquet, add white wine or the vinegar and salt enough to make it almost briny, and Cayenne enough to make it hot
3. Throw in the crawfish and let them boil about twenty minutes or until a bright red
4. Set them to cool in their own water and serve on a platter piled high in pyramidal shape, and garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon
5. Serve with salt and pepper, oil and Chili vinegar, each person making the dressing as it suits his taste

title: Boiled Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light.
3. Beat the whites to a stiff froth, and stir carefully into the yolks and the sugar.
4. Moisten the cornstarch with a little cold milk, and stir into the boiling milk.
5. Continue to stir until it commences to thicken.
6. Then add the sugar and the eggs.
7. Stir and let it cook for one moment longer, and take from the fire.
8. Add the vanilla, and turn into a dish to cool.
9. Serve very cold.

title: Boiled Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- salt
- flour
- milk
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean and pluck them, preserving the skin from rents
2. Salt them for 30 hours previous to cooking
3. Flour a clean white cloth and boil them in it
4. A moderate sized duck will take about 1 hour's boiling
5. Make a rich onion sauce with milk
6. Send it to table with the duck

title: Boiled Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Large, Fine, Rosy Apples
- A Plain Paste
- Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a Plain Paste and cut into squares.
2. Put in the center of each a fine, juicy apple, pared and cored, or a half apple, pared and cored.
3. Wrap the fruit up in the dough and place these in small dumpling cloths.
4. Put them into boiling water and let them boil for at least an hour.
5. On taking out the apple, if so desired, the inner core may be filled with some delicate jelly or marmalade.
6. Serve with Hard, Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Boiled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Have ready a saucepan of boiling water.
2. Use only fresh eggs.
3. Put them in the boiling water without cracking the shells.
4. If you desire soft-boiled eggs, let the eggs boil from two minutes to two minutes and a half by the clock, keeping the exact time, minute by minute. The whites will then be set.
5. If you desire the yolk to be set also in the soft-boiled egg, let the eggs boil three minutes, but not a second longer.
6. For hard-boiled eggs, five minutes is sufficient.
7. Bear in mind always that the water must be boiling hard before you put the egg into it, and that the exact time for boiling must be followed by the clock or with the watch in hand.

title: Boiled Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- sprig of onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- eight or ten cloves
- about two dozen allspice
- bit of lemon peel
- red pepper pod
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the fish thoroughly.
2. Make a small letter 'S' with knife on the back.
3. Pass twine around the body of the fish so as to secure it.
4. Have ready a kettle of boiling water and throw in a sprig of onion, thyme and bay leaf, eight or ten cloves, about two dozen allspice, all mashed fine; a bit of lemon peel and a red pepper pod.
5. When the water has boiled long enough to have extracted the flavor of these ingredients, drop the fish in carefully, so as to avoid breaking.
6. Let it boil about ten minutes and then take out carefully.
7. Put into a strainer and drain quickly.
8. Place on a bed of parsley with garnishes of lemon and serve either a Mayonnaise or Genoise Sauce or Sauce Hollandaise.

title: Boiled Fowls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- flour
- cold water
- parsley
- butter
- oysters or celery sauce

## Directions

1. Flour a white cloth and put the fowls in cold water
2. Let them simmer for 1 of an hour
3. Serve with parsley and butter, or oysters or celery sauce

title: Boiled Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh young Green Peas
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Shell the peas, and when you have a pint, put the peas into cold water, drain and put into a saucepan of boiling water.
2. Add a teaspoonful of salt to prevent the peas from cracking, and let them boil rapidly for at least twenty minutes.
3. To ascertain if they are done, take one out and press with a fork.
4. As soon as done, drain off all water; put a large tablespoonful of butter into the saucepan with the peas, season with pepper to taste, pour into a vegetable dish and serve hot.

title: Boiled Green Trout
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Medium-Sized Trout, or 3 Large Ones
- A Kettle of Boiling Water
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Onion
- A Clove of Garlic (if desired)
- 10 Cloves
- 2 Dozen Allspice
- A Seedless Red Pepper Pod
- A Bit of Lemon Peel
- Parsley and Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Select fine fresh Trout
2. Clean and wash the fish thoroughly
3. Make a small letter S with knife on the back; pass twine around the body of the fish so as to secure it
4. Have ready a kettle of boiling water and throw in a sprig of onion, thyme and bay leaf, eight or ten cloves, about two dozen allspice, all mashed fine; a bit of lemon peel and a red pepper pod
5. When the water has boiled long enough to have extracted the flavor of these ingredients, drop the fish in carefully, so as to avoid breaking
6. Let it boil about ten minutes and then take out carefully
7. Put into a strainer and drain quickly
8. Place on a platter on a bed of parsley, with chopped parsley thrown over, and garnish with parsley and sliced lemon
9. Serve with a drawn butter Hollandaise Sauce, or Sauce Genoïse

title: Boiled Grits
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Grits
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Salt

## Directions

1. Wash the grits in fresh cold water, and throw off the refuse
2. Wash again and drain
3. Into two quarts of cold water put the grits
4. Add the salt, and stir frequently while they are coming to the boiling point
5. Set back on the stove and let them cook slowly for about an hour
6. Serve hot with any meat, with gravy, or serve with milk, as oatmeal, as a preliminary to breakfast, or simply eat with butter

title: Boiled Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- A Ham
- 2 Blades of Mace
- 1 Dozen Cloves
- 4 Bay Leaves
- Black Pepper and Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Wash the ham well in cold water, scraping off all portions of mold or salt.
2. Have a large boiler of water on the stove; or, better still, the furnace.
3. Throw in two blades of mace, a dozen cloves and three or four bay leaves.
4. Put the ham in the water and let the fire be slow, allowing the water to heat gradually.
5. Do not permit it to come to a good boil for two hours at least, and be careful to skim carefully, so that all rejected substances may not impregnate the ham.
6. Keep it simmering gently, allowing twenty minutes to every pound.
7. When done, let the ham cool in its own liquor, and then put the ham on a board, cover with another board, and lay a weight over.
8. Leave under weight several hours.
9. Carefully remove the skin without taking off the fat.
10. Sprinkle it in patches with black pepper and ornament the shank bone with quilled paper, or a paper frill.
11. Serve it cold with a garnish of parsley.

title: Boiled Hominy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Hominy
- 2 Quarts of Water
- Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Hominy should always be soaked over night in cold water.
2. Wash the hominy, and put into two quarts of water to soak.
3. In the morning turn both hominy and water into a saucepan, and let it boil slowly for three or four hours.
4. Serve with sugar and cream.
5. It may also be eaten with butter and salt and pepper.

title: Boiled Icing
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and one-half lbs. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- whites of 7 eggs

## Directions

1. One and one-half lbs. of loaf sugar, boiled in 1/2 pint of water until it ropes
2. Beat the whites of 7 eggs to a stiff froth
3. Put the syrup on to boil, and stir it until it is milk-warm
4. Put in the eggs, and beat the whole 1 hour

title: Boiled Icing, Very Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of white sugar
- a cup of water
- whites of three eggs
- flour or corn starch
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil until very thick, a pound of white sugar in a cup of water
2. It should be as thick as for candy
3. when boiled, pour it gently on the beaten whites of three eggs
4. Beat this rapidly until well mixed with the eggs
5. flour the cake with flour or corn starch, and ice it with a knife in the usual way
6. You must use flavoring to suit your taste

title: Boiled Leg of Mutton, Caper Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 leg of mutton
- 1 herb bouquet
- salt and pepper to taste
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Rub the leg of mutton well with salt and pepper
2. Have ready a pot of boiling water, into which you have thrown the herbs, bay leaf, salt and pepper, allowing a teaspoonful each of the two latter ingredients
3. Put the leg of mutton into the water, being very careful to have it well covered with water, else the meat will blacken
4. Let it boil gently but steadily, allowing fifteen minutes to every pound of meat
5. When done, place on a dish and serve with a Caper Sauce
6. In serving slice nicely and put a few drops of lemon on each slice, and pour over the Caper sauce

title: Boiled Macaroni, Italian Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Macaroni
- 1 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into convenient lengths, and set to boil in a kettle filled with boiling water and in which you have thrown a spoonful of salt and black pepper.
2. Be careful to keep the lengths of macaroni firm.
3. When cooked till tender, take off and strain the water.
4. Take one tablespoonful of butter and one of flour and put them on the fire, blending well.
5. Have one pound of Parmesan cheese grated; add one-half of it to the flour and butter, and one pint of the water in which the macaroni was boiled; the mixture must not be allowed to brown; stir briskly.
6. Place the macaroni by lengths into a dish, season well with salt and pepper, and warm a few minutes in the oven.
7. When warm, take out the dish and sprinkle over it one-quarter of the pound of cheese that still remains.
8. Pour the hot sauce over this, and sprinkle the rest of the cheese on top.
9. Serve hot.

title: Boiled Okra
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- okra
- fresh butter
- pepper
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Use small and tender okra
2. Wash them, and cut off a small piece from each end
3. Boil them till very tender throughout
4. Drain well, and transfer them to a deep dish
5. Lay among them some bits of fresh butter, and season with pepper
6. Cover the dish, that the butter may melt the sooner
7. Or make a sauce of 1 pint of milk boiled, then add 1 lb. of good fresh butter, divided into 4 pieces, each piece rolled in a little flour
8. Stir the butter in gradually and smoothly as the milk is taken off the fire
9. Pour this sauce over the dish of okra, and keep it covered till it has gone to table

title: Boiled Okra
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Young Okra
- 1 Tablespoonful Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A pint of water
- A teaspoonful of salt
- Tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the okra well in cold water, and put in a porcelain-lined or agate saucepan
2. Add a pint of water and a teaspoonful of salt
3. Cover the pot, and let the okra simmer for about half an hour
4. Take from the pot, season with salt and pepper to taste
5. Pour over a tablespoonful of Tarragon vinegar and set to cool
6. Serve as a salad, and with all meats, such as daube, roast, etc.

title: Boiled Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Nice, Small White Onions
- 1 Tablespoonfal Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Throw the onions in their skins into cold water, and peel them.
2. Then put them into a saucepan of boiling water.
3. Add a teaspoonful of salt, and let them boil about forty minutes, or until you can pierce them easily with a fork.
4. Then put into a dish, and drain off all water.
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste and pour over a Drawn Butter Sauce (see Beurre a la Maitre d'Hotel), and serve hot.
6. The large Spanish onions will require about an hour longer to boil tender.

title: Boiled Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Parsnips
- 1/2 Pint of Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. If parsnips are young, simply scrape them and lay them in cold water.
2. If the parsnips are old, pare them and cut them in quarters, or, better still, split lengthwise.
3. Let the young parsnips cook in salted boiling water in a porcelain-lined saucepan for forty-five minutes; let the older ones cook for an hour and a quarter.
4. When done, take them out of the saucepan, and drain and serve on a heated dish with a Drawn Butter Sauce.

title: Boiled Parsnips With Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Parsnips
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as above in peeling and boiling the parsnips.
2. When done, drain and put into a heated dish, and serve with a Cream Sauce poured over them.
3. Serve the parsnips with boiled salt or fresh fish or boiled corn beef.

title: Boiled Perch
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fresh Perch
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic (if desired)
- 10 Cloves
- 2 Dozen Allspice
- A Bit of Lemon Peel
- 1 Red Pepper Pod, Without the Seed
- Parsley
- Lemon
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Select fine, fresh Perch and clean and wash the fish well
2. Make a small letter S with a knife on the back
3. Pass a piece of twine around the body, so as to secure it
4. Have ready a kettle of boiling water, and throw in a sprig of onion, thyme and bay leaf, the cloves and allspice, mashed fine, a bit of lemon peel and the red pepper pod
5. When the water has boiled long enough to have fully extracted the flavor of these ingredients, drop the fish in carefully, so as to avoid breaking
6. Let it boil ten minutes, and then take out carefully and drain quickly in a strainer
7. Place in a dish on a bed of parsley, sprinkle chopped parsley over it and garnish prettily with parsley and sliced lemon
8. Serve with a Drawn Butter Sauce or Sauce Genoise

title: Boiled Plum Pudding. Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of grated bread crumbs
- a pint of boiling milk
- a pound of chopped suet
- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- half a pound of sifted flour
- one dozen eggs
- one pound of raisins
- one pound of currants
- half a pound of citron
- one tablespoonful of ground cinnamon
- one tablespoonful of cloves
- one tablespoonful of allspice
- one grated nutmeg
- a glass of brandy
- the rind and juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Prepare all the ingredients except the beating of the eggs, the day before making the pudding
2. Take one pound of grated bread crumbs, pour over them a pint of boiling milk
3. Add a pound of chopped suet, half a pound of butter, one pound of sugar, half a pound of sifted flour, one dozen eggs, one pound of raisins, one pound of currants, half a pound of citron
4. Add one tablespoonful of ground cinnamon, one of cloves and allspice, also one grated nutmeg, a glass of brandy, the rind and juice of two lemons
5. Tie it in a piece of thick, unbleached cotton, allowing room for the pudding to swell
6. Boil five hours
7. Serve with butter and sugar sauce

title: Boiled Potatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Put potatoes in just water enough to cover them, with the skins on
2. Boil them constantly till done
3. Squeeze them in a dry cloth
4. Send them to the table very hot

title: Boiled Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients
