- 6 Potatoes, of Uniform Size, If Possible
- Boiling Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash and scrub the potatoes well, and put them on in their jackets in a pot of boiling water, which has been well salted.
2. Let them cook until they are soft enough to be pierced with a fork.
3. When done, take them out and drain dry.
4. Put into steamer, sprinkle with salt, and cover and let them stand over the kettle (lid open) on the fire for a few minutes for the water to evaporate.
5. After five minutes take off and peel quickly, and serve in a covered vegetable dish.

title: Boiled Red Fish or Boiled Red Snapper
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Redfish or Red Snapper
- 1 Sliced Onion
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Tablespoonful of French Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Hollandaise Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and thoroughly clean the fish, and then lay on a pan and open the flesh on either side by making the letter S with the knife. This is done to prevent the fish from puffing out or drawing up, and to insure thorough cooking and a perfect shape afterwards.
2. Tie the fish well with cord or twine, wrapping it around the body.
3. Never encase a boiled fish in a cloth; put the fish in a deep saucepan and add boiling water sufficient for the fish to swim in it.
4. Throw in a tablespoonful of spice (well mashed), a sliced onion, three bay leaves, a sprig of thyme and sweet marjoram, a sprig of parsley (all chopped very fine), a tablespoonful of French vinegar, and salt and pepper to taste.
5. Cover well and let it boil for ten minutes.
6. After ten minutes, the fish is cooked. Prepare a Cream Sauce and serve immediately.

title: Boiled Rice, Italian Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- A Slice of Breakfast Bacon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Grated Parmesan
- 1 Pinch Saffron
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Wash one cup of rice.
2. Take boiling water, using about a quart.
3. Add a slice of bacon and a tablespoonful of grated Parmesan cheese, and a pinch of saffron.
4. Let it boil well for five minutes.
5. Then add the rice gradually, continuing to cook according to the recipe for boiled rice.
6. When done, remove the bacon, dot the top with bits of butter, set in the stove to dry for ten minutes, and serve hot.

title: Boiled Salt Mackerel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salt mackerel
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- Chopped parsley
- Sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Soak the mackerel overnight, and in the morning take out of the water, wash thoroughly, taking off every portion of salt, and wash again.
2. Have ready a deep pan of boiling water; place the mackerel within and let it boil ten or fifteen minutes until done, which can be known by the flesh beginning to part from the bones.
3. Serve whole on a platter garnished with parsley.
4. Pour over the mackerel melted butter and chopped parsley, and bring to the table very hot.

title: Boiled Sheepshead
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Sheepshead
- 1 Sliced Onion
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Tablespoonful French Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and thoroughly clean the fish, and then lay on a pan and open the flesh on either side by making the letter "S" with the knife
2. Tie the fish well with cord or twine, wrapping it around the body
3. Put the fish in a deep saucepan and add boiling water sufficient for the fish to swim in it
4. Throw in a tablespoonful of spice (well mashed), a sliced onion, three bay leaves, a sprig of parsley (all chopped very fine), a tablespoonful of French vinegar, and salt and pepper to taste
5. Cover well and let it boil for ten minutes
6. Prepare a Cream Sauce and serve immediately

title: Boiled Shrimps
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Fine River Shrimp
- A Large Bunch of Celery and Celery Tops
- 2 Dozen Allspice
- 2 Blades of Mace
- 1 Dozen Cloves
- 4 Sprigs each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1 Red Pepper Pod
- Cayenne
- Black Pepper
- Salt
- Parsley Sprigs to Garnish

## Directions

1. Select fine large River shrimp for this purpose
2. Into a pot of water put a great quantity of salt, almost enough to make a brine
3. Pepper a great bunch of celery and celery tops, chopped fine
4. Add two dozen allspice, two blades of mace, one dozen cloves, mashed fine
5. Add thyme, parsley, bay leaf, chopped fine
6. Add Cayenne and a red pepper pod
7. When this has boiled so that all the flavor of the herbs have been thoroughly extracted, throw in the shrimp
8. Let them boil ten minutes and then set the pot aside and let the shrimp cool in their own water
9. Serve in a platter on a bed of cracked ice, and garnish with parsley sprigs

title: Boiled Spaghetti, Italian Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Spaghetti
- 1 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Spaghetti a l'Italienne is prepared in the same manner as Boiled Macaroni, Italian Style.
2. Spaghetti is a more delicate form of macaroni.

title: Boiled Sponge Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound of loaf sugar
- nearly half a pint of water
- the peel of a lemon
- eight eggs, yolks only
- four eggs, whites only
- ten ounces of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Put loaf sugar in a stew pan with nearly half a pint of water and thin-cut lemon peel
2. Let it simmer twenty minutes
3. Beat the yolks of eight eggs and the whites of four for ten minutes
4. Pour in the boiling syrup and beat it well for half an hour
5. Grease your cake pan well and put a paper in the bottom
6. Stir ten ounces of sifted flour gently into the mixture
7. Pour the batter immediately into the pan
8. Bake in a rather quick oven about half an hour
9. Have the oven in baking order before you put the flour in

title: Boiled Sweet Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Drawn Butter Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well, using a cloth or brush, and removing every particle of earth
2. Clip the edges of the roots that adhere
3. Put them to boil in a kettle of boiling water, and let them cook till they may be easily pierced with a fork
4. Drain off the water, and set the kettle on the back of the stove
5. Cover and let the potatoes steam a few minutes
6. Take them out after five minutes or so, and peel and serve them with Drawn Butter Sauce, or cut in slices and spread butter over them, and set in the oven for a few seconds, and serve hot with daubes, fish, grillades, etc.

title: Boiled Trout
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large fresh trout
- salt
- boiled parsley
- melted butter
- catsup

## Directions

1. Buy large fresh trout, clean very nicely, wash them well
2. sprinkle well with salt
3. boil them for 15 minutes
4. when done, send in on a drainer, with boiled parsley over them
5. Use melted butter and catsup as a dressing

title: Boiled Turkey and Celery Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey hen
- two pounds of fat veal dressing
- veal bones
- turkey giblets
- carrot
- onions
- head of celery
- sweet herbs
- puree of celery or oyster sauce

## Directions

1. Draw a fine, young turkey hen, and remove the angular part of the breast bone.
2. Take two pounds of fat veal dressing and stuff the turkey with it.
3. Put over the fire to cook the veal, bones, and turkey giblets, to make some white soup stock; season this and let it boil until you want to put the turkey on to cook.
4. Now truss your turkey and put it in a boiling pot with a carrot, two onions, a head of celery, and a bunch of sweet herbs.
5. Pour over the turkey the stock from the veal and giblets; cover with it, if enough; if not, put in water to cover it and set it to boil.
6. When it has boiled one hour, put it on the back of the stove, and let it simmer and braise, until dinner.
7. Take off any strings that may look badly; dish it up.
8. Pour over it a well-made puree of celery, or oyster sauce, and send to table.

title: Boiled Turkey with Oyster Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- oysters
- bread crumbs
- butter
- mace
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and truss it the same as for baking.
2. Stuff the turkey with oysters, bread crumbs, butter and mace, all mixed and seasoned.
3. Put it on the fire in a kettle of water not hot, but slightly warm; do not drop it into boiling water or it will break the skin and spoil the appearance of the turkey.
4. Cover it close, and when the scum rises take it off.
5. Let the boiling continue for one hour, then put the pot containing the turkey on the coolest part of the stove, and let it simmer for half an hour.
6. Serve with oyster sauce in a sauce boat.

title: Boiled Turnip Tops a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Peck of Turnip Greens
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt
- 1 Pound of Fresh Pork or 1 Pound of Salt Pork
- Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the turnip tops, and put into a kettle of boiling water with a piece of fresh or salt pork.
2. Let them boil slowly till tender, and then season well with salt and pepper.
3. When tender, take out and chop, but not too fine, or leave them just as they are.
4. Drain of all water, and serve as you would boiled cabbage, piling the turnip tops around the dish, and the salt meat or pork in the center.

title: Bombe Glacé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- 1/2 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract
- 1/2 Pint of Strawberry Sherbet

## Directions

1. Line Bombe Glacé molds about three-fourths of an inch thick with Strawberry Water Ice
2. Fill the center with the Biscuit Glacés prepared as above
3. Ensure the biscuit is freezing cold when put into the mold
4. Pack in salt and ice, and set to freeze several hours

title: Boned Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- white veal
- fat bacon
- mushrooms
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- sweet herbs
- egg yolks
- truffles
- boiled smoked tongue
- calf's udder or veal
- turkey or capons or fowls
- cloth
- bones
- calf's feet or gelatine
- onions
- cloves
- green onions
- sweet basil
- thyme
- mace
- pepper corns
- wine or brandy

## Directions

1. Chop up one pound of white veal, with a pound of fat bacon
2. Season high with chopped mushrooms, parsley, pepper, salt, and a bunch of sweet herbs
3. When chopped fine, pound them in a mortar or pass them through a sausage grinder
4. Add to this the yolks of three eggs, and place it by in a basin for use
5. Peel a pound of truffles, and cut up a boiled smoked tongue, a pound of fat bacon, or a pound of calf's udder or veal
6. Bone a turkey, or two fine capons, or fowls, and draw the skin from the legs and pinions inside
7. Take the turkey on a napkin
8. Cut slices from the thick breast and place it on the skin where it seems to be thin
9. Distribute the flesh of the fowl as evenly as you can on the skin
10. Season it slightly with pepper and salt
11. Spread a layer of the prepared force-meat in the basin, let it be an inch thick
12. Place the cut-up tongue, bacon and veal
13. Lay a row of chopped truffles and a layer of the force-meat until the skin is covered, or as full as it will hold
14. Sew it up the back, the ends tied, like a cushion, or roly-poly
15. Butter a cloth and put it tightly over the turkey skin
16. Tie it up tightly
17. Place it in a round stew-pan with the bones and any trimmings of veal or poultry at hand
18. Add to it two boiled calf's feet, or an ounce of gelatine
19. Add two onions stuck with four cloves, a bunch of parsley, six green onions, a bunch of sweet basil, and a bunch of thyme, two blades of mace, and a dozen pepper corns, or whole peppers
20. Moisten all with half a pint of wine or brandy

title: Boned Turkey Gelatine Truffee a la Gelée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Turkey Hen
- 2 Pounds of Young Veal
- 1 Pound of Fat Fresh Pork
- 1 Pound of Lean Fresh Pork
- 1/2 Pound of Cooper's Gelatine
- 1/2 Can of Truffles
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Minced Parsley and Thyme
- 3 Minced Bay Leaves
- 1 Lemon
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 1 Glass of Brandy
- 1 Wineglass of Sherry
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Turnip
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 2 Gallons of Water
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Each of Grated Cinnamon and Allspice
- 1/2 Nutmeg
- Salt and Black Pepper
- 2 Raw Eggs
- 1 Onion

## Directions

1. Select a young hen turkey that has been hand-picked and not scalded
2. Clean it thoroughly and place on table with breast down
3. Cut the turkey open from the neck to the rump down the backbone with a sharp knife
4. Run the knife between the bones and flesh to the wings, unjointing and separating bones from body without breaking flesh
5. Remove each bone as you reach the joint, leaving the small bones in the wing tips
6. Carefully slit out the bones of the leg and run the knife between bones and flesh to the breast bone
7. Skillfully separate the flesh from the bone and remove the carcass
8. Spread out the turkey and wipe inside and out with a damp towel
9. Rub well with salt and pepper inside and out, then set aside in a cool place
10. Take two pounds of young veal, one pound of young fat fresh pork, and one pound of lean fresh pork and mince as fine as possible
11. Season the minced meat with one-half grated nutmeg, one tablespoonful minced parsley, one tablespoonful minced thyme, three minced bay leaves, one teaspoonful salt, one teaspoonful black pepper, one teaspoonful grated cinnamon, one-quarter teaspoonful grated allspice, and the juice of one onion
12. Mix thoroughly and add two raw beaten eggs, one wineglass of Sherry, and one of Brandy, stirring well
13. Add one-quarter of a box of chopped truffles and mix well
14. Lay the turkey open and carefully cut a layer of meat in nice slices from the inner part
15. Put in a thick layer of stuffing, then a layer of the meat using the whole liver sliced in strips as alternate layers
16. Put in the rest of the stuffing as a layer and bring the turkey together
17. Sew up the turkey so it retains its original shape
18. Roll the turkey in a clean towel and tie it securely at both ends and around the middle
19. Take all the bones of the turkey, skinned feet, cleaned head, and place in a large pot
20. Add two pounds of veal cut in pieces and two calves' feet
21. Put in two carrots, one turnip, several sprigs of thyme and parsley, three bay leaves, and a large piece of celery
22. Add two gallons of water and let boil very hard for an hour
23. Add the turkey tied in the towel and let it boil for two hours
24. Remove the turkey from the towel and roll the towel out very smoothly again
25. Place the turkey back in it while hot and roll carefully again, tying at both ends and across the middle
26. Place on a table with a board on top and a fifteen-pound weight over it
27. Leave in a cool place but not in the ice box to cool gradually and naturally
28. After five or six hours you may put it in the ice box
29. Let the pot with water and bones continue boiling for two hours longer
30. Strain the whole through a strainer into another pan, then strain through a towel to remove cinnamon, herbs, and dregs
31. Skim off all grease from the top and put on fire again to simmer
32. Add one lemon cut in four or five pieces and season with salt to taste
33. Put one-quarter pound of Cooper's gelatine in a bowl and add one pint of water in which the turkey was boiled
34. Stir well and let the gelatine melt
35. Pour the melted gelatine into the boiling mixture and beat as you would a cake, mixing thoroughly
36. Break three raw eggs in another bowl and mash the shells and add
37. Add one small wineglass of water and beat well again as you would a cake
38. Prepare a flannel bag of size sufficient to hold a half gallon
39. Pour the eggs into the broth beating very thoroughly and rapidly through and through for ten minutes while it remains on the fire
40. When the mixture begins to boil and curdle, take off the fire and strain in the flannel bag
41. Let this fall into tin cups or cans and set on ice
42. The next morning it will be hard
43. Unroll the turkey and put it in a dish
44. Cut the jelly from the can into fancy shapes and ornament the turkey with it, placing a fancy border around the dish

title: Bordelaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Shallots
- 1/2 Glass Claret
- 3/4 Pint of Sauce Espagnole
- A Dash of Red Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut two shallots very fine; put in a saucepan with a half glassful of Claret; reduce one-half.
2. Add three-quarters of a pint of good Sauce Espagnole (see recipe) and a dash of red pepper.
3. Cook for twenty minutes and serve hot.
4. In serving this sauce, the flavor may be increased by adding a dozen round slices of blanched Marrons.

title: Bordelaise Sauce, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Onion or 2 Shallots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Peel the onion or shallots and chop fine.
2. Put in a saucepan with one tablespoonful of olive oil; let the onion sauté well.
3. Pour the sauce over tenderloin filets or sirloin steaks when it is desired to serve these a la Bordelaise.
4. A tablespoonful of Red Wine may be added to the sauce.

title: Boston Brown Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pints of Indian meal
- half a pint of wheat flour
- one cup of sweet milk
- one cup of sour milk
- a teaspoonful of soda
- three tablespoonfuls of molasses
- one tablespoonful of yeast
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Mix one and a half pints of Indian meal, half a pint of wheat flour, one cup of sweet milk, one cup of sour milk with a teaspoonful of soda in it, three tablespoonfuls of molasses, one tablespoonful of yeast, and a pinch of salt
2. Put it in a warm place to rise
3. Let it bake steadily for four hours
4. Warm by steaming it when wanted to use

title: Boston Caramels
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint bowl of bakers' chocolate grated
- two bowls of yellow sugar
- one bowl of New Orleans molasses
- one half a cup of milk
- a piece of butter the size of a small egg
- vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil about twenty-five minutes
2. Pour in buttered tins
3. Mark deeply with a knife

title: Bouches de Dames
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract
- 1 Cup of Currant or Other Jelly for Filling
- Transparent Icing to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare a sponge or cup cake batter.
2. Have ready a baking sheet, which you will cover with white writing paper.
3. Lay the batter out on the paper by forcing it through a biscuit cutter in rounds that will measure one inch and a half in diameter.
4. Dredge the surface with white sugar very lightly.
5. Bake them for about fifteen minutes or less, until they are a light color.
6. Trim nicely and place them upon a wire drainer.
7. Place circular pieces of currant or other preserves on top.
8. Garnish with a meringue or Transparent Icing.
9. Set in the stove or the sun to dry, and in ten minutes they will be ready to serve.

title: Bouchés With Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- The Juice of 1 Orange or 1 Tablespoonful of Extract
- 1 Cup of Orange Marmalade
- Transparent Icing to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake drops as directed above, with sponge or cup cake batter
2. Place thin slices of oranges cooked in syrup on the Bouchés
3. Gloss them over with Transparent Icing
4. Flavor the batter of these Bouchés with the zest of an orange or with Curaçoa Liqueur

title: Bouchés With Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Noyau Extract
- 1 Cup of Preserved Peaches
- Transparent Icing to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the drop cakes as directed in Bouchés de Dames
2. Place halves of peaches, preserved in syrup, over them
3. Gloss over with Transparent Icing, flavored with Noyau, and slightly tinged with Carmine
4. Do not allow any excess of moisture from the preserves
5. The syrup should become entirely absorbed into the bouchés before you attempt to gloss it with the icing

title: Bouchés With Pineapples
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 1 Cup of Preserved Pineapples
- Transparent Icing to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the drop cakes one inch and a half in diameter
2. Place smaller slices of thin, preserved pineapple upon each
3. Gloss or glacer over with Transparent Icing, flavoring with pineapple syrup

title: Boudin Blanc
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of the White Meat of Fowl (left over)
- 1 Pound of Lean Pork
- 1 Pound of Fat Pork
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1/4 Cup of Soft Bread
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1/8 Teaspoon of Ground Spices
- 1/4 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Onion
- 1 Teaspoonful Cayenne
- Salt and Pepper, 1 Teaspoonful Each

## Directions

1. Cut the meat and mince
2. Season highly with the salt and pepper and Cayenne
3. Add the minced onion and garlic and mix well
4. Mix with half a cup of the soft bread, wet and squeezed well
5. Cook all for about fifteen minutes in one pint of cream
6. When reduced take off the stove
7. Add the beaten yolks of two eggs and stir well
8. Cool the mixture
9. Fill the prepared entrails and tie either end
10. Place them in a pot containing half milk and half water
11. Boil them for about twenty minutes
12. Prick gently, place in buttered papers and broil gently

title: Boudins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Hog or Beef Blood (1 pint)
- 1 1/2 Pound of Hog Fat
- 2 Onions
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Season Highly
- 1/4 Clove of Garlic
- 1/2 teaspoonful each of Allspice, Mace, Clove and Nutmeg, finely ground
- 1/2 teaspoonful each of Fine Herbs

## Directions

1. Mince the onions fine and fry them slightly in a small piece of the hog fat
2. Add the minced garlic
3. Hash and mince the remaining fat very fine
4. Mix the fat thoroughly with the beef blood
5. Mix in the onions and season highly with the spices and fine herbs
6. Take the prepared casings or entrails and fill with the mixture
7. Tie the sausage casing at the further end before attempting to fill
8. Tie the other end, making the sausage into strings of about two feet
9. Wash them thoroughly on the outside after filling
10. Tie again in spaces of three inches or less in length
11. Place them to cook in a pot of tepid water, never letting them boil
12. Let them remain on the slow fire till you can prick the sausage with a needle and no blood will exude
13. Take them out, let them dry and cool
14. Fry in boiling lard or broil to serve

title: Boudins à la Reine
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Chicken
- 2 Small Onions
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Place the mixture in a frying pan and fry in butter, using about a tablespoonful
2. Add about one pint of milk
3. Beat the chicken thoroughly in this
4. Add a grated nutmeg
5. Take off the fire and add two eggs, well beaten
6. Fill custard cups with the mixture
7. Place in the oven setting in a pan of boiling water and covering with paper
8. Bake for twenty minutes
9. Take off the paper and let them brown
10. Serve hot

title: Bouillabaisse
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Red Snapper
- 6 Slices of Redfish
- 1/2 Bottle of White Wine
- 1/2 Lemon
- 6 Large Fresh Tomatoes, or 1/2 Can
- 3 Onions
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 3 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 6 Allspices
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1 Good Strong Pinch of Saffron
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut off the head of the Red Snapper and boil it in about one and a half quarts of water to make a fish stock.
2. Put one sliced onion and a herb bouquet consisting of thyme, bay leaf and parsley into the water.
3. When reduced to one pint, take out the head of the fish and the herb bouquet and strain the water and set it aside for use later on.
4. Take six slices of Redfish and six slices of Red Snapper of equal sizes and rub well with salt and pepper.
5. Mince three sprigs of thyme, three sprigs of parsley, three bay leaves and three cloves of garlic very fine, and take six allspice and grind them very fine, and mix thoroughly with the minced herbs and garlic.
6. Rub each slice of fish well with this mixture till every portion is permeated by the herbs, spice and garlic.
7. Take two tablespoonfuls of fine olive oil and put into a very large pan, so large that each slice of the fish may be put in without one piece overlapping the other.
8. Chop two onions very fine and add them to the heating oil.
9. Lay the fish slice by slice in the pan and cover, and let them "etouffer," or smother for about ten minutes, turning once over so that each side may cook partly.
10. Take the fish out of the pan and set the slices in a dish.
11. Pour a half bottle of white wine into the pan and stir well.
12. Add a half can of tomatoes, or six large fresh tomatoes sliced fine, and let them boil well.
13. Add half a lemon, cut in very thin slices, and pour over a pint of the liquor in which the head of the snapper was boiled.
14. Season well to taste with salt, pepper and a dash of Cayenne.
15. Let it boil until very strong and till reduced almost one half.
16. Lay the fish slice by slice, apart one from the other, in the pan, and let boil five minutes.
17. In the meantime prepare one good pinch of saffron, chopped very fine, and add a little of the sauce in which the fish is boiling to dissolve well.
18. When well melted, and when the fish has been just five minutes in the pan, spread the saffron over the top of the fish.
19. Take out of the pan and lay each slice on toast which has been fried in butter.

title: Bouille-Abaisse
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- garlic
- olive oil
- fish
- tomatoes
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper
- thyme
- sweet-bay
- parsley
- white wine
- water
- bread

## Directions

1. Chop some onions and garlic very fine, fry them in olive oil.
2. When slightly colored add some fish cut up in slices.
3. Add a few tomatoes scalded, peeled and sliced, some salt, black and red pepper, thyme, sweet-bay, parsley, and half a bottle of white wine, and enough water to cover the fish.
4. Put it over a brisk fire and boil a quarter of an hour.
5. Put slices of toasted bread in a deep dish, place the fish on a shallow dish with some broth, and pour the balance on the bread and serve hot.

title: Bouillon
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds of beef, without bone or fat
- 6 quarts of cold water
- 4 cloves
- allspice
- a small cup of fresh or canned tomatoes
- 1 teaspoon of salt
- 1 spoon of celery seed
- 1 bay leaf
- a piece of red pepper pod, without seeds
- 1 clove of garlic
- a sprig of thyme
- 1 onion
- 1 small bunch of celery
- 1 medium-sized carrot, chopped fine

## Directions

1. Put the beef and water into the soup kettle and bring to a boil
2. Add cloves, allspice, tomatoes, salt, celery seed, bay leaf, red pepper pod, and garlic
3. Set aside and let it simmer gently, never allowing the soup to crack
4. After two and a half hours add a sprig of thyme, onion cut in pieces, small bunch of celery, and medium-sized carrot chopped fine
5. Replace the cover and let the bouillon boil gently for two and one-half hours more, making five hours of actual boiling when not intended for invalid use
6. If you decide not to clarify the soup, set it aside and let it settle, then carefully pour off the upper portion without disturbing the sediment
7. Add about a tablespoonful of celery and a little cayenne

title: Brabant Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Boiled Potatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper
- Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes into small dice-shaped pieces and fry them for a few minutes in the lard
2. When half brown, take out of the lard and finish frying in the butter to a light brown
3. When ready to serve, add three sprigs of chopped parsley, and salt and pepper, and the juice of a lemon, and serve hot

title: Braised Tongue With Aspic Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- onions
- celery
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- brandy
- sugar
- mace
- thyme
- parsley
- Cox’s gelatine
- cold water

## Directions

1. Boil the tongue until tender, then place it in a stew-pan with two onions, a head of celery, four cloves, and salt and pepper;
2. cover it with the liquor it was boiled in; add a glass of brandy, a tablespoonful of sugar, a blade of mace, a bunch of thyme, and a bunch of parsley.
3. Let it simmer gently for two hours.
4. Take out the tongue, strain the liquor it was boiled in, and add to it a box of Cox’s gelatine which has been soaked in a goblet of cold water.
5. Heat it and pour over the tongue. Serve cold.

title: Brandy Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- fine freestone peaches
- boiling water
- brandy
- fruit water
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine freestone peaches, yellow or white, not overripe
2. Scald them with boiling water, cover well, and let them boil till tender
3. Take them out, and drain on a platter, and put in the jars and cover with brandy, and let them stand one week
4. Make a syrup, allowing a half pint of the water in which the peaches were boiled to one pound of granulated sugar
5. Take out the peaches, drain them, and put into glass jars
6. Let the syrup cool, and mix equal parts of this with equal parts of the brandy, in which the peaches were put up
7. Pour over the peaches, and seal

title: Brandy Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Gill of Brandy
- 1 Gill of Boiling Water

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar together to a cream, and add the sugar, beating until light and very white.
2. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, putting in one at a time.
3. Whip these well together, and add the boiling water and the brandy.
4. Set over the fire in a bain-marie and stir till creamy and no longer.
5. Serve either hot or cold.
6. Do not let it boil.

title: Brandy Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Glass of Fine Old French Cognac
- 1 Tablespoonful of the Best Butter
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Cloves
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon and Allspice

## Directions

1. Have a nice porcelain-lined saucepan
2. Melt the butter and sugar over a clear fire, blending well, and adding almost immediately the ground cloves, cinnamon and allspice
3. Let it stew slowly and add the brandy or good old Bourbon or Rye whisky very carefully, so that it will not take fire
4. Stir well, and let it bubble up once or twice, and then take off the fire and add the grated nutmeg

title: Bread and Butter Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- citron
- raisins
- sweetmeats
- 6 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1 spoonful of rose water
- sugar to the taste

## Directions

1. Cut the bread in thin slices, butter them and put a layer into a well buttered dish
2. strew citron and raisins, or sweetmeats, over it
3. then another layer of bread and fruit, and so on until the dish is filled
4. beat 6 eggs with 1 pint of milk, a little salt, nutmeg, and a spoonful of rose water
5. sweeten to the taste, and pour it over the bread
6. let it soak 1 or 2 hours before baking
7. bake 1 hour

title: Bread Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teacups of risen dough
- a teacup of sugar
- a cup of butter
- 2 eggs
- a few raisins (stoned and cut)
- a mite of soda
- cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Mix together the risen dough, sugar, butter, eggs, and raisins
2. Add a mite of soda and cream of tartar
3. Mix the cream of tartar with the dough
4. Dissolve the soda in a little milk and add lastly

title: Bread Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- yeast
- 5 eggs
- 1 lb. sugar
- ½ lb. butter
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make up a loaf of bread with milk and yeast
2. Next morning add to it 5 eggs, 1 lb. of sugar, ½ lb. of butter, and 1 nutmeg
3. Beat it well
4. Set it in a mould to rise until evening

title: Bread Dough Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- bread dough
- jam or preserves

## Directions

1. roll out, and spreading it very thickly with any kind of jam or preserves
2. roll it over carefully, and tie up in a nicely floured square stout cloth
3. have ready a pot of boiling water, and drop in the pudding
4. keep it boiling briskly ½ hour
5. keep a kettle of water on the fire, to renew the water as it evaporates

title: Bread Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Boiling Milk
- 2 Cups of Fine Bread Crumbs
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/2 Cup of Sifted Flour
- Soda

## Directions

1. Soak the bread in the boiling milk, and as it cools beat it to a very smooth paste.
2. Then add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light, and the soda, dissolved in a little warm water.
3. Finally add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and a half cupful of sifted flour to bind well.
4. Bake on a hot griddle, butter, and serve with Louisiana Molasses or Syrup.

title: Bread Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 3/4 Cup of Sifted Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Soak the stale bread crumbs in the milk for a half hour.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs till very light, and the whites to a stiff froth.
3. Add a teaspoonful of salt to the bread, and then pour in the melted butter, and mix thoroughly.
4. Add the flour, into which you will have sifted the baking powder, and beat well.
5. Lastly, add the whites of the eggs, and put the muffins into the muffin rings or gem pans, and bake for about a half hour in a quick oven.

title: Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Stale Bread
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup Raisins
- 1 1/2 Pints of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well, and mix, and then, when very light, add the milk.
2. If you use stale cake, add two tablespoonfuls of sugar; if you use stale bread, add half a cup of sugar.
3. Mix thoroughly.
4. Wet the bread and squeeze it.
5. Then mix in it a cup of seeded raisins.
6. Beat the egg mixture thoroughly into this, and place in the oven and bake for an hour.
7. Serve with Hard Cream, Brandy or Lemon Sauce.

title: Bread Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 loaf of bread
- 1 pint of boiled milk
- yolks of 6 eggs
- whites of 3 eggs well beaten
- 2 tablespoons of flour
- nutmeg
- 1 lb. of raisins

## Directions

1. Pour boiled milk on a loaf of bread
2. Mix into it when soft the yolks of 6 eggs and the whites of 3 eggs well beaten, flour, nutmeg, and raisins
3. Boil 2 hours and serve with sauce

title: Bread Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Ounces of Fresh Bread Crumbs
- 3/4 Cup of Cold Water
- 3/4 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Cup of Cream or Milk
- 6 Whole Peppers
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Crumble the bread and place in a saucepan with the water; add the butter, salt and peppers.
2. Cook for five minutes and add the milk.
3. Cook five minutes longer, remove the peppers and serve hot.

title: Breaded Ox Tails
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ox Tails
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- 3 Sprigs of Chopped Parsley
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Egg
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the tails and cut them at the joints; then cut again, into two pieces of about four inches in length
2. Have a pot of boiling water; season this well with chopped parsley, thyme, bay leaf, and salt and pepper and Cayenne to taste
3. Boil the ox tails til tender; when done, remove from the fire and let them cool in the water in which they were boiled
4. Beat an egg well, roll the bits of tail in the egg, and then roll in grated bread crumbs
5. Drop into a pot of boiling grease and fry to a golden brown
6. Take out and drain and serve with a sauce

title: Breakfast Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- cream or new milk
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Take dough early in the morning and make it into a thin batter with cream or new milk
2. Set it to rise
3. Put it thin on a hoe and bake it quickly
4. Have ready melted butter to put over it

title: Breasts of Chicken Louisiana Style
body:
## Ingredients

- The Filets of 2 Chickens
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Small Onion, sliced
- 1 Minced Bay Leaf
- 1 Blade of Mace
- 4 Cloves
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Form the white meat neatly into filets by patting and flattening
2. Season well with salt and pepper
3. Put the butter into the stewpan and add the sliced onion, the bay leaf (whole) and the spices
4. Let all simmer without browning
5. Lay in the filets of chicken, being careful not to let them brown
6. Let them simmer gently and add one cup of the water in which you have broiled the dark meat of the chicken
7. Let all simmer gently for an hour
8. When done, arrange the filets tastefully on a dish, garnish with parsley sprigs and Croutons of bread nicely shaped in diamond form and fried in butter

title: Breasts of Chicken, Queen Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Breasts of Chicken
- 2 Truffles
- 4 Mushrooms
- 2 Ounces of Chicken Forcemeat
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Gill of Madeira Wine
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Mushroom Liquor
- 1 Pint of Hot Sauce a la Reine

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the small filet found under the breast of each chicken and set aside on a dish for further use
2. Take a small, sharp knife and make an incision three inches long and one inch deep in the inner side of each breast
3. Season lightly with salt and pepper
4. Stuff each breast in the incision made, using two ounces of chicken forcemeat mixed with two truffles and four mushrooms, all finely minced
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a stewpan and lay the breasts in gently
6. Take each small filet, press gently into shape, and flatten
7. Make several small incisions and place within a fine slice of truffle, about an inch in diameter
8. Carefully lay on top of each breast lengthwise
9. Brush lightly with melted butter
10. Pour into the pan, but not over the breasts, the wine and mushroom liquor
11. Cover tightly and set in the oven for fifteen minutes
12. Send to the table hot

title: Breeze Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- i box of gelatine
- 1 pint of boiling water
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Whites of 3 eggs
- 3 eggs
- Yolks of the other eggs
- A quart of milk

## Directions

1. Dissolve i box of gelatine in 1 pint of boiling water
2. Add 2 cupfuls of sugar and the juice of 2 lemons
3. After this has become cool (not cold), break into it the whites of 3 eggs
4. Beat all to a stiff froth
5. Pour into moulds
6. When ready for use, turn out, pouring over the following custard: make a soft custard with 3 eggs added to the yolks of the other eggs and a quart of milk

title: Bride's Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of White Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 4 Cups of Sifted Flour
- ½ Cup of Cornstarch
- The Whites of 12 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Cup of Sweet Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Extract of Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth.
2. Sift the flour and baking powder.
3. Sift the sugar.
4. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream.
5. Beat till very light.
6. Mix the cornstarch with the sweet milk and add to the sugar and butter, beating well.
7. Then add gradually the flour, mixing thoroughly, and beating light.
8. Add the extract of lemon, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
9. Bake one hour in a moderate oven, using the broom wisp test.
10. If not done, bake a little longer.

title: Bride's Cake. A Splendid Recipe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-fourths of a pound of butter
- one pound of white sugar
- beaten whites of seventeen eggs
- one pound of sifted flour
- lemon or rose flavoring

## Directions

1. Wash and cream three-fourths of a pound of butter
2. Add one pound of white sugar
3. Beat them well together
4. Add the beaten whites of seventeen eggs alternately with a pound of sifted flour
5. Flavor with lemon or rose
6. Bake

title: Brioche
body:
## Ingredients

- Slightly sweetened egg and milk batter
- Yeast

## Directions

1. Set to rise over night
2. Form into a Brioche, or cake, with a central cake for a head
3. Arrange other cakes to the number of six or eight around the central cake
4. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Brioche Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Small Brioches
- 1 Tablespoonful of Essence of Vanilla
- Lemon or Orange Flower Water
- Creole Fritter Batter
- Powdered White Sugar
- Sweetened milk

## Directions

1. Cut the Brioche into slices, more or less thin, and soak them in sweetened milk, to which you have added the essence of orange flower water, vanilla or lemon.
2. Drop them into a light fritter batter, or simply drop them into boiling lard.
3. Fry to a golden yellow.
4. Sprinkle with white sugar, and serve.

title: Brioches
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of the Best Flour
- ⅛ Ounce of Yeast
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- ⅛ Ounce of Salt
- 1 Pound of the Best Butter
- 12 or 8 Eggs
- Milk or tepid water

## Directions

1. Take a pound and a half of the best quality of flour, and put it in a wooden bread trough.
2. Make a hole in the center of the flour, and put in a half ounce of yeast, dissolved in a little warm water.
3. Take milk or tepid water to make the dough, using milk if you want it to be very rich and delicate, and water if you have not the milk.
4. Knead and mix the flour with one hand, while adding the milk or water with the other.
5. Make a dough that is neither too stiff nor too soft, and when perfectly smooth set the dough to rise in a moderately warm place, covering with a cloth.
6. If you use milk to make the dough, the milk must be scalded, that is, it must be heated to the boiling point and then allowed to grow tepid.
7. Let the dough rise for five or six hours, and when increased to twice its bulk, take it and add the reserved half pound of flour, into which you will have sifted the salt.
8. Add six eggs, beaten very light, with the sugar and butter, and mix all well together, kneading lightly with your hands, and adding more eggs if the dough is a little stiff.
9. Knead the dough by turning it over on itself three times, and set to rise again for an hour or three-quarters of an hour.
10. Cover with a cloth.
11. At the end of this time take it up and work again lightly, and then form into Brioches, that is, work the dough gently with your hands, and break it into pieces about the size of an egg.
12. Pat these gently, forming them into neat rolls, a little flattened, and then make one roll about twice the size of the others.
13. Have ready a baking pan with a buttered sheet of paper in it, and set the central roll in the middle.
14. Arrange around the other rolls, having at least six or eight, to form a perfect-looking cake.
15. Cover the pan with a clean, stiff cloth, and set the cake to rise for an hour longer.
16. When well risen, set in an oven a few degrees cooler than that used for baking Bread.
17. If the rolls are large, let them bake an hour and a half; if medium, one hour, and if very small, a half hour.
18. Some bakers and cooks glace the Brioche lightly with a beaten egg, which is spread lightly over the top, before placing in the oven.
19. After the Brioches are baked, set them to cool, if you do not wish to eat them hot.
20. When serving, whether hot or cold, sprinkle lightly with powdered white sugar.

title: Broiled Beefsteak
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Steak for Broiling Purposes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Chopped Parsley and Lettuce Leaves to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Dredge the meat well with salt and pepper and then brush lightly with butter.
2. Place it on the hot gridiron and let it broil quickly for four minutes; then turn on the other side for four minutes longer.
3. When done take off, place in a hot dish, butter nicely.

title: Broiled Canvasback Ducks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Ducks
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- Salt and Pepper
- Drawn Butter Sauce
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Minced Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the duck nicely, as for broiling a chicken, wipe well and split down the middle of the back in the same manner as for a chicken.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Rub the duck well with olive oil of the best quality, and place on the broiler.
4. Turn it over at least twice, so that it will cook thoroughly through and through without burning.
5. Let it cook from seven to ten minutes on either side.
6. Place on a dish that is very hot, pour over a Drawn Butter Sauce, in which you will have squeezed the juice of a lemon, and mixed some minced parsley.
7. Decorate with water cress or parsley sprigs.
8. Bring to the table covered and very hot, and serve on heated plates.

title: Broiled Ceps on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Ceps
- 3/4 Pint of Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Sliced Lemon
- Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Drain the Ceps from their oil
2. Season well with salt and pepper
3. Roll in fresh bread crumbs finely grated
4. Broil nicely on double broiler
5. Arrange nicely on toast
6. Pour over each slice some of the Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel
7. Garnish nicely with sliced lemon and serve hot

title: Broiled Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- A Spring Chicken
- Melted Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select spring chickens for broiling
2. Clean the chickens nicely and singe
3. Split down the middle of the back, laying the chicken open
4. Break the breastbone with a mallet and flatten out the chicken
5. Season well with salt and pepper and brush with melted butter
6. Have the broiler ready over a moderate fire
7. Place the chicken between the double broiler
8. Let the fowl broil slowly for about a half hour if the chicken is very tender, otherwise three-quarters of an hour
9. Keep a plate over it all the time to retain its flavor better
10. Turn the chicken as needed

title: Broiled Chickens
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of chickens
- pepper
- salt
- egg yolks
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Split a pair of chickens down the back
2. Wipe the inside and season with pepper and salt
3. Prepare some beaten yolks of eggs and bread crumbs
4. Dip the outside of the chickens in the batter
5. Put them on a gridiron on a bed of bright coals
6. Lay the chickens on the gridiron with the inside down
7. Broil them 20 minutes
8. Just before taking them from the fire, add bits of butter

title: Broiled Eels
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of eel
- 1 sprig of thyme
- 1 teaspoonful of black pepper
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- A drawn butter sauce, or sauce a la Tartare

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eel as for frying.
2. Cut in two-inch pieces and boil till disgorged.
3. Then roll in oil and pepper and salt.
4. Mix well and broil quickly over a clear fire.
5. Serve with melted or drawn butter sauce and pickles, or a sauce a la Tartare.

title: Broiled Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish slices
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- white paper
- drawn butter or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Cut some slices of fish about 1 inch thick
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Wrap each piece in half a sheet of white paper, well buttered
4. Twist the ends of the paper and broil them over a fire of bright coals for 10 minutes
5. Serve in the butter, with drawn butter or anchovy sauce

title: Broiled Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- sweet oil or butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, without cutting off the head and tail.
2. When the fish is large, split down the back; else broil whole.
3. Have a clear moderate fire.
4. Before placing on the broiler, rub the fish well with salt and pepper, mixed in a little sweet oil or a little butter.
5. If the fish is small, broil on a quick, clear fire; if large, the fire must be moderate.
6. Expose first the flesh side to the fire, and then the skin.
7. When the fish is done through and through, remove the gridiron from the fire, and loosen the fins from the broiler with a knife.
8. Place the hot dish over the fish and turn the two back again, thus separating the gridiron from the fish and placing the latter in the dish.
9. Butter well, season with a little pepper and salt, if deemed necessary.
10. Pour over a tablespoonful of chopped parsley and lemon juice.
11. Serve with garnishes of sliced lemon and parsley, or garnishes of delicate green lettuce leaves.

title: Broiled Frogs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Frog Legs
- 1 Cup of Boiling Lemon Juice and Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- 1 Teaspoonful of Black Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Lettuce Leaves and Parsley, or Sliced Lemons and Olives to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the Frogs; scald well in boiling lemon juice and salt
2. Dry with a clean towel
3. Mix thoroughly a little black pepper, salt and olive oil, or butter melted, and rub the frogs thoroughly, rolling them over and over
4. Take out and put on a double wire broiler, being careful to turn frequently to prevent scorching
5. When done, place in a platter of delicate lettuce leaves

title: Broiled Green Trout
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Medium-Sized Trout, or 3 Large Ones
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- Parsley
- Salt and Pepper
- Sweet oil or butter oil

## Directions

1. Clean the fish without cutting off the head or tail
2. Broil whole over a clear, moderate fire
3. Before placing on the broiler rub the fish well with salt and pepper, mixed in a little sweet oil or a little butter oil
4. If the fish is small, broil on a quick, clear fire; if large, the fire must be moderate, or the outside of the fish will be charred before the inside is done
5. When the fish is done through and through, which can easily be determined by the flesh parting easily from the bone, remove the gridiron from the fire and loosen the fish from the broiler with a knife, being careful not to break the flesh
6. Place the hot dish over the fish, and, with a dexterous movement, turn the two back again, thus separating the gridiron from the fish and placing the latter in the dish
7. Butter well, season with a little pepper and salt, if deemed necessary, and pour over a tablespoonfull of chopped parsley and lemon juice
8. Serve with garnishes of parsley and sliced lemon

title: Broiled Green Trout
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Medium-Sized Trout, or 3 Large Ones
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Sliced Lemon and Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the fish without cutting off the head or tail
2. Rub the fish well with salt and pepper, mixed in a little sweet oil or a little butter oil
3. Broil whole over a clear, moderate fire
4. If the fish is small, broil on a quick, clear fire; if large, broil accordingly

title: Broiled Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Thin Slices of Boiled Ham
- 6 or 8 Slices of Buttered Toast

## Directions

1. Always use boiled ham for broiling.
2. Slice it about a half inch thick, according to the number to be served, and trim off the rough edges.
3. Have the broiler very hot, lay the slices of ham upon it and brown well.
4. Serve with buttered toast.

title: Broiled Ham With Cucumber Garnish
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 slices of boiled ham
- Pepper
- Cucumbers
- Butter
- Salted vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut thin as many slices of ham as desired and broil evenly over hot coals.
2. When well brown, butter and add pepper, sprinkling.
3. Serve with slices of cucumber that have been steeped in salted vinegar several hours ranged around it.

title: Broiled Kidneys
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Kidneys
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Lemon Juice
- Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the kidneys well and slice them; cut into thin pieces of about three inches long and two inches wide
2. Run a wooden or silver skewer through to hold them together, and season well with salt and pepper
3. Brush with a little butter, and put on a double broiler and broil for about five minutes, turning over the broiler to allow each side to cook
4. Place on a platter and pour over melted butter and chopped parsley and lemon juice, and serve hot, as you would broiled steak

title: Broiled Lamb Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Lamb Chops
- Butter, Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Season the chops well with salt and pepper and brush with melted butter and a few broad crumbs.
2. Have the gridiron hot and place the chops upon it.
3. In a few seconds turn the chops and let the other sides cook.
4. Let them remain on the griddle until the chops are firm under pressure of a fork.
5. Place on a platter, butter thickly and sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve very hot.

title: Broiled Lamb Kidneys
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Kidneys
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for broiling Sheep Kidneys
2. Serve with melted butter and lemon juice and chopped parsley, thrown over
3. Where the skewer is used in broiling to keep the kidneys from separating, the skewer must be drawn out before buttering and serving

title: Broiled Liver
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of beef's liver
- 1 tablespoonful of melted butter
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Chopped parsley to garnish
- Bacon slices

## Directions

1. Wash and slice the liver into thin pieces of about three inches in length and one-quarter inch in thickness.
2. Run a skewer through to prevent from curling up.
3. Season well with salt and pepper, brush lightly with butter, and place on a double broiler, stringing on the skewer with alternate slices of bacon.
4. Broil as you would a tenderloin steak for about five minutes, and serve with a sauce of melted butter and chopped parsley poured over.

title: Broiled Mackerel
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 No. 1 salted mackerel
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak a No. 1 salted mackerel over night
2. wipe it dry in the morning
3. have ready some clear, bright coals
4. heat and grease the gridiron
5. lay the mackerel on it with the flesh side down
6. when cooked turn it, by placing a dish on it
7. slip the skin side on the gridiron
8. Butter it and serve up hot

title: Broiled Mackerel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salt mackerel
- 1 cup of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- Black pepper
- Sliced lemon
- Olives
- Parsley sprigs
- Cream sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the mackerel and wash thoroughly as directed above, only using boiling water.
2. Have ready some milk, seasoned well with black pepper and soak the mackerel in the milk until thoroughly impregnated.
3. Take out and wipe dry with a towel.
4. Dredge the mackerel with butter, and place between a double broiler, over a slow fire, broiling about fifteen or twenty minutes, the under side being allowed to broil first.
5. When done, take off and pour over melted butter and chopped parsley.
6. Garnish the dish with sprigs of parsley, sliced lemons and olives, and serve hot.

title: Broiled Mutton Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Mutton Chops
- Butter
- Salt and Pepper
- Bread Crumbs
- Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Season the chops well with salt and pepper and brush with melted butter and a few bread crumbs.
2. Have the gridiron very hot and place the chops upon it.
3. In a few seconds turn the chop and let this side cook.
4. The blood will be running out, and the chop is done.
5. Place on a platter, butter thickly and sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve very hot.

title: Broiled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen fine oysters
- 1 tablespoonful of parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- Sprigs of thyme
- 1 sprig of sweet basil
- 2 shallots
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- Grated bread crumbs
- Asparagus tips
- Sliced lemon
- 1 dozen well washed oyster shells

## Directions

1. Blanch three dozen fine oysters in their own water and drain.
2. Chop a tablespoonful of fine parsley, bay leaf and thyme, using a sprig each of the latter and a sprig of sweet basil.
3. Cut up two nice shallots very fine and add.
4. Place a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan; add a little oyster juice and sauté, or shake the oysters without making a bouillon.
5. When two minutes have passed, take off the stove.
6. Place the oysters in the well washed shells.
7. Sprinkle over a little bread crumbs, and put on top of each a pinch of butter.
8. Have ready the gridiron or broiler very hot; place the shells between the double broiler, set over the fire to broil for about four or five minutes.
9. Serve with delicate garnishes of asparagus tips and sliced lemon.

title: Broiled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Oysters to Each Person
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- Melted Butter and Chopped Parsley
- Sliced Lemon and Sprigs of Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Allow six or eight oysters for each person. The oysters must be large and fat, else they will shrivel to nothing in cooking.
2. Drain the oysters through a colander, lay them on a dish and wipe with a dry, clean towel.
3. Melt butter and dip in the oysters, seasoned well with salt and Cayenne on both sides.
4. Have ready the gridiron (use always the double wire broiler) and test the heat by dropping a little water on it.
5. If the water hisses, the broiler is quite ready.
6. Place the broiler in a warm place — just over the oven will do.
7. Butter and place the oysters on it and return to moderate coals.
8. As soon as the oysters are browned on one side, turn on the other and brown.
9. Have ready a heated dish and serve the oysters, pouring over melted butter and chopped parsley (chopped very fine).
10. Garnish with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon and serve immediately.

title: Broiled Oysters on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Oysters to Each Person
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 6 or 8 Pieces of Buttered Toast or Milk Toast
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Broil the oysters according to the recipe given above.
2. Have ready a heated dish; sprinkle the oysters with salt and pepper and pour over melted butter.
3. Serve on small pieces of buttered toast, or milk toast.
4. Sprinkle with finely-chopped parsley.

title: Broiled Oysters With Sauce Espagnole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Oyster Liquor to Every 2 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sifted Flour
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- Chopped Parsley
- 6 or 8 Squares of Buttered Toast

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters and allow about one pint of the oyster liquor to every two dozen oysters.
2. Have ready a porcelain-lined saucepan and put the liquor on to boil.
3. As the scum rises skim it carefully.
4. Put one tablespoonfuls of butter into a frying pan, and when it begins to heat, add gradually two tablespoonfuls of sifted flour.
5. Mix well and brown.
6. Pour over this the oyster liquor and stir constantly till it begins to boil, seasoning with salt and pepper (Cayenne) and parsley chopped very fine.
7. Stand the sauce in a vessel in hot water (bain-marie) until wanted.
8. Proceed to broil the oysters in the same manner as in the recipe first given.
9. Place squares of buttered toast in a dish, lay the oysters on top, pour over the sauce, and serve immediately.

title: Broiled Pababotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Pababottes
- 6 Fine Strips of Bacon
- 6 Slices of Buttered French Toast
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- Sliced Lemon and Parsley Sprigs to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean the Pababotte well, removing the entrails, and be particularly careful to throw away the gizzard.
2. Rub the birds with salt and pepper and then with melted butter.
3. Tie a strip of very finely-sliced bacon around the body of each bird, joining the bacon with a skewer.
4. Place the birds on a broiler over a slow fire and let them cook fifteen, twenty or even thirty minutes, according to the size of the birds.
5. Turn frequently, so that they may broil without burning.
6. When done, take off the broiler; have ready the slices of buttered French toast, and place a bird upon each slice.
7. Trim away the rough edges of the toast.
8. Pour over the birds a little of the juice that has run from them in broiling, and let this soak down into the toast.
9. Pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley, and add a little lemon juice, if desired.
10. Garnish with slices of lemon and parsley sprigs and bring to the table hot.

title: Broiled Partridges
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Young Partridges
- 6 Slices of French Toast
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- The Juice of a Lemon
- Parsley Sprigs and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds
2. Cut them in two by splitting down the back
3. Rub with salt and pepper and melted butter
4. Place on a broiler and let them broil from fifteen to twenty minutes, allowing from seven to ten minutes to either side during the broiling process, according to the size of the bird
5. Turn frequently to avoid burning
6. Have ready the buttered French toast; place the birds upon it
7. Pour over a little of the juice that ran from the bird while broiling and let it soak down in the bread
8. Pour over melted butter and chopped parsley, and add a little lemon juice, if desired
9. Garnish the dish nicely with sprigs of parsley or watercress and send to the table hot

title: Broiled Perch
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Perch
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner indicated under the heading General Rule for Broiling Fish
2. If large, split the fish down the middle of the back, else broil whole
3. Serve with garnishes of parsley and sliced lemon

title: Broiled Pigeons
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Young Squab
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- 6 or 8 Pieces of Buttered Toast
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean nicely inside and out.
2. Wipe with a damp towel.
3. Split down the back and spread open as you would a broiled chicken.
4. Have the gridiron very hot.
5. Rub the pigeon inside and out with salt and pepper, and brush lightly with butter.
6. Place the broiler over a moderate furnace fire, from which all the gas has been exhausted, and let it broil slowly ten minutes on the inner side and five minutes on the outer.
7. In the meantime toast a piece of bread for every pigeon that you broil.
8. Moisten well with butter.
9. Place the squab upon the toast, allowing one squab for each piece of toast, sprinkle with chopped parsley and butter, and serve hot.
10. Rub the pigeon with a little lemon juice, as that renders the flesh nice and white.

title: Broiled Pigeons a la Crapaudine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pigeons
- The Yolk of an Egg
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- A Tomato Sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons nicely, inside and out.
2. Carefully cut the breast from the loin joints, without separating entirely.
3. Raise the breast up from the shoulder joints, and pass it over the head of the pigeon, without separating it from the shoulders.
4. Press it down very firmly with your hands or a masher.
5. Have ready the yolk of one egg, well beaten in a cup of milk.
6. Season well with salt and pepper.
7. Soak the pigeons in it well, so that they will absorb the milk and be thoroughly impregnated.
8. Roll over and over, so that they will gather up the seasoning.
9. Pass them through bread crumbs, rolling and then patting each pigeon with your hands, so that the crumbs will hold.
10. Brush each with a little melted butter.
11. Have ready a double broiler, well heated, but on a slow fire.
12. Place the pigeons on it, broiling very slowly.
13. Broil for fifteen or twenty minutes, allowing from seven to ten minutes to each side.
14. Serve with Tomato Sauce.

title: Broiled Quail on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Quails
- 6 Strips of Bacon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 6 Slices of Buttered Toast
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Parsley Sprigs to Garnish

## Directions

1. Rub the bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter.
2. Tie a strip of very finely-sliced bacon around the body of the bird, joining with a skewer, and place on a broiler over a slow fire, and let it cook for ten, fifteen or twenty, or even thirty minutes, according to the size of the bird.
3. Turn frequently, so that it may cook well without burning.
4. When done take off the broiler.
5. Have ready always buttered French toasts, and place the birds upon them, allowing a slice of toast for each bird.
6. Trim away the rough edges of the toast.
7. Always pour over the bird a little of the juice that has run from it in broiling, and let it soak down into the toast.
8. Pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley, and lemon juice, if you like.
9. Garnish with sliced lemon and parsley sprigs, and bring to the table hot.

title: Broiled Snipe on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Snipe
- 6 Slices of Buttered French Toast
- 6 Strips of Bacon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Parsley Sprigs and Sliced Lemon to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the bird by hand picking, singing and trussing neatly, following the Invariable Rule for Broiling Birds
2. Rub the bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter
3. Tie a strip of very finely-sliced bacon around the body of the bird joining with a skewer
4. Place on a broiler over a slow fire, and let it cook for ten, fifteen or twenty, or even thirty minutes, according to the size of the bird
5. Turn frequently, so that it may cook well without burning
6. When done take off the broiler
7. Have ready always buttered French toasts, and place the birds upon them allowing a slice of toast for each bird
8. Trim away the rough edges of the toast
9. Pour over the bird a little of the juice that has run from it in broiling, and let it soak down into the toast
10. Pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley, and lemon juice if you like
11. Garnish with sliced lemon and parsley sprigs, and bring to the table hot

title: Broiled Soft-Shell Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Soft-Shell Crabs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Flour, Sifted
- Butter
- Sliced Lemon
- Parsley Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the crab and wash in cold water
2. Dry with a clean towel and season well
3. Season a pint of milk with salt and black pepper, and soak the crabs in it so as to thoroughly impregnate them with the milk
4. Pat lightly with a little flour and brush over with melted butter
5. Place between the broiler and broil till a delicate brown over a slow fire, which will generally require about fifteen minutes to cook thoroughly
6. Serve on a platter, nicely garnished with parsley sprigs and lemon cut in quarters
7. Pour over the crabs a little melted butter and chopped parsley

title: Broiled Spanish Mackerel
body:
## Ingredients

- A Spanish Mackerel, or 3 Pounds Weight
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- Parsley to Garnish
- Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as the Pompano, serving whole
2. Where the fish is large, split downwards, in the middle of the back, and broil in the same manner as the Pompano
3. Place on a dish garnished with parsley and slices of lemon
4. Serve with a Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel

title: Broiled Sweetbreads
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Parboil the sweetbreads and then remove all nerves.
2. Cut into halves.
3. Brush with melted butter and place on the gridiron.
4. Broil nicely, and when well colored, take off, pour melted butter over them, season again, and serve very hot.

title: Broiled Teal Duck
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- butter
- buttered toast
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Split the duck like a partridge down the back
2. Broil on clear coals, butter freely
3. Pepper and salt when broiled, just before putting on the butter
4. Serve on buttered toast

title: Broiled Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tomatoes
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Take six tomatoes and cut them in halves on the cross.
2. Do not peel.
3. Put them on the broiler and broil on a slow fire till tender.
4. Turn the broiler from one side to the other often, to prevent burning.
5. In about ten minutes they will be done.
6. Put on a hot dish; put a little melted butter, a little chopped parsley and the juice of a lemon on each, and serve hot.

title: Broiled Venison
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- venison slices
- butter
- black pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the slices medium size
2. Thickly butter them
3. Sprinkle with black pepper and a little salt
4. Place the slices on a gridiron and broil them in a hurry
5. Send it to the table very hot with a little melted butter over it

title: Broiled Woodcock on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Woodcock
- 6 Slices of Buttered French Toast
- 6 Fine Strips of Breakfast Bacon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper
- Parsley Sprigs and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds by hand picking.
2. Singe and wipe well.
3. Rub the bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter.
4. Tie a strip of very finely-sliced bacon around the body of the bird, joining with a skewer.
5. Place on a broiler over a slow fire and let it cook for ten, fifteen, twenty, or even thirty minutes, according to the size of the bird.
6. Turn frequently so that it may cook well without burning.
7. When done take off the broiler.
8. Have ready buttered French toasts and place the birds upon them, allowing a slice of toast for each bird.
9. Trim away the rough edges of the toast.
10. Pour over the bird a little of the juice that has run from it in broiling, and let it soak down into the toast.
11. Pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley, and lemon juice, if you like.
12. Garnish with sliced lemon and parsley sprigs, and bring to the table hot.

title: Broth in haste
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cut some rare roast meat or broiled steak very fine
- A teacupful of the cut meat
- A pint and a half of boiling water
- A cracker (rolled fine)

## Directions

1. To a teacupful of the cut meat put a pint and a half of boiling water; cover it, and set it on the fire for ten minutes;
2. Season to taste.
3. Roll a cracker fine, and put in with the meat.

title: Brown Betty Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- 2 Cups of Chopped Apples
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1/2 Spoonful Each of Ground Spices

## Directions

1. Utilize stale bread crumbs for this pudding.
2. Butter the bottom of a baking pan, and put in a layer of the apples, which you will have stewed nicely.
3. Put over this a layer of bread crumbs, and sprinkle with sugar and dot with butter.
4. Sprinkle lightly with the spices.
5. Continue this till you have used up all the apples.
6. Put a layer of bread crumbs on top.
7. Place in a moderate oven, and bake to a nice brown.
8. Serve hot, with Cream Sauce or Hard Sauce.

title: Brown Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of Indian meal and rye flour
- yeast
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald the meal
2. When lukewarm, mix in the flour, adding yeast and salt
3. Knead as for other bread
4. Bake from 2 to 3 hours

title: Brown Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half pints corn meal
- 1½ pints graham flour
- 1½ cups molasses
- 2 teaspoons soda dissolved in a little boiling water and mixed with the molasses
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 pint of water
- a little salt

## Directions

1. pour into a well greased 3-quart tinpail
2. steam 3 hours and bake one hour

title: Brown Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cut Parsley (not chopped)
- 3 Tablespoonfuls Juice of Lemon or Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a saucepan, and when it begins to smoke it is browning.
2. Then add two tablespoonfuls of cut parsley, and let it brown half a minute longer.
3. Then add three tablespoonfuls of the juice of a lemon or Tarragon Vinegar, and let it simmer two minutes longer, and serve with Stingaree or Rai aux Beurre Noir (see recipe), calves' brains or crawfish boiled.

title: Brown Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- a pinch of salt
- mace to the taste
- flour enough to make a soft dough
- lard for frying
- sugar for sifting

## Directions

1. roll and cut into small cakes
2. throw them into boiling lard
3. turn them over
4. take them out with a fork, dripping all the lard from them
5. sift sugar over each one whilst hot

title: Brown Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour
- 3/4 lb. of sugar
- 3 eggs, leaving out the yolk of one
- 1 egg yolk
- rose water
- mace
- pounded almonds
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Mix butter, flour, sugar, and eggs together in a stiff paste
2. Roll it out and cut it into strips
3. Mix the yolk of 1 egg with rose water
4. Spread it on the top of the cakes
5. Strew mace with pounded sugar and almonds on the top of the cakes
6. Bake them on tin sheets

title: Brown Fricasee
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or veal (chopped fine)
- pepper
- salt
- ginger
- 4 eggs
- stale bread (a large plateful)
- flour
- tomato or walnut catsup

## Directions

1. Chop the beef or veal very fine and remove all the stringy parts
2. Season it with pepper, salt and a little ginger
3. Beat up 4 eggs very light
4. Take a large plateful of stale bread and make them in balls
5. Roll them in flour and fry them
6. Take 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, mix with cold water
7. Put some seasoning and hot water into the frying pan
8. After it has boiled throw it over the balls and let it stew about 15 minutes before dishing them in tomato or walnut catsup

title: Brown Fricassee
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or veal
- pepper
- salt
- ginger
- 4 eggs
- stale bread
- flour
- water
- hot water
- tomato or walnut catsup

## Directions

1. Chop the beef or veal very fine and take out all the stringy parts
2. Season it with pepper, salt and a little ginger
3. Beat up 4 eggs very light
4. Take a large plateful of stale bread and make them in balls
5. Roll them in flour and fry them
6. Take 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, mixed with cold water
7. Put some seasoning and hot water into the frying pan
8. After it has boiled throw it over the balls
9. Let it stew about 15 minutes before dishing them in tomato or walnut catsup

title: Brown Onion Sauce for Poultry, Etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions (three)
- butter (a spoonful)
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- sage
- parsley
- thyme
- liquor in which the fowl was cooked
- catsup (a tablespoonful)

## Directions

1. Slice three onions after peeling them; fry them a bright brown in a spoonful of butter;
2. sprinkle a little flour in, and let it brown also;
3. add salt, pepper, and also sage, if for goose or duck, and parsley and thyme if for chickens or roast meat;
4. add a cup of the liquor in which the fowl was cooked, let it boil up and add a tablespoonful of catsup.

title: Brown Roux
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Melt some butter very slowly
2. stir into it browned flour till it is a fine, even brown
3. add as much to the butter as will make it the thickness of paste, stirring well with a wooden spoon for fifteen or twenty minutes, till it is of a yellowish brown color
4. Great care must be taken, for if the fire is too hot, it will become bitter and unfit for use
5. When cold it should be thick enough to cut with a knife

title: Brown Roux
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour

## Directions

1. Melt the tablespoonful of butter slowly
2. Add gradually the flour, sprinkling it in and stirring constantly, till every portion is a nice, delicate brown
3. Never make it too brown, because it must continue browning as the other ingredients are added

title: Brown Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Neck or Brisket of Veal
- Bones of Beef
- 1 Gallon of Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- 1/2 Can Mushrooms or 1/2 Can Truffles
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Cloves Garlic
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Wineglass of Sherry

## Directions

1. Take a good quantity of bones, place in a gallon of boiling water, and make a strong consommé, seasoning well with salt and pepper.
2. Take a piece of the brisket or neck of the beef, and roast rare, so that the blood spurts out when pricked with a needle.
3. After roasting cut it in pieces of about one inch square.
4. Take two tablespoonfuls of lard and three of flour, and brown slightly, stirring all the time.
5. After browning, add the water of the consommé, which has been reduced to about half a gallon, pouring it in slowly and stirring constantly.
6. Then add all the pieces of the roast beef which you have cut.
7. Add three carrots, two cloves of garlic, one onion, an herb bouquet (tied together of thyme, parsley and bay leaf), and let the whole boil well two hours, stirring every five minutes until reduced to the consistency of starch.
8. Then strain well through a strainer or sieve, season to taste, and set back on the stove to cook a few minutes longer.
9. Taste, and if sufficiently seasoned take it off and allow it to get cool.
10. This sauce is then used as a foundation sauce, and will keep for at least one month in our climate of New Orleans, if put in a cool place in winter or the ice box in summer.

title: Brown Taffy
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of New Orleans sugar
- water
- 1 lb. of butter

## Directions

1. Boil until it cracks
2. When almost done, stir constantly to prevent burning
3. When done pour on a marble slab, or in a flat dish
4. When cool, pull till light brown

title: Browned Snipe A La Fauvet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- snipe
- browned stuffing
- truffles
- bacon
- soup-stock
- buttered paper
- cock's combs
- scallops
- goose liver
- champignons
- Madeira sauce

## Directions

1. Dress fourteen snipe, stuff them with a little browned stuffing, to which add two hashed truffles.
2. Bend the skin back carefully while stuffing, and then replace it so the birds will retain as nearly as possible their natural appearance.
3. Place the snipe so prepared and larded with bacon, into a frying pan.
