4. Put some strips of bacon over them to keep them sufficiently together in order that the skins may not shrink much while cooking.
5. Moisten them with a little soup-stock, cover them with buttered paper and let them cook in the oven for forty minutes.
6. Drain them, lightly trim the lower side, and lay them on a little mound of uncooked, but slightly browned stuffing, breast up, in the bottom of a dish, and ice them.
7. Keep the dish hot in the oven for some minutes.
8. Remove the skin and eyes from the heads of the snipe after cooking them and stick a small truffle in each bill, and lay between each two birds, one of the heads with the truffle up.
9. Garnish the dish with stewed cock's combs, scallops, goose liver, and champignons.
10. Add a little Madeira sauce, boiled down and permeated with the flavor of the game.
11. Ice the snipe and truffles, and serve with a separate sauce.
12. Let everything be very hot.

title: Brussels Sprouts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Brussels Sprouts
- ¾ Gallon of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Pick the sprouts carefully, rejecting all loose, dead leaves
2. Throw the sprouts into cold fresh water so that any lurking insects may be drawn out
3. Wash and pick carefully after the sprouts have remained about twenty minutes in the water
4. Put them into half a gallon of boiling water and add immediately a tablespoonful of salt and a quarter of a spoon of bicarbonate of soda
5. Let the sprouts boil uncovered for twenty minutes or just long enough to make them tender all through
6. Drain in a colander, season well with pepper and salt
7. Serve in a heated dish with a Drawn Butter Sauce poured over

title: Brussels Sprouts Sautéd in Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Brussels Sprouts
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the Brussels Sprouts carefully
2. After washing, drain thoroughly and boil them in salted water for ten minutes
3. Take out of the hot water, drain and put into cold water
4. Drain again and put them in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter
5. Season according to taste with salt and pepper
6. Add a teaspoonful of chopped parsley
7. Let them cook slightly for ten minutes more, or less if very tender
8. Serve hot

title: Brussels Sprouts à la Crème
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Brussels Sprouts
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- ½ Cupful of Cream or Milk
- A Pinch of Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare and boil the Brussels Sprouts as in the preceding recipe
2. Drain thoroughly and put in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter
3. Season with salt and pepper to taste and a pinch of nutmeg
4. Add a half cup of cream or milk
5. Toss lightly for five or ten minutes but do not let them boil
6. Place on a hot dish, garnish nicely and serve hot

title: Buckwheat Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- warm water
- good buckwheat
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 handfuls cornmeal
- 1 wineglassful good yeast
- warm milk

## Directions

1. Take as much warm water as you will require for the size of the family
2. Thicken this with good buckwheat to a fritter batter
3. Add 1 teaspoonful of salt if 2 quarts is made, 2 handfuls of cornmeal, and 1 wineglassful of good yeast
4. Warm milk in the place of warm water is a great improvement
5. Be particular not to scald the yeast or the cakes will be heavy

title: Buckwheat Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Cups of Buckwheat Flour
- 1½ Pints of Sweet Milk or Water
- ⅜ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- ⅜ Teaspoonful of Salt
- ½ Teaspoonful of Soda or Saleratus

## Directions

1. Take a small earthen crock, or a pitcher, and put the warm milk or water, or half milk and water, into it, and then add the salt and the buckwheat flour.
2. Beat till perfectly smooth, and then, when you have a stiff batter, add the yeast, dissolved in a little water.
3. Beat this till smooth, and then cover well and set to rise over night.
4. In the morning add a half teaspoonful of soda or saleratus, to remove any sourness that may have accrued during the night.
5. Bake on a griddle, like Batter Cakes.

title: Buckwheat Griddle Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three pints of warm water
- half a gill of baker's yeast or an inch square of turnpike cake
- a heaping teaspoonful of salt
- half a small teaspoonful of saleratus
- buckwheat flour
- a large handful of cornmeal optional
- an egg optional
- a cup of milk optional

## Directions

1. Put three pints of warm water into a stone jar
2. Add half a gill of baker's yeast or an inch square of turnpike cake dissolved in a little warm water
3. Add a heaping teaspoonful of salt and half a small teaspoonful of saleratus
4. Gradually stir in enough buckwheat flour to make a nice batter
5. Beat it perfectly smooth
6. Cover it and set it in a moderately warm place until morning
7. A large handful of cornmeal may be put with the flour if desired, and if the meal is added it will require an egg and a cup of milk

title: Bullfrogs
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound of the Best Flour
- ⅛ Ounce of Yeast
- ½ Cup of Sugar
- ⅛ Ounce of Salt
- 1 Pound of the Best Butter
- 12 or 8 Eggs

## Directions

1. The dough for Bullfrogs is just the same as that for the Brioche, only a half pound of flour is used, instead of a pound, and a cup of butter.
2. Bake in the same manner as Brioche.

title: Burgundy Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Good Old Burgundy
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- 1 Orange
- A Dash of Old French Cognac
- ¾ Pint of Seltzer Water
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- ½ Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and lemon and orange juice and add the liquors and water
2. Decorate with the sliced lemon and sprigs of green mint

title: Burnt Sugar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Granulated Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Take one pound of granulated sugar, and set it on the fire, and stir with a wooden spoon until thoroughly dissolved and burnt black
2. Add one quart of water, and let it boil until thoroughly dissolved
3. Reduce to half the quantity and strain and bottle for use

title: Burr Artichokes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- burr artichokes
- salted water
- salt
- drawn-butter sauce
- vinegar
- mustard
- oil

## Directions

1. Get them young
2. Wash them in salted water
3. Put them to boil
4. Boil until you can pull off a leaf easily
5. Salt them and serve with drawn-butter sauce, with vinegar in it, or mustard and oil, as preferred

title: Butter and Flour Sauce or White Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter (a tablespoonful)
- flour (a tablespoonful)
- water (a cup of cold)
- butter (a quarter of a pound, if for a number of guests)
- lemon juice
- parsley (chopped)

## Directions

1. Mix a tablespoonful of butter and one of flour; mix over the fire, with a cup of cold water, stirring all the time.
2. When this boils, take a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, if for a number of guests, and stir in the butter quickly, adding a cup of cold water by degrees, to keep the butter from oiling;
3. finish with the juice of a lemon, and strain. It must be served hot, and made only a few moments before it is wanted. It gets oily if kept long.
4. Add a spoonful of chopped parsley.

title: Butter Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 pint of milk
- 2 wineglasses of the best brewers' yeast
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Set it to rise
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Butterbeans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Butterbeans
- 2 Pints of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful (heaping) of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Soak the butterbeans for about a half hour in water.
2. Pour off this cold fresh water, and then put them in a porcelain lined saucepan, or one of agate, and cover with two pints of water.
3. Let them boil well for about an hour, or less, if they are very tender.
4. As soon as they crush easily under pressure, take off the fire, drain off water, season well with salt and pepper.
5. Butter well with a heaping tablespoonful of butter, add a teaspoonful of parsley, minced very fine, and serve hot.

title: Buttered Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Beet Roots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Cook beets very tender
2. Slice nicely
3. Pour over them a tablespoonful of melted butter
4. Sprinkle nicely with salt and pepper
5. Vinegar may also be added at the table according to taste

title: Buttermilk Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of wheat flour
- 1 dessertspoonful of super-carbonate of soda
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 1 1/2 tumblers of buttermilk

## Directions

1. Mix these ingredients, and knead them into soft dough
2. Place in a greased pan, and set it to rise in a slightly warm oven
3. When risen, bake

title: Buttermilk Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Pint of Buttermilk
- 1 Dessertspoonful of Soda
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- ½ Pound of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Warm the milk, add the sugar, butter and soda, the latter dissolved in a little warm water
2. Add the flour and set to rise
3. When well risen knead lightly, place in a baking pan, let it rise again for half an hour, and bake in a moderate oven

title: Buttermilk Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Buttermilk
- 3 Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, and then add the sugar, which has been sifted fine.
2. Beat to a cream.
3. Add the yolks of the eggs, and continue beating till very light.
4. Mash the soda in the buttermilk, dissolving thoroughly, first in a half cupful, and then mixing well with the remainder of the milk.
5. Add this to the eggs and butter.
6. Then gradually add the sifted flour, and beat till very smooth.
7. Lastly add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
8. Bake for forty-five minutes in a moderate oven.

title: Buttermilk Pie-Crust—Very Wholesome
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of buttermilk
- one large teacupful of lard
- one teaspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of soda
- flour enough to form a soft dough

## Directions

1. Mix the lard and flour by rubbing them together
2. then add the other ingredients

title: Buttermilk Rolls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1 quart of flour
- 1 quart of buttermilk
- 1 large spoonful of soda
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix cream of tartar into flour
2. Dissolve soda in the buttermilk
3. Mix flour and buttermilk well and quickly
4. Add salt lastly
5. Pour into tins or make into rolls
6. Bake quickly

title: Button Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- small round tomatoes
- cold vinegar
- bag of spice

## Directions

1. Put small round tomatoes whole into cold vinegar of the best quality
2. You may add a bag of spice, if you choose

title: Cabbage and Corned Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fine head of cabbage
- 1 pound of corned beef
- 1 red pepper pod
- 1 teaspoonful of chili pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the meat in cold water and put it in a large kettle
2. Cover with cold water
3. Let it simmer gently for two hours
4. Prepare the cabbage according to directions in the above recipe
5. Add the cabbage and let all boil for two hours longer
6. When done, put the cabbage in a dish with the meat in the center
7. Serve with tomato catsup or horseradish or mustard sauce

title: Cabbage Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Head of Cabbage (green and white mixed)
- 1 Round Steak
- 2 Large Slices of Lean Ham
- 2 Pounds of Creole Sausage
- 1 Pod of Cayenne Pepper, Without the Seeds
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- Salt and Black Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Shred the cabbage and wash each leaf separately and thoroughly to avoid insects.
2. Chop the entire head very fine, into pieces about half the size of dice.
3. Cut the steak or brisket into small squares, also the ham, and fry in the deepest kettle you have, putting the meat into the pot when the lard is very hot.
4. When it begins to brown, add a chopped onion and the sausage, and then add the chopped cabbage, stirring and pouring in enough water to prevent it from burning.
5. Throw in the red pepper pod and a dash of Cayenne, and salt to taste.
6. Add a little black pepper.
7. Stir often and allow the ingredients to cook well, gradually adding, if necessary, a little water, and stirring frequently to prevent burning.
8. Take one pint of new milk and two tablespoonfuls of flour and mix thoroughly, so as not to be lumpy.
9. Stir this into the gumbo while boiling, and continue stirring for five minutes.
10. Serve with boiled rice.

title: Cabbage Sauteed With Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- A fine head of white cabbage
- 1 cupful of cream or milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- A pinch of black pepper
- Salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the cabbage according to recipe
2. Blanch in hot water for ten minutes
3. Drain and throw into cold water
4. Drain again and chop up the cabbage
5. Put into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter
6. Add salt to taste and the black pepper
7. Take a tablespoonful of flour and blend well with a little milk
8. Mix well with a cup of cream or milk
9. Add this to the cabbage and mix well
10. Let all cook for three-quarters of an hour or an hour till done
11. Arrange neatly on a hot dish and serve

title: Cabbage Stewed With Sausage
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen fine sausage (chaurice preferred)
- 1 head of cabbage
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- Salt to taste
- 1 inch of red pepper pod
- 1 pound fresh pork

## Directions

1. Wash the cabbage well after having cut it into quarters and allowed it to soak half an hour
2. Cut the cabbage into shreds of about one inch in width and five in length
3. Scald the cabbage with boiling water for about fifteen minutes
4. Throw off this water and cover it again with boiling water and let it boil for twenty minutes
5. Prick each sausage in several places
6. Cut the meat into pieces of about two inches in length and one in thickness
7. Put the lard in the frying pan and fry the sausage and meat until they are about half done
8. Drain the cabbage and turn into the sausage and meat and the fat drippings
9. Cover and stew gently where it will not scorch for at least forty minutes
10. Season with salt and add an inch of red pepper pod
11. Let all simmer till the cabbage is quite done
12. Put in a dish with the sausage and meat piled in the center and the cabbage heaped around as a border

title: Cabinet Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sponge cake
- citron
- raisins
- currants
- spice
- 1 quart of milk
- 8 eggs
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Butter a mould, and place a layer of sponge cake all around it, then citron, raisins, currants, and any spice you like
2. then add more cake and more fruit until the mould is filled
3. boil 1 quart of milk, and pour it hot on 8 well beaten eggs
4. then pour all into the mould
5. tie it in a cloth, boil it 2 hours, and serve it with cold sauce of butter and sugar, creamed well together

title: Cacao Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of cacao beans
- 2 quarts of brandy
- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 quart of water
- 1 tablespoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Roast the beans to a light brown and grind as you would grind coffee
2. Infuse in two quarts of brandy for six days
3. Make a syrup with the sugar and water; reduce to one-half and let it cool
4. Add the cacao and brandy
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Strain and bottle for use

title: Cafe Au Lait
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee, ground
- boiling water
- cream, fresh and sweet
- milk

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as in the making of Cafe Noir, allowing the usual quantity of boiling water to the amount of coffee used.
2. When made, pour the coffee into delicate china cups, allowing a half cup of coffee to each cup.
3. Serve, at the same time, a small pitcher of very sweet and fresh cream, allowing a half cup of cream to a half cup of coffee.
4. The milk should always be boiled, and the cream very hot.

title: Cafe Noir
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee, ground
- freshly boiled water

## Directions

1. Have the water heated to a good boil. Set the coffee pot in front of the stove; never on top, as the coffee will boil, and then the taste is destroyed.
2. Allow one cup, or the ordinary mill, of coffee to make four cups of the liquid, ground and put in the strainer, being careful to keep both the strainer and the spout of the coffee-pot covered; to prevent the flavor from escaping.
3. Pour, first, about two tablespoonfuls of the boiling water on the coffee grounds, or, according to the quantity of coffee used, just sufficient to settle the grounds.
4. Wait about five minutes, then pour a little more water, and allow it to drip slowly through; but never pour water the second time until the grounds have ceased to puff or bubble, as this is an indication that the grounds have settled.
5. Keep pouring slowly, at intervals, a little boiling water at a time, until the delightful aroma of the coffee begins to escape from the closed spout of the coffee pot.
6. When you have produced a rich, fragrant concoction, serve in fine china cups, using no preference than sugar for sweetening.

title: Cake For Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of milk or water
- 2½ cups of flour
- 1 spoon of yeast powder
- For sauce: 2 eggs
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 1 pint of boiling water
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix the cake ingredients, flavor to taste
2. For sauce: stir in 1 pint of boiling water, a little salt, and season
3. Let it thicken, but not turn

title: Cake or Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Stale Cake
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup Raisins
- 1 1/2 Pints Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Utilize left-over cake in this pudding.
2. Beat the eggs well, and mix and then, when very light, add the milk.
3. If you use stale cake, add two tablespoonfuls of sugar; if you use stale bread, add half a cup of sugar.
4. Mix thoroughly.
5. Spread the bottom of the pan with the raisins, and then put a layer of cake over them, sliced very thin.
6. Sprinkle this with raisins, and continue adding the cake and raisins alternately till all are used.
7. Add a little essence of lemon or vanilla to the egg mixture, and pour all this over the cake.
8. Set in the oven and bake for an hour.
9. Serve with Hard or Lemon Sauce.

title: Cake Without Butter
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the weight of 3 eggs in sugar
- the weight of 2 eggs in flour
- 5 eggs well beaten
- a little orange flower water

## Directions

1. When the 5 eggs are well beaten, gradually add the sugar
2. Add the flour and orange flower water
3. Beat all well together
4. Bake it in a floured tin

title: Cake Without Eggs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of butter
- 3 cups of sugar
- 1 pint of sour cream
- 1 1/2 pints of flour
- 1 lb. of chopped raisins
- 1 spoonful of soda
- spice to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake about 1 hour

title: Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sifted Flour
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 1/4 Cups of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and the butter to a cream, and then add the milk, stirring carefully.
2. Add the flour, and beat well and hard.
3. Add the salt, flavoring extract and baking powder.
4. Mix all thoroughly, and bake in a moderate oven for a half hour.

title: Calf or Pig Brains Fried
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt water
- wheat flour or beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- butter or lard
- pepper
- salt
- lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the brains in salt water, and wipe dry.
2. Dip in wheat flour or in beaten egg and then in bread crumbs.
3. Fry in butter or lard.
4. Season with pepper, salt and lemon sliced.

title: Calf's Feet A la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's Feet
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sifted Flour
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1/4 Can of Mushrooms
- Yolk of 1 Egg
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the calf's feet; then take out the larger bones and cut in pieces of about an inch square.
2. Prepare a Sauce a la Poulette by putting one tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan, and, as it melts, add two tablespoonfuls of sifted flour.
3. Add about one pint of the water in which the calf's feet have been boiled.
4. Stir well and throw in the calf's feet, salt and pepper to taste, and, if desired, about a quarter of a can of chopped mushrooms.
5. Let it boil about five minutes, and then take off the stove and add the yolk of an egg (beaten well), the juice of one lemon, and serve.

title: Calf's Feet Plain
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Calfs Feet
- 3 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Gill of Vinegar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 12 Whole Pepper Corns
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Salt
- 1 Herb Bouquet

## Directions

1. Split each of the calf's feet in two
2. Carefully remove the larger bones and cut the meat into pieces of about one inch square
3. Soak well in fresh water for one hour
4. Wash and drain thoroughly
5. Put two tablespoonfuls of flour and three quarts of cold water into a saucepan
6. Stir well, mixing thoroughly
7. Place the feet in the mixture and add one onion chopped fine, twelve whole peppers, one carrot cut into fine shreds, the herb bouquet and two tablespoonfuls of salt
8. Let the feet boil briskly for about one hour
9. Take from the fire and drain well
10. They are now ready to serve with any sauce that may be desired

title: Calf's Head a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Calf's Head
- Yolk of 1 Egg
- 1 Lemon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- Water
- Salt and Pepper
- Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the calf's head as in the recipe given for Calf's Head Soup
2. Boil it according to recipe
3. Cut one lemon fine and add to the boiling calf head, which is boiled simply in water, and salt and pepper
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of good vinegar and let it cook till done
5. Make a Sauce a la Poulette
6. Put the calf's head in the sauce and let it boil for a half hour
7. Take the yolk of one egg and beat it as you would an omelet
8. Add to the calf's head and serve

title: Calf's Head A la Tortue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Calf's Head
- 2 Large Onions
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Lemon
- Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Wineglass of Sherry
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Slice the onions and mince the garlic
2. Put the butter into a stew pot, and as it melts add the onions
3. Add one tablespoonful of flour, sifted well, and as this becomes brown, add one pint of consomme or water
4. Add the chopped thyme and bay leaf and the peel of one lemon, cut very fine, and the juice
5. Let all this simmer for about ten minutes
6. Cut up the calf's head and add it to the mixture
7. After fifteen minutes add a half can of mushrooms
8. In a few minutes add one small glass of Sherry wine
9. Let it all cook about ten minutes, then season well according to taste
10. Let all cook about half an hour longer
11. When ready to serve, place the calf's head in the middle of the dish, pour the gravy over and range the mushrooms around
12. Garnish with pieces of a flat omelet made from the two eggs and cut into diamond shapes, alternating with toast buttered and cut into diamond-shaped Croutons

title: Calf's Head Boiled or Baked
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf's head
- salt
- water
- butter
- flour
- lemon juice
- tomatoes
- pepper
- sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Have a head nicely cleaned, and soak it in salt and water to make it look white.
2. Remove the eyes.
3. Take out the tongue and salt it.
4. Of the brains make a separate dish.
5. To boil the head put it in a pot of lukewarm water and boil till very tender.
6. Serve with sauce made of butter, flour and water, some lemon juice and tomatoes.
7. If to bake, dredge flour over it, put on bits of butter, season with pepper, salt, and sweet herbs, set in a hot oven and baste with the water in which it was boiled.

title: Calf's Head Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head with eyes, teeth, and nose bone removed
- calf's liver, lights, feet and heart
- water
- salt
- 6 very small onions
- 1 coffee cup of very finely sifted sweet marjoram
- 1.5 teaspoons black pepper
- 1.5 teaspoons red pepper
- 2 tablespoons salt
- small one of mace (powdered)
- 2 nutmegs
- 1 tablespoon of cloves
- 3 tablespoons of butter
- 8 tablespoons of flour
- 4 pounds of crackers
- 1 pint wine
- 1 bottle of claret
- 6 or 8 eggs
- fresh lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the calf's head thoroughly; remove the eyes, teeth, and nose bone, making the skin smooth and white as possible
2. Clean the liver, lights, feet and heart
3. Put all these into an iron pot
4. Cover with water and add salt enough to make the scum rise, about 2 tablespoonfuls
5. Boil all until the meat cleaves from the skull bone, and the liver is tender to the touch of the fork
6. Put the meat in a separate pot, cover with water and boil until the meat slips from the bone
7. Boil the feet separately as they require so much more boiling than the head
8. Fish out the head with a long skimmer, then pour out the liquor
9. Wash the pot and strain the whole through a hair sieve into the pot
10. Separate the head from the bone, cut it into pieces of proper size to give each person
11. Divide the feet and liver also, and put into the strained liquor with 6 very small onions minced very fine, 1 coffee cup of very finely sifted sweet marjoram, 1.5 teaspoons black pepper, 1.5 teaspoons red pepper, 2 tablespoons salt, small one of mace (powdered), 2 nutmegs, 1 tablespoon of cloves, 3 tablespoons of butter mixed with 8 of flour (nicely browned), 4 pounds of crackers, 1 pint wine, and a bottle of claret
12. Let the whole simmer slowly till dinner time
13. 6 or 8 eggs cut up and put in as it goes to table, with small pieces of fresh lemon

title: Calf's Head Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fine calf's head
- 5 quarts cold water
- 1 onion
- 1 lemon
- 1 turnip
- 1 carrot
- 4 level teaspoonfuls flour
- 1 level teaspoonful mushroom catsup
- 1 level teaspoonful Worcestershire sauce
- salt and pepper to suit taste
- butter
- hard-boiled eggs
- wine

## Directions

1. Select a fine calf's head, not too large. If large, reserve half and the tongue and brains to make another dish.
2. Get the butcher to crack the head well and remove the brains.
3. Wash the head thoroughly in cold water, then pour boiling water through the nose and throat passages until they are perfectly clean, and scrub out the ears thoroughly, washing very clean.
4. Rinse all well in cold water, and be very sure that the head is very sweet and clean before attempting to cook it.
5. Put the head in a kettle with five quarts of cold water, and set it over a moderate fire.
6. When it begins to boil well, skim thoroughly, till every particle of scum has been taken off.
7. Set it back and let it simmer until the meat is quite tender. This will require about two hours and a half.
8. Remove the head; take the meat from the bones; skin the tongue, and set away to cool.
9. Return the bones to the kettle, with the vegetables, which have been washed and cut fine; as also, the spices and the liver.
10. Simmer gently again for two hours, and when cool, strain.
11. Set aside to cool, and when the soup is cold, remove all the fat.
12. Put the butter in a saucepan and melt, adding the flour until nicely browned, but be careful not to burn it.
13. Add by degrees the boiling soup, stirring constantly.
14. Boil, keeping up a gentle stir, for about five minutes.
15. Add the meat of the head and the liver, having first cut them into dice, and bring to a boil at once.
16. Take the saucepan from the fire, and add the catsup, salt, pepper and wine.
17. Slice the hard-boiled eggs and the lemon and place them in the tureen, and pour the soup over them and serve.

title: Calf's Liver Fried
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Liver
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the liver very fine into pieces of about three inches in length and one in width
2. Season well with salt and pepper
3. Slice two onions very fine
4. Take a tablespoonful of lard or butter and put into the frying pan
5. When it heats, add the onions
6. As they brown, place on top the slices of liver
7. Let them brown on one side about two minutes and a half
8. Turn on the other side and let brown two minutes and a half longer
9. Serve with the onion sauce, to which add a teaspoonful of vinegar

title: Callers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- about 5 cents worth of rice
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 dessertspoonful of yeast powder
- enough flour to make a light batter
- whites of 3 eggs, well beaten
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Soak and pound the rice in a mortar
2. Let it stand over night
3. Add the yolks of 3 eggs
4. Put in a dessertspoonful of yeast powder
5. Add enough flour to make a light batter
6. Add the whites of the eggs well beaten
7. Have a pot of boiling lard and cook as for fritters

title: Calves Brains Brown Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Calves Brains
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- Sauce aux Beurre Noir

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains as mentioned above by boiling
2. Place in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter
3. Cut up a tiny onion and add also a sprig of thyme, bay leaf and parsley, all minced very fine
4. Add to the butter
5. Add the brains cut in slices a half inch thick
6. Season again to taste
7. Fry for five minutes and serve with a Sauce aux Beurre Noir

title: Calves Brains Fried
body:
## Ingredients

- Calves Brains
- 1 Onion
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Lard for frying
- Fried parsley for bed
- Boiled green peas for garnish

## Directions

1. Plunge the brains into cold water to disgorge them of all blood and remove the fine skin and blood that surrounds them
2. Blanch with scalding water
3. In five minutes take them out of the hot water and put them into a saucepan and cover with cold water
4. Add a tiny onion sliced fine, parsley and bay leaf whole
5. Let them simmer gently for five minutes
6. Take from the fire and drain
7. When cold, cut into pieces of a square inch and dip in a batter or tomato sauce, then in grated bread crumbs, patting gently
8. Drop into boiling lard and fry to a golden brown
9. Take out and drain of grease and serve on a bed of fried parsley
10. Garnish with boiled green peas

title: Calves Liver a la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Calf's Liver
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 1 Turnip
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Pint of Broth or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- White Wine or Sherry

## Directions

1. Wash the liver and lard it well with needles
2. Put a tablespoonful of lard or butter into the frying pan
3. When hot, add immediately the onion, carrot and turnip, all sliced very fine
4. Add the fine herbs, nicely minced
5. Let these brown and add the liver
6. Pour over this about two spoons of White Wine or one of Sherry
7. Add about a pint of consomme or boiling water
8. Season highly
9. Cover the saucepan well and set back on the fire
10. Let it simmer for about half an hour and serve

title: Calves' and Pigs' Feet Fried in Batter
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- calves' or pigs' feet
- flour
- eggs
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- hot lard
- toast

## Directions

1. Wash and cook the feet tender, the day before using.
2. When wanted, wash and roll them in a little flour to dry.
3. Make a batter of flour, eggs, milk, and a little salt and pepper (one egg is sufficient to two feet).
4. Take out the largest bones and roll the feet in batter, or lay them in a pan with hot lard, and pour the batter over them.
5. Fry a delicate brown and serve on toast.

title: Calves' Feet Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two calves' feet
- one gallon of water
- whites of six eggs
- half a pint of wine
- half a pound of white sugar
- juice of four lemons
- rind of one lemon grated

## Directions

1. Take two calves' feet, add to them a gallon of water which you must reduce by boiling to a quart
2. Strain it while hot, and set away to get cold
3. When cold take off the fat, and remove any settlings which may be in the bottom
4. Melt the jelly in a stew-pan, and add to it the whites of six eggs, well beaten, half a pint of wine, half a pound of white sugar, the juice of four lemons, and rind of one grated
5. Boil this a few minutes, and pass it through a flannel strainer

title: Calves' Feet Jelly Made with Gelatine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of water
- one pint of white wine
- six teaspoonfuls of brandy
- six lemons, juice and peel
- six eggs, the whites slightly beaten, the shells crushed
- three pounds of white sugar
- four ounces of gelatine

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine in one quart of the measured water; let it remain for one-half an hour
2. Mix the ingredients named with the other two quarts, and let all boil twenty minutes
3. Strain it through a flannel bag without squeezing
4. Wet the jelly mould in cold water
5. Pour the jelly in, and leave it to cool, or put it on ice until wanted

title: Canapes of Chicken Livers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Chicken Livers
- 1 Onion
- 6 Slices of Toast
- Dash of Anchovy Essence
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- Salt and Red Pepper to Taste
- Pimentos and Red Chili to Garnish

## Directions

1. Saute the Chicken Livers with a finely minced onion till tender
2. Pound them to a paste, adding first a dash of Anchovy Essence, one tablespoonful of butter, salt and red pepper to taste
3. Cut the bread the full width of the loaf and trim the edges nicely
4. Fry in butter to a golden brown
5. Take out and drain
6. Place in a silver dish, or in the dish in which they are to be served
7. Pile up the chicken liver preparation in pyramidal shape on top of the toast
8. Smooth nicely all around with a knife
9. Set in the oven for two or three minutes
10. Decorate the edges of the bread with slices of Pimentos and rings of Red Chilis
11. Serve hot

title: Canapes, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Toast
- 1 Cup of Forcemeat of Game (White Meat)
- ¾ Cup of Forcemeat of Game (Dark Meat)

## Directions

1. Prepare triangular-shaped pieces of toast
2. Butter nicely
3. Spread over with a game forcemeat (Woodcock, Snipe, Reed Birds, etc.)
4. Decorate the edges with a forcemeat of game of different color, for effect
5. Serve

title: Canapés of Potted Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box of Potted Ham
- 2 Ounces of Grated Boiled Ham
- Thin Slices of Green Gherkins
- 6 Slices of Toast

## Directions

1. Prepare delicate strips of Toast, spread with Potted Ham to a quarter of an inch in thickness.
2. Sprinkle lightly with grated ham and decorate the edges, and cover if desired, with thinly-sliced Green Gherkins.

title: Canapés of Potted Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box of Potted Tongue
- Strips of Cooked Pork Tongue
- 6 Slices of Toast

## Directions

1. Prepare the toast and cut into delicate circles.
2. Spread with a layer of Potted Tongue one-quarter of an inch in thickness.
3. Decorate with strips of cooked Red Tongue in lattice forms, that is with strips laid one over the other, like a lattice work.

title: Candied Pumpkin
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of pumpkin
- Brown sugar and water
- A little juice of the lemon
- Mace, or other spices (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel a piece of pumpkin, and cut it in thin slices
2. Make a nice, thick syrup of brown sugar and water, and put the pumpkin into it, with a little of the juice of the lemon
3. Boil this until the pumpkin is nicely candied
4. Mace, or other spices, may be used for flavoring instead of lemon, if preferred

title: Candy
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of good molasses
- 5 lbs. of brown sugar
- 1 lb. of butter

## Directions

1. Boil together until they candy well
2. Pour into buttered dishes
3. Pull until very white

title: Candy Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- Water to Dilute
- Flavoring Extract to Taste

## Directions

1. Pound and sift a pound of sugar that has been doubly refined, first through a coarse and then through a fine sieve
2. Put in an earthen vessel, and add the flavoring extract, whether of lemon, orange, cinnamon, cloves, rose or violet or vanilla or carmine
3. Dilute the sugar with a little water and the liquid
4. Set the saucepan on the fire, being careful to select a saucepan that has a spout
5. As soon as the mixture begins to bubble up about the sides of the saucepan, stir it once well and take from the fire, and drop it in small lumps through the spout of the saucepan upon sheets of tin, and let it stand two hours to dry
6. Then put in the Stove a few seconds to finish drying
7. As they become hard and brilliant, take them out, else they will lose their flavor

title: Canned Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Quarts of Corn
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard to Each Quart of Corn
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the corn on the cob until no milk will exude if the grains are pricked with a needle
2. cut the corn off the cob, and pack into cans or stone jars, putting first a layer of salt half an inch deep, and then a layer of corn two inches deep, and continuing with these alternate layers until the jar is nearly filled
3. The top layer must be of salt, and about an inch thick
4. Pour over all this melted lard in proportion of about a tablespoonful to a quart of the corn
5. Set on the fire, in a bain-marie, and let all the bubbles come to the surface
6. Take off, stick a silver fork down into the jar, to see that all bubbles have ceased to form
7. Seal hermetically, and then set in a bain-marie, at a temperature of from 80 to 100 degrees, and let it cool
8. Keep in a cool place, and use as wanted

title: Canned Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Quarts of Green Peas
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Select the youngest and most tender peas, put them in boiling water, and let them cook for about thirty minutes.
2. Drain lightly, and can immediately, excluding all air.

title: Canned Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Quarts of Mushrooms
- Salted water

## Directions

1. Cook the mushrooms for three-quarters of an hour in salted water.
2. Drain lightly and can them, excluding all air.
3. Submit to the bain-marie, letting them cool gradually.
4. Keep in a cool place.

title: Canned Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Quarts of Mushrooms
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the mushrooms for three-quarters of an hour in salted water
2. drain lightly and can them, excluding all air
3. Submit to the bain-marie, letting them cool gradually
4. Keep in a cool place

title: Cantaloupe Sweet Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one ripe cantaloupe
- vinegar
- three pounds of loaf sugar
- five pounds of cantaloupe
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- mace

## Directions

1. Take a ripe cantaloupe, quarter it, remove the seeds and cut it into pieces an inch square
2. Put the cut pieces in a stone crock
3. Pour on scalding vinegar
4. When it cools heat it again, and return it to the cantaloupe
5. Repeat this next day
6. On the fourth day take out the fruit and add fresh vinegar to cover it
7. To every quart of this vinegar add three pounds of loaf sugar, and five pounds of cantaloupe
8. Put to them nutmeg, cinnamon and mace, to taste
9. Put all in a porcelain-lined kettle and simmer until the fruit can be pierced with a straw
10. Pack it in jars and keep in a cool place

title: Caper Sauce for Boiled Mutton, Etc., Etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter sauce (half a pint)
- capers (two tablespoonfuls)
- salt

## Directions

1. Take half a pint of butter sauce and add two tablespoonfuls of capers and a little salt.

title: Caramel
body:
## Ingredients

- About a half pint of brown sugar
- Two pints of water

## Directions

1. Put the brown sugar in a pan on a slow fire
2. Let it burn or parch, slowly stirring all the time
3. When it turns a dark brown, add two pints of water and stir well
4. Bottle it and put it away
5. Use a few drops at a time to color and thicken gravies and soup broths

title: Caramel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Continue boiling till the sugar reaches a light brown color
2. Take the vessel off immediately, or it will burn
3. On taking the vessel from the fire, insert the bottom in cold water to prevent the sugar from burning

title: Caramel Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pint of Gelatine
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Water
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Gill of Sherry Wine
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Caramel

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as for Coffee Bavarian Cream, using the same ingredients, except the eggs and coffee, substituting instead of the coffee, a gill of the best Sherry Wine and two tablespoonfuls of Caramel

title: Caramel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1½ Cups of Flour, Sifted
- 1 Cup of Cornstarch
- Whites of 7 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, add the sugar, and beat till very light
2. Add the milk
3. Mix well
4. Sift the flour, cornstarch and baking powder together, and add gradually to the mixture, beating vigorously all the while
5. Add the vanilla and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
6. Mix all quickly and lightly, turn into a long family pan, and bake for about half an hour in a moderate oven
7. Apply the broom wisp test
8. When done, take out of the oven and set to cool
9. When cool, boil together 2 Cups of Brown Sugar, 1 Cup Sweet Cream, 2 Teaspoonfuls Vanilla, and 1 Tablespoonful of Butter until it sugars, and spread over the top and sides
10. Alternatively, make a mixture of ¾ Pound Brown Sugar, ¾ Pound Chocolate, ¼ Cup Milk, 1 Tablespoonful Butter, and 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vanilla
11. Grate the chocolate, and set all to boiling together until thick enough to spread over the top and sides of the cake

title: Caramel Chestnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Chestnuts
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonfull of Butter

## Directions

1. Take fine, large Chestnuts, and transfix them on a hatpin or on small pointed sticks
2. Dip them in the Caramel, and then dry and Glacie them

title: Caramel Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Caramel

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of milk and one pint of cream together.
2. Add four ounces of powdered white sugar and the yolks of three eggs, nicely beaten together till very light.
3. Add one teaspoonful of Caramel.
4. Let it reduce one-half.
5. Strain, garnish nicely with any fruits, and serve cold.

title: Caramel Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- 2 teacups of sugar
- 1 scant cup of flour
- 1 quart of cream

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and 1 cup of sugar together
2. Add the flour
3. Have the milk boiling and put into the mixture
4. Stir over the fire until it is as thick as mush
5. Put the other cup of sugar dry into a pan and stir over the fire until it is melted
6. Add this to the mush and set it away to cool
7. When cold, strain into the cream and freeze

title: Caramel Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oranges
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonfull of Butter

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter the oranges in their natural divisions, being careful not to break through the pith or allow the juice to escape
2. String them on fine wire, or take up on the point of a hatpin and dip into the Caramel
3. When sufficiently coated, place them in the stove for a few minutes to dry and Glacie, or Glaze

title: Caramel Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sherry
- 2 Ground Cloves
- 1 Stick of Cinnamon
- Grated Zest of 1/2 Lemon

## Directions

1. Put the sugar in a saucepan and let it melt and brown to a golden yellow.
2. Add the water and the wine, or essence and spices, etc., and set to cool after it has boiled three minutes.

title: Caramels
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Clarified Sugar
- Sufficient Water to Melt

## Directions

1. Take one pound of clarified sugar and add just sufficient water to melt
2. Boil it till it is very brittle and has the slightest odor of beginning to burn
3. Pour it on a buttered slab or plate
4. As soon as it is cool enough to receive an impression from the finger, cut it out with a common case knife into small squares of about an inch in size
5. Glace with another coating of sugar
6. Let them dry and keep them tightly closed

title: Cardinal Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Fine Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Pint of the Best Claret or Any Fine Red Wine
- 1 Pint of Old Port Wine
- 1 Pint of Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cloves
- 1 Pint of Gelatine or the Whites of 10 Eggs, Beaten to a Froth
- 2 Pounds of White Sugar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Raspberry Juice or a Few Drops of Cochineal

## Directions

1. Select fine Louisiana oranges, when in season, otherwise fine, ripe oranges
2. Set them on a plate in the oven, and let them bake till very tender, without letting the juice exude
3. Make a boiling syrup of the sugar, water and the cloves
4. Drop the roasted oranges into the syrup, add a pint of the best Claret or any good red wine, and set the mixture to cool, letting the oranges steep in the wine and syrup
5. When it grows cold, cut the oranges and press out all the juice, mashing them into the steeped syrup, and then strain all through a fine sieve into the ice cream freezer
6. Add the gelatine (Calf's Foot Jelly) or the whites of ten eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
7. Stir well and add the raspberry juice, if raspberries are in season, otherwise color with a few drops of Cochineal, so that the punch will be of a bright cardinal color
8. Cover immediately and set to freeze as hard as possible for three or four minutes
9. Take a wooden spoon and detach the frozen punch from the bottom, mix in with the inner liquid mixture, and cover and freeze again for three or four minutes
10. Repeat this four or five times
11. Serve in punch glasses

title: Carmine
body:
## Ingredients

- Cochineal
- Brazil wood
- nitric acid
- muriatic acid
- bismuth

## Directions

1. Pound the Cochineal in a mortar
2. add two gallons of water, and let it boil for an hour and a quarter
3. Add about three drachms of nitro-muriate of tin, made by taking two drachms of nitric acid, two drachms of muriatic acid and one drachm of bismuth, and dissolving under a gentle heat
4. Continue boiling the Cochineal ten minutes longer
5. strain the mixture through a fine cloth, and let it stand for eighteen hours
6. Pour off the water, and add the solution of tin, in three pints of water, and let it stand for five days longer, changing the water every twenty-four hours
7. When free from any taste or smell the Carmine has been sufficiently washed, and is ready for use
8. Put into china saucers and dry in the stove

title: Carolina Boiled Rice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Pick your rice clean and wash it in two cold waters
2. Do not drain off the last water until you are ready to put the rice on the fire
3. Prepare a saucepan with water and a little salt in it
4. When it boils, sprinkle in the rice
5. Boil it hard twenty minutes, keeping it covered
6. Take it from the fire and pour off the water
7. Set the saucepan on the back of the stove with the lid off to allow the rice to dry and the grains to separate

title: Carrageen Moss
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1/2 an ounce of moss

## Directions

1. To 1 quart of milk add 1/2 an ounce of moss
2. Boil it till thick
3. Strain it, and pour it into moulds
4. Flavor it with anything you prefer

title: Carraway Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sifted loaf sugar
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- some carraway seeds
- 3 well beaten eggs
- a little rose water

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with the flour and butter
2. Add some carraway seeds
3. Make it into a stiff paste with the eggs and rose water
4. Roll it out thin
5. Cut it into round cakes
6. Prick them with a fork
7. Bake them upon floured tins in a quick oven

title: Carrigeen Moss for Invalids
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of Irish moss
- half a pint of boiling water
- sugar
- nutmeg
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash and pick a tablespoonful of Irish moss and put it into a tin cup
2. pour on it half a pint of boiling water
3. set it on the coals for a short time
4. when it is all dissolved add sugar and nutmeg to taste
5. This may be made with milk, to resemble custard

title: Carrot Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Large Carrots
- 2 Large Onions, Cut Fine
- 1 Quart of New Milk
- 1 Turnip
- 1 Teaspoon of Corn Starch
- 2 Sticks of Celery
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Sprig of Thyme
- Sprig of Parsley
- 3 Cloves
- 1 Large Teaspoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper and Sugar to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the vegetables thoroughly, cutting them fine and boiling until tender in three pints of water.
2. When very soft, mash them and press through a sieve.
3. Mash the carrots very fine.
4. Return to the fire, and, adding about two quarts of boiling water, cover and simmer gently for a while.
5. Add one teaspoonful of cornstarch that has been blended well with a little milk.
6. Add the boiling milk and cook for about two minutes more.
7. Serve with Croqutons.

title: Carrots a la Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Carrots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Onion, Minced Very Fine
- Salt, Pepper, Thyme and Bay Leaf

## Directions

1. Boil the carrots according to recipe given above, and then cut into thin slices.
2. Fry an onion in butter, add the carrots.
3. Sprinkle well with salt and pepper, and add minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf.
4. Fry ten minutes, and serve hot.

title: Carrots a la Maitre d'Hotel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Good-Sized Carrots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots and boil them at the same time that you are making your soup, or bouillon, leaving them whole.
2. When done and ready to serve, skim out of the soup, and place in a dish.
3. Cut them into pieces, more or less large, and then place them in a frying pan with a tablespoonful of butter, minced parsley, thyme, bay leaf.
4. Add salt and pepper to taste, and when they have cooked for about ten or fifteen minutes pour over a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel, and serve hot.

title: Carrots Sautés à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 nice tender carrots
- 1 tomato
- 1 square inch ham
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 6 fine chaurice or sausages
- 3 shallots
- 1 onion
- ½ pint of bouillon
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 gill white wine
- Thyme, parsley, bay leaf
- ¾ clove garlic

## Directions

1. Boil the carrots for one hour and a half.
2. Cut into dice or nice slices.
3. Put the butter in the saucepan, and add the onions, minced very fine, and the shallots, greens and whites.
4. Let these brown for a few minutes, and then add the half square inch of ham and three chaurice whole.
5. Let these simmer for three minutes, and add the minced herbs.
6. Add the tomato and its juice, mincing it well.
7. Let all simmer for three minutes more, till the tomato has browned, and add a half pint of bouillon and one gill of white wine.
8. Let all this simmer for ten minutes, and then add the carrots, nicely seasoned.
9. Stir well, cover and let them simmer for about half an hour.
10. Serve hot.

title: Casserole of Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Louisiana Rice
- 3 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/4 Cup of Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Egg
- Mushroom Sauce

## Directions

1. Make a Fricassee of Chicken with White Gravy.
2. Boil the rice according to recipe and then mash the rice thoroughly and add the butter and season with salt and pepper.
3. Take a raised pie pan or casserole and press the rice into this, and set away to cool.
4. When cool, cut out the center of the rice and fill the wall and bottom with the white fricassee of chicken.
5. Cover the top with the rice which you have cut out, laying on lightly, so as not to press the chicken sauce through.
6. Beat an egg well and brush over this.
7. Set in the oven and bake.
8. Serve with Mushroom Sauce.

title: Cauliflower a la Maitre d'Hotel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large or 2 Medium-Sized Cauliflowers
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- A Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower whole, as in the first recipe.
2. Bring them to the table whole, like blooming flowerlets, in a dish nicely garnished, and serve with a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel and lemon cut in quarters.

title: Cauliflower a la Vinaigrette
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large or 2 Medium-Sized Cauliflowers
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower as above, and serve a la Vinaigrette, that is, with vinegar, pepper and salt.

title: Cauliflower au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ordinary-Sized Head of Cauliflower
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1/2 Pint of Grated Parmesan and Gruyere Cheese, Mixed
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower as in the first recipe.
2. When boiled, take it off the fire and take out of the cheesecloth in which it was enveloped.
3. Let it cool.
4. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the frying pan, and as it melts add a tablespoonful of flour.
5. Let these blend nicely, without browning, and add immediately half a pint of milk and half a pint of grated Parmesan or Gruyere cheese.
6. Mix this thoroughly in the sauce, and let it cook well for about ten minutes.
7. Put the cauliflower in a pan, or the dish in which it is to be served, having greased the dish with butter.
8. Take some grated cheese, sprinkle well over the cauliflower, and then cover the cauliflower with the sauce, forcing it down into every nook and crevice.
9. When these crevices are full, and the cauliflower seems to have absorbed all, wipe the edges and all around the dish with a napkin.
10. Then add grated bread crumbs, sprinkling them over the cauliflower; dot it in about a dozen places with little bits of butter.
11. Set it in the oven, let it brown, and serve hot.
12. Let it bake about twenty minutes, or until brown.

title: Cauliflower Boiled With Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Medium-Sized Cauliflowers
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- A Tablespoonful of Salt
- A Pinch of Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower as in recipe Boiled Cauliflower, Cream Sauce.
2. After picking and washing thoroughly put in a saucepan and cover with cold water.
3. Add the salt and pepper and a tablespoonful of butter.
4. Let it cook for a half hour and then take the cauliflower from the pan and drain through a colander.
5. Place them on a dish and add a sauce made of one tablespoonful of butter, one of vinegar and a dash of salt and pepper, all mixed thoroughly, and serve hot.

title: Cauliflower Omelet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled cauliflower
- well-beaten egg
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Take the white part of a boiled cauliflower after it is cold
2. Chop it very small and mix with it a sufficient quantity of well-beaten egg, to make a very thick batter
3. Fry it in fresh butter in a small pan
4. Send it hot to table

title: Cauliflower Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Boiled Cauliflower
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Teaspoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- A French Dressing (plain)

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower as directed (See recipe)
2. Then separate the flowerets; mix them with parsley, and cut the remainder very fine and mix also
3. Let it cool
4. Serve with a French dressing, after adding first an extra teaspoonful of Tarragon vinegar

title: Cauliflower With White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large or 2 Small Cauliflowers
- A Teaspoonful of Salt
- A White Sauce or Sauce Allemande

## Directions

1. Separate the cauliflower, piece by piece, having taken off the outer leaves and cut off the rough stalk.
2. Place them in a pot of boiling water, with a teaspoonful of salt, and let them boil rapidly for about half an hour, till the stalks are tender.
3. When done, take them out gently with a skimmer, that you may not break them.
4. Place in a bowl and pour over a Sauce Blanche or Sauce Allemande.

title: Cauliflower, with White Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- pepper
- butter
- salt
- drawn butter
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the green stalks
2. If the heads are large, divide them into quarters
3. Wash and boil them with a little pepper, butter and salt
4. Serve with drawn butter or white sauce, when they become soft and tender

title: Caviar Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of French Toast
- ½ of a Box of Russian Caviar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the toast and cut in delicate circles
2. Put half the contents of a small box of Russian Caviars into a saucepan
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of cream
4. Heat one and a half minutes on the stove
5. Stir constantly
6. Pour this over the toast
7. Place on a dish on a folded napkin and serve
8. Alternatively, the circles of toast may be used as a foundation, the edges being spread with Anchovy butter, with an onion ring at its base
9. Fill this decorated ring with the Caviars
10. Place on a folded napkin on a dish and serve

title: Cedar, or Carolina Rice Birds
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sweet herbs
- gravy
- light paste

## Directions

1. Stew them with butter and sweet herbs
2. Bake them in a light paste, with plenty of gravy

title: Celery and Sweet Herbs Vinegar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two gills of celery seed
- sharp vinegar

## Directions

1. Take two gills of celery seed
2. pound them and put them in a bottle
3. fill the bottle with sharp vinegar
4. shake it every day for two weeks
5. then strain and bottle it for use

title: Celery au Naturel
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery
- Salt and water

## Directions

1. Scrape and wash the celery nicely.
2. Cut off the long outer leaves, leaving the tips nice and crisp.
3. Set in a celery glass or bowl, with about one inch of salt and water, and serve as an appetizer, or hors d'oeuvre, at the beginning of the meal.

title: Celery aux Petit Pois
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 stalks of celery
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 cup of milk or bouillon
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the celery into pieces and blanch and boil for about thirty minutes.
2. Drain.
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and add the flour, blending without browning.
4. Add the milk and salt and pepper to taste.
5. When it begins to boil add the chopped celery.
6. Let all simmer for twenty minutes longer, and then take off the fire and add the well-beaten yolk of an egg.
7. Season again to taste, and serve.

title: Celery Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 heads of celery
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 ounces of Parmesan cheese
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Batter

## Directions

1. Cut the celery into stalks three inches long.
2. Tie the stalks in bundles allowing three to a bundle.
3. Boil till tender in salted water, then take out.
4. Remove the strings and drain.
5. Season with salt and pepper, and the grated Parmesan cheese.
6. Dip in batter and fry and serve as a vegetable.

title: Celery Mayonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Crisp White Celery
- A Mayonnaise Sauce

## Directions

1. Chop the celery, or rather cut fine, as indicated in the above recipe
2. Mix the Mayonnaise with it
3. Garnish nicely with celery tips and serve

title: Celery Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- Hearts of 3 heads of celery
- ½ cupful of grated ham
- ½ cupful of cream
- ¾ cup of fine bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the celery hearts till tender, then drain and pound to a paste.
2. Mix with a cupful each of grated ham, cream and fine bread crumbs; season to taste with salt and pepper and add a tablespoonful of butter.
3. Steam the mixture till it thickens, then fill small patty cases with it and serve hot.

title: Celery Puree For Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight heads of celery
- half a pound of butter
- white soup stock
- sugar
- pepper
- salt
- a cup of milk or cream

## Directions

1. Chop up six or eight heads of celery, boil them a few minutes
2. Drain and put them in a saucepan, with half a pound of butter, some white soup stock, a little sugar, pepper and salt
3. Cook till soft, then strain it through a sieve
4. Heat it again and add a cup of milk or cream

title: Celery Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Crisp French Celery
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- French Dressing

## Directions

1. Cut the celery into pieces of about a quarter of an inch
2. Chop two hard-boiled eggs, not too fine, and mix well with the chopped celery
3. Blend all with French dressing and serve

title: Celery Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- bunch of celery
- water
- pint of milk
- spoonful of butter
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Mash a bunch of celery
2. Boil it soft in water
3. Cut the sticks into pieces 2 inches long
4. Make a sauce with a pint of milk, a spoonful of butter, flour, and salt to taste
5. Put the celery in and let it boil up once
6. Serve

title: Celery With Beef's Marrow
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 fine heads of celery
- 2 dozen slices of beef's marrow
- 1 pint of Madeira sauce
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the green leaves of the celery and pare nicely.
2. Wash well and drain.
3. Tie each head near the end where the green portion has been taken away.
4. Put them into boiling salted water and let them blanch for ten minutes.
5. Take out of the water and drain through a colander.
6. Make one pint of Madeira sauce, add the celery to this and let it cook for a quarter of an hour.
7. Take the celery, place on a dish and untie.
8. Add to the sauce in the saucepan about two dozen slices of beef marrow cut half an inch thick; cook for two minutes; do not allow the marrow to break.
9. Put in the dish with the celery, pour the sauce over and serve hot.

title: Celery à l'Espagnole
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 stalks of celery
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 onion
- Salt and pepper
- 1 tablespoonful vinegar
- Parsley
- Tarragon vinegar and oil

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the celery well, and then chop it fine.
2. Chop an onion very fine, and also several sprigs of parsley.
3. Take a hard-boiled egg and cut fine.
4. Mix all these together, pour over a little tarragon vinegar and oil, if desired, and serve as a salad.

title: Ceps on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ceps
- The juice of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- 6 slices of toast

## Directions

1. Drain the ceps from their oil, slice nicely and fry lightly in a frying pan.
2. When thoroughly heated take from the pan, sprinkle lightly with chopped parsley and lemon juice.
3. Arrange daintily on slices of toast and send to the table hot.

title: Chambord Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Large Onion Minced
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Large Tomatoes
- 1 Truffle, If Desired
- 6 Thinly Sliced Mushrooms
- 1 Pint Oyster Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Cloves, Mashed
- 4 Allspice, Ground

## Directions

1. Brown the onion in the butter, but do not let it burn.
2. Add three large tomatoes, chopped fine, with their juice and the finely-minced herbs, the thinly-sliced truffles and mushrooms.
3. Let these brown well for about ten minutes.
4. Then add the pint of oyster water, and season to taste.
5. Add, if you have them, three or four crawfish, chopped fine, and one dozen oysters.
6. Let all boil twenty minutes longer, and season to taste.
7. Serve with Baked Red Snapper and other baked fish.

title: Champagne Cocktail
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of wine
- two dashes of Angostura bitters
- two bits of lemon peel
- one small spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Put the bitters and lemon peel in the glass first
2. pour in the wine
3. put in one small spoonful of sugar
4. stir

title: Champagne Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Champagne
- 1 Lemon, Sliced
- 1 Sliced Orange
- A Dash of Old French Cognac
- 1/2 Pint of Seltzer Water
- A Dash of Curacoa
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water; add the liqueurs, and the sliced orange and lemon, and the ice
2. Stir well and let cool
3. Serve in small, thin tumblers, and decorate with a piece of fresh green mint on top

title: Champagne Punch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Temperance Beverage mixture
- a bottle of champagne
- a bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Add to the Fine Temperance Beverage mixture a bottle of champagne, and a bottle of white wine
2. You have a very delicious punch for festive occasions

title: Champagne Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Bottle of Champagne
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Quart of Rhine Wine
- 0.5 Pint of Brandy
- 0.5 Pint of Curacoa
- 2 Quarts of Seltzer Water
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Take a large punch bowl and dissolve in it the above quantities of sugar, lemon juice, Rhine Wine, Champagne, Seltzer Water and Curacoa.
2. Mix well, and set to cool and serve in small punch glasses.

title: Champagne Punch à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Quart Bottle of Champagne
- 1 Quart of Best White Wine
- 0.5 Pint of Curacoa
- 2 Quarts of Seltzer Water
- 0.5 of a Grated Pineapple
- 0.5 of a Sliced Pineapple
- 3 Dozen Strawberries
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Take a large punch bowl, and dissolve in it one pound of sugar, one pint of lemon juice, one quart of White Wine, one quart bottle of Champagne, two quarts of Seltzer Water, one-eighth of a pint of Curacoa, and one-half of a grated pineapple.
2. Mix well.
3. Put in a large piece of ice, decorate with strawberries and sliced pineapple, let it cool, and serve in small cup glasses.

title: Champagne Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass of Champagne
- 2 cloves
- 6 whole peppers
- 1 bay leaf
- 3/4 of a pint of Sauce Espagnole
- 1/8 teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put the Champagne, cloves, peppers, bay leaf and sugar in a saucepan
2. Set on the fire and reduce for five minutes
3. Moisten the mixture with three quarters of a pint of Sauce Espagnole
4. Let it cook for fifteen minutes longer
5. Strain well and serve

title: Chancellor's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. loaf of stale bread
- butter
- raisins or almonds
- currants
- sugar
- nutmeg
- lemon peel
- 4 eggs
- ½ pint of sweet cream or milk
- small glass of good brandy
- glass of lemon juice
- white wine

## Directions

1. Butter and slice very thin 1 lb. loaf of stale bread
2. Butter very well a large sized tin pudding dish or pan
3. Stick raisins or almonds in the bottom and sides of the pan gracefully
4. Put a layer of bread
5. Lay over it a layer of currants, raisins, sugar, and chopped almonds, nutmeg and grated lemon peel
6. Lay on a layer of bread, raisins, currants, etc., in alternate layers, until all is in the pan
7. Pour over it batter made of 4 eggs well beaten, ½ pint of sweet cream or milk, a small glass of good brandy, glass of lemon juice, butter and a little more sugar
8. Tie a strong double paper over the pan and place it in a boiling bottle of water, being careful that the water does not reach the top of the pan
9. Let it boil for 1½ hours
10. Turn it out on a deep dessert plate or dish
11. Pour over it boiled custard, seasoned with white wine
12. Stick a few whole blanched almonds and serve it up for table

title: Charlotte Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of yeast dough
- lemon or bitter almond for flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream the butter with the yeast dough
2. Mix in the eggs, sugar, and flavoring
3. Bake as you would light bread

title: Charlotte Des Pommes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 or 15 apples
- loaf sugar
- pounded cinnamon
- grated lemon peel
- 2½ ounces fresh butter
- bread
- melted butter
- apricot jam or marmalade

## Directions

1. Pare, core and mince 14 or 15 very nice apples; put them into a frying pan with some loaf sugar, a little pounded cinnamon, grated lemon peel, and 2½ ounces of fresh butter; fry them ½ hour over a quick fire, stirring them constantly
2. Butter a shape of the size the charlotte is intended to be; cut strips of bread 2 inches wide, and long enough to reach from the bottom to the rim of the shape, so that the whole be lined with bread; dip each bit into melted butter, and then put a layer of the fried apples, and one of apricot jam or marmalade, and one of bread dipped into butter; begin and finish with it
3. Bake it in an oven for nearly 1 hour; turn it out to serve it; it may be boiled and served with a sweet sauce

title: Charlotte Polonaise
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint sweet milk
- 2 ounces grated chocolate
- 2 ounces white sugar
- 1 lb. macaroons
- 1/2 pint milk
- 4 ounces blanched almonds
- 1 dozen blanched bitter almonds
- 4 ounces pounded citron
- 4 ounces white sugar
- sponge cake
- wine
- rich custard
- white of egg
- fine sugar
- raspberry or other jelly
- icing

## Directions

1. Boil in 1/2 pint of sweet milk, until perfectly dissolved, 2 ounces of grated chocolate, 2 ounces of white sugar, 1 lb. of macaroons, which must be first broken into small pieces
2. In another 1/2 pint of milk, mix 4 ounces of blanched almonds finely pounded and very sweet, 1 dozen blanched bitter almonds, 4 ounces of pounded citron, and 4 ounces of white sugar
3. Let this mixture also boil a few minutes
4. Set them both away to cool
5. Cut a sponge cake into slices 1/2 an inch thick
6. Wet them well with wine and spread them alternately with the chocolate and almond mixtures
7. Put them one upon another, until the dish is filled
8. Then pour a rich custard over them, and when they are well soaked, cover the whole with a floating island, made with white of egg, whipped to a froth, and sweetened and flavored with fine sugar and raspberry or other jelly
9. Instead of the custard and floating island, you may cover the cake thickly with icing, such as is used for pound cake
10. The polonaise must not be put into the oven that the icing may be dried, but should be prepared an hour or two before it is wanted that the icing may become glossy

title: Charlotte Russe
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Good Cream
- 3/4 Cup of Powdered White Sugar
- 1/4 Box of Gelatine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sherry Wine
- 1/2 Pound of Lady Fingers

## Directions

1. Line a glass dish with the lady fingers, or with small slices of cake.
2. Soak the gelatine in cold water, just covered, for half an hour.
3. Whip the cream, and then dissolve the soaked gelatine with the Sherry Wine.
4. Add the Vanilla Extract.
5. Add the sugar to the cream carefully, and then strain in the gelatine very carefully.
6. Stir immediately, and then pour the cream over the lady fingers, and set away to harden.

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- quarter of a pound of sugar
- half a pint of cream
- lemon or vanilla for flavoring
- sponge cake or lady fingers

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of three eggs to a high froth with a quarter of a pound of sugar and half a pint of cream, until it is quite thick and light
2. Flavor to your taste with lemon or vanilla
3. Pour it into a cake-lined mould
4. Place some of the sliced cake or lady fingers on top of the mould and over the cream
5. Set it on ice
6. When wanted turn it on a dish and serve

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of isinglass or gelatine
- one and a half pints of milk
- half a pound of white sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- vanilla
- one pint of cream
- strips of sponge cake

## Directions

1. Boil one ounce of isinglass or gelatine, in one and a half pints of milk
2. sweeten it with half a pound of white sugar
3. beat in the yolks of six eggs
4. flavor it with vanilla
5. When this mixture begins to stiffen as it grows cold, stir into it one pint of cream whipped to a froth
6. Ornament the glass dish it is to be served in, with strips of sponge cake
7. pour the Charlotte Russe in
8. Set it in a cool place until wanted

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake or lady fingers
- whipped cream, custard or blancmange
- the whites of three eggs
- a quarter of a pound of sugar
- half a pint of cream

## Directions

1. beat the whites of three eggs to a high froth, with a quarter of a pound of sugar, and half a pint of cream, until it is quite thick and high
2. flavor this to your taste with lemon or vanilla
3. pour it into a cake-lined mould
4. place some of the sliced cake or lady fingers on top of the mould and over the cream
5. set it on ice
6. when wanted turn it on a dish and serve

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Six eggs, yolks only
- 2½ dozen ladies' fingers
- 6 ounces of granulated sugar
- ½ ounce of gelatine
- 1 pint of pure sweet milk
- 1 pint of thick sweet cream
- ice and salt
- tablespoonful of extract of vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the gelatine in the milk cold and put on to boil in a bain Marie
2. Stir now and then
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar
4. When the milk comes to a boil, add the eggs and sugar to the milk
5. Stir constantly to let the custard thicken well and to prevent curdling
6. Strain through a swiff muslin cloth and set aside in a cool place to get cold and to thicken
7. Put cream in a pan and put this in a larger pan which has been filled with ice and salt
8. When cream gets very cold, whip it up with a fork or egg beater until it is very light
9. Line mould with ladies' fingers
10. Gradually slip the cream into the custard, which must be quite thick, until all is used and the custard is free from lumps
11. Flavor with a tablespoonful of extract of vanilla
12. Pour the mixture into the mould
13. Pack in ice and salt to stand for not less than 2 hours

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Three ounces of isinglass
- 1 quart of water
- vanilla
- yolks of 3 eggs
- white of 1 egg
- 3 pints of cream
- sponge cake
- ice

## Directions

1. Put isinglass in 1 quart of water with vanilla sufficient to flavor it
2. Boil it down to a pint, or sufficient to make a strong jelly
3. When done, strain through a sieve
4. Take the yolks of 3 eggs and the white of 1 and beat them well
5. Stir them in the jelly while hot
6. Sweeten to your taste and put it to cool
7. Take 3 pints of cream and beat it to a high froth and let it drain on a sieve
8. When the jelly is cold, stir in the froth
9. Butter the moulds and have a nice sponge cake ready to cut
10. Lay it round so as to fit close and cut the same in narrow pieces
11. Lay in the bottom so as not to move them
12. Pour in the froth and put it on the ice to cool for 3 hours
13. Have a cloth dipped in hot water and wipe over the mould
14. Turn the mould bottom upwards on the dish and take it off
15. Be careful not to make it too warm, or the shape will be spoiled

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 ounce of isinglass
- 1 cup of sweet milk
- 1 pint of rich cream
- powdered loaf sugar
- vanilla or rose essence
- whites of 7 eggs
- sponge cake

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1/2 ounce of isinglass in a cup of sweet milk
2. Have ready a pint of rich cream
3. Stir into the cream powdered loaf sugar until it is very sweet
4. Flavor with vanilla, rose or any other essence
5. Beat up the whites of 7 eggs very light
6. Stir the dissolved isinglass into the bowl of cream after it is sweetened and flavored
7. Set the bowl into a tub of ice and stir until it thickens
8. Add last the whites of eggs
9. Put the mixture into a mould lined with sponge cake
10. Place the mould on ice
11. The cake can be joined with a little isinglass to make it stick
12. The charlotte may be made in the morning for a late dinner, or in winter over night
13. Turn the mould after cutting it round and the charlotte will come out

title: Charlotte Russe, Without Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box of gelatine
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 10 eggs
- 1 pint of cold water
- 1 pint of boiling water
- vanilla
- lady fingers or sponge cake

## Directions

1. Pour over the gelatine 1 pint of cold water and allow it to soak at least 10 minutes
2. Pour over 1 pint of boiling water
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar
4. Boil the milk in a porcelain or brass kettle
5. When milk comes to a boil, pour in the gelatine and let the mixture come to a boil again, stirring all the time
6. As soon as it boils, pour over the beaten eggs and sugar, stirring briskly to mix it well
7. Have the kettle thoroughly scraped at the bottom, then pour the whole back into it
8. Put it over a moderate fire to thicken, being careful not to let it curdle
9. The moment it begins to simmer, take it off and pour out, stirring occasionally until it cools
10. When cool, add the beaten whites and flavor with vanilla
11. Line a pan with lady fingers or sponge cake and pour in the mixture
12. Set it in the coolest place possible to congeal
13. To prevent the lady fingers floating out of their places, pour a little of the mixture in and let it congeal to fix them at the bottom and sides of the pan
14. When that is done, pour in the rest of the mixture and it will congeal within the cake

title: Chartreuse of Partridge
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Partridges
- A Fine Tender Head of Cabbage
- 12 Chaurice (Sausage)
- ¾ Pound of Salt Pork
- 3 Small Onions
- 2 Turnips
- 2 Carrots
- 4 Cloves
- 1½ Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- ¾ Cup of Green Peas
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Pint of Veal or Chicken Broth
- 1 Pint of Consomme
- ¾ Pint of Demi Glace, or Madeira Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the partridges and cabbage exactly as for the recipe Partridges and Cabbage.
