2. Butter a three-pint mold lightly; cut the turnips, carrots and onion into small even pieces, using a vegetable tube; put a layer of the cut vegetables in the bottom of the mold.
3. Lay on top a layer of the cooked cabbage, cut the partridges into pieces and place a layer of them on the cabbage, filling in the hollow spaces with cabbage chopped fine and the chopped vegetables.
4. Fill in further with the sliced sausage and lay on top six slices of the salt pork; then place another layer of the partridges, fill in the hollow places with the sliced turnips and carrots and onions and the sausage.
5. Place on top another layer of cabbage, covering the top well with the cabbage and pressing down very carefully; decorate the mold prettily around the edges with the sliced carrots and turnips and place in a tin baking pan and set in a moderate oven for fifteen minutes.
6. Have at hand a hot dish turn the mold upside down and carefully draw it off the preparation. Send to the table hot and serve with Demi Glace, or Madeira Sauce.

title: Chaufausen Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chaufausen Wine
- 1 Pint of Cold Water
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- 6 Cloves
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Inch of Cinnamon
- 1 Ounce of Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Put the sugar, cinnamon, zest of lemon, cloves and water into a saucepan and set on a good fire and let them come to a boil, which will be in about five minutes.
2. Then add the cornstarch diluted in a tablespoonful of cold water and stir it briskly into the water.
3. After three minutes of stirring add one pint of Chaufausen wine, let it cook for two minutes longer and then take off the fire, drain through a sieve and serve with puddings, especially Apple Pudding.

title: Chaurice
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of lean, fresh pork
- 2 pounds of fat fresh pork
- 2 large onions, minced very fine
- 1 clove of garlic, minced very fine
- 1 teaspoonful of cayenne pepper and chili pepper (very hot)
- 1 teaspoonful of red pepper
- 3 teaspoonfuls of salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls finely ground black pepper
- 1 sprig of thyme, well minced
- 3 sprigs of parsley, finely minced
- 2 bay leaves, chopped or minced very fine
- 1/8 teaspoonful of allspice, very fine
- Sausage casings

## Directions

1. Hash the pork as fine as possible — fat and lean — and mix together.
2. Season highly with the salt and black pepper and cayenne, chili and red pepper (pimento).
3. Mince the onion and garlic as fine as possible, then add to the chaurice.
4. Mince the herbs as fine as possible, and add, and then mix the finely ground spices thoroughly with the chaurice.
5. Hash all together, and when well mixed, take the casings (that have been well cleaned by the butcher), scald them and wash thoroughly again.
6. Dry them and fill with the mixture, tying them in the lengths you desire.
7. Fry in boiling lard for breakfast, always having sufficient to have the sausage swim in it, and serve, after draining of all grease, on a hot dish with minced parsley thrown over as a garnish.

title: Chaurice With Creole Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of chaurice (about 6 to a pound)
- 1 clove of minced garlic
- 1/2 can of tomatoes
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of black pepper
- 1 large onion
- 1/2 spoon of lard
- About 1/2 cup of boiling water

## Directions

1. Place a half teaspoonful of lard in the frying pan or stewpan, and when it heats, add the chopped onion.
2. Let this brown slightly and then add the minced garlic.
3. Add the half can of tomatoes.
4. As this browns, put in the sausage which you have pricked gently.
5. Cover and let them simmer for about five minutes, then add the seasonings to taste.
6. Add about a half cup of boiling water.
7. Cover well and let all simmer for twenty minutes longer.

title: Chaurice With Puree of Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of chaurice
- 4 Irish potatoes
- 1 egg, well beaten
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prick the sausages and lay them in the bottom of a pan.
2. Make a soft puree of potatoes and pour this over the sausage.
3. Spread a beaten egg very evenly on top, sprinkle with bread crumbs, and place in the oven and let it bake a half hour.

title: Cheap and Reliable Gold and Silver Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of butter
- four cups of sugar
- two cups of sweet milk or water
- eight cups of sifted flour
- four teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- two teaspoonfuls of soda
- yolks and whites of eight eggs

## Directions

1. cream butter and sugar together
2. beat separately the yolks and whites of eight eggs
3. Take half the batter; use the yolks for the gold cake, and the whites for the silver cake
4. Flavor differently, as with rose and lemon

title: Cheap Fruit Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of sifted flour
- a teacupful of sugar
- half a cup of butter
- one cupful of raisins
- two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- one teaspoonful of soda
- two tablespoonfuls of mixed spices
- cold water

## Directions

1. To one quart of sifted flour, add a teacupful of sugar, half a cup of butter, one cupful of raisins, two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar, and one of soda, two tablespoonfuls of mixed spices
2. rub thoroughly together the flour, cream of tartar, soda and butter
3. stir in sufficient cold water to make a stiff batter
4. add the spices and raisins
5. pour it into a small tin pan
6. bake one hour

title: Cheap Gingerbread Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of butter
- three and a half cups of flour
- one cup of milk
- one cup of molasses
- one teaspoonful of saleratus
- rich sauce

## Directions

1. Take a cup of butter, rub it up with three and a half cups of flour, one cup of milk, one cup of molasses, and one teaspoonful of saleratus
2. Steam three hours
3. Serve with a rich sauce

title: Cheap Jelly Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of fine white sugar
- one cup of milk or water
- two tablespoonfuls of butter
- one egg
- two cups of flour
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- one-half teaspoonful of carbonate soda
- nutmeg or lemon flavoring
- plum or currant jelly

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, milk or water, and butter
2. Add egg and flour
3. Add cream of tartar and carbonate soda
4. Flavor with nutmeg or lemon
5. Bake
6. Fill with plum or currant jelly

title: Cheap Plum Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A cup of chopped suet
- A cup of raisins
- A cup of currants and citron mixed
- A cup of sweet milk
- Two eggs
- A cup of molasses
- A teaspoonful of soda
- Three and a half cups of sifted flour or bread crumbs
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Combine the chopped suet, raisins, currants and citron mixed, sweet milk, eggs, molasses, and soda
2. Add three and a half cups of sifted flour or bread crumbs, and a little salt
3. Boil three or four hours
4. Serve with hard sauce of beaten butter, sugar and nutmeg; or with butter, sugar and wine sauce

title: Cheap Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- 1 tumbler of brown sugar
- 1 lb. of melted butter
- 1 quart of flour
- 1 tumbler of milk with 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Pour eggs well beaten with a tumbler of brown sugar
2. Add 1 lb. of melted butter
3. Add 1 quart of flour
4. Add 1 tumbler of milk with 1 teaspoonful of soda
5. Mix all well together
6. Pour into a pan, and bake in a quick oven
7. When done, serve it with wine and sugar sauce

title: Cheap White Cakes. For Tea
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sifted flour
- one ounce of butter
- a quarter of a pound of fine sugar
- one egg
- half a teaspoonful of caraway seeds
- milk enough to make a paste

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar into flour
2. add egg and caraway seeds
3. add milk to make a paste
4. roll out to quarter-of-an-inch thickness or thinner
5. cut in small round cakes
6. bake on tin plates in a quick oven ten or twelve minutes

title: Cheese Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 Pound of Butter
- 0.5 Pound of Flour
- 5 Ounces of Grated Swiss Cheese
- 0.5 Tablespoonful of Mustard
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream.
2. Add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten, and mix well.
3. Add gradually the grated cheese, mustard and Cayenne.
4. Add the flour gradually, beating in thoroughly and make a stiff dough.
5. Roll it out and cut into square or round biscuits.
6. Bake in a rather slow oven for twenty minutes and serve.

title: Cheese Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of butter
- 6 ounces of sugar
- 4 eggs
- a glass of brandy or wine
- a little rose water
- a teaspoonful of nutmeg or mace
- a handful of currants
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add eggs, brandy or wine, rose water, nutmeg or mace, and currants
3. Bake it in puff paste

title: Cheese Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Swiss Cheese
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 1 Gill of Sauce Piquante

## Directions

1. Take six slices of bread cut the whole width of the loaf, one-half inch in thickness, and hollow out one-half of the inner portion.
2. Toast this nicely and spray the inner part with Piquante Sauce.
3. Have ready six slices of toasted or baked Swiss cheese; fit a slice into each cavity in the sliced toast.
4. Set in the oven for a few minutes and serve very hot.

title: Cheese Fondu
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Grated Parmesan or Gruyere Cheese
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sifted Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Eggs
- 1/4 Cup of Fresh Milk or Cream
- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put the butter into a saucepan and melt, and add the flour, blending without allowing it to brown
2. Add immediately the boiling milk, and let it boil for two minutes
3. Remove from the fire and stir in the yolks of three eggs, well beaten
4. Add salt, pepper, nutmeg and the grated cheese
5. Mix all thoroughly
6. Have ready a small pudding dish, or several small souffle dishes and butter these well
7. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth and beat them into the mixture
8. Fill the dishes about two-thirds full
9. Bake in a moderate oven for about twenty-five minutes
10. Serve immediately

title: Cheese Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 ounces of good grated cheese
- 2 ounces of flour
- Yolks of 3 eggs
- A little salt and pepper
- J pint of milk
- A little more milk
- A large tablespoonful of butter
- Whites of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Add to 7 ounces of good grated cheese, 2 ounces of flour, the yolks of 3 eggs, well beaten, and a little salt and pepper
2. Mix all together in a pan
3. Add J pint of milk
4. Place this mixture on the fire, taking care not to let it burn
5. When it has been cooking for a short time, add a little more milk and a large tablespoonful of butter
6. Take it from the fire and allow it to cool
7. Beat up the whites of 6 eggs, very stiff, and add it gradually to the rest
8. When the whole is well mixed together, put it in a dish or deep plate, and place it in a cooking stove moderately heated
9. As soon as the mixture has risen well, and is of a good color, serve it

title: Cheese Ramakins
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Cheese
- 1.5 Gills of Milk
- The Yolks of Three Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Ounces of Bread
- 0.5 Tablespoonful of Prepared Mustard
- The Whites of Three Eggs
- Cayenne and Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Put the milk on to boil, and add the bread, which you will have minced fine.
2. Stir the milk and bread until very smooth, and then add the butter, stirring well, and finally the grated cheese.
3. Stir this for five minutes, letting it boil, and then take off the fire and add the beaten yolks of three eggs.
4. Have the whites ready, beaten to a stiff froth, and stir them in very gently.
5. Season to taste.
6. Grease a baking dish with butter and pour the mixture into the dish, set in a quick oven, and let it bake for a quarter of an hour.
7. Serve hot.

title: Cheese Soufflé
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 Pound of Grated Cheese
- 1 Gill of Fresh Cream or Milk
- 3 Creole Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Pinch Cayenne
- Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Grate the cheese very fine and then add to it the boiling milk.
2. Add gradually the pepper, Cayenne and salt.
3. Add the butter and flour, rubbed well together.
4. When the cheese is well dissolved, take the mixture off.
5. Add nutmeg and the beaten yolks of three eggs and the whites of two eggs beaten to a froth.
6. Stir the whole well.
7. Place all in a shallow earthen dish, add a little butter that has been well melted and put in the stove for a few minutes till it begins to solidify well.
8. Take out and spread on top the white of one egg, beaten to a stiff froth.
9. Set one minute in the stove, let it brown slightly and serve immediately.

title: Cheese Straws
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Grated Parmesan or Gruyere Cheese
- 1 Cup Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Yolk of an Egg
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Blend the flour and cheese together and add at the same time the salt and Cayenne.
2. Moisten with a well-beaten egg and one tablespoonful of melted butter, and work all gently into a paste.
3. Roll out on a biscuit board into thin strips of not more than an eighth of an inch in thickness, and cut into strips of four inches in length and one-eighth of an inch in width.
4. Place on buttered sheets of paper and bake in a very hot oven until a light brown.
5. It is very pretty to make little rings of some of the strips and pass the others through them in little bundles like gathered bound bits of straw.

title: Cherry and Noyeau Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Soaked Gum Arabic
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Gill of Cherry Juice
- 3/4 Gill of Noyeau
- The Whites of 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Work the gum, Noyeau and sugar to an elastic paste
2. Use the cherry juice and whites of the eggs and some of the sugar for the Royal Icing
3. Proceed as in Chocolate Cream Bonbons with the white Noyeau Cream forming the inside and the cherry-colored icing the outside

title: Cherry Charlotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound Cherries
- 1 Pound White Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Soft of Bread

## Directions

1. Seed the cherries, and butter the bottom of a pudding dish.
2. Cover the bottom with a layer of the bread crumbs which must be soft.
3. Add a layer of the cherries, which have been cooked like the apples, into a marmalade.
4. Sprinkle with sugar, and add another layer of cherries, and then a layer of bread crumbs, alternating till the dish is filled, letting the last layer be of crumbs.
5. Dot the last layer with bits of butter.
6. Put in an oven and bake for an hour.
7. Serve cold with cream.

title: Cherry Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cherries
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and water into a saucepan, and when it makes a syrup add the cherries
2. Let them boil up

title: Cherry Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cherries
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Water
- a glassful of Currant Jelly

## Directions

1. To one pound of stoned cherries allow the above amount of sugar.
2. Put the sugar and water into a saucepan, and when it makes a syrup add the cherries.
3. Let them boil up several times.
4. Then drain them with the skimming spoon, and put them in a dish, and add to the juice a glassful of Currant Jelly.
5. Let it boil to a thin jelly, and pour it, when half cold, over the cherries.

title: Cherry Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fine Cherries
- 1 Egg
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Madeira or Malaga Wine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar
- The Zest of a Lemon
- Bread slices
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the cherries by taking out the stones.
2. Make them into a thick marmalade, adding a tablespoonful of Madeira or Malaga Wine.
3. Soak nice slices of bread in milk and egg, seasoned well with a tablespoonful of sugar and the grated zest of a lemon.
4. Take the slices, when well-soaked, and spread the marmalade well over them, making it adhere very thickly and closely into the meshes of the bread.
5. Then fry in boiling lard.
6. Sprinkle nicely with powdered sugar, and serve hot.
7. Alternatively, mix the cherries in Creole Fritter Batter, drop by spoonfuls into the boiling lard, fry to a golden brown, drain in the oven, and sprinkle with powdered white sugar and serve hot.

title: Cherry Jam
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries (they should be the short stem); take equal quantities of sugar and fruit, and boil slowly till it is almost a jelly; it is delicious and keeps well

title: Cherry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Cherries
- ¾ of a Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe cherries.
2. Stone them, but be careful not to allow them to lose their juice.
3. Arrange in layers as directed above.
4. Set on the fire and boil till the syrup is very clear and begins to form a jelly when a portion is turned upon a plate.
5. Bottle as directed.

title: Cherry Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- Sugar - half a pound to every pound of cherries if ripe and sour, a quarter of a pound otherwise

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries
2. Allow half a pound of sugar to every pound of cherries if the cherries are ripe and sour, and a quarter of a pound otherwise
3. Set over the fire until the heat causes the juice to flow freely
4. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Strawberry or Blackberry Jelly

title: Cherry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cherries
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water

## Directions

1. Stew the cherries gently, as for a marmalade
2. Prepare the pie crust according to directions, and proceed as in directions for making pies

title: Cherry Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Cherries
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together for five minutes
2. Stone the cherries and add them to the syrup
3. Set to cool
4. Press through a sieve till all the juice is extracted from the cherries
5. Set to freeze and serve as directed

title: Cherry Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Pints of Cherry Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup

## Directions

1. Seed the cherries, and stem, pound and mash them well, and let them stand for thirty-six hours in a cool place.
2. Then strain through a bag.
3. Cook the sugar and water till it forms the syrup called "Le Petit Casse," or crack, till it instantly snaps asunder when placed between the fingers, after dipping them in cold water to enable you to test.
4. Then add the fruit syrups and mix thoroughly.
5. Let it boil for about five minutes, take off the fire, let it get almost cold, and then put up in bottles.

title: Cherry Water
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Cherries
- 1 Lemon
- 8 Ounces of Sugar

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries and press the juice from the pulp into a porcelain vessel
2. Add a little water and the juice of one lemon
3. Stir well and let stand for two hours
4. Wash the stones well and pound them fine, and add to the cherries, and add eight ounces of sugar
5. Let all stand one hour
6. Strain the mixture and stir well and filter
7. Set on ice till ready to use
8. Serve in glasses with crushed ice

title: Cherry Water Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pints of cherry water
- 4 ounces of orange flower water
- 4 pounds of sugar
- 1 quart of water

## Directions

1. Take seven pints of cherry water and distill it to nearly one-half
2. Add four ounces of orange flower water
3. Let it stand for four or five hours
4. Dissolve four pounds of sugar in a quart of distilled and filtered Mississippi water, and set it over the fire till well-blended and dissolved
5. Take it off, and let it cool
6. Add the cherry water, strain and bottle

title: Chervil Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chervil
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Serve cut fine between bits, in the same manner as Lettuce Salad, With a French Dressing

title: Chervil Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of dried or fresh chervil leaves
- 1 quart of best French vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a wide-mouthed bottle, and half fill it with either fresh or dry chervil leaves.
2. Fill the bottle with the best vinegar, and set it in hot water till it reaches the boiling point.
3. Then remove from the fire.
4. When cool cork and in about two weeks it will be ready for use.

title: Chestnut Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Chestnuts
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts and blanch them, and pound sufficient quantity to make two ounces reduced to flour.
2. Or roast two dozen chestnuts and peel them, and pound them till very fine, and blend with a little milk till you have a thick paste.
3. Add the yolks of two eggs, well beaten, and two tablespoonfuls of the best butter.
4. Beat well, and add four ounces of powdered white sugar.
5. Beat all this very light, and put into one pint of boiling milk, or half milk and half cream.
6. Let it boil for about ten minutes, and then take off.
7. Strain, decorate in a glass dish, and serve cold.

title: Chestnut Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of large roasted chestnuts
- 1 pint of boiling stock
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Roast the chestnuts, and peel and mash them very fine
2. Make a Brown Roux with the flour and butter
3. Add the boiling stock
4. Let it boil for about five minutes
5. Add the mashed chestnuts, stirring constantly, and seasoning to taste
6. Let it boil for two minutes
7. Take off and serve hot with Broiled Dindonneau (turkey chicks) or Roast Turkey

title: Chestnut Sauce For Turkey Or Fowls
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of veal stock
- half a pound of chestnuts
- peel of half a lemon
- a cupful of cream or milk
- cayenne
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the dark shell of the chestnuts, and scald them until the inner skin can be taken off
2. Put them into the saucepan with the stock, the lemon peel cut very thin, some cayenne and salt
3. Let it simmer till the chestnuts are quite soft
4. Rub it through a sieve
5. Add the seasoning and cream, and let it simmer for a few minutes, taking care it does not boil, and stirring constantly

title: Chestnut Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- lean fresh beef, mutton or venison
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- water
- 1 quart or more of chestnuts
- fresh butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a soup of the lean of fresh beef, mutton or venison in the best manner
2. Season with cayenne pepper and a little salt, allowing rather less than a quart of water to each pound of meat
3. Skim and boil it till the meat drops from the bone
4. Strain it and put it in a clean pot
5. Have ready a quart or more of nice chestnuts, boiled and peeled
6. Put them into the soup with some small bits of fresh butter rolled in flour
7. Boil the soup 10 minutes longer before it goes to the table

title: Chestnut Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Quarts of Oyster Water, or A Good Round of Beef or Veal
- 1 Quart of Chestnuts
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Cayenne Pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Make a good broth of the veal or beef; season with the Cayenne pepper and salt. Follow the rule given for making soups, by allowing a pound of meat to each quart of water.
2. Skim and boil till the meat falls into rags; then strain and put in a clean porcelain pot.
3. In the meantime, shell the chestnuts and throw them into boiling water until the skin comes off easily.
4. Put them into a saucepan with some of the soup water, and boil about thirty minutes, till quite soft.
5. Press through a colander; add butter, pepper and salt.
6. Then add to the soup.
7. Make dumplings the size of a marble with fresh butter rolled in flour, and add.
8. Boil the soup about fifteen minutes longer and serve.
9. On fast days use the oyster water instead of the beef broth, following the recipe in all other particulars, and adding a half tablespoonful of butter to the purée before pressing through the colander.

title: Chestnuts With Brown Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chestnuts
- 1 Pint of Brown Sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts in the same manner as Stewed Chestnuts, as far as boiling
2. Make a Brown Sauce (see Sauce Espagnole)
3. Add the chestnuts to it and sauté for fifteen minutes longer
4. Let all simmer gently and serve hot

title: Chicken a la Jardiniере
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Chicken
- 6 Small Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Head of Cauliflower
- 1/2 Cup of Green Peas
- 3 Small Artichokes
- 1/4 Can of Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut and stew the chicken as in Fricassee Brown Gravy
2. After adding the water, add a half dozen small onions, and let it simmer for an hour or until tender
3. Then add one-quarter can of mushrooms, a small half head of cauliflower (nicely chopped), a half cup of green peas and several cooked artichokes
4. Set upon a quick fire, mix well and add a pint of good broth or water
5. Let all cook for twenty minutes longer and serve hot

title: Chicken a la Jardinniere
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Chicken
- 6 Small Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Head of Cauliflower
- 1/2 Cup of Green Peas
- 3 Small Artichokes
- 1/4 Can of Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut and stew the chicken as in Fricassee Brown Gravy.
2. After adding the water, add a half dozen small onions, and let it simmer for an hour or until tender.
3. Add one-quarter can of mushrooms, a small half head of cauliflower (nicely chopped), a half cup of green peas and several cooked artichokes.
4. Set upon a quick fire, mix well and add a pint of good broth or water.
5. Let all cook together.

title: Chicken A la Tartare
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Spring Chicken
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf, Minced Fine
- 1 Chopped Onion
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the chicken according to the above recipe, adding the chopped vegetables and herbs.
2. Season to taste.
3. When done, place on a hot dish, butter nicely and serve with a Sauce a la Tartare.

title: Chicken and Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Chicken
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham
- 2 Small Turnips
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Onions
- 1 Small Piece Lemon Peel
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Small Piece of Red Pepper Pod
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Whole Cloves, Without the Seed
- 3 Sliced Tomatoes
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Butter
- 1 Cup of Well-washed Rice
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut and season the chicken well with salt and pepper.
2. Put the butter into the saucepan and let it melt, and add the seasoned chicken.
3. Let it brown well, and add the vegetables, all chopped very fine.
4. Add the minced herbs and garlic, and after this the spices.
5. Let all simmer gently for ten minutes, and pour over two quarts of boiling water.
6. Stir and season again to taste and set back on the stove and let it simmer steadily and slowly for three-quarters of an hour.
7. When two-thirds cooked, add one cup of well-washed rice, stir well, seasoning again to taste.
8. Do not let the rice become mushy. Let the grains stand out.
9. Let all cook for twenty minutes longer and serve, taking out first the pieces of chicken and ranging the rice around as a garnish.
10. Serve with the sauce poured over.

title: Chicken Balls, Queen Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Chicken
- 2 Small Onions
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Make the mixture in exactly the same manner as croquettes.
2. Place the mixture in a frying pan and fry in butter, using about a tablespoonful.
3. To this add about one pint of milk.
4. Beat the chicken thoroughly in this, add a grated nutmeg, then take off the fire and add two eggs, well beaten.
5. Fill custard cups with the mixture, place in the oven setting in a pan of boiling water and covering with paper.
6. Let them bake thus as you would a cup custard for twenty minutes.

title: Chicken Broth
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good large chicken
- cold water
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken carefully and cut it up, being careful to mash all the bones with an ax.
2. Place in a saucepan of cold water, and let it simmer gently for four or five hours, until it is boiled down to about two cups of broth.
3. Season with a little salt and pepper, omitting all vegetables.

title: Chicken Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Toast
- 1 Cup of Chicken Forcemeat
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Gill of Cream
- The Whites of 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 2 Ounces of Parmesan Cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare a Chicken Forcemeat
2. Prepare six slices of toast, cut square or in circles
3. Add an ounce of butter and one gill of cream to the chicken forcemeat
4. Work well together, and then set to cool
5. Spread the toast lightly with butter
6. Spread over each slice a portion of the chicken forcemeat to the thickness of one-quarter of an inch
7. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese
8. Set in the oven and bake for five minutes
9. Decorate in the center with delicately cut pieces of the white of hard-boiled eggs
10. Serve

title: Chicken Consomme
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large chicken cut in pieces
- 3 quarts of cold water
- 1 onion
- 1 small stalk of celery
- 1 carrot
- 1 turnip
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put the chicken into the salt and water and let it simmer gently until the scum begins to rise; then skim.
2. Add the other ingredients.
3. Boil gently for two hours, and serve with slices of toast.

title: Chicken Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Chicken
- 2 Small Onions
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Cayenne and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Eggs
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the chicken as directed in the recipe for boiling.
2. Then, when cold, remove all the tough fibers and nerves.
3. Hash the chicken well and season with the minced vegetables and sweet herbs, mixing all thoroughly.
4. Then take a cup of the soft of the bread, wet it and squeeze, and soak in milk, in which you have beaten two eggs.
5. Mix all this with the chicken very thoroughly and season to taste.
6. When well mixed form the meat into cylindrical shapes and brush with a little butter.
7. Then roll in a beaten egg and roll again in powdered bread crumbs.
8. Fry in boiling lard and serve hot on a plate garnished with fried parsley.

title: Chicken Curry
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- curry powder
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut up the chicken and stew as usual for the table
2. When done add a tablespoonful of curry powder
3. Serve rice with the dish

title: Chicken Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Raw Chicken Breasts
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- Bread Soaked In Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Thyme and Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut up the chicken and pound in a mortar
2. Add an equal quantity of bread soaked in milk or water and well squeezed
3. Add the butter and the yolks of the eggs
4. Blend well and season highly with salt and pepper and the minced herbs, and add a pinch of grated nutmeg
5. Mix all together and roll into balls, and use as desired

title: Chicken Fricassee a la Marengo
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salad oil
- truffles
- parsley
- chives or small green eschalots
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- button mushrooms
- brown gravy sauce
- lemon juice
- fried bread
- crayfish

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken up as for a fricassee
2. Put it in a sauce-pan with a wineglassful of salad oil
3. Allow it to cook rather briskly for twenty minutes
4. Put in with it a quarter of a pound of truffles cut up, a bunch of parsley, six chives or small green eschalots, a bruised clove of garlic, and pepper and salt
5. Let them stew for twenty minutes
6. Pour off the oil and take out the parsley
7. If only one chicken is used, throw in half a pint of button mushrooms, a ladleful of brown gravy sauce, and the juice of a lemon
8. Garnish this dish with pieces of fried bread and large crayfish

title: Chicken Gombo With Oysters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a young chicken or the half of a grown one
- a quart of fresh green okras
- a large slice of ham
- half a gallon of boiling water

## Directions

1. Take a young chicken, or the half of a grown one;
2. cut it up, roll it in salt, pepper and flour, and fry it a nice brown, using lard or drippings, as if for fricassee.
3. Cut up a quart of fresh green okras, and take out the chicken and fry the okra in the same lard.
4. When well browned return the chicken to the pot and boil.
5. Add to it a large slice of ham; a quarter of a pound will be about right for this gombo.
6. Pour onto the chicken, ham and okra, half a gallon of boiling water, and let it boil down to three pints.
7. Ten minutes before serving pour into the boiling soup two dozen fine oysters with half a pint of their liquor.
8. Let it come to a good boil, and serve it with well-boiled rice.

title: Chicken Patties, Queen Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Small Young Chicken
- 12 Rounds of Puff Paste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1/2 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Egg

## Directions

1. Roast or broil the chicken nicely.
2. Make a Puff Paste.
3. Cut a dozen rounds with a biscuit cutter; mark a smaller round or top for a cover.
4. Brush with a beaten egg, and mark on the surface of each with the cutter, dipping it each time in hot water, so that the marked outline may remain perfect.
5. Set in a brisk oven and let them brown nicely for twelve minutes.
6. Then remove the covers gently with a knife and fill with the following garnishing.
7. Remove all the chicken meat from the bone and chop very fine.
8. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a sautoire or stewpan and add a tablespoonful of sifted flour.
9. Stir till smooth.
10. Pour in gradually a half pint of hot milk till the same reaches the consistency of a thick cream.
11. Season to taste with salt and pepper and a little nutmeg, and add one-half can of mushrooms finely chopped and the chicken.
12. Stir constantly and let it come to a boil.
13. Then remove from the fire and fill the patties.
14. Set the covers on, serve on a hot dish.

title: Chicken Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Bay Leaf and Parsley
- Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken into small pieces of about two inches in length and make a plain fricassee.
2. Prepare a Vol-au-Vent Paste and fill a tin pan of about two quarts with the Paste.
3. Pour in the chicken and gravy, and let it bake in the oven till the top crust is nicely browned.
4. Always bake the under crust first.

title: Chicken Pie, a la Reine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- veal
- fat ham
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- white sauce
- butter
- flour
- milk or water
- mushrooms
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- paste

## Directions

1. Cut two chickens up as for frying
2. Lay some veal cut in small pieces in the bottom of your pie dish
3. Cut up over the veal a slice of fat ham
4. On this place your chickens
5. Place hard-boiled yolks of eggs in among the chicken
6. Take half a pint of white sauce, made with butter, flour, and milk or water
7. Pour this over the chickens
8. Season with a cup of chopped mushrooms, some parsley, pepper and salt
9. Cover your pie with a good paste
10. Bake for an hour or two

title: Chicken Pot Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- fat salt pork
- butter
- pepper
- light crust

## Directions

1. Cut up a chicken
2. Parboil it, save the liquor it was boiled in
3. Wash out the kettle, or take another one
4. In it fry three or four slices of fat salt pork
5. Put it in the bottom of the dish in which the pie is to be made
6. Put in the chicken and the liquor
7. Also a piece of butter the size of a teacup
8. Sprinkle in some pepper
9. Cover with a light crust
10. Bake an hour

title: Chicken Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cold Boiled Chicken
- 3/4 Pint of Mayonnaise Sauce
- 1 Head of Crisp Fresh Lettuce
- Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Sliced Beets
- Celery Tips

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into small dice
2. Chop half of the lettuce very fine, and season well with salt and pepper
3. Make a bed of the remainder of the lettuce leaves, and place first a layer of the chicken and then of the lettuce, until you have used all
4. Spread the Mayonnaise Sauce over the top nicely, and garnish prettily with slices of cold hard-boiled eggs, sliced beets, celery tips, etc.

title: Chicken Salad for a Small Company
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- yolks of two
- a spoonful of mixed mustard
- an ounce of sweet oil
- a saltspoonful of salt
- black pepper
- four tablespoonfuls of good vinegar
- one-half a teaspoonful of sugar
- boiled or baked chicken
- three heads of celery
- pepper
- salt
- six or seven heads of white-heart lettuce

## Directions

1. Boil four eggs hard, throw them in cold water
2. When cool, take the yolks of two in a bowl, pour over them a spoonful of mixed mustard, an ounce of sweet oil, a saltspoonful of salt, and a little black pepper
3. Mix this carefully, pouring in the oil a little at a time
4. When it is smooth, pour in four tablespoonfuls of good vinegar, and one-half a teaspoonful of sugar
5. The chicken is supposed to be already boiled or baked
6. When cold, pick all the flesh from the bones and pile it in the centre of a glass bowl, or dish
7. Mix with it three heads of celery, cut up fine, and season it with pepper and salt
8. About the time you wish it served, take six or seven heads of white-heart lettuce, split them, and place them closely around the cut-up chicken, and pour over it all the dressing

title: Chicken Salad, Mayonnaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of Cold Chicken, or Freshly Boiled
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Celery
- 1 Onion
- Celery, Asparagus Tips and Boiled Beets to Garnish
- A Sauce à la Mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Pick out all the white meat into small pieces of about an inch or less
2. Add chopped celery of the whitest fiber and very tender
3. Mix thoroughly, using good judgment in having parts of the celery and chicken in the proportion of one-third celery
4. Chop an onion very fine, and add
5. Season all with salt and pepper to taste
6. Place on a dish and spread over a nice Mayonnaise dressing
7. Garnish prettily with celery tips, asparagus tips, olives, and very delicately sliced red beets, and sliced lemon

title: Chicken Saute a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Spring Chickens
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 6 Large Fresh Tomatoes, or 1/2 Can
- 6 Fresh, Sweet Green Peppers
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Large Onions
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Boiling Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take two spring chickens and clean nicely and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter into a stewpan, and, when it melts, add the chicken.
4. Let this brown slowly for a good five minutes.
5. Have ready three large onions sliced.
6. Add these to the chickens and let them brown.

title: Chicken Saute with Oyster Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- oysters
- lemon juice
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Cut up the chicken as for frying
2. Roll each piece in salt, pepper, and sifted flour
3. Fry a light brown
4. Pour off most of the grease the chicken was fried in
5. In the same pan put three dozen oysters with a pint of their juice, and a spoonful of lemon juice
6. Let them simmer a few minutes
7. Serve with pieces of fried bread around the dish

title: Chicken Soufflé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chopped Left-Over Chicken
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 3 Fresh Eggs
- 1/4 Cup of Stale Bread
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Butter

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a saucepan and add the flour, and mix nicely without browning.
2. Then add the milk and stir constantly till it boils.
3. Add the bread crumbs and cook for one minute longer.
4. Then take from the fire and add the chicken, which has been hashed very fine and seasoned well with salt, pepper and Cayenne, judging according to the taste.
5. Beat the yolks of the eggs and add, mixing thoroughly.
6. Then beat the whites to a stiff froth and stir very carefully into the mixture.
7. Grease the bottom of a baking dish with butter and put the mixture in this, baking for twenty minutes in a quick oven.
8. Serve immediately while hot, or it will fall.

title: Chicken Soup. Yellow and Very Rich
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of veal
- Half as much beef or lamb
- One small chicken cut up

## Directions

1. Boil them in three quarts of water, skim off all the scum as it rises;
2. Slice aleek or...

title: Chicken Soup. Yellow And Very Rich
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of veal
- half as much beef or lamb
- one small chicken cut up
- three quarts of water
- two sliced aleeks or onions
- grated large carrot or two small ones
- two tablespoonfuls of salt
- one of pepper
- a spoonful of butter worked in flour

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of veal, half as much beef or lamb, and one small chicken cut up; boil them in three quarts of water, skim off all the scum as it rises; slice aleek or two onions, grate a large carrot or two small ones; put all these to the soup; add two tablespoonfuls of salt and one of pepper. Let it boil gently for two hours, then add a spoonful of butter worked in flour; cover this for fifteen minutes, and serve in a tureen. Take the chicken into a deep dish, put over it butter, pepper, and sprigs of parsley; or you may chop the chicken up, season with pepper, salt, butter, and an egg; form into balls, roll them in flour, and drop them in a few minutes before serving.

title: Chicken Stewed With Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Nice Chicken of a Year Old
- 1 Pint of Green Peas
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Chopped Onions
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Pint of Fresh Milk
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf

## Directions

1. Cut and season the chicken nicely.
2. Put it in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter, and let it simmer nicely for about ten minutes without browning.
3. Add two nicely-chopped onions and let these brown slightly.
4. Add a square inch of ham, chopped very fine, and minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf, one sprig each.
5. Add the garlic, nicely minced.
6. Let all brown together, slightly simmering all the time.
7. Pour in one pint of boiling water, and set back on the stove and let simmer gently for an hour and a quarter.
8. About twenty minutes before serving add one pint of milk, and let all cook for twenty minutes.
9. Serve with the green peas heaped around the chicken, which should be placed in the center of the dish.
10. Pour the gravy over, and bring to the table.

title: Chicken With Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Spring Chicken
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Chopped Onions
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham to Season
- Thyme, Parsley, Bay Leaf

## Directions

1. Cut into joints and season a nicely cleaned chicken.
2. Put it in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter, and let it simmer for about ten minutes, browning slightly.
3. Add two nicely-chopped onions, and let these brown slightly.
4. Add one-half of a square inch of ham, chopped very fine indeed.
5. Add thyme, parsley and bay leaf, following carefully the order given in adding the ingredients.
6. A minute later add the garlic, which has been minced very fine.
7. Let all brown together for ten minutes.
8. Cut the mushrooms into halves, put them with their water into the pot, stirring well.
9. Let them simmer five minutes.
10. Add a wineglass of Sherry or Madeira, stir and cover the pot closely, so that it can smother well.
11. If the sauce appears too thick add about a half cup of broth or boiling water.
12. Season to taste, and let all cook very slowly for an hour longer over a steady fire.

title: Chicken With Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Chicken
- 0.75 Cup of Rice

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the chicken as in Poulet au Riz, only do not cut up the chicken, but stew whole.
2. When three-fourths done, add the rice.
3. In serving place the chicken in the center of the dish and heap the rice around.

title: Chicken à la Reine
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Chickens of 1 Year Old
- 1/2 Pound of Nice Bacon
- 1 Carrot, cut fine
- 1 Onion, cut fine
- 1 Quart of Broth or Water
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens and truss as for roasting.
2. Then dredge inside and out with salt and pepper.
3. Cut the bacon into very thin strips, about the width of a match, and cover the bottom of the stewpan.
4. Lay over this the carrots and onions, sliced fine, and put another layer of salt meat in delicate strips.
5. Put the chickens in this and cover well and set inside of a hot oven.
6. After twenty minutes add the boiling broth or water and the bunch of sweet herbs.
7. Let the chickens cook for two hours, turning them at the end of one hour and basting occasionally.
8. Put the chicken in a hot dish, boil the gravy down to a half quart, skim off all the grease and pass through a sieve and pour over the chickens and serve.

title: Chickens Royal
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- good gravy and broth
- mushrooms
- morels
- truffles
- cocks-combs
- petty-patties
- sweet-breads

## Directions

1. Lard the chickens, and force the bellies and pass them off
2. Stew them in good gravy and broth, gold color
3. Make a ragoo of mushrooms, morels, truffles and cocks-combs
4. When your chickens are done enough dish them up, lay your ragoo over and garnish with petty-patties and fried sweet-breads

title: Chili Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tomatoes
- 4 green peppers
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 1/2 cups of vinegar
- Cayenne and chili pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the vinegar
2. Add the chopped tomatoes and green peppers and the minced onion, adding a tablespoonful of sugar
3. Let all boil one hour
4. Season to taste
5. Strain and serve with any fish or meats

title: Chocolate
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cake of Grated French or German Chocolate
- 1 Quart of New Milk
- Sugar to taste or two tablespoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil
2. In the meantime mix the grated chocolate with a little hot water, to blend thoroughly
3. Stir this in the milk as it begins to simmer, and let it boil up once
4. Serve hot, each one sweetening to taste, or allow two tablespoonfuls of sugar to the entire mixture
5. Serve hot, with or without Whipped Cream

title: Chocolate Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Large Tablespoonfuls of Chocolate
- 1 Pint Milk
- 1 Pint Cream
- 1/4 Cup Sugar
- 3/4 Pint of Gelatine
- Yolks of 4 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla or Rum
- 2 Ounces of Best French Chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare the gelatine and cream according to general directions
2. Grate the chocolate and set the milk to boil
3. Add the grated chocolate, stirring slowly until all is dissolved
4. Take the saucepan from the fire, and add the sugar, mixing well, and the vanilla and rum
5. Turn it into a tin vessel to cool; set the vessel over cracked ice, and stir constantly till it begins to thicken
6. Stir the whipped cream in carefully, and pour the mixture into a mold, and set in a cool place to harden
7. Serve with whipped cream

title: Chocolate Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Powdered French Chocolate
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 10 Ounces of Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar to a cream.
2. Add the chocolate, and mix well.
3. Then add the flour, alternating with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Add one tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract.
5. Bake upon wafer sheets in small cakes to a light color.

title: Chocolate Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- one pound of flour
- four eggs
- one half pint of milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- three whites of eggs
- three cups of sifted sugar
- chocolate to taste

## Directions

1. Sift the cream of tartar and soda into the flour
2. Mix all ingredients well together, and bake in two cakes
3. Beat three whites of eggs with three cups of sifted sugar, and add chocolate to taste
4. Spread a layer of this icing between the cakes and on the top and sides

title: Chocolate Cake A la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Flour
- ¾ Cups of Sugar
- ¼ Cup of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Chocolate, or 2 Ounces
- ¾ Cup of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 1 Heaping Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, and gradually add the sugar, beating constantly
2. When very light, add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till these are very light
3. Add the milk and the chocolate, which you will have dissolved in four tablespoonfuls of boiling water
4. Mix thoroughly, and add by degrees the flour, beating all the while
5. Give a very vigorous beating and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
6. Stir them very carefully into the batter, and add the vanilla, and finally the baking powder
7. Mix all very quickly and lightly, and set to bake in buttered pans in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour

title: Chocolate Caramels
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of sugar
- one cup of molasses
- one cup of milk
- one spoonful of butter
- one spoonful of flour
- half a pound of bakers' chocolate

## Directions

1. Butter your saucepan
2. Put in the sugar, molasses and milk
3. Boil fifteen minutes
4. Add butter and flour, stirred to a cream
5. Boil five minutes longer
6. Add the chocolate grated
7. Boil until quite thick
8. Butter tin flat pans
9. Pour in the mixture half an inch thick
10. Mark it in squares before it gets hard

title: Chocolate Caramels
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of new milk
- quarter of a pound of grated chocolate
- one cup and a half of white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil all these together until it will pull like candy
2. Try a little, and if stiff enough to pull, pour it on a buttered dish
3. Mark it off in squares with a knife as it cools
4. It will break easily when cold

title: Chocolate Caramels
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of chocolate
- three pounds of dark brown sugar
- one-eighth pound of butter
- one small teacup of milk
- vanilla, or grated lemon or orange-peel

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of chocolate, three pounds of dark brown sugar, one-eighth pound of butter, and one small teacup of milk very quickly over a hot fire
2. Stir constantly
3. Season with vanilla, or grated lemon or orange-peel
4. When it becomes hard on being dropped into water, take it off the fire
5. Stir for a few moments before pouring into buttered dishes
6. Before it is quite cool, cut into little squares

title: Chocolate Caramels
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of molasses
- 1 cup of grated chocolate
- 1 cup of milk
- 2 teaspoonfuls of vanilla
- 1 tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Boil 25 minutes
2. Set to cool in buttered pans
3. Mark in squares

title: Chocolate Caramels
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Chocolate
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonfull of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Set the sugar to boil
2. Scrape the chocolate in the milk, and add it to the boiling sugar, and stir in the butter
3. Then boil till it forms a syrup
4. Take from the fire and turn upon a greased slab or upon a buttered dish or pan that is quite square
5. When it begins to cool sufficient to receive an impression from the finger, cut into small squares with the case-knife and set in a cool, dry place to harden
6. A good test as to whether the mixture is done is to drop a little from the end of a spoon into a cup of cold water. If it begins to harden instantly, it is ready to take from the stove

title: Chocolate Caramels (Second Recipe)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of sugar
- 1 cup of water
- 1 lb. of chocolate
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 cup of butter

## Directions

1. Dissolve the chocolate in the water
2. Add 1 cup of milk, and 1 cup of butter
3. Let it boil till it will drop from a spoon, generally about 1 hour
4. Pour in a flat dish and cut in squares

title: Chocolate Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Pint of Cream
- Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 4 Ounces of Sugar
- 2 Ounces of Grated Chocolate

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of milk and one pint of cream.
2. Add, while boiling, three yolks of eggs, beaten well with four ounces of sugar.
3. Stir constantly, and let it reduce one-half.
4. Add two ounces of grated chocolate.
5. Let it boil three or four minutes.
6. Take off and serve when very cold.

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cupful of grated chocolate
- 1 cup of water
- 1 cupful of sweet milk
- 1 heaping tablespoonful of cornstarch
- milk to dissolve cornstarch
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 cupful of grated chocolate and 1 cup of water together
2. Add 1 cupful of sweet milk and let it boil again
3. Add 1 heaping tablespoonful of cornstarch dissolved in a little milk
4. Sweeten to the taste
5. Flavor with vanilla

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of scraped chocolate
- 4 ounces of white sugar
- 1 pint of boiling milk
- 1 ounce of dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Melt 6 ounces of scraped chocolate and 4 ounces of white sugar in 1 pint of boiling milk
2. Stir in 1 ounce of dissolved isinglass
3. When the whole has boiled, pour it into moulds

title: Chocolate Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Finest White Icing Sugar
- 2 Ounces of Gum Arabic
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 4 Ounces of French Chocolate

## Directions

1. Soak two ounces of the finest Gum Arabic in a gill of hot water
2. Add two pounds of the finest icing sugar and the whites of two eggs
3. Strain the soaked gum through a piece of muslin bag into a bowl
4. Add the essence to the strained gum and fill in with as much icing as it will hold, till it forms a stiff, elastic body
5. Dissolve four ounces of French Chocolate with about a tablespoonful of water and incorporate this with the whites of two eggs of Royal Icing
6. Fill a biscuit forcer with the vanilla preparation and push it through upon a sheet of paper well-dredged with sugar
7. With a small knife cut off the cream as it is pressed out into pieces of about the size of a filbert
8. Place them in the baking pan in the oven for a few minutes to dry their surfaces
9. Dip these white balls into the Chocolate Icing, holding one at a time on the tip of the fork, so as to be able to turn it all around and ice each part
10. Put on a wire tray and set to dry in the oven for about ten minutes

title: Chocolate Cream Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half a cup of cream
- two cups of white sugar
- chocolate
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix cream with white sugar
2. Boil and stir fully five minutes
3. Set the dish into another of cold water
4. Stir until it becomes hard
5. Make into small balls about the size of marbles
6. With a fork roll each one separately in the chocolate, which has in the meantime been put in a bowl over the boiling teakettle and melted
7. Put on brown paper to cool
8. Flavor with vanilla, if desired

title: Chocolate Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Milk
- ½ of a Cake of Vanilla or French Chocolate
- ¾ Cup of White Sugar
- 6 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve the chocolate in a little milk, and then stir into the boiling milk
2. Let it boil about four minutes
3. Set to cool
4. When almost cool, add the yolks of all the eggs and the whites of three, saving the remainder for the meringue
5. Stir these eggs well into the chocolate, and add the essence of vanilla
6. Put into pie pans, in which you will already have baked the under crust
7. Set in the oven and let the custard grow to a set state
8. When half done, spread over the whites of the eggs, which have been whipped to a stiff froth, with two tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
9. Let the pies brown lightly, and serve cold

title: Chocolate Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. Baker's prepared cocoa
- 1 quart rich milk
- 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls loaf sugar
- 8 eggs
- chocolate macaroon

## Directions

1. Make some strong chocolate, allowing 1 lb. of the best, which is Baker's prepared cocoa, to a quart of rich milk, first fixing the milk, and scrape the chocolate to a smooth paste
2. Boil them together 1 hour
3. While warm, stir in 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls of loaf sugar
4. Set it away to cool
5. Have ready 8 wellbeaten eggs, and stir them gradually into the chocolate
6. Bake the mixture in cups, and serve them up with a chocolate macaroon laid on the top of each

title: Chocolate Eclairs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Cup of Water
- 4 Eggs
- 1.5 Cup of Water
- 2 Ounces of Chocolate
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a Cream Puff Paste as directed in Cream Puffs recipe
2. Put into a tube or a pastry bag and press out upon well-buttered tins in the shape of Lady Fingers
3. This will give the eclairs the right shape, making them about five inches long
4. Set them to bake in a Quick oven twenty or thirty minutes
5. Fill in with a mixture made by melting two ounces of chocolate and four ounces of powdered sugar, and set away to cool
6. You may ice by dipping one end into the icing, and then setting away to dry
7. The eclairs may be filled with preserved fruits, Orange Icing, Whipped Cream, or any marmalade

title: Chocolate Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 yolks of eggs
- ½ lb. of sugar
- ½ lb. of chocolate
- boiling water
- 1 quart of cream and milk

## Directions

1. Have 6 yolks of eggs and ½ lb. of sugar well strained together as for cake
2. Roll out ½ lb. of chocolate
3. Pour a teacupful of boiling water on it, a little at a time, until it is well mixed
4. Boil 1 quart of cream and milk together
5. When it boils, mix it with the chocolate, a little at a time
6. Put it on the fire and when it boils, pour it on the eggs, mixing it all the time
7. Put it again on the fire and stir it until it becomes thick
8. It must not boil
9. When cold, freeze it

title: Chocolate Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- 1/4 Pound of the Best French Chocolate
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Boil the milk, and add to it a half pound of the best French chocolate, grated
2. Let it boil and thicken, beating till smooth
3. Add a quarter of a pound of white pulverized sugar
4. When cool add half a quart of cream and the Vanilla Extract
5. Pour into the freezer, after mixing well, and proceed as in directions for making and freezing cream

title: Chocolate Ice Cream With Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Chocolate
- 6 Ounces of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 8 Yolks of Eggs
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve the chocolate in a little water, and add it to the boiling milk
2. Take off, and pour over the eggs and sugar, which you will have beaten to a thick cream
3. Add the Vanilla, and set to cool
4. Add the remaining cream, and freeze as directed

title: Chocolate Icing
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine loaf sugar
- half a pint of cold water
- six ounces of grated chocolate
- vanilla

## Directions

1. add one pound of fine loaf sugar to half a pint of cold water
2. boil over a brisk fire until the sugar, when pressed with the fingers, presents the appearance of strong glue
3. add six ounces of grated chocolate
4. flavor with vanilla

title: Chocolate Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Chocolate
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Melt two ounces of chocolate in a little boiling water
2. Add two cups sugar, and stir till smooth
3. Stir in the well-beaten whites of three eggs and one teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract
4. Proceed as in Plain Icing

title: Chocolate Kisses
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of sugar
- one ounce of finely-powdered chocolate
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and chocolate together
2. Mix it with the whites of four eggs well beaten
3. Drop on buttered paper
4. Bake

title: Chocolate Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- A Layer Cake
- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk or Water
- 4 Ounces of Chocolate
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of White Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a layer cake
2. Boil four ounces of chocolate in one cup of milk or water
3. Add one cup of sugar, and let it boil till thick
4. Spread between the layers of cake
5. Or take four ounces of chocolate, melt with a little boiling water, just sufficient to make a paste, and work till smooth
6. Beat the whites of two eggs to a stiff froth, add four tablespoonfuls of white sugar, and then proceed as in Almond Layer Cake, having a layer of chocolate on top

title: Chocolate Loaf Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1½ Cups of Sifted Flour
- ¾ Cup of Milk
- Yolks of 8 Eggs
- White of 1 Egg
- ½ Cake of Grated Chocolate
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Dissolve the chocolate in the cup of milk, and boil till thick
2. Beat the butter to a cream, and gradually add the sugar, beating constantly
3. Add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light
4. Sift the flour, and add the baking powder, and sift again
5. Add the flour gradually to the mixture, beating all the while
6. Add the chocolate, and beat very vigorously
7. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and add to the mixture
8. Add the essence, beating lightly again, and turn into a greased pan and bake in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour or an hour

title: Chocolate Macaroons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of the best prepared cocoa
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- a little sifted flour
- sweet oil or good butter

## Directions

1. Scrape down the cocoa very fine
2. Beat the whites of 4 eggs to a stiff froth
3. Beat into the white of egg 1 lb. of powdered sugar in turn with the chocolate
4. Add a little sifted flour if the mixture appears too thin
5. Grease the bottom of some oblong tin pans very slightly with sweet oil or good butter
6. Form the mixture into small, thick cakes and lay them in the pan not close together
7. Bake them a few minutes
8. Sift sugar over them while warm

title: Chocolate Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Ounces of Ground Almonds
- 1.5 Pounds of Sifted White Sugar
- 4 Ounces of Grated French Chocolate
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl, and let it form a stiff paste
2. Lay the macaroons out upon the wafer sheet in the form of lady fingers
3. Bake at a very moderate heat till a light brown

title: Chocolate Mange
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- half a box of gelatine
- one cup of sugar
- six ounces of grated chocolate
- cream or wine sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Make in the same manner as gelatine blanc mange
2. Stir six ounces of grated chocolate in the boiling milk for seasoning
3. Eat with cream or wine sauce

title: Chocolate Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces of French isinglass
- cold water
- 1 quart of new milk
- half cake of vanilla chocolate
- cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve 3 ounces of French isinglass in a very little cold water
2. Put on a quart of new milk to boil
3. Grate half cake of vanilla chocolate and stir in the milk
4. Let it simmer and add the dissolved isinglass
5. Let all simmer 5 minutes
6. Pour into a mould
7. When cold and stiff, turn out and serve with cream

title: Chocolate Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 6 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- % Cup of Sweet Milk
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- % Cake of Grated Chocolate
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and then add the milk.
2. Sift the flour and baking powder together, and stir into the cake gradually.
3. Then add the vanilla essence and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Divide the cake batter in two, and mix the grated chocolate into one half.
5. Then put a dark layer of cake in the pan, then a light, and continue so alternating until all the batter is used.
6. Bake in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour.

title: Chocolate Paste for Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half a cup of chocolate
- one-half cup of milk
- one cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil chocolate in milk
2. Add sugar
3. Boil until it is a thick paste

title: Chocolate Puddings
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Ounces of Grated Chocolate
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 3/4 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Powdered Crackers
- 3/4 Cup of Butter
- 1 Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Soak the cracker crumbs in water for a few minutes, to dissolve them, and then squeeze out thoroughly.
2. Dissolve the chocolate in a little milk, and add the other ingredients.
3. Pour all into a buttered dish, and let bake for twenty minutes.
4. Then take out, and let it cool slightly.
5. Spread over the top a meringue made of one cup of powdered sugar and the whites of three eggs.
6. Set back in the oven to brown slightly for two minutes, and serve cold.

title: Chopped Cabbage Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of chopped onions
- three gills of white mustard seed
- three tablespoonfuls of ground mustard
- three tablespoonfuls of celery seed
- one pound of brown sugar
- three quarts of good vinegar
- two gallons of chopped cabbage

## Directions

1. Put together one pint of chopped onions, three gills of white mustard seed, three tablespoonfuls of ground mustard and the same of celery seed
2. Add a pound of brown sugar and three quarts of good vinegar
3. Cook this compound slowly until it begins to thicken
4. Pour it hot upon two gallons of chopped cabbage, which should be shaved or chopped very thin
5. This pickle is ready to bottle for use when it has boiled fifteen minutes

title: Chow-Chow Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a peck of green tomatoes
- a quarter of a peck of pickling-beans
- a quarter of a peck of white onions
- one dozen cucumbers
- green peppers
- one head of cabbage
- ground mustard
- celery seed
- salt
- cider vinegar
- two tablespoonfuls of sweet oil
- two tablespoonfuls of white sugar

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of a peck each, of green tomatoes, pickling-beans, and white onions, scald the onions separately
2. Add one dozen cucumbers, green peppers, and a head of cabbage chopped
3. Season with ground mustard, celery seed, and salt to taste
4. Pour over these the best cider vinegar to cover them
5. Let all boil two hours
6. While hot add two tablespoonfuls of sweet oil and the same of white sugar
7. Bottle and seal up carefully in wide-mouthed glass jars

title: Christmas Plum Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a half of raisins
- half a pound of currants
- three-quarters of a pound of bread-crumbs
- half a pound of flour
- three-quarters of a pound of beef-suet
- nine eggs
- one wineglassful of brandy
- half a pound of citron and orange-peel
- half a nutmeg
- a little ground ginger

## Directions

1. Chop the suet as fine as possible, and mix it with the bread-crumbs and flour
2. Add the currants washed and dried, the citron and orange-peel cut into thin slices, and the raisins stoned and divided
3. Mix it all well together with the grated nutmeg and ginger
4. Stir in nine eggs well beaten, and the brandy
5. Mix it thoroughly together, that every ingredient may be moistened
6. Put it into a buttered mould
7. Tie it over tightly, and boil it for six hours
8. This pudding may be made a week before using, boiled in a cloth, and hung up in a dry place
9. When required put into a saucepan of boiling water and boiled for two hours or two hours and a half
10. Turn out, and serve with sauce

title: Cider Apple Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- new sweet cider
- apples

## Directions

1. Boil down new sweet cider, till about as thick as good molasses
2. When cold, strain it through a sieve
3. As soon as it boils put in the apples, pared, quartered and cored
4. Stew over a slow fire till the fruit is perfectly tender

title: Cinnamon Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Powdered French Chocolate
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 10 Ounces of Pulverized Sugar
- Ground Cinnamon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cinnamon Extract
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the cinnamon to a cream.
2. Add the chocolate, and mix well.
3. Then add the flour, alternating with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Add one tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract.
5. Bake upon wafer sheets in small cakes to a light color.

title: Cinnamon Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3 pints of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of beaten cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Roll them in flour and mould them thin
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Citron Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- The Zest of 1 Citron
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 12 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar to a cream.
2. Add the flour gradually, and the juice of the lemon and citron zest.
3. Beat well.
4. Bake on paper sheets in the form of small wafers, very thinly spread, in a moderate oven to a light color.

title: Citron Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- one pound of flour
- eight eggs
- one pound of blanched almonds
- half a pound of sliced citron
- flour
- half teaspoonful of soda
- sweet milk
- or one tablespoonful of yeast powder

## Directions

1. Add to the batter one pound of blanched almonds, cut small, and half a pound of sliced citron dredged with flour
2. beat all up well
3. Beat in a half teaspoonful of soda, moistened with sweet milk, or if preferred, a tablespoonful of yeast powder, rubbed in the flour before mixing
4. Beat this mixture well, and bake it in a cake-pan with buttered paper in the bottom
5. cover the top of the cake with something to protect it from the heat of the stove, until the bottom is nearly done

title: Citron Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 12 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Cream
- 1 Glass of Wine
- 1 Cup of Chopped Citron
- 1 Teaspoonful of Almond Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Add the sugar, and beat till very light
3. Add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light
4. Add the cream, and mix thoroughly, and then add by degrees the citron, mixing thoroughly
5. By degrees, add the flour, well-sifted
6. Beat well
7. Add the wine, and lastly the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
8. Mix all quickly and lightly; add the almond extract, mix lightly, and then set to bake in a buttered pan in a moderate oven for one hour

title: Citronelle
body:
## Ingredients

- The Zest of 30 Lemons
- The Zest of 4 Oranges
- 3½ Quarts of Alcohol
- 2½ Pounds of Sugar
- ⅛ Drachm Each of Cloves and Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate the zests of the lemons and oranges, and grate the nutmeg and grind the cloves.
2. Mix well.
3. Pour over the alcohol, and steep for fifteen days.
4. Distill in a "bain-marie," or water bath, and add the syrup made with two and a half pounds of sugar and one quart of water.
5. Bottle and keep in a cool, shady place.

title: Clam Chowder
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 to 100 small clams
- water
- salt pork
- biscuits
- milk or warm water

## Directions

1. Put into boiling water from 50 to 100 small clams
2. When all their shells have opened, take them out and extract all the hard part and throw it away
3. Slice thin as much salt pork as will produce half a pint of liquid or gravy
4. Take out all the pork, leaving the liquid in the pot
5. Add to it a layer of clams, then a layer of biscuits soaked in milk or warm water
6. Next another layer of clams

title: Clam Chowder
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 to 100 small clams
- salt pork
- biscuits
- milk or warm water
- pepper
- mace
- onions
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Put clams into boiling water and when all shells have opened, take them out and extract all the hard part and throw it away
2. Slice thin as much salt pork as will produce half a pint of liquid or gravy
3. Take out all the pork, leaving the liquid in the pot
4. Add to it a layer of clams, then a layer of biscuits soaked in milk or warm water
5. Add another layer of clams, then another layer of soaked biscuits
6. Add more clams, seasoned with pepper and mace
7. If desired, add three or more onions, boiled and sliced
8. Add some nice potatoes, boiled, peeled and cut very fine
9. Cover the whole with a nice paste, and bake it in an iron oven

title: Clam Fritters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- clam juice
- 3 eggs
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 1 pint of cream
- flour
- butter
- lard

## Directions

1. Strain the clams from the juice
2. Cut the clams fine
3. Beat up 3 eggs very light
4. Stir in the clams
5. Cut up the parsley with a little salt and pepper
6. Grate some nutmeg
7. Add these to the clams
8. Stir in 1 pint of cream
9. Slowly dredge in some flour until it is of the consistency of fritters
10. Have the pan hot and put in half butter and half lard
11. Let it boil
12. Drop in a spoonful of the fritter batter
13. Serve hot

title: Clam Pie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 full quart of clams
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 coffee cup of cream
- pepper
- salt
- rich paste

## Directions

1. Open clams and take off the shells
2. Chop them quite fine
3. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of butter and a coffee cup of cream
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Bake it in a rich paste

title: Claret Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Good Claret
- 1 Pony of Curacoa
- 1 Orange, Sliced
- A Dash of French Cognac
- ½ Cup of Loaf Sugar
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and lemon juice
2. Add the Claret, Curacoa, Brandy and sliced orange and lemon juice
3. Put to cool

title: Claret Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of the Best Claret
- ½ Pint of Ice Water
- ¾ Cup of Powdered White Sugar
- 2 Lemons, Sliced Thin

## Directions

1. Slice the lemons and let them stand for ten minutes with the sugar
2. Add the water and wine
3. Mix well and serve in glasses with crushed ice or freeze as directed above

title: Claret Punch, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ Quarts of Claret
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- ½ Quarts of Seltzer or Appolinaris Water
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- 2 Lemons, Sliced Very Thin

## Directions

1. Slice the lemons very thin
2. Mix all the above ingredients together
3. Add the sliced lemons as a garnish
4. Stir well, set to cool, and serve in small glass cups or punch glasses

title: Clareville Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 pint of milk or cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Put the batter in buttered pans, and bake a light brown in a slow oven

title: Clear Gravy Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh beef
- pepper
- salt
- sage
- carrots
- celery
- turnips

## Directions

1. Wash a piece of fresh beef and put in on the night before it is wanted
2. Boil it several hours until perfectly tender
3. Take it off and strain it
4. In the morning separate every particle of fat from it and put it over the fire
5. Season it with pepper and salt
6. Put in a spoonful of sage to color it
7. Prepare the vegetables nicely: carrots, celery, and turnips, cut small and boiled in the soup till tender

title: Clear Pea Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of good beef or veal soup stock
- a quart of young green peas
- heads of lettuce
- a sprig of mint
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Into this put a quart of young green peas, heads of lettuce, and a sprig of mint
2. Add salt and pepper to taste

title: Clear Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- lean beef
- bones
- ham
- cold water
- salt
- onions
- carrots
- celery
- cayenne pepper
- vermicelli
- maccaroni
- pates
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Have 5 or 6 pounds of lean beef from above the knee cut into small pieces and the bones chopped
2. Add a slice of ham cut fine
3. Put this on in about 6 quarts of cold water
4. Let the soup come to a boil and skim it well
5. Throw in a tablespoonful of salt and a half tea cup of cold water to help the soup throw up the scum better
6. Carefully take off all scum until it ceases to rise
7. Add one large or two medium sized onions, two carrots cut fine, and one or two pieces of celery
8. Let this boil slowly from 6 to 8 hours, or until the broth is reduced to one-half the quantity
9. Your meat should be cooked to pieces
10. Pass all through a cullender and strain all through a coarse cloth
11. Put this in a cold place until the next day, when it will be jellied
12. Skim all the grease carefully from the top
13. Take the jelly, or soup as it may be, and pour it carefully into another vessel without disturbing the bottom
14. Heat it well
15. Have a clean flannel cloth, or bag, well wet, which dip in ice water
16. Pass a small quantity of the broth at a time through the bag, constantly shaking the dregs from the flannel and wetting it with ice water
17. Season highly with cayenne pepper and salt to the taste
18. Heat it now to serve, adding vermicelli, maccaroni or pates, as you like
