19. If the soup is cloudy before you strain it, beat the whites of three eggs stiff and pour them into the soup and let all come to a good boil
20. Let it set about five minutes before you strain

title: Clove and Cinnamon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- 6 Cloves
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Inch of Cinnamon
- 1 Ounce of Cornstarch
- Water
- 1 Pint of Chaufausen Wine

## Directions

1. Put the sugar, cinnamon, zest of lemon, cloves and water into a saucepan and set on a good fire and let them come to a boil, which will be in about five minutes
2. Add the cornstarch diluted in a tablespoonful of cold water and stir it briskly into the water
3. After three minutes of stirring add one pint of Chaufausen wine, let it cook for two minutes longer and then take off the fire
4. Drain through a sieve and serve with puddings, especially Apple Pudding

title: Clove Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Cloves
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Sour Cream
- 1 Egg
- ¼ Cup of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda
- A Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and yolk of the egg to a cream
2. Add the cream and soda blended
3. Mix thoroughly
4. Add the cloves, and mix well
5. Sift the flour and salt together, and add gradually, beating all the while
6. Give several vigorous beats, and set to bake in a buttered tin in a moderate oven for forty-five minutes

title: Cochineal
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Powdered Cochineal
- 1 Ounce of Burnt Alum
- 2 Ounces of Salts of Tartar
- 2 Ounces of Cream of Tartar
- 12 Ounces of Sugar
- 2 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Boil the water in a porcelain-lined vessel, and add the other ingredients
2. Remove from the fire, and then strain at once through a sieve or cloth, and bottle for use

title: Cocoa
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Cocoa

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil in a farina boiler, and moisten the Cocoa with a little milk
2. Pour it into the milk just as it begins to simmer, stirring all the while
3. Let it boil up once
4. Serve hot, with or without Whipped Cream

title: Cocoanut Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- three cups of flour
- four eggs
- icing
- grated cocoanut

## Directions

1. Make a batter of one cup of butter, two cups of sugar, three cups of flour and four eggs
2. Bake in jelly cake pans
3. Spread a layer of icing between each cake with grated cocoanut on top of the icing
4. finally, ornament the top with a thick layer of cocoanut

title: Cocoanut Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cocoanut
- whites of 7 eggs
- 3 teacups of sifted flour
- 2 teacups of sifted white sugar
- ½ cup of butter
- ½ cup of milk or water
- 2 teaspoons yeast powder
- For icing: 3 whites of eggs
- 1 lb. of sugar

## Directions

1. Grate 1 cocoanut and set aside
2. Beat the whites of 7 eggs very light
3. Mix 3 teacups of sifted flour, 2 teacups of sifted white sugar, ½ cup of butter, and ½ cup of milk or water
4. Beat well together, and add 2 teaspoons yeast powder
5. Bake in 4 jelly cake pans
6. Make an icing of 3 whites of eggs and 1 lb. of sugar beaten together until stiff enough to stand as you drop it from the beater
7. Flavor icing to taste
8. Spread the icing on one of the cakes, then sprinkle cocoanut over, and lightly press with palm of the hand
9. Place another cake upon the first, and continue the process until all are used
10. Use icing only on the top of the last cake, or spread grated cocoanut over the others

title: Cocoanut Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 10 eggs
- 2 coconuts, grated
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 1/2 lb. of sifted flour
- rose water

## Directions

1. Whip the whites of the eggs
2. Grate the nuts and add them
3. Add the sugar and flour
4. Stir this well and flavor with rose water
5. Pour into pans
6. Bake for 1/2 of an hour

title: Cocoanut Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cocoanut
- one cup of sugar
- the white of an egg

## Directions

1. Grate a cocoanut, place it in a skillet over the fire, and stir until it is as dry as flour
2. Beat one cup of sugar and the white of an egg to a froth
3. Mix well, and make into small cakes
4. put them on buttered paper and bake
5. The oven should not be very hot

title: Cocoanut Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cocoanut
- half a pound loaf sugar
- two tablespoonfuls of water

## Directions

1. pare the cocoanut and grate half a pound
2. allow the same quantity of loaf sugar
3. Dissolve the sugar in two tablespoonfuls of water, place it on the fire
4. when the syrup is boiling hot, stir in the cocoanut
5. Continue to stir it until it is thick like candy
6. pour it out on a buttered pan, and cut it across in shapes, or use a round cake cutter

title: Cocoanut Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Four cups of water
- two and a half cups fine white sugar
- four spoonfuls of vinegar
- a piece of butter as large as an egg
- one cup of desiccated cocoanut

## Directions

1. Boil water, sugar, vinegar, and butter till thick, or about three quarters of an hour
2. Just before removing, stir in one cup of desiccated cocoanut
3. Lay in small, flat cakes on buttered plates, to cool and harden

title: Cocoanut Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 4 eggs
- 3 pints of milk
- cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of 4 eggs to a stiff froth
2. Boil 3 pints of milk down to a quart and pour it on the eggs, stirring it very slowly
3. Sweeten it to the taste
4. After this has cooled, grate a cocoanut in it
5. Set it to freeze

title: Cocoanut Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coconut grated
- 1 pint milk
- 3 eggs
- sugar
- butter the size of a walnut
- rind of 1 lemon
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate 1 nut into 1 pint of milk and 3 eggs
2. Add sugar to the taste, butter the size of a walnut, the rind of 1 lemon, and a little nutmeg

title: Cocoanut Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a grated cocoanut
- beaten whites of four eggs
- half a pound of white sugar

## Directions

1. flavor, mix, and bake on paper in drops

title: Cocoanut Drops
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 5 cocoanuts
- 1 cup of sugar to 2 of the cocoanuts

## Directions

1. Grate the nuts fine, and dry it in a moderate oven
2. Beat up the whites of the eggs
3. Add the sugar, allowing 1 cup to 2 of the cocoanuts, and beat to a paste
4. Stir in the cocoanut, and drop in a pan or on a sheet of paper
5. Bake for 10 minutes

title: Cocoanut Drops (Second Recipe)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of grated cocoanut
- 1 lb. of white powdered sugar
- whites of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs to a stiff froth
2. Drop in buttered pans and bake

title: Cocoanut Fingers
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Butter
- 4 Ounces of Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 2 Ounces of Flour
- 4 Ounces of Grated Cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the butter, sugar and eggs

title: Cocoanut Jumbles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cocoanut, grated
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour
- 8 eggs
- cocoanut and sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Rub 1 lb. of butter into 1 lb. of flour
2. Add 8 eggs and the grated cocoanut to form a stiff dough
3. Make the jumbles with the hands
4. Grate cocoanut and sugar over them
5. Bake them

title: Cocoanut Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Cake
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 2 Cups of Grated Cocoanut
- White Sugar to Sprinkle

## Directions

1. Make a good layer cake
2. Beat the whites of two eggs till very frothy
3. Add one cup of powdered sugar, and beat till very stiff
4. Grate the cocoanut, and then proceed as in filling the Almond Cake, having a layer of the cocoanut on top, sprinkled with white sugar

title: Cocoanut Loaf Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound of Butter
- 2 Cups Grated Cocoanut
- 5 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Milk
- ¼ Cup of Butter
- ¾ Pound of Flour
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- The Juice and Grated Zest of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a rich cream
2. Add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light
3. Add the milk, and the flour by degrees, beating all the while, and then add the lemon
4. Beat well
5. Add the cocoanut, and stir in carefully and well
6. Sift the baking powder over the whites of the eggs, which you will have beaten to a stiff froth, and add these, beating lightly and thoroughly
7. Have ready two loaf pans, and line them with very thin buttered paper
8. Divide the batter into two even parts, filling each pan about three-quarters full
9. Set in a moderate oven, and bake for forty-five minutes
10. When done, cover the top with finely-grated cocoanut, and sift powdered sugar over it
11. Set it to cool

title: Cocoanut Meringue
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- whites of 9 eggs
- grated cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs to a froth
2. The sugar should be worked in slowly with a wooden spoon
3. Have ready a pan well greased and floured
4. Drop a tablespoonful and sprinkle well with grated cocoanut
5. Bake in a slow oven till a light brown

title: Cocoanut Pound Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three coffee-cupfuls of flour
- one coffee-cupful of butter
- two coffee-cupfuls of white sugar
- one cupful of milk
- whites of six eggs
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- one-half teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- grated cocoanut from a small one

## Directions

1. The cocoanut should be laid in water as soon as the shell is broken; take out a piece at a time to pare it; lay it in a dry cloth as soon as pared, and cover it up, that the air may be kept out and the moisture absorbed
2. Cream the butter
3. add the sugar, and beat well
4. put in the milk, slowly
5. the whites of the eggs, well beaten, alternately with flour
6. the cocoanut last of all
7. Bake in a moderate oven; increase the heat at the last
8. It takes about one-half or three-quarters of an hour to bake

title: Cocoanut Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Grated Cocoanut
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Put the milk in a boiler and add the cornstarch as it commences to boil, first moistening the cornstarch with a little water.
2. Stir until very smooth.
3. Then add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten, and then the sugar.
4. Lastly, add the whites, beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Then add the grated cocoanut, turn into the baking pan, and bake for half an hour.
6. Serve cold, with Vanilla Sauce.

title: Cocoanut Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small cocoanuts
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 10 yolks and 3 whites of eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- brandy or wine

## Directions

1. Grate 1 large or 2 small cocoanuts, the day before, that they may dry
2. Take 1 lb. of sugar, 10 yolks and 3 whites of eggs, beat them very light
3. Add a tablespoonful of butter to the sugar, and put the nut in just before it is baked
4. Season with a little brandy or wine
5. Bake it in puff paste

title: Cocoanut Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sugar
- yolks of 10 eggs
- ½ pint of cream
- ½ gill of brandy
- juice of 1 lemon
- peel of 1 lemon grated
- ½ lb. of cocoanut

## Directions

1. Combine 1 lb. of sugar, the yolks of 10 eggs, ½ pint of cream, ½ gill of brandy, the juice of 1 lemon, with the peel grated, ½ lb. of the cocoanut
2. Bake it in a nice paste

title: Cocoanut Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of milk
- 6 eggs
- J lb. sugar
- i lb. of butter
- 1 large cocoanut grated

## Directions

1. Put this, with the butter and sugar, over the fire
2. Melt and cream together
3. Add the eggs, well beaten
4. Add the milk
5. Eat cold

title: Cocoanut Pudding or Pies
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cocoanut
- the milk of the cocoanut
- white sugar
- butter
- five eggs well beaten
- a cup of milk
- the grated rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Break a cocoanut and save the milk.
2. Peel off the brown skin, then throw each piece into cold water, and let it stay a few minutes to cool.
3. Take the pieces out, wipe dry and grate.
4. Add their own weight of white sugar and half the weight of butter.
5. Rub the butter and sugar to a cream, add five well beaten eggs, and a cup of milk.
6. Throw into the mixture the milk of the cocoanut and the grated rind of a lemon.
7. Bake in a pudding-dish, or make it into pies with a bottom crust.
8. Ornament the top of the pies with fancy twists of paste.

title: Cocoanut Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cocoanuts
- 1/2 lb. best white sugar
- 2 quarts water

## Directions

1. After taking the milk from the nuts, break and take off the dark rind
2. Grate the white meat, and pour over it 2 quarts of water
3. Let it stand awhile
4. Strain through a strong piece of muslin, or use a fruit press, so that you abstract all the richness
5. Add the milk taken from the nuts, and the sugar
6. It is then ready for the freezer

title: Cocoanut Silver Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- a cup of milk
- whites of six eggs
- three cups of sifted flour
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- half a teaspoonful of soda
- a small cocoanut

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of butter and two cups of sugar
2. add a cup of milk, the whites of six eggs, and three cups of sifted flour with one teaspoonful of cream of tartar, and half a teaspoonful of soda mixed in the flour
3. Grate a small cocoanut, dry it in a skillet over the fire by stirring it about ten minutes
4. Stir the cocoanut into the batter
5. Bake in a moderate oven about three-quarters of an hour

title: Cocoanut Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 calves' feet
- 6 or 7 blades of mace
- yellow rind of a lemon
- 1 gallon of water
- half a pound of grated cocoanut
- 1 pint of cream or rich milk
- 3 ounces of fresh butter
- arrowroot or rice flour
- 1 small nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put the calves' feet into a soup kettle with mace and grated lemon rind
2. Pour on a gallon of water, cover the kettle and let it boil very slowly, skimming it well till the flesh is reduced to rags and has dropped entirely from the bones
3. Strain it into a broad white-ware pan and set it away to get cool
4. When it has congealed, scrape off the fat and sediment, cut up the cake of jelly and put it into a clean porcelain kettle
5. Mix the grated cocoanut with a pint of cream or rich milk and three ounces of butter divided into three parts, each bit rolled in arrowroot or rice flour
6. Mix it gradually with the cocoanut and add it to the calves' feet stock in the kettle, seasoned with a small nutmeg grated
7. Set it over the fire and boil it slowly about a quarter of an hour, stirring it well
8. Put it into a tureen and serve it up with French rolls or light milk biscuits and powdered sugar

title: Cocoanut Tarts
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cocoanuts
- yolks of 10 eggs
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- rose water
- nice puff paste

## Directions

1. Season cocoanut, yolks of eggs, sugar, and butter with rose water
2. Put the butter in cold and bake in a nice puff paste

title: Coconut Fingers
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Butter
- 4 Ounces of Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 2 Ounces of Flour
- 4 Ounces of Grated Cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the butter, sugar and eggs together for five minutes, blending thoroughly
2. Then add the flour, and immediately after the grated cocoanut
3. Beat well, and have ready buttered tins
4. Divide the mixture into fingers of about four or five inches in length
5. Bake in a moderately quick oven for thirty minutes
6. When cool dust with powdered sugar

title: Coconut Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Grated Coconut
- 2 Eggs
- ⅜ of a Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs very light
2. Add the sugar and beat with the eggs until very light
3. Add the grated nutmeg, and finally the coconut
4. Make a pie crust, using half the quantity, as a coconut pie is always an uncovered pie
5. Fill with the mixture, and bake in the oven for about half an hour

title: Coconut Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- calves' feet
- mace
- lemon
- water

## Directions

1. Take eight calves' feet that have been scalded and scraped but not skinned
2. Put them into a soup kettle with 6 or 7 blades of mace and the yellow rind of a lemon grated
3. Pour on a gallon of water
4. Cover the kettle and let it boil very slowly, skimming it well, till the flesh is reduced to rags and has dropped entirely from the bones
5. Strain it into a broad white-ware pan and set it away to get cool
6. When it has congealed, scrape off the fat and sediment
7. Cut up the cake of jelly, or stock, and put it into a clean porcelain vessel

title: Coconut Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Calves' Feet
- 1/2 Pound of Grated Coconut
- 1 Gallon of Water
- 1 Pint of Cream or Rich Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour or Arrowroot
- 3 Ounces of Fresh Butter
- Blades of Mace
- The Grated Rind of 1 Lemon
- Nutmeg
- French rolls or milk biscuit

## Directions

1. Scald the calves' feet, and scrape thoroughly without skinning.
2. Put them into the soup kettle with a gallon of cold water, and cover the kettle well.
3. Let the feet come to a slow boil and skim carefully.
4. Add the blades of mace and let the soup boil slowly till the meat is reduced to rags and has fallen from the bones.
5. Strain into a white porcelain dish or pan, and set it away to cool.
6. After it has congealed, scrape off fat and sediment, and a beautiful jelly will remain.
7. Cut up this cake of jelly and put it into a thoroughly cleansed, white porcelain soup kettle.
8. Grate the coconut very fine, till about a half pound is on hand.
9. Mix this with the pint of rich cream or milk, and add the butter which has been rolled smoothly in the arrowroot or flour.
10. Mix this carefully and gradually with the calves' feet stock or soup, and season with grated nutmeg.
11. Set back on the fire and allow to boil slowly for about fifteen minutes, stirring almost constantly.
12. Pour into the tureen and serve with French rolls, or milk biscuit, made very light and thin.

title: Cod au Beurre Roux
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- A little browned butter
- A little flour
- Sugar
- One onion
- Tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. For cod au beurre roux, boil a piece of cod and separate it into flakes; brown some butter, dredge in a little flour, and a little sugar in powder, and in this fry some slices of onion a fine brown;
2. Throw in the vinegar, boil it up, pour over the fish, and serve it with crisp parsley.

title: Cod Au Beurre Roux
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of cod
- some butter
- a little flour
- a little sugar in powder
- slices of onion
- vinegar
- crisp parsley

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of cod and separate it into flakes
2. Brown some butter
3. Dredge in a little flour, and a little sugar in powder
4. In this fry some slices of onion a fine brown
5. Throw in the vinegar
6. Boil it up
7. Pour over the fish
8. Serve it with crisp parsley

title: Coddled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 oysters to each person
- 6 slices of bread
- 1 large tablespoonful of butter
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of black pepper
- A dash of Cayenne
- 2 sprigs of parsley chopped very fine
- 1 bay leaf minced fine
- 3 cloves
- 1 blade of mace
- 1 pint of oyster liquor

## Directions

1. Toast five or six slices of bread to a nice brown and butter them on both sides.
2. Drain the liquor from the oysters and put it in a saucepan.
3. When hot, add a large lump of butter.
4. Have ready a baking dish and place the toast within.
5. Lay the oysters on the toast, having seasoned well with salt, Cayenne pepper, chopped parsley, bay leaf, mace and cloves.
6. Put the liquor of the oysters over the toast until it is well absorbed.
7. Set in an oven and bake for five or six minutes with a quick fire.

title: Codfish Bacalao
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of salted codfish
- 1 large onion, chopped fine
- 1 pint of rich tomato sauce
- 2 cloves of garlic, chopped fine
- 1 red pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- Pepper to taste
- 6 tomatoes
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Parsley
- Croutons
- 1 heaping tablespoonful of butter
- 4 sprigs of parsley
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- Cayenne
- Salt
- 1 gill of olive oil or 2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Soak the codfish well overnight, and in the morning boil for about forty-five minutes, until very tender, beginning with cold water.
2. After it has boiled, scald again thoroughly, and pick out all the bones and set away to cool.
3. Take six large fresh tomatoes, or a half can of tomatoes, and add a heaping tablespoonful of butter, four sprigs of parsley, thyme and two bay leaves, all chopped very fine.
4. Add two chopped onions and a clove of garlic, chopped fine, and which has been fried in a little butter.
5. Set the saucepan, with the sauce, into boiling water, and add pepper, Cayenne and a pinch of salt to taste.
6. Stew very gently for about two hours or longer, if necessary.
7. Strain the sauce and make a roux with one tablespoonful of butter and two of flour; stir and let it brown lightly, and stir in the sauce.
8. Boil about four minutes longer until rich and thick.
9. Fry the remaining large onion and clove of garlic, chopped very fine, in a gill of olive oil, or two tablespoonfuls of butter if the taste of the oil is disliked, and when it browns add this to the sauce and a red sweet pepper, finely chopped.
10. Cut about a dozen croutons in dice or diamond shapes, from the soft part of the bread, and fry in boiling lard.
11. Heat a dish, put the codfish into it, pour over the tomato sauce, border the dish with the fried croutons, and set in the oven, allowing it to bake ten or fifteen minutes longer.

title: Codfish Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of picked codfish
- 2 cups of mashed potatoes
- 1/4 cup of cream
- Bread crumbs
- 2 beaten eggs
- Pepper and Cayenne to taste
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of black pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the codfish overnight and boil until tender.
2. Pour off this water, and scald again with hot water; pick fine, scald again and then drain thoroughly, pressing out all the water.
3. Mash the potatoes, and melt about three tablespoonfuls of butter and mix well in the potatoes.
4. Add the codfish and mix thoroughly, seasoning with about a teaspoonful of black pepper, and a dash of Cayenne to taste.
5. Add this to the cream, and again mix.
6. Mold the codfish into round or oval balls.
7. Roll in the egg, which has been well beaten, and pass through the bread crumbs, patting gently.
8. Lay them in a frying basket, if you have one, and sink into the boiling lard, or otherwise drop into the boiling lard, having tested the heat with a bit of bread.
9. When a golden brown, lift out the basket, or skim out the balls with a skimmer; drain well of all the fat by laying on a heated brown paper, and then serve hot on a dish garnished with sprigs of parsley.

title: Codfish Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Boiled Salt Codfish
- 1 Green Pepper
- 3 Young Onions
- 1 Teaspoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- Capers to Garnish

## Directions

1. Boil the codfish, or utilize left-over fish; mince finely and mix thoroughly with the minced green peppers and young onions.
2. Season with one teaspoonful of Tarragon vinegar.
3. Spread on triangular-shaped pieces of toast placed on a dish on a folded napkin.
4. Decorate nicely with capers and serve.

title: Coffee and Cognac Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Finest White Icing Sugar
- 2 Pounds of Gum Arabic
- 2 Ounces of Essence of Mocha Coffee
- 1/2 Gill of Fine Old Cognac

## Directions

1. Work gum, brandy and icing sugar into a paste
2. Cover with the Royal Icing flavored with Essence of Coffee
3. Proceed in the same manner as Chocolate Creams

title: Coffee Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Strong Cup of Cafe Noir
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 3/4 Pint of Gelatine
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Water
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine and whip the cream
2. After half an hour, pour it into the boiling coffee, and dissolve thoroughly
3. Add the sugar, and stir thoroughly
4. Add the milk and let it boil up once, and remove from the fire
5. Stir in the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
6. Stir steadily and carefully until all the ingredients have thoroughly blended
7. Set it on the fire to thicken slightly, stirring all the time, but be careful not to let it boil, or the eggs will curdle
8. Set aside to cool slightly; pour it into a basin, and set over cracked ice, and add the whipped cream as it thickens
9. Turn into a mold, and set in a cool place to harden
10. Serve with whipped cream

title: Coffee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cafe Noir (Black Coffee)
- 4 Eggs
- 4 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Seeded Raisins
- 1 Cup of Currants, Washed and Dried
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Cloves
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Soda, Dissolved in Warm Water

## Directions

1. Wash and cream the butter
2. Add the sugar and beat till very light
3. Add the yolks of the eggs
4. Beat them into the butter and sugar till very light
5. Add the molasses, and mix thoroughly
6. Take the seeded currants and raisins and blend them with one-half of the flour and add gradually
7. Add by degrees the remainder of the flour, beating vigorously
8. Add the coffee, and mix well
9. Add the soda, dissolved in hot water
10. Add the spices, mix well, and finally add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
11. Mix thoroughly but lightly, divide into two parts, set in two greased paper-lined tins, and bake from forty-five minutes to one hour in a moderate oven

title: Coffee Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of molasses
- one cup of strong coffee
- two eggs
- five cups of flour
- one teaspoonful of soda
- one cup of currants
- one cup of raisins
- spice to taste

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and mix well
2. Bake

title: Coffee Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of molasses
- 1 egg
- 1 cup of warm coffee
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 4 cups of flour
- 1 lb. of raisins or currants
- 1 tablespoonful of cloves
- 1 tablespoonful of cinnamon
- 1 tablespoonful of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, butter, molasses, and egg well together
2. Add 1 cup of warm coffee with 1 teaspoonful of soda
3. Add 4 cups of flour, 1 lb. of raisins or currants, 1 tablespoonful each of cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg

title: Coffee Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of good clear coffee
- one pint of cream

## Directions

1. Take three cups of good clear coffee and sweeten it well
2. Boil with it a pint of cream until reduced one-third

title: Coffee Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- five spoonfuls of white sugar
- four eggs
- half a cup of corn starch
- cold milk
- a cup of hot strong coffee
- sponge cake

## Directions

1. Boil one quart of milk with five spoonfuls of white sugar
2. Beat four eggs separately
3. Throw the whites into the boiling milk for two minutes and dip them out with a skimmer as soon as they are cooked
4. Beat the four yolks of the eggs with half a cup of corn starch wet with a little cold milk
5. Set it aside until you can put into the hot milk a cup of hot strong coffee
6. Pour in the mixed corn starch and eggs, give it a little boil and take it off
7. Place the pure white boiled eggs on the rich brown custard
8. Serve with sponge cake

title: Coffee Frappée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of coffee (black and very strong)
- A dash of old French Cognac
- 2 teaspoonfuls of sugar
- Finely-crushed ice

## Directions

1. Take a large glass, mix the above ingredients together, fill with finely-crushed ice, and pour into a small Burgundy glass, and serve very cold.

title: Coffee Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of New Milk
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Strong Cafe Noir (Black Coffee)

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and take from the fire
2. Whip the cream to a stiff froth
3. Add the sugar and yolks of eggs, thoroughly beaten, to the milk and coffee, sweetening to taste
4. Set aside to cool, and add the whipped cream
5. Proceed to freeze as directed

title: Coffee Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of ground Mocha coffee
- 1 glass of boiling water
- 1 lb. sugar
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 4 glasses of boiling milk
- 6 ounces of sugar
- 1 ounce of clarified isinglass

## Directions

1. Infuse 1 lb. of ground Mocha coffee in a glass of boiling water
2. When the coffee is precipitated, pour it off clear
3. Boil 1 lb. sugar to a rich syrup
4. Pour the coffee to it
5. Set it on a slow fire, that the sugar may dissolve gently
6. When it is perfectly melted, stir in gently the yolks of 8 eggs, 4 glasses of boiling milk, and 6 ounces of sugar
7. Put it on a moderate fire, stirring with a wooden spoon
8. When it begins to simmer, pass it through a fine sieve
9. Let it stand till lukewarm
10. Mix into it 1 ounce of clarified isinglass, and finish it by dissolving the isinglass
11. Pour into moulds to cool

title: Colbert Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pint of Madeira Sauce
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of consommé
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- The juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Put a pint of very thick Madeira Sauce in a saucepan
2. Add gradually the butter and consommé
3. Mix well without allowing the mixture to boil
4. When ready to serve add the juice of half a lemon and a teaspoonful of chopped parsley

title: Cold Brisket of Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- brisket
- salt
- pepper
- saltpetre
- mace
- cloves
- allspice

## Directions

1. Cut brisket from the bones and wash
2. Lay in a large dish
3. Cover well with salt, pepper and a little saltpetre
4. Cover to stand several days, as the weather will permit
5. Wipe off the dressing and place on a clean dish
6. Powder mace and cloves and allspice together and spread thickly over the beef
7. Roll it over and over and tie up well
8. Sew up tightly in a cloth and boil slowly all day
9. Serve cold

title: Cold Chicken Escalloped
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold chicken
- gravy or hot water
- salt
- pepper
- sweet fresh butter
- powdered cracker
- celery or pickle

## Directions

1. Mince cold chicken without the skin
2. Wet it with gravy or hot water
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. To the minced meat of one chicken, put two ounces of sweet, fresh butter, cut small
5. Rub tin or silver scallop pans with butter
6. Strew over the bottom powdered cracker
7. Lay the minced chicken in
8. Strew cracker over the top
9. Bake in a hot oven long enough to brown the top
10. Serve with celery or pickle

title: Cold Daube à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of the rump or round of beef
- Two pounds of veal rump
- Two pigs' feet
- Half pound of salt fat meat
- Five large onions
- Two turnips
- Five carrots
- Two cloves of garlic
- Three bay leaves
- Tablespoonful of lard
- Glass of sherry
- Three sprigs of thyme and parsley
- Salt, pepper and cayenne
- Three cloves
- Six allspice

## Directions

1. Cut the salt meat into shreds, roll well in cayenne and black pepper
2. Chop finely several sprigs of thyme and three bay leaves, one clove of garlic, three sprigs of parsley
3. Mash well three cloves and six allspice
4. Roll the strips of salt meat, which must be about three inches in length and one-half inch thick, in this mixture
5. Make incision into the rump of meat and force in the strips of fat meat and the spices
6. Rub the whole well with salt and pepper, judging according to taste
7. Proceed to cook according to the recipe for Beef à la Mode
8. Let the daube cook about four hours when you intend to serve it cold
9. In another pot, place a veal steak of about two pounds, and two pigs' feet
10. Season well with salt and pepper and cayenne, and cover well with four quarts of water
11. Let them boil and add one bay leaf, one sprig of thyme, one-half clove of garlic and one onion, all minced very fine
12. Add two cloves mashed into almost a jelly, and one glass of sherry or Madeira wine
13. Let these boil well with the veal and pigs' feet
14. When the veal and pigs' feet are cooked very tender, take them out of the pot and mince the meat of each very fine
15. Return to the sauce, and again season highly, according to taste
16. After the daube has cooked four or five hours, take off the stove and pour over the sauce and set all in a cool place
17. Serve the next day cold, cutting into thin slices

title: Cold Hash
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a chicken
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- liquid the chicken was boiled in

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken until it is tender
2. Take out all the bones
3. Chop the meat fine
4. Season with butter, pepper and salt
5. Pour in enough of the liquid the chicken was boiled in to make it moist
6. Simmer for a few moments
7. Put it in a mould to cool
8. Slice nicely when serving

title: Cold Jelly Daube
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds round of beef
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 clove
- vinegar
- lard
- 3 pigs feet
- 8 calves feet
- carrot

## Directions

1. Season 4 pounds round of beef well with salt, pepper, a little garlic, 1 bay leaf, and 1 clove
2. Lard well through and through
3. Sprinkle with a tablespoonful of vinegar and let it stand for 24 hours
4. Put the daube in a pot with a small piece of lard
5. Cover with heavy cover on a slow fire and let it smother for about 4 hours until thoroughly done
6. Take off the cover and let it fry a light brown
7. Remove from the fire
8. In a separate pot have 3 pigs feet and 8 calves feet boiling steadily till they leave the bone, skimmed well at the first boil
9. Add one carrot to boil

title: Cold Jelly Daube
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Four lbs. round of beef
- salt
- pepper
- a little garlic
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 clove
- a tablespoonful of vinegar
- a small piece of lard
- 3 pigs feet
- 8 calves feet
- one carrot
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Season beef well with salt, pepper, a little garlic, 1 bay leaf, and 1 clove
2. Lard well through and through
3. Sprinkle with a tablespoonful of vinegar, and let it stand for 24 hours
4. Put the daube in a pot with a small piece of lard
5. Cover with heavy cover on a slow fire, and let it smother for about 4 hours, until thoroughly done
6. Take off the cover, let it fry a light brown, then remove from the fire
7. In a separate pot, have 3 pigs feet and 8 calves feet boiling steadily till they leave the bone, skimming well at the first boil
8. Add one carrot to boil about 1 hour, then slice in length and place at bottom of bowl when the daube is to be served
9. Break 2 eggs, whites and shells to be beaten together and boiled with jelly briskly, then strain through thick flannel or cloth
10. Mix the daube gravy with jelly; let it rest about 1/2 an hour, then pour slowly over the daube
11. Set it in a cold place, and when cold, take the lard from the top with a spoon
12. With a round edge knife detach carefully the daube and turn into a round plate
13. Place the plate on top of the bowl and turn it over to avoid breaking the jelly

title: Cold Red or Other Cold Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- bones
- onions
- parsley
- milk or cream
- flour
- yolks of 1 or 2 eggs
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Set the cold fish away with some of the bones
2. put these bones on to stew, with onions and parsley cut up
3. make a thickening of milk, or cream, flour, the yolks of 1 or 2 eggs, pepper and salt
4. when the onions are done, take out the bones, and cut up the fish, as picked crabs
5. put it into the above gravy, and stew till quite hot
6. then add the above thickening

title: Cold Slaw
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- raw egg
- condensed milk
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Shave cabbage into shreds
2. Mix 1 well beaten raw egg in 1 teacup condensed milk, 1 cup vinegar
3. Boil till it thickens
4. Pour this over the cabbage
5. Sprinkle with salt

title: Cold Slaw With Hot Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a firm white head of cabbage
- two cups of strong vinegar
- a quart of cut cabbage
- one teaspoonful of mustard
- salt
- a tablespoonful of butter
- three teaspoons of white sugar
- yolks of two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Chop fine a firm white head of cabbage, or better than that, slice it with a patent slicer
2. Lay it in very cold water for an hour
3. Take it out, drain it, and when drained thoroughly, place it in the dish it is to be served in
4. Take two cups of strong vinegar to a quart of cut cabbage, stir in it one teaspoonful of mustard and salt, a tablespoonful of butter, and three teaspoons of white sugar
5. Make this all hot, and at the last moment stir in the yolks of two or three eggs
6. Stir rapidly and pour on to the chopped cabbage in the dish
7. It should be served instantly or the sauce will harden

title: Cold Wild Duck
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of Cold Duck
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Tumbler of Currant Jelly
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Port Wine

## Directions

1. Cut the cold Wild Duck nicely in thick slices.
2. Warm the butter in the saucepan, add the wine and jelly, thoroughly blended; mix well.
3. Serve the sauce with the slices of cold duck.
4. The duck may also be served very deliciously with Currant Jelly alone, and buttered toast.

title: Collared Calf's Head With Brains. Cold Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half or whole calf's head
- water
- sage leaves
- an onion
- meat of head and brains
- slices of ham
- pepper
- salt
- chopped tongue
- an eschalot
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Boil half or the whole calf's head, as you require.
2. Cover it with water and let it simmer for two hours.
3. Take it up, remove the bones, and put them back into the broth.
4. Let it continue to stew, adding to it sage leaves, and an onion.
5. Cut the meat of head and brains into a stew-pan, adding to it some slices of ham, pepper and salt, the chopped tongue and an eschalot.
6. Beat the brains up with two eggs, before putting them in, which should be the last thing.
7. Let these cook two hours.
8. Pour all in a mould and fill up with the liquor from the head, which should be boiled to a jelly.

title: Colored Transparent Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Pound of Fine White Sugar
- Half the White of 1 Egg
- Flavoring and Coloring According to Taste

## Directions

1. For Green Icing, use the Extract of Spinach and flavor with Cedrat
2. For Rose Pink, flavor with the Essential Oil of Roses and add a few drops of Cochineal
3. For Violet Icing, flavor with Syrup of Violet
4. Proceed as in Plain Icing

title: Commencement Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of flour
- 3 lbs. of sugar
- 3 lbs. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 pint of wine
- 1 pint of yeast
- 4 lbs. of fruit
- 1 ounce of citron
- 1 ounce of nutmeg
- mace to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Divide the quantity and bake in moulds, which hold about 2 lbs.

title: Common Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. and a half of flour
- 1 lb. of good brown sugar
- 8 well beaten eggs
- 1 ounce of carraway seeds
- 1 tablespoonful of fresh yeast
- 1 tablespoonful of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of water

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, eggs, and carraway seeds together
2. Add the fresh yeast, milk, and water
3. Let it stand a short time
4. Bake it in a floured tin

title: Common Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sugar
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- the yolks of 5 eggs
- 8 spoonfuls of cream
- 1 wineglass of brandy
- spice to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in an oven

title: Compote of Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Apples
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1/2 Cup of Water
- Lemon peel
- Lemon juice
- Grated nutmeg
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pare the apples, cut them in quarters, cut out the cores, and pierce them several times, to drain the juice
2. Boil six apples in one pound of sugar and half a cup of water, and add the finely cut peel of a lemon, together with the lemon juice
3. When they are well blanched, take them out, without letting them cook too much, and place in a dish
4. Put the apples that you have cut into quarters into the syrup, and let them boil to a jelly in the juice
5. Then drain well, and pour over the apples
6. Sprinkle with a little grated nutmeg and powdered sugar

title: Compote of Banana Cassa
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Banana Cassa
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Granulated Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Allspice, Cinnamon and Mace
- 1/2 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla or Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Take the ripe fruit, peel it, and cut into slices of about an inch in thickness
2. Remove the seeds and place the fruit in a bowl of water, to which you will add a small piece of lime; or simply immerse in lime-water
3. Let it soak for three hours
4. Remove it and rinse in cold water
5. Prepare a syrup, allowing one and a quarter pounds of granulated sugar to one quart of water, and when it begins to boil well add the fruit, ground spices and nutmeg, and let all boil till the fruit becomes transparent
6. If flavoring is preferred to the natural taste, add, according to taste, a tablespoonful of Extract of Lemon or Vanilla
7. Let it cool and serve as a dessert

title: Compote of Blackberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Blackberries
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine berries, but not too ripe
2. Wash them in cold water, and drain through a sieve
3. Boil the sugar, and add the berries, and let them boil up once very gently
4. Put them into a dish, pour the jelly over and serve

title: Compote of Coconut
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Coconut
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Pound of Fine White Loaf Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Divide the coconut into quarters and peel off all the brown skin.
2. Soak the clean, white pieces in cold water, with a little lemon juice and a little salt.
3. Cut them in thin slices first, and then in thick threadlike shreds.
4. Drain the shreds in a clean sieve, and wash them in another water, to free them entirely from oil.
5. Drain well.
6. Boil one pound of the finest white loaf sugar.
7. Remove it from the fire, and throw in the prepared coconut, and set back on the fire.
8. Let it come to a good boil, stirring lightly with a silver fork.
9. Lift them out with the fork, after they have boiled up once, and place the coconut shreds in a sieve.
10. Let the sugar boil up well again.
11. Add the juice of a lemon to the syrup, boil it very thin, and then throw in the coconut shreds, mixing them very lightly in the syrup.
12. Separate the shreds carefully with two silver forks, holding one fork in each hand.
13. Dish up in a raised pile in a dish.
14. When cold, pour over the clear syrup, flavoring with vanilla extract, and serve.

title: Compote of Cranberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cranberries
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Wash and drain the cranberries.
2. Then boil as in Cranberry Jelly, only do not let the cranberries get mashed.
3. Take them out with a skimmer, and add a half pound of sugar to the syrup, and the juice of a lemon.
4. Boil to a syrup and pour over the berries and serve cold.

title: Compote of Currants
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Currants
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Select nice berries and pick clean and wash well in cold water, and drain them through a sieve.
2. Boil the sugar and add the berries.
3. Let them boil up once very gently.
4. Put them in a dish, pour the syrup over and serve.

title: Compote of Damsons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Damsons
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Pick off the stems of the damsons and prick them all over with a pin.
2. Let them simmer in sugar, which you will have boiled to a syrup for about fifteen minutes.
3. Then proceed as in a Compote of Cherries.

title: Compote of Dried Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Dried Fruit
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Allow the dried fruit to soak in cold water a long while, say about two hours, to swell up.
2. Then boil, and afterwards pour into a syrup of boiling sugar, and proceed as in other compotes.

title: Compote of Figs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Figs
- 3/4 Cup of Water
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Use figs that are not quite ripe.
2. Break off the stalks, and prick them all over, and let them simmer in a syrup.
3. Then proceed in exactly the same manner as for Compote of Cherries.

title: Compote of Lemons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Dozen Lemons
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. A Compote of whole lemons is prepared in exactly the same manner as the above, using twice the amount of sugar, or sugar to taste.
2. When squeezed thoroughly and boiled they make an excellent Creole remedy for colds and coughs.

title: Compote of Malaga Grapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Malaga Grapes
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Wash the grapes, then cut a slit in the grape on one side, and take out the seeds.
2. Put the fruit in cold water, and let it boil.
3. Take off the fire, and let it stand uncovered for a time, and then put in cold water.
4. When cold lift them out with a skimming spoon.
5. Put the grapes in clarified sugar, which you will have boiled almost to a thread, and let them boil up once.
6. Skim them out, and put in a dish.
7. Boil the sugar to half the quantity in syrup, and pour over, and serve.

title: Compote of Mespilus or Japan Plums
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Mespilus
- 3/4 Cups of Water
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Peel, stone and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Compote of Cherries.

title: Compote of Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of Half a Lemon
- 3/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, and cut them into halves crosswise, and take out the seeds and the inner pulp.
2. Put the sugar and the water on to boil, and after ten minutes add the lemon juice.
3. Then put a few of the oranges at a time into the hot syrup, and let them stew gently.
4. After a half hour take them out, lay in a dish, pour over the hot liquid syrup and serve either hot or cold.

title: Compote of Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Peaches
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- % Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Take well-grown, somewhat unripe peaches
2. Cut them in two, and take out the stones
3. Put the peaches into boiling water, and let them stand till they are soft, and then throw in cold water till cold
4. Peel them and put them into the sugar, adding a little water, and let them boil up several times
5. Drain with the skimmer, lifting them into the dish gently
6. Pour over the reduced syrup, and serve with milk or whipped cream

title: Compote of Pears
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 Large, Fine Pears
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of a Lemon
- 1/4 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Scrape the pears spirally from head to stalk with a tapering tin cutter, to take out the cores
2. Put the sugar and one-half cup of water on the fire, and add to it four or five pears, which you will have cut into pieces
3. Place the whole pears in it, and let them simmer, adding the lemon juice to keep them white
4. When done, place the pears in order in a dish
5. Let the syrup stew down to a nice jelly, and then pour over and serve

title: Compote of Pigeons
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Squab
- A Half Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Onion
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of White Wine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Cloves ground very fine
- 3 Allspice ground very fine
- Salt and Pepper
- 3/4 Cup of Consomme
- Thin Strips of Bacon
- Toasted Croflton

## Directions

1. Clean the squabs nicely; singe, draw and truss, with their legs inside.
2. Rub well with salt and pepper and three cloves, ground very fine, and three allspice, also ground very fine.
3. Take a tablespoonful of butter and melt in a saucepan.
4. Add the sliced onion, and as it browns add the sliced carrot.
5. Let this simmer gently for three or four minutes, and then add a minced sprig of thyme and parsley, and one bay leaf, and the clove of garlic, minced very fine.
6. Let all this brown, and then place on top the pigeons, which you will have bound in thin strips of bacon tied around the body.
7. Add two tablespoonfuls of white wine and cover well.
8. Let this simmer for about fifteen minutes, till the pigeons are nicely browned.
9. Add a half cup of consomme if you have it, if not, a half cup of boiling water.
10. After ten minutes add a half can of mushrooms.
11. Let all simmer gently for an hour longer, being careful not to let the pigeons go to pieces.
12. Place each pigeon on a slice of toasted Croflton, and garnish with the mushrooms.
13. Pour over the gravy, and serve hot.

title: Compote of Pineapple
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pineapple
- % Pound of Sugar
- % Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple, and cut in slices, very thin
2. Put it into a syrup made of half a pound of sugar, and let it boil up once
3. Take out, and let the syrup simmer a few minutes longer
4. Pour over the pineapple, and serve

title: Compote of Plantain, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Plantains
- % Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of Half a Lemon
- 1 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the plantains and cut into halves or quarters
2. Put the sugar and water on to boil, and, after ten minutes, add the lemon juice
3. Put the pieces of plantain, a few at a time, into the hot syrup and let them stew gently
4. After half an hour, or forty-five minutes if not tender, take them out, lay in a dish
5. Pour over the hot syrup, and serve either hot or cold

title: Compote of Plantain, Old Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Plantains
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Rum
- Kirsch or Maraschino Sauce

## Directions

1. Have ready a pile of hot ashes in the oven
2. Put the plantains (unpeeled) into the ashes and cover well with ashes
3. Let them roast thus for half an hour
4. Take out, peel, cut into quarters and sprinkle with sugar
5. Set in the oven a few minutes and serve hot, with a Rum, Kirsch or Maraschino Sauce

title: Compote of Plums
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Plums
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- % Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Pick off the stems of the plums and prick them all over with a pin
2. Let them simmer in sugar, which you will have boiled to a syrup for about fifteen minutes
3. Proceed as in a Compote of Cherries

title: Compote of Pomegranates
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Pomegranates
- % Pound of Sugar
- % Cup of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Juice or Maraschino

## Directions

1. Cut a circle about the size of a five-cent piece out of the peel of the pomegranate, using a sharp-pointed knife
2. Split down the sides of their skins, and carefully remove all the bright ruby pips, without bruising them
3. Throw these into a dish
4. Make a syrup, flavored with orange juice or Maraschino, and pour over and serve

title: Compote of Prunes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Prunes
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Glass of Claret

## Directions

1. Soak the prunes over night in cold water, and drain
2. Put them into a saucepan with water, cover the saucepan, and let them boil over a moderate fire
3. When they begin to soften, add a glass of red wine and a quarter of a pound of sugar
4. Let it boil, stirring frequently, till the prunes are cooked well
5. Take them from the fire, rub a piece of sugar over an orange or a lemon, and when it is completely saturated with the zest, put into a dish
6. Pour the prunes in with their juice, and serve

title: Compote of Raspberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Raspberries
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine Raspberries, but not too ripe.
2. Wash them in cold water, and drain through a sieve.
3. Boil the sugar, and add the berries and let them boil up once very gently.
4. Put them into a dish, pour the Jelly over and serve.

title: Compote of Whole Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the oranges, with their peel, gently in water for twenty minutes.
2. Turn the oranges very thinly and spirally, scoring out, if possible, all the inner pulp and peeling.
3. Make a syrup by boiling the sugar, and add the oranges.
4. Let them simmer for twenty minutes longer.
5. Take off, and allow the oranges to remain in their juice for several hours.
6. Dish them up in a compote dish, and pour over their syrup on the instant of sending to the table.

title: Conserve of Grapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fruit Juice
- ½ Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the grapes for one hour.
2. Press them through a sieve.
3. Add a half pound of sugar to every pint of liquid.
4. Proceed as directed above.

title: Consomme with Poached Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3 quarts of consomme or bouillon
- salt

## Directions

1. Break the eggs and drop them one by one into boiling salted water, being careful not to allow the water to boil when once the eggs are in it.
2. Have the frying pan to one side of the stove, and cook slowly until the eggs are firm.
3. When firm, carefully remove with a spoon or perforated skimmer, and lay in cold water for a moment, until the edges are trimmed evenly.
4. Transfer to the tureen and pour the boiling soup very gently into the tureen and serve.
5. One egg and about a half pint of broth should be allowed to each person.

title: Consommé
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds of lean beef
- 2 large-sized onions
- 2 carrots
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 piece of cabbage
- salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Select six pounds of lean beef, rump of beef and some bones, and cut the meat into small pieces, the bones also being mashed
2. Put this on in about six quarts of cold water
3. When it comes to a boil, skim well
4. Add a teaspoonful of salt to help the scum rise more thoroughly, and skim as it rises
5. Take two large sized onions, two carrots, a piece of cabbage and two pieces of celery; chop fine and add to the soup
6. Let it boil six hours, or until the broth is reduced about one-half the quantity
7. Pass all through a colander and then strain through a coarse flannel cloth
8. Season highly with cayenne pepper and salt to the taste
9. If cloudy or muddy before straining, crush the shells of two eggs and put them into the soup and let it come to a good boil, set it back about ten minutes and then strain
10. Add vermicelli, macaroni, or patés according to taste

title: Consommé Of Beef And Fowl
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of lean beef
- a fowl half roasted and cut in pieces
- stock or plain broth
- two carrots
- two onions
- a head of celery or a pinch of celery seed
- a little thyme
- a whole pepper
- mace
- a bay leaf

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of lean beef and a fowl half roasted and cut in pieces, put into a saucepan, which must be filled with stock or plain broth; skim it well, salt it to taste, and add two carrots, two onions, a head of celery or a pinch of celery seed, also a little thyme, a whole pepper, mace, and a bay leaf. Let it simmer gently for three or four hours, then strain through a coarse cloth; free it entirely from fat, and clarify it with the white of an egg.

title: Cooking Crabs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen crabs
- 1 pint of water
- 1 pint of vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of salt

## Directions

1. Add 1 pint of water, 1 pint of vinegar, and 2 tablespoonfuls of salt to an iron pot
2. After the mixture boils, put the crabs in with a cloth over them, excluding the air
3. Let them boil from 12 to 15 minutes

title: Coquilles of Chicken, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- The Breast of a Roasted Chicken
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Truffle
- 4 Mushrooms
- 1 Dozen Godiveau Quenelles
- 1/2 Pint of Madeira Sauce
- Grated Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into dice-shaped pieces; take an ounce of butter and place in a saucepan; add the chicken.
2. Chop four mushrooms and one truffle and add.
3. Make a dozen small Godiveau Quenelles (see recipe), and add to the mixture in the saucepan.
4. Pour over a half pint of Madeira Sauce (see recipe), stir well and let all cook for five minutes.
5. Take a half dozen table shells and fill with this mixture.
6. Sprinkle grated fresh bread crumbs over the tops, spread a little melted butter over each and place the shells on a baking dish.
7. Place in a very hot oven and let them brown to a beautiful golden color, which will require about six minutes.
8. Place a folded napkin on a dish, set the shells upon it and send to the table hot.

title: Corn and Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Corn, Cut from the Cob
- 1 Pint of Fresh Tomatoes, Peeled and Chopped
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sprigs Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sugar

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a frying pan, and when it heats well without browning, add the tomatoes.
2. Let them simmer for about five minutes, stirring well, and then add the minced herbs.
3. Let these stew for three minutes, and add the corn, which has been scored and cut from the cob.
4. Mix all thoroughly.
5. Add salt and pepper to taste, and a teaspoonful of sugar, or less, according to taste.
6. Let all stew or sauté for about twenty minutes, and then stir in a teaspoonful more of butter.
7. Serve hot, after cooking ten minutes longer.

title: Corn Baked in a Dish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet corn
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- egg

## Directions

1. Cut sweet corn from the ear
2. Add plenty of milk
3. Season with salt, pepper and butter
4. Turn into a baking dish
5. Beat an egg and spread over the top
6. Bake 20 minutes

title: Corn Batter Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six spoonfuls of flour
- six of corn meal
- a little salt
- four eggs
- a cup of milk

## Directions

1. Take six spoonfuls of flour, and six of corn meal
2. Add a little salt and sift them together
3. Make a batter with four eggs, and a cup of milk
4. Stir in the flour and meal
5. Make it a soft batter
6. Bake in small tins for breakfast

title: Corn Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rounds of beef
- saltpetre
- common salt

## Directions

1. Rub the rounds first with saltpetre and then with common salt
2. They will make their own brine, in which they should be turned every day
3. A round of small beef only requires ten days in winter; it grows hard if kept longer; in warm weather less time suffices

title: Corn Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Corned Beef
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Turnips
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 2 Onions
- 1 Clove of Garlic

## Directions

1. Put the corn beef on to boil in a large pot of cold water
2. The pot should be well covered
3. When it begins boiling well, set it back to cook gently, and allow twenty-five minutes to each pound of beef
4. When within two hours of being cooked, add two carrots, two turnips, a stalk of celery, two onions, one clove of garlic, chopped fine, and let these boil with the beef
5. Serve with the vegetables ranged whole around the dish

title: Corn Beef Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Corn Beef, Cooked
- 1 Pint of Left-Over Potatoes
- 1 Grated Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Broth or Water
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Slices of Buttered Toast
- Sliced Lemon

## Directions

1. Mix the meat thoroughly after mincing fine together with the chopped potatoes
2. Grate the onion and a half clove of garlic if desired, and chop the herbs fine and mix thoroughly with the beef, seasoning highly
3. Put the butter into the frying pan, add the meat and the broth or water, and stir constantly till it boils
4. Spread, after it has cooked for about twenty minutes on slices of buttered toast
5. Pour over the gravy and garnish with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon

title: Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of cornmeal
- 1 cup of breakfast hominy boiled
- 1 egg
- a pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of yeast powder
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients with sufficient milk to make a soft batter
2. Bake quickly in a slightly larded hot pan
3. Serve instantly

title: Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups cornmeal
- 1 cup flour
- 1½ pints milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of molasses
- 1 tablespoonful yeast powder
- little salt

## Directions

1. bake in pans, in a not very hot oven

title: Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of cornmeal
- 1 cup of breakfast hominy (boiled)
- 1 egg
- a pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of yeast powder
- sufficient sweet milk

## Directions

1. Make a soft batter with the cornmeal, hominy, egg, salt, yeast powder, and sweet milk
2. Bake quickly in a slightly larded hot pan
3. Serve instantly

title: Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and a half pints of rich buttermilk
- cornmeal
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- a cup of new milk
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Thicken the buttermilk with cornmeal to the consistency of batter
2. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of soda in a cup of new milk
3. Add a little salt and beat very light
4. Pour this into buttered pans
5. Bake two hours
6. Serve hot

title: Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of thick sour cream
- 1 spoonful of fresh butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- hot water
- 1 tablespoonful of sifted flour
- white cornmeal

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of soda in hot water
2. Combine the sour cream, fresh butter, soda solution, and sifted flour
3. Add white cornmeal to form a soft dough
4. Work well with the hands
5. Bake in a buttered pan
6. Serve very hot

title: Corn Bread Rusk
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- six cupfuls of cornmeal
- 4 cupfuls of wheat flour
- 2 cupfuls of molasses
- 2 teaspoonfuls of soda
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix the cornmeal, wheat flour, molasses, soda, and salt well together
2. Knead it into dough
3. Make 2 cakes of it
4. Pour into tin or iron pans
5. Bake 1 hour

title: Corn Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of sour milk
- sifted cornmeal
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of soda
- a cup of milk
- 1 teacup of good molasses
- 1 large spoonful of lard or butter

## Directions

1. Thicken the sour milk with sifted cornmeal
2. Dissolve a teaspoonful of soda in a cup of milk
3. Add the salt, molasses, and lard or butter
4. Beat these ingredients well together
5. Pour it into a well greased iron baking pan with an iron cover
6. Put it in the fire-place when the fire is raked for the night
7. Put fire and hot ashes over and around it
8. In the morning you will have a nice brown loaf for breakfast

title: Corn Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Ears of Corn
- 3/4 Pint of Milk
- 1 Egg
- 1/2 Cup of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter in exactly the same manner as the Corn Fritters recipe.
2. Bake on a griddle like batter cakes, and serve hot, with generous layers of butter between.

title: Corn Dodgers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Indian Meal
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the meal with boiling water.
2. Add the melted lard and the salt.
3. Use sufficient boiling water to make a very stiff batter or soft dough.
4. Then take up a handful of the mixture, and mold it with your hands into oval mounds, tossing the cake of dough lightly between your hands in a dexterous manner, and leaving the impression of your fingers across.
5. Bake the pones thus formed in a quick oven.

title: Corn Dodgers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Indian Meal
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald the meal with boiling water.
2. Add the melted lard and the salt.
3. Use sufficient boiling water to make a very stiff batter or soft dough.
4. Take up a handful of the mixture, and mold it with your hands into oval mounds, tossing the cake of dough lightly between your hands in a dexterous manner, and leaving the impression of your fingers across.
5. Bake the pones thus formed in a quick oven.
6. Serve at dinner.

title: Corn Fritters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- uncooked corn ears
- 1 cup flour
- 1 egg
- milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Grate corn from uncooked ears
2. For batter, mix 1 cup flour, 1 egg, and milk enough to soften the whole sufficiently
3. Add salt and pepper
4. Fry brown in thin cakes

title: Corn Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Corn Meal
- 1 Pint of Boiling Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Eggs
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- Flavoring to Taste
- 1/2 Spoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the cornmeal together, and add the melted butter and the milk, stirring and beating well.
2. Add the salt and any flavoring extract preferred.
3. Lastly, add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Fry in boiling lard, dropping in the lard by spoonfuls.

title: Corn Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Ears of Corn
- 1/2 Pint of Milk
- 1 Egg
- 3/4 Cup of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Grate the corn.
2. Beat the egg well, whites and yolks together, and by degrees add the corn, beating in thoroughly and very hard.
3. Add a tablespoonful of melted butter, and then stir in the milk.
4. Add a tablespoonful of flour, or just sufficient to thicken and bind, and then fry like fritters in boiling lard, dropping in a deep spoonful at a time.
5. Serve hot.

title: Corn Gems
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Cornmeal
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Sweet Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Baking Powder
- Salt

## Directions

1. Boil one-half of the milk and pour it over the cornmeal
2. Add the butter instantly and stir well
3. When thoroughly mixed add the cold milk and the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
4. Add the salt and then the flour, into which you will have sifted the baking powder
5. Mix thoroughly and then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
6. Pour into greased or buttered gem pans, filling only half full
7. Bake about a half hour in a hot oven

title: Corn on the Cob
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Ears of Corn
- 2 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Only young and tender corn should be boiled
2. Have ready a pot of boiling water
3. Remove the green outside husks and the silk, as far as possible
4. Put the corn into the kettle of boiling water
5. Let it boil rapidly twenty or thirty minutes, if the ears are large
6. Serve immediately after removing again all the silk
7. Heap the corn on a platter and serve to each person an ear with a small butter plate of butter, pepper and salt

title: Corn Oysters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of grated green corn
- one cup of flour
- one dessertspoonful of salt
- one teaspoonful of pepper
- an egg
- lard

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Drop and fry in hot lard

title: Corn Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ears of corn
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful pepper
- 1 egg
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix together 12 ears of corn, 1 cup of flour, 1 teaspoonful of salt, 1 teaspoonful of pepper, and 1 egg
2. Stir well and fry in butter

title: Corn Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- eggs
- 1 tablespoonful wheat flour
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Grate the corn while green and tender with a coarse grater into a deep dish
2. To 2 ears of corn allow 1 egg
3. Beat the whites and yolks separately
4. Add them to the corn with 1 tablespoonful of wheat flour and 1 tablespoonful of butter
5. Add salt and pepper to the taste
6. Lay them in hot butter with a spoon and fry them on both sides

title: Corn Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Ears of Tender Corn
- 1 Quart Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of White Sugar
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Score the corn down each row of grains, and grate it from the cob.
2. Beat the whites of the egg and the yolks separately.
3. After beating the yolks, add them to the sugar and butter, which you will have rubbed well together.
4. Beat all this very light, and then add the milk and a half teaspoonful of salt.
5. Blend well, and add the grated corn.
6. Beat again, and blend thoroughly, and add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth.
7. Stir in well, and set the mixture in the oven with a piece of brown paper on top.
8. Bake slowly for about an hour, and serve hot.

title: Corn Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Corn Salad
- A Plain French Dressing
- Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Beets

## Directions

1. Take one pint of fresh Doucette and pare off the outer stale leaves, if there are any; cut off the roots
2. Wash the Doucette well in two waters, drain in a napkin and place in the salad bowl
3. When ready to serve add a plain French dressing but not before
4. Mix well, so that every portion will be impregnated with the dressing
5. Serve very cold with a garnish of two hard-boiled eggs, sliced or cut in quarters, or of two medium-sized beets

title: Corn Sauted With Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Fine Ears of Corn
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Cup of Milk or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Boil the corn, and then cut the grains from the cob with a sharp knife.
2. Put in a saucepan and add one tablespoonful of butter.
3. Add the milk, and season nicely to taste.
4. Let the corn boil for about ten minutes and serve hot.

title: Corn Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- young corn
- water
- pork
- seasoning
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Take young corn and cut the ears across
2. Grate them in water, 2 ears to a pint
3. About 6 quarts will make a good tureen of soup
4. To this quantity put a piece of pork and seasoning to your taste
5. Add vegetables if you like
6. It must boil 3 hours

title: Corn Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds lower ribs of beef
- 6 quarts cold water
- 1 quart sliced fresh tomatoes
- 1 quart corn sliced from the cob
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 pod cayenne pepper without seeds
- salt and black pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Put the meat and water into a soup pot and as soon as the scum begins to rise, skim carefully
2. Add the tomatoes and the corncobs
3. Cook for four hours or so longer
4. Take out the corncobs and add the corn cut fine
5. Add salt and pepper to suit the taste and a pod of cayenne pepper without the seeds
6. Cook one hour longer and serve with slices of toast bread

title: Corn Wafers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of meal
- 1 gill of milk
- 1 gill of water
- 1 dessertspoonful of butter
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Combine the meal, milk, water, butter, and salt
2. Bake a light brown

title: Cornmeal Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
- 1 pint of cornmeal
- 2 eggs
- some new milk
- a spoonful of yeast

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the cornmeal
2. Make it a batter with 2 eggs and some new milk
3. Add a spoonful of yeast
4. Set it by the fire 1 hour to rise
5. Butter little pans and bake it

title: Cornmeal Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 2 Cups of Cornmeal
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 3 Eggs
- 3 Cups of Sour Milk or Warm Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Scald the meal and mix with the milk.
2. Add the flour, into which you will have mixed the salt.
3. Beat the eggs very light and add, and when all has been beaten very light add a teaspoonful of soda, dissolved in a little warm water.
4. Bake on a griddle to a nice brown.
5. If sweet milk is used, be careful to use, instead of the soda, two teaspoonfuls of baking powder.

title: Cornmeal Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of cornmeal
- ¾ cup of sifted wheat flour
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 2 large spoonfuls of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 pint of buttermilk
- a large pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve the soda in the buttermilk
2. Mix all ingredients
3. Pour into rings and bake a nice brown in the oven

title: Cornmeal Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Cornmeal
- 1/4 Cup of Sifted Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 1/2 Cups of Buttermilk or Sweet Milk
- 2 Spoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the meal.
2. Scald the milk and add the butter.
3. When lukewarm add to the cornmeal and beat well.
4. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and beat well.
5. Add the flour, into which the yeast powder must previously be sifted.
6. Mix well and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
7. Beat steadily for about ten minutes, and then pour into greased muffin rings or gem pans, and bake for twenty minutes, in a quick oven.
8. If buttermilk is used, add a teaspoonful of soda, dissolved in the milk.

title: Cornmeal Mush
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Cornmeal
- 2 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1¼ Teaspoonfuls of Salt

## Directions

1. Set the water to boil in a porcelain-lined or agate stewpot; add the salt.
2. When the light scum comes on top, skim it off.
3. Add the fresh, sweet white cornmeal, putting a handful at a time into the water, and stirring with a spoon, or a pudding stick, round and round, as the meal falls lightly from the hand.
4. When one handful is exhausted, refill it, and continue stirring and letting the meal fall by degrees, until the pudding stick will stand in it.
5. Continue stirring, and when sufficiently cooked, which will be in a half hour, as the bubbles begin to puff up, turn into a bowl.
6. Bring to the table either hot or cold and eat with milk, butter, sugar, syrup or with meat and gravy.

title: Cornmeal Pone
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of the Finest Indian Meal
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- A Heaping Teaspoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Pint of Sour Milk or Buttermilk
- 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Pour just enough boiling water over the meal to scald it through and through, without making a dough or batter.
2. Stir it well, and let it grow tepid.
3. It will be then quite dry.
4. Beat two eggs very light, and add to the meal, and add a melted tablespoonful of lard or butter.
5. Mix this well, and add the milk or buttermilk, and beat till the batter is very smooth.
6. Dissolve one teaspoonful of soda in a little boiling water, and add to the mixture, and grease a baking pan.
7. Turn the mixture in and bake in a quick oven for about thirty-five minutes, till a golden brown.
8. Take the pone from the pan whole, and place on a hot platter, and serve, cutting it at the table into slices about three inches long and two wide, and serving with butter.

title: Cornmeal Rusk
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cups of cornmeal
- 4 cups of flour
- 2 cups of molasses
- 2 teaspoonfuls of saleratus

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Knead it into dough
3. Make into 2 cakes
4. Bake them as you would pone, for a half hour

title: Cornmeal Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Cornmeal
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Soda, dissolved in hot water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
2. Add the milk and the soda, dissolved in a little hot water
3. Add the melted butter and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Add the flour gradually making a nice batter, not too thick nor yet too thin
5. Beat till all is very smooth
6. Proceed to bake as for Plain Waffles

title: Cornstarch Blanc Manger
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Dissolve the cornstarch lightly in one pint of milk, and then add the sugar and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
2. Put this mixture into a pint of boiling milk.
3. Let it come to a boil.
4. Flavor with the lemon; pour into cups to cool.
5. When very cold, turn out, and serve with Jelly and cream.
6. This will serve six persons. Serve, if you wish, with a Cream Sauce.

title: Cornstarch Blanc Mangier
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Dissolve the cornstarch lightly in one pint of milk
2. Add the sugar and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Put this mixture into a pint of boiling milk
4. Let it come to a boil
5. Flavor with the lemon
6. Pour into cups to cool
7. When very cold, turn out, and serve with Jelly and cream

title: Cotelettes en Papilottes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- dry stale bread crumbs
- raw eggs
- thyme
- parsley
- marjoram
- onion
- celery leaves or seed
- 1 dozen olives
- letter paper
- lard
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Trim the cutlets nicely
2. Prepare a stuffing of dry, stale bread crumbs, mixed with the yolks of some raw eggs
3. Season highly with thyme, parsley, marjoram, onion and celery leaves or seed, etc.
4. Cut up 1 dozen olives fine, and mix in
5. Put plenty of the dressing on each cutlet, and wrap it carefully in letter paper
6. Lay them side by side in a flat, common pot
7. Add a little lard and let them sweat for 1 hour
8. Broil quickly on hot coals, and serve and eat with lemon juice

title: Cottage Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs, separately beaten
- 1 common sized teacup of brown sugar
- 2 teacups of molasses
- 1 teacup of beaten ginger
- 2 teacups of butter
- 1 teacup of cream or milk with a small piece of soda dissolved in it
- 3 cups of flour
- orange or lemon peel for flavoring

## Directions

1. Make it the consistency of pound cake
