2. Flavor with orange or lemon peel
3. Bake it either in a large size or small tins

title: Cottage Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls rennet wine

## Directions

1. Make 1 quart of milk lukewarm
2. Add 3 tablespoonfuls of rennet wine
3. After having seasoned the milk to the taste, set it by a short time, when it will be fit for use

title: Cottage Plum Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound and a half of flour
- Four or five eggs
- A pinch of salt
- A little nutmeg
- One pound of raisins
- Half a pound of currants
- Sugar to taste
- A little milk
- Peel of a lemon grated
- A few pieces of citron cut thin

## Directions

1. Make a thick batter with five well-beaten eggs, one pound and a half of flour, and a sufficient quantity of milk
2. Add the currants washed and picked
3. Add the raisins stoned
4. Add a little nutmeg and sugar to taste
5. Mix all well together
6. Boil it in a basin or floured cloth for quite five hours
7. The peel of a lemon grated, and a few pieces of citron cut thin may be added

title: Cottage Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of butter
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of milk
- two eggs
- one teaspoonful of soda
- one pint of sifted flour
- two spoonfuls of cream of tartar
- powdered sugar
- one tablespoonful of butter for sauce
- one cup of powdered sugar for sauce
- lemon extract or lemon juice for sauce
- half a pint of boiling water for sauce

## Directions

1. Mix butter, sugar, milk, eggs, soda, sifted flour, and cream of tartar like cake.
2. Bake quickly in shallow tin pans.
3. Dredge the top with powdered sugar, which gives a nice crust.
4. For the sauce: beat together one tablespoonful of butter, one cup of powdered sugar, lemon extract or lemon juice for seasoning, with half a pint of boiling water until it foams.

title: Cottage Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 1/2 Cups of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and the yolks of the eggs, and then add the milk and the flour.
2. Add the whites, beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Beat carefully into the pudding.
4. Add the baking powder, and pour into a buttered pan, and cook in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour.
5. Serve this pudding hot, with Vanilla, Cream or Lemon Sauce.

title: Cough Lozenges
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Gum Dragon
- 2 Ounces of Orange Flower Water
- 2 Pounds of Fine Sugar Icing
- 50 Drops of Paregoric
- 20 Drops of Ipecacuanha
- 1 1/2 Ounces Syrup of Squills

## Directions

1. Work the gum on the slab with about one-third of the sugar
2. Work in gradually the syrup of squills
3. After working well add the rest of the sugar and again work
4. Add the Paregoric and the Ipecacuanha
5. Proceed to finish these old-fashioned lozenges in the manner directed in the general recipe

title: Country Batter Pudding With Fruit, Cheap and Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of buttermilk
- one egg
- one large teaspoonful of soda
- salt
- flour
- one quart of chopped fruit (pie-plant, berries, strawberries, peaches, etc.)
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. To a quart of buttermilk add one egg, a large teaspoonful of soda, a little salt, and flour enough to make a thick batter.
2. Pour it over a quart of chopped fruit, such as pie-plant, berries, strawberries, peaches, etc.
3. Beat it a little.
4. Tie it tightly in a bag, drop it in a kettle of hot water, and let it boil two hours.
5. Alternatively, this pudding may be poured into a cake pan and baked, if not convenient to boil it. Put in plenty of fruit.
6. Serve with sugar and cream.

title: Country Fried Chickens
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a young, fat chicken
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- a cup of lard
- slices of fat bacon
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Take a young, fat chicken, cut it up, pepper and salt it, dredge it over with flour, and set it by
2. Mix a cup of lard, and some slices of fat bacon in a frying pan and let the lard get very hot
3. Drop in a few pieces of the chicken, always allowing room in the pan for each piece to be turned without crowding
4. As fast as you fry the pieces, put them on a dish over hot water to keep the heat in them while you make the gravy
5. Pour off some of the grease the chicken was fried in, and then dredge into the frying pan some flour, let this brown nicely
6. Pour into it a cup of sweet milk, little at a time; let it froth up
7. Place your chicken back into the gravy for three minutes
8. If you like the chicken brown and dry, pour the gravy under it on the dish for serving

title: Country Green Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one peck of tomatoes
- eight green peppers
- cabbage
- brine
- vinegar
- horseradish
- spices

## Directions

1. Chop eight green peppers fine
2. Soak the tomatoes and pepper twenty-four hours in weak brine
3. Drain off the brine, and add to the green tomatoes a head of finely chopped cabbage
4. Scald all in boiling vinegar twenty minutes
5. Skim it out from the vinegar, and place in a large jar
6. Add three pints of grated horseradish and such other spices as you please
7. Fill the jars with strong cold vinegar and tie up for use

title: Country Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a large handful of hops
- 3 pints of water
- flour
- a heaping teaspoonful of salt
- a large teacupful of lively yeast

## Directions

1. Put hops into a vessel with water and boil half an hour, or until the strength is extracted
2. Strain it through a sieve on to 8 or 10 spoonfuls of flour
3. Stir until all the dry flour is moistened, or as thick as griddle cakes
4. Add a heaping teaspoonful of salt
5. When about blood-warm, stir in a large teacupful of lively yeast

title: Courtbouillon a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- Six Fine Slices of Redfish or Red Snapper (equal to 3 Pounds)
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 12 Well-Mashed Allspice
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 3 Sprigs of Sweet Marjoram
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 clove of Garlic
- Large Fresh Tomatoes or a Half Can
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 Glass of Claret
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Slice the Redfish in fine, clear-cut pieces, after having thoroughly washed and cleaned it.
2. Make a Roux by putting one tablespoonful of lard in a deep can or kettle.
3. When hot add gradually two tablespoonfuls of flour, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
4. Throw in about ten or twelve well-mashed allspice, and three sprigs each of chopped thyme, parsley, bay leaf and sweet marjoram, one clove of garlic and one large onion, chopped very fine.
5. Add six fresh, large tomatoes, chopped fine, or one-half can of tomatoes.
6. Pour in one glass of good claret.
7. Add about one quart of water, and let it boil well.
8. Add salt and Cayenne to taste.
9. When this has boiled about five minutes add the fish, putting in slice by slice.
10. Add the juice of a lemon.
11. Let all boil about ten minutes.
12. Serve with French fried potatoes, mashed potatoes or potato croquettes.

title: Courtbouillon Of Any Sort Of Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of water
- 1 pint of white wine
- 2 ounces of butter
- large bunch of parsley
- 3 leeks
- 1 clove of garlic
- bunch of thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- basil
- slices of onion
- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- fish

## Directions

1. Tie together a large bunch of parsley, 3 leeks, 1 clove of garlic, a bunch of thyme, 2 bay leaves, and a little basil
2. Boil the fish, or rather let it simmer in the courtbouillon with 1 pint of water, 1 pint of white wine, 2 ounces of butter, some slices of onion and carrots, salt and pepper until done
3. Remove the fish and strain the liquor
4. Place fish in the dish and pour liquor over it
5. Garnish the sides of dish with slices of lemon

title: Courtbouillon Of Perch
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- perch
- 1.5 ounces of butter
- flour
- 2 or 3 small onions
- small bunch of sweet herbs
- 1/2 pint of wine

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish
2. Place them in a stewpan with the butter rolled in flour, the sweet herbs minced fine, and the small onions
3. Pour over them the white wine and let them simmer until cooked enough
4. Garnish sides of dish with thin slices of lemon

title: Courtbouillon Of Red Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced red fish of from 2 to 3 or 4 pounds
- butter
- flour
- boiling vinegar
- wine
- salt
- pepper
- laurel leaf
- 2 slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Rub the fish well with butter and roll in flour
2. Pour over it a large wine-glass of boiling vinegar
3. Place the fish in a stewpan with wine enough to cover it
4. Add salt, pepper, a laurel leaf or two, and 2 slices of lemon
5. Let all simmer gently
6. Remove the fish and strain the liquor
7. Pour it over the fish and serve with dish garnished with slices of lemon

title: Cousin Jane's Buns
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- 3 yolks and 2 whites of eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of yeast
- 2 tablespoonfuls of spice

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, butter, eggs, yeast, and spice
2. Bake

title: Crab Apple Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Crab apples
- Water
- Loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the apples, cut them up, remove all defects, remove the seeds and the blossom end; but do not pare them
2. Lay them in your preserving-kettle, and cover them with water
3. Boil them until they are soft, but do not let them mash up from too much boiling
4. Drain off all the water, and mash the apples with the back of a silver spoon
5. Put this in a jelly bag, and place a deep dish under it to collect the juice
6. To every pint of the juice allow a pint of loaf sugar
7. Boil it and skim it
8. It will be ready to dip out into tumblers in half an hour, if you have complied with these directions
9. Always dip jelly out with a silver spoon, as any other kind darkens fruit

title: Crab Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Toast
- 8 Hard-Shelled Crabs
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Small Onion
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Gill of Broth or Water
- 2 Ounces of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 2 Ounces of Grated Swiss Cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs
2. Pick out all the meat from the claws and bodies, and season nicely with salt and pepper
3. Put one ounce of butter into the saucepan, and add a finely minced onion
4. Let this cook on a slow fire for two minutes, but by no means allow either butter or onion to brown
5. Add a tablespoonful of flour, and stir constantly for a minute and a half
6. Add a gill of broth or water
7. Stir well and let this mixture cook for five minutes, stirring constantly
8. Add the crab meat and let it cook for fifteen minutes longer, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon
9. Turn the mixture into a dish and let it cool for about a quarter of an hour
10. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and add immediately a tablespoonful of flour and blend well
11. Let this cook for three minutes, stirring all the time
12. Add two ounces of grated Parmesan and two ounces of grated Swiss cheese
13. Stir all well together, blending thoroughly
14. Turn into a vessel to cool
15. Cut six slices of bread the full length and width of the loaf, using preferably the Pan Bread or delicate French loaf
16. Let the thickness of each slice be about a quarter of an inch
17. Neatly trim off the crust
18. Fry the bread in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter till they have reached a golden brown
19. Let them cool, draining off all butter
20. Divide the crab forcemeat and the cheese separately into six equal parts
21. Place a layer of the crab forcemeat one-quarter of an inch thick on each slice of toast
22. Take the six portions of cheese and roll each into a ball-shaped form about two inches in diameter
23. Arrange them on each portion of toast nicely and equally
24. Place in a dish and brown in the oven for five minutes
25. Send the Canapes to the table hot in the same dish in which they were baked

title: Crab Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Crabs
- 1 Cup of Wet Bread, Squeezed Well
- 1 Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs clean and pick
2. Season the meat well with salt and pepper
3. Chop the onion fine, also the herbs
4. Put a tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan, and as it melts, add the chopped onion
5. When it begins to fry add the crab meat which has been mixed thoroughly with the chopped thyme, bay leaf, parsley and garlic
6. Add a dash of Cayenne and put in the frying pan with the onion
7. Add the bread which has been thoroughly squeezed, and mix all and fry about three minutes
8. Take off the fire and when cool form the mixture into cylindrical shapes of about two or three inches in length and one in thickness
9. Roll in grated bread crumbs and fry in boiling lard
10. Serve hot on a dish nicely garnished with parsley and sliced lemon

title: Crab Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- The Meat of 12 Crabs
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of White Pepper
- A Dash of Cayenne
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 12 Mushrooms, if desired
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the onion very fine and fry in one tablespoonful of butter until a golden brown
2. Add a tablespoonful of flour and moisten with a quarter of a pint of water, or oyster juice, till the sauce begins to thicken well
3. Season with the salt and pepper and a dash of Cayenne
4. Add the clove of garlic, finely minced, and the herbs
5. Add the crab meat, finely minced, and the mushrooms, if desired
6. Cook for a half hour in the saucepan, and then take off the fire and add the yolks of the eggs
7. Stir again for a moment, cool and roll into balls and use as desired

title: Crab Gombo, With Okra
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six large crabs
- a cup of lard
- a cup of cut onions
- a small bunch of parsley
- two tablespoonfuls of browned flour
- a quart of sliced okra
- a slice of lean ham
- a quart of good veal or beef stock
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a teaspoonful of black and red pepper

## Directions

1. Take six large crabs, throw them in cold water for a few moments.
2. When cool cut off the limbs—while they are living if possible, as this renders them more delicate; clean them, and put them to fry, shells and all, in a pot containing a cup of lard, a cup of cut onions, a small bunch of parsley, and two tablespoonfuls of browned flour.
3. Let them cook about fifteen minutes, and then pour on them two pints of boiling water and a quart of sliced okra;
4. let it all stew gently for half an hour, and add a slice of lean ham and a quart of good veal or beef stock (made by boiling two pounds of veal or beef in two quarts of water until reduced to a quart);
5. season with a teaspoonful of salt, and same of black and red pepper, and let all boil for half an hour.
6. This soup can be made in the oyster season by putting in a quart of oysters and two quarts of their liquor instead of the boiled beef stock.

title: Crab Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Hard-Shell or Soft-Shell Crabs
- 1 Onion
- 6 Large Fresh Tomatoes
- 2 Pints of Okra, or Fifty Counted
- 1/2 Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme or Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Two Level Spoons of Butter
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Scald the hard-shell crabs and clean according to recipe already given, taking off the dead man's fingers and the spongy substances
2. Remove the sandbags on the under part
3. Cut off the claws, crack and cut the body of the crab in quarters
4. Season nicely with salt and pepper
5. Put the lard into the pot, and when hot throw in the bodies and claws
6. Cover closely, and after five or ten minutes add the skinned tomatoes, chopped onions, thyme and parsley, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching
7. After five minutes add the okras, sliced fine
8. When well-browned without the semblance of scorching, add the bay leaf, chopped fine, and the juice of the tomatoes
9. Pour over about two quarts and a half of boiling water
10. Set back on the stove and let it simmer well for about an hour, having thrown in the pepper pod
11. When nearly ready to serve, season according to taste with Cayenne and added salt
12. Pour into a tureen and serve with boiled rice

title: Crab Jambalaya
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen fine, large crabs
- 1.5 cups of rice
- 3 quarts of broth
- 3 tomatoes
- 2 onions
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 2 sprigs of thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- 1/2 teaspoonful of chili pepper
- salt and black pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs
2. Cut in pieces, cutting the bodies into quarters
3. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in making shrimp jambalaya

title: Crab Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Crabs
- Sliced Stale Bread
- Butter
- Milk
- Cayenne
- Salt
- Chopped thyme
- Bay leaf
- Sliced lemon
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil according to recipe one dozen fine large crabs and pick out all the meat
2. Season nicely with salt, Cayenne and chopped thyme and bay leaf
3. Take stale bread and slice very thin
4. Lay in a little milk to moisten
5. Butter a baking pan and cover the bottom with a layer of bread
6. Put in a layer of crab meat and lay over at intervals slices of lemon cut very fine and thin
7. Dot here and there with bits of butter, and then spread over another layer of bread
8. Then another layer of crabs, and repeat till the meat is used up
9. Lay on top a thick sprinkling of bread crumbs, dotted with butter
10. Place in the oven and bake for about twenty minutes
11. Serve hot

title: Crab Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Fine Large Crabs
- 12 Celery Stalks
- 1 Dozen Olives
- A Mayonnaise Sauce
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cayenne
- Sliced hard-boiled eggs
- Sliced beets
- Asparagus
- Celery tips
- Parsley
- Sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs according to recipe
2. Clean and pick out all the meat
3. Season well with salt and pepper and a dash of Cayenne
4. Chop the celery fine and mix with the crab meat
5. Place on a dish in pyramidal shape and pour over nicely a Sauce a la Mayonnaise
6. Garnish tastefully with sliced hard-boiled eggs, sliced beets, asparagus or celery tips on top and around with sprigs of parsley and asparagus tips, with sliced lemon and sliced hard-boiled egg alternating

title: Crab Salad a la Mayonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of the Meat of Crab (hard shell)
- Mayonnaise
- Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Crisp Lettuce Leaves
- Sliced Beets
- Celery Tips

## Directions

1. Boil and pick crabs sufficient to give a pint of meat (See recipe for Boiling Crabs)
2. Season well with salt and pepper
3. Place in a dish, on a bed of crisp lettuce leaves, spreading over them the Mayonnaise Sauce, and garnish nicely with hard-boiled eggs, sliced beets and tips of celery

title: Crab Salad No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Crabs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of French Vinegar
- 1 Saltspoonful Salt
- 1 Head Lettuce
- Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Pick the boiled crabs carefully, keeping the pieces as large as possible
2. Lay in a salad bowl
3. Mix a dressing of two tablespoonfuls of sweet oil, one of French vinegar, one saltspoon of salt, black pepper and Cayenne, according to taste, and pour over the crabs which have been cooled in the ice box
4. Clean the lettuce well and put a row of crisp leaves around the edge of the salad dish
5. Garnish nicely with sliced lemon and hard-boiled eggs sliced

title: Crab Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 young fat crabs
- 12 fine, ripe tomatoes
- 1 large onion
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 spoonful of butter
- 2 spoonfuls of lard
- grated bread or crackers
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- sweet marjoram
- thyme
- half teaspoonful of lemon juice
- peel of a lemon
- water

## Directions

1. Open and cleanse the crabs of deadmen's fingers and sand
2. Cut them into 4 parts and extract the meat from the crabs and the fat from the top of the shells
3. Scald and skin 12 fine, ripe tomatoes
4. Squeeze the pulp from the seeds and juice and chop fine
5. Pour boiling water over seed and juice and strain it off, using it to make the soup with more water if required
6. Stew in soup pot one large onion and one clove of garlic in one spoonful of butter and two of lard
7. Put in the tomatoes and after stewing a few minutes, add the meat from the claws, then the crabs, and lastly the fat from the top shells
8. Sift over it grated bread or crackers
9. Season with salt, pepper, parsley, sweet marjoram, thyme, half teaspoonful of lemon juice and the peel of a lemon
10. Pour in water in which seeds were scalded and boil moderately for one hour

title: Crab Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen large crabs
- one tablespoon of lard
- two tablespoons of butter
- one large onion
- one clove of garlic
- one whole can of tomatoes, or 18 or 20 fresh ones
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper
- the rind of a lemon
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- about one-half dozen grated soda crackers
- boiling water
- fat from the crabs
- a little thyme
- parsley
- sweet marjoram

## Directions

1. Have ready one dozen large crabs; separate the fat from the back shell and bodies; cleanse them from the dead men's fingers
2. Boil the claws and pick the meat from them
3. Have ready a large stew-pan, into which put one tablespoonful of lard, two of butter, one large onion cut up into not very small pieces; one clove of garlic
4. Allow this to brown a little, then add one whole can of tomatoes, or 18 or 20 fresh ones
5. After they have stewed 10 minutes, add the meat from the crab claws, then the crabs
6. Season with salt and black and red pepper, the rind of a lemon, and one tablespoonful of the juice
7. Sift over this about one-half dozen grated soda crackers
8. Add boiling water to make the required quantity of soup, and allow it to boil moderately for two hours
9. A little while before serving, add the fat from the crabs and a little thyme, parsley and sweet marjoram

title: Crab Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Crabs
- 6 Ripe Tomatoes
- 1 Sprig Thyme
- 3 Sprigs Parsley
- 1 Sprig Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Clove Garlic
- 1 Teaspoonful Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Lard
- 1 Lemon
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne

## Directions

1. Cleanse the crabs thoroughly and extract all the meat from the body and claws
2. Scald and skin the tomatoes, and squeeze the pulp from the seeds and juice; chop very fine
3. Pour boiling water over the seeds and juice and strain
4. Chop the onion and garlic and stew with the tablespoonful of butter and lard
5. As they begin to brown, add the tomatoes, cover, and after simmering a few minutes, add the well-seasoned meat of the crab
6. Sift over this some grated bread or crackers and season with Cayenne, sweet marjoram and thyme
7. Pour in tomato water and add about a quart or more of water
8. Let it boil moderately for about an hour
9. Add the juice of two lemons and serve

title: Cracked Wheat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cracked Wheat
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 teaspoonful of Salt
- sugar and cream

## Directions

1. Soak the grain over night in a quart of cold water
2. In the morning set the kettle containing the grain in another kettle of cold water
3. Add the salt
4. Let it gradually heat and boil for at least an hour and a half
5. It should be thoroughly cooked
6. Serve with sugar and cream for breakfast

title: Cracker Anchovy Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Soda Crackers or Butter Crackers
- 2 Dozen Anchovies
- 1 Ounce of Anchovy Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the Anchovies into halves
2. Butter one side of six crackers with the Anchovy butter
3. Lay on this four Anchovies sliced in half
4. Cover each with a cracker
5. Place on a folded napkin on a dish and serve
6. A very pretty conceit at luncheons is to tie the crackers across and around with narrow green ribbon, making a delicate knot in the center

title: Crackers
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Rich Milk
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Gill of yeast
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Flour sufficient to make a light dough

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and add the butter, and let it grow tepid.
2. Then add the yeast and dissolve well.
3. Add the salt, and now add the flour sufficient to make a light dough.
4. Set to rise, and when twice its bulk work lightly, and roll out very thin, and cut into pieces about two and a half inches square.
5. Stick well with a fork through and through, and bake in a slow oven, as you would soda crackers.

title: Crackling Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Meal
- ½ Teaspoonful of Salt
- Cold water
- Cracklings

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 pint of meal, a half teaspoonful of salt, and cold water enough to make a thick batter.
2. Mix the cracklings, already fried, in the batter.
3. Pour a large tablespoonful at a time on a griddle.
4. Fry to a golden brown.
5. Serve crisp, requiring no butter or other accompaniment.

title: Cracksels
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Rich Milk
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Gill of Yeast
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and add the butter, and let it grow tepid.
2. Then add the yeast and dissolve well.
3. Add the salt, and now add the flour sufficient to make a light dough.
4. Set to rise, and when twice its bulk work lightly, and roll out very thin, and cut into pieces about two and a half inches square.
5. Stick well with a fork through and through, and bake in a slow oven, as you would soda crackers.

title: Cranberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Pints of Cranberries
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- Plain Pie Crust

## Directions

1. Cook the cranberries as for Cranberry Sauce
2. Do not strain the berries
3. Prepare the pie crust
4. Bake the lower crust, and fill with the fruit, and proceed as in general directions for making pies

title: Cranberry Pie or Tarts
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of cranberries
- a pint of water
- a pound of clean brown sugar
- corn starch

## Directions

1. Pick a quart of cranberries free from imperfections
2. Put a pint of water to them, and put them in a stew-pan over a moderate fire
3. Add a pound of clean brown sugar, and stew them gently until they are soft
4. Mash them with a silver spoon and turn them into a dish to become cold
5. Make them in pies or tarts
6. If they are strained and are too thin to jelly, add a spoonful of corn starch to thicken

title: Cranberry Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stew cranberries till soft; when soft, stir in sugar;
2. scald a few minutes and strain, or not, just as you please;
3. it is good either way.

title: Cranberry Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of cranberries
- one teacupful of water
- one teacupful of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and pick a quart of cranberries
2. Put them into a stew-pan, with a teacupful of water, and the same of brown sugar
3. Cover the pan and let them stew gently for one hour
4. Mash them smooth with a silver spoon
5. Dip a quart bowl in cold water, pour in the stewed cranberries, and leave till cold
6. Serve with roast pork, ham, turkey or goose

title: Cranberry Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Cranberries
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the cranberries in cold water and pick well, rejecting all those that float on top or are in any manner over-ripe and spoiled
2. Put them in a porcelain-lined saucepan with one pint of water
3. Let them boil over a moderate fire, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon and mashing the fruit as much as possible
4. When the berries have cooked about twenty minutes, remove the saucepan from the fire
5. Add sugar stirring in sufficient to sweeten nicely
6. Let them cook at least ten or fifteen minutes longer after adding the sugar
7. Put into an earthenware bowl and let the sauce cool
8. Never strain the sauce
9. Let them stand at least overnight or twenty-four hours in a cool place before serving
10. Serve with Roast Turkey

title: Cranberry Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pints of Cranberry Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 3/4 Ounce of Soluble Essence of Lemon

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Currant Syrup, adding twice the quantity of sugar, and adding the essence after the syrup has somewhat cooled and you are about to bottle it.

title: Cranberry Tarts with Apples
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of cranberries
- half a pound of sugar
- a spoonful of water
- a half dozen apples
- paste

## Directions

1. Mix half a pint of cranberries with half a pound of sugar and a spoonful of water
2. Let them simmer a little until soft
3. Peel and cut thin a half dozen apples
4. Put a rim of paste around a pie plate
5. Strew in the apples
6. Pour the cranberries over the apples and cover with a nice crust
7. Bake for an hour to cook the apples

title: Crapaudine Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of Sauce Piquante
- 3 chopped mushrooms
- 1 teaspoonful of dry mustard
- 2 teaspoonfuls of tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Put a half pint of very light Piquante Sauce on the fire
2. Add the mushrooms finely chopped and a teaspoonful of dry mustard which has been well diluted in two teaspoonfuls of tarragon vinegar
3. Let the sauce boil for five minutes and serve hot

title: Crawfish Baked à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Crawfish
- 2 Livers
- one-half Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Bouquet of Fine Herbs
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Boil according to recipe fifty large fine crawfish
2. When cooked, allow them to cool in their own water
3. Clean them, picking off the shells and leaving the crawfish whole
4. Take out all the smallest ones, and cut off the tail ends of the largest and place with the small ones
5. Take the remainder of the large crawfish and cut up, and make a dressing with two chopped livers, parsley, the minced contents of one-half can of mushrooms, the bouquet of fine herbs consisting of thyme, bay leaf, sweet marjoram, etc.
6. Chop a half dozen shallots and add to the dressing, and season highly with Cayenne and salt and black pepper to taste
7. Cut up the yolks of two eggs and mix with a cup of the soft portion of bread, which has been wet and thoroughly squeezed of all water
8. Place two tablespoonfuls of butter in a frying pan and add the dressing when it begins to heat; cook about ten minutes and then place in the dish in which the crawfish are to be served, making a bed of the dressing
9. Arrange with symmetry and grace the reserve crawfish upon this bed, cover lightly with the rest of the stuffing, and dot with small bits of butter, after sprinkling with grated bread crumbs
10. Pour over all a Cream Sauce and the juice of a lemon
11. Place in the oven and let it bake about ten or fifteen minutes, and serve with Cream Sauce, seasoned with lemon juice

title: Crawfish Bisque
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Dozen Fine Large Crawfish
- 3 Onions
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Bunch of Celery
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 6 Cloves
- 2 Blades of Mace
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Liquor
- A Dash of Cayenne
- 1 Seedless Cayenne Pod
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 cup of rice
- 1 cup of bread
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash about eight dozen fine, large crawfish thoroughly, being careful to cleanse of every particle of dust or sand
2. Set to boil in about a gallon of water
3. When boiled, take the fish out of the water and save the water
4. Pick out two dozen of the largest crawfish; pick out the inside of the tails and save the heads, cleansing them of every particle of meat
5. Set this meat to one side with the shells of the head
6. Pick the meat from the rest of the crawfish, saving all the shells
7. Take one large onion, a carrot, a bunch of celery, a sprig of thyme, one bay leaf, three sprigs of parsley, six cloves and two blades of mace, one clove of garlic; chop all very fine and put into the pot of water in which the crawfish were boiled
8. Add all the picked meat, except the reserved tails, and all the shells of the bodies and heads, except the reserved heads
9. Add one cup of rice and let it all boil till the mixture becomes thick and wishy
10. When it is well cooked, take it off the fire and mash the shells thoroughly, and the meat also, and strain all through a sieve
11. Take about a tablespoonful of butter and two quarts of oyster liquor and add this to the soup, seasoning to taste with Cayenne, salt and black pepper
12. Set to boil slowly
13. Chop an onion very fine and let it brown in a tablespoonful of butter
14. Squeeze thoroughly a cup of bread wet with water
15. When well squeezed, mix with a little milk, sufficient to make a paste, season to taste and mix with the well-seasoned crawfish meat
16. Chop another onion and put in melted butter, and add the crawfish stuffing, letting all fry about ten minutes, adding a finely-chopped sprig each of thyme and parsley and a bay leaf, and mixing thoroughly
17. Take off the fire and stuff the reserved heads of crawfish
18. Put on every stuffed head a dot of butter, and set in the oven and bake ten minutes
19. Place the stuffed heads in the tureen and pour the soup over
20. Serve hot with Croutons of buttered toast, passing the latter in a separate dish

title: Crawfish Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Crawfish
- A Sauce a la Mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Boil the crawfish, pick the meat out of the shells, heads and tails, break them into pieces, and prepare in exactly the same manner as Shrimp Salad

title: Crawfish Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Fine, Large Crawfish
- 1 1/2 Pounds of a Fillet of Veal
- 1 Slice of Ham
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Half Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Parsnip
- 1 Dozen Almonds
- 1 Dozen Allspice
- 4 Shallots
- 6 Tomatoes or a Half Can
- Croutons
- lard

## Directions

1. Wash the crawfish thoroughly over and over again to take away every particle of dust
2. Boil them in plain water and save the water
3. Take out the crawfish and take off all the shells, putting the meat aside
4. Pound the shells fine; pound one dozen almonds fine and mix thoroughly with the meat of the crawfish, and pound this in a mortar
5. Take one pound and a half of a fillet of veal and a slice of ham and cut in small pieces
6. Cut up the onions, carrots and parsnips
7. Put one tablespoonful of lard in a kettle, and when it begins to heat, add the herb bouquet (sweet basil, parsley, bay leaf), the onions, parsnip, shallots, clove of garlic chopped fine
8. As these brown, add the veal and ham
9. Add two tablespoonfuls of flour and butter rubbed, and the mushrooms, chopped finely
10. Let these simmer for about five minutes and then add the tomatoes, allspice and cloves
11. After ten minutes, when the mixture is well browned, add the pounded crawfish shells and the pounded meat and almonds
12. Pour over all the water from the boiled crawfish and set it back on the stove and let it simmer for about two hours
13. Skim off all the grease when near time for serving
14. Strain through a sieve, and serve with Croutons of toast, cut in slices, placed in the bottom of the tureen

title: Crayfish Bisque A La Creole
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cray-fishes
- bread
- onions
- salt
- black pepper
- egg
- garlic
- ham
- turnip
- carrot
- flour
- water
- tomatoes
- thyme
- sweet bay
- parsley
- rice
- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the cray-fishes, boil and drain them.
2. Separate the heads from the tails.
3. Clean out some of the heads, allowing two or three heads to each person.
4. Peel the tails.
5. Chop up a part of them, add to them some bread, onions, salt, black pepper and an egg or two.
6. With this dressing, stuff the heads that you have cleaned out.
7. Chop the claws and the parts adhering to them.
8. Fry a little garlic, onions, ham, one turnip, one carrot, and a little flour.
9. Add some water, the chopped claws, a few tomatoes, thyme, sweet bay, parsley and a little rice stirring often to avoid scorching.
10. When well boiled, strain through a colander.
11. After straining, put back to the fire and season to taste.
12. Put the stuffed heads into the oven until brown.
13. When ready to serve, put them and the tails in a soup dish and pour the soup over them.
14. Before serving, add a little butter and nutmeg, stirring until the butter is melted.

title: Crayfish Bisque a la Creole
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cray-fishes
- bread
- onions
- salt
- black pepper
- egg
- garlic
- ham
- turnip
- carrot
- flour
- water
- tomatoes
- thyme
- sweet bay
- parsley
- rice

## Directions

1. Wash the cray-fishes, boil and drain them
2. Separate the heads from the tails
3. Clean out some of the heads, allowing two or three heads to each person
4. Peel the tails
5. Chop up a part of them, add to them some bread, onions, salt, black pepper and an egg or two
6. With this dressing, stuff the heads that you have cleaned out
7. Chop the claws and the parts adhering to them
8. Fry a little garlic, onions, ham, one turnip, one carrot, and a little flour
9. add some water, the chopped claws, a few tomatoes, thyme, sweet bay, parsley and a little rice
10. stir often to avoid scorching
11. When well boiled

title: Crayfish Bisque. A Creole Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fifty crayfish
- one-third the quantity of bread soaked in milk
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a bunch of thyme
- two leaves of sage
- a small piece of garlic
- a chopped onion
- a teacup of water
- three spoonfuls of lard or butter
- a slice of ham or bacon
- two onions chopped fine
- plain beef stock

## Directions

1. Parboil the fish, pick out the meat, and mince or pound it in a mortar until very fine; it will require about fifty crayfish.
2. Add to the fish one-third the quantity of bread soaked in milk, and a quarter of a pound of butter, also salt to taste, a bunch of thyme, two leaves of sage, a small piece of garlic and a chopped onion.
3. Mix all well and cook ten minutes, stirring all the time to keep it from growing hard.
4. Clean the heads of the fish, throw them in strong salt and water for a few minutes and then drain them.
5. Fill each one with the above stuffing, flour them, and fry a light brown.
6. Set a clean stewpan over a slow fire, put into it three spoonfuls of lard or butter, a slice of ham or bacon, two onions chopped fine;
7. dredge over it enough flour to absorb the grease, then add a pint and a half of boiling water, or better still, plain beef stock.
8. Season this with a bunch of thyme, a bay leaf, and salt and pepper to taste.
9. Let it cook slowly for half an hour, then put the heads of the crayfish in and let them cook fifteen minutes.
10. Serve rice with it.

title: Crayfish-Broth for Purifying the Blood
author: Lafcadio Hearn

title: Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of the Finest White Icing Sugar
- 2 Ounces of Gum Arabic
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Gill of Any Essence Desired

## Directions

1. Soak two ounces of the finest Gum Arabic in a gill of hot water
2. Use two pounds of the finest icing sugar, the whites of 2 eggs, and a few drops of essence
3. Strain the soaked gum through a piece of muslin bag into a bowl, and add the essence to it
4. Fill in with as much icing as it will hold, till it forms a stiff, yet elastic body
5. Fill a biscuit forcer with the preparation and push it through upon a sheet of paper, well-dredged with sugar
6. With a small knife cut off the cream, as it is pressed out, into pieces of about the size of a filbert
7. Place them in the baking pan in the oven for a few minutes, merely to dry their surfaces
8. Dip these white balls into the chocolate icing, holding one at a time on the tip of the fork, so as to be able to turn it all around and ice each part
9. Put on a wire tray, set to dry in the oven for about ten minutes

title: Cream Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 pint sour cream
- flour
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- 1 cup new milk
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Beat very lightly 2 eggs
2. Stir them into 1 pint of sour cream
3. Sift in as much flour as will make a stiff batter
4. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of soda in a cup of new milk
5. Add this and a little salt
6. Mix them well and pour into rather a shallow pan
7. Bake quickly a nice brown
8. Serve hot

title: Cream Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of butter
- a half pint of water
- two cups of sifted flour
- five eggs well beaten
- a quarter of a spoonful of soda dry

## Directions

1. Boil a cup of butter with a half pint of water
2. while it is boiling, stir in two cups of sifted flour
3. let it cool
4. when cool, add five eggs well beaten, and a quarter of a spoonful of soda dry
5. Drop this mixture with a teaspoon on tins
6. bake in a quick oven

title: Cream Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 1 pint of milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Melt as much butter in a pint of milk as will make it as rich as cream
2. Make the flour into a paste with this
3. Knead it well
4. Roll it out frequently
5. Cut in squares
6. Bake on a griddle

title: Cream Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/2 pint of clotted cream
- a little salt
- 3/4 lb. of butter
- the yolk of egg for gilding

## Directions

1. Put the flour upon a pie-board
2. Make a hole in the middle, put in the clotted cream and a little salt
3. Mix the paste lightly
4. Let it stand for 1 hour
5. Add the butter
6. Roll it out 6 times, the same as puff paste
7. Form it into small cakes
8. Gild them with the yolk of egg
9. Bake in an oven

title: Cream Cakes with Filling
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of milk
- one-half a cup of flour
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and flour together
2. add the eggs and sugar
3. flavor the custard with lemon
4. take the first or outside cakes, and split each one gently
5. place in it the cream or custard, which must be cold before you introduce it
6. Put into each cake about a teaspoonful of the cream

title: Cream Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of light brown sugar
- one teacup of water
- two tablespoonfuls of butter
- one tablespoonful of vinegar
- two tablespoonfuls of flavoring extract

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of light brown sugar, one teacup of water, two tablespoonfuls of butter, one of vinegar, and two of flavoring extract
2. Dissolve the sugar in the water but do not stir it
3. Set it on to boil and let it boil briskly for twenty minutes
4. Try it by dropping a spoonful in a glass of cold water
5. If cooked enough to pull, butter some dishes and pour it into them
6. When cool enough to handle, pull it until it becomes as white as cream

title: Cream Doughnuts Without Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of cream sweet or sour
- five eggs
- a cup of sugar
- soda optional
- two tablespoonfuls of yeast powder or baking powder optional
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix cream, eggs and sugar
2. If the cream be sour, add soda to sweeten it; if sweet, put in two tablespoonfuls of yeast powder or any good baking powder
3. Add flour to mix
4. Roll out
5. Fry in boiling lard

title: Cream Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 2 Cups of Pulverized Sugar
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Rich Cream
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as in Frosting, only omit the cornstarch, and add an equal measure of rich cream
2. Flavor with Vanilla Extract
3. Boil the cream

title: Cream Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Sponge Cake
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Pulverized Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a layer sponge cake
2. Prepare a filling by beating together the yolks of two eggs, one cup of pulverized sugar, two tablespoonfuls of cream, and one teaspoonful of Vanilla
3. Beat and mix thoroughly to a thick cream, and spread between the layers, with a layer of cake on top

title: Cream Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 1/2 Teaspoonfuls of Yeast Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks of the eggs separately.
2. Then add the cream and salt, and finally the flour, into which you will have blended the yeast powder.
3. Beat well and fill the muffin ring or gem pans one-half full.
4. Place in a quick oven and bake for twenty minutes.

title: Cream of Asparagus Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Bunch of Asparagus
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Rich Cream
- 2 Even Tablespoonfuls of Flour or Corn Starch
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the Asparagus, tie it in a bunch and put in a saucepan of boiling water.
2. Let it boil gently for about three-quarters of an hour, or until perfectly tender.
3. Take it from the water, cut off the tips or points and put them aside until wanted.
4. Put the milk on to boil in a farina boiler.
5. Press the Asparagus stalks through a colander, and add them to the milk.
6. Rub the butter and cornstarch or flour together until perfectly smooth, and add to the boiling milk, stirring constantly till it thickens.
7. Add the Asparagus tops, salt and pepper, and serve without Croutons, as the Asparagus tips form a beautiful garnish.

title: Cream of Barley Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Barley, or a Half Cup
- 1 Pint of Cream or Milk
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 3 Quarts of Water (boiling)
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Scald the barley and then put into a kettle with three quarts of boiling water.
2. Let it boil about three hours.
3. Take it off and mash thoroughly, and strain through a sieve.
4. Add the hot milk to the stock of the barley, season with salt and pepper, and let it come to a boil.
5. Take off and add the yolks of two eggs.

title: Cream of Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Cup of Butter
- ¾ Cup of Powdered White Sugar
- ¼ Tablespoonfuls of Milk or Cream
- The Juice and Rind of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a thick cream, and add the milk or cream gradually
2. Place the mixture in a bowl and stand in a small tin of boiling water (bain-marie) and stir slowly till the sauce is very creamy, but do not allow it to simmer or boil
3. It will take just three or four minutes
4. As the thick cream forms, take from the fire, add the lemon and serve with any pudding

title: Cream of Celery Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery Stalks
- 2 Quarts of Milk or Cream
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Small Onion
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the celery and onion and cut into fine pieces.
2. Then place them in a porcelain-lined saucepan and let boil for about a half hour.
3. Take off and mash, and press through a colander.
4. Set the milk to boil in a farina boiler, and as it heats well, add to it the water and celery that have been pressed.
5. Rub smoothly together the flour and butter, and then stir into the boiling soup, stirring constantly till it thickens to a cream of the right consistency.
6. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve hot.
7. It is very delicious served with slices of delicately toasted and buttered Croutons.
8. Serve on a separate dish and garnish with sprigs of parsley and slices of hard-boiled eggs.

title: Cream of Corn Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn
- 2 Level Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Butter
- Milk
- Buttered toast

## Directions

1. Slit the corn in two and grate from the cobs.
2. Put the cobs into boiling water and let them boil slowly about an hour, till the water is reduced to three quarts.
3. Take the cobs out and drain over the kettle.
4. Add the corn and let it boil till very soft, which will require about thirty minutes.
5. Take the soup off and press all through a sieve.
6. Season highly and set back to simmer gently, adding the flour and butter, thoroughly rubbed together.
7. Stir constantly till the soup thickens, and then add the boiling milk.
8. Cook a moment only, take off the fire, stir in the beaten yolks and serve hot with buttered toast cut in dice shape.

title: Cream of Corn Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Grated Corn
- 3 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Pint of Hot Milk or Cream
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Level Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Slit the corn in two and grate from the cobs.
2. Put the cobs into the boiling water and let them boil slowly about an hour, till the water is reduced to three quarts.
3. Then take the cobs out and drain over the kettle.
4. Add the corn and let it boil till very soft.
5. This will require about thirty minutes.
6. Take the soup off and press all through a sieve.
7. Season highly and set back to simmer gently, adding, in the meanwhil, the flour and butter, thoroughly rubbed together.
8. Stir constantly till the soup thickens, and then add the boiling milk.
9. Cook a moment only, take off the fire, stir in the beaten yolks of eggs.

title: Cream of Onion Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Onions
- 3/4 Ounce of Butter
- 1 1/2 Pints of Cream
- 2 Ounces of Flour
- 2 Pints of Boiling Water
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and boil in salted water until very tender; then drain and dry well with a cloth; put them on the fire in a saucepan, with one ounce of butter; add the other ingredients, except the remaining half ounce of butter.
2. When the soup comes to a boil, press through the sieve, and return to the fire; add the remainder of the butter and serve.

title: Cream of Rice Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Pint of Milk
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Pepper and Salt to Taste
- Cream
- Croutons or Crackers

## Directions

1. Wash the Rice thoroughly, rubbing dry.
2. Put it into a saucepan with one quart of cold water; when swelled add one quart of boiling water, and when it begins to get very tender add the remaining quart of boiling water.
3. Add the pepper and salt.
4. Take from the fire, mash the rice well and rub all through a sieve.
5. Beat up the yolks of the eggs well with a few tablespoonfuls of cream.
6. When quite smooth stir in carefully a few spoons of the boiling rice water, and then pour the eggs and cream or milk into the saucepan with the rice, which you will have returned to the stove.
7. Mix briskly and then draw aside and stir for two or three minutes, being very careful not to allow the mixture to boil when once the eggs will have been added.
8. Serve hot with Croutons or Crackers.

title: Cream of Roses
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 pounds of rose leaves
- 2 quarts of brandy
- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 quart of water
- a few drops of cochineal

## Directions

1. Pound the rose leaves in a stone vessel, and then pour over the brandy, and infuse for six days
2. Distill the liquor by boiling to one-half in a bain-marie
3. Mix it thoroughly with one pound of sugar, dissolved in a quart of water or in rose water
4. Color with a few drops of cochineal, and filter and bottle for use

title: Cream of Spinach Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a Peck or Four Pints of Spinach
- 2 Ounces of Fresh Butter
- Two Quarts of Oyster Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Granulated Sugar
- 1/4 of a Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Wash and boil one-half peck, or four pints of Spinach; this quantity will measure about one pint when cooked, chopped and pounded into a fine paste.
2. Put it into a stew pan with four ounces of fresh butter, the grated nutmeg and a teaspoonful of salt.
3. Let it cook for ten minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Add two quarts of oyster juice (or other than fast days consommé may be used, or good bouillon).
5. Let all boil up, and then press through a strainer.
6. Set it over the fire again and just at the boiling point mix with it a tablespoonful of butter and a teaspoonful of granulated sugar.
7. Serve hot with Croutons.

title: Cream of Tartar Biscuit for Breakfast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of sifted flour
- three teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- one of soda
- a little salt
- two large spoonfuls of shortening
- warm water or milk

## Directions

1. To one quart of sifted flour, add three teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar and one of soda mixed in the flour, and a little salt
2. Add two large spoonfuls of shortening
3. Mix soft with warm water or milk
4. Bake

title: Cream of Tomato Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Pure Tomato Juice
- 1 Gill of Rice
- 3 Onions
- 8 Allspice
- 4 Cloves
- A Sprig of Thyme
- A Pinch of Sugar to Taste
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Stew the Tomatoes for about two hours, and then extract the Juice.
2. Add the other ingredients, and boil for about an hour and a half; then strain.
3. The rice, being creamy, should now make the soup as thick as cream.
4. Serve with Croutons or Quenelles.

title: Cream of Vanilla Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonfuls of Vanilla Essence
- ¾ Gill of Maraschino or Good Cognac

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar well together, and add the vanilla essence
2. Beat thoroughly
3. Boil the milk and add the beaten egg to the milk as it comes to a boil
4. Stir briskly till it comes to a boil once more
5. Take from the fire and add the maraschino or brandy according to taste

title: Cream of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Chopped Veal
- 1 Cup of Chopped Ham
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 1/2 Cup of Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Utilize the left-over filet of veal and chop very fine
2. Add one cup of minced ham
3. Prepare a Cream Sauce in the proportions to make just a half cup
4. Season the chopped veal with this, add a dash of Cayenne and grated nutmeg
5. Spread upon hot buttered toast and serve at breakfast

title: Cream or Milk Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Bread
- 1 Pint of Hot Cream or Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Toast the bread nicely, and butter well on both sides.
2. Lay in a dish, and pour over hot milk.
3. Serve hot.

title: Cream Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Fine White Confectionery Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonfull of Vinegar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cream of Tartar
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Moisten the sugar with the water and set to boil
2. Add vinegar and Cream of Tartar
3. Let it boil till brittle
4. Then take from the fire and add the Lemon Extract
5. Turn quickly on a wet slab or buttered plates
6. When it begins to cool, pull till very, very white, and then cut into small squares of about a half inch

title: Cream Peanuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Fine White Sugar
- 1 Teacupful of Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Extract of Vanilla
- 3/4 Pound of Peanuts, Shelled

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water till it comes to a thread
2. Flavor highly with extract of vanilla
3. Take from the fire and stir until it is a creamy white
4. Throw in the peanuts
5. Drop in a little white granulated sugar
6. Put them on a table on a piece of white paper and shake well till each stands apart
7. Roll again lightly in white powdered or granulated sugar and let them dry

title: Cream Pecans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Pecans
- The White of an Egg
- ½ Pound of Finest White Confectionery Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pecans in halves, being careful not to break the meat.
2. Take the white of an egg and beat well with its weight in water till it forms a cream.
3. Work in with your fingers the finest white confectionery sugar till it forms a smooth paste.
4. Take a small piece of this paste, roll it, and put it between two halves of the pecans, and then lightly roll in the paste, flattening the pecan somewhat.
5. The coating outside must be very, very light, so that the delicate brown of the pecan meat shows through.
6. Set the pecans to dry, and serve on dainty china saucers, setting a saucer to each guest.

title: Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- Whites of Four Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- ¾ Cup Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Essence or Lemon Juice

## Directions

1. Blend the flour and butter well together
2. When light add the milk gradually
3. Add the essence of vanilla or the juice of a lemon
4. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Stir them well into the boiling mixture
6. Fill the pie pans, which you have filled with crust, baked according to directions
7. Place in the oven and bake until a nice brown
8. Serve very cold

title: Cream Puddings
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Eggs
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Pint Flour
- 1 Pint Milk
- 1 Grated Lemon
- 1 Pint of Rich Cream
- A Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and the grated lemon.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs well, and add to the mixture.
3. Then add the flour, well-sifted; beat light, and add one pint of milk and one pint of rich cream.
4. Beat the whites to a stiff froth and add.
5. Bake a half hour in a buttered dish.
6. Serve with Lemon Sauce.

title: Cream Puffs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of water
- 1/2 cup of butter
- 1 cup of sifted flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup of milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cornstarch or fine flour
- cold milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Put 1 cup of water on the fire
2. When hot, melt in 1/2 cup of butter
3. When it boils, beat in 1 cup of sifted flour
4. If lumpy, stir till perfectly smooth
5. Set it aside to cool
6. When cold, stir in, one at a time, three eggs without first beating them
7. Drop the batter on tins in small spoonfuls
8. Bake 1/2 hour in a quick oven
9. For the cream: put 1 cup of milk to boil
10. Beat the yolks of 2 eggs very stiff
11. Add 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
12. When the milk boils, pour it on the eggs, stirring all the time
13. Wet 2 tablespoonfuls of cornstarch or fine flour with a little cold milk
14. Add it to the custard and return it to the fire
15. Stir until quite thick
16. When cold, flavor with vanilla
17. The cream is put in the puff by lifting the top with a knife and dropping in the cream with a spoon

title: Cream Puffs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of water
- 1 cup of best butter
- 1 cup of sifted flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups of milk
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cornstarch
- fine flour
- cold milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Put 1 cup of water on the fire
2. When hot melt in 1 cup of best butter
3. When it boils beat in 1 cup of sifted flour
4. If lumpy, stir until perfectly smooth
5. Place it aside to cool
6. When cool, stir in 1 at a time 3 eggs without first beating them
7. Drop the batter on tins in small spoonfuls
8. Bake 1 hour in quick oven
9. For the cream put 1 1/2 cups of milk to boil
10. Beat yolks of 2 eggs very light with 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
11. When the milk boils, pour it on the eggs, stirring all the while
12. Wet 2 tablespoonfuls of cornstarch in fine flour with a little cold milk
13. Add it to the custard and replace on the fire
14. Stir until quite thick
15. Flavor when cold with vanilla
16. Place the cream in the puff by lifting up the top with a knife and dropping in the cream with a small spoon

title: Cream Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1/2 Cup of Butter
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Set the water to boil, and while boiling, stir in the butter
2. Add the flour, and stir continually till the paste leaves the sides of the saucepan
3. Set the mixture to cool
4. When cool, stir one after another, the three eggs in, without beating them
5. After adding the last egg, beat very vigorously for about four minutes
6. Drop by tablespoonfuls on buttered tins, and set in the oven to bake from twenty to thirty minutes
7. Watch carefully so that they will not burn
8. Rub the cornstarch in a little water and add it to the boiling milk
9. Let it boil three minutes, stirring constantly
10. Beat the eggs, without separating, and the sugar till light, and add to the boiling milk
11. Add the vanilla, stirring all well
12. When cold make an opening inside, through the side of the paste, with a sharp knife, and fill it with the custard
13. Fill the cakes and set away to cool

title: Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 2 gills of fresh milk or cream
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in the saucepan
2. Add the flour gradually, letting it blend without browning in the least
3. Add the boiling milk or cream and stir without ceasing
4. Add salt and white pepper to taste
5. Serve immediately with boiled fish

title: Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Sweet, Fresh Cream
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Nutmeg, Grated
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla, or Sherry or Madeira

## Directions

1. Add the sugar essence or wine to the cream
2. Stir constantly till thoroughly dissolved
3. Beat vigorously, and then add the nutmeg and set to cool
4. Serve with stewed apples and other stewed fruits, or fruit puddings

title: Cream Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of a pound of butter
- one small tablespoonful of wheat flour
- one teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- one teaspoonful of young onions, or eschalots, chopped fine
- one saltspoonful of salt
- one saltspoonful of pepper
- one grated nutmeg
- one glass of cream, or rich milk

## Directions

1. Put a quarter of a pound of butter in a stew-pan, with a small tablespoonful of wheat flour, a teaspoonful of chopped parsley, and the same of young onions, or eschalots, chopped fine
2. Add a saltspoonful of salt, and the same of pepper, and a grated nutmeg
3. Mix these well together
4. Add a glass of cream, or rich milk
5. Set it over the fire, and stir it with a silver spoon until it is ready to boil; if it is too thick, add more milk
6. This sauce should be stirred for fifteen minutes

title: Cream Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pints of rich cream
- 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- powdered nutmeg
- a dozen bitter almonds (slightly broken up) or a dozen peach leaves

## Directions

1. Boil 1½ pints of rich cream with 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, some powdered nutmeg, and a dozen bitter almonds slightly broken up, or a dozen peach leaves
2. When boiled, take it off the fire and strain it
3. If it is to be eaten with boiled pudding or with dumplings, send it to table hot
4. Let it get cold if you intend it as an accompaniment to fruit pies or tarts

title: Cream Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Cream
- 2 Cups of Flour
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Essence

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar to a cream
2. Add the cream, and gradually add the flour and baking powder, sifted
3. Add the whites of the eggs, beat lightly, and bake in a quick oven thirty minutes

title: Cream-Iced Meringues With Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sifted White Sugar
- The Whites of 12 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence
- 3 Ounces of Currant, Orange or Any Preserves
- 1/2 Cup of Transparent Icing

## Directions

1. Prepare as in Creole Meringues recipe.
2. Spread some preserves thinly inside the Meringues before garnishing them with the cream.
3. Ice with Transparent Icing.

title: Creamed Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of tender white cabbage
- A cream sauce

## Directions

1. Take a fine delicate head of white cabbage
2. Cut it in quarters and soak in cold water for an hour
3. Remove all the hard parts and cut the remainder into fine shreds
4. Put it into the stewing pan and pour over boiling water enough to cover
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Let it boil at least thirty minutes and then drain in a colander
7. Have ready a heated dish
8. Turn the cabbage into this and pour over a cream sauce

title: Creamed Celery
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cut celery
- 1 cupful of milk or cream
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut fresh, crisp celery into pieces of an inch, until you have a pint.
2. Wash thoroughly, and put in boiling water and cook until tender, which will be in about twenty-five minutes or a half hour.
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan, and add the flour, blending without allowing to brown.
4. When perfectly smooth, add the cream or milk, and let it come to a boil.
5. Add salt and pepper to taste.
6. Drain out the celery, and add it to the sauce, and stir gently, letting it cook twenty minutes longer.
7. Serve hot. The dish should be kept covered while cooking.

title: Creamed Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Select a fine one-year-old chicken and clean, singe and boil according to the above recipe, first having cut in joints, however.
2. In boiling always simply cover the chicken with water, otherwise you will have chicken soup, all the nutriment of the chicken being absorbed by the soup.
3. When cooked for an hour or longer, if the chicken is now very tender, take out of the saucepan and place in a dish and pour over a Cream Sauce, and serve.

title: Creamed Codfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Picked Codfish
- 1 Pint and a Half of Cream
- The Yolk of 1 Egg
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Soak the codfish overnight, and then let it boil about forty minutes.
2. Take off and scald again and drain, and again scald and drain, allowing it to stand each time about four or five minutes before changing the water.
3. Put one large tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan; when melted add the flour and blend, without browning.
4. Add the milk, stirring constantly until it boils, and then add the fish, seasoning highly with pepper and Cayenne.
5. Let it boil about ten minutes longer, and take off the fire.
6. Add the yolk of an egg which has been beaten thoroughly.

title: Creamed Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Finely Ground Coffee
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The yolks of 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Boil two ounces of finely ground and powdered coffee in one pint of milk.
2. Add one-half pint of cream as it begins to boil.
3. Add the yolks of three eggs, which have been well beaten with four ounces of powdered sugar.
4. Let it reduce one-half.
5. Take off and strain till clear.
6. Let it cool and serve.

title: Creamed Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Fine Ears of Corn
- 1 Pint of Milk Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/2 Spoonful of Black Pepper
- 1 Dessertspoonful Butter

## Directions

1. Score the corn down the center of each row of grains and then cut from the cob
2. With the knife press out all the pulp from the cob, leaving the hull on the cob
3. Set a porcelain or agate saucepan on the fire
4. Put into this the corn cobs, which you will have cut into pieces
5. Cover with water and let them boil until you have extracted all the juices
6. When the liquid is reduced to about one pint, add the corn and let it boil for about twenty minutes
7. Stir in a quarter of a pint of milk, season with salt and pepper to taste
8. Add a teaspoonful of butter and serve hot

title: Creamed Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Small, White Onions
- 1 Tablespoonfal Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- ½ Pint of Milk or Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the onions as directed in the Boiled Onions recipe.
2. When very tender, take off the fire and drain.
3. Put one tablespoonful of flour into a saucepan, and add a tablespoonful of butter.
4. Set on the fire, and let all blend well together, rubbing very smooth, without browning.
5. Then add half a pint of milk.
6. Stir continually till it boils.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and pour over the onions, and serve hot.

title: Creamed Pineapple
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large, ripe pineapples
- powdered loaf sugar
- rich cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut into 4 pieces 2 large, ripe pineapples
2. Stand them up successively in a deep dish, and grate them from the rind
3. When all is grated, transfer it to a large glass bowl, and make it very sweet by mixing it with powdered loaf sugar
4. Whip to a stiff froth a sufficiency of rich cream, adding to it some sugar, and heap it high upon the grated pineapple

title: Creamed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Cream Sauce
- Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes whole.
2. Pour over them a Cream Sauce, remembering always to keep them whole.
3. Add the juice of a lemon.
4. Serve hot with fried chicken.

title: Creamed Strawberries
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, large, ripe strawberries
- powdered sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take fine, large, ripe strawberries; hull, or stem them, and set them on ice till just before they are wanted
2. Put a large quantity of powdered sugar in a bowl with a layer of strawberries and sugar, until the bowl is full
3. Have ready a whipped cream, which must be placed on the top of the fruit

title: Creamed Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Cut large turnips in quarters for this dish.
2. Small ones may be boiled whole.
3. When tender, drain and put into a colander and press out all water.
4. Make a rich Cream Sauce and pour over the turnips.
5. Serve with boiled leg of mutton, either by placing the turnips around the leg of mutton on the dish and pouring the sauce over, or separate, as a vegetable.

title: Creamed-Iced Meringues
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sifted White Sugar
- The Whites of 12 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence
- 3 Ounces of Stiff Whipped Cream
- 1/2 Cup of Transparent Icing

## Directions

1. Make Meringues much smaller than ordinary Meringues—about the size of a bird's egg.
2. Proceed as in Creole Meringues recipe.
3. Fill with stiff, whipped cream, taking great care when filling that none of the cream shall ooze from between the two halves of the Meringues after they have been stuck together.
4. If cream oozes, wipe it off very carefully.
5. Hold each Meringue separately on a silver fork, and dip them all over in Transparent Icing, and let them dry.

title: Creole Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Grated Lemon
- 4 Ounces of Orange Flower Marmalade
- 4 Ounces of Apricot or Peach Marmalade
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 3 Ounces of white pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the fruits and lemon thoroughly together.
2. Add three ounces of white pulverized sugar and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Place the mixture in small cakes on white paper.
4. Bake lightly.
5. Cover with sugar beaten with the white of an egg, in meringue form.
6. Bake to a light brown.

title: Creole Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Grated or Minced Boiled Ham
- 1 Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Peeled Tomato
- 1 Minced Green Pepper
- 6 Slices of Buttered Toast
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Ounces of Parmesan Cheese
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Grate and mince only the lean portion of ham till you have a cupful
2. Put this in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter
3. Add the finely minced onion and garlic
4. Let this cook for three minutes
5. Add the finely-cut tomato and minced green pepper
6. Season to taste with salt and pepper

title: Creole Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Grated or Minced Boiled Ham
- 1 Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Peeled Tomato
- 1 Minced Green Pepper
- 6 Slices of Buttered Toast
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Ounces of Parmesan Cheese
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Grate and mince only the lean portion of ham till you have a cupful.
2. Put this in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter, and add the finely minced onion and garlic.
3. Let this cook for three minutes, and then add the finely-cut tomato and minced green pepper.
4. Season to taste with salt and pepper; add a dash of Cayenne, and let the preparation stew down dry.
5. Spread on strips of buttered toast and dredge with grated Parmesan cheese.
6. Set in the oven in the dish in which it is to be served and bake for five minutes and send to the table hot.

title: Creole Coconut Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Pound of Grated Coconut
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- ¾ Pound of White Powdered Sugar
- Whites of 6 Eggs
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Vanilla
- 1 Tablespoonful of Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter to a light cream, and then add the wine and the essence of vanilla
2. Gradually add the coconut, beating in as lightly as possible
3. Add the scalded milk, which has been allowed to cool
4. Finally beat in lightly the whites of the eggs, which have been whipped to a very stiff froth
5. Do this latter with a few swift strokes, so that the coconut may stand out fresh and flaky, like snow
6. Bake in pie crusts which have already been set in the oven
7. Serve cold, sprinkling over white powdered sugar

title: Creole Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cloves and Cinnamon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract
- 2 Cups of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Cup of Sweet Milk
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Soft Batter

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the milk and the baking powder, and gradually add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Beat the batter hard for ten minutes, after adding sufficient flour to make a nice soft batter
4. Pour into baking tins, and bake to a delicate brown
5. When cold ice with Plain Icing

title: Creole Fig Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Figs
- 3/4 Pound of Grated Bread Crumbs
- 1 Teacupful of Milk
- 2 1/2 Ounces of Sugar
- 3 Ounces of Butter
- 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the figs fine, and beat the sugar and the yolks of the eggs to a thick cream.
2. Add the melted butter and the bread crumbs, which have been wet and squeezed thoroughly.
3. Add the milk and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth.
4. Butter a mold and sprinkle with flour.
5. Steam lightly for three hours.
6. Serve with a Hard, Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Creole Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's Liver
- A Slice of Pork Fat
- 1 Onion
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take calf's liver and pork fat, in the proportions of two-thirds liver and one-third fat
2. Grind both together very, very fine
3. Mince an onion, and two sprigs each of thyme and parsley, and one bay leaf, and mix with the ground meat
4. Add a half teaspoonful of grated nutmeg and salt and pepper to taste
5. Mix well
6. Put one tablespoonful of hot butter in a frying pan and throw in the chopped meat
7. Let all blend well together without cooking for about two minutes, stirring all the time
8. Take the mixture off, and, when it cools, form into little balls about the size and shape of a pecan
9. Roll these in flour, and then parboil in boiling water that has been well seasoned with pepper and salt
10. Place around the meat and pour the sauce over and serve hot

title: Creole French Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Tablespoonfuls of the Best Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- 1 Teaspoonful Mustard
- The Yolk of a Hard-Boiled Egg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Blend the oil and salt and pepper in the manner above indicated, and then add these to the mustard drop by drop, alternating with the vinegar.
2. When well blended add the well-mashed yolk of a hard-boiled egg.
3. Stir well, and serve with lettuce, celery or potato salad.

title: Creole Jambalaya
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and a half cups of rice
- 1 pound of fresh pork
- 1 slice of ham
- 1 dozen fine chaurice (pork sausage)
- 2 onions
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 2 sprigs of thyme
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 cloves ground very fine
- 3 quarts of beef broth or hot water
- 1/2 spoonful of chili pepper
- salt, pepper and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the pork very fine, lean and fat, into pieces of about half an inch square
2. Chop the onions very fine, and mince the garlic and fine herbs
3. Grind the cloves
4. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the saucepan, and add the onions and pork, and let them brown slowly
5. Stir frequently, and let them continue browning slightly
6. When slightly brown, add the ham, chopped very fine, and the cloves of garlic
7. Add the minced herbs, thyme, bay leaf and parsley and cloves
8. Let all brown for five minutes longer
9. Add a dozen fine chaurice, cut apart, and let all cook five minutes longer
10. Add the three quarts of water or broth
11. Let it all cook for ten minutes
12. When it comes to a boil add the rice, which has been carefully washed
13. Add a half teaspoonful of chili pepper, and salt and cayenne to taste
14. Let all boil for a half hour longer, or until the rice is firm
15. Serve hot
16. Stir often, to mix all well

title: Creole Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Ounces of Shredded Almonds
- 4 Ounces of Ground Almonds
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 2 Eggs
- 4 Ounces of Wheat Flour
- The Zest of 2 Oranges

## Directions

1. Blanch and wash and dry the almonds
2. Grind four ounces
3. Shred twelve ounces by cutting the almonds lengthwise into thin shreds
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the whites separately, and then beat them together with the sugar, rubbing till very light and smooth
5. Add the grated zest of two Louisiana oranges, that has been rubbed on sugar
6. Incorporate all the other ingredients
7. Roll the paste out into balls about the size of an egg
8. Place them on buttered wafer paper on a baking sheet
9. Set in a moderate oven, and bake to a light color

title: Creole Meringues
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Sifted White Sugar
- The Whites of 12 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the whites to a stiff, snowy froth, and then gradually add the sugar and vanilla, beating until the mixture is stiff enough to cut with a knife.
2. Cut stout sheets of foolscap paper into strips measuring at least two inches in width, or place the sheet of paper, slightly buttered, on the table.
3. Take a tablespoon and gather up the meringue mixture as nearly as possible in the shape of an egg, and drop slopingly from the spoon upon the strips of paper at a distance of at least an inch apart.
