4. Draw the edge of the spoon very sharply around the edge of the meringue, to give it a smooth, round shape, resembling an egg.
5. Sprinkle sifted sugar over them, and let them remain in this state for about three minutes.
6. Take hold of the strips at either end, and shake lightly, to remove all excess of sugar.
7. Place the strips in rows about one inch apart on the wet board or baking tin, and as soon as the sugar begins to dissolve slightly on their surface, push them into the oven, which must be at a very moderate heat, and bake them a very light fawn color.
8. When the meringues are done, remove each one very carefully and separately from the paper.
9. If you desire to fill the meringues, scoop out the soft white part, and smooth the interior of the meringues.
10. Place them with the rounded side downward on the baking tin in the oven to dry for a few minutes.
11. When thoroughly crisp, without browning, place them between sheets of paper in a dry place.
12. When using meringues for desserts, garnish the interior with whipped cream, beaten to a stiff froth, and flavored with vanilla, orange, lemon, orange flower water, or any kind of fancy liqueur, stick the two halves together lightly and serve.

title: Creole Mustard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Creole Mustard
- 1 Pint of White Wine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Cloves
- 1 Tablespoonful of Celery Seed
- 1 Tablespoonful of Ground Allspice
- 2 Blades of Ground Mace
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Cider and Tarragon Vinegar in equal parts

## Directions

1. Put a pint of white wine into a clean saucepan
2. Add the mixed ground spice, crushed clove of garlic, celery seeds and salt
3. Steep on the rear part of the stove for one hour
4. Mix the mustard, ground to a flour, into a smooth paste with equal parts of cider and Tarragon Vinegar
5. Strain out the spices from the wine and add to the paste while boiling hot
6. Mix thoroughly and bottle for use

title: Creole Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fine, ripe tomatoes
- 2 onions
- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of minced ham
- 1/2 clove of garlic
- bread crumbs
- salt, pepper and Cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Scald and skin six fine, ripe tomatoes and chop them fine
2. Chop two onions and mince the garlic very fine
3. Add a large spoonful of bread crumbs
4. Fry them with a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan till quite brown
5. Add the tomatoes and salt, pepper and Cayenne to taste
6. Let all stew for at least an hour
7. Prepare the eggs as for Ham Omelet
8. When the tomatoes are quite done have ready a heated frying pan and a half tablespoonful of butter
9. Pour this into the pan
10. As they become set pour in the center the tomatoes
11. Fold the omelet over and cook for two minutes longer
12. Roll gently into a dish and serve hot

title: Creole Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cupfuls of Orange Juice
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Cupful of Fine Old Port Wine
- % Cupful of Brandy
- 2 Small Cupfuls of Sugar
- 2 Ripe, Delicate-Skinned Lemons

## Directions

1. Wash the lemons and dry, and then slice them very thin, without grating or squeezing into a bowl
2. Take the sugar and water and make a boiling syrup, and pour over the lemons, and let the whole mixture cool
3. Add the orange juice
4. Take out the slices of lemon, and save them for later use
5. Strain the punch into an ice cream freezer, add the slices of lemon and liqueurs, and freeze as hard as possible for three or four minutes
6. Take a wooden spoon and detach the frozen punch from the sides of the freezer and from the bottom, mix in with the inner liquid mixture, and cover and freeze again for three or four minutes
7. Repeat this four or five times
8. Serve in punch glasses

title: Creole Salpicon
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Crawfish
- 3 Tomatoes
- 1 Dozen Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Pint of Boiling Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 12 Whole Black Peppers

## Directions

1. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter into a saucepan and add the flour.
2. Stir constantly for about five minutes.
3. Then moisten well with the milk, pouring it in gradually and then whisking.
4. Add the grated nutmeg, salt to taste, and the twelve whole peppers.
5. Add a herb bouquet, minced fine, and let all cook for a quarter of an hour.
6. Then rub well through a sieve and put into a saucepan with the tomatoes, skinned and chopped fine, and the meat of the crawfish cut into dice-shaped pieces, and the minced mushrooms.
7. Let all cook for five minutes and serve.

title: Creole Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful Worcestershire sauce
- ½ teaspoonful cayenne pepper
- 1 ounce butter
- 2 tomatoes
- 1 tablespoonful onions
- 1 tablespoonful parsley

## Directions

1. Chop the tomatoes, onions and parsley very fine
2. Boil all together 10 minutes
3. Strain

title: Creole Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tomatoes
- 6 Shallots
- 1 Chopped Sweet Pepper
- 1 Glass Sherry
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Make a Tomato Sauce quite brown.
2. Add the chopped shallots and sweet pepper, and when these are browned, add one wine glass full of Sherry wine, seasoning highly.
3. Serve with meats.

title: Creole Timbales
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Raw Chicken Breasts
- 1 Cup of Bread, Soaked in Milk
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Yolks of Four Eggs
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Veloute Sauce
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 6 Plain Pancakes
- 1 Gill Madeira Sauce

## Directions

1. Cut up into medium-sized pieces two raw chicken breasts; pound them in a mortar with the same quantity of bread soaked in milk, a half tablespoonful of fresh butter and the yolks of four eggs.
2. Season with salt and pepper and a dash of Cayenne; mix all well together.
3. Mix in a bowl with three tablespoonfuls of Veloute Sauce.
4. Butter the half dozen small timbale molds and line them with plain pancake.
5. Fill the molds with the chicken mixture and cover with small round pieces of pancake.
6. Steam them in a moderate oven for about ten minutes.
7. Take out of the oven and remove the timbales from the mold; garnish a hot dish nicely with sprigs of parsley, place the timbales upon it.
8. Pour over a gill of hot Madeira Sauce and send to the table hot.

title: Creole Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Ounces of Flour
- 8 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- 8 Eggs
- 1 Wineglassful of Noyau
- 1 Pint Whipped Cream
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- A Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and yolks of the eggs to a cream.
2. Add the Noyau, salt, Vanilla and flour, and incorporate thoroughly till light.
3. Add the whites of the eggs and the cream.
4. Mix all lightly together, taking great care that all are well-mixed.
5. Heat the wafer irons over a clear charcoal fire.
6. When hot brush inside with a little clarified butter.
7. Pour a large spoonful of butter into the under-sheet of the wafer irons, then close in.
8. Bake to a golden brown color.
9. Sprinkle lightly with sugar.

title: Cress Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- Cress
- Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as lettuce, washing and bringing to the table firm and crisp
2. In this salad use for dressing only Tarragon vinegar, salt and pepper to taste

title: Cromesquies with Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Roasted Chicken
- 2 Truffles
- 1 Pint of Veloute Sauce
- 6 Pieces of Crepinette
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Roast the chicken, then bone and hash the meat.
2. Make a pint of Veloute Sauce; chop the truffles very fine and place the chicken and the truffles and sauce in a saucepan together.
3. Season to taste with salt and pepper, and add a dash of nutmeg.
4. Cover and let all cook for ten minutes, occasionally stirring to prevent burning.
5. Take from the fire and let cool by pouring into a flat dish.
6. Spread out to about an inch in thickness and divide into six parts.
7. Prepare six pieces of Crepinette and roll each one of the six parts of chicken into the skin.
8. Have ready a flour batter and dip each Crepinette into the batter and fry in boiling lard for five minutes, or until slightly brown.
9. Drain thoroughly on a cloth, place a nice folded napkin on a dish; set the cromesquies upon it; decorate with fried parsley and serve hot.

title: Croquets of Cold Meat
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- any kind of boiled or roasted meat
- a piquante sauce
- bread
- capers
- mushrooms
- salt
- spices
- fine herbs
- egg
- grated bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Take any kind of boiled or roasted meat and mix them together, so that it is clear of bones
2. Make a hash of it, and mix it with a piquante sauce
3. The mixture must be very thick; if it be too thin, put a little bread with it
4. Add capers, mushrooms, salt, spices and fine herbs, boiled together
5. Let it get cold on a chopping board or dish
6. Make balls of it and press them flat
7. Roll them in egg and grated bread, and fry them in butter, brown on both sides

title: Croquettes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. veal
- broth from boiling veal
- ½ loaf of bread
- 3 eggs
- 2 heaping tablespoons of butter
- part of a nutmeg or powdered mace
- salt and pepper
- parsley
- thyme
- sage
- onion

## Directions

1. Boil veal till done
2. Take out the bones, skins and strings
3. Chop very fine
4. Take enough of the broth the veal was boiled in and ½ loaf of bread to make a panada as thick as mush
5. Stir into this while on the fire, 3 eggs and 2 heaping tablespoons of butter, till the whole looks like thin scrambled eggs
6. Turn this out on the meat, stirring it well
7. Add part of a nutmeg or powdered mace and salt and pepper, in fact, any seasoning to taste
8. Add a little parsley, thyme and sage chopped very fine, and a little onion chopped and fried

title: Croquettes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey, beef, chicken, or sweet bread
- grated bread crumbs
- grated lemon peel
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- hard boiled egg yolk
- milk
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Chop the meat very fine
2. Add grated bread crumbs
3. Season with a little grated lemon peel, salt, pepper, a very little nutmeg, and yolk of a hard boiled egg
4. Moisten the whole with enough milk to make the ingredients adhere
5. Form into cones or pear-shaped balls
6. Dip each into a batter of eggs, well beaten
7. Roll in bread crumbs
8. Fry brown in butter
9. Serve with fried parsley

title: Croquettes—Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter
- flour
- salt
- red pepper
- black pepper
- parsley
- onion
- egg
- grated bread
- lard

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken tenderly
2. When cold, chop fine
3. Put some of its liquor in pan with butter the size of a hen's egg
4. Mix in a little flour, salt, red and black pepper, parsley, and small onion
5. Add the minced chicken
6. When cold, shape the mixture
7. Dip in egg with grated bread
8. Fry in lard

title: Croquignoles
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 4 Ounces of Bitter Almonds
- Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1½ Pounds of Fine Sugar

## Directions

1. Scald, skin and wash and dry the almonds, as directed in "Macarons," and then pound them into a pulp adding the whites of the eggs gradually, remembering always that they must be beaten to a stiff froth.
2. Then work in the sugar, and you will have a firm paste.
3. Drop these in small balls upon wafer paper, spread upon a baking sheet of tin or iron, in distinct and separate rows.
4. Pass a little brush, moistened with water, lightly on their surface, and bake in a very slack oven till a very light color.

title: Crows'-Nest With Cinnamon
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cooking apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon
- water
- light pie crust
- cream
- butter for hard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut nice sour cooking apples into a baking dish, small or large as you need.
2. Put sugar, cinnamon, and lemon over them.
3. Throw in a cup of water, and cover the dish with a crust of light pie crust.
4. Be sure to cut air-holes in the crust before putting in to bake.
5. Put it in the oven, and bake until the apples are tender.
6. Hat it with cream and sugar, or hard sauce of butter and sugar; beat together until firm enough to slice like butter.
7. Grate a little nutmeg over the sauce, if cinnamon is not liked.

title: Crullers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of sugar
- one cup of butter
- three eggs
- one cup of sour milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- flavoring to taste
- boiling lard for frying

## Directions

1. Combine sugar, butter, eggs, sour milk, and soda
2. Add flavoring to taste
3. Fry in boiling lard or bake lightly in the stove

title: Crullers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- three eggs
- half a pint of sour milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- half a nutmeg
- flour
- sugar for topping
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Combine butter, sugar, eggs, sour milk, soda, and half a nutmeg
2. Add flour to roll thin
3. Cut in fancy shapes
4. Put two pounds of lard in a deep skillet and heat until very hot
5. Fry the crullers, replenishing lard once or twice as it becomes brown and scorched
6. Crullers should be a light brown of uniform color
7. Sprinkle sugar over them when done

title: Crullers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 3 cups brown sugar
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoonful yeast powder
- flour to roll

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Roll out
3. Cut out with a biscuit cutter
4. Cut from the centre with an apple corer
5. Twist into fanciful shapes
6. Fry in a potato fryer in plenty of boiling lard

title: Crumpets
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 quart of milk
- yeast

## Directions

1. Make into stiff batter, as for pancakes
2. Add yeast
3. Make over night in cool weather, and set to rise

title: Crumpets
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- ¾ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and set to cool
2. Add the salt and gradually beat in the flour
3. Beat till very smooth and add the yeast
4. Beat continuously for ten minutes and then set in a warm place to rise
5. After several hours beat well again and add the melted butter
6. Mix thoroughly and then turn into greased muffin rings
7. Bake till a beautiful brown, turning first on one side and then on the other
8. Send to the table very hot
9. Break open with your fingers and butter nicely

title: Crystallised Watermelon
body:
## Ingredients

- Watermelon
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Take the bright red heart of the watermelon
2. Cut into dainty and fanciful forms, and place in a dish in the sun to dry
3. Make a syrup as directed for Crystallized Fruits
4. With the point of a long hatpin or hairpin immerse the watermelon in it
5. Hang to dry from these points

title: Crystallized Orange Blossoms
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange Blossoms
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the petals one by one from the flowers, and put them into water
2. Drain this off, and then squeeze the flowers with your hands to bruise them, and drain on a sieve
3. Make a fine syrup, equal in bulk to the flowers, clarify it and add the flowers
4. Let the syrup boil up about seven times
5. Remove it from the fire and let it stand till the sugar forms a coating all around the petals
6. Drain them, separate them on a paper for an hour or so, till perfectly dry
7. Put in boxes lined with white paper, and keep in a cool, dry place

title: Crystallized Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oranges
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- Sufficient Water to Dissolve

## Directions

1. Crystallize oranges in quarters, keeping the skins from being punctured

title: Crystallized Rose Petals
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Rose Petals
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select beautiful red or pink rose petals that have been freshly gathered, and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Crystallized Violets

title: Crystallized Violets
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Violet Petals, Freshly Gathered
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Orange Blossoms, only do not squeeze the petals in your hands

title: Crème de Menthe Frappée
body:
## Ingredients

- A small sherry glass filled with crushed ice
- Two tablespoonfuls of crème de menthe (white or green)

## Directions

1. Fill a glass with finely-crushed ice.
2. Pour over the crème de menthe, and serve very cold.

title: Crème de Noyau Frappée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 claret glass filled with crushed ice
- 2½ tablespoonfuls of crème de noyau
- Seltzer

## Directions

1. Put two tablespoonfuls of crème de noyau in a glass of finely-crushed ice and seltzer and serve at almost freezing point.

title: Cucumber and Onion Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen fine crisp cucumbers
- four large onions
- salt
- pepper
- boiling vinegar
- mace
- nutmeg
- ginger

## Directions

1. Take a dozen fine crisp cucumbers and four large onions
2. Cut both in thick slices
3. Sprinkle salt and pepper on them and let them stand
4. Next day drain them well and scald them in boiling vinegar; cover close after scalding
5. Next day scald again with a bag of mace, nutmeg and ginger in the vinegar
6. Place them in jars and cork close
7. If the vinegar seems to have lost its strength, replace with fresh, and put the bag of spices in again to keep the flavor

title: Cucumber Pickles in Whiskey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- brine
- one part whiskey
- three parts water
- spices

## Directions

1. Prepare your cucumbers by letting them stay a few days in brine, or if time is an object scald them in brine
2. If you have no vinegar convenient drop your scalded cucumbers into a mixture of one part whiskey and three parts water
3. Secure them carefully from the air
4. By Christmas they will be fine, firm, green pickles, and the whiskey and water will be excellent vinegar
5. Add spices after they are pickled

title: Cucumber Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Cucumbers
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Wash and slice two nice young cucumbers, and use a plain dressing of vinegar, salt and pepper

title: Cucumber Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Nice, Tender Cucumber
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Prepared Mustard
- The Yolk of One Egg
- Seasoning to Taste
- Juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the cucumber, and add the mustard, mixing thoroughly.
2. Add the juice of one lemon and the yolk of one egg, beaten thoroughly.
3. This is a delicious salad dressing.

title: Cucumbers as a Garnish
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Cucumbers
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- 1 Large Onion
- Salt
- Black Pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and slice three fine cucumbers.
2. Make a marinade of one teaspoonful of salt, one of black pepper and a tablespoonful of vinegar.
3. Add one finely-sliced onion.
4. Let the cucumbers marinate in this for an hour.
5. Drain and use as a garnish for cold meats, especially cold Bouilli.

title: Cucumbers as a Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine Cucumbers
- Salt
- A French Salad Dressing

## Directions

1. Wash the cucumbers, cut off the bitter end, and pare the skin to a sufficient depth to remove the green portion.
2. Score them lengthwise with a table fork.
3. Put them into a salad earthen bowl and sprinkle with salt.
4. Set in an ice box for three hours.
5. Cut into delicate slices and serve with a French Salad Dressing, the dressing being served in a separate dish.

title: Cullis
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of lean veal, cut in slices
- 2 lbs. of lean ham
- 2 ounces butter
- a handful of chopped mushrooms
- 3 onions
- 1 carrot
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- rind of a lemon
- 1/2 teaspoon of mixed allspice, mace and cloves
- 4 quarts of stock
- brown roux

## Directions

1. Put 6 lbs. of lean veal, cut in slices, and 2 lbs. of lean ham into a stewpan, with 2 ounces butter, a handful of chopped mushrooms, 3 onions, one carrot, a bunch of sweet herbs, the rind of a lemon, and 1/2 teaspoon of mixed allspice, mace and cloves
2. Let it just brown at the bottom and add 4 quarts of stock
3. let it boil 3 or 4 hours
4. strain it off
5. thicken with brown roux, and boil it well for ten minutes, stirring all the time
6. pass through a tamise

title: Cumberland Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and a half lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 12 ounces of butter
- 10 eggs
- 1 glass of orange or lemon brandy
- a few cloves, mace, or nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat as pound cake
2. Bake in small tins

title: Cup Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of flour
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of cream
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda and cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Beat light
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Cup Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ cups of unsifted flour
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- whites of 8 eggs
- ½ cup of sweet milk
- 2 teaspoonfuls of yeast powder

## Directions

1. sift yeast powder in the flour
2. cream butter and sugar together
3. add the whites of the eggs well beaten
4. add the flour
5. add the milk

title: Cup Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 4 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Sour Cream
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy
- 1 Tablespoonful of Rose or Vanilla Extract
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Soda
- Sweet Milk

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and then add the cream
3. Mix thoroughly, and sift in gradually the flour, beating thoroughly
4. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and the brandy and rose water
5. Dissolve a half teaspoonful of soda in a little sweet milk and add, being very careful that there are no lumps in the soda
6. Beat well for a few minutes and bake at once, in small tins or cups, which you will grease well with butter and only half fill with the cake batter

title: Cup Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Eggs
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch (If desired)
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs together until light, and then add the sugar, and beat till very light.
2. Now add the milk and the nutmeg, and put a pan into the oven.
3. Fill the pan with boiling water.
4. Pour the custard into cups or into a smaller pan, and set in the boiling water, and let it bake till firm in the center.
5. Set to cool, and serve in the cups if they have been used.
6. Otherwise serve in dessert plates.

title: Curacao
body:
## Ingredients

- The Zest of 1 Dozen Fine Louisiana Oranges
- 3 Fine Lemons
- 3 Quarts of Fine Old French Cognac
- 3½ Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Grate the zests of the fruit as carefully as possible, so as to preserve the fine aromatic oils, and without touching the white pulp.
2. Put the zests in a large glass bottle or vessel that can be closed tightly, and pour over the brandy.
3. Shake well, and let it stand for fifteen days, each day shaking the bottle well, to agitate the juices.
4. Make a syrup with the sugar and water, first beating into the sugar the whites of two eggs, to make the infusion very clear.
5. Mix with the water and strain.
6. Add it to the syrup of brandy and lemons, bottle and cork well.
7. Shake the bottles and let them stand for fifteen days longer, agitating each day.
8. At the end of this time filter the liquor and bottle tightly for use.

title: Curd Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of curds
- 8 eggs (leaving out 4 whites)
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- grated nutmeg
- a little flour
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Mix curds, eggs, sugar, nutmeg, and flour all well together
2. Heat butter in a frying-pan
3. Drop them in and fry like fritters

title: Curing Hams
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- green ham of 18 lbs.
- 1 dessertspoonful of saltpetre
- 1 pound of brown sugar
- fine salt
- ground black pepper

## Directions

1. Apply 1 dessertspoonful of saltpetre and 1 pound of brown sugar to the fleshy side of the ham and about the hock
2. Cover the fleshy side with fine salt 1 inch thick
3. Pack away in tubs, to remain from 3 to 6 weeks, according to size
4. Before smoking, rub off all the salt from the ham
5. Cover well with ground black pepper, particularly about the bone and hock
6. Hang up and drain for 2 weeks
7. Smoke with green wood 8 weeks, till the rind is brown

title: Currant and Raspberry Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts of currants and raspberries
- 1 pound of sugar to every pint of berry juice

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of currants and raspberries and strain the juice.
2. Allow one pound of sugar to every pint of berry juice.
3. Proceed as for Currant Jelly.

title: Currant Charlotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Currants
- 1 Pound of White Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Soft of Bread

## Directions

1. Make a marmalade of the currants, and using the same proportion as for Cherry Charlotte.
2. Proceed exactly as for Cherry Charlotte.

title: Currant Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe currants
- boiled cream
- cinnamon finely beaten

## Directions

1. Bruise currants that are quite ripe in boiled cream
2. Put in some cinnamon, finely beaten
3. Sweeten to the taste
4. Strain it through a sieve

title: Currant Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants, fully ripe
- 1 pound of fine white sugar to every pint of juice

## Directions

1. Clean, pick, wash and boil the currants.
2. To every pint of juice add one pound of fine white sugar.
3. Let it boil for twenty minutes, always skimming it while on the fire.
4. Do not let it remain longer than twenty minutes, or it will grow dark.
5. Pour immediately into cups.

title: Currant Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants that are fully ripe
- 1 pound of fine white sugar to every pint of juice

## Directions

1. Take currants that are fully ripe
2. Clean, pick, wash and boil
3. To every pint of juice add one pound of fine white sugar
4. Let it boil for twenty minutes, always skimming it while on the fire
5. Do not let it remain longer than twenty minutes, or it will grow dark
6. Pour immediately into cups

title: Currant Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Tumbler of Currant Jelly
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Gill Water
- 1 Gill of Port or Madeira Wine

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, and add the jelly, blending well, and then add the gill of wine and water.
2. Add a little salt and sugar to taste.
3. The sauce is much finer when made of wine without water, but this is a question of taste.
4. If the wine only is used, double the proportions, or according to taste.
5. This sauce is served with Venison and other game.

title: Currant Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Currants
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- Plain Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Clean and pick and wash the currants well
2. Drain nicely, and then put into a saucepan with one-half cup of sugar
3. Stew nicely, and then proceed as in the directions given for making Apple Pie

title: Currant Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Currant Juice
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- one quart of currants

## Directions

1. Mash one quart of currants
2. Boil the sugar and water for five minutes
3. Add the currants and strain through a napkin or sieve, being careful to press very hard to extract the juice of the currants
4. Set to freeze as directed

title: Currant Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Currant Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup

## Directions

1. Pick, seed, wash and dry the currants carefully.
2. Then pound them well and set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours.
3. Make a Plain Syrup and proceed as in the General Directions for Making Fruit Syrups.

title: Currant Syrup with Raspberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Currant Juice
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- 6 Pounds of Currants
- 1 Pound of Sour Cherries
- 1 Pound of Raspberries
- 1 1/4 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Pick, wash, seed and dry the currants carefully.
2. Seed the cherries, and stem them and the raspberries, pound and mash them well, and let them stand for thirty-six hours in a cool place.
3. Then strain through a bag.
4. Cook the sugar and water till it forms the syrup called "Le Petit Casse," or crack, till it instantly snaps asunder when placed between the fingers, after dipping them in cold water, to enable you to test.
5. Then add the fruit syrups, and mix thoroughly.
6. Let it boil for about five minutes, take off the fire, let it get almost cold, and then put up in bottles.

title: Currant Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Quarts of Currant Juice
- 1.33 Quarts of Cold Water
- 3 Pounds of Granulated Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as Blackberry Wine

title: Curried Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- 1 Gill of Rich Cream or 2 Gills of Good Milk
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Curry Powder
- A Pinch of Corn Starch
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- The Oyster Water
- 1/2 Cup of Louisiana Rice Boiled a la Creole, for Border

## Directions

1. Take four dozen fine, large oysters and drain the oyster liquor into a saucepan, being careful to extract all pieces of shell, and set it to boil.
2. Wipe the oysters dry with a clean towel.
3. Put in another saucepan one tablespoonful of butter and let it melt; then add two tablespoonfuls of flour, stirring constantly and rubbing smoothly; do not let it brown.
4. Add about one gill of rich cream, or two gills of good milk, to the boiling oyster juice, and stir all this into the flour slowly, avoiding the formation of any lumps, and stirring constantly.
5. Let this boil about two minutes.
6. Take one-half teaspoonful of curry powder and a pinch of cornstarch or flour and rub smoothly with a few drops of cold milk.
7. Stir this into the oyster juice; season a la Creole with Cayenne, salt, chopped thyme, etc., and, as it boils up, drop in the oysters; let them cook about three minutes and serve on a dish with a border of Louisiana Rice, boiled so as to appear like snowflakes, the grains standing apart.
8. Sprinkle chopped parsley over the oysters to form a garnish.

title: Curry
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of fresh butter
- 1 medium sized onion
- 1 whole tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of curry powder
- 1 chicken jointed and cut up into small pieces, or 2 lbs. of mutton, lamb or veal without fat
- 1 teacup of water
- pineapple
- cocoanut
- potato
- green peas
- rice

## Directions

1. Put 1 tablespoonful of fresh butter into a saucepan and let it boil
2. Add a medium sized onion, cut into thin slices
3. Fry the onion a deep brown
4. To the fried onion add a whole tablespoonful of butter and 1 of curry powder
5. Stir well and cook for a few moments
6. Add the meat and stir the whole over the fire
7. Add a teacup of water
8. Let the whole simmer over a gentle fire for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally
9. Remove the saucepan to the top of the stove and let the contents simmer for 1½ to 2 hours, must not let it boil
10. Add cocoanut, potato, green peas to taste, only just long enough before the curry is done to insure their being thoroughly cooked
11. Serve rice separately, having been well drained and put into cold water to boil

title: Curry
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of fresh butter
- 1 medium sized onion
- chicken or mutton, lamb or veal

## Directions

1. Put 1 tablespoonful of fresh butter into a saucepan
2. Let it boil
3. Add a medium sized onion, cut into thin slices
4. Fry the onion a deep brown
5. Have ready a chicken jointed and cut up into small pieces, or 2 lbs. of mutton, lamb or veal

title: Curry of Cold Roast Fowl
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two large onions
- two apples
- two ounces of butter
- a dessertspoonful of curry powder or paste
- half pint of gravy or soup-stock
- one spoonful of lemon juice
- two tomatoes
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Fry the fowl and the onions in butter to a light brown color.
2. Stew the apples, or fry them also.
3. Put all, onions, apples, gravy and fowl, with the tomatoes and lemon juice into a stewing pan.
4. Let it stew thirty minutes.
5. Serve with boiled rice. If curry paste is used instead of curry powder, no lemon is required.

title: Curry of Crawfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- 3 Dozen Crawfish
- 2 Ounces of Butter
- 1 Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Juice of Half a Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Curry Powder
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Quart of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil the crawfish according to recipe.
2. Clean and pick the crawfish the same as for a fricassee.
3. Put two ounces of butter in the frying pan.
4. Cut one onion in slices, add it to the butter, letting it brown nicely.
5. Then add the well-seasoned crawfish, and fry them to a golden brown.
6. Add one clove of garlic, finely minced, and minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf.
7. Let this brown.
8. After five minutes, add a quart of boiling water.
9. Stir well.
10. Season to taste with salt, pepper and a dash of Cayenne.
11. Simmer gently until the crawfish are very tender.
12. When done, add the juice of half a lemon, and mix one even tablespoonful of Curry Powder and one of flour with a little water.
13. Bring it to a smooth paste by rubbing well, and add it to the crawfish.
14. Stir constantly, and let it boil five minutes longer.
15. Serve with a border of boiled rice heaped around it.

title: Custard and Apple Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 apples
- Paste
- Sugar
- Boiled custard

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in slices 4 apples
2. Put a paste in the bottom of the dish
3. Lay a covering of apples and sugar
4. Put a boiled custard over the top, and bake it

title: Custard Cocoanut Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cocoanut grated
- a quart of milk
- four eggs
- a cup of sugar
- paste

## Directions

1. Grate one cocoanut.
2. Beat sugar and eggs light, then stir in the milk, and last the cocoanut and such flavoring as you may prefer.
3. Pour this into a deep pan lined with paste.
4. Put fancy strips of paste across it, and bake lightly.

title: Custard for Pies
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of new milk
- 2 or 3 bitter almonds
- a stick of cinnamon
- a piece of lemon peel
- 7 or 8 good sized lumps of sugar
- yolks of 6 eggs
- almond or rose water

## Directions

1. Put almonds, cinnamon, lemon peel, and sugar into milk
2. Let the whole simmer gently till the flavor is extracted
3. Strain and stir till cool
4. Beat the yolks of 6 eggs and mix well with the milk
5. Stir the whole over a slow fire till it is the thickness of rich cream
6. Flavor it with almond or rose water, using 1 ounce

title: Custard Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs per quart of milk
- sugar
- essence

## Directions

1. Make your custard, allowing 5 eggs to a quart of milk
2. Use much more sugar than for ordinary custard
3. Flavor with whatever essence may be preferred
4. Freeze

title: Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 3 Eggs
- ½ Cup of Sugar
- Vanilla or Lemon Extract
- A Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Line the pie pans with the pie crust
2. Beat the eggs

title: Custard Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- ¾ Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil
2. Beat the eggs and sugar to a thick cream and very light, and then stir them into the boiling milk
3. Stir over the fire till the sauce begins to thicken, and no longer
4. If you stir too long the sauce will curdle
5. Take it from the fire and add the Vanilla and serve cold

title: Custard Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the milk.
2. Blend the butter and the flour nicely, and add to the boiling milk.
3. Stir constantly over the fire for ten minutes.
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar together, and add these to the milk.
5. Then set the mixture to cool.
6. When cool, beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and add to the mixture.
7. Turn into a buttered baking dish, and bake in a quick oven for twenty minutes.
8. Serve with Cream Sauce, while hot, otherwise it will fall.

title: Cutlets of Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tenderloin Trout Cutlets
- 1 Cup of Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Flour
- Parsley
- Sliced Lemon
- Mayonnaise Sauce or Sauce a la Tartare

## Directions

1. Cut the fish (Tenderloin Trout) into filets or square pieces
2. Remove all the bones, dip in milk which has been freely salted and peppered, and roll in flour
3. Drop into the well-heated lard so that the filets will swim and fry to a golden brown
4. When done, drain on a brown paper and serve on a bed of fried parsley, with garnishes of parsley and sliced lemon
5. Serve with either a Mayonnaise Sauce, a Sauce a la Tartare or a Tomato Sauce

title: Cutlets of Veal a la Milanaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Veal Cutlets
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- A Bed of Macaroni or Spaghetti
- Tomato Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as above.
2. Serve the cutlets on a bed of boiled macaroni or spaghetti.
3. Pour over the whole a Tomato Sauce.

title: Damson Cheese
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- damsons
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the fruit in a sufficient quantity of water to cover it
2. Strain the pulp through a very coarse sieve
3. To each lb. add 4 ounces of sugar
4. Boil it till it begins to candy on the sides, then pour it into tin moulds

title: Dandelion Greens
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Dandelion Greens
- A Ham Shank
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut off the coarse roots; wash the leaves thoroughly.
2. Steep in salt and water for five hours to remove the bitterness.
3. Boil a ham shank for two hours, throw in the dandelions, and cook gently for forty-five minutes.
4. Drain and chop fine; season with butter, pepper and salt.
5. Mince the ham very fine and sprinkle over the greens.
6. Spread over sliced hard-boiled eggs and serve hot.

title: Dandelion Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh White Dandelion
- A Plain French Dressing
- Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Beets

## Directions

1. Cut off the roots and green portion of the leaves; wash and steep in salt and water
2. When they become crisp, drain and press dry
3. Rub the salad bowl with a clove of garlic and season the dandelions with French dressing
4. This salad may also be served with two hard-boiled eggs cut in quarters or sliced and laid over; or with two medium-sized beets, sliced, and seasoned

title: Dandelion Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh White Dandelion
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Cut off the roots and green portion of the leaves; wash and steep in salt and water.
2. When they become crisp, drain and press dry.
3. Rub the salad bowl with a clove of garlic and season the dandelions with French dressing.
4. This salad may also be served with two hard-boiled eggs cut in quarters or sliced and laid over; or with two medium-sized beets, sliced, and seasoned with a plain French dressing.

title: Danish Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of rich milk
- 4 eggs
- 1½ spoonfuls of flour
- sugar
- ½ lb. of butter
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 wineglass of wine
- yolk of 1 egg
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart of rich milk and let stand till almost cold
2. Beat 4 eggs for ½ hour with 1½ spoonfuls of flour
3. Mix the milk and sugar with it
4. Tie it up in a close cloth, well floured
5. Boil it 1 hour
6. Make a sauce of ½ lb. of butter, 1 cup of sugar, with 1 wineglass of wine, the yolk of 1 egg, and a little nutmeg, and serve with the pudding

title: Daub
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lbs. round of beef
- strips of pork
- a small piece of onion
- garlic
- salt and pepper
- 1 pint of vinegar
- water
- 1 ounce of gelatine

## Directions

1. Make incisions in the beef
2. Put strips of pork, a small piece of onion and garlic, salt and pepper into the incisions
3. Pat it away for several hours
4. Pour over it 1 pint of vinegar and let it remain all night
5. In the morning let it brown a little
6. Fill the saucepan full of water and cook it slowly 4 or 5 hours before taking it up
7. Add 1 ounce of gelatine dissolved in a little cold water
8. Pour all in a mould
9. In the morning skim off the grease

title: Daube
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt meat
- Large onions
- Turnips
- Carrots
- Clove of garlic
- Tablespoonful of lard
- Glass of sherry, Madeira or claret
- Salt, pepper and cayenne
- Bay leaves
- Sprigs of thyme and parsley
- Rump of beef

## Directions

1. Cut the fat of the salt meat into thin shreds
2. Chop the onion and bay leaf very fine, as also the garlic, thyme and cloves
3. Rub the shreds well with salt and pepper
4. Take the rump of beef and lard thickly by making incisions about three or four inches in length and inserting the pieces of salt fat and spices, onion and thyme and garlic, mixed thoroughly
5. Take two large onions and cut into quarters and put in a saucepan with one tablespoonful of lard
6. Let the slices brown and then lay on top the rump of beef, well larded
7. Cover closely and let it simmer very slowly till well browned
8. Add the chopped bay leaf and parsley
9. When brown add five carrots cut into squares of an inch, and two turnips, cut in the same manner, and two large onions, chopped fine
10. Let the whole brown, keeping well covered, and cooking slowly over a slow but regular fire
11. Keep the cover very tight on the pot
12. When it has simmered about ten minutes, turn the daube on the other side, cover closely and let it simmer ten minutes more
13. Cover with sufficient boiling water to cover the daube; or use boiling consommé or pot-au-feu, and if possible, a glass of sherry or Madeira wine; or if you have neither, a glass of good claret
14. Season according to taste with salt, cayenne and black pepper
15. Cover the pot tight and set it back on the stove, letting it smother slowly for about three hours, or until tender
16. Serve hot or cold

title: Daube Glacee of Beef, for Cold Suppers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- thick round of beef
- salted pork
- bacon
- pepper
- salt
- sugar
- vinegar
- parsley
- garlic
- calf's feet
- pig's feet

## Directions

1. Take a thick round of beef—from four to six inches is the best size—make holes in it and stuff them with salted pork or bacon; roll each piece, before it is drawn through the beef, in pepper, salt, sugar, and vinegar, with minced parsley, and a very little minced garlic.
2. If the weather is cold it will be better to keep the meat till the next day before cooking it.
3. Boil two calf's feet or four pig's feet until they drop to pieces; pick out the bones and strain the liquor; set it away to jelly, or put it on ice to make it jelly.
4. The next morning, put one half the jelly in a large stew pan, then add the beef, and cover it with the remainder of the jelly.
5. Paste the pan over very tight or cover it extremely well, so that none of the flavor can escape.
6. Cook this about four hours; when done, take out, cover with the liquor, and set it aside till it is jellied.

title: Delicate Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two cups of sugar
- half a cup of butter
- one cup of sweet milk
- three cups of flour
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- half a teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients
2. Bake in squares

title: Delicate Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Nearly 3 cups of flour
- 2 cups of powdered sugar
- 1 cup of sweet milk
- whites of 6 eggs
- 1 cup of butter
- yeast powder
- lemon flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix flour, powdered sugar, sweet milk, beaten egg whites, butter, and yeast powder
2. Flavor with lemon
3. Bake

title: Delicate Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 Cups of Flour
- 1 3/4 Cups of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Cornstarch, Sifted With the Flour
- 1/2 Cup of Butter
- 3/4 Cup of Milk
- Whites of 6 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls Baking Powder
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Extract of Lemon
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Salt

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and beat it and the sugar to a cream.
2. Add the milk, and stir carefully.
3. Add gradually the flour, into which has been sifted the salt and the cornstarch, and mix thoroughly.
4. Add the flavoring extract, and, lastly, the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Sift the baking powder over the whites.
6. Mix quickly and lightly with the batter, and bake forty-five minutes in a moderate oven.

title: Delicate Cake of Corn Starch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- one-fourth of a pound of butter
- whites of eight eggs
- quarter of a pound of corn starch
- quarter of a pound of common flour
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- half spoon of fine soda

## Directions

1. Beat all very light
2. add to the flour a teaspoonful of cream of tartar, and a half-spoon of fine soda
3. Flavor with lemon or rose

title: Delicate Rusks for Convalescents
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of new milk
- one cup of hop yeast
- flour
- half a pint of milk
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of butter
- one egg
- one nutmeg

## Directions

1. Add flour to half a pint of new milk and one cup of hop yeast to make a batter
2. Set the sponge at night
3. In the morning add half a pint of milk, one cup of sugar, one cup of butter, one egg, one nutmeg, and flour to make it sufficiently stiff
4. Let it rise, then roll it and cut it out
5. Let it rise again and then bake

title: Delicious Bread Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- butter
- currants
- citron
- a quart of milk
- four eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Butter some slices of bread, cut thin, and lay them in a dish, with currants and citron between
2. Pour over it a quart of milk, with four well-beaten eggs, and sugar sufficient to sweeten to taste
3. Bake
4. Serve with sauce

title: Delicious Pound Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound of butter
- one pound of flour
- nine eggs, yolks only
- one pound of sifted sugar
- twelve eggs, whites only
- brandy or wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cream three quarters of a pound of butter
2. Sift a pound of flour gradually into the butter and cream them together
3. Beat the yolks of nine eggs light in another pan
4. Stir a pound of sifted sugar into the eggs
5. Mix well
6. Beat the whites of twelve eggs to a froth
7. Add the whites to the yolks and sugar
8. Pour this into the pan containing the butter and flour
9. Beat all well together
10. Add a little brandy or wine and nutmeg
11. Bake carefully in a large pan
12. Ensure the oven is not hotter on the top than at the bottom
13. Allow the cake to rise and bake from the bottom

title: Demi-Glace
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Sauce Espagnole
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 1 Glass of Mushroom Liquor
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Teaspoonful of Pepper
- Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. To one pint of Sauce Espagnole add a glass of Madeira wine and a glass of mushroom liquor, a herb bouquet and a teaspoonful of pepper.
2. Carefully remove all fat and set on the fire and cook for thirty minutes.
3. Strain and use when needed.
4. This sauce is used in all recipes where Madeira Sauce is indicated as a foundation sauce.

title: Derby Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of raisins
- 1 lb. of good brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 pint of new milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together
2. Roll it out thin
3. Cut it into round cakes
4. Lay them on baking tins
5. Bake in a moderately heated oven for 10 minutes

title: Des Tomates en Conserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Dozen Tomatoes
- A Bain Marie

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the Tomatoes to loosen the skins.
2. Then skin the Tomatoes, and bring to a hard boil for twenty minutes.
3. Then drain and can as quickly as possible, excluding all air.
4. Set in a bain-marie to cool, and keep in a cool, dark place.

title: Devil's Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Onion
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Pickle a Finger Long
- 1 Teaspoonful of Mustard
- 2 Gills of Consomme
- Salt and Cayenne
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Brown the onion in butter, and add the two cloves of garlic, minced very fine.
2. When brown, add one pickle, minced very fine, and add a teaspoonful of mustard prepared.
3. Then add two gills of consomme and one glass of White Wine, and the juice of a lemon, and allow it to cook slowly.
4. Season with salt and hot pepper (piment fort), and serve with shell fish, chicken, sweetbreads, etc.
5. This is a hot sauce.

title: Deviled Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1/2 of a Clove of Garlic
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- Salt and Pepper
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil the chicken according to the above recipe.
2. Mince the meat fine.
3. Make a sauce by putting into a saucepan one tablespoonful of butter, and as it melts add one onion, minced very fine; a sprig of minced parsley, one minced bay leaf, and a half clove of minced garlic.
4. Let it simmer gently without browning and then add one tablespoonful of flour, well sifted.
5. Mix thoroughly, and add three tablespoonfuls of vinegar or a wineglass of White Wine.
6. Stir well and add one pint of the water in which the chicken was boiled.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste and a slight dash of Cayenne.
8. Add one teaspoonful of prepared mustard.
9. Let it simmer three minutes longer, and as it comes to a boil pour over the chicken and serve.

title: Deviled Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Large Crabs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Tablespoon of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Half Pint of Cream
- 1/4 Nutmeg Grated
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Yolks of 4 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs according to recipe
2. Take out and drain after they have cooled in their own water
3. Break off the claws, separate the shells, remove the spongy portions and the dead man's fingers, and then pick out the meat
4. Put the cream on to boil, rub the flour and butter together well and add to the boiling cream
5. Stir and cook for two minutes
6. Take from the fire and add the crab meat, the yolks of the hard-boiled eggs mashed very fine, the chopped parsley, grated nutmeg, salt and Cayenne
7. Clean the upper shells of the crabs and fill them with the mixture
8. Brush over with a beaten egg, sprinkle with bread crumbs and put in a quick oven to brown; or plunge the crabs into the boiling fat or lard until a nice brown

title: Deviled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen oysters
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 pint of cream
- The yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 blade of mace
- 3 sprigs of parsley
- Salt and Cayenne
- Sprigs of parsley or asparagus tips
- Sliced lemon
- Olives
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take three dozen fine, large oysters, drain and chop them into middling fine pieces.
2. Rub together one tablespoonful of butter and two tablespoonfuls of flour, very smoothly.
3. Place in a saucepan one-half pint of cream, and when it is coming to a boil, stir in the flour and butter.
4. Have ready the yolks of two eggs well beaten.
5. As soon as the milk boils, take from the fire and add the eggs, one tablespoonful of parsley chopped fine, one bay leaf chopped fine, mace, and a sprig of finely-chopped thyme.
6. Add salt and Cayenne to taste, and add the oysters.
7. Take the deep shells of the oysters, which have been washed perfectly clean, and fill with this mixture.
8. Sprinkle lightly with bread crumbs.
9. Put a pinch of butter on top, and set in the baking pan and brown.
10. The oven should be very quick, and only five minutes are needed for the browning.
11. Serve the oysters thus baked in their shells, and garnish the dish with sprigs of parsley or asparagus tips and sliced lemon and olives.

title: Devonshire Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- risen cream
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Set the risen cream in a warm place, and as it rises skim it off
2. Sweeten it and grate nutmeg over it

title: Dickson Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 2 cups of brown sugar
- 4 cups of flour
- 2 cups of butter
- 1 cup of cream
- 2 cups of molasses
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 cup of ginger

## Directions

1. Add brown sugar to eggs
2. Add flour, butter, cream, molasses, soda, and ginger
3. Beat very light and bake in cups or a pan

title: Diet Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 eggs
- 1 pound of sugar
- 14 ozs. of flour

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with a whisk till quite thick
2. Mix the sugar with them, and beat till light
3. Beat the whites well, and add them by degrees
4. Put the flour in gradually
5. Bake in a quick oven

title: Diet Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- a little mace
- grated nutmeg
- a little rose water
- 6 eggs
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, sugar, mace and nutmeg together
2. Stir well together
3. Beat the eggs very light, add salt
4. Mix all the ingredients well
5. Bake it slowly in a pan

title: Dimples
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- three quarters of a pound of sugar
- three quarters of a pound of blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of three eggs very stiff
2. Add gradually three quarters of a pound of sugar and beat till it is well mixed
3. Blanch almonds and cut them into pieces as small as peas
4. Stir them into the egg and sugar
5. Drop the mixture in spots as large as a half penny on white paper upon a tin
6. Bake in a cool oven

title: Domestic Fruit Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups dried apples
- 3 cups molasses
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- spice
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak 3 cups of dried apples over night in cold water
2. Chop them in the morning, and put them on the fire with 3 cups of molasses
3. Stew until soft, but not pulpy
4. When cold, mix with 3 cups of flour, 1 cup of butter, 3 eggs and a teaspoon of soda
5. Season with plenty of spice, not forgetting a little salt
6. Bake in a steady oven
7. This will make 2 good sized loaves

title: Domestic Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pints of cream
- peel of a small lemon
- crumbs of a small loaf of bread
- 1 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 4 well beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Sweeten 1 1/2 pints of cream, and boil it with the peel of a small lemon
2. Cut the crumbs of a small loaf of bread
3. Put it in the cream
4. Boil it for 8 minutes, stirring constantly
5. When thick, add 1 lb. of fresh butter beaten to a cream
6. Add 1 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg, and 4 well beaten eggs
7. Beat all well together for some minutes
8. It may be baked or boiled
9. The water should boil quick when it is put in, and moved for a minute, to cause the ingredients to mix
10. When the pudding is done, a pan of cold water should be ready, and the pudding dipped into it, as soon as it comes out of the pot

title: Doughnuts
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 3 lbs. of butter
- 4 eggs
- ½ cup of the best brewers' yeast
- 1½ pints of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of powdered cinnamon
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 tablespoonful of rose water
- lard for frying
- loaf sugar for coating

## Directions

1. fry them in lard
2. grate fine loaf sugar over them

title: Doughnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 pint of milk
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 3/4 cake of compressed yeast
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- flour in sufficient quantity to make a dough

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, and then add the butter, and let it stand and cool.
2. When it has cooled, add the yeast and the sugar, and beat in the flour gradually.
3. Beat well, and then cover, and set the mixture in a warm place over night, with only moderate warmth.
4. In the morning beat the eggs till very light, and stir them into the butter.
5. Add more flour, sufficient to make a dough, which must be soft.
6. Knead this dough lightly, and stand it away to rise.
7. When it has risen well, take one-half of the dough and roll it on the biscuit board, and then cut into doughnuts, using a large cake or biscuit cutter.
8. Take a small cutter and make a small hole in the center of each doughnut.
9. Spread a clean towel over the table, and dust it lightly with flour, and let the doughnuts stand upon it for half an hour, being well covered.
10. Let the frying kettle be ready with boiling fat or lard, deep enough that the doughnuts can swim in it.
11. Put the doughnuts into the boiling lard, and let them fry to a golden brown.
12. When a beautiful dark golden brown, drain out of the pan with a skimmer, place on a hot dish, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and serve hot.

title: Doughnuts With Hop Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of light hop or potato yeast sponge
- a pint of new warm milk
- three beaten eggs
- a cup of butter or lard
- one large cup of sugar
- a large spoonful of cinnamon
- salt
- flour
- boiling lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix the yeast sponge with warm milk, beaten eggs, butter or lard, sugar, cinnamon and salt
2. Beat this well and sift in flour to make a soft dough
3. Set this in a warm place to rise
4. When it is light roll it out on the board a little thicker than pie crust
5. Cut with a knife in squares of about three inches
6. Let them stand a little
7. Fry them in plenty of boiling lard in a pot half full of boiling lard to ensure their being light and nicely browned

title: Doughnuts Without Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- a pint of sour milk or buttermilk
- three quarters of a pound of sugar
- a small teaspoonful of saleratus dissolved in a little hot water
- two well beaten eggs
- flour
- half a teaspoonful of lemon extract
- half a nutmeg grated
- boiling lard for frying

## Directions

1. Combine butter, sour milk or buttermilk, sugar, saleratus, and beaten eggs
2. Add enough flour to make a smooth dough
3. Flavor with half a teaspoonful of lemon extract and half a nutmeg grated
4. Rub a little flour over a breadboard or table
5. Roll the dough to a quarter of an inch in thickness
6. Cut it in squares, diamonds, or round cakes
7. Fry in boiling lard
8. These cakes may be made in rings and fried

title: Drawn Butter
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- two teaspoonfuls flour
- 1/2 lb. butter
- 4 tablespoons boiling water

## Directions

1. Stir flour and butter into 4 tablespoons of boiling water
2. Stir until all of the butter is melted

title: Drawn Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Chopped Parsley
- Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. This sauce is made simply by melting butter and adding a little chopped parsley.
2. Add the juice of a lemon, if desired.
3. It is used as a garnish for broiled meat, fish, chicken, etc.

title: Dried Cherries
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar
- whisky

## Directions

1. Stew the cherries with a little sugar for a short time; then spread them on dishes and set them in the sun
2. As they dry pour the syrup over them, a little at a time, for 3 or 4 days; when dry, pack them away in boxes or jars, putting a layer of cherries with a layer of sugar, and a little whisky sprinkled between

title: Dried Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Dried Apples, Peaches or any Kind of Dried Fruit
- 2 Cups of Louisiana Molasses
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 1/4 Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- 3/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cloves and Mace
- 3/4 Pound of Stoned Raisins
- 3/4 Grated Nutmeg
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Salt
- The Juice of a Lemon
- 1 1/2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder or 1 Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Soak the fruit over night.
2. Chop very fine, and simmer in a saucepan with the molasses for two hours and a half.
3. Let it cool.
4. Seed the raisins.
5. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, until very light.
6. Add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till light.
7. Add the milk and then the fruit and molasses, stirring carefully.
8. Beat hard for five minutes.
9. Add the flour, which you will have sifted with the baking powder, and beat vigorously for five minutes.
10. If you use soda, dissolve it at this point into a tablespoonful of boiling water, and add, mixing well.
11. Add the raisins, and beat vigorously, and the spices and salt.
12. Add the juice of the lemon, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
13. Bake in a moderate oven for two hours.
14. Use the broom wisp to test; if it comes out clean and dry, then the cake is done.

title: Dried or Split Pea Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Dried or Split Peas
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 1 Small Bunch of Celery
- 1 Bunch of Parsley
- 1 Quart of Good Milk or Cream
- 2 Onions
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Soak the peas overnight after washing them in cold water and rejecting all that float.
2. In the morning drain off the water and cover the peas again with one quart of boiling water, setting them back on the stove and letting them cook slowly until tender.
3. Cut up the onion and parsley and celery into fine pieces and add to the boiling peas.
4. When perfectly tender remove from the stove and press through a sieve or colander and add the salt and pepper.
5. Return the soup to the fire and let it boil up once.
6. Just before serving add the rich cream or milk, stirring well.
7. Serve with Croqutons or Oyster Crackers.

title: Dried Split-Pea Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a good beef marrow-bone of one or two pounds weight
- the remains of roast beef-bones and gravy
- a slice of ham
- a gallon of cold water
- two cups of split peas or small white beans
- two carrots
- two turnips
- two large onions or three small ones
- a stalk of celery cut in pieces
- a bunch of thyme
- a teaspoonful of mixed black and red pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put these in a pot with a gallon of cold water
2. Throw in the pot two cups of split peas or small white beans, two carrots, two turnips, two large onions or three small ones, a stalk of celery cut in pieces, a bunch of thyme, and a teaspoonful of mixed black and red pepper
3. When the vegetables are quite soft, which will be in about two hours, take the soup from the fire
4. Strain it through a sieve or coarse cloth
5. Add salt, and put on the fire again and boil for a few moments
6. Then pour it over toasted bread

title: Drop Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1/2 lb. butter
- 5 eggs
- mace and nutmeg to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Drop them on tin sheets
3. Bake them in a quick oven

title: Dry or Baked Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chopped Meat, Left-Over
- ½ Pint of Water, or Left-Over Broth
- 1 Pint of Cooked Chopped Tomatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- Potatoes
- Pepper
- Cayenne
- Salt

## Directions

1. Chop and mince the meat very fine
2. Chop the potatoes fine, or in square inch pieces
3. Mince the parsley, bay leaf, onion and garlic fine
4. Mix all together with the meat and potatoes and season highly with pepper and Cayenne, salting to taste
5. Add the tablespoonful of butter and bake in a moderate oven for about one hour

title: Duchess Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen boiled potatoes
- the yolk of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Take one dozen boiled potatoes, mash them, and form into small square cakes.
2. Criss-cross them gracefully with a knife, brush with the yolk of an egg beaten in water or milk, and set in the oven to bake to a delicate brown.

title: Duck Roasted
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- potatoes
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- water
- giblets
- gravy
- catsup
- brown flour
- lemons

## Directions

1. Pick, draw and singe the duck; wash it out carefully and stuff it with potatoes, mashed with butter, onions, and parsley.
2. Put it down to a good fire or in a hot oven, pour in a cup of water.
3. Let it roast for half an hour if it is fat and tender, longer if tough.
4. As soon as the duck is cleaned, boil the giblets, and before serving, chop them up fine with some of the gravy from the duck, two tablespoonfuls of catsup, a lump of butter, and a little brown flour.
5. Have lemons cut on side dishes, or serve with brown duck sauce.

title: Duck Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions (six large)
- butter (two large spoonfuls)
- pepper
- salt
- mushroom catsup
- vinegar (a cup)
- wine (a cup)

## Directions

1. Boil six large onions; change the water two or three times, while it is boiling, which takes away the strong taste.
2. When soft, chop and put them in a saucepan with two large spoonfuls of butter, a little pepper and salt;
3. now add either mushroom catsup, a cup of vinegar or a cup of wine, whichever is preferred.

title: Ducks a la Mode
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 fine ducks
- butter
- flour
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- 1/2 pint of red wine
- 2 eschallots
- 1 anchovy
- sweet herbs
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the ducks into quarters
2. Fry them in butter a little brown
3. Pour off all the fat and throw a little flour over them
4. Add 1/2 pint of good gravy, 1/2 pint of red wine, 2 eschallots, 1 anchovy and a few sweet herbs
5. Cover them close
6. Let them stew 15 minutes
7. Take out the herbs
8. Skim off the grease and let the sauce be as thick as cream
9. Send it to table
10. Garnish it with lemon

title: Ducks Stewed With Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Ducks
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare the Ducks for cooking exactly as in the Stewed Wild Ducks recipe and proceed to cook accordingly.
2. Immediately after adding the boiling water add a can of mushrooms, and continue cooking according to recipe.
3. The mushrooms add a delicious flavor to the dish.
4. Serve hot, using the mushrooms as a garnish.

title: Ducks à la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Canvas-Back Ducks
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Tomatoes (fresh or canned)
- 12 Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1/2 Glass of Madeira Wine
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 Pint of Sauce Espagnole
- 1/2 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1/2 Ounce of Salt Pork

## Directions

1. Prepare the Ducks, and cook according to the first recipe provided.
2. After placing finally in the saucepan, add twelve small onions which have been nicely glazed.
3. Add the two carrots cut into small dice and which have been cooked in salted water for two minutes, before adding to the ducks.
4. Also add a half ounce of salt pork cut into half-inch pieces.
5. Let these cook for fifteen minutes with the ducks and serve on a hot dish with Croutons.

title: Dutch Blanc Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce of isinglass
- 1 quart of water
- yolks of 8 eggs
- wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 ounce of isinglass in a quart of water
2. Let it boil down to 1 pint
3. Add to this the yolks of 8 eggs, well beaten
4. Season highly with wine and sugar to the taste
5. Put it in a shape
6. Turn it out when cold

title: Duxelle Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Madeira Sauce
- 1/2 Glass of White Wine
- 12 Mushrooms
- 2 Shallots
- 1/2 Ounce of Beef Tongue
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put half a pint of Madeira Sauce and a half glass of White wine in a saucepan.
2. Add the mushrooms, which must be chopped very fine.
3. Then add the shallots, which will have also been chopped fine and browned in butter.
4. Let this reduce slightly and add half an ounce of finely chopped cooked beef tongue.
5. Let all boil for five minutes and serve hot.

title: Easy Cake for Young Cooks
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- a teaspoonful of yeast powder per cup of flour
- the yolks of three eggs
- one cup of fine white sugar
- half a cup of water mixed with extract or wine
- the whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and to each cup put a teaspoonful of yeast powder
2. Beat the yolks of three eggs and one cup of fine white sugar together with half a cup of water mixed with extract or wine
3. beat this well in the yolks and sugar (only half a cup)
4. froth up the whites of the eggs, add them
5. last of all, beat in the flour with the powder in it
6. Bake quickly in square or jelly cake pans

title: Eau Sucre
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass of fresh water
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Allow one glass of fresh water to one tablespoonful of sugar, or to a half glass allow a half tablespoonful
2. Stir till the sugar is dissolved
3. Drink after a hearty meal

title: Economical Green Pea Family Soup With Egg Dumplings
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of shelled English peas for a large family
- a pint for a small family
- a veal bone or half a chicken
- a broiled steak, bones, nice scraps, or a small beef marrow bone
- a gallon of water
- one coffeecupful of the green peas
- the rest of the peas
- two eggs
- a spoonful of milk and flour
- a spoonful of butter
- a little eschalot
- lettuce heads

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a veal bone or half a chicken; if a pint only of peas is used add any broiled steak, bones, nice scraps, or a small beef marrow bone; set it on the fire with a gallon of water and let it boil two hours
2. Tie up in a muslin bag, one coffeecupful of the green peas; let the others stand in a cool place until wanted
3. Put this bag of peas into the pot with the beef and chicken stock, and let them boil until the peas are perfectly done
4. Skim out the peas, meat and bones, and add the rest of the peas, and let them boil gently
5. While these are cooking pour the peas in the bag into a pan and mash them smoothly
6. Then add to them a batter made with two eggs, a spoonful of milk and flour
7. Add to the boiling peas a spoonful of butter and a little eschalot, if the flavor of onion is liked
8. Then drop the batter in gently, a little at a time, in small round dumplings, and when they boil up your soup is ready to serve
9. This is an excellent spring soup, and is improved by adding lettuce heads, but they must be taken out before the dumplings are put in, as they give a dark color if left in too long

title: Economical Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- an unbaked loaf of bread
- new milk

## Directions

1. Put as much milk as will make a batter with the bread
2. Beat it well
3. Let it stand one hour
4. Bake it in small ones on the hoe, thick enough to split

title: Economical Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup boiling water
- 2 cups sugar
- 4 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons yeast powder
- salt
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Pour 1 cup of boiling water over 2 cups of sugar
2. Separate whites and yolks of 4 eggs, and beat well the whites to a stiff froth
3. Add the yolks to the sugar and hot water, beating quickly
4. Add 2 cups of flour in which 2 teaspoons of yeast powder have been mixed
5. Add a small pinch of salt and flavoring preferred
6. Lastly, add the whites, mixing them as quickly and as lightly as possible
7. Bake at once in a quick oven

title: Eels a la Maitre d'Hotel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of eel
- 1 sprig of thyme
- 1 bay leaf
- 6 cloves
- 1 dozen allspice
- 1 onion chopped fine
- 1 clove of garlic
- A sauce a la Tartare
- A half cup of lemon or citron juice
- Fried potato balls
- Salt water
- A glass of Claret
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut and clean the eel as directed and boil about twenty minutes in salt and water, putting a great quantity of salt into the kettle, and adding the thyme, bay leaf, cloves, allspice, chopped onion, garlic and a glass of Claret.
2. Remove the eels after cooking twenty minutes and serve on a dish garnished with parsley and fried potato balls.
3. Serve with a sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel, only be careful to add about a half cup of citron or lemon juice to the sauce.

title: Eels a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Eel
- 1 Cup of Hot Vinegar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Basil
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Onion Minced Very Fine
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- Juice of 2 Lemons
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eels and put in a pot of boiling water.
2. To be sure that they disgorge thoroughly add a cup of hot vinegar.
3. After boiling fifteen minutes, take them out and cut into three-inch pieces.
4. Take one tablespoonful of butter, put into a frying pan, and add the eels and one can of mushrooms poured over.
5. Add two tablespoonfuls of flour rubbed smoothly in butter as the eels begin to fry.
6. Pour over, when this begins to brown, a glassful of white wine.
7. Add parsley, thyme, bay leaf, sweet basil, and an onion minced like the herbs, very fine.
8. As the grease rises, floating, skim it off.
9. Add a little Cayenne, and salt to taste, if necessary.
10. When quite done, take the yolks of three eggs and mix thoroughly with the juice of two lemons.
11. Take the eels off the fire and add the lemon juice and eggs and be careful not to set back on the fire when once these are added, or the eggs will curdle.
12. Place on a dish and garnish with Croutons and slices of lemon and serve hot.

title: Egg and Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- milk
- butter
- cornstarch
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs for five minutes till hard
2. Take out of the water and cool
3. Take off the shells and separate the whites from the yolks
4. Rub the yolks through a sieve and chop the whites very fine
5. Have the milk ready to boil and rub the butter and cornstarch together
6. Add to the boiling milk
7. Then add the whites of the eggs and salt and pepper to taste
8. Prepare buttered toast and cover it with a layer of white sauce
9. Add a layer of the yolks of the eggs
10. Add another layer of the sauce and another layer of the yolks
11. Add the remainder of the sauce
12. Sprinkle the top with a little salt and pepper
13. Set in the oven and let it stand two minutes
14. Serve hot

title: Egg and Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful of sherry or port wine
- 1 tablespoonful or teaspoonful of sugar
- Grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the yolk of the egg to a cream in a tumbler, and add the sugar, and beat till very light.
2. Add the sherry or port, according to taste.
3. Beat the whites to a stiff froth, and then beat it into the yolk and wine.
4. Add a little grated nutmeg.

title: Egg Balls for Mock-Turtle Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
- white of two raw ones
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a paste of the yolks of four hard-boiled eggs and the white of two raw ones; season with salt and cayenne pepper.
2. Take bits of the paste the size of small marbles, run them in flour and roll into balls; fry carefully in butter and drop into the soup.

title: Egg Corn Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cornmeal
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter or Lard
- 1 Pint of Milk (Buttermilk if Possible)
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the meal with boiling water sufficient to melt.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs very, very light.
3. Add the corn meal and melted butter, and the salt, and beat till very light, moistening with the milk.
4. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Beat all well together.
6. Pour into a buttered or greased pan, or into shallow tins, and bake quickly.

title: Egg Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Cup of Bread, Wet and Squeezed Thoroughly
- 1 Chopped Onion
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Teaspoonful of Butter
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Sage
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wet the bread and squeeze thoroughly
2. Chop the eggs fine and mix with the bread
3. Mince the herbs and add
4. Season well with salt and pepper
5. Chop the onion and fry it in one tablespoonful of butter
6. As it browns add the bread, into which you have mixed the sifted sage, if desired
7. Add, as it fries, the half square inch of ham, minced very fine
8. Season again to taste, and let all fry about ten minutes
9. Take off the stove and stuff the fowl or fish and proceed with the arrangement for baking

title: Egg Kisses
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four whites of eggs
- one-half pound powdered sugar
- extract of rose or lemon

## Directions

1. beat well
2. Flavor with extract of rose or lemon
3. bake quickly

title: Egg Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of wheat flour
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 dessertspoonful of butter
- 2 eggs
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs light
2. Add the other ingredients, stirring them well together
3. Put into patties and bake in rather a quick oven

title: Egg Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Cake of Compressed Yeast, or 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the Muffin batter as above and set to rise over night.
2. In the morning prepare the eggs as above indicated.
3. Blend and beat till light.
4. Then turn the muffin batter into greased muffin rings, and bake about twenty minutes in a hot oven.
5. The Muffins must be eaten hot.

title: Egg Plant
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- beaten egg
- pounded cracker
- hot lard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Parboil egg plant
2. Slice it and dip each piece in beaten egg and roll it in pounded cracker
3. Drop it in hot lard and fry brown
4. Season with salt and pepper

title: Egg Plant
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
