## Ingredients

- egg plant
- egg
- pounded cracker
- lard
- salt

## Directions

1. Parboil egg plant
2. Slice it and dip each piece in beaten egg and roll it in pounded cracker
3. Drop it in hot lard and fry brown
4. Season with salt

title: Egg Plants
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plants
- bacon
- onion
- bread crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Parboil them, after splitting them in half
2. Scrape out the middle
3. Chop up the middle with little slips of bacon, onion cut up small, and crumbs of bread mixed with a raw egg
4. Fill the skins with this stuffing and bake them nicely
5. Grate bread on the surface of them

title: Egg Plants
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plants
- little slips of bacon
- onion
- crumbs of bread
- raw egg

## Directions

1. Parboil them, after splitting them in half
2. Scrape out the middle, which you chop up with little slips of bacon, onion cut up small, and crumbs of bread mixed with a raw egg
3. Fill the skins with this stuffing and bake them nicely
4. Grate bread on the surface of them

title: Egg Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 2 Chopped Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Bay Leaf, Minced Fine
- 1 Onion
- 6 Peppers
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Pint of Veal or Chicken Broth
- Juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Chop the onions and put in the saucepan with the butter and bay leaf.
2. Stir in the flour to thicken and moisten with the broth.
3. Mix well, and add the nutmeg, and salt and pepper to taste.
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs separately with the juice of half a lemon.
5. Pour gradually into the sauce, but do not let it boil after they are added.
6. Press through a sieve, and when ready to serve, sprinkle with two chopped hard-boiled eggs and a teaspoonful of minced parsley.

title: Egg Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- yolks of 5 hard boiled eggs
- a little cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Melt 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
2. stir into it the yolks of 5 hard boiled eggs
3. mash very smooth
4. add a little cayenne pepper and salt

title: Egg Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of chicken water
- 1 dessertspoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- a little salt
- 4 or 6 hard boiled eggs, chopped fine

## Directions

1. Just before serving the chickens, take 1/2 pint of the chicken water
2. stew into it a dessertspoonful of butter, a tablespoonful of flour, and a little salt
3. set it on the fire and let it thicken, stirring all the time
4. add 4 or 6 hard boiled eggs, chopped fine

title: Egg Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- yolks of 4 or 5 eggs
- cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Melt 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
2. Stir into it the yolks of 4 or 5 eggs, hard boiled, mashed very smooth
3. Add a little cayenne pepper and salt

title: Egg Sauce With Lemon
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- a cup of melted butter
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice or vinegar or catsup

## Directions

1. Boil six eggs
2. When cold, take off the shells, and slice them into a cup of melted butter
3. Add pepper and salt
4. Stir constantly while heating
5. Add the juice of a lemon, or vinegar, or catsup as preferred

title: Egg Toast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a glass of thick cream
- a cup of white sugar
- 3 macaroons pounded
- a few almonds
- a little grated lemon
- 8 egg yolks and 3 egg whites
- slices of fried bread and butter

## Directions

1. Let the cream, sugar, pounded macaroons, almonds, and grated lemon boil
2. Add the yolks of 8 eggs and whites of 3 eggs
3. Beat the whole up over a bath
4. Serve on slices of fried bread and butter for tea

title: Egg-Nog
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of ten eggs
- ten tablespoonfuls of pulverized sugar
- three pints of new milk
- one pint of the best brandy
- whites of ten eggs

## Directions

1. Take the yolks of ten eggs; add to them ten tablespoonfuls of pulverized sugar, three pints of new milk, and one pint of the best brandy
2. Beat up the whites the last thing, and stir in, after the liquor is poured in

title: Egg-Nog
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 fine, fresh Creole eggs
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 pound of white granulated sugar
- 1 gill of fine French Cognac
- 1 grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks to a cream, add the sugar, and beat to a cream.
2. Blend all thoroughly, beating till very, very light.
3. Pour over the boiling milk, stirring well.
4. When thoroughly blended add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and the liquor, and serve hot.

title: Eggplant Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Young Eggplants
- ¾ Pint of Milk
- Salt and Pepper
- Flour

## Directions

1. Slice the eggplants nicely and thin.
2. Boil them in milk in which you have put salt and pepper to taste.
3. Pass the eggplant in flour, dusting lightly, and fry in boiling lard.
4. The eggplant must float in the lard.
5. Drain on brown paper in the mouth of the oven and serve hot.

title: Eggs a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1/2 pint of sauce a la poulette

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard and slice.
2. Pour over a Sauce a la Poulette.
3. Serve hot.

title: Eggs And Butter Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- a cup of butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- lemon
- walnut catsup

## Directions

1. Boil six eggs hard
2. When cold, peel them and put them into a cup of butter, melted
3. Mix with a little flour, make it hot
4. Stir in pepper and salt
5. Add lemon and walnut catsup as preferred

title: Eggs Fondus
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 4 heaping teaspoonfuls of Gruyere cheese, grated
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a saucepan.
2. Add the butter, the grated cheese, a little salt and pepper.
3. Place the saucepan on a hot fire.
4. Stir the mixture around and around till the edges begin to thicken.
5. When of the consistency of a thick starch take off the fire.
6. Serve immediately on buttered toast.

title: Eggs Scrambled in Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of finely minced boiled ham
- 1 tablespoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Mince the ham very fine.
2. Break the eggs, one by one, into a saucer, and add to the bowl in which you have minced the ham.
3. Mix all together.
4. Place a tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan.
5. Add the eggs and ham, stir briskly.
6. When it comes to the consistency of starch take off the fire.
7. Serve hot on buttered toast.

title: Eggs Scrambled with Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of fruit marmalade

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for eggs scrambled in ham, using the marmalade instead of the ham.

title: Eggs with Asparagus Tips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of asparagus tips
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of milk
- salt and pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Boil the asparagus tips.
2. Put the eggs into a saucepan, with the butter, after seasoning well with salt and pepper, and mixing the milk.
3. Stir a second, and throw in the asparagus.
4. Proceed to scramble as in the preceding recipe.
5. Serve on buttered toast.

title: Eggs with Browned Butter and Vinegar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put four ounces of butter into an omelet pan over the fire
2. as it begins to sputter, break the eggs into it without disturbing the yolks
3. season with pepper and salt
4. fry the eggs carefully and remove them on to the dish in which they are to be served
5. Put two ounces more butter in the pan
6. fry it of a brown color
7. put to the butter two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
8. pour it over the eggs and serve

title: Eggs, Au Gratin, for Lent
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- grated cheese
- sweet milk
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard
2. peel and cut them in slices
3. lay them in a deep dish in close circular rows
4. Make a sauce of a tablespoonful of butter, the yolks of four eggs, a little grated cheese and half a cup of sweet milk
5. Stir this over the fire until it thickens
6. pour it over the eggs
7. strew some bread crumbs on the top
8. bake for about ten minutes
9. send to table hot

title: Elderberry Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Peck of Elderberries
- 2.5 Gallons of Water
- 0.5 Pound of Sugar
- 0.5 Pint of Hop Yeast

## Directions

1. Bruise the Elderberries
2. Dilute with the water and boil for ten minutes
3. Strain well and press out the juice from the berries
4. Measure the whole, and add the sugar
5. While half warm add the yeast
6. Let the whole ferment for ten days
7. Cork well
8. After three months draw off the wine and bottle for use

title: Elderflower Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Tender White Elder Flowers
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- White of 2 Eggs
- Creole Fritter Batter

## Directions

1. Select a sufficient quantity of beautiful, tender white flowers, and soak them in the whites of two eggs, beaten to a snowy froth, and well blended with white sugar.
2. Dip them by spoonfuls into the fritter batter, and fry according to directions.

title: Elixir of Violets
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Syrup of Violets
- 4 Ounces of Filtered Raspberry Juice
- 4 Pounds of Sugar
- 2 Quarts of the Best Brandy or Alcohol
- 2 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup with the sugar and water, and let it cool.
2. Mix the violet syrup and the Raspberry Juice, and add it to the alcohol.
3. Add the sugar syrup, when cool, mix all thoroughly, strain and bottle tightly.

title: Elysian Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of pippins
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 fresh lemon
- 6 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Grate 1 lb. of pippins
2. Stir to a cream 1 lb. of sugar, the same of butter
3. Add the grated apples and the rind of 1 fresh lemon
4. Whip very light 6 eggs
5. Beat all well together
6. Line a dish with puff paste
7. Pour in the batter, and bake 1/2 an hour

title: Endive Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Endives
- French Dressing
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chervil, Chopped Very Fine

## Directions

1. Prepare the endives in the same manner as the lettuce.
2. When ready to serve, add the chervil and the French dressing.
3. Serve immediately.

title: Endive With Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Heads of Chicory
- 4 Tablespoonfuls Butter
- 2 Glasses of Cream or Milk
- A Pinch of Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Pick nicely three heads of Chicorfie casting away all the outer green leaves.
2. Then wash the heads carefully in fresh cold water; drain and wash again, and blanch for ten minutes in boiling salted water; remove and throw into cold water to cool.
3. Then drain of all the water, and chop the Chicorfie very fine.
4. Put in a saucepan with four tablespoonfuls of butter and let cook for a quarter of an hour.
5. Pour two glassfuls of milk or rich cream over it; add a pinch of grated nutmeg, and salt and pepper to taste.
6. Mix all thoroughly together for five minutes on the stove; then remove, put in a dish, garnish nicely with Croutons fried in butter, and serve hot.

title: Endives With Gravy
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Heads of Chicory
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1/2 Pound of Bacon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Pint of Veal or Chicken Consomme

## Directions

1. Clean and pick the Chicorfie well, pare off all the outer leaves, and wash the heads well in several waters.
2. Then drain and put to blanch in salted water for ten minutes; at the end of this time, throw them in the cold water to cool; drain and cut into quarters.
3. Put the piece of bacon in the bottom of a sautoire or stewpan, and add an onion and carrot and herb bouquet, minced very fine.
4. Lay the Chicorfie on top of this, season with a teaspoonful of salt and a half teaspoonful of black pepper, and cover with a buttered paper.
5. Then set the sautoire in the oven, and let the Chicorfie cook for ten minutes, when it will be a golden brown.
6. Moisten with half a pint of veal or chicken consomme, cover and again set in the oven for thirty minutes.
7. Arrange the Chicorfie on a hot dish; strain the sauce, pour over and serve.

title: Epicurean Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- one tablespoonful strong dry mustard
- pepper
- salt
- one-half pint oysters

## Directions

1. Take 3 tablespoonfuls of butter and one of strong, dry mustard and put them in a chopping dish
2. Add pepper and salt
3. When they commence boiling put in one-half pint of oysters without the liquor
4. Have a hot dish in readiness
5. Take out the oysters with a fork when cooked, and continue to replace them until all are done

title: Epigram of Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Breasts of Lamb
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Pepper
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter or Olive Oil
- Grated Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Take two breasts of Lamb; tie them and put them to boil in soup stock for forty-five minutes
2. Then drain well and extract all the bones
3. Press them down with a heavy weight on top
4. When thoroughly cold, cut each breast into three triangular-shaped pieces
5. Dip them in olive oil, or melted lard, or butter, and season with the salt and pepper
6. Roll each piece in fresh bread crumbs grated
7. Broil on a slow fire, allowing four minutes to each side
8. Serve with a pint of hot Macedoine or any garnish that may be desired, arranging the breast over the garnish

title: Eugene Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- cream
- 1 large spoonful finely powdered sugar
- 1 quart thick cream
- bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Break 5 eggs; beat the yolks with some cream and a large spoonful of finely powdered sugar
2. Put into a saucepan 1 quart of thick cream, with a few bitter almonds, and set it on the fire
3. When it has once boiled, take out the almonds, and add the beaten eggs
4. Stir all together, and keep it hot for some time, without allowing it to boil, observing to stir always the same way
5. When thick enough, pour it into cups for the table

title: Eve's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 apples
- 6 ounces of suet
- 6 ounces of pounded cracker
- 6 ounces of currants
- 6 ounces of white sugar
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix together 6 eggs, 6 apples chopped fine, 6 ounces of suet, 6 ounces of pounded cracker, 6 ounces of currants, 6 ounces of white sugar, a little salt and nutmeg
2. boil it 3 hours
3. serve it with wine, sugar and butter sauce

title: Everton Toffy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of brown sugar
- one-quarter of a pound of butter
- lemon or vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. In a shallow vessel, melt together brown sugar and butter
2. Stir well together for fifteen minutes, or until the mixture becomes brittle when dropped in water
3. Add lemon or vanilla flavoring before the cooking is complete
4. Butter a flat plate
5. Pour the toffy on it to cool
6. When partly cold, mark it off in squares with a knife

title: Excellent Family Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a peck of sifted flour
- half a pint of family yeast, or a gill of brewers' yeast
- new milk, or milk and water warm
- a cup of shortening
- a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Take a peck of sifted flour, half a pint of family yeast, or a gill of brewers' yeast
2. wet all up soft with new milk, or milk and water warm
3. Add a cup of shortening, and a teaspoonful of salt
4. Knead it faithfully, and set it in a warm place to rise
5. It is better to take the dough when risen, and work it down again; but some dislike the trouble, and bake it as soon as it rises

title: Express Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- ½ lb. of sugar
- ½ lb. of butter
- grated nutmeg
- rich paste for edge of dish

## Directions

1. beat up eggs
2. put them into a stewpan with sugar, butter, and grated nutmeg
3. put it on the fire, stirring it till it thickens
4. pour it into a bowl to cool
5. set a rich paste around the edge of the dish
6. pour in the pudding
7. bake it in a moderate oven

title: Family Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ quarts of flour
- 2 eggs
- 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 pint of water
- 1 gill of yeast
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar, and 4 tablespoonfuls of the flour
2. Stir the yeast and water with the salt well together, and add gradually to the egg
3. Sprinkle some of the flour in the bottom of the breadpan
4. Pour in the batter
5. Sprinkle some of the flour on the top, and set it in a cool place to rise
6. When risen enough, work in the remainder of the flour
7. Make the batter at 12 o'clock in winter, and 4 in summer

title: Family Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of sifted flour
- ½ pint of family yeast
- new or skim milk
- a little shortening
- a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Wet the flour with milk, adding shortening and salt
2. Knead it well
3. Have the dough soft if wheat flour, and stiff if made of rye

title: Family Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of rice
- 6 ounces of flour
- the yolks and whites of 9 eggs
- 1 lb. of lump sugar pounded and sifted
- 1 ounce of carraway seeds

## Directions

1. Beat this 1 hour
2. Bake it for the same length of time in a quick oven

title: Family Pie Crust, Short
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sifted flour
- half a pound of sweet lard or beef drippings
- a dessertspoonful of salt
- cold water
- a bit of volatile salts, the size of a small nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put a pound of sifted flour into a bowl, work into it half a pound of sweet lard or beef drippings, with a dessertspoonful of salt
2. When it is thoroughly mixed put to it enough cold water to bind together
3. Flour the paste slab, or table, and rolling pin
4. Take a part of the paste and roll it to less than a quarter of an inch in thickness
5. A bit of volatile salts, the size of a small nutmeg, dissolved in a little hot water and put to the paste, will make it more light and delicate

title: Famous Apple Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange or lemon
- i lb. of apples
- Yolks of 8 eggs
- J lb. of sugar
- i lb. of butter
- Nice puff paste

## Directions

1. Boil until tender, 1 orange or lemon, with i lb. of apples
2. Beat until they can be passed through a wire sieve
3. Add the yolks of 8 eggs, J lb. of sugar, i lb. of butter
4. Mix all well
5. Fill the dish
6. Put nice puff paste on the rim, and bake 1 hour

title: Fancy Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 loaf of French bread
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Slice a loaf of French bread and pour 1/2 pint of milk over it
2. take another 1/2 pint of milk and mix with 4 well beaten eggs, sugar and cinnamon to the taste
3. dip the slices in the mixture, and fry in butter or lard

title: Fancy Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- A loaf of French bread sliced
- J pint of milk
- Other J pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- Sugar and cinnamon to the taste
- Lard or butter for frying

## Directions

1. Take a loaf of French bread sliced, J pint of milk poured over it
2. Mix the other J pint with 4 well beaten eggs
3. Add sugar and cinnamon to the taste
4. Dip each slice of bread in the mixture, and fry it in lard or butter
5. Serve it with a sauce of sugar, wine and nutmeg

title: Fancy Punch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a tablespoonful of sugar
- a little raspberry
- a little lemon, lime and pineapple juice
- Two parts of water to one of whiskey or brandy
- plenty of ice
- fruits in season

## Directions

1. Shake and strain in punch glass
2. put fruits in season when serving
3. use regular bar glass

title: Fare or Stuffing
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bunches of spinach
- white meat of a roast or boiled chicken
- 1 calf's brain
- 6 raw eggs
- 1 bottle of sweet oil
- salt
- pepper
- a pint of flour
- 2 eggs
- a little water
- grated parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil 2 bunches of spinach, chop fine with the white meat of a roast or boiled chicken
2. Add 1 calf's brain, previously boiled and cleaned
3. Stir the whole well in a bowl with 6 raw eggs, 1 bottle of sweet oil, and season with salt and pepper to taste
4. Take a pint of flour, add 2 eggs, with a little water, making the paste as stiff as possible
5. Roll out flat and thin, put in a layer of the stuffing all the length of the paste
6. Double the paste over and cut lengthways with a paste wheel, and also cut crossways, making small squares
7. Put squares of paste in a pot of boiling water, and boil steadily for 1/2 hour
8. Strain and put 1 layer of squares in bottom of deep dish
9. Sprinkle grated parmesan cheese
10. Pour some of the daube gravy with mushrooms, previously made thin
11. Add another layer of squares, cheese and gravy, and so on
12. Serve hot

title: Farina
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Cup of Farina
- 1 Quart of Water or Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Add the salt to the milk or water
2. Sift in slowly sufficient Farina to make a thick gruel
3. Set in a double boiler
4. Let it cook for about a half hour, stirring frequently while it boils

title: Farina Gruel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Boiling Water
- 1 Cup of Fresh Milk
- 1 Large tablespoonful of Farina
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of White Sugar
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Take a cup of boiling water, one cup of milk, and a pinch of salt
2. Slightly salt the water
3. Set one boiler within another, the latter boiler being filled with boiling water
4. Stir the Farina into the cup of boiling water, and let it boil, stirring constantly, till it thickens
5. Add the milk, stirring it gradually, and let it boil about fifteen minutes longer
6. Sweeten, and when it is cool give to the child
7. Warm when it is needed, with a little boiling water

title: Fast-Day Broth
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Carrots
- 6 Large White Onions
- 6 Turnips
- 1/2 Pound of Beans or Dried Split Peas
- 1 Small Head of Cabbage
- 1 Parsnip
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1/2 Pound of Butter, or 1 Large Tablespoonful of Lard
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Red Pepper Pod, Without the Seed
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into fine, thin slices the carrots, turnips and parsnips
2. Cut and chop fine the cabbage, celery and onions
3. Put all in a saucepan and add one glass of water and a quarter of a pound of butter, using the butter preferably to the lard
4. Add the parsley, chopped very fine
5. Let all boil till the water has evaporated
6. Add one pint of red or white beans or split peas, which have been soaked overnight
7. Add three quarts of water and the pepper pod
8. Let all simmer well for three hours
9. If the beans are perfectly tender at this point, drain or press through a colander
10. Return to the fire and add the seasonings
11. Let all boil up once and then serve with Croutons

title: Federal Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coffee cup of butter
- 1 1/2 coffee cups of sugar
- 1 coffee cup of milk
- 3 coffee cups of flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together
2. Bake

title: Fig Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Butter
- 2 Cups Sugar
- 1 Cup Milk
- 3 Cups of Seeded Raisins
- 1 Pound of Figs, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Egg
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/4 Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream and add the sugar.
2. Beat very light, and add the egg.
3. Beat well and add the milk; carefully and gradually add the flour, into which you have sifted the baking powder.
4. Add the raisins and the figs, and beat thoroughly; add the grated nutmeg, and bake for an hour in a moderate oven.
5. Bake slowly, and use the broom wisp test.
6. If not done, bake longer, till the straw comes out clean and dry.

title: Fig Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh Figs
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- Creole Fritter Batter

## Directions

1. Pare the figs, taking off every particle of the skin.
2. Then cut up nicely, sprinkle with sugar and let them stand for half an hour.
3. Then add the juice to fritter batter and proceed as in Apple Fritters.

title: Fig Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Sponge Cake
- 1 Pound of Chopped Figs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/2 Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Make a layer cake — any good white, sponge or cream
2. Bake in jelly tins
3. Boil one pound of chopped figs, one cup of sugar, and one-half cup of water, till the figs form a jelly
4. Spread, when cool, between the layers of cake, while they are still hot, and proceed as in Almond Layer Cake

title: Fig Pickles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- salt
- water
- pure, strong vinegar
- light brown sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves
- horse radish

## Directions

1. Gather the figs when fully swollen, but not quite ripe, leaving the stems on
2. Soak them 10 or 12 hours in moderately strong brine (salt and water)
3. Take them out of this brine
4. Rinse with clear water and place in glass or stone jars
5. Have a kettle of pure, strong vinegar
6. Add a gallon of light brown sugar to each gallon of vinegar, with such spices as you prefer for flavoring, cinnamon and a few cloves
7. Place this kettle of vinegar over the fire, stirring and dissolving the sugar
8. When the vinegar comes to a simmer, pour it over the figs, covering them about an inch
9. Repeat this process of scalding the vinegar and pouring over figs about 3 times
10. A small piece of horse radish is an improvement

title: Fig Preserves
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- sugar
- water
- alum

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water syrup as directed in previous recipe.
2. Let the figs be firm, not dead ripe or they will boil to a mass.
3. They should be laid in alum the day before they are to be preserved, then taken out, washed, and put into the boiling syrup.
4. Boil for three hours, or until transparent.
5. Bottle as usual and seal up with wax.

title: Fig Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- Figs
- Sugar
- Water
- Lemons
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Take the weight in sugar of the quantity of figs to be preserved; the figs must be ripe.
2. Cover the figs with cold water for twelve hours.
3. Simmer in water enough to cover them until tender, and spread out upon a sieve to cool and harden.
4. Make a syrup of the sugar, a cup of cold water being allowed for every pound.
5. Boil until clear of scum, put in the figs, and simmer for ten minutes.
6. Take them out and spread upon the dishes in the sun.
7. Add the lemons and the ginger; boil the syrup thick, give the figs another boil of fifteen minutes and fill the jars three-quarters of the way up to the top.
8. Then, fill up with boiling syrup, cover, and, when cold, seal up.

title: Fig Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Six ounces of suet
- 6 ounces of bread crumbs
- 6 ounces of sugar
- J lb. of figs chopped
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 nutmeg
- J glass brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of yeast powder

## Directions

1. Steam 3 hours
2. Serve with a wine sauce

title: Fig Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh Figs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 1/2 Cup of Butter

## Directions

1. Peel the figs and cut fine.
2. Then make a batter of the eggs, flour and milk, according to recipe.
3. When smooth, add the fruit.
4. Turn into a baking pan, and bake for an hour.
5. Serve with Wine Sauce.

title: Figs a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Ripe Figs
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Stem and peel and cut the figs in quarters.
2. Place in a dish, sprinkle well with sugar, and serve with their own syrup, ice cold.

title: Filbert Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 4 Ounces of Filberts
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 8 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Flour

## Directions

1. Blanch, peel and pound the almond and filbert kernels in a mortar till finely pulverized, adding the white of an egg, to keep the almonds from turning oily.
2. Beat the whites of three eggs to a froth.
3. Add the yolks, beaten separately to a cream with the sugar.
4. Incorporate thoroughly, and beat well.
5. Add four tablespoonfuls of flour, sifted well, and mix well.
6. Place in small cakes on wafer sheets of paper.
7. Bake in a moderate oven till a very light color.

title: Filet of Beef Larded
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- Lard sufficient to lard thoroughly
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 4 Cloves, if desired
- 1½ Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Glace
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine (or Water)
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Trim the filet nicely, removing the outer muscular skin.
2. Lard the filet well, using larding needles. The lard must be very thin, like a shoestring.
3. Fill the needles with the lard and push them through the filet as far as they will go.
4. Repeat this process all down the center and along the sides of the filet, about an inch apart, having the rows neat and even.
5. When well larded, dredge well with salt and pepper, rubbing this thoroughly into the beef.
6. Cut up one small onion, one bay leaf, and mash four cloves, and place in the bottom of the baking pan.
7. Lay the larded filet on this bed, the larded side being uppermost.
8. Put small bits of butter equal to a half tablespoonful on top, and bake in a quick oven thirty minutes.
9. To ascertain if sufficiently done, stick a fork into the filet; if the blood bubbles out, it is ready to serve.
10. Prepare the following brown sauce: Take one tablespoonful of butter and one of Glace, and three of water; rub smoothly and melt in a saucepan, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
11. When brown, add one glass of Madeira or Sherry wine and add a half cup of water.
12. Season well with salt and pepper.
13. Pour over the filet, which must be placed in a hot dish, and serve.

title: Filet of Beef Larded With Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- ½ Can of Mushrooms
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Glace
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine, if desired
- ½ Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Trim and lard the beef according to the directions given in the Filet of Beef Larded recipe, and bake in the same manner—rare.
2. When it has cooked for a half hour in a quick oven, it will be done.
3. Make a sauce as follows: Take one tablespoonful of butter and one of Glace and three of water; melt the butter and add the Glace, browning nicely without burning, and stirring constantly.
4. When brown, add one glass of Madeira or Sherry wine, if desired, and one-half pint of water.
5. Season well with salt and pepper.
6. Add a half can of mushrooms, chopped very fine.
7. Stir well and let it boil about ten minutes, so as not to be too thick nor yet too thin.
8. Place the filet in a hot dish and pour the sauce over and serve hot.

title: Filet of Beef Smothered With Mushrooms or Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- 1 Can of Mushrooms or ½ Can of Truffles
- 6 Onions, sliced fine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Sprigs each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Pint of Hot Water
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Sherry or White Wine

## Directions

1. Smother the beef, using a filet according to the recipe given for Smothered Beefsteak
2. After it has cooked about a half hour add one can of mushrooms and let it continue to simmer gently for an hour and a half longer
3. When ready to serve, add, if possible, a gill or a small wineglass of Sherry or White Wine and boil ten minutes longer
4. Put the filet in a dish, place the mushrooms over and around as a garnish, pour over the sauce and serve
5. If truffles are used instead of the mushrooms, add one-half can and proceed in exactly the same manner as when using the mushrooms

title: Filet of Beef With Stuffed Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- 1 Dozen Uniform-Sized Tomatoes
- 1 Cup of Mushrooms
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- ¾ Cup of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Grated Onion
- Sprigs of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Smother the filet according to the directions given above, adding two chopped tomatoes to the sauce
2. Take the tomatoes and cut off the stem end, scoop out the soft inside, being careful to retain the skins in proper shape
3. Take a half cup of mushrooms, one-half cup of stale bread crumbs which have been wet and squeezed, one clove of garlic chopped very fine, one grated onion, and a sprig of chopped parsley
4. Chop the mushrooms fine
5. Place a tablespoonful of butter into a frying pan, and when melted, add the bread crumbs which have been seasoned with salt and pepper and Cayenne, and mixed thoroughly with the chopped onion or garlic and the parsley
6. When these begin to fry, add the chopped mushrooms, stirring constantly for about five or eight minutes
7. Serve with the Filet and Stuffed Tomatoes

title: Filet of Beef With Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 6 Onions, sliced fine
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Sprigs each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- ½ Can of Tomatoes or 6 Large Fresh Ones
- 1 Pint of Hot Water
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Smother the filet in exactly the same manner as already described
2. When cooked for about a half hour, add one-half can of tomatoes and their juice, or six large fresh tomatoes sliced in their juice
3. Let the mixture simmer for an hour and a half longer
4. Season well and serve, pouring the gravy over the filet

title: Filet of Beef With Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Filet of Beef
- ½ Can of Truffles
- ½ Glass of Sherry
- 1 Pint of Broth or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Glace
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as in the preparation of Filet of Beef Larded.
2. When it has baked for a half hour, make a sauce as follows: Put one tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan; add two tablespoonfuls of Glace and add a half wineglass of Sherry and one pint of broth or water.
3. Let it boil slowly for ten minutes, and add one-half can of truffles, chopped very fine, if a la Périgeux; if aux truffes, cut in dice.
4. Let the sauce boil slowly twenty minutes longer, and then pour over the filet, serving hot.

title: Filet of Hare, Sauce Poivrade
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Hares
- 2 Onions
- 2 Carrots
- A Half Glass of White Wine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Cup of Broth or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take two fine Hares, clean neatly and cut the filets neatly from the rack.
2. Lard the surface carefully with fine needles.
3. Season well with salt and pepper.
4. Make a marinade with half a glass of White wine, one onion and one carrot, minced very fine.
5. Let all steep together for two hours.
6. Place the butter or lard in a baking dish with an onion and carrot, sliced fine.
7. Put the filets of Hare over this and set in the oven and let it cook for a half hour.
8. Baste frequently with the Hare's own juices.
9. Place the filets on a hot dish, add a tablespoonful of broth to the gravy in which the Hares were cooked.
10. Let all come to a boil on the stove.
11. Strain the gravy and pour over the filets.
12. Bring to the table hot and serve with a Sauce a la Poivrade.

title: Filet of Lamb Roasted and Larded
body:
## Ingredients

- A Filet of Lamb
- Lard Sufficient to Lard Thoroughly
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 4 Cloves, if desired
- 1½ Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Glace
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine, or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Trim the filet nicely, removing the outer muscular skin.
2. Lard the filet well, using larding needles. The lard must be very thin, like a shoestring.
3. The larding is done by filling the needles with the lard and pushing them through the filet as far as they will go.
4. Repeat this process all down the center and along the sides of the filet, about an inch apart, and have the rows neat and even.
5. When well larded, dredge well with salt and pepper, rubbing this thoroughly into the meat.
6. Cut up one small onion, one bay leaf, and mash four cloves, and place in the bottom of the baking pan.
7. Lay the larded filet on this bed, the larded side being uppermost.
8. Put small bits of butter equal to a half teaspoonful on top, and bake in a quick oven thirty minutes.
9. To ascertain if sufficiently done, stick a fork into the filet; if the blood bubbles out, it is ready to serve.
10. Prepare a Brown Sauce: Take one tablespoonful of butter and one of Glace and three of water, smoothly rubbed, and melt in a saucepan, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
11. When brown, add one glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine and a half cup of water.
12. Season well with salt and pepper.
13. Pour over the filet, which must be placed in a hot dish, and serve with fresh, young green peas.

title: Filet of Lamb à la Béchamel
body:
## Ingredients

- A Filet of Lamb
- A Sauce à la Béchamel

## Directions

1. Roast the lamb as in the manner given, and prepare a 'Sauce à la Béchamel.' (See recipe.)
2. Slice the lamb and pour over the sauce and serve.

title: Filling for Chocolate Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoonfuls of grated chocolate
- enough cream or milk to wet it
- 1 cupful sugar
- 1 egg well beaten
- vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Stir the ingredients over the fire until thoroughly mixed
2. Flavor with vanilla

title: Financier Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 6 Stoned Olives
- 12 Mushrooms
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne
- 1 pint of consommé

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, then remove from the fire and add the flour
2. Blend with a wooden spoon till smooth
3. Moisten with one pint of consommé till it reaches the consistency of cream
4. Then add the chopped mushrooms, stoned olives, pepper, salt and Cayenne
5. Before serving add the wine
6. Serve hot

title: Fine Rolls
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of butter
- a half pint of milk
- two spoonfuls of small beer yeast or a cup of home-made yeast
- a little salt
- two pounds of flour

## Directions

1. Warm half a cup of butter in a half pint of milk
2. add two spoonfuls of small beer yeast, or a cup of home-made yeast, and a little salt
3. pour this on to two pounds of flour
4. Let it rise an hour
5. knead it
6. make into loaves or rolls

title: Finest Puff Paste, for Puffs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one egg
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- cold water
- one pound of butter divided in six parts

## Directions

1. Heap one pound of flour in the centre of the bread-board, or slab; make a hollow in the centre
2. Break one egg into it, then add a teaspoonful of salt and a piece of butter the size of an egg
3. Mix these lightly together with a little cold water, adding the water a little at a time, until the flour is made a nice paste
4. Work it together, and roll it out to half an inch in thickness
5. Divide a pound of butter in six parts, spread one part over the paste, then fold it and roll it out again, until you can perceive the butter through
6. Spread over another part, fold it up, and roll out again, and so continue until all the butter is used, and the paste has been worked over six times
7. Flour the slab and rolling-pin, and roll it out to a quarter of an inch in thickness

title: Fish Au Gratin
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- butter
- onions
- white wine
- cullis
- bread crumbs
- stock
- lemon
- mushrooms
- parsley
- Worcestershire or Sultana sauce
- sugar
- cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the fins, and make an incision in the back
2. butter a saucepan, and put into it a teaspoonful of finely chopped onions and a wineglassful of white wine
3. place the fish in the pan, pouring over it 6 tablespoonfuls of cullis
4. sprinkle fine bread crumbs over it, and stick a few small pieces of butter about it
5. put it in a moderate oven for 20 minutes or 1/2 hour
6. carefully lift it into a silver or plated dish
7. Put into the saucepan 4 tablespoonfuls of stock
8. let it boil 5 minutes, stirring all the time
9. add the juice of a lemon, a teaspoonful of chopped mushrooms, 1 of minced parsley, and 1 of Worcestershire or Sultana sauce, a little sugar, cayenne pepper, and salt
10. beat all together, and pour over the fish
11. around which put some whole mushrooms and sliced lemon
12. Put it into the oven for 1/2 of an hour
13. pass the salamander over it and serve very hot

title: Fish Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of any fish
- 1 large onion
- 1 sprig of thyme
- 3 sprigs of parsley
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 egg
- Bread crumbs
- A dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Take the remains of any fish, season well with chopped parsley, onions, thyme and Cayenne, and a touch of garlic if the flavor is liked.
2. Then mince all well, adding a tablespoonful of butter and mix with one-third bread crumbs, mashed well.
3. Beat the yolk and white of an egg and roll the balls, shaped in the form of a cake, in this.
4. Pat with bread crumbs and fry in butter to a pale brown.

title: Fish Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- herbs
- pepper
- salt
- bread
- parsley
- white of an egg
- melted butter
- bread raspings

## Directions

1. Take any kind of fish, and cut off the flesh
2. put the heads, bones, fins, etc., on the fire, with a little water
3. season with herbs, a little pepper and salt, and stew for gravy
4. mince the flesh of the fish fine, and mix it with a third of bread, parsley, pepper and salt
5. add the white of an egg, and a small quantity of melted butter
6. Form it into the shape of a cake
7. cover it with raspings of bread
8. fry it a pale brown

title: Fish Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Minced Left-Over Fish
- 1 Tablespoonful of French Mustard Dressing
- 1 Gill of Sauce Piquante
- 2 Ounces of Parmesan Cheese
- 6 Slices of Toast

## Directions

1. Utilize any kind of white-fleshed Fish, Red Fish, Red Snapper, Sheephead or Trout.
2. Prepare a forcemeat and season with the French Mustard and the Piquant Sauce.
3. Spread a layer one-quarter of an inch thick over delicate strips of toast, dredge with grated Parmesan Cheese.
4. Set in the oven, and bake for five minutes and serve hot.

title: Fish Chowder
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of fresh fish, preferably redfish or sheepshead
- 3 medium-sized potatoes
- 1 onion
- 1 clove of garlic
- 3 sprigs each of thyme, parsley and bay leaf
- 1/4 can of tomatoes
- 1/4 cup of milk
- 1 quart of boiling water
- 2 ounces of salt pork or ham chopped very fine
- Grated oyster crackers
- Salt, Cayenne and black pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of fresh fish of any kind, preferably the redfish or sheepshead.
2. Take three medium-sized potatoes and one onion and cut into slices.
3. Take two ounces of salt pork, wash well and chop very fine.
4. Put the pork into a frying pan, and when it is hot add the sliced onion.
5. Smother slightly, and add chopped thyme, parsley, bay leaf, one clove of garlic very fine, and Cayenne and black pepper to taste.
6. Let this simmer for about ten minutes longer.
7. Pour over this one quart of boiling water, and add fish and half a can of tomatoes and the potatoes.
8. Season to taste, and cover the pan and let the contents simmer for half an hour.
9. A half cup of milk may be added, if desired.
10. Take oyster crackers, place in a bowl and pour the chowder over and serve hot.

title: Fish Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Firm Fish
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1/2 Pint of Cream or Milk
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Minced Thyme and Parsley
- Salt and White Pepper to Taste
- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. The left-over fish may be utilized for these Quenelles, or take a half pound of any firm fish — Sheepshead, Redfish or Red Snapper
2. Take out all the bones and remove the skin
3. Pound the fish well in a mortar, and add gradually the well-whipped whites of three eggs
4. Add gradually the cream or milk, and season to taste with salt and pepper, using white pepper
5. Add the grated nutmeg and minced herbs
6. Mix thoroughly, drain through a sieve, form into little balls, and use when needed

title: Fish Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cold Boiled Fish
- 1 Head of Lettuce
- Mayonnaise Sauce

## Directions

1. Use cold boiled left-over fish, picking nicely into bits of about an inch and a half square.
2. Follow the same directions as in the above recipes, only do not mix lettuce and fish in layers.

title: Fish Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of anchovies
- 1 pint of port wine
- 1/2 pint of strong vinegar
- 1 onion
- a few cloves
- a little allspice
- whole pepper
- a few blades of mace
- a handful of thyme, green or dried
- 1 large lemon, sliced with the skin

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients into a saucepan
2. cover close
3. stew gently until the anchovies are dissolved
4. strain and bottle for use

title: Fish Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Fish of Almost Any Variety
- 4 Onions, Chopped Fine
- 6 Tomatoes, Chopped Fine
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Salad Oil
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 3 Pints of Water
- Cayenne pepper
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the onions and fry them in the salad oil.
2. Cut the tomatoes fine and add onions, and put in all the other ingredients, except the fish, adding the flour to make a good roux.
3. When brown add the water, and, after it has boiled about a half hour, add the slices of fish.
4. When they are firm remove the herb bouquet, add Cayenne pepper, and salt and pepper to taste, and serve the fish soup in a tureen, pouring it over crusts of dried toast.

title: Flannel Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart new milk
- flour
- 2 eggs well beaten
- 1 large spoonful yeast
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Thicken 1 quart of new milk with flour to the consistency of fritter batter
2. Add 2 eggs well beaten, 1 large spoonful of yeast, and a little salt
3. Mix all well together
4. Set it to rise at night for breakfast
5. Do not stir in the morning
6. Bake on the griddle as buckwheat cakes

title: Flannel Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. flour
- 4 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls yeast
- 1 pint milk
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Sift 2 lbs. of flour and mix a teaspoonful of salt with the flour
2. Set the pan before the fire to rise
3. Warm the milk and stir it into the flour so as to make a stiff batter
4. Beat the eggs very light and stir them into the yeast
5. Add the eggs and yeast to the batter
6. Beat all well together
7. If it is too stiff, add a little more warm milk
8. Cover the pan closely and set it to rise near the fire
9. Bake it when quite ready

title: Flannel Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour sifted
- 4 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of yeast
- 1 pint of milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix a teaspoonful of salt with the flour, and set the pan before the fire to rise
2. Warm the milk and stir it into the flour, so as to make a stiff batter
3. Beat the eggs very light and stir them into the yeast
4. Add the eggs and yeast to the batter, and beat all well together
5. If it is too stiff, add a little more warm milk
6. Cover the pan closely and set it to rise near the fire
7. Bake it when quite light
8. Have the baking irons hot, grease them, pour on a ladleful of batter, let it slowly bake, and when done on one side, turn it to the other
9. Butter them and send them to the table hot

title: Flannel Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- ⅜ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1¼ Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Heat the milk, and when it grows tepid, add the yeast, dissolved in a little hot milk or water, and flour sufficient to make a stiff batter.
2. Set to rise over night, or for at least three hours in the morning.
3. In the morning add the yolks of the eggs, beaten light, and the melted butter, and mix well.
4. Then add the whites beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Beat well, and then bake on a griddle.

title: Flannel Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 1/4 Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Heat the milk, and when it grows tepid, add the yeast, dissolved in a little hot milk or water, and flour sufficient to make a stiff batter.
2. Set to rise over night, or for at least three hours in the morning.
3. In the morning add the yolks of the eggs, beaten light, and the melted butter, and mix well.
4. Then add the whites beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Beat well, and then bake on a griddle.

title: Flannel Cakes Without Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart buttermilk
- wheat flour
- 1 large tablespoonful butter
- a little salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Thicken 1 quart of buttermilk with wheat flour
2. Add 1 large tablespoonful of butter, a little salt, and 2 teaspoonfuls of carbonate of soda
3. Pour on a griddle iron or in rings

title: Flirtation Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- custard ingredients for a rich boiled custard
- whites of 2 eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a rich boiled custard, and when cold, beat to a froth the whites of 2 eggs
2. Drop it in spoonfuls on the top of the custard
3. Set it before the fire to brown the whites slightly

title: Floating Island
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- 3/4 Cup of Currant Jelly

## Directions

1. Put the milk to boil in a farina boiler.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar till very light, and stir them into the milk as it begins to boil.
3. Let it thicken one minute.
4. Take from the fire, and add the vanilla and set aside to cool.
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth.
6. When the mixture is cold pour it into a glass dish, heap on the whites of the eggs, and dot here and there with bits of Currant Jelly.
7. Dust these with powdered sugar, and serve very cold.

title: Floating Island, Without Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of five eggs
- a little currant jelly
- one pint of cream
- a teaspoonful of extract
- one pint of sweet milk
- yolks of two eggs
- half a cup of white sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of five eggs with a little currant jelly until they are quite thick
2. Sweeten a pint of cream, add a teaspoonful of extract, pour it in the bowl
3. Drop your whites of eggs and jelly by spoonfuls on the cream
4. If you can not procure cream, you may make a substitute of a custard, made of a pint of sweet milk, yolks of two eggs, and half a cup of white sugar

title: Flounder à la Normande
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Flounder
- 8 Shallots
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Cups of Bouillon or Water
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Croutons Cut in Dice Shape

## Directions

1. Cut open the Flounder down the back.
2. Dig inside under bone to right and left, without breaking the meat.
3. Chop the bay leaves, thyme and clove of garlic very, very fine and mix with a half teaspoonful of salt and a half teaspoonful of black pepper and a dash of Cayenne.
4. Rub the Flounder all over on the inside and under the cutting and outside with this preparation, seasoning thoroughly.
5. Chop the shallots, green and white, very fine.
6. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a flat saucepan or stewing dish, and put the shallots with the butter.
7. Lay the Flounder on top of the shallots and butter, and let it cook slowly on a slow fire for about ten minutes.
8. Turn on the other side and cook ten minutes longer.
9. In the meantime take another saucepan and put into it one tablespoonful of butter and two of flour.
10. Dissolve this immediately with two cups of bouillon or water so that the sauce will be white.
11. Let it cook about five minutes, and then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and one can of mushrooms.
12. Let all cook ten minutes longer, and then take the Flounder out of the saucepan in which it has been cooking and lay on a flat silver dish.
13. Mix the sauce in which it has been cooking with the mushroom sauce.
14. Taste the sauce and add salt, pepper and Cayenne, according to taste.
15. Pour this sauce over the fish, around which you will have placed Croutons cut as dice and fried in the dish.
16. Cover the fish and put it in the bake oven for fifteen minutes.
17. Serve hot in the dish in which it was baked.

title: Flounder à la Nouvelle Orleans
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-pound Flounder
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 6 Allspice
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Basil
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- Croutons
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select a fine, fresh Flounder, parboil, by scalding well after it has been cleansed.
2. Slit open in scores on top and put in butter and salt, and set to bake in an oven.
3. When it is two-thirds cooked, take off and pour over a cream sauce made of two tablespoonfuls of flour, one tablespoonful of butter, spice, thyme, parsley, sweet basil and bay leaf, a can of mushrooms, all chopped fine, and cook about five minutes.
4. After pouring this over the fish, put back in the oven and let it bake till done, which will be in about fifteen minutes.
5. Prepare Croutons, or crusts of bread cut in diamond shape, fry these in butter and use as a garnish for the fish.

title: Flounder à la Orly
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Filets of Flounder
- A Cup of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Egg
- Bread Crumbs
- A Tomato Sauce
- Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Have the fish dealer cut the Flounder in filets and trim, neatly removing all the bones.
2. Beat an egg with milk, and dip in the slices of fish.
3. Roll in bread crumbs and fry in butter.
4. Serve with parsley garnish, with a Tomato Sauce.

title: Flounders Sautée
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- flounders
- eggs
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the fish
2. dip them in a couple of eggs well beaten
3. put 6 tablespoonfuls of olive oil in a frying pan
4. place over the fire
5. when quite hot put in the fish
6. let it remain 5 minutes
7. turn over and fry the other side

title: Flounders à la Tartare
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Flounders
- 3 Eggs
- 10 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- A Bunch of Parsley
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- A Sauce a la Tartare

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the Flounders.
2. Beat well two or three raw eggs.
3. Have ready a frying pan, with eight or ten tablespoonfuls of olive oil.
4. Place over the fire. When well heated, dip the fish into the eggs, roll well and place in the frying pan.
5. Cook for about five minutes, turn on the other side, and cook about the same period.
6. Drain by placing on a heated brown paper.
7. Garnish a dish with a bed of fried parsley, lay the Flounders upon it and garnish with sliced lemon.
8. Serve with Sauce a la Tartare.

title: Foaming Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Gill of Boiling Water
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine or a Tablespoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
2. Melt the sugar in the boiling water, and then add a wine-glass of Sherry
3. Stir in the frothy whites, and set to cool

title: Foamy Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Gill of any Liqueur Desired

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as in the Whipped Cream recipe, using a gill of any liquor desired

title: Foamy Cream With Rum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Gill of Rum
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Whip a pint of fresh sweet cream, adding a gill of rum and whipping till it reaches a stiff froth
2. Transfer to a china bowl and set on ice till ready to use
3. Have a dessert dish ice cold when ready to serve and drop the cream into this spoon by spoon, with greatest care so that it will not fall
4. Pile up high in a pretty pyramid shape and serve immediately

title: Foies Gras Loaf Jellied
body:
## Ingredients

- Foies Gras
- Slice of Fat Fresh Pork
- Slice of Lean Pork, Grated
- Quarter Can of Mushrooms
- Quarter Can of Truffles
- Calf's Foot
- Bay Leaf
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Sprig Each of Thyme and Parsley
- Quarter Teaspoonful of Ground Allspice, Cinnamon, Cloves and Mace
- Three-Quarter Cup of the Soft of Bread
- Juice of One Lemon
- Bits of Lemon Peel
- Tablespoonful of French Brandy
- Egg, well beaten

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as the Loaf of Foies Gras, only the sweetbreads are omitted
2. In their place add an egg, well beaten, and a piece of grated lean and fat fresh pork meat
3. Add a half can of mushrooms and a quarter of a can of truffles, and a piece of calf's foot
4. Cook into a mixture of the consistency of gravy, mash well and strain in a sieve after seasoning very highly
5. Add the juice and let this simmer for about five minutes

title: Fonche Romaine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two wineglassfuls of water
- one wineglassful of whiskey
- half wineglassful of Jamaica rum
- sugar to taste
- lemon to taste
- ice

## Directions

1. Shake
2. use plenty of ice
3. Strain and serve in fancy glass

title: Fonds L'Artichaux, Stuffed
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- French artichokes in cans
- chicken
- egg yolks
- butter
- flour
- onion
- chicken broth
- mushrooms
- truffles
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- lemon juice
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Get the very best French artichokes in cans
2. Boil a chicken in very little water
3. When done, chop it as fine as possible
4. Beat the yolks of 3 eggs and stir them into the chicken water
5. Put 1 spoonful of butter in a frying pan
6. Thicken it with a little flour
7. Fry some onion minced very fine in it
8. Pour in some of the chicken broth
9. Stir it until it is like a rich cream
10. Stir in the chicken
11. Chop some mushrooms very fine, also 2 or 3 truffles, and add to it
12. Season with salt, pepper, parsley, and a little lemon juice
13. Let it get cold
14. Put it in the artichokes in pyramid shape
15. Cover each one with very finely sifted bread crumbs
16. Put them in a pan
17. Pour over them the balance of the chicken broth
18. Let them simmer slowly, taking great care to preserve their shape
19. Put a few mushrooms or truffles sliced into the gravy
20. Serve them upon a dish dressed with sprigs of parsley or sliced lemons, and the sauce in a boat

title: Force-Meat Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of beef or veal and chopped chicken about an inch in thickness
- a little of the liver and tongue of the calf
- 1/2 dozen small onions
- 1 tablespoonful of sweet marjoram
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful powdered black pepper
- 1 teaspoonful mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of cloves (powdered)
- 3 eggs
- 3 grated crackers (sifted)
- 1/2 gill of good sherry wine
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- lard or butter for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the beef or veal and chopped chicken about an inch in thickness.
2. Add a little of the liver and tongue of the calf, a half dozen small onions, one tablespoonful of sweet marjoram, one grated nutmeg, a teaspoonful each of powdered black pepper and mace, and a half teaspoonful of cloves (powdered), three eggs, three grated crackers (sifted), half a gill of good sherry wine, a tablespoonful of butter and two teaspoonfuls of salt.
3. Chop up and mix thoroughly together.
4. Roll in balls and fry slowly in lard or butter.
5. Serve with the soup.

title: Foundation Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Pound of Flour
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- ¾ Pint of Water
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour well; mix with the salt; then rub the flour and butter lightly together, being careful not to let it harden under the touch.
2. When the butter and flour are well kneaded, add the water gradually, kneading little by little, till well kneaded.
3. Do not work the dough; simply mix lightly and thoroughly.
4. Flour the table lightly, turn the paste out upon it, and press in the center and around the edges, repeating this operation three or four times.
5. Then flatten the paste, lay on a dish and cover with a towel and set in a cool place till ready to use, which should be in about twenty-five minutes, at least.

title: Fowls in Fillets with Pistachios
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- sweet herbs
- butter
- small champignons
- flour
- broth
- pistachoes
- eggs
- cream
- lemon

## Directions

1. Spit your fowls and let them roast
2. When done, take them off and cut off the wings and the white of the breasts
3. Keep a small sauce ready in a stewpan, made with sweet herbs, a little good butter, small champignons cut in slices
4. Put it over the fire, with a dust of flour in it
5. Stir and moisten it with a ladleful of good broth
6. See it be of a good taste
7. Scald the pistachoes and cut into slices, put them in and make a thick sauce with 4 or 5 yolks of eggs
8. Beat the same up with cream
9. Put in the white and legs of fowls, with juice of a lemon
10. Cut your wings only in two
11. Place the slices of your fowls in the bottom of the dish with your sauce over it, and serve up hot for an entry

title: Francatelli's Lemon Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- the juice and grated rind of six lemons
- one pint of milk or cream
- six ounces of sponge cake or macaroons
- eight yolks
- whites of four eggs
- one pound of sugar
- a little salt
- puff paste
- cut-up almonds
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix in a basin the juice and grated rind of six lemons, one pint of milk or cream, six ounces of sponge cake or macaroons, eight yolks, and the whites of four eggs (whipped to a froth), one pound of sugar, and a little salt
2. Work all these materials together for at least ten minutes
3. Put a border of puff paste around a pie-dish, then pour in the batter
4. Strew cut-up almonds over it
5. Bake
6. Sift powdered sugar over it, and serve

title: Francatelli's Spanish Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of milk or water
- four ounces of butter
- two ounces of sugar
- five ounces of flour
- essence to taste
- three eggs
- a small pinch of soda

## Directions

1. Put half a pint of milk or water into a stew-pan over the fire, with four ounces of butter and two ounces of sugar
2. As soon as these begin to boil, withdraw the stew-pan from the fire, and stir in five ounces of flour
3. Stir well for a few minutes, add essence to taste, and, one by one, three eggs and a small pinch of soda
4. Drop this paste on a baking-sheet in small round balls (the size of a hickory nut), and bake a light brown in a quick oven

title: Frangipani
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Shelled Almonds
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- 2 Eggs
- ¼ Spoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- ¼ Teaspoonful of Orange Flower Water
- ¼ Gill of Rum

## Directions

1. Prepare a pie paste, and peel three ounces of shelled almonds
2. Put them into a mortar and pound to a paste, with the powdered sugar and a raw egg
3. When reduced to a very fine paste, add the melted butter, ground cinnamon and the orange flower water
4. Beat the yolk of an egg well, and then beat the white to a stiff froth and mix thoroughly
5. Add the rum and mix again
6. Line the pie pans with the pie paste, and fill with the preparation of almonds
7. Set in a moderate oven for thirty-five or forty minutes
8. Set to cool for a half hour
9. Prepare a Glace a l'Eau by putting one ounce of white granulated sugar into a saucepan, with one tablespoonful of cold water, and let it come to a boil
10. Take off the fire and add immediately a tablespoonful of curacoa
11. Mix thoroughly, and then glaze the surface of the cake with this
12. Let it cool and send to the table

title: Fraseati Croquantes
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Flour
- 6 Ounces of Sugar
- 2 Ounces of Ground Almonds
- 2 Whole Eggs and 3 Yolks
- 1 Ounce Aniseed

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a bowl, and beat light, and add the sugar and beat very light.
2. Add the almonds, pounded to a powder, and the aniseed.
3. Beat all together till well incorporated.
4. Add the flour and beat thoroughly.
5. Roll the paste out on a clean dry marble slab in the form of a long, thick rope.
6. Take a knife and cut it into pieces about the size of a guinea egg.
7. Roll each between the palms of your hands, and shape them into oval balls.
8. Make a slight incision into each cake by pressing the back of the knife across the surface.
9. Brush the egg over them, and sprinkle lightly with granulated sugar.
10. Bake in a moderate heat to a light color.

title: French Artichokes Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tender Fresh Artichokes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Sauce, according to Taste

## Directions

1. Strip off the coarse outer leaves, or, better still, cut the stalks close with a pair of scissors, and trim the sharp points from the leaves, removing about a quarter of an inch of each.
2. Cut the stalks about an inch from the bottom.
3. Throw in cold water and wash well, adding a little vinegar to draw out any lurking insects.
4. Have on the stove a pot of boiling water, and add a teaspoonful of salt.
5. Throw in the artichokes and boil gently until it is possible to draw out a leaf easily, or until the outer leaves are tender.
6. Take from the fire and drain upon a dish, placing them upside down, so that the water may all run off.
7. Stand on their stalks in another dish when thoroughly drained, and serve hot with a Drawn Butter Sauce, Sauce a la Maltre d'Hotel or a Sauce a la Hollandaise.
8. The time for boiling an artichoke depends entirely on the age and size of the vegetable and requires all the way from twenty-five minutes to an hour.

title: French Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Yeast
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1⅜ Ounces of Sugar
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough

## Directions

1. Dissolve the yeast, salt and sugar into the water and mix in flour sufficient to make a nice smooth dough of medium degree, not too stiff, nor yet too soft.
2. Work until it no longer adheres to the hand or bowl, and then cover with a cloth and set to rise until it has doubled its size.
3. When it begins to sink, work it well again and set to rise anew.
4. When well risen divide the dough into equal pieces, and mold into round ball shapes, or into long loaves of about two inches in thickness.
5. Lay the loaves on a board previously sprinkled with flour and at sufficient distance apart not to touch one another, and set to rise again.
6. Let them rise to nearly double the previous size.
7. Have the oven heated, transfer the loaves into it, wash over with eggs mixed with water, make diagonal cuts half way across on each loaf, half way through, and close the oven.
8. When baked, brush off the flour, wipe the tops with a damp cloth and the bread is ready to serve.

title: French Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs, yolks and whites beaten well and separately
- 1 lb. of pounded and sifted loaf sugar
- grated peel of a lemon
- 1/2 lb. of sifted flour
- 1/2 lb. of sifted, dried-ground rice
- 4 ounces of sweet almonds, pounded
- 1 ounce of bitter almonds, pounded
- 1 tablespoonful of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks and whites well and separately
2. Mix all ingredients gradually
3. Beat them well
4. Paper the pan
5. Bake the cake for one hour

title: French Chicken Salad
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two nice chickens
- yolks of six hard-boiled eggs to each fowl
- a cup of olive oil
- a cup of vinegar to two fowls, a half cup to one
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- fresh lettuce heads
- six more hard-boiled eggs
- red beets

## Directions

1. Roast one or two nice chickens, season them well, and when cooked, put them by to cool
2. Just before serving the dish, carve the fowls in small pieces, taking out all the large bones
3. Make a dressing of the yolks of six hard-boiled eggs to each fowl, mash the yolks very smooth with a wooden spoon and pour gently on them in a little stream a cup of olive oil
4. Beat the eggs all one way till they are creamed
5. Add now a cup of vinegar to two fowls, a half cup to one, pepper, salt, and drop a little vinegar on the fowl, then pour on the dressing
6. Arrange on the dish, cool, fresh lettuce heads quartered, and slice six more hard-boiled eggs over all as a garnish
7. Mix red beets with the green lettuce and yellow eggs as a handsome addition

title: French Dressing No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- A Saltspoon Each of Black Pepper and Salt
- Chopped Onion and Parsley
- The Juice of Half an Onion

## Directions

1. Mix these in the order given above, adding the onion juice and parsley, well chopped, last.
2. Serve with salads.

title: French Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled fish
- 1 ounce of flour
- 1 quart of milk
- 2 small peeled onions
- parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 sprig of thyme
- grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 pound fresh butter
- white pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Have ready a boiled fish, pick all from the bones
2. Put in stewpan 1 ounce of flour, to which add, by degrees, a quart of milk, mixing it very smoothly
3. Add 2 small peeled onions, a little parsley, a bay leaf, a sprig of thyme, tied together, a little grated nutmeg, teaspoonful of salt, teaspoonful of pepper
4. Place over fire
5. Stir in 1 pound fresh butter, and pass it through a sieve
6. Lay a little sauce on the bottom of your dish
7. Then a layer of the fish
8. Season lightly with white pepper and salt
9. Then another layer of the sauce, until the fish is used up
10. Finish with sauce

title: French Fried Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Boiling Lard
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and then cut into three-inch dice or sections like an orange.
2. Have ready a frying pan of very hot, boiling lard, and let them fry to a golden yellow or brown.
3. Do not burn.
4. Take them out with a skimmer, and drain in a heated colander.
5. Stand the colander in the mouth of the oven while you fry the remainder of the potato.
6. When done, sprinkle with salt, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve hot.

title: French Fritter Batter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sifted Flour
- 3/4 Cup of Cold Water
- 2 Eggs
- 1/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Best Olive Oil
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy or Orange Flower Water
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the whites well into a stiff froth.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs into the flour until very light, and add the sugar, blending well.
3. Add brandy or orange flower water, and beat light, and then add the water and oil, making the batter of the consistency of a very thick starch.
4. Now add the whites of the egg, beat well, and proceed to drop in the fruit.

title: French Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pints of Sifted Flour
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Ounce of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- Jelly
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light; then add the flour and melted butter, and then add the warm milk.
2. Beat all till very light.
3. Add a half teaspoonful of baking powder, and then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Beat all till very smooth.
5. Drop a tablespoonful at a time upon the hot griddle; turn quickly and bake on the other side, allowing about two minutes in all.
6. Place on a hot platter, butter well and spread with jelly; then roll up, dust with powdered sugar and serve very hot.

title: French Jumbles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lbs. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1 very large spoonful of butter
- 1/2 lb. of almonds
- 1 nutmeg
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Form and bake

title: French Loaf Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- five cups of sugar
- three cups of butter
- two cups of milk
- ten cups of sifted flour
- six eggs
- three small nutmegs
- one teaspoonful of saleratus
- one pound of raisins
- one-third of a pound of citron

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar to a cream
2. then add part of the flour, the milk and the beaten yolks of the eggs
3. then add the rest of the flour and the whites of the eggs
4. add the fruit as you get the cake ready for the oven
5. season to taste
6. Bake one hour

title: French Loaves
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Compressed Yeast
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough

## Directions

1. Make the dough as in Bakers' Bread, and after it has risen and begins to drop, scale the dough off, and cut and mold them into little loaves of about the length of a man's hand.
2. Mold them round and set them to rise again.
3. When they are very light, which will be in a few minutes, take them up and work them well separately.

title: French Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound of flour
- 4 eggs
- 3/4 pint of cold milk
- 4 ounces of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl.
2. Break in the eggs; beat well, and add one ounce of powdered sugar.
3. Mix thoroughly with a spatula, and then add the cold milk, pouring it in gradually.
4. Mix well for six minutes.
5. Have ready a clean griddle; butter lightly, and when hot, drop on it about two large spoonfuls of the batter at a time.
6. Let the cakes bake two minutes on each side, and then turn and bake two minutes on the other.
7. Turn the pancake on a dish; sprinkle generously with powdered sugar.
8. Continue baking the cakes and turning on the dish, sprinkling each in turn with the powdered sugar, till all is used.

title: French Pancakes With Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Flour
- 4 Eggs
- 1/2 Pint of Cold Milk
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Glass of Jelly

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter exactly as in the above recipe
2. When the pancakes are nicely cooked, arrange them neatly on a dish, and spread over each a half tablespoonful of apple, strawberry, raspberry, currant, peach or any kind of jelly desired
3. Roll them up nicely and dress on a dessert dish and sprinkle powdered white sugar over them
4. Send to the table hot

title: French Pilau
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair fowls
- rice
- butter
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil a pair of fowls
2. When done, take them out and put the rice in the same water, first taking out some of the liquor
3. When the rice is done, butter it well
4. Cover the bottom of the dish with 1/2 of it
5. Put the fowls on it and add the remainder of the liquor
6. Cover the fowls with the other 1/2 of the rice
7. Make it smooth and spread over it the yolks of 2 eggs, well beaten
8. Bake in a moderate oven

title: French Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Pints of Fresh Milk
- 2 Quarts of White Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Ounces of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sugar (which may be omitted, according to taste)
- ¾ Cake of Compressed Yeast, Dissolved in Warm Water

## Directions

1. Boil the milk, take off the stove, and add the butter, sugar and salt.
2. Then add the well-beaten eggs.
3. Stir in about one and a half pounds of flour, more or less, to make a stiff batter, and beat till air bubbles accumulate all over the surface.
4. Then cover with a close cloth and set to rise over night in a warm place.
5. Then take up the dough and knead lightly with the remaining flour to make a nice, soft dough, and shape with your hands into nice, small rolls or balls.
6. Set them together in the baking pan, which you will have lightly sprinkled with flour.
7. Then set to rise for an hour longer.
8. Set in the oven, and bake about a half hour.

title: French Rolls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of lukewarm milk
- 1 quart of flour
- 2 ounces of butter
- 2 eggs
- a teaspoonful of salt
- 6 spoonfuls of yeast

## Directions

1. Melt the butter
2. Add to it the eggs and salt
3. When cool, stir in the yeast, and flour sufficient to mould it
4. Set it in a warm place
5. When light, mould it into small rolls
6. Lay them on flat buttered tins, and let them remain 20 minutes before baking

title: French Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fleshy part of a rump of beef
- thyme
- parsley
- salt
- small white onions
- carrots
- cloves
- allspice
- 2 teaspoonfuls of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Take the fleshy part of a rump of beef and put it on the fire early in the morning to boil
2. Skim all the grease off
3. Put in thyme, parsley and salt with the rump
4. After the soup is sufficiently boiled and the grease carefully taken off, put in the small white onions and a few carrots cut round
5. Add cloves and allspice
6. Just before you are ready to dish the soup, put two teaspoonfuls of brown sugar to turn it dark

title: French Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Flour
- 8 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- 1 Wineglassful of Brandy
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a cream and add to the flour, milk, salt and sugar
2. Work all together very light in a bowl, until you have a very smooth batter
3. Add the brandy and beat vigorously ten minutes longer
4. Have ready the wafer irons and a clear charcoal fire
5. Brush the irons with butter and place the wafers within and bake on both sides to a light brown color
6. If desired, the wafers may be rolled into fancy shapes

title: French-Creole Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sifted Sugar (White Pulverized)
- The Grated Peel of 1 Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water
- 12 Eggs
- 3/4 Pound of Sifted Flour
- 1/4 Pound of Mashed Rice (Dried)
- 1 Tablespoonful of Baking Powder
- 4 Ounces Sweet Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Bitter Almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, after having blanched and peeled the almonds and pounded them to a fine powder in a mortar
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs very light, and gradually add the rice, which has been well-mashed
