3. Mix the almonds thoroughly with the flour; then add these gradually to the sugar, beating vigorously
4. Add the grated zest of the lemon and the essence, and finally add the whites of the eggs and mix lightly and quickly
5. Butter a paper and line the bottom and sides of the pan, and bake the cake in a moderate oven for one hour

title: Fricandeau of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- A Rump of Veal of Two Pounds Weight
- 1 Pint of Broth
- 2 Onions
- 2 Carrots
- ½ Dozen Slices of Bacon
- ½ Can of Green Peas or Mushrooms
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper
- Lard
- ½ Glass of White Wine

## Directions

1. Take one whole piece of the rump of the veal, cut about two inches in thickness, and about the size of a large saucepan or frying pan in length and width.
2. Lard the veal well with larding needles, and rub well with salt and pepper.
3. Slice two onions, two carrots, two sprigs of thyme and two bay leaves, and a half dozen slices of thinly-sliced fat bacon, two inches long and about the thickness of a dollar.
4. Place the bacon in strips in the bottom of the saucepan, and lay over this a layer of the sliced carrots and onions.
5. Put about a dozen little dots of lard over this at intervals, and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
6. Lay the veal on top of this, and then cover up with a layer of the sliced onions and carrots, and lay strips of bacon on top.
7. Cut a piece of paper the size of the saucepan, and cover it up.
8. Place in a good oven, and let it bake three-quarters of an hour longer, slowly.
9. Watch carefully.
10. When done, take out the meat and place it in a dish.
11. Take the sauce and add, if possible, one-half glass of white wine, and let it boil with the vegetables a moment.
12. Then add one pint of broth or water, and let it cook well.
13. Strain after it boils fifteen minutes.
14. Add, if desired, a half can of mushrooms, or a half can of green peas, and let it boil for ten minutes longer.
15. Then add the Fricandeau of Veal and let it warm well for about ten minutes and it is ready for the table.

title: Fricandellons Of Cold Veal Or Mutton
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- bread
- milk
- egg
- parsley
- thyme
- lemon peel
- pepper
- salt
- butter or lard
- bones
- onion
- butter
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very fine, soak a thick slice of bread in boiling milk, mash it, and mix it with the cold meat.
2. Add a beaten egg (or two if you have more than a quarter of a pound of meat), some chopped parsley and thyme, a little grated lemon peel, pepper and salt; make this into cakes, and fry in butter or lard.
3. Serve them dry on a serviette, accompanied with a gravy made from the bones of the minced meat which must be cooked with an onion, a little butter and flour, and milk; when brown it is ready.

title: Fricassee Of Crabs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six nice fat crabs
- onions
- butter
- lard
- flour
- chopped parsley
- green onion
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Take six nice fat crabs, wash them, and while alive chop off the claws
2. Then clean the rest of the crabs carefully and lay them in a dish
3. Chop up two onions fine
4. Fry them in a tablespoonful of butter and lard mixed
5. When brown and soft stir in a large spoonful of flour
6. Which must also brown nicely
7. Throw in some chopped parsley and a little green onion
8. And when they are cooked pour on a quart of boiling water—this is the gravy
9. Now put in the crabs without parboiling
10. Let them simmer in the gravy for half an hour
11. And serve with boiled rice
12. Parboiling crabs destroys their flavor
13. They should be alive to the last moment

title: Fricassee Of Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- fried onions
- parsley
- tomatoes
- a little garlic

## Directions

1. All large fish make nice fricassee.
2. Cut the fish into slices and lay it in a gravy made of fried onions, parsley, tomatoes and a little garlic;
3. fry it in butter and serve.
4. Add sauce if liked.

title: Fried Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Apples
- Boiling Lard
- 3/4 Cup of White Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and cut into round slices.
2. Have ready a pan of boiling lard, and fry to a delicate golden brown.
3. Place in a colander, heated, and in which you have placed a piece of brown paper.
4. Drain in the mouth of the oven.
5. Place in a dish, and sprinkle with sugar, and serve hot with roast meat, roast pork, etc.

title: Fried Apples as a Relish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, fair apples
- hot lard, or pork fat

## Directions

1. Wash fine, fair apples without paring
2. Cut them in slices an eighth of an inch thick
3. Fry in hot lard, or pork fat
4. Serve with fried pork

title: Fried Artichokes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Artichokes
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter

## Directions

1. Pluck off the coarse scales of the artichokes, and then throw the vegetable into cold water.
2. Let it stand for an hour.
3. Then drain.
4. Cut the meat into delicate slices, and fry in butter, just as you would potato chips.
5. Serve with Filet of Beef, Veal Saute, Smothered Chicken, etc.

title: Fried Bananas
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Bananas
- Boiling Lard
- White Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the bananas, and cut them in two, lengthwise.
2. Slice lengthwise about a quarter of an inch in thickness.
3. Have ready a pan of boiling lard.
4. Lay the bananas in it, and fry brown, first on one side and then on the other, sifting with a little powdered sugar.
5. When done, skim out of the pan, place neatly in a dish, sprinkle with powdered sugar again, and serve hot, as an entrée.

title: Fried Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Spring Chicken
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- 2 Eggs
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken into joints
2. Dredge well with salt and pepper
3. Make a nice batter with the eggs and flour and roll the chicken in this, patting lightly
4. Place in the hot lard in the frying pan and let it cook for about three-quarters of an hour, watching carefully that it may not burn
5. Serve on a platter garnished with chopped parsley and cresses

title: Fried Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair of young chickens
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- lard
- 1 pint of cream
- mace
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up a pair of young chickens
2. Lay them in a pan of cold water to extract the blood
3. Wipe them dry
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Dredge them with flour
6. Fry them in lard with both sides a rich brown
7. Take them out of the pan and keep them near the fire
8. Skim carefully the gravy in which the chickens have been fried
9. Mix it with 1 pint of cream
10. Season with a little mace, pepper and salt, adding some parsley

title: Fried Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of young chickens
- water
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut up a pair of young chickens
2. Lay them in a pan of cold water to extract the blood
3. Wipe them dry
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Dredge them with flour
6. Fry them in lard
7. Both sides should be of a rich brown
8. Take them out of the pan and keep them near the fire

title: Fried Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair young chickens
- water
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- lard
- 1/2 pint cream
- mace
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up a pair of young chickens
2. Lay them in a pan of cold water to extract the blood
3. Wipe them dry
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Dredge them with flour
6. Fry them in lard with both sides a rich brown
7. Take them out of the pan and keep them near the fire
8. Skim carefully the gravy in which the chickens have been fried
9. Mix it with 1/2 pint of cream
10. Season with a little mace, pepper and salt, adding some parsley

title: Fried Chicken with Batter
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- small tablespoonful of butter
- wheat flour
- salt
- 1 pair of chickens

## Directions

1. Make a light batter with 3 eggs, a small tablespoonful of butter, a little wheat flour, and salt to the taste
2. Joint the chicken
3. Put the chicken into the batter
4. Grease the frying pan
5. Throw the mixture of chicken and batter into it and fry a good brown

title: Fried Chicken, Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Spring Chicken
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- Salt and Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1/2 Pint of Milk

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken at the joints
2. Dredge well with salt and pepper, and a little flour
3. Put the lard into the frying pan, and, when hot, add the chicken, letting it fry slowly for three-quarters of an hour until done
4. Be careful not to burn
5. When done, arrange the pieces on a hot dish
6. Pour off all the fat that remains in the frying pan but one tablespoonful
7. Add to this a tablespoonful of sifted flour
8. Mix thoroughly and then pour in a half pint of rich cream or milk
9. Season well with salt and pepper, let it come to a slight boil and pour over the chicken and serve

title: Fried Cod Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cod
- bread crumbs
- yolk of an egg
- salt
- pepper
- boiling lard

## Directions

1. Cut the fresh cod into slices 1 inch in thickness
2. dry it in a towel
3. have ready bread crumbs, and the yolk of an egg well beaten
4. salt and pepper the fish
5. dip each slice first in the egg and then in the bread crumbs
6. prepare boiling lard and lay them into it
7. fry till a nice brown
8. drain off all the fat from each slice and serve hot

title: Fried Codfish
body:
## Ingredients

- Codfish
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Parsley and Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Soak the Codfish overnight, and boil twenty minutes, or until very tender.
2. Take out and cut into slices of one-inch thickness, and dry with a clean towel.
3. Have ready a pint of milk, and season well with pepper only; season the Codfish, rubbing a little black pepper to taste and a dash of Cayenne over it.
4. Soak the fish in the milk.
5. Have ready some crushed bread crumbs and a well-beaten egg.
6. Dip each slice first into the egg, and then roll in bread crumbs, patting lightly.
7. Drop into boiling lard; the fish must swim in the lard.
8. When fried a golden brown, take out with a skimmer, drain off all fat, and serve hot on a bed of fried parsley, garnished with sliced lemon.

title: Fried Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Ears Young, Tender Green Corn
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Minced Onion

## Directions

1. Score the corn along each row, and then cut from the cob with a knife.
2. Press out all the pulp and corn juice from the cob.
3. Mix all and season well with salt and pepper.
4. Mince the onion fine, and blend with the lard, which you will have put into the frying pan.
5. Add the corn when the onions begin to brown slightly, and keep stirring and stirring till the grain is cooked, which will be in about fifteen or twenty minutes.
6. Serve hot.

title: Fried Corn Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Indian Meal
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- A well-beaten egg
- Milk
- Fat bacon skin for greasing

## Directions

1. Make the dough as above, adding a well-beaten egg and milk instead of water.
2. Beat till very light.
3. Drop the stiff batter in large spoonfuls on a frying pan that you will have greased well with fat bacon skin.
4. Fry to a golden brown.
5. After each cake is taken from the skillet, wipe the latter off and regrease with the fat bacon before putting the next cake upon it.
6. Serve hot at breakfast or dinner.

title: Fried Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Large Crabs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 6 Tablespoonfuls of Butter or Lard
- Stale Bread Crumbs, Grated
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Bay Leaf
- Onions
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs according to recipe.
2. Cut off the claws and crack and cut the crabs into quarters as for stewing.
3. Season well with pepper and salt.
4. Have ready a pint of milk.
5. Mix pepper and salt in proportions of about two teaspoonfuls each.
6. Have ready a pan of boiling lard and a plate of grated bread crumbs.
7. Dip the crabs into the milk and then roll in the bread crumbs and drop into the boiling lard, frying about ten minutes or until a golden brown.
8. Serve on a platter with the claws piled in the center, the bodies of the crabs grouped nicely around, and garnish with sprigs of parsley.

title: Fried Croakers
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Small or Six Large Croakers
- 1 Pint of Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Flour
- Parsley
- Sliced Lemon
- Grease

## Directions

1. Fill a small pan with milk, and add salt and pepper to taste
2. Roll the Croakers in the milk and then take out and roll in dry, sifted flour
3. Have ready a pan of grease that has been heated very hot without boiling
4. Drop in the Croakers and cook till brown
5. The fish must swim in the grease
6. Lift carefully out of the frying pan and serve on a bed of fried parsley, with garnishes of sliced lemon

title: Fried Cucumbers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- flour
- lard
- hot water
- wine
- walnut catsup
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- butter
- mustard

## Directions

1. Slice 8 middle sized cucumbers
2. Flour them slightly and fry a light brown in a little lard
3. Pour off the lard and add to the cucumbers 4 tablespoonfuls of hot water, 2 of wine, 2 of walnut catsup, pepper, salt, and sliced onion
4. Add a lump of butter dipped in flour
5. Stew about 15 minutes

title: Fried Eels
body:
## Ingredients

- A 2-pound eel
- 2 eggs
- Mashed bread crumbs
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Hot lard

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eel and place in boiling water and vinegar till thoroughly disgorged.
2. Cut into lengths of two inches.
3. Wipe dry with a clean towel.
4. Beat two eggs well and add mashed bread crumbs.
5. Roll the eel well in this preparation and fry in hot lard, following implicitly the directions given for frying, and remembering that the fish must always swim in the grease.
6. Drain off all grease and serve on a bed of parsley.

title: Fried Eggplant
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Young Eggplants
- 2 Eggs
- Flour to Make a Light Batter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the eggplants very thin, paring them if large, and leaving the skin on if very young and tender.
2. Make a light batter with the eggs and flour.
3. Season the eggplant well with salt and pepper.
4. Soak the slices in the batter.
5. Lift out and fry in the boiling lard.
6. When done on one side, turn on the other with a cake turner.
7. Remove the eggplants.
8. Drain them on brown paper in the mouth of the oven, and serve hot on a flat and open dish or platter.

title: Fried Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lard
- salt
- black pepper
- parsley garnish (optional)

## Directions

1. Heat the lard until very hot.
2. Break the eggs gently into a saucer, one by one, and drop gently into the lard, without breaking the yolks.
3. With the spoon take up a little of the hot lard and drop gently over the top of the egg, if you wish it to be quite done.
4. Otherwise simply fry till the yolk is set.
5. Slide out on a batter cake turner, and place in a dish.
6. Sprinkle with salt and black pepper, and add if you wish a little parsley garnish.
7. Serve very hot.

title: Fried Frogs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- hind-quarters of the frogs
- warm water
- cold vinegar
- salt
- scalding water
- chopped parsley
- flour
- butter or sweet oil
- pepper
- crisped parsley

## Directions

1. Use only the hind-quarters of the frogs.
2. After washing them in warm water, soak well;
3. then put them into cold vinegar with a little salt, and let them remain one or two hours, after which throw them into scalding water, and remove the skin without tearing the flesh.
4. Wipe them dry, dust flour on them and fry in butter or sweet oil, with plenty of chopped parsley.
5. When brown, dust pepper and a little salt over them, and garnish with crisped parsley.
6. Stewed frogs are seasoned with butter, wine, beaten eggs and parsley chopped fine.

title: Fried Frogs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Frog Legs
- 1/2 Cup of Lemon Juice and a Teaspoon of Salt
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Sifted Bread Crumbs
- Parsley, Sliced Lemon and Radishes to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Scald the Frogs' legs about three minutes in boiling water and add a half cup of lemon juice and salt
2. Take out of the water and dry with a clean towel
3. Season with salt and pepper and dip into a batter made of the well beaten yolks and whites of two eggs and sifted bread crumbs
4. Pat the Frogs well and drop into the lard, heated to a boiling point, and fry to a golden brown
5. Take them from the lard and drain well by placing on soft brown paper, heated
6. Place a snow-white folded napkin in a dish, and lay the frogs upon it and garnish with fried parsley and sliced lemon, or place the Frogs in a bed of fried parsley laid in the dish and garnish with decorated radishes and sliced lemons

title: Fried Grits or Hominy
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled grits or hominy
- Salt and pepper
- Flour
- Beaten egg
- Lard

## Directions

1. After the grain is boiled, let it cool.
2. Season with salt and pepper, and spread on a biscuit board.
3. When perfectly cold, cut into slices and dust each slice with a little flour.
4. Brush again with a beaten egg, and fry in lard till a light brown.

title: Fried Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Thin Slices of Ham
- Pepper to Taste
- Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Slice the ham thin.
2. Heat the frying pan very hot.
3. Lay in the ham in its own fat and fry over a quick fire.
4. After taking the ham out of the pan, drop in the eggs.
5. If you do not like eggs fried on both sides, baste the eggs with the hot grease, and be sure to cook the yolks whole.
6. When they are well set, without being hard, take the eggs out and lay one on each slice of ham.
7. Garnish with parsley.
8. Sprinkle the eggs with salt and pepper very lightly and serve.

title: Fried Irish Potatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- lard

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes
2. Peel and mash them into small cakes
3. Fry them in lard of a rich brown on both sides

title: Fried Lamb
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a loin of lamb
- butter
- anchovies
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut a loin of lamb into thin slices
2. Beat them and lay them in water to take out the blood
3. Fry them in butter
4. Make the sauce with butter, anchovies and lemon

title: Fried Liver a la Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of liver of the beef
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 large onions
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the onions nicely.
2. Put one tablespoonful of butter into the frying pan and add the onions.
3. When brown, take the liver which you have cut into slices of about three inches in length and one half inch in thickness, and season well with salt and pepper, and lay it over the onions.
4. Stir well.
5. Cover and let it fry for about three minutes, and then turn over and let it cook three minutes more.
6. Pour a teaspoonful of vinegar on top and again season to taste.
7. Let it simmer three or four minutes longer and serve hot.

title: Fried Liver and Bacon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Calf's Liver
- 1/2 Pound of Breakfast Bacon
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Black Pepper to Taste
- Parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Slice the liver into pieces of about three inches in length and one-quarter of an inch in thickness
2. Slice the bacon very thin, having as many slices of the bacon as of the liver
3. Put the bacon in the frying pan and fry brown
4. Place it in a heated dish and set over a pot of boiling water and cover to keep warm
5. Dust the liver with flour, after having seasoned well with salt and pepper
6. Fry it in the bacon fat
7. When it cooks about five minutes, allowing two minutes and a half to each side, take out
8. Arrange on the same dish with the bacon in alternate slices
9. Garnish nicely with parsley and serve

title: Fried Meat. Grillade.
body:
## Ingredients

- round of meat
- salt
- pepper
- tablespoonful of lard
- sliced onion
- one clove of garlic
- one chopped tomato
- parsley

## Directions

1. The round of the meat is always selected for Grillades, and one steak will serve six persons. The steak is cut into pieces of about six or eight squares and each piece is called a grillade.
2. Season well with salt and pepper, rubbing these into the meat thoroughly and letting it soak well into the fibres.
3. Have ready a hot pan, and place within a tablespoonful of lard, and, when hot, add a sliced onion and one clove of garlic, chopped very fine.
4. Let this brown, and then add one chopped tomato.
5. Place the Grillades in this, letting them soak thoroughly.
6. Cover with a tight cover, and set back, letting them fry slowly, so as to absorb all the lard and juices.
7. Serve on a hot dish, when brown, with garnishes of parsley.

title: Fried Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful Olive Oil
- 6 Shallots, Minced Very Fine
- Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf, Minced Fine
- 1 Clove of Garlic, Minced Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- One tablespoonful of White Wine or Sherry
- French toast

## Directions

1. Put the oil into a frying pan, and when heated, add the shallots, minced very fine
2. Let these brown slightly, and add the minced garlic and fine herbs
3. Let these brown for three minutes or so, and then add the mushrooms
4. Stir well and fry for about five minutes
5. Add one tablespoonful of White Wine or Sherry, and serve the mushrooms on slices of French toast

title: Fried Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Dozen Nice, Tender Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Salt
- Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Throw the onions into cold water, peel them, and then cut them into thin slices.
2. Cover them with boiling water, and let them boil about twenty minutes.
3. Drain off the water thoroughly, and put them into a frying pan with a large tablespoonful of butter, and season to taste.
4. Let them fry slowly for about ten minutes.
5. Turn frequently, to prevent them from burning.
6. Alternatively, peel and pare and slice into round-shaped pieces.
7. Dip the pieces in milk, and then in grated bread crumbs or flour, and fry them in boiling fat for about ten minutes.
8. Lift them out of the fat, drain well, and serve on a hot dish with fried parsley as a garnish.

title: Fried Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 oysters to each person
- Salt and pepper
- Finely grated bread crumbs
- Parsley
- Boiling lard

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters, allowing about six or eight to each person to be served.
2. Salt and pepper and then roll oysters in bread crumbs, grated very fine.
3. Drop in the frying pan of boiling lard, having sufficient lard to allow the oysters to swim in the grease.
4. Remove when a golden brown and place on brown paper and drain.
5. Serve on a platter garnished with parsley or on a bed of fried parsley.

title: Fried Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- 3 or 4 eggs
- grated bread
- grated nutmeg
- mace
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Choose the largest and finest oysters
2. Beat the yolks of 3 or 4 eggs
3. Mix with them grated bread and a small quantity of grated nutmeg, mace, and a little salt
4. Stir this batter well
5. Dip the oysters into it
6. Fry them in lard, till they are of a light brown color
7. Take care not to do them too much
8. Serve them up hot

title: Fried Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 oysters
- yolk of 4 eggs
- bread crumbs
- powdered biscuit
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- butter
- corn meal

## Directions

1. Take 100 oysters and dry them in a coarse towel
2. Beat very light the yolk of 4 eggs
3. Add some bread crumbs or powdered biscuit, pepper, salt and a little mace
4. Stir these well together, and dip your oysters into the mixture
5. Fry them in boiling butter till they become a light brown
6. Corn meal may be substituted for the bread or biscuit

title: Fried Oysters à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 oysters to each person
- 1 egg
- 1 glass of milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of black pepper
- Grated cracker or bread crumbs
- Butter oil or lard
- Parsley
- Sliced lemon
- Pickle

## Directions

1. Select the firmest and largest oysters, allowing six or eight to each person.
2. Drain in a colander and dry with a soft linen towel.
3. Beat an egg thoroughly and mix with a glass of milk and a half teaspoonful of salt and pepper.
4. Mash bread crumbs or crackers in another dish.
5. Dip the oysters one by one in the milk and roll gently in the bread crumbs, patting softly with the hands.
6. Drop into a deep frying pan with sufficient lard or butter oil for the oyster to swim in it.
7. In from three to five minutes the oysters will be done.
8. Take them out with a skimmer, being careful not to break, and drain on a piece of soft brown paper.
9. Serve on a bed of fried parsley, with garnishes of sliced lemon and pickle.

title: Fried Parsley
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard

## Directions

1. Pick off the delicate leaves and branches of very young parsley, wash well, drain.
2. Put in a frying pan, in which you will have placed a tablespoonful of lard and allowed to reach a medium hot state.
3. Fry slowly and drain and use as a garnish or as needed.

title: Fried Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Parsnips
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Drippings of Roast Beef
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Brush and scrape or peel the parsnips.
2. Boil as directed in salted water till tender.
3. When done, drain off the water and cut the parsnips into slices lengthwise of about half an inch in thickness.
4. Put two tablespoonfuls of the drippings of the roast beef in the saucepan, and add a teaspoonful of butter.
5. When hot add the sliced parsnips.
6. When they are brown on one side, turn on the other and let this brown also.
7. Place on a hot platter, sprinkle with chopped parsley and salt and pepper, and serve with roast meats.

title: Fried Plantains
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- plantains
- flour
- lard
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Use plantains that are perfectly ripe and yellow all over
2. Peel them
3. Split them in long slips, and dredge slightly with flour
4. Have ready a frying pan filled with boiling lard
5. Put in the plantains and fry them well
6. When done, take them up on a perforated skimmer and drain back the lard into the frying pan
7. Dish, and send them to table with powdered sugar to eat with them

title: Fried Pork Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Pork Chops
- Grated bread crumbs
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the pork chops and season well with salt and pepper.
2. Roll in grated bread crumbs and fry in boiling lard twenty-five minutes.
3. Continue until they have reached a rich brown.
4. Take out, place on a platter and serve with pickles or a Sauce aux Cornichons.

title: Fried Potatoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- very hot lard
- fine salt

## Directions

1. Wash and pare a sufficient quantity for the meal
2. Slice them in the machine, taking care to bear down lightly, so as to have the slices very thin
3. Have ready a vessel of very hot lard, and drop the sliced potatoes into it
4. Let them remain till they begin to brown
5. Take them out with a wire ladle
6. Scatter a little fine salt over them, and serve while hot

title: Fried Potatoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salted water
- boiling fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Pare and cut the potatoes in thin slices
2. Throw them as you cut them into salted water to cool, and make them crisp
3. Put them piece by piece on a dry towel and wipe dry
4. Drop them into boiling fat, enough to float them
5. As they brown dip them out with a skimmer, and salt them a little

title: Fried Potatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cold potatoes that have been boiled
- butter

## Directions

1. Mash cold boiled potatoes
2. Make them into flat cakes
3. Fry them in butter

title: Fried Shrimps
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Fine Lake Shrimp
- 1 Cup of Milk
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Fried Parsley and Parsley Tips and Olives to Garnish

## Directions

1. Use Fine Lake shrimp for this recipe
2. Boil first according to the recipe given for Boiled Shrimps
3. Take off the fire, pick off shells and season well
4. Take a pan of milk, season well with salt and pepper
5. Roll the shrimp well in this, then roll them in grated bread crumbs or yellow cornmeal (the latter being preferable)
6. Fry in boiling lard
7. The shrimp must swim in the lard
8. When they are a nice golden brown, skim out with a skimmer and drain on heated brown paper
9. Serve on a hot dish on a bed of fried parsley and garnish with parsley tips and olives

title: Fried Soft-Shell Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Soft-Shell Crabs
- 1 Quart of Milk
- Bread Crumbs
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash carefully, removing all sand, but do not scald or blanch them, as this destroys the fine flavor completely
2. Remove the spongy, feathery substances under the side points called the "man-eaters"
3. Remove also the sand bag or "sand pouch" from under the shells just between the eyes
4. Remove the "tablier" or "apron"
5. Wash well in cold water and dry with a clean towel
6. Take a pint of milk and season well with pepper and salt; season the crabs and crack them in the milk, rubbing thoroughly, so that the milk may thoroughly impregnate them
7. Take out and roll in a little sifted flour
8. Pat lightly with the hand, shake off superfluous flour and fry in boiling grease, being always careful to have sufficient grease in the pan for the crabs to swim in it
9. When a delicate golden brown, take out of the grease with a skimmer
10. Drain on a piece of heated brown paper, and serve on a bed of fried parsley, with garnishes of sliced lemon
11. Serve with Sauce à la Tartare

title: Fried Speckled Trout
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Filets of Speckled Trout
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Onion
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Gills of Mustard
- 1/4 Pint of Mayonnaise Dressing
- Parsley
- Sliced Lemon
- Salt and Pepper
- Cracker or bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Select fine Spotted Trout and skin the fish and clean thoroughly
2. Cut the Trout into filets or steaks
3. Take the yolks of two eggs and a little milk and beat together
4. Add salt and pepper and soak the Trout well in this, rolling over and over
5. Take it out of the pan and roll in cracker or bread crumbs
6. Pat the fish a little all over with your hands and then fry in the same manner as Croakers
7. Serve on a bed of fried parsley, with garnish of sliced lemon
8. For the Sauce a la Tartare: Take one large onion and four or five sprigs of parsley and chop fine
9. Squeeze the juice out well and parboil, chopping fine and squeezing out the water
10. Prepare about one-half pint of Mayonnaise Dressing and add about two gills of mustard
11. Mix well and add to the parsley and onion and juices
12. Serve the sauce cold

title: Fried Stingaree
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Young Stingaree
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- A Handful of Chopped Parsley
- The Juice of 1 Lemon, or 1 Spoon of French Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Egg and Bread Crumbs
- Boiling Lard
- Fried Parsley
- Sauce a la Poivrade

## Directions

1. Prepare the Stingaree wings as directed in the above recipe.
2. Cut it into square pieces of about three inches.
3. Let these soak in the vinegar which has been charged with the salt and minced parsley.
4. Roll in egg and bread crumbs and fry in boiling lard.
5. Drain and serve on a bed of fried parsley, with a Sauce a la Poivrade.

title: Fried Sweet Potatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut sweet potatoes in long slices and fry in lard, or half boil them, peel, cut round, and fry quickly
2. Drain them and serve up as dry as possible

title: Fried Sweet Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Potatoes
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the boiled potatoes lengthwise in slices
2. Fry in very hot butter until brown, and serve hot

title: Fried Sweetbreads Breaded
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 1 Egg
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and parboil the sweetbreads and then trim off all tendons and nerves.
2. Cut into pieces of about two inches long and roll first in a well-beaten egg and then in bread crumbs.
3. Drop into boiling fat and fry till a golden brown.
4. Serve with a Cream Sauce.

title: Fried Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ripe tomatoes
- fresh butter
- 2 or 3 sliced green peppers
- salt
- 1 or 2 onions

## Directions

1. Peel 1 dozen ripe tomatoes, and fry them in a little fresh butter, together with 2 or 3 sliced green peppers
2. Sprinkle on them a little salt, then add 1 or 2 onions sliced, and let the whole cook thoroughly

title: Fried Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Nice, Large, Firm Tomatoes
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes very thin, and then dust with salt and pepper.
2. Beat the egg very light.
3. Dip the tomatoes first in this and then in the bread crumbs, and drop into the lard, covering the bottom of the pan.
4. When brown on one side, turn on the other.
5. Turn carefully with the cake turner into a heated dish.
6. Keep warm while you are frying the rest, and serve very hot.

title: Fried Tripe
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Tripe
- 1 Egg
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Parsley and Lemon to Garnish
- Sauce Piquante or Sauce Poivrade

## Directions

1. Prepare the tripe, boil well, and cut into pieces of three inches in length and one in width.
2. Roll it in a beaten egg and then roll in grated bread crumbs.
3. Drop in boiling lard and fry to a golden brown.
4. Take off the fire and place on a bed of fried parsley and garnish with sliced lemon.
5. Serve with a Sauce Piquante or a Sauce Poivrade.

title: Fritter Batter à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Brandy
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Cold Water
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter, Melted

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs well, and add the flour, beating very light
2. Add the melted butter and the brandy, and thin with water to the consistency of a very thick starch
3. Add the whites of eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Dip the fruit into this, immersing well at one dipping
5. Lift out with a large cooking spoon, drop into the boiling lard, and fry to a golden brown
6. Sprinkle nicely and lightly with powdered sugar

title: Fritters Souffle à la Vanille
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1/2 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- The White of 1 Egg
- 1 Spoonful of Whipped Cream
- 1 Vanilla Bean
- Powdered White Sugar

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a saucepan and infuse the vanilla bean, and let the milk reduce to one-half.
2. Then remove the bean, and add a tablespoonful of butter.
3. When it comes to a good boil, add the flour, well sifted.
4. Stir briskly till a stiff paste is formed that will not adhere to the saucepan.
5. Then take off the fire and add an ounce of powdered white sugar that has been beaten well with the yolks of two eggs.
6. Have ready the white of one egg beaten to a stiff froth, and mix a spoonful of whipped cream with this, and add to the paste.
7. Mix well, and then roll the paste out on a flour board that has been slightly sprinkled with flour, and sprinkle lightly on top with the flour.
8. Cut into small cakes, drop into very hot lard, and let them cook to a golden brown.
9. Then arrange nicely on a dish, sift powdered sugar over them, and send to the table hot.

title: Frog Lemonade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sugar
- 3 quarts of water
- juice of 2 dozen lemons
- juice of 1 pineapple
- 2 bottles of seltzer water
- 3 dozen fine, large strawberries
- slices of pineapple
- slices of lemon
- a large piece of ice
- crushed ice

## Directions

1. Take a large punch bowl
2. Put into it one pound of sugar, three quarts of water, the juice of two dozen lemons, the juice of one pineapple, two bottles of seltzer water
3. Mix all well
4. Add three dozen fine, large strawberries, and decorate with slices of pineapple and lemon sliced very thin
5. In case there is not sufficient sugar, sweeten again to taste
6. Put a large piece of ice in the bowl
7. When ready to serve, fill glasses one-quarter full of crushed ice, and fill up with the lemonade, being careful to have several strawberries and a slice or two of lemon and pineapple in each glass

title: Frogs Sautéed à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Frog Legs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Large Onions
- 6 Fresh Tomatoes or a Half Can
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 6 Green Sweet Peppers
- 1 Cup of Consommé or Boiling Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the Frogs' legs
2. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter in a saucepan and add the legs
3. Let this brown well, being careful not to burn
4. After ten minutes of very slow cooking on a good fire, take three large onions and slice them and let them brown with the frogs
5. Add one-half dozen nice large fresh tomatoes, or a half can; cover and let these brown
6. Cook very slowly, adding salt and pepper to taste, thyme, bay leaf, and two cloves of garlic, all chopped very fine indeed
7. Let the mixture smother slowly over the fire, and add one-half dozen green sweet peppers sliced very fine, being careful to extract all the seeds
8. Stir well and let it smother twenty minutes longer, stirring frequently to prevent burning
9. When well smothered, that is, when the Frogs' legs are tender, add one cup of broth or consommé; if not, add one cup of boiling water, and let it cook again for half an hour very slowly and well covered
10. Serve hot

title: Frogs à la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Frog Legs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoon Flour
- 1 Lemon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the Frogs' legs and cut them in small pieces of about an inch in size
2. Have ready a stew pot and put in the Frogs' legs, with salt and pepper to taste, and half a lemon cut fine
3. Cover well with water and let it cook till the meat is tender
4. When cooked, drain and put the meat aside
5. Make a sauce: Take one tablespoonful of butter and two spoonfuls of flour; rub the flour smoothly and put in a sauce pan with the butter to melt
6. Add two cups of water and stir well
7. When it begins to boil well, add a half can of mushrooms
8. Season with salt and Cayenne
9. Add the frogs and season again to taste, and let this boil ten minutes
10. Beat the yolks of two raw eggs well, take the frogs off the fire and stir in the yolks thoroughly
11. Add the juice of one-half lemon and serve

title: Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 2 Cups of Pulverized Sugar
- ½ Cup of Cornstarch
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Milk

## Directions

1. Take the whites of two eggs, two cups of pulverized sugar, and one-half a cup of cornstarch
2. Blend the cornstarch with a little milk, to make a thick paste
3. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten light, and the pulverized sugar
4. Beat all to a stiff froth
5. Frost the cake while hot

title: Frosting For Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg
- ten teaspoonfuls of finest powdered sugar
- a teaspoonful of lemon extract

## Directions

1. To each egg used take ten teaspoonfuls of finest powdered sugar, and a teaspoonful of lemon extract
2. Beat quickly
3. allow at least five minutes for each spoonful of sugar

title: Frozen Apricots
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine, Ripe Apricots
- 2 cups of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water or Cream

## Directions

1. Pare the apricots, cut them in two, remove the stones, and cut in small pieces.
2. Mash the fruit and the sugar together, add the water or cream, and turn into the freezer and proceed to freeze as in Ice Cream.

title: Frozen Bananas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Bananas
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Sugar or Cream
- The Juice of 2 Oranges

## Directions

1. Peel the bananas, cut them very fine and mash well.
2. Add the cream or water and the orange juice, turn into the freezer and pack away in ice and rock salt.
3. Set to freeze according to directions given for freezing Ice Cream.

title: Frozen Champagne Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Lemons
- 2 Oranges
- 1 Pound of White Powdered Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- % Pint of Champagne
- ½ Gill of Brandy

## Directions

1. Extract all the juice from the oranges and lemons
2. Dissolve the sugar in boiling water, and when cold add the juice
3. Freeze very hard for four or five minutes, and when firm add the liquors, turning long enough to mix well, and then proceed to freeze as in Creole Punch
4. Serve in punch glasses

title: Frozen Cherries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Cherries
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries, mix them with the sugar and add the water.
2. Mix well and turn into the freezer and proceed to freeze as in directions for Ice Cream.
3. Serve with Whipped Cream or rich milk.

title: Frozen Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Oranges
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Pare the oranges, and grate or cut them into fine pieces.
2. Mix well with the sugar and set aside for an hour.
3. Add the water and mix thoroughly.
4. Turn into a freezer and proceed to freeze as in Ice Cream.

title: Frozen Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Large Peaches
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Take twelve large peaches or one can, two cups of sugar, and one pint of water.
2. Cut the peaches fine, and mix all the ingredients together.
3. Then freeze in the ice cream freezer according to directions already given.
4. Serve with rich cream or milk.

title: Frozen Peaches and Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- nice soft, ripe peaches
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Peel and stone nice soft, ripe peaches
2. Sprinkle enough sugar on them to make them very sweet
3. Chop them up fine until they are a pulp
4. Add to them as much cream as you have peaches
5. Put them into the freezer and turn it briskly until the cream is well frozen

title: Frozen Pineapple
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Pineapple
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapples.
2. Cut out the eyes, and then grate the fruit, or cut it very fine.
3. Add the sugar and water and stir well.
4. When the sugar is thoroughly dissolved, turn the mixture into a freezer, and proceed to freeze as in Ice Cream.

title: Frozen Rice Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of rice
- 1 quart of milk
- a pint of cream
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 6 oranges

## Directions

1. Prepare the rice as in the Rice Cream Pudding recipe, boiled, but omit the fruits and use only the grated rind of an orange in making the pudding.
2. When cold, add the juice of an orange and the cream, beaten or whipped to a froth.
3. Four tablespoonfuls of wine or lemon juice may be substituted for the orange juice, according to taste.
4. Freeze the same as you would ice cream, and serve with an Orange Sauce.

title: Frozen Strawberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Strawberries
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Mash the berries with the sugar and set aside for an hour.
2. Add the water and mix well with the strawberries.
3. Turn the mixture into the freezer and proceed to freeze as in Ice Cream.
4. Serve with rich milk or Whipped Cream.

title: Frozen Tapioca Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One teacup of pearl tapioca
- cold water
- hot water
- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 quart of cream

## Directions

1. Soak tapioca over night in cold water
2. Pour hot water on the soaked tapioca and boil until a clear jelly
3. When cold, add the whites of 3 eggs, 1 lb. of sugar, 1 quart of cream
4. Whip well, strain and flavor
5. Freeze

title: Fruit Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 lbs. flour
- 5 lbs. sugar
- 5 lbs. butter
- 10 lbs. currants
- 5 lbs. raisins stoned and cut
- 37 eggs
- 2 ounces cloves
- 2 ounces nutmeg
- 1 ounce cinnamon
- 1 ounce mace
- 2 lbs. citron
- 0.5 pint brandy
- 0.5 pint wine
- 0.5 pint rose water

## Directions

1. Bake 5 hours or more

title: Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pound of Grated Lemon and Orange Peel, Mixed
- 3/4 Pound of Raisins
- 1/4 Pound of Preserved Cherries
- 1/4 Pound of Sultanas
- 1/2 Pound of Preserved Figs
- 1/4 Pound of Citron
- 1/4 Pound of Preserved Apricots
- 3/4 Pound of Sifted Flour
- 1/4 Pound of Preserved Pineapple
- 1/4 Pound of Butter
- 1/4 Pound of Mashed Almonds
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 3/4 Pound Of White Pulverized Sugar
- 1/4 Tablespoonful of Mace
- 8 Eggs
- 1/4 Tablespoonful Ground Cinnamon
- The Juice of 1 Orange
- 1/4 Tablespoonful of Cloves
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 1/2 Tablespoonful Ground Allspice
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Wineglassful of Good Brandy
- 1 Wineglassful of Sherry Wine or Jamaica Rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the fruit first.
2. Stone the raisins, picking carefully.
3. Pick over the sultanas carefully, and wash them well.
4. Cut the orange and lemon zest very fine, almost grating.
5. Cut the citron very, very fine.
6. Cut all the remaining fruit into quarter-inch dice, or very, very small pieces.
7. Beat the butter to a rich cream, and then add the sugar, and beat all till very, very light.
8. Beat the yolks and the whites of the eggs together till very, very light, and then add them to the sugar and butter, and then beat again very, very light.
9. Sift the salt and baking-powder and flour together, and gradually add it to the mixture, beating very vigorously till light.
10. Mix all the fruit together, dredging with flour, so as to keep them apart, and then add the spices to the batter.
11. Mix well, and add the fruit.
12. Mix thoroughly, and then add the brandy or rum.
13. Beat all together well once more, and then line a cake pan with buttered paper, and turn the mixture into it, and bake in a very slow, steady oven for four and a half hours.

title: Fruit Ginger Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Flour
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Louisiana Molasses
- 1/4 Pound of Raisins
- 3/4 Pound of Currants, Washed and Dried
- 3/4 Cup of Sour Cream
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda or Baking Powder (Level)
- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Ginger
- 3/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cinnamon and Cloves

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and warm the molasses and beat with the sugar and butter
2. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and the milk and spices and ginger, mixing thoroughly
3. Add the soda, dissolved in a tablespoonful of boiling water and mix well
4. Mix the seeded raisins and currants well and then add the flour by degrees, beating vigorously
5. Finally add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth, and beat thoroughly
6. Put in two shallow pans in the oven for about two hours
7. Use the broom wisp test before taking the cake out of the oven

title: Fruit Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 Ounces of Sugar
- 1/2 Ounce of Any Fruit Juice

## Directions

1. Make in exactly the same manner as Orange Pastilles
2. Use one-half an ounce of juice and three and a half ounces of sugar, and add a little more sugar if it appears liquid after taking from the fire

title: Fruit Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of hot milk
- sifted Indian meal
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- a little molasses
- 1 pint of whortleberries or chopped sweet apple
- cranberries

## Directions

1. Take a pint of hot milk, and stir in sifted Indian meal till the batter is stiff
2. Add a teaspoonful of salt and a little molasses
3. Stir in a pint of whortleberries or chopped sweet apple
4. Tie in a cloth that has been wet, and leave room for it to swell, or put it in a pudding pan, and tie a cloth over
5. Boil 3 hours
6. The water must boil when it is put in
7. You can use cranberries, and use sweet sauce

title: Fruit Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Peaches, Apples, Apricots or Pears
- 1 Cup Sugar
- ¼ Cup Water
- 1 Pint of Cream
- Whites of 2 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy

## Directions

1. Pare the fruit; take out the stones or core; slice and put into a saucepan with the water and sugar, and let it stew gently until very tender
2. Press through a colander or sieve
3. Set the cream to boil, and then beat it into the fruit thoroughly
4. Take off the fire, add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth, set to cool, and serve with fruit puddings made of the same fruits

title: Fruit Toasts with Cherries, Apricots or Raspberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of French Toast
- 1 Cup of Fruit Marmalade
- Cream to Serve

## Directions

1. Prepare thin slices of very dry French toast, and butter generously.
2. Take a dish, and lay on each slice of toast generous allotments of the fruits you wish to serve.
3. Pour all remains of syrup over the toast, and serve with cream.

title: Galas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Cup of Rice
- 3 Cups Water (boiling)
- 3 Eggs
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- Powdered White Sugar
- Boiling Lard
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour

## Directions

1. Put three cups of water in a saucepan and let it boil hard.
2. Wash half a cup of rice thoroughly, and drain and put in the boiling water.
3. Let it boil till very soft and mushy.
4. Take it out and set it to cool.
5. When cold, mash well and mix with the yeast, which you will have dissolved in a half cup of hot water.
6. Set the rice to rise overnight.
7. In the morning beat three eggs thoroughly, and add to the rice, mixing and beating well.
8. Add a half cup of sugar and three tablespoonfuls of flour, to make the rice adhere.
9. Mix well and beat thoroughly, bringing it to a thick batter.
10. Set to rise for fifteen minutes longer.
11. Then add about a half teaspoonful of grated nutmeg, and mix well.
12. Have ready a frying pan, in which there is sufficient quantity of lard boiling for the rice cakes to swim in it.
13. Test by dropping in a small piece of bread. If it becomes a golden brown, the lard is ready, but if it burns or browns instantly it is too hot.
14. Take a large deep spoon, and drop a spoonful at a time of the preparation into the boiling lard, remembering always that the cake must not touch the bottom of the pan.
15. Let fry to a nice brown.
16. Take the Galas out of the frying pan and drain off the lard by laying in a colander or on heated pieces of brown paper.
17. Place in a hot dish, and sprinkle over with powdered white sugar, and eat hot with Cafe au Lait.

title: Game Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- The Breasts of Any Birds
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Use judgment in regard to quantity
2. Take two breasts of partridges, cut into pieces and pound in a mortar
3. Add the same quantity of bread that has been wet with milk or water and squeezed well
4. Add the butter and the yolks of four eggs, and season highly with salt and pepper and a pinch of grated nutmeg
5. Mix thoroughly and press all through a sieve
6. Two well-pounded truffles may be added
7. Use as desired

title: Game Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Small Birds
- 1 Dozen Eggs
- A Rich Pie Crust
- 1 Dozen Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 2 Cups of Egg Dressing
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Take one dozen small birds. Snipe, Quail, Woodcock, etc., and clean well, inside and out.
2. Stuff each one with a dressing the same as for turkey, using either egg or oysters as desired.
3. Loosen the joints with a knife but do not separate them.
4. Put them in a stewpan, with water enough to cover them, and let them cook till nearly tender.
5. Season with salt and pepper again and two tablespoonfuls of butter.
6. Thicken the gravy with one tablespoonful of flour, let cook for ten minutes more and then remove and set to cool.
7. Butter a pudding dish and line the sides with a rich pie crust.
8. Have ready the hard-boiled eggs, cut in slices.
9. Put in a layer of the eggs and a layer of the birds until the dish is full.
10. Pour over the gravy and then cover the pie with a crust and bake to a light brown.

title: Gateau de Pommes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. loaf sugar
- 1 pint spring water
- 2 lbs. apples
- 1 large lemon
- isinglass (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar in a pint of spring water till it becomes sugar again
2. Pound and core 2 lbs. of apples
3. Put all together in a stewpan, grate in the pulp of a large lemon, and stew it till it becomes a stiff jelly
4. Put it into a mould; it will turn out when cold
5. If the apples are flat, you may add a little lemon juice
6. A custard put in the dish is an improvement; if not stiff enough, add a little isinglass
7. Some think it better to leave out 1 lb. of sugar and reduce the water

title: Gateau De Pommes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- half lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 pint of spring water
- 2 lbs. of apples
- 1 large lemon
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil half lb. of loaf sugar in a pint of spring water till it becomes sugar again
2. Pound and core 2 lbs. of apples
3. Put all together in a stewpan
4. Grate in the pulp of a large lemon and stew it till it becomes a stiff jelly
5. Put it into a mould
6. It will turn out when cold
7. If the apples are flat, you may add a little lemon juice
8. A custard put in the dish is an improvement
9. If not stiff enough, add a little isinglass

title: Gateau Savoie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon or Rose Extract
- Milk

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream.
2. Add the sugar gradually, beating all the while.
3. Beat the yolks until they are very light, and add to the mixture.
4. Add the milk.
5. Mix well, and add half of the flour, which you will have sifted with the baking powder.
6. Add the well-beaten whites of the eggs, and the remainder of the flour, and the extract of Lemon or Rose.
7. Finally add the baking powder, mix quickly and lightly, turn into a Turk's head pan, which you will have lined with a greased paper, and bake for forty-five minutes in a moderate oven.

title: Gelatine Blanc Mange
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- one ounce of Nelson's or Coxe's gelatine
- one cup of cold water
- half a pound of fine white sugar
- cream for serving

## Directions

1. Soak an ounce of Nelson's or Coxe's gelatine in a cup of cold water for one hour
2. Add the soaked gelatine to one quart of milk
3. Add to this half a pound of fine white sugar
4. Let it simmer very gently on the fire in a stewpan until all the gelatine is dissolved
5. Strain it and pour it in a mould
6. When it begins to thicken, put it on ice and serve it with cream

title: Gelatine Blanc Mange with Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of new milk
- half a box of gelatine
- cold water
- one cup of sugar
- four eggs
- vanilla or lemon extract

## Directions

1. Set one quart of new milk on to boil
2. Soak half a box of gelatine in cold water for ten or fifteen minutes
3. Stir the soaked gelatine into the boiling milk
4. When the gelatine is dissolved, stir into the milk one cup of sugar
5. Take the jelly from the fire, and while the mixture is very hot, stir in four eggs
6. Season with vanilla or lemon extract
7. Pour into moulds and eat with cream

title: Gelatine Dessert
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one box of Cox's gelatine
- one quart of boiling water
- one teaspoonful of tartaric acid
- four cups of sugar
- beaten whites of three eggs
- one tablespoonful of good extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the gelatine and stir until dissolved
2. Add tartaric acid and sugar, let dissolve and bring to a boil
3. While boiling, stir in the beaten whites of three eggs
4. Let this boil up once again and take it off the fire
5. When nearly cool, add a tablespoonful of good extract of lemon
6. Strain the mixture into moulds or cups
7. Set it in a cool place or on ice to become firm

title: Gelatine Snow Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of good gelatine
- two spoonfuls of water
- half a pint of boiling water
- three-quarters of a pound of white sugar
- the juice of two lemons
- the rind of two lemons
- whites of two eggs
- yolks of two eggs
- one pint of milk

## Directions

1. Take two tablespoonfuls of good gelatine, throw over it two spoonfuls of water, let it soak ten minutes
2. Pour over it half a pint of boiling water, three-quarters of a pound of white sugar, and the juice of two lemons with the rind thrown in
3. Let it come to a boil, take it off immediately, strain it, let it cool a little
4. When it begins to thicken add the beaten whites of two eggs
5. Beat all thoroughly, and pour it in a mould on ice to get firm
6. When cold and firm, send it to table in the middle of a glass basin or dish, and pour around it a custard made from the yolks of the eggs, and a pint of milk sweetened and flavored to taste
7. Serve with sponge cake

title: General Rule for Boiling Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the fish thoroughly
2. Make a small letter "S" with knife on the back
3. Pass twine around the body of the fish so as to secure it
4. Never wrap or tie in a cloth
5. Have ready a kettle of boiling water
6. Throw in a sprig of onion, thyme and bay leaf, eight or ten cloves

title: Genoese Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of sugar
- four eggs
- half a pound of flour
- a small glass of brandy or wine

## Directions

1. Bake in a square sheet
2. ice it and cut into diamonds
3. ornament with dots or stripes of any kind of bright jelly or preserves

title: Genoese Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Glassful of Claret
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1/2 Pint of Water
- Salt, Pepper, Nutmeg, and Allspice to Taste

## Directions

1. Melt a tablespoonful of butter, stir in two tablespoonfuls of flour, and mix well until smooth.
2. Then add a wine-glassful of Claret, stirring all well.
3. To this add about half a pint of water, and season with pepper and salt and a little nutmeg and allspice.
4. Let the sauce simmer and reduce to about one-half.
5. Add parsley as a garnish and serve with boiled fish or boiled meat.

title: German Ladies Fingers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of five eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of blanched almonds
- rind of a lemon
- sifted flour
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of five eggs with half a pound of sugar
2. Add half a pound of blanched almonds, cut fine or pounded
3. Grate the rind of a lemon, mix well, and add gradually enough sifted flour to make into a dough
4. Roll out and cut in strips the length and size of the forefinger
5. wet them with the beaten white of two eggs, and bake

title: German Ladies' Fingers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of five eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of blanched almonds
- yellow part of one lemon grated
- half a pound of flour
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Beat for one hour the yolks of five eggs with half a pound of sugar
2. Add half a pound of blanched almonds pounded fine and the yellow part of one lemon grated
3. Mix well and add half a pound of flour very gradually
4. Roll out the paste and cut it into strips the length and size of the forefinger
5. Beat lightly the whites of two eggs and wet the fingers

title: German Pickle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sound, ripe cucumbers
- 3 quarts of cucumber pieces
- 3 cups of vinegar
- 4 cups of water
- 1 quart of vinegar
- 1 pint of sugar
- cinnamon bark
- 1 teaspoonful of pimento

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers and remove the seeds
2. Cut lengthways into strips an inch wide
3. To 3 quarts of the pieces add 3 cups of vinegar, and 4 of water
4. Soak 24 hours, stirring once or twice
5. Put 1 quart of vinegar on the fire
6. Add 1 pint of sugar, a little cinnamon bark, 1 teaspoonful of pimento, tied in a piece of cloth
7. Scald all together
8. Add the cucumbers and boil till soft
9. Put in glass jars

title: German Pickle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- damsons
- brown sugar
- vinegar
- 1 ounce of cinnamon
- 2 ounces of mace
- 2 ounces of allspice
- 2 ounces of cloves

## Directions

1. Stick the damsons with a fork to prevent splitting them
2. Weigh 1 lb. of brown sugar to every 3 lbs. of damsons
3. Make a syrup by letting them boil a very short time with the spices
4. Pour into a jar when cold
5. Pour the syrup from the damsons for 3 mornings and make it boiling hot, then pour over the fruit, but not until nearly cold
6. On the 4th morning put the fruit into the jars also, and remain till quite done (but not too much)

title: German Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 6 eggs
- 6 spoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 spoonful of butter
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. add 6 eggs, well beaten, 6 spoonfuls of flour, 1/2 spoonful of butter, 1 nutmeg, and 1 teaspoonful of salt to 1 pint of milk
2. pour it into a well greased pan, and bake for 1 hour
3. to be eaten with wine, sugar and butter sauce

title: German Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 1 lb. fresh butter
- 8 eggs
- ½ lb. sifted flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls strong fresh yeast
- 1 wineglass rose water
- fresh butter for greasing
- sugar flavored with powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Warm a quart of milk and cut up in it 1 lb. of the best fresh butter
2. Stir it about to soften in the warm milk
3. Beat 8 eggs till very thick and smooth
4. Stir them gradually into the milk and butter, in turn with ½ lb. of sifted flour
5. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of strong, fresh yeast
6. Cover the pan with a clean cloth and set it in a warm place to rise
7. When the batter has risen nearly to the top and is covered with bubbles, it is time to bake
8. First stir in a wineglass of rose water
9. Heat your waffle iron in a good fire
10. Grease its inside with the fresh butter used for the waffle mixture
11. Fill it and shut the iron closely
12. Turn it on the fire so that both sides of the cakes may be equally well done
13. Take them out of the iron by slipping a knife underneath
14. Grease and prepare the iron for another waffle
15. Butter them and send them to the tea table very hot
16. Serve with a glass dish of sugar flavored with powdered cinnamon

title: Giblet Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Turkey Giblets
- 1 Cup of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put the giblets or simply the gizzard into a saucepan and cover well with water.
2. Let them simmer as long as the turkey roasts, then cut them fine and take the turkey out of the pan on which it has been roasted.
3. Add the giblets and stir well, and then add a cup of the water in which the giblets have been boiled.
4. Season to the taste and serve in a sauce dish, pouring over the dressing when serving the turkey.

title: Giblet Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Hard-Boiled Yolk for Each Person
- 2 1/2 Cups of Chicken Broth
- 3 Quarts of Boiling Water or Broth
- 1 Onion, Carrot and 1/2 Turnip, chopped
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Port or Madeira Wine
- Parsley
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Leaf each of Sage and Bay
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour and 1 of Butter
- The Giblets, heart, liver, etc., of Two Turkeys or Four Chickens
- Pepper
- Salt
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the onion fine and put it into the stewpan with the butter; let it brown, and then add the chopped vegetables, whole giblets, etc.
2. Fry until nicely browned; but do not let it burn.
3. Slit the giblets with a knife, that the juices may run out in boiling, and put all into the soup kettle, with pepper, salt, sage, parsley, and the three quarts of consomme or boiling water.
4. Add bones or lean meat, cooked or raw, that are left, preferably the meat of the chicken, and let all simmer for five hours.
5. Then strain.
6. Mash one liver fine and add it to the broth; season with Cayenne pepper, lemon juice to taste, and two tablespoonfuls of Madeira or Port wine.
7. Boil three minutes, and have in the tureen one hard-boiled yolk of an egg for each person.
8. Pour the soup over it and serve hot.

title: Gilt-Edged Consomme
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fine chicken
- 1 good soup bone
- 1 slice of fine ham
- 1 gallon of water
- 2 eggs, whites and shells
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 1/2 small parsnip
- 1/2 carrot
- 1/2 head of celery
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- salt and pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Have the fowl thoroughly cleaned, and put the chicken, beef and ham into a kettle of cold water of the quantity mentioned, and boil slowly for five hours, being careful to keep the pot well covered.
2. Chop the onion and vegetables and fry them in a little butter, and add all the seasonings to the soup.
3. Boil two hours longer, and set away overnight in an ice box.
4. The next day remove all the fat; from the top take out the jelly, leaving the thickest part of the sediment.
5. Mix in the shells and the whites of eggs and boil quickly for about ten minutes.
6. Set it on the hearth to settle.
7. Pour the soup through a thin bag without squeezing; if it does not come out perfectly clear, pass it through again.
8. It should be a beautiful golden-brown color.

title: Gin Fiz—No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half tablespoonful of sugar
- a little lemon juice
- two wineglassfuls of seltzwater
- one wineglassful Tom or Holland gin
- teaspoonful of white of an egg
- ice

## Directions

1. Shake well and strain into fancy glass

title: Gin Fiz—No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half tablespoonful of sugar
- a little lemon juice
- two wineglassfuls of celestine vichy
- one wineglassful Tom or Holland gin
- teaspoonful of yolk of an egg
- ice

## Directions

1. Shake well and strain into fancy glass

title: Ginger Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Bruised Ginger Root
- 2 Ounces of Cream of Tartar
- 4 Quarts of Boiling Water
- The Juice and Rind of 1 Lemon
- 1½ Pounds of Granulated Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Yeast, or ½ of a Cake

## Directions

1. Bruise and pound the ginger well, and put it into a bowl with the boiling water
2. Add the juice of a lemon and the well-grated zest
3. Add the Cream of Tartar and the sugar, mixing and stirring constantly
4. When it is lukewarm add the yeast, dissolved in a little water
5. Mix well and cover tightly, and let it stand for six hours
6. Then strain it and put it into bottles with patent tops and keep in a cool place

title: Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 1 lb. butter
- 2 ounces of ginger
- 1 gill of cream, warmed
- 1 quart of molasses

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients well together
2. Roll them out and bake quickly

title: Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 9 eggs
- 1 pint of molasses
- 1 cup of ginger
- 2 teaspoonfuls of soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Ginger Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 3 Pints of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Ground Ginger
- 1 Teaspoonful Ground Cinnamon
- 1 Pint of Sweet Milk or Sour Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda or Baking Powder
- eggs
- molasses

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, and then add the sugar.
2. Beat till very light, and then beat the yolks of the eggs till creamy and add to the butter and sugar.
3. Beat very light.
4. Now add the molasses and stir well, mixing thoroughly.
5. Then add the milk and mix well into the flour, with which you will have sifted the baking powder, and beat till very smooth.
6. Then add the ground ginger and cinnamon; mix well.
7. Finally add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth; mix well and pour into two well-greased, shallow tins.
8. Bake in a moderate oven for forty minutes, using the broom wisp test before taking out of the oven.
9. Sprinkle with white sugar and serve, either hot or cold.
10. If soda is used, dissolve it in a tablespoonful of boiling water and add to the molasses; then add to the butter and sugar before adding the flour and milk.

title: Ginger Bread Nuts
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of white powdered sugar
- 1 ounce of grated ginger
- peel of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Rub butter, sugar, ginger, and lemon peel into flour
2. Bake in a slow oven

title: Ginger Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 teacups flour
- 0.5 cup molasses
- 0.5 cup brown sugar
- 0.5 cup hot water
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 heaped tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 0.5 teaspoon cloves

## Directions

1. Mix molasses and sugar, into which put the butter, adding also hot water
2. Beat yolks and add
3. Add 1 teaspoon of cinnamon, 1 of ginger, and 0.5 of cloves
4. Add whites well beaten, and add flour last of all
5. Dissolve the soda in a little water
6. Place immediately in a moderate oven
7. Bake about 0.75 of an hour

title: Ginger Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 3/4 Cup of Milk or Sour Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda or Baking Powder
- 3/4 Cup of Butter or Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Ground Ginger, or 1 Tablespoonful Each of Ground Cinnamon and Ginger
- 1 1/2 Cups of Louisiana Molasses

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs to a cream and then add the melted butter, beating till very light.
2. Sift the baking powder and the flour together, and add the ginger, mixing well.
3. Then add the milk and the molasses to the butter and eggs, mixing thoroughly.
4. When well blended, add the flour and ginger and baking powder (mixed) and beat vigorously.
5. Finally add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth, turn into a well-greased tin, and bake in a moderate oven for forty-five minutes.
6. Use the broom wisp test before taking the cake out of the oven.

title: Ginger Cake Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of molasses
- ½ lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of good brown sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of ground ginger
- 1 gill of cream
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Heat together well with a spoon: molasses, butter, brown sugar, ground ginger, and cream
2. Add as much sifted flour as will make it stiff enough to bake
3. When done, serve it up with cold sauce of sugar and butter creamed together

title: Ginger Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cups of flour
- 3 cups of molasses
- 1 cup of sour milk
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of soda dissolved in water
- 1 tablespoonful of ginger
- salt
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Ginger Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Gill of Lard
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Soda
- molasses
- ginger

## Directions

1. Melt the molasses, lard and ginger together and blend well
2. When thoroughly melted and warmed, beat for about ten minutes
3. Dissolve the soda in a tablespoonful of boiling water and add to the molasses
4. Mix it thoroughly, and then add the flour, using good judgment and adding just enough of the three cups of sifted flour to make a stiff batter
5. Beat thoroughly and vigorously
6. Have ready several greased, shallow pans and pour the mixture into them
7. Bake for ten minutes in a quick oven

title: Ginger Cookies
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacup of sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 spoonful soda
- 1 spoonful ginger
- 1 spoonful vinegar
- 7 cups of flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Ginger Cup Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of flour
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of molasses
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of ginger
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 3 eggs
- 1 lb. of stoned and chopped raisins

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in small pans

title: Ginger Nuts
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 2 quarts of molasses
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of candied orange peel, cut small
- 1 ounce of strong ginger, ground
- 1 ounce of allspice
- 1 lb. of butter oiled

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together and set it by for some time
2. Roll it out in pieces the size of a walnut
3. Put them in rows on a baking plate
4. Press them flat with the hand
5. Bake them in a slow oven for 10 minutes

title: Ginger Nuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Molasses
- 1.5 Pound of Brown Sugar
- 0.75 Pound of Butter
- A Pinch of Salt
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Ginger
- 0.5 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 0.75 Pint of Milk or Water

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Work in the flour and mix well
3. Add the molasses and mix well
4. Then add the milk, into which you will have dissolved the soda thoroughly, and beat well
5. Add the ginger and continue working lightly till thoroughly mixed
6. Then roll out and cut in various fanciful shapes and bake to a light brown

title: Ginger Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Ounce of Lemon Juice
- 3 1/2 Ounces of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Essence of Jamaica Ginger

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for Orange Pastilles

title: Ginger Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Five cups of flour
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 1/2 cup of molasses
- 1 cup butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda in 1/2 cup of sour milk
- 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of ginger

## Directions

1. Mix 5 cups of flour, 2 cups of sugar, 1 1/2 cup of molasses, 1 cup butter, 1 teaspoonful of soda in 1/2 cup of sour milk, 3 eggs, and 1 tablespoonful of ginger
2. To be eaten with sauce; but just as good cold, without the sauce

title: Ginger Snaps
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter and lard mixed
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of molasses
- half a cup of water
- one tablespoonful of ginger
- one teaspoonful of soda in hot water
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix butter and lard
2. Combine with sugar, molasses, water, and ginger
3. Dissolve soda in hot water and add
4. Add flour enough to roll the dough soft

title: Ginger Snaps
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Lard or Butter
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 0.75 Cup of Sour Cream
- 1 Tablespoonful of Ground Ginger
- 2 Eggs
- 2.5 Teaspoonfuls of Soda
- Flour Sufficient to Roll Thick

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients, making the batter slightly stiffer
2. Cut into fancy shapes and bake in a quick oven

title: Ginger Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Spoon of Ground Ginger
- 4 Ounces of Molasses
- 8 Ounces of Flour
- 8 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- 1 Wineglassful of Brandy
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a cream and add it to the flour and milk, salt and sugar, molasses and ginger.
2. Work all well together very light in a bowl, until you have a smooth batter.
3. Add the brandy and Ginger Extract and beat vigorously ten minutes longer.
4. Heat the wafer irons over a clear charcoal fire.
5. Brush the irons with butter.
6. Place the wafers within and bake on both sides to a light fawn color.

title: Ginger-Nuts
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of flour
- one pound of butter
- one quart of molasses
- four tablespoonfuls of allspice
- four tablespoonfuls of cinnamon
- eight tablespoonfuls of ground ginger

## Directions

1. Roll thin
2. cut out in the shape of the small ginger-nuts sold at the confectioners'
3. bake in a rather quick oven

title: Glaze
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Pounds Rump of Beef
- 5 Pounds of Beef Bones
- 2 Calfs Feet
- 1 Large Herb Bouquet
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 3 Large Carrots
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Roast five pounds of the rump of beef well and brown, but rare
2. Chop it in small pieces and put in a pot with two gallons of water
3. Add the bones and calf's feet, all raw
4. Add a large herb bouquet, one stalk of celery and three large carrots
5. Let the whole come to a boil
6. As the scum rises skim
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste
8. Let all boil till reduced to one quart
9. Strain this, and it will make a jelly or glace when cold

title: Glazed Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Dozen Small Onions of Uniform Size
- 1 Tablespoonful Sugar
- 1 Cup Water
- 1 Large Teaspoonful Butter
- Salt to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour

## Directions

1. Select small Creole onions of uniform size, top the heads and the stems, and remove the skins, but not too closely, lest they should break up when boiling.
2. Take a frying pan, large enough for the onions to lie in it, side by side.
3. Put the butter in it first, and when melted add the onions.
4. Sprinkle with the sugar and water, and season with salt to taste.
5. Set on the back of the Stove, where they can simmer gently for an hour.
6. When nearly done, and tender all through, add a tablespoonful of flour, mixed in water, blended well.
7. Set in the oven, with a paper on top.
8. Let them stand for about half an hour, and use as a garnish for beef, veal, etc.

title: Glazed Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips of Uniform Size
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of flour, mixed in water

## Directions

1. Select fine, small and tender turnips of uniform size.
2. Top the heads and the stems, and remove the skins, but not too closely, lest they should break up when boiling.
3. Take a frying pan large enough for the turnips to lie in it, side by side.
4. Put the butter in first, and when melted add the turnips.
5. Sprinkle with the sugar and water, and season with salt to taste.
6. Set on the back of the stove, where they can simmer gently for an hour.
7. When nearly done, and tender all through, add a tablespoonful of flour, mixed in water, blended well.
8. Set in the oven, with a paper on top.
9. Let them stand for about half an hour and use as a garnish for beef, veal, etc.

title: Godiveaux Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pound of Suet
- 1/2 Pound of Lean Veal
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Gill of Cold Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Minced Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Raw Eggs
