- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Remove all the stringy tissue from the suet and pound in a mortar
2. Hash the veal well and mix with the meat
3. Take a tablespoonful of flour and blend well with half a gill of cold milk and a tablespoonful of melted butter and add to the suet and veal and blend well
4. Season highly with salt and pepper, and add a pinch of grated nutmeg
5. Add the yolks of two raw eggs and the white of one egg, and, when well blended, strain all through a sieve
6. Roll into balls and use as needed

title: Gold Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of sugar
- yolks of 28 eggs
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 1/2 lbs. of butter
- yeast powder

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, egg yolks, flour, butter, and yeast powder
2. Bake

title: Gold Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup of butter
- 2 cups of sugar
- yolks of 6 eggs, beaten very stiff
- 1/2 cup of milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- flour
- vanilla, bitter almond, rose water, cinnamon, or nutmeg for flavoring
- currants and raisins

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the beaten egg yolks
3. Add the milk with soda dissolved in it
4. Mix the cream of tartar with flour enough to make it as thick as cup cake
5. Add to the mixture
6. Flavor as desired
7. Bake

title: Gold Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1/4 Cup of Butter
- 3 Cups of Flour
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Add the sugar and beat till very light
3. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs and beat vigorously till light
4. Gradually add the flour, into which you will have sifted the baking powder
5. Bake for forty-five minutes in a moderate oven

title: Golden Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Gill of Brandy or Sherry

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar until creamy
2. Set the saucepan in a kettle of boiling water over the fire
3. Add the eggs that have been beaten well with the brandy or sherry
4. Stir until the sauce becomes a thick cream
5. Take from the fire, add the juice of a lemon and serve very hot

title: Gombo Filee With Oysters, No. Z
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of flour
- a tablespoonful of lard
- two quarts of oysters
- a cupful of cut leeks or onions
- a large slice of ham
- some parsley

## Directions

1. Fry a tablespoonful of flour in a tablespoonful of lard.
2. Let it brown slowly so as not to scorch.
3. Boil the liquor of two quarts of oysters, and when it is boiling throw in a cupful of cut leeks or onions, a large slice of ham, some parsley, and stir in the browned flour.
4. Let this cook fifteen minutes;
5. then pour in two quarts of oysters.
6. Let them boil a few minutes, season with salt and pepper;
7. take out the parsley and sift in half a cup of dried and pounded fresh filee;
8. if not fresh more will be required.

title: Gombo with Crabs, or Shrimp
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of beef
- half a pound knuckle of ham
- lard
- crabs
- shrimps
- okra pods
- onion
- red pepper
- salt
- water
- chicken (optional)

## Directions

1. To a pound of beef add half a pound knuckle of ham; chop up both in inch pieces and fry them brown in two tablespoonfuls of boiling lard;
2. Add to them four large crabs cut up, or a pound of peeled shrimps, or both if desired;
3. Cut into this four dozen small okra pods, one large onion, a little red pepper, and salt to taste.
4. Let all simmer on a slow fire for about twenty minutes; then fill up with warm water, enough to cover the contents two inches deep.
5. Let this boil for two hours. If it becomes too thick, add as much water as required.
6. If preferred a chicken can be used instead of the beef.

title: Goose à la Chipolata
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Young Goose
- 1/2 Pound of Chaurice
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Dozen Large Chestnuts, nicely roasted and skinned
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- The Juice of 2 Lemons
- 4 or 5 Bits of Lemon Peel
- 2 Onions
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Spoon of Flour

## Directions

1. Place the lard or butter in the stewpot and when it melts add the onions, which have been nicely sliced
2. As they brown add the goose, which has been cleaned, singed and nicely cut at the joints into pieces, and well rubbed with salt and pepper, and the sausage, which must be cut in halves
3. Let simmer for about ten minutes, until every portion is slightly browned
4. Add the minced herbs and garlic
5. After three minutes add the spoonful of flour, mixing well
6. Let it all simmer for ten minutes longer
7. Pour in the can of mushrooms and their water, and add immediately the chestnuts
8. Let the goose cook till tender and serve hot

title: Goose, with Chestnuts a la Chipolita
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- sixty large chestnuts
- two ounces of butter
- one onion
- sprig of parsley
- one pint of good broth
- juice of two oranges
- half a pound of currant jelly
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Get the goose ready as usual
2. Peel sixty large chestnuts by scalding
3. Put them in a stew pan with two ounces of butter, one onion chopped fine, and a sprig of parsley
4. Chop and mix all together and stuff the goose with it
5. Mix with the chestnuts one pint of good broth, and stew them down in it before stuffing the goose
6. Boil down the gravy very much
7. When the goose is served, add the juice of two oranges, half a pound of currant jelly, and a lemon peel in the gravy
8. Pour this over the goose when it goes to the table

title: Gooseberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Gooseberries
- 1½ Cups of Sugar
- A Plain Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Top and tail the gooseberries
2. Line two deep pie dishes with a plain Pie Paste
3. Fill with berries
4. Add nearly one cup of sugar to each pie, and proceed as in recipe for Apple Pie
5. Set in the oven and let the pies bake for three-quarters of an hour

title: Gooseberry Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of stewed gooseberries
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 2 lbs. of fresh butter
- 2 lbs. of grated bread
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Stew gooseberries with all the juice made very sweet
2. Add 1 lb. of white sugar, 2 lbs. of fresh butter, 2 lbs. of grated bread, 3 eggs

title: Governor's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cocoanut
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- whites of 6 eggs
- nutmeg
- sugar to the taste

## Directions

1. To 1 large cocoanut add 1/2 lb. of butter, the whites of 6 eggs, nutmeg and sugar to the taste
2. to be baked in a rich paste

title: Graham Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups Graham flour
- 1 cup white flour
- 1 teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1 heaping tablespoonful sugar or molasses
- 2 cups lukewarm water

## Directions

1. Mix the batter until it reaches the consistency of oatmeal porridge
2. Pour into buttered tins
3. Let it rise till light
4. Bake in a moderate oven
5. Milk may be used instead of water, if you have it

title: Graham Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- good, fresh, ground unbolted flour
- three quarts of wheat meal
- one half a cup of good yeast
- three spoonfuls of molasses
- water
- flour enough to mould it
- a little soda

## Directions

1. Get good, fresh, ground unbolted flour, and sift it through a common hair sieve
2. Take three quarts of this wheat meal, one half a cup of good yeast, and three spoonfuls of molasses
3. Mix to a sponge with water
4. Work in flour enough to mould it
5. Put a little soda in the batter before moulding, as it is more disposed to ferment than fine or bolted flour
6. Proceed as you do with common wheat bread

title: Graham Muffins For Dyspeptics
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of Graham flour
- one half cup of brown sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoonfuls of yeast
- warm water or milk
- a little soda

## Directions

1. Take a quart of Graham flour, one half cup of brown sugar, one teaspoon of salt, two tablespoonfuls of yeast, warm water or milk enough to soften it sufficiently to stir readily with a spoon
2. When it is light, stir up again and drop in rings and bake
3. If made over night, add a little soda in the morning
4. Bake soft

title: Grand Brule a la Boulanger
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two wineglasses of best French brandy
- one half wineglass of kirsh
- one half wineglass of maraschino
- a small quantity of cinnamon and allspice
- about ten cubes of white sugar

## Directions

1. Pour into a large silver bowl two wineglasses of best French brandy, one half wineglass of kirsh, the same of maraschino, and a small quantity of cinnamon and allspice
2. Put in about ten cubes of white sugar; do not crush them, but let them become saturated with the liquor
3. Remove the lumps of sugar, place in a ladle and cover with brandy
4. Ignite it, then lift it with the contents from the bowl, but do not mix
5. After it has burned about fifteen minutes serve in wine glasses

title: Grape Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the grapes from the stems; wash and drain them; mash them with a spoon; put them in the preserving kettle, and cover them closely with a large plate; boil them 10 minutes; then pour them into the jelly bag, and squeeze out the juice
2. Allow a pint of juice to 1 lb. of sugar; put the sugar and juice into the kettle, and boil them 20 minutes, skimming them well; fill the glasses while the jelly is warm, and tie them up with brandy papers

title: Grape Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes
- sugar (1 lb. per pint of juice)

## Directions

1. Pick the grapes from the stems
2. Wash and drain them
3. Mash them with a spoon
4. Put them in the preserving kettle, and cover them closely with a large plate
5. Boil them 10 minutes
6. Pour them into the jelly bag, and squeeze out the juice
7. Allow a pint of juice to 1 lb. of sugar
8. Put the sugar and juice into the kettle, and boil them 20 minutes, skimming them well
9. Fill the glasses while the jelly is warm, and tie them up with brandy papers

title: Grape Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Malaga Grapes
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water five minutes
2. Mash the grapes and add all to the sugar and water
3. Set to cool
4. Press through a fine cloth, pressing hard to extract all the juice of the grapes
5. Set to freeze and serve as directed

title: Grape Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Gallon Plain Syrup
- 2 Pints of Grape Juice
- 3/4 Pint of Grape or Catawba Wine

## Directions

1. Pick, wash, stem and seed the grapes; then pound and mash well, and set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours.
2. Then strain through a bag, pressing out all the juice; make a Plain Syrup, and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Currant Syrup, and add the wine when you are about to bottle.

title: Grassets, Reed Birds, Robins, Larks, Broiled or Roasted
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Birds
- 6 or 8 Slices of Toast
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 6 or 8 Strips of Bacon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sliced Lemon and Sprigs of Parsley or Watercress to Garnish

## Directions

1. These birds are always broiled or roasted, following the invariable rules
2. They should be broiled over a clear fire, and do not require much more than five minutes to broil; ten minutes to roast in a quick oven
3. Serve whether broiled or roasted, on buttered French toast, and garnish with cresses of parsley sprigs
4. All these little birds should be broiled en brochette, that is, a skewer should be run through the body
5. Salt and pepper after, and pour melted butter and chopped parsley over them
6. If roasted, they may be served with a brown gravy

title: Green (Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Young Spinach
- A Few Grains of Powdered Alum
- sugar
- alcohol

## Directions

1. Pound one pound of young Spinach to a pulp
2. carefully squeeze all the juice out of it
3. Set to boil, and the moment it begins to curdle remove from the fire
4. strain all the juice from it
5. add to the curd the same weight in sugar, a few grains of powdered alum, and a little alcohol, and bottle for use
6. Keep in a cool place

title: Green Corn Fritters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- a cup of milk
- a pint of boiled green corn grated
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- lard

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs with a cup of milk
2. Add a pint of boiled green corn grated
3. Throw in flour enough to make a batter thick enough to drop from a spoon
4. Add salt and pepper to taste
5. Beat it very hard
6. Drop into boiling lard one spoonful at a time

title: Green Corn Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Finely-Grated Green Corn
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs separately, and then add the yolks, well beaten, to the corn.
2. Add the salt, melted butter and the milk, and mix well.
3. Then add sufficient flour to make a thin batter, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Bake on a hot griddle, butter generously, and serve very hot.

title: Green Corn on the Cob
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Get it as fresh from the field as possible
2. When it is well silked, throw it into boiling salted water
3. Boil for only fifteen minutes; too long boiling takes out the sweetness from the grain

title: Green Corn Soup. Very Delicate
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- at least a pint of corn
- a quart of sweet milk
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a piece of butter the size of a hen’s-egg
- pepper to suit your taste

## Directions

1. Cut corn from the cob until you have at least a pint
2. Cover it with a quart of sweet milk
3. Let it boil half an hour
4. Add a teaspoonful of salt
5. Skim it carefully
6. Then throw into it a piece of butter the size of a hen’s-egg and pepper to suit your taste
7. Serve with rolls or toasted bread

title: Green Corn, Planter's Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Ears of Corn
- 2 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Husk the corn and pull off the silk, leaving one layer of leaves close to the kernels
2. Put to cook in cold water
3. When the water begins to boil, after ten minutes, add the salt
4. Do not let the corn boil longer than five minutes after adding the salt

title: Green English Peas, to Stew
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- quart of green peas
- cold water
- pint of boiling water
- large lump of butter
- flour
- teaspoonful of white sugar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Shell a quart of green peas for a small mess
2. Wash them in cold water
3. Put them on to cook in a stew pan with a pint of boiling water, or enough to cover them
4. Let them cook half an hour
5. Stir in a large lump of butter rolled in flour
6. Let this cook a few minutes
7. Add a teaspoonful of white sugar, same of salt and pepper
8. Serve while hot

title: Green Gage Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ripe Greengages
- 3 Ounces of Apple Marmalade
- ½ Pound of Feuillage Paste

## Directions

1. Prepare a half pound of Feuillage Paste and line six tart molds with it
2. Spread nicely on the bottom of each three ounces of Apple Marmalade
3. Take the greengages, wipe well and remove the stones
4. Cut into quartered pieces and lay nicely over the marmalade
5. Sprinkle two ounces of powdered sugar evenly over them
6. Bake in a moderate oven for twenty-five minutes
7. Remove and let cool
8. Spread apple marmalade nicely over the top and send to the table

title: Green or Herb Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- A Veal Brisket
- 1 Large Slice of Lean Ham
- Equal Parts of the Leaves of Young Cabbage, Radish, Turnips, Mustard, Spinach, Watercress, Parsley and Green Onions
- 1 Large Red or White Onion
- 1/2 Red Pepper Pod
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Clove
- 2 Allspice
- Cayenne to Taste
- Salt and Black Pepper to Taste
- Lard

## Directions

1. Soak and wash the leaves thoroughly, being careful to wash each leaf separately, to be sure there lurk no insects in the folds or ridges.
2. Trim by taking off all the coarse midrib of the leaves, which will make the gumbo taste coarse and unpalatable.
3. Boil the leaves together for about two hours and then parboil by adding a teaspoonful of cooking soda.
4. Strain and chop very fine, being careful to save the water in which they were boiled.
5. Cut the brisket of veal and the sliced ham into small pieces and dredge with black pepper and salt, and chop one large white or red onion.
6. Put a heaping teaspoonful of lard into a deep frying pan, and when hot, add the chopped veal and the ham.
7. Cover and let it simmer for about ten minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
8. Then add the chopped onion and a little sprig of parsley chopped fine.
9. When it comes to a rich brown, add the greens, and when these are browned, pour over four quarts of the water in which the leaves have been boiled.
10. Throw in the finely chopped bay leaf, thyme, sweet marjoram, and the red pepper pod and the clove and allspice, mashed fine.
11. Set it back on the stove and let it boil for about one hour longer, adding the Cayenne or hot pepper.
12. Serve with boiled rice.

title: Green Pea Soup, Without Meat, For Lent
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pints of green peas
- two quarts of water
- three or four onions
- two heads of celery
- a carrot
- a turnip
- salt to taste
- butter

## Directions

1. Put two pints of green peas in two quarts of water, boil until the peas are very soft; then add three or four onions, two heads of celery, a carrot, and a turnip, all cut small; season with salt to taste, add a little butter, and boil for two hours. If it becomes too thick, add one pint of boiling water. The peas may be boiled the day before, and kept over for convenience, if desired. This recipe is intended for green peas but it may be made with dried peas also, and the longer they boil, the better the soup will be. Do not add the vegetables until the day it is wanted.

title: Green Peaches Pickled
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- green peaches
- salt
- water
- grape leaves
- saleratus
- vinegar
- whole pepper
- allspice
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Brush the down from green peaches (cling-stones)
2. Put them in salt and water, with grape leaves and a bit of saleratus
3. Set them over a moderate fire to simmer slowly until they are a fine green, then take them out, wipe them dry, and smooth the skins
4. Take enough vinegar to cover them, put to it whole pepper, allspice, and mustard seed, making it boiling hot
5. Turn it over the peaches
6. Repeat the scalding three successive days

title: Green Peas
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- young, freshly shelled green peas
- salt
- 1 teaspoonful pounded loaf sugar
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash peas clean
2. Put them in a bag and the bag into plenty of boiling water, with a little salt and a teaspoonful of pounded loaf sugar
3. Boil them till tender, taking from 1½ to 1 hour
4. Never let them stand in the water after they are done
5. Season them with butter, salt and pepper

title: Green Peas
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- salt

## Directions

1. Put the peas in a covered vessel with a little salt
2. Place this in another vessel filled with water
3. Boil until the peas are tender

title: Green Peas
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- salt
- a teaspoonful of pounded loaf sugar
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Use young and freshly shelled green peas
2. Wash them clean
3. Put them in a bag and the bag into plenty of boiling water with a little salt and a teaspoonful of pounded loaf sugar
4. Boil them till tender, taking from 3/4 to 1 hour
5. Never let them stand in the water after they are done
6. Season them with butter, salt and pepper

title: Green Peas a la Bourgeoise
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pints of green peas
- fresh butter
- parsley
- lettuce cut in fours
- a little sugar
- yolk of 2 eggs
- a little cream

## Directions

1. Wash 1.5 pints of green peas
2. Put them into a stewpan with fresh butter, parsley, lettuce cut in fours, and a little sugar
3. Let these boil in their own juice over a slow fire
4. When all the liquid is boiled away, add a thickening made of the yolk of 2 eggs and a little cream
5. Let the whole remain a few minutes on the fire

title: Green Peas à la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Peas
- 1½ Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Ounce of Chopped Onion
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Minced Fine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- ½ Pint of Cream or Milk or Water

## Directions

1. Shell and boil the peas until done, about forty minutes if the peas are large.
2. Add sprigs of parsley, thyme and bay leaf, finely minced.
3. Drain off all water and add one tablespoonful and a half of butter.
4. Blend the flour, and add the milk, preferably, mixing together and stirring well, to prevent browning or burning.
5. Serve hot.

title: Green Peas, Country Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Green Peas
- 3 Small Carrots
- 1-8 of a Head of Green and White Cabbage
- A Tablespoonful of Butter
- ½ Pint of Consomme of Veal or Chicken
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Shell the peas; take three small carrots, one-eighth of a head of cabbage, and one-quarter head of lettuce, and cut into small dice-shaped pieces.
2. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan and let the dice-shaped vegetables smother for about fifteen minutes over a slow fire without browning.
3. Add the green peas and the consomme, and let all cook for a half hour, stirring frequently to prevent burning.
4. Season to taste with salt and pepper, and use a sprinkling of chopped parsley as a garnish.

title: Green Peas, French Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh Green Peas, or 1 Can
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- ½ Cup of Water
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Lettuce Heart
- 1 Onion
- 1 Teaspoonful of Powdered Sugar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and wash and drain them.
2. Put them into a saucepan, with one tablespoonful of butter; add the herb bouquet, the onion whole, and the lettuce heart, and cover with cold water, and let them simmer slowly for about twenty minutes, or until tender.
3. Drain off the water, remove the onion and herb bouquet, lay the lettuce heart on a dish, and add another tablespoonful of butter to the peas.
4. Let them cook for five minutes longer.
5. Pour the peas over the lettuce heart and send to the table hot, and serve with chops or cutlets as a vegetable.

title: Green Peas, Old Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Young Green Peas
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Cream
- The Yolk of 1 Egg
- 1 Teaspoonful of Powdered Sugar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Shell and clean the peas carefully.
2. Put them in a saucepan with three tablespoonfuls of butter, and cover with a cup of water; season with a pinch of salt, and let them cook for twenty minutes, or until tender.
3. Take three tablespoonfuls of cream and beat with the yolk of one egg; add a half pinch of white pepper, and mix thoroughly with the peas.
4. Add a teaspoonful of powdered sugar, stir well, and let all cook together for five minutes and serve hot.

title: Green Pepper Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen green peppers
- 1 dozen red peppers
- 1/2 large head of cabbage
- 3 tablespoonfuls of mustard seed
- 1 teaspoonful of ground cloves
- 1 tablespoonful of ground allspice
- 2 tablespoonfuls of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of salt per pepper
- cold water
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the tops from the peppers and remove the seeds
2. Put a teaspoonful of salt into each pepper, cover with cold water, and soak for twenty-four hours
3. Chop the cabbage very fine, and mix well with the ground spices, mustard seed and salt
4. When thoroughly blended, stuff the peppers with this mixture
5. Put on the tops, and stand upright in stone jars, and cover with cold vinegar

title: Green Pepper Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Tomatoes
- 2 Green Peppers
- 1 Large Onion
- French Dressing, Plain

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes, onions and green peppers nicely and thin.
2. Arrange on a dish, placing a layer of tomatoes, an alternate layer of onion and green pepper, and tomatoes mixed.
3. Dress either before bringing to the table, or at the table, with French dressing.

title: Green Peppers Sauted
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen large green peppers
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 chopped shallots
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- A dash of sweet paprica
- Salt to taste
- 1 pint of consomme
- Chopped yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- Fancy slices of toast

## Directions

1. Plunge the peppers into hot boiling fat and rub off the skin.
2. Cut them into halves and cut off the stem and take out the seeds.
3. Carefully cut away the ribs.
4. Let the peppers saute for ten minutes in a tablespoonful of butter.
5. Moisten with a pint of consomme; season with the chopped shallots, parsley and sweet paprica.
6. Let all cook slowly for a half hour, or until done.
7. Prepare fancy slices of toast, and serve the peppers on these with a delicate garnish of chopped yolks of hard-boiled eggs.

title: Green Peppers à l'Espagnole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Green Peppers
- 3 Tomatoes
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Parboil the peppers so that they will peel easily, and scald the tomatoes.
2. Peel them, removing the seeds of the peppers.
3. Cut the peppers into one-inch pieces, slice the tomatoes, and serve with plain French dressing as a Salad.

title: Green Rémoulade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Raw Yolk of Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1/8 Clove of Garlic, Minced Very Fine
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- About Two Tablespoonfuls of Spinach or Parsley Juice

## Directions

1. Make in exactly the same manner as Sauce Rémoulade
2. Color with the juice of spinach or parsley, using about two tablespoonfuls of either

title: Green Tomato Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of green tomatoes
- 1 dozen onions
- 1 ounce of cloves
- 1 ounce of mustard seed
- 1 tablespoonful of white mustard seed
- 1 cupful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of red pepper or the pods cut into strips
- 1 ounce of cloves
- 1 ounce of black pepper
- 2 quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes and the onions
2. Put a layer of tomatoes and a layer of onions, and sprinkle with salt, and continue till all are used up
3. Let them stand over night
4. In the morning drain off all the liquor that has accumulated, and put the vegetables into a porcelain-lined pot with all the other ingredients, and cover with two quarts of vinegar
5. Let all simmer gently for a quarter of an hour, and then put away in stone jars, letting the vinegar cover the pickles, and boiling while hot

title: Green Tomato Soy
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ bushel of green tomatoes
- 1½ dozen of white onions
- salt
- vinegar
- sugar
- sweet oil
- box of mustard
- black pepper
- mace
- cloves
- allspice
- mustard seed
- coriander
- celery seed

## Directions

1. Take ½ bushel of green tomatoes and 1½ dozen of white onions
2. Slice them thin and put them in jars, with a tablespoonful of salt between each layer
3. Let them stand 24 hours
4. Drain off the water and boil them 10 or 15 minutes in vinegar
5. Take them out and put them again in jars, a layer of tomatoes and onions, spice, a little sugar, and a tablespoonful of sweet oil, until the jar is filled
6. Mix a box of mustard in the vinegar, and pour over them till all are covered
7. Use black pepper, mace, cloves, allspice, mustard seed, coriander and celery seed as spices to be bruised

title: Green Tomato Soy, or Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a peck of green tomatoes
- a pint of salt
- a dozen raw onions
- two tablespoonfuls of black pepper
- two tablespoonfuls of allspice
- a quarter of a spoonful of ground mustard
- half a pound of white mustard seed
- a tablespoonful of red pepper
- strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice a peck of green tomatoes thin
2. salt them thoroughly, using a pint of salt
3. Let them stay in this all night
4. in the morning drain them from the salt
5. wash them in cold water
6. put them in a kettle with a dozen cut-up raw onions, two tablespoonfuls of black pepper, same of allspice, a quarter of a spoonful of ground mustard, half a pound of white mustard seed, and a tablespoonful of red pepper
7. Cover all with strong vinegar
8. boil it until it becomes like jam
9. Stir it frequently while it is boiling or it will scorch

title: Green Tomato Sweet Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- seven pounds tomatoes
- salt
- four and a half pounds of sugar
- one ounce of cinnamon
- one ounce of cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice tomatoes until you have seven pounds
2. Sprinkle them with salt
3. Let them stand twenty-four hours
4. Soak them for the same length of time in fresh water to get the brine from them
5. When drained off and ready, allow four and a half pounds of sugar, one ounce of cinnamon, one ounce of cloves, and enough vinegar to cover them
6. Boil the compound together
7. Pour it over the tomatoes
8. Let them stand twenty-four hours
9. Then bring all to a boil
10. Tie away in jars
11. Keep in a cool place away from the light

title: Green Turtle Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Turtle Meat, or a 2-Pound Turtle
- 2 Fine Large Onions
- 1 Square inch of Ham
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1/2 of a Small Lemon
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine
- 6 Cloves
- 4 Allspice
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay Leaf
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Select two pounds of fine Green Turtle meat. If the turtle is bought whole, first cut off the head by hanging the turtle with the head downwards and using a very sharp knife to cut off the head as close as possible.
2. To remove the shells, first separate the upper from the lower shell, being exceedingly careful to avoid touching the gall bladder.
3. Clean the turtle and the entrails by cutting open and washing thoroughly in cold water.
4. Put the meat and entrails into a saucepan and parboil about ten minutes. Save this stock of water.
5. Chop an onion very fine, and the ham into very fine pieces.
6. Cut the turtle meat into one-inch pieces, mash the cloves and the allspice very fine and chop the thyme and bay leaf.
7. Brown the onions in a tablespoonful of butter or lard, and add immediately the turtle meat.
8. Brown together slightly and after a few minutes add the chopped ham.
9. Add two cloves of garlic chopped fine, and the thyme, bay leaf minced fine, cloves and allspice ground, all mixed together, and lay on the turtle.
10. Stir almost constantly to prevent burning, and add two tablespoonfuls of flour that has been well rubbed, stirring constantly all the time.
11. Dissolve the meat with the water in which the turtle was parboiled, adding gradually until a certain consistency is reached. About three quarts of water will be the required amount.
12. Season with salt, black pepper and Cayenne to taste, and boil slowly for fully an hour, stirring almost constantly.
13. After cooking one hour taste, and if not seasoned sufficiently season again and taste.
14. Chop one-quarter of a small lemon and put it in the soup.
15. When well done—that is when no blood exudes from the turtle after sticking it with a fork—pour into the tureen.
16. Add the whites and yolks of two hard-boiled eggs chopped fine, and one good glass of Sherry wine, and the soup is ready to serve.

title: Green Turtle Steaks
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Turtle Steaks
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sliced Lemon and Parsley to Garnish
- Currant Jelly Sauce or Sauce Poivrade

## Directions

1. Select the female turtle, as the meat is best. If bought alive from the market, clean according to recipe. Otherwise the butcher may prepare it as is frequently done in the New Orleans market.
2. Cut the meat into thick slices or steaks, about the size of a filet of beef, and batter down with the hands to make smooth and regular.
3. Fry in butter.
4. Season well with salt and pepper and garnish with parsley and lemon, and serve with Currant Jelly Sauce or the delightful Sauce Poivrade.

title: Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 3 Cups of Sour Milk or Warm Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 3 Eggs, Beaten Very Light
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and meal, and pour on the milk or warm water.
2. Make a batter somewhat stiffier than for Buckwheat Cakes, and add the eggs, well-beaten, the whites and yolks separately, and finally add the soda, dissolved in a little warm water.
3. Bake on a griddle, making the cakes large and generous.
4. Serve with Louisiana Syrup.

title: Grillades Breaded. Grillades Panées.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 round of veal
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- 1 beaten egg
- 1/2 cup of bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. The round of the veal is always used for this.
2. Cut the veal into squares of about four inches.
3. Season well with salt, pepper and Cayenne.
4. Beat an egg well and take each grillade and soak it well in the egg.
5. Roll in bread crumbs grated.
6. Have ready a pan of boiling grease, sufficient for the grillades to swim in it.
7. Fry to a nice brown and serve very hot.

title: Grillades With Gravy. Grillades a la Sauce.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 round steak
- 1 tomato
- 1 large onion
- salt
- pepper
- Cayenne
- tablespoonful of lard
- one clove of garlic
- tablespoonful of flour
- two tomatoes, sliced
- half tablespoonful of vinegar
- cup of water

## Directions

1. Select a nice round steak and beat well.
2. Cut into grillades of about four inches square and season highly with salt and pepper and Cayenne.
3. Put a tablespoonful of lard into the frying pan, and when it heats, add a chopped onion and one clove of garlic.
4. As these brown, add one tablespoonful of flour, making a Brown Roux.
5. Then add two tomatoes, sliced, with their juices.
6. As this browns lay the grillades upon it.
7. Cover closely, and as it browns on one side, turn on the other.
8. Then add a half tablespoonful of vinegar and a cup of water.
9. Stir well and set back on the stove and let it simmer slowly for about a half hour.
10. This is very nice served with hominy at breakfast, or with red or white beans and boiled rice at dinner.

title: Ground Rice Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 large spoonfuls of rice
- cold milk
- 1 quart of boiling milk
- 5 eggs
- lemon
- sugar to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix 2 1/2 large spoonfuls of rice in a little cold milk
2. stir it into 1 quart of boiling milk
3. let it boil 15 minutes, stirring it constantly
4. when cold, add 5 eggs, a little lemon, sugar to the taste, and bake it 1 hour
5. put a paste, or not, as you prefer, on the dish

title: Groundnut Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of shelled ground nuts
- 2 spoonfuls of flour
- 1 pint of oysters
- 1 pint and a half of water
- 1 or 2 red peppers

## Directions

1. Beat up the ground nuts well
2. Add two spoonfuls of flour and mix well
3. Put to them a pint of oysters and a pint and a half of boiling water
4. Throw in a red pepper or two if small

title: Gruel Batter Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of thick gruel
- 1 large tablespoonful of butter
- 9 eggs
- a little salt
- 2 spoonfuls of flour and cornmeal
- 1 small teaspoonful of soda
- warm water

## Directions

1. Cook the butter in the gruel
2. Remove from the fire and pour into a wooden bowl
3. Stir until almost cool
4. Add the eggs and meal
5. Dissolve 1 small teaspoonful of soda in warm water and add lastly
6. The cornmeal should be white and fresh

title: Gumbo Aux Herbes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage leaves
- beet leaves
- turnip leaves
- mustard leaves
- spinach
- cresses
- parsley
- green onions
- cooking soda
- veal brisket
- ham
- large onion
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- hot water
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of cabbage leaves, beet leaves, turnip leaves, mustard leaves, spinach, cresses, parsley and green onions
2. Soak them and wash them thoroughly
3. Trim them by taking off the coarse mid rib of the leaf
4. Boil them for at least two hours
5. Add to the water a teaspoonful of cooking soda
6. Strain them and chop up as you would spinach
7. Take a piece of veal brisket and a slice of ham and cut into small pieces
8. Add a large onion, chopped fine, salt and pepper
9. Fry all in a heaping spoonful of lard to a rich brown
10. Add the greens, stirring with a wooden spoon
11. Add as much hot water as will make sufficient gumbo for the family
12. Serve with boiled rice

title: Gumbo d'Herbes - Green Gumbo
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- equal portions of young cabbage leaves
- beet leaves
- turnip leaves
- mustard leaves
- spinach
- cresses
- parsley
- green onions
- beef
- ham
- hot pepper

## Directions

1. Take equal portions of young cabbage leaves, beet leaves, turnip leaves, mustard leaves, spinach, cresses, parsley, green onions, and place them in boiling water
2. When well boiled, chop them fine
3. Make a good soup (brown gravy) in which you put beef and ham to fry a little
4. Add your vegetables (or herbs)
5. Stir often and let them fry about one hour, keeping the pot well covered
6. Add the required quantity of hot water, and let it all boil gently for about two hours
7. By adding hot pepper, you will have a real Creole Gumbo

title: Gumbo File
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Tender Chicken
- 2 Large Slices or ½ Pound Lean Ham
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter or 1 of Lard
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Water
- 2 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Half Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- Salt and Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of File

## Directions

1. Clean and cut up the chicken as for a fricassee
2. Dredge with salt and black pepper, judging according to taste
3. Cut the ham into dice shapes and chop the onion, parsley and thyme very fine
4. Put the lard or butter into the soup kettle or deep stewing pot, and when hot, put in the ham and chicken
5. Cover closely and fry for about five or ten minutes
6. Then add the onion and parsley and thyme, stirring occasionally to prevent burning
7. When nicely browned add the boiling water and throw in the oyster stock, which has been thoroughly heated
8. Add the bay leaf chopped very fine, and the pepper pod, cut in two, and set the gumbo back to simmer for about an hour longer
9. When nearly ready to serve dinner, and while the Gumbo is boiling, add the fresh oysters
10. Let the gumbo remain on the stove for about three minutes longer, and then remove the pot from the fire
11. Have ready the tureens, set in a bain-marie or hot water bath
12. Take two tablespoonfuls of the File and drop gradually into the pot of boiling hot Gumbo, stirring slowly to mix thoroughly
13. Pour into the tureen or tureens and serve with boiled rice, about two spoonfuls of rice to one plate of gumbo

title: Haly's Buckwheat Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of fresh buckwheat flour
- half a cup of yeast
- one tablespoonful of salt
- one and a half quarts of milk and water warmed
- a teaspoonful of soda or saleratus
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Beat all well with a large spoon
2. Pour the mixture in a tall jar as in that it rises better than in a flaring or open crock
3. In the morning add a teaspoonful of soda or saleratus just before frying the cakes
4. Grease the griddle and fry them brown
5. Eat with syrup or honey

title: Ham and Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 slices of ham
- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- parsley

## Directions

1. Soak the ham in hot water before frying.
2. Cut slices of about half an inch in thickness, of sufficient size to lay an egg upon them.
3. Lay the ham in the hot frying pan, and let it fry until the fat becomes transparent.
4. Take the slices out and put them on a hot dish.
5. Break the eggs, one by one, into a saucer, and slip them into the frying pan.
6. Fry in the same lard in which you have fried the ham.
7. When the yolks are quite set, take them out, and lay one egg on each slice of ham.
8. Garnish nicely with parsley, and serve hot.

title: Ham Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Lean Ham
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 1 Tablespoonful of French Mustard
- 1 Gill of Cold White Sauce
- 1 Boiled Onion
- 1 Cooked Garlic
- 2 Ounces of Grated Parmesan Cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare the toast nicely, cutting the slices the full width of the bread, paring the edges nicely and toasting and buttering well.
2. Lay on each piece of toast a thin slice of very lean ham, which has been lightly spread with French mustard.
3. Spread lightly over this a cold White Sauce to which has been added while cooking a finely-minced onion and clove of garlic, and a dash of Parmesan cheese.
4. Dredge the top of the Canapé with Parmesan cheese, then sprinkle lightly with finely-grated bread crumbs.
5. Set in the oven for five minutes and bake and send to the table hot.

title: Ham Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of finely chopped boiled ham
- 2 cups of mashed potatoes
- The yolks of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- A dash of cayenne
- Bread crumbs
- Boiling fat

## Directions

1. Chop the ham fine and add to the mashed potatoes.
2. Add the cream and butter and the yolks of two eggs, beaten well.
3. Beat all together until smooth, then add a dash of cayenne.
4. Mold the ham into cylinder shapes of about a finger in length and roll in the beaten egg that remains.
5. Roll in bread crumbs grated and fry in the boiling fat.

title: Ham Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped ham
- 1/2 grated onion
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks to a cream and add the other ingredients
2. Rub all these smoothly together
3. Add the whites of eggs, beaten to a froth
4. Beat all thoroughly together
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan
6. When it melts add the omelet
7. Let it stand, shaking occasionally to prevent from sticking to the pan, till the eggs are quite set
8. Fold as in a plain omelet
9. Turn into a hot dish and serve

title: Ham Soufflé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of minced ham
- 3 eggs, beaten with the whites and yolks separate
- 1 teaspoonful of finely chopped parsley
- Pepper to taste
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Mix together the chopped parsley, ham and yolks of eggs and a dash of cayenne pepper.
2. Beat all very hard till it becomes light.
3. Add the whites of the eggs, which have been beaten to a froth.
4. Beat together sufficiently to mix well.
5. Fill a dish and bake in an oven for eight or ten minutes and serve with a cream sauce.

title: Ham Toast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- lean ham
- yolk of an egg
- pepper
- butter
- square bits of toast

## Directions

1. Grate some lean ham
2. Mix with it the yolk of an egg, pepper it, and fry it in butter
3. Pour on square bits of toast, and brown it with a salamander

title: Ham Toast for Luncheon
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of an egg
- a tablespoonful of sweet milk
- grated or finely chopped ham
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Beat the yolk of an egg with a tablespoonful of sweet milk.
2. Set it on the fire to warm, and thicken it with grated or finely chopped ham.
3. Let it simmer a few moments and pour it on buttered toast.

title: Hampton Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the yolks of 8 eggs and the whites of 5
- 1 lb. of pounded loaf sugar
- 1/2 lb. of sifted flour
- the grated peel of a small lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites together
2. Add the sugar
3. Add the flour and lemon peel
4. Beat all well together
5. Bake it in a floured tin

title: Hampton Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls of yeast
- 1 pint of flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well
2. Add the yeast and flour
3. Add as much water as will make a stiff batter
4. When light, bake them in hoops for breakfast

title: Hard Brandy Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 1/2 Cup of Butter
- 1 Wineglassful of Brandy
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream.
2. Add the brandy and the powdered cinnamon and serve cold.

title: Hard Fig-Leaf Yeast Made Without Hops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint cup of fig-leaves
- a quart of cold water
- Irish potatoes enough to fill a pint-cup
- a tablespoonful of brown sugar
- flour
- corn-meal
- a cup of cold water
- a spoonful of brown sugar
- two quarts of flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Take a pint cup of the leaves, put them to a quart of cold water, and boil them until a strong tea or decoction is made
2. put away to cool
3. pour off the tea carefully, leaving the dregs and leaves
4. Now boil and wash Irish potatoes enough to fill a pint-cup
5. put them to the tea of fig-leaves
6. beat them up with a tablespoonful of brown sugar and flour, to make a stiff batter
7. put it in a covered vessel to rise
8. When this yeast is light and frothing, thicken it immediately with corn-meal, until it is thick enough to be rolled out like biscuit
9. Roll it out, cut and dry the cake, turning them very often until dry
10. When you wish to make bread, take one of the cakes in the morning, put it in a covered mug or pitcher
11. put on it a cup of cold water, and when it is dissolved, put to it a spoonful of brown sugar, and make a batter of the water and yeast cake
12. Make this batter as stiff as pound-cake batter
13. when it rises well, mix with two quarts of flour, and the bread will be most excellent
14. Use lard as usual in making the bread up for baking

title: Hard Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of a pound of butter
- half a pound of pulverized white sugar
- one wineglass of wine or brandy
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat a quarter of a pound of butter to a cream
2. Stir into it half a pound of pulverized white sugar
3. Beat it until it is light
4. A wineglass of wine or brandy may be added
5. Grate nutmeg over it
6. Put it on ice if the weather is warm

title: Hard Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Powdered White Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream and gradually add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
2. Add the flavoring and heap on a small butter dish, sprinkle with grated nutmeg and serve cold

title: Hard Sauce a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- Vanilla
- Brandy

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter to a rich cream
2. Add a teaspoonful of vanilla, or a tablespoonful of good brandy, and serve cold

title: Hard-Egg Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or two hard-boiled eggs
- Finely-minced parsley

## Directions

1. Make a White Sauce.
2. Add three or two hard-boiled eggs, chopped, but not too fine, and a little finely-minced parsley as a garnish.
3. This sauce is served with boiled fish or boiled chicken or other fowl.

title: Hare or Rabbit Roasted
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- grated bread crumbs
- suet or butter
- a chopped onion
- the liver of the rabbit chopped
- a lemon peel grated
- eggs
- a little claret
- butter
- the drippings in the pan
- a little cream or milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbits and wash them through several waters
2. Stuff with grated bread crumbs, suet or butter, a chopped onion, the liver of the rabbit chopped, and a lemon peel grated
3. Moisten with eggs and a little claret
4. Put this in the rabbit and sew it up
5. Baste with butter, and cook for two hours
6. Make the gravy with the drippings in the pan, a little cream or milk, and flour

title: Hare, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine, Tender Hare
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Dozen Small Onions
- 3 Tomatoes
- 1 Ounce of Minced Ham
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1/2 Glass of White Wine
- 1/2 Glass of Red Wine
- 1 1/2 Tablespoonfuls of Salt
- 1 1/2 Tablespoonfuls of Pepper
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Grated Nutmeg
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, draw and thoroughly wash a fine tender Hare and preserve the liver and heart
2. Cut the Hare into pieces at the joints
3. Make a marinade by taking a half glass of White Wine, one large finely-sliced onion, the thyme and bay leaves finely minced, and place in a stone jar
4. Add a half teaspoonful of grated nutmeg and a tablespoonful of salt, and place in this mixture the cut-up Hare, and let all steep for six hours
5. Lift the pieces out carefully and have ready a saucepan into which you will have placed a teaspoonful of butter
6. Add twelve small onions glazed, one ounce of ham minced fine
7. Put the Hare into the pan and let all brown nicely for about ten minutes
8. Add the flour finely rubbed, stir well and let brown
9. Add the tomatoes peeled and sliced fine, let all brown ten minutes longer
10. Add the red wine and the consomme or water and stir till it begins to boil
11. Season according to taste with salt and pepper
12. Let all cook for three quarters of an hour
13. Add the heart and liver which you will have finely chopped and thoroughly mixed together
14. Let all cook for a quarter of an hour longer and serve with toasted Croutons

title: Hare, Roasted and Stuffed
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Hares
- 3 Onions
- 1 Carrot
- 3 Apples
- 2 Ounces of Sausage Meat
- 6 Mushrooms
- 1 Lemon
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Cloves
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Glass of White Wine or Cider
- 1 Pint of Consomme
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Slices of Bacon
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Pepper
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Salt
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Select two fine Hares, and cut them in half
2. Separate the hindquarters from the fore and then bone them down to the legs, but do not bone the legs
3. Place the Hares in an earthen dish that is quite deep
4. Make a marinade by pouring in a glassful of white wine, add a small lemon nicely sliced, and a small onion minced fine, one sprig of thyme and one bay leaf, all minced very fine
5. Season this preparation with a tablespoonful of salt and a tablespoonful of pepper and two mashed cloves
6. Take the saddles of the Hares and roll them well in this, and let the entire Hares steep well in the marinade for twelve hours
7. Chop an onion very fine and put it in a saucepan on the stove, and when well heated, put in a tablespoonful of butter, cook for one minute
8. Add two ounces of fine sausage chopped very fine, six mushrooms chopped very fine, a teaspoonful of minced parsley, a teaspoonful of salt and a half teaspoonful of pepper
9. Mix well and let all cook for about five minutes
10. Take three fine apples and cut them fine, carefully removing the cores, place them in a clean saucepan on the fire with a half glassful of good White Wine or the best Cider
11. Let this boil about five minutes, and then add the stuffing and mix well together
12. Set the mixture to cool
13. Take the Hares from the marinade and stuff the boned saddles very carefully and evenly, and give a nice round, even shape
14. Tie them to keep them firm, then place a fine slice of bacon over each saddle, tying firmly
15. Cut up a carrot and onion into fine slices and place in the bottom of the roasting pan
16. Lay the Hares over these and pour one pint of consomme over the Hares
17. Place them in a hot oven and roast for three-quarters of an hour, basting frequently with their own gravy
18. Remove from the oven and untie
19. Place the Hares on a hot dish nicely decorated with dice-shaped Croutons, and pour the gravy over the Hares and serve very hot

title: Harrison Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 6 cups of flour
- 4 cups of sugar
- 2 cups of butter
- 1 cup of cream
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake
3. Eat with fresh butter

title: Hash (Hachis)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cold Meat
- 1 Onion
- 1 Pint of Chopped Potatoes (Uncooked)
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1/4 of a Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter or Lard
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Take the remains of cold roast, stew, bouilli, steaks or fowl, and mince very fine.
2. To every quart of meat allow one onion, a quarter of a clove of garlic, chopped fine, and one pint of chopped (uncooked) potatoes, and two hard-boiled eggs, chopped fine.
3. Mix all this with the minced meat, add salt, pepper and Cayenne to taste.
4. Put into a stew pan with a tablespoonful of butter or lard, and let it simmer gently.
5. After ten minutes add a half pint of hot water.
6. Let it cook ten minutes longer and serve.

title: Hash en Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cold Meat
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water or Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Slices of Bread

## Directions

1. Cut the left-over roast, bouilli or steak into small squares
2. To each Pint of these little squares allow one tablespoonful of butter, one tablespoonful of flour and a half pint of boiling water
3. Put the butter into a frying pan, and as it melts add the flour, being careful not to let it burn
4. When browned add the water, or preferably milk, and stir until it begins to boil
5. Then add the hashed and seasoned meat, and season again to taste
6. Set the hash on a moderate fire and let it simmer for fifteen minutes
7. In the meantime, toast slices of bread and butter them
8. Set them in a hot dish, spread each slice with the hash very thickly and pour the sauce over and serve

title: Hashed Beef, Plain
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- onion
- mushroom or tomato catsup
- broth stock or gravy-soup
- bread

## Directions

1. Slice some beef in very thin pieces, season with pepper and salt, and shake a little flour over it.
2. Chop a medium sized onion and put it (without the beef) into a stew-pan with a tablespoonful of mushroom or tomato catsup.
3. Boil for a few minutes, then add a pint of broth stock, or gravy-soup; boil it down to half the quantity.
4. Five minutes before serving, throw in the cold sliced beef; let it boil five minutes and serve on toasted bread.

title: Health Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Grains of Saffron
- 6 Grains of Musk
- 6 Grains of Ambergris
- 1/2 Drachm of Basil Seed
- 1/2 Drachm of Orange Peel
- 18 Grains of Mace
- 18 Grains of Nutmeg
- 1/2 Drachm of Storax Calamite
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar
- Gum Tragacanth

## Directions

1. Pound the Musk, Storax and Ambergris to a sugar, so as to extract all the resinous properties
2. Make the whole into a paste with a half pound of sugar and just enough Gum Tragacanth to give it sufficient consistency
3. Mix the paste thoroughly and then cut into small tablets that will weigh about a drachm each

title: Herb Gumbo
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- herbs (spinach, lettuce and mustard tops predominate; green cabbage, raddish, and turnip tops in small quantities)
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- 1 lb. of brisket of veal
- a pinch of flour
- onions
- ½ lb. of raw ham
- red or green pepper
- rice

## Directions

1. Thoroughly boil herbs together
2. Chop herbs fine
3. Put in a pot 1 tablespoonful of lard
4. When hot, put in 1 lb. of brisket of veal
5. Smother slowly for ½ hour
6. Add a pinch of flour
7. Let veal fry brown
8. Add onions to taste
9. Add ½ lb. of raw ham cut in small pieces
10. Put in herbs for a little while
11. Add boiling water, say about 1½ pints
12. Let stew together for about ¾ hour
13. Add red or green pepper and serve with rice in separate dish

title: Hindoo Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of almonds
- a small tablespoonful of fresh butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 3 gills of water

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 lb. of almonds, and fry them in a small tablespoonful of fresh butter, until they are of a light brown
2. Wipe with a towel and put them into a bowl or pan
3. Make a syrup with 1 lb. of sugar and 3 gills of water; boil it to a thread
4. Care must be taken to boil it to the exact candying point
5. Pour it boiling upon the almonds, and stir them until the sugar hardens around them

title: Hoe Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 spoonfuls of hominy
- 2 spoonfuls of rice flour
- a little butter
- milk sufficient to make it soft

## Directions

1. Combine the hominy, rice flour, butter, and milk to make a soft dough
2. Bake on a griddle or on a hoe

title: Holiday Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 6 ounces of butter
- 1 ounce of the best powdered ginger
- 1 ounce of carraway seeds ground
- 1 ounce of sweet pepper
- 2 lbs. of treacle
- 1 lb. of sugar
- a large teaspoonful of soda mixed in boiling water

## Directions

1. melt butter and treacle
2. mix with flour and other ingredients
3. bake in a slow oven for 2½ hours

title: Hollandaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- The Juice of Half a Lemon
- Yolk of 1 Egg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Melt the butter; add the juice of half a lemon; mix well and take off the stove and add the yolk of one egg, well beaten.
2. Add a teaspoonful of chopped parsley, beating steadily.
3. This sauce is very light, and as soon as removed from the fire is served hot with the fish.

title: Home Made Yeast Powder
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. cream of tartar
- 1 lb. soda

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly 2 lbs. of cream of tartar and 1 lb. of soda
2. Use 1 teaspoonful of the mixture to 1 pint of flour, sifting it into the dry flour

title: Home-Made Chow-Chow
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Head of Cauliflower
- ½ Head of Cabbage
- 1 Quart of Tiny Cucumbers
- 1 Quart of Small Button Onions
- 2 or 3 Pods of Green and Red Peppers
- ½ Pound of French Mustard
- ½ Teacupful of Celery Seed
- 1 Teacupful of Horseradish
- 1 Whole Garlic
- 1 Pint of String Beans
- 1 Gill of Salad Oil
- ¾ Gallon of Vinegar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Mustard Seed
- 1 Cupful of Brown Sugar
- ¼ Ounce of Tumeric
- Salt and water for brine

## Directions

1. Shred the cabbage as for cold slaw, and cover the bottom of a stone jar with the cucumbers and the cabbage.
2. Then cover with a brine of salt and water strong enough to float an egg.
3. Let them stand for twenty-four hours.
4. At the end of that time boil the cauliflower, the onions and the beans in separate pots till tender.
5. Then drain off all water.
6. Mix them with the cucumbers and the cabbage.
7. Put the vinegar into a porcelain-lined kettle, and let it come to a boil.
8. Add the mustard seed, the celery seed, the grated horseradish, the minced garlic, and the pepper pods, cut fine.
9. Let all boil for about five minutes, and stir constantly till it begins to thicken.
10. Then add the sugar and make a paste of the Tumeric, the mustard and the salad oil, moistening with a little cold vinegar, and pour into the mixture.
11. Stir well and pour while boiling hot over the vegetables.
12. Put it away in stone jars, and in about ten days it will be ready for use.

title: Home-Made Creole Light Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Flour
- ½ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 1½ Pints of Milk or Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and put into a wooden bread trough or a pan, and make a hole in the center of the flour
2. Add warm water or milk sufficient to make a nice, smooth, rather stiff batter
3. Mix the yeast with a little tepid water till well-dissolved, and add
4. Set to rise over night in a warm place
5. In the morning take up and again make a hole in the center of the dough, and add half a tablespoonful of melted lard or butter, one and one-half teaspoonfuls of salt, and one and one-half pints of warm milk or water
6. Add the flour and knead well about ten times
7. When it forms a smooth dough, form into a cake, and set to rise again
8. When risen to about twice its size, take it up and knead again about five times, that is, work one piece of dough over the other about five times, and set to rise again
9. Always, when setting to rise, sprinkle the bottom of the pan with a little flour
10. After an hour, take up the bread and knead for about ten minutes, till you have a nice dough, that can be worked without sprinkling the biscuit board with flour
11. Form the dough into two loaves and place them side by side in the baking pan
12. Bake in a moderate oven for about three-quarters of an hour
13. Serve hot or cold

title: Home-Made Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of good flour
- one-quarter of a pound of good sugar
- a tablespoonful of salt
- two gallons of water

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of good flour, one-quarter of a pound of good sugar, and a tablespoonful of salt in two gallons of water
2. Boil for two hours
3. bottle it for use

title: Home-Made Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Large Potatoes
- A Handful of Hops
- 3 Pints of Water
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- 1/2 Cup of Yeast
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Batter
- Cornmeal to Blend

## Directions

1. Take six large potatoes, and pare them, and then boil in about three pints of water.
2. Take a handful of hops, and tie them in a muslin bag, and boil with the potatoes.
3. When these are thoroughly cooked, drain the water on sufficient flour to make a good batter, and set the mixture on the stove for a few minutes, till the flour is well-scalded.
4. Do not let it boil or simmer under any consideration.
5. Take it from the fire, and let it cool.
6. Then mash the potatoes and add them to the flour, and add a half cup of sugar and a half cup of yeast.
7. Let it stand in a warm place till it has thoroughly risen.
8. Then add cornmeal that has been sifted and dried, and knead well, until you have a dough thick enough to roll out, and that will crumble when dry.
9. Cut this dough into cakes, and spread on a board and place in the shade to dry.
10. Then keep in a box in a dry place.

title: Hominy Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- warm hominy
- salt
- butter
- cream
- 1 or 2 eggs
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Season warm hominy with salt, butter, and cream
2. Add 1 or 2 eggs
3. Add a little flour to stiffen it
4. Fry on a griddle
5. Serve hot

title: Hominy Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Hominy
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 3 Eggs
- 3 Cups of Sour Milk or Warm Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Boil and mash the hominy, or utilize left-over hominy.
2. Add the flour, into which you will have mixed the salt.
3. Beat the eggs very light and add, and when all has been beaten very light add a teaspoonful of soda, dissolved in a little warm water.
4. Bake on a griddle to a nice brown.
5. If sweet milk is used, be careful to use, instead of the soda, two teaspoonfuls of baking powder.
6. Serve with Louisiana Molasses or Syrup.

title: Hominy Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Fine Hominy
- 3 Eggs
- 1/4 Cup of Melted Butter
- 3 Cups of Fresh Milk or Sour Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of White Sugar
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda, if Sour Milk is Used
- 1/4 Cup of Flour

## Directions

1. Mash the hominy through a sieve, and then stir in the milk, salt, yeast powder and sugar.
2. Add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light, and then the flour, well-sifted.
3. Lastly, add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and bake in a quick oven for twenty minutes.
4. If buttermilk is used, add a half teaspoonful of soda, instead of the yeast powder.

title: Hominy Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of hominy liquor
- a lump of fresh butter
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 lemon
- powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Take from the hominy, when nearly done, 1 pint of the liquor
2. Put into it, while hot, a lump of fresh butter, 4 eggs, the juice of half a lemon with the rind grated
3. Sweeten to the taste with powdered loaf sugar, and bake it in a rich paste

title: Honey Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Teacupful of Honey
- 1 Teacupful of Sugar
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Melted Butter
- 1 Pint of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Carraway Seeds

## Directions

1. Take one teacupful each of honey and sugar, and mix well
2. Add a half teaspoonful of melted butter and the yolks of two well-beaten eggs
3. Sift one teaspoonful of baking powder into a pint of flour and add
4. Beat all together till very light, and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Finally, add a teaspoonful of carraway seed, if desired, and bake in a moderate oven from half an hour to three-quarters of an hour

title: Hop Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of hops
- 3 pints of water
- 3 or 4 potatoes
- flour
- yeast
- 1 teacup of warm water
- 1 teacup of brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon of ginger
- 1 tablespoon of salt

## Directions

1. Boil hops in water strong
2. Put potatoes in a crock and mash very fine
3. Put the flour on top
4. Pour the hop water on boiling hot, mixing well with the potatoes and flour
5. Let it stand until milkwarm
6. Add some yeast to make it rise
7. When it gets light, add 1 teacup of warm water, 1 of brown sugar, 1 teaspoon of ginger, 1 tablespoon of salt
8. Stir it down well when you put in the ingredients
9. Let it rise and fall several times
10. Put away for use

title: Horseradish Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a spoonful of mustard
- three tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- salt
- two spoonfuls of cream
- horseradish
- a clove of garlic

## Directions

1. To a spoonful of mustard add three tablespoonfuls of vinegar and a little salt
2. If you have it, put in two spoonfuls of cream
3. Grate into this as much horseradish as will thicken it
4. Mash a clove of garlic into the mixture

title: Horseradish Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup Cream
- Grated Horseradish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1/2 Pint of Consomme or Broth

## Directions

1. Grate the horseradish in sufficient quantity for use, and place it in a saucepan with the boiling stock.
2. Let it boil about ten minutes, or less, until tender.
3. Season to taste.
4. In the meantime rub the eggs in a bowl with the cream, beating and mixing thoroughly.
5. Add these to the horseradish, stirring constantly, but do not let the sauce boil, or the horseradish will curdle.
6. Serve with roast meats or with baked fish.

title: Hot Boiled Icing For Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of powdered sugar
- two large tablespoonfuls of water
- whites of four eggs
- lemon or vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve one pint of powdered sugar in two large tablespoonfuls of water
2. set it on the fire to boil
3. While this syrup is heating on the stove, beat the whites of four eggs to a strong froth
4. take off the boiling syrup, and beat it to the white of eggs, holding it high over the pans, and pouring it in a stream on the eggs
5. flavor with lemon, or vanilla
6. spread it on the cake, while the icing is warm
7. set the cake for a few moments in the oven to harden the icing

title: Hot Claret Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Claret
- 1½ Pounds of Sugar
- 6 Oranges
- 3 Lemons
- ¾ Ounce of Cinnamon (Whole Stick)

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from the oranges and lemons
2. Take the peeling of the oranges and lemons and boil with one quart of water and the sugar and cinnamon
3. Strain, and then add the claret and lemon juice and orange juice
4. Let all come to a boil again and serve hot

title: Hot Lemonade
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Lemons
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the lemons whole and unpeeled till the water is reduced to one quart
2. Sweeten and serve

title: Hot Slaw with Vinegar Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- two cups of strong vinegar
- one teaspoonful of mustard
- salt
- one tablespoonful of butter
- three teaspoons of white sugar
- yolks of two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Slice the cabbage with a patent slicer
2. Lay it in very cold water for an hour
3. Take it out, drain it thoroughly
4. Place it in the dish it is to be served in
5. Take two cups of strong vinegar to a quart of cut cabbage, stir in one teaspoonful of mustard and salt, one tablespoonful of butter, and three teaspoons of white sugar
6. Make this all hot
7. At the last moment stir in the yolks of two or three eggs; stir rapidly
8. Pour the sauce on to the chopped cabbage in the dish
9. Serve instantly or the sauce will harden

title: Hot Spiced Port
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Lump of Sugar
- 2 Cloves
- 3 Allspice
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Old Port Wine
- A Dash of Grated Nutmeg
- A Bit of Lemon Peel

## Directions

1. Take a small mixing glass and dissolve the sugar in a little warm water
2. Add the Allspice and Cloves, ground well
3. Add the Port Wine
4. Fill with hot water and add a bit of lemon peel
5. Stir well
6. Grate a little nutmeg on top and serve very hot

title: Hot Spiced Rum
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two lumps of sugar
- two wineglasses boiling water
- one wineglass Jamaica rum
- a little butter
- cloves and allspice

## Directions

1. Serve in small bar glass

title: Hot Whisky Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Best Old Bourbon
- 3 Lumps of Loaf Sugar
- 1/4 of a Lemon
- Hot Water to Fill One Tumbler

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from a quarter of a lemon and dissolve with the sugar
2. Add the whisky and let all dissolve well
3. Fill with hot water; stir well, and add a little grated nutmeg, if desired, and serve hot

title: How to Blanch Sweetbreads
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of sweetbreads
- salt

## Directions

1. Select three fine pairs of sweetbreads and clean and trim nicely.
2. Soak them for at least two and a half hours in cold fresh water, pouring off the water from time to time till three separate waters have been used.
3. About three-quarters of an hour may be allowed for the first two waters.
4. Add a pinch of salt to each water.
5. After soaking for the time specified, drain the sweetbreads and place them in a saucepan of cold water and set on the fire.
6. Add a half teaspoonful of salt and let them blanch till they come to a boil.
7. Then drain and set them in cold water to freshen.
8. Drain thoroughly, press them into shape and lay on a napkin in a cool place.
9. The sweetbreads should be pressed down gently with a pound weight, in order to flatten well.

title: How to Boil Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Always purchase the best quality of Macaroni.
2. Avoid breaking the macaroni as much as possible.
3. Immerse it whole in a large saucepan of boiling water; add a tablespoonful of salt and one of butter.
4. Let the macaroni cook from twenty to twenty-five minutes.
5. Remove from the fire and drain in a colander.
6. If not intended for immediate use, cover at once with cold water.
7. When cool, drain and use as needed.

title: How To Boil Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of water
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 cup of rice

## Directions

1. Put a quart of water on the fire, and let it boil well, with a teaspoonful of salt
2. Wash a cup of rice well in cold water
3. When the water commences to boil well add the rice
4. Stir occasionally, and gently with a wooden spoon
5. As soon as the grains commence to soften, do not stir or touch the rice again
6. Let it continue to boil rapidly for about twenty minutes, or until the grains begin to swell out
7. Take the cover off and pour off the water, and set the pot in the oven, so that the rice may swell up
8. Let it stand in the oven about ten minutes
9. Do not let it brown, but simply dry
10. Take it off, and let it stand a few minutes
11. Pour out into a dish

title: How to Ice Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of pulverized sugar
- whites of 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of rose or orange flower water or vanilla essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Have the eggs set in the refrigerator for about an hour before using.
2. Break the whites into a cold, shallow dish, and throw in a handful of sugar at a time, beating meanwhile, and adding at short intervals, till you have used up all the sugar.
3. Beat the eggs and the sugar and a teaspoonful of rose or orange flower water or vanilla essence together till the eggs are very light.
4. When the mixture is stiff and smooth, begin to lay a thin coating of the ice over the cake, using a knife to spread it, and occasionally dipping the knife into the lemon juice, which will enable you to smooth it nicely.
5. When it is covered with this first coat, set in a warm place to harden — or in a moderate oven, where you may let it remain about four minutes, or in the sunshine where there is no dust around.
6. When it is hardened it will be ready for the next coat.
7. This coat will be much smoother and whiter than the first.

title: How to Make a Rice Border
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt to Taste
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the rice using one cup to one quart of boiling water.
2. Boil rapidly for fifteen minutes.
3. Pour off any water that remains on top.
4. Set in the oven to dry for about ten minutes, then drain.
5. Season with salt and pepper, and press into a well-buttered border mold.
6. Put it in the oven and let it bake ten minutes.
7. Take out.
8. Place a dish on the mold.
9. Turn it upside down, and remove the mold.

title: How to Make Curry
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion of medium size
- butter
- a little more than a teaspoonful of curry powder
- 1 breakfast cupful of gravy or good stock
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- a small piece of butter
- 1 clove
- Harvey or Worcestershire sauce
- lemon peel (chopped fine)
- lemon juice (about a teaspoonful)
- about a teaspoonful of chutney
- ½ teaspoonful of anchovy sauce
- a pinch of ground mace
- salt
- about a dessertspoonful of milk or cream
- meat, chicken, or vegetable (cooked and cut into pieces)
- rice (boiled)

## Directions

1. Peel and slice an onion of medium size into a saucepan in which some butter is boiling (just sufficient to fry the onion)
2. Let the onion fry till it is of a nice light brown color
3. Add a little more than a teaspoonful of curry powder and let this fry with the onions about half a minute, stirring all the time
4. Have a breakfast cupful of gravy or good stock and mix upon a plate about a teaspoonful of flour with a small piece of butter
5. When quite smooth, incorporate this with the gravy or stock and add to the onion and curry powder, stirring all the time
6. Add one clove, a little Harvey or Worcestershire sauce, a little lemon peel (chopped fine) and juice, about a teaspoonful of chutney, ½ teaspoonful of anchovy sauce, a pinch of ground mace, and salt to taste
7. Let this sauce simmer on the fire for about two hours or longer
8. Add about a dessertspoonful of milk or cream (after this is added it must not boil, or it will curdle)
9. Have the meat, chicken, or vegetable that is to be curried, ready cooked and cut into convenient pieces, put it into the curry sauce for about five minutes, heat thoroughly, but do not boil
10. Serve with rice, boiled, in a separate dish, and handed around before the curry

title: How to Mix Absinthe in Every Style
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a sherry glass of absinthe
- plenty of fine ice
- about two wineglassfuls of water

## Directions

1. Put in the water, drop by drop, on top of absinthe and ice
2. stir well, but slowly
3. It takes time to make it good

title: How To Prepare Rice For Cooking
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- cold water

## Directions

1. Pick the rice clean
2. Wash it well in cold water before attempting to cook, rubbing the rice well with the hands, to get all the dust off
3. Pour off the first water, and add fresh
4. Pour off this, and add fresh again
5. The rice will then be ready to cook

title: How To Wash Rice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water

## Directions

1. Pour upon it water enough to cover it
2. Stir it round briskly with the hand for several seconds
3. Pour off the water, and add fresh
4. Stir as before, and repeat this several times

title: Huckle or Whortleberry Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of picked huckleberries
- pie paste
- a teacupful of brown sugar
- half a teacupful of water
- a teaspooonful of flour
- a saltspoonful of salt
- half a nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put a quart of picked huckleberries into a basin of water
2. Take off whatever floats
3. Take up the berries by the handful
4. Pick out all the stems and unripe berries, and put the rest into a dish
5. Line a buttered pie dish with a pie paste
6. Put in the berries half an inch deep
7. To a quart of berries put a teacupful of brown sugar, and half a teacupful of water
8. Dredge a teaspooonful of flour over
9. Throw in a saltspoonful of salt, and half a nutmeg grated
10. Cover the pie, cut a slit in the centre, or make several incisions on either side of it
11. Press the two crusts together around the edge
12. Trim it off neatly with a sharp knife
13. Bake in a quick oven for three-quarters of an hour

title: Huckleberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Huckleberries
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- A Plain Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as in the recipe given for Cherry Pie

title: Hunters' Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 3 Tomatoes
- 2 Onions
- 6 Mushrooms
- 1 Pint of Consommé
- salt
- pepper
- herb bouquet
- juice of a lemon or six drops of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the flour and butter into a saucepan and blend well
2. Moisten with one pint of consommé or water
3. Add the chopped tomatoes, onions and mushrooms and season with a pinch of salt and pepper
4. Add an herb bouquet and let it boil for an hour
5. Before serving add the juice of a lemon or six drops of vinegar
6. If you have fresh game two tablespoonfuls of blood may be added but do not let it boil after this

title: Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- 6 Eggs
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Any Extract

## Directions

1. Put the milk or cream on to boil
2. If cream is used, six eggs will be sufficient; if milk, use eight
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light, and then beat the whites to a stiff froth
