4. Take the milk off the fire, and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring it all the while
5. Then add the whites, mixing thoroughly, and two tablespoonfuls of extract of either Lemon or Vanilla
6. Set to cool, and then turn into the freezer, pack and stir around in the ice for about ten minutes

title: Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- arrowroot or cornstarch
- 1 tablespoon
- 1 quart cold milk or cream
- whites of 4 to 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Thicken a quart of milk with arrowroot or cornstarch, a tablespoonful to the quart
2. Sweeten and let it boil
3. When cold, add a quart of cold milk, or cream if you have it
4. Flavor to your taste
5. When the cream is put into the freezer and is just beginning to thicken, whip up very light the whites of 4 to 6 eggs, and add this to the cream

title: Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts sweet milk
- 1 quart cream
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 teacup starch
- 2 teacups powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the milk in a porcelain or granite vessel to boil
2. Beat the eggs and sugar till very light and free of lumps
3. Dissolve the starch in a little cold milk and add to the sugar and eggs
4. When the milk boils, pour it slowly into the sugar, eggs and starch, beating all the time
5. Wash the vessel in hot water, slightly butter with the fingers, and return to the fire to thicken
6. Stir constantly till thick, and set away to cool
7. When ready to freeze, add the cream whipped, and flavor to taste with vanilla

title: Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight eggs
- 1 box of Eagle Brand condensed milk

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and the milk together
2. Flavor to suit the taste
3. Beat the whites to a froth and sweeten to the taste
4. Beat all together
5. Put in water enough to fill a gallon freezer

title: Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 3 quarts of milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs separately and very light
2. Sweeten to the taste, stirring the sugar into the yolks
3. Stir in the milk
4. Stir in the whites last
5. Flavor to the taste
6. Freeze

title: Ice Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- i lb. each of macaroons, ratifees, sponge cake, and sugar
- i pint of milk
- 1 or 2 eggs
- 1 lb. of fruit cut small (dried cherries, angelica and raisins)
- 1 pint of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soak i lb. each of macaroons, ratifees, sponge cake, and sugar in a light custard made with i pint of milk and 1 or 2 eggs
2. When ready to freeze, add 1 lb. of fruit cut small (dried cherries, angelica and raisins)
3. Place in freezer
4. Stir until partially congealed
5. Add a pint of whipped cream, and freeze hard

title: Iced Chocolate Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of vanilla chocolate
- half a pound of sugar
- yolks of eight eggs
- one pint of rich, sweet milk
- half a pint of whipped cream
- one and a half ounces of isinglass

## Directions

1. Grate half a pound of vanilla chocolate
2. Put it in a stew-pan with half a pound of sugar, the yolks of eight eggs, and one pint of rich, sweet milk
3. Stir over the fire until it begins to thicken
4. Strain through a sieve into a basin
5. Add half a pint of whipped cream, and one and a half ounces of isinglass
6. Mix well and pour into a mould
7. Set it on ice if the weather is warm

title: Iced Lemonade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Lemons
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 2 Quarts of Water
- Cracked Ice

## Directions

1. Choose the finest and freshest ripe lemons
2. Rasp the rind with white, crushed sugar, in order to extract the fine aromatic oil that lurks in the zest
3. Squeeze the juice of the lemon, and strain it, and add the sugar and the necessary quantity of water
4. In making cold lemonade for six persons, allow six ounces of sugar to five lemons, and one quart of water
5. Increase the quantity in proportion to number to be served
6. Serve with crushed ice

title: Iced Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Whole Tomatoes
- 1 Pint Mayonnaise Sauce
- A Garnish of Chopped Ice

## Directions

1. Take the tomatoes whole.
2. Lay on a bed of lettuce or cress.
3. Garnish with chopped ice, and serve very cold with Mayonnaise Sauce.

title: Icing
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of powdered or flour sugar
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Put the sugar to the eggs before you beat it at all
2. beat till it is stiff
3. Spread it on the cake with a wet knife, wetting it in cold water each time you use it
4. Set it in front of the stove to dry, or in an oven with the least particle of heat
5. The cake must be nearly cold
6. You can flavor the icing with rose, orange, or lemon; if the latter, add a very small portion of grated rind

title: Icing
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of powdered or flour sugar
- the whites of four eggs
- rose, orange, or lemon for flavoring optional

## Directions

1. Put the sugar to the egg whites before you beat it at all
2. Beat till it is stiff
3. Spread it on the cake with a wet knife, wetting it in cold water each time you use it
4. Set it in front of the stove to dry or in an oven with the least particle of heat
5. The cake must be nearly cold
6. You can flavor the icing with rose, orange, or lemon; if the latter, add a very small portion of grated rind

title: Icing
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 2 eggs
- 2 teacups granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoonful citric acid
- extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar until clear, with just enough water to moisten it
2. Beat the eggs to a stiff froth, and pour boiling sugar very slowly into the eggs
3. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful citric acid and a small tablespoonful of water, putting enough in to flavor delicately, and add a little extract of lemon

title: Icing
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 2 eggs
- 2 teacups of granulated sugar
- water
- 1 teaspoonful of citric acid
- lemon extract

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar until clear with just enough water to moisten it
2. Beat the eggs to a stiff froth
3. Pour the boiling sugar very slowly into the eggs
4. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of citric acid in a small tablespoonful of water, putting enough in to flavor delicately
5. Add a little extract of lemon

title: Icing
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sugar
- 2 wineglasses of water
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. To 1 lb. of sugar, put 2 wineglasses of water
2. Set it on the fire and let it simmer
3. Have 6 eggs beaten to a froth
4. Add the syrup to the eggs after it has cooled a little
5. Beat it 1 hour, and then use it

title: Imperial Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pound of Flour
- The Juice and Rind of 1 Lemon
- 1 Pound of Blanched Almonds
- 1/2 Pound of Citron
- 3/4 Pound of Raisins
- 9 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel and pound the almonds in a mortar
2. Seed the raisins, and cut the citron into shreds and quarter-inch dice, very fine
3. Grate the zest of the lemon
4. Beat the butter to a cream
5. Add the sugar gradually, beating till very light
6. Add the yolks of the eggs beaten to a cream and beat till very light
7. Mix the sifted flour and baking powder with the almonds, and add them gradually to the mixture, beating vigorously
8. Add the raisins, dredging with flour, and the citron and mix well
9. Add the juice of the lemon, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth
10. Turn into a buttered tin and bake for an hour in a moderate oven
11. Use the broom-wisp test before taking out of the oven

title: Imperial Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ripe Pineapple
- 4 Oranges
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- The Finely-Grated Zest of 2 Lemons
- 1 Pint Equal Parts of Maraschino, Noyeau, Kirsch and Curacao
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Pint of Champagne
- The Whites of 8 Eggs
- 3 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of the water and the sugar
2. Peel and grate the pineapple very fine
3. Grate the zest of two lemons very fine, and mix with the pineapple
4. Add the juices of the oranges and lemons
5. Mix thoroughly, and pour over all the boiling syrup
6. Let it stand till cold
7. Strain through a fine sieve into the ice cream freezer, pressing out all the juice of the pineapple as you strain
8. Whip the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and add, mixing well
9. Add the liqueurs, stir well, and then proceed to freeze as in Creole Punch
10. When you have detached the frozen punch from the sides and bottom of the freezer for the third time, add the champagne
11. Let it continue freezing for several turns more, and serve immediately in punch glasses

title: Indian Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Indian meal
- cold water
- boiling water
- a little salt
- yeast

## Directions

1. Mix Indian meal and cold water as for a thick gruel
2. Stir the mixture into boiling water
3. Let it boil 1/2 hour
4. Stir in a little salt
5. Take it from the fire and let it remain until lukewarm
6. Stir in yeast and Indian meal, making it the consistency of common dough
7. When light, take it out and put into buttered pans
8. Let it remain a few minutes
9. Bake it about 2 1/2 hours

title: Indian Breakfast Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- corn-meal
- salt
- saleratus
- two well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Take a quart of milk scalding hot
2. stir into it as much corn-meal as will make a thick batter
3. add of salt and saleratus in fine powder, each a teaspoonful
4. when a little cooled, add two well-beaten eggs
5. bake in buttered pans, in a quick oven

title: Indian Breakfast Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk or milk and water
- fine corn meal
- two eggs
- salt
- a teaspoonful of saleratus
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a quart of milk or milk and water scalding hot
2. Pour half of it hot on as much fine corn meal as it will wet
3. Let it cool
4. Beat up two eggs and add them, beating the eggs light
5. Add a little salt and a teaspoonful of saleratus
6. Thin the batter a little with the rest of the milk
7. Butter pan and pour in the mixture
8. Bake in a quick oven

title: Indian Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box of Potted Ham
- 1 Ounce of Chutney
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 2 Ounces of Grated Parmesan Cheese

## Directions

1. Cut six slices of bread into delicate circles, and fry in butter.
2. Spread first with Potted Ham and then with Chutney.
3. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan Cheese.
4. Set in the oven to brown for five minutes, and serve hot.

title: Indian Meal Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces of mush
- 6 ounces of butter
- 6 ounces of sugar
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 white
- nutmeg
- mace
- wine

## Directions

1. Mix the butter in the mush while it is warm
2. beat the eggs very light, mix the sugar with them, and add them to the mush when cool
3. season with nutmeg and mace to the taste, also a little wine

title: Indian Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of boiling water
- cornmeal
- flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- 1 wine glass of yeast

## Directions

1. Stir into the boiling water as much cornmeal as will make a nice batter
2. When warm, put in as much flour as will make a stiff batter
3. Add salt and yeast
4. Set it to rise
5. When risen, pour into the muffin rings, and bake slowly in an oven
6. Serve them with nice fresh butter

title: Indian Pound Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 1 pint of powdered sugar
- 1 pint of Indian meal sifted
- 1 pint of wheat flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 nutmeg grated
- 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1/2 glass of wine and brandy mixed

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Beat the eggs very light
3. Stir the meal and eggs alternately into the butter and sugar
4. Add the spice and liquor
5. Stir all well
6. Butter a tin pan and put in the mixture
7. Bake it in a moderate oven

title: Indian Pound Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pounded sugar in the weight of 8 eggs
- sifted Indian meal in the weight of 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 grated nutmeg or 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Indian Pound Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Cup of Flour
- 1 Cup of Indian Meal
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pound Sugar
- 8 Eggs
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cinnamon
- 3/4 Glass of Sherry Wine and Brandy, Mixed

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar to a cream, and add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
2. Beat till very light, and then add the meal and flour, well blended
3. Beat light
4. Add the spices and liquor, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Beat thoroughly, but lightly, and turn into a buttered tin
6. Bake in a moderate oven for an hour and a half

title: Indian Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Indian Meal
- 1/2 Cup Butter
- 1 Cup Sugar
- 2 Cups Milk
- 3/4 Cup of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Seeded Raisins
- salt
- 1 teaspoonful of extract of cinnamon
- 1/4 of a grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pour sufficient boiling water on the meal to wet it thoroughly, and then beat in the butter
2. Add the sugar, beating thoroughly, and the salt
3. Then add the milk, stirring well, also the molasses and the raisins
4. Add one teaspoonful of extract of cinnamon, and one-quarter of a grated nutmeg
5. Mix thoroughly
6. Bake for two and a half or three hours, and serve cold with cream

title: Indian-Meal Griddle Cakes, Without Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk or milk and water
- one pint of corn meal
- four tablespoonfuls of flour
- one tablespoonful of salt
- a teaspoon of butter

## Directions

1. Beat up all ingredients
2. Bake on a griddle or in shallow pans

title: Invariable Rule for Broiling Birds
body:
## Ingredients

- Birds
- Salt and Pepper
- Melted Butter
- Strip of Very Finely-Sliced Bacon
- Buttered French Toasts
- Chopped Parsley
- Lemon Juice
- Sliced Lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds by hand picking.
2. If of the very small variety, such as grassets, reed birds, robins, etc., do not pick out the entrails.
3. Rub the bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter.
4. Tie a strip of very finely-sliced bacon around the body of the bird, joining with a skewer.
5. Place on a broiler over a slow fire, and let it cook for ten, fifteen or twenty or even thirty minutes, according to the size of the bird.
6. Turn frequently, so that it may cook well without burning.
7. When done, take off the broiler.
8. Place the birds upon buttered French toasts, allowing a slice of toast for each bird.
9. Trim away the rough edges of the toast.
10. Pour over the bird a little of the juice that has run from it in broiling, and let it soak down into the toast.
11. Pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley, and lemon juice if you like.
12. Garnish with sliced lemon and parsley sprigs, and bring to the table hot.

title: Invariable Rule for Roasting Birds
body:
## Ingredients

- Birds
- Salt and Pepper
- Melted Butter
- Truffle
- Lump of Butter
- Pinch of Chopped Thyme
- Chopped Parsley
- Bay Leaf
- Pinch of Four Spices
- Strips of Bacon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- Buttered French Toast
- Sprigs of Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds, cleaning out the entrails of the larger birds, and leaving the small ones untouched.
2. Rub with salt and pepper and melted butter.
3. In the larger birds, if you can afford it put a truffle or two, for stuffing, and in all put a little lump of butter and a little salt and pepper, a pinch of chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf, and a small pinch of the four spices.
4. Bind with strips of bacon, and place in a baking pan with a tablespoonful of butter.
5. Let them bake or roast thirty minutes or less, according to size.
6. Prepare the gravy by simply adding a tablespoonful of water, letting it cook for two minutes.
7. Strain; let it cook for two minutes more, and pour upon the breast of the bird.
8. Serve always on buttered French toast.
9. Garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley and lemon, and serve hot.

title: Irish Potato Snow Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- the yolk of an egg
- a small piece of butter
- bread crumbs
- salt
- good lard

## Directions

1. After boiled tender, drain off the water
2. Let the potatoes steam till they break to pieces
3. Mix them with the yolk of an egg, a small piece of butter, bread crumbs, with salt to the taste
4. Form them into balls
5. Fry them in good lard or brown them in an oven

title: Irish Potatoes Mashed
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes
2. Mash them in a mortar with butter; to a tablespoonful of butter use 8 or 10 common sized potatoes
3. Add salt to the taste

title: Irish Potatoes, Mashed and Browned
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- sweet butter
- a little milk
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil them without peeling
2. Peel them while hot
3. Mash them up with sweet butter, a little milk, pepper and salt
4. Smooth over with a knife blade, and slightly brown in the oven

title: Irish Stew
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- loin of mutton or ribs of beef
- white potatoes
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Take from one to three pounds of loin of mutton, or ribs of beef; cut it into chops.
2. Add by weight as many white potatoes, sliced, as there is beef.
3. Throw in from two to six chopped onions, according to size, some pepper and salt, and a large spoonful of butter to each pound of meat.
4. Let all stew gently for two hours and serve with boiled rice or macaroni.

title: Irish Stew
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- square, thick piece of beef from the round or sirloin
- water
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut a square, thick piece of beef from the round or sirloin and trim off the fat
2. Put it into a stewpan with water enough to cover it
3. Season it slightly with salt and pepper
4. Let it stew slowly till tender all through
5. Then add potatoes, being first pared and cut into quarters

title: Isabella Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of butter
- four cups of sugar
- sixteen eggs
- six cups of sifted flour
- two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
- vanilla or lemon for flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar together
2. add the beaten yolks
3. alternately add flour and beaten whites
4. put yeast powder in the flour
5. flavor with vanilla or lemon
6. bake in a four-quart cake mould with oiled bottom and sides and paper on bottom
7. finish with chocolate icing

title: Isinglass Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of water
- one ounce of isinglass
- one pound of sugar
- one cup of pale wine
- rind of a lemon
- juice of a lemon
- grated rind of lemon

## Directions

1. Boil in one pint of water one ounce of isinglass
2. When well dissolved, add to it one pound of sugar and a cup of pale wine
3. When the water is boiling, add to it the rind of a lemon
4. When taken off the fire, add the juice and grated rind of lemon
5. Strain this mixture and whisk it till it begins to thicken
6. Pour it into the vessel you wish to mould it in and set in a cool place or on ice to harden

title: Italian Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts of cream
- 2 lemons
- 3 glasses of white wine
- 1| ounces of isinglass
- 4 small teacupfuls of hot water

## Directions

1. Put 3 quarts of cream into 2 bowls
2. Sweeten one sweet enough for the two
3. Add the juice of 2 lemons and 3 glasses of white wine
4. Add the other quart of cream
5. Stir the whole very hard
6. Boil 1| ounces of isinglass with 4 small teacupfuls of hot water until reduced to 1/2
7. Strain the isinglass, lukewarm, into the other ingredients
8. Put it into moulds

title: Italian Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints of cream
- 6 ounces of powdered loaf sugar
- juice of two large lemons
- 2 glasses of white wine
- 2 ounces of isinglass
- four small teacupfuls of water

## Directions

1. Put 2 pints of cream into 2 bowls
2. With 1 bowl mix 6 ounces of powdered loaf sugar, the juice of two large lemons, and 2 glasses of white wine
3. Add the other pint of cream and stir the whole very hard
4. Boil 2 ounces of isinglass with four small teacupfuls of water until it is reduced to half
5. Stir the isinglass, lukewarm, into the other ingredients
6. Put them into a glass dish to congeal

title: Italian Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 8 Shallots, Greens and White
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- juice of 1/2 of a Lemon
- 1/2 Can Mushrooms
- Consommé
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. If mushrooms are used, make a white sauce, that is, let the butter and flour blend without browning
2. Add a half cup of consommé and a half can of chopped mushrooms, the white of the shallot (chopped very fine) and the juice of half a lemon
3. If a brown sauce, add the shallots to the butter and flour, which you will have browned, using the chopped white and green of the shallots
4. Then add a half pint of consommé, and let it simmer for about an hour, and add the juice of a lemon and serve
5. The white sauce is used for fish, the brown for meats
6. Always season to taste

title: Jam
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe fruit
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. You must weigh your fruit (say blackberries), and allow three quarters of a pound of good sugar to each pound of fruit
2. Crush the fruit and sugar, with a biscuit beater, until they are well mashed
3. Add a gill of water to each pound of fruit
4. Boil gently (not rapidly like jelly) until it becomes a jelly-like mass
5. When done, put it into glasses, or small earthenware pots and when cold, cover up like jelly

title: Jambalaya au Congri
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- 1 Pint of Cowpeas
- 1 Large Onion
- 1/4 Pound of Salt Meat
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Chop the small meat, after washing, into dice, and mince the ham.
2. Boil the cowpeas and the salt meat and ham together.
3. Add the onion, minced very fine.
4. Boil the rice according to recipe for boiled rice.
5. Chop the meat well.
6. After the peas and the rice are cooked, pour the rice into the pot of peas, which must not be dry, but very moist.
7. Mix well.
8. Let all simmer for five minutes, and then serve hot.

title: Jambalaya Au Congri
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of rice
- 1 pint of cowpeas
- 1 large onion
- 1/2 pound of salt meat
- 1 square inch of ham

## Directions

1. Chop the salt meat, after washing, into dice, and mince the ham
2. Boil the cowpeas and the salt meat and ham together
3. Add the onion, minced very fine
4. Boil the rice according to recipe for boiled rice
5. Chop the meat well
6. After the peas and the rice are cooked, pour the rice into the pot of peas, which must not be dry, but very moist

title: Jambalaya of Fowls and Rice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a fowl
- a cup of raw rice
- a slice of ham minced
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut up and stew a fowl
2. When half done, add a cup of raw rice, a slice of ham minced, and pepper and salt
3. Let all cook together until the rice swells and absorbs all the gravy of the stewed chicken, but it must not be allowed to get hard or dry
4. Serve in a deep dish

title: Japan Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 5 beaten eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of rose water or a nutmeg
- 1 lb. of flour
- sugar for sifting

## Directions

1. Stir together sugar and butter until white
2. Add 5 beaten eggs, rose water or nutmeg
3. Add 1 lb. of flour
4. Drop it with a large spoon on flat tins that have been buttered
5. Sift sugar over them

title: Jaune Mange
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- an ounce of isinglass
- water
- the juice of two large oranges
- a gill of white wine
- the yolks of four eggs

## Directions

1. Break up and boil an ounce of isinglass in rather more than half a pint of water until it is melted
2. strain it
3. then add the juice of two large oranges, a gill of white wine, and the yolks of four eggs beaten and strained
4. sweeten to taste
5. stir it over a gentle fire till it boils up
6. dip a mould into cold water and pour the preparation into it

title: Jellied Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beef tongue
- 2 calf's feet
- 4 pints of strong consomme
- 1 glass of sherry wine
- Spices
- 2 onions
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Parboil the tongue and two calf's feet.
2. Take out of the hot water and skin and clean the tongue well, and take the bones out of the calf's feet.
3. Mince two onions very fine, and fry them in a tablespoonful of butter.
4. Let them brown, and lay on these the well-seasoned tongue and calf's feet.
5. Let them simmer ten minutes, and then add one pint of consomme, and five minutes after, one glass of white wine.
6. Let these smother, keeping well covered, for an hour and a half.
7. Take the tongue out, and let the calf's feet cook and reduce a half hour longer.
8. After this add the tongue for two minutes longer.
9. Put all into a bowl or dish, and let it cool.

title: Jellied Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- The Filet or Part of Lower Shoulder Blade of Veal
- 6 Peppercorns
- 2 Calves' Feet
- 1 Blade of Mace
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Gill of French Vinegar
- 1 Dozen Cloves Mashed Fine
- 1 Allspice Mashed Fine
- 3 Quarts of Beef Broth
- Pepper, Cayenne and Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into fine pieces, season well, and put it in a kettle with the calves' feet
2. Season highly with pepper, Cayenne and salt to taste
3. Add three quarts of beef broth, or pot-au-feu
4. Add the minced vegetables, herbs and the peppercorns
5. Let it boil gently until it forms a jelly, which will be in about two and a half hours
6. Take out the veal and calves' feet, and carefully remove all the bones, if any, and place in a mold
7. Let the liquor in which it was boiled boil until it is reduced to about a quart, adding, in the meantime, the good vinegar
8. When reduced, pour over the meat and set it in a cold place over night
9. When cold, turn out of the mold and garnish nicely with sliced lemon and parsley sprigs and serve in slices

title: Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces isinglass
- 3 quarts water
- 1.5 lbs. powdered sugar
- 4 lemons
- 1 pint good wine
- 3 eggs
- mace

## Directions

1. Combine 2 ounces of isinglass, 3 quarts of water, 1.5 lbs. of powdered sugar, 4 lemons, 1 pint of good wine, 3 eggs, mace to the taste

title: Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of fruit
- sugar (proper quantity)

## Directions

1. After the juice is pressed from the fruit, add the proper quantity of sugar to it
2. Heat it until the sugar is dissolved
3. After this is effected, no further heat is required

title: Jelly Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs beaten separately
- 0.5 lb. sugar
- 0.5 lb. butter
- 6 ounces flour
- jelly
- 1 teaspoonful yeast powder

## Directions

1. Cream the flour and butter
2. Beat the sugar and yolks together, and add to flour and butter
3. Beat well, and last add the beaten whites
4. If accustomed to using yeast powder, add 1 teaspoonful
5. Bake in shallow tin pans
6. When cool spread on the jelly
7. Pile up
8. Trim off the edges, and sift sugar over the top

title: Jelly Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the cup cake recipe above
- 1 pint of fresh milk, boiling hot
- 2 tablespoons of cornstarch dissolved in a little cold milk
- grated cocoanut

## Directions

1. bake the cake in jelly pans, or whole and sliced
2. dissolve cornstarch in cold milk
3. add to boiling milk
4. stir continually until as thick as blanc mange
5. put on a layer of this and grated cocoanut

title: Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 4 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Sour Cream
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract
- 3 Ounces of Currant, Grape or any Jelly Desired

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter exactly as for Cup Cake or Sponge Cake
2. Butter the small tins and pour in the batter in such thin layers as to allow the cakes to be just a half inch thick when baked
3. Let them bake for a few minutes to a light brown
4. As they are taken from the oven lay them on the table and spread a layer of Currant or Grape Jelly between
5. Add alternate layers of cake and jelly, reserving the prettiest cakes for the top layers
6. Do not put jelly on the top
7. Ice or powder with sifted sugar

title: Jelly Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Cake
- 1 Cup of Pineapple, Peach, Raspberry or any Jelly

## Directions

1. Make a layer cake, and spread, while hot, with any kind of jelly, peach, lemon, raspberry, apple, strawberry, or the like

title: Jelly Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of flour
- 1 cup of sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 lemon
- jelly

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs separately
2. Mix flour, sugar, eggs, and soda with juice and rind of 1 lemon
3. Bake it thin in square pans
4. Spread the bottom with jelly and roll in as soon as taken from the oven

title: Jenny's Sponge Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ten eggs
- one pound of sifted fine sugar
- half a pound of flour
- lemon or orange-flower water flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat ten eggs separately
2. Put the yolks with a pound of sifted fine sugar in a bowl
3. Beat it again very light
4. Put in the whites
5. Stir in half a pound of flour last of all
6. Do not beat the batter after the flour is stirred in
7. Flavor with lemon or orange-flower water
8. Pour the mixture into pans lined with buttered paper
9. Do not place more than one and a half inches of batter in each pan
10. Bake twenty minutes
11. If the oven is too hot, cover the top of the pans with a paper or pasteboard to prevent scorching

title: Jerusalem Artichokes Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Jerusalem Artichokes
- Sauce a la Maltre d'Hotel or a la Creme

## Directions

1. Wash the artichokes in cold water, and scrape them.
2. Then throw them into cold water, and let them soak for an hour or so.
3. Take out and drain.
4. Put them in a saucepan; cover with boiling water, and let them boil slowly until tender; watch carefully, as they will easily harden again.
5. Serve with a Sauce a la Maltre d'Hotel or a Cream Sauce.

title: Johnny Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Pints of Indian Meal
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Sweet Milk or Buttermilk or Hot Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter, or 1 of Lard
- ¼ Teaspoonful of Soda

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs till very light, and add the cornmeal, and beat till light.
2. Add the melted butter and the milk or water.
3. If buttermilk is used, you may use the soda, dissolving it in two tablespoonfuls of boiling water.
4. Do not use the soda with the sweet milk.
5. Make a dough or batter thick enough to be spread into cakes a half inch in thickness and about five inches in diameter, the cakes being formed round.
6. Place in buttered tins, and bake in a moderate oven for about a half hour, frequently brushing across while baking with melted butter about four or five times.
7. Serve hot for lunch or tea, split and buttered, or served with fresh, sweet butter and buttermilk.

title: Julienne Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Potatoes
- Boiling Lard

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the potatoes into long, thin strips.
2. Fry in boiling lard.
3. Serve with beefsteak, grillades, etc.

title: Julienne Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds of Lean Beef
- 5 Quarts of Water
- 2 Turnips
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Onions
- 2 Leeks
- A Small Stalk of Celery
- 3 Tomatoes
- A Small half-Head of White Cabbage
- 1 Gill of green Peas
- A tablespoonful of lard

## Directions

1. Set the beef and water in a close vessel where they will heat gradually
2. After boiling five or six hours add the following vegetables, which have been carefully prepared
3. Cut the vegetables into long, thin shreds
4. Take a tablespoonful of lard, heat and add the vegetables, letting them fry or smother until a golden brown
5. Add to the boiling broth
6. If fresh peas are used they must be boiled apart
7. If canned peas, simply add to the broth after throwing in the vegetables
8. Let them cook in the broth one hour longer and serve hot with the vegetables

title: Jumballaya
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. rice
- cold roast chicken or remnants of turkey
- slice of ham
- 1 tablespoonful lard
- 1 pint hot water

## Directions

1. Wash rice and soak it an hour
2. Cut up a cold roast chicken or the remnants of a turkey and a slice of ham, which fry in a tablespoonful of lard
3. Stir in the rice, and add slowly, while stirring in a pint of hot water
4. Cover your pot, and set where it can cook slowly

title: Jumbles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs, well beaten separately
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of white sugar for rolling
- mace and orange peel to the taste
- 2 grains of soda

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, flour, sugar, and butter as you would beaten cake, except that the batter must be stiff enough to roll in cakes
2. Roll them in white sugar
3. Add mace and orange peel to taste

title: Jumbles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of cream
- rind of 3 lemons
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients into a paste
2. Roll in the form of rings before baking
3. Bake

title: Jumbles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 lb. flour sifted
- 1 lb. butter
- 1 lb. pounded loaf sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of rose water
- 1 grated nutmeg
- mixed mace and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar and butter to a cream
2. Beat the eggs very light
3. Put them all at once into the pan of flour, butter and sugar
4. Add the spice and rose water
5. Stir the whole with a knife
6. Take a portion of the dough and lay it on the board
7. Roll it lightly with the hands into long thin rolls

title: Jumbles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Butter
- 0.75 Pound of Flour
- 4 Eggs
- 0.75 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Rose Water
- 0.5 Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the eggs, thoroughly beaten, and then add the Rose Water and the nutmeg
3. Gradually add the flour, and beat well
4. Beat till very light before adding the flour
5. Dust a paper on a baking sheet with sugar, instead of flour, and roll out the paste to about one-eighth of an inch in thickness
6. Then use a small cutter with a round center, and take the centers out, thus forming pretty rings
7. Bake in a moderate oven till a light brown
8. Take them out and sift powdered sugar over them

title: Kendall Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs. of white sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- ½ pint of milk
- ½ lb. of butter
- some mace
- a little wine
- flour

## Directions

1. beat all ingredients with a sufficient quantity of flour to roll out
2. cut into pretty shapes

title: Kentucky Corn Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen ears of corn
- 1 teacup milk
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 teaspoonful sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the grains down through the middle
2. Scrape out the heart
3. Mix this with 1 teacup of milk, 3 eggs, 1/2 cup of butter with 1 teaspoonful of sugar
4. Mix well and bake in a moderate oven

title: Kidney and Mushroom Stew
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- flour
- butter
- plain beef stock, or beef gravy
- one chopped onion
- one cupful of mushrooms
- mushroom catsup

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys into slices, wash and dry them carefully
2. Pepper and salt them, roll them in flour, and fry in butter till of a delicate brown color
3. Pour some plain beef stock, or beef gravy, in the pan
4. Add a chopped onion, and stew for half an hour
5. Put in a cupful of mushrooms, and cook for fifteen minutes
6. Mushroom catsup will serve as a substitute. Use one-half the quantity of catsup

title: Kidney Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kidneys
- 6 eggs
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Cut the fat from the kidneys and wash well
2. Cut into small pieces
3. Mix these with the eggs, which you will have prepared as for a plain omelet
4. Proceed as in Ham Omelet
5. Serve with Tomato Sauce

title: King of the Carnival Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Coloring Extracts: Spinach Green, Essence of Vanilla, Orange Juice or Saffron, and Creme de Violettes

## Directions

1. Make a Plain Ice Cream
2. Divide into three distinct and equal portions
3. Color one with Spinach Green to obtain the green effect
4. Flavor another with Essence of Vanilla, and tinge with orange juice or a bit of saffron, to obtain the golden yellow
5. Color the third with a deep Creme de Violettes, to obtain the royal purple
6. Freeze in separate molds
7. Pack into one freezer, and when frozen remove according to directions for taking ice cream whole from a mold
8. Cut in slices and serve

title: King's Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a half flour
- half ounce yeast
- warm water
- milk or tepid water
- half pound reserved flour
- salt
- six eggs
- sugar
- butter
- beaten egg for glazing
- dragees and caramels for decoration

## Directions

1. Put flour in a wooden bread trough
2. Make a hole in the center of the flour and put in half ounce of yeast dissolved in a little warm water
3. Add milk or tepid water to make the dough, using milk if you want it very rich and delicate, and water if you have not the milk
4. Knead and mix the flour with one hand while adding the milk or water with the other
5. Make a dough that is neither too stiff nor too soft
6. When perfectly smooth set the dough to rise in a moderately warm place, covering with a cloth
7. Remember that if you use milk to make the dough it must be scalded, that is, must be heated to the boiling point, and then allowed to grow tepid
8. Let the dough rise for five or six hours, and when increased to twice its bulk, take it and add the reserved half pound of flour into which you will have sifted the salt
9. Add six eggs beaten very light with the sugar and butter, and mix all well together, kneading lightly with your hands, and adding more eggs if the dough is a little stiff
10. Knead the dough by turning it over on itself three times, and set to rise again for an hour or three-quarters of an hour
11. Cover with a cloth
12. At the end of this time take it up and work again lightly
13. Form into a great ring, leaving a hole in the center
14. Pat gently and flatten a little
15. Have ready a baking pan with a buttered sheet of paper in it and set the central roll in the middle
16. Cover the pan with a clean stiff cloth and set the cake to rise for an hour longer
17. When well risen, set in an oven a few degrees cooler than that used for baking bread
18. Let bake for an hour and a half; if medium, one hour, and if very small, a half hour
19. Glace the Brioche lightly with a beaten egg spread lightly over the top before placing in the oven
20. Decorate with dragees, caramels, etc.

title: Kirsch Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Gill of Kirsch
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Pineapple Syrup
- 2 Lemons
- 2 Dozen Strawberries

## Directions

1. Take fine, fresh Louisiana oranges, and press out all the juice, and mash the pulp and strain through a sieve
2. To each pint of juice allow one gill of Kirsch and two of the juice of two lemons
3. Sweeten to taste
4. Flavor with Pineapple Syrup, add whole strawberries when in season, and freeze as in Creole Punch
5. Serve in glasses

title: Kirsch Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Gill of Kirsch
- 1 Pint of Cold Water
- ¾ Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water in a saucepan and set on the stove on a brisk fire
2. Let it come to a boil
3. Add a tablespoonful of cornstarch which has been diluted in a tablespoonful of water
4. Stir well for three minutes, and then take from the fire and add immediately a half gill of Kirsch
5. Mix well, strain through a sieve and serve with puddings

title: Kisses
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Take the whites of eggs
2. Beat them very light and mix with them enough sifted sugar to make them very stiff
3. Drop them on white paper the size you want them
4. Let them remain in a slow oven 20 minutes
5. 4 eggs will make a cake basket full

title: Kisses, or Sugar Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- three well-beaten eggs
- half a pound of sifted flour
- half a grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Rub to a cream half a cup of butter, with one cup of sugar
2. Add three well-beaten eggs, half a pound of sifted flour, and half a grated nutmeg
3. Drop this mixture on buttered tins, by the spoonful; let them be two or three inches apart
4. Sprinkle sugar over them
5. Bake quickly

title: Kummel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of the Best Rectified Alcohol
- 30 Drops of Kummel Oil
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 2 Pounds of the Finest White Loaf Sugar

## Directions

1. Pour the boiling water over the sugar and stir till dissolved.
2. Bring to a boil, and let it continue boiling from fifteen to twenty minutes.
3. Strain, and when luke warm, add the oil and the alcohol.
4. Bottle and keep for at least two months before using.

title: La Colle
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Brown Sugar
- 2 Cups of Louisiana Pecans

## Directions

1. Boil the brown sugar down to almost one pint of syrup
2. Add two cups of pecans, cut or mashed in various irregular sizes
3. Boil until it passes like a thread between the fingers
4. Pour into small white paper cases about four inches long and two inches wide
5. Set to dry

title: Lady Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one pound of sugar
- five and one-half ounces of butter
- whites of seventeen eggs
- two or three drops of oil of bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Cream the well-washed butter
2. add the sugar and cream again
3. alternate the whites with flour
4. flavor last of all
5. bake in a square or long pan

title: Lady Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 4 of the small measures of yeast powder
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- whites of 20 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful essence of lemon
- 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix flour, yeast powder, sugar, butter, egg whites, lemon essence, and vanilla
2. Cover the pan
3. Keep the stove hot enough to make the cake rise nicely
4. Add a little more fuel if necessary
5. Bake about 2 hours

title: Lady Cake (White)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1% Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- % Cup of Butter
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- % Cup of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Peach Extract

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, add the eggs, beating the whites to a stiff froth, and mixing well.
2. Add the milk, and gradually add the flour, blending thoroughly, and beating till very light.
3. Add the essence, and bake in a moderate oven for one hour.

title: Lady Fingers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of flour

## Directions

1. Flavor to taste
2. Drop by teaspoonfuls, and bake quickly

title: Lady Fingers
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 of a Pound of Flour
- 4 Ounces of Powdered White Sugar
- 5 Eggs

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and the yolks of the eggs into a bowl and beat thoroughly till very light
2. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth and add the flour
3. Mix well and add immediately the whites of the eggs
4. Beat all together gently for a few minutes
5. Have ready a long sheet of paper, spread on a baking tin
6. Take a tablespoon and drop the batter along the paper, in lengths of four or five inches, being careful to leave an empty space of an inch between each cake
7. Set to bake in a very moderate oven
8. Bake to a very light brown, for twenty minutes, in a moderate oven

title: Lamb en Blanquette
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Brisket of Lamb
- 1 Onion
- 2 Carrots
- 1/2 Dozen Cloves
- 1 Leek
- 1 Bouquet of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms or Green Peas
- Salt and Pepper
- 1 Pint of water
- The juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the brisket of the lamb into pieces of two square inches
2. Put in a stew pot and cover with a half gallon of water, and add salt and pepper and two onions and one carrot, chopped fine
3. Let it boil till very tender
4. Take the meat out of the saucepan and keep the water in which it was boiled
5. Take another saucepan and put a tablespoonful of butter in it, and as it melts add a tablespoonful of flour
6. Let it brown lightly, and add one pint of the water in which the meat was boiled
7. Stir well, making it very light, and not thick
8. Add one-half can of mushrooms, and let the whole boil about fifteen minutes, so as to be very light
9. Put in the meat, which is already cooked
10. Let it simmer for about fifteen minutes longer, and take off the fire and add the yolks of two eggs, well beaten, two tablespoonfuls of the gravy, and the juice of one lemon
11. Serve hot

title: Lamb Sweetbreads in Cases
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb Sweetbreads
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Shallot
- 3 Mushrooms
- 1/4 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Gill of Sauce Espagnole

## Directions

1. Clean and pare and blanch the sweetbreads.
2. Then set aside to cool.
3. Lard nicely with very fine larding needles.
4. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter in a saucepan and add the sweetbreads.
5. Pour over one glass of Madeira Wine, cover well with the onion, shallot, mushrooms, clove of garlic and parsley, all minced very fine.
6. Cover with a piece of buttered paper and set in the oven for ten minutes or until they are a nice golden brown color.
7. Then take out of the oven and lay the sweetbreads on a dish.
8. Set the saucepan back on the stove and add a gill of Sauce Espagnole.
9. Let all cook for five minutes.
10. Have ready six small boxes of buttered paper; pour a little of the gravy in the bottom of each case, then place a sweetbread in each and set in a baking dish.
11. Set the dish in the oven for five minutes.
12. Then arrange a napkin nicely in a dish, set the cases of sweetbreads upon it and send to the table hot.

title: Lamb Tongues with Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tongues
- 1 Onion
- 1 Pint of Broth or Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- A Tomato Sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues as in the recipe given above, omitting the vegetables
2. When done, place the tongues on a hot dish
3. Pour over a rich Tomato Sauce and serve

title: Lamb's Brains à la Remoulade
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb's Brains
- 1 Pint of White Wine
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 2 Shallots
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 6 Capers
- 3 Small Vinegar Pickles
- 1 Tablespoonful of Parsley
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Creole Mustard
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Chives
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Plunge the Lamb's Brains into cold water and let them stand for an hour, changing the water several times
2. Prepare in exactly the same manner indicated for the preparation of Sheep Brains
3. After removing from the water and taking off the skin, drain of all water
4. Have ready a saucepan of boiling water and season it with salt and pepper and an herb bouquet of parsley, bay leaves and thyme
5. When the herbs begin to boil add a pint of white wine to the water, as it boils up again drop in the brains and let them cook for ten minutes
6. Remove the herb bouquet and strain the brains through a sieve
7. Place on a hot dish
8. Chop the shallots very fine, mince the garlic and mash the yolks of the eggs
9. Put the vinegar into a small saucepan and add the shallots, the garlic and let all boil till the vinegar is reduced about one-half
10. Mash the yolks of eggs in the sweet oil and cut up the capers and add all to the vinegar
11. Add the parsley and the vinegar pickles chopped fine and let all come to a boil
12. Add the chives and two teaspoonfuls of Creole mustard
13. Mix well and pour all over the brains and send to the table very hot

title: Lamb's Feet à la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Lamb's Feet
- 1 Pint of Broth
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the feet well and boil
2. When the water is reduced, take out the feet, cut in pieces, taking out all the bones
3. Put back in the saucepan, add a tablespoonful of butter blended well with a tablespoonful of flour
4. Stir well and add two sprigs of parsley, minced very fine, and the juice of one lemon
5. Let this simmer for ten minutes longer and serve hot

title: Lamb's Feet, White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Lamb's Feet
- 1 Pint of Broth
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- The Yolk of 1 Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Clean the feet well, and, after boiling, take out all the bones, cut in little pieces of about two inches or less
2. Season nicely and cook in a pint of their own water over a slow fire
3. Add the juice of a lemon
4. Throw in the beaten yolk of an egg to bind nicely, and serve hot

title: Lamb's Head Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb's head
- 2 quarts of water
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- herbs
- turnip
- carrot
- celery
- heart and liver

## Directions

1. Prepare the head, and put to it 2 quarts of water
2. Boil it until the head becomes so tender that the bones may be taken out
3. Cut it into small pieces and put them back into the pot
4. Cut up into small pieces half of the heart and liver, and add them
5. Season with pepper, salt, a little onion (chopped), a few herbs, turnip, carrot and celery
6. Boil all together over a slow fire for several hours, skimming well
7. If the water boils away too much add more, which must be boiling

title: Lancers Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 lb. of powdered white sugar
- 2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 heaping tablespoonful of mixed nutmeg and cinnamon
- a small glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the sifted flour crumbled fine
2. Add a heaping tablespoonful of mixed nutmeg and cinnamon
3. Moisten it entirely with butter
4. Add a small glass of brandy
5. Roll it out into a large, thick sheet
6. Cut it into round cakes about the size of saucers
7. Bake them on flat tins, slightly buttered

title: Lapland Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 pint new cream
- 1½ pints flour

## Directions

1. Beat 5 eggs very light with 1 pint of new cream
2. Beat in well 1½ pints of flour
3. Bake in tins or cups on a quick fire

title: Large Thread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar a little longer
2. Try as before; if a thread forms that can be pulled two inches without breaking, this degree is reached

title: Layer Cake of Fresh Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of White or Gold Cake
- 3 Apples
- 2/3 of a Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange or Lemon Extract
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a good White Cake or Gold Cake.
2. Pare and grate three apples, and cook them with two-thirds of a cup of sugar.
3. When they come to a thick jelly, take off the stove and flavor with Lemon or Orange extract.
4. Take the whites of two eggs, beat till very light.
5. Add four tablespoonfuls of white sugar, and beat till very stiff, so stiff that the whites can stand alone.
6. Spread a layer of this on the top of the bottom layer of cake.
7. Add a layer of apples.
8. Spread another layer of frosting.
9. Cover with a layer of cake, and so continue till all is used up.

title: Leg of Mutton Boiled A L'Anglaise
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a fat, fine leg of mutton
- warm water
- salt
- turnips
- cream
- butter
- pepper
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Select a fat, fine leg of mutton, put it on the fire in warm water
2. When it boils skim it, and let it simmer gently for two hours and a half
3. Throw in a tablespoonful of salt
4. When the mutton is done garnish with turnips mashed in cream, butter, pepper and salt, and send it to table with a sauce boat of caper sauce No. 11

title: Lemon and Maraschino Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Soaked Gum Arabic
- 1 Ounce of Lemon Sugar
- 1/2 Gill of Maraschino
- The Whites of 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Use the same amount of soaked Gum Arabic, icing sugar, whites of eggs and one ounce of lemon sugar, made by rasping the lemon on the sugar, and half a gill of Maraschino
2. Follow the same directions as for Orange Bonbons, with the Maraschino forming the cream for the inside and the lemon sugar for the outside

title: Lemon Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 12 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon or 1 Tablespoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar to a cream.
2. Add the flour gradually, and the juice and zest of a lemon.
3. Beat well.
4. Bake on paper sheets in the form of small wafers, very thinly spread, in a moderate oven, to a light color.

title: Lemon Cheese Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 fresh lemons
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar pounded
- ½ lb. of good fresh butter
- yolks of 6 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Grate with care the oily rind of 3 fresh lemons
2. Rub this with loaf sugar pounded until perfectly incorporated with sugar
3. Add by degrees fresh butter
4. Beat very light the yolks of 6 eggs
5. Mix all well together
6. Line a dish with puff paste and put in the above mixture
7. Bake ¾ hour
8. Serve hot

title: Lemon Cheese-Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- peel of two lemons
- half a pound of sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- one-half pound of butter
- juice of the lemons
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Boil the peel of two lemons until tender, and pound them
2. Take half a pound of sugar, the yolks of six eggs, and one-half pound of butter
3. Stir all well together
4. Add the juice of the lemons last
5. Lay puff paste in your pans
6. Fill them half full of the mixture
7. Bake lightly

title: Lemon Citron
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon peels (nice, fresh)
- water
- sugar (equal to their weight)

## Directions

1. Turn water on nice, fresh lemon peels
2. Soak them till all the white pulp can be scraped off
3. Boil till soft
4. Preserve them in their weight in sugar

title: Lemon Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 3 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract
- Sufficient flour to make a nice dough

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and butter to a cream
2. Add the sugar, and beat well
3. Add the extract of lemon, and then the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Add sufficient flour to make a nice dough, just stiff enough to mold, and then roll thin, and bake in a quick oven to a very light brown

title: Lemon Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 large lemons
- 20 spoonfuls spring water
- 6 eggs
- 10 ounces loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Take 5 large lemons; pare them as thin as possible, and steep the parings with the juice of the lemons in 20 spoonfuls of spring water
2. Strain into a silver saucepan through a jelly bag
3. Add the whites well beaten of 6 eggs, and 10 ounces of loaf sugar
4. Set the saucepan over a very slow fire, stirring all the time one way
5. Skim it, and when scalding hot, pour it into glasses

title: Lemon Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 fresh lemons
- 1 quart of water
- sugar
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Pare the rind very thin from 4 fresh lemons
2. Squeeze the juice and strain it
3. Pour the rind and juice into 1 quart of water
4. Sweeten to your taste
5. Add the whites of 6 eggs beaten to a froth
6. Put it on the fire and keep stirring until it thickens, but do not let it boil
7. Pour it into a bowl
8. When cold, strain it through a sieve
9. Put it on the fire again and add the yolks of the eggs
10. Stir until quite thick
11. When cold, serve up in small glasses with a nice macaroon on each glass

title: Lemon Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of water
- one tablespoonful of flour or corn starch
- yolks of three eggs
- one cup of brown sugar
- juice of a lemon
- whites of three eggs
- one cup of pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a cup of water and stir into it a tablespoonful of flour or corn starch
2. Beat the yolks of three eggs with a cup of brown sugar
3. Add the juice of a lemon strained and beat it up with the yolks and sugar
4. Pour this in a paste and bake it
5. While the custard is baking, take the whites of the three eggs and beat them up with a cup of pulverized sugar
6. Spread this icing on the baked custard and brown it slightly

title: Lemon Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of finely powdered sugar
- lemon juice
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pour just enough lemon juice on the sugar to dissolve it
2. Boil to the consistency of thick syrup
3. Drop this in plates
4. Put in a warm place to harden
5. Add grated lemon peel and proceed

title: Lemon Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Creole Fritter Batter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 2/3 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a plain Creole batter for fruit fritters, and add the juice of a lemon, and sugar to taste.
2. Proceed as directed in cooking and serving Apple Fritters.

title: Lemon Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 6 lemons
- 2 quarts of water
- 2 lbs. of white sugar
- whites of 24 eggs

## Directions

1. Take the juice of 6 lemons to 2 quarts of water
2. Steep the rinds for some time in water and strain it
3. Add 2 lbs. of white sugar and the whites of 24 eggs
4. Beat until they will adhere to the bowl
5. Keep the freezer in motion until the cream be of the consistency of snow

title: Lemon Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream or Milk
- 6 Eggs
- 1/4 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Put the milk or cream on to boil
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light, and then beat the whites to a stiff froth
3. Take the milk off the fire, and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring all the while
4. Add the whites, mixing thoroughly, and two tablespoonfuls of extract of lemon
5. Set to cool, and then turn into the freezer, pack, and stir around in the ice for about ten minutes
6. Finish as in recipe for making and freezing Cream

title: Lemon Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces isinglass
- 1 pint water
- juice of 12 large oranges
- juice of 3 lemons
- 3 lbs. sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve isinglass in 1 pint of water
2. Press the juice of 12 large oranges and 3 lemons
3. Boil 3 lbs. of sugar in 1 pint of water to a syrup
4. Strain the juice and isinglass into the boiling syrup, and stir it a little while
5. When nearly cold, pour into moulds

title: Lemon Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lemons
- 1 pound of refined sugar
- 1/2 can of Gelatine
- 1 quart of water

## Directions

1. Grate three or four lemons into a pound of refined sugar, and press out the juice.
2. Boil one-half a can of Gelatine in one quart of water.
3. When reduced to one-half the quantity, add the juice and sugar.
4. Set all to boil on a very hot coal fire.
5. Pour immediately into a glass or porcelain dish.
6. Fill the glasses and set in a cool place.

title: Lemon Jelly Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the cup cake recipe
- juice and rind of 8 lemons
- ½ lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. beat the eggs and sugar very light
2. melt the butter and pour it on the eggs and sugar
3. pour boiling water over the lemons
4. grate the outside after pressing out the juice
5. add rind and juice
6. boil slowly and stir constantly until of the consistency of blanc mange

title: Lemon Jelly Without Lemons
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a box of Cox's gelatine
- one quart of boiling water
- a teaspoonful of tartaric acid
- four cups of sugar
- the beaten whites of three eggs
- a tablespoonful of good extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Take a box of Cox's gelatine, pour over it one quart of boiling water, and stir until it is dissolved
2. Add a teaspoonful of tartaric acid, and four cups of sugar
3. let it dissolve and bring it to a boil
4. while boiling, stir in the beaten whites of three eggs
5. let this boil up once again and take it off the fire
6. when nearly cool, add to it a tablespoonful of good extract of lemon
7. Strain the mixture into moulds or cups, and set it in a cool place, or on ice, to become firm

title: Lemon Jelly, A Beautiful Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Isinglass
- Water
- Lemon juice
- Sugar
- Rinds of lemons
- Saffron

## Directions

1. Set an ounce of isinglass in a pint of water on the stove in a stew-pan
2. Stir the isinglass until it dissolves
3. Let it boil a few minutes, then add a pint of lemon juice sweetened with a pound and a half of sugar, or a little more, if it is wished very sweet
4. Stir this in with the rinds of six lemons, and boil all together
5. After boiling for about five minutes, put a teaspoonful of saffron in to color it yellow, and strain through a flannel bag
6. Fill your jelly-glasses with it; when cool, it is a most beautiful dish for a collation

title: Lemon Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- The Grated Rind and Juice of 1 Lemon
- 4 Layers of Cake
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Egg
- 1 Teaspoonful of Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Flour

## Directions

1. Make a good layer sponge or cup cake
2. Prepare a good layer filling of creamed lemon by taking one cup of sugar, one egg, the grated rind and juice of one lemon, one teaspoonful of water and one of flour
3. Blend the sugar, water and flour, and add the well-beaten egg
4. Add the rind and juice of the lemon
5. Boil in a bain-marie (a kettle set in a kettle of hot water), and let it thicken
6. Spread between the layers, and ice, or sprinkle with sugar

title: Lemon Meringue Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Milk or Water
- 1½ Cups of White Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour or Cornstarch
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- The Juice and Grated Zest of 2 Lemons

## Directions

1. Have ready the pie pans, with the baked crust
2. Beat the sugar and the yolks well together, and add the juice and grated zest of the lemons
3. Blend the flour and milk gradually, and pour it through a sieve into the eggs
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Fill the lined pans with this, and set in the oven to bake for twenty or thirty minutes
6. Add gradually three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar to the whites of the eggs, beating steadily all the time, and when it is all absorbed beat it to a stiff froth
7. Place this over the top of the pie by spoonfuls, and then smooth lightly with the spoon
8. Place in the oven, and let it brown

title: Lemon Mince
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large lemons
- lemon peel
- 6 large apples
- 1 lb. of suet
- 1 lb. of currants
- ½ lb. of sugar
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Squeeze 2 large lemons
2. Boil the peel till tender enough to beat to a mush
3. Combine with apples, suet, currants, sugar, and candied fruit

title: Lemon Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Ounce of Water
- 3 1/4 Ounces of Sugar
- 1/4 Ounce of Lemon Juice

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for Orange Pastilles

title: Lemon Pickle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 lemons
- 2 lbs. of salt
- 8 or 9 cloves of garlic
- mace
- nutmeg
- cayenne
- allspice
- ½ ounce of each spice
- ½ lb. of flour of mustard
- 1½ gallon of good vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the lemons into 6 pieces each
2. Put on them salt, garlic, mace, nutmeg, cayenne, allspice and flour of mustard
3. Add vinegar
4. Boil the whole for ½ hour
5. Put it in a jar, and set it by for 8 weeks, stirring it every day
6. After which, pour it into small bottles, and close them very tight

title: Lemon Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three lemons
- a cup and a half of powdered sugar
- three tablespoonfuls of butter
- yolks of four eggs
- rich puff paste
- whites of four eggs
- powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the rind and express the juice of three lemons
2. Rub together a cup and a half of powdered sugar and three tablespoonfuls of butter
3. Beat up the yolks of four eggs, and add to the butter and sugar, lastly the lemon
4. Bake on a rich puff paste without an upper crust
5. While the pie is baking beat up the whites of the four eggs with powdered loaf sugar
6. Spread it over the top of the pie when done
7. Set back in the oven a few moments to brown lightly

title: Lemon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Corn Starch or 2 of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice and Inner Fruit and Zest of Two Grated Lemons

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter to a cream
2. Add the eggs, the yolks and the white beaten separately, and then add the hot water and the juice and meat of the lemon and the grated zest
3. Moisten the flour or cornstarch with a little cold water, and stir into the mixture
4. Line the pie pans with plain paste or pie crust
5. Bake the under crust a few minutes
6. Fill the pans with the mixture, cover with an upper crust, and bake in a quick oven for half an hour

title: Lemon Pie, Without Corn Starch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a lemon
- one cup of sugar
- two tablespoonfuls water
- yolks of three eggs
- nice crust
- whites of three eggs
- a cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Take the juice and grated rind of a lemon, one cup of sugar, two tablespoonfuls water, and yolks of three eggs
2. Bake in a nice crust
3. Make an icing of the whites and a cup of sugar
4. Pour it over the pie
5. Put it back in the oven, and brown lightly

title: Lemon Pie. Rich
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- two lemons
- one cup and a half of sugar
- puff paste or plain family crust

## Directions

1. Beat all together except the whites of three eggs
2. Beat the whites of three eggs stiff with sugar
3. When the pies are cold spread this icing on top and brown lightly
4. Make the crust of puff paste, or in any way that is liked

title: Lemon Pound Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 ounces of sugar
- 14 ounces of flour
- 14 ounces of butter
- 15 eggs, leaving out the whites of 3
- rind and juice of fresh lemons

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Lemon Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar for Every Pound of Lemons
- The Juice of 1 Lemon for Every Pound of Fruit
- A Tablespoonful of Alum for Every Quart of Water
- Salt
- Ginger Root

## Directions

1. Cut the lemon in halves, after paring, and remove the seeds.
2. Then cut it up into a number of thin slices, and put them into a vessel with cold water, adding a tablespoonful of alum to every quart of water, or a little salt.
3. After several hours put the lemon into cool, fresh water, and let it remain an hour, and drain.
4. Then put it into a kettle and cover with two quarts of boiling water.
5. Add the rinds of the lemon or citron, and a few pieces of ginger root.
6. Let all boil till perfectly clear.
7. Then take out the fruit and drain dry.
8. Then prepare a syrup, allowing one pound of sugar for every pound of fruit, and add the juice of a lemon for each pound of fruit.
9. Put the citron or lemon in without the ginger, and allow it to boil for three-quarters of an hour.
10. It will become a beautiful, clear amber color.
11. Proceed to bottle or seal in jars immediately.

title: Lemon Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Three lemons
- juice of 1 lemon
- 3 cups of sugar
- 7 soda crackers
- 3 cups of cold water

## Directions

1. Grate three lemons and the juice of 1
2. Soak 7 soda crackers 1 hour in 3 cups of cold water
3. Mix with 3 cups of sugar
4. Bake in a nice paste

title: Lemon Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 nice lemon with a smooth thin rind
- 3 eggs
- 1 lb. of fine white sugar
- 1 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 tablespoonful of wine and brandy mixed
- 1 teaspoonful of rose water
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Grate the rind of the lemon
2. Cut it in half, and squeeze the juice of the lemon into the plate with the grated rind, taking out all the seeds
3. Mix the juice and rind well together
4. Stir the sugar and butter with a spoon till it is perfectly light and of the consistency of cream
5. Beat the eggs until they are quite smooth and as thick as a boiled custard
6. Stir the eggs gradually into the pan of butter and sugar
7. Add the seasoning by degrees; then the juice and rind of the lemon
8. Mix all the ingredients well together
9. Have ready a nice puff paste, enough to cover the edges and bottom of the dish
10. Pour the mixture in and bake the pudding 1/2 hour, in a moderate oven

title: Lemon Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of marmalade
- 2 or 3 grated biscuits
- crumbled stale bread
- 3 even tablespoonfuls of butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- lemon chips

## Directions

1. To a dish that will hold 2 1/2 pints, put 4 tablespoonfuls of marmalade
2. add 2 or 3 grated biscuits, or the same quantity of crumbled stale bread, 3 even tablespoonfuls of butter, 3 eggs beat very light, 1 pint of milk
3. add some lemon chips and bake it

title: Lemon Pudding. Very Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six eggs
- Three lemons
- Six tablespoonfuls of corn starch
- One large spoonful of butter
- One cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the corn starch in a pint and a half of water
2. Stir in the butter
3. Let it get cool
4. Stir in the yolks of the eggs
5. Add the juice of the lemons, and the grated rind
6. Add one cup of sugar
7. Bake this lightly in a pudding dish
8. When cold pour it over a meringue, or icing, made with the whites of the eggs, and sufficient sugar to make a thick icing
9. Put it back in the oven
10. Let it brown lightly

title: Lemon Salad
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 lemons
- 1 ounce isinglass
- 1 quart cream

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of 2 or 3 lemons into a dish
2. Squeeze the juice of 3 upon it
3. Sweeten it well
4. Dissolve 1 ounce of isinglass in a very little water
5. Strain it in a quart of cream, which you will boil
6. Put it into a jug, and pour it as slowly as possible, that the mixture may froth
7. Do not move the dish until the contents are quite cold
8. The cream should be poured in as hot as the safety of the dish will permit

title: Lemon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1/2 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Juice of an Onion
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- The Juice of Half a Lemon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and flour, blending well without browning
2. Add a half pint of water, or consomme, the juice of one lemon, and let it simmer twenty minutes
3. Season to taste
4. Take from the fire, add the yolks of two well-beaten eggs and the juice of a lemon and serve immediately
5. No sauce into which the yolks or eggs are beaten must be set on the fire after adding these
6. If necessary to keep warm, set in a bowl of boiling water (a bain-marie) till ready to serve
7. It is best served immediately

title: Lemon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Cornstarch
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- ½ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Egg
- The Juice and Grated Zest of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the well-beaten egg, and then add the cornstarch, which you will have dissolved in a teaspoonful of water
3. When well-blended pour in slowly the boiling water, stirring all the time, and set over the fire till it thickens
4. Stir about three minutes
5. Take from the fire and add the juice and rind of a grated lemon
6. This sauce is much improved by adding the white of an egg beaten to a stiff froth, after you have taken it from the fire

title: Lemon Sauce for Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter sauce (half a pint of No. 10)
- lemon juice (one)
- lemon (sliced, one)

## Directions

1. To half a pint of butter sauce No. 10, add the juice of a lemon and another lemon sliced; take out the seeds, and let all boil together.
2. This is good with broiled Spanish mackerel or pompano, also with broiled fish.

title: Lemon Sherbet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ten fine lemons
- three quarts of cold water
- two and one-half pounds of loaf sugar
- whites of three eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place lemons to three quarts of cold water sweetened with two and one-half pounds of loaf sugar
2. Just before placing in the freezer, beat up the whites of three eggs with a little sugar and stir in
3. Place the mixture of lemons, sugar, water and eggs in the freezer
4. Pack ice and salt around it

title: Lemon Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rich lemonade
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make any quantity of rich lemonade, it is better made of limes
2. Make it very sweet
3. Freeze it

title: Lemon Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- The Juice of 10 Lemons
- The Grated Zest of 3 Lemons
- 1 Pint of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and water to boil.
2. Grate the zest or yellow rind of three lemons, and add to the syrup, and let it boil for five minutes.
3. Then set away to cool.
4. Extract the juice from the lemons, and grate the fruit.
5. Mix this with the syrup, and then strain through a thin muslin cloth.
6. Turn into the freezer, and proceed as in Ice Cream.

title: Lemon Sugar
body:
## Ingredients

- The Zest of 3 Lemons
- 1 Pound of Loaf Sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the zest or outer rinds of the lemons on pieces of loaf sugar, and then set to dry
2. When dry, pound very fine, sift, and put in a jar and cork tight

title: Lemon Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Lemon Juice
- 15 Ounces of Sugar
- 3/4 Ounce of Extract of Lemon

## Directions

1. Press the juice from the lemons and grate the zest of four into the syrup; set aside in a cool place for six or eight hours.
2. Then strain carefully through a bag.
3. Make a Plain Syrup, using the proportions of fifteen ounces of sugar to a pint and a half of water.
4. Prepare a gallon of syrup, and then proceed as in the General Directions for Making Syrups.
5. Add the essence when about to bottle.

title: Lemon Wine Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of eggs
- Sugar
- Zest of half a lemon or one tablespoonful of lemon or one tablespoonful of lemon essence
- 2 Gills of Madeira Wine

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar well together, and when they come to a rich cream set on the stove and whip well for two minutes
2. Then add the zest of a lemon or of one tablespoonful of a lemon, or one tablespoonful of lemon essence, and drop in gradually the Madeira wine
3. Stir without ceasing for two minutes more, and then serve very hot with Fruit, Bread Puddings, etc.

title: Lenten Mince Pies
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of the Best Apples, Cored and Minced
- 2 Pounds of Raisins, Stoned and Minced
- 8 Hard-Boiled Eggs, Chopped Fine
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Spices
- Juice and Rind of 2 Lemons, Grated
- The Juice of an Orange, and Rind, Grated
- 2 Pounds of Currants
- 2 Pounds of Sugar, or Sugar to Taste
- 3/4 Pint Each of Brandy and Sherry

## Directions

1. Mix all well together in lesser proportions, according to the number of pies you wish to make, and proceed as in the mince pie recipe

title: Lentil Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Lentils
- A Vinaigrette Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare lentils in the same manner as Bean Salad.
2. Serve with Vinaigrette Sauce.

title: Lentil Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Lentils
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1 Onion
- 1 Small Bunch of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter or Lard
- 1 Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- 1 Stalk of Celery, Chopped Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the lentils, and, if dried, soak them over night
2. Drain off the water and put them in a saucepan with the cold water
3. Allow them to come gradually to a boil
4. Set them back on the stove and let them simmer gently for about two hours
5. Melt the butter in the saucepan and fry in it the minced onion, celery, parsley, thyme and bay leaf, and let these brown
6. Add them to the lentils
7. Boil about an hour longer, and, if particularly tender, press all through a colander
8. Return to the fire and add the seasonings
9. Let them boil up once and serve with Croutons

title: Lettuce Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Heads of Lettuce
- French Dressing
- 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Take fresh, crisp lettuce of sufficient quantity for the number to be served, three young heads being enough for six.
2. Dip in cold water examining each leaf, and pick over carefully, and select the fresh crisp leaves.
3. Place all these in a salad bowl, and garnish nicely with sliced hard-boiled egg.
4. Never dress the lettuce before bringing to the table.
5. Bring to the table, and let the sauce, always a plain French dressing or Creole dressing preferred, be made at the table.

title: Lettuce Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Head of Lettuce
- 1 Spoon of French Vinegar
- 1 Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Gill of Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Flour
- Dices of Stale Bread
- 1 Pinch of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Broth

## Directions

1. Prepare a good broth and cook till it is reduced to three pints; this will serve six persons.
2. Chop the lettuce fine and stew it with a tablespoonful of butter, adding the pinch of sugar and one spoon of French vinegar.
3. Keep stirring constantly, so that it will not burn.
4. Then add the flour (which has been rolled smoothly in butter), the pepper and salt, throw in a dash of Cayenne pepper.
5. Break in the egg and stir thoroughly.
6. Then pour on the broth.
7. Place the dice of bread in the tureen, and add the gill of cream to the soup before pouring over the bread.

title: Light Bread, Invariably Good
