author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- nine pint cups of flour
- one pint cup of good yeast made from hops
- two pint cups of warm water
- a pint cupful of warm milk

## Directions

1. Take nine pint cups of flour, one pint cup of good yeast made from hops, two pint cups of warm water and a pint cupful of warm milk
2. Make into a sponge, let this rise
3. when risen, knead it with all your strength, work more flour into it, and let it rise again
4. When it is light, you must bake it in loaves

title: Light Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 4 potatoes
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- a little salt
- flour
- 1 small teacup of home made yeast

## Directions

1. Pass the potatoes through a cullender with the milk
2. Mix enough flour to make it the thickness of muffins
3. Add yeast and let it stand 4 hours
4. Add sufficient flour to make it as stiff as bread dough
5. Mould it off and put it in pans
6. Let it remain for 3 hours
7. Bake in a quick oven 20 minutes

title: Light Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 4 or 5 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- 2 Fresh Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. To one pint of milk add the same quantity of cream, and four or five tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar.
2. Let it boil till reduced to one-half the quantity.
3. Take off the stove, and whip in the whites of two fresh Creole eggs, and, when it is as white as snow, add a half pint of whipped cream.
4. Place upon the fire, and let it simmer gently, stirring continually.
5. Add a tablespoonful of orange flower water, or other essence.
6. Take off, let it cool, and serve when cold.

title: Light Flour Puffs For Breakfast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tumbler of sifted flour
- a tumbler of milk
- two eggs
- a teaspoonful of yeast powder
- a teaspoonful of melted butter or lard
- salt

## Directions

1. Put a teaspoonful of yeast powder in the flour before sifting
2. beat the eggs separately
3. Mix all together
4. add a teaspoonful of melted butter or lard just before baking in little fancy pans
5. Put salt in the flour with the yeast powder
6. bake as quickly as you can

title: Light Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 pint of molasses
- 6 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- juice of 1 lemon
- rind of 1 lemon
- raisins and citron

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of soda in the juice of a lemon and add the rind
2. Mix all ingredients together
3. Bake in cake pans
4. Raisins and citron are a great improvement

title: Light Ginger Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- ginger and mace to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Put in small cups or tins and bake in a slow oven

title: Lightened Loaf
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 pint of sugar
- 1 pint of raisins
- 1 lb. of butter
- 5 eggs
- ½ pint of yeast
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix a part of the flour, milk and yeast at night to rise
2. Work in the other materials in the morning

title: Lima Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Lima Beans
- 3 Pints of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Sort the beans as you shell them, and save the very large ones for a purge.
2. Soak the small ones over night.
3. Drain the water when about to cook, and put in a porcelain or agate saucepan, and cover with three pints of boiling water.
4. Boil them until very tender, which will require at least two hours.
5. After they have boiled one hour, add a teaspoonful of salt, or salt to taste.
6. When done, drain the beans and return to the saucepan.
7. Add a half pint of cream or milk, a tablespoonful of flour, blended well with butter, salt and pepper to taste, a sprig each of thyme and parsley and bay leaf minced very fine.
8. Let all simmer for ten minutes, and then serve hot.

title: Lima, or Butter Beans
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- lima beans
- cold water
- boiling water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Shell them
2. Lay them in cold water for an hour or so before cooking
3. When ready to cook, put them in a stew pan in boiling water enough to cover them
4. Let them boil fast and keep them covered while cooking
5. Examine them in an hour, and if soft, pour off nearly all the water
6. Stir in a lump of butter, some pepper and salt

title: Lime Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Lime Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 3/4 Ounce of Soluble Essence of Limes

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Lemon Syrup, and add the essence when about to bottle.

title: Liqueur Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- Essence or Liquor to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar
2. Remove the saucepan from the fire, and immerse it in a pan containing cold water, so that the sugar may cool quickly
3. Place a lid over the pan, to prevent the sugar forming a crust on top by contact with the air
4. Roll the sugar into balls or pellets, flatten out and work the flavoring into them
5. Set them in a box or mold and thinly cover their surface with a little starch powder to prevent forming lumps
6. Dry in a moderately warm closet for about fifteen hours
7. As you remove the bonbons place them on a dry sieve and brush off any remains of starch with a long-haired soft brush

title: Liqueur Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Yolks of 8 Eggs
- A Gill of Any Kind of Liqueur

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and take from the fire
2. Add the sugar and the eggs, beaten to a thick cream
3. Add a gill of any kind of fancy liqueur
4. Turn into a mold and freeze as directed

title: Liqueur Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- A Small Wineglassful of Any Kind of Cordial or Liqueur
- 3 1/4 Ounces of Coarse Sifted Sugar
- Cochineal or Carmine to Color, If Desired

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for Orange Pastilles

title: Liquid Yeast of Parched Corn and Hops, Which Does Not Turn Sour
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- hops
- water
- potatoes
- vinegar
- sugar
- salt
- good yeast
- boiled potatoes
- flour

## Directions

1. Take two teacupfuls of corn and parch it thoroughly, being careful not to burn it
2. Add a good handful of hops
3. Boil in water enough to cover well for an hour and a half
4. Pare six good-sized potatoes and boil them for half an hour with the corn and hops
5. Sift the potatoes through a colander when done and strain the liquor through a cloth onto the potatoes
6. Add a tablespoonful of vinegar, one cup of sugar, and a half cup of salt
7. Put in cold water enough to make up a gallon
8. Add a teacupful of good yeast to raise it
9. Put the whole in a jug and set it without corking in a warm place till it begins to work
10. Cork it and put it in the cellar; the longer it stands the better it becomes
11. At noon take five or six boiled potatoes and mash them very fine
12. Stir in a teacupful of flour and pour on a quart of boiling water
13. Put in a cup nearly full of yeast and set the ferment in a warm place till night
14. Set a soft sponge with warm water and flour, adding the ferment
15. It will be ready to mould up hard the first thing in the morning
16. Let it rise till quite light, then mould it out in loaves
17. Rise again and bake in the usual way

title: Little Drop Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- four eggs
- half a pound of flour
- quarter of a pound of butter

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients
2. Mix and bake

title: Liver And Ham Force Meat For Stuffing
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- calf’s liver
- fat ham or bacon
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- chopped mushrooms
- parsley
- shallots

## Directions

1. Take a calf’s liver, or the livers of three or four turkeys, or geese; lay them in cold water, till ready to use them;
2. cut with them the same quantity of fat ham or bacon; throw them into a saucepan, and let them fry a good brown;
3. season with salt, pepper, spices, chopped mushrooms, parsley and three shallots.
4. When soft, chop them fine, or else pass them through a sausage grinder.
5. This recipe can be used for raised pies, or as an addition to turkey stuffing.

title: Liver and Truffle Stuffing for a Pig or Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles (a pound)
- butter
- fat bacon (one-half pound)
- black pepper
- garlic (a clove)
- salt
- sweet basil
- thyme (dried and powdered)
- veal liver (half a pound)
- bread crumbs (a quarter of a pound)
- eggs (two beaten)
- sweet oil
- butter

## Directions

1. Pare and cut into small pieces a pound of truffles, put them into a stewpan with a large spoonful of butter, one-half pound of fat bacon, chopped very fine;
2. add a spoonful of black pepper, a clove of garlic, a little salt, a bunch of sweet basil and thyme, dried and powdered;
3. add also half a pound of nice veal liver, boiled and grated.
4. Set this all on the fire, let it cook until the truffles are soft, then mash with a wooden spoon;
5. take it off to cool it, and stuff the pig with the forcemeat.
6. Baste the pig with sweet oil, which is better than butter.
7. It is supposed the pig comes from the butchers all ready for stuffing and baking.
8. If the stuffing is desired for a turkey, add a quarter of a pound of bread crumbs and two beaten eggs, and baste the turkey with butter, instead of oil.

title: Loaf Cake, Plain
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of sweet milk
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of yeast
- flour
- two eggs well beaten
- fruit and spice to taste

## Directions

1. stir in flour to make it quite thick, and let it rise over night
2. In the morning add two eggs well beaten, fruit and spice to taste
3. let it rise till hen
4. Bake in a slow oven

title: Loaf of Foies Gras
body:
## Ingredients

- Foies Gras
- Quarter Can of Mushrooms
- Quarter Can of Truffles
- Two Shallots
- Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- Leaf of Rose Geranium
- Quarter Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cinnamon, Allspice, Cloves and Mace
- Young Sweetbread
- Quarter Cup of the Soft of Bread
- Yolk of an Egg
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Half Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- Milk

## Directions

1. Choose sufficient livers for the number of guests and place them in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter
2. Let them simmer gently and add a half can of mushrooms and a quarter of a can of truffles
3. Add two shallots, nicely minced; a sprig each of thyme, bay leaf and parsley, minced fine; salt and pepper to taste, and a half teaspoonful of prepared mustard
4. Add the leaf of one geranium, minced fine, and a quarter teaspoonful each of ground cinnamon, allspice, cloves and mace
5. Mix thoroughly and let it simmer in the juice of the mushrooms for about twenty minutes
6. Take a young sweetbread and cook according to recipe for Plain Fried Sweetbreads, and add a half cup of the soft of the bread, well moistened with milk
7. Mix this with the hashed sweetbreads, and add the yolk of an egg
8. Place this in a mortar with the foies gras and mix well
9. Turn into a pan and brush lightly with the beaten yolk of an egg, and sprinkle grated bread crumbs over
10. Set in a pan of boiling water (Bain-marie), and bake in the oven for about a half hour

title: Lost Bread or Egg Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Orange Flower Water
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- Slices of Stale Bread
- The Finely-Grated Zest of a Lemon
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy (if desired)

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a bowl, beat them till very light, add the Orange Flower Water and the Brandy, if desired, and then add the sugar, and beat thoroughly.
2. Add the grated zest of a lemon, mix well, and then cut the bread into slices or round pieces, taking off the crust, or still again into diamond shapes, and soak them well for a half hour in the mixture.
3. Have ready a frying pan of boiling lard, lay in the bread, and fry to a golden brown.
4. Lift the slices out with a skimmer, and drain on brown paper in the mouth of the oven.
5. Then place on a hot dish, sprinkle with powdered sugar, add a little grated nutmeg, if desired, and serve hot.

title: Louisiana Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- Two Breasts of Chicken
- 1/2 of a Red Tongue
- 2 Ounces of Grated Lean Boiled Ham
- A Dash of Curry Powder
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Thick Velouté Sauce
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Parmesan Cheese

## Directions

1. Mince very fine the cooked breasts of the chicken, and cut the ham and tongue into small dice shapes.
2. Mix well with the chicken, and season with salt and a dash of Cayenne.
3. Add a dash of Curry Powder, and then work the entire forcemeat well with two tablespoonfuls of thick Velouté Sauce.
4. Spread the mixture in layers one-quarter of an inch thick on each slice of delicate circles of toast.
5. Dredge lightly with Parmesan Cheese, set in the oven for five minutes and bake.
6. Send to the table hot.

title: Louisiana Hard-Times Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sifted sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- whites of six eggs
- one pound of flour
- two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
- one cup of cold water
- two teaspoonfuls of brandy, wine or extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Cream half a pound of butter, with one pound of sifted sugar
2. Add to this the beaten yolks of six eggs
3. Beat this again, and set it by until you beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
4. Sift a pound of flour, and put into it two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
5. Then pour in alternately a little flour and beaten eggs until all is used
6. Then mix in a cup of cold water and two teaspoonfuls of brandy, wine or extract of lemon
7. Butter a four-quart cake-pan or mould, have the oven ready, and pour in the mixture, and bake immediately
8. Cover the top of the cake while baking

title: Louisiana Orange Flower Macaroons
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a coffee cup of freshly gathered petals of the orange
- two pounds of dry, sifted white sugar
- whites of seven eggs

## Directions

1. Cut orange petals with a pair of scissors into sugar; this keeps their color fresh
2. Beat the whites of seven eggs to a stiff froth
3. Add to the orange flowers and sugar
4. Drop this mixture on white paper in small cakes
5. Bake in a slow oven; do not let them brown

title: Louisiana Orange Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar to every Gallon of juice
- 0.33 of a Gallon of Water
- The White of 1 Egg
- 1 Cake of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. Take fine Louisiana oranges, and wash them well
2. Dry with a cloth, and peel off the yellow outer rind very thin
3. Strain all the juice through a hair sieve
4. Put the sugar, the crushed eggshell and white of the egg, beaten to a froth, in a porcelain-lined saucepan, and add the water
5. Boil to a syrup, stirring constantly to prevent the egg from hardening
6. Let the syrup boil till it looks clear when seen through the froth of the egg
7. Strain the syrup, and pour it over the grated orange zest
8. Let it stand for twenty-four hours
9. Add the orange juice, and strain
10. Put it into a cask with a small cake of compressed yeast
11. Close tightly, but leave the bung out of the cask until the wine ceases to ferment
12. When fermentation ceases, add a glass of good brandy for each gallon of wine
13. Close the cask, drive in the bung, and let the wine stand for at least nine months
14. Bottle it, and about four months after it has been bottled it will be ready for use

title: Louisiana Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of White Burgundy Wine
- 2 Quarts of Seltzer Water
- 1 Pint of Brandy
- 1 Pint of Good Old Cognac
- 1 Pint of Curacoa
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- ½ Dozen Cherries
- 2 Dozen Strawberries
- ¼ of a Sliced Pineapple
- 2 Lemons, Sliced Very Thin
- 1½ Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Mix the Seltzer Water and the sugar
2. Add the wine, brandy, Curacoa and lemon juice
3. Stem and wash the strawberries and the cherries
4. Add, and decorate the punch with thin slices of lemon and pineapple
5. Set to cool and serve in small punch glasses

title: Louisiana Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- The Juice of 1 Large Louisiana Orange
- The Juice of 4 Lemons
- ¾ Pound of Pulverized Sugar
- ½ Pint of Water
- 1 Gill of Kirsch
- 1 Gill of Fine Rum
- 1 Gill of Champagne

## Directions

1. Put the sugar into a bowl and squeeze over the juices of the orange and lemons
2. Mix well till all the sugar is impregnated with the juices
3. Add a half-pint of cold water, and then add the Kirsch, and stir for five or six minutes
4. Strain all through a fine sieve into the ice cream freezer
5. Remove the sieve and pour into the freezer the rum and champagne
6. Cover well, and let the mixture freeze for about five minutes
7. Remove the cover and detach the mixture from the sides and bottom, and cover again

title: Louisiana Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Lettuce Heads
- 6 Pickled Cucumbers
- 2 Dozen Pickled Onions
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Use, in this fancy salad, lettuce, pickled cucumbers, pickled onions, cut in dice, and serve with a French dressing.

title: Louisiana Salpicon
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Lake Shrimp
- 1 Dozen Mushrooms
- 1 Truffle
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 3 Tomatoes

## Directions

1. Clean and scale the Shrimp and boil according to recipe.
2. Then follow in all particulars the recipe given for Salpicon Creole, with the exception that White Wine is used instead of milk, and a truffle is added to the minced vegetables.
3. Send to the table hot.

title: Love Knots
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 5 spoonfuls of white sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda dissolved in 2 teaspoonfuls of cold water
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- flour enough to roll

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs, sugar, soda mixture, and butter together
2. Add flour to make a dough to roll
3. Cut the sheet in slips
4. Tie them in love knots
5. Fry in pure white lard

title: Lozenges
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pounds of Finest White Icing Sugar
- 2 Ounces of Any Essence Preferred
- 1 Ounce of Gum Tragacanth
- 2 Ounces of Tepid Water

## Directions

1. Take one ounce of Gum Tragacanth and soak it in two ounces of tepid water for about six hours
2. Squeeze and wring out through a cloth
3. Add about a pound and a half of fine icing sugar and the desired essence in the proportion of about two ounces of essence to the quantities given
4. Work this prepared gum on a marble slab as you would knead dough until it is firm and white, yet elastic
5. Gradually work in the sugar and add the essence and work till smooth and very elastic
6. Roll the paste out with fine sugar dredged on the slab and cut into ten-cent size pieces and into diamond shapes
7. Place them in rows upon sugar-powdered baking sheets and dry in the oven
8. Keep them in a well-stopped jar in a cool place

title: Lunch Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- % Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- % Cup of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Peach Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and eggs to a cream, and add the milk.
2. Then add gradually the flour, into which you will have sifted the baking powder.
3. Add one tablespoonful of Peach Extract, and bake in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour.

title: Lyonnaise Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Cold Boiled Potatoes, Cut in Dice
- 2 Onions
- 2 Large Tablespoonfuls Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take cold boiled potatoes, and cut them into slices.
2. Slice the onions fine.
3. Put the butter into the frying pan and as it melts add the onions.
4. Let them fry until half done.

title: Lyonnaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Tomatoes
- Equal Parts of Onions
- 1/2 Spoon of Butter
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf, Minced Fine
- Sherry to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Make a good Tomato Sauce
2. Add to this the equal parts of onion browned in butter
3. Stir well, add a little lemon juice, and serve with any meats

title: Macaroni and Grated Cheese
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of macaroni
- grated cheese
- salt
- butter
- new milk

## Directions

1. break macaroni into lengths, and throw it into cold water to soak, an hour or so after breakfast
2. Boil it an hour
3. take it out of the pot and put in the bottom of the pan a layer of the boiled macaroni and then a layer of grated cheese
4. strew over the top a teaspoonful of salt and some lumps of butter as big as a nutmeg
5. fill up the pan with new milk and bake until browned on top, but never let it get dry; it is better to put water in, if your milk has given out, than to let it get the least dry

title: Macaroni au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Macaroni or Spaghetti
- 1 Pound of Parmesan Cheese (Grated)
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Pint of Milk

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni by its length in water until soft.
2. Do not let it cook too much, but just enough to be soft, and lift out of the water without breaking.
3. This will require about a half hour of rapid boiling.
4. When done, take out of the water in which it was boiled, and season well with salt and black pepper.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter, blended well with flour, into a pint of milk.
6. Let it boil two minutes.
7. Place a layer of the spaghetti or macaroni in the pan in which it is to be baked, seasoning again to taste, and mix in a layer of the grated cheese.
8. Sprinkle with pepper and salt.
9. Put in alternately a layer of the macaroni and a layer of cheese, and so on until three-fourths of the cheese is used.
10. Do not break the macaroni or spaghetti.
11. Pour over this the boiling milk.
12. Take the remaining quarter pound of cheese and sprinkle thickly on top.
13. Dot here and there with bits of butter, and put in a quick oven and let it bake to a nice brown.
14. Serve in the dish in which it was baked.

title: Macaroni Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cold Boiled Macaroni
- 1 Cup Boiling Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Cheese
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put the butter into a saucepan, and as it melts add the flour, but do not let it brown.
2. Add the cup of boiling milk and stir well.
3. When it begins to thicken add the grated cheese, and let it simmer for a few minutes longer.
4. Take from the fire and add the beaten yolks of four eggs.
5. Have the macaroni cut into tiny bits.

title: Macaroni Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Macaroni
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cooked Smoked Tongue, Cut Very Fine
- 1 Minced Truffle
- 1 Egg
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Fresh Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the Macaroni.
2. Then drain well.
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, add the Macaroni, and sprinkle over the grated cheese and the minced smoked tongue and truffle.
4. Toss all together nicely and then spread out evenly.
5. Then set aside to cool.
6. When cool, cut the preparation into six equal parts and roll each in grated Parmesan cheese, then in a well-beaten egg, and afterwards in freshly-grated bread crumbs.
7. Have ready a pan of boiling lard, fry the Croquettes in the lard for five minutes, then drain well, place on a dish on a folded napkin and serve hot.

title: Macaroni in a Mould
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- meat
- sweet herbs
- butter
- pepper
- eschalot
- eggs
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Boil macaroni till it is tender
2. Line a mould with it, fitting it in closely
3. Make a mince of any kind of meat, raw or cooked
4. Season with sweet herbs, butter, pepper chopped eschalot, and a couple of eggs
5. Fill the mould with this and boil for twenty minutes
6. Serve with white sauce No. 10 put around the macaroni

title: Macaroni in a Mould
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- meat, raw or cooked
- sweet herbs
- butter
- pepper
- eschalot, chopped
- eggs
- white sauce No. 10

## Directions

1. Boil macaroni till it is tender
2. line a mould with it, fitting it in closely
3. Make a mince of any kind of meat, raw or cooked; season with sweet herbs, butter, pepper chopped eschalot, and a couple of eggs
4. fill the mould with this and boil for twenty minutes
5. Serve with white sauce No. 10 put around the macaroni

title: Macaroni or Spaghetti Milanaise Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Macaroni
- 1/2 Pound of Cold Boiled Ham
- 1 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Onion
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Can Tomatoes
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni in water and salt as in the recipe given for Boiling Macaroni.
2. When tender, drain well through a colander.
3. While the macaroni is boiling, put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan, and as it melts, add the grated onion.
4. Let this brown, and then add the ham which you will have minced very fine.
5. Let this brown; add the cloves of garlic, minced very fine, and the herbs, minced very fine.
6. Add almost immediately, as these begin to brown, a half can of tomatoes, or six fresh large tomatoes.
7. As it stews, add a half pound of grated Parmesan cheese, and let all stew for about ten minutes.
8. Add the macaroni or spaghetti, and let all simmer gently for about twenty minutes longer.
9. Serve hot, and pass at the same time a small plate of grated Parmesan cheese to each person.

title: Macaroni or Spaghetti with Daube
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Macaroni
- A Veal or Beef Daube
- 1 Pint of Hot Water or Broth

## Directions

1. Prepare a daube.
2. After it has cooked about an hour and a half, and is about two-thirds done, add the macaroni according to the number to be served, using between a quarter and a half pound for six, and cutting the macaroni into lengths of about five inches, to facilitate serving.
3. Let it boil for about three-quarters of an hour in the daube.
4. If there is not sufficient gravy for it to cook well, add a half pint or a pint more of hot water or hot broth, according as the macaroni appears to absorb after it has been in the pot four or five minutes.
5. Cook until very tender, and on serving place the daube in the center of the dish and heap the macaroni around.

title: Macaroni Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Broth
- 1/4 Pound of Macaroni

## Directions

1. Prepare a good Pot-au-Feu, or Bouillon, according to directions given
2. Break the macaroni into two-inch length pieces
3. Add to the boiling broth about a half hour or so before serving
4. Alternatively cook the macaroni separately in salted boiling water about ten or fifteen minutes, drain thoroughly, and drop into the boiling broth about fifteen minutes before serving
5. Serve, optionally with Parmesan cheese, grated

title: Macaroni with Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Macaroni
- 1 Gill of Tomato Sauce
- 1 Gill of Madeira Sauce, or Wine
- 3/4 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni in salted water for twenty minutes, adding a tablespoonful of butter and an onion, with two cloves stuck in it.
2. Then drain the macaroni of all water.
3. Place it in a saucepan with a gill of Tomato Sauce and one of Madeira Sauce or Madeira Wine.
4. Add a quarter of a pound of grated Parmesan cheese.
5. Season well with salt and pepper.
6. Add a dash of Cayenne, and let the mixture cook slowly for ten or fifteen minutes, tossing frequently to prevent burning.
7. Place the Macaroni on a hot dish, pour the sauce over it, and serve with grated Parmesan cheese passed in a separate dish.

title: Macaroni, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Macaroni
- 3/4 Can of Tomatoes, or 6 Fresh Ones
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni according to the recipe given above.
2. When done, drain through a colander without breaking the lengths.
3. Season well with Salt and pepper.
4. Put one tablespoonful of butter in a frying pan and add one tablespoonful of flour; blend well and as it browns add the tomatoes, which have been chopped fine in their own juice.
5. Let this stew, after stirring well for about ten minutes, and when it begins to boil add the macaroni or spaghetti, mixing well without breaking the lengths.
6. Let it boil up once, and then serve hot.

title: Macaroni, Neapolitan Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pound of Macaroni
- 1/2 Pint of Sauce Espagnole
- 1/2 Pint of Tomato Sauce
- 3/4 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 6 Mushrooms
- 2 Truffles
- 1 Ounce of Smoked Beef Tongue
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1/2 Bottle of Claret
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 2 Sprigs of Celery

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni, and cut into pieces of about two inches long, after draining thoroughly.
2. Place it in a saucepan with a half pint of Tomato Sauce and Sauce Espagnole, and add the cheese.
3. Add the herb bouquet, tied together.
4. Cut the truffles and mushrooms and carrot into dice-shaped pieces; mince the celery and add.
5. Add the beef tongue cut into small dice-shaped pieces.
6. Add the Claret.
7. Let all cook for about fifteen minutes, tossing frequently in the meantime.
8. When ready to serve, remove the herb bouquet and send to the table hot.

title: Macaroon Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 fresh lemons
- 1 quart of water
- sugar
- whites of 6 eggs
- yolks of 6 eggs
- macaroons

## Directions

1. Pare the rind very thin from 4 fresh lemons
2. Squeeze the juice and strain it
3. Pour them both into 1 quart of water
4. Sweeten to your taste
5. Add the whites of 6 eggs beaten to a froth
6. Put it on the fire and keep stirring it until it thickens, but do not let it boil
7. Pour it into a bowl
8. When cold, strain it through a sieve
9. Put it on the fire again and add the yolks of the eggs
10. Stir until quite thick
11. When cold, serve up in small glasses
12. On each glass lay a nice macaroon

title: Macaroon Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Macaroons
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water
- 1/2 a White Praline
- 2 Ounces of Powdered White Sugar
- The Yolks of 4 Fresh Eggs

## Directions

1. Crumble six macaroons, soak them in a pint of milk.
2. Add one tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water, in which you have put one-half of a mashed Praline.
3. Add two ounces of sugar and the yolks of four fresh Creole eggs, well beaten till light.
4. Set on the stove and let it simmer constantly till reduced almost one-half.
5. Let it cool, and serve in the glass in which it was cooled.

title: Macaroon Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Dozen Macaroons
- Orange Juice or Extract of Orange
- Two tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar
- The Whites of the Eggs

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil.
2. Blend the butter and the flour, and stir into the boiling milk.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs with half a cup of sugar, very light, and add this to the milk, stirring constantly for one minute, and take from the fire to cool.
4. Add a tablespoonful of orange Juice or extract of orange.
5. When cool, crumble one dozen macaroons over the top of the dish.
6. Make a meringue with the whites of the eggs and two tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar.
7. Beat to a stiff froth, pile on the custard, and set in the oven for a few minutes to brown.
8. Serve very cold.

title: Macaroon Pudding Iced
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroons
- dried cherries
- seedless raisins
- Madeira or sherry wine
- a pint of milk
- two eggs
- flavoring
- half a pound of white sugar
- butter and sugar sauce

## Directions

1. Line a mould with macaroons, as described for Iced Cabinet Pudding.
2. Fill the mould with dried cherries, seedless raisins and macaroons, in layers.
3. Pour a little Madeira or sherry wine over them.
4. Pour over all a custard made of a pint of milk, two eggs and flavoring, sweetened with half a pound of white sugar.
5. In summer cover the mould up in ice and salt until wanted.
6. In winter steam it and serve with butter and sugar sauce.

title: Macaroons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of blanched almonds or peach kernels
- 1 white of an egg, beaten to a stiff froth
- refined sugar

## Directions

1. Throw peach kernels in cold water to take off the bitter taste if using those
2. Drain and pound in a mortar until they become a stiff paste
3. Take the white of an egg and beat it to a stiff froth
4. Mix it with the almonds
5. Sift in as much refined sugar as will turn it to a stiff dough
6. Form it into small cakes
7. Put them on sheets of tin which must be greased
8. Bake them in a very moderate oven

title: Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sweet Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 2 Pounds of Sifted Sugar
- The Whites of 6 Eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch or scald the almonds by throwing them into a pan of boiling water, and letting them remain on the fire until you can take one of the almonds between your fingers and easily push off the skin
2. Drain and cool the almonds in cold water, and drain again
3. Rub the almond skins or hulls off with your hands in a cloth, throw the almonds in cold water with a little salt, wash clean, drain and dry them in another cloth
4. Pound them to a pulp that will be as smooth as flour
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
6. Add a little of the sugar and a little of the eggs as you pound, to prevent the almonds from becoming oily
7. Keep the paste firm as you add the remainder of the sugar and the eggs
8. Have ready a baking sheet on which you will have placed a buttered piece of wafer paper
9. Lay the macaroons out on this, by dropping them from the spoon, in the form of a guinea fowl egg
10. Place the macaroons about an inch apart, so that the balls will not touch one another
11. Pass a wet paste brush gently over their surface
12. Put them in the oven, and bake at a very moderate heat till they are a light, delicate brown or fawn color
13. Take out and set to cool, and take away any excess of wafer paper that may cling to the edges
14. Keep in a dry place

title: Macedoine of Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Lemon Ice Cream
- The Juice of 3 Lemons
- ½ Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract
- 2 Ounces of Stoned Cherries
- 2 Ounces of Pineapple, Cut in Dice
- 2 Ounces of Strawberries
- 2 Ounces of Grapes
- 2 Ounces of Peaches
- 2 Ounces of Apricots
- 2 Ounces of Oranges
- 1 Ounce of Candied Cherries
- 1 Ounce of Angelica
- 1 Ounce of Candied Pineapple
- 1 Gill of Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Prepare a quart of Lemon Ice Cream
2. Add to this a half teaspoonful of vanilla, 2 ounces each of stoned cherries, pineapple cut into dice shape, 2 ounces of strawberries, 2 ounces of grapes, apricots, peaches and grapes cut into small pieces, and the candied fruits in equal proportions
3. Add a gill of Sherry wine, mix all the fruits lightly
4. Turn into a freezer and proceed to freeze as in Ice Cream
5. Prepare the cream, mix the candied fruits and fresh fruits together; have ready a square ice cream mold, and place one-fourth of the lemon cream at the bottom
6. Divide the fruits into three parts, and place one portion on top of the cream
7. Cover this with another portion of the cream, and lay on top the fruits
8. Again cover with the cream, and lastly with the fruits
9. Fill up the mold with the remaining portion of the cream, cover closely, pack in broken ice and rock salt, and set to freeze for two hours
10. Just before serving have ready a vessel with warm water, lift the mold and wash off rapidly, being very careful to remove all salt and ice
11. Carefully take the Macedoine of Fruits from the mold, turn into a cold dessert dish and serve immediately

title: Madame John's Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 7 Ounces Butter
- The Whites of 16 Eggs, Whipped to a Stiff Froth
- 1 Pound Sifted Flour
- Juice of 1 Orange
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Cream the sugar and butter together till very light.
2. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff snow, after which stir in gradually the flour and one teaspoonful of baking powder.
3. Flavor with the juice of one orange, or with a tablespoonful of Orange Extract, and bake for from thirty to forty-five minutes in a quick oven.
4. Use the broom-wisp test.

title: Madeira Sabayon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered White Sugar
- 2 Gills of Madeira Wine
- The Zest of Half a Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar well together, and when they come to a rich cream set on the stove and whip well for two minutes
2. Add the zest of a lemon or one tablespoonful of a lemon, or one tablespoonful of lemon essence, and drop in gradually the Madeira wine
3. Stir without ceasing for two minutes more, and then serve very hot with Fruit, Bread Puddings, etc.

title: Madeira Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Gills of Espagnole Sauce or Brown Sauce
- 1 Gill of Truffles, Cut in Two
- 1 Gill of Mushrooms, Cut in Two
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Make a Sauce Espagnole and let it boil for about five minutes
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, and the mushrooms and truffles, cut in pieces
3. Let them boil for ten minutes, and then stir in the wine
4. If you have not the Madeira, use Sherry wine
5. Serve with Filet of Beef Roasted, etc.

title: Madeira Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sugar
- ¼ Grated Nutmeg
- ½ Cup of Butter
- ½ Cup of Boiling Water
- 2 Wineglasses of Madeira

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and the sugar to a cream, using powdered sugar, and then add one cup of boiling water
2. Stir in gradually two wineglasses of good Madeira wine or Sherry, and add one teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
3. Put into a double boiler and stir until the sauce is hot, but do not let it boil
4. Serve, either hot or cold, with any pudding

title: Madeleines
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Eggs
- 0.75 Pound of Powdered Sugar
- 0.75 Pound of Sifted Flour
- 0.75 Pound of Butter
- 0.25 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- Grated Rind of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream and add the yolks of the eggs, the lemon rind and the vanilla, and beat till very, very light
2. Then add the flour, beating constantly, and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Turn into buttered tins, and bake in a quick oven till a delicate brown
4. Serve cold

title: Maigre Oyster Gombo
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 oysters with their juice
- one large onion
- a tablespoonful of flour
- red pepper
- a large spoonful of butter
- one or two tablespoonfuls of filee

## Directions

1. Take 100 oysters with their juice, and one large onion;
2. slice the onion into hot lard and fry it brown, adding when brown a tablespoonful of flour and red pepper.
3. When thick enough pour in the oysters.
4. Boil together twenty minutes.
5. Stir in a large spoonful of butter and one or two tablespoonfuls of filee;
6. then take the soup from the fire and serve with rice.

title: Maigre Shrimp Gombo For Lent
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of shrimps
- three dozen okra pods
- two onions
- a pod of pepper
- a little parsley
- half a pound of dry cod-fish (soaked and chopped fine)

## Directions

1. Boil a pint of shrimps in a quart of water; give them only one boil up;
2. then set them to drain and cool, reserving the water they were boiled in.
3. Chop up three dozen okra pods, two onions, a pod of pepper, and a little parsley, and fry them brown in a little lard or butter;
4. add to the okra the shrimps and the strained water in which they were boiled.
5. Let all boil for an hour, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. When shrimp and crabs can not be procured, half a pound of dry cod-fish, soaked an hour or two, and chopped fine, will do very well.
7. All gombo should be thickened with a little flour—browned if preferred—and stirred in just before adding the water; then boil an hour.

title: Mais Tac-Tac
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Louisiana Molasses
- 3/4 Pound of Indian Corn (Parched)

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of Louisiana molasses
2. As it comes to the boiling point, throw in about three-quarters of a pound of parched Indian corn, parched to a blossom
3. Stir well
4. Pour into little paper cases, about five or six inches in length, three in width and one and a half in depth
5. Let these cool before touching

title: Maitre d'Hotel Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- The Juice of 1/2 a Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Pint of Consommé
- yolk of one egg, well beaten

## Directions

1. Put the butter and the flour in the saucepan and let them blend without burning
2. Mix well over a slow fire, and add one pint of consommé
3. Add the juice of half a lemon and the chopped parsley, and let all boil about fifteen minutes
4. When it reaches this point take off the stove and add the yolk of one egg, well beaten
5. Mix well, stirring round, and serve with boiled fish, etc.
6. Never add egg while the sauce is on the fire, as it will curdle immediately

title: Maizena Blanc Mange
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a pound of maizena
- three pints of milk
- quarter of a pound of white sugar
- lemon rind
- cream or stewed fruit for serving

## Directions

1. Put two and a half pints of the milk on to boil
2. Wet the maizena with the remaining half pint of milk
3. When the milk boils add a quarter of a pound of white sugar and some lemon rind, sliced or grated
4. Let this boil a little, then stir in the mixed maizena
5. When cooked five minutes, pour it into moulds or bowls
6. Wet the bowls first with cold water to prevent the jelly sticking to the sides
7. When firm and cold, eat it with cream or any kind of stewed fruit

title: Maizena Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of powdered sugar
- six eggs
- one pound of corn starch

## Directions

1. Rub half a pound of butter to a cream with one pound of powdered sugar
2. Beat six eggs lightly
3. Add one pound of corn starch
4. Flavor and bake in small patty pans

title: Mallard Ducks or Canvasback Ducks Roasted
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Wild Ducks
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean the ducks as you would a chicken, without scalding, however, rinse out the inside and wipe well inside and out with a wet towel.
2. Do not wash the duck unless you have broken the gall bladder, as the washing destroys their flavor.
3. Rub the inside well with salt and pepper, and rub outside as thoroughly.
4. Place a three-inch lump of butter on the inside.
5. Truss nicely and place the ducks in a baking pan, and brush the tops with melted butter.
6. Pour over two tablespoonfuls of water, and set in a very hot oven, and allow them to bake twenty minutes, if they are not very large, and thirty minutes, if larger than the ordinary size of Canvasback ducks.
7. A wild duck is never cooked dry. It must reach the point where the blood will not run if the flesh is pierced with the fork in carving.
8. When done, place the ducks in a very hot dish, and serve with their own gravy poured over them.
9. Garnish nicely with parsley or water cress.
10. Serve with Currant Jelly.
11. Always have the plates very hot in which you serve the ducks at table.

title: Mandarin Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream or Milk
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- The Grated Zest of 2 Mandarins
- The Juice of 1 Dozen Mandarins, With the Grated Pulp

## Directions

1. Peel the mandarins, grate the rind or zest of two very fine, extract the juice of twelve, and after taking out the seeds, mash the inner fruit or pulp through a sieve
2. Put the milk or cream on to boil
3. Beat the yolks of eggs and sugar till very light, and beat the whites to a stiff froth
4. Take the milk from the fire and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring all the while
5. Set to cool, then add the whites of the eggs, and finally the grated mandarin pulp, zest and juice, and mix well
6. Turn all into a freezer and proceed as in the directions given for making Ice Cream

title: Mangoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- mangoes
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- alum
- cabbage leaves
- 1 lb. ginger
- 1 lb. grated horse radish
- 1/2 lb. garlic
- 2 ounces black mustard seed
- 2 ounces white mustard seed
- 2 ounces celery seed
- 2 ounces ground mace
- 2 ounces ground cloves
- small cucumber
- long white pepper
- olive oil
- worsted string
- whole red pepper
- brown sugar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Put the mangoes into salt and water and let them remain for nine days
2. Split them and take out the insides
3. Put them in a kettle and cover them with vinegar and water
4. Put in a lump of alum the size of a hickory nut
5. Put cabbage leaves around them
6. Put them on the stove where they will keep warm, but not boil, and keep them there until green
7. Take 1 lb. of ginger, crack it but don't powder it fine, 1 lb. of grated horse radish, 1/2 lb. of garlic, 2 ounces of black mustard seed, 2 ounces of white mustard seed, 2 ounces of celery seed, 2 ounces of ground mace, 2 ounces of ground cloves
8. In each mango put a small cucumber and a long white pepper
9. Mix the stuffing with olive oil
10. Fill the mangoes and tie them with a worsted string
11. Pour boiling vinegar over them two or three times
12. Put a whole red pepper in each jar
13. Fill the spaces with cucumbers
14. Put a little unmixed mustard and brown sugar in the top of each jar
15. Seal them up and put them away for a year or more

title: Manque
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 8 Eggs
- The Whites of 3 Eggs, Beaten to a Froth
- % Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Flour
- The Grated Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter and yolks of the eggs to a very light cream, and then add the grated zest of the lemon.
2. Gradually add the flour, which you will have mingled and sifted with the baking powder.
3. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Beat lightly and quickly, and place in a buttered cake pan, and bake for thirty minutes in a moderately quick oven.

title: Manzoni
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 8 Eggs
- The Whites of 3 Eggs, Beaten to a Froth
- 1/4 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Flour
- The Grated Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter and yolks of the eggs to a very light cream, and then add the grated zest of the lemon
2. Gradually add the flour, which you will have mingled and sifted with the baking powder
3. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Beat lightly and quickly, and place in a buttered cake pan, and bake for thirty minutes in a moderately quick oven

title: Maraschino Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 8 Eggs
- A Gill of Any Kind of Maraschino
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and take from the fire
2. Add the sugar and the yolks of the eggs, beaten to a thick cream
3. Set to cool
4. Add a gill of Maraschino and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Turn into a mold and freeze as directed

title: Marble Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of four eggs
- one cup of white sugar
- half a cup of butter
- half a cup of sweet milk
- one teaspoonful of cream tartar
- half a teaspoonful of soda
- yolks of four eggs
- one cup of brown sugar
- half a cup of molasses
- half a cup of sour milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- spices to suit the taste

## Directions

1. Make white part with egg whites, white sugar, butter, sweet milk, cream tartar and soda
2. make black part with yolks, brown sugar, molasses, butter, sour milk, soda and spices
3. put first black, then white dough, until all is in
4. bake

title: Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- % Cup of Butter
- % Cup of Milk
- 1% Cups of White Pulverized Sugar
- 2% Cups of Flour
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Extract of Lemon
- % Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Brown Sugar
- The yolks of 4 Eggs
- % Cup of Milk
- 1% Cups of Flour
- 2 Ounces of Melted Chocolate
- 1 Teaspoonful Vanilla
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cloves (if desired)
- 1 Teaspoonful Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, and gradually add the sugar, beaten thoroughly till very light.
2. Then add the milk, and stir carefully.
3. Add one-half of the flour, evenly, divided.
4. Beat vigorously, and add the Lemon Extract.
5. Mix well, and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth, and the remainder of the flour.
6. Then stand this mixture to one side while you make the dark part of the cake.
7. Beat the butter and the sugar to a cream, till very light, and then add the beaten yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light.
8. Add the milk, and stir carefully.
9. Then add the flour, and blend and beat till smooth.
10. Dissolve the chocolate in a little of the milk, and add, mixing well, and then add the vanilla essence and the baking powder to the mixture.
11. Add the baking powder to the first mixture, and mix thoroughly and lightly.
12. Then grease a piece of brown paper, and line the cake pan, and put in first a spoonful of the white mixture and then one of the dark, and continue alternating thus till all is used.
13. Set in a moderate oven, and bake for three-quarters of an hour.
14. Try the broom-straw test, and if it comes out clean and dry, the cake is done.
15. If not, bake a quarter of an hour longer.
16. The cake must cool in the pan in which it was baked.

title: Marble Cake (Dark Part)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of molasses
- 2 cups of brown sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- yolks of 7 eggs
- 2 tablespoons of cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon of allspice
- 1 nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons of cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoon of soda
- 4 cups of flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients for dark part

title: Marble Cake (Light Part)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of white sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of cream, either sweet or sour
- 2 teaspoons of cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoon of soda
- 3 1/2 cups of flour
- whites of 7 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients for light part
2. Alternate layers of light and dark
3. Draw a knife through 2 or 3 times
4. Bake

title: Marble Cake—Black Part
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- one cup of brown sugar
- half a cup of molasses
- half a cup butter
- half a cup of sour milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- plenty of all kinds of spices to suit the taste

## Directions

1. Put first black, then white, dough, until all is in
2. then bake

title: Marbled Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 cochineals
- a wineglass of water
- a small pinch of soda
- sponge cake or white cake

## Directions

1. Soak cochineals in a wineglass of water, adding a small pinch of soda
2. Pour the cake into the pan for baking
3. At the same time pour with it this water, mixing it well with the cake

title: Marbled Glacées
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Cup of Mixed Preserved Fruits

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake as directed in Biscuits Glacés aux Confitures recipe.
2. Place on the baking sheet.
3. Drop different-sized small bits of fine preserves, orange, apricot, damson, red currant, etc., on the biscuit, and spread smoothly, so as to form a beautifully marbled pattern.
4. Cut out the cakes in any fanciful design.
5. Cover with Transparent Icing.

title: Marlborough Apple Tarts, Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen tart apples
- sugar
- melted butter
- juice and grated rind of two lemons
- a cup of milk
- four eggs
- half a nutmeg
- pastry
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Quarter, and stew a dozen tart apples
2. To each teacup of this pulp, rubbed through a sieve, add a teacup of sugar, half a cup of melted butter, the juice and grated rind of two lemons, a cup of milk, four eggs and half a nutmeg
3. Beat all together and bake in pans lined with pastry, with a rim of puff paste around the edge

title: Marlborough Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of grated apples
- half a pound of fine white sugar
- half a pound of butter
- six eggs
- the peel of one lemon grated
- the strained juice of two lemons
- pie paste

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of grated apples, half a pound of fine white sugar, half a pound of butter, six eggs well beaten, the peel of one lemon grated, and the strained juice of two
2. Line the dish with pie paste, put the pudding in, and bake in a quick oven

title: Marmalade
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe fruit (peaches, quinces, apples, oranges or cranberries)
- Three quarters of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit
- A little water (half a cup to a pound)

## Directions

1. Boil fruit to a pulp with a little water
2. Crush the fruit
3. Put in three quarters of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, add a little water (half a cup to a pound)
4. Boil until it is a jellied mass
5. When done, put it in glass or white earthenware

title: Marrowfat Peas
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- marrowfat peas
- water
- milk
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil the marrowfat peas in water
2. When soft, pour off the water
3. Fill up with milk, or milk and water
4. Boil a little longer
5. Season with butter, pepper and salt
6. Thicken with a teaspoon of flour stirred in among the peas

title: Marsh-Mallow Paste
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of gum arabic
- one pint of water
- half a pound of fine sugar
- whites of four eggs
- powdered starch
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Dissolve gum arabic in water; strain it
2. Add sugar and place over the fire, stirring constantly till the sugar is dissolved and all is the consistency of honey
3. Add gradually the whites of four eggs, well beaten
4. Stir the mixture till it becomes somewhat thin and does not adhere to the finger
5. Pour all into a pan slightly dusted with powdered starch
6. Flavor to the taste, just before pouring out to cool
7. When cool divide into small squares

title: Marshmallow Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of White Gum Arabic
- 1 Pint of Water
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Extract of Vanilla
- 1/2 Pound of the Finest White Sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve the Gum Arabic in one pint of water and strain, and add one-half pound of the finest white sugar, and place over the fire, stirring constantly all the time until the syrup is dissolved
2. Then gradually add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Stir in the mixture until it becomes thin and does not adhere to the fingers
4. Then flavor with the Vanilla Extract, and drop in tablets of about the size of a five-cent piece upon a sheet dusted with powdered starch
5. Make the shape of the tablets slightly convex, and in two hours remove from the papers, dry for a few minutes in the stove and put away for use

title: Maryland Biscuit
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- lump of butter or lard
- new milk

## Directions

1. Take any quantity of flour the family may require, put in salt and lump of butter or lard, rub it well in the flour
2. Then moisten it with new milk, work it well, and beat it with a rolling pin until perfectly light
3. Bake rather slowly a light brown

title: Mashed Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Parsnips
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the parsnips until so tender that they break easily under pressure.
2. Mash them well, after draining off all water.
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan, and add a tablespoonful of flour.
4. Blend, without browning, and add a half Pint of milk or cream.
5. Stir well, and as the mixture begins to boil, add the parsnips.
6. Mix thoroughly, season with salt and pepper, and serve in a dish, heaping up the parsnips in pyramidal shape.
7. Serve with veal cutlets.

title: Mashed Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash and pare the turnips, and cut them into quarters, and cover with boiling water and boil until very tender, which will be in about thirty to forty-five minutes, according to the tenderness of the turnips.
2. If you boil them whole it will take an hour and a quarter.
3. If you cut the turnips as soon as tender take out of the water and drain through a colander of all water.
4. Then press them lightly, to squeeze all remaining water out, and hash well.
5. Add a tablespoonful of butter, salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
6. Serve with roast meats or roast ducks.

title: Massepains (Plain)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Bitter Almonds
- The Whites of 4 Eggs
- 1½ Pounds of Sifted Sugar

## Directions

1. Scald, skin, wash and dry the almonds.
2. Then pound them to a flour in a mortar with the whites of the eggs.
3. When well-pulverized, beat well.
4. Mix in the sugar by pounding it, and then take up the paste and put it in a bowl.
5. Beat well, keeping it very firm. If soft, add sugar, to render firmer.
6. Strew the wafer sheet, spread on a baking sheet, with white pulverized sugar.
7. Cut the massepains into lengths of two or three inches, and twist them into fancy shapes, diamonds, hearts, rings, triangles, etc., on the wafer sheet.
8. Then set in the oven, and bake to a very light color.

title: Massepains Soufflés
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Bitter Almonds
- 1½ Pounds of Sifted Sugar
- 1 Ounce of Vanilla Sugar
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 White of Egg Royal Icing

## Directions

1. Prepare the paste exactly as above, and when you take it up in the bowl add the Royal Icing, keeping the paste firm.
2. Spread the massepains upon wafer sheets, using only the ring shape, and bake in a slack oven till a very light color.

title: Matelote d'Anguille
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of eel, or a 2-pound fish
- 1 large onion
- 1 pint of button onions
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 glass of good Claret
- 1 can of mushrooms
- 1 pint of oyster water
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 sprig of thyme
- Croutons to garnish
- Parsley
- Cayenne and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eel, using about two pounds.
2. Cut in one inch or two-inch pieces, as desired.
3. Take one large onion and a half pint of button onions, or a clove of garlic, and chop fine.
4. Make a roux with one tablespoonful of lard, adding, when hot, the chopped onions and parsley, and gradually adding, when these begin to brown, the two tablespoonfuls of flour.
5. Pour in one good glass of Claret and a can of mushrooms.
6. Add a pint of oyster stock or oyster water, and let cook a while.
7. Season to taste with Cayenne and salt, adding bay leaf and thyme.
8. When it boils, add the eels that have been thoroughly cleansed and placed in cold water and allowed to stand about fifteen minutes.
9. When these are added to the matelote, let it simmer about an hour.
10. Serve with fancy garnish of croutons.

title: Matrimony
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen common sized peaches
- sugar
- 1 quart of cream or rich custard

## Directions

1. Pare and cut into small pieces 2 dozen common sized peaches
2. Cover them thickly with sugar and let them stand 3 or 4 hours
3. Beat them into a quart of cream, or a rich custard
4. If cream, sweeten it before freezing it

title: Mayonnaise
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of 1 raw egg
- 1 teaspoonful of made mustard
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- sweet oil
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar
- a little red pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. The mustard and salt to be well rubbed together
2. add the egg
3. pour on very slowly the sweet oil, rubbing hard all the time, till as much is made as is wanted
4. add a tablespoonful of vinegar
5. when these ingredients are mixed, they should look perfectly smooth
6. if it curdles, add a little more mustard or a little vinegar
7. With shrimps or oysters, a little red pepper rubbed in is an improvement

title: Mayonnaise de Saumon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of salmon
- 2 heads of crisp lettuce
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Sauce a la mayonnaise

## Directions

1. To one can of salmon allow two good sized heads of young lettuce.
2. Make a bed of the crisp hearts of the leaves, and tear the other leaves into small pieces with a fork, making very delicate shreds.
3. Drain the oil from the can of salmon, and separate the fish into flakes.
4. Take a cupful of boiling milk and a tablespoonful of butter and two of flour, and stir over the fire until quite smooth.
5. Add to this a tablespoonful of chopped parsley and the salmon.
6. Season with pepper and salt, and let it cook about ten minutes.
7. Set away to cool.

title: Mayonnaise Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolk of 1 Egg
- Sweet Oil
- Lemon
- Vinegar
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Take the yolk of one fresh egg, raw, and put it in a bowl. The egg and the oil must be cold, and in summer it is well to keep the soup plate in which you make the dressing on cracked ice in a pan, so that the oil will not run.
2. Put the yolk in a plate; add, drop by drop, a little sweet oil from the bottle.
3. When you have dropped about a spoonful being careful to work it into the yolk of the egg drop by drop and blend all the time, take a lemon and drop a few drops into the mixture.
4. It will at once begin to harden as you stir it in.
5. Continue stirring till the egg grows hard, and then steadily, drop by drop, let the oil fall, working it all the time with your fork into the egg.
6. Have another spoon, begin to drop in the lemon juice, working it the same way again till it hardens the egg.
7. Then begin again with the oil and work again, and again drop the lemon till you have the juice of half a lemon and about two gills of oil, finishing with the oil.
8. When the egg begins to curdle, add a little salt, but do not add this salt till the Mayonnaise is complete.
9. Serve very cold, with salads, etc.

title: Mayonnaise of Salmon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of Salmon
- 2 good sized heads of young lettuce
- 1 cupful of boiling milk
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- pepper and salt
- Sauce a la Mayonnaise
- sliced hard-boiled eggs
- celery tips

## Directions

1. Make a bed of the crisp hearts of the lettuce leaves and tear the other leaves into small pieces with a fork, making very delicate shreds
2. Drain the oil from the can of Salmon and separate the fish into flakes
3. Take a cupful of boiling milk and a tablespoonful of butter and two of flour and stir over the fire until quite smooth
4. Add to this a tablespoonful of chopped parsley and the Salmon
5. Season with pepper and salt and let it cook about ten minutes
6. Set away to cool and then place on the bed of lettuce leaves
7. Cover with a Sauce a la Mayonnaise and garnish nicely with sliced hard-boiled eggs and celery tips

title: Mayonnaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolk of 1 Egg
- Sweet Oil
- Lemon
- Vinegar
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Take the yolk of one fresh egg, raw, and put in a bowl
2. The egg and the oil must be cold, and in summer it is well to keep the soup plate in which you make the dressing on cracked ice in a pan, so that the oil will not run
3. Put the yolk in a plate; add, drop by drop, a little sweet oil from the bottle
4. When you have dropped about a spoonful, being careful to work it into the yolk of the egg drop by drop and blend all the time, take a lemon and drop a few drops into the mixture
5. Continue stirring till the egg grows hard, and then steadily, drop by drop, let the oil fall, working it all the time with your fork into the egg
6. Have another spoon, begin to drop in the lemon juice, working it the same way again till it hardens the egg
7. Then begin again with the oil and work again, and again drop the lemon till you have the juice of half a lemon and about two gills of oil, finishing with the oil
8. When the egg begins to curdle, add a little salt, but do not add this salt till the mayonnaise is complete
9. Serve very cold, with salads, etc.
10. When making for large salads, as a garnish use three yolks of eggs and other ingredients in proportion

title: Meat Balls (Boulards)
body:
## Ingredients

- Several Slices of Meat (Raw or Cold Cooked)
- 1 Tomato
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Cup of Cracker Crumbs
- A Pinch of Ginger
- 1/2 Cup of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select slices of beef cut very thin from the round of the cross rib.
2. Take one tomato, one onion, one carrot, a stalk of celery, several sprigs of parsley, one bay leaf, a sprig of thyme, three hard-boiled eggs, and chop very fine.
3. Mix this with one tablespoonful of butter, a half cup of cracker crumbs and a pinch of ginger.
4. Salt and pepper to taste.
5. Take each slice of meat, and make a roll of it, folding the dressing within and folding over the edges that it may be retained.
6. Tie each with thin twine.
7. Have boiling lard or suet on the fire, drop in the boulards rolled in bread crumbs, set them back on the stove, cover well, and let them simmer gently for about two hours, adding a half cup of water to prevent scorching.
8. Keep the pot covered.
9. After two hours, drain the boulards well by laying them on heated brown paper.
10. Place them in a hot dish, garnish it with sliced hard-boiled eggs, parsley and olives, and serve.

title: Meat Balls. Boulettes.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of raw or left-over meat
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 onion
- juice of a lemon
- salt
- pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Take one pound of steak from the upper round and mince and chop very fine.
2. Add to it one tablespoonful of lemon juice, one onion (well grated), one tablespoonful of melted butter, salt, pepper and Cayenne to taste.

title: Meat Soufflé (Souffle de Boeuf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cold Meat
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Cup of Cold Milk
- 2 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put two level tablespoonfuls of butter into a frying pan, and when it is hot add two tablespoonfuls of flour, rubbing smoothly and letting it brown.
2. Then add gradually one cup of cold milk.
3. Stir this until it boils.
4. Add one-half teaspoonful of salt; a little pepper and one cupful of chopped meat, or fowl, that has been left over.
5. When this comes to a boil, add the yolk of two beaten eggs.
6. Let it cook a moment longer and set to cool.
7. Then beat the whites of the eggs and when the meat mixture is cold, fold them in carefully.
8. Turn this into a buttered dish and bake in a moderate oven twenty minutes.
9. Serve as soon as removed from the fire.

title: Melon Mangoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- late small smooth green melons
- salt
- water
- sliced horseradish
- very small cucumbers
- nasturtiums
- small white onions
- mustard seed
- whole pepper
- cloves
- allspice
- vinegar
- coarse needle and thread

## Directions

1. Get late small smooth green melons not larger than a teacup
2. Cut out a piece from the stem end large enough to allow you to take the seeds from the inside
3. Scrape out all the soft part and cover with the piece cut out
4. Lay them in rows in a stone or wooden vessel
5. Make a strong brine of salt and water and pour it over the melons
6. Let them remain in the brine twenty-four hours
7. Scald the pickles and cull them
8. Rinse the melons in cold water and wipe each one dry
9. Fill each melon with a cucumber, one or two small onions, sliced horseradish and mustard seed
10. Put on the piece belonging to it and sew it with a coarse needle and thread
11. Lay them in a stone pot or wooden vessel with the cut side up
12. Strew cloves and pepper over them
13. Make vinegar enough to cover them boiling hot and put it over them
14. Cover with a folded towel and let them stand one night
15. Drain off the vinegar, make it hot again and pour it on
16. Repeat this scalding four or five times until the mangoes are a fine green
17. If you wish to keep them till the next summer, choose the most firm, put in a jar and cover with cold fresh vinegar
18. Tie thick paper over them

title: Meringue Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Six large, nice apples
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 6 eggs
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- whites of 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Stew 6 large, nice apples, and mash through a sieve
2. Mix in 1 lb. of butter and 1 lb. of sugar
3. Add 6 eggs beaten very light
4. Add the juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
5. Bake the pudding
6. Save out the whites of 3 eggs, beat them up with 3 tablespoonfuls of sifted sugar to a stiff froth
7. After the pudding is baked and nearly cold, lay the sugar and egg over the top
8. Set it in a cool oven for a few minutes, until it is crusted, but not browned

title: Meringue Pudding. Very Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of bread crumbs
- a quart of milk
- four eggs
- a cup of sugar
- a lump of butter (the size of an egg)
- peach or nutmeg for flavoring
- acid preserve or jelly, such as plums, apples, grapes, or currants
- a full cup of white sugar
- lemon extract

## Directions

1. Make one pint of milk boiling hot, pour it over the bread crumbs, and beat it smooth.
2. When cool, add a cup of sugar, and the yolks of the four eggs; also a lump of butter (the size of an egg).
3. Beat all well together, thin it by adding the rest of the milk, flavor it with peach or nutmeg, and set it in the oven to bake.
4. Bake only long enough to cook the eggs.
5. When slightly brown on top, take the pudding out of the stove, and set it to cool.
6. When cool, spread over it a layer of acid preserve or jelly.
7. Make an icing or meringue of the whites of the eggs, beaten up with a full cup of white sugar; flavor this with lemon extract.
8. Put the pudding again in the stove, and brown.

title: Meringues
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 10 eggs
- 10 tablespoonfuls of sifted crushed sugar, very finely powdered
- glaizing sugar
- delicate preserve
- flavoring as desired

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites to a stiff froth, adding the sugar slowly
2. When well beaten and quite stiff, put it in the form of a large egg on paper
3. Ornament with the glaizing sugar
4. Lay the paper on a tin in a moderate oven
5. When of a light brown, take them out
6. Remove from beneath all that which is not cooked with a spoon
7. Place them in the oven when cold to dry
8. Put any kind of delicate preserve in each and flavor as fancied

title: Military Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Bottle of Claret
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1 Bottle of Seltzer Water
- The Juice and Rind of 3 Lemons
- 1 Cordial Glass of Benedictine
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Cordial Glass of Cherry Bounce
- A Few Strawberries or French Cherries

## Directions

1. Take one bottle of claret and pour into a punch bowl with two quarts of water
2. Add one bottle of Seltzer water and a half pound of sugar and the juice and rind of three lemons
3. Stir all well and add a dash of old French Cognac, a cordial glass of Benedictine and of Cherry Bounce, and put sufficient ice in the bowl to make the Punch very cold
4. Serve with a few Strawberries or French Cherries in each glass and place a sprig of fresh young mint on top

title: Milk Biscuit or Rolls
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of milk
- half a pound of butter
- nearly two quarts of flour
- two eggs
- a cup of yeast

## Directions

1. Warm a pint of milk and half a pound of butter
2. Pour this into nearly two quarts of flour
3. Add two eggs and a cup of yeast
4. Knead it very well
5. Make into round balls
6. Flatten each one on the palm of your hand and prick it with a fork
7. Bake

title: Milk Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of good butter
- 1 pint of new milk, warmed
- salt
- 1 wineglass of good yeast
- 3 eggs beaten
- grated nutmeg
- 1 lb. of flour

## Directions

1. Stir this into the flour, mix well, and add a wineglass of good yeast, 3 eggs beaten, and grated nutmeg
2. Set it to rise
3. When risen, sift on the board 1 lb. of flour
4. Mix all together, make it into cakes, and bake quickly

title: Milk Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Flour
- ½ Teacupful of Yeast or ½ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 1 Pint of Fresh Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Ounces of Butter

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, and then turn it into the bread trough or pan
2. Add immediately the butter and the salt
3. When it becomes tepid add the yeast and about three-quarters of a pint or half a pint of flour, using always good judgment in adding sufficient, more or less, to make a thick batter
4. Beat thoroughly till air bubbles accumulate all over the surface of the batter
5. Cover and set in a warm place over night
6. Very early in the morning work in the rest of the flour, and take it out of the pan and put on a board and knead quickly and lightly until the dough is perfectly smooth and does not cling to the hands or the board
7. Put it back in the trough or pan and cover with a cloth, and set to rise again in a warm place
8. When it has increased to twice its bulk, turn again upon the board, and then divide the dough into two parts
9. Mold these lightly and gently into small loaves, and stand away to rise for about half an hour
10. Set in the oven, which should be moderately quick, and bake for three-quarters of an hour

title: Milk of Almonds
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of almonds
- 1 ounce of bitter almonds
- 8 ounces of loaf sugar
- 3 pints of filtered river water
- 1/2 gill of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Blanch and peel the almonds, and soak them in cold water for several hours
2. Pound them thoroughly into a pulp, adding as you pound a half gill of orange flower water
3. Put this into an earthen vessel with eight ounces of the finest and whitest loaf sugar
4. Add three pints of clear, filtered water, and then stir all well together
5. Return to the mortar
6. Pound well and strain
7. Stir well and set the vessel on ice
8. At the end of an hour it will be ready for use

title: Milk Porridge
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk
- a tablespoonful of flour
- cold milk
- a little salt
- loaf sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make a quart of milk boiling hot
2. make a tablespoonful of flour into a batter with cold milk
3. add a little salt and stir it in the boiling milk
4. stirring it constantly for five minutes while it boils
5. flavor with anything agreeable
6. Sweetened with loaf sugar, and nutmeg grated plentifully over it

title: Milk Porridge
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Indian Meal
- 1 Spoon of White Flour
- 3 Cups of Milk
- 1¼ Cups of Boiling Water
- A Pinch of Salt

## Directions

1. Bring the flour to a paste with a little cold water, and also scald the meal with a little hot water.
2. Have the water boiling in the proportion given above; add the meal, and then add the flour, stirring constantly.
3. Let it boil for about twenty minutes, and add the pinch of salt and the milk, stirring almost constantly.
4. Let all cook for ten minutes more, stirring often.
5. Serve while hot, with sugar and milk.

title: Milk Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- A Glass of Good Rich Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy or Whisky
- A Small Quantity of Crushed Ice

## Directions

1. Dissolve the whisky or brandy and sugar together
2. Mix well, and pour over the milk
3. Add a small quantity of crushed ice and serve

title: Milk Punch as a Restorative
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped ice
- a large tablespoonful of white sugar
- a wineglass of gin, rum or brandy
- fresh milk

## Directions

1. half fill a large tumbler (it should hold a pint) with chopped ice
2. add to it a large tablespoonful of white sugar
3. beat it a little with the ice
4. pour on it a wineglass of gin, rum or brandy
5. fill up with fresh milk

title: Milk Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart fresh milk
- 2 cups white sugar
- whites of 2 eggs
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Put in the freezer
2. When it begins to congeal, add the juice of 3 lemons

title: Mince Meat
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh beef tongue, boiled and chopped very fine
- 2 lbs. of suet, picked and chopped fine
- 4 lbs. of raisins
- 2 lbs. of Sultana plums
- 3 lbs. of currants, washed and dried
- 2 lbs. of white sugar
- 1 pint of brandy
- 1 bottle of wine
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- mace
- allspice
- apples

## Directions

1. Boil and chop beef tongue very fine
2. Combine with suet, raisins, plums, currants, sugar, brandy, wine, nutmeg, cinnamon, mace, and allspice
3. Mix ingredients well together and keep in a closely covered jar
4. The day you bake the pie, add a few apples cut up very fine
5. Line the dish with light paste, covering the top with the same and bake in a moderate oven

title: Mince Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Beef or Mutton Suet
- 1 Pounds of Apples, Pared and Chopped
- 2 Pounds of Raisins, Seeded and Chopped
- 1 Pound of Sultana Raisins, Picked and Washed
- 2 Pounds of Currants, Picked and Washed
- 1 Pound of Citron, Cut Very Fine
- 3/4 Pound of Lemon Peel, Grated Very Fine
- 2 Pounds of Brown Sugar
- Pound of Candied Lemon Peel, if desired
- 2 Grated Nutmegs
- 1 Tablespoontul Each Of Ground Cloves and Allspice
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Ground Mace
- 1 Tablespoontul of Fine Salt
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Ground Cinnamon
- 1 Quart of Good Sherry or Madeira
- 1 Quart of the Best Brandy
- The Juice of Two Lemons
- The Juice of 2 Oranges, and the Rind, Grated Fine

## Directions

1. The meat should be good and lean, and should be boiled the day before needed, or early in the morning, and allowed to cool
2. In boiling the meat, plunge it into boiling (not cold) water, for the boiling water enables it to retain its juices
