3. Boil steadily till tender, then stand away to cool
4. Chop the meat very fine, and clear away all gristle and skin
5. Shred the suet, and chop, or rather mince it very fine
6. Pare and core the apples, and chop very fine
7. Stone the raisins, and cut the citron fine
8. Grate the orange and lemon peel very fine
9. Mix the meat and suet together, chopping again, and add all the ingredients, mixing well together
10. Add the juices of the lemon and orange, and the grated rinds, and then mix all well again, and pack in a stone jar
11. Pour over this the quart of brandy, and the wine, and cover very tightly, and set in a very cool place
12. When ready to use the meat, dish out the quantity you desire, and thin to the desired consistency with good Sherry or Madeira wine

title: Mince Meat for Pies
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of beef chopped fine
- one peck of apples
- two pounds of raisins
- two pounds of currants
- one pound of citron
- one-half pound of suet
- three pounds of sugar
- powdered cinnamon, cloves and nutmeg, a spoonful each
- a bottle of champagne cider

## Directions

1. Moisten with a bottle of champagne cider
2. When you bake the pies, place a spoonful of butter on each pie
3. do not put butter in the jar with the meat

title: Mince Meat, for Christmas Pies
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a fresh beef tongue
- one pound of suet
- one-half peck of apples
- two pounds of currants picked and washed carefully
- one pound of citron sliced
- half an ounce each of powdered cloves, allspice, cinnamon and ginger
- three pints of cider
- half a pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Boil a fresh beef tongue tender, let it get cold, then chop it fine
2. add one pound of suet, one-half peck of apples, two pounds of currants picked and washed carefully, one pound of citron sliced, half an ounce each of powdered cloves, allspice, cinnamon and ginger, three pints of cider, with half a pint of brandy
3. sweeten to taste, then pack away in a crock
4. Keep it cool, or it will ferment
5. Add apples when you bake the pie

title: Mince Pie Meat
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice tender piece of beef which is free from gristle, skin or strings
- hot water

## Directions

1. Put the meat in hot water, enough to cover it
2. boil it gently until turning a fork in it will break it
3. set it to become cold, then take out all the bone and gristle parts
4. If the tongue is used peel off the skin, chop it very fine

title: Mince Pie Without Meat
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of currants
- one pound of peeled and chopped apples
- one pound of suet chopped fine
- one pound of moist brown sugar
- quarter of a pound of chopped and stoned raisins
- the juice of four oranges and two lemons
- the peel of one lemon chopped
- a wine-glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Mix all carefully and put in a cool place
2. Eat this pie hot, and when it is baked, put in a tablespoonful of butter, but put none in the mixture

title: Mince Pie. How to Fill and Bake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice puff paste
- pie mixture
- butter

## Directions

1. Line a pie dish with a nice puff paste, rolled to twice the thickness of a dollar piece
2. Put in the pie mixture half an inch deep, and spread it to within a finger width of the edge
3. roll out a puff paste crust, turn a plate the size of the one on which the pie is made on to it
4. with a knife cut the paste around the edge of the plate
5. then take the plate off, make three small incisions with the end of the knife on each side of the middle
6. take it carefully up and cover the pie with it
7. press it lightly with the finger against the bottom crust
8. put it in a quick oven for three-quarters of an hour

title: Mince Pies Without Meat
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of the best apples, cored and minced
- 3 lbs. of raisins stoned and minced
- 3 lbs. of hard boiled eggs, cut very fine
- 3 lbs. of powdered sugar
- ½ ounce of salt
- ½ ounce of cinnamon
- ½ ounce of nutmeg
- ½ ounce of mace
- 8 finely powdered cloves
- grated peel of 4 lemons
- juice of 2 lemons
- ½ pint of brandy
- ½ pint of wine
- 4 lbs. of currants
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together and put into a deep pan
2. Have ready, washed and dried, currants and as you make the pies add them with candied fruit

title: Minced Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Cold Minced Lamb
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 4 Ounces of Butter
- 1/2 Pint of Veal Stock
- 1 1/2 Gills of Cream
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 chopped onion
- 1 tablespoonful of blended flour
- Croûtons (buttered)

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a frying pan
2. Add one chopped onion and brown slightly
3. Add the mushrooms, and season to taste
4. Add the half pint of veal broth, if you have it; if not, boiling water or milk, and let it simmer a few minutes
5. Thicken with a tablespoonful of blended flour
6. Add the minced lamb and a gill and a half of cream
7. Let all simmer, stirring constantly
8. When done, which will be in about ten minutes, take off the fire
9. Add the yolks of two eggs, beaten, and stir constantly
10. Place in a hot dish, garnish with Croûtons (buttered) and serve

title: Minced Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Cold Minced Lamb
- ½ Can of Mushrooms
- 4 Ounces of Butter
- ¾ Pint of Veal Stock
- 1½ Gills of Cream
- Yolks of 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a frying pan; add one chopped onion and brown slightly; add the mushrooms, and season to taste.
2. Then add the half pint of veal broth, if you have it; if not, boiling water or milk, and let it simmer a few minutes.
3. Thicken with a tablespoonful of blended flour.

title: Minced Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Gill of Oyster Juice
- A Half Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Gill of White Wine
- 3 Yolks of Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Gill of Rich Cream
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Parsley and Chives and Thyme, chopped fine

## Directions

1. Scald the oysters in their own water.
2. Drain and mince, but not too fine.
3. Put into the saucepan a tablespoonful of butter, and, when melted, add the parsley (chopped fine), the herbs and the mushrooms.
4. Then add the flour, which has been rubbed smoothly in a gill of oyster juice, and, after it stews about five minutes, add the white wine; if this is not obtainable, add another gill of oyster juice.
5. Mix thoroughly, and then add the minced oysters, and stew gently until the sauce is absorbed and the mince forms a thick batter.
6. Be very careful not to scorch.
7. Remove from the fire and add in the yolks of the eggs, which have been beaten smoothly, in the cream.
8. Set it back on the fire and let it remain about one minute, and serve on large toasted and buttered Croutons, with garnish of lemon and parsley and olives.

title: Minced Veal and Poached Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cold veal
- half a pint of sauce
- a cup of cream
- pepper
- salt
- six poached eggs
- slices of red tongue or ham

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of cold veal very fine.
2. Boil half a pint of sauce till it begins to thicken or glaze.
3. Add a cup of cream and the minced veal.
4. Season with pepper and salt.
5. When dished put six poached eggs around it, alternately with slices of red tongue or ham.

title: Mint Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Freshly-Gathered Mint
- The Zests of 5 Lemons
- 7 Pints of Fine Old French Cognac
- 2 Drachms of Essence of Peppermint
- 4 Pounds of Sugar
- 2 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Gather the mint very fresh, and wash lightly in fresh water.
2. Cut it very fine, and add the grated zests of the lemons.
3. Pound well in a stone jar, and macerate or steep for eight days in seven pints of good brandy.
4. Then distill it, or boil to one-half, covering the spout of the boiler with a piece of kid in which you have cut small holes.
5. Then add a half drachm of essence of peppermint.
6. Dissolve four pounds of sugar in three pints of water, and let it cool.
7. Then mix it thoroughly with the mint infusion and let it stand for a half hour.
8. Then filter and bottle it, and keep in a cool, shady place.

title: Mint Julep à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cut glass filled with water
- 3 lumps of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of good brandy or whisky
- The juice of half a lemon
- A bit of lemon or orange peel
- 6 sprigs of fresh young mint
- A few ripe strawberries
- Crushed ice
- Crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Take one large cut glass, half fill with water, add six sprigs of fresh mint, three lumps of sugar.
2. Stir well, till the sugar is absorbed.
3. Add a tablespoonful of good brandy or whisky, and stir well.
4. Add a little lemon and orange peel, and the juice of half a lemon, if desired.
5. Fill the glass with crushed ice, and decorate on top with sprigs of mint.
6. Place a few ripe strawberries on top of the mint, sprinkle lightly with crushed sugar, and serve.

title: Mint Juleps Made of Whiskey, Brandy, Gin, etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half tablespoonful of powdered sugar
- one wine-glass of water
- one of whiskey, brandy or gin
- one-half dozen sprigs of mint
- plenty of fine ice
- strawberries and pineapples or any fruit in season

## Directions

1. Use plenty of fine ice
2. decorate with strawberries and pineapples, or any fruit in season

title: Mint Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good handful of mint, chopped very fine
- 1 tablespoonful of tarragon vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of sugar
- 1 pint of white beef stock
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop one good handful of fresh mint and put it in a bowl
2. Add a teaspoonful of tarragon vinegar and one teaspoonful of sugar
3. Add one pint of good white beef stock
4. Mix all together and place in a bain marie or hot-water bath—that is, stand in a saucepan of hot water on the fire and let it warm without boiling
5. Serve with roast lamb

title: Mint Sauce for Spring Lamb
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- spearmint (a cup of tender green)
- sugar (half a cup)
- vinegar (a cup)

## Directions

1. Wash carefully a cup of tender green spearmint, chop it fine, and mix with it half a cup of sugar and a cup of good vinegar.

title: Minute Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Eggs
- 5 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Milk
- The Juice and Rind of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs to a cream, and then add the sugar, beating thoroughly till very, very light.
2. Then blend the flour and milk until smooth, and add.
3. Add the juice of the lemon and the grated zest, and beat well again.
4. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth, turn into a buttered dish and bake fifteen minutes in a quick oven.

title: Mirontous
body:
## Ingredients

- The Left-Over Bouilli
- 3 Large Onions
- 6 Shallots
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Pickles
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Slice the onions fine; brown in one tablespoonful of butter.
2. Chop the shallots and add to the onions, then add the garlic, thyme and bay leaf, all chopped very fine, and season with salt, Cayenne and black pepper to taste.
3. When the whole is browning nicely, add a tablespoonful of flour and water, or left-over broth, sufficient to cover.
4. Season this to taste and then take two pickles, about one finger long, slice very fine, and add.
5. Let all boil about fifteen minutes and then lay the cold bouilli, which has been thickly sliced, in the sauce.
6. Set it to bake in the oven about twenty minutes.
7. Garnish with buttered toast and serve hot.

title: Miss Beecher's Potato Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- flour
- salt
- hot water
- brewers' yeast or home-brewed yeast

## Directions

1. Mash six boiled potatoes
2. Mix in half a coffeecup of flour and two teaspoonfuls of salt
3. Add hot water until it is a batter
4. Beat all well together
5. When it is blood-warm add to it one-half cup of brewers' yeast or a whole cup of home-brewed yeast
6. When this is light, put it in a bottle and cork it tight for use
7. Keep it as cool as possible

title: Miss Lester's Tea Rusk
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of flour
- one half pint of milk
- one quarter of a pound of butter
- two eggs
- mace
- nutmeg
- a cup of yeast

## Directions

1. Add mace, nutmeg and a cup of yeast to the flour, milk, butter and eggs
2. Set it to rise
3. Make up into rusks
4. Bake on buttered tins when light
5. Serve hot

title: Miss Shattuck's Brown Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of rye meal
- two quarts of Indian meal
- two tablespoonfuls of molasses
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix one quart of rye meal, two quarts of Indian meal, and two tablespoonfuls of molasses thoroughly with sweet milk
2. Let it stand two hours
3. Bake in a slow oven

title: Mississippi Corn Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of buttermilk
- two eggs
- three spoonfuls of butter
- a teaspoonful of saleratus
- meal

## Directions

1. Stir meal into the buttermilk until it is as thick as buckwheat batter
2. Add two eggs, three spoonfuls of butter, and a teaspoonful of saleratus
3. Bake in squares about one inch thick
4. It will require half an hour in a hot oven

title: Mixed Fruit Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- black currants
- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix cherries, black currants and raspberries together
2. Dilute the juice and add the usual amount of sugar
3. Let it ferment
4. Bottle it

title: Mixed Marmalade
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of quinces
- 1 peck of pears
- 1 peck of peaches
- 1 peck of apples
- 3 lbs. of good sugar to each peck of fruit

## Directions

1. Pare and cut quinces in small pieces
2. Take the rinds and cores and boil them in water
3. Strain it and boil the quinces till quite soft
4. Add 1 peck of pears
5. When done, add 1 peck of peaches
6. When quite done, add 1 peck of apples
7. Add 3 lbs. of good sugar to each peck of fruit

title: Mixed Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cauliflower
- 1/2 head of cabbage, cut as for slaw
- 1 quart of tiny cucumbers
- 1 quart of small button onions
- 2 or 3 pods of green and red peppers, chopped fine
- 3/4 pound of French mustard
- 1/2 teacupful of celery seed
- 1 teacupful of grated horseradish
- 1 whole garlic, minced very fine
- 1 pint of string beans
- 1 gill of salad oil
- 1 gallon of vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mustard seed
- 1 cupful of brown sugar
- 1/2 ounce of turmeric
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Take young cauliflower heads, small onions, peppers, cucumbers cut in dice, nasturtiums and string beans in the proportions given above, and boil till tender
2. Drain very dry and pack in wide-mouthed bottles
3. Boil in one pint of cider vinegar, one tablespoonful of sugar, half a teaspoonful of salt and two tablespoonfuls of mustard seed and spices
4. Pour over the pickles, mix well, and seal carefully

title: Mocha Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of the Best Mocha Coffee
- The Zest of 1 Orange
- 7 Pints of Brandy
- 4 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Roast the coffee to a light brown without the least trace of burning.
2. Then grind it quickly, while it is hot, and infuse immediately in seven pints of good brandy.
3. Add the zest of the orange, cut very fine, and let it remain thus for six days.
4. Then place in a vessel in a bain-marie, and distill till reduced to one-half.
5. Dissolve the sugar and water over a slow fire, then let it cool, and mix it with the coffee mixture.
6. Filter it, bottle and preserve in a cool, shady place.

title: Mock Charlotte Russe
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half lb. of gelatine
- cold water
- boiling water
- 1½ pints of milk
- yolks of 6 eggs
- vanilla
- whites of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Soak gelatine in a little cold water
2. When soaked, dissolve in ½ pint of boiling water
3. Make sweet custard with 1½ pints of milk and the yolks of 6 eggs
4. Flavor with vanilla
5. Add gelatine to the custard and set in a cool place to congeal
6. When it begins to harden, add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
7. Beat well through the custard
8. Pour into a dish lined with sponge cake

title: Mock Eggs for Turtle Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh egg
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- about a teaspoonfull of butter

## Directions

1. Break and beat thoroughly one fresh egg
2. Take the yolks of three hard-boiled eggs, and rub them into a fine paste with about a teaspoonfull of butter
3. Mix this with the raw egg and roll into pellets of the identical size and shape of the turtle eggs
4. Let them lie in boiling water about two minutes
5. Drop into the soup

title: Mock Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls good preserve
- cream
- 1/2 ounce isinglass
- 1/2 pint water

## Directions

1. Take about 3 tablespoonfuls of some good preserve
2. Rub it through a sieve with as much cream as will fill a quart mould
3. Dissolve 1/2 ounce of isinglass in 1/2 pint of water
4. When almost cold, mix it well with the cream
5. Put it into a mould
6. Set it in a cool place, and turn out the next day

title: Mock Mince Pies. Very Good
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six crackers
- one and a half cups of warm water
- one cup of good brown sugar
- one cup of raisins
- one cup of molasses
- one-half cup of cider or strong vinegar
- half a cup of butter
- a lemon and its rind
- a nutmeg
- one teaspoonful of cloves
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak six crackers in one and a half cups of warm water
2. add to them one cup of good brown sugar, one cup of raisins, one cup of molasses, and one-half cup of cider or strong vinegar
3. Beat in half a cup of butter, season with a lemon and its rind, a nutmeg, one teaspoonful of cloves, and ground cinnamon

title: Mock-Turtle Soup No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- two calf’s feet
- two onions
- a few cloves
- pepper
- allspice
- mace
- sweet herbs
- water
- walnut catsup
- mushroom catsup
- mushroom powder
- mushrooms
- the jelly of the meat
- hard eggs
- force-meat balls
- the juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Put into a pot a knuckle of veal, two calf’s feet, two onions, a few cloves, pepper, allspice, mace and sweet herbs; cover them with water; tie a thick paper over the pot, or cover it close.
2. Let it stew four hours; Remove from the fire and let it cool.
3. When cold take off the fat very nicely, cut the meat and feet into bits an inch square, remove the bones and coarse parts; then place over the fire again to warm.
4. Add a large spoonful of walnut catsup, one of mushroom catsup, a little mushroom powder, or a few mushrooms, and the jelly of the meat.
5. When hot, serve with hard eggs, force-meat balls, and the juice of one lemon.

title: Mock-Turtle Soup. Excellent, No. 3
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf’s head
- water
- beef
- veal
- onions
- carrot
- turnip
- sweet herbs
- tongue of the calf’s head
- brains of the calf’s head
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- sweet basil
- catsup
- lemon juice
- sherry
- eggs balls

## Directions

1. Clean a calf’s head nicely, split it and take out the brains; put the head into considerably more water than will cover it.
2. Let it boil gently, and skim it carefully; when very tender take it out and cut in small pieces.
3. Put into the boiling soup three pounds of beef and a knuckle of veal with all the bones broken fine.
4. Add to this four or five onions, a carrot and turnip sliced, and a bunch of sweet herbs.
5. Let it boil gently for three hours.
6. Parboil the tongue and brains of the calf’s head, and add them when the soup is nearly done.
7. Let it cool and take off the fat.
8. To finish it for the table, melt a quarter of a pound of nice fresh butter, add a handful of flour and stir over the fire till the butter and flour are brown;
9. Add to this a little of the soup, a few sprigs of parsley and sweet basil; boil it for fifteen minutes and add it to the soup, together with two tablespoonfuls of catsup, the juice of a lemon, and salt to taste.
10. It is usual to add a pint of sherry.
11. When dished in the tureen, put in two dozen egg balls.

title: Molasses Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Louisiana Molasses
- 1 Cup of Boiling Milk or Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder or Soda
- 3% Cups of Flour (Sifted)
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Cup Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Ground Ginger
- % Teaspoonful of Cloves

## Directions

1. Melt the butter.
2. Dissolve the soda in the boiling water, using about one tablespoonful, and add it to the molasses.
3. Then beat the sugar and butter to a cream, until very light, and add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
4. Add these to the molasses, and then stir in the cream.
5. Beat till smooth, and then add gradually the flour, beating till very light and smooth.
6. Now add the ginger and cloves, or a teaspoonful of cinnamon, and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
7. Place in a shallow cake pan, buttered, and bake in a moderate oven from thirty to forty-five minutes.

title: Molasses Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of molasses
- one pound of brown sugar
- juice of two lemons
- extract
- a pint of parched pinders, or pecan meats, or almonds cut up
- butter

## Directions

1. Let the molasses and sugar boil moderately, without stirring it, for two hours
2. If not thick enough to pull then, let it boil a little longer
3. Put in your extract
4. When the candy is cool enough to handle, put into the pot parched pinders, pecan meats, or almonds cut up
5. Butter two large dishes and pour out the candy

title: Molasses Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Louisiana Molasses
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Pound of Granulated Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Soda
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar until it becomes quite thick when dropped into water
2. Add the molasses and the vinegar and butter
3. Boil till it hardens when dropped into cold water
4. Stir in a small half teaspoonful of bicarbonate of soda
5. Pour into buttered tins
6. As soon as it begins to cool sufficiently pull till white
7. Moisten the hands while pulling with ice water or butter
8. The sticks may be single, twisted, braided or flattened, according to taste

title: Molasses Candy of Our Grandfathers' Time
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of molasses
- butter the size of an egg
- one teaspoonful of essence of wintergreen

## Directions

1. Stew over a brisk fire till it will harden on being dropped into cold water
2. Add essence of wintergreen when it is almost done
3. Pull it while warm, with buttered hands
4. Cut in sticks

title: Molasses Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Soda
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Ginger
- 0.75 Teaspoonful of Cloves
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Cloves

## Directions

1. Melt the molasses
2. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and add, mixing in the soda, dissolved in a tablespoonful of boiling water
3. Add the ginger and the ground cloves
4. Take off the fire, and beat till cool
5. Then add the eggs, beaten well

title: Molasses Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Soda
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Ginger
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Cloves
- Flour

## Directions

1. Melt the molasses.
2. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and add, mixing in the soda, dissolved in a tablespoonful of boiling water.
3. Add the ginger and the ground cloves.
4. Take off the fire, and beat till cool.
5. Then add the eggs, beaten well, and flour sufficient to make a nice, soft dough, just stiff enough to roll.
6. Cut into small cakes, and bake in a quick oven to a delicate brown.

title: Molasses Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of molasses
- three eggs
- a large spoonful of butter
- rich crust

## Directions

1. Take one pint of molasses
2. Beat into it three eggs and a large spoonful of butter
3. Pour the mixture into a rich crust, and bake

title: Molasses Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of New Orleans Molasses
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- The Juice of 2 Lemons
- 3 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 3/4 Teaspoonful Each of Nutmeg and Cinnamon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter

## Directions

1. Blend the lemon juice and molasses well, and gradually beat in the flour, which you will have moistened with a little water
2. Add the spices and the butter, melted, and the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light with the sugar
3. Add the whites, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Mix well
5. Line the pie pans with a pie crust, bake, fill with the mixture, and bake again for half an hour

title: Molasses Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of New Orleans Molasses
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 3/4 Cup of Melted Butter
- 3/4 Cup of Sherry Wine
- 3 Cups Flour
- 3/4 Teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1 1/2 Teaspoonful Each of the Four Spices

## Directions

1. Dissolve the baking powder in the flour, mixing thoroughly
2. Melt the butter and add it to the molasses, and then add the milk, mixing thoroughly
3. Add the flour, gradually mixing well, and then add the wine, and lastly the spices
4. Blend all well together
5. Pour into a well-buttered pan, and let it bake for about two hours
6. Serve with Foamy Sauce

title: Molasses Toast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- West India molasses

## Directions

1. Make a thick slice of very nice toast, evenly browned on both sides, but not the least burnt
2. Lay it in a pint bowl
3. Pour over it a small ½ pint of the best West India molasses, having stirred into the molasses a heaped tablespoonful

title: Molasses Toast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a thick slice of very nice toast
- a small pint of the best West India molasses
- a heaped tablespoonful of ground ginger
- a pint of hot water

## Directions

1. Lay the toast in a pint bowl
2. Stir the ground ginger into the molasses
3. Mix the molasses with a pint of hot water
4. Pour the whole over the toast
5. Cover it with a plate for a few minutes
6. Eat it while warm, previous to going to bed

title: Morue a la Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of picked codfish
- 1 pint and a half of cream
- The yolk of 1 egg
- 1 large tablespoonful of butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Pepper and Cayenne

## Directions

1. Soak the codfish overnight, and then let it boil about forty minutes.
2. Take off scald again and drain, and again scald and drain, allowing it to stand each time about four or five minutes before changing the water.
3. Put one large tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan; when melted add the flour and blend, without browning.
4. Add the milk, stirring constantly until it boils, and then add the fish, seasoning highly with pepper and Cayenne.
5. Let it boil about ten minutes longer, and take off the fire.
6. Add the yolk of an egg which has been beaten thoroughly, and serve hot, with plain boiled potatoes buttered.

title: Moselle Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Moselle Wine
- 1 Sliced Lemon
- 1 Sliced Orange
- A Dash of Good Brandy
- A Dash of Maraschino
- 1/2 Pint of Seltzer Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 of a Grated Pineapple
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- 3/4 Tablespoonful of Sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water, add the wine and the liqueurs, and the juice of the lemon and sliced orange and lemon, and the grated pineapple
2. Stir well
3. Let cool, and serve in small thin tumblers
4. Decorate the top with sprigs of fresh green mint

title: Motley Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- % Pound of Butter
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 12 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Peach Extract
- 1 Teaspoonful of Fruit Coloring

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream and then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
2. Beat until very light.
3. Then add gradually the flour, into which you will have sifted, in the second sifting, the baking powder.
4. Mix thoroughly and beat vigorously.
5. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Now divide the batter into two parts, and put one spoonful of Peach Extract into one, and one spoonful of fruit coloring into the other.
7. Mix well, and drop by spoonfuls into the buttered cake pan, first a spoonful of the white, and then one of the pink, until all the batter is used up.
8. Bake from forty-five minutes to one hour in a moderate oven.

title: Mrs. Clay's Premium Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 eggs, whites only
- 1 lb. of sugar
- ¾ lb. of flour
- ½ lb. of butter
- whisky and lemon to taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Mrs. Coolidge's Oyster Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pints of oysters
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 pint of water
- 3 soft crackers
- pepper
- mace
- butter the size of an egg
- 1 small onion
- celery

## Directions

1. Strain the liquor from 1.5 pints of oysters into a saucepan with 1 pint of milk and 1 pint of water
2. Add 3 soft crackers pounded fine, a little pepper, mace, butter the size of an egg, small onion, and celery chopped fine if you have it
3. Boil all together a few minutes
4. Add the oysters and let it boil up once
5. Serve immediately

title: Mrs. Joy's Chow-Chow Pickle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- two large heads of cabbage
- 8 large onions
- 2 teacups of salt
- 1 gallon of vinegar
- 1 teacup of white mustard seed
- 1 teacup of celery seed
- 1 teacup of grated horse radish
- 1 whole garlic
- 2 or 3 pods of green or red peppers
- 2 teacups of brown sugar
- 1 teacup of the best sweet oil
- 2 ounces of turmeric
- 1 teacup of mustard

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage as for slaw, but not so fine
2. Slice 8 large onions
3. Mix the onions with the cut cabbage, and sprinkle about 2 teacups of salt over them
4. Mix the salt thoroughly in the cabbage, and let them stand not less than 12 hours
5. Drain all the water from the cabbage through a muslin cloth
6. Put the cabbage in a porcelain kettle and cover it with a gallon of vinegar, 1 teacup of white mustard seed, 1 teacup of celery seed, 1 teacup of grated horse radish, 1 whole garlic minced fine, 2 or 3 pods of green or red peppers cut fine
7. Let this come to a boil, and add 2 teacups of brown sugar
8. Set this aside until the next day to get cold
9. Have a paste made of 1 teacup of the best sweet oil, 2 ounces of turmeric and 1 teacup of mustard, made quite thick and stirred into the oil
10. Mix this paste in well with the hand into the vinegar and cabbage
11. It will be fit for use in 10 days

title: Mrs. L. Wayne's Bread Receipt
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half yeast cake
- ¼ cup cold water (whole cake in cold weather)
- 1 quart lukewarm water
- 1 tablespoon white sugar
- ½ cup lard
- 1 tablespoon salt
- flour

## Directions

1. beat hard with hand
2. add flour to make thick dough (use gallon tin bucket, with tight fitting cover)
3. when risen to top, add flour to make dough stiff, in bucket
4. knead on board 15 minutes, use as little flour as possible, keeping dough soft
5. make in loaves
6. cover to rise
7. when to the top of pans, bake in moderately hot oven

title: Mrs. Meyer's Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart flour
- 3 eggs
- ¼ lb. butter
- 2 spoonfuls of yeast
- about ½ pint of water

## Directions

1. beat light at night
2. bake in rings

title: Muffin Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints of flour
- a half pound of butter
- 5 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of good yeast

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks of eggs separately
2. Work the butter well in the flour
3. Put in the eggs
4. Beat all very light
5. Add 3 tablespoonfuls of good yeast
6. Bake it in a pan with oven rather quick at the bottom and clear at the top until it begins to bake
7. Then quicken the fire by degrees

title: Muffin Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 4 Eggs
- 1/2 Pound of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and add the butter.
2. When it grows lukewarm, add the yeast, dissolved in about three tablespoonfuls of warm water.
3. Add the salt and flour, and beat well for about ten minutes.
4. Then set in a warm place over night.
5. In the morning beat the yolks of the eggs well, and then the whites to a stiff froth; add the yolks and beat well, and then add the whites, and mix all thoroughly.
6. Beat till very light, let it stand for about a quarter of an hour, and then bake in a buttered pan for about twenty minutes in a quick oven.

title: Muffins And Crumpets With Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pints of milk
- four eggs
- a small teacupful of yeast or a yeast cake
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- a teaspoonful of salt
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Take two pints of milk, four eggs, and a small teacupful of yeast, or a yeast cake
2. melt a piece of butter in a little of the milk
3. add a teaspoonful of salt
4. thicken with sifted flour until it is like buckwheat batter
5. Set it to rise for eight or ten hours
6. bake in muffin rings, or pour it like batter cakes, on a hot griddle
7. Butter them when cooked this way, just as they come from the griddle

title: Muscadine Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Quarts of Muscadine Juice
- 1.33 Quarts of Cold Water
- 3 Pounds of Granulated Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as Blackberry Wine

title: Mushroom Catsup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper
- cloves

## Directions

1. Lay fresh mushrooms in a deep dish
2. strew a little salt over them, then a fresh layer of mushrooms and salt, till you get in all the mushrooms
3. Let them stay in this brine three days
4. then mash them fine
5. add to each quart a spoonful of vinegar, half a spoonful of pepper and a teaspoonful of cloves
6. pour all this in a stone jar
7. place the jar in a pot of boiling water
8. let it boil two hours
9. then strain it without squeezing the mushrooms
10. Boil the juice fifteen minutes
11. skim it well
12. let it stand a few hours to settle
13. bottle and cork it well
14. Keep it cool, or it will ferment

title: Mushroom Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1/2 can of mushrooms
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Stew the mushrooms a few minutes
2. Chop them fine
3. Make a plain omelet
4. When it is ready to fold, place the mushrooms across the center
5. Fold twice over
6. Let it cook two minutes longer and serve hot

title: Mushroom Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of broth (white) or boiling water
- Lemon juice
- 1 can of mushrooms
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Make a brown roux, melting the butter in the saucepan, and adding the flour, and stirring till well browned
2. Stir in the boiling stock, or water, if you have not the stock
3. Add the mushrooms, and salt and pepper to taste
4. Add the juice of half a lemon and let it cook for about fifteen minutes longer
5. Pour the sauce over the filet, and serve hot

title: Mushroom Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- water
- butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the mushrooms, cut them in small pieces, and put them in a saucepan;
2. cover them with water, and let them boil soft;
3. then stir in butter, mixed in flour, until it is thick enough to form a nice sauce;
4. add pepper and salt.

title: Mushroom Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound good macaroni
- 1/2 pound fresh mushrooms or a half can of mushrooms
- 1 carrot and onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 2 pints consomé or bouillon
- 1 pint cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- salt and pepper to taste
- three teaspoonfuls salt
- two quarts boiling water

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces of about three inches
2. Wash and put into a stewpan with two quarts of boiling water and three teaspoonfuls of salt
3. Let the macaroni boil half an hour
4. Put the butter and flour in a small stewpan and beat to a cream
5. Add the chopped onion, carrot and pepper with remaining salt and broth
6. Heat slowly
7. When the sauce begins to boil, set it back where it will only simmer for about twenty minutes
8. Add the cream and strain the sauce
9. Pour the water from the macaroni and in its place put the sauce and mushrooms
10. Cook for five minutes and serve hot

title: Mushrooms With Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1/2 Cup of Cream
- The Yolk of 1 Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Put the mushrooms with their juice, into a saucepan, porcelain-lined or agate.
2. Let them simmer for fifteen minutes.
3. Then add one tablespoonful of butter, blended well with the same amount of flour, and mixed thoroughly with the cream.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Stir well.
6. Bring it to a good boil, and remove from the fire, and stir in the yolk of an egg, which has been beaten with Sherry Wine.
7. Serve immediately.

title: Mustard Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- The Yolk of 1 Egg, If Desired
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Blend the mustard and the oil, adding the latter, drop by drop at first, and then proceeding more confidently.
2. Whenever the dressing appears to be curdling, add a few drops of vinegar, and work rapidly till it becomes smooth again.
3. Add the salt and pepper, and when the dressing is finished, use it for celery salad, fish, tomatoes, potatoes, etc.

title: Mutton Broth
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds of the neck of the mutton
- 4 quarts of cold water
- 1/4 cup of rice
- 1 onion
- 2 large turnips
- 1 bay leaf
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the neck of the mutton, or wipe it with a damp towel, and put it into the kettle with the cold water.
2. Let it come to a slow boil and skim carefully.
3. Cover well and let it simmer for about four hours.
4. Remove from the stove and pour into an earthern vessel to cool.
5. When cold, remove all the fat from the surface.
6. Return to the kettle and add the rice, the sliced turnip and the bay leaf, and season to taste.

title: Mutton Chops, Braseur's Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Mutton Chops
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 3 Shallots, Chopped Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Select six thick chops from the loin of the mutton.
2. Trim neatly and season well with salt and black pepper and a dash of Cayenne.
3. Rub lightly with butter on either side and broil on a hot charcoal fire.
4. Have ready a hot dish and pour over the chops a sauce of melted butter, seasoned nicely with salt and pepper, the juice of one lemon and three minced shallots.
5. Serve hot.

title: Mutton Cutlets
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Mutton Cutlets
- Butter
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. The cutlets are slices from the thick part of the leg of the mutton and are very excellent eating.
2. Trim off the outer skin and broil in the same manner as mutton chops.
3. They are very delicious served for dinner with a garnish of Puree of Spinach.

title: Mutton Feet a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Mutton Feet
- Boiling Water
- 1/2 Lemon
- Salt
- Cream Sauce
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of French Vinegar
- 1 Gill of Water
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms (if desired)

## Directions

1. Scald the mutton feet in boiling water and remove every vestige of wool that may adhere, cleaning and scraping the feet.
2. Then place them in a pot, cover well with boiling water, add half of a lemon (including peel and meat) to the water, and salt well.
3. In the meantime, prepare a Sauce a la Poulette as follows: Make a Cream Sauce and add the juice of one lemon, or half a tablespoonful of good vinegar.
4. Take the mutton feet out of the water in which they have been boiled, take out the big bones from the feet.
5. Put the mutton feet into the Sauce a la Poulette, add a gill of water, let all simmer about five minutes, and then take off the fire and add the yolks of two eggs, beaten well, stirring well into the sauce.
6. Serve hot.

title: Mutton Feet à la Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Mutton Feet
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Pint of Broth
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the mutton feet in the same way as indicated in the above recipe.
2. When done take out the large bones and cut the feet into two-inch pieces.
3. Cut two onions very fine, mincing them, and brown in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter.
4. When slightly brown, add a tablespoonful of flour and mix well, making a nice Brown Roux.
5. Add about a pint of the broth in which the mutton feet have been boiled.
6. As it boils, skim off the grease and let it simmer for about ten minutes.
7. Add the mutton feet and let them simmer ten minutes longer and serve hot, with Croutons of bread fried in butter.

title: Mutton Feet à la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton feet
- Half of a lemon (including peel and meat)
- Salt
- Cream Sauce
- Juice of one lemon or half a tablespoonful of good vinegar
- A gill of water
- Yolks of two eggs
- Half can of mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Cover mutton feet well with boiling water, add half of a lemon (including peel and meat) to the water, and salt well.
2. Prepare a Cream Sauce and add the juice of one lemon, or half a tablespoonful of good vinegar.
3. Take the mutton feet out of the water in which they have been boiled and take out the big bones from the feet.
4. Put the mutton feet into the Sauce à la Poulette, add a gill of water, and let all simmer about five minutes.
5. Take off the fire and add the yolks of two eggs, beaten well, stirring well into the sauce.
6. Serve hot. Many of the Creoles add a half can of mushrooms to the mutton feet before putting in the sauce.

title: Mutton Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Hashed Mutton
- 6 Left-over Tomatoes or Freshly Boiled Tomatoes
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- Croutons
- Butter for Frying

## Directions

1. After having taken off all the rough edges of the roast and cut out the gristle and hard membrane, hash the mutton into pieces of about one inch in size.
2. Take six left-over tomatoes, or freshly boiled, and cut into quarters.
3. Chop fine one herb bouquet.
4. Place a tablespoonful of butter or a half tablespoonful of lard into the stew-pot, and as it melts add the mutton seasoned well, and a few minutes later the fine herbs.
5. Mince the clove of garlic if the flavor is liked and add.
6. Stir constantly without browning much, and add a tablespoonful of flour.
7. Let this brown very slightly and then add the tomatoes.
8. Cover and let all simmer for about twenty minutes, and then pour over a pint of boiling water.
9. Season again to taste and set back on the stove and let it simmer gently for about three-quarters of an hour.
10. Cut some Croutons and fry them in butter; place on a dish and serve with the hash.

title: Mutton Haunch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton haunch
- vinegar
- water
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- butter
- port wine
- jelly

## Directions

1. Let it lie in vinegar and water a few hours before it is put to cook
2. Rub it all over with pepper and salt, and when going to put it in the oven, cover it with a paste made of flour and water, to keep in the juices while baking
3. Allow fifteen minutes to each pound of mutton
4. When half done, take off the flour paste, baste the meat well and dredge flour over it
5. Half an hour before serving, stir into the pan a quarter of a pound of butter, baste the meat freely, dredge flour over it again, and brown
6. Serve with port wine and jelly in the gravy, or if preferred, use one of the sauces mentioned for roast mutton

title: Mutton Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of the brisket of mutton
- 6 Irish potatoes
- 3 large onions
- 1 bay leaf
- 1/2 clove of garlic
- 1/4 tablespoonful of lard
- 2 quarts of water
- 1 square inch of ham, chopped very fine
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces of about an inch square and season well with salt and pepper
2. Put one-quarter of a tablespoonful of lard into the stewpot, and when it melts add the thinly sliced onions
3. Let these brown for a few minutes and then add the mutton and the ham, chopped very fine
4. Let this continue browning, and when slightly browned, add one tablespoonful of finely sifted flour and stir well
5. Then add the finely-minced bay leaf and a half clove of garlic, minced fine
6. Brown lightly, for a mutton stew must never be dark

title: Mutton Stew with Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of the Brisket of Mutton
- 3 Large Onions
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- 1/4 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1 Square Inch of Ham, Chopped Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces of about an Inch square and season well with salt and pepper.
2. Put one-quarter of a tablespoonful of lard into the stew-pot, and when it melts add the thinly sliced onions.
3. Let these brown for a few minutes and then add the mutton and the ham, chopped very fine.
4. Let this continue browning, and when slightly browned, add one tablespoonful of finely sifted flour and stir well.
5. Then add the finely-minced bay leaf and a half clove of garlic, minced fine.
6. Brown lightly, for a mutton stew must never be dark.
7. After twenty minutes, add two quarts of boiling water and let it boil for about ten minutes longer, seasoning to taste.
8. Then add the potatoes, cut into halves, and let the mixture cook for three-quarters of an hour longer, making one hour and a half in all.
9. Let it simmer gently all the time, so that the meat may be perfectly tender.

title: Mutton Stew with Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of the Brisket of Mutton
- 6 Turnips
- 3 Large Onions
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- 1/4 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1 Square Inch of Ham, Chopped Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces of about an inch in length and thickness, and season well.
2. Proceed to make the stew as mentioned above, only instead of adding the potatoes, add turnips parboiled, and cut into halves or quarters.

title: Mutton Stuffed with Mushrooms
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of mushrooms
- chopped parsley
- chopped onion
- a tablespoonful of grated lean ham
- a tablespoonful of grated fat ham
- salt
- pepper
- the yolks of four eggs
- flour
- brown gravy
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Chop up half a pint of mushrooms, put them in a stew pan with some chopped parsley and onion, and a tablespoonful of grated lean and same of fat ham
2. Season with salt and pepper, add the yolks of four eggs, stir it all together
3. Introduce it in the leg by taking out the bone or by making incisions in the mutton
4. Bake very brown, froth it up by dusting flour over it, and serve with a good brown gravy, in which some currant jelly is melted

title: Mutton that will Taste like Venison
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a hind quarter of lamb or mutton
- brown sugar
- half a pint of wine
- half a pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a hind quarter of lamb or mutton; rub it well all over with brown sugar, half a pint of wine, and same of vinegar
2. Let it stay in this pickle for a day or two, if the weather is cold
3. When it is wanted, wash it, dry it, and roast it, or it may be cut into steaks, or made into a pie like venison

title: My Own Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk
- a cup of maizena or corn starch
- cold water
- a cup of white sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- jelly or jam
- whites of four eggs
- a cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Let a quart of milk be set on to boil
2. While it is getting hot, mix a cup of maizena or corn starch with enough cold water to form it into a thick batter
3. Add to this a cup of white sugar and the yolks of four eggs
4. Take the milk off and stir eggs, maizena, and sugar, into the milk
5. Beat all together a few minutes
6. Pour the mixture into a baking dish and bake it lightly about ten minutes, or long enough only to cook the eggs
7. Take the pudding out, and while hot put over it a layer of jelly or jam
8. Beat up the whites of the eggs with a cup of sugar, put this over the jelly and brown

title: Myrtle Orange Preserve, or Home-Made Limes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- salt
- water
- sugar
- orange leaves

## Directions

1. Pluck the oranges before they turn yellow; they should be a rich dark green.
2. Cut a hole in the stem end and take out all the white pulp and seeds.
3. Scrape them carefully, grate the rind so as to break the oil cells, and allow the strong oil to escape.
4. Wash them and throw them into strong salt and water; let them stay in it for three days.
5. Soak them in fresh water three days.
6. Boil them in clear water, slowly, in a brass kettle; cover them with a few orange leaves while boiling, which will green them, and boil until they are tender, then set them up to cool.
7. Weigh as much sugar as you have oranges, and allow pound for pound.
8. Boil the syrup clear and then put in the oranges; boil gently for half an hour, or until green and yellow.
9. Use only a silver spoon in making this preserve.

title: Naples Biscuit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- half a pound of fine white sugar
- half a pound of sifted flour
- essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs light
2. add half a pound of fine white sugar, and half a pound of sifted flour
3. Flavor with essence of lemon

title: Naples Biscuit
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 12 whites and 10 yolks of eggs
- 2 glasses of wine

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. These should gradually harden in the oven till quite crisp
3. Frequently turn in the tins

title: Naples Biscuit
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 1 lb. sugar
- 12 whites of eggs
- 10 yolks of eggs
- 2 glasses of Madeira wine
- 1 teaspoonful of mace

## Directions

1. Drop on pans
2. Let them harden gradually in the stove until quite crisp, turning frequently

title: Nasturtium Seeds
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cider vinegar
- green seeds of nasturtiums

## Directions

1. Keep a large glass jar of cold cider vinegar
2. Put in the green seeds of nasturtiums after the flowers are off, and the seeds full grown, but not hard
3. Remove the stalks
4. To flavor them with spice, boil the vinegar with a bag of spice in it, and pour it on hot, leaving the spice in the jar

title: Neapolitan Blanc Manger
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Cornstarch
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- The Yolk of 1 Egg
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract
- 1 Tablespoonful of Grated Vanilla Chocolate
- Whipped Cream

## Directions

1. First make the cornstarch blanc manger preparation, and then separate it into four parts.
2. Put the first into a mold, and then stir into it, while warm, one heaping tablespoonful of grated Vanilla chocolate.
3. Pour the white preparation into the same mold, on top of this.
4. Then a layer of the chocolate, and then the white.
5. Cover the last layer with the yolk of an egg, and set to cool and harden.
6. Serve cut into thin slices, with whipped cream.

title: Neapolitan Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Vanilla Cream
- 1 Pint of Pistache Ice Cream
- 1 Pint of Strawberry or Raspberry Cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the ice cream according to recipes
2. Have at hand a brick form or mold that will hold three pints
3. Lay at the bottom of the mold Pistache Ice Cream
4. Place over this the Vanilla Cream and on top of this the Raspberry or Strawberry Cream
5. Cover the mold tightly and set to freeze for two hours
6. Plunge the mold into warm water, wash off all the salt and dry
7. Carefully uncover, and unmold the cream in a cold dessert dish or on a piece of paper
8. Take a long, sharp knife, dip into warm water, cut the brick of cream through the center, and then divide the pieces into three or four parts

title: Nectar
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of White Honey
- 2 Ounces of Coriander
- 6 Ounces of Fine White Pulverized Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Ounce of the Fresh Zest of Lemon
- 1 Ounce of Cloves
- ½ Ounce of Benzoine
- 1 Ounce of Storax Calamite
- ½ Drachm of Tincture of Vanilla
- 4 Ounces of Orange Flower Water
- 5 Quarts of Highly Rectified Spirits

## Directions

1. Pound to a powder all the ingredients which require it, mix with the honey, benzoin, etc., pour over the alcohol, and steep for fifteen days.
2. Distill the liquor till it is reduced to four quarts, using a water bath, or bain-marie, for the distillation.
3. Make a syrup of six pounds of fine white sugar, add the Vanilla, color a deep red with Cochineal, and add to the liquor.
4. Blend and shake well, bottle and keep in a cool place.

title: Nectarine Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream or Milk
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Nectarines or 1 Pint Can
- 6 Eggs

## Directions

1. Peel the nectarines, stone and mash well, with half of the sugar
2. Let them stand for an hour or more, and press through a sieve
3. Set the milk to boil; beat the yolks of the eggs and the remaining half of the sugar to a cream, and beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
4. Take the milk off the fire and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring all the while
5. Set to cool
6. Add the mashed nectarines and sugar, and mix well; mix all thoroughly, turn into a freezer and proceed as in the general directions for making Ice Cream

title: Nesselrode Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pint of Chestnuts
- 3/4 Pint of Almonds
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- 1/2 Pound of Mixed French Candies of the Finest Kind
- 1/2 of a Grated Pineapples, or 1/2 of a Can
- The Yolks of Six Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence
- sugar
- 4 tablespoonfuls of Sherry

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts and throw them into boiling water to blanch
2. Then take off the brown skin, and set them in a saucepan and cover with boiling water, and let them boil for twenty minutes
3. Then drain and press them through a colander
4. Shell the almonds and blanch them for twelve minutes in boiling water
5. Then take them out and pound them, and cut the fruit into very small pieces
6. Put the sugar and the water on to boil together, and let the mixture boil for ten minutes
7. Then beat the yolks of the eggs very, very light, and add them to the boiling syrup
8. Let it boil up once, and take off, and then beat very lightly with an egg beater, till cool
9. Add all the fruits, the almonds and chestnuts, and the pint of cream, and a tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence
10. Beat well, and add four tablespoonfuls of Sherry
11. Put the pudding into a freezer to freeze and ripen, and let it stand covered with a carpet for four or five hours, or till frozen, renewing the ice and salt and draining off water as necessary

title: New Orleans Gumbo
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey or fowl
- piece of fresh beef
- lard
- onion
- water
- 100 oysters
- oyster liquor
- pulverized fillet

## Directions

1. Take a turkey or fowl, cut it up with a piece of fresh beef
2. Put them in a pot with a little lard, an onion, and water sufficient to cook the meat
3. After they have become soft, add 100 oysters with their liquor
4. Season to your taste
5. Just before taking up the soup, warm it until it becomes mucilaginous, and add two spoonfuls of pulverized fillet

title: New Orleans Toddy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One lump of sugar
- one tablespoonful of water
- one wineglassful of whiskey or brandy
- one lump of ice

## Directions

1. Use small bar glass

title: New York Cup Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 cups of sifted flour
- 3 cups of powdered white sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of rich milk
- 1 glass of wine
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- a small teaspoonful of pearl ash
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Warm the milk and cut up the butter in it
2. Beat the eggs very light
3. Stir them into the milk, in turn with the flour
4. Add the spice and wine
5. Dissolve pearl ash in a little vinegar and add lastly
6. Stir all very hard

title: New York Rusk
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 8 ounces of sugar
- 6 ounces of butter
- 5 spoonfuls of yeast
- 1 pint of milk at tepid heat

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour
2. Then add the sugar
3. Then add the yeast
4. If the milk is not sufficient, add a little warm water
5. Make the dough stiff
6. Make them after breakfast
7. Bake for tea on tin sheets

title: Niagara Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 2 teacups of brown sugar
- 4 cups of flour
- 2 cups of butter
- 1 cup of cream
- 2 cups of molasses
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 cup of ginger

## Directions

1. Add brown sugar to eggs
2. Add flour, butter, cream, molasses, soda, and ginger
3. Beat very light and bake very light

title: Nice and Cheap Jumbles. No Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of clabber
- a teaspoonful of soda
- grated orange or lemon peel
- a good quart of sifted flour
- sugar for sprinkling
- strips of citron

## Directions

1. Stir soda into the clabber
2. roll dough and cut in rounds with a hole in the middle
3. bake in a quick oven
4. sprinkle sugar over them and stick strips of citron in each cake when placing in baking pan

title: Nice Boiled Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- six eggs
- one cup of white sugar
- half cup of sugar
- rose, lemon, or peach-leaf flavoring

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil
2. Beat the whites of the eggs with a half cup of sugar, and drop into the boiling milk for two minutes
3. With a skimmer remove the boiled whites, and put on a dish to cool
4. When the whites are taken off, stir into the milk the yolks and sugar, previously well beaten up together
5. Add rose, lemon, or peach-leaf flavoring
6. Run this through a sieve into the bowl you expect to serve it in
7. Pile up the whites on the custard

title: Nice Boiled Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- six eggs
- one cup of white sugar
- rose, lemon, or peach-leaf flavoring

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil
2. Beat the whites of the eggs with a half cup of sugar
3. Drop the beaten whites into the boiling milk for two minutes
4. With a skimmer remove the boiled whites and put on a dish to cool
5. When the whites are taken off, stir into the milk the yolks and sugar, previously well beaten up together
6. Add rose, lemon, or peach-leaf flavoring
7. Run this through a sieve into the bowl you expect to serve it in
8. Pile up the whites on the custard

title: Nice Drop Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound sugar
- one-quarter pound of butter
- four well beaten eggs
- one-half pound of currants
- a spoonful of brandy
- grated nutmeg or lemon peel
- flour sufficient for a stiff batter

## Directions

1. Cream together sugar and butter
2. add four well beaten eggs
3. add one-half pound of currants, a spoonful of brandy, grated nutmeg or lemon peel
4. add flour sufficient for a stiff batter
5. Beat well
6. Drop by spoonfuls on buttered tins
7. bake in a quick oven

title: Nice Family Cake With Fruit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of fine sugar
- two cups of butter
- five cups of sifted flour
- half a pound of chopped raisins
- half a pound of dried currants
- brandy, and nutmeg, or extract of nutmeg, or lemon

## Directions

1. Combine three cups of fine sugar, two cups of butter, five cups of sifted flour, half a pound of chopped raisins, and half a pound of dried currants
2. flavor with brandy, and nutmeg, or extract of nutmeg, or lemon
3. Put the same flavor in the frosting, if the cake is to be used for special occasions

title: Nice Force meat, for Stuffings, Etc.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold chicken
- veal
- beef
- pepper
- salt
- sweet herbs
- nutmeg
- minced ham
- garlic
- raw eggs (two)
- butter
- marrow or drippings

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of cold chicken, veal and beef; shred small and mix together;
2. season with pepper, salt, sweet herbs, and a little nutmeg, 7. e., if intended for white meat or anything delicately flavored, but if meant for a savory dish add a little minced ham, and garlic;
3. pound or chop this very fine (it is well, and saves trouble, to run it through a sausage chopper);
4. and make it in a paste with two raw eggs, some butter, marrow or drippings;
5. stuff your joint, or poultry, and if there is some not used, roll it round the balls, flour them and fry in boiling lard.
6. This is a nice garnish for a side dish.

title: Nice Muffins
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk
- one quarter of a pound of butter
- four eggs
- enough flour
- a cup of yeast

## Directions

1. To a quart of milk, one quarter of a pound of butter, four eggs, and enough flour to form a very stiff batter, add a cup of yeast
2. set it to rise three hours
3. bake in greased muffin rings
4. Split, butter, and serve them hot

title: Nice Rice Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of boiled rice
- warm water
- a tablespoonful of butter
- three or four eggs
- a quart of sweet milk
- sugar
- picked and seeded

## Directions

1. Take two tablespoonfuls of boiled rice
2. If it is very dry, wash it with a little warm water
3. Put it in a pan, add a tablespoonful of butter, three or four eggs beaten light, a quart of sweet milk, sugar enough to make it quite sweet, and one cup of picked and seeded

title: Nice Rice Custard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of boiled rice
- a little warm water
- a tablespoonful of butter
- three or four eggs beaten light
- a quart of sweet milk
- sugar enough to make it quite sweet
- one cup of picked and seeded raisins
- nutmeg
- essence of lemon or vanilla

## Directions

1. If the rice is very dry, wash it with a little warm water
2. Put it in a pan
3. add a tablespoonful of butter, three or four eggs beaten light, a quart of sweet milk, sugar enough to make it quite sweet, and one cup of picked and seeded raisins
4. Flavor with nutmeg and essence of lemon or vanilla
5. Bake lightly
6. Do not allow it to remain in the oven long, as the milk will become watery and thus destroy the jelly-like consistency of the custard
7. The raisins may be omitted if they are objectionable

title: Nice Rolls or Light Biscuit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one spoonful of sugar
- a small lump of butter
- a gill of yeast, or a yeast cake
- a quart of flour
- warm milk, or milk and water
- a tablespoonful of lard or butter

## Directions

1. Beat together one egg, one spoonful of sugar, a small lump of butter and a gill of yeast, or a yeast cake
2. Add to this a quart of flour, and enough warm milk, or milk and water, to form a dough
3. Work it and set it to rise
4. When it has risen, take down your bread-board and flour it well
5. Roll your dough out on the board, and spread over it a tablespoonful of lard or butter
6. Sprinkle a dust of flour over the butter
7. Roll it up into rolls and bake quickly

title: Nonpareil Corn Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Indian Meal
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 2½ Cups of Sweet Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar (if desired)
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs very, very light, the whites and yolks separately.
2. Melt the lard or butter, and sift the Baking Powder into the sifted meal and flour.
3. Stir this into the yolks of the eggs and the milk.
4. Blend thoroughly.
5. Add the whites, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Beat well together, and bake quickly and steadily in a quick oven for about a half hour.
7. Serve hot, with butter.

title: Noodle Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Quarts of Good Bouillon or Consomme
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare a good Bouillon or Consomme
2. Beat the yolks of three eggs and the white of two together until very light
3. Add one cup of flour, one half teaspoonful of salt, and mix with cold water, making a stiff paste
4. Roll very thin
5. Roll each strip to form a tube
6. Cut in strips and grease
7. Simmer a few at a time in boiling salt water for about twenty minutes
8. Simmer the noodles in the soup about fifteen minutes

title: Noodle Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- cold water
- 1 quart bouillon or consomé
- boiling salt water

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of three eggs and the white of two together until very light
2. Add one cup of flour and one half teaspoon of salt
3. Mix with cold water to make a stiff paste
4. Roll very thin
5. Roll each strip to form a tube and cut in strips
6. Grease and simmer a few at a time in boiling salt water for about twenty minutes
7. Simmer the noodles in the soup about fifteen minutes

title: Noodles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough with two eggs, a little salt, and sufficient flour
2. Roll this out very thin
3. Shake on a little flour and rub it in
4. Fold the dough over and roll it up
5. Cut it fine with a knife
6. Have ready a pot almost full of boiling water into which you have put a little salt
7. Drop the noodles in and boil them for five or six hours
8. Pour the water off
9. Fry the noodles in plenty of butter

title: Norfolk Black Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 12 eggs
- 2 lbs. of raisins
- 2 lbs. of currants
- 1 lb. of citron
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mixed spice (mace and cinnamon)
- 2 nutmegs, powdered
- 1 large glass of wine
- 1 glass of brandy
- 1/2 glass of rose water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake for 4 or 5 hours
3. Ice it next day

title: Norfolk Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of boiling water
- a large handful of hops
- 1 1/2 lbs. of flour
- a large teacupful of good yeast
- 1 teaspoonful of pearl ash per bottle

## Directions

1. Have ready 2 quarts of boiling water
2. Put into it a large handful of hops and let them boil 20 minutes
3. Sift into a pan 1 1/2 lbs. of flour
4. Strain the liquor from the hops
5. Pour half of it over the hops
6. Let the other half of the liquor stand till it is cool
7. Pour it gradually into the pan of flour, mixing it well
8. Stir into it a large teacupful of good yeast
9. Put it in bottles and cork tightly
10. In each bottle put a teaspoonful of pearl ash

title: Normandy Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of sauce velouté
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom liquor
- 2 tablespoonfuls of fish stock
- The yolks of 2 eggs
- The juice of half a lemon
- 1 teaspoonful of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Make a pint of sauce velouté and add the mushroom liquor
2. Reduce for about ten minutes and add two tablespoonfuls of fish stock or oyster juice; if not at hand add hot water
3. Let it all boil again, and then add the yolks of two eggs and the juice of a lemon
4. Strain through a fine sieve, and add a teaspoonful of fresh butter and serve with fish

title: Nougat
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- 1 Pound of Shredded Almonds, Dried
- A Few Drops of Essence of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the almonds on a baking sheet or plate at the entrance of the oven and let them heat through and through
2. In the meanwhile put the sugar in a pan or porcelain-lined saucepan, and set on a moderate stove
3. Keep stirring with a wooden spoon until it begins to melt
4. Then quicken the stirring, and as soon as the sugar begins to form upon the surface in small white bubbles, like pearls, immediately throw in the almonds
5. Stir all gently until thoroughly mixed, and then pour out on a wet marble slab, and flatten to one-sixth of an inch in thickness
6. As soon as it begins to thicken, cut into pieces of about three inches long and two wide, or, if you have fancy molds, line them thinly with the Nougat, having previously slightly buttered them on the inside
7. Cut the edges of the Nougat even and level before it becomes cold, for then it is brittle, and breaks easily

title: Nougat Fruit Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit cake batter
- two pounds of seedless raisins
- one pound of citron
- one pound of blanched and cut almonds
- one pound of grated cocoanut
- a cup of sweet milk
- wine, brandy and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make the batter the same as for fruit cake
2. instead of the same quantity of fruit, add two pounds of seedless raisins, one pound of citron, one of blanched and cut almonds, and one pound of grated cocoanut
3. Pour over the cocoanut a cup of sweet milk
4. Add the wine, brandy and nutmeg

title: Nougat Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Nougat
- 8 Eggs
- % Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Cream
- 3 Drops of Essence of Peach Kernels
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Make some Nougat using about a pint
2. Bruise and mash it, and blend with one gill of orange flower water
3. Add to this the sugar and the yolks of the eggs, beaten to a thick cream
4. Set the milk to boil
5. Take from the fire and add the above ingredients, stirring well, and adding at the end the Essence of Peach Kernels, and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
6. Then freeze in the manner above directed

title: Noyau Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 8 Eggs
- A Gill of Noyau

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and take from the fire, and then add the sugar and the yolks of the eggs, beaten to a thick cream
2. Add a gill of Noyau Extract and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Set to cool, and then turn into a mold and freeze as above directed

title: Noyeau Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 ounces isinglass
- 2 lemons
- noyeau
- cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1.5 ounces of isinglass
2. Add 2 lemons and as much noyeau as pleasant to the taste
3. Add enough cream to fill the mould, and whisk it well

title: Noyeau Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1| ounces of isinglass
- 2 lemons
- noyeau to taste
- cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1| ounces of isinglass
2. Add 2 lemons and as much noyeau as pleasant to the taste
3. Add enough cream to fill the mould
4. Whisk it well

title: Nut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Nut Meats, Cut Fine
- 2 Teaspoonfuls Baking Powder
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Extract of Almond

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, and add the sugar, and beat till very light
2. Then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light
3. Add the milk, and mix carefully
4. Then sift the baking powder and flour together, and add the flour, beating till very smooth
5. Add half the beaten whites, which must be of a stiff froth, and mix well
6. Then add the nuts (pecans, mashed almonds, peanuts and Brazilian nuts, mixed, and cut or mashed very fine)
7. Add the remainder of the whites of the eggs, the Almond Extract and the baking powder, and mix well
8. Pour into two square, flat pans, lined with buttered paper, and bake in a moderate oven forty-five minutes
9. Use the broom-straw test. If the straw comes out clean and dry, the cake is done; otherwise bake it a little longer
10. Keep the heat of the oven steady and moderate

title: Nut Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of nut kernels
- 1 lb. of sugar
- whites of 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs to a stiff froth
2. Mix with nut kernels and sugar
3. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
4. Bake

title: Nutmeg Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-fourths lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- yolks of 10 eggs and whites of 5
- 2 nutmegs

## Directions

1. Mix three-fourths lb. of butter, 1 lb. of sugar, the yolks of 10 eggs and the whites of 5, and 2 nutmegs
2. Bake either with or without paste

title: Oak Hill Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts of flour
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 2 ounces of beaten ginger
- a little allspice
- 1 lb. of butter rubbed in very fine
- 4 eggs beaten up light
- 1 quart of molasses

## Directions

1. Add the brown sugar to the flour
2. Add the ginger and allspice
3. Rub in the butter
4. Add the beaten eggs
5. Add the molasses to make it up
6. Roll it in thin cakes
7. Bake them

title: Oat Flakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Teacupful of Oat Flakes
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- sugar and cream, or simply cream or milk

## Directions

1. Put the quart of water into a porcelain-lined saucepan, and let it come to a boil
2. Add the salt, and when it is boiling stir in gradually the oat flakes
3. Keep stirring to prevent burning
4. Let the preparation boil for about fifteen or twenty minutes
5. Serve with sugar and cream, or simply cream or milk

title: Oatland Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 8 eggs
- 8 spoonfuls of sugar
- flour enough to roll out
- a small lump of butter melted
- 1 spoonful of powdered ginger and mace

## Directions

1. roll in sugar
2. bake in shapes

title: Oatmeal
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Oatmeal
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- cream

## Directions

1. Soak the oatmeal, when the coarse Scotch Oats are used, over night
2. Cook for a half hour in the morning, boiling constantly, and salt to taste
3. It is always best to set in a double boiler
4. Serve with cream

title: Oil Mangoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of race ginger
- 1 lb. of horse radish
- 1 lb. of mustard seed
- ½ lb. of garlic
- 2 ounces black pepper
- 2 ounces of cloves
- ½ ounce of mace
- mangoes
- salt
- water
- 1 pint of made mustard
- sweet oil
- boiling vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak and dry the race ginger well
2. Do the horse radish the same way
3. Wash the mustard seed well
4. Soak and bleach the garlic
5. Lay the mangoes in salt and water till they are yellow
6. Scald the mangoes until they are green
7. Soak the ingredients well and beat them together in a mortar
8. Mix them with a pint of made mustard and as much sweet oil as will make the ingredients into a paste
9. Fill the mangoes, and put what is left between the layers
10. Pour over them as much boiling vinegar as will cover them

title: Oil Mangoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter of garlic
- 1 lb. of scraped horse radish
- 1 lb. of white mustard seed
- 1 lb. of black mustard seed
- 1 ounce of long pepper
- 2 ounces of black pepper
- 2 ounces of turmeric
- 1 ounce of mace
- 1 ounce of cloves
- 1 lb. of race ginger
- 4 ounces of olive oil
- salt
- vinegar
- allspice
- mustard

## Directions

1. Fill the mangoes with salt, let them stand 24 hours
2. Wipe them clear from salt
3. Mix the garlic, scraped horse radish, white mustard seed, black mustard seed, long pepper, black pepper, turmeric, mace, cloves, race ginger cut up fine, and olive oil together
4. Put them in the mangoes and sew them up
5. Have some vinegar spiced well with common spices, such as black pepper, allspice, horse radish and mustard
6. Boil it well and pour it over the mangoes
7. Tie the jar closely

title: Okra and Corn Fricassee
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of cut okra
- a cupful of hot lard or fat of side meat
- a pint and a half of corn
- flour
- a half cup of milk
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put a pint of cut okra in a frying pan in which there is a cupful of hot lard, or the fat of side meat
2. Let it fry a little, then cut into it a pint and a half of corn
3. Fry it until it is thoroughly cooked
4. Pour off some of the grease, and dredge in a little flour, and a half cup of milk
5. Pepper and salt must be added just before dishing it up

title: Okra Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Onion
- 6 Large Fresh Tomatoes
- 2 Pints of Okra, or Fifty Counted
- 1/2 Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- 2 Large Slices of Ham
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme or Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Two Level Spoons of Butter
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut up the chicken
2. Cut the ham into small squares or dice and chop the onions, parsley and thyme
3. Skin the tomatoes, and chop fine, saving the juice
4. Wash and stem the okras and slice into thin layers of one-half inch each
5. Put the lard or butter into the soup kettle, and when hot add the chicken and the ham
6. Cover closely and let it simmer for about ten minutes
7. Add the chopped onions, parsley, thyme and tomatoes, stirring frequently to prevent scorching
8. Add the okras, and when well-browned, add the juice of the tomatoes
9. When well fried and browned, add the boiling water (about three quarts)
10. Set on the back of the stove, letting it simmer gently for about an hour longer
11. Serve hot, with nicely boiled rice

title: Okra Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Boiled Young Okras
- French Dressing

## Directions

1. Boil the okra as directed.
2. When cold, dress nicely with vinegar, salt and pepper, or, if preferred, the plain French dressing, and serve very cold.

title: Okra Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of water
- 4 handfuls of cut okra
- 1 handful of lima beans
- 3 cymblins
- small piece of fresh meat or fowl
- 5 tomatoes
- butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Let water boil
2. Put into it 4 handfuls of cut okra
3. Half an hour after, put in a handful of lima beans, 3 cymblins and a small piece of fresh meat or fowl
4. Before you take up the soup, put in 5 tomatoes cut in slices
5. When all are well boiled and mixed together, take butter rolled in flour, but not to make the soup too thick
6. Season with pepper and salt
7. Make it in a stone vessel and stir it with a wooden spoon
8. Put your soup on early, that it may simmer on the fire

title: Okra Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 peck of okra
- 1/2 peck of potatoes
- shin or leg of beef
- 10 quarts of cold water
- cayenne or black pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut up in small pieces a quarter of a peck of okra
2. Skin one-half a peck of potatoes
