3. Put them with a shin or leg of beef into 10 quarts of cold water
4. Boil it gently for 7 hours, skimming if well
5. Season with cayenne or black pepper and salt

title: Okra Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- two soup plates of okra
- 5 quarts of water
- salt
- meat
- 1 soup plate and a half of tomatoes
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut up in fine slices two soup plates of okra
2. Put into a soup pot with 5 quarts of water and a little salt at 10 o'clock
3. At 11 o'clock put your meat into the soup pot
4. At 12 o'clock peel a soup plate and a half of tomatoes, and after straining them through a cullender throw them into the soup pot
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Allow all the ingredients to boil till 3 o'clock, when it is fit to be served up

title: Okra Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Beef without fat or bone
- 2 Cups of Okra, chopped fine
- 1/2 Pound of Butter
- 4 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Onion, sliced and chopped
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small pieces
2. Season well with butter, pepper and salt
3. Fry it in the soup kettle with the onion and butter, until very brown
4. Add the cold water and let it simmer for an hour and a half
5. Add the okra and let it simmer gently for three or four hours longer

title: Okra Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Okra, or Fifty Counted
- 6 Fresh Tomatoes
- 2 Onions Chopped Fine
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Quarts of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Red Pepper Pod, Without the Seed

## Directions

1. Wash and stem the okra and then slice it very fine.
2. Chop the tomatoes fine, being careful to preserve the juice.
3. Chop the onions fine and fry them in the butter.
4. Then add the chopped thyme, bay leaf, parsley and tomatoes and the pepper pod, and, after letting it stew about five minutes, add the okra, stirring constantly almost, as it burns quickly.
5. When well browned, add the juice of the tomatoes.
6. Then add the hot water, and set on the back of the stove and let it simmer well for about an hour and a half.
7. Season to taste and serve hot, with Croutons.

title: Old-Fashioned Creole Apple Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Large, Fine, Rosy Apples
- 1 Large Kitchen Spoonful of Butter
- 2 Large Kitchen Spoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Seeded Raisins
- The Grated Zest of 1 Orange

## Directions

1. Drop the dumpling into the boiling water without wrapping them in cloths.
2. After they have boiled an hour, beat one large kitchen spoonful of butter and two of sugar to a thick cream and stir into the water with the dumplings.
3. Add one cup of seeded raisins and the grated zest of one orange.
4. Let all boil twenty minutes longer.
5. Put the dumplings into a dish, pour the sauce over, and serve hot or cold.

title: Old-Fashioned Creole Cocktails
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of good old Bourbon, Cognac, gin, sherry or vermouth
- 2 lumps of loaf sugar
- A piece of ice about the size of an egg
- A bit of lemon peel
- The juice of 1 orange
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- Seltzer
- Freshly-gathered mint
- A few cherries

## Directions

1. Take a large glass, put the lump sugar in, and mix with about three tablespoonfuls of seltzer, dissolving the sugar well.
2. Add the lemon peel and the juices of the orange and lemon.
3. Add one tablespoonful of good old Bourbon or Sherry or any of the above-mentioned liqueurs, according to the cocktail desired.
4. Stir well, and serve with delicate sprigs of freshly-gathered mint on top.

title: Old-Fashioned Creole Plum Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Beef Suet
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Flour
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Yolks of 8 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Raisins
- 1 Pound of Currants
- 1/2 Pound of Citron
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 2 Glasses of Brandy
- 1 Nutmeg
- A Pinch of Cinnamon
- Milk

## Directions

1. Chop finely two pounds of beef suet.
2. Take one and a half pounds of flour, mix with suet; add to this one pound of sugar, the yolks of eight eggs, one pound of raisins, stoned and floured; one pound of currants, one-quarter pound citron, chopped fine; one glass Madeira wine, two glasses brandy, one nutmeg, grated, and a little cinnamon.
3. Beat the whole well together, adding milk till of proper consistency.
4. Wrap in a cloth, previously scalded and floured, leaving room for the pudding to swell; boil six hours, and serve with Brandy or Hard Sauce.

title: Old-Time Sweet Pickled Cucumbers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- brine
- vine leaves
- pulverized alum
- vinegar
- ginger tea
- one quart of strong vinegar
- one pint of water
- three pounds of sugar
- four pounds of cucumbers
- one ounce of cinnamon
- cloves
- mace
- white ginger

## Directions

1. Put your cucumbers in brine for eight days
2. Slice them without soaking; let the slices be an inch thick
3. When cut, soak them until the salt is nearly out, changing the water very often
4. Put them in a kettle with vine leaves laid between the layers
5. Cover them well with leaves and sprinkle pulverized alum all through them to harden and green them
6. Cover with vinegar and set them on the back of the stove until they become green
7. Take the cucumbers out and boil them a little in ginger tea (half an hour will be enough)
8. Make a syrup of one quart of strong vinegar and one pint of water, three pounds of sugar to four pounds of cucumbers, with one ounce of cinnamon, cloves, mace and white ginger to every ten pounds of fruit
9. Make this syrup hot and put in the cucumbers and boil them until clear
10. When they are clear take them out and boil the syrup until it is thick enough to keep
11. Pour it over the cucumbers which should have been placed in jars ready for the syrup

title: Olive Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Stuffed Olives
- 6 Slices of Buttered Toast
- 6 Anchovies
- Capers and Minced Olive to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the toast and cut into delicate circles.
2. Place on each circle a coiled Anchovy, and set a Stuffed Olive in the center of the coil.
3. Decorate lightly with Olives and Capers minced very fine, and serve.
4. Alternatively, this Canapé may be prepared by frying the circles of toast in butter, and spreading them with Anchovies pounded to a paste, and decorating on top with minced Capers and Olives.

title: Omelet for One Person
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- parsley
- butter
- green onion
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs—yolks and whites separately
2. in a bowl put a tablespoonful of water, a little parsley, a teaspoonful of butter, and a little green onion, if liked
3. beat the eggs into this
4. whisk all very rapidly for a few minutes
5. pour it into a pan, where there is a tablespoonful of butter just hot enough to color the eggs
6. cook them very slowly
7. roll up the omelet as it cooks until it is like a rolled pancake
8. pepper and salt it at the last moment of cooking

title: Omelet Souffle Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of ten eggs
- yolks of ten eggs
- three quarters of a pound of powdered sugar
- juice and grated rind of a lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of ten eggs to a stiff froth.
2. Beat the yolks with three quarters of a pound of powdered sugar, and the juice and grated rind of a lemon.
3. Mix all together lightly.
4. Butter a thick-bottomed dish which will just hold the pudding.
5. Put it immediately in the oven, and bake it fifteen or twenty minutes.
6. Serve it just as it comes from the oven.
7. It should quiver like a golden jelly when served.
8. The oven must not be too hot, or it will scorch; the heat should be as usual to bake pies.

title: Omelet Soufflé
body:
## Ingredients

- the whites of 6 eggs
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- the juice of half a lemon, or a spoonful of orange flower water or Kirsch
- 3 tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Have a baking dish ready, greased with butter
2. Be sure that the oven is very hot
3. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
4. Beat the yolks and the sugar to a cream
5. Add the juice of half a lemon or a tablespoonful of orange flower water or Kirsch
6. Add the whites of the eggs
7. Stir carefully and heap all quickly into the baking dish
8. Bake about fifteen minutes, till the top is a delicate brown
9. Serve immediately, as it will fall if allowed to stand

title: Omelet with Oysters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten eggs
- a gill of cream
- a tablespoonful of sweet butter
- a spoonful of chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- a pint of chopped oysters

## Directions

1. Break eight or ten eggs in a basin
2. whip them up well
3. add a gill of cream, a tablespoonful of sweet butter, a spoonful of chopped parsley, pepper and salt to taste
4. beat it again very light
5. stir in a pint of chopped oysters
6. when the butter is hot put in the omelet
7. When the eggs have partly set, roll the omelet in form of a cushion, which you can do by using the tin slice
8. Brown delicately
9. serve with a little melted butter or some sauce you prefer

title: Omelet with Parmesan Cheese
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cream
- grated cheese
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Break six eggs into a bowl
2. add a gill of cream, four ounces of grated cheese, some pepper and a little salt
3. beat the whole together
4. pour into a pan
5. roll up and bake as directed
6. Butter it well before sending to table

title: Omelet with Parmesan Cheese
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- a gill of cream
- four ounces of grated cheese
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Break six eggs into a bowl
2. add a gill of cream, four ounces of grated cheese, some pepper and a little salt
3. beat the whole together
4. pour into a pan
5. roll up and bake as directed
6. Butter it well before sending to table

title: Omelet with Sugar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- seven spoonfuls of powdered sugar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs, whites and yolks separately, with seven spoonfuls of powdered sugar
2. Flavor with lemon
3. bake like a pudding for ten or fifteen minutes, or just long enough to set the eggs
4. Do not bake longer as it will spoil the jelly-like consistency of the omelet

title: Omelette au Naturel
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- water
- sweet butter

## Directions

1. Break eight eggs into a bowl
2. add a teaspoonful of salt, half as much pepper
3. beat up the whole very hard and throw in a tablespoonful of water
4. Have the omelet-pan on the fire with a cup of sweet butter heated to a gentle heat
5. pour the eggs into the heated butter
6. raise it as it cooks, with a skimmer-spoon
7. turn in the brown edges, or turn one half over the other, as it keeps in the lusciousness of the omelet
8. Keep gently rolling it, as it cooks, until, when done, it is round like a small roly-poly pudding

title: Omelette Souffle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- four dessertspoonfuls of white sugar powdered
- yellow rind of a lemon
- quarter of a pound of butter
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs, the whites and yolks separately
2. put to the yolks four dessertspoonfuls of white sugar powdered, and the yellow rind of a lemon chopped very fine
3. mix them thoroughly
4. whip the whites to a high froth and add them to the yolks
5. Put quarter of a pound of butter into the pan, over a brisk fire
6. as soon as it is completely melted pour in the mixture
7. stir it that the butter may be completely incorporated with the eggs
8. When it is so, put it in a buttered dish and set it over hot embers or ashes
9. strew powdered sugar over the top and color it with a hot shovel
10. Serve as soon as possible, as it soon falls

title: Omelette Souffle in a Mould
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six fresh eggs
- three spoonfuls of rice flour
- a tablespoonful of orange-flower water
- butter

## Directions

1. Break six fresh eggs, separate the whites from the yolks
2. put with the yolks three spoonfuls of rice flour and a tablespoonful of orange-flower water
3. stir these well together
4. whip the whites of the eggs to a high froth
5. mix them with the yolks
6. Pour the mixture into a buttered mould, about half full
7. bake it in a moderate oven for half an hour
8. When done turn it on to a dish and serve quickly

title: Omelette Soufflé
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 6 eggs
- dessertspoonful potato flour
- 1 dessertspoonful orange flower water
- salt
- whites of 6 eggs
- 2 ounces butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put the yolks of 6 eggs in a large basin
2. Add a dessertspoonful of potato flour and 1 dessertspoonful of orange flower water
3. Add a very little salt
4. Stir these together with a wooden spoon for about 10 minutes
5. Whip the 6 whites and mix them in lightly with the batter
6. Put 2 ounces of butter into a saucepan and set it on the fire
7. As soon as the butter begins to sputter, pour the batter into it
8. Set the pan over the fire and as the batter becomes partially set round the sides and at the bottom, toss it over gently
9. Turn the omelette out neatly in the form of a dome on a silver dish greased with butter
10. Put it in the oven and bake it for about 12 minutes
11. Shake some sugar over it and serve immediately

title: One, Two, Three, Four Cup Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Butter
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 4 Eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream.
2. Add the sugar.
3. Beat till very light.
4. Beat the eggs (yolks and whites together) till very light, and add them gradually to the butter and sugar, beating all the while.
5. Beat vigorously.
6. Add the sifted flour, and beat thoroughly until very light.
7. Line a round cake pan with the buttered paper, and fill with the mixture.
8. Set in a moderate oven, and bake for one hour and a quarter.

title: Onion Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- fresh butter
- eggs
- milk
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel and slice 10 or 12 mild onions, and fry them in fresh butter
2. Drain them well when you take them up
3. Mince them as fine as possible
4. Beat 4 eggs until very thick and light
5. Stir them gradually into 1 pint of milk in turn with the minced onions
6. Season the whole with plenty of grated nutmeg, and stir it very hard
7. Put into a deep white dish, and bake it about 1 hour
8. Send it to table as a side dish, to be eaten with poultry

title: Onion Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 large onion minced very fine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs as for a plain omelet
2. Stew the onions in the butter till quite tender
3. Stir in the omelet once
4. Let it cook as in a plain omelet
5. Roll in folds and serve hot

title: Onion Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 dozen onions
- salt
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Boil the onions till quite tender, with a little salt in the water
2. Take them out and chop them fine
3. Stew them in a small quantity of sweet cream

title: Onion Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 onions
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- Lemon juice
- Salt and pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the onions until quite tender, adding salt and pepper
2. When soft, mash well and pass through a sieve
3. Take one spoon of butter and one of flour and melt, blending together without burning, or allowing to brown
4. In this cream dissolve the puree of onions, boiling gently for ten minutes and stirring well
5. Add the juice of a lemon, a teaspoon of vinegar, and serve with cutlets of lamb, fried sweetbreads, etc.

title: Onion Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Large Onions, Sliced Very Thin or Chopped
- 1/4 Cup of Flour
- 1 Large Spoon of Butter
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 2 Large Potatoes, Mashed Fine
- Dice of Bread or Toast
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Fry the onions in the butter, until reddish brown.
2. Then add the flour and stir until browned, gently; do not burn.
3. Put the boiling water in gradually, stirring perfectly smooth, and adding the salt and pepper; mix well and boil one minute.
4. Then pour it into the kettle and set back.
5. Before serving, add the milk warmed, and rubbed with mashed potatoes until they are a smooth paste.
6. Simmer a few moments.
7. Have the pieces of toast ready in the tureen and pour in the hot soup.

title: Onion Stuffing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Stale Bread, Wet and Squeezed Thoroughly
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Each of Chopped Parsley and Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Large Onion, Chopped Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for bread stuffing, using also one large onion, chopped very fine, and mixed thoroughly

title: Onions Boiled and Fried
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- water
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Trim and peel them
2. Boil them in water until quite tender
3. Dish them
4. Season with salt, pepper and butter

title: Onions Boiled and Fried
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- lard
- beefsteak gravy

## Directions

1. Trim and peel them, and boil in water until quite tender, then dish them
2. Season with salt, pepper and butter
3. Or cut in slices and fry a light brown
4. Wash in two or three waters after being sliced, then put into hot lard and some of the beefsteak gravy, fry gently until a light color, and serve around the steak

title: Orange and Lemon Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One hundred oranges
- Ten gallons of boiling water
- Twenty-five pounds of lump sugar
- A few tablespoonfuls of good yeast

## Directions

1. Take the outer rind of one hundred oranges pared, so that no white appears
2. Pour upon them ten gallons of boiling water, let it stand ten hours and keep slightly warm
3. While still warm, add the juice of the oranges, mixed with twenty-five pounds of lump sugar, and a few tablespoonfuls of good yeast
4. Let it ferment five days, or until the fermentation has ceased, and the wine is clear
5. Bottle it

title: Orange Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Louisiana Oranges
- The Yolks of 5 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/2 Box Gelatine, or 1/2 Pint

## Directions

1. Whip the cream and prepare the gelatine according to general directions.
2. Set the milk to boil, and when it begins to boil, add the gelatine.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs very light, and add the sugar, beating very light.
4. Strain the milk and gelatine, and add the sugar and yolks stirring constantly till well mixed.
5. Set over the fire for two minutes, and then set it away to cool.
6. When cold, pour in the orange juice, which you will strain through a sieve.
7. Set the basin in a pan of cracked ice, and stir until it begins to thicken.
8. Stir in the whipped cream very carefully, and proceed to turn into a mold to harden.
9. Always wet the mold first with cold water before turning in the cream.
10. When cold, serve with whipped cream.

title: Orange Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- The Zest of 1 Orange
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 12 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Orange

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar to a cream.
2. Add the flour gradually, and the juice and zest of the orange.
3. Beat well.
4. Bake on paper sheets, in the form of small wafers, very thinly spread, in a moderate oven, to a light color.

title: Orange Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ripe oranges
- 1 lb. of the best loaf sugar
- 1 lb. of good butter
- 14 ounces of flour
- 10 eggs

## Directions

1. Roll the oranges under your hand on the table till quite soft
2. Break up the loaf sugar, and on some of the pieces rub off the yellow rind of the oranges
3. Cut the oranges and squeeze the juice through a strainer
4. Powder the sugar and mix the orange juice with it
5. Wash and squeeze the butter in a pan of cold water till you have extracted all the salt and milk
6. Cut up the butter in the pan of sugar and orange, and stir it hard till perfectly light
7. Sift the flour into a pan
8. Beat the eggs very light and thick
9. Stir them by degrees into the butter and sugar, alternately with the flour, a little of each at a time
10. Beat the whole very hard for some time after all the ingredients are in
11. Put the mixture into a large, shallow, square pan, well buttered
12. Set immediately into a brisk oven until properly baked

title: Orange Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Ripe Oranges
- 1 Pound Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 14 Ounces of Flour
- 10 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Wash the oranges. Then dry and roll them under your hand on a board till very soft
2. Grate the zest of the oranges, and then cut up the fruit fine, and squeeze through a strainer till all the juice is extracted
3. Wash the butter till you extract all the salt, and then beat it to a cream
4. Add the sugar, and beat thoroughly, and then add the orange zest and juice, and beat till very light
5. Add the yolks of the eggs, well-beaten, and again beat the mixture till light
6. Then, by degrees, add the flour, adding alternately the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
7. Beat the whole for a half hour, and then bake in a moderate oven for forty-five minutes or an hour, using the broom-wisp test
8. If you ice the cake, add orange juice to the icing

title: Orange Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients for queen cake (omitting wine, brandy and rose water)
- grated yellow rind of 4 large, ripe oranges
- juice of 4 large, ripe oranges

## Directions

1. Make a mixture precisely as for queen cake, only omit the wine, brandy and rose water
2. Substitute the grated yellow rind and the juice of 4 large, ripe oranges
3. Stir into the batter in turn with the eggs and flour
4. Flavor the icing with orange juice

title: Orange Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 large cup of brown sugar
- molasses enough to roll thin
- a great deal of orange peel, well powdered

## Directions

1. mix it with molasses enough to roll thin
2. season with orange peel, well powdered

title: Orange Cheese-Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- peel of four oranges
- half a pound of sugar
- one-quarter of a pound of butter
- yolks of six eggs
- juice of the oranges
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Boil the peel of four oranges in two waters, to take out the bitter taste
2. When tender, pound up with half a pound of sugar, one-quarter of a pound of butter, and the yolks of six eggs
3. Beat the mixture well and add the juice of the oranges; if the oranges are large the juice of two will be sufficient to make two pies
4. Put puff paste in your pans, fill them half full of the confection, and bake lightly

title: Orange Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of four oranges
- peel of one orange
- a pint of water
- half a pound of sugar
- beaten whites of five eggs
- yolks of five eggs
- a cup of cream or sweet milk

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice of four oranges, and put it with the peel of one into a sauce-pan
2. Add to this a pint of water, half a pound of sugar, and the beaten whites of five eggs
3. Mix carefully, place it over a gentle fire, or it will curdle, stir it in one direction until it looks thick
4. Strain it through a gauze sieve
5. Add to it, when cold, the yolks of five eggs, and a cup of cream or sweet milk
6. Set it on the fire until hot enough to cook the eggs, or nearly ready to boil them
7. Take it off, stir until cold, and set it on ice, or freeze it as you choose

title: Orange Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 4 oranges
- rinds of 2 oranges grated
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 1/2 pint cream

## Directions

1. Take the juice of 4 oranges, and the rinds of 2, grated, the yolks of 8 eggs
2. Mix it well together and stir it over a chafing dish of coals
3. Add 1/2 a pint of cream, and stir it all one way

title: Orange Cream Bonbons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Orange Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 3/4 Gill of Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. Work the orange sugar, using one ounce, with the whites of the eggs and make a Royal Icing
2. Work the gum, Orange Flower Water, using half a gill, and the icing sugar into a stiff paste
3. Proceed as in Chocolate Creams, finally covering with the Royal Icing with the white cream forming the interior of the Bonbons and the orange or yellow the outside

title: Orange Flower and Rose Conserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Rose Water
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Orange Petals
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Sugar

## Directions

1. Take two ounces of powdered orange flower petals, one and one-half pounds of sugar, and eight ounces of rose water.
2. Steep the petals in distilled water, and then add the sugar, and set to heat over a moderate fire.
3. Add the rose water.
4. When it begins to jelly turn into a jelly glass.

title: Orange Flower Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- % Pound of Sugar
- 1 Ounce of Candled Orange Flowers
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Bruise the orange flowers, and mix and stir them with the boiling milk
2. Take from the fire, and add the sugar, and dissolve well, and then the beaten yolks of the eggs, mix well
3. Freeze in the manner above indicated

title: Orange Flower Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Orange Blossoms
- 10 Pints of Brandy
- 4 Pounds of Sugar
- 1¾ Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Steep two pounds of orange blossoms, well bruised and pounded, in ten and a half pints of good brandy for fifteen days.
2. Add a syrup made of four pounds of sugar and one and three-quarters pints of water.
3. Filter and bottle.

title: Orange Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Large Louisiana Oranges
- Creole Fritter Batter
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Pare the oranges, taking off every particle of the white inner skin.
2. Then slice nicely, and take out the seeds.
3. Sprinkle with sugar, and let them stand for an hour.
4. Then add the juice to the fritter batter, and proceed as in Apple Fritters.
5. Alternatively, place the oranges thus sliced over a slow fire, add a little water and several tablespoonfuls of sugar, and let them cook till you have a light marmalade.
6. Take it off, let it cool, cover the slices of orange well with the jellied juice, dip in the batter, and proceed as directed in recipe for Apple Fritters.

title: Orange Granit
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Oranges
- 1.5 Pints of Orange Juice
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Peel six oranges, removing every particle of pulp.
2. Slice nicely and place in a dish, and sprinkle over them the granulated sugar.
3. Set aside for several hours to make a syrup.
4. Squeeze the juice from the remaining six oranges and add to the syrup formed from the other oranges, which must be carefully drained of all syrup.
5. Add the water; mix well and strain.
6. Then turn into a freezer and freeze till a light frosting appears.
7. Serve in small glasses.

title: Orange Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 12 Oranges
- The Grated Rind of 2 Oranges, Rubbed on Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the milk or cream in a farina boiler
2. Take off the fire, and while hot pour this over the yolks of the eggs, which have been beaten to a thick cream, with the sugar; add orange juice and rind of the oranges
3. Stir till all begins to thicken
4. Then add the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
5. Set to cool
6. Turn into the freezer, and proceed in the manner above indicated

title: Orange Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- 1/2 can of Gelatine
- 1 quart of water

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Lemon Jelly.

title: Orange Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 8 oranges
- 6 lemons
- peel of the fruit
- 1 pint of cold water
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 1/2 ounces of isinglass
- beaten whites of 7 eggs

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of the fruit and steep it in a pint of cold water
2. When the flavor is extracted, mix the water with the juice of 8 oranges and 6 lemons
3. Add 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar, 1 1/2 ounces of isinglass, and the beaten whites of 7 eggs
4. Put all into a saucepan and stir till it boils
5. Let it boil a few minutes
6. Strain it till clear through a jelly bag
7. Put it into a mould or glasses

title: Orange Jelly, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Louisiana oranges
- 1/2 box of Gelatine
- 1/2 gill of Curacao
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Extract the juice of the oranges.
2. Soak the gelatine, if prepared, and set the bowl in boiling water till the gelatine melts.
3. Add the juice of the orange, and add sugar to taste.
4. Add a wineglassful of the best Curacao, and strain the jelly through a coarse piece of swig.
5. Set in a mold to harden.

title: Orange Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Pound or Cup Cake
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 3 Cups of Sugar
- The Grated Rind and Juice of 1 Orange
- Juice of Half a Lemon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Orange Extract

## Directions

1. Make a good layer, pound or cup cake
2. Then make a filling of the whites of two eggs, three cups of sugar, the grated rind and juice of one orange, half a lemon's juice, two teaspoonfuls of orange extract
3. Mix all thoroughly, and spread between the layers of the cake

title: Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oranges
- 2 Lemons
- ¾ of a Pound of Sugar for every Pound of Fruit

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe Louisiana oranges.
2. Remove the rind and grate it.
3. Grate the pulp, preserving carefully every particle of juice.
4. Extract the seeds.
5. Allow the grated rind and juice of two lemons to every six oranges.
6. Weigh the whole and allow for every pound of fruit three-fourths of a pound of sugar.
7. Cook the whole, adding the grated rind of the oranges.
8. When done turn into jelly tumblers.
9. Test by turning a portion on a plate.
10. When it begins to jelly, which will be in about twenty-five minutes, turn into the tumblers and seal.

title: Orange Marmalade
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, large, ripe oranges with thin deep colored skins
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar to each lb. of oranges
- cold water
- beaten white of egg, allowing 1 white of egg to every 2 lbs. of sugar
- 1/4 pint of clear water to each pound of sugar
- brandy paper

## Directions

1. Weigh oranges and allow 1 lb. of loaf sugar to each lb. of oranges
2. Pare off the yellow outside of the rind from half of the oranges as thin as possible
3. Put the rind into a pan with plenty of cold water
4. Cover it closely to keep in the steam and boil it slowly until it is so soft that the head of a pin will pierce it
5. Grate the rind from the remaining oranges
6. Take out all the pulp and juice, removing the seeds and core
7. Put the sugar into a preserving kettle with 1/4 pint of clear water to each pound
8. Mix it with some beaten white of egg, allowing 1 white of egg to every 2 lbs. of sugar
9. When the sugar is all dissolved, put it on the fire and boil and skim it till it is quite clear and thick
10. Take the boiled parings and pound them to a paste in a mortar
11. Put this paste into the sugar and boil and stir it 10 minutes
12. Put it into the pulp and juice of the oranges and the grated rind
13. Boil all together for 1/4 hour till it is a transparent mass
14. When cold, put it up in glass jars with brandy paper on the top

title: Orange Marmalade Made With Honey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe oranges
- Strained honey
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Quarter a dozen large ripe oranges
2. Remove the rind, seeds and filaments, but save all the juice
3. Put the juice and pulp into a porcelain kettle, with an equal quantity of strained honey, adding one-third as much sugar as honey
4. Boil until very thick, sweet and clear
5. When cold, put it in small jars

title: Orange Marmalade Made with Honey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A dozen large ripe oranges
- Strained honey in equal quantity to juice and pulp
- One-third as much sugar as honey

## Directions

1. Quarter a dozen large ripe oranges
2. Remove the rind, seeds and filaments, but save all the juice
3. Put the juice and pulp into a porcelain kettle, with an equal quantity of strained honey, adding one-third as much sugar as honey
4. Boil until very thick, sweet and clear
5. When cold, put it in small jars

title: Orange Marmalade, Delicious
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Cold water
- White sugar
- Peel

## Directions

1. Quarter the oranges and take out the seeds and white strings
2. To every pound of pulp, add a cup of cold water, and let it stand thus for twenty-four hours
3. Boil some of the peel in several waters until quite tender
4. To each pound of pulp, add one-quarter of a pound of boiled peel, and one and a quarter pounds of white sugar
5. Boil this slowly until it jellies, and the bits of peel are quite transparent

title: Orange or Lemon Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon or Orange

## Directions

1. Make a Plain Icing, stirring in at the start the juice of one lemon or one orange
2. Proceed as in Plain Icing

title: Orange Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- The Strained Juice of 2 Oranges
- 1/4 Ounce of Orange Sugar
- 3 1/4 Ounces of Coarse Sugar

## Directions

1. To make the orange sugar, rub the rind of the oranges on the sugar and dry
2. Then proceed to add the water and three and a quarter ounces of sugar and flavoring
3. As soon as it begins to dissolve in the pan, take a small wooden spoon and stir it for a few minutes
4. Then remove it from the fire, stirring continuously, and drop the pastilles upon a sheet of paper, cutting them off in drops about the size of a pea with a curved piece of wire or a hairpin
5. When they are quite dry, turn the paper upside down, brush the reverse side with a paste brush slightly moistened with water, and shake off the pastilles with the point of a knife upon a dry sieve
6. Then move the sieve to and fro over a very slow fire, so that all the moisture may be removed from the drops, and keep them in well-bottled jars

title: Orange Peel Syrup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- peel of 3 sweet oranges
- 3 pints of cold water
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Put the peel of 3 sweet oranges in 3 pints of cold water
2. Boil down to 1 quart
3. Add 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
4. Boil together

title: Orange Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and sliced pulp of two large oranges
- grated yellow rind of one orange
- yolks of three eggs
- a cupful of sugar
- whites of three eggs
- a cup of milk
- puff paste

## Directions

1. To the juice and sliced pulp of two large oranges, add the grated yellow rind of one orange
2. Beat the yolks of three eggs with a cupful of sugar
3. Beat the whites to a high froth and add to them a cup of milk
4. Mix all the above together
5. Have ready a nice puff paste, and bake the mixture in it

title: Orange Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Louisiana Oranges and Their Juice
- The Rind of 1 Orange, Grated
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoontul of Cornstarch
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 3/4 Teacupful of Boiling Water

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Blend the cornstarch with sufficient cold water to moisten and mix well, and stir into the teacup of water, which should be boiling in a saucepan
3. Let it cook, stirring constantly, for two minutes only
4. Add the butter and sugar, and stir well
5. Remove from the fire, and add the well-beaten yolks of two eggs
6. Line the pie plates with pie crust and bake the under crust
7. Add the custard, and let it get slightly brown in the oven
8. Spread over the whites of two eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, with two tablespoonfuls of fine white powdered sugar
9. Let it bake for about three minutes longer in a quick oven, and serve cold

title: Orange Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- fine white powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges
2. Slice nicely and weigh
3. Allow equal quantities of fine white powdered sugar
4. Place the fruit in a deep dish, and sprinkle the sugar over it, between the slices, in alternate layers
5. Let it remain over night
6. Drain off the syrup in the morning, and put it in a kettle
7. When it gets very hot put in the fruit
8. Let it cook slowly till the slices look very clear
9. Skim them out and put them in the jars
10. Fill to overflowing and then seal

title: Orange Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Large Louisiana Oranges
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pound of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Gill of Sherry and Brandy, Mixed
- 1 Stale, Small Sponge Cake
- 6 Eggs

## Directions

1. Grate the rind of the oranges, and squeeze out the juice after removing the seeds
2. Grate the inner meat
3. Beat the butter and sugar to a thick cream, and then add the wine
4. Add these to the oranges, grated with the rind
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and the yolks separately
6. Add them in order to the mixture
7. Place in a moderately quick oven, and let it bake for about a half hour
8. When done, let it cool slightly
9. Place a meringue on top, as in above recipe, and set back to bake two minutes more
10. Serve cold

title: Orange Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one large orange of a deep color and smooth, thin rind
- 1 lime
- ½ lb. of powdered loaf sugar
- ½ lb. of fresh butter
- 8 eggs
- 1 glass of mixed wine and brandy
- 1 teaspoonfal of rose water
- 5 ounces of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Have ready a sheet of puff paste made of 5 ounces of sifted flour and ½ lb. of fresh butter
2. Lay the paste in a buttered dish
3. Trim and notch the edges
4. Put in the mixture
5. Bake it 1 hour in a moderate oven
6. Grate loaf sugar over it before you send it to table

title: Orange Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow rinds of 2 fine oranges
- ½ lb. of sugar
- ½ lb. of butter, free of salt
- yolks of 16 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Pare the yellow rinds of 2 fine oranges very thin
2. Beat it extremely fine in a marble mortar
3. Add to it ½ lb. of sugar, ½ lb. of butter, free of salt
4. Beat the yolks of 16 eggs very light, and add to them the other ingredients
5. Mix and beat until they are all of a color
6. Lay a puff paste with a double rim in a dish
7. Pour in the pudding and bake it

title: Orange Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 fine oranges
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of butter, free of salt
- yolks of 16 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Pare the yellow rinds of 2 fine oranges very thin
2. Beat it extremely fine in a marble mortar
3. Add to it 1/4 lb. of sugar
4. Beat the yolks of 16 eggs very light, and add to them the other ingredients
5. Mix and beat until they are all of a color
6. Lay a puff paste with a double rim in a dish
7. Pour in the pudding and bake it

title: Orange Punch, New Orleans Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Glass of Rum
- ¾ Glass of Best Brandy
- 2 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Pound of Loaf Sugar
- 1 Small Glass of Cherry Bounce
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water, and the rum and brandy
2. Add the juice of six oranges, and the grated peel of three
3. Let all infuse for one hour
4. Then set to cool
5. When ready to serve add one small glassful of Cherry Bounce
6. Serve in small glass cups

title: Orange Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Oranges
- The Zest of 3 Oranges
- 1 Pint of Brandy
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the zest of three fine Louisiana oranges, very, very fine.
2. Place this oil or zest which you will have rubbed on one pound of powdered sugar in a jar.
3. Add one pint of good brandy or alcohol, and the juice of three fine oranges, from which you will have taken out the seeds.
4. Let the infusion stand for three days.
5. Filter and bottle for use.

title: Orange Roly Poly
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Dough from Roly Poly recipe
- White Sugar
- Lemon or Orange Sauce

## Directions

1. Seed the oranges and lay them thinly sliced all over the sheet of dough.
2. Sprinkle lightly with white sugar, and roll up and fasten closely in the pudding cloth.
3. Boil for one hour, and eat with Lemon or Orange Sauce.

title: Orange Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Louisiana Oranges
- 3/4 Pound of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Glass of Rum or Brandy

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges whole, removing the peel entirely down to the tips of the stem end.
2. Cut in slices, and cut out the seeds.
3. Pile the oranges in a neat heap in a dish.
4. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.
5. Boil some sugar to a syrup, using about half a pound to half a pound of oranges.
6. Add a glass of rum or brandy, and, when cold, pour over the oranges and serve.

title: Orange Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1/4 Cup of Water
- The Juice and Grated Zest of 2 Oranges
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar, butter and yolks of the eggs to a cream
2. Then add the water and the juice and grated zest of two oranges
3. Set to boil in a double boiler; add, if desired, when it begins to simmer well, one tablespoonful of wine
4. Take off the fire and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a froth
5. Serve either hot or cold
6. If served cold, add the whites of the eggs at the moment of serving, as also the grated nutmeg
7. Serve with Orange Pudding, Orange Roly-Poly, etc.

title: Orange Sherbet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- juice from a dozen oranges
- boiling water
- peel from oranges
- boiling water and sugar (a pint to a pound)

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from a dozen oranges
2. Pour boiling water on the peel and cover it closely
3. Boil water and sugar (a pint to a pound) to a syrup and skim it clear
4. When all are cold, mix the syrup, juice and peel with as much water as may be necessary to make a rich orangeade
5. Strain it and set the vessel containing it on ice

title: Orange Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Large Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Quart of Water
- The Juice of 2 Lemons
- The Zest of 3 Oranges
- 1 Pint of Sugar

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and water to boil.
2. Grate the zest or yellow rind of three oranges, and add to the syrup, and let it boil for five minutes.
3. Then set away to cool.
4. Extract the juice from the oranges and lemons, and grate the fruit.
5. Mix this with the syrup, and then strain all through a thin muslin cloth.
6. Turn into the freezer, and proceed as in Ice Cream.

title: Orange Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Louisiana Oranges
- Snow Cream

## Directions

1. Slice the oranges, and removing the seeds, sprinkle sugar over them before adding the snow cream.
2. Prepare this dish in exactly the same manner as Pineapple Snow.

title: Orange Syrup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe and thin-skinned oranges
- A pound and a quarter of white sugar to every pint of juice

## Directions

1. Select ripe and thin-skinned fruit
2. Squeeze the juice, and to every pint, add a pound and a quarter of white sugar
3. Boil it slowly, and skim as long as any scum rises
4. Take it off, let it grow cold, and bottle it
5. Secure the corks well

title: Orange Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Orange Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1/4 Ounce of Lemon Juice

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Lemon Syrup, and bottle when almost cold.

title: Orange Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Pure cider vinegar
- Fine white sugar in the proportion of one and two-thirds pounds to every quart of juice

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges
2. Slice them very thin and cover with pure cider vinegar, and let them stand for three or four days
3. Mash them through a cloth, straining as long as the juice runs clear
4. To every quart of juice allow one and two-thirds pounds of fine white sugar
5. Boil all together for about ten minutes, and skim very carefully, until no scum rises upon the surface
6. Take the mixture from the fire, and when it cools somewhat, bottle it
7. Put a tablespoonful of this extract in a glass of cold ice water in warm weather

title: Orange Transparent Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Pound of Fine White Sugar
- Half the White of 1 Egg
- The Zest of 1 Orange
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Essence or Orange Juice

## Directions

1. Make Orange Transparent Icing by flavoring with a little orange sugar, rubbing against the zest of the orange, or tinging with Saffron and flavoring with Curaçao

title: Orange Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 Oranges
- 1 Quart of Good French Cider Vinegar
- Sugar in proportion of two-thirds of a pound to 1 quart of orange syrup

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges
2. Slice them very thin and cover with pure cider vinegar
3. Let them stand for three or four days
4. Mash them through a cloth, straining as long as the juice runs clear
5. To every quart of juice allow one and two-thirds pounds of fine white sugar
6. Boil all together for about ten minutes
7. Skim very carefully, until no scum rises upon the surface
8. Take the mixture from the fire, and when it cools somewhat, bottle it

title: Orange Wafers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- best oranges
- water
- loaf sugar (1 1/2 lbs. per lb. of pulp)

## Directions

1. Take the best oranges and cut them in half
2. Take out the seeds and juice
3. Boil them in 3 or 4 waters (always hot) until they are tender
4. Beat them to pulp in a marble mortar and rub them through a hair sieve
5. To 1 lb. of this pulp allow 1 1/2 lbs. of loaf sugar
6. Take 1/2 of the sugar and boil it with the oranges till it becomes ropy
7. Take it from the fire, and when cold, make it up in paste with the other 1/2 of the sugar
8. Make but a little at a time, for it will dry fast
9. With a rolling pin roll them out as thin as tiffany, upon paper
10. Cut them round with a wineglass
11. Let them dry, and they will look clear

title: Orange Water
body:
## Ingredients

- zest of 6 Louisiana oranges
- 4 ounces of orange blossoms
- 7 pints of brandy
- 4 pounds of fine white sugar
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Macerate the zests of the oranges and orange blossoms and orange juice in the brandy for eight days
2. Distill in a bain-marie, or water bath
3. Add a syrup made of four pounds of sugar and three pints of water
4. Strain and filter, and bottle for use

title: Orange Whips
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 oranges
- gelatine
- whites of 2 or 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Strain the juice of 6 or 8 oranges, sweetened to taste
2. Dissolve 1 box of gelatine
3. Put the orange juice to it
4. Allow it to jelly slightly
5. Beat the whites of 2 or 3 eggs to a froth
6. Mix all well together
7. Fill moulds, and set on ice

title: Orangeade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oranges
- juice of 1 lemon
- 4 ounces of sugar
- 1 1/2 pints of water

## Directions

1. Use ripe, juicy Louisiana oranges
2. Peel one orange and cut it in thin slices lengthwise, and then put it into a vessel with four ounces of sugar and a pint and a half of water
3. Squeeze the juice of the lemon and two more oranges into another vessel, and stir well
4. Pour it on the sliced orange
5. Let it stand for a few minutes, and it is ready to use
6. Strain if you desire

title: Oranges, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Louisiana Oranges
- % Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges and cut in slices
2. Sprinkle with sugar, to form a syrup
3. Let them stand for several hours in a cool place
4. Serve either as a preliminary to breakfast or as a dessert

title: Orgeat Paste or Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Peeled Almonds
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. To one pound of skinned almonds add one pound of sugar
2. Pound as directed to a pulp in a mortar, occasionally adding a few drops of water to prevent the almonds from running to oil
3. Use Orange Flower Water
4. Add fine white sifted sugar till you have sufficient to make a paste
5. Work till well-amalgamated and elastic

title: Orgeat Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sweet Almonds
- 4 Ounces of Bitter Almonds
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Water
- 2 Ounces of Orange Flower Water
- The Zest of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Throw the almonds into cold water after shelling them until the peeling or skin comes off easily
2. Mash them and pound them in a mortar till they are reduced to a fine powder, adding from time to time a little water and the well-grated zest of the lemon
3. Moisten the paste with one-half of the water, and then squeeze it hard through a linen cloth
4. Return the paste to the mortar, throw over the rest of the water, mix thoroughly, pounding well, and then squeeze again through the towel
5. Put the sugar into a farina boiler, and let it boil to the degree of Le Petit Cassé, or crack, that is, till it forms a thick syrup that will not cling to the teeth in tasting, or instantly snaps asunder between the fingers after testing in cold water
6. Take the syrup off the fire and add the milk of almonds, stirring well
7. Return it to the fire, and let it simmer gently till it begins to boil
8. Let it boil for several minutes
9. Take it off the fire and let it cool
10. When cold, add the orange flower water and mix well
11. Drain all through a cloth or bag, and fill and seal the bottles
12. Shake the bottles daily to preserve the exact mingling of the mixture

title: Orgeat Syrup Without Orange Flowers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A pound of sugar to every pint of water
- A gill of orange water, or rose water, to every four pounds of sugar used
- Two tablespoonfuls of pure essence of bitter almonds to every four pounds of sugar used

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of a pound of sugar to every pint of water
2. Boil this a few minutes, skim it clear
3. When cold, to every four pounds of sugar used, allow a gill of orange water, or rose water, and two tablespoonfuls of pure essence of bitter almonds
4. Serve it in iced water

title: Ornamental Dish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- jelly or blanc mange
- 3 lemons
- 4 eggs
- blanc mange

## Directions

1. Make the foundation of the nest of jelly or blanc mange
2. Rasp the skin of 3 lemons and preserve it
3. Lay it around and on the jelly like straw
4. Take out the contents of 4 eggs through a small hole
5. Fill the shells with blanc mange
6. When cold, break off the shells and lay the mange eggs in the nest

title: Ox Tails à la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ox Tails
- 2 Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Turnip
- ½ Can of Green Peas
- ¼ Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Pint of Consommé or Boiling Water
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine or Water

## Directions

1. Cut the ox tails into pieces of three or four inches in length
2. Chop two onions fine, and put the whole into a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter
3. Let them brown a little and add two large carrots, cut into dice, and one turnip cut the same way
4. Brown these with the ox tails
5. Add one-half inch of ham, well chopped and let it brown, and then add two sprigs of thyme and one bay leaf and one clove of garlic, chopped very fine
6. Let these ingredients all brown about two minutes over a hot fire
7. Then add one glass of Sherry wine or water
8. Let all brown two minutes longer, and add one pint of consommé or boiling water
9. Season again to taste, and add a half can of green peas
10. Let all boil until the ox tails are tender to the touch, and serve hot

title: Ox-Tail Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ox Tail
- A Bunch of Soup Herbs
- 1 Head of Celery
- 4 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Large Onion
- 2 Carrots
- 3 Cloves
- A Sprig of Parsley
- A Small Slice of Lean Ham or Beef
- Salt and Pepper
- A tablespoonful of lard
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 5 Quarts of Consomme or boiling water
- 3 tablespoonfuls of barley
- Cayenne
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sherry wine

## Directions

1. Cut the tail in pieces from the joint, and then cut again into the size of a peanut, or one inch and a half in length
2. Chop the onions very fine
3. Put the onion and a tablespoonful of lard into a frying pan and add the ox tail
4. Cook slowly until it begins to brown
5. Add the carrot, cut in pieces about the size of a green pea, and about a square inch of ham, chopped very fine
6. Let this brown
7. When it begins to brown nicely, add the thyme, bay leaf, three cloves, one clove of garlic, all chopped very fine
8. Let this continue to brown, being careful not to burn
9. Add one tablespoonful of flour, dredged in lightly and stirred
10. When all is nicely browned, add about five quarts of Consomme, if you have it; if not, five quarts of boiling water and three tablespoonfuls of barley
11. Let it cook together about four hours, simmering gently
12. Season with salt, pepper and cayenne to taste
13. When ready to serve, add two tablespoonfuls of sherry wine
14. Serve one joint to each person

title: Ox-Tail Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ox-tails
- carrots
- turnips
- onions
- celery
- cloves
- mace
- sugar
- red pepper
- vegetables (peas, carrots, turnips, celery)

## Directions

1. Cut each joint of two ox-tails with a meat-saw, steep them in water for two hours; then place them in a stew-pan with three carrots, three turnips, three onions, two heads of celery, four cloves, and a blade of mace.
2. Fill up the stew-pan from the boiling stock-pot; boil this over a slow fire until done and the joints quite tender.
3. Take them out, cool them, and clarify the broth.
4. Strain this into a soup-pot, put with it the pieces of ox-tail, some olive shaped pieces of carrot and turnip which have been boiled in a little of the broth;
5. Add to this when it has boiled half an hour a small lump of sugar and a little red pepper.
6. This soup is excellent, and may be served with any kind of vegetables strained in it, such as puree of peas, carrots, turnips, or celery.

title: Oyster Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Chopped Oysters
- 2 Pints of Chopped Sausage Meat
- 1 Egg
- Grated Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. To every pint of chopped oysters add one pint of chopped sausage meat.
2. Roll in bread crumbs; season highly.
3. Add one egg and roll in bread crumbs.
4. Make into small cakes and fry In boiling lard.
5. Serve hot.

title: Oyster Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Hollandaise Sauce
- Parsley Sprigs

## Directions

1. Blanch the oysters and then mince very fine.
2. Mix with two tablespoonfuls of Hollandaise Sauce, and then spread over thin strips of Buttered toast.
3. Sprinkle lightly with Parsley, which has been grated so fine as to be almost a dust.
4. Put a bit of butter on top of each Canapé, set in the oven for a few minutes, and send to the table hot.

title: Oyster Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 3 Gill of Rich Cream
- 1 Gill of Oyster Liquor
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne
- A Well Beaten Egg
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Boiling Lard

## Directions

1. Take two dozen oysters and boil them in their own liquor
2. Stir constantly and boil for about five minutes
3. Remove from the fire
4. Take out the oysters and chop very fine
5. Put them into a saucepan with about one gill each of rich cream and oyster liquor
6. Rub together two tablespoonfuls of flour and one of butter
7. Add this and the oysters to the boiling milk and cream
8. Stir until it thickens and boils
9. Add the yolk of two eggs
10. Stir this over the fire for about one minute, and then take off and add parsley, chopped fine, salt and Cayenne
11. Mix well and place in a dish to cool
12. Roll in a beaten egg to bind and form into cylinders of about a finger in length
13. Roll in bread crumbs mashed fine, and fry in boiling lard or oil

title: Oyster Croquettes à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Fine Oysters
- 1 Cup of the White Meat of a Chicken Minced
- 6 Finely Chopped Mushrooms
- 1 Teaspoonful of Onion Juice
- 3/4 Cup of Cream
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- Salt and Cayenne Pepper to Taste
- Butter

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters about three minutes in their own liquor. Drain and chop the oysters fine.
2. Take a half cup of the liquor in which the oysters have been boiled, set it on the fire and add the chopped oysters.
3. Then add the half cup of cream, the chopped mushrooms and the minced chicken.
4. Stir thoroughly into this boiling mixture the butter and flour which have been rubbed smoothly.
5. Add the chopped parsley, onion Juice, salt and Cayenne, and mix well.
6. Then add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten.
7. Let it cook about two minutes and turn it out into a dish to cool.
8. When cold, roll Into cylinders about two inches in length and one inch In diameter.
9. Pass through bread crumbs and fry in boiling lard.
10. Serve immediately on a bed of fried parsley.

title: Oyster Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Cup of Bread, Wet and Squeezed
- 1 Onion, Chopped Very Fine
- 1/4 Square Inch Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Sage
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf, Minced Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wet the soft of the bread and squeeze thoroughly till you have one cup, judging the quantity of stuffing always by the size of the fowl to be stuffed, and adding more in proportion, if needed.
2. Season the bread well with salt and pepper, and add the minced herbs, mixing well.
3. Take a tablespoonful of butter and put in the frying pan.
4. As it melts, add the onion, which must be chopped very fine.
5. Let this brown for about five minutes, and while frying add the bread and stir well.
6. Then add the square inch of ham, minced very fine.
7. Mix well and let all fry well.
8. Season again to taste.
9. Then add the two dozen oysters, cut in two, with all the hard portions taken off.
10. Mix all well, and fry for a few minutes longer.

title: Oyster Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 2 Cups of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Good Baking Powder
- Parsley or Asparagus Tips to Garnish

## Directions

1. Take two dozen large oysters, drain in a colander and remove any pieces of shell or grit that may adhere
2. Chop the oysters fine
3. Take two eggs and beat until very light
4. Add a cup of milk and rub in smoothly two cupfuls of flour and one teaspoonful of salt
5. Beat until perfectly smooth
6. Add one-half teaspoonful of good baking powder
7. Mix well and then drop in the oysters which must be dry
8. Drop into boiling lard or oil
9. When browned on one side, turn on the other, being careful not to use a fork or to pierce them
10. Use a skimmer in removing from the pot, and drain on brown paper
11. Serve on a dish in which you have placed a folded napkin and garnish with sprigs of parsley or asparagus tips

title: Oyster Gombo With Filee, No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a grown chicken
- fifty oysters
- a half-pound of ham

## Directions

1. Take a grown chicken, fifty oysters, and a half-pound of ham to flavor the gombo.
2. Cut up two onions fine, fry them in lard and thicken the gravy with flour; a teaspoonful will be enough.
3. Cut up the chicken and ham, and put them to fry with the onions.
4. Let all cook gently till brown, then put in a pint of boiling water and
5. boil the chicken until it is almost in pieces.
6. Half-an-hour before dinner pour in the oysters and their liquor.
7. When ready for the table take a large spoonful of fresh powdered sassafras leaves or filee, wet it with a little of the soup, and stir it into the soup.
8. If not thick or ropy enough, stir in another spoonful.
9. Do not let the soup boil after the filee is put in, but remove it from the fire, or serve it immediately.

title: Oyster Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Oysters
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Liquor
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Quart of Hot Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Large White Onion
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Filé

## Directions

1. Put the lard into a kettle, and when hot add the flour, making a brown roux.
2. When quite brown without burning, add the chopped onions and parsley.
3. Fry these, and when brown, add the chopped bay leaf.
4. Pour in the hot oyster liquor and then add the hot water.
5. When it comes to a good boil, just before serving, add the oysters which have been well drained, without pouring water over them.
6. Cook for about three minutes longer and take off the stove and stir gradually two tablespoonfuls of Filé into the boiling hot gumbo.
7. Have the tureen ready in a bain-marie, or hot-water bath, and pour in the gumbo and cover.
8. Bring to the table immediately and serve with boiled rice, allowing about six or eight oysters to each person.

title: Oyster Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- Delicate French Loaves of Bread
- 2 Dozen Oysters to a Loaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- Broiled or Creamed Oysters

## Directions

1. Take delicate French loaves of bread and cut off, lengthwise, the upper portion
2. Dig the crumbs out of the center of each piece, leaving the sides and bottom like a square box
3. Brush each corner of the box and the bottom with melted butter, and place in a quick oven to brown
4. Fill with broiled or creamed oysters
5. Cover with each other and serve

title: Oyster Pan Roast
body:
## Ingredients

- A Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sliced Lemon and Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters, heat a deep frying pan, drop in a generous lump of butter
2. When it melts, add the oysters, covering and shaking the pan constantly over a hot fire
3. Have ready a dish well buttered and nicely garnished with parsley and lemon slices
4. When the oysters are brown, turn quickly into the dish and add salt and pepper and melted butter, into which you have dropped finely chopped parsley, and serve hot

title: Oyster Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Oysters
- 3/4 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Small Onion, Grated
- Pepper, Salt, Chopped Parsley and Lemon Juice
- Flour
- Puff paste

## Directions

1. Prepare a puff paste (see recipe) and lay on the ice to cool.
2. Boil the oysters in their own liquor. Drain, put the butter in the saucepan, and when it is heated, add the grated onion and rub in the flour until smooth.
3. Add a gill of mushroom Juice and pepper, salt and Cayenne to taste, and the mushrooms chopped in quarters.
4. Then add the oysters and let all stew about five minutes, adding the lemon Juice.
5. A tablespoonful of cream will improve the oysters. If this is used, omit the lemon Juice.
6. Line the small tins with the puff paste and put In each three or four oysters according to the size of the pate.
7. Cover with the paste and bake in a quick oven about fifteen minutes.

title: Oyster Pickle. Very Easy And Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four dozen oysters
- pepper
- mace
- salt
- wine vinegar
- crackers
- fresh vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash four dozen oysters
2. Let them be fine and large, with plenty of their own liquor
3. Pick them carefully
4. Strain their liquor and to it add a dessertspoonful of pepper
5. Two blades of mace, a tablespoonful of salt, and a cup of strong wine vinegar
6. Simmer the oysters in this five minutes
7. Then put them in small jars
8. Boil the pickle again, and when cold add a cup of fresh vinegar
9. And fill up the jars, cork them, and set away for use

title: Oyster Pie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rich puff paste
- oysters
- eggs
- butter
- mace
- salt and pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a rich puff paste in the dish
2. Lay oysters over the bottom of the dish
3. Boil some eggs hard, and put 2 or 3 pieces with butter, mace, salt and pepper
4. Shake in a little flour
5. Add more oysters and eggs, till the dish is full
6. Cover with a crust and bake

title: Oyster Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Dozen Large, Fine Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 6 Yolks of Eggs
- Spices, Thyme, Bay Leaf, Parsley
- 1 Slice of Grated Bread
- Pie Crust
- Flour
- Cayenne
- Rich, hot cream or boiled milk

## Directions

1. Take an earthen dish which will hold about three and a half pints and line the sides with rich old crust. Set in the stove and let it bake a few minutes.
2. In the meantime, take about one pint and a half of the oyster liquor and put on the fire, after having drained well.
3. Add the seasonings of chopped herbs and Cayenne.
4. Rub a tablespoonful of flour into the butter and add to the liquor, stirring constantly.
5. Mash the grated crumbs; add to this, and mix well.
6. Chop the hard-boiled eggs fine.
7. Then pour the oysters into the pan of pie crust, sprinkle some of the chopped eggs and grated bread crumbs over, and put a teaspoonful of butter in small bits here and there over this.
8. Then roll out very fine and thin a layer of the pie crust.
9. Place this over the preparation and ornament here and there, all around, with neat notches or designs, which can be easily formed with the end of a spoon or the prongs of a fork.
10. Cut a hole in the center in the shape of the letter X.
11. Set in a moderately quick oven and bake till brown.
12. In the meantime, melt one tablespoonful of butter, add the remaining oyster liquor and season with pepper and salt.
13. When it is about to come to a boil, add one-half cup of rich, hot cream or boiled milk, and when the pie is nearly brown, put a funnel into the opening in the center and pour in as much of the liquor as the pie will hold.
14. Place a delicate garnish of pastry leaves over the whole and bake a minute or so longer.

title: Oyster Plant a la Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- A Bunch of Fresh Oyster Plant
- 1/2 Pint of Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the roots of the plant thoroughly.
2. Then throw them into cold water immediately as you scrape them, or they will turn black and appear unpalatable.
3. Have ready a kettle of boiling water, and cook for an hour, or longer, till tender.
4. Drain and serve with a Cream Sauce poured over.

title: Oyster Plant a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- A Bunch of Salsify or Oyster Plant
- 1/2 Pint of Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as in the manner indicated above, using instead of the Cream Sauce a Sauce a la Poulette.

title: Oyster Plant Sauted in Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- A Good Bunch of Oyster Plant
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- A Tablespoonful of Salt
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- A Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- A Pinch of Black Pepper
- Two Tablespoonfuls of the Best Butter
- A Quart of Cold Water

## Directions

1. Scrape the oyster plant well and throw into cold water into which put two tablespoonfuls of vinegar to prevent the salsify from turning black.
2. Take from the water, drain and cut into pieces one and a half inches in length.
3. Put into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of vinegar and flour well mixed and cover with a quart of cold water.
4. Add a tablespoonful of salt, cover well and let boil slowly for three quarters of an hour.
5. Drain and return to the saucepan and add two tablespoonfuls of the best butter, the pepper, chopped parsley, juice of a lemon, and a pinch of black pepper.
6. Mix well and let it heat for five minutes, tossing almost constantly.
7. Put into a deep dish and serve hot.

title: Oyster Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Large Oysters
- 2 Crisp Heads of Lettuce
- The Yolks of Three Eggs
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Mustard
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil about four dozen large oysters in their own liquor, season with salt and pepper.
2. Drain and set aside to cool.
3. Take two crisp heads of lettuce leaves and arrange nicely in the salad bowl.
4. Turn the oysters into the center of the leaves and pour over them the following dressing.
5. Take the yolks of three raw eggs, half a teaspoonful of mustard, and a little salt; beat these together until they begin to thicken, and add gradually olive oil, as in making Mayonnaise, until it begins to thicken.
6. Add a little vinegar to thin and serve with the oysters.

title: Oyster Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen oysters
- The oyster water
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Herb bouquet

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters in their own water
2. Add a nice herb bouquet while boiling
3. Take a tablespoonful of butter and one of flour and put into a saucepan, and mix well without browning
4. Water this with the juice of the oysters, sufficient to make one pint; season to taste
5. Let it boil for ten or fifteen minutes, and when it reaches a thick consistency, serve with freshly added oysters, taking the old ones out
6. This is a sauce for boiled fish, etc.

title: Oyster Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- oyster liquor
- 3 or 4 blades of mace
- melted butter
- thick cream or rich milk

## Directions

1. When the oysters are opened, take care of all the liquor, and boil them in it
2. take the oysters out and put to the liquor 3 or 4 blades of mace
3. add to it some melted butter, with thick cream, or rich milk
4. put in the oysters and give them a boil
5. when that is done take them off the fire

title: Oyster Sauce for Boiled Fowl or Turkey
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen large oysters
- their liquor
- a little water
- 1 or 2 spoonfuls of flour
- 2 ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put into a stewpan, with their liquor, 2 dozen large oysters and a little water
2. when it boils, take out the oysters with a silver spoon, and drain them on a hair sieve
3. let the liquor settle, and pour it off from the sediment
4. put it into a stewpan with 1 or 2 spoonfuls of flour, and 2 ounces of fresh butter
5. let it stand until the flour is a little fried
6. add the liquor, which must be made quite hot

title: Oyster Sauce For Boiled Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three dozen oysters
- half a pound of butter
- flour
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- a cup of cream

## Directions

1. Put three dozen oysters in a stewpan
2. Save their liquor in a bowl
3. Mix with the oysters half a pound of butter and flour, worked together
4. Season with cayenne pepper and salt
5. Thin this with the liquor from the oysters and a cup of cream
6. Let it boil ten minutes and serve on the turkey

title: Oyster Sausage
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of oysters
- 1/4 pound of veal
- 1/4 pound of suet
- bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- flour
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop oysters with veal and suet, and some bread crumbs
2. Add one egg and a little flour
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Pound them in a mortar
5. Fry in small cakes

title: Oyster Sautées
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 oysters to each person
- 3 slices of fat bacon
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- Salt and pepper
- 6 or 8 squares of buttered toast
- Olives
- Pickles
- Sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters well and dry with a clean towel.
2. Sprinkle them with pepper and salt, and roll in a little flour.
3. Place the bacon, cut into thin slices, in a frying pan, and let all the fat fry out.
4. Remove the bacon from the pan and place in the oysters, covering the bottom.
5. As they turn a golden brown on one side, turn over on the other.
6. Serve on squares of buttered toast, with garnishes of olives, pickles and sliced lemon.

title: Oyster Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Large Fresh Oysters
- 1 Quart Rich Milk
- The Oyster Liquor
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 3 Sprigs Parsley, Chopped Fine
- 1 Dozen Pepper Corns
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. In purchasing the oysters, be careful to make the vendor add the oyster juice when intended for soup
2. Drain the oysters through a colander and set them over the ice box to keep fresh and cold
3. Strain the liquor and put it into a soup kettle, adding the chopped parsley and pepper-corns
4. Let it come to a boil
5. Boil the milk separately in a saucepan, as boiling the milk and oyster juice together is likely to curdle the milk
6. When the milk comes to a boil, add to the oyster juice and put in the tablespoonful of butter
7. Stir the soup constantly, throwing in the oysters and continuing to stir until it comes to a boil again
8. Under no circumstances allow the oysters to boil, as that destroys their flavor and makes them tough
9. Watch for the ruffling of the edges, which indicates the right condition
10. At this point the soup must be served immediately
11. Serve with sliced lemon and oyster or wafer crackers

title: Oyster Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 oysters
- 2 quarts of water
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 pint of rich milk or cream
- mace
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Strain the oysters through a colander and set the liquor to boil
2. When the scum has all risen and been taken off, add two quarts of water, a tablespoonful of butter, one pint of rich milk or cream, mace, nutmeg, pepper and salt to taste
3. Boil these ingredients together
4. Just before serving up, throw in the oysters
5. If the soup be too thin, stir in a little wheat flour a few minutes before serving up

title: Oyster Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of oysters with the liquor
- 3 quarts of water
- 8 slices of lean ham
- 2 onions
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 tablepoonfuls of wheat flour
- 1 pint of rich milk
- 1 quart of raw oysters
- 1 teaspoonful of white pepper
- half teaspoonful of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put 3 quarts of water, 8 slices of lean ham, and 2 onions into a pot with the oysters and liquor
2. Boil this down to half the quantity
3. Run it through a cullender and put the liquor alone back into the pot and let it come just to a boil
4. Beat up the yolks of 6 eggs with 4 tablepoonfuls of wheat flour and 1 pint of rich milk
5. Stir this into the oyster liquor after it has been strained and made boiling hot
6. Add a quart of raw oysters and let the whole thicken a little on the fire
7. Add 1 teaspoonful of white pepper and a half of grated nutmeg

title: Oyster Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints of large, fresh oysters
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- flour
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- 1 saucer full of chopped celery
- 1 quart of rich milk
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take the liquor from the 3 pints of oysters, strain it, and set it on the fire to make the soup
2. Roll the butter in flour
3. Put the butter, bunch of sweet herbs, chopped celery, and rich milk into the liquor
4. As soon as the ingredients boil, take out the herbs and put in the oysters just before you send it to table
5. Season with pepper to your taste

title: Oyster Soup for Six Persons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of oysters
- water
- 1 handful of chopped ham
- a few blades of mace
- one-fourth of a large onion
- salt
- pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 quarter pound of butter
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 pint of cream

## Directions

1. Strain and drain two quarts of oysters; if you have less than a quart of liquor, make up that amount with water
2. Add a handful of chopped ham, fat and lean, a few blades of mace, one-fourth of a large onion, salt and pepper to taste
3. Boil one hour, then strain
4. Make a tablespoonful of flour and a quarter pound of butter into a soft paste and set aside
5. Put oysters into liquor and give them 8 or 10 minutes to plump up
6. Stir in the paste and leave until dinner is ready
7. Put the soup on again until it comes to a boil
8. Add a pint each of milk and cream
9. Stir all the time until it boils again, then dish and serve immediately

title: Oyster Soup Without Milk
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Bayou Cook Oysters
- The Oyster Liquor
- 1 Large Onion
- 4 Sprigs Parsley
- 1 Quart Boiling Water
- 1 Tablespoonful Lard
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Sifted Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter

## Directions

1. Put the tablespoonful of lard into the soup kettle
2. Have ready one onion and some parsley, chopped very fine
3. When the lard is hot, stir in two tablespoonfuls of sifted flour and make a Brown Roux, stirring constantly to prevent burning
4. When the Roux is of a light brown color, add the chopped onions and parsley, continuing to stir, being exceedingly careful to avoid burning
5. Strain the oyster juice of about four dozen oysters into the Roux, mixing thoroughly to avoid bits of shell
6. Mix with about a quart of boiling water and pour
7. When it shows signs of coming to a boil, add the oysters and a teaspoonful of butter
8. At the boiling point remove from the stove and serve with oyster soda crackers or dry toast

title: Oyster Soup. Delicate
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- the liquor of oysters
- a pint of water or milk (milk is preferable)
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- toasted bread-crumbs that have been toasted and pounded
- a quart of oysters to two quarts of liquor

## Directions

1. Take the oysters from their liquor
2. To every quart of the liquor add a pint of water or milk (milk is preferable)
3. Season with salt, pepper, butter, and toasted bread-crumbs that have been toasted and pounded
4. When this has boiled, put in a quart of oysters to two quarts of liquor
5. Let all boil a few minutes, and serve

title: Oyster Stuffing for Poultry
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Quart of Stale Bread, Wet and Squeezed
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sage
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Liver and Gizzard of the Fowl
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- Chopped Onions

## Directions

1. Drain the oysters
2. Wet the stale bread with hot water, squeezing thoroughly
3. Chop fine the liver and gizzard of the fowl, and put a tablespoonful of lard into the frying pan
4. Mix in the chopped onions and add the chopped liver and gizzard
5. As it begins to brown, throw in the chopped herbs, and then add the bread which has been mixed well and seasoned with the chopped sage
6. Mix well
7. Add to this one tablespoonful of butter and stir, blending all thoroughly
8. Add the pint or so of oyster water, and as it is reduced mix in the oysters
9. Stir for three or four minutes and take off and dress the fowl

title: Oyster Stuffing For Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four dozen nice plump oysters
- a tumblerful of bread crumbs
- a tumblyful of nice beef suet
- three eggs
- salt
- pepper
- a little butter
- a teaspoonful of mace
- some cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Take three or four dozen nice plump oysters, wash and beard them
2. Add to them a tumblerful of bread crumbs
