3. Chop up a tumblerful of nice beef suet
4. Mix together, and moisten with three eggs
5. Season with salt, pepper, a little butter, a teaspoonful of mace, and some cayenne pepper
6. Roll force-meat into cakes, and fry them
7. They are pretty laid around a turkey or chicken

title: Oyster Toast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of oysters
- a little cream or fresh butter
- some pepper and salt
- thin slices of toast
- fresh butter
- a thin slice of fresh lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scald a quart of oysters in their own liquor
2. Take them out and pound or chop them to a paste
3. Add a little cream or fresh butter, and some pepper and salt
4. Get ready some thin slices of toast moistened with boiling water
5. And spread with fresh butter
6. Then, spread over the butter the oyster paste
7. Put a thin slice of fresh lemon on each piece
8. And lay parsley on the platter
9. Serve this very hot or it will not be good

title: Oysters a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Gill of Sherry
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Rich Cream
- Toasted and Buttered Croutons
- Sliced Lemon and Parsley Sprigs to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare as in the above recipe for Curried Oysters, using a gill of sherry instead of the milk and cream, and omitting the curry powder.
2. Having dropped the oysters into the boiling oyster juice, remove from the fire after cooking three minutes.
3. Beat well the yolks of two eggs with two tablespoonfuls of rich cream, add to the oysters, and serve with toasted and buttered Croutons, a garnish of sliced lemons and parsley sprigs.

title: Oysters and Bacon
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen oysters
- Thin slices of breakfast bacon
- Minced parsley
- Sauce piquante

## Directions

1. Wrap each oyster in a very thin slice of breakfast bacon.
2. Lay on a broiler over a baking pan in a hot oven.
3. Remove when the bacon is brown.
4. Each must be fastened with a wooden toothpick.
5. Serve with minced parsley and pepper sauce, or Sauce Piquante.

title: Oysters au Parmesan
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Dozen Oysters
- Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- 1 Gill of White Wine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt
- Cayenne
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme

## Directions

1. Brown the bread crumbs in a little butter, and butter a shallow dish and stew with bread crumbs
2. Drain the oysters and dry with a clean towel; season highly à la Creole
3. Place them, one by one, on the bread crumbs
4. Strew chopped parsley over them, and the grated cheese, using good judgment as to quantity
5. Sprinkle lightly with bread crumbs again, and pour over all a gill of white wine
6. Place in the oven, which should be very quick; let them remain about fifteen minutes, till quite brown
7. Take out and pour over a little drawn butter, and serve with lemon garnish

title: Oysters en Brochettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Large, Fat Oysters
- Thin Slits of Bacon
- A Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonfuls of Minced Parsley
- Sliced Lemon and Olives to Garnish

## Directions

1. Have ready a furnace with red hot coals; take fine sliced breakfast bacon and cut into thin slits about the size of the oyster.
2. Drain three dozen large, fat oysters.
3. Take a long skewer, of silver or metal that is not dangerous, and string it first with a slit of bacon and then an oyster, alternating this until it is filled, the extreme ends, terminating with the bacon.
4. Hold the oysters over the clear fire and broil until the edges begin to ruffle, when they are done.
5. In the meantime prepare some drawn butter by placing about a tablespoonful in a cup before the fire to melt.
6. Place the oysters in a hot dish, alternating with slices of bacon, sprinkle with pepper and salt, and pour over the drawn butter mixed with about one tablespoonfuls of parsley, chopped fine.
7. Garnish with slices of lemons and whole olives, and serve.
8. The oysters and bacon may be served on the skewers, if they are not charred or blackened; but the other is the far daintier method.

title: Oysters Fried
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- large oysters
- a cup of lard
- wheat flour
- rolled cracker
- beaten egg
- mushroom catsup
- Harvey’s or Worcestershire sauce

## Directions

1. Take large oysters from their own liquor
2. Dry and lay them in a towel till you heat
3. Very hot, a cup of lard in a thick-bottomed pan
4. Dip each oyster in wheat flour, or rolled cracker, until it will hold no more
5. Then lay it in the pan
6. The fire must be moderate, or the oysters will scorch before cooking through
7. They will brown on one side in five minutes, then turn them
8. Oysters may be dipped in beaten egg and rolled cracker, and then fried

title: Oysters Served in a Block of Ice
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oysters to Each Person
- A Square Block of Ice
- Black Pepper and Cayenne
- Sprigs of Parsley and Radishes to Garnish
- Lemon Cut in Quarters

## Directions

1. Have your dealer send a square block of Ice of the size desired and make a hollow in the center of the block by placing a flat-iron on the top, scooping out with the iron the shape desired.
2. Place a folded napkin on a platter and stand the block of ice upon it.
3. Pepper the oysters nicely with Cayenne and black pepper, and place in the ice.
4. Take sprigs of parsley and decorate the platter, placing between decorated radishes, and alternate slices of lemon.
5. Serve the oysters with lemon cut in quarters.
6. The cavity should be square and deep, leaving walls of ice about two inches in thickness.
7. Smilax may be substituted for the parsley or mixed with it.

title: Oysters Stewed With Champagne
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a quart of oysters
- grated bread which has been toasted, beaten and sifted
- some cut parsley
- a little pepper and salt
- bits of butter cut thin
- a pint of champagne

## Directions

1. Put into a silver chafing dish a quarter of a pound of butter
2. Lay in a quart of oysters
3. Strew over them grated bread which has been toasted, beaten and sifted
4. Some cut parsley and a little pepper and salt
5. Cover the top with bits of butter cut thin
6. Pour on a pint of champagne
7. Cover and cook
8. This may be done in a pan or oven

title: Oysters Stewed With Milk
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of fine oysters
- one-half pint of their own liquor
- a half a pint of milk
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- crackers
- dressed celery

## Directions

1. Take a pint of fine oysters, one-half pint of their own liquor and a half a pint of milk
2. Boil the liquor, take off the scum
3. Put in a quarter of a pound of butter
4. Pepper and salt to taste
5. And serve crackers and dressed celery with them

title: Pababotte à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Pababottes
- 6 Truffles
- 6 Thin Slices of Bacon
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- The Zest of a Lemon
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Olives and Sprigs of Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the Pababotte as you would a chicken, and take out the entrails.
2. Separate the gizzards, and be sure to throw them away, retaining all the rest of the entrails for stuffing.
3. Chop the remaining entrails very fine, and season well with salt and pepper.
4. Fry them in about a quarter of a spoon of butter.
5. Take the Pababotte and rub well with salt and pepper, and put a small piece of butter, about the size of a peanut, with a little salt and pepper, in the Pababotte.
6. Place in the interior one truffle.
7. Bind a strip of thin bacon around the body.
8. Place a tablespoonful of butter in a baking dish, and set the Pababotte in it, and add about two tablespoonfuls of water.
9. Set the dish in a Quick oven, and let the birds roast thirty minutes, turning over once, so that they may be perfectly done.
10. When the entrails are done, add two inches of the zest of the lemon and a little juice.
11. Take slices of toast, allowing one slice for each bird, and spread over each a coating of the entrails, or farcie.
12. Place a bird on each slice of toast, after taking off the binding of bacon, or leaving it on, according to taste.
13. Add one spoon of water to the gravy in which the Pababottes have been cooking, strain it, then warm for two minutes, and pour hot on top of the breast of the bird, allowing it to melt down into the French toasts.
14. Garnish the dish nicely with sprigs of parsley and olives, and serve hot.

title: Pain Perdu, Or Lost Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of fresh milk
- a cup of sugar
- two beaten eggs
- any flavoring
- six slices from a loaf of bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a pint of fresh milk, and sweeten it with a cup of sugar
2. stir two beaten eggs in it
3. season with any flavoring you like
4. Cut six slices from a loaf of bread
5. soak each piece of bread a few minutes in the custard of milk and sugar
6. take the pieces out one by one, and fry them in butter made hot in a frying pan
7. pile them up and serve hot

title: Pain Perdu, or Lost Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of fresh milk
- a cup of sugar
- two beaten eggs
- flavoring of choice
- six slices of bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Sweeten the milk with sugar
2. Stir two beaten eggs into the milk
3. Season with flavoring of choice
4. Cut six slices from a loaf of bread
5. Soak each piece of bread a few minutes in the custard of milk and sugar
6. Take the pieces out one by one and fry them in butter made hot in a frying pan
7. Pile them up and serve hot

title: Pan Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Compressed Yeast
- ¼ Pound of Lard
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough
- ⅛ Ounces of Sugar, If Desired

## Directions

1. Make the same dough as for Bakers' Bread, only work the loaves twice over.
2. Then put them in the oven and dredge lightly with flour.
3. As they begin to rise make a light paste of flour and water or egg and water and spread very lightly over them.
4. This is brushed lightly with lard, to keep from getting too crusty.
5. Bake in a rather hotter oven than the other bread.

title: Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 3/4 pound of sugar
- 1 cup of milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites of the eggs together.
2. Add the flour, and beat very light.
3. Add the milk, pouring gradually, and having the batter no thicker than cream.
4. Add the salt, and mix well.
5. Have a wide pancake pan, and let it be very hot.
6. Grease it with butter, or better still, with a piece of fat bacon.
7. Pour in batter sufficient to just cover the bottom of the pan.
8. In a minute, or perhaps less time, the cake must be ready to turn.
9. Toss the cake and catch it in the pan, and bring the brown side up without failure.
10. Continue baking and tossing the pancakes, rolling them up with sugar and cinnamon and butter mixed, or buttered and rolled in molasses.
11. Serve hot.

title: Pancakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 yolks of eggs
- 1 lb. of flour well sifted
- cream
- whites of 3 eggs
- fresh butter
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Break 5 yolks of eggs in 1 lb. of flour well sifted
2. Mix it well with cream until the batter is very thin
3. Beat up the whites of 3 eggs very light and mix it with the batter
4. Have ready fresh butter creamed, and to each pancake put 2 tablespoonfuls in the pan, which must be made hot
5. Fry them a light brown
6. Turn a plate up in the dish, and lay them on one another, powdering each with white sugar

title: Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pint of Cold Milk
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- Flour
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl
2. Break in the eggs; beat well, and add one ounce of powdered sugar
3. Mix thoroughly with a spatula, and then add the cold milk, pouring it in gradually
4. Mix well for six minutes
5. Have ready a clean griddle; butter lightly, and, when hot, drop on it about two large spoonfuls of the batter at a time
6. Let the cakes bake two minutes on each side, and then turn and bake two minutes on the other
7. Turn the pancake on a dish; sprinkle generously with powdered sugar
8. Continue baking the cakes and turning on the dish, sprinkling each in turn with the powdered sugar, till all is used

title: Parfait Amour
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Zest of Lemons
- 4 Ounces of Zest of Limes
- 2 Drachms of Cloves
- 3 Sprigs of Rosemary Leaves
- A Handful of Orange Blossoms
- 2½ Gallons of Alcohol
- 10 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Grate the zests of the lemons and limes carefully, without touching the white inner pulp.
2. Bruise the rosemary leaves and orange blossoms and steep in the alcohol fifteen days.
3. Make a syrup with the sugar and water, add the liqueur, distill and filter, color lightly with Cochineal, and bottle for use.

title: Parisian Charlotte Russe
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Stale Lady Fingers
- 1/2 Box of Gelatine
- 1 Cup Grated Cocoanut
- 1/2 Pound of Macaroons
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine in cold water for about half an hour.
2. Put one-half of the cream to one side, and whip the rest.
3. Put the remaining pint to boil, and stir into it the sugar and eggs, beaten together till very light.
4. Stir about one minute, and as it thickens add the gelatine, which you will have dissolved either in a little warm water or Sherry Wine.
5. Then add the lady fingers and a teaspoonful of the vanilla; add the macaroons and the grated cocoanut.
6. Turn all this into a bowl or tin basin, place it on ice, and stir continually until it begins to harden.
7. Add the whipped cream, and stir all very carefully.
8. When well-mixed, wet a fancy dish of glass or a mold, turn the Charlotte Russe into it, and stand on the ice to harden.

title: Parisian Nougats
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Scalded or Split Pistachio Kernels
- 6 Ounces of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- A Few Drops of Prepared Cochineal
- Small bits of granulated sugar
- Seeded raisins or cleaned currants

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar till brittle, and then add the Pistachio Kernels or Peanuts and the Cochineal
2. Add the flavoring and mix gently
3. Then pour the Nougat out on a marble slab, slightly buttered
4. Flatten out the Nougat by means of a hard lemon slightly rubbed with butter to prevent sticking
5. Roll to about one-sixth of an inch in thickness, and before it cools sprinkle all over with small bits of granulated sugar, free from dust, and seeded raisins or cleaned currants
6. Press these lightly in with the fingers, and then divide the sheets of Nougat while hot into small diamond shapes, about two and a half inches long and one in width

title: Parisian Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of sponge cake
- preserve (red or black currant)
- sherry
- whites of four eggs
- powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Lay slices of sponge cake at the bottom of a glass dish.
2. Spread over them a layer of preserve (red or black currant is very good for the purpose).
3. Place over that more slices of sponge cake, then another layer of jam.
4. Do this until you have filled the dish.
5. Pour over it sufficient sherry to soak the cake properly.
6. Beat up the whites of four eggs with sufficient powdered loaf sugar to make it a very stiff froth.
7. Cover the top of the cake completely with the froth, and bake.

title: Parlor Punch (Moran's)
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of white sugar
- a little lemon juice
- two wineglassfuls of English black tea
- one wineglassful of whiskey
- one-half wineglassful of Jamaica rum
- a little raspberry syrup
- plenty of small ice

## Directions

1. Shake well
2. Strain in fancy glass

title: Parsley and Butter Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter sauce (half a pint of No. 10)
- parsley (chopped, half a cup)
- lemon juice (one)

## Directions

1. Take half a pint of butter sauce No. 10, and add half a cup of chopped parsley and the juice of one lemon.
2. Pour hot water on the parsley before chopping.

title: Parsley Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful and a half of butter
- 1 tablespoonful and a half of flour
- Half a cup of water or white broth
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful and a half of finely minced parsley
- 1 tablespoonful of cream

## Directions

1. To the recipe for white sauce add one tablespoonful and a half of finely minced parsley
2. You may also add a tablespoonful of cream
3. This is nice with boiled fish or boiled chicken

title: Parsley for Garnishing Purposes
body:
## Ingredients

- Several bunches of fresh parsley
- Slightly salted water
- Ice

## Directions

1. Take several bunches of fresh parsley, trim off all the coarse stems.
2. Wash in slightly salted water, drain and place in a colander.
3. Set over a watertight vessel.
4. Shave some ice, cover the parsley with it and let it keep fresh and cool for table garnishes.

title: Parsley Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of cut parsley

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Plain Omelet
2. Mix a tablespoonful of cut parsley in the omelet before putting in the frying pan

title: Parsnip Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Large Parsnips
- 2 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the parsnips until very, very tender.
2. Drain and mash through a colander.
3. Beat two eggs very light, and add the parsnips, using proportions of three large parsnips to the eggs.
4. Form the parsnips into little balls or boulettes, and fry in boiling lard, or make into little cakes and fry on a griddle.

title: Parsnip Fritters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- salted water
- egg
- spoonful of milk
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Boil the parsnips in salted water until they are done
2. Make a batter of an egg, a spoonful of milk and flour, pepper and salt
3. When the parsnips are cool enough to handle cut them in rounds
4. Dip them in the batter and drop them into hot lard
5. Fry a light brown, turn them and fry the other side
6. Drain them from the grease when brown on both sides

title: Parsnip Fritters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- beaten eggs
- milk
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Boil and peel a dozen parsnips
2. Split and cut them in pieces
3. Make batter, allowing 4 beaten eggs to 1 pint of milk and 4 tablespoonfuls of flour
4. Have ready over the fire a frying pan with boiling lard
5. Put in a large spoonful of batter upon every piece of parsnip, and cover with another spoonful of batter
6. Proceed thus till you have used up the parsnips
7. When done, drain them from the lard, and serve them hot at breakfast or dinner

title: Parsnip Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Parsnips
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Water
- 2 Eggs
- % Pound of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the parsnips until very tender.
2. Cut into long, narrow strips.
3. Make a batter by mixing the flour with the yolks of the eggs, beaten well.
4. Add the salt and pepper, and gradually one cup of water, till it is absorbed.
5. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Mix thoroughly.
7. Add the parsnips to the batter.
8. Dip out one at a time, in a spoon of butter, and fry in boiling lard.
9. Serve as an entree at dinner.

title: Partridge and Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Partridges
- A Fine Tender Head of Cabbage
- 1½ Chaurice (Sausage)
- ¾ of a Pound of Salt Pork
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 4 Cloves
- ¾ Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Pint of Veal or Chicken Broth (White)
- 1 Pint of Beef Broth or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges, selecting large and older partridges in preference to the young. Clean, singe, draw and wipe well. Then truss them neatly, rub with salt and pepper and butter and place in a roasting pan.
2. Take a fine, tender head of cabbage, clean thoroughly and cut into four parts. Wash the cabbage well in cold water and put into boiling salted water for five minutes.
3. Take the cabbage out of the water and drain well; make a hollow in the center of each piece of cabbage; place within the partridges, cover with the other pieces and tie together.
4. Put in a saucepan the quarter of a pound of salt pork which has been well scalded and washed of all salt and cut into six slices. Add one carrot cut into four pieces, one whole onion into which you will have stuck four cloves, the herb bouquet, the sausage and one pint each of white veal broth or chicken broth, and one pint of water or beef broth.
5. Season with a small pinch of salt, and a good pinch of pepper, and place the cabbage in this preparation.
6. Put the partridges in the oven and let them roast for ten minutes. Then remove and take the cabbage from the mixture, make a hollow in the center of the cabbage, place within the partridges and cover with the remaining portion of cabbage; tie each half separately together.
7. Return to the saucepan, placing a piece of buttered paper over to keep all air from escaping. Put the lid on the saucepan, set in the oven and let the partridges cook thus for an hour.
8. Remove the lid and paper, skim off all that may adhere to the surface, drain the cabbage and slice; dress neatly on a hot dish.
9. Untruss the partridges and lay them on the cabbage, placing on each dressed section a piece of sliced boiled pork, a sausage cut in half; slice the carrots nicely in round pieces, and use these as a decoration, placing them artistically around the dish.
10. Strain the sauce in which the partridges were cooked and let it reduce slightly. Serve with the cabbage and partridge, bringing it to the table in a separate bowl and pouring over the cabbage when serving.

title: Partridge With Sour Orange Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Partridges
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Juice of a Sour Orange
- Toasted Croutons
- Watercress or Parsley to Garnish
- Drawn Butter Sauce

## Directions

1. Broil the Partridges according to recipe for Broiled Partridge
2. Prepare a Drawn Butter Sauce and add the juice and zest of a sour orange
3. Pour over the birds and let it soak down into the toast
4. Serve hot, with garnishes of parsley or watercress

title: Partridge, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Partridges
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 3 Large Tomatoes
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- ⅛ Glass of Sherry or Madeira Wine
- 1 Cup of Water or Consomme
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the Partridges; singe, draw and wipe well
2. Cut up the birds as for Fricasseed Chicken
3. Rub well with salt and pepper and place in a stewpan, and let them brown well on either side
4. Add the finely-chopped onion and the herbs, minced very fine
5. Let these brown, and add the tablespoonful of flour
6. Let brown nicely, and add the chopped tomatoes and their juice
7. Cover and let simmer about five minutes
8. Add the wine and a cup of water or consommé
9. Cover closely and let all cook for fifteen minutes
10. Serve hot, using toasted Croutons for a garnish

title: Partridges, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fine Partridges
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Finely Chopped Onion
- 12 Whole Mushrooms
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- ¼ Glass of Sherry or Madeira Wine
- 1 Cup of Water or Consomme
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Clean the Partridges; singe, draw and wipe well
2. Cut up the birds as you would a young chicken
3. Rub well with salt and pepper, and place in a stewpan with two tablespoonfuls of butter
4. Let them brown well on either side, about three minutes
5. Add the finely-chopped onion and carrot, and the minced herbs
6. Let these brown for two minutes
7. Add the flour and let all brown nicely
8. Add a cup of water or consommé and the wine and the chopped mushrooms
9. Cover closely and cook for fifteen minutes
10. Serve, using toasted Croutons as garnish

title: Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of beef suet
- a bottle of the best olive oil

## Directions

1. Render 6 lbs. of beef suet
2. strain it through a hair sieve into a clean pan
3. to this add a bottle of the best olive oil
4. stir it well and then put into jars, each containing about 3 lbs.
5. cover tightly, and keep in a very cool place

title: Paste for Croquants
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ lb. of flour
- ½ lb. of loaf sugar
- yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Sift ½ lb. of flour and ½ lb. of loaf sugar together
2. mix them well with a knife
3. stir in as many yolks of eggs as will make it into a stiff paste
4. this must be well stirred
5. roll it on a flour board, and cut into shape of leaves with a cutter
6. bake a light brown, as other paste

title: Pastilles to Relieve Thirst
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Grains of Tartaric Acid
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 20 Drops of Lemon Essence
- Sufficient Gum Tragacanth to Make a Paste

## Directions

1. Take eight grains of Tartaric Acid, one pound of sugar, twenty drops of Lemon Essence, and make a paste with all, adding a sufficient quantity of mucilage or Gum Tragacanth to make a paste
2. Cut out into tablets and dry in the open air

title: Patent Flour
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of flour
- 5 teaspoonfuls of dry soda
- 6 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- 6 teaspoonfuls of salt
- milk
- flour
- a little shortening

## Directions

1. Pulverize 6 lbs. of flour
2. mix 5 teaspoonfuls of dry soda all through it, then 6 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar, and 6 of salt
3. incorporate all these
4. add milk and flour, a little shortening
5. make into a soft dough
6. bake in tins

title: Patent Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of potatoes
- 1 pint of water
- flour
- 1 pint of cold water
- 1/2 pint hop tea

## Directions

1. Put potatoes in water and boil it until reduced to 1 teaspoonful of water
2. Peel the potatoes and mash them into a light teacupful of flour with the boiling water in which they were boiled
3. After mixing well, add 1 pint of cold water in small quantity at a time
4. Add 1/2 pint hop tea
5. Set it to rise

title: Patties of Foies Gras
body:
## Ingredients

- Terrine of Foies Gras
- Pound of Fat Pork
- Pound of Goose Fat
- Half Can of Truffles
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Glass of Sherry Wine
- Puff Paste

## Directions

1. Procure the fat livers of geese
2. Chop the livers, fat pork and goose fat very fine, allowing pound for pound of the fat meat and goose fat to the same quantity of livers
3. Season well with salt and pepper, and moisten well with Sherry wine
4. Chop a half can of truffles and mix
5. Put all in a quart or pint measure baking pan which you will have lined with Puff Paste
6. The pan must be about two and a half or three inches deep
7. Bake this paste, then fill in with the foies gras
8. Cover with a light cover of the dough, and decorate around the edges with the clippings of dough that remain
9. Place the pie in the oven, and let it bake for about an hour to a nice brown, covering for the first three-quarters of an hour with a piece of paper, to prevent burning
10. When done, serve in the dish in which it was baked

title: Pea Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- split peas
- water
- parsley
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- celery seed
- butter

## Directions

1. To 2 quarts of split peas, put 2 gallons of water, a handful of parsley, a little cayenne pepper, salt and celery seed, to suit taste
2. Put the peas in soak the night before
3. After boiling pass through a sieve
4. Add a large lump of good butter

title: Peach Batter Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Cups of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 8 Peaches
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites of the eggs till very light, and mix, and add the milk
2. Beat steadily until smooth
3. Then add the melted butter, the salt and the baking powder
4. Have the peaches stewed till almost cooked, and stir them into the pudding, after cutting fine
5. Then turn the pudding into the mold or pan in which it is to be baked
6. Bake for an hour, and serve hot, with Cream, Brandy or Hard Sauce

title: Peach Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh or Canned Peaches (1/4 Can)
- Madeira Wine
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- Grated Lemon Peel
- Fruit Fritter Batter a la Creole

## Directions

1. If the peaches are fresh, peel and stone them, and cut in halves.
2. Then sprinkle them with the grated zest of a lemon and sugar, and pour over them sufficient Madeira Wine for them to be saturated.
3. Cover and set aside for two hours.
4. Then drain off the liquor.
5. Make a Fruit Fritter Batter a la Creole, and have ready a saucepan filled deep with boiling lard.
6. Dip the peaches, one by one, into the batter, and drop from the spoon into the lard, and let them fry to a golden brown.
7. Then proceed in exactly the same manner of serving as in the directions given for Apple Fritters.

title: Peach Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck of very ripe peaches
- 2 lbs. of the best white sugar
- 1 quart of cream
- 1 quart of milk

## Directions

1. Pare and cut finely 1/2 peck of very ripe peaches
2. Stir in well 2 lbs. of the best white sugar
3. Let it stand for 2 or 3 hours
4. Stir them well together
5. Put into a preserving kettle to simmer for 20 minutes, stirring all the time to prevent scorching
6. Pour them into a pan
7. When quite cold, add 1 quart of cream and 1 quart of milk
8. Stir them together
9. Put instantly in the freezer to prevent curdling

title: Peach Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ripe Peaches or 1 Can
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Quart of Cream

## Directions

1. Put the milk to boil, and when hot take from the fire, and add the yolks of the eggs, which have been beaten to a thick cream with the powdered sugar
2. Set to cool for thirty minutes
3. Add the peaches, which you will have pared, stoned and mashed, and stir them quickly into the cream
4. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Mix all thoroughly together and turn into the freezer
6. Turn the crank rapidly for a few minutes, till thoroughly mixed, and then cover and let stand for two hours

title: Peach Jam
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches
- powdered loaf sugar, same weight as pulp
- 1/2 ounce of bitter almonds, blanched and pounded

## Directions

1. Gather the peaches when ripe
2. Peel and stone them
3. Put them into a preserving pan and put them over the fire till hot
4. Rub them through a sieve
5. Add to 1 lb. of pulp the same weight of powdered loaf sugar and 1/2 ounce of bitter almonds, blanched and pounded
6. Let it boil 10 or 15 minutes
7. Stir and skim well

title: Peach Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Wipe your peaches perfectly dry, after washing them (they should be free-stones and perfectly ripe); cut them in quarters; crack the stones and break the kernels small; put the peaches and the kernels into a covered jar; set them in boiling water, and let them boil till they are soft; strain them through a jelly bag
2. Add 1 lb. of loaf sugar to 1 pint of juice; put all in the preserving kettle; boil them 20 minutes

title: Peach Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, just ripe
- Water sufficient to cover
- 1 pound of sugar to every pint of fruit juice

## Directions

1. Choose peaches that are just ripe, and no more.
2. Pare and cut in two, and take out the stone.
3. Boil them in water sufficient to cover well.
4. Strain through a hair sieve, and pour the decoction into earthen dishes.
5. To every pint of liquid add a pound of sugar.
6. Set on the fire, let it boil twenty minutes, skimming while on the fire.
7. Pour immediately into cups, glasses or preserve jars.

title: Peach Kernel Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound Each of Peach or Apricot Kernels
- 4 Pints of Brandy
- 2½ Pounds of Sugar
- 2 Pints of Water

## Directions

1. Pound the peach or apricot kernels — some also pound the peach stones — steep them for one whole month in four pints of brandy in an earthen jar.
2. At the end of that time add a syrup made of two and a half pounds of sugar and two pints of water.
3. Mix all well together, and then filter as directed above, and bottle and seal, and keep in a cool, shady place.

title: Peach Leathers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Add sugar to taste
2. Boil until they will spread smoothly over a dish
3. Dry chiefly in the stove and then in the sun, covering with a net
4. When sufficiently dry, warm slightly
5. Cut in strips
6. Roll in sugar and eat

title: Peach Mangoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- large free-stone peaches
- brine
- vinegar
- white mustard seed
- horseradish
- powdered ginger
- red pepper
- onions
- garlic

## Directions

1. Steep some large free-stone peaches in brine for two days
2. Wipe each peach carefully, and cut a hole in it just sufficient to allow the seed to come out
3. Throw them into cold vinegar until you make the stuffing
4. Take fresh white mustard seed which has been wet with vinegar, and allowed to swell a few hours, scraped horseradish, powdered ginger, a few pods of red pepper, a few small onions, or a clove of garlic
5. Mix all with vinegar, and add half as much chopped peach
6. Stuff the peaches hard with this mixture
7. Replace the piece cut out, and tie it up tight with packthread
8. Boil a quart of vinegar for each dozen peaches
9. Season it with the same spices as the stuffing
10. Boil the spices in a small bag, and then put in the peaches

title: Peach Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Peaches
- ¾ of a Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Allow three-fourths of a pound of sugar for every pound of fruit.
2. Take ripe fruit; pare and cut into small pieces.
3. Place in the preserving kettle, with a layer of fruit and a layer of sugar, and so alternate till all is in.
4. Cook over the fire as directed.
5. Watch carefully that they do not burn.
6. When they begin to form a jelly take off the fire and put away in stone jars or glasses.

title: Peach Meringue Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Peaches
- Sugar to Taste
- Whites of 6 Eggs
- 6 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- Rich pie crust

## Directions

1. Pare and stone the peaches, and stew according to recipe.
2. Line the bottoms of two pie pans with a rich pie crust, and then fill in with the peaches, which you will have sweetened to taste.
3. Bake in a quick oven twenty or twenty-five minutes.
4. Beat the whites of six eggs to a stiff froth with the sugar, which should be fine white powdered.
5. Add a teaspoonful of vanilla.
6. When the meringue can stand alone, cover the tops of the pies three-quarters of an inch thick, after baking, and set back in the oven and bake for two or three minutes, to a nice, delicate brown.

title: Peach or Apple Compote, for Dessert
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- loaf sugar
- water
- apples or peaches
- lemon

## Directions

1. Dissolve and boil a pound of loaf sugar in a pint of water.
2. Skim it, pare six or eight apples, or a dozen peaches, throw them into the boiling syrup, and cook until tender and transparent.
3. Lemon improves the apples, but peaches are better without it.

title: Peach Pickles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe, sound, cling-stone peaches
- vinegar
- brown sugar
- cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the down with a brush like a clothes brush from ripe, sound, cling-stone peaches
2. Make a gallon of good vinegar hot
3. Add to it four pounds of brown sugar
4. Boil and skim it clear
5. Stick five or six cloves into each of the peaches, then pour the hot vinegar over them
6. Cover the vessel and set it in a cold place for eight or ten days
7. Drain off the vinegar, make it hot, skim it, and again turn it over the peaches
8. Let them become cold, then put them into glass jars and secure as directed for preserves

title: Peach Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Peaches
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- A Plain Paste

## Directions

1. Pare and cut the peaches into slices
2. Stew the peaches and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apple Pie
3. Sprinkle the tops of the covers with fine white powdered sugar when ready to serve

title: Peach Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Fruit
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the syrup as above directed.
2. Then peel the peaches and cut them into halves, removing the stones.
3. Skim the syrup, and drop in the peaches, one at a time, and let them boil till tender, from fifteen to twenty minutes.
4. They should be so tender that they may be pierced with a broom straw.
5. Then remove the kettle from the fire, carefully drain out the peaches and put them in the jars.
6. Then boil the syrup twenty minutes longer, or until it forms a thick syrup, removing the scum as it rises.
7. Pour off any remains of thin syrup that may have adhered to the peaches in the jar, and fill in with the syrup, and set aside to cool.
8. When cold, screw the tops of the jars on.
9. Label and set away.

title: Peach Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Five eggs
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- a dinner plate full of peaches, peeled and cut up fine
- 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of milk
- flour
- a pinch of salt
- a dessertspoon of butter

## Directions

1. Beat 5 eggs with 2 cupfuls of sugar, well mixed together
2. Add a dinner plate full of peaches, peeled and cut up fine
3. Add 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of milk, and as much flour
4. Add a pinch of salt and a dessertspoon of butter
5. Bake in a slow oven
6. Eaten hot or cold

title: Peach Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 dozen ripe, soft, free-stone peaches
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Get 2 or 3 dozen ripe, soft, free-stone peaches
2. Peel them and pass them through a cullender
3. Add water sufficient to weaken it
4. Sweeten to the taste
5. Freeze

title: Peach Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Peaches
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 2 Lemons
- 1 Quart of Water

## Directions

1. Pare and cut the peaches in halves, remove the stones.
2. Cut the peaches up very fine and sprinkle with a half pound of sugar, mash well together and set aside for an hour.
3. Then set the water and the remaining half of the sugar to boil.
4. After five minutes remove from the fire and let cool.
5. Add the peaches and mix well.
6. Then press all through a fine cloth sieve, extracting all the juice from the peaches.
7. Set to freeze and serve as directed, in glasses.

title: Peach Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Peach Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1/2 Ounce of Peach Essence

## Directions

1. Pare and stone the peaches, then pound and mash well and set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours
2. Strain through a bag, pressing out all the juice
3. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apricot Syrup
4. Add the essence as the syrup cools and you are about to bottle it

title: Peach Tart
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe juicy free-stone peaches
- sugar
- cream
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Take ripe, juicy, free-stone peaches, pare them and cut them into small pieces (leaving out the stones, half of which must be cracked, and the kernels blanched and mixed with the peaches); mix in a sufficient quantity of sugar, to make them very sweet, and set the peaches away till the sugar draws out the juice
2. Then stew them (without water) till quite soft; take them out, mash them with the back of a spoon, and set them away to cool
3. Have ready some shells of fine puff paste baked a light brown; when cool, put the peaches into the shells; having first mixed the stewed fruit with cream; grate white sugar over them

title: Peaches and Apricot Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- salt water
- vinegar
- cloves
- pepper corns
- sliced ginger root
- white mustard seed
- salt
- apricots

## Directions

1. Take peaches fully grown, but not mellow
2. Cover them in strong salt and water for one week
3. Take them from the brine and wipe them carefully, rubbing each peach to see if it is firm
4. Put to a gallon of vinegar half an ounce each of cloves, pepper corns, sliced ginger root, white mustard seed, and a little salt
5. Scald the peaches with this boiling vinegar, repeat this three times
6. Add half as much fresh vinegar, and cork them up in jars
7. Keep them dark and cool
8. Apricots may be pickled in the same way

title: Peaches and Apricots in Brandy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Nice smooth peaches not too ripe
- Sugar in weight equal to the fruit
- Half a pint of water to each pound of sugar
- Equal quantities of syrup and French brandy

## Directions

1. Put peaches in a vessel and cover them with weak lye
2. Take them out in two hours, and wipe carefully to get off the down and outside skin, and lay them in cold water
3. Weigh the fruit, add their weight in sugar, and half a pint of water to each pound of sugar
4. Boil and skim this syrup, put in the peaches
5. When the syrup is clear of scum, let them boil for twenty minutes or half an hour
6. Take them out and lay them on dishes to cool
7. Boil the syrup for an hour longer, or until reduced one-half and quite thick
8. When cold, put the peaches or apricots in jars, and cover them with equal quantities of the syrup and French brandy

title: Peaches and Cream Frozen
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of nice yellow peaches
- sweet cream
- a quart of milk
- half a pound of sugar
- yolks of four eggs

## Directions

1. Peel and stone a quart of nice yellow peaches
2. Put them in a bowl, sweeten them well, and chop very fine
3. If you have sweet cream, put to the fruit a quart of it; if you have not, take a quart of milk, sweeten it with half a pound of sugar, let it boil, and when boiling, pour it on to the beaten yolks of four eggs
4. When this custard cools, you may add the chopped peaches, which should be well sweetened
5. Pour all in the freezer and set it where it can be frozen

title: Peaches in Brandy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine peaches
- Three quarters of a pound of white sugar to every pound of fruit
- Water
- A pint of brandy to a pint of the sugar syrup from the peaches
- Rosin

## Directions

1. Soak fine peaches in lye until you can remove the fuzzy outside
2. Wipe them, and turn them into cold water
3. Weigh them, and to every pound of fruit, put three quarters of a pound of white sugar
4. Make a syrup like that for preserves, only using less water
5. Boil the peaches in the syrup until they are tender
6. Take them out of the kettle, and place them in jars
7. Fill up the jars with a brandy syrup, made of a pint of brandy, to a pint of the sugar syrup from the peaches
8. Cook them very carefully, and dip the mouths of the jars in rosin melted
9. Keep them in a cool dark place

title: Peanut Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Fresh Peanuts
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Gill of Water
- 1/2 Pint each of Milk and Cream
- 1 1/2 Ounces of Powdered White Sugar
- 1 Ounce of Gelatine

## Directions

1. Peel four ounces of fresh peanuts, and pound them into a flour.
2. Add the grated zest of a lemon, and a little water, sufficient to make all into a thick paste.
3. Boil equal quantities of milk and cream to equal a pint and a half, and add an ounce and a half of white powdered sugar.
4. Let it cool, and add a piece of gelatine, blended with one spoon of water.
5. Mix well, and then strain through a sieve.
6. Put the cream back on the fire to heat, and add the peanuts.
7. Let all come to a good boil.
8. Take off and set it to cool in a cool place, and serve cold.

title: Peanut Pralines
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Peanuts
- 1 Pound of Brown Sugar
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Shell the peanuts and break into bits
2. Set the sugar and water to boil
3. As it begins to simmer add the peanuts and the butter
4. Stir constantly and as it bubbles up once take from the fire
5. Pour from the spoon on the marble slab or a buttered plate
6. Set away to harden

title: Pear Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Fresh Pears or 4 Canned Pears
- Brandy or Rum (If desired)
- Grated Lemon Peel
- Powdered Sugar
- Batter a la Creole
- Brown paper

## Directions

1. Peel and core the pears, which will be all the nicer if they are a little tart.
2. Take out the seeds and core.
3. Cut them into slices, more or less thick or thin, according to taste.
4. Soak them in brandy or good whisky or rum for the space of two hours, sprinkling with the zest of a lemon and sugar, according to judgment.
5. Make a batter a la Creole, and have ready a deep saucepan of boiling lard.
6. Drain the pears.
7. Dip the slices, one at a time, into the batter.
8. Lift out with a large kitchen spoon, drop into the boiling lard, and fry to a golden brown.
9. Then lift out with a skimmer, and set on brown paper, in the mouth of the oven, and drain.
10. Sift powdered white sugar over them, and serve hot, piling high in pyramidal shape, and sprinkling again with powdered white sugar.
11. Serve as an entremet or as a dessert.

title: Pear Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears, just ripe
- Water sufficient to cover
- 1 pound of sugar to every pint of liquid

## Directions

1. Choose pears that are just ripe, and no more.
2. Pare and cut in two, and take out the stones.
3. Boil them in water sufficient to cover well.
4. Strain through a hair sieve, and pour the decoction into earthen dishes.
5. To every pint of liquid add a pound of sugar.
6. Set on the fire, let it boil twenty minutes, skimming while on the fire.
7. Pour immediately into cups, glasses or preserve jars.

title: Pear Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3½ Pounds of Pears
- 2 Pounds of Sugar
- Grated rinds of several lemons

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and cut them into quarters, extracting all the seeds.
2. Then throw them into fresh water, sufficient to half cover, and set them on the fire.
3. When they begin to grow soft, mash them with a wooden spoon.
4. Then add the sugar and the grated rinds of several lemons; mix well.
5. Set all back upon the fire.
6. Stir the mixture while boiling till it is of the proper consistency.
7. Pour it into jars or glasses.

title: Pear Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Pears
- 1/4 Cup of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- A Plain Paste

## Directions

1. Peel and core and slice six fine pears.
2. Put them in a vessel with the sugar and water, and stew.
3. Prepare a plain paste, and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Apple Pie.

title: Pearl Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 1 Pound of Flour
- 1/2 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and the sugar to a cream
2. Beat the eggs, whites and yolks separately, and add the yolks
3. Then beat till very light
4. Add the soda, dissolved in a tablespoonful of boiling water, and then add the milk and beat carefully
5. Add the flour gradually, and beat vigorously
6. Then add the spices and essence, and lastly the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
7. Bake for an hour in a moderate oven

title: Pecan Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of butter
- one and one-half cups of sugar
- two eggs
- three-quarters of a cup of sweet milk
- two cups of sifted flour
- one and one-half teaspoonfuls of soda
- one teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- one cup of pecans picked out and cut fine

## Directions

1. Bake in a small cake pan

title: Pecan Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Cup or Sponge Cake
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Minced Louisiana Pecans
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Make a good Layer Cake or Cup Cake
2. Beat the whites of three eggs to a stiff froth
3. Add three tablespoonfuls of sugar, and one cup of minced and pounded pecans
4. Add one teaspoonful of extract of lemon, and fill the cake, leaving a layer of the filling on top

title: Pecan Pralines
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Brown Sugar
- ½ Pound of Freshly-Peeled and Cut Louisiana Pecans
- 1 Spoon of Butter
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Set the sugar to boil, and as it begins to boil add the pecans, which you will have divested of their shells and cut some into fine pieces, others into halves, and others again into demi-halves.
2. Let all boil till the mixture begins to bubble, and then take off the stove and proceed to lay on a marble slab, as above, to dry.
3. Be careful to stir the mixture constantly till the syrup begins to thicken and turn to sugar.
4. Then take from the stove and proceed to turn on the marble slab.

title: Pepper Mangoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large, green peppers
- salt
- water
- nice white cabbages
- celery seed
- mustard seed
- turmeric
- spices
- pure sweet oil
- boiling vinegar

## Directions

1. Take large, green peppers and put them in salt and water
2. Let them stand 9 days
3. The day before you are to take them out, get nice white cabbages and chop them very fine
4. Sprinkle them well with salt and let them stand all night
5. In the morning drain off the water, and mix with them celery seed, mustard seed, and a little turmeric, and all kinds of spice
6. Moisten the whole with pure sweet oil
7. Take the seed out of the peppers and stuff the pods with the above mixture
8. Cover them with boiling vinegar and tie them up closely

title: Pepper Pot
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Plain Tripe
- 2 Potatoes
- 1 Pound of Honeycomb Tripe
- Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Knuckle of Veal
- 3 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Onion
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Cayenne to Suit the Taste
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. The knuckle of the veal is best for this. Wash and put into the soup kettle, covering with water and bring it to a slow boil.
2. Carefully skim off the scum.
3. Let it simmer gently for three hours.
4. The tripe should be prepared the way before. Wash it thoroughly in cold water and boil for about seven hours.
5. Put away in the ice-box till needed.
6. Chop the parsley and herbs fine and one-half of the dried pepper pod, and add to the boiling knuckle of veal, and also the potatoes, which have been cut into dice.
7. Cut up the tripe into pieces of about one inch square.
8. Take out the knuckle of veal and cut any meat into small pieces, and add all with the tripe to the soup.
9. At the boiling point, season with salt and pepper.
10. Throw croutons into the soup, let it simmer for about ten minutes more and serve.

title: Pepper Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carrot, minced fine
- 2 sprigs each of thyme and parsley
- 1 onion, minced fine
- 1 pint of consommé
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 wineglass of sherry or Madeira
- 1/2 grated lemon
- 1 small piece of celery
- Salt and black pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- A dash of cayenne

## Directions

1. Put the butter in the saucepan, and as it melts, add the flour
2. Let it brown slowly, and then add one pint of consommé
3. Let it boil, and add the minced herbs and vegetables and the zest or outer skin of half a grated lemon
4. Let all boil slowly for an hour and a half
5. Add a wineglassful of sherry or Madeira and season with salt and black pepper (hot) and a dash of cayenne
6. Let it boil for ten minutes longer, take off the stove and strain, and serve with any game

title: Pepper Sauce for Venison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carrot, minced fine
- 2 sprigs each of thyme and parsley
- 1 onion, minced fine
- 1 pint of consommé
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 wineglass of sherry or Madeira
- 1/2 grated lemon
- 1 small piece of celery
- Salt and black pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- A dash of cayenne
- Half glass of currant jelly

## Directions

1. Make sauce poivrade in exactly the same manner as the pepper sauce recipe
2. When it is to be served with venison add a half glass of currant jelly, and the sauce allowed to boil ten minutes longer

title: Pepper Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of small cherry or chili peppers
- 3/4 pint of best French vinegar

## Directions

1. Fill a pint bottle about half full of small cherry peppers or chili peppers, red and green.
2. Fill up with the best French vinegar or cider.
3. Bottle well, and in about a week it will be ready for use.

title: Peppermint Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cupful of Sugar
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Essence of Peppermint
- A Pinch of Cream of Tartar
- Water just sufficient to moisten

## Directions

1. Crush the sugar very fine, and boil for five minutes with water just sufficient to moisten
2. Then take from the fire, and add the Cream of Tartar, and after mixing well add the Essence of Peppermint
3. Beat briskly until the mixture begins to whiten well, and then drop upon white paper and dry in the open air
4. If it sugars before all is dropped, add a little water and boil again for two minutes

title: Perlaro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Flour
- 1 Pound of Butter, or Half Butter and Lard
- 12 Eggs
- 1½ Ounce of Yeast
- 1 Gill of Brandy
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water
- Milk or Tepid Water to Form a Dough
- 1 Pound of Figs or Stoned Cherries

## Directions

1. Make the same dough as for the Brioche.
2. At the moment of adding the leavened flour and the reserved flour, add the stoned cherries or peeled figs, the latter cut in halves.
3. Then proceed as for Brioches, and at the last kneading add the orange flower water and the brandy.
4. Bake as you would the Brioche.

title: Persicot
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Bitter Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Cinnamon
- 2½ Gallons of Alcohol or Fine Brandy
- 6 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds and pound and mix with the cinnamon, and steep for fifteen days in the alcohol.
2. Distill in a water bath, add the syrup, color with Cochineal or Caramel, and bottle for use.

title: Perslcot
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Bitter Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Cinnamon
- 2.5 Gallons of Alcohol or Fine Brandy
- 6 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds and pound and mix with the cinnamon, and steep for fifteen days in the alcohol
2. Distill in a water bath
3. Add the syrup
4. Color with Cochineal or Caramel
5. Bottle for use

title: Petit Brule
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- an ordinary-sized, thick-skinned orange
- two lumps of sugar
- cinnamon
- fine French brandy (cognac)

## Directions

1. Take an ordinary-sized, thick-skinned orange; cut through the peel entirely around the orange like the line of the equator
2. Force off the peel by passing the handle of a spoon between it and the pulp
3. Into the cup thus formed put two lumps of sugar and some cinnamon
4. Fill with fine French brandy (cognac)
5. Ignite it and pour into glasses

title: Petits Fours
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sweet Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 2 Pounds of Sifted Flour
- The Whites of 8 Eggs
- Royal Icing to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the paste as above directed, keeping it somewhat stiffier and firmer, however.
2. Add two whites of eggs of Royal Icing (see recipe), and work both together till thoroughly incorporated.
3. Fill the biscuit forcer, and push the macaroons through upon buttered wafer paper, as directed.
4. Bake in a very moderate oven. Otherwise the excessive heat will cause the macaroons to run into one another, and produce a useless mass.
5. Put the Petits Fours in pretty plaited paper cases.

title: Picayune Frogs à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- A Half Dozen Picayune Frogs
- 3 Eggs
- A Cup of Sifted Bread Crumbs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Gills of Milk or Fresh Cream
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/4 of an Onion Chopped Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Lemon Juice
- Lard for frying
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Take a half dozen Picayune Frogs, the more delicate the better
2. Cut off the hind legs and skin
3. Scald the legs about four minutes in boiling water, adding salt and lemon juice to the water
4. Take out and dry with a clean towel
5. Beat the yolks and whites of two or three eggs, and mix in bread crumbs sifted
6. Have ready a pot of heated lard
7. Rub the Frog legs well with pepper and salt, and put into the pot of hot lard
8. Let them fry to a nice golden brown
9. Make a sauce: Put one tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan over the fire
10. As soon as it melts, add the flour, which has been rubbed smoothly
11. Stir in gradually, and when blended, add by degrees the boiling milk, stirring constantly to prevent burning
12. Take a teaspoonful of chopped parsley and a half of a chopped onion that has been well grated
13. Have ready two fresh eggs, beaten in a bowl
14. Warm the Frogs' legs in the sauce, and when it begins to simmer stir the sauce briskly into the eggs
15. Return to the back of the stove a minute or two, being careful not to let the sauce boil after the eggs have been added, else they will curdle the sauce
16. Serve hot

title: Pickle Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful and a half of butter
- 1 tablespoonful and a half of flour
- Half a cup of water or white broth
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Chopped gherkins or other vinegar pickles, about two or three

## Directions

1. To the recipe for white sauce add chopped gherkins, or any other vinegar pickles, using about two or three
2. Add just before serving
3. Serve with fish

title: Pickled Cabbage
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of whole black pepper
- allspice
- cloves
- cinnamon
- 2 quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Lay slices of red cabbage into a jar, and strew salt over each layer
2. Put 2 tablespoonfuls of whole black pepper, allspice, cloves and cinnamon in a bag, and scald them in 2 quarts of vinegar
3. Pour the vinegar over the cabbage, and cover it tight
4. It will be fit for use in 3 days

title: Pickled Cabbage
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- nicely headed cabbages
- 1 gill of salt
- cold water
- cold vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of turmeric
- 2 gallons of the best vinegar
- 2 lbs. of brown sugar
- 1 tablespoonful best cayenne pepper
- 2 ounces of turmeric

## Directions

1. Quarter the cabbages
2. Put them in a porcelain skillet of cold water with 1 gill of salt, and let them boil until they may be pierced easily with a straw
3. Place them on a dish carefully to drain
4. While draining, take a jar of cold vinegar and stir in 2 tablespoonfuls of turmeric
5. Place the cabbage in this vinegar and let it remain 48 hours
6. Take 2 gallons of the best vinegar, 2 lbs. of brown sugar, 1 tablespoonful best cayenne pepper, 2 ounces of turmeric and tie them up in 6 muslin bags
7. Boil two of these bags with the sugar and pepper in the vinegar, and while boiling, pour upon the cabbage, after having been placed in a dry place with the other bags of turmeric
8. Made and fit for use in one week

title: Pickled Cauliflower
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe, full-blown cauliflowers
- boiling water
- one ounce of mace
- one quart of good cider vinegar
- white pepper
- white mustard seed
- ginger

## Directions

1. Take large, ripe, full-blown cauliflowers
2. Divide the pieces equally
3. Throw them into a kettle of boiling water
4. Boil them until a little soft, but not as much as if for the table
5. Take the pieces out and let them cool
6. Scald an ounce of mace, to each quart of good cider vinegar
7. Pour it hot on the cauliflower
8. Spices such as are usually used in pickling, improve this recipe, and should be tied in a bag and thrown in with the pickle at the last
9. Use white pepper, white mustard seed and ginger as the spices suitable for this pickle

title: Pickled Cauliflower
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen cauliflowers
- 2 teaspoonfuls of mustard per quart of vinegar
- 1/2 cup of white sugar per quart of vinegar
- salt and water

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower for about twenty minutes in salt and water
2. Remove and drain, and break carefully, and let them get cold
3. Pack in a jar and pour over them hot spiced vinegar which you will have strained, having first stirred into each quart of vinegar two teaspoonfuls of French mustard and a half cup of white sugar
4. Cover the cauliflower well with the mixture and bottle tight

title: Pickled Cauliflowers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe, fullblown cauliflowers
- salt
- mace
- nutmeg
- best cider vinegar
- sweet oil

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and stalks, and divide the blossom into pieces or clusters of equal size
2. Throw them into a porcelain kettle of boiling water (adding a little salt)
3. Let them simmer, and skim them well
4. When they come to a boil, take them up with a perforated skimmer, and lay them on a sieve to drain
5. Put them into stone jars (3 parts full)
6. Season with mace and nutmeg infused in sufficient of the best cider vinegar, and simmer it for 1 hour
7. When it comes to boil, take it off the fire, and pour it hot over the cauliflower in the jar, filling it quite up to the top, and adding sweet oil at the last
8. Cover it while warm, and tie leather over the top

title: Pickled Cherries
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Morella cherries
- mace
- cinnamon
- brown sugar (2 large tablespoonfuls to 1 quart of vinegar)

## Directions

1. Remove the stems
2. Prepare the vinegar with mace and cinnamon, with sufficient quantity of brown sugar (say 2 large tablespoonfuls to 1 quart of vinegar)
3. Boil all together for a few minutes
4. Put it over the cherries when cold
5. Put them away in jars, carefully excluding the air

title: Pickled Cherries
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries with stems
- 1 pint of vinegar per quart
- 2 large teaspoonfuls of sugar per quart
- a dozen cloves per quart
- allspice
- 5 or 6 blades of mace per quart

## Directions

1. Let the vinegar and spices boil together for about five minutes, and then turn into an earthen jar and let them cool.
2. Then strain and fill the pickle jars with the cherries about three-quarters full, and pour the cold vinegar over them.
3. Bottle tight, and keep in a cool place.

title: Pickled Cucumbers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Wipe and put them in salt and water, strong enough to bear an egg
2. Let them remain 6 or 7 days
3. Scald in vinegar to green them
4. Put them in cold spiced vinegar

title: Pickled Cucumbers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- strong brine
- cucumbers
- fig leaves
- grape leaves
- vinegar
- water
- alum
- horse radish
- garlic
- 2 lbs. of brown sugar
- turmeric
- cloves
- mace
- nutmeg
- allspice
- celery seed
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Make a strong brine and let it boil in a bell metal skillet
2. Place cucumbers in a jar with layers of fig and grape leaves, alternately, until filled
3. Pour the boiling brine over the cucumbers
4. Cover up closely
5. Repeat the next day with the same brines, but fresh leaves
6. If sufficiently green, scald with vinegar and water on the day following
7. Let them remain 24 hours
8. Boil strong vinegar with a small piece of alum, horse radish and garlic, with 2 lbs. of brown sugar and a small quantity of turmeric, cloves, mace, and nutmeg, allspice, celery seed and cayenne pepper, say ½ ounce of each

title: Pickled Cucumbers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- water
- cucumbers
- cabbage leaves
- alum
- vine leaves
- vinegar
- cloves
- mace
- mustard seed
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a brine of salt and water strong enough to bear an egg
2. Pour it over the cucumbers and cover them with cabbage leaves
3. Let them stand for a week until they are quite yellow
4. Pour off the water
5. Take a porcelain kettle and cover the bottom with fresh leaves
6. Put in the cucumbers with a piece of alum
7. Cover them closely with vine leaves and then with a dish or cloth to keep in the steam
8. Fill up kettle with clear water and hang it over the fire, but not in a blaze
9. Keep the fire under the kettle moderate and keep them over the fire in a slow heat for several hours
10. If they are not then a pretty green, repeat the process
11. When they are well greened, take them out of the kettle and drain them on a sieve
12. Put them into a clean stone jar
13. Spice the vinegar with cloves, mace, mustard seed and sugar
14. Boil well for 5 or 6 minutes

title: Pickled Damsons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Damsons
- brown sugar of the best kind

## Directions

1. Do these in the same way as the plums
2. Allow brown sugar of the best kind

title: Pickled Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four dozen eggs
- scalding vinegar
- whole pepper
- cloves
- allspice
- ginger
- cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Boil three or four dozen eggs for half an hour
2. Let them cool and then take off the shells
3. Place them in wide-mouthed jars
4. Pour over them scalding vinegar seasoned with whole pepper, cloves, or allspice, ginger, and a few cloves of garlic
5. When cold, bung them down very close
6. Let them be well covered with the vinegar
7. In a month they will be fit for use

title: Pickled English Walnuts
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- nuts
- salt
- water
- common vinegar
- good vinegar
- allspice
- cloves
- horse radish
- made mustard
- red pepper
- garlic

## Directions

1. Put the nuts in strong salt and water for 12 days, changing the water several times
2. After which put them in fresh water a short time
3. Wipe them dry and put them in the sun until they become black
4. Put them in common vinegar for several weeks
5. Spice the good vinegar with allspice and cloves, horse radish, made mustard, red pepper, garlic, and a little salt
6. Boil the vinegar and pour it over the nuts while hot

title: Pickled Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds of fruit
- 3 pounds of white sugar
- 1 pint of best cider vinegar
- 1 ounce each of cloves, allspice, mace and cinnamon
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put the fruit into a kettle with alternate layers of sugar and spices, and add the vinegar.
2. Let all boil for about five minutes, and then skim out the fruit and pack in glass jars as it begins to cool.
3. Let the syrup boil till thick, and then pour the boiling syrup over.
4. Bottle tight, and examine from time to time.
5. If they show signs of fermenting, open the jars or set them uncovered in a kettle of boiling water, and heat the contents till they begin to bubble and cork again.

title: Pickled Lemons
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- small lemons with thick rind
- salt
- vinegar
- brine of the lemons
- pepper
- ginger
- two ounces of mustard seed
- two cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Take small lemons with thick rind
2. Rub them hard with a piece of flannel
3. Slit them through the rind in four quarters, but not through the pulp
4. Fill the slits with salt hard pressed in
5. Set them upright in a crock four or five days, until the salt melts
6. Turn them each day in their own liquid until they get tender
7. Make the pickle to cover them of vinegar, some of the brine of the lemons, pepper and ginger
8. Boil this pickle and skim it well
9. When cold put it over the lemons with two ounces of mustard seed and two cloves of garlic, to six lemons

title: Pickled Lemons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large lemons
- 1 lb. of salt
- 6 large cloves of garlic
- 2 ounces of horse radish sliced thin
- 1 ounce of cloves, mace, nutmeg, and cayenne
- 2 ounces of flour of mustard
- 2 quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wipe the lemons
2. Put on them salt, garlic, horse radish, cloves, mace, nutmeg, cayenne, and flour of mustard
3. Add vinegar
4. Boil 1 hour
5. Set the jar by and stir it daily for 6 weeks
6. Keep it closed

title: Pickled Mangoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 pepper mangoes or young melon mangoes or muskmelons
- a piece of alum the size of a pecan
- 1/2 pound of ginger
- 1/4 ounce each of black pepper, mace, allspice, turmeric
- 1/4 pound of garlic
- 1/2 pint of grated horseradish
- 1/2 pint each of black and white mustard seed
- 1/2 teacupful of the best olives
- 1/2 head of fine young cabbage
- 4 green tomatoes
- brine
- pure water
- green grape leaves
- vinegar
- cider vinegar
- 1 pound and 1/2 of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Take two dozen pepper mangoes or young muskmelons that are no larger than an orange
2. Let them lie for two weeks in a brine strong enough to float an egg
3. Soak them in pure water for two days, changing the water every day
4. Remove the seed by cutting a slit down the side of the mangoes, but do not take the meat out
5. Rinse in cold water
6. Put a layer of green grape leaves in a kettle, and lay on this a layer of mangoes, and then a layer of leaves, and so continue till all are used up
7. Let the top layer be of grape leaves
8. Add a piece of alum about the size of a pecan, and pour vinegar over the mixture, and let all boil for fifteen or twenty minutes
9. Take out the leaves, and let the mangoes remain in this vinegar for five or six days
10. Prepare a stuffing of half a pound of ginger which has been soaked in brine for two days and cut into small dice; one-half ounce each of black pepper, mace, allspice, turmeric; one-quarter of a pound of garlic which has been soaked in brine for twenty-four hours and dried; a half pint of grated horseradish, and a half pint each of black and white mustard seed
11. Bruise all the spices well till fine, and mix them with a half teacupful of the best olive oil
12. Take a half head of fine solid cabbage, and chop fine; add a half pint of finely-chopped onions, a few small cucumbers, and a few green tomatoes which have lain in a brine overnight
13. Drain all well and then select any imperfectly shaped mangoes, or any that have become too soft to stuff, and chop these fine with the spices
14. Mix all this stuffing well together, and stuff the mangoes
15. Tie them with pieces of twine, or sew them up, put in a stone jar, and pour over them the very best cider vinegar
16. Set them in a dry place and cover well
17. At the end of a month add a pound and a half of brown sugar, and bottle for use

title: Pickled Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 small onion buttons
- 1 tablespoonful of allspice
- 1 tablespoonful of black pepper and 1 red pepper pod to each pint of vinegar
- 1 dozen white pepper corns
- 1 dozen cloves
- 4 blades of mace
- strong brine
- cold water

## Directions

1. Select the smallest white button onions
2. Remove the outer skin, and then remove the inner skin with a silver knife
3. Pack them in well-washed and carefully dried jars
4. Pour over the onions a strong brine, and let them stand for twenty-four hours
5. At the end of this time pour off this brine and add another, and let them stand twenty-four hours longer
6. On the fourth day make another strong brine, and let it come to a boil, and throw in the onions, and let them boil just five minutes
7. Throw them into cold water for several hours
8. At the end of three hours drain off all water and pack closely in jars, allowing one tablespoonful of black pepper and a red pepper pod to every pint of vinegar

title: Pickled Onions
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- water
- strong brine
- good vinegar
- mace
- horse radish
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Put the onions in salt and water strong enough to bear an egg
2. Change the water every day
3. Make a strong brine and pour it on boiling hot for 3 days
4. Lay them on a sieve to drain
5. Put them in the jar
6. Fill it with good vinegar
7. Add mace and horse radish
8. If you wish them white, put salt and oil over the top
9. If you prefer them yellow, add from 2 to 3 tablespoonfuls of turmeric with the vinegar
10. Tie them up very close and they will keep any length of time for table use

title: Pickled Onions
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- water
- good vinegar
- mace
- horse radish
- turmeric

## Directions

1. Put the onions in salt and water strong enough to bear an egg
2. Change the water every day
3. Make a strong brine, pour it on boiling hot for 3 days
4. Lay them on a sieve to drain
5. Put them in the jar
6. Fill it with good vinegar, add mace and horse radish
7. If you wish them white, put salt and oil over the top
8. If you prefer them yellow, add from 2 to 3 tablespoonfuls of turmeric with the vinegar
9. Tie them up very close

title: Pickled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 or 100 Large, Fine Oysters
- 12 Blades of Mace
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Allspice
- 1 Level Tablespoonful of Cloves
- 1 Pepper
- 2 Sliced Lemons
- 1/2 Pint of White Vinegar
- Salt to Taste
- 1/2 Dozen Pepper Corns
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters in their own liquor until the edges begin to ruffle
2. Take a half pint of white wine vinegar and a half pint of the oyster water and set to boil, adding the blades of mace, cloves, allspice, pepper corns and a dash of Cayenne
3. Salt to taste
4. As soon as they come to a good boil, pour the oysters into the boiling liquor
5. Care must be taken to have the oysters very cold, as they will make the pickles slimy otherwise
6. After adding the oysters to the boiling liquid, set it aside to cool
7. Put in a very cool ice box and serve cold

title: Pickled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 or 100 Large, Fine Oysters
- 12 Blades of Mace
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Allspice
- 1 Level Tablespoonful of Cloves
- 1 Pepper
- 2 Sliced Lemons
- 1/2 Pint of White Vinegar
- Salt to Taste
- 1/2 Dozen Pepper Corns
- White wine

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters in their own liquor until the edges begin to ruffle.
2. Then take a half pint of white wine

title: Pickled Oysters
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fine large oysters
- butter
- their own liquor
- vinegar
- ground mace
- cloves
- allspice
- whole pepper
- sweet oil

## Directions

1. Put oysters over a gentle fire in their own liquor, and a small lump of butter to each hundred oysters
2. Let them boil ten minutes, when they are plump and white
3. Take them from their liquor with a skimmer and spread them on a thickly folded cloth
4. When they are firm and cold take half as much of their own liquor and half of good vinegar, make this hot
5. Take a stone crock, put in a layer of oysters, a spoonful of ground mace, a dozen cloves, allspice, and whole pepper alternately
6. If to be kept, put them in glass jars with a little sweet oil on top
7. Stop them and seal tight, and they will, if kept in a cool place, be good for months

title: Pickled Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- 2 pints of vinegar
- allspice and mace

## Directions

1. Save the liquor when opening the oysters
2. Scald them in it
3. Take them out of the liquor
4. To 1.5 pints of liquor add 2 pints of vinegar, with allspice and mace
5. Boil these ingredients together for 1 hour
6. When cold pour it over the oysters

title: Pickled Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- mace
- pepper
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters till quite done, in their own liquor, with as much mace and pepper as will season them
2. Pour off and strain the liquor and vinegar sufficient to cover the oysters
3. Stir in salt to your taste

title: Pickled Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- weak lye
- strong salt and water
- strong vinegar
- mace
- cloves
- horse radish
- white mustard seed
- turmeric

## Directions

1. Wash your peaches in weak lye
2. Rub them with a coarse cloth to remove the fur
3. Put them in strong salt and water for 7 or 8 days
4. Wipe them dry
5. Put them carefully into the jars
6. Cover them with strong vinegar
7. Add mace, cloves, horse radish, white mustard seed and turmeric

title: Pickled Plums
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large fine plums
- white sugar powdered
- best cider vinegar
- nutmeg
- mace
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take large fine plums, perfect and quite ripe
2. To every quart of plums allow ½ lb. of the best white sugar powdered, and a large pint of the best cider vinegar
3. Melt the sugar in the vinegar
4. Put it, with the fruit, in a porcelain kettle
5. Previously prick all the plums to the stone with a large needle
6. Lay among them some small muslin bags filled with broken nutmeg, mace, and cinnamon
7. Give them one boil up, skimming them well
8. Put them warm into stone jars, with the bags interspersed
9. Cork them immediately

title: Pickled Pork
body:
## Ingredients

- 23 Pounds of Pork
- Coarse Salt Sufficient to make a Brine
- 1 Ounce of Saltpetre
- 12 Bay Leaves
- 2 Dozen Onions
- 12 Cloves
- 6 Allspice

## Directions

1. Pork should be pickled about twenty hours after killing.
2. To twenty-five pounds of pork, allow one ounce of saltpetre.
3. Pulverize saltpetre thoroughly and mix with a sufficient quantity of salt to thoroughly salt the pork.
4. Cut the pork into pieces of about two pounds, and slash each piece through the skin, and then rub thoroughly with the salt and saltpetre mixture till the meat is thoroughly penetrated through and through.
5. Mash the cloves very fine and ground the allspice.
6. Chop the onions.
7. Take a small barrel and place at the bottom a layer of salt, then a layer of coarsely chopped onions, and sprinkle over this a layer of the spices and minced bay leaves.
8. Place on this a layer of the pork; pack tightly; then place above this a layer of the salt and seasonings and continue with alternate layers of pork and seasonings till all the pork is used up.
9. Conclude with a layer of the minced herbs and spices and have a layer of salt on top.
10. Cover the preparation with a board on which a heavy weight must be placed to press down the meat.
11. It will be ready for use in about ten or twelve days.

title: Pickled Radish Pods
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- radish pods
- salt
- water
- white vinegar
- mace
- black pepper
- horse radish

## Directions

1. Put the pods, which must be gathered when very young, into salt and water all night
2. Boil the salt and water they were laid in
3. Pour it upon the pods, and cover the jar close, to keep in the steam
4. When it is nearly cold, pour it on again, and continue to do so till the pods are green
5. Drain them on a sieve and make a pickle for them of white vinegar, mace, black pepper and horse radish
6. Pour it boiling hot upon the pods
7. Tie them down, and put them away for use

title: Pickled String Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds of beans
- 1 quart of best cider vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of alum
- 1 ounce of cloves
- 6 red pepper pods

## Directions
