1. Take young tender beans, and cut the two extremities lightly, and string carefully.
2. Throw them into fresh water for several hours.
3. Wash well and drain, and put them into earthen jars.
4. Pour over boiling vinegar, spiced as for Tomato Pickle, and allow one-quarter of a teaspoonful of powdered alum to each quart of vinegar.
5. Cover and cork well.
6. Three days after pour off this vinegar and boil again and pour over the pickles, and let them stand again for three days.
7. Add a few cloves and pieces of red pepper pods, and bottle closely.

title: Pickled Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fair, ripe tomatoes
- pounded mace
- cloves
- cinnamon
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Prick the skins of fair, ripe tomatoes
2. Spread them in layers
3. On each layer put pounded mace, cloves and cinnamon
4. Pour cold vinegar over the whole

title: Pickled Watermelon Rind
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of watermelon rind
- 1/2 pint of best French vinegar
- 1 pound of brown sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of mixed ground cloves, mace, cinnamon and allspice

## Directions

1. Cut the watermelon rind into strips, cook in clear water and drain.
2. To four pounds of rind take one pound of brown sugar and half a pint of vinegar, and make a syrup, to which add the ground spices.
3. Boil the watermelon rind in this syrup till quite tender.
4. Put in jars while hot and seal.

title: Pie Crust
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Sifted Flour
- 1 1/2 Cups of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt
- Nearly a Cup of Tepid Water

## Directions

1. Rub the flour and butter very lightly together, being careful not to allow it to harden under touch.
2. Keep on kneading a little butter at a time into the flour till you have used up all and the dough feels greasy to the touch.
3. Always remember to add the salt before putting in the butter.
4. Add, little by little, the water, kneading gently, till you have a nice, soft dough, lifting out the portion that is wet and continuing to knead as you mix the flour and water.
5. Always be very careful in adding the water, and never wet the flour twice in the same place.
6. Mix all together lightly with your hands when all is moistened, using always your own good judgment in adding water.
7. Do not work the dough; simply mix lightly and thoroughly.
8. Dredge the board lightly with flour.
9. If you intend to make two pies, cut the dough into four parts.

title: Pie-Crust
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Three and a half cups of flour
- one cup of sweet lard
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one teaspoonful of baking powder
- a cupful of very cold water

## Directions

1. Mix with a knife, using the hands as little as possible
2. Roll and cut after the crust is on the pie-plate

title: Pigeon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Young Wild Pigeons
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig Thyme
- 1 Sprig Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig Parsley
- Pie Paste
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the young pigeons into small pieces of about two inches in length and make a plain fricassee.
2. Prepare a Vol-au-Vent Paste and fill a tin pan of about two quarts with the paste.
3. Pour in pigeons and gravy, and let bake in the oven till the top crust is nicely browned.
4. Always bake the under crust first.
5. Alternatively, after cleaning the pigeons, stuff each daintily with oyster or egg dressing and then loosen the joints with a knife without separating them.
6. Put into a stewpan and make a plain fricassee as indicated.
7. Let them cook until tender, and season with salt and pepper.
8. Fill the pie dish with the pie paste, put in the birds, pour over the gravy, cover with a crust and bake.

title: Pigeon Pie, Very Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- their own livers
- a little bacon
- some butter
- parsley
- rolled cracker or a small piece of bread
- salt
- slices of veal or beef
- chopped parsley
- mushrooms
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- plain beef-stock or water
- gravy

## Directions

1. Truss the pigeons and stuff them with their own livers, a little bacon, some butter, parsley, and rolled cracker or a small piece of bread
2. Salt to taste
3. Cover the bottom of the baking dish with slices of veal or beef
4. Season with chopped parsley, mushrooms, pepper, salt, and butter
5. Place the pigeons on this
6. Lay between each two pigeons the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
7. Cover with a nice pie crust
8. Parboil the pigeons a little, also the beef, before putting them in the pan
9. Keep the water they were boiled in to fill up the pie
10. Add plain beef-stock or water as the pie bakes to keep it very moist

title: Pigeon Stew
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour
- butter
- soup-stock or gravy
- wine or stock

## Directions

1. Pick and wash the pigeons, stuff them with bread crumbs, parsley, pepper, salt and butter mixed.
2. Dust with flour, and put into a pan to brown.
3. Add butter and a little soup-stock or gravy.
4. Stew gently until tender.
5. Before dishing add a glass of wine if approved, if not, a little more stock, if the gravy has become too thick.

title: Pigeons and Crawfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pigeons
- 2 Dozen Crawfish
- 1 Slice of Fat Bacon
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Juice of a Lemon
- 1 Cup of Bouillon
- Buttered Croftons

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons nicely, and rub inside and out with the juice of a lemon.
2. Rub with salt and pepper, and brush with melted butter.
3. Place thin strips of fat bacon in the bottom of a saucepan, lay the pigeons on this, and cover with another thin layer of strips of bacon.
4. Cover with butter and set in a slow oven, and let them simmer gently.
5. Take a small square inch of ham, chop or mince very fine, and fry in a tablespoonful of butter.
6. Add an onion and a carrot, chopped fine.
7. Let these brown, and then add an herb bouquet, minced very fine.
8. When brown, add a cup of bouillon, and let it boil for ten minutes.
9. Pour this sauce over the baking pigeons, and let them cook slowly for about an hour, or until done.
10. In the meantime boil about two dozen nice crawfish according to recipe, and when the pigeons are done, place them on buttered Croftons and place between each a garnish of crawfish.
11. Add about a half cup of the broth in which the crawfish have been boiled to the gravy in the baking dish.
12. Let it simmer for five minutes till reduced slightly, and pour over the pigeons.

title: Pigs' Feet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Pigs Feet
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 3 Blades of Mace
- 1 Dozen whole cloves
- 1 Whole red pepper pod
- 1 Pint of good cider vinegar
- Salt, pepper and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Select young and tender pigs' feet.
2. Clean and scrape well and soak in cold water several hours.
3. Split and crack the feet in several places.
4. Put them in a stewpot and cover with cold water.
5. Let them simmer until tender.
6. When done, lay in a crock.
7. Boil the vinegar, mace, cloves, bay leaves and pepper pod together a few minutes.
8. Season the feet with salt and pepper, and pour the spiced vinegar over while boiling hot.
9. Cover the crock and set to cool.
10. The feet will be ready for use in twenty-four hours.

title: Pigs' Feet, Sauce Robert
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pigs' Feet
- 1 Tablespoonful of butter or olive oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of salt
- 3/8 Tablespoonful of pepper
- Grated bread crumbs
- 1/2 Pint of Sauce Robert

## Directions

1. Boil three good-sized Pigs' Feet in salted water.
2. When tender, take out of the water and drain thoroughly.
3. Split the feet in two and place in a dish.
4. Season well with salt and pepper.
5. Rub them with the olive oil or butter.
6. Roll the feet in grated bread crumbs.
7. Put them to broil, allowing four minutes to each side of the feet.
8. Prepare a hot Sauce a la Robert.
9. Pour this sauce in a warm dish.
10. Lay the feet nicely over it and send to the table hot.

title: Pilaff of Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken About Four Pounds
- 0.5 Cup of Rice
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken as you would for a fricassee.
2. Put in a stewpan and cover well with water.
3. Add salt and pepper to taste, having previously rubbed the fowl with salt and pepper.
4. Let the chicken simmer gently for about an hour.
5. Take a half cup of rice and wash it thoroughly.
6. Add it to the chicken.
7. Salt again to taste.
8. Cover and let all simmer for about twenty minutes longer.
9. Make a Tomato Sauce.
10. Dish the chicken and rice together, setting the chicken in the center of the dish and the rice around for a border.
11. Serve hot.

title: Pineapple and Orange Ambrosia
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Ripe Pineapple
- 6 Oranges
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Glass of Sherry or White Wine

## Directions

1. Slice one large, ripe pineapple and six oranges.
2. Put in a glass bowl one layer of oranges, sprinkle pulverized sugar over them; then one layer of pineapples, also sprinkled with sugar and continue till all the fruit is used.
3. Pour a wineglassful of Sherry or White Wine over the top.
4. Let it stand about two hours before serving.

title: Pineapple Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Grated Pineapple
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3/4 Pint of Gelatine
- 3/4 Cup of Cold Water

## Directions

1. Cover the gelatine with the water, and let it soak for half an hour
2. Stand it over boiling water, and let it melt
3. Grate the pineapple, and add the sugar, and let it simmer gently till reduced to a liquid
4. Stir the gelatine into the pineapple, and dissolve thoroughly
5. Place it in a tin vessel, and stand over cracked ice, stirring constantly
6. When it begins to thicken, add the whipped cream, and set away to harden, after turning into a mold
7. Serve with whipped cream

title: Pineapple Cream Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- ⅓ a Pineapple
- ⅓ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Gill of White Wine
- Cream Puff Paste

## Directions

1. Make a Cream Paste.
2. Prepare the pineapple as in the above recipe.
3. Dip in the Cream Puff Paste and proceed as in Apple Fritters.

title: Pineapple Eclairs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Cup of Water
- 4 Eggs
- 3/4 Cup of Water
- 2 Ounces of Preserved Pineapple or Pineapple Mousse

## Directions

1. Make Pineapple Eclairs in the same manner as Chocolate Eclairs
2. Fill them in with preserved pineapple or Pineapple Mousse

title: Pineapple Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- ⅓ a Pineapple
- ⅓ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Gill White Wine
- Fritter Batter

## Directions

1. Slice the pineapple, and cut the slices in halves.
2. Sprinkle with sugar and White Wine, and let them soak for an hour.
3. Then proceed as in Apple Fritters.
4. Or, simply sprinkle with sugar, let them stand one hour, add the juice to the fritter batter, and proceed as above.

title: Pineapple Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large, Ripe Pineapple
- 1 Quart of Cream or Milk
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple, and then grate and pound and mash the pulp with one-half of the sugar
2. Press through a sieve
3. Put the milk or cream on to boil
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the remaining half of the sugar very light, and then beat the whites to a stiff froth
5. Take the milk off the fire, and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring all the while
6. Add the whites, mixing thoroughly
7. Add the pineapple and juice and mix well
8. Set to cool and then turn into the freezer, pack and stir around in the ice box for about ten minutes, and proceed as in the recipe for making and freezing Cream

title: Pineapple Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapple, pared
- 3/4 of a pound of white sugar to every pound of grated pineapple

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapple, take out the eyes and grate carefully, or pick into fine pieces.
2. To every pound of pineapple allow three-quarters of a pound of white sugar.
3. Put the pineapple and the sugar to boil in a porcelain-lined kettle and cook over a moderate fire for ten or fifteen minutes.
4. Pour into jelly glasses or jars, cover tightly, and set in a cool place.

title: Pineapple Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 1 1/2 Cups of Pulverized Sugar
- 9 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon or Pineapple Extract
- 1 Pineapple
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare four layers of sponge cake
2. Pare the pineapple, being careful to cut out all the eyes and the core
3. Grate the fruit very fine
4. Place a layer of the fruit over the bottom layer of the cake, sprinkle with a little powdered sugar and then spread over this a little Cream Sauce, which has been made to the consistency of Cream Puff Filling
5. Put a second layer of the cake over this, cover nicely with the grated pineapple, sugar and Cream Filling, and proceed thus till the top layer is placed over the pineapple
6. Then ice or sprinkle with white sugar

title: Pineapple Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Four layers of sponge cake
- Pineapple
- Powdered sugar
- Cream Sauce (made to the consistency of Cream Puff Filling)

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapple, being careful to cut out all the eyes and the core.
2. Grate the fruit very fine.
3. Place a layer of the fruit over the bottom layer of the cake.
4. Sprinkle with a little powdered sugar.
5. Spread over this a little Cream Sauce, which has been made to the consistency of Cream Puff Filling.
6. Put a second layer of the cake over this.
7. Cover nicely with the grated pineapple, sugar and Cream Filling.
8. Proceed thus till the top layer is placed over the pineapple.
9. Ice or sprinkle with white sugar.

title: Pineapple Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Pineapple
- ¾ of a Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapple and remove the eyes.
2. Grate carefully, preserving the juice.
3. Allow three-fourths of a pound of sugar to every pound of the grated fruit.
4. Proceed as directed above.

title: Pineapple Mousse
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pineapple
- 3 Teaspoonfuls of Vanilla
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Jamaica Rum
- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1 Pound of Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple, and cut one-half into slices
2. Lay them in a bowl and sprinkle with powdered sugar and one-third of the rum
3. Cover and set aside till needed
4. Grate the remainder of the pineapple into an earthen vessel, and add sugar sufficient to sweeten to taste, and pour the vanilla and the rest of the rum over it
5. Mix carefully with the cream, which should be at least three times the weight of the pineapple
6. Turn all into an ice cream mold and proceed to freeze as directed
7. Serve with slices of pineapple and its syrup around the frozen composition, which should be turned out of the mold according to directions given for making and freezing cream

title: Pineapple Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Pineapple
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- A Plain Pie Paste

## Directions

1. Peel and slice into very fine, thin slices one pineapple.
2. Place with the juice in a saucepan with the sugar, and stew slightly.
3. Prepare a plain pie paste, and proceed in the same manner as for Apple Pie.

title: Pineapple Preserves
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fine pineapples
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Take fine pineapples, cut off all the rough parts, and each apple in quarters, shaping each piece alike.
2. Boil the pineapples in just enough water to cover them, and put to this water all the cuttings, so as to make the syrup as rich in flavor as possible.
3. When the pieces are tender, take them out, weigh them, and make a syrup of a pound of sugar to each pound of fruit, allowing a cup of the water the pineapples were boiled in, to each pound of fruit.
4. Strain the water over the sugar, mix it, and let it boil fifteen minutes, by itself.
5. Skim it, and put in the pineapples, letting them boil until they are clear and perfectly tender.

title: Pineapple Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- fine powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapple and remove the eyes
2. Slice it nicely and weigh it
3. Allow equal quantities of fine, powdered sugar
4. Place the fruit in a deep dish and sprinkle the sugar over it, between the slices, in alternate layers
5. Let it remain overnight
6. Drain off the syrup in the morning, and put it in a kettle
7. When it gets very hot put in the fruit
8. Let it cook slowly till the slices look very clear
9. Skim them out and put them in the jars
10. Fill to overflowing, and then seal

title: Pineapple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Pineapple
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Pound of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the milk in a saucepan and add the butter
2. Sift the flour and add gradually to the mixture, stirring briskly for three minutes
3. Take off the fire, and add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light
4. Stir well, and add the powdered sugar
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and add gradually to the milk
6. Take a pudding mold and butter well; sprinkle with sugar and put a layer of the milk-and-egg preparation about half an inch thick at the bottom
7. Put over this a layer of finely-sliced pineapple, then add another layer of the milk and eggs and a layer of the pineapple, and continue till all the pineapple and the mixture are used up
8. Set the mold in a tin pan of warm water, and place in the oven and cover with buttered paper
9. Let the pudding steam for one hour; then remove from the oven, turn into a pudding dish, and serve with a Sauce au Kirsch

title: Pineapple Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 very nice pineapples
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take 2 or 3 very nice pineapples
2. Pare and grate them into a bowl
3. Put the grated pineapple on a sieve and let it drain well
4. Press it down to get out every drop of juice
5. Weaken it as much as you please with water
6. Make it very sweet, as all things when frozen have less sweetness

title: Pineapple Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Grated Pineapple
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 Pint of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Peel and slice and pound the pineapple to a pulp.
2. Then rub through a strainer.
3. Put the sugar and water to boil for five minutes.
4. Then set away to cool.
5. Extract the juice from the lemon and mix this with the syrup.
6. Add the pineapple and juice, and then strain all through a thin muslin cloth.
7. Turn into the freezer and proceed as in ice cream.

title: Pineapple Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pineapple
- The Whites of Four Eggs
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1/4 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sherry

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapple, and grate it.
2. Beat the whites of the eggs until foamy, and then add by degrees the sifted sugar.
3. Add one tablespoonful of Sherry.
4. Stir the whipped cream, which must be very cold from standing on ice.
5. When all is very stiff beat in the pineapple, pulp and juice, adding as much as the cream and the meringue will hold without becoming too soft.
6. Place in glasses, and serve very cold.

title: Pineapple Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Pineapple Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1 Ounce of Lemon Juice

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapples, cutting out the eyes and the core
2. Cut into very fine pieces and mash and pound well, or grate the pineapple
3. Set aside for thirty-six hours in a cool place
4. Strain through a fine muslin bag, pressing out all the juice
5. Make a Plain Syrup and proceed as in the General Directions for Making Fruit Syrups

title: Pineapple Syrup for Ice Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapples
- white sugar (1 lb. per lb. of fruit)

## Directions

1. Pare and slice the pineapples, and to each lb. of fruit add 1 lb. of white sugar
2. Put in the jar first a layer of sugar pulverized, then a layer of pines
3. Then one of sugar and another of pines, until the jar is full
4. Tie up tight, to exclude the light and air
5. This is delicious, and keeps well
6. Not to be scalded

title: Pineapple Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pineapples
- 1 Quart of Pure Cider Vinegar
- Fine white sugar in the proportion of two-thirds of a pound to one quart of the juice

## Directions

1. Slice the pineapples
2. Cover with pure cider vinegar, and let them stand for three or four days
3. Mash them through a cloth, straining as long as the juice runs clear
4. To every quart of juice allow one and two-thirds pounds of fine white sugar
5. Boil all together for about ten minutes, and skim very carefully, until no scum rises upon the surface
6. Take the mixture from the fire, and when it cools somewhat bottle it
7. Put a tablespoonful of this extract in a glass of cold ice water in warm weather and serve as a summer drink

title: Pink Pralines
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Fine White Sugar (Granulated)
- 1 Freely-Grated Cocoanut (Small Size)
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cochineal

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as with White Pralines, only add about a tablespoonful of Cochineal to the pralines, just before taking off the fire.
2. Proceed to drop on a marble slab, as above.

title: Piquant Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 1 sprig each of thyme, parsley and bay leaf
- 2 pickles, 2 inches in length
- 1 teaspoonful of strong French vinegar
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Cayenne or hot pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of consommé or water

## Directions

1. Chop two onions very fine
2. Smother in a tablespoonful of butter
3. When well cooked, without burning, add one tablespoonful of consommé or water
4. Add two cloves of garlic, minced very fine, and the herbs minced very fine
5. Season to taste with hot pepper
6. Take two pickles about two inches in length, and cut into thin slices of about a quarter of an inch in thickness
7. Put this into the sauce, with a teaspoonful of strong vinegar, and let the whole boil about five minutes
8. Serve with boiled beef, boiled beef tongue, boiled pork tongue, or any boiled meats

title: Piquant Tomato Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen ripe tomatoes
- sliced onions
- meat gravy
- half a pint of brown gravy
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Mash half a dozen ripe tomatoes (pick out the seeds)
2. Put them in a stewpan with sliced onions, and a little meat gravy
3. Let them simmer till nearly dry
4. Add half a pint of brown gravy, left of cold meat, and let it cook twenty minutes
5. Strain and season with cayenne pepper, salt and lemon juice
6. Tarragon vinegar may be used instead of the lemon juice

title: Pistachio Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- % Pound of Sugar
- Yi Pound of Shelled, Scalded and Cleaned Pistachio Nuts
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Spinach Greening, or 6 Drops of Spinach Green
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water or Almond Extract

## Directions

1. Wash and boil the Spinach for five minutes, and then drain through a colander
2. Reduce it to a pulp, and then squeeze out the juice through a piece of fine muslin
3. Wash, scald, clean and pulverize the pistachios to a pulp
4. Set half the milk to boil in a farina boiler
5. Add the sugar, and stir till well dissolved
6. Take off and add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very, very light
7. Stir and add the nuts, and stir till it thickens
8. Stand away to cool
9. When cool, add the flavoring extract and the remaining cream, reserving a small portion to work into the Spinach, which you will add and blend smoothly
10. When the color is a light green, turn into a freezer and proceed as directed

title: Pistachio Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Bitter Almonds
- 6 Ounces of Shredded Pistachio Kernels or Peanuts
- 12 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and then pound them well
2. Mix with the whites of the eggs till thoroughly pulverized
3. Mix with the shredded pistachio kernels or peanuts, and stir well
4. Fill a biscuit forcer with the preparation, and drop the macaroons in round balls about the size of a hickory nut upon a wafer sheet of paper
5. Spread upon a baking sheet
6. Bake in a moderate oven till very slightly colored

title: Pistachio Soufflés
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Ounces of Pistachio Kernels or Peanuts
- 1½ Pounds of Fine Sugar
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- 4 Drops Essence of Roses
- 2 Whites of Eggs of Royal Icing

## Directions

1. Scald, skin, wash and dry the pistachios or peanuts.
2. Pound them with two ounces of sugar, adding occasionally some of the whites of the eggs, until reduced to a pulp.
3. Then add all the sugar, gradually working it into the paste on a marble slab, being careful to have the slab and the hands very clean.
4. Roll out paste with sugar to the thickness of a quarter of an inch on the slab.
5. Spread the icing evenly over, and cut into various fanciful shapes.
6. Place the cakes on the sheet wafer or lay on baking sheet, and bake in a slow heat to a very light color.

title: Plain Beef Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Five pounds of the leg or shin of beef
- One gallon of water
- A teaspoonful of salt
- Two heads of celery
- Five carrots
- Three onions
- Four turnips
- Two tomatoes
- A bunch of sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Boil four hours and a half.
2. Cut the meat in two or three pieces, and put them into a pot with a gallon of cold water, which gradually soaks out the juices of the meat before coming to the boil.
3. Salt well, then skim as the soup heats.
4. Boil slowly with a regular heat for about four hours;
5. Then add two heads of celery, five carrots cut small, two tomatoes, three onions sliced and fried, and the sweet herbs tied up in muslin.
6. The turnips should be added half an hour before serving.
7. If any portion of the meat is required for the table, take it from the soup about two hours before dinner.
8. Let the remainder be left in the soup, which must be strained through a hair sieve before it is served.

title: Plain Boiled Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips
- Drawn Butter Sauce
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Boil the turnips whole, selecting very tender ones.
2. They will require about an hour of boiling.
3. Always use hot water in putting on the turnips.
4. Drain off water, when tender, and serve with a Drawn Butter Sauce, pepper and salt.
5. Serve with roast beef, mutton or roast duck.

title: Plain Charlotte Russe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of isinglass
- one pint of water
- one-half pint of milk
- yolks of four eggs
- one-fourth of a pound of sugar
- vanilla or lemon for flavoring
- one quart of cream
- lady fingers or sponge cake

## Directions

1. Boil one ounce of isinglass in a pint of water until reduced one-half
2. While it is boiling, make a custard of one-half pint of milk, yolks of four eggs, and one-fourth of a pound of sugar
3. Flavor the custard with vanilla or lemon
4. Take a quart of cream and whip it up to a fine froth
5. When the isinglass is nearly cold, so that it will not curdle the cream, stir it and the cream into the custard
6. Beat all thoroughly and set it on ice
7. Line a glass dish with lady fingers and pour in the cream, or slice any light sponge cake and lay it on the bottom and sides of the glass bowl
8. Lay fine fancy sugar-drops on top if desired

title: Plain Chicken Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- fat meat
- onions
- Irish potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- sweet butter
- eggs
- milk
- crust

## Directions

1. Take two nice chickens, or more if they are small
2. Cut them up as for frying
3. Put them in a pot to stew with some slices of fat meat
4. Let them cook for half an hour
5. Add a few onions and four Irish potatoes sliced small
6. Season with pepper, salt, a little parsley, and a quarter of a pound of sweet butter
7. When it is cooked well there should be gravy enough to cover the chickens
8. If you want it very nice, beat up two eggs, and stir into the stew with half a pint of milk
9. Line a five-quart pan with a crust made like soda biscuit, only more shortening
10. Put in the chickens and gravy
11. Cover with a top crust
12. Bake until the crust is done

title: Plain Chicken-Broth for an Invalid
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- A young fowl cut into four parts
- One quart of cold water
- A little salt
- The white heart of a head of lettuce
- A handful of chervil
- Two tablespoonfuls of pearl barley

## Directions

1. Cut a young fowl into four parts, wash well in cold water, put the pieces in a stewpan with one quart of cold water and a little salt;
2. Let it boil gently, skim it well;
3. Add the white heart of a head of lettuce and a handful of chervil.
4. Boil the broth for an hour, then strain it into a bowl.
5. Two tablespoonfuls of pearl barley added to the broth when first put on makes it quite nourishing for an invalid.

title: Plain Cold Slaw with Vinegar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- pepper
- salt
- one cup of cold, sharp vinegar
- hard-boiled eggs
- onions

## Directions

1. Chop or slice the cabbage
2. Soak the cabbage until it is cool and firm
3. Dust pepper on it
4. Throw in a little salt
5. Pour over it a cup of cold, sharp vinegar
6. Slice hard-boiled eggs and put over the cabbage when sent to table
7. Sliced onions can also be used, seasoned with salt, pepper and vinegar
8. Mix a little sweet, sliced vegetable pepper with the onions

title: Plain Corn Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of the Finest Indian Meal
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- A Heaping Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Pour just enough boiling water over the meal to scald it through and through, without making a dough or batter.
2. Stir it well, and let it grow tepid.
3. Add a melted teaspoonful of lard or butter.
4. Mix this well, and add water, and beat till the batter is very smooth.
5. Dissolve one teaspoonful of soda in a little boiling water, and add to the mixture, and grease a baking pan.
6. Turn the mixture in and bake in a quick oven for about thirty-five minutes, till a golden brown.
7. Take the bread from the pan whole, and place on a hot platter, and serve, cutting it at the table into slices about two inches long and three wide, and serving with butter.

title: Plain Doughnuts
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds or pint cups full of light risen dough
- half a pound of butter
- one half pound of sugar
- one half pint of milk
- three eggs
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- flour
- boiling lard for frying

## Directions

1. Add butter, sugar, milk, eggs, cinnamon and nutmeg to the risen dough
2. Cover it and set it to rise
3. When light, add a little flour to stiffen the dough
4. Cut it into shapes
5. Fry in boiling lard

title: Plain French Dressing for Salads
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Tablespoonfuls of the Best Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar, According to Taste
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Black Pepper

## Directions

1. First put the oil into a small bowl.
2. Then add gradually the salt and pepper until all are thoroughly mixed.
3. Then add gradually the vinegar, stirring continually for about a minute.
4. It is now ready to pour over the salad, and remember that it must be mixed thoroughly.

title: Plain Fritter Batter for Meats, Poultry, etc.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Flour
- 1 Cup of Water
- 2 Eggs
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the flour together, and add the melted butter and the salt.
2. Then add the water, and beat well, and finally add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Some add a half teaspoonful of baking powder according to taste.

title: Plain Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 4 Eggs
- The Zest of Half a Lemon
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- Flavoring to Taste
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the whites separate.
2. Sift the baking powder into the flour, and add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten.
3. Beat well, and add the milk, and flavoring of orange, vanilla or brandy to taste.
4. Add the zest of a lemon, grated very fine, and salt in quantity given above.
5. Lastly, add the whites, beaten to a stiff froth, and have the batter of such consistency that it will pour from the spoon.
6. Drop it in the boiling lard by large kitchen spoonfuls, and let it fry to a golden yellow.
7. Lift out with a skimmer, and drain and place on a heated dish, and sprinkle freely with powdered white sugar, and serve hot.
8. In arranging them in the dish, make the fritters rise into a pretty pyramid and sprinkle with the sugar.

title: Plain Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Brown Sugar
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 10 Eggs
- 2 Pounds of Seeded Raisins
- 2 Pounds of Currants
- 1 Pound of Citron
- 1/4 Pound of Almonds
- 1 Nutmeg
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Allspice and Cinnamon
- 1/8 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Mace and Cloves
- 3/4 Pound of Sifted Flour
- The Zest Each of an Orange and Lemon
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- The Juice of 1 Orange and 1 Lemon
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Wineglassful of Good French Brandy
- 1 Wineglassful of Sherry Wine or Jamaica Rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the fruit by seeding the raisins, washing and drying the currants, cutting the citron into shreds and dice, and mashing the almonds.
2. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream and add the eggs, which you will first beat, without separating whites and yolks, till very light.
3. Beat these into the sugar and butter till very light and then add the flour gradually and the spices.
4. Sift the flour with the baking powder and salt, and then mix the fruit into it before adding to the cake.
5. Beat vigorously.
6. Then add the grated zest of the orange and lemon, and the juices of both.
7. Stir well and add the brandy and wine or rum.
8. Mix well.
9. Divide the batter into two pans, which you will have lined with greased paper, and bake in a steadily moderate oven for four hours.
10. Use the broom wisp test, and if it comes out clean and dry the cake is done.
11. If not, let the cake bake from a quarter to a half hour longer, watching carefully.
12. When done, put the cake into an earthenware bowl, with a flat bottom.
13. Pour over a pint of the best brandy and half a pint of Sherry Wine.
14. Make the vessel air tight by tying around a linen cloth, and put on the cover.
15. Set the cake away for about three weeks, and then open and turn.
16. Let it remain three weeks longer, and the cake is ready to cut.
17. If you wish to use it sooner, do not add the liquor after baking, but set the cake away to cool in the pan in which it was baked.
18. Then place in a cool place after taking out and removing the paper, and wait eight days before cutting.

title: Plain Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lemon Juice
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- Essence of Rose, Orange or Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs, and sift in gradually the sugar, beating all the while
2. Add in the beginning a tablespoonful of Rose or Lemon Essence or Orange Flower Water
3. Beat very, very light, till sufficient almost to stand alone
4. Have ready in another bowl a little lemon juice
5. Begin to lay on the icing in a thin coat over the cake with a knife, occasionally dipping the knife into the lemon juice, to make a nice, smooth icing
6. When it is covered with this coat, set in the oven or the sun to harden
7. Add another coating as before

title: Plain Mince Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Meat
- 2.5 Pounds of Apples
- 2 Pounds of Seeded Raisins
- 2 Pounds of Currants
- 1 Pint of Brandy
- 3/4 Pint of Good Sherry
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Cinnamon, Cloves, Nutmeg and Mace, Grated
- 2 Pounds of Brown Sugar
- The Juice and Rind of 1 Lemon
- The Juice and Rind of 1 Orange
- 3/4 Pound of Mutton or Beef Suet
- 1/4 Pound of Chopped Citron

## Directions

1. Proceed to prepare the meat and the pies as in the mince meat recipe above

title: Plain Mock-Turtle Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf’s-head
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- cloves
- sweet herbs
- onions
- white wine
- force-meat balls
- a pound of lean veal
- half a pound of salt pork
- brains of the calf’s-head
- curry powder

## Directions

1. Boil a calf’s-head until very tender; take out the head, strain the liquor, and skim off the fat when cold, and keep till following day.
2. Cut up the meat of the head and brain, and add to the liquor; place over the fire, after seasoning to taste with pepper, salt, mace, cloves, sweet herbs, and onions.
3. Let it stew an hour, then add a tumbler of white wine, and it is ready for the force-meat balls.
4. For the balls, chop a pound of lean veal with half a pound of salt pork; add the brains of the calf’s-head, seasoned with pepper, salt, mace, cloves, sweet herbs, or curry powder.
5. Make into balls the size of the yolk of an egg; boil part in the soup, fry the rest for a separate dish.

title: Plain Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- yeast
- warm water
- warm milk
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a stiff batter with flour, yeast and warm water, at night
2. In the morning, have a little warm milk and eggs, and flour to make it sufficiently stiff to bake

title: Plain Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Break the whites and yolks separately
2. Beat the whites till they come to a light froth
3. Beat the yolks till they are quite light
4. Beat the whites and yolks together
5. Season well
6. Melt the butter in a frying pan, letting it grow hot but not brown
7. Pour in the mixture of egg
8. Let it stand about two minutes, shaking occasionally to prevent it from sticking to the pan
9. Continue shaking over a quick fire until the eggs are set
10. Roll the omelet, folding it in two or three rolls and making it long and narrow
11. Take a hot dish and turn the omelet into it
12. Garnish with parsley and serve hot immediately

title: Plain Paste For Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sifted Flour
- 1 Large Heaping Kitchenspoonful of Butter or Lard
- ¾ Cup of Tepid Water
- ½ Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Blend the salt and the flour, and then gradually mix in the butter, if for apple dumplings or rolls.
2. Use lard if intended for dumplings for chicken stews, veal stews, etc.
3. Add the water by degrees, using good judgment always, and lifting the dough out as you wet it, never wetting twice in the same place.
4. Mix and knead lightly, and then sift a light coating of flour on the board, and roll out with the rolling pin, and use for all purposes in this book where Plain Paste is indicated.
5. If necessary to keep the paste awhile, place in a napkin in the ice box.

title: Plain Peach Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten fine, nearly ripe peaches
- brine
- vinegar
- whole pepper
- ginger
- mace
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Wipe off the down with a flannel rag from eight or ten fine, nearly ripe peaches
2. Put them into brine strong enough to bear up an egg
3. In two days drain them from this brine, and scald them in boiling vinegar, and let them stay in all night
4. Next day boil in a quart of vinegar, one ounce of whole pepper, one of broken-up ginger, eight blades of mace, and two ounces of mustard seed
5. Pour this boiling on the peaches, and when cool, put them in jars, and pack away carefully in a cool place

title: Plain Plum Pudding for Children
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- One pound of bread crumbs
- Three quarters of a pound of stoned raisins
- Three quarters of a pound of currants
- Three quarters of a pound of suet
- Four eggs
- Milk to moisten, say about one pint

## Directions

1. Let the suet be finely chopped
2. Stone the raisins
3. Wash, pick and dry the currants well
4. Mix them with the other dry ingredients
5. Stir all well together
6. Beat and strain in the eggs
7. Add just enough of the milk to make it mix properly
8. Tie it up in a well floured cloth
9. Put it into boiling water
10. Boil for five hours
11. Serve with butter and sugar sauce, or wine sauce

title: Plain Pudding Without Eggs or Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of chopped and stoned raisins
- Half a pound of suet
- One pound of flour
- A cup of bread crumbs
- Two tablespoonfuls of molasses
- A pint of milk
- Nutmeg grated
- A lemon peel chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the suet very fine and mix it with the flour
2. Add the bread crumbs, lemon and nutmeg, with the stoned raisins, to a pint of milk
3. Mix all together and put in the molasses
4. Keep it closely covered in a cool place
5. When it is wanted, pour it in a floured cloth and boil it five hours
6. Serve with rich sauce

title: Plain Tapioca Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- pearl tapioca
- sugar
- nutmeg
- rich cream

## Directions

1. Boil the pearl tapioca as you do rice
2. When cool sweeten it to the taste
3. Grate nutmeg over it
4. Pour rich cream over it and serve

title: Plain Tea Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of butter, or a large spoonful of lard
- one and a half cups of sugar
- one teacupful of milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- seven cupfuls of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients
2. Roll thin
3. Bake

title: Plain Veal And Ham Pie. Easily Made
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- ham
- salt
- mushrooms
- parsley
- eschalots
- soup stock
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- paste

## Directions

1. Cut a pound of veal and a pound of ham into slices, salt them slightly.
2. Chop a cupful of mushrooms, a bunch of parsley, some eschalots, and fry them lightly.
3. Add to them a pint of soup stock, boil it together for five minutes and pour it into the piepan where you have placed your ham and veal.
4. Put a dozen hard-boiled yolks of eggs in among the contents of the pie, cover it with a nice paste and bake it one hour and a half.

title: Plain Vermicelli Soup, No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a soup-bone, weighing from two to three pounds, or a brisket of beef
- four quarts of water
- two onions
- two carrots
- two turnips
- salt to taste
- a quarter of a pound of vermicelli

## Directions

1. Put a soup-bone, weighing from two to three pounds, or a brisket of beef, into four quarts of water; add two onions, two carrots, and two turnips; salt to taste, and place over the fire to boil for three hours; then remove and strain; put back on the stove, and add a quarter of a pound of vermicelli, and let it boil till tender; serve with tomatoes.

title: Plain Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Cups of Milk
- 1⅛ Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and salt and baking powder together
2. Beat the yolk of the egg till very light
3. Add the melted butter to the milk, which should be scalded and grown lukewarm
4. Mix in the whites of the eggs
5. Add the flour gradually, making a nice, light batter, adding more milk if it appears stiff
6. Have your waffle irons ready, thoroughly heated
7. Grease the interior of the irons with melted lard using a brush or cloth
8. Pour the batter into a pitcher and fill the irons until the elevations are lightly covered
9. Close the irons quickly and turn it over
10. Bake the waffle about two minutes, or till a nice, delicate brown
11. Carefully remove from the irons and place in a hot plate or waffle dish in the mouth of the oven
12. Send to the table very hot

title: Plantain Meringue Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Plantains
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sweet Spices
- Whites of 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and stew the plantains, mash through a sieve, and proceed as in recipe for Apple Meringue Pie.

title: Plantain Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Plantains
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- A Plain Paste

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and stew the plantains, and proceed in the same manner as for Apple Pie.

title: Plum Cake or Old-Fashioned Creole Wedding Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/4 Pounds Flour
- 1 3/4 Pounds Sifted Sugar
- 1 1/4 Pounds Butter
- 1 Pound of Seeded and Chopped Raisins
- 1 Pound Chopped and Dried Cherries
- 1 Pound of Currants, Washed, Cleansed and Dried
- 12 Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Salt
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Shredded Orange, Lemon and Green Citron Peel, Combined
- 3/4 Pint of French Brandy
- 1/2 Gill of Caramel or Burnt Sugar Coloring
- 8 Ounces of Ground Almonds
- The Zest of 4 Oranges
- 1 Ounce of Ground Cloves
- Cinnamon, Nutmeg and Coriander Seed in Equal Proportions
- Two teaspoonfuls of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Work the butter until it becomes very creamy, using a wooden spoon.
2. Then add the sugar, working to a thick cream, and add the yolks of the eggs, well-beaten.
3. Beat till light, and add by degrees the flour, salt and two teaspoonfuls of baking-powder, working the batter all the time.
4. Then add the fruit, which will have dredged slightly with flour, working them well into the batter.
5. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth, and when all are well incorporated, pour the mixture into a baking pan, lined with double sheets of buttered paper, and place on top thick sheets of paper which you set in the oven.
6. Bake in a moderate oven, and keep the heat regular and steady all the time, being careful not to increase or diminish it during the baking.
7. Bake for two hours and a half.

title: Plum Preserves
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Get plums before they are dead ripe.
2. Allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, dissolve and boil the sugar and water (allowing half a pint of water to a pound).
3. Boil the syrup until it is thick, then put in the plums and boil them until they are transparent.
4. Bottle them in sealed jars.

title: Plum Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Raisins
- 1 Pound of Currants
- 1/4 Pound of Citron
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pound of Suet
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 6 Eggs
- 3/4 Pint of Milk
- 3/4 Cup of Brandy
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 1/2 Ounce Each of Ground Cloves, Mace, Cinnamon and Allspice
- The Grated Zest of 1 Lemon
- The Grated Zest of Half an Orange

## Directions

1. Seed the raisins and chop them fine.
2. Seed the currants, after washing and picking over very carefully.
3. Mince the citron, and grate the zest of the orange and lemon.
4. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and then beat in the yolks of the eggs, which have been previously beaten to a perfect degree of lightness.
5. Add the flour, beating in by degrees till very light, and the whites of the eggs, which have been beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Add the spices and beat in well.
7. Mince the suet and the fruits very fine and add, and lastly add the brandy, which you will beat thoroughly into the pudding.
8. When all is well-mixed, have ready the pudding cloth; wet with hot water, and wrung perfectly dry.
9. Dredge it slightly with flour, and wrap the pudding in it.
10. Sew it up, and put into a kettle of boiling water, and let it boil for six hours.
11. Serve with a Hard or Brandy Sauce.

title: Plum Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of flour
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 lb. of suet chopped fine
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1 lb. of stoned raisins
- ½ lb. of currants
- ¼ lb. of citron
- sugar
- butter
- wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Combine 1 quart of flour, 1 pint of milk, 1 lb. of suet chopped fine, 1 lb. of brown sugar, 8 eggs, 1 lb. of stoned raisins, ½ lb. of currants, ¼ lb. of citron
2. Make a sauce of sugar and butter creamed together, with wine and nutmeg
3. Boil it 6 hours

title: Plum Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of beef suet
- 1 lb. of raisins
- 1 lb. of currants
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- spicing to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix 1 lb. of flour, 1 lb. of beef suet, 1 lb. of raisins, 1 lb. of currants, 4 eggs, 1 pint of milk, and spicing to the taste
2. Tie it in a bag
3. Allow no room for swelling and boil 4 hours

title: Plum Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half lbs. of raisins
- J lb. of currants
- i lb. of bread crumbs
- J lb. of flour
- J lb. of beef suet
- J lb. of citron and orange peel
- i nutmeg
- 9 eggs
- 1 wineglass of brandy
- A little ground ginger

## Directions

1. Chop the suet as fine as possible, and mix it with the bread crumbs and flour
2. Add the currants, washed and dried
3. Add the citron and orange peel cut in thin slices, and the raisins stoned and divided
4. Mix all together with the grated nutmeg and ginger
5. Stir in the 9 eggs well beaten, and the brandy, and again mix, so that all the ingredients may be moistened
6. Put it into a buttered mould or cloth
7. Tie it tightly, and boil it for six hours

title: Plum Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Plum Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup

## Directions

1. Stone the plums, pound and mash well and set aside in a cool place for thirty-six hours
2. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for Apricot Syrup

title: Poached Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar, optional

## Directions

1. Have the frying pan filled with boiling water.
2. Add salt.
3. Some add also a tablespoonful of vinegar, but this is a matter of taste.
4. The eggs must be absolutely fresh.
5. Break the eggs into the boiling water.

title: Poached Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling water
- salt
- vinegar (optional)
- black pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Have the frying pan filled with boiling water.
2. Add salt and optionally a tablespoonful of vinegar.
3. Break the eggs into a saucer, one by one, and gently slip off into the water, without breaking the yolk.
4. Break another and another, until you have four in the pan, and allow the eggs to stand apart.
5. Let them boil thus on the water, till the white forms a thin veil over the yolks.
6. Take them up gently and neatly round off the ragged edges.
7. Sprinkle the top with a little black pepper.
8. Place on buttered toast, and serve immediately.

title: Poached Eggs and Ham
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- ham
- butter

## Directions

1. Poach your eggs in a tin dipper, as directed
2. when done put them on round slices of broiled or fried ham

title: Poached Eggs with Toast and Anchovy Paste
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- anchovy paste

## Directions

1. Toast six pieces of bread, shape them round, before browning
2. keep them where they will be hot until you poach the eggs
3. Take a tin dipper, half fill it with boiling water, and drop it gently into the pot again, holding it so that none of the water from the pot can get into the dipper
4. keep it firm by holding it yourself or getting it held for you
5. break a nice fresh egg into the dipper
6. let it stand until the white is firm
7. Lay each egg on one of the slices of toast
8. use butter and salt on the toast for both egg and toast
9. break each egg in this way until your six eggs and six pieces of toast are used
10. butter very freely
11. serve hot
12. Anchovy paste may be spread on the toast before the eggs are put on

title: Poches in Large Glasses
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiskey, brandy, gin, white wine, claret or catawba
- fine ice
- one tablespoonful of white sugar
- a little raspberry syrup
- strawberries and pineapple
- seltzer water

## Directions

1. Fill the glass half full of fine ice
2. put in one tablespoonful of white sugar
3. add a little raspberry syrup, strawberries and pineapple
4. pour in your liquor
5. fill up with seltzer water
6. Stir all rapidly

title: Pocket Books For Tea
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of light and warm yeast
- a cup of warm, sweet milk
- two eggs beaten
- a cup of sugar
- a spoonful of grated orange peel and nutmeg
- flour
- salt
- a lump of butter as big as an egg
- warmed butter
- a beaten egg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a cup of light and warm yeast, a cup of warm, sweet milk, two eggs beaten, a cup of sugar, a spoonful of grated orange peel and nutmeg
2. add to this, flour enough to make a thin batter
3. set it in a warm place to rise
4. pour this batter into sifted flour, enough to form into a rather stiff dough
5. add salt and a lump of butter as big as an egg
6. Work it thoroughly
7. set it in a tureen to rise again
8. When it is risen, flour the board and roll out the dough half an inch thick
9. smear the surface with butter
10. cut into strips about six inches long, and two inches wide
11. fold them over and over
12. lay them within an inch of each other on a warm and greased baking tin, or pan
13. swab the tops over with warmed butter and a beaten egg
14. set them to rise, which will require an hour
15. Just before you put them in the oven, sift some sugar over them

title: Pomegranate Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 Ripe Pomegranates
- 4 Quarts of Good French Brandy
- 3 Pounds of Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the pomegranates in slices and steep them for fifteen days in the brandy.
2. Squeeze through a cloth, add a syrup made with three pounds of sugar, and filter and bottle for use.

title: Pomegranate Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ripe Pomegranates
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Essence of Vanilla
- 3 Drops of Cochineal

## Directions

1. Press the pips of six or eight ripe pomegranates with a wooden spoon through a strainer to produce a pint of juice.
2. Make a syrup by boiling one quart of water and one pint of sugar.
3. Add, when cooling, the pomegranate juice, a half teaspoonful of Essence of Vanilla, the juice of a lemon, and a few drops of Cochineal.
4. Proceed to freeze as directed.

title: Pommes de Terre à la Diable
body:
## Ingredients

- nice, fresh new potatoes
- lard
- two tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- a teaspoonful of prepared mustard
- a dash of Cayenne
- two teaspoonfuls of vinegar
- the yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Take nice, fresh new potatoes and boil them.
2. When done, take off and let evaporate, being careful not to have cooked them too much.
3. Take a deep frying pan, and heat the lard to the boiling point.
4. When it begins to boil, drop in the potatoes, and let them fry for about five minutes.
5. Lift out with a skimmer, drain quickly, and put them into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of melted butter into which you have rubbed well a teaspoonful of prepared mustard.
6. Add a dash of Cayenne and two teaspoonfuls of vinegar.
7. Let it cook for three or four minutes, shaking constantly, and then take off.
8. Add the yolks of two eggs, well beaten, and serve immediately with Grillades à la Sauce or Grillades Panees.

title: Pompano à la Maître d'Hôtel
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Pompano, or Small Ones in Weight to Equal Three Pounds
- A Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- A Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- A Sliced Lemon
- Parsley to Garnish
- Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Split the fish in the middle of the back if the fish is large; if small, broil whole
2. Season well by rubbing with salt and pepper, mixed with a little sweet oil
3. Put the Pompano on the broiler and see that it is browned well on both sides, using always the double wire broiler
4. When done, place in a heated dish (heat by placing in a bain-marie or hot water bath and dry thoroughly)
5. Butter the fish nicely and squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
6. Garnish with parsley and sliced lemon, and serve with a Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel

title: Ponche Romaine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two wineglassfuls of water
- one wineglassful of whiskey
- half wineglassful of Jamaica rum
- sugar and lemon to taste
- plenty of ice

## Directions

1. Shake
2. Strain and serve in fancy glass

title: Poor Man's Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 2 eggs
- a pinch of salt
- water

## Directions

1. Add butter, eggs, and salt to flour
2. Mix with water

title: Pop-Corn Balls
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six quarts of pop corn
- one pint of molasses

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of molasses about fifteen minutes
2. Put the corn into a large pan
3. Pour the boiled molasses over it
4. Stir it briskly until thoroughly mixed
5. With clean hands make into balls of the desired size

title: Pop-Corn Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- one and a half cups of brown sugar
- one tablespoonful of vinegar
- a lump of butter the size of an egg
- two cups of popped corn

## Directions

1. Boil molasses, brown sugar, vinegar, and butter until thick
2. Chop two cups of popped corn rather fine
3. Put it into the boiling candy
4. Pour it all on the buttered plates
5. Cut in squares to be eaten without pulling

title: Pop-Corn Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cupful of White Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 3 Quarts of Nicely-Popped Corn

## Directions

1. Put the butter, water and sugar into a boiler
2. Boil till it begins to almost run to candy
3. Throw in the nicely popped corn
4. Stir vigorously over the fire until the sugar is evenly distributed over the corn
5. Take the kettle from the fire and stir until it cools a little
6. If you wish, pile it into mounds and roll into balls while still hot enough for the grains to adhere

title: Pop-Overs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Pint of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs separately till very light and frosty
2. Stir in the unskimmed milk and the flour that has been twice sifted with the baking powder
3. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Pour the mixture into buttered cake tins
5. Bake in a quick oven till a light brown, and serve either hot or cold

title: Pork and Apple Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- crust
- nice fat salt pork
- apples
- allspice
- pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make the crust in the usual manner
2. Spread it over a deep plate
3. Cut nice fat salt pork very thin, and slice some apples
4. Place a layer of apples, then a layer of pork
5. Sprinkle with allspice, pepper, and sugar between each layer
6. Have three or four layers, and let the last one be apples
7. Sprinkle in sugar and spice
8. Cover with a top crust, and bake an hour

title: Pork Tenderloins
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pork Tenderloins
- A tablespoonful of lard
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Have the tenderloins cut thin and split lengthwise without separating.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Have ready a very hot frying pan, place a tablespoonful of butter or lard within and add the tenderloin.
4. Turn every two minutes, not leaving them very long on either side at a time.
5. Be careful to cook through and through, smothering over a low fire.
6. Serve with Apple Sauce or Currant Jelly.

title: Portugal Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- one pound of flour
- six eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of lemon juice or white wine
- one pound of seeded raisins or citron
- one and a half pounds of blanched almonds cut fine
- one grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make a batter with butter, sugar, flour, and eggs
2. add lemon juice or white wine
3. add raisins or citron dredged with flour
4. add blanched almonds cut fine and grated nutmeg

title: Pot au Feu
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of beef
- 1 cup of water
- carrot
- turnip
- greens
- salt

## Directions

1. To each 1 lb. of beef, add a cup of water
2. Put in at the same time a carrot, a turnip and other greens
3. Let simmer slowly
4. Add salt to suit taste

title: Pot Au Feu
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. beef
- 1 cup of water
- a carrot
- a turnip
- other greens
- salt

## Directions

1. Put beef, water, carrot, turnip and other greens in at the same time
2. Let simmer slowly
3. Add salt to suit taste

title: Pot-au-Feu à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of Lean Beef
- 6 Quarts of Cold Water
- 2 Small Turnips
- 2 Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Parsnip
- 1 Cup of Cut-up Tomatoes
- 2 Whole Cloves
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 5 Allspice
- 2 Irish Potatoes
- Small Piece of Lemon Peel
- Small Piece of Red Pepper Pod
- Bunch of Celery Leaves (Chopped)
- Bunch of Parsley (Chopped)
- Pinch of Salt
- Pinch of Black Pepper
- Sprig of Cabbage

## Directions

1. Put the meat into cold water, heating by slow degrees in order that it may gradually penetrate the meat, softening it and dissolving the non-nutritive portion
2. As the scum becomes thicker remove it
3. After having skimmed well, set the soup back where it can be kept on a gentle but steady boil

title: Potage Croute-au-Pot
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts good consomé
- 10 pieces dried toast
- 1 carrot, cut fine
- 1 head of celery, cut fine
- 1 turnip, cut fine
- salted boiling water

## Directions

1. Prepare a good consomé
2. Parboil the vegetables in salted boiling water
3. When tender, drain off the water and add to them about two and one-half quarts of the boiling consomé
4. Let them simmer until they are very tender
5. Prepare the toasts and put them into a saucepan with enough consomé to cover them
6. Simmer gently until the toasts have absorbed all the consomé and show signs of drying up
7. Add a little hot consomé and detach them from the saucepan
8. Lay them in the tureen and pour the soup with the vegetables very gently over them
9. Serve immediately

title: Potato and Raisin Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 ounce of sugar
- lemon peel
- 6 ounces of grated potatoes
- 1 lb. of raisins

## Directions

1. Mix 2 ounces of butter, 4 eggs, 1 ounce of sugar and lemon peel well together
2. add 6 ounces of grated potatoes
3. add 1 lb. of raisins, and bake it in a form or mould by 2 degrees of heat for half an hour
4. serve it with or without snow

title: Potato Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potatoes
- the yolk of an egg
- flour
- egg
- bread crumbs
- very nice lard

## Directions

1. Mix mashed potatoes with the yolk of an egg
2. Roll them into balls in a little flour, with egg and bread crumbs
3. Fry them in very nice lard or brown them in a hot oven

title: Potato Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 potatoes
- 1 kitchenspoonful of butter
- three-quarter pint of milk
- 2 eggs
- cold left-over meat or pork, minced very fine
- salt and pepper
- minced parsley and shallots or onions
- boiling lard

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes as above, or use cold left-over potatoes for this dish.
2. Mash the potatoes well, and add an equal quantity of cold left-over meat or pork, minced very fine.
3. Season well with salt and pepper, minced parsley and shallots or onions.
4. Mix well with the yolks of two eggs, and form into balls of medium size.
5. Brush with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and fry in boiling lard.
6. Serve with garnish of chopped parsley or with meat gravy.

title: Potato Balls for Breakfast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pare and boil dry the potatoes
2. Put them into a hot pan and mash with a lump of butter and a little salt
3. Beat this well and make it into little cakes or roll it into balls
4. Dip them into egg and sprinkle with bread crumbs
5. Fry a nice brown

title: Potato Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- milk
- 1 or more beaten eggs
- butter or drippings of cold beef
- thin slices of beef without the fat

## Directions

1. Boil some potatoes till well done all through
2. Peel them and put them into a large pan
3. Mash them smoothly, adding as you proceed some milk and 1 or more beaten eggs, well mixed, to the potatoes
4. Rub the bottom of a white pudding dish with nice butter or some drippings of cold beef
5. Cover it with a thick layer of mashed potatoes
6. Put in thin slices of beef (without the fat), enough to cover the potatoes
7. Then a layer of mashed potatoes, then beef, then potatoes, till the dish is full, heaping them up in the centre
8. Bake it in an oven

title: Potato Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a half pint of warm water
- a half pint of yeast
- 6 moderate sized potatoes
- 1 dessertspoonful of salt
- 3 pints of flour

## Directions

1. Combine the warm water and yeast
2. Add the potatoes and salt
3. Stir in 3 pints of flour lightly

title: Potato Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- mealy potatoes
- salt
- butter
- flour
- yeast
- lukewarm water or milk

## Directions

1. Boil thoroughly and mash fine, mealy potatoes
2. Add salt and a very little butter
3. Rub them with twice their quantity of flour
4. Stir in the yeast, moisten with lukewarm water or milk, till stiff enough to mould
5. It will rise quicker than common wheat bread, and should be baked as soon as risen

title: Potato Chips
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Potatoes
- Boiling Lard
- Parsley Sprigs to Garnish

## Directions

1. Have two medium-sized potatoes, and slice them just as thin as possible.
2. Drop a few pieces at a time into the lard, and be sure to have the lard about three inches deep.
3. Stir the slices occasionally, to keep them from overlapping the others.
4. When a light brown, take out of the pan and drain on a piece of brown paper, which you have put in a colander.
5. Stand in the open oven, and continue frying.
6. As you finish the second frying turn the first from the colander into a heated dish, and so continue till all the potatoes are fried.
7. Serve hot.
8. Garnish, if you wish, with parsley sprigs.

title: Potato Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of fine cornmeal
- a pint of milk
- a half pound of sweet potatoes
- a pound of butter
- 1 pound of brown sugar
- 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil and mash smooth the potatoes
2. Beat the eggs and stir them into the butter
3. Add the milk, and lastly the meal
4. Beat the whole well together
5. Bake in a pan

title: Potato Croquets
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six boiled potatoes
- three tablespoonfuls of grated ham
- a little pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- the yolks of three eggs
- egg
- bread crumbs
- hot lard
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take six boiled potatoes
2. Add three tablespoonfuls of grated ham, a little pepper, salt, and chopped parsley, also the yolks of three eggs
3. Form into balls
4. Dip in egg and roll in bread crumbs
5. Fry in hot lard
6. Garnish with parsley

title: Potato Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Mashed Potatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Onion Juice
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- A Dash of Cayenne
- A beaten egg for binding
- Bread crumbs
- Boiling lard

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks to a cream and add them to the potatoes.
2. Mix well and then add the cream and all the other ingredients.
3. Mix well, and put into a saucepan over the fire, and stir until the mixture leaves the side of the vessel.
4. Take off the fire, and set to cool.
5. When cold, form into cylinders of about two and a half inches in length and one in width.
6. Roll first in a beaten egg, to bind, and then in bread crumbs.
7. Fry to a golden brown in boiling lard.
8. When done, lift out of the lard with a skimmer, and drain on brown paper in the mouth of the oven.
9. Serve for breakfast, or as a garnish for meats.

title: Potato Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- mealy potatoes
- water
- 1 quart of flour

## Directions

1. Boil mealy potatoes soft
2. rub them through a sieve while hot
3. put them in a stewpan over the fire with as much water as will make them the consistency of mush
4. sift 1 quart of flour in, and make into a paste
5. knead it till very light

title: Potato Pone
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 good sized sweet potatoes
- 2 eggs
- a good spoonful of butter
- 2 coffee-cups of sugar
- milk
- powdered ginger

## Directions

1. Wash potatoes well
2. Grate them
3. Season with eggs, butter, sugar, milk, and powdered ginger
4. Bake and eat hot with butter, or when cold, slice and fry

title: Potato Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs. well mashed potatoes
- 1 lb. butter
- 6 eggs
- ½ lb. sugar rolled fine
- 1 glass of brandy
- nutmeg, mace, or lemon

## Directions

1. While potatoes are warm, put in butter
2. Beat 6 eggs with ½ lb. of sugar rolled fine
3. Mix all well together
4. Put in 1 glass of brandy, with nutmeg, mace, or lemon
5. Bake it in a nice paste

title: Potato Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lbs. of well mashed potatoes
- 1 lb. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1 glass of brandy
- nutmeg
- mace
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take 1 1/2 lbs. of well mashed potatoes
2. while they are warm, put in 1 lb. of butter
3. beat 6 eggs with 1/2 lb. of sugar rolled fine
4. mix all well together
5. put in 1 glass of brandy, with nutmeg, mace, or lemon
6. bake it in a nice paste

title: Potato Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Cold Mashed Potatoes
- 2 Eggs
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Cream or Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes.

title: Potato Quenelles
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 eggs
- minced parsley
- a half onion minced very fine
- powdered bread crumbs
- boiling lard

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes, peel and mash very fine.
2. Add the butter and minced parsley, and a half onion minced very fine.
3. Add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light, and then form the potatoes into balls and throw them for two minutes into boiling water.
4. Take out, brush with the whites of the eggs, roll lightly in powdered bread crumbs, and fry in boiling lard.
5. Drain and serve with fish, meat or any sauce.

title: Potato Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Large Cold Boiled Potatoes
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 9 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley

## Directions

1. Pare and peel the potatoes, if freshly boiled, and let them cool.
2. Prepare the salad dressing, following implicitly the directions given for plain French dressing, only here, the quantities are larger in proportion.
3. Add the vinegar, stirring constantly. A dash of mustard may be added, if desired.
4. Mince the onion very fine, and cut the potatoes into dice or slices, and mix them carefully with the onion.
5. Then add the dressing, turning the potatoes into it without breaking.
6. Sprinkle all with parsley, nicely chopped, and serve cold.

title: Potato Salad
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of cold potatoes
- six eschalots
- pepper
- salt
- two tablespoonfuls of oil
- one of made mustard
- half a cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice a pint of cold potatoes, put them in a dish
2. Chop over them six eschalots, pepper and salt them
3. Pour over them a dressing of two tablespoonfuls of oil, one of made mustard, and half a cup of vinegar

title: Potato Salad
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold potatoes sliced
- chopped eschalots
- pepper
- salt
- mustard
- oil
- tomato catsup
- vinegar
- sprigs of parsley

## Directions

1. Slice cold potatoes, add to them chopped eschalots, and season with pepper, salt, mustard, oil, tomato catsup and vinegar
2. Garnish with sprigs of parsley

title: Potato Salad
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes (Irish)
- 5 large hard boiled eggs
- 4 potted ham
- 2 tablespoons sweet pickle
- 2 tablespoons salt and pepper
- 2 tablespoons of Prince of Wales salad sauce
- 2 yolks (raw) and 1 cooked
- 1 tablespoon Worcester sauce
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- pepper
- sweet oil about 1 pint
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Slice boiled potatoes and hard boiled eggs
2. Mix potatoes, eggs, potted ham, sweet pickle, salt and pepper thoroughly before making the dressing
3. For the dressing, stir the oil into the raw yolk as you do for mayonnaise dressing
4. Add 2 tablespoons of Prince of Wales salad sauce
5. Add the cooked yolk
6. Add 1 tablespoon Worcester sauce
7. Add 1 teaspoon sugar and pepper to taste
8. Mix all other ingredients
9. Pour the dressing on the potatoes, 1 spoonful or more at a time
10. Mix well
11. Squeeze a few drops of lemon juice
12. Add the whites heated stiff
13. Garnish with eggs, pickles, etc., according to your taste

title: Potato Salad
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- good Irish potatoes
- 3 egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil good Irish potatoes very dry
2. Let them cool thoroughly
3. Slice and pour a dressing over them, made of the yolks of 3 eggs, oil, vinegar, pepper, salt and parsley

title: Potato Salad
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- good Irish potatoes
- yolks of 3 eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil good Irish potatoes very dry
2. Let them cool thoroughly
3. Slice them
4. Pour a dressing over them made of the yolks of 3 eggs, oil, vinegar, pepper, salt and parsley

title: Potato Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes with their jackets on.
2. Peel them and set them before the fire, to allow all the water to evaporate.
3. Sprinkle lightly with salt.
4. Rub them through a sieve or colander.
5. Let the potato fall in light, flaky drops, like snow flakes.
6. Do not touch the flakes as they fall.
7. Serve on the dish in which the flakes have fallen.

title: Potato Souffle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large, smooth potatoes
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- whites of 4 eggs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes very clean and bake them
2. Cut lengthwise, scrape out the insides carefully, so as not to break the skins
3. Mash smoothly, adding the boiling milk, butter, seasoning and half the beaten whites of the eggs
4. Put into potato skins, place in a pan
5. Cover with remainder of whites, and bake a light brown

title: Potato Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Mashed Potatoes
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- 2 Eggs
- 3/4 Cup of Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes well, pressing them through a colander.
2. Add the butter, and beat till smooth and light.
3. Add the cream and the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and salt and pepper to taste.
4. Add the whites, which must be beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Heap this into a dish, leaving the mound rough and uneven, so that the dish will look pretty with the tips nicely browned.
6. Set in the oven and bake to a beautiful brown, and serve in the dish in which it was baked.

title: Potato Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Potatoes
- 2 Onions, Cut Fine
- 1 Cup of Cream or Rich Milk
- 1 Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. After washing and peeling the potatoes, put them into a saucepan with the onions and add about two quarts of cold water.
2. Bring to a boil.
3. Allow to cook about forty minutes, if the vegetables are then very tender, mash and pass all through a sieve.
4. Return to the fire, and add the seasoning and butter.
5. Bring to a boil, and add the cream and a beaten egg.
6. Serve immediately with Croqutons.

title: Potato Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- small onion
- water
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- fresh butter
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pare and slice thin half a dozen fine potatoes and a small onion
2. Boil them in three pints of water till so soft that you can pulp them through a cullender
3. When returned to the pot add a very little salt and cayenne pepper
4. Add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, divided into bits
5. Boil it 10 minutes longer
6. When you put it into the tureen, stir in 2 tablespoonfuls or more of sweet cream

title: Potato Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. potatoes
- 1 lb. flour
- 4 eggs
- milk
- 1 cup yeast

## Directions

1. Mix 1 lb. of potatoes, 1 lb. of flour, 4 eggs, and as much milk as will make it the consistency of fritters
2. Add 1 cup of yeast

title: Potato Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Irish Potatoes
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Flour
- 1½ Pints of Milk
- ½ Cake of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. Mash the boiled, left-over potatoes through a sieve, and then add the milk and the sifted flour.
2. Add the yeast, which you will have dissolved in a little warm water, and set the whole to rise for two hours.
3. At the end of that time add the eggs, beaten separately, and mix well.
4. Set to rise again for a half hour, till light, and then proceed to bake as in Plain Waffles.

title: Potato Yeast
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large Irish potato
- flour
- hop tea
- 1 gill of good yeast

## Directions

1. Peel and boil soft a large Irish potato
2. Rub it through a sieve
3. Add an equal quantity of flour
4. Make it liquid with hop tea
5. When a little warmer than new milk, add a gill of good yeast
6. Stir it well
7. Keep it closely covered in a small pitcher

title: Potatoes a la Maitre d'Hotel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes whole.
2. Serve hot, with a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel poured over them.

title: Potatoes as a Garnish
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- Beef, Veal or Mutton Drippings

## Directions

1. Select nice, round potatoes
2. Wash them clean, boil, or better, steam them
3. Peel and lay them in the pan with the beef or mutton or veal
4. Let them brown awhile before the meat is done, basting them when you baste the meat with the drippings from the roast
5. Place as a garnish around the roast, and bring to the table hot

title: Potatoes au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Cold Boiled Potatoes
- 3/4 Pint of Cream
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 3 1/2 Heaping Tablespoonfuls of Gruyere or Parmesan Cheese
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- 3/4 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Put the butter in the frying pan, and as it melts, add the flour, and let it blend, rubbing smooth, without browning.
2. Add the consomme or water and the cream, and stir constantly till the mixture boils.
3. Take the saucepan from the fire, and add the cheese, well grated, and the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
4. Salt and pepper to taste.
5. Beat all thoroughly till light.
