6. Mash the potatoes well, and place, first, a layer of the sauce in the saucepan, and then of the potatoes, and so on till the sauce forms the top layer.
7. Sprinkle this lightly with bread crumbs, and set in the oven and let bake ten minutes.
8. When it comes to a nice brown, serve in the dish in which it was baked.

title: Potatoes in Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, chopped
- 1/2 pint of cream
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Put into a stewpan a good sized piece of butter, a dessert-spoonful of flour, some salt, pepper and parsley, chopped
2. Add 1/2 pint of cream
3. Put this sauce on the fire and stir it till it boils
4. Slice the potatoes and throw into the sauce

title: Potatoes With Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 Small New Potatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Parsley
- Drawn Butter Sauce

## Directions

1. Select small, round new potatoes, about the size of a marble
2. Boil them for about half an hour or twenty minutes, according to the touch when pierced with a fork
3. Drain off water, peel quickly, and put into a saucepan, with a tablespoonful of butter
4. Let them brown lightly, and serve with Drawn Butter Sauce, in which you have put a tablespoonful of chopped parsley

title: Potatoes With Drawn Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes according to the boiled potatoes recipe.
2. Peel and pour over them a tablespoonful of melted butter, in which you have mingled chopped parsley.
3. Salt and pepper to taste.

title: Potatoes with Thick Batter
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ozs. butter
- 1 oz. of flour
- 1/2 pint of water
- boiled potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- fine chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Stir butter, flour and water over the fire until it is melted into a sauce
2. Put the boiled potatoes into the sauce
3. Dish it very hot, seasoning it with salt, nutmeg and pepper
4. You may also mix fine chopped parsley with it

title: Potatoes à la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Potatoes
- 1 Pint Gravy or Broth
- 1 Onion
- 1 Leek
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take cold boiled potatoes and cut into quarters, and then drain and put into a saucepan
2. Cover with any meat gravy or soup stock that may have remained, and chopped onion, chopped leek, pepper and salt
3. Set on the stove and let stew or simmer for half an hour

title: Potatoes à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Potatoes
- 1 Spoonful of Flour
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Vinegar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper
- Water

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes
2. Take out of the water, drain and set to the side of the stove for five minutes, to evaporate
3. Blend one tablespoonful of flour with two of butter, and, as it melts, add about three large tablespoonfuls of water
4. Let it come to a boil, and take off the stove and add the yolk of an egg, which has been beaten very light
5. Add two teaspoonfuls of vinegar
6. Beat well, and pour over the potatoes, and serve either hot or cold à la salade

title: Potatoes à la Parisienne
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 potatoes
- 1 kitchenspoonful of butter
- half pint of milk or water
- salt
- a teaspoonful of orange flower water
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and let them boil till tender in salt and water.
2. Take them out of the water, and grate and mash them into a paste.
3. Put this potato paste in a saucepan with a kitchenspoonful of butter and a half pint of milk or water, more or less, according to the dryness of the potatoes.
4. Season lightly with salt, and add a teaspoonful of orange flower water, to give a pleasant taste.
5. Let all boil together, stirring constantly until a smooth and thick paste is formed.
6. Then turn the mixture into another pan, in which you have already beaten two eggs until very, very light.
7. Mix thoroughly, and make small boulettes or balls of this paste.
8. Have ready a pan of boiling lard, place the boulettes in it, and when they are a beautiful golden brown take them out, drain and serve hot.

title: Potted Beef (Terrine de Boeuf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Rounds of Beef
- 1 Slice of Suet (Gros de Boeuf)
- 3/4 Can Mushrooms
- 4 Yolks of Eggs
- 1 Dozen Allspice
- 4 Cloves
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne
- 1 Glass of Brandy

## Directions

1. Chop the slices of beef very fine with the suet, and season with the mashed spices, the herbs, minced very fine, and mix thoroughly with the beaten yolks of the eggs.
2. Pour over all the brandy and mix.
3. Line the bottom of the baking pan with strips of lean bacon and dot of beef on top with bits of butter.
4. Bake for two hours in a quick oven.

title: Potted Calf's Head
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf's head or half a beef's head
- a cow-heel
- black pepper
- salt
- thyme
- parsley
- garlic

## Directions

1. Boil a calf's head or half a beef's head with a cow-heel until very tender.
2. When done, pick out all the bones and chop the meat and tendons very fine.
3. Strain the liquor they were boiled in, and set it away to cool.
4. Skim off the fat and pour the jelly over the meat.
5. Season with a teaspoonful of black pepper, salt, and thyme, powdered.
6. Boil all together for a few minutes, and pour into bowls or jelly moulds.
7. Serve with parsley.
8. Add a little garlic if the flavor is liked.

title: Potted Pigeons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- bread crumbs
- sweet marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- chopped pork or butter
- pork
- powdered cloves
- flour
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Wash the pigeons
2. Prepare dressing with bread crumbs, plenty of sweet marjoram, a little salt, pepper, 1 egg, and either chopped pork or piece of butter
3. Stuff the pigeons and lay on their backs in a pan
4. Put pork about an inch in size on breast of each
5. Throw over them a very little salt and powdered cloves
6. Cover all thick with flour from dredging box
7. Pour boiling water over the breasts
8. Cook about 2 hours, basting frequently

title: Poules d'Eau
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pair of Poules d'Eau
- 6 Turnips
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Onions, Chopped Fine
- 1 Square Inch of Ham, Minced Fine
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Before cooking parboil a few minutes if there is the slightest odor of fish
2. Add a small peeled carrot or onion to the water to absorb the flavor of fish

title: Poulet a la Eugenie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 chickens
- water
- small piece of pork
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- celery seed
- small delicate onion

## Directions

1. Prepare 1 or 2 chickens for boiling
2. Cover well with water and put in a small piece of pork
3. Boil until the chicken falls from the bone
4. Select the white meat and mince or mash in a mortar until it becomes paste
5. Put in a skillet
6. Place this where it will keep warm and add nearly as much butter as you have chicken
7. Add a little salt, pepper, mace and celery seed if you choose
8. Boil a small, delicate onion, chopped fine and mix through it when simmered
9. Let it become quite firm and cold before pressing into jelly jars

title: Poulet Saute a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Spring Chickens
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 6 Large Fresh Tomatoes, or 1/2 Can
- 6 Fresh, Sweet Green Peppers
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Large Onions
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Boiling Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take two spring chickens and clean nicely and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter into a stewpan, and when it melts, add the chicken.
4. Let this brown slowly for a good five minutes.
5. Add three large onions sliced and let them brown. Every inch must be nicely browned, but not in the slightest degree burned.
6. Add two tablespoonfuls of flour; let this brown.
7. Add a half dozen large, fresh tomatoes nicely sliced, or a half can of tomatoes, and let these brown.
8. Cook very slowly, allowing the mixture to simply simmer.
9. Add chopped parsley, thyme and bay leaf and two cloves of garlic finely minced.
10. Let all brown without burning.
11. Cover and let it smother over a slow but steady fire.
12. If you have sweet green peppers, add a half dozen, taking the seeds out before adding and slicing the peppers very fine.
13. Stir well.
14. Let all smother steadily for twenty minutes at least, keeping well covered and stirring occasionally.
15. Add one cup of Consomme, if you have it; if not, one cup of boiling water.
16. Let it cook again for a full half hour, very, very slowly over a very steady fire, and season again to taste.
17. Cook ten minutes more, and serve hot.

title: Poulet à la Eugenie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 chickens
- water
- small piece of pork
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- celery seed
- small delicate onion

## Directions

1. Prepare 1 or 2 chickens for boiling
2. Cover well with water and put in a small piece of pork
3. Boil until the chicken falls from the bone
4. Select the white meat and mince or mash in a mortar until it becomes paste
5. Put in a skillet and place where it will keep warm
6. Add nearly as much butter as you have chicken
7. Add a little salt, pepper, mace and celery seed if you choose
8. Boil a small, delicate onion, chopped fine
9. Mix through it when simmered
10. Let it become quite firm and cold before pressing into jelly jars

title: Poulette Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- The yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- 1 pint of consommé or water
- Juice of an onion
- Salt and pepper to taste
- The juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and flour, blending well without browning
2. Add a half pint of water, or consommé, the juice of one lemon, and let it simmer twenty minutes
3. Season to taste
4. Take from the fire, add the yolks of two well-beaten eggs and the juice of a lemon and serve immediately
5. If necessary to keep warm, set in a bowl of boiling water (a bain-marie) till ready to serve

title: Pound Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Pound of Flour
- 10 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lemon or Vanilla Extract
- a gill of brandy

## Directions

1. Wash the salt from one pound of butter. Then take a tablespoon and take out one heaping tablespoonful of butter from the pound
2. After washing the butter, beat it with the white pulverized sugar to a thick cream
3. Have the baking powder and the flour sifted together
4. Beat the yolks of the eggs well, and then add them to the sugar and butter, beating till very light
5. Then add a part of the flour, and beat light
6. Add a part of the whites of the eggs and mix thoroughly
7. Then alternate with the flour and the whites of the eggs until all are used up
8. Add the essence of lemon or vanilla and a gill of brandy, and continue beating vigorously for a half hour
9. Put the batter in a cake pan (a Turk's head), lined with a buttered paper, and bake for one hour in a moderate oven
10. Use the broom-straw test. If the cake is not quite done, bake a quarter of an hour longer
11. Never touch or move a pound cake in the oven till the center is set

title: Pound Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 whites of eggs
- 1 lb. of white sugar
- ¾ lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour

## Directions

1. Cream butter
2. Mix the sugar
3. Put in flour and eggs alternately
4. Bake 2 hours

title: Pound Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 10 Eggs
- 1 Pound Sifted Flour
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Wineglassful of Brandy
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla, Lemon or Rose Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Wash the butter and drain, and take out a heaping teaspoonful
2. Then beat the remainder to a rich cream with the sugar, and add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
3. Beat till very light, and add a part of the flour, into which you will have sifted the baking powder
4. Then add a part of the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and continue alternating with the flour and the eggs till all are used up
5. Then add the grated nutmeg, the brandy and the essence, and continue beating steadily for a half hour
6. Bake in a moderate oven for one hour
7. Use the broom-wisp test. If it comes out clean and dry the cake is done; otherwise continue baking for a quarter of an hour longer

title: Pousse Cafe - No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Maraschino
- curacoa
- kirsh-wasser
- brandy
- Peychaud bitters

## Directions

1. Mix in equal parts of each
2. dash with Peychaud bitters
3. Serve in liquor glasses

title: Pousse Cafe - No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Bernardine
- brandy
- curacoa
- Angostura bitters

## Directions

1. Mix in equal parts of each
2. dash with Angostura bitters

title: Pousse Cafe - No. 3
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Brandy
- maraschino
- cassis
- Boker's bitters

## Directions

1. Mix in equal parts
2. dash with Boker's bitters

title: Pousse Cafe - No. 4
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- La grande chartreuse (yellow)
- brandy (French)
- la grande chartreuse (green)
- Peychaud bitters

## Directions

1. Mix in equal parts
2. dash with Peychaud bitters

title: Pousse Café
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of anisette
- 1 teaspoonful of curacoa
- 1 teaspoonful of Benedictine or Chartreuse
- 1 teaspoonful of good old Bourbon or Cognac

## Directions

1. Mix all the above ingredients in a small glass, and serve cold, or otherwise, as preferred.

title: Premium Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of loaf sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- yolks of 5 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 cup of milk
- whites of 5 eggs
- 4 cups of flour
- juice and peel of one lemon

## Directions

1. rub the butter and sugar to a cream
2. stir in the yolks of eggs well beaten
3. dissolve soda in milk
4. add the milk
5. beat the whites of the eggs to a froth
6. add them
7. sift in flour as light as possible
8. add the juice and peel of one lemon with peel grated

title: Premium Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 14 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Flour
- 3/8 Pound Butter
- 1 Wineglassful of Good Whisky
- The Juice and Grated Zest of 1 Lemon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and add one-half of the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
2. Then add the flour gradually, beating thoroughly and well, till very light
3. Now add the whisky and the lemon juice, and the grated zest of the lemon, and finally add the remainder of the whites and the baking powder, beating till very light
4. Bake in a moderate oven from forty-five minutes to an hour

title: Premium Mustard Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of small cucumbers, gherkins, or green tomatoes
- salt water
- vinegar
- one ounce of white mustard seed
- two ounces of turmeric
- three ounces of sliced ginger
- two ounces of shredded horseradish
- one-half pound of mustard
- three pounds of brown sugar
- one-half pint of sweet oil
- one ounce each of celery seed, black pepper, cloves, mace
- one teaspoonful of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Soak three quarts of small cucumbers, gherkins, or green tomatoes, in strong salt water for three days
2. Put them into fresh cold water for a day or two
3. Scald them in plain vinegar and set them by in a place to cool
4. Take a gallon of vinegar, add to it one ounce of white mustard seed, two ounces of turmeric, three of sliced ginger, two of shredded horseradish, one-half pound of mustard, three pounds of brown sugar, one-half pint of sweet oil, one ounce each of celery seed, black pepper, cloves, mace, and one teaspoonful of cayenne pepper
5. Boil all these ingredients for fifteen minutes and pour it on the cucumbers, gherkins, or other scalded vegetable

title: Preserved Apples
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- equal weights of white sugar and apples
- 1 pint of water to every 3 lbs. of sugar
- grated peel of 1 or 2 lemons
- 2 pieces of white ginger

## Directions

1. Peel, core and chop the apples fine
2. Allow 1 pint of water to every 3 lbs. of sugar
3. Boil the sugar quite thick, skimming it well
4. Add the apples to the grated peel of 1 or 2 lemons and 2 pieces of white ginger
5. Boil until the apples look clear and yellow
6. This will keep for years

title: Preserved Citron
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- citron
- a pound and a quarter of sugar per pound of rind
- grapevine leaves
- pulverized alum
- water

## Directions

1. Pare off the green skin and all the soft part of the rind
2. cut the firm part in strips, or any shape you fancy
3. Allow a pound and a quarter of sugar to each pound of rind
4. line your porcelain kettle with grapevine leaves and fill with the rind
5. scatter a little pulverized alum over each layer
6. Cover with vine-leaves three thick
7. pour on water enough to reach and wet these and cover with a close lid
8. Let them heat together for three hours, but the water must not actually boil
9. Take out the rind, which will be well greened by this process
10. throw at once into very cold water
11. Let it soak for four hours, changing the water for fresh every hour
12. Then make a syrup, allowing two cups of water to every pound and a quarter of syrup
13. Boil and skim until no more scum comes up
14. put in the rind and simmer gently nearly an hour
15. Take it out and spread on dishes in the sun until firm

title: Preserved Cranberries
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries
- loaf sugar (1 lb. per lb. of cranberries)
- water (1/2 pint per lb. of sugar)

## Directions

1. Wash the fruit and weigh it
2. To each lb. of cranberries allow 1 lb. of loaf sugar, dissolved, 1/2 pint of water to 1 lb. of sugar
3. Set it on the fire in a preserving kettle, boiling it 10 minutes
4. Put in the fruit and boil slowly till it is soft and a ruby color
5. Put away in glass jars, with brandied paper on the top
6. Exclude the air with kid tightly sealed over the jars

title: Preserved Figs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- white figs, nearly ripe
- white sugar, equal weight with the figs

## Directions

1. Score figs across, as deep as the outside skin
2. Make a syrup of white sugar, equal weight with the figs, which you may clarify if you see proper
3. Put in the figs and boil them gently until they look clear
4. Take them off the fire and again boil the syrup till very thick and rich
5. Pour it over the figs hot

title: Preserved Figs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- white or brown figs (a little more than half ripe)
- sugar (3/4 lbs. per lb. of fruit)
- lemon peel
- ginger

## Directions

1. Pick the white or brown figs when a little more than half ripe
2. Peel them very thin
3. To 1 lb. of fruit put 3/4 lbs. of sugar
4. Make a syrup, and put the figs into it with lemon peel and ginger
5. Let them boil till clear, stirring frequently

title: Preserved Huckleberries
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- huckleberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take them just as they begin to ripen, pick and weigh them, allow a pound of fruit to a pound of sugar.
2. Stew them until quite clear, and the syrup becomes thick.

title: Preserved Orange Peel
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- orange peels
- salt
- water
- syrup

## Directions

1. Put the peels in salt and water for 3 days, changing it each day
2. Then put them in fresh water until they are tender
3. Make a rich syrup, and finish as other preserves

title: Preserved Peppers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- small round peppers (green)
- vine leaves
- alum (a little)
- lemon or ginger
- water (1 pint per 1 1/2 lbs. of sugar)
- sugar (1 1/2 lbs.)
- white of egg (beaten to stiff froth)

## Directions

1. Take the small round peppers while they are green
2. Extract the seeds and cores with a sharp penknife
3. Put the outsides into a kettle with vine leaves and a little alum to give them firmness, and assist in keeping them green
4. Flavor with lemon or ginger
5. Allow 1 pint of water to 1 1/2 lbs. of sugar
6. Mix with it some white of egg beaten to a stiff froth
7. Boil and skim it, and when the scum ceases to rise, put in the fruit

title: Preserved Pineapple
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapples
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapples and cut them in thick slices
2. Weigh the slices and to each lb. of fruit put 1 lb. of sugar
3. Dissolve the sugar in a very small quantity of water
4. Stir it and set it over the fire in a kettle
5. Boil it 10 minutes
6. Put in the fruit, letting it remain ½ hour
7. Put the preserve in a pan or dish to cool
8. Have the jars ready to put away for use
9. Cover it well with the jelly
10. Exclude it well from the air and it will keep some time

title: Preserved Pineapple
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapples
- sugar (1 lb. per lb. of fruit)
- water (very small quantity)

## Directions

1. Pare the pineapples and cut them in thick slices
2. Weigh the slices, and to each lb. of fruit put 1 lb. of sugar
3. Dissolve the sugar in a very small quantity of water
4. Stir it and set it over the fire in a kettle
5. Boil it 10 minutes
6. Put in the fruit, letting it remain 1/2 hour
7. Put the preserve in a pan or dish to cool
8. Have the jars ready to put away for use
9. Cover it well with the jelly
10. Exclude it well from the air, and it will keep some time

title: Preserved Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of tomatoes
- 2 lbs. of sugar

## Directions

1. Make the syrup first
2. Put the fruit in and let it remain a short time over a slow fire
3. They do not require a great deal of cooking

title: Preserved Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes (small and green)
- clarified syrup
- orange (1 per 2 lbs. of tomatoes) or lemon (2 per 3 lbs. of tomatoes)

## Directions

1. Take the tomatoes while small and green
2. Put them in cold clarified syrup, with 1 orange, cut in slices, to every 2 lbs. of tomatoes
3. If very superior preserves are wanted, add, instead of the oranges, 2 fresh lemons to 3 lbs. of tomatoes
4. Simmer them over a slow fire for 2 or 3 hours

title: Preserved Watermelon Rind
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Watermelon rind
- Lime
- Cold water
- Alum water
- Grape-leaves
- Ginger tea
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. In a bucket of cold water, put a handful of lime, stir it in, and when it settles clear, pour it over the watermelon rind you intend preserving
2. Let it stay in the weak lime-water one day
3. Soak it a few hours, and get the taste of the lime from the rind
4. Put it in alum water and scald for ten minutes
5. Put grape-leaves in with the alum water while scalding; they will make the rind green
6. Take the rind from the alum, and put it in cold water for a few hours
7. When cold, boil it in strong ginger tea until it is soft, and tastes of the ginger
8. Make the syrup of one and a half pounds of sugar to each pound of rind, and a half pint of water to each pound of sugar
9. Let it cook slowly, skim it, and when it looks clear, put in the rind, and let it cook slowly until clear and transparent
10. The rind should be cut into beautiful shapes, and preserved with care

title: Pressed Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- brisket of beef
- salt
- saltpetre

## Directions

1. Salt a brisket of beef with salt and saltpetre for 5 days
2. Boil it gently till extremely tender
3. Put it under a large weight or in a cheese press till perfectly cold
4. Serve cold for sandwiches

title: Prince Albert's Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of butter
- one-half pound of grated bread crumbs
- one-half pound of sugar
- the juice of two lemons with the rinds grated in
- six eggs well beaten
- a glass of brandy
- four tablespoonfuls of marmalade
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Take one-half pound of butter, one-half pound of grated bread crumbs, one-half pound of sugar, the juice of two lemons with the rinds grated in.
2. Add six eggs well beaten, a glass of brandy and four tablespoonfuls of marmalade.
3. Steam this pudding in a mould and serve with wine sauce.

title: Puff Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. 2 ounces of flour
- water

## Directions

1. Divide the flour in half and put one half into a tin pan
2. Divide the butter into 4 parts, and put 1 part into the pan with the flour
3. Mix it all with water
4. Roll it out and flour it
5. Add another part of the butter, fold it up, roll it out again
6. Add another part of the butter and so on until all is used
7. In mixing it, use only a knife

title: Puff Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Flour
- 1 Pound of Good Butter, or Half Lard and Half Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Cup of Ice Water
- 1 Egg

## Directions

1. Have the flour dry and fresh and the butter of the freshest.
2. Wash the butter well, till it becomes clear and elastic to the touch, and cut it into four equal parts, and place it in a bowl, which you will have cooled with ice water.
3. Set it away to cool and harden.
4. Have your flour sifted and ready, and the egg beaten and at hand.
5. Put the flour into a bowl and add to it a lump of butter; work it with your hand, handling always as lightly as possible.
6. Add the egg, then salt, and, if the paste is intended for cakes, a teaspoonful of sugar.
7. Work all well, but very lightly, with the fingers and thumb, and then gradually add the ice water, working it well and lightly into the flour.
8. When all is worked in, knead lightly for about five minutes, just as though you were making bread.
9. Cut the dough into halves; sprinkle the board lightly with flour.
10. Take the rolling pin and roll the paste out very thin, rolling each half into a half sheet.
11. Break the butter into bits; take about one-quarter of the remainder, quickly spread this over the paste.
12. Dredge it very lightly with flour and lay over it the other sheet.
13. Dredge this with flour lightly and pound with the rolling pin lightly.
14. Roll this from you into a long thin sheet.
15. Spread a thin coating of butter over this, and then fold the paste over from the sides to the center, and then back again, till you have three folds.
16. Turn and roll this again till quite thin; add a second quarter of butter broken into bits.
17. Dredge lightly with flour and fold and roll as before, always in the same direction — from you.
18. Repeat this process, folding and rolling, till all the butter is used.
19. Cut the paste into three or four parts, according to quantity, and set in the ice box in tin plates for twenty minutes, or until you are ready to use.
20. Bake in a very hot oven, being careful not to set the crust too near the inner fire part of the oven as it will bake unevenly.

title: Puffs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight potatoes
- two well-beaten eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- a cup of milk

## Directions

1. Mash seven or eight potatoes smoothly
2. Mix in with them two well-beaten eggs, two tablespoonfuls of melted butter, also well-beaten, and a cup of milk
3. Pour it into a pan and bake in a hot stove

title: Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Sweet Milk
- 1/2 Pound of Flour
- 2 Ounces of Butter
- Salt
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and the yolks separately till very, very light.
2. Warm the milk and add the melted butter.
3. When cold, mix with the yolks of the eggs, and add the flour and salt, beating till very light.
4. Now add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Stir lightly, turn into buttered tins, filling each about half full, and bake to a light brown.

title: Pumpkin Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Mashed, Stewed Pumpkin
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Mace, Cinnamon and Allspice
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy, if desired

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of pumpkin, cut into dice-shaped pieces, putting it on with just enough water to keep from burning, say about a quarter of a cupful.
2. Let it stew slowly for an hour, at least, or until tender.
3. Drain and press through a colander.
4. Add a good tablespoonful of butter and a quarter of a teaspoonful of salt.
5. Mix well and let it cool.
6. When cool, put the pumpkin, using one pint, into a large bowl, and add the pint of milk, the ground spices, and mix all well together, and add the above amount of sugar, or sugar to taste.
7. Beat four eggs well and add to the mixture.
8. Add a tablespoonful of brandy, if desired.
9. Line the pie pans, and bake the under crust.
10. Fill with the mixture, and bake in a quick oven for half an hour.
11. When cold, sprinkle lightly with white powdered sugar, and serve.

title: Pumpkin Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ lb. of stewed pumpkin
- 3 eggs
- ½ lb. of fresh butter or 1 pint of cream
- ¼ lb. of powdered white sugar
- ¼ glass of wine and brandy, mixed
- teaspoonful of mixed spice (mace and nutmeg)
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take ½ lb. of stewed pumpkin, 3 eggs, ½ lb. of fresh butter or 1 pint of cream, ¼ lb. of powdered white sugar, ¼ glass of wine and brandy, mixed, a teaspoonful of mixed spice (mace and nutmeg), or if you prefer it, add a little cinnamon
2. Put a nice paste in the dish
3. Pour the mixture in
4. Bake it ¾ hour in a moderate oven
5. Grate sugar over it when cool

title: Pumpkin with Salt Meat
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- salt meat
- brown sugar
- molasses
- smoked meat

## Directions

1. Slice the pumpkin and put it in the oven with brown sugar, or good molasses
2. Slice some smoked meat and lay it in among the pumpkin
3. Cook it tender

title: Punch Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Gill of Good Cognac or Rum
- 5 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Juice of 3 Oranges
- The Zest of Half a Lemon
- The Grated Rind of Half an Orange
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Essence

## Directions

1. Put the rum or brandy, the sugar, orange rind, lemon zest and vanilla essence into a saucepan and set on the stove
2. As soon as the ingredients light into a flame, cover tightly with a lid and let all infuse together for two minutes
3. Then take from the fire and add the juice of three Louisiana oranges
4. Serve very hot as a pudding sauce.

title: Puree of Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Young Green Peas
- Bunch of Parsley
- 2 Young Onions
- 2 Quarts of Good Milk or Broth
- Pepper and Salt

## Directions

1. Cut the onions and parsley fine, and boil with the peas until all are quite tender, in boiling water, for about a half hour.
2. Then drain.
3. Rub all through a sieve or colander, and add them to the boiling broth or milk.
4. Do not allow this to boil after adding the peas.
5. Season and serve with dainty Croutons.
6. To keep hot, stand the soup on a bain-marie, or kettle of boiling water.

title: Puree of Green Peas a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Green Peas
- 1 Cup of Milk or Cream
- A Pinch of Salt and White Pepper
- 1 Teaspoonful of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Shell and clean the peas well; then put them in a saucepan with the cream and a half cup of water, and let them simmer till they become quite soft.
2. Remove the pan from the fire; rub the peas through a fine sieve; season well with the salt and pepper and sugar; add a tablespoonful of butter; beat the butter in well with the peas; set on the stove for five minutes and serve hot.

title: Puree of Green Peas a la St. Germain
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Green Peas
- 1 Pint of Chicken Broth
- 1 Pint of Sweet Cream
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 2 Sprigs of Mint
- A Pinch Each of Salt and White Pepper
- 1 Teaspoonful of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 8 Chicken Quenelles to Garnish

## Directions

1. Shell and clean the peas and put them in a saucepan or sautoire, with one pint of chicken broth and one pint of sweet cream.
2. Add an herb bouquet, in which you will have tied two sprigs of mint.
3. Let the peas cook for twenty minutes, or until very tender, and then remove the herb bouquet and mint; take from the fire, and run the peas through a sieve.
4. Season with salt and pepper and a little powdered sugar; add a tablespoonful of butter; set on the fire five minutes longer, and then serve on a hot dish with Chicken Quenelles to garnish.

title: Puree of Jerusalem Artichokes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Artichokes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- a pint of water
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and skin the artichokes, and boil until tender in a pint of water.
2. Press them through a colander, and return to the fire in a saucepan in which you have placed a tablespoonful of butter, salt and pepper.
3. Stir well, and let them simmer for five minutes longer, and serve with a Cream Sauce.

title: Puree of Red Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Dried Red Beans
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/4 Pound of Ham or Lean Salt Pork
- 1/2 Pint of Cream or Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the beans by washing and soaking them over night, or at least five or six hours, in fresh, cold water.
2. Drain off this water and put the beans in a pot of cold water.
3. Add the ham or salt pork, and the herbs and onion and carrot, minced fine.
4. Let the water heat slowly and boil the beans until they will mash very easily under pressure.
5. Remove the beans from the fire and take out the bits of ham.
6. Press the beans through a colander.
7. Add a tablespoonful of butter as you return them to the pot in which they have been boiled, and a half pint of cream or milk, or sufficient according to quantity to make the puree of the consistency of thick starch or mashed potatoes.
8. Season with salt and pepper, and serve.

title: Pyramid of Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- preserve or jam
- yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Roll out some nicely prepared puff paste, an inch in thickness
2. cut the paste into any shape you may fancy
3. make each smaller until the top one is not larger than a cent
4. between each piece of paste spread some nice preserve or jam
5. turn up the edges of the paste, and brush the sides and top with the yolk of an egg well beaten
6. lay the pyramid on a baking tin, and bake a nice brown
7. serve hot

title: Quail Roasted in Grape Leaves
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Quails
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 6 Slices of Buttered Toast
- 12 Grape Leaves
- Green Grape Jelly

## Directions

1. Follow the directions given for roasting quails, only do not wrap the quails in strips of bacon.
2. Instead rub the bodies well with butter and then envelop the birds in fresh grape leaves.
3. Set in a baking pan and proceed to roast according to the directions given above.
4. Garnish a dish nicely with fresh young grape leaves, place the quails on slices of toast and lay upon the leaves and send the dish to the table hot.
5. Serve the quails with Green Grape Jelly.

title: Quails Broiled With Bacon
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Quails
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt
- 3/4 Tablespoonful of Pepper
- 6 Slices of Bacon
- 6 Slices of Toast
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel
- Watercress and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the quails, singe and wipe well.
2. Split them through the back without separating the breast and break the leg bones.
3. Rub well with salt and pepper and a little melted butter, mixed together.
4. Put the quails on a broiler and let them broil on a moderate fire for fifteen minutes, allowing seven and a half minutes to either side, and turning frequently to prevent burning.
5. Have ready a hot dish with six slices of buttered toast, lay the quail on top and pour over a little melted butter (Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel).
6. Decorate the dish with parsley sprigs, on which lay six nicely broiled slices of breakfast bacon.

title: Queen Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Flour
- 4 Ounces of Sugar
- 1 Pound of Currants
- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Teacupful of Cream
- 8 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Rose Water

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter to a cream
2. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
3. Add the cream, and then one-half of the flour, and next one-half of the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Add the remainder of the flour into which you will have dredged the currants that have been well-washed cleansed and picked and dried
5. Mix thoroughly, and add the remainder of the whites and the rose water
6. Beat as you would a pound cake
7. Pour into small, buttered tins and bake immediately to a light brown

title: Queen Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cream Puff Filling
- Cream Puff Batter
- ½ Cup of Grated Almonds
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine
- Cold Sherry Wine Sauce or French Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a Cream Puff Batter and a Cream Puff Filling.
2. If the flavor of almonds is desired, add a few finely-grated almonds that have been steeped in Sherry Wine.
3. Have ready the frying pan, with boiling lard.
4. Roll the cream batter into small balls, and fry till they swell to the size of an egg.
5. Skim out with a skimmer, and drain.
6. When cold, split one side with a very sharp knife, and fill with the filling.
7. Close, and continue till all are filled.
8. Serve cold with Sherry Wine Sauce or Peach Sauce.

title: Queen Mab's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of new milk
- thin rind of 1 lemon
- 6 or 8 bitter almonds
- pinch of salt
- 1/2 ounce of isinglass
- sugar to the taste
- cream
- wine

## Directions

1. Throw into a pint of new milk, the thin rind of 1 lemon, 6 or 8 bitter almonds and a pinch of salt
2. keep it at the boiling point till strongly flavored
3. add 1/2 of an ounce of isinglass
4. when dissolved strain the milk
5. sugar to the taste
6. put it in a mould, and when cold, eat it with a rich sauce of cream, wine and sugar

title: Queen Mab's Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of new milk
- Thin rind of 1 lemon
- 6 or 8 bitter almonds
- A pinch of salt
- J of an ounce of isinglass
- Sugar to the taste

## Directions

1. Throw into a pint of new milk, the thin rind of 1 lemon, 6 or 8 bitter almonds and a pinch of salt
2. Keep it at the boiling point till strongly flavored
3. Add J of an ounce of isinglass
4. When dissolved strain the milk
5. Sugar to the taste
6. Put it in a mould, and when cold, eat it with a rich sauce

title: Queen Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Sweet Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder or 1/2 Cake of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. If yeast is used, set the batter to rise over night, as in Egg Muffins, and then proceed in exactly the same manner, adding the eggs in the morning.
2. Beat the eggs separately till very light, add to the batter, and pour into muffin rings or gem pans and let the mixture rise a half hour longer.
3. Then bake for twenty minutes in a quick oven.
4. If yeast powder is used, beat the yolks of the eggs to a cream, add the scalded milk and butter, and add the flour.
5. Beat till very light, and add the yeast powder, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Turn into gem pans or muffin rings, and bake for twenty minutes in a hot oven.

title: Queen Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1/4 Pound of Rice
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- 1/4 Blade of Mace
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Quarts of White Veal Broth
- Salt and Pepper
- Chopped onion
- Bay leaf
- 1 Carrot
- Small bunch of celery
- 1 Cup of rice
- 1 Tablespoonful of butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of flour
- 1 Glass of milk
- 1/4 Grated nutmeg
- Croutons of bread fried in butter

## Directions

1. Take a fine large chicken, clean it nicely and put it whole into a pot containing about five quarts of water.
2. Add chopped onion, thyme, bay leaf, one carrot, a small bunch of celery, and one cup of rice.
3. Let the chicken simmer well, for about four hours, and, when well cooked, take out the chicken from the broth.
4. Cut off the white meat and cut it into pieces about the size of dice.
5. Then strain the broth, mashing the rice well.
6. Make a puree by taking another saucepan, putting in one tablespoonful of butter and one of flour, letting it melt together without browning.
7. Moisten this well with the soup and a glass of milk, and season with salt and pepper, and one-quarter of a grated nutmeg and add to the broth.
8. Then add the chicken, which has been cut up.
9. Put in the tureen little dice of croutons of bread fried in butter.
10. Pour the soup over and serve hot.

title: Queen Victoria’s Favorite Green Pea Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of green peas
- a double-handful of parsley
- four stalks of green mint
- a good handful of green onions
- two quarts of veal or beef stock
- a half pound of butter
- a spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Place it on the fire, throw in the above peas, mint and onions
2. Let them all boil; when they are thoroughly done take them out, drain them and pound them well together
3. Put them in the stewpan again with the liquor
4. Warm it and run it through a sieve
5. Add at the last moment a half pound of butter and a spoonful of sugar
6. Serve with fried bread

title: Queen's Drops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a pound of butter
- quarter of a pound of sifted sugar
- two eggs
- six ounces of flour
- almonds, vanilla, or lemon for flavoring
- butter for paper

## Directions

1. Beat up a quarter of a pound of butter and a quarter of a pound of sifted sugar
2. Add two eggs and six ounces of flour
3. Flavor with almonds, vanilla, or lemon
4. Butter some paper and place it on baking-sheet or pans
5. Drop the mixture in drops about the size of a nutmeg
6. Bake in a hot oven

title: Quick Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Cake of Compressed Yeast, or 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, and add the butter.
2. When it grows lukewarm, add the yeast, the flour and salt, and beat all well for about ten minutes.
3. Then set the bowl in a warm place, cover with a cloth, and let the mixture rise for about two hours.
4. When very light, beat well again, and add two eggs well beaten.
5. Turn into muffin rings or gem pans, and let them rise a half hour longer.
6. At the end of that time place them in the oven and bake for about twenty minutes.
7. If yeast powder is used, beat into the batter, and proceed to bake immediately.

title: Quick Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Eggs
- 1½ Cups of Sugar
- 2 Cups of Flour
- ¾ Cup of Cold Water
- 1 Teaspoonful Lemon Extract
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar to a cream
2. Add the water, and mix well
3. Then gradually add the flour, alternating with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a thick froth
4. Bake from thirty to forty-five minutes in a quick oven

title: Quick Way of Pickling Cabbage
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 nice white cabbages
- salt
- water
- weak vinegar
- turmeric
- good cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Quarter the cabbages; if very large more than quarter them
2. Put them in salt and water to remain 24 hours
3. Boil them till tender in weak vinegar with a little turmeric in it
4. Put them in jars and pour over them good cold vinegar
5. Tie them up

title: Quince Cheese
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fine ripe quinces
- brown sugar (1/2 lb. per lb. of cut quinces)
- water

## Directions

1. Take fine ripe quinces, pare and core them
2. Cut them into pieces and weigh them
3. To each lb. of the cut quinces allow 1/2 lb. of the best brown sugar
4. Put the cores and parings into a kettle with water enough to cover them, keeping the lid of the kettle closed
5. When they are all boiled to pieces and quite soft, strain off the water over the sugar
6. When it is entirely dissolved, put it over the fire and boil it to a thick syrup, skimming it well
7. When no more scum rises, put in the quinces
8. Cover them closely, and boil them all day over a slow fire, stirring them and mashing them down with a spoon till they are a thick smooth paste
9. Take it out and put it into buttered deep pans or dishes
10. Let it set to get cold
11. It will then turn out so firm that you may cut it into slices like cheese
12. Keep it in a dry place in broad stone pots

title: Quince Jam
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- loaf sugar
- fruit

## Directions

1. Put equal weight of loaf sugar with the fruit; mash them well together, and stew them until they become sufficiently

title: Quince Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- 1 pound of white sugar to every pint of fruit liquid

## Directions

1. Pare and core the quinces, and cut into slices.
2. Boil until well done, which may be determined by running a fork through them.
3. Strain the liquor through a flannel bag, and measure it, allowing one pound of white sugar to every pint of juice.
4. Place the whole on a fire in the preserving kettle, and watch closely.
5. Do not let it boil over.
6. Try occasionally on a saucer.
7. When it begins to grow solid it is done.
8. Bottle as directed.

title: Quince Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare the quinces as before directed
2. Take off the stems and blossoms
3. Wash them clean and cut them in slices without paring
4. Fill the pan and pour in water to cover them
5. Stew them gently, putting in a little water occasionally, till they are quite soft
6. Pour them in a jelly bag
7. Let all the juice run through, pressing it

title: Quince Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Quinces
- ¾ of a Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Take ripe quinces and cut them into small pieces.
2. Steam them in sufficient water to cover them and let them cook till they can be mashed with a wooden spoon.
3. When well-mashed in the water, pour in the sugar, allowing three-fourths of a pound for every pound of fruit.
4. Proceed to arrange in layers as directed above.
5. Add the mixture and cook until it assumes a bright red color.
6. Test by pouring on a saucer just sufficient to see if it will jelly.
7. When it reaches this degree of consistency, proceed to run into jars or glasses.
8. When cold it will be quite solid.

title: Quince Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe quinces
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pare the ripe quinces, and then cut them into slices or rings
2. Weigh them and put them into the preserving kettle and cover with water
3. When they are quite tender, pour off the superfluous water, leaving about half a pint to every pound of fruit
4. Add sugar, pound for pound
5. Let the whole boil till the fruit is tender
6. Carefully drain out the fruit and lay it in the jars
7. Return the kettle to the fire, and let the syrup boil to a jelly
8. Fill the jars with it

title: Quince Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 quinces
- little water
- lemon peel
- sugar
- 4 eggs
- ½ pint of cream
- mace and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare quinces, cut them in quarters
2. Stew them with a little water and lemon peel
3. Let them cook gently till soft
4. Rub them through a sieve
5. Mix with sugar till very sweet
6. Add 4 eggs, ½ pint of cream, mace and nutmeg
7. Bake it in paste

title: Quince Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 quinces
- water
- lemon peel
- sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint of cream
- mace
- nutmeg
- paste

## Directions

1. Take 6 quinces, pare them, cut them in quarters, and stew them with a little water and lemon peel
2. let them cook gently till soft
3. then rub them through a sieve
4. mix with sugar till very sweet
5. add 4 eggs, 1 pint of cream, mace and nutmeg
6. bake it in paste

title: Rabbit en Matelote
body:
## Ingredients

- A Pair of Rabbits
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard or Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 6 Fresh, Large Tomatoes, or a Half Can
- 1 Large Onion, Chopped Fine
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme, Sweet Marjoram, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1 Glass of Good Claret, or the Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Quart of Water or Consommé
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Bash of Cayenne
- Allspice
- 1 Clove of Garlic

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, wash and cut the rabbit into pieces at the joints.
2. Put the lard or butter into a deep stewpan or kettle.
3. When hot, add gradually two tablespoonfuls of flour, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
4. Throw in about ten or twelve well-mashed allspice, and three sprigs each of chopped thyme, parsley, bay leaf and sweet marjoram, one clove of garlic, and one large onion, chopped very fine.
5. Add six fresh large tomatoes, chopped fine, or one-half can of tomatoes.
6. Pour in one glass of good claret, add about one quart of water, and let it boil well.
7. Then add salt and Cayenne to taste.

title: Rabbit Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of chopped mushrooms
- a bunch of parsley
- two shallots chopped
- pepper
- salt
- thyme or sweet herbs
- a glass of white wine
- a half cup of brown gravy from veal or chicken
- the juice of half a lemon
- a cup of sweet milk
- a piece of butter, as large as a hen's egg
- paste

## Directions

1. Season mushrooms, parsley, and shallots with pepper, salt, and thyme or sweet herbs
2. Cook them a light brown
3. Throw in a glass of white wine, a half cup of brown gravy from veal or chicken, and the juice of half a lemon
4. If you have no gravy on hand, add to the rabbits a cup of sweet milk, and a piece of butter, as large as a hen's egg
5. Toss all up on the fire fifteen or twenty minutes
6. Make a nice paste, line the sides of the pan
7. Pour in the stewed rabbit, and cover with paste
8. Bake until a light brown
9. Eat cold or hot

title: Rabbit Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbits
- cold water
- butter
- onions
- celery
- cold water
- peas (green or dry)
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut one or two rabbits into joints; lay them for an hour in cold water; dry and fry them in butter until they are half done;
2. Place the meat in a saucepan with four or five onions and a head of celery cut small; add to these three parts of cold water and a cup of peas, either green or dry;
3. Season with pepper and salt, then strain and serve it. Some like it unstrained.

title: Rabbit Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Young Rabbits
- 2 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Blade of Mace
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Cup of Rice
- Cayenne Pepper, 1/2 Pod
- Salt to the Taste

## Directions

1. The rabbits should be well skinned and singed.
2. Wash thoroughly in warm water; this is very important.
3. Then cut the meat into small pieces and put into the soup pot, with the quantity of water given.
4. Chop the onion, mace and bay leaf and add.
5. Place on a very moderate fire, and let it simmer gently until the meat has grown very tender. This will require about two hours or less.
6. Add the salt, pepper and rice, and simmer for an hour longer.
7. Pour into the tureen over croutons and serve.
8. The Creoles add two tablespoonfuls of sherry or port wine, thus increasing the delicacy of the flavor.

title: Rabbit, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- A Pair of Rabbits
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Onion
- 1 Slice of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- ½ Box of Mushrooms
- The Zest of a Lemon
- ½ Bottle of Claret Wine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons to Garnish
- 1 Quart of Consommé or Water

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit; clean and draw, and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Rub well with salt and pepper; put a tablespoonful of butter into the saucepan with the rabbit and let it brown slowly.
3. When nearly brown, add the onion, chopped fine, and let this brown slightly.
4. Then add the ham, minced very fine, and the clove and garlic and bay leaves and thyme, minced very fine.
5. Stir with the rabbit, and let these brown for about two minutes; then add a tablespoonful of flour and brown for a few minutes.
6. Add a half bottle of Claret wine and let all simmer for five minutes; then add a quart of consommé or water, and let all cook for about one hour.
7. Season according to taste.
8. Add a half can of mushrooms, chopped fine, and the zest of a lemon and again season to taste.
9. Let all cook for a half hour longer and serve on a hot dish, with croutons fried in butter.

title: Ragout Souse
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- souse
- butter
- flour
- boiling water
- beaten cloves
- allspice
- 1 glass of Madeira wine
- oysters

## Directions

1. Fry the souse of a light brown
2. In another vessel make a gravy of a large lump of butter rolled in flour with a little boiling water
3. Add to it beaten cloves and allspice, with 1 glass of Madeira wine
4. A dozen or two of oysters dressed with the souse adds much to its excellence

title: Rainbow Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Layers of Sponge or Pound Cake
- 3 Ounces of Pink Icing
- 3 Ounces of Violet Icing
- 3 Ounces of Chocolate Icing
- 3 Ounces of Vanilla Icing
- 3 Ounces of White Icing

## Directions

1. Make a good layer cake, either Pound or Sponge.
2. Prepare the Cream Icings.
3. Place these between the alternate layers in the order given.
4. Ice the cake nicely.

title: Raised Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- wheat flour
- 4 eggs
- a gill of yeast
- a spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Make a thick batter of milk and wheat flour
2. Add eggs and beat light
3. Add yeast and butter
4. Let it rise some hours
5. As you take them out of the iron, butter and sprinkle them

title: Raised Wheat Bread With Potatoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one half a cup of hop yeast, or yeast made from turnpike cake
- two boiled hot Irish potatoes
- a pint of water
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Take one half a cup of hop yeast, or yeast made from turnpike cake will do, also two boiled hot Irish potatoes, mash them
2. add to the yeast and potatoes one pint of water
3. Make a sponge of this by beating in sifted flour until it is a soft dough
4. Set it to rise by the stove
5. when it is light, pour the sponge in the bread tray and mould it rather stiff with sifted flour
6. knead it well and set it to rise again
7. When it is light, work in a little more flour, shape it in loaves in the baking-pans
8. and when light the second time bake it

title: Raisin Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Layers of Pound or Cup Cake
- 1/4 Pound of Raisins
- 1/4 Pound of Citron
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cloves, Cinnamon, Allspice and Nutmeg
- Frosting to Cover

## Directions

1. Make a good Pound or Cup Cake and bake in tins, taking out enough to fill three tins.
2. Add to the remaining batter one-half teaspoonful each of ground cloves, allspice, cinnamon and nutmeg, and one-quarter pound each of raisins, seeded, and citron, chopped very fine.
3. Mix thoroughly, and bake in two layers.
4. Place between the other layers, alternating with a frosting, as in Almond Layer Cake.

title: Raisin Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pounds of Stoned Raisins
- 1 1/2 Pints of Sugar
- 1 1/2 Pints of Flour
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 3/4 Pint of Milk
- 6 Eggs
- 3/4 Gill of White or Sherry Wine
- 1/2 Gill of Brandy
- 2 1/2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and baking powder together.
2. Then beat the butter and sugar to a cream, add the yolks of the eggs, and beat till very light.
3. Beat the whites to a stiff froth, and then add alternately the whites and flour to the mixture, beating vigorously till light.
4. Now add the wine and brandy.
5. Flour the raisins, and shake lightly, till each becomes slightly coated on each side, and add to the cake batter, and then begin to stir lightly, stirring just sufficient to mix.
6. Have ready a deep baking pan or two, lined with buttered paper, and bake in a slightly quick oven for from forty-five minutes to an hour.
7. Have a thick layer of paper over the cake for the first half hour of baking.

title: Raisin Wine with Elder Flowers
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six pounds of raisins
- Six gallons of water
- Twelve pounds of white sugar
- Half-pint of yeast
- Half a pound of elder flowers

## Directions

1. Boil six pounds of raisins in six gallons of water
2. When soft, rub them to a pulp, and pass through a colander to get rid of the stones
3. Add this pulp to the water it was boiled in
4. Put to it twelve pounds of white sugar and a half-pint of yeast
5. When clear, suspend half a pound of elder flowers in it to flavor the wine
6. Withdraw the flowers and bottle off the wine

title: Raspberry Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Raspberry Juice
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1/4 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Pint of Gelatine

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Strawberry Bavarian Cream

title: Raspberry Cordial
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- loaf sugar
- brandy

## Directions

1. Squeeze the fruit through a flannel bag
2. To every quart of juice add a pound of loaf sugar
3. Put it in a stone jar and stir it constantly for half an hour
4. Allow it to stand for three days, then strain it again
5. Add to each quart of juice a quart of fine brandy

title: Raspberry Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of stiff raspberry jelly
- cream
- sugar
- wine

## Directions

1. Put 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of stiff raspberry jelly into a large bowl
2. In another bowl froth the cream, having seasoned it with sugar and wine
3. Skim the froth as it rises and add it to the jelly
4. Beat and stir the jelly until you add frothed cream enough to make it of a handsome color and not too sweet to the taste

title: Raspberry Granit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Raspberries
- 1 Tablespoonful of Raspberry Extract
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Mash the berries and proceed in exactly the same manner as for Strawberry Granit.

title: Raspberry Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1 Quart of Raspberries or 2 Tablespoonfuls of Raspberry Extract
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- The Yolks of 6 Eggs
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil, add the sugar, and when well-dissolved take from the fire, add the eggs, well beaten to a cream, and set aside to cool.
2. Then, when cool, add the remaining half of the sugar, which you will have blended with the berries, which have been mashed well, and allowed to stand for several hours, and then press through a sieve.
3. Mix well with the milk, add the lemon juice, turn into the freezer, and freeze as above directed.
4. If the berries are too pale to impart a sufficient rosy tinge to the cream, add a few drops of Raspberry Extract.
5. If not in season use two tablespoonfuls of Raspberry Extract, and proceed as in recipe for Vanilla Cream.

title: Raspberry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Raspberries
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe fruit and weigh.
2. Allow equal weights of fruit and sugar.
3. Proceed to boil as directed.
4. When they begin to jelly, they are ready to be turned into glasses.

title: Raspberry Jam
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take the weight of raspberries and sugar; pick the berries very carefully, and lay them, with the sugar, in a stone jar overnight
2. In the morning boil it until it begins to jelly; the great secret of making nice jam, and preserving the raspberry flavor, is to boil it enough

title: Raspberry Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pick all the leaves and stems from the raspberries
2. Put them on in bell metal skillets
3. Mix a little powdered sugar with them and mash them well
4. Stew only a short time
5. Take them off and pass them well through a clean sieve to extract as much of the pulp as possible
6. To every pint of juice put 1 lb. (dead weight) of loaf sugar
7. Return it to the skillet and stew until you think it sufficiently thick, which you may discover by cooling a little

title: Raspberry Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- 1/2 pound of sugar to every pound of berries

## Directions

1. To every pound of raspberries allow a half of a pound of sugar.
2. Put the berries in a porcelain-lined kettle, and cover them with the sugar and let them stand for two hours.
3. Set over a moderate fire and bring the mixture to a boiling point.
4. Skim while boiling.
5. Pour immediately into cups and glasses, and cover tightly and place in a cool dry place.

title: Raspberry or Blackberry Charlotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Raspberries or Blackberries
- 1 Pound of White Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Soft of Bread

## Directions

1. Stew the berries, making a marmalade.
2. Proceed exactly as in the recipe for Cherry Charlotte.

title: Raspberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Berries
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful Sweet Spices, Blended Equally
- 1/4 Cup of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Flaky pie crust

## Directions

1. Pick, wash and stem the berries; then put them in a porcelain-lined saucepan, and let them stew gently, adding the water and the butter.
2. Season well with a little ground cinnamon, mace and allspice.
3. When done, take off and allow to cool a little.
4. Make a flaky pie crust, and line the tin plates, after greasing slightly with butter, and bake the bottom crust slightly brown.
5. Take out and fill in with the berries, and then cover the top with a thin layer of crust.
6. Decorate the edges, and set in the oven to bake.
7. When a nice, delicate brown, take out and let them cool.
8. Remove from the tin plate by slightly loosening the outer edge with a knife.
9. Turn over into a china plate, sprinkle with white sugar, and serve cold or hot.

title: Raspberry Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Raspberries
- % Pound of Powdered Sugar
- % Pound of Butter
- 1 Gill of Sherry Wine and Brandy Mixed
- 1 Stale Small Sponge Cake
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Stew the berries and proceed in the same manner as for Orange Pudding
2. Bake for about twenty minutes or half an hour after adding the fruit
3. Serve cold, with a Cream or Hard Sauce

title: Raspberry Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Pounds of Raspberries
- 2 Gallons of Brandy
- 7 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Steep the berries, which you will have mashed well, for one month, or at least fifteen days, in the brandy.
2. Add a syrup made of the sugar and water.
3. Filter and proceed to bottle as directed.

title: Raspberry Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pints of Raspberry Juice or Pulp
- 1 1/2 Pints of Pickled Raspberries
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 3 Drops of Cochineal
- The Juice of 1/2 Lemon

## Directions

1. Stem the berries, and add the sugar and lemon, and stand aside in their juice for an hour and a half.
2. Then strain through a fine sieve, and add the reserved juice.
3. Turn into the freezer, and proceed to freeze according to directions given under heading Ice Cream.

title: Raspberry Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Raspberry Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1 Ounce of Raspberry Essence

## Directions

1. Pick, stem and wash the berries
2. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Strawberry Syrup
3. Add the essence when about to bottle

title: Raspberry Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Raspberries
- 1 Quart of Good French Vinegar
- White sugar in the proportion of one pound to every pint of the liquor

## Directions

1. Put one quart of raspberries into a jar or deep vessel, and pour over the vinegar
2. Let them stand for twenty-four hours, and then strain through a jelly bag, and add the other quart of raspberries, and let them stand for twenty-four hours longer
3. Strain them again, and again add the fruit, repeating this operation of straining and letting stand twenty-four hours three times
4. Strain through a muslin bag, and add one pound of white sugar to every pint of the liquor
5. Boil the whole in a porcelain kettle for half an hour
6. When cold, bottle it and keep it in a cool place

title: Ratafia of Four Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Pounds of Fine, Ripe Cherries
- 3 Pounds of Sour Cherries
- 1 Pound of Currants
- 3 Pounds of Raspberries
- 1 Pint of the Best French Brandy
- 4 Ounces of Sugar to Every Pint of Fruit Liquor

## Directions

1. Stone the fruit and stem the berries
2. Put all together in a bowl, and mash well
3. Let the fruit stand for two hours
4. Press out the juice through a cloth or sieve
5. Add one pint of the best French brandy and four ounces of sugar to every pint of liquor obtained
6. Put in a stone jar, cover tight, and let it stand for a month
7. At the end of the time strain and filter
8. Bottle and keep in a cool place

title: Ravigote Sauce (Cold)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Shallots
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Pickle
- 1 Tablespoonful of Mustard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- The Yolk of an Egg
- 4 Sprigs of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Chop the shallots, greens and white all very fine, and mince the cloves of two garlics very fine
2. Put these in a bowl, and add one pickle of about three inches long, chopped very fine
3. Drain the pickle first of all water
4. Add a good bunch of parsley, chopped very fine
5. Mix all this together in a bowl, and add one tablespoonful of mustard
6. Mix well
7. Add a good tablespoonful of vinegar and salt and pepper to taste
8. Beat the yolk of an egg and mix well in the sauce
9. Serve cold, with cold meats, turkey or fowl

title: Ravigote Sauce (Hot)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Shallots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vinegar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sauce Velouté

## Directions

1. Chop the parsley very fine
2. Have ready a Sauce Velouté
3. Add the other ingredients
4. Mix well
5. Place in a saucepan and set in boiling water and let it heat
6. Serve hot with fish, white meats of chicken, etc

title: Ravioli à la Avegno
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Four lbs. round of beef
- a tablespoonful of lard
- a little flour
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- water
- 1 pound of dry mushrooms

## Directions

1. Lard the beef
2. Put in a pot with a tablespoonful of lard
3. Smother on a slow fire
4. When ready to fry, sprinkle with a little flour
5. Fry brown
6. Add onions, salt, and pepper to taste, and water enough to cook thoroughly
7. About 1/2 an hour before done, put in 1 pound of dry mushrooms
8. This daube is made for its gravy to the ravioles

title: Raw Oysters on Half Shell
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oysters to Each Plate
- Cracked Ice
- Maunsell White or Black Pepper, Cayenne and Vinegar
- Lemon, Sliced or Cut in Quarters

## Directions

1. Allow six oysters to each person where the bivalve is used to begin the dinner or breakfast.
2. Have the oysters opened in their shell and remove one half of the shell.
3. Drain the water from the oyster shell, without disturbing the oyster, and place in plates, with cracked ice.
4. Sprinkle with a quarter of a sliced lemon in the center of the plate.
5. Serve with black pepper and Cayenne, if desired, or the famous Maunsell White, sold in all New Orleans oyster saloons.

title: Red Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Red Beans
- 2 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Minced Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Bunch of Parsley
- Sprig of Thyme
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the beans and soak them overnight in lukewarm water.
2. Drain and put them in a saucepan with the cold water.
3. Allow them to come gradually to a boil; then set them back, and let them simmer gently for about two hours.
4. Melt the butter in a saucepan and fry in it the onion, parsley, thyme and bay leaf until brown.
5. Add these to the beans and boil about an hour and a half longer.
6. If the beans are perfectly tender at this point, press the whole through a colander.
7. Return to the fire, and add the seasonings.
8. Let them boil up once and serve with the Croqutons.
9. Some think that the flavor is enhanced by beating up an egg in the tureen and pouring the boiling soup gradually over it, stirring constantly.

title: Red Beans and Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Dried Red Beans
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Pound of Ham or Salt Meat
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the beans and soak them over night, or, at least five or six hours, in fresh, cold water.
2. When ready to cook, drain off this water and put the beans in a pot of cold water, covering with at least two quarts.
3. Let the water heat slowly.
4. Then add the ham or salt pork, and the herbs and onion and carrot, minced fine.
5. Boil the beans at least two hours, or until tender enough to mash easily under pressure.
6. When tender, remove from the pot, put the salt meat or ham on top of the dish, and serve hot as a vegetable, with boiled rice.

title: Red Beans, Burgundy Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Red Beans
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Onion
- 2 Cloves
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Glass of Claret Wine
- 6 Small Glazed Onions

## Directions

1. Pick and wash the beans and let them soak in cold water for six hours.
2. Drain thoroughly, and put in a saucepan, with sufficient fresh cold water to cover.
3. Add a tablespoonful of butter, and a medium-sized onion, with two cloves stuck in it.
4. Boil for about twenty minutes, and then add a glass of Claret.
5. Stir well, and let the beans cook for three-quarters of an hour longer, stirring frequently to keep from scorching.
6. Remove from the fire, take out the herb bouquet and onion, pour the beans into a hot dish, and decorate the edges with a half dozen small glazed onions.
7. Serve hot.

title: Red Beans, Plain Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Dried Red Beans
- 1/2 spoonful of Lard or Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Soak the beans in cold water over night, or at least five or six hours, and drain off the water, and place them in a pot of cold water, using at least a quart of water to a pint of beans.
2. Let them boil for at least an hour and a half or two hours, and then season nicely with salt and pepper.
3. Add a half tablespoonful of lard or butter, let them cook for fifteen minutes more, and serve in their own juice.

title: Red Pepper Catsup a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Pepper Pods
- 2 Quarts of Best French Vinegar
- 1 Quart of Water
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Grated Horseradish
- 5 Onions
- 1 Garlic

## Directions

1. Take four dozen pods of ripe green peppers, two quarts of vinegar, one of water, three tablespoonfuls of grated horseradish, five onions sliced, and one garlic
2. Boil all together till the onions are very soft
3. Mash all together to a paste and strain, rubbing through the sieve till nothing remains but the seeds and skins
4. When cold, bottle and cork tightly for use

title: Red Snapper a la Chambord
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- red snapper fish
- bacon
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- six laurel leaves
- thyme
- sliced onions
- three cloves
- forcemeat balls or pigeons a la Gautier
- iced sweetbreads
- small glaces
- pope's eyes of a shoulder of veal
- crabs
- fowl livers
- truffles
- cock's combs
- cock's kidneys
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Clean your fish, and be careful not to damage it, and replace the roe
2. Take off the scales
3. lightly raise the skin on one side, and lard it with bacon from fin to tail
4. put it in a pan, and moisten with white wine
5. Add salt, pepper, parsley, six laurel leaves, some thyme, sliced onions and three cloves
6. cover the head with strips of bacon
7. put it into the oven, covering your fish-kettle with leaves of foolscap paper, and letting it simmer for an hour
8. When about to serve, drain it and put it on a platter
9. garnish it all round with forcemeat balls, or better, with pigeons a la Gautier, iced sweetbreads, small glaces, pope's eyes of a shoulder of veal, crabs, fowl livers, truffles, cock's combs and cock's kidneys
10. Strain the sauce through a silken sieve
11. if not sufficiently seasoned, put into a pan two spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, and two spoonfuls of the dressing of your snapper
12. let it boil down one-half
13. put your small garnishes into it, and pour the sauce around the fish
14. Serve after having jellied and browned it

title: Red Snapper a la Chambord
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Red Snapper, 3 Pounds in Weight
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 1/2 Can Mushrooms
- 3 Large Tomatoes
- 2 Large Onions
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 6 Allspice
- 3 Cloves
- 1 Bottle of White Wine
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the Red Snapper carefully.
2. Cut a space six inches square on the surface of the upper side of the fish, and carefully remove the skin within the enclosed space.
3. Lard this space closely with very fine larding needles, and fill in with spice, thyme, clove and bay leaf, all minced very fine.
4. Rub thoroughly inside and outside with salt.
5. Make a good stuffing by taking one dozen oysters, one cup of stale bread crumbs, wet and squeezed of all water; one large onion, chopped fine; a half tablespoonful of salt and black pepper to taste.
6. Mix well and fry in a pan with a tablespoonful of butter.
7. Stuff the body of the fish and sew up with soft thread.
8. Lard well and, after rubbing thoroughly with the lard, place in the oven.
9. Pour over immediately a bottle of white wine and let the fish bake well in the wine.
10. Prepare the following sauce: Take one large tablespoonful of butter, one large chopped onion, one sprig of thyme, one of bay leaf.
11. Brown the onions and butter, being careful not to burn, and put in three large tomatoes.
12. Add the chopped herbs, brown and add a pint of oyster water, which has been heated with blanching the oysters.
13. Season the sauce with pepper and salt to taste.
14. Put the fish in the dish in which it is to be served, and garnish with the oysters, placing them over the fish and mingling between.

title: Red Snapper au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine 3-Pound Red Snapper
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Large Onion
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 3/4 Tea Cup of Water
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Select a fine, large fish, clean and wash thoroughly.
2. Make a dressing by taking one cup of stale bread, wet and squeezed of all water; one large onion and three sprigs of parsley.
3. Chop the onions and parsley fine and mix with the bread crumbs and fry in a spoonful of butter, seasoning well with salt and pepper.
4. Stuff the fish and sew up with a soft thread.
5. Rub the fish thoroughly with salt and pepper and butter.
6. Put small pieces of butter all over the fish and add a few pieces on the bottom of the baking pan.
7. Pour in water to the depth of two inches, cover the pan and bake on the outside of the oven, about an hour and a half.

title: Red Snapper With Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Red Snapper, 3 Pounds in Weight
- 12 Large Tomatoes or a Can
- 3/4 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Large Onions
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 6 Allspice
- 3 Cloves
- 1/4 Inch of Garlic, or 1 Clove
- 1 Cup of Stale Bread Crumbs
- 1 Bottle of White Wine
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter, or Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Red Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish, following the directions given in the recipe for Red Snapper a la Creole to the point of larding thoroughly
2. After larding pour over immediately the bottle of white wine, and let the fish bake well in the wine
3. If wine is not available, use a cup of warm water instead
4. When the fish is done, put one tablespoonful of olive oil or one large tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and one large chopped onion and let brown
5. Add one tablespoonful of flour, and let the same brown
6. Take the sliced and chopped fresh tomatoes, or strain a can of tomatoes in lieu of the former, and add to the sauce
7. Add immediately a small glass of the best white wine, and a half can of mushrooms chopped fine
8. Place in the sauce the bouquet of sweet herbs, thyme, bay leaf, and add an inch of garlic, minced very fine
9. Season with red pepper only
10. Pour this sauce over the baked fish after placing it in the dish in which it is to be served
11. Set in the oven a few minutes and bring to the table hot

title: Reddie Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of powdered cinnamon
- 3 well beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour
2. Mix in the sugar and cinnamon
3. Add the eggs and mix all into a dough
4. Roll it out
5. Cut it into round cakes
6. Bake in a quick oven

title: Reed Bird Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Reed Birds
- 1 Salpicon Royal
- 1/2 Pound of Foundation Paste
- Bacon
- Madeira Sauce

## Directions

1. Pick and clean the birds and season well; then bind each with a thin strip of bacon
2. Make a Foundation Paste (Pâté-à-Foncer) and line six patty molds with this
3. Put in a moderate oven and bake for fifteen minutes; then empty them and let them dry well in the open oven for five minutes
4. Let them cool
5. Fill the bottom of each mold with a tablespoonful of Salpicon Royal, and place in each mold two of the nicely prepared birds
6. Lay the patties on a baking dish or roasting pan and set in the oven, whose heat must be moderate, and let them roast for fifteen or twenty minutes
7. Remove from the oven, moisten each patty with a tablespoonful of hot Madeira Sauce, and send to the table hot

title: Remoulade Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Raw Yolk of Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic, Minced Very Fine
- 3/4 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs till hard.
2. Remove the shells and set aside the whites, which you will have crumbled fine for a garnish.
3. Put the yolks into a bowl, and mash very fine, till perfectly smooth.
4. Add the mustard, and mix well, and the seasonings of vinegar and salt and Cayenne to taste.
5. Then add the olive oil, drop by drop, working in the egg all the time, and then add the yolk of the raw egg, and work in thoroughly, till light.
6. Then add the juice of half a lemon.
