7. Mix well, increasing the quantity of oil or vinegar, according to taste, very slightly.
8. It is now ready to be served with cold meats, fish or salads.

title: Rice Blanc Mange
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacupful of the best rice
- 1 pint of cold water
- 1 pint of new milk
- 1 tablespoonful of white sugar

## Directions

1. Put a teacupful of the best rice into 1 pint of cold water
2. Let it stand until the rice cracks, and looks perfectly white
3. Add 1 pint of new milk, and 1 tablespoonful of white sugar
4. Stir it, and let it gently simmer until all the milk is absorbed, stirring frequently
5. Pour it into a mould to cool
6. The rice may be flavored with vanilla, and eaten with cream

title: Rice Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint rice
- 1 pint flour
- 2 spoonfuls butter
- yolks of 3 eggs
- whites of 2 eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Make up 1 pint of rice, 1 pint of flour, 2 spoonfuls of butter, yolks of 3 eggs, and whites of 2 eggs with milk, not too stiff

title: Rice Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of rice
- 1 quart of water
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- 2 of yeast
- milk or water

## Directions

1. Boil rice in a quart of water till dry and soft
2. Put it into 2 lbs. of flour
3. Mix it well, add 2 teaspoonfuls of salt, 2 of yeast, and enough milk or water to make it a proper consistency
4. Bake it in moulds, when well risen

title: Rice Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cold Boiled Rice
- 2 Cups of White Indian Meal
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Melted Butter
- 2 Heaping Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1½ Pints Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites of the eggs together until very light, and then pour in the milk, mixing gradually.
2. Add the well-prepared meal, into which you will have mixed the salt and baking powder.
3. Beat well.
4. Then add the melted butter and the rice, which you will have pressed through a sieve.
5. Mix all thoroughly and beat till very light.
6. Then grease the bottom of a shallow pan and turn the mixture in and bake half an hour in a hot oven.
7. Serve hot, buttering the slices freely.

title: Rice Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of rice that has been boiled soft
- a teacup of flour
- two eggs well beaten
- a pinch of salt
- enough milk
- a tablespoonful of melted butter or lard

## Directions

1. Take a pint of rice that has been boiled soft
2. add to it a teacup of flour, two eggs well beaten, a pinch of salt, and enough milk to make a nice thick batter
3. throw into the batter a tablespoonful of melted butter or lard
4. bake on a hot griddle

title: Rice Cream Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of boiled rice
- 3/4 cup of sugar
- 1 ounce of pineapple
- 2 ounces of raisins
- 2 ounces of currants
- 1 ounce of grated orange peel
- 1 quart of milk
- the yolks of 6 eggs
- 3/4 of a grated nutmeg
- the whites of two eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the rice well, and then drain through a colander and set to boil with the milk.
2. When it has cooked for twenty minutes, add all the fruits, being careful to have the pineapple cut into dice, and the raisins seeded, and the currants picked, washed and dried.
3. Add the orange peel and grated nutmeg, and finally the eggs, which have been well beaten in the sugar till very light.
4. Let all simmer for just one minute.
5. Take off the stove, place in a baking pan, and set to brown nicely in a quick oven for about twenty-five minutes.
6. When well browned, have ready a meringue, which you will have made by beating the whites of two eggs to a froth, and add to this two tablespoonfuls of powdered white sugar.
7. Spread all this over the pudding.
8. Let it brown slightly in a hot oven.
9. Serve either hot or very cold.

title: Rice Croquettes With Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ Cup of Rice
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Large Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- ½ Cup of Currants
- ½ Cup of Raisins
- ¾ Cup of Citron
- ¾ Teaspoonful of Vanilla Essence
- The Yolks of Two Eggs

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a farina boiler, and add the rice which you will have washed well.
2. Boil until very thick.
3. Seed the raisins and prepare the other fruits.
4. Beat the eggs well and add them to the rice, and then stir in the sugar.
5. Beat until very smooth.
6. Then take from the fire, and add the essence of vanilla, the raisins and currants, and the citron.
7. Turn out all into a dish to cool.
8. Then form into pretty pyramids, and dip first in a well-beaten egg, and then in bread crumbs.
9. Fry in boiling lard.
10. Drain well.
11. When about to serve, put a small piece of Currant Jelly on the top of each croquette.
12. Then dust the whole with powdered sugar, and serve with Sauce a la Vanille.

title: Rice Croquettes With Parsley
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- The Yolks of 4 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well, and put it on to boil in a farina boiler with the milk, or use cold boiled rice, and set it to boil with the milk, after pressing through a sieve.
2. If the rice has not been cooked, let it boil about an hour. If it has already been cooked, twenty minutes will suffice.
3. When very thick take from the fire and beat until very smooth, mashing all the grains.
4. Then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and cook for about eight or ten minutes longer.
5. Add the parsley and seasoning, using the white pepper.
6. Take from the fire and mix well, and turn out on a plate, and let it cool.
7. When cool, form it into pretty cylinders of about three inches in length and one and a half in thickness.
8. Roll these in a beaten egg, to bind, and then in bread crumbs, and fry in boiling lard.
9. Drain and serve with any daube, or with any meat cooked with gravy.

title: Rice Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Rice
- ¾ Cup Sugar
- ¾ Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- The Peel of a Quarter of an Orange, Cut In Dice

## Directions

1. Boil the rice very soft, and then add the milk, and let it come to a good boil.
2. Add the orange peel, cut in quarter dice.
3. Beat the eggs and sugar well together till very light, and add to the boiling custard.
4. Cook for one minute longer.
5. Then take from the fire, and add the vanilla and one-quarter of the grated nutmeg.
6. Place all in a dish to cool.
7. Sprinkle the top with grated nutmeg, and serve cold.
8. If you wish to have a baked custard, place the custard in a pan or in cups, set in the oven to brown, and serve hot with a Cream Sauce.

title: Rice Dumpling
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Rice
- 1 Egg
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and take out the cores.
2. Fill the interiors with sugar and cinnamon.
3. Beat the egg and the rice together, add the flour, and knead to a dough.
4. Cover the apples with a thick coating of rice, and then tie each in a dumpling cloth, and put them in a pot of boiling water.
5. Let them boil for three-quarters of an hour.
6. When done, turn out carefully on a dish, and serve with a Hard or Cream Sauce.
7. Or the dumplings may be baked in the oven.

title: Rice Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound of Mashed, Cold, Boiled Rice
- ½ Dozen Apples
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Egg
- Sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and take out the cores.
2. Fill the interiors with sugar and cinnamon.
3. Beat the egg and the rice together and add the flour, knead to a dough.
4. Cover the apples with a thick coating of rice.
5. Tie each in a dumpling cloth and put them in a pot of boiling water.
6. Let them boil for three-quarters of an hour.
7. When done, turn out carefully on a dish and serve with a Hard or Cream Sauce.
8. Or the dumplings may be baked in the oven.

title: Rice Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup of flour
- 3 cups of ground rice
- 8 apples, tart and not overripe
- 2 quarts of milk
- sugar and cinnamon
- 1/2 of the peel of an orange
- 3/4 of a grated nutmeg
- butter

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and take out the cores, leaving the apples whole.
2. Take the ground cinnamon and sugar, and mix well, and fill the cores with this mixture.
3. Boil the rice in milk till it comes to the consistency of flour, having added the grated peel of an orange and a half teaspoonful of grated nutmeg and a half cup of flour.
4. Take off the fire, and let it cool.
5. Cover each apple all over with a very thick coating of the rice, and tie each dumpling in a cloth very tightly, and put them in a pot of cold water.
6. Bring the water to a quick boil, and boil the apples for three-quarters of an hour.
7. When done, untie the cloth and place the dumplings carefully on a large dish.
8. Sprinkle each with a little grated nutmeg, put on top of each a dot of butter, set in the oven for five or ten minutes to brown, and serve with a Hard or Cream Sauce.

title: Rice Flour Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Milk
- ½ Split Vanilla Stick
- 6 Ounces of Ground Rice
- ¼ Pound of Sugar
- 2 Ounces Butter
- Yolks of 8 Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Pineapple
- 2 Ounces of Apricots
- 3 Ounces of Cherries
- 1 Ounce of Orange Peel
- A Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of milk, and add to it while boiling the split vanilla stick.
2. Cut all the fruit and orange peel into small dice, throwing away the seeds.
3. Drop the ground rice like a shower of rain into the boiling milk, stirring it continuously with a whisk of the hand.
4. When it begins to soften, set it back, and let it cook for fifteen minutes longer.
5. Then mix in the cut fruits, and add the butter, which you will have melted.
6. Add the eggs, which have been beaten very light in the sugar.
7. Mix thoroughly, and add the orange peel cut into quarter dice.
8. When the mixture is very light, set it to cool by spreading it out on a baking sheet, covered with white paper.
9. When cold, divide it into small balls, roll these in powdered Macaroons, dip in beaten egg, and then roll in white bread crumbs.
10. Fry in boiling lard.
11. Drain in a heated colander or on a piece of brown paper.
12. Then dress the croquettes nicely on a dish, sprinkle with vanilla sugar, and serve each separately with a Cream Sauce.

title: Rice Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cold Rice
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Orange or Lemon Essence
- A Pinch of Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take one cup of cold rice, mash it well, and then pass through a sieve.
2. Season with the orange essence or lemon, a pinch of cinnamon, and a tablespoonful of butter.
3. Add the beaten yolks of two eggs.
4. Then make the rice into small boulettes or balls, roll in a beaten egg, fry to a golden brown, sprinkle with white sugar and serve.

title: Rice Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- Left-over rice
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of yeast powder
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Take the left-over rice and mash very fine
2. Take three eggs, a half cup of flour, one teaspoonful of yeast powder, and sugar to taste, and beat all into a light, thick batter
3. Cook by dropping a spoonful at a time into boiling lard

title: Rice Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1⅔ Cups of Cold Boiled Rice
- 1½ Cups of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Heaping Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Large Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and set it to cool.
2. Press the rice through a sieve, and then add the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, then the salt, yeast powder and flour, blended, and beat well.
3. Then add the milk, blending thoroughly, and finally the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Mix thoroughly and bake on a hot griddle.

title: Rice Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Boiled Rice
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Milk or Water
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 1/4 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sugar

## Directions

1. Mash the rice through a sieve.
2. Sift the flour, baking powder and salt together, and add the sugar to the milk.
3. Add this to the flour and mix in the rice till free from any lumps.
4. Then add the eggs, the whites and yolks beaten separately, and mix into a very smooth batter.
5. Have the griddle well-heated.
6. Bake the cakes to a nice brown, and serve with Louisiana Syrup.

title: Rice Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ Pints of Milk
- ¾ Pound of Sugar
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 4 Ounces of Boiled Rice
- 1 Gill of Curaçoa
- ¾ Pint of Milk of Almonds
- A Compote of 8 Oranges

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in the milk till very soft. Do not mash.
2. Set the pint and a half extra milk to boil, and add the rice and the milk of almonds and the sugar and eggs, beaten to a cream.
3. Make a custard. Take from the fire, and add the Curaçoa, and mix well.
4. Set to freeze as above directed, and when cold turn out of the mold, place in a dish and garnish around with the oranges, pour the syrup over all, and serve.

title: Rice Meringue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- 6 Creole eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 pint milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- the grated rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the rice thoroughly, and boil it in a quart of boiling water.
2. When very soft, drain the rice of all water by pressing through a colander, and add it to the milk.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar together till very light and add the butter.
4. Add the juice and the grated rind of a lemon, and mix thoroughly.
5. Place the whole mixture into a baking dish, and bake for half an hour in a quick oven.
6. Beat the whites to a stiff froth, and add gradually six tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar (white), beating them well all the time.
7. Continue beating till the whites are stiff enough to stand alone.
8. Pour this over the top of the rice, and set it back in the oven a few minutes to brown.
9. It may be served either hot or cold.

title: Rice Meringue Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of rice
- one quart of milk
- sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- lemon rind or essence
- nutmeg
- whites of four eggs
- one cup of white sifted sugar
- lemon, rose or vanilla for seasoning

## Directions

1. Boil half a cup of rice in a quart of milk until it is thoroughly done.
2. Sweeten to taste, and let it cool.
3. Beat in the yolks of four eggs.
4. Flavor with lemon rind or essence and nutmeg.
5. Bake in a pudding-dish.
6. When cool, pour over it the whites of your eggs, beaten with a cup of white sifted sugar.
7. Bake light brown.
8. Season to taste with lemon, rose or vanilla.

title: Rice Milk
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- flour of rice
- rose water

## Directions

1. Thicken milk with the flour of rice, and season with rose water
2. Put into cups

title: Rice Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Boiled Rice
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 1 1/2 Pints of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Mash the rice through a close sieve and beat the yolks very light, and add.
2. Add the milk and butter, and then sift in the flour and half a teaspoonful of baking powder.
3. Add the sugar and the whites of the eggs.

title: Rice Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Boiled Rice
- 1 Cup of Flour
- 3 Eggs
- 1½ Pints of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- ½ teaspoonful of baking powder

## Directions

1. Mash the rice through a close sieve
2. Beat the yolks very light and add
3. Add the milk and butter
4. Sift in the flour and half a teaspoonful of baking powder
5. Add the sugar and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
6. Add the salt
7. Bake in muffin rings in a quick oven for about fifteen or twenty minutes

title: Rice Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces of butter
- 1 lb. of ground rice
- water
- flour

## Directions

1. Rub butter well into ground rice
2. Moisten it with water
3. Roll it out with a little flour

title: Rice Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of rice
- a quart of milk
- sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- lemon rind or essence
- nutmeg
- whites of four eggs
- a cup of white sifted sugar
- lemon, rose or vanilla for seasoning

## Directions

1. Boil half a cup of rice in a quart of milk until it is thoroughly done
2. Sweeten to taste, and let it cool
3. Beat in the yolks of four eggs
4. Flavor with lemon rind or essence and nutmeg
5. Bake in a pudding-dish
6. When cool, pour over it the whites of your eggs, beaten with a cup of white sifted sugar
7. Bake light brown
8. Season to taste with lemon, rose or vanilla

title: Rice Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of soft boiled rice
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 quart of milk
- 8 eggs
- few sticks of cinnamon
- sugar to the taste

## Directions

1. scald the milk
2. add a few sticks of cinnamon, and while warm, stir into it the rice, butter, and eggs, which must be first beaten
3. sweeten to the taste, and bake in a dish

title: Rice Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of rice flour
- 1/4 pound of sugar
- 1/2 pint cream
- 6 fresh Creole eggs
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- 1/2 teaspoontul salt
- the grated peel of half an orange
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Boil the rice well, and when very soft add the half pint of cream, and let it come to a boil.
2. Beat the butter and sugar and the yolks of the eggs together until very light.
3. Add the rice, which has been boiled in the milk.
4. Set on the fire, and add the grated peel of a half orange, and stir continually till it thickens.
5. Add the salt and stir well.
6. Take from the fire, and add the vanilla.
7. Have the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff thick froth.
8. Pour this over the rice.
9. Set in the oven a few minutes to brown, and serve immediately while very hot.

title: Rice Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Half Cup of Rice
- 3 Pints of Broth or Consomme

## Directions

1. Prepare the clear Pot-au-Feu or Consomme
2. When nearly done add one half cupful of rice, which has been thoroughly washed and dried
3. Cook for about twenty-five minutes longer, or until done
4. Serve

title: Rice Soup, Without Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Rice
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Spoon of Butter
- 1 Pint of Milk
- Pepper and Salt
- Cream

## Directions

1. Wash the rice thoroughly, rubbing dry.
2. Put it in a saucepan with one pint of cold water; when swelled, add one pint of boiling water; and when it begins to get very tender, add the remaining pint of boiling water.
3. Add the pepper and salt.
4. Beat up the yolks of the eggs with a few tablespoonfuls of cream.
5. When quite smooth, stir in carefully a few spoonfuls of the boiling rice water, and then pour the eggs and cream into the saucepan, stirring very briskly.
6. Draw aside and stir for two or three minutes, but do not allow the soup to boil when once the eggs are added.

title: Rice Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rice
- 2 eggs
- 1 spoonful butter
- ½ pint milk

## Directions

1. Boil 2 cups of rice till quite soft
2. Make it into a thick batter with 2 eggs, 1 spoonful of butter, ½ pint of milk
3. Beat it till quite light
4. Bake in a waffle iron

title: Rice Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints well boiled rice
- 1 large spoonful butter
- 1 pinch salt
- 2 eggs
- 1 pint milk
- 1 pint sifted flour
- 1 teaspoonful yeast

## Directions

1. Stir into 2 pints of well boiled rice 1 large spoonful of butter and a pinch of salt
2. When cool, add 2 eggs well beaten, 1 pint of milk, 1 pint of sifted flour, and 1 teaspoonful of yeast
3. Let it stand 2 hours before baking

title: Rice Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Cups of Softly Boiled Rice
- 2 Ounces of Butter
- 1 Pint of Scalded Milk
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Wheat Flour

## Directions

1. The rice must be cold and well mashed.
2. Melt the two tablespoonfuls of butter into the milk, which has been allowed to cool.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the whites separately, making the latter come to a stiff froth.
4. Mix the rice and milk.
5. Beat thoroughly, and then add a half teaspoonful of salt and one of baking powder, and the flour.
6. Put the yolks into the batter, first blending well, and lastly add the whites, and beat well again.
7. The waffle iron should be very hot, and well greased in every part.
8. Always have a little brush with which to grease the waffle irons.
9. Pour the batter into a pitcher, so that you may more easily fill the irons.
10. Open the irons, pour the batter from the pitcher and fill the iron quickly.
11. Then close quickly and set on the fire.
12. As soon as the edges are set, turn the iron and bake on the other side.
13. Two minutes should be all the time required to bake a waffle nicely.
14. The waffle must be baked evenly.
15. Always select the simple waffle baker with four compartments in preference to the more complicated one.

title: Rice Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Boiled Rice
- 1½ Pints of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Flour or 2 Cups of Rice Flour
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Mash the rice through a sieve.
2. Beat the yolks light, and add, and then add the salt, the baking powder and the milk.
3. Beat well, and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Now add the rice flour to make a thin batter, and proceed to bake as above.
5. Be careful to grease the irons very well, so that the rice may not adhere.

title: Rice With Compote of Oranges
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 Cup Rice
- 1.5 Pints Milk
- 1 Quart Cream
- 1.5 Cups Sugar
- Yolks of 8 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- The Grated Peel of an Orange
- 12 Sweet Louisiana Oranges
- 1 Pound Sugar
- 1 Gill Water
- 1/4 of a Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the rice clean, and boil according to recipe in about a pint and a quarter of water
2. In half an hour take off and drain all water, and press through a sieve
3. Add it to the milk, and let it boil slowly a half hour longer without burning
4. Whip the cream to a stiff froth, and add the drain to the rice or milk, and set the whipped cream to cool until it is needed
5. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar until they are very light
6. Add them to the boiling rice, stirring constantly and well, and let it cook for two minutes, adding in the meantime the grated peel of the orange
7. Take the mixture from the fire
8. Add the tablespoonful of vanilla, and the grated nutmeg
9. Mix well and set out to cool
10. Remove the dasher from the ice cream freezer, and when the mixture has become very cool, turn it into the freezer and let it set packed in rock salt and ice for three hours
11. Take a dozen sweet Louisiana oranges, and peel and cut them crosswise into halves
12. Take out the cores with the sharp point of a penknife, and set them in a dish ready for use
13. Put a pound of sugar to boil with one gill of water, and after ten minutes add the juice of half a lemon
14. Put a few pieces at a time of the oranges into this boiling liquid, and lay them out side by side in a flat dish
15. Pour over them the syrup that remains from the boiling, and set the dish in the ice box to cool
16. When ready to serve, wipe thoroughly the outside of the can that contains the pudding, and all around the edges, so as to remove any traces of salt
17. Wet a towel in boiling water and stand the can upon it
18. Open the can
19. Put a round dish on top, and then turn quickly upside down, and remove the can
20. If the pudding adheres, repeat the applications of the hot towel at the bottom and around
21. Place the oranges on top and all around the pudding, and pour over them the syrup, which has become cool, but not frozen
22. Serve immediately

title: Rice, As Cooked in Japan
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour on just enough cold water to prevent the rice from burning to the pot
2. Use a pot with a close fitting cover
3. Set it on a moderate fire
4. The rice is steamed rather than boiled until it is nearly done
5. Take off the cover of the pot and allow the surplus steam and water to escape
6. Add the necessary salt before boiling

title: Rice-Milk for Children
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- rice

## Directions

1. To every quart of milk, allow two ounces of rice
2. Wash the rice and put it with the milk in a close-covered stewpan
3. Set it over a slow fire, and let it simmer gently for one hour and a half

title: Rice-Milk for Children
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- two ounces of rice

## Directions

1. Wash the rice and put it with the milk in a close-covered stewpan
2. set it over a slow fire
3. let it simmer gently for one hour and a half
4. It will scorch on a fierce fire

title: Rich Bride's Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of sifted flour
- four pounds of sweet fresh butter
- two pounds of white powdered sugar
- six eggs for every pound of flour
- an ounce of ground mace or nutmeg
- a tablespoonful of lemon extract or orange-flower water
- four pounds of currants
- four pounds of raisins
- two pounds of citron
- one pound of blanched almonds
- half a pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Wash currants through several waters and pick clean from grit
2. Spread currants on a folded cloth to dry
3. Stone and cut raisins in two
4. Cut citron in slips
5. Chop or slice one pound of blanched almonds
6. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar to a smooth paste
7. Beat the butter and flour together
8. Add the yolks and sugar to the butter and flour
9. Add the spice and half a pint of brandy
10. Beat the whites of the eggs to a froth and add them
11. Stir all together for some time
12. Strew half a pound of flour over the fruit and mix it through
13. By degrees stir the fruit into the cake
14. Butter large tin basins and line them with white paper
15. Put in the mixture two inches deep
16. Bake in a moderate oven two hours
17. Prepare the fruit the day before making the cake

title: Rich Lemon Sauce, for Puddings
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh lemon
- one teacupful of sugar
- one teacupful of butter
- one large teaspoonful of wheat flour
- half a pint of boiling water
- one half nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil a fresh lemon in plenty of water, until a straw will penetrate it
2. Cut it in slices, and each slice in quarters
3. Add a teacupful of sugar, and the same of butter, with a large teaspoonful of wheat flour worked into it
4. Put all together into a stew-pan, and stir in gradually half a pint of boiling water
5. Keep it over the fire for ten minutes, stirring it all the time
6. Serve with half a nutmeg grated over

title: Rich Plum Pudding Without Flour
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound and a half of grated bread
- One pound and a half of raisins
- One pound and a half of currants
- One pound of beef-suet
- Peel of one large lemon
- Three ounces of almonds
- A little nutmeg or mixed spice
- Sugar to taste
- Three quarters of a pound of candied orange, lemon and citron
- Eight or nine eggs
- Half a pint of milk
- Two wineglassfuls of brandy

## Directions

1. Stone the raisins
2. Wash and pick the currants
3. Chop the suet very fine
4. Mix with them a pound and a half of grated bread
5. Add the candied peel cut into shreds
6. Add the almonds blanched and minced
7. Add the mixed spice and sugar to taste
8. When all are thoroughly blended, stir it well together with eight or nine well-beaten eggs, two glassfuls of brandy, and half a pint of milk
9. Tie it in a cloth
10. Boil it for five hours or five hours and a half, or divide it into equal parts, and boil it in moulds or basins for half the time

title: Rich Soda Biscuits with Cream of Tartar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of flour
- two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- a tablespoonful of lard or butter
- a tablespoonful of soda
- a cup of water
- milk, or milk and water
- a little salt

## Directions

1. To each quart of flour add two teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar sifted through it
2. Put in a tablespoonful of lard or butter
3. Dissolve a tablespoonful of soda in a cup of water and pour it on the flour
4. Mix with milk, or milk and water, to a soft dough
5. Add a little salt
6. Roll out on the floured biscuit board
7. Cut with the biscuit cutter and bake quickly

title: Rich Syrup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cold water (1 pint)
- white sugar (2 lbs.)
- white of egg (1/2 of one)
- cold water (1 teaspoonful)

## Directions

1. One pint of cold water to 2 lbs. of white sugar
2. When it is dissolved, set it over a moderate fire
3. Beat 1/2 the white of an egg, and put it to the sugar before it gets warm
4. Watch it, and when it is ready to run over, put in 1 teaspoonful of cold water
5. After the boiling ceases, put it on the fire again
6. Do this 3 times, and the last time take it off and let it settle
7. Take off the scum, and strain the syrup through a jelly bag

title: Rich Wedding Cake, Or Black Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- nine eggs
- one pound of butter
- one pound of brown sugar
- one teacupful of molasses
- one ounce of grated nutmeg or ground mace
- one teaspoonful of ground allspice
- one teaspoonful of cinnamon
- a gill of brandy
- three pounds of currants
- three pounds of raisins
- half a pound of flour
- one pound of citron

## Directions

1. Beat one pound of flour with nine eggs, the whites and yolks beaten separately, one pound of butter beaten to a cream, one pound of brown sugar, one teacupful of molasses, one ounce of grated nutmeg or ground mace, one teaspoonful of ground allspice, one teaspoonful of cinnamon, and a gill of brandy
2. Pick, wash, and dry three pounds of currants
3. stone and cut three pounds of raisins
4. strew half a pound of flour over them, mix it well through
5. stir them with one pound of citron, cut in slips, into the cake
6. Line tin pans with buttered paper
7. put the mixture in, an inch and a half or two inches deep
8. bake in a

title: Rich Wedding Cake, or Black Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- nine eggs, the whites and yolks beaten separately
- one pound of butter
- one pound of brown sugar
- one teacupful of molasses
- one ounce of grated nutmeg or ground mace
- one teaspoonful of ground allspice
- one teaspoonful of cinnamon
- a gill of brandy
- three pounds of currants
- three pounds of raisins
- half a pound of flour
- a pound of citron

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks of eggs separately
2. Beat butter to a cream
3. Beat the mixture of butter, sugar, molasses, and spices well
4. Pick, wash, and dry three pounds of currants
5. Stone and cut three pounds of raisins
6. Strew half a pound of flour over currants and raisins and mix well
7. Stir the currants and raisins with citron cut in slips into the cake mixture
8. Line tin pans with buttered paper
9. Put the mixture in an inch and a half or two inches deep
10. Bake in a moderate oven an hour and a half or two hours
11. Ice according to directions

title: Richmond Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 1/2 pints of new milk
- 2 large spoonfuls of butter
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 3 spoonfuls of good yeast

## Directions

1. Sift 2 lbs. of flour
2. Warm 1 1/2 pints of new milk and 2 large spoonfuls of butter
3. Stir into the flour
4. Beat 3 eggs very lightly, a little salt
5. Beat all well together till thoroughly mixed
6. Then add 3 spoonfuls of good yeast
7. Set it to rise
8. When risen, bake in muffin rings in an oven

title: Ring Jumbles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- four eggs
- one and a quarter pounds of flour, or enough to make a soft dough

## Directions

1. Line a pan with buttered paper, form the dough into rings
2. Bake quickly and sift sugar over them

title: Risen Corn Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cornmeal
- 1 pint of Milk or Water
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Yeast

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well, and use boiling water or milk to blend the cornmeal, eggs and salt together.
2. Then add the yeast, which you will have dissolved in a little hot water.
3. Set the bread to rise for three or four hours and then bake in tins or in a greased pan, like a pone of bread, or make it into loaves.

title: Risen Corn Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of scalding milk
- 1 quart of sifted cornmeal
- a spoonful of good lard
- 3 well beaten eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of yeast

## Directions

1. Pour the scalding milk on the sifted cornmeal
2. When cool, stir in the lard, eggs, and salt
3. Stir this with 2 tablespoonfuls of yeast
4. Beat all together and set it to rise
5. When risen, bake in tins

title: Risen Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pint of milk
- ½ pint of water
- 1 egg
- 1 teacupful of yeast
- 2 large lumps of loaf sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix the milk, water, egg, yeast and sugar
2. Make the milk lukewarm
3. Mix with flour to the consistency of pound cake
4. Rise over night

title: Risen Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1½ Quarts of Milk
- ⅜ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 3 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, add the butter, and let it grow tepid.
2. Sift the flour and salt together, and add the milk and butter, and finally the yeast.
3. Beat all continuously for five or ten minutes, and then cover well and set in a warm place for about two hours.
4. Beat the whites and the yolks of the eggs separately, and add first the yolks to the batter, after it has risen well, and then add the whites.
5. Beat in thoroughly, and set to rise again for about a quarter of an hour.
6. Then beat lightly and pour into a pitcher.
7. Prepare the waffle irons as indicated above, and proceed to bake in the same manner.

title: Rissoles
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Cold Roast Beef or Veal, or Left-Over Meat of any Kind
- 2 Ounces of Stale Bread, Wet, and Squeezed Thoroughly
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Minced Parsley
- 1 1/2 Ounce of Flour
- 1 1/4 Tablespoonfuls of Milk or Water
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Each of Salt and Black Pepper
- A Dash of Cayenne
- 1 Bay Leaf, Chopped Fine, With Sprigs of Parsley and Thyme

## Directions

1. Mince the meat finely and season well.
2. Mix the ingredients thoroughly with it, adding, if you have it, minced Chaurice or sausage meat, or a little cold ham minced.
3. Form it into balls, using two tablespoonfuls for each ball.
4. Brush lightly with milk, toss in a little flour, pat to get off all superfluous flour, and brown nicely in boiling lard.
5. Drain off all lard and serve on a platter, garnished with parsley sprigs.

title: Riz Au Lait
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coffee cupful of rice
- 1 quart of new milk
- a large spoonful of butter
- a little salt
- grated nutmeg
- a little ground cinnamon
- 1 pint more of new milk
- 1 lb. of raisins

## Directions

1. Wash a coffee cupful of rice well
2. Put it into 1 quart of new milk over night to soak
3. In the morning add a large spoonful of butter, a little salt, grated nutmeg, and a little ground cinnamon, and 1 pint more of new milk
4. Put it into the oven, and when it is warm, stir the pudding without removing it from the oven
5. Stone 1 lb. of raisins, and add at this time
6. Let it cook until the rice is perfectly done

title: Riz de Veau
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- onion
- thyme
- parsley
- cloves
- allspice
- lemon
- eggs

## Directions

1. Fry slightly, putting in a little flour before it becomes too brown, as the sauce must be a pale yellow
2. Add water to the sauce
3. Put in a little onion, thyme and parsley, chopped very fine
4. Add 2 or 3 cloves and allspice
5. Let the whole simmer till well done
6. Add the juice of a lemon, and a little of the peel
7. Stir in the yolks of 2 eggs well beaten

title: Roast Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- First four ribs of beef
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lard

## Directions

1. Have the butcher skewer the roast so that it will have a nice shape when it comes on the table and will retain all the juice of the beef.
2. Leave the bones in the roast, as the meat will be far sweeter than when taken out.
3. Rub the meat well with salt and pepper, dredge slightly with lard and set in a hot oven.
4. Every now and then baste the beef with its own juices and let it cook, adding no water, as sufficient fat runs from the beef to baste with.
5. Allow fifteen minutes to every pound of meat if one likes the meat rare, otherwise allow twenty minutes.
6. Occasionally stick a needle into the beef. If the blood spurts up, the meat is ready to serve.
7. Serve on a dish in its own gravy.

title: Roast Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 3/4 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for roasting a turkey
2. The chicken must bake in a quick oven, allowing fifteen minutes to every pound
3. A roasted chicken may be stuffed or not, according to taste, with a stuffing of oysters, eggs or truffles in exactly the same manner as turkey

title: Roast Chickens
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- bread and butter stuffing
- butter
- fat bacon
- sweet herbs
- flour
- hot water
- parsley
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Draw them and stuff with rich bread and butter stuffing
2. Baste them with butter and a little fat bacon, seasoned with sweet herbs
3. Brown nicely
4. Make gravy by sifting in a tablespoonful of flour and a cup of hot water
5. Add a little chopped parsley
6. Serve with hard-boiled eggs on the dish with the chickens

title: Roast Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- bread
- butter
- onion
- sage
- pepper
- salt
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Well pluck the ducks without tearing the skin, removing all the plugs
2. Clean the inside thoroughly with a little warm water
3. Stuff them with bread and butter, onion, sage, pepper and salt
4. Roast them before a brisk fire, but not too close
5. Baste very frequently
6. They will take from 1/2 hour to 1 hour
7. When the breast plumps, they will be done
8. Serve them with a rich brown gravy

title: Roast Fowl
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- butter
- black pepper
- bread for bread sauce or parsley and butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fowl thoroughly
2. Roast it 20 minutes, unless a very fine one; a fine fowl will take 3 quarters of an hour
3. Place a small lump of butter, well covered with black pepper, within the fowl previous to roasting
4. Serve with bread sauce, or parsley and butter; egg sauce is sometimes sent to table with it

title: Roast Fowls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- butter
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- flour
- salt
- sausage meat, truffles, or chestnuts (optional)

## Directions

1. Truss the fowls nicely
2. Make a stuffing of butter and some pepper
3. Dry up the butter with a few bread crumbs
4. Baste it well
5. Add flour and salt before you take it from the fire
6. Optionally stuff the fowl with good sausage meat, truffles, or chestnuts

title: Roast Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Goose
- 1 Onions
- 1 Cup of Mashed Potatoes
- 1/2 Teaspoonfuls of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 4 Apples
- 1 1/2 Tablespoonfuls of Lard
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Pick the goose by hand picking, never scald it
2. Singe and clean
3. Season well
4. For the dressing: take one cup of mashed potatoes, four apples peeled nicely and cored, and four onions, one-half teaspoonful of sage powdered well, one-half teaspoonful of thyme, and pepper and salt to taste
5. Place the apples and onions and herbs in a saucepan and add water sufficiently to cover nicely
6. Let all cook together till soft
7. Mash well and rub through a sieve
8. Add the cup of mashed potatoes and mix well, seasoning with salt and pepper
9. Stuff the body and craw, sew up and truss the goose
10. Put into the roasting pan, rubbing a half tablespoonful of lard over it and pouring over a half cup of water, boiling
11. Baste the goose very frequently, say every ten minutes
12. Roast allowing twenty-five minutes to every pound, generally requiring at least an hour and a half
13. If a Green Goose, cover from the beginning with a piece of buttered paper
14. Serve with Apple Sauce

title: Roast Goose, with Sage and Onion
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fine fat goose
- four onions
- sage leaves dried and powdered
- cupful of stale white bread crumbs
- teaspoon of black pepper
- little cayenne
- teaspoon of salt
- cup of milk or beef water
- butter
- apple sauce

## Directions

1. Draw a fine fat goose
2. Peel four onions and boil them ten minutes in plenty of water to take from them the strong taste
3. When the onions have boiled take them from the fire, chop fine, and add to them a large spoonful of sage leaves dried and powdered
4. Add a cupful of stale white bread crumbs, a teaspoon of black pepper, a little cayenne, and a teaspoon of salt
5. Mix all together with a cup of milk or beef water, and stuff the goose with it
6. Put it in the oven and brown it nicely
7. Baste often with butter
8. When done dish it with its own rich brown gravy, and send to table with a boat of apple sauce

title: Roast Haunch of Venison
body:
## Ingredients

- A Haunch or Saddle of Venison
- Melted Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Watercress to Garnish
- Currant Jelly

## Directions

1. Prepare the haunch or saddle of Venison in the same manner as you would the roast beef.
2. Pour a cup of water over the venison when putting in the oven, for it is a dry meat, and requires a little moistening if roasted.
3. Bake in a quick oven, allowing ten minutes to the pound.
4. To prevent the hoof and hair just above changing color in cooking always bind this with a coarse piece of muslin, in four or five pieces of thickness, covering the hoof and hair.
5. Wet with cold water, and bind a buttered paper tightly around and over it.
6. Baste every ten minutes, with melted butter first, and then with the drippings of the Venison.
7. When half cooked, turn the venison over, so that the other side may cook.
8. Unbind the hoofs and garnish them with quilled paper.
9. Place the venison on a dish garnished with Watercress.
10. Serve with Currant Jelly.

title: Roast Lamb à la Béarnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- The Hind Quarter of a Lamb
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 6 Minced Shallots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Parsley and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Select a fine, white hind-quarter of the lamb; lard it in the same manner as given for larding 'Roast Filet of Beef.' (See recipe.)
2. Rub well with butter on top and sprinkle over thickly with the soft of bread crumbs, minced parsley, thyme, bay leaf, salt and pepper and minced shallots.
3. Set in the stove and cover with a buttered brown paper.
4. Let it roast; allowing eighteen or twenty minutes to the pound, in a quick oven, and when done, take off the paper.
5. Sprinkle again lightly with grated bread crumbs; let it brown and set in a hot dish.
6. Sprinkle over with lemon juice; garnish the dish with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon, and serve, carving in slices and placing a quarter of a lemon on each plate.

title: Roast Lamb, Mint Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- The Hind Quarter of a Lamb
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Parsley to Garnish
- Mint Sauce

## Directions

1. Select a fine, fresh, white hind-quarter of lamb.
2. Roast in exactly the same manner as indicated in 'Roast Leg of Mutton' (see recipe), only allow about twenty minutes to the pound in cooking.
3. Serve with garnish of parsley and a Mint Sauce. (See recipe.)
4. Roast Lamb is always served with fresh, young green peas and asparagus tips.

title: Roast Leg of Mutton
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine, fat leg of mutton
- fat bacon
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Select a fine, fat leg, cut holes in it, and lard it with fat bacon
2. Season with parsley, pepper, and salt and put it to bake in a slow oven
3. Roast it for two hours, and serve with tomato sauce

title: Roast Leg of Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 leg of mutton
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Select a fine, tender leg of mutton
2. Wipe thoroughly with a damp towel and dredge with salt and pepper, thoroughly rubbing, so that the meat may be penetrated by the seasoning
3. Place the mutton in a baking pan, set in a quick oven and bake, basting every ten minutes or so, allowing twelve minutes to every pound
4. Press with the fingers or stick with a fork; the juice will spurt out, and it is then ready to serve
5. Decorate the bone with a quilling of white paper, and serve in its own sauce
6. Place the dish on which mutton is placed on a very hot plate

title: Roast Loin of Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- a filet of mutton
- salt and pepper to taste
- garnish of green peas

## Directions

1. The filet of mutton is a square cut from the loin
2. Proceed to dredge with salt and pepper, and roast in exactly the same manner as leg of mutton
3. Serve with a garnish of green peas piled around

title: Roast Loin of Pork
body:
## Ingredients

- A Loin of Pork
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Parsley to Garnish
- Apple Sauce

## Directions

1. Score the loin in close lines across and down, about a half inch apart
2. Dredge well with salt and pepper and place in the oven, letting it cook slowly and long
3. Allow at least twenty-five minutes to every pound
4. Baste every five minutes for the first half hour and every ten minutes thereafter
5. When cooked thoroughly, take out of the baking pan and put in a hot serving dish
6. Garnish nicely with parsley
7. Serve with Apple Sauce and a little horseradish

title: Roast Loin of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of veal
- 1 tablespoonful of butter or lard
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon to garnish

## Directions

1. Trim and cut the veal nicely of the heavier portion of the fat, leaving enough, however, to render it sweet and juicy.
2. If freshly cut, and not handled too much by the butcher, merely wipe the loin with a damp towel.
3. Dredge it thickly with salt and pepper, and separate the articulations or joints, that the meat may cook thoroughly.
4. Rub well with a tablespoonful of butter or lard, and place in a very quick oven for about fifteen minutes.
5. Raise the damper of the stove and cover the veal with a piece of brown buttered paper and let it cook slowly, allowing at least twenty minutes to each pound of veal.
6. Keep the oven at a steady, regular heat.
7. About twenty minutes before serving take off the buttered paper and let the roast brown nicely, augmenting the fire a little.
8. Take out, place on a hot dish, garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon.
9. Serve with its own gravy.

title: Roast Pababotte
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Pababottes
- 6 Truffles (If desired)
- 3 Sprigs of Chopped Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf, Minced
- 6 Thin Strips of Bacon
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- 6 Slices of French Toast
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sprigs of Parsley and Thin Slices of Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the Pababottes as indicated in above recipe.
2. Rub with salt and pepper and melted butter.
3. Put a truffle or two into each Pababotte if you can afford it, and put in each bird a little lump of butter about the size of a peanut, a pinch of salt and pepper, and a pinch of chopped thyme and parsley and bay leaf.
4. Bind the birds with the strips of bacon and place in a baking pan with a tablespoonful of butter.
5. Let them bake or roast for thirty minutes or less, according to size.
6. When done, place each bird on a slice of buttered French toast.
7. Pour over a bit of the gravy which you will have made by adding to the birds while cooking.
8. Just four minutes before serving, add a tablespoonful of water, letting it cook for two minutes.
9. Strain this gravy, and let it cook two minutes more.
10. Pour a little over the breast of each bird, so that it will soak down into the toast.
11. Garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon and serve hot.

title: Roast Partridge
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Young Partridges
- 6 Thin Slices of Bacon
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Toasted Bread
- Parsley or Watercress to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean, singe and draw and wipe the birds neatly.
2. Rub each bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter.
3. If it can be afforded, stuff the bird with truffles, placing one or two in each bird.
4. Place within the bird a pinch of salt and pepper, a lump of butter about the size of a peanut, and a pinch of chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf, all minced very fine.
5. Bind the birds with thin strips of bacon, and fasten each strip with a skewer.
6. Put the Partridges in a baking pan in a brisk oven, and add two tablespoonfuls of butter and one of water.
7. Let them bake from twenty-five to thirty minutes, according to size.

title: Roast Partridge on Buttered French Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Salt and pepper
- Melted butter
- Truffles
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Bay leaf
- Thin strips of bacon
- Buttered French toast
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean, singe and draw and wipe the birds neatly
2. Rub each bird well with salt and pepper, and then with melted butter
3. Stuff the bird with truffles, placing one or two in each bird
4. Place within the bird a pinch of salt and pepper, a lump of butter about the size of a peanut, and a pinch of chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf, all minced very fine
5. Bind the birds with thin strips of bacon, and fasten each strip with a skewer
6. Put the Partridges in a baking pan in a brisk oven, and add two tablespoonfuls of butter and one of water
7. Let them bake from twenty-five to thirty minutes, according to size, basting occasionally with their own juice
8. When done, have ready the buttered French toast; place the birds upon the toast
9. Prepare gravy by adding two tablespoonfuls of water to the Partridge juice after removing the birds
10. Let it cook for two minutes, then strain and let it cook two minutes more
11. Pour the gravy upon the breast of the birds, so that it will soak into the toast
12. Garnish the dish nicely with sprigs of parsley or watercress and serve hot

title: Roast Partridge, Bread Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Fine Young Partridges
- Slice of Toast Bread
- 1½ Ounces of Fresh Bread Crumbs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- ½ Cup of Cold Water
- ½ Cup of Cream or Milk
- ⅜ Teaspoonful of Salt
- 6 White Peppers
- Parsley sprigs or watercress

## Directions

1. Prepare the Partridges and roast according to recipe for Roast Partridge
2. Make a bread sauce as follows: Crumble one and a half ounces of fresh bread crumbs and place in a saucepan with a half cup of cold water
3. When the water heats, add a tablespoonful of butter, six whole white peppers and a half teaspoonful of salt
4. Cook for five minutes
5. Add a half cup of rich milk or cream
6. Let the whole cook five minutes more
7. Remove the white peppers
8. Place the Partridges on the toasted bread, and garnish the dish nicely with parsley sprigs or watercress
9. Send to the table hot with the sauce in a separate dish
10. Pour the sauce over the Partridges when serving

title: Roast Pig Stuffed
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pig, Four or Five Weeks Old
- 3 Large Onions
- 2 Cups of Bread Crumbs
- 3 Sprigs of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 2 Ounces of Butter
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Powdered Sage
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Herb Bouquet

## Directions

1. Wash the young pig well, cleaning again, and scraping thoroughly and taking out all remaining hair from the ears and nostrils
2. Wash again thoroughly in cold water, inside and out, shaking the pig vigorously, head downward
3. Turn upwards and pour cold water over it
4. Wipe dry inside and out with a coarse towel
5. Rub well inside with salt and pepper and minced parsley, thyme and bay leaf
6. Wet the bread crumbs and squeeze thoroughly
7. Add the sprigs of minced parsley and hard-boiled eggs and the powdered sage
8. Mix well and season all highly with black pepper and salt using about a teaspoonful of salt and a half teaspoonful of black pepper
9. Place two ounces of butter in a frying pan on the stove, and when it melts, add the minced onions
10. Let them brown, and then add the dressing, stirring well, and letting it fry for five minutes
11. Stuff the pig and sew up the opening
12. Truss the fore legs forward and the hind feet forward, and close under the body
13. Wipe the pig carefully with a damp towel, and place a corn cob in its mouth to keep it open
14. Rub the pig all over the outside with butter, dredging lightly with salt and pepper
15. Place in a moderate oven, and bake steadily for two and a half or three hours, according to size and age
16. Baste frequently, and when half done, rub again with butter until the pan is saturated
17. Continue basting at intervals
18. When done, take out of the oven and place on a hot dish
19. Garnish the dish with parsley
20. Take the corncob out of the mouth and place instead a nice, rosy apple
21. Serve very hot, with Apple Sauce

title: Roast Pigeons
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of fat bacon
- their giblets
- the livers
- the stock the liver was boiled in

## Directions

1. Truss them when plucked and drawn
2. Lay thin slices of fat bacon on their breasts
3. Bake them three-quarters of an hour
4. Make a gravy with their giblets, which should have been boiling for the purpose
5. Chop up the livers, etc., brown them and serve with the pigeons
6. Thin the gravy with the stock the liver was boiled in

title: Roast Saddle of Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- a saddle of mutton
- salt and pepper to taste
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. A saddle of mutton is two loins
2. Proceed to roast in exactly the same manner as for a single leg
3. Serve with Currant Jelly

title: Roast Snipe on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Snipe
- 6 Slices of Buttered French Toast
- 6 Strips of Bacon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Sliced Lemon and Sprigs of Parsley or Watercress to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper
- Truffle (optional)
- Four Spices (pinch)

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds as in the Invariable Rule for Roasting Birds
2. Rub with salt and pepper and melted butter
3. If desired, put a truffle into each bird for stuffing, and in all put a little lump of butter and a little salt and pepper, a pinch of chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf, and a small pinch of the four spices
4. Bind with strips of bacon, and place in a baking pan with a tablespoonful of butter
5. Let them roast thirty minutes or less, according to size
6. Remove and place always on buttered French toast on a hot dish, and cover and set over a pot of boiling water to keep warm and juicy
7. Meanwhile prepare a gravy by adding a tablespoonful of water to the gravy made in cooking the birds
8. Let it cook for two minutes, then strain and let it cook for two minutes more
9. Pour a little upon the breast of each bird so that it will soak down into the toast
10. Garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley or watercress and slices of lemon, and serve hot

title: Roast Spare Ribs
body:
## Ingredients

- Spare Ribs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Garnish of Parsley and Radish
- Butter

## Directions

1. Dredge the spare ribs lightly with salt and pepper, after having washed well and wiped dry with a coarse towel
2. Place them in the baking pan and dredge with butter
3. Place them in the oven and cover with a piece of buttered paper
4. Allow twenty minutes to every pound in cooking
5. About twenty minutes before serving take off the buttered paper, dredge again with melted butter, and let it brown nicely
6. Serve with a garnish of parsley and radishes

title: Roast Turkey
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Turkey
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Dressing According to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey according to the directions given.
2. Make a nice stuffing either of oysters, egg, truffles or chestnuts.
3. Rub the turkey well with salt and pepper inside and out, and then rub the inside with butter, using about a half tablespoonful.
4. Stuff first the space from which you took out the craw and then sew up the slit in the skin, fastening the skin by a piece of thread tied around the neck or folding it over and fastening with a small skewer.
5. Then stuff the body of the turkey.
6. Push the legs under the skin near the rump, cross them and fasten them with a small skewer or tie with a piece of twine.
7. Turn the wings back, under the body of the fowl.
8. Rub the turkey all over with butter or lard, and place in the baking pan that has been greased lightly.
9. Bake the turkey in a quick oven, allowing about fifteen minutes to every pound.
10. Baste every ten minutes or so with its own drippings.
11. When done, remove the twine and the skewer and place on a hot dish, garnished nicely with parsley, and serve.
12. The turkey breast should always be carved in delicate slices.
13. In making the dressing of any kind, always take up the liver and heart, which you have seasoned well and minced very fine, and add to the turkey, dressing and mixing thoroughly.

title: Roast Turkey a la Perigord
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- hen turkey
- three or two pounds of truffles
- two pounds of fat ham or bacon
- turkey liver
- quarter of a pound of veal liver
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped thyme
- garlic
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Choose a fine young hen turkey; make an incision at the back of the neck, and through this take out the entrails.
2. Cut away the vent, and sew up the place with coarse thread; singe off the hairs and scald the legs to get off the black skin.
3. The neck should be cut off close into the back, and the crop left entire.
4. Break the breast bone and take it out.
5. Lay a little salt on the turkey, and cover it up, while you prepare the stuffing.
6. Wash the truffles, peel them and slice them; throw them into water, and scald them.
7. Add fat ham or bacon, also the turkey liver, and a quarter of a pound of veal liver; season this with pepper, salt, nutmeg, chopped thyme, and a clove of garlic.
8. Set the stew-pan, containing all these ingredients, on a slow fire, and let them cook for an hour, stirring them occasionally, with a wooden spoon.
9. Mash them all up and let it get cool.
10. When cool, stuff the turkey full of the truffle dressing, and fill the crop also; sew it up carefully, and tie it with a string, then truss the turkey.
11. If time allows, put it away for the next day.
12. It should then be roasted, keeping it well basted with the liquor the truffles were boiled in, and butter added to it.

title: Roast Turkey With Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Young Hen Turkey
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Proceed in the manner of Roast Turkey With Truffles, substituting mushrooms instead.

title: Roast Turkey With Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Young Hen Turkey
- 1 Pound of Lean Ham, Cut into Dice
- 2 Pounds of Truffles
- ⅛ Nutmeg
- ⅛ of a Teaspoonful of Pepper
- 1 Bay Leaf, Minced Fine

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey for roasting as directed in the above recipe.
2. Put a saucepan on the fire and put in the ham cut into dice.
3. When hot add two pounds of the very best truffles and the grated nutmeg, the pepper and a minced bay leaf.
4. Stir over the fire for about fifteen minutes.
5. Then take off and let cool.
6. When it is cold stuff the place at the neck of the turkey whence you take the craw, and sew up and arrange as indicated in the directions for dressing a turkey.
7. Stuff the body of the turkey with the remainder of the truffles and sew it up and truss it.
8. Set it in the oven and roast according to the above recipe, serving with a Sauce aux Truffles.

title: Roast Veal With Fine Herbs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-Pound Filet of Veal
- 1 Cup of Broth or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Onion
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 2 Sprigs of Sweet Marjoram
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Cloves, Mace and Allspice
- Salt and Cayenne to taste
- Bread crumbs
- 1 Egg

## Directions

1. Have the butcher cut the filet of veal square, wipe with a damp towel and then take one tablespoonful of lard and dredge the roast nicely.
2. Season well with salt and Cayenne; mince the onion, bay leaf, marjoram, parsley and thyme, and mix these with the ground spices; add the juice of a lemon, and pour all over the meat.
3. Place in a quick oven for about fifteen minutes.
4. After this, lessen the heat, place on top of the veal a buttered piece of brown paper, and let it roast slowly, allowing twenty minutes for each pound of veal.
5. When almost done, take off the paper and let the roast brown nicely for twenty minutes longer.
6. Then take the roast out of the gravy and place in a hot dish in the oven.
7. Take the gravy, stir well, mixing all the herbs that have run out of the beef. Add one cup of broth or water and the juice of one lemon, and mix this thoroughly.
8. Grate some bread crumbs, beat well in one egg, and pour this over the veal, let it brown nicely and serve with the sauce.

title: Roast Woodcock on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Woodcock
- Slices of Buttered Toast
- 6 Strips of Bacon
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper
- Truffle for stuffing
- Four spices
- Sliced Lemon and Sprigs of Parsley or Watercress to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the birds as in the Invariable Rule for Roasting Birds.
2. Rub with salt and pepper and melted butter.
3. If you can afford it, put a truffle in each bird for stuffing.
4. In all put a little lump of butter and a little salt and pepper, a pinch of chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf, and a small pinch of the four spices.
5. Truss neatly and bind with strips of bacon.
6. Place in a baking pan with a tablespoonful of butter.
7. Let them bake or roast thirty minutes or less, according to size.
8. Remove from the oven and place on buttered French toast on a hot dish, cover and set over a pot of boiling water to keep warm.
9. Prepare gravy by adding a tablespoonful of water to the gravy made when cooking the birds.
10. Let it cook for two minutes, then strain.
11. Let it cook for two minutes more and pour upon the breast of the bird so that it will soak down into the toast.
12. Garnish nicely with sprigs of parsley and lemon, and serve hot.

title: Roasted Beefsteaks
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Tenderloin or porterhouse steaks
- fat of the meat
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- blood from the steak
- tomato sauce or catsup

## Directions

1. Have a clear fire of coals to broil on; rub the gridiron with a little fat of the meat
2. Lay on the steak without salting, let it broil gently until one side is done
3. Turn the steak and catch the blood as you turn it, to make the gravy rich
4. If the steak is large and firm, take a quarter of a pound of butter and work into it pepper and salt
5. When the steak is done lay it on to this seasoned butter, keep it hot until the butter melts, turn the steak in it a few times
6. Put the blood with the gravy and serve hot, with tomato sauce or catsup

title: Roasted Duck
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- duck or ducks
- salt
- pepper
- sage and onion stuffing
- mashed potatoes
- bread
- butter
- onions
- egg

## Directions

1. Clean, draw and truss the duck, or ducks, wash them nicely, salt and pepper them
2. Get ready a sage and onion stuffing or stuff with mashed potatoes, or bread, butter, onions, pepper and salt mixed, and bound together with an egg

title: Roasted Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Fine, Large, Fat Oysters
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Buttered Toast

## Directions

1. Clean the oyster shells thoroughly, set them on the top of the stove or place in a baking pan until the shell is easily removed
2. Remove the flat outer shell
3. Butter the oyster in the deep shell and serve very hot with salt and pepper
4. As the shells open put in a little butter

title: Roasted Quail
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Quails
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 6 Slices of Toast
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sliced Lemon and Parsley Sprigs, or Watercress to Garnish

## Directions

1. Select six fine, fat, tender quail.
2. Pick, singe, clean and wipe them well.
3. Butter the inside of each quail nicely and sprinkle lightly within with salt and pepper.
4. Rub lightly on the outside with butter, then truss the bird and bind the body round with a thin strip of bacon.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a roasting pan and set the birds in the pan and cook in the oven from twenty to thirty minutes, according to size.
6. Have ready the buttered toast.
7. Place on a hot dish, lay a bird on each slice of toast.
8. Add a little butter to the gravy in which the quails have been roasted, a tablespoonful of water and the juice of one lemon.
9. Let this cook for three or four minutes, strain and set on the stove for two minutes longer and pour over the breast of the birds so that it will soak into the bread.
10. Garnish the dish nicely with parsley and sliced lemon or sliced lemon and watercress, and send to the table hot.

title: Roasted Quail with Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Tender Quails
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- 6 Truffles
- 8 Thin Strips of Bacon
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Parsley Sprigs and Sliced Lemon to Garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the quail and butter inside and throw in a little salt and pepper.
2. Stuff with truffles, and bind the body, after rubbing, with a strip of bacon.
3. Set in the oven in a baking pan in which you have placed a tablespoonful of butter, and let it roast twenty or thirty minutes, according to size.
4. Have ready buttered toast.
5. Put the birds on the toast.
6. Add a little butter to the gravy in which they have been roasted, and a tablespoonful of water, and the juice of a lemon.
7. Let this cook for three or four minutes, strain, set on the stove for two minutes longer, and pour over the breast of the bird, so that it will soak into the bread.
8. Serve with a nice garnish of parsley and sliced lemon, and with green peas as an entree.

title: Roasted Salmon
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large piece of fine fresh salmon
- flour
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of fine fresh salmon, cut from the middle of the fish
2. well cleaned and carefully scaled
3. wipe it dry in a clean coarse cloth
4. dredge it with flour
5. put it on the spit, and place it before a clear bright fire
6. Baste it with fresh butter
7. roast it well — seeing that it is thoroughly done to the bone

title: Roasted Squab
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Young Squab
- 6 or 8 Thin Slices of Fat Bacon
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Truffles (if desired)
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 6 or 8 Slices of Buttered Toast

## Directions

1. Use squab only for roasting.
2. Clean nicely, and then truss the pigeon as you would a turkey, only use wooden skewers to hold the wings and legs in place.
3. Take a slice of nice fat pork and fasten it around the body of each pigeon, passing over the breast.
4. Put a bit of butter about the size of a pecan in each bird, and if you can afford to do so, you may stuff with truffles.
5. Put the pigeons in the roasting pan, and add a tablespoonful of butter and about two tablespoonfuls of water.
6. The oven should be hot, but must not be scorching.
7. Baste the birds frequently, and let them roast from fifteen to twenty minutes, according to their size.
8. Prepare toasted bread, one slice for each pigeon.
9. Butter well, and then remove the fat pork and place the pigeons on the toast.
10. Pour over each a little of the gravy which has been made in the roasting pan, allowing it to soak into the bread.
11. Serve hot, with a jelly, preferably Cranberry Sauce.

title: Roasting Ear Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- corn from the cob
- eggs
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cut as much corn from the cob as you require; a dozen ears make a large pudding
2. To every three ears allow an egg, a spoonful of butter, a little pepper and salt, to suit your taste
3. Fill and cover it with sweet milk
4. Let this bake an hour

title: Robert Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 2 Pickles, 2 Inches In Length
- 1 Teaspoonful of Strong French Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Cayenne or Hot Pepper
- Sauce Piquante
- A Teaspoonful More of Prepared Mustard
- Two More Tablespoonfuls of Minced Parsley
- The Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Make a Sauce Piquante
2. Add a teaspoonful more of prepared mustard, and two more of minced parsley, the juice of a lemon
3. Let it boil up once
4. Serve with steak, pork chops, liver sauté, turkey or goose

title: Roe
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Roe
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Juice of a Lemon
- Lemon Sliced in Quarters
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. First wash the eggs well and cut out fibres.
2. Butter a silver dish or, if you have not one, a nice agate.
3. Lay the roe in the dish.
4. Split them open, if large, and put a dot of butter on top of each.
5. Sprinkle nicely with salt and pepper.
6. Place in the oven, bake a few minutes, and serve hot with lemon juice.

title: Roll Jelly Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of flour
- yeast powder

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well
2. Add the sugar
3. Add the flour
4. Bake in a shallow square pan
5. When done, take it out
6. Spread on the jelly
7. Roll at once

title: Roll Jelly Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of flour
- yeast powder
- jelly for filling

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well
2. Add the sugar
3. Add the flour
4. Bake in a shallow square pan
5. When done, take it out and spread on the jelly
6. Roll at once

title: Roll Pudding of Any Kind of Fruit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- light paste
- any kind of fruit

## Directions

1. Make a light paste, roll out lengthwise, spread any kind of fruit over the paste, and roll it up in the dough.
2. Wrap it up in a cloth, tie it carefully, and boil it one hour.

title: Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Quart of Water
- ¾ Ounce of Yeast
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- ¼ Pound of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar (if desired)

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar (if desired), the yeast and salt, with the milk and the water.
2. Then add flour sufficient to make a nice dough of medium firmness.
3. Add the lard, and work the dough until smooth.
4. Then form into a round mass and set to rise.
5. When the dough has doubled its size, work anew and mold into small round balls and set into greased baking pans at a little distance apart from one another.
6. Set to rise again and when they have doubled their size bake in a brisk oven.

title: Rolls For Breakfast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of flour
- half a pint of milk
- a spoonful of salt
- two well-beaten eggs
- a half cup of yeast
- two tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Sift at night a quart of flour
2. add half a pint of milk, a spoonful of salt, two well-beaten eggs, and a half cup of yeast
3. Work it well, cover it, and set it in a warm place to rise
4. Next morning work in two tablespoonfuls of butter
5. mould the dough into rolls
6. Rub over each roll a little butter
7. bake

title: Rolls With Milk
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of yeast
- new milk
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Add as much milk as will make a slack dough
2. Add salt and set it to rise
3. In the morning work it up with very little flour
4. Bake in a brisk oven

title: Roly Poly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. veal
- 1 lb. lean beef
- 1/2 lb. pork
- 1/2 loaf bread
- 1 chopped onion
- spices
- 3 eggs
- beef stock, the shin of veal, or calves' feet

## Directions

1. Chop veal, beef, and pork fine
2. Chop 1/2 loaf bread fine, wet it and squeeze the water out
3. Add 1 chopped onion
4. Add spices to taste
5. Beat 3 eggs well into the mixture
6. Form into a roll
7. Bake 1.5 hours, basting all the while with beef stock, the shin of veal, or calves' feet
8. Set it away to cool
9. Cut in slices and serve

title: Roly Poly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. veal
- 1 lb. lean beef
- 1/4 lb. pork
- 1/4 loaf bread, chopped fine
- 1 chopped onion
- spice to taste
- 3 eggs
- beef stock, shin of veal, or calves' feet for basting

## Directions

1. Chop bread fine, wet it and squeeze the water out
2. Mix chopped onion and spice to taste
3. Beat 3 eggs well into the mixture
4. Form into a roll
5. Bake 1.5 hours, basting all the while with beef stock, shin of veal, or calves' feet
6. Set it away to cool
7. Cut in slices and serve

title: Roly Poly
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Flour
- 5 Ounces of Suet
- 1 Pint of Fresh Fruit, Jam or Jelly
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut away all the fibers from the suet, and then mince very fine.
2. Add the flour and salt to it, and mix well together, and gradually add sufficient water to make a dough.
3. Roll it out about half an inch in thickness on a baking board, and then spread over thinly with minced fresh fruits, mingled, or with fruit Jam or preserves, singly or mingled.
4. Roll it up, and put in a cloth, leaving room for it to swell.
5. Then plunge into boiling water, and let it boil for two hours.
6. Serve with Hard, Cream or Brandy Sauce.

title: Roman Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Lemon Sherbet
- 1/4 Pint of Champagne
- 1/2 Pint of Jamaica Rum
- 1 Gill of Maraschino
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a Lemon Sherbet, and freeze it very hard.
2. Then add the liquors slowly, and beat well.
3. Turn into a freezer, pack and cover it well, and let it stand for four or five hours.
4. The punch will not freeze perfectly on account of the alcohol.
5. Serve in a rather liquid state, in glasses.

title: Root Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 Pint of Root Beer Extract
- 10 Pounds of Sugar
- 10 Gallons of Lukewarm, Filtered Mississippi River Water
- 1 Pint of Yeast, or 1.5 Cakes of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar thoroughly in the water
2. Add the Root Beer Extract and the yeast
3. If using cake yeast, dissolve it first in a little water
4. Stir until thoroughly mixed
5. Bottle in strong bottles or in jugs, corking and tying the corks very securely
6. Set the beer away in a place of even temperature
7. It will be ready for use in twenty-four hours after bottling, or even ten, but will be far more effervescing if allowed to stand three or four days

title: Rosa Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 2 lbs. of butter
- 2 lbs. of sugar
- yolks of 4 eggs
- whites of 8 eggs
- 1 glass of wine
- 1 glass of rose water

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Lay them on tin sheets in small lumps
3. Bake in a quick oven

title: Rose Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Red Rose Extract and Vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as in recipe for Ice Cream, using the same ingredients, and flavor with two tablespoonfuls of Red Rose Extract and Vanilla.

title: Rose Pastilles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Water
- 3 1/4 Ounces of Sugar
- 4 Drops of Essence of Roses
- 3 Drops of Cochineal

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for Orange Pastilles

title: Rose Soufflés
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds Powdered White Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Orange Flower Water
- A Pinch of Carmine Powder

## Directions

1. To two pounds of powdered sugar add the whites of two eggs and one ounce of Orange Flower Water and a little pinch of Carmine Powder.
2. Make of all a firm, consistent paste, adding more sugar, if necessary.
3. Roll out on a sheet of paper, sprinkled with sugar, then cut the paste into little pieces, which you will form into balls about the size of a nut.
4. Place them on a wafer sheet on a baking sheet at a distance of two inches apart, so that they will not touch one another, should they run.
5. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Round of Beef a la Baronne
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fat round of beef
- pepper
- salt
- claret wine
- mace
- pickled capers
- nasturtiums
- white onions
- parsley
- bread

## Directions

1. Boil a fat round of beef for half an hour, take it up and put in a deep dish.
2. Cut gashes in the sides of the meat, put pepper and salt into each gash; fill the dish the meat is in with claret wine.
3. Set it in to bake, adding as it goes in the stove three blades of mace, a cup of pickled capers, or nasturtiums, three white onions cut small, and a bunch of parsley cut fine.
4. Stew or bake all together until the meat is tender.
5. Toast some slices of bread very brown, lay them in the bottom of a dish, lay in the beef and pour the gravy around it, unless it is preferred in a sauce boat.

title: Round of Beef Stewed Brown
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- sweet basil
- thyme
- parsley
- flour
- bacon
- turnips
- carrots
- salsifies
- celery
- tomato catsup
- tomatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Make incisions in the beef and stuff with chopped onions, salt, pepper, and sweet basil, thyme and parsley.
2. Dredge the meat with flour, lay some slices of bacon over it, and put it to brown in a close oven.
3. Slice two turnips, four carrots, four salsifies, three stalks of celery and two onions; add a quarter of a cup of tomato catsup or two large tomatoes; season with salt and put all in the oven to cook with the meat.
4. After it has been cooking in the oven two hours and is brown, add a cup of water with the vegetables.
5. Cover again closely, and let this stew for one or two hours more, or until the meat and vegetables are tender.

title: Royal Cocktail (Moran's Own)
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One lump sugar
- two dashes of Boker's bitters or Angostura bitters
- two tablespoonfuls of Belfast ginger ale
- one wineglassful of whiskey or brandy
- one lemon peel
- plenty of ice

## Directions

1. Shake well
2. strain in fancy glass

title: Royal Ginger Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 pint of molasses
- 1 gill of cream
- 1 cup of ginger

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, and butter well together with molasses, cream, and ginger
