2. Roll out and bake in a slack oven

title: Royal Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Fine White Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lemon Juice
- 1 Tablespoonful of Any Kind of Essence Desired

## Directions

1. Make a Royal Icing of the finest possible white loaf sugar, sifted, and mixed or worked into a paste with the whites of eggs and lemon juice in the proportions given for Plain Icing
2. The paste must be softer than Plain Icing
3. Flavor with any kind of essence, according to fancy

title: Royal Salpicon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 12 Mushrooms
- 2 Truffles
- 1 Pint of Sauce Allemande

## Directions

1. Clean, pare and blanch the sweetbreads.
2. Then cut into very small pieces and put them into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter.
3. Let them sauté a moment and then add the mushrooms and truffles all cut very fine.
4. Add a pint of Sauce Allemande, and let the mixture cook on a slow fire for six or eight minutes, tossing gently all the while.
5. Add a teaspoonful of butter, stir well and use for any garnishing desired.

title: Royal Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Fine Louisiana Oranges
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Orange Syrup

## Directions

1. Take fine, fresh Louisiana oranges, and press out all juice, and mash the pulp and strain through a sieve.
2. To each pint of juice allow two tablespoonfuls of brandy and two of orange syrup.
3. Sweeten to taste and freeze and serve according to the directions given for Creole Punch.

title: Rum Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 glass of Jamaica rum
- 1 teaspoonful of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks well
2. Add the milk and then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
3. Beat all together
4. The longer the eggs are beaten, the lighter will be the omelet
5. Make a plain omelet
6. Fold and turn quickly into a hot dish
7. Place three spoonfuls of sugar on top of the omelet
8. Bring to the table hot
9. Pour the rum over the omelet and around
10. Set the rum on fire with a match
11. With a tablespoon dash the burning rum over the omelet till all the sugar has melted over and all the rum has evaporated
12. When it ceases burning serve immediately

title: Rum Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ Pints of Best Jamaica Rum
- ½ of a Pint of Brandy
- 2 Quarts of Apollinaris or Seltzer Water
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Pint of White Wine
- 2 Lemons, Sliced Thin
- 1½ Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water
2. Add the liquors, stir well
3. Add the lemon juice and the bits of sliced lemon
4. Set to cool and serve in small punch glasses

title: Rum Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Gill of Jamaica Rum
- 6 Ounces of Granulated Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Caramel
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cornstarch
- 1 Pint of Water
- The Zest of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and caramel and lemon zest in a saucepan, and when it begins to boil add a tablespoonful of cornstarch diluted in four tablespoonfuls of cold water
2. Let all cook for three minutes, and then remove from the fire, and add the rum
3. Mix well, then strain through a sieve into a sauce bowl and serve hot with fruit puddings especially.

title: Rusks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Warm Milk
- ¼ Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Cake of Compressed Yeast
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Batter

## Directions

1. Dissolve the yeast in the warm milk, and then add flour sufficient to make a thin batter.
2. Set to rise over night.
3. In the morning beat the sugar and the butter to a cream, and add the eggs, well beaten.
4. Add these to the batter, beat well, add the salt, and sufficient flour to make a soft dough.
5. Then mold the dough into balls with your hands, or into small loaves.
6. Set to rise until they have reached about twice their bulk.
7. Then place in the oven and bake for thirty minutes.

title: Russian Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Carrots
- 2 Parsnips
- 1 Cup of Cold Minced Fowl
- 3 Anchovies
- 1 Dozen Olives
- 3 Caviares
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sauce a la Tartare
- 1 Teaspoonful of Mustard

## Directions

1. This salad is made of cooked carrots, parsnips, beets, cold roast beef, cold ham, a truffle (if it can be afforded), all cut into fancy or dice-shaped pieces.
2. Use one ounce of each of the meats, or simply one cup of cold minced fowl, as it may not be convenient to have all these meats at hand in households.
3. Add six boned anchovies, and one dozen olives and two caviares, and serve with Tartare Sauce, or with a French salad dressing, to which mustard has been added.

title: Rye and Indian Bread for Dyspeptics
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of rye flour
- a pint of Indian meal
- a cup of boiling water
- a cup of yeast
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Scald the Indian meal with a cup of boiling water
2. When lukewarm, mix in the rye flour and a cup of yeast
3. Add a little salt
4. Knead it as for other bread
5. Bake for two hours

title: Rye Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Compressed Yeast
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- Rye and Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough
- A Few Kummel Seeds

## Directions

1. Make the dough a little stiffer than for White Bread, allowing one-half of wheat flour and one-half rye
2. Allow the same amount of yeast, and add a few Kummel seeds and a little more salt than for White Bread
3. Set to rise
4. After one rising, work again as in Bakers' Bread, divide it into loaves, and set it to rise again
5. When about twice its original size, bake in a moderate oven

title: Rye Mush
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 Cup of Rye Meal
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- salt
- sugar and cream

## Directions

1. Sift the meal into the boiling water, and stir constantly while doing so
2. Add the salt, and continue stirring till the mixture begins to boil
3. Cover and let it cook slowly for at least an hour and a quarter
4. Serve hot for breakfast, with sugar and cream

title: Saddle of Venison, Currant Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- A Saddle of Venison Weighing About 5 Pounds
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/2 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 1 Gill of Consomme
- Currant Jelly Sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the Venison neatly and remove all the sinews from the surface.
2. Take fine larding needles and lard closely.
3. Tie the saddle around four times.
4. Slice the carrot and onion and put in the resting pan.
5. Place the Saddle of Venison on top of these sprinkle lightly with a pinch of salt, and spread a half tablespoonful of butter over.
6. Set in a brisk oven and roast for forty minutes, frequently basting the venison with its own gravy.
7. Before taking it from the pan, remove the cord which binds it and place the saddle in a hot dish.
8. Pour the Madeira wine and a gill of veal consommé into the pan, set on the stove and let it come to a boil.
9. Skim the gravy of all fat and strain over the venison.
10. For the sauce: Take a half pint of Currant Jelly and stir till it is thoroughly dissolved.
11. Put in a saucepan a wineglassful of good old Port wine, and set on the stove and let it come gradually to a boil.
12. Add the currant jelly and mix till thoroughly dissolved.
13. Add a tablespoonful of Sauce Espagnole and let it again come to a boil.
14. Serve with the venison, sending each to the table separately.

title: Sago Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal Parts of Sago Marmalade and Rice Croquettes
- Creole Fritter Batter
- Port Wine Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare Croquettes of Rice, adding equal parts of Sago Marmalade.
2. Dip in fritter batter, and fry to a golden brown.
3. Serve with Port Wine Sauce.

title: Sago Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- % Pound of Sago
- 1/4 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Essence

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and add the sago, and stir briskly for ten or fifteen minutes
2. Take off the stove and set to cool
3. Add the sugar and mix thoroughly
4. Add the eggs and a tablespoonful of Vanilla Essence and mix thoroughly
5. Butter a pudding mold or pan and fill with the mixture
6. Set in a tin pan and fill the latter with hot water
7. Place in the oven and let it steam for half an hour
8. Remove from the fire, turn into a dessert dish and send to the table hot
9. Serve with a Cream Sauce

title: Sago Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pints of Broth
- 2 Ounces of Sago

## Directions

1. Soak the sago overnight
2. Allow two ounces to every three pints of broth or Consomme
3. Boil for one hour before serving, stirring occasionally

title: Salad A La Russe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- snap beans
- water
- salt
- butter
- black pepper
- vinegar
- green peas
- artichoke hearts
- asparagus
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut up all kinds of vegetables, such as carrots, turnips, snap beans, etc.
2. Boil them in water with salt and butter, then drain and season lightly with salt, black pepper and vinegar.
3. Add a few cooked green peas, mashed and well drained.
4. Put all in a salad dish in the form of a pyramid, and lightly cover it over with mayonnaise.
5. If you have the hearts of artichokes put them around the dish, as a wreath, with a little asparagus mixed in.
6. Keep as cool as possible until served.

title: Salad Sauce or Dressing for Lettuce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs (two hard-boiled yolks)
- mustard (a tablespoonful)
- sweet cream (a tablespoonful)
- sweet oil (two tablespoonfuls)
- egg (yolk of a raw)
- salt
- sugar (a spoonful)
- vinegar (one tablespoon and a half)

## Directions

1. Take the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs, rub them to a paste in a bowl with a tablespoonful of mustard and one of sweet cream;
2. add gradually two tablespoonfuls of sweet oil; when well mixed add the yolk of a raw egg, to give the paste a delicate smoothness;
3. a little salt, a spoonful of sugar, and one tablespoon and a half of fine vinegar.

title: Sally Lunn
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of warmed sweet milk
- a cup of yeast
- one cup of sugar
- one quart of sifted flour
- four eggs
- a cup of lard and butter melted together

## Directions

1. Pour the mixture into a cake mould after it has been well beaten
2. let the mould be warmed and well greased
3. Set it to rise in a warm place
4. let it rise until very light
5. bake like a cake
6. With a sharp knife divide the cake, severing the top from the bottom crust
7. butter both
8. set the top crust down on the under half
9. bring it to table hot

title: Sally Lunn
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 2 cups of butter
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 pint of milk
- 1/2 pint of yeast
- juice and rind of 1 lemon
- flour

## Directions

1. Make it early in the morning in winter, and about 12 o'clock in the summer
2. Work flour into the mixture to make it as stiff as pound cake
3. Bake it for tea
4. Eat it with fresh butter

title: Sally Lunn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1½ Pints of Flour
- ¾ Cupful of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- ¾ Cup of Sugar
- ¾ Cake of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. Warm the butter in a pint of milk till the milk reaches the boiling point but do not let it boil
2. Add the salt and a tablespoonful of sugar and let it cool
3. When tepid add the flour, well sifted, and beat thoroughly into the mixture
4. Add the yeast, dissolved in a little hot milk or water
5. Beat continuously for at least five minutes
6. When the batter begins to break into blisters, cover it and set to rise for the night
7. In the morning add the yolks of the eggs, beaten till very light, and the whites, beaten to a stiff froth
8. Mix carefully and dissolve a half teaspoonful of soda in the mixture if it seems any way sour
9. Turn the whole into a shallow buttered dish and set to rise for fifteen minutes longer
10. Bake about twenty or twenty-five minutes in a moderately quick oven till it is a light brown

title: Sally Lunn (Second Recipe)
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of risen yeast
- a cup of warm sweet milk
- one-half a cup of white sugar
- a large spoonful each of lard and butter mixed and warmed
- four eggs well beaten
- three and one-half cups of sifted flour
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Pour a cup of risen yeast into a bowl
2. add a cup of warm sweet milk, one-half a cup of white sugar, and a large spoonful each of lard and butter mixed and warmed
3. also add four eggs well beaten, three and one-half cups of sifted flour, and a little salt
4. Beat all this well
5. pour into a warm and well greased cake pan
6. set it to rise in a warm place in winter, and a cool one in summer
7. If made up at night, add a little more flour, or make the dough a little stiffer, and allow it to rise in a tureen or crock, and pour it in the pan and let it rise a little before baking
8. bake like a cake

title: Sally Lunn or Tea Buns
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1/2 quart of good yeast
- flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter melted in sweet milk
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Add yeast to milk
2. Put in as much flour as will make a stiff batter
3. Let it stand 6 or 8 hours
4. Add melted butter and eggs to sugar
5. Work in sufficient flour to make it the consistency of dough
6. Let it stand 1 hour
7. Work it off, forming it into buns or in a large mould
8. Let it stand 1/2 hour
9. Bake it in a quick oven

title: Salmi of Ducks, Venison or Birds
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 cup of soup broth
- 2 whole leeks
- carrots
- thyme
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- pieces of game
- 1 cup of claret wine
- juice of ½ a lemon
- bread cut into diamonds
- slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Put 1 tablespoonful of butter into the saucepan, stirring in 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, letting it melt without browning
2. Add 1 cup of soup broth, 2 whole leeks, carrots, a little thyme and parsley, pepper and salt
3. Let it boil for half an hour
4. Withdraw the vegetables, put in the pieces of game, and let simmer until done
5. Add 1 cup of claret wine and juice of ½ a lemon
6. Have ready some fried pieces of bread cut into diamonds, lay them around the dish, put in the game in the middle, pour the sauce over the whole
7. Garnish the dish with slices of lemon

title: Salmon Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Salmon
- 1-3 the Quantity of Chopped Celery
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Sliced Lemon
- Olives
- 1 Pickled Beet
- A Plain French Dressing
- crisp bed of lettuce

## Directions

1. Flake the salmon and heap into a salad bowl
2. Mix with chopped celery using one-third of the latter in proportion to the quantity of salmon
3. Add a Plain French Dressing
4. When ready to serve turn into a salad dish on which you have arranged a crisp bed of lettuce
5. Garnish with sliced lemon, olives, hard-boiled eggs, and one daintily sliced pickled beet

title: Salpicon à la Montglas
body:
## Ingredients

- The Breasts of 2 Small Chickens, or Any Game
- 1 Ounce of Lean Ham
- 1 Gill of Madeira Sauce
- 1 Gill of Tomato Sauce
- 6 Mushrooms
- 1 Truffle

## Directions

1. Mince the breasts of the chicken or game very fine, mince the ham, truffles and mushrooms, and put all into a saucepan with a gill of Madeira Sauce and one gill of Tomato Sauce
2. Let all cook for five minutes, and then use as a garnish

title: Salpicon, Financier Style
body:
## Ingredients

- The Legs and Breasts of a Chicken
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Dozen Small Godiveau Quenelles
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Truffle
- 8 Mushrooms
- 1 Pint of Madeira Sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into dice-shaped pieces, and put into a saucepan with the butter
2. Add the square inch of ham, the truffles and mushrooms, all minced fine
3. Add twelve small Godiveau Quenelles
4. Add a pint of Madeira Sauce
5. Set on the stove, and let all cook for five or eight minutes, and the Salpicon is ready to be served as a garnish for any dish desired

title: Salpicon, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- The Breasts of 2 Fine Partridges
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 12 Whole Peppers
- 1 Glass of Madeira Wine
- 1 Glass of Good Sherry Wine
- 6 Blanched Chicken Livers
- 1 Carrot
- 1/2 Onion
- 1 Ounce of Cooked Beef Tongue
- 2 Truffles
- 6 Mushrooms
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Square Inch of Lean Ham
- 1 Quart of Consommé or Broth

## Directions

1. Cut the breasts of the Partridges into dice-shaped pieces, and then put them into a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter.
2. Pour over a glass of good Sherry wine and set on a hot fire.
3. Add the blanched chicken livers, the truffle cut into dice-shaped pieces and the mushrooms cut very fine.
4. Have already prepared a Fumet of Game, made by placing one carrot, one-half of a small onion, one bay leaf, one square inch of ham, one sprig of thyme, and the carcass of the partridge into a covered saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter.
5. Let these brown well and add a glass of Madeira Wine.
6. Let it come to a boil, then moisten with one quart of broth or consommé, and salt and pepper to taste; add twelve whole black peppers.
7. Let all cook for three-quarters of an hour, until it is reduced to a pint, and strain through a napkin.
8. Add this to the salpicon and let all cook for five minutes longer.
9. Use as a garnish for any dish desired.

title: Salsify Fried in Batter
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- cold water
- olive oil
- French vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- eggs
- milk
- flour
- hog's lard
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scrape the salsify, throw it for a few moments into cold water
2. Parboil it, drain it and cut into lengths of three inches
3. Steep until cold in a bowl with two tablespoonfuls of olive oil, one of French vinegar, pepper and salt
4. Let it remain in this, occasionally turning it until ready to fry it
5. Make a batter with eggs, milk, and flour
6. Dip the salsify in this batter, and fry in hog's lard
7. Fry parsley with it and serve

title: Salsify Fritter
body:
## Ingredients

- A Bunch of Salsify
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Water
- 2 Eggs
- 1/2 Pound of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Teaspoonful of Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape the salsify and boil as directed in recipe Boiled Parsnips till very tender.
2. Then cut into long, narrow strips.
3. Make a batter by mixing the flour with the yolks of the eggs, beaten well.
4. Then add the salt and pepper, and gradually one cup of water, till it is absorbed.
5. Then add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
6. Mix thoroughly.
7. Add the salsify to the batter.
8. Dip out one spoon at a time of the batter, and fry in boiling lard.
9. Serve as an entree at dinner.

title: Salsify Fritters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- water
- 5 eggs
- 1 pint milk
- 1 large spoonful butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrape the salsify and lay it in water to prevent its becoming black
2. Boil it quick and mash it fine
3. Prepare a batter with 5 eggs, beaten very light with yolk and white separate
4. Add 1 pint of milk and a large spoonful of butter
5. Thicken the whole with flour to the consistency of fritter batter
6. Stir in the salsify
7. Season with pepper and salt to the taste
8. Fry them nicely

title: Salsify Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- salt
- red pepper
- eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Scrape and boil the salsify
2. Beat fine in a mortar
3. Season with salt and red pepper
4. Mix them in a batter of eggs and a very little flour
5. Drop the size of an oyster and fry a light brown

title: Salt and Pepper to Taste
body:
## Ingredients

- Turnips
- Butter
- Salt and Pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Wash and pare the turnips, and cut them into quarters, and cover with boiling water and boil until very tender, which will be in about thirty to forty-five minutes, according to the tenderness of the turnips.
2. If you cut the turnips as soon as tender take out of the water and drain through a colander of all water.
3. Press them lightly, to squeeze all remaining water out, and hash well.
4. Add a tablespoonful of butter, salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
5. Serve with roast meats or roast ducks.

title: Salt Rising Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of new milk
- a teaspoonful of salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a pint of new milk, warm from the cow if possible
2. Put in a teaspoonful of salt
3. thicken it with flour to the consistency of batter cakes
4. Set this in a warm place to rise
5. make your biscuit or bread up with it, and some new milk, or milk and water warmed together

title: Salt, Or Milk, Rising For Bread, And How To Bake It
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of new milk
- nearly a pint of boiling water
- a teaspoonful of fine salt
- flour
- fine flour
- a little lard
- a cup of water

## Directions

1. Take a pint of new milk, stir in nearly a pint of boiling water
2. then salt it with a teaspoonful of fine salt
3. thicken this with flour enough to make a thick batter
4. Set it in a warm place to rise
5. it is ready to mix into bread
6. Mix the yeast in a soft dough with fine flour, a little lard, and a cup of water
7. mould it, and set it to rise
8. When well risen, bake it a nice brown
9. Wrap it in a damp cloth for a few minutes, and let it cool slowly before it is cut

title: Salted Almonds
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Almonds
- 1 Ounce of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Take about a pint of almonds and shell them
2. Blanch or scald them in boiling water till the skin comes off easily
3. Dry well and place in a flat tin dish
4. Add about an ounce of butter and set the plate in a moderate oven until the almonds are a golden brown
5. Take them out of the oven and add a heaping tablespoonful of salt
6. Stir them round and round, dredging quickly with the salt, and turn them into a dish to cool
7. Pick out the almonds and throw aside the extra salt
8. When cool, place in a box for use, covering tightly

title: Salted Peanuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whole Peanuts
- 1 Tablespoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Take blanched whole peanuts and proceed in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for Salted Almonds

title: Sandwiches. Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet butter
- mixed mustard
- sweet oil
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolk
- tongue or boiled ham
- bread
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of nice sweet butter, three tablespoonfuls of mixed mustard, the same of sweet oil, a little salt, pepper and the yolk of an egg.
2. Put it over the fire and stir till it thickens; set it by to cool and chop fine some tongue or boiled ham.
3. Cut the bread thin, then spread on the dressing and over it put a layer of ham or tongue.
4. Press the slices of bread hard together, trim the edges and garnish with curled parsley.

title: Sangaree, Hot or Cold
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Claret
- 1 Cup of Boiling Water
- 6 Whole Cloves
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 12 Whole Allspice
- 2 Inches of Stick Cinnamon
- Grated Nutmeg to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the water and spices for fifteen minutes, till the water is thoroughly flavored with the spices
2. Then remove from the fire and dissolve the sugar in it
3. Put the Claret into a pitcher
4. Strain the water and Sugar into it
5. Flavor with the nutmeg, and add more sugar, if necessary, to taste
6. Serve hot immediately

title: Sardine Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Triangular Slices of Toast
- 1 Box of Sardines
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 6 Anchovies
- A Dash of Red Pepper

## Directions

1. Pound the sardines and the hard-boiled eggs to a paste, season with a dash of Red Pepper and the juice of a lemon and spread the buttered toast with the mixture.
2. Decorate in the center with a coiled Anchovy.
3. Or, simply pound the sardines to a paste, season with a dash of red pepper and the juice of a lemon, and spread on the slices of buttered toast.

title: Sardine Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box Sardines
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Cut the sardines into pieces of about half an inch in length.
2. Season nicely with a French dressing and serve.

title: Sardines in Oil
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Box of Sardines
- Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Take the sardines carefully from the box and avoid breaking them.
2. Decorate a dish nicely with sprigs of parsley or watercress and lay the sardines upon it and serve.

title: Sassafras Mead
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Bunches of Sassafras Roots
- 1.25 Pints of Honey
- 3.5 Pints of Louisiana Molasses
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cream of Tartar
- 0.5 Teaspoonful of Carbonate of Soda

## Directions

1. Take the roots of sassafras, and make about two quarts of Sassafras Tea
2. Strain well
3. Set to boil again, and when it boils add one and one-half pints of honey, and three and one-half pints of Louisiana Syrup or Molasses
4. Add a tablespoonful of Cream of Tartar
5. Stir well and set to cool
6. When cool strain it
7. Take about a dozen clean bottles and fill with the mixture
8. Cork very tight, and put in a cool place
9. In a day it will be ready for use
10. When serving this Mead, take a glass and fill half full with ice water
11. Add a tablespoonful of the Mead and stir in a half teaspoonful of Carbonate of Soda

title: Sauce a l'Aurore, for Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster spawn
- butter (a small piece of fresh)
- sieve
- white sauce
- lemon juice (a large spoonful)

## Directions

1. Pound the spawn of a lobster very smooth, with a small piece of fresh butter, and press it through a sieve in the white sauce and a large spoonful of lemon juice, and set it over a clear fire to simmer for a minute or two, taking care it does not boil.

title: Sauce Flamande
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 yolks of eggs beaten
- juice of 1/2 a lemon
- a little grated nutmeg
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar
- a good sized piece of butter

## Directions

1. Put 4 yolks of eggs beaten, juice of 1/2 a lemon, a little grated nutmeg, a tablespoonful of vinegar, and a good sized piece of butter into a saucepan
2. set it on a gentle fire
3. stir well, but do not let it boil

title: Sauce for Batter Puddings
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 6 eggs
- 4 spoonfuls of sifted sugar
- butter
- 1 pint of boiling cream
- 1 wineglass of brandy

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of 6 eggs with sugar and butter mixed together
2. Have ready a pint of boiling cream to mix with the yolks
3. Put it on the fire and stir it until it is of the consistency of sauce
4. Add to it a wineglass of brandy

title: Sauce Froide
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- chives
- burnet
- oil
- eggs (three yolks of hard-boiled)
- vinegar (two tablespoonfuls)
- mustard (made)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince quite fine some parsley, chervil, tarragon, chives and burnet; mix them in five or six tablespoonfuls of oil, or three yolks of hard-boiled eggs rubbed down smooth;
2. add two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, some made mustard, salt and pepper; beat all together until

title: Sauce Froide
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- chives
- burnet
- five or six tablespoonfuls of oil or three yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- made mustard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince quite fine some parsley, chervil, tarragon, chives and burnet
2. Mix them in five or six tablespoonfuls of oil, or three yolks of hard-boiled eggs rubbed down smooth
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, some made mustard, salt and pepper
4. Beat all together until it is smooth and thick
5. Serve in a sauce-boat

title: Sauce Piquant
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a large spoonful of sweet butter
- two onions
- two carrots
- thyme
- two cloves
- two eschalots
- a bunch of parsley
- garlic
- flour
- a cup of beef or veal stock
- half a cup of strong vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put a large spoonful of sweet butter in a stewpan
2. Slice into it two onions, two carrots, a little thyme, two cloves, two eschalots and a bunch of parsley
3. Add a clove of garlic if liked
4. Let them cook until the carrot is soft
5. Shake in a little flour
6. Let it cook five minutes more
7. Add a cup of beef or veal stock, and half a cup of strong vinegar
8. Skim and strain through a sieve
9. Add salt and pepper when boiling

title: Sauce Piquante
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of vinegar
- very little pepper
- thyme
- 5 tablespoonfuls of broth or clear gravy
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix in a stewpan 1/2 pint of vinegar, a very little pepper and thyme
2. boil away to half the quantity
3. add 5 tablespoonfuls of broth or clear gravy
4. let that again boil away to half
5. add a little salt

title: Sauce Piquante
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of vinegar
- pepper
- thyme
- 5 tablespoonfuls of broth or clear gravy
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix in a stewpan 1/2 pint of vinegar, a very little pepper and thyme
2. Boil away to half the quantity
3. Add 5 tablespoonfuls of broth or clear gravy
4. Let that again boil away to half and add a little salt

title: Sauce Piquante for Cold Meat
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions (two)
- butter
- carrot
- sweet herbs (dried thyme or marjoram)
- eschalots (two)
- parsley
- garlic (a clove)
- flour (a spoonful)

## Directions

1. Slice two onions, fry them in butter; put them in a stewpan with a carrot, some sweet herbs, such as dried thyme or marjoram, two eschalots, some parsley and a clove of garlic;
2. dredge in a spoonful of flour. When the carrot is perfectly done, mash it in the stewpan with a wooden spoon, and when smooth add to it a cup of soup stock.
3. When this boils up, throw in a cup of strong vinegar. Add salt and pepper, and strain.

title: Sauce Robert
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four large onions
- three ounces of butter
- one spoonful of flour
- half a pint of veal, or beef gravy, or soup
- salt
- pepper
- one dessertspoonful of made mustard

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces four large onions
2. Brown them with three ounces of butter and a spoonful of flour
3. When yellow-brown, pour on them half a pint of veal, or beef gravy, or soup
4. Let all simmer for half an hour
5. Season with salt and pepper, and at the moment of serving, add a dessertspoonful of made mustard

title: Sauce Rémoulade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Raw Yolk of Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Tarragon Vinegar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil
- 1/8 Clove of Garlic, Minced Very Fine
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- The Juice of Half a Lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs till hard
2. Remove the shells and set aside the whites, which you will have crumbled fine for a garnish
3. Put the yolks into a bowl and mash very fine, till perfectly smooth
4. Add the mustard and mix well, and the seasonings of vinegar and salt and Cayenne to taste
5. Add the olive oil, drop by drop, working in the egg all the time, and then add the yolk of the raw egg
6. Work in thoroughly, till light
7. Add the juice of half a lemon
8. Mix well, increasing the quantities of oil or vinegar, according to taste, very slightly
9. Serve with cold meats, fish or salads

title: Sauce Tartar
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of 1 hard boiled egg
- yolks of 2 raw eggs
- salt
- pepper
- 1 pint of oil
- tarragon vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped gherkins
- 1 ditto of chopped capers
- 1 ditto of eschallots and parsley
- 2 of French mustard
- a very little cayenne pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mash the yolk of a hard boiled egg
2. add the yolks of 2 raw ones, with salt and pepper
3. mix with a wooden spoon, dropping slowly a pint of oil, and moisten with tarragon vinegar to taste, but keep it thick
4. add a tablespoonful of chopped gherkins, 1 ditto of chopped capers, ditto of eschallots and parsley, 2 of French mustard, a very little cayenne pepper, and sugar, and more salt, if required

title: Sauce Tartare
body:
## Ingredients

- A Mayonnaise Sauce
- 8 Shallots
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Pickle
- A Handful of Parsley, Minced Fine
- 1 Teaspoonful Mustard
- Salt, Cayenne and Black Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the Mayonnaise
2. Put in a bowl a half dozen shallots, greens and all, and chop fine
3. Add a handful of parsley, chopped fine; and the half-minced clove, and one whole pickle, well chopped
4. Mix all this together and put in a cloth and strain out the juice by pressing
5. Add this juice to the Mayonnaise, and add one teaspoonful of mustard, salt, Cayenne and black pepper to taste
6. Serve with filet of trout, etc

title: Sauce À l'Aurore, For Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- spawn of a lobster
- fresh butter
- white sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pound the spawn of a lobster very smooth, with a small piece of fresh butter, and press it through a sieve in the white sauce and a large spoonful of lemon juice
2. Set it over a clear fire to simmer for a minute or two, taking care it does not boil

title: Saucisses
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Lean Beef
- 2 Pounds of Lean Pork
- 1 Pound of Lean Veal
- 1 Pound of Fat Pork
- 2 Large Onions Minced Very Fine
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cayenne Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful Black Pepper
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Salt
- 3 Bay Leaves, Minced Very Fine
- 1/4 Spoon Each of Ground Cloves, Mace, Allspice and Grated Nutmeg
- 1 Teaspoonful Each of Minced Thyme and Sweet Marjoram

## Directions

1. Chop and hash the meat (fat and lean) very fine, mincing it
2. Season highly with salt and pepper and Cayenne, mixing well
3. Add the minced onion and garlic, mix well
4. Add the finely minced herbs and spices and mix thoroughly
5. Fill the casings which you have gotten from the butcher and washed again thoroughly
6. Fill them with the mixture, in lengths of about two feet or one foot and a half, stuffing tightly
7. Tie at both ends and let them stand overnight in a deep brine
8. If used for breakfast, take out as much as desired, wipe dry and cut into slices and fry, or fry the sausage the whole length in boiling lard
9. Slice nicely, garnish with chopped parsley and serve

title: Saucissons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Fresh Pork, Very Lean
- 1 Pound of Fat
- 2 Pounds of Filet of Beef
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cayenne Pepper
- 1 Teaspoonful of Black Pepper
- 3 Teaspoonfuls of Salt
- 1 Bay Leaf, Chopped Fine
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Ground Allspice, Cloves, and Nutmeg
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Fine Herbs
- 1 Clove of Garlic

## Directions

1. Mince and hash the meat very fine, mixing the beef and pork and fat
2. Season highly with the Cayenne, salt and pepper, mixing thoroughly
3. Season next with the minced onion and garlic and mix well
4. Season with the minced herbs and spices, mixing all thoroughly
5. Fill the casings, which are never very large for Saucissons
6. Tie them in sausages of about a finger in length, or three inches
7. Fry in boiling lard and serve whole, placing several on each plate

title: Sauerkraut
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Head of Cabbage
- 1 Pint of Vinegar
- 1 Bottle of White Wine
- 1 Glass of Brandy or Whisky
- Salt in Sufficient Quantity to Allow 3 Ounces for each Layer of Cabbage

## Directions

1. Take a large head of cabbage, and take off the green leaves.
2. Shred the cabbage into fine pieces, of about five inches long and one wide.
3. Get an earthern vessel or a keg, and line the bottom and sides with the green leaves of the cabbage.
4. Put in a layer of salt, of about three ounces, and lay over this a layer of cabbage leaves of about three inches in thickness.
5. Cover again with a layer of salt, and pound down well, and so continue until you have used up the cabbage.
6. Pour over this sufficient vinegar to cover, and also, if possible, a bottle of White Wine and a glass of brandy or whisky.
7. Take some boards or the cover of the keg and line them with cabbage leaves, and cover the keg closely.
8. Put the cover on the keg, or the board over the bowl, with a fifteen-pound weight on top.
9. Set it in a place of even moderate temperature.
10. Bore a hole in the bottom of the keg, and insert a piece of wood.
11. When the cabbage begins to ferment, take the piece of wood out, and let the liquor from the fermentation flow through this canal. This will be in about four or five days.
12. After this first operation open the keg and renew the vinegar and wine, skimming the fermentation from the top, and so continue until the cabbage is clear and without odor.
13. Place the Chou-Croute in a cool place.
14. When ready to use, take it out and let it soak for two or three hours in cool fresh water, and when quite fresh-looking put it into a saucepan and cook as you would cabbage, with salt meat, pork, sausage or corned beef.

title: Sausage
body:
## Ingredients

- Bouilli from the day before
- Chopped parsley
- A few spices
- Salt
- Cayenne pepper
- Beef extract from bouillon
- 1 Pound of pork
- A few truffles
- 1 Wineglass of Madeira
- Entrails

## Directions

1. Take the bouilli of the day before, mince and add chopped parsley, a few spices, salt and Cayenne pepper, and a little beef extract saved from the bouillon
2. Take a pound of pork and add, mixing thoroughly
3. When the whole is well mixed, add a few truffles and a little Madeira
4. Fill some entrails that have been thoroughly cleansed with this meat and shape the sausage as one desires
5. Boil in butter and serve alone

title: Sausage Forcemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Fresh Pork
- 2 Square Inches of Lean Raw Ham
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- A Pinch of Grated Nutmeg
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Hash the pork; season well with salt and pepper, according to taste, adding a pinch of grated nutmeg and the chopped herbs and minced ham
2. Hash all very fine and make into small balls and use as desired

title: Savarin
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of the Best Flour
- ⅜ Ounce of Yeast
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- ½ Ounce of Salt
- 1 Pound of the Best Butter
- 12 or 8 Eggs
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Wineglass of Brandy or Jamaica Rum

## Directions

1. Make the same dough as for the Brioche, and at the last kneading place it in a cake pan lined with a buttered paper.
2. Set it to rise for an hour longer.
3. Then place it in the oven, and brush over lightly with a beaten egg.
4. Let it bake for about an hour and a half, according to the size, and then take from the oven and soak in a syrup made of one pound of sugar and a pint of water, and boiled to a syrup, and into which you will add a wineglass of brandy or Jamaica rum.

title: Savory Friar's Omelet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of nice potatoes
- ½ lb. of carrots
- 1 egg yolk
- salt and pepper
- about 1 teaspoonful of butter
- fine bread crumbs
- 1 or 2 small pieces of butter

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes and carrots
2. When cooked quite tender, rub the potatoes and carrots through a wire sieve with a wooden spoon
3. Add the yolk of an egg, salt and pepper to taste, and about 1 teaspoonful of butter
4. Rub and beat all up into a thick paste
5. Have a pudding dish well buttered and scattered all over with fine bread crumbs
6. Put in the mixture
7. Add bread crumbs on the top, and 1 or 2 small pieces of butter
8. Bake for about ¾ hour, or till the crumbs are nice and brown
9. Turn out the whole on a dish and serve hot

title: Savory Jelly For Cold Turkey Or Meat
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef
- veal or beef bones
- half a pound of sweet ham or bacon
- thyme
- basil
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- whites and shells of three eggs
- three blades of mace
- a cup of wine or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put in the pot two pounds of beef
2. If you have veal or beef bones, break them and throw them in also
3. Add half a pound of sweet ham or bacon
4. Add all the sweet herbs, such as thyme, basil, parsley and marjoram
5. Last of all, add salt and pepper to taste
6. Boil for three or four hours
7. When it is sufficiently boiled, take off, strain, and put away to cool
8. Take off all the fat and sediment
9. Clarify by throwing into it the whites and shells of three eggs
10. Add three blades of mace and a cup of wine or lemon juice
11. Place it again on the fire, let it boil a few times, and strain it through a jelly-bag

title: Savory Sauce for a Roast Goose
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of made mustard
- half a teaspoonful of cayenne pepper
- three spoonfuls of port wine

## Directions

1. When mixed, pour this (hot) into the body of the goose before sending it up

title: Savory Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds lean beef
- 4 quarts water
- 1 onion, chopped fine
- 3 sprigs parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 sprig thyme
- 1/2 cup rice
- 3 tablespoonfuls oatmeal
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful pepper

## Directions

1. Put the meat in four quarts of cold water and let it simmer for three hours
2. One hour before serving, add one-half cup of rice which has been soaked in water until soft and three tablespoonfuls of oatmeal
3. Add one tablespoonful of salt and one-fourth teaspoonful of pepper
4. Add parsley, sprig of thyme and one onion chopped fine
5. Boil an hour longer and serve very hot

title: Savoy Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- good crushed sugar in weight of 12 eggs
- flour in weight of 7 eggs
- juice of 2 nice lemons
- rind of 1 lemon or 4 spoonfuls of rose water

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites separate
2. Add the flour and sugar
3. Stir them lightly with the juice of 2 nice lemons and the rind of 1, or 4 spoonfuls of rose water
4. Stir all well together
5. Bake on tins in a moderately hot oven

title: Savoy Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve eggs
- J lb. of sugar
- 5 ounces of butter
- Grating of 2 lemons
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Take 12 eggs, all the whites taken out, J lb. of sugar, 5 ounces of butter, well creamed, the grating of 2 lemons and juice of 1
2. Bake it in a thin paste

title: Scalloped Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- The Meat of 1 Dozen Picked Crabs
- A Dash of Grated Nutmeg
- Bread Crumbs
- 1 Egg
- Pepper and Salt to Taste
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil and pick the crabs according to recipe given above
2. Beat an egg thoroughly and add to the meat which has been seasoned highly with Cayenne and salt to taste
3. Take one clove of garlic, chop fine and add
4. Sift into this mixture fine grated bread crumbs or cracker crumbs, and mix thoroughly
5. Beat an egg, roll the crabs into boulets or graceful meat balls, and then bind by rolling lightly in the egg
6. Roll in the bread crumbs, grated nicely, and then drop into boiling lard, and fry until a pale golden brown, which will generally require about three minutes
7. Wash and clean the shells thoroughly and wipe dry
8. Set a boulet in the center of each and garnish prettily with sprigs of parsley and sliced lemon
9. Serve very hot

title: Scalloped Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Fine Oysters
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Blade of Mace
- 4 Cloves
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1/2 Cup of Rich Milk or Cream
- 1/2 Cup of Oyster Liquor
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Select about four dozen fine oysters.
2. Have ready a porcelain-lined baking dish, or any good dish that will not darken the oysters.
3. Drain the oysters in a colander, strain the liquor to remove all pieces of shell and save it.
4. Butter the baking dish and place in a layer of oysters, well seasoned, a la Creole, with Cayenne, salt, chopped mace, cloves, thyme, parsley and bay leaf, chopped very fine.
5. Place over a layer of bread crumbs, about a half-inch in thickness.
6. Place here and there little dots of butter and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
7. Add another layer of seasoned oysters, and then another layer of bread crumbs, until the dish is full.
8. Then mix a half cup of rich cream and milk and a half cup of the oyster liquor, and pour over the dish.
9. Sprinkle the last layer with bread crumbs and dot gently with bits of butter.
10. Place in a quick oven and bake about fifteen or twenty minutes, or until a nice brown.

title: Scalloped Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of oysters
- butter
- powdered bread crumbs or crackers
- allspice
- pepper corns
- mace
- small glass of cooking Maderia

## Directions

1. Scald 1 quart of oysters in their own liquor, and drain them
2. Butter a pan
3. Sprinkle powdered bread crumbs or crackers well over the bottom and sides
4. Put a layer of oysters, then of bread crumbs
5. Next, add small pieces of butter, a few whole allspice and pepper corns, a very little mace
6. Continue this until the pan is full
7. Cover last with crumbs
8. Add small glass of cooking Maderia
9. Bake 1 hour

title: Scalloped Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- bread crumbs
- butter
- pepper and salt
- grated crackers or small champion biscuit
- ham

## Directions

1. Take the oysters from their liquor
2. Place them in the bottom of a dish or pan
3. Cover them with bread crumbs, butter, pepper and salt
4. Add another layer of oysters, then the bread crumbs, etc.
5. Continue this till the dish is full
6. Cover the top either with grated crackers or small champion biscuit
7. Bake about 30 minutes
8. Serve with slices of ham

title: Scalloped Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- oysters
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- cloves
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Toast several slices of bread quite brown, and butter them on both sides
2. Take a baking dish and put the toast around the sides
3. Pour the oysters into the dish and season to your taste, with butter, pepper and salt
4. Add mace or cloves
5. Crumb bread on the top of the oysters, and bake with quick heat for 15 minutes

title: Scalloped Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tomatoes
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as in Tomatoes au Gratin, only bake the tomatoes in layers in small cups or silver shells.

title: Scallops Of Mutton, With Mushrooms
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton scallops
- wine
- button mushrooms
- truffles
- broth or soup stock
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Saute, or fry the scallops brown, then pour off the fat.
2. Add a glass of wine, a dozen button mushrooms, three ounces of truffles cut in pieces, and a cup of broth, or the stock of plain soup without vegetables.
3. Simmer gently, and finish by adding the juice of a lemon.

title: Scallopéd Oysters.—No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- their own liquor
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- butter
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Lay the oysters in a shallow pan or dish with a little of their own liquor
2. Some pepper, salt, chopped parsley, butter, and grated bread crumbs
3. Have a layer of bread crumbs on the top of the pan
4. And set it in the oven to bake a light brown
5. They should be served hot with tomato or walnut catsup poured over them

title: Scallopéd Oysters.—No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- pounded crackers
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Procure any quantity of oysters desired, and place in a baking dish
2. Put alternate layers of oysters and pounded crackers
3. Season each layer with salt, pepper and butter
4. When filled, pour on enough milk to soak the crackers
5. And bake forty minutes
6. Serve hot

title: Scotch Barley Broth. Cheap And Substantial
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of Scotch barley
- four or five pounds of shin beef sawed into small pieces
- cold water
- three onions
- two heads of celery
- a couple of turnips cut into thin pieces
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash half a pound of Scotch barley in cold water; put it in a pot with four or five pounds of shin beef sawed into small pieces, cover it with cold water and set it on the fire. When it boils skim it well, and then add three onions. Set it near the fire to simmer gently for two hours. If much fat rises skim again; then add two heads of celery and a couple of turnips cut into thin pieces. Season with salt, and let it boil for an hour and a half. Take out the meat on a platter and cover to keep warm; then pour the soup in a tureen and serve.

title: Scotch Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. and a quarter of flour
- ¾ lb. of sugar
- ⅝ lb. of butter
- lemon to taste

## Directions

1. bake 20 minutes in rather a slow oven

title: Scrambled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fresh eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- parsley
- buttered toast or broiled ham

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a saucer, one by one, and then transfer to a bowl.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Have the frying pan very hot.
4. Put into it the butter, and add immediately the eggs.
5. Keep stirring around and around and across for about three or four minutes.
6. Judge by the consistency of the egg, which must be like a thick mush as you take it from the fire.
7. Keep stirring a few seconds longer after you have taken the pan off the fire.
8. Put the eggs into a hot dish, and garnish with parsley.
9. Serve immediately with buttered toast or broiled ham.

title: Scuppernong Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Quarts of Scuppernong Juice
- 1.33 Quarts of Cold Water
- 3 Pounds of Granulated Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as Blackberry Wine

title: Seasoned Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gill of rich milk
- a wineglass of rose water
- 4 ounces of powdered white sugar
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 quart of good sweet cream
- preserves

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 gill of rich milk, a wineglass of rose water, and 4 ounces of powdered white sugar
2. Add the yolks of 2 eggs well beaten
3. Stir all into 1 quart of good sweet cream
4. Set it over hot coals
5. Let it boil, stirring it all the time
6. Take it off and when cool enough, pour it into a glass bowl
7. When cool, eat with it any sort of preserves

title: Sheep Brains
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound of Brains
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Grated Breadcrumbs
- Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Plunge the brains into cold water to disgorge them of all blood and remove the fine skin and blood that surround them.
2. Blanch with scalding water.
3. In five minutes take them out of the hot water and put them into a saucepan and cover with cold water.
4. Add a tiny onion, sliced fine, parsley and a whole bay leaf.
5. Let them simmer gently for five minutes.
6. Take from the fire and drain.
7. When cold cut into pieces of a square inch and dip in a batter or tomato sauce, and then in grated bread crumbs, patting gently.
8. Drop into boiling lard and fry to a golden brown.
9. Take out and drain off grease, and serve on a bed of fried parsley.
10. A garnish of boiled green peas is also very pretty and palatable.

title: Sheep Brains, Brown Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound of Brains
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Brown Butter Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains in exactly the same manner as indicated in the above recipe.
2. Serve with Brown Butter Sauce.

title: Sheep Kidneys
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Kidneys
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Lemon juice
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Slice the kidneys very thin and wash well, then scald and wipe dry.
2. Season well.
3. Pass a skewer through each kidney and brush with melted butter.
4. Place on a double broiler and cook for five minutes, allowing two and a half minutes to each side.
5. Place on a hot dish and pour over melted butter and a little lemon juice.
6. Garnish nicely with parsley and serve hot.

title: Sheep Tongues Smothered
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tongues
- 1 Large Onion, Cut Fine
- 2 Carrots, Cut Fine
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Minced Very Fine
- 3 Pickles
- ¼ of a Cup of Capers
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- 2 Slices of Bacon

## Directions

1. Scald and blanch the tongue removing the skins.
2. Throw them into cold water, dry and piqué or lard very delicately with larding needles.
3. Season well with salt and pepper.
4. Slice the bacon into fine strips and lay in the bottom of a saucepan.
5. Place the lamb tongues over this.
6. Place on top another fine layer of bacon in very fine strips.
7. Add the minced carrots, onion, herbs, and salt and pepper again to taste.
8. Let it simmer for about fifteen minutes and then moisten with about a pint of boiling water or broth.
9. Let it cook over a slow fire about three hours.
10. Take out the tongues and place them on a hot dish.
11. Strain the sauce through a sieve, set back on the stove a few seconds, and add one-quarter of a cup of capers and three pickles, sliced fine.
12. Stir well and let it boil up once.
13. Pour over the tongues and serve.

title: Sheepshead a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- A 3-Pound Sheepshead
- 2 Onions
- 1 Bunch of Parsley
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 4 Bay Leaves
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Quart of Boiled Cream
- Yolks of 4 Eggs
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the Sheepshead as for boiling
2. When quite done, take out of the water and flake off all the flesh from the bones
3. Have ready a quarter of boiled cream or milk and beat the yolks of four eggs and mix with the cream
4. Chop one large onion, a bunch of parsley, a sprig each of thyme and bay leaf, and add to the cream and eggs
5. Let it boil up once, and while boiling, throw in three tablespoonfuls of flour, rubbed perfectly smooth, in a little cream, and about two tablespoonfuls of butter
6. Remove from the fire
7. Have ready a deep dish, well buttered, and put in a layer of fish and then a layer of the sauce, until the dish is full
8. Sprinkle over with bread crumbs
9. Place in the oven and bake about a half hour, or until brown

title: Sherry Cobbler
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Lemonade
- 1/2 Bottle of Sherry or any Other Wine
- Small Bits of Lemon Peel

## Directions

1. To one quart of lemonade add half a bottle of Sherry and proceed to freeze as directed above, or hand around in glasses half full of crushed ice
2. Add a little lemon peel

title: Shirred Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a thin dish, in which they are to be served, having first buttered the bottom of the dish or pan.
2. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper.
3. Pour over a little melted butter.
4. Place in a quick oven, and let them bake until the yolks are set.
5. Serve in the dish in which they have been cooked.

title: Short Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 4 tablespoonfuls butter or 2 of lard
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- A pinch of soda, or 1/4 teaspoonful yeast powder
- Tepid water

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and yeast powder together, if you desire it instead of soda
2. Otherwise sift the flour, add the salt, and mix the lard or butter thoroughly together, rubbing between your hands till there is not a lump left
3. Add sufficient tepid water to make a nice, rather stiff dough
4. Roll out into a thin sheet, about half an inch thick, and criss-cross with a knife lightly in diamond shapes
5. Set in the oven and bake for about fifteen minutes to a light brown
6. Bring to the table split open and buttered, and eaten while hot

title: Short Cake for Fruits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 eggs, well beaten
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- A pinch of soda, or 1/4 teaspoonful yeast powder
- Sour milk or buttermilk if using soda, or sweet milk if using yeast powder
- Strawberries, raspberries, or other fruits for filling

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and yeast powder together, if you desire it instead of soda
2. Otherwise sift the flour, add the salt, and mix the butter thoroughly together, rubbing between your hands till there is not a lump left
3. Add the well-beaten eggs and milk to make a soft dough
4. Divide dough in two, one for the upper and the other for the lower crust
5. Sprinkle thickly between with fruits such as strawberries, raspberries, etc.
6. Bake in the oven
7. The dough should be soft and crisp

title: Shoulder of Mutton Smothered with Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Shoulder of Mutton
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 1/2 Stalk of Celery
- 4 Cloves
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 6 Turnips
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. If the mutton does not appear very tender the process of smothering it will make it so. It is well to beat the leg well with a rolling pin, and you will be sure of good and tender eating.
2. Season well.
3. Slice an onion and one carrot very fine; chop fine a half stalk of celery, and put these, with the shoulder of mutton, into a deep baking pot.
4. Cover well and let the mutton juice permeate the vegetables and brown them.
5. Then add the minced bay leaves and cloves; cover and let these brown, and after ten minutes add one quart of boiling water.
6. Season well again and set on a steady fire, allowing fifteen minutes to every pound.
7. An hour before serving add six whole turnips, which have been peeled and parboiled, and let these remain smothered with the mutton.
8. Serve with the turnips as a garnish.

title: Shrewsbury Biscuit
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 3 ounces of ginger
- 5 eggs
- 1 1/2 lbs. of white sugar
- nutmeg or orange peel for seasoning

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Season with nutmeg or orange peel
3. Bake them in a slow oven

title: Shrewsbury Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- five well beaten eggs
- a nutmeg
- some essence
- about a quart of flour
- currants

## Directions

1. Beat to a cream half a pound of butter, and three-quarters of a pound of sugar
2. add five well beaten eggs, a nutmeg, some essence, and about a quart of flour
3. Sift the flour, mix it well, and drop the mixture with a spoon on buttered tins
4. Add currants if you wish

title: Shrewsbury Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- five well beaten eggs
- a nutmeg
- some essence
- about a quart of flour
- currants (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat to a cream half a pound of butter, and three-quarters of a pound of sugar
2. add five well beaten eggs, a nutmeg, some essence, and about a quart of flour
3. Sift the flour, mix it well
4. drop the mixture with a spoon on buttered tins
5. Add currants if you wish

title: Shrewsbury Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of flour
- 2 large or 3 small eggs
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 quart of good brown sugar
- 2 nutmegs
- orange peel to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in an oven

title: Shrimp Gumbo File
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Fine Lake Shrimp
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Liquor
- 1 Quart of Hot Water
- 1 Large White Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Salt and Black Pepper to Taste
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Filé

## Directions

1. Scald and shell the shrimp, seasoning highly with the boiling water.
2. Put the lard into a kettle, and when hot, add the flour, making a brown roux.
3. When quite brown, without a semblance of burning, add the chopped onion and the parsley.
4. Fry these, and when brown, add the chopped bay leaf.
5. Pour in the hot oyster liquor and the hot water, or use the carefully strained liquor in which the shrimp have been boiled.
6. When it comes to a good boil, and about five minutes before serving, add the shrimp to the gumbo and take off the stove.
7. Then add to the boiling hot liquid about two tablespoonfuls of the Filé, thickening according to taste.
8. Season again with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Serve immediately, with boiled rice.

title: Shrimp Jambalaya
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 Cups of Rice
- 80 Lake Shrimp
- 3 Tomatoes
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 0.5 Teaspoonful of Chili Pepper
- Salt and Pepper
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Parsley
- Cayenne to Taste
- 3 Quarts of Broth or Water, or Oyster Water

## Directions

1. Chop two onions very fine, and put them in a saucepan to brown with a tablespoonful of butter.
2. After a few minutes add a tablespoonful of flour and stir well.
3. Then add chopped thyme, bay leaf and parsley, and two cloves of garlic, minced very fine.
4. Let all of this fry five minutes longer, and be careful not to let it burn or brown too much.
5. Add a half teaspoonful of Chili pepper, and three large tomatoes, chopped fine, and also add the juice.
6. Let all brown or simmer for ten minutes longer.
7. When cooked, add three quarts of broth or water, or oyster water which has been heated to the boiling point.
8. Let all boil well, and then add the lake shrimp, which you will already have boiled according to recipe.
9. Let the mixture boil again for five minutes, and add one cup and a half of rice, which has been well washed.
10. Mix all well, and let boil for a half or three-quarters of an hour longer, stirring every once in a while.
11. Serve hot.

title: Shrimp Pie
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- picked shrimp
- 2 large slices of bread
- 2 glasses of wine
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg and mace

## Directions

1. Have a large plate of picked shrimp
2. Cut off the crust from 2 large slices of bread, and make the crumb into a paste with 2 glasses of wine and 1 large spoonful of butter
3. Add as much pepper, salt, nutmeg and mace, as you like
4. Mix the shrimps with the bread
5. Bake in a dish or shells

title: Shrimp Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Lake Shrimp
- 2 Slices of Stale Bread
- 2 Glasses of White Wine
- 1 Blade of Mace
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1/2 of a Ground Nutmeg
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Tablespoonfull of Butter
- 5 Tomatoes
- 1/2 of a Celery Stalk
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Pint of Oyster Liquor
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil and pick about 100 shrimp.
2. Take two large slices of stale bread and break off the crusts, grating this fine.
3. Moisten the bread with two glasses of white wine, and season highly with salt, pepper, a dash of Cayenne, ground nutmeg, chopped mace, thyme and parsley.
4. Mix the shrimp with the bread and bake in a dish.
5. Sprinkle over the grated crusts and dot with butter.
6. To make the sauce: take a pint of shrimp, boiled and picked; put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan.
7. Add the shrimp and four or five tomatoes, chopped fine; a little celery, thyme, one bay leaf, chopped fine; parsley (chopped) and mix thoroughly.
8. Let it cook for about three or four minutes and add a half pint of oyster stock.
9. Pour this sauce over the sliced pie.

title: Shrimp Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 River or Lake Shrimp
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Bunch of Celery
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Salt and Pepper
- Sliced Lemons
- Beets
- Celery Tips
- Mayonnaise Sauce

## Directions

1. Boil and pick the shrimp.
2. If Lake shrimp are used, serve whole; if River shrimp, slice in two.
3. Season well with salt and pepper.
4. Chop celery fine and add a little onion.
5. Place the shrimp in the salad dish and pour over all fine Mayonnaise Sauce.
6. Garnish with sliced hard-boiled eggs, sliced lemon, beets and celery tops.

title: Shrimp Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Cold Boiled Shrimp
- 1 Head of Crisp Lettuce
- Mayonnaise Sauce
- Salt and pepper
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Sliced beets
- Celery

## Directions

1. Take Lake Shrimp and River Shrimp combined, if you have them.
2. Cut the larger Lake Shrimp into two.
3. Season well with salt and pepper.
4. Chop some lettuce. Season well.
5. Place first a layer of shrimp and then of lettuce, and spread over all a Mayonnaise Sauce.
6. Garnish nicely with sliced hard-boiled eggs, sliced beets and celery and lettuce tips, and serve very cold.

title: Shrimps in Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 River Shrimp
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Tomato Catsup
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil the shrimp and pick.
2. Put them into a salad dish.
3. Season well with black pepper and salt and a dash of Cayenne.
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of tomato catsup to every half pint of shrimps.
5. Garnish with lettuce leaves and hard-boiled egg and serve.

title: Sicilian Biscuit Dropped on Tins
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- twelve ounces of powdered and sifted sugar
- ten ounces of flour
- vanilla for flavoring
- butter
- white sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and sugar together in a stewpan on the fire, until the batter feels warm to the touch
2. Remove it from the fire, and stir it thoroughly until it becomes cold
3. Add the flour and flavor with vanilla
4. Butter some paper and place it on the baking tins or pans
5. Drop the cake mixture in round or ovals on the buttered paper
6. Sift white sugar over the biscuit
7. Bake in a slow oven

title: Silver Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two coffee-cups of butter
- two pints of fine white sugar
- beaten whites of eighteen eggs
- four pints of flour
- one pint maizena or corn starch flour
- two coffee-cups of water
- almond flavoring
- two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder

## Directions

1. Cream two coffee-cups of butter with two pints of fine white sugar
2. add the beaten whites of eighteen eggs, and four pints of flour
3. thin this mixture gradually as you beat in the flour and eggs, by pouring in two coffee-cups of water
4. Flavor with almond
5. sift the flour and add two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
6. bake in a large pan

title: Silver Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pints of sugar
- two cups of butter
- two cups of cold water
- whites of eighteen eggs
- four pints of flour
- two teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
- almond flavoring

## Directions

1. cream the butter and sugar together
2. Add two cups of cold water
3. beat to a froth the whites of eighteen eggs, mix them with the butter and sugar
4. mix flour and yeast powder together, and stir gently into the batter
5. Flavor with almond

title: Silver Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. sugar
- 1 lb. sifted flour
- 6 ounces butter
- whites of 14 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter to a cream
2. Add the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
3. Add the flour
4. Bake

title: Silver Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of pulverized sugar
- ¾ lb. of flour
- 7 ounces of butter
- whites of 14 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder

## Directions

1. put the powder in the flour before sifting
2. cream the butter and sugar
3. add the eggs
4. add the flour
5. put 2 or 3 thicknesses of paper around the pan
6. put an iron lid over the top until the cake is pretty well cooked through

title: Silver Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of butter
- 2 cups of white sugar
- whites of 6 eggs, beaten stiff
- 1 cup of sweet milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 4 cups of flour
- vanilla, bitter almond or rose water for flavoring

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the beaten egg whites
3. Mix the soda into the sweet milk and add to the mixture
4. Stir the cream of tartar into the flour and add to the cake
5. Flavor with vanilla, bitter almond or rose water
6. Bake

title: Silver Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Cup of Butter
- 1 1/2 Cups of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- The Whites of 6 Eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Add the well-beaten sugar and beat well
3. Add the yolks of the eggs and beat till very light
4. Add the milk and mix carefully
5. Sift the flour and baking powder together, and add gradually to the mixture
6. Turn into a buttered pan and bake in a moderate oven for forty-five minutes

title: Silver Cake (Second Version)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lbs. of flour
- yeast powder
- whites of 28 eggs
- 2 lbs. of sugar
- 12 ounces of butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour, yeast powder, egg whites, sugar, and butter
2. Flavor to taste
3. Bake

title: Silvery Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla or Lemon
- 2 Cups of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and the butter to a cream
2. Set the milk to boil, and as it begins to simmer, add the sugar and butter
3. Let it simmer gently for a few minutes, and then take from the fire and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Add the essence, stirring till thoroughly mixed
5. Add the brandy and serve, either hot or cold, with any berry pudding.

title: Simple Okra Gombo
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- veal brisket
- okra pods
- onion
- red pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Chop a pound of beef and half a pound of veal brisket into squares an inch thick; slice three dozen okra pods, one onion, a pod of red pepper, and fry all together.
2. When brown pour in half a gallon of water; add more as required.

title: Simple Okra Gombo
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of beef
- half a pound of veal brisket
- three dozen okra pods
- one onion
- a pod of red pepper

## Directions

1. Chop a pound of beef and half a pound of veal brisket into squares an inch thick;
2. slice three dozen okra pods, one onion, a pod of red pepper, and fry all together.
3. When brown pour in half a gallon of water;
4. add more as it boils away.
5. Serve with rice as usual.

title: Six-Ounce Plum Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of stoned raisins
- six ounces washed and dried currants
- six ounces of bread crumbs
- six ounces of suet
- six eggs
- half a nutmeg
- half a lemon
- half a glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Mix six ounces of stoned raisins, six ounces washed and dried currants, six ounces of bread crumbs, six ounces of suet and six eggs
2. Flavor with half a nutmeg, half a lemon and half a glass of brandy
3. Mix all these ingredients together
4. Put the pudding into a mould, or floured cloth, and boil three hours

title: Small Oranges Preserved Whole
body:
## Ingredients

- small green sweet oranges
- thin clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Take small, green, sweet oranges, and pierce them several times with a penknife till very soft
2. Let them stand for three days in water, which must be renewed fresh every day
3. Put them in a dish after the third day, pour thin clarified sugar over them
4. Prepare a syrup as directed
5. Add the oranges when it begins to boil
6. Cook till they are tender, and proceed in the usual way

title: Small Thread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Dip the forefinger into iced water, and then into the boiling sugar, and instantly again into the iced water, to prevent burning the finger
2. Pinch between the index finger and thumb
3. If a small thread forms, which breaks when the attempt is made to pull apart through the fingers, the proper degree is reached

title: Smothered Breast of Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- Shoulder and Breast of a Lamb
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Carrot
- 2 Onions
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- ½ Can of Tomatoes
- A Sprig Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Bread Crumbs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Pepper and Salt to Taste
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Select nice, fresh breast and shoulder of lamb. Have the butcher remove all the bones; wash or wipe carefully with a damp towel.
2. Take one cup of bread crumbs, which have been wet and squeezed, and season well with one grated onion and clove of garlic, and chopped parsley, thyme and bay leaf, and spices to taste, put in a frying pan with one tablespoonful of Butter, and fry about five minutes.
3. Place this dressing into the open side of the lamb, and roll it up in its own meat, and tie it securely with thin strips of twine that the dressing may not escape in baking.
4. Slice the carrot, onion and turnip very fine, and fry in a tablespoonful of butter in a deep pan.
5. When brown, add the lamb and cover and let it simmer for about fifteen minutes.
6. Then add the tomatoes and let them brown; then add just enough boiling water to cover the meat (one pint).
7. Set the pot back on the stove and let it simmer gently and steadily for about three hours, or according to the size of the roll.
8. Serve with the vegetables dished around and with its own gravy.

title: Smothered Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard

## Directions

1. After cleaning the young chicken, split down the back and dredge with salt and pepper
2. Put a tablespoonful of lard into the frying pan, and, when it is hot, add the chicken
3. Let it simmer gently for about fifteen minutes, then add a half cup of water, and set back on the stove, and let it simmer gently and steadily for about an hour
4. Serve with a garnish of chopped parsley

title: Smothered Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Young Hen
- 1/4 Pound of Nice Bacon
- 1 Lemon
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Onions
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 1/2 Cups Broth
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Thyme
- Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the chicken nicely, and, after taking out the entrails, truss it as in roasting turkey
2. Place in the frying pan small pieces of fat bacon, cut in very slender strips of about the size of your finger
3. Place over this slices of lemon, very fine, and cover again with slender bits of bacon
4. Moisten this with a half cup of water and lay over two carrots, cut in thin slices, and two onions, cut likewise, and a teaspoonful each of thyme, parsley and one bay leaf, minced fine
5. Place on top of this the chicken and cover closely
6. Let it cook on a good fire for three-quarters of an hour, or a half hour, if the chicken is exceedingly tender
7. When done, take out the chicken, add one-half cup of broth to the liquor in which it has been boiling
8. Stir well and season highly, and pour over the chicken and serve

title: Smothered Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Small Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Quart of Broth
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Put the onions into cold water, and remove their skins.
2. Then put them into a saucepan, and cover with a soup stock (pot-au-feu or bouillon), or otherwise use water.
3. Let them stew slowly for an hour and a half, till they are almost falling to pieces.
4. Then drain the onions through a colander, and save the stock.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a frying pan, and add a tablespoonful of flour, and make a Brown Roux.
6. When brown, add a half pint of the broth in which the onions were boiled.
7. Season well with salt and pepper and a dash of Cayenne.
8. Put the onions into this and let them simmer gently for about twenty minutes longer.
9. Serve hot.

title: Smothered Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 Parsnips
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish

## Directions

1. Boil the parsnips as directed, and then cut into strips of the length of the parsnip, and half an inch in width.
2. Put a big cookingspoonful of butter into the saucepan, and add the parsnips.
3. Sprinkle well with salt and pepper.
4. Cover and let them fry, but only slightly brown, on either side.
5. Serve with chopped parsley as a garnish.

title: Smothered Quail
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fine Fat Quails
- 1/2 Carrot
- 1/2 of an Onion
- 3/4 Cupful of Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 6 Thin Strips of Bacon

## Directions

1. Select fine fat quails, clean, singe and wipe well.
2. Truss neatly and cover with a thin layer of bacon.
3. Place two tablespoonfuls of butter in a saucepan.
4. Place the quails in the pan and add half of an onion and carrot minced very fine.
5. Cover and let the quails brown to a nice golden color.
6. Moisten with a half cup of water and set the pan in the oven.
7. Cover with buttered paper and let the quails cook for twenty minutes.
8. Serve on a hot dish nicely garnished with parsley sprigs or lettuce leaves.

title: Smothered Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh tongue of beef
- 1 pint of liquor in which the tongue was boiled
- 2 onions, minced very fine
- 3 sprigs each of thyme and parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 cloves
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 glass of white wine (if desired)
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 square inch of ham, well chopped
- 2 carrots sliced

## Directions

1. Parboil the tongue for about ten minutes.
2. Skin and clean well.
3. Chop an onion very fine, and brown this in a tablespoonful of butter.
4. When brown, add the tongue, which you will have arranged by fastening the thick part to the tip with a skewer, as for roast beef.
5. Let it cook, smothering slowly, for fifteen minutes, and then add another onion sliced nicely.
6. Let this brown, and add one square inch of ham, well chopped, two carrots sliced, and a bay leaf, and two sprigs of thyme, minced fine.
7. Brown again, and add a pint of broth.
8. Season well, and add a glass of white wine, and then let it smother for one hour and a half longer, turning every quarter, so that every part may cook thoroughly.
9. Serve with the sauce in which it was cooked, or with a Sauce Piquante.

title: Snap Beans, Stewed and Boiled
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- snap beans
- boiling water
- half cup of broth
- cup of milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pick and snap them when green and tender
2. Cut them small
3. Throw into boiling water
4. Let them cook gently for two hours
5. Stir in a half cup of broth, and a cup of milk
6. Let them stew in this for half an hour longer
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste

title: Snow Balls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 10 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls of mace
- 2 teaspoonfuls of nutmeg
- 1 large wineglass of brandy
- 1 large wineglass of wine
- 1 lb. of citron
- 1 lb. of almonds
- rose water

## Directions

1. Blanch almonds and beat fine with rose water
2. Separate the whites of the eggs from the yolks
3. Mix the yolks and sugar together
4. Beat the whites to a very light froth, then add to the yolks and sugar
5. Cream the butter light and mix in by degrees
6. Beat all together very light
7. Mix in the flour, beat it till quite smooth
8. Add the mace and nutmeg, then the wine and brandy, and lastly the raisins, citron and almonds
9. Stir all well together
10. Butter the cups, fill them nearly full, and bake in a quick oven

title: Snow Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake
- wine
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls finely powdered sugar
- 1 glass sweet wine
- 1 pint cream

## Directions

1. Put some thin slices of sponge cake in the bottom of a dish
