2. Pour in wine enough to soak it
3. Beat up the whites of 3 eggs very hard
4. Add to it 2 tablespoonfuls of finely powdered sugar, a glass of sweet wine, 1 pint of cream
5. Beat well and pour over the cake

title: Snow Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fresh, Sweet Cream
- 8 Tablespoonfuls of White Powdered Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Coffeespoon of Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. Add to one pint of fine, fresh, sweet cream, eight tablespoonfuls of white powdered sugar, the whites of two eggs, well beaten to a froth, one coffeespoon of Orange Flower Water, and whip all together steadily till the cream rises, taking it out as it does, and putting in another bowl, as in Crème Fouettée, till all is drained.
2. Place it in a little basket, lined with a fine linen napkin, and serve as quickly as possible.

title: Snow Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of Four Eggs
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 1/4 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sherry

## Directions

1. Whip the cream and set on ice.
2. Beat the whites of the eggs to a foam, and then add the sifted white sugar, and beat steadily till the mixture becomes very stiff and glossy.
3. Add the Sherry and Vanilla.
4. Stir the whipped cream carefully into the meringue, and set it in small custard glasses to get cold.
5. Serve very cold, as a summer dessert.

title: Snow Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- One half package of Coxe's gelatine
- 1 cup of cold water
- 1 J cups of sugar
- 1 cup of boiling water
- Juice of 1 lemon
- Whites of 4 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- Yolks of 4 eggs
- Grated rind of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Pour over gelatine 1 cup of cold water, and add 1 J cups of sugar
2. When soft, add 1 cup of boiling water, juice of 1 lemon and the whites of 4 well beaten eggs
3. Beat all together until very light
4. Put in glass dish
5. Pour over it custard made as follows: heat 1 pint of milk with yolks of 4 eggs and grated rind of 1 lemon and boil

title: Snow Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- % Box of Gelatine
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- 2 Cups of Sugar
- The Whites of 3 Eggs
- The Juice of 3 Lemons
- The Yolks of the Eggs
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in the boiling water
2. When nearly cool, add one cup of powdered sugar and the juice of one lemon
3. Beat well and lightly, and strain
4. When cold, beat as white as snow, and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Beat all thoroughly, and pour into a mold
6. Stand in a cold place for four hours to harden
7. Beat the yolks of the eggs together with a half cup of sugar till very light
8. Stir them into the milk, which should be boiling
9. Let them cook, stirring constantly, for two or three minutes
10. Add a teaspoonful of Vanilla, and set to cool
11. Serve with the pudding, pouring the sauce upon it

title: Snowballs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- 1 pint milk
- 1/2 cup white sugar
- the whites of six eggs
- the juice of one lemon
- two tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- a cream sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the rice with the milk, and add the whites of three eggs, well beaten with the sugar.
2. Stir well, and flavor with the juice of one lemon.
3. Take from the fire as it thickens well, and set in a dish to cool.
4. Form the rice into small balls of about two and a half inches square.
5. Have the rest of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth, with two tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar.
6. Cover the tops of the balls with the mixture and place in the stove to heat.
7. Let the balls remain about four minutes, without browning.
8. Take out, and serve with a Cream Sauce or just as they are.

title: Soda and Milk Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Heaping Teaspoonful of Lard
- 2 Cups of Sour Milk
- ⅜ Teaspoonful of Soda
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and add the salt and lard, rubbing them in thoroughly with your hands.
2. Then dissolve the soda in a little warm water and add to the milk, and work rapidly and lightly.
3. Then lay on a biscuit board or a block of wood, and beat till the surface of the dough is full of air bubbles, or blisters.
4. Roll the dough out to about a quarter of an inch in thickness, cut into round biscuits with a biscuit cutter, or square ones with a knife.
5. Stick here and there over the surface with a fork, and bake for about six minutes in a quick oven.

title: Soda Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of flour
- a teaspoonful of tartaric acid
- milk or buttermilk
- a little saleratus or soda

## Directions

1. Rub the tartaric acid into the flour perfectly dry
2. Make a batter of milk or buttermilk
3. Add a little saleratus or soda

title: Soda Cracker Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 soda crackers
- 3 teacups of cold water
- 2 lemons
- 2 teacups of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak 4 soda crackers in 3 teacups of cold water
2. Add 2 lemons and 2 teacups of sugar

title: Soda Cracker Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Four soda crackers
- 3 teacups of cold water
- 2 lemons
- 2 teacups of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak four soda crackers in 3 teacups of cold water
2. Add 2 lemons and 2 teacups of sugar

title: Soda Crackers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- 2 cups sweet milk or water
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour several times and add the salt
2. Mix well
3. Rub in the butter thoroughly
4. Add the soda, which you will have dissolved in a little boiling water, and the milk, and mix all well together
5. Knead well and put upon the biscuit board and beat with a rolling pin for upwards of half an hour, frequently rolling the dough over, and beating hard until the air bubbles cover every part, above and below
6. Roll out into a nice square even sheet of dough, about one-eighth of an inch in thickness, and cut into nice square cakes
7. Stick through and through with a fork here and there over the surface in even rows
8. Bake in a moderate oven till they are hard and crisp, but not brown
9. Hang in a muslin bag for about two days to thoroughly dry

title: Soda or Milk Biscuit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of sifted flour
- the yolk of an egg
- a teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- a little milk
- a teaspoonful of salt
- milk

## Directions

1. To a pound of sifted flour, put the yolk of an egg
2. Dissolve a teaspoonful of carbonate of soda in a little milk
3. Put it and a teaspoonful of salt to the flour, with as much milk as will make a stiff paste
4. Work it well together
5. Beat it for some minutes with a rolling-pin
6. Roll it very thin
7. Cut it in round or square biscuits
8. Bake in a moderate oven until they are crisp

title: Soda Teacakes Without Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of butter
- one teaspoonful of fine soda
- one cup of milk
- half a nutmeg
- flour

## Directions

1. beat it to a cream
2. Dissolve a teaspoonful of fine soda in a cup of milk, and pour it into the batter
3. add half a nutmeg and flour to make a stiff batter
4. Bake in tin squares or a shallow pan, cook twenty minutes

title: Soft Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups of flour
- 2 cups of molasses
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 8 eggs
- 1 teacup of allspice and ginger mixed

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in a pan or small cups

title: Soft-Shelled Crabs, Fried
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- rolled cracker
- hot lard
- salt
- favorite sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the crabs properly
2. Dip them into rolled cracker
3. And fry them in hot lard salted
4. They must be dried carefully before frying
5. Or they will not brown well
6. Serve with any favorite sauce

title: Sontag Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of fresh butter washed in rose water
- 1 lb. of treacle
- 1 nutmeg grated
- a little pounded mace and cinnamon
- 1 ounce of pounded ginger
- 1 ounce of blanched sweet almonds
- 2 well beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Melt the butter with the treacle
2. When nearly cold, stir in the eggs and the rest of the ingredients
3. Mix all well together
4. Make it into round cakes
5. Bake them upon tins

title: Sorrel au Gras
body:
## Ingredients

- Sorrel
- Croutons
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the sorrel in exactly the same manner as spinach, or Epinards au Jus.
2. Reject all fibrous portions.
3. After you have scalded it, drain thoroughly.
4. Then chop and press through a colander.
5. Garnish nicely with Croutons fried in butter.

title: Sorrel au Maigre
body:
## Ingredients

- Sorrel
- Milk
- 2 Eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as Sorrel au Gras, only, instead of using broth or gravy, add milk and the well-beaten yolks of two eggs.

title: Sorrel Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- A Small Bunch of Fresh Sorrel
- 3 Quarts of Boiling Water
- 1 Cup of Cream or Rich Milk
- 1 Cup of Mashed Potatoes
- 4 Eggs
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 3 Leaves of Lettuce
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- A Pinch of Nutmeg
- Pepper and Salt to Taste

## Directions

1. Wash the leaves and stem them, the entire length of the leaf.
2. Chop them fine until you have a quantity equal to a pint or two teacupfuls.
3. Chop the other vegetables and put these and the sorrel into a saucepan with the butter.
4. Cover and let them stew gently for ten minutes.
5. Add the flour, which has been well mixed with a little water.
6. Pour gradually, stirring always, into the three quarts of boiling water.
7. Beat the yolks of the eggs and mix with a little cream or milk in a tureen.
8. Rub the rest of the cream or milk smooth with the mashed potato and put into the soup.
9. Add the seasonings.
10. Prepare toast in the form of dice, rubbing them first with the raw onion.
11. Pour some of the boiling soup over the eggs in the tureen and mix very carefully.
12. Put in the pieces of toast, and then add the remainder of the soup.
13. Cover and stand five minutes in a warm oven, and serve hot.

title: Souffle Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- one cup of flour
- one spoonful of sugar
- a piece of butter as large as an egg
- yolks of five eggs
- whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, flour, and butter together.
2. After the batter becomes cold, stir in the yolks of five eggs.
3. Just before baking, stir in the whites.
4. Bake in a quick oven, and serve with sauce.

title: Souffle Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces of sifted flour
- 2 1/2 ounces of granulated sugar
- 1 pint of sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Put on your milk to boil, reserving about a gill to wet the flour
2. When the milk comes to a boil, add the flour which has been wet with the milk, also the butter and sugar
3. Let this thicken well
4. Put it in a bowl to get cold, at least for 1 or 2 hours before you are ready to use it
5. About 1/2 hour before you are ready to use, have the yolks well beaten, which you add by degrees to the cold paste
6. Last of all, add the whites, which have been whipped until very light
7. Have a yellow bowl buttered, put the paste in it, and bake in a hot oven a good 1/2 hour
8. Serve with a hard sauce of butter and sugar, flavored as you wish
9. Serve immediately, as it will fall by standing

title: Soufflés
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Powdered White Sugar
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. To two pounds of powdered sugar add the whites of two eggs and one ounce of Orange Flower Water.
2. Make of all a firm, consistent paste, adding more sugar, if necessary.
3. Roll out on a sheet of paper, sprinkled with sugar, and cut into little pieces, which you will form into balls about the size of a nut.
4. Place them on a wafer sheet on a baking sheet at a distance of two inches, so that they will not touch one another, should they run.
5. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One large leek
- Two carrots
- One bunch of parsley
- Two turnips
- A potato
- One head of celery
- Two leeks
- Two turnips
- Five or six small potatoes
- Six tomatoes
- A leek
- A bunch of parsley
- Six potatoes
- A carrot
- Some cabbage
- Tomatoes
- Potatoes
- Okra
- Green peas
- Lettuce
- New potatoes
- Vermicelli
- Macaroni
- Onion

## Directions

1. Soup must have time to cook, and should always boil gently, that the meat may become tender, and give out its juices. Allow a quart of water and a teaspoonful of salt for each pound of meat. Soup meat must always be put down in cold water. Skim well before it comes to the boiling point, and again skim off superfluous fat before putting in the vegetables. The vegetables most used in soups are carrots, leeks, parsley, turnip, celery, tomatoes, okras, cabbage, cauliflower, peas and potatoes.
2. One large leek, two carrots, one bunch of parsley, two turnips and a potato, will be enough for one pot of soup. One head of celery, two leeks, two turnips, and five or six small potatoes will be enough another time. Six tomatoes skinned, the juice strained from the seeds, a leek, a bunch of parsley, and six potatoes will answer for another style; a carrot, some cabbage, tomatoes, and potatoes will do another time. Okra alone is vegetable enough for a gombo, unless onion is liked with it. Green peas, lettuce, and new potatoes are enough for spring lamb soup. Vermicelli and macaroni are for chicken, lamb or veal soup, with the addition of onion if liked.
3. It is well to prepare the vegetables when the meat is put over the fire to boil; allow a quart of water to a pound of meat. Trim and scrape carrots, then cut or grate them. Wash parsley and cut it small. Pare turnips and cut them in slices a quarter of an inch thick. Cut leeks in thick slices. Cut celery in half lengths; the delicate green leaves give a fine flavor to the soup.
4. Pour boiling water on tomatoes, which will cause the skins to peel off easily; when cool, squeeze out the seeds, and reserve the juice for use in soup.
5. Shave cabbage in thin slices. Slice okra for gombo or okra soup. Pare the potatoes, shell the peas, and cut off green corn from the cob, for all these add fine flavor to soup.
6. To color soup brown, use browned flour or a little burnt sugar. Spinach leaves give a fine green color. Pound the leaves, tie them in a cloth, and squeeze out all the juice which add to the soup five minutes before serving. This is also used to give color to mock-turtle soup.
7. You may color soup red by putting in the strained juice of tomatoes, or the whole tomato, if it is run through a sieve; grated carrot gives a fine amber color; okra gives a pale green.
8. For white soups, which are made of veal, lamb, and chicken, white vegetables are best, such as rice, pearl barley, vermicelli, and macaroni; the thickening should then be made of unbrowned flour.

title: Soup Et Bouilli
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six or eight pounds of a brisket of beef
- Three carrots
- Four turnips
- Two onions
- Six cloves
- Two heads of celery
- One clove of garlic
- A bunch of sweet herbs
- A little salt
- A piece of butter
- A little flour
- A French roll
- A tablespoonful of French mustard

## Directions

1. Put the beef into a pot and cover it with water, and when it boils take off the scum as it rises;
2. Then draw it to the side of the fire to stew slowly for five or six hours, with the carrots, turnips, celery, garlic, bunch of sweet herbs, and the onions stuck with cloves.
3. When done lay the bouilli on a hot dish, and strew over it some carrots, turn, and the stalks of celery, previously boiled and cut into shapes.
4. Add to it a sauce made of a little of the soup, thickened with flour fried in butter, and seasoned with pepper and salt.
5. Strain the soup over a French roll placed at the bottom of the tureen and serve.
6. The bouilli may have a spoonful of French mustard added to the soup sauce.

title: Soupe la Reine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- water
- thyme
- sweet-bay
- parsley
- onion
- flour
- rice
- eggs
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Boil two chickens in water with thyme, sweet-bay and parsley
2. When cooked (not to pieces), take them out of the water
3. cut up the breasts in small pieces the size of dice
4. fry a few pieces of onion without coloring them
5. add a little flour and the water that the chickens were boiled in
6. add a little rice and the balance of the chickens, meat and bones, chopped fine
7. Boil all together
8. when thoroughly cooked strain through a colander and put back to boil, stirring constantly
9. When it comes to a boil remove it from the fire
10. add the beaten yolks of a few eggs and a little cold milk, stirring continually
11. Keep the soup in bain-marie
12. When ready to serve put the small pieces of the breasts in a soup-dish and pour the soup over them

title: Soupe Maigre, Without Meat, For Lent
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of butter
- Six onions sliced
- Two heads of celery cut small
- One-half a head of white cabbage
- A bunch of chopped parsley
- Three rolled crackers
- Two quarts of boiling milk, or milk and water
- Two well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Melt half a pound of butter in a stewpan, put in six onions sliced;
2. Add two heads of celery cut small, one-half a head of white cabbage, and a bunch of chopped parsley;
3. Let them boil twenty minutes, then stir in three rolled crackers;
4. Pour in two quarts of boiling milk, or milk and water;
5. Let this boil up gently for half an hour, and just before serving stir in two well-beaten eggs.

title: Sour Orange Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One gallon juice of sour oranges
- Four gallons of water
- Twenty pounds of sugar
- A quart of uncooked orange juice

## Directions

1. Take one gallon juice of sour oranges, four gallons of water, and twenty pounds of sugar
2. Boil this mixture in a vessel large enough to hold it, and skim it as it boils until no more scum rises
3. Pour it into a flannel bag and strain
4. Put it in a cask, adding to it a quart of uncooked orange juice
5. Let it ferment

title: Sour Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Small Cucumbers
- Boiling brine
- 24 Whole Spices
- 1 Ounce of Mustard Seed
- 12 Small Red Peppers
- 6 Blades of Mace
- 8 Cloves
- 2 Onions
- Boiling vinegar

## Directions

1. Take about 100 small Cucumbers, and place them in a jar.
2. Cover them with boiling brine that will be strong enough to bear an egg.
3. Let them stand for several days.
4. Then take them out of the brine, wipe them well, and place them in clean jars.
5. Add two dozen whole spices, one ounce of mustard seed, and six blades of mace, eight cloves, two chopped onions, and the red peppers.
6. Cover them with boiling vinegar and cork.
7. In several weeks they will be ready for use.

title: Sour Stew
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tablespoonful of butter
- sliced onion
- fish
- boiling water
- six eggs
- four lemons
- teaspoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan a tablespoonful of butter and a sliced onion
2. Let it simmer
3. Put in the fish, sliced about an inch thick
4. Add boiling water enough to cover the fish
5. Make a sauce of six eggs, four lemons, and a teaspoonful of flour
6. Add this mixture to the stew when cool

title: Sour-Milk Doughnuts Without Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of flour
- three eggs
- three-fourths of a pound of sugar
- half a cup of shortening
- a teaspoonful of soda
- buttermilk
- boiling lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix flour, eggs, sugar, and shortening
2. Add a teaspoonful of soda and mix to a soft dough with buttermilk
3. Roll out and cut them
4. Fry in boiling lard

title: Southern Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- nice tart apples
- sugar
- 1 lemon
- stale rusks
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Lay alternately in a baking dish slices of nice tart apples
2. On these sprinkle sugar, and the grated oily rind of 1 lemon, and then crumbs of stale rusks which have been soaked in milk
3. Then more slices of apples, sugar and crumbs of rusks
4. Cut very thin slices of butter and lay thinly on the top
5. Over this sift thickly pulverized sugar
6. Bake 1 hour, and send to table in the same dish

title: Soy
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a common size jar of anchovies
- 1 quart of mushroom catsup
- 1 quart of walnut pickle
- 1 gallon of Maderia wine
- black mustard seed

## Directions

1. Bruise and strain the anchovies
2. Add mushroom catsup, walnut pickle, Maderia wine and a little black mustard seed
3. Boil ½ hour
4. Bottle and cork tight
5. Seal with wax and in 10 days the soy will be fit for use

title: Spanish Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of good brown sugar
- 4 well beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the flour till quite fine and smooth
2. Add the brown sugar
3. Roll fine
4. Mix all together with the beaten eggs
5. Break the paste into small bits
6. Bake upon floured tins

title: Spanish Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Butter
- 1 Pound of Sifted Flour
- 1 Pound of Good Brown Sugar
- 6 Well-Beaten Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream.
2. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs, and then alternately the flour, with which the baking powder has been sifted, and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Bake in a buttered cake pan for about an hour in a moderate oven.

title: Spanish Canapés
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Circular Pieces of Buttered Toast
- 1 Cup of Finely Minced White Fleshed Fish
- 3 Sweet Pickles
- 1 Tablespoonful of Madras Chutney
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Hollandaise Sauce
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Parmesan Cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare circular pieces of buttered toast.
2. Make a mixture of a cup of any white-fleshed fish, the Madras Chutney moistened with the Hollandaise Sauce and minced pickles, all pounded together.
3. Spread this over the toast and dredge with grated Parmesan Cheese.
4. Set in the oven and bake for five minutes.

title: Spanish Courtbouillon
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Redfish or Red Snapper (equal to 3 pounds)
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Large Onion
- 6 Tomatoes
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Basil
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Cup of White Wine
- 2 Pints of Water
- Salt and Pepper
- Vinegar or White Wine

## Directions

1. Buy in the French Market four or six slices of Redfish.
2. Make a Roux by putting one tablespoonful of lard into the stew pan, and when it is hot, stir in gradually two tablespoonfuls of flour.
3. Add one large chopped onion, six tomatoes (chopped), the chopped parsley, clove of garlic, sweet basil and thyme, all chopped very fine.
4. Add two bay leaves whole.
5. When it browns nicely without burning, pour in about two pints of water and let it come to a boil.
6. Rub the fish well with salt and pepper, and pour over it a cup of boiling vinegar, if you have no white wine.
7. Put the fish slice by slice into the pot and let it simmer for about a half hour, or until the flesh begins to be soft.
8. Remove from the fire, take out of the pot, and lay the slices in a dish.
9. Take the bay leaves out of the gravy, pour the gravy over the fish, and serve with garnishes of sliced lemon.

title: Spanish Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- The Yolks of 5 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/2 Pint of Gelatine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 1 Pint of Cream
- The Whites of 5 Eggs
- Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine and whip one pint of cream.
2. Set the milk to boil, and add the gelatine, which you will have dissolved in two tablespoonfuls of milk.
3. Have the eggs and sugar beaten very light, and, as the milk boils up, add the sugar and eggs, but do not let them boil, or the eggs will curdle.
4. Stir well, and turn the gelatine, which you will have dissolved well, into the milk.
5. Then remove from the fire and strain, and add the essence of vanilla.
6. Have the mold ready, clean and well rinsed and turn the cream into it.
7. Set it on ice to harden.
8. When ready to serve, make a cream by whipping the whites of the eggs into the cream which has been whipped with powdered sugar.
9. Turn the cream into a dish, heap this delicious meringue cream over it, and serve very cold.

title: Spanish Flummery
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce isinglass
- 1 pint water
- yolks of 8 eggs
- juice of 2 large lemons
- rind of 1 lemon
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1 quart good Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Boil isinglass in water till dissolved
2. Beat up the yolks of 8 eggs, the juice of 2 large lemons, and the rind of 1, 1/2 lb. of sugar
3. Add all these to the isinglass, stirring it over the fire till it boils
4. Add to it 1 quart of good Madeira wine
5. Strain it through a sieve
6. Keep stirring it till nearly cold, and then pour it into moulds

title: Spanish Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Ounces of Sweet Almonds
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- Yolks of 12 Eggs
- Grated Zest of 2 Oranges
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar almost to a syrup
2. Add the pounded almonds, and let them simmer gently for five minutes
3. Add the flavoring and let them simmer five minutes longer over a slow fire of smothered charcoal
4. Stir occasionally
5. At the end of ten minutes add the yolks of the eggs, beaten very light
6. Stir the paste over the kitchen fire with a quickened heat until it becomes firm and compact
7. Remove from the fire, and when it is cool roll it in your hands, which should be slightly greased with oil of sweet almonds
8. Lay them out in small cakes on wafer paper, spread on a baking sheet
9. Bake at a quick heat

title: Spanish Omelet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- a cup of chopped ham
- two small onions
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Beat up six eggs until quite light
2. add to them a cup of chopped ham and two small onions minced very fine
3. The onions should be cooked a little before being put into the eggs, or they will not be cooked enough
4. When mixed together put it into a thick-bottomed pan and commence rolling
5. When it is a light brown, give it the last roll
6. let it lie a moment in the pan, then dish it
7. Put fresh butter as it goes to table

title: Spanish Rolls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of good flour
- a pinch of salt
- 1 spoonful of yeast
- 2 eggs well beaten
- ½ pint of milk
- 1 ounce of butter

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, salt, yeast, eggs and milk together
2. Knead it and set it to rise
3. Next morning work in the butter
4. Make the dough into small rolls and bake them
5. The top crust should not be hard

title: Spanish Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- Sliced Tomatoes
- 2 Dozen Pickled Onions
- 1/2 Pint of Mayonnaise Dressing

## Directions

1. Make of sliced tomatoes and pickled onions, prettily arranged around a small bed of Mayonnaise heaped in the center.

title: Spanish Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Brisket or Veal
- Bones of Beef
- 1 Quart of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1/2 Can Mushrooms or 1/4 Can Truffles
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Wineglass of Sherry
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take a good quantity of bones, place in a quart of boiling water, and make a strong consommé, seasoning well with salt and pepper
2. Take a piece of the brisket or neck of the beef, and roast rare, so that the blood spurts out when pricked with a needle
3. Cut it in pieces of about one inch square
4. Take two tablespoonfuls of lard and three of flour, and brown slightly, stirring all the time
5. Add the water of the consommé, which has been reduced to about half a pint, pouring it in slowly and stirring constantly
6. Add all the pieces of the roast beef which you have cut
7. Add three carrots, two cloves of garlic, one onion, a herb bouquet, tied together, of thyme, parsley and bay leaf
8. Let the whole boil well two hours, stirring every five minutes, until reduced to the consistency of starch
9. Strain well through a strainer or sieve
10. Season to taste, and set back on the stove to cook a few minutes longer
11. Add one wineglass or two tablespoonfuls of Sherry to dissolve, and a half pint of broth
12. Set it to boil again, and add a half can of mushrooms or truffles, as desired
13. Serve hot with meats, fish and fowl

title: Spanish Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Gill of Brandy or Rum (if desired)
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar
- ⅛ Ounce Each of Powdered Nutmeg and Cinnamon
- 6 Slices of Stale Bread
- Lard for Frying

## Directions

1. Beat the egg and sugar well, and add the milk and the rum or brandy if desired.
2. Have ready the slices of stale bread, cut in diamond shapes or square, and let them soak for about a half hour in the mixture.
3. Lay into a pan of boiling lard, and fry to a golden brown.
4. Skim out and drain on brown paper in the mouth of the oven, and place on a hot dish.
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and grated cinnamon and nutmeg, and serve with milk or cream.

title: Spanish Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Ounces of Flour
- 2 Ounces of Sifted Sugar
- 3/4 Pint of Chocolate Water
- 12 Drops of Essence of Vanilla
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Gills of Cream
- 2 Ounces of French Chocolate
- 1/2 Pint of Boiling Water

## Directions

1. Prepare the chocolate water by dissolving well two ounces of French chocolate in a half pint of boiling water
2. Place the sugar, vanilla, eggs and cream in a pan, work all vigorously into a smooth, light batter
3. Add the cold Chocolate Water, beat all together well for ten minutes longer
4. Bake the wafers as directed in recipe for French Wafers, and curl in the form of cornucopias while still warm and able to retain impressions

title: Speckled Trout
body:
## Ingredients

- Filets of Speckled Trout
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Parsley
- Sliced Lemon
- Pepper and Salt
- Sweet oil

## Directions

1. Skin and cut the fish, after cleaning, into filets or square pieces, slicing across the back
2. Remove all the bones and rub well with pepper and salt, and a little sweet oil mixed with the pepper and salt
3. Place on the gridiron or double broiler and broil carefully, turning the broiler frequently to prevent the fish being charred before it is done
4. When nicely browned, place on a bed of parsley and garnish with sliced lemon
5. Serve with Sauce a la Tartare

title: Spice Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups of sifted flour
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of molasses
- ½ cup of sour milk
- 1 teaspoon of soda
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon of ginger
- 1 teaspoon of spice
- 1 teaspoon of cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon of cloves
- 1 teaspoon of mace

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Spice Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- ¾ lb. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 1 small spoonful of cinnamon
- 1 small spoonful of cloves
- 1 wineglass of brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 cup of sour cream

## Directions

1. beat the butter and sugar together until smooth
2. add the flour and eggs by degrees
3. add the brandy and spice alternately
4. dissolve soda in sour cream
5. stir it all well
6. put in the mould to bake

title: Spice Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Flour
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 of a Pound of Butter
- 9 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Ground Allspice
- 1 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Ground Mace
- 1 Wineglasstul Brandy
- 1 Wineglasstul of Sherry or Madeira
- 1 Teaspoonful of Soda
- 1 Cup of Sour Cream
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and the sugar to a cream, and then add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs.
2. Beat till very light.
3. Add half the flour gradually, alternating with half the whites of the eggs, which you will have beaten to a thick froth.
4. Then add the spices, the brandy and the wine.
5. Now add gradually the rest of the flour, and then the remainder of the whites of the eggs.
6. Finally add the sour milk, into which you will have dissolved one teaspoonful of soda in a tablespoonful of boiling water.
7. Stir well, and turn into a buttered Turk's head tin, and bake for one hour in a moderate oven.
8. Use the broom-straw test.

title: Spice Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 3 Eggs
- 0.75 Cup of Sweet Milk
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Molasses
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Ground Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the molasses and the milk
3. Blend well, add the spices, and the nutmeg and lemon extract, and then add the flour, sifted with the baking powder
4. Beat well for fifteen minutes, and then drop on tins lined with buttered paper
5. Bake in a quick oven to a delicate brown

title: Spiced Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds round of beef
- 1 teaspoonful of saltpetre
- 1 tablespoonful of brown sugar
- salt
- allspice
- salt pork
- water

## Directions

1. Take a round of beef of 10 pounds with 1 teaspoonful of saltpetre and 1 tablespoonful of brown sugar to stand in it about 2 days
2. Rub in a slop-bowl full of salt and let stand 10 days, turning it every day
3. Sprinkle over about a spoonful of allspice
4. Cut salt pork in strips size of your finger and have them well dipped in ground allspice
5. Gash the beef with a knife and stuff in thick as you please on both sides
6. Put in a large kettle and cover well with water and the juices from the meat
7. Boil slowly about 6 hours
8. When cold turn and trim

title: Spiced Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- round of beef
- 1 ounce of saltpetre
- one pound of brown sugar
- salt
- a tablespoonful of allspice
- 2 tablespoonful of black pepper
- 2 teaspoonfuls of cloves
- 1 teaspoonful of mace

## Directions

1. Rub well on both sides of the round with 1 ounce of saltpetre
2. Rub in one pound of brown sugar
3. Rub all with salt, into which you will mix a tablespoonful of allspice, 2 of black pepper, 2 teaspoonfuls of cloves and 1 of mace
4. Let it stand 30 days, turning it every other day, and bathing it with the liquor that runs from it

title: Spiced Molasses Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Louisiana Molasses
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Butter
- 2 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Vinegar
- 2 Teaspoonfuls Soda
- 1/4 Teaspoonful Each of Cloves, Allspice and Cinnamon (ground)
- 1 Grated Nutmeg
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1/4 Teaspoonful of Salt

## Directions

1. Warm the molasses and dilute with the vinegar.
2. Beat the butter and the sugar to a cream, and then add the eggs, whites and yolks, well-beaten together.
3. Then add gradually one-half of the flour and the molasses.
4. Add the grated spices and salt.
5. Beat well.
6. Add the soda, dissolved in two tablespoonfuls of boiling water, and beat well.
7. Bake in a moderate oven from thirty to forty-five minutes.

title: Spiced Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 lbs. of nice peaches
- 4 lbs. of sugar
- 1 pint of good vinegar
- whole allspice

## Directions

1. Pare, stone and halve the peaches first
2. Boil the peaches in water till tender
3. Put in vinegar and sugar, with a little whole allspice till done

title: Spiced Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 lbs. of good, ripe peaches
- 4 lbs. of sugar
- 1 pint of vinegar
- cloves
- mace
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Rub peaches with a coarse towel and halve them
2. Put 4 lbs. of sugar and 1 pint of vinegar in a kettle with cloves, mace and cinnamon, each 1 ounce
3. When the syrup is formed throw in the peaches, a few at a time, so as to keep them whole
4. When clear, take out and put in more until all are done
5. Boil the syrup till quite rich
6. Pour over the peaches

title: Spiced Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 lbs. of nice peaches
- 4 lbs. of sugar
- 1 pint of good vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the peaches in water till tender

title: Spiced Tongue
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh tongue
- salt
- saltpetre
- 1 tablespoonful each of allspice, cloves and nutmeg
- 1 lemon peel
- 1 teaspoonful ginger
- 1 teaspoonful thyme
- 1 teaspoonful summer savory
- 1 piece of lemon
- 1 ounce of suet
- pepper

## Directions

1. Corn a fresh tongue 2 days, with salt and a little saltpetre mixed
2. Lay it on one side and bore holes with a steel used for knives
3. Staff these holes with a mixture of 1 tablespoonful each of allspice, cloves and nutmeg, 1 lemon peel grated, 1 teaspoonful ginger, 1 teaspoonful thyme, 1 teaspoonful summer savory, 1 piece of lemon, 1 ounce of suet chopped fine, salt and pepper to taste
4. Place in a dish and under a heavy press for 24 hours
5. Add water to its own brine and boil until it will skin
6. Cut off its roots even or nicely
7. Begin at the small end to roll
8. Bandage very tightly
9. Stuff the paste into the middle
10. Boil 3 hours
11. When done put under heavy press again

title: Spiced Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1.5 Gallons of the Best Cider or White Wine
- 1 Ounce Each of Cloves, Allspice, Mace, Celery and Mustard Seed
- 1 1/3 Ounces Each of Black Pepper, Turmeric and White Ginger

## Directions

1. Mix all the spices together with the vinegar and the bits of ginger
2. Place in an earthen jar and cover closely

title: Spinach a la Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Spinach
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Powdered Sugar
- 6 Croutons
- A Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the spinach as for Plain Boiled Spinach.
2. When ready to take from the saucepan, have ready slices of buttered toast, or Croutons fried in butter.
3. Roll the Croutons in sugar before frying.
4. Heap the spinach in little mounds upon them, sprinkle lightly with sugar, and pour over a Cream Sauce, and serve hot.

title: Spinach a la Cuisiniere
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Spinach
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 6 Toasted Croutons
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the spinach as Plain Boiled Spinach, hash fine, and put in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter.
2. Season with salt and pepper, add a tablespoonful of flour, mixed with a cup of milk and a spoon of melted butter, and serve with toasted dice Croutons on the spinach.

title: Spinach a la Maitre d'Hotel
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Spinach
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- A Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. Prepare the spinach as directed in recipe Plain Boiled Spinach.
2. Pour over a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel, cook a few minutes, and serve hot.

title: Spinach au Jus a la Bourgeoise
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Spinach
- 2 Hard-Boiled or Poached Eggs
- 1 Cup of Gravy or Consomme

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach according to the Plain Boiled Spinach recipe.
2. When ready to take from the saucepan, after adding the butter and seasonings, put it in a saucepan and pour over a cup of roast beef gravy or consomme.
3. Let it cook for twenty minutes, and serve hot.

title: Spinach with Hard-Boiled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Spinach
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Hard-Boiled Eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach and prepare to the point indicated in recipe for Spinach Plain Boiled.
2. Then take the spinach from the saucepan, and arrange in a mound on a dish, and garnish with slices of hard-boiled eggs.

title: Spinach, Plain Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Peck of Fresh Young Spinach
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter

## Directions

1. Select young and tender spinach.
2. Carefully pick it over, and reject all wilted leaves and coarse fibers and nerves.
3. Cut off the roots.
4. Wash and drain it well in cold water, and press out all the water.
5. Put the spinach in a kettle, and add one cupful of water.
6. Place on a moderate fire and let it simmer for ten minutes.

title: Spinach, Plain Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Peck of Fresh Young Spinach
- 1 Cupful of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select young and tender spinach and carefully pick it over, rejecting all wilted leaves and coarse fibers and nerves.
2. Cut off the roots.
3. Wash and drain it well in cold water, and press out all the water.
4. Put the spinach in a kettle, and add one cupful of water.
5. Place on a moderate fire and let it simmer for ten minutes.
6. As soon as it appears to wilt, take off the fire, for that is all the cooking that is needed.
7. Then drain the spinach of all water through a colander, and then press through a very fine sieve, to drain off the remaining water from the vegetable.
8. Chop the spinach very fine.
9. Then put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan, and add the spinach to it.
10. Stir well.
11. Add a few spoonfuls of water, just sufficient to thin, if a little thick, and let it heat thoroughly.
12. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Splendid Fruit Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one and one-fourth pounds of white sifted sugar
- yolks of twelve eggs
- whites of twelve eggs
- a pound of sifted flour
- two pounds of currants
- two pounds of large raisins
- one pound of citron
- a quarter of a pound of sifted flour
- a grated nutmeg
- a glass of wine
- a glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Wash and cream one pound of butter
2. cream one and one-fourth pounds of white sifted sugar with the butter
3. add the yolks, beaten lightly, alternately, with the whites beaten to a stiff froth, of twelve eggs
4. stir in carefully a pound of sifted flour
5. The day before, wash and dry two pounds of currants, pick and seed two pounds of large raisins, and slice one pound of citron
6. Pour all this fruit into a large pan, and dredge it well with a quarter of a pound of sifted flour
7. stir all well into the butter
8. add a grated nutmeg, a glass of wine, and the same of brandy
9. Bake in a large cake mould very carefully, four hours

title: Sponge Biscuit
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar (weight of 10 eggs)
- flour (weight of 6 eggs)
- 10 eggs (whites and yolks separated)
- juice and rind of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks separately
2. When the whites are sufficiently light to adhere to the side of the dish, add the yolks and the sugar
3. Add the flour and the juice and rind of lemon
4. Bake in cups or deep tins half full
5. The bottom of the oven should be warmer than the top

title: Sponge Biscuit with Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a teacup of melted butter
- a teaspoonful of salt
- a cup of good yeast
- a pint of lukewarm water
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir half a teacup of melted butter, a teaspoonful of salt and a cup of good yeast, into a pint of lukewarm water
2. Add flour to make a stiff batter
3. Set this to rise
4. When light drop this mixture onto flat buttered tins, several inches apart
5. Let them stay in a warm place fifteen minutes, before being put in the oven to bake
6. Bake them quickly to a light brown color

title: Sponge Biscuit without Yeast, Made with Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of thick cream
- four eggs
- a little salt
- soda
- flour
- a cup of sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix half a pint of thick cream, four eggs, a little salt, soda, and flour enough to make a stiff batter
2. If too stiff to drop nicely, thin it with a cup of sweet milk
3. Drop on tins
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Sponge Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of wheat flour
- three quarts of boiling water
- twelve spoonfuls of family yeast, or six of brewers'
- flour to mould it
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix three quarts of wheat flour and three quarts of boiling water thoroughly
2. Let them remain until lukewarm
3. Add twelve spoonfuls of family yeast, or six of brewers'
4. Place it where it will be warm and keep the air from it
5. Leave it to rise
6. When it is light, work in flour to mould it, and a little salt
7. Let it stand for a second rising
8. Shape into loaves and bake

title: Sponge Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loaves of bread
- 3 quarts of wheat flour
- 3 quarts of boiling water
- 12 spoonfuls of family yeast
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix the bread, flour and boiling water thoroughly
2. Let the mixture remain until lukewarm
3. Add the yeast
4. Put it in a warm place to rise
5. When light, knead in flour to make it sufficiently stiff
6. Mould off and let it stand for a minute rising
7. Bake it

title: Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Pulverized Sugar
- 3/4 Pound Flour
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs very light, and then add the sugar, and beat till very light.
2. Then add the juice of the lemon, and add gradually one-half of the flour.
3. Have the whites of the eggs beaten to a very stiff froth, so clear that they may be cut with a knife.
4. Add one-half to the cake, and then add the remaining half of the flour.
5. Beat well.
6. Now add the remaining half of the whites, and beat lightly.
7. Pour into a cake pan, lined at the bottom and around with buttered paper, and bake in a quick oven from forty-five minutes to one hour, using the broom-straw test.
8. Cover the cake with a layer of thick brown paper when you first put it in the oven.
9. Keep the heat of the oven steady.

title: Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 cups white sugar
- 3 1/2 cups of flour
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 tablespoon of yeast powder

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups of flour
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of cold water
- 8 eggs
- a little soda dissolved in vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 14 eggs
- yolks of 7 eggs
- 10 ounces flour
- 1 lb. sugar

## Directions

1. Combine whites of 14 eggs, yolks of 7 eggs, 10 ounces of flour, and 1 lb. of sugar

title: Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the weight of 10 eggs in very fine sugar
- the weight of 6 eggs in flour

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with the flour
2. Beat the whites alone
3. By degrees, mix the whites and flour with the other ingredients
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Sponge Cake Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- stale sponge or other plain cake
- a little more than a pint of milk
- two or three beaten eggs
- sugar and butter

## Directions

1. Crumble stale sponge or other plain cake into a little more than a pint of milk and two or three beaten eggs
2. Bake it
3. Serve with a sauce of sugar and butter beaten together

title: Sponge Cake Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 stale sponge cakes
- the peel of a lemon
- the juice of a lemon
- cold milk
- 3 eggs
- a teacupful of orange juice
- a little sugar
- a thick slice of butter
- paste

## Directions

1. Grate 3 stale sponge cakes and with them the peel of a lemon, and all the juice
2. Mix them in cold milk until they are quite soft
3. Beat 3 eggs together, and stir them by degrees into the biscuits
4. Add a teacupful of orange juice, and a little sugar, a thick slice of butter well melted
5. Mix all well together
6. Put it in a dish with paste round the edges
7. Bake it slowly

title: Sponge Cake With Butter
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the weight of 14 eggs in sugar
- the weight of 8 eggs in flour
- the weight of 6 or 7 eggs in butter
- the rind and juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and flour together
2. Beat the eggs and sugar light
3. Add the rind and juice of lemon
4. Combine all ingredients
5. Bake in cups

title: Sponge Ginger Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sour milk
- one cup of molasses
- one-half cup of butter
- two eggs
- one and a half teaspoons of saleratus
- one tablespoonful of ginger
- flour

## Directions

1. Warm the butter, molasses and ginger
2. Add the milk, flour and saleratus
3. Make batter as thick as pound cake
4. Bake as quickly as you can

title: Sponge Ginger Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of molasses
- piece of butter the size of an egg
- 1 tablespoonful of white ginger
- 1 teaspoonful of soda dissolved in milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Warm 1 pint of molasses
2. Stir in while warm a piece of butter the size of an egg, 1 tablespoonful of white ginger, 1 teaspoonful of soda dissolved in milk
3. Strain this into the mixture
4. When cool, sift in as much flour as will make it stiff
5. Roll it out in cake and bake on tins

title: Spring Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 eggs
- the weight of 14 eggs in sugar
- the weight of 7 eggs in flour
- the rind and juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in an oven

title: Spruce Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- Sprigs of spruce
- One ounce of hops
- A spoonful of ginger
- A gallon of water
- A pint of Louisiana Molasses or a pound of brown sugar
- A teacupful of yeast (home-made)

## Directions

1. Take sprigs of spruce and boil until you have an ounce of strong essence
2. Take one ounce of hops and a spoonful of ginger, and put them into a gallon of water
3. When well-boiled, strain the mixture, and add a pint of Louisiana Molasses or a pound of brown sugar, and then add the extract of Spruce
4. When cool add a teacupful of yeast (home-made), and put all into a clean, tight cask, and let it ferment for twenty-four hours or two days
5. Then bottle for use

title: Squab With Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pigeons
- 1 Pint of Green Peas
- 1/2 Inch of Ham
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig Thyme
- 1 Sprig Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Cup of Consommé or Broth

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons nicely, leaving them whole, as you would a fowl that is to be roasted, and truss nicely.
2. Take two onions and slice well, and place in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of melted butter.
3. Let them brown slightly, and lay the pigeons that have been rubbed well with salt and pepper, inside and out, on top of the onions.
4. Cover closely and let them smother.
5. Add for one pigeon one-half or one-quarter of an inch of nice ham, minced very fine, to give a good seasoning.
6. Add one sprig of thyme and one bay leaf, and the clove of a garlic, minced very fine.
7. Let this smother very slowly for ten or fifteen minutes.
8. When well browned, moisten with a cup of consommé or broth, and add one pint of fresh green peas, or one can.
9. Cover tight, and let all simmer over a slow fire for one hour, or more if the pigeons are not very tender.
10. Serve on a platter, placing the pigeons in the center and heaping the green peas around.

title: Squash
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Young Squash
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut the squash into quarters and pare and remove the seeds.
2. Then cut into small squares of about an inch.
3. Wash in cold water, and then put into a porcelain-lined or agate saucepan, and half cover with water, for squash is a watery vegetable.
4. Let it simmer gently for about twenty minutes or half an hour.
5. Then drain through a colander, pressing gently.
6. Mash the squash very fine after draining, and return to the saucepan.
7. Add two tablespoonfuls of butter, and salt and pepper to taste, and stir until thoroughly heated, and it begins to simmer gently.
8. Do not cease stirring, or it will burn.
9. Serve hot.

title: Squash, Stewed
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- squashes
- sweet milk or cream
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- ham

## Directions

1. If not very young, peel the squashes
2. Steam or boil them until tender
3. Season them with sweet milk or cream, and a little butter, pepper and salt
4. Let them stew down in this until they are thick, and of the consistence of mashed potatoes

title: Squirrel or Rabbit Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Squirrel or Rabbit
- 2 Slices or 1/2 Pound of Lean Ham
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Large Onion
- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Water
- 1/2 Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Skin, clean and cut up the squirrel or rabbit, as for a fricassee
2. Dredge well with salt and black pepper
3. Cut the ham into dice shapes, and chop the onion, parsley and thyme very fine
4. Put the lard or butter into a deep stew pot and, when hot, put in the squirrel or rabbit
5. Cover closely and fry for about eight or ten minutes
6. Then proceed in exactly the same manner as for Chicken Gumbo
7. Add the file at the time indicated
8. Serve with boiled Louisiana rice

title: Squirrel, or Young Rabbit Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three young squirrels or rabbits
- two ounces of butter
- handful of chopped mushrooms
- bunch of parsley
- two shallots
- pepper
- salt
- thyme or sweet herbs
- glass of white wine
- half cup of brown gravy from veal or chicken
- juice of half a lemon
- paste

## Directions

1. Cut up two or three young squirrels or rabbits
2. Put them in a saucepan to cook with two ounces of butter, a handful of chopped mushrooms, a bunch of parsley and two shallots chopped
3. Season with pepper and salt, and a little thyme or sweet herbs
4. Cook them a light brown
5. Throw in a glass of white wine, a half cup of brown gravy from veal or chicken, and the juice of half a lemon
6. Toss all up on the fire fifteen or twenty minutes
7. Make a nice paste, line the sides of the pan, pour in the stewed rabbit, and cover with paste
8. Bake until a light brown, and eat cold or hot

title: Stage Planks or Ginger Bread Without Butter or Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Molasses
- 1 Cup of Sour Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful of Ground Ginger
- 1 Gill of Lard
- 3 Cups of Flour
- 1 Teaspoonful of Baking Soda

## Directions

1. Melt the molasses, lard and ginger together and blend well.
2. When thoroughly melted and warmed, beat for about ten minutes.
3. Then dissolve the soda in a tablespoonful of boiling water and add to the molasses.
4. Mix it thoroughly, and then add the flour, using good judgment and adding just enough of the three cups of sifted flour to make a stiff batter.
5. Beat thoroughly and vigorously.
6. Have ready several greased, shallow pans; pour the mixture into them and bake for ten minutes in a quick oven.

title: Steamed Cabinet Pudding, Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- brioche, or any kind of cold sweet roll, or Sally Lunn
- sponge cake, or macaroons
- currants, or seedless raisins
- chopped citron, or other dried fruit
- six yolks of eggs
- a pint of milk or cream
- six ounces of sugar
- a glass of brandy
- the grated rind of a lemon
- extract of lemon
- wine or hard butter sauce

## Directions

1. Butter a pudding mould, and line it with brioche, or any kind of cold sweet roll, or Sally Lunn, that has been left over.
2. Fill the mould with layers of sponge cake, or macaroons, alternately with currants, or seedless raisins, chopped citron, or other dried fruit.
3. Make a boiled custard of six yolks of eggs, a pint of milk or cream, six ounces of sugar, a glass of brandy, and the grated rind of a lemon.
4. Moisten the macaroons with extract of lemon, and then pour over the custard.
5. Steam the pudding.
6. Serve with wine or hard butter sauce beaten up with a little wine.

title: Steamed Corn Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Flour
- 1 Cup of Louisiana Molasses
- 2 Cups of Sweet Milk
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Scald the meal.
2. Beat the eggs very light, and beat the milk into them till very light.
3. Add the molasses and yeast powder, and blend well.
4. Then add the flour, mixing and beating till thoroughly blended.
5. Tie in a cloth, as you would roll a pudding, and place in a steamer, on top of a pot of boiling water, and cover first with a close cloth, and then with the steamer cover, and steam for three hours.
6. Then turn into a hot dish, and eat as a plain dessert, with a Brandy or Hard or Cream Sauce.

title: Steamed Oatmeal
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Teacupful of Oatmeal
- 1 Quart of Boiling Water
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- cream

## Directions

1. Add the oatmeal to the water, and then add the salt, and set the steaming vessel over a pot of cold water
2. Let it gradually heat, and then steam for an hour and a half
3. Keep closely covered
4. When done, serve with cream

title: Steamed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Dozen Oysters
- A Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Have ready a pot of boiling water
2. Drain the oysters in their shells and put them in a shallow tin pan, the bottom being perforated
3. Cover and put them over the steamer
4. Let them stand about ten minutes, put into a hot dish, season with pepper and Cayenne, and serve with drawn butter sauce
5. If one has not the perforated tin, steam the oysters in their shells by washing the shells thoroughly on the outside, placing the oysters in the steamer and covering, letting them remain about fifteen minutes over the boiling water, or until the shells open easily

title: Steamed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Nice Potatoes
- A Pot of Boiling Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well, scrubbing thoroughly, to take off every particle of earth that adheres.
2. Then put them in a potato steamer, and set over a pot of boiling water. Cover tight and steam till you can pierce with a fork.
3. When done, remove the jackets, or skin, and sprinkle with salt and pepper; add a tablespoonful of butter in which you have mingled chopped parsley; and serve immediately.

title: Steeven Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pint rice
- 1½ pints flour
- 1 lb. butter
- 1 salt spoonful salt
- 5 eggs
- 1 quart milk

## Directions

1. Pick and wash ½ pint of rice
2. Boil it very soft
3. Drain it and let it get cold
4. Sift 1½ pints of flour over the pan of rice
5. Mix in 1 lb. of butter that has been warmed by the fire and 1 salt spoonful of salt
6. Add 5 eggs beat very light
7. Stir them gradually into a quart of milk
8. Beat all together very light
9. Bake it in muffin rings or waffle irons
10. Send them to the table very hot
11. Eat them with butter, honey or molasses

title: Stew Ducks with Green Peas
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter
- two tablespoonfuls of flour
- a pint of broth or gravy made from veal, or from water in which the ducks or chickens have been boiled
- ducks
- a quart of green peas
- an onion
- a sprig of parsley

## Directions

1. Truss the ducks as for baking and boiling, and put them away in the pantry
2. Put two ounces of butter in a stew-pan on the fire, stir in two tablespoonfuls of flour, stir until it becomes brown or a fawn color
3. Pour in a pint of broth or gravy made from veal, or from water in which the ducks or chickens have been boiled
4. Stir this while cooking, and when it boils, put in the ducks
5. Let them cook for half an hour, or until done or nearly so
6. Add a quart of green peas, an onion chopped, and a sprig of parsley
7. Allow these to stew gently until done
8. Remove the parsley and the ducks, and if there is too much sauce, cook it down a little
9. Dish up, pour the peas and gravy over the ducks and serve

title: Stew, or Fricassee of Chicken
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- toasted bread
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the chicken, cut it up as for frying
2. Lay it in a stew-pan with water to cover it
3. Add a teaspoonful of salt and half as much pepper
4. Set it to boil very gently, take off all scum as it rises
5. When the chicken is tender, which will be in an hour, take a tea-cup of butter, a tablespoonful of flour worked up in it, and a bunch of parsley, put them in the stew-pan with the chicken
6. Let all stew twenty minutes
7. Serve on toasted bread
8. Egg-balls around the toast add much to the beauty of this dish

title: Stew, or Fricassee of Chicken
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- salt
- pepper
- a teacup of butter
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a bunch of parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the chicken, cut it up as for frying, lay it in a stew-pan with water to cover it
2. Add a teaspoonful of salt and half as much pepper; set it to boil very gently, take off all scum as it rises
3. When the chicken is tender, which will be in an hour, take a teacup of butter, a tablespoonful of flour worked into it, and a bunch of parsley, put them in the stew-pan with the chicken
4. Let all stew twenty minutes, and serve

title: Stewed Apples
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2¼ lbs. apples
- 1½ lbs. coarse white sugar
- whites of 3 eggs
- water
- orange peel
- citron

## Directions

1. Put more than a quart of water to the beaten sugar; clarify the sugar with the whites of 3 eggs
2. As the apples are peeled, they must be dropped in water; when the syrup is sufficiently clear, put them in; when half done, add orange peel and cut citron

title: Stewed Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Apples, Large and Firm
- Sugar to Taste
- The Zest of Half an Orange
- 1 Blade Mace
- 1 Stick Ground Cinnamon
- Ground Allspice
- Half a Cup of Water

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and cut into quarters or semi-quarters, removing the cores and seeds.
2. Add half a cup of water.
3. Put them into an agate or a porcelain-lined saucepan.
4. Strew generously with sugar.
5. Let them simmer gently for half an hour.
6. Then add the zest of half an orange, grated, and the ground spices.
7. Let all simmer gently till they form a rich tender compote, that is, until not the least semblance remains of being uncooked.
8. This compote may be served hot during the meal, or it may be used as a dessert, with cream or milk.
9. Apples may be stewed whole in the same manner, only remember to extract the core with a fine knife.

title: Stewed Beets
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil them first
2. Scrape and slice them
3. Put them into a stewpan, with a piece of butter, rolled in flour, with finely chopped parsley, a little vinegar, salt and pepper
4. Set the pan on hot coals and let the beets stew for 1 hour

title: Stewed Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Left-Over Biscuits
- ¾ Pound of Butter
- 1½ Pints of Milk

## Directions

1. Take a porcelain-lined saucepan, cut the biscuit in halves, lay in the bottom of the saucepan, place a layer of butter over this, and then a layer of biscuit, and so on till all are used up.
2. Pour milk over the whole, let it come to a boil, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Cabbage
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cabbage
- pot liquor
- water
- milk
- butter
- clarified drippings
- cream
- flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Drain cold cabbage left from dinner from the pot liquor in which it was boiled
2. Simmer for half an hour in water, or milk and water
3. Pour off all the water when it is tender
4. Stir in the pot a lump of butter or clarified drippings
5. Let it cook gently
6. Throw in a cup of milk or cream
7. Thicken it with flour
8. Season with pepper and salt
9. Serve with the cream gravy poured over the cabbage

title: Stewed Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cabbage
- 1 pound ham or salt pork
- Salt to taste
- Pinch of red pepper pod
- 1 tablespoonful of lard
- 2 chaurice or sausage

## Directions

1. Parboil the cabbage after cutting into quarters
2. Let it boil well about half an hour
3. Take it out of the water and drain nicely, separating the leaves down to the heart as it cools
4. Cut the ham into pieces of about two inches long
5. Take a tablespoonful of lard and put in a stewing pan
6. Put into this the ham or salt meat and let it fry well
7. Add two chaurice or sausage
8. As these brown well, moisten with half a cup of boiling water and let simmer gently for fifteen minutes
9. When well browned, add little by little the cabbage, stirring it well
10. Let it simmer gently for an hour and a half or longer, covering well and stirring frequently to prevent burning
11. Add an inch of red pepper pod cut fine and salt to taste
12. Serve hot

title: Stewed Carrots
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- fresh butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Half boil the carrots
2. Scrape them nicely, and cut them into thick slices
3. Put them into a stewpan with as much milk as will barely cover them, a very little salt and pepper, and a sprig or two of chopped parsley
4. Simmer them till perfectly tender
5. When nearly done, add a piece of fresh butter rolled in flour
6. Send them to table hot

title: Stewed Carrots
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Carrots of Good Size
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 3/4 Pint of Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots and throw them into cold water for about a quarter of an hour.
2. Then put them in a saucepan, and cover with boiling water.
3. Add the salt, and let them cook for an hour and a half, boiling steadily.
4. After this time expires, take them out and drain off all water.
5. Then cut neatly into one-half inch dice pieces, or slice thin.
6. Put a tablespoonful of butter in the frying pan, and add the flour, blending well, but without browning.
7. Then add the milk, and let it simmer to a rich cream sauce.
8. Add the carrots to this, and let them simmer gently for about twenty minutes.
9. Then add a little chopped parsley and sugar, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Ceps
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Ceps
- 1 Onion, Minced Fine
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1/4 Cup of Milk

## Directions

1. Drain the Ceps from their oil
2. Slice nicely
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan
4. Add the minced onion and the clove of garlic minced very fine
5. Moisten with a half cup of milk
6. Let the mixture simmer gently for twenty minutes
7. Serve the Ceps on slices of toast with the sauce poured around

title: Stewed Chestnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Chestnuts
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts
2. Throw them into a saucepan of boiling water for twenty minutes
3. Take them out and remove the dark outer skin
4. Remove the boiling water in the saucepan and add the chestnuts
5. Let them cook for twenty minutes more, or until they may be easily pierced with a fork
6. Take them out and drain
7. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan
8. Add the flour and blend gradually, without browning
9. Add the pint of milk and then add the chestnuts
10. Let all cook for fifteen minutes longer
11. Season to taste

title: Stewed Chicken, Brown Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken into pieces at the joints.
2. Season well with salt and black pepper.
3. Chop the onions fine.
4. Put a tablespoonful of lard into the stewpot, and, when hot, add the onion.
5. Let it brown slightly and then add the flour, which has been well sifted.
6. Let this brown and add the chicken.
7. Let all simmer a few minutes and then add the chopped thyme, parsley and bay leaf.
8. The latter must be minced very fine.
9. Stir well and often.
10. When every piece is nicely browned, add one pint and a half of boiling water or soup broth.
11. Stir until it begins to boil.
12. Season again to taste.
13. Cover and let it simmer gently for an hour, or until tender.
14. Dish up the chicken, pour over the hot sauce and serve hot.

title: Stewed Chicken, White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Chicken
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls Flour
- 1 Pint Fresh Milk
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken into joints and clean the gizzard, liver and open the heart.
2. Season well with salt and pepper, and put all into a stewpan on a moderate fire.
3. Cover well with boiling water.
4. Let simmer for an hour and a half or quarter if the chicken is very young; longer if the chicken is old.
5. Add the juice of two large onions.
6. Cook until tender.
7. When the chicken is done, blend together one large tablespoonful of butter and of flour in a frying pan without browning; add a pint of milk and mix well.
8. Add this to the chicken, mixing and stirring constantly till it boils.
9. Salt and pepper to taste.
10. Take from the fire and add the beaten yolks of two eggs and a little chopped parsley.
11. Serve hot.

title: Stewed Codfish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Small Potatoes, or Left-Over Salt Cod, Enough for Six Persons
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Dozen White Onions
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour Blended With Two of Milk

## Directions

1. If the potatoes are not left-over from the day before, wash and peel, and also the onion.
2. Boil the potatoes till tender.
3. Soak the fish overnight, and boil for twenty minutes, or until tender.
4. Cut the fish into pieces of about two or three inches in length.
5. Put a tablespoonful of lard in the stewpan and lay in the potatoes and then the onions, and the Codfish on top.
6. Add enough cold water to cover the whole, and let it simmer until the fish is well cooked.
7. Take out the fish, and allow the potatoes and onions to simmer on.
8. Remove every piece of bone from the fish, and trim edges nicely.
9. Dissolve in another stewpan the butter and flour as directed; let this simmer gently without browning.
10. Put in the potatoes, onions and fish in the order given.
11. Pour over this the quart of cream or milk.
12. Let it simmer for about ten minutes more till the milk comes to a boil, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Crabs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 crabs
- white wine
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- bread crumbs
- yolks of 2 eggs
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take 3 or 4 crabs and pick the meat out of the body and claws
2. Take care that no spongy part be left among it, or any of the shell
3. Put this meat into a stew pan with a little white wine, some pepper and salt, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Heat all this together and put in some crumbs of bread, the yolks of 2 eggs beat up, and 1 spoonful of vinegar
5. Stir all well together
6. Make some toasted sippets, lay them on a plate and pour in the crabs
7. Serve it up hot

title: Stewed Crabs Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Live Crabs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter or Lard
- 1 Stalk Chopped Celery
- 1 Dozen Fresh Tomatoes or 1 Can
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove
- Garlic
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Large Onion

## Directions

1. Boil a dozen fine large crabs about five minutes in order to kill them.
2. Take off the fire and place in a dish.
3. When sufficiently cooled, cut off the claws and crack, separating the joints.
4. Remove the "apron" or "tablier" of the crab and the "dead man's fingers," and take off the spongy substance.
5. Remove the shell, cut the body of the crab into four parts, cutting down the center and across.
6. Chop a large onion very fine and brown with butter or lard, using a tablespoonful of either.
7. Add a dozen fine, large, fresh tomatoes, chopped fine, in their liquor, and brown nicely.
8. Stir in chopped celery, thyme, parsley, one bay leaf, chopped fine; pepper and salt to taste, and a dash of Cayenne pepper.
9. Add one clove of garlic, chopped fine.
10. Taste and add more seasoning if necessary.
11. Let the mixture cook ten minutes, then add the crabs and let them cook ten minutes longer.
12. Never add water to this sauce, as the liquor of the tomatoes is sufficient and makes an excellent sauce.
13. Serve with boiled rice or potatoes boiled whole.

title: Stewed Duck
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- 1 pint of good gravy
- salt
- cayenne
- 2 teaspoonfuls of fine ground rice
- 1 glass of port wine

## Directions

1. Cut the ducks into joints and lay them into a stewpan with a pint of good gravy
2. Let it come to a boil
3. As the scum rises, remove it
4. Season with salt and cayenne
5. Let it stew gently three-quarters of an hour
6. Mix smoothly 2 teaspoonfuls of fine ground rice with a glass of port wine
7. Stir it into the gravy
8. Let it have 7 or 8 minutes to amalgamate with the gravy
9. Dish and send it to table very hot

title: Stewed Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- bread
- butter
- onions
- lard
- slips of bacon
- giblets
- water
- pepper
- salt
- mace or cloves
- lemon juice or lemon pickle
- flour

## Directions

1. Truss the ducks and stuff them with bread, butter and onions
2. Place them with lard in a frying pan
3. Have prepared in an iron pot slips of bacon, giblets, water, pepper, salt, a little mace or cloves
4. Put in the ducks and let them stew gently but constantly for 2 hours
5. Flour the ducks each time that you turn them in the pot
6. Add lemon juice or lemon pickle
7. Thicken with butter rolled in flour

title: Stewed Ducks With Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Ducks
- 6 Turnips
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Onions, Chopped Fine
- 1 Square Inch of Ham, Minced Very Fine
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the Ducks, and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the pot, and as it melts, add the onions, chopped fine.
3. Let this brown, and then add the pieces of Ducks.
4. Let them brown, and add the minced ham.
5. Immediately after add the turnips, sliced or cut in quarters, a tablespoonful of sifted flour.
6. Stir well, let the flour brown slightly, and add the minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf, and one clove of garlic, minced very fine.
7. Stir well again, and let it smother for about fifteen minutes, stirring frequently, so that it will not burn.
8. Then add water, almost sufficient to cover the Ducks, and stir well.
9. Cover tight, and let the mixture smother for a half hour longer.

title: Stewed Ducks, French Marshal Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Fine Ducks, French or Canvas-Back
- 12 Godiveau Quenelles
- 12 Mushrooms
- 2 Onions Chopped Fine
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine
- Croutons to Garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the Ducks exactly as in the recipe for Stewed Ducks With Turnips, omitting the turnips.
2. Add ten minutes before serving, twelve small Godiveau Quenelles and the wine.
3. Garnish the dish with Croutons and twelve nicely cooked mushrooms, cut in two.
4. Send to the table hot.

title: Stewed Ducks, Peasant Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Fine Ducks, French or Canvas-Back
- 1 Dozen Glazed Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Cup of Green Peas
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Glass of Madeira or Sherry Wine
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- Croutons fried in butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the Ducks exactly as in the recipe for Stewed Ducks With Turnips, only the turnips are omitted.
2. Add two carrots cut into dice pieces, and twelve glazed onions and the green peas.
3. A quarter of an hour before serving add a glass of Madeira wine.
4. Serve on a hot dish, with Croutons fried in butter, using the onions as a garnish with the Croutons.

title: Stewed Eggplant
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Eggplants
- ¾ Can of Tomatoes
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Parboil the eggplant for about thirty minutes.
2. Take out of the boiling water and let cool slightly.
3. Skin and cut into pieces half an inch square.
4. Chop two onions very fine.
5. Take one tablespoonful of butter, and brown the onion in it.
6. As it browns, add half a can of tomatoes, or six fresh, chopped fine.
7. Add the square inch of ham, chopped very fine.
8. Add two cloves of garlic, minced very fine, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Let this simmer for three or four minutes, and then add the eggplant sufficient to make a pound.
10. Let all cook, smothering slowly and well, keeping tightly covered, and stirring often to prevent burning.
11. Season again to taste.
12. After it has cooked for half an hour serve very hot.

title: Stewed Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- 1 pound good butter
- 2 blades of mace
- 10 cloves
- cinnamon
- red pepper
- salt
- water
- 1 pint port wine
- lemon or horse radish

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well by putting it in a dish and pouring vinegar over it to take off the slime and scales
2. Put some butter into a frying pan and make it hot
3. Put the fish into the pan and let it remain over the fire for 6 minutes
4. Take it out and put it into the kettle in which it is to be stewed
5. Sprinkle some flour into the pan, put it over the fire for 3 minutes and pour it over the fish
6. Take 1 pound of good butter, roll it well in flour, and put it to the fish
7. Add 2 blades of mace, 10 cloves, a little cinnamon, red pepper, and salt, with water sufficient to keep it from burning
8. Put it over a slow fire to stew
9. When half done, add a pint of port wine
10. When done, put in a dish, pour the gravy over it, and garnish with lemon or horse radish

title: Stewed Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- one cup of good, boiling vinegar
- one tablespoonful of lard
- two tablespoonfuls of flour
- salt
- pepper
- large chopped onion
- parsley
- one clove of garlic
- one sprig of thyme
- bay leaf
- two blades of mace
- eight or ten allspice
- Cayenne
- pint or glass of Port Wine
- crotons
- slices of lemon
- prepared horseradish

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and slice and pour over one cup of good, boiling vinegar.
2. Make a roux by putting one tablespoonful of lard into the stewpan, and when hot add gradually two tablespoonfuls of flour rubbed smoothly.
3. When quite brown, take the fish, which has been previously rubbed with salt and pepper, and place in the pot.
