4. Let it simmer gently a few minutes, and then add a large chopped onion, parsley, one clove of garlic, one sprig of thyme, a bay leaf, two blades of mace and eight or ten allspice.
5. Let these brown and cover with water sufficient to prevent burning.
6. Put the fish on a slow fire to stew, and when half done, add a little Cayenne, and, if possible, add a pint or glass of Port Wine.
7. When done, place the fish in a dish, pour the gravy over it, and garnish with crotons, with alternate slices of lemon and prepared horseradish.

title: Stewed Foies Gras
body:
## Ingredients

- Terrine of Foies Gras
- Thin Strips of Bacon
- Carrot
- Onion
- Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- Wineglassful of White Wine
- Two Spoonfuls of French Brandy
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Get the prepared Foies Gras and cut them into slices or filets
2. Lard them with a larding needle
3. Place at the bottom of the saucepan small strips of bacon, cut very thin and fine
4. Add one carrot, nicely sliced and one onion, nicely sliced, and a tablespoonful of chopped parsley
5. Cover this with narrow strips of bacon, and moisten with sufficient white wine to cover well, and two spoonfuls of brandy
6. Add the juice of a lemon and let it simmer well for a few minutes
7. Add the livers, and let them simmer for ten minutes longer
8. Season to taste, cook five minutes more and serve hot

title: Stewed Frogs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Frog Legs
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1/2 Pint of Water
- 1/2 Pint of Oyster Water
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Sweet Marjoram
- 10 Allspice
- 1 Clove
- The Yolk of an Egg
- Croutons
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- Salt and Pepper
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Take the legs of one dozen frogs and prepare the same as for frying.
2. Take a tablespoonful of butter and put in a frying pan.
3. When it begins to melt, add a tablespoonful of flour and stir constantly.
4. When it begins to brown nicely, add a half pint of water and a pint of oyster water.
5. Throw in the Frog legs as it begins to boil, and add salt and pepper, a little Cayenne, a sprig of thyme, bay leaf and sweet marjoram, eight or ten allspice, one clove.
6. Let it simmer about fifteen minutes and take off the fire.
7. Have ready the yolk of a beaten egg and add, blending well, and serve immediately with garnishes of Croutons, and fried in a little butter, with oysters laid upon them.

title: Stewed Goose with Mushrooms and Chestnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- Spoon of Flour
- Onions
- Goose, cleaned, singed and cut into pieces
- Salt and Pepper
- Sausage
- Minced herbs and garlic
- Can of Mushrooms and their water
- Chestnuts

## Directions

1. Place the lard or butter in the stewpot and when it melts add the onions, which have been nicely sliced
2. As they brown add the goose, which has been cleaned, singed and nicely cut at the joints into pieces, and well rubbed with salt and pepper, and the sausage, which must be cut in halves
3. Let simmer for about ten minutes, until every portion is slightly browned
4. Add the minced herbs and garlic
5. After three minutes add the spoonful of flour, mixing well, and let it all simmer for ten minutes longer
6. Pour in the can of mushrooms and their water, and add immediately the chestnuts
7. Let the goose cook till tender and serve hot

title: Stewed Green Corn
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen fresh, tender ears of corn
- cold water
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cut corn off the cob, and put it in a stew pan with a quart of cold water
2. No salt at first
3. Let it cook half an hour and then stir in a lump of fresh butter, a spoonful of flour, and salt and pepper to taste
4. If too dry, add a cup of sweet milk, or water, if the milk is not convenient

title: Stewed Hare With Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Hare
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Dozen Small Onions
- 1 Ounce of Ham
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 1/2 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare in exactly the same manner as the Hare Creole Style, only do not use the tomatoes or red wine
2. Use instead of the claret, one pint of broth or consomme, and add a half can of mushrooms about ten minutes before serving

title: Stewed Irish Potatoes, A Nice Breakfast Dish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six potatoes
- cold, salted water
- cold water
- half a cup of sweet milk
- a little salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- a spoonful of butter, rolled in flour or a teaspoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Wash, peel, and slice six potatoes
2. Throw them for a few moments into cold, salted water
3. Take them out in five minutes and place them in a stew pan on the fire
4. Cover them with cold water
5. When tender, throw off all the water
6. Pour over them half a cup of sweet milk, a little salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
7. Thicken them with a spoonful of butter, rolled in flour, or a teaspoonful of flour, beaten in carefully to prevent it from lumping
8. Stew a few moments and serve in a covered dish

title: Stewed Kidneys
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Kidneys
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1½ Tablespoon of Butter
- ½ Glass of White Wine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sherry Wine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Sprig Each of Chopped Parsley, Thyme and Bay Leaf, Very Fine

## Directions

1. Select perfectly fresh kidneys; wash them well, and then slice very thin
2. Season well with salt and pepper
3. Put one and a half tablespoonfuls of butter into the saucepan; when melted and very hot, add the kidneys and chopped herbs, being very careful to stir constantly and very fast, to prevent burning
4. Add a half glass of White Wine, if possible, and one cup of water or consommé
5. Let it boil up once, and the kidneys are ready to be served

title: Stewed Lamb Chops With Green Peas
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- butter
- water
- green peas
- sweet butter

## Directions

1. Season the chops with pepper and salt; roll in flour and fry to a pale brown.
2. When done, if the chops are very fat, pour some of it into the stock-pot and cover the chops with boiling water.
3. Parboil a pint of green peas; add them to the chops, together with a large spoonful of sweet butter.
4. Dredge in a spoonful of flour, and let all stew gently for half an hour.

title: Stewed Lamb Tongues
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tongues
- 1 Onion
- 1/4 of a Small Carrot, Cut Fine
- 1/4 of a Small Turnip, Cut Fine
- 1 Pint of Broth or Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme and Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean the tongues; wash well and boil in clear water for an hour and a half
2. Throw them into cold water and remove the skins
3. Cut the vegetables fine, and put them with the butter into a saucepan
4. Add a pint of broth or water, and then add the finely-minced herbs
5. Add the tongues and let them simmer gently for two hours
6. Serve hot, with the gravy poured over

title: Stewed Mushrooms on Toast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- tablespoonful of butter
- salt
- pepper
- powdered mace
- slice of bread

## Directions

1. Pull out the stems of the mushrooms, and peel them
2. Melt a tablespoonful of butter in a stew pan
3. Throw into the butter a little salt, pepper, and powdered mace (if liked)
4. Lay the mushrooms in this, upper side down
5. Stew till they are tender, which will be in about twenty minutes
6. Fry a slice of bread until it is a light brown
7. Arrange the mushrooms over it
8. Serve hot

title: Stewed Mushrooms on Toast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- powdered mace
- bread
- lard

## Directions

1. Pull out the stems of the mushrooms, and peel them
2. Melt a tablespoonful of butter in a stew pan
3. Throw into the butter a little salt, pepper, and powdered mace if liked
4. Lay the mushrooms in this, upper side down
5. Stew till they are tender, which will be in about twenty minutes
6. Fry a slice of bread until it is a light brown
7. Arrange the mushrooms over it
8. Serve hot

title: Stewed Mushrooms on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 6 Slices of Toast
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Drain the mushrooms of their liquor, and place in a stewpan with the butter; season to taste with salt and pepper; cover and let them cook for ten minutes, tossing almost constantly.
2. Add the juice of a lemon and the chopped parsley.
3. Place six slices of toast on a dish, garnish these nicely with the mushrooms and serve.

title: Stewed Mushrooms, Spanish Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint or 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Olive Oil
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chives
- 1 Dozen Whole Peppers
- Salt and Black Pepper to Taste
- Three tablespoonfuls of Olive Oil

## Directions

1. Drain the mushrooms of their liquor
2. Cut them in lozenge-shaped pieces, and put them in a dish and sprinkle with a tablespoonful of olive oil, and salt and pepper to taste
3. Let the mushrooms soak in this marinade for two hours
4. Take them out and put in a saucepan and let them stew for ten minutes
5. Make a sauce of three tablespoonfuls of olive oil, the clove of garlic minced very fine, a tablespoonful of minced chives, and blend well
6. Add this to the mushrooms and let them saute for five minutes longer on a very slow fire, without boiling
7. Serve hot

title: Stewed Okra, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Okras
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 3 Tomatoes
- 1 Onion
- 1 Green Pepper
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Cayenne

## Directions

1. Wash the okras and pare the ends
2. Place in a saucepan with one tablespoonful of butter
3. Add a finely-minced onion and clove of garlic and green pepper
4. Let all cook for six or eight minutes, and then add the three tomatoes, chopped fine; also add the juice of the tomatoes
5. Season to taste with salt and pepper; add a dash of Cayenne and a teaspoonful of chopped parsley
6. Add the okras, and let all simmer slowly for twenty minutes
7. Place in a hot, deep serving dish, and cover and send to the table

title: Stewed Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart oysters
- liquor
- slice of stale bread
- tablespoonful butter
- pepper
- salt
- celery

## Directions

1. Put 1 quart of oysters with their liquor into a chopping dish or stew pan
2. Crumble in a slice of stale bread, tablespoonful of butter, pepper and salt to your taste
3. Stew from ten to fifteen minutes
4. The addition of a little celery cut up is a great improvement

title: Stewed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dozen large oysters
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 pint of oyster liquor
- 1 pint of rich cream or milk
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Take about four dozen large oysters, drain in a colander.
2. Mix one tablespoonful of flour and one of butter together.
3. Put one pint of oyster liquor on the fire and add the flour and butter blended.
4. Have ready in another saucepan a pint of rich, hot cream.
5. After five minutes, add this to the oyster liquor, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
6. Salt and pepper to taste.
7. Let it boil up once and then add the oysters.
8. After three minutes serve.

title: Stewed Oysters (French)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 oysters
- 3 hard boiled eggs
- 1 large tablespoonful of flour
- butter
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 of allspice
- cayenne
- 1 large fresh lemon

## Directions

1. Drain the juice from 100 oysters
2. Pour the juice into a saucepan
3. Let it slowly simmer
4. Skim it very carefully
5. Rub the yolks of 3 hard boiled eggs and 1 large tablespoonful of flour well together
6. Stir this into the juice
7. Add small pieces of butter, a teaspoonful of salt and 1/2 of allspice, a very little cayenne, and the juice of a large fresh lemon
8. Let this simmer for 10 minutes
9. Just before dishing add the oysters

title: Stewed Oysters (Very Delicate)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints of oysters
- 1 pint of rich cream
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful butter

## Directions

1. Strain oysters through a cullender
2. Put 1 pint of rich cream in a clean saucepan
3. Mix 1 teaspoonful of flour and 1 tablespoonful butter together
4. When mixed and the cream begins to heat, stir in the flour and butter slowly
5. Let it simmer for a few minutes, then add the oysters
6. Cook them ten minutes and dish hot for table

title: Stewed Oysters On Toast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- the oysters from their liquor
- whole pepper
- two blades of mace
- three cloves
- a piece of butter as large as a hen’s egg for two dozen oysters
- crackers
- dressed celery

## Directions

1. Take the oysters from their liquor, let it settle
2. Then strain and add some whole pepper, two blades of mace, and three cloves
3. Put over a moderate fire in a block-tin covered sauce-pan
4. Mix a little flour with a piece of butter, as large as a hen’s egg for two dozen oysters
5. And stir in the boiling liquor
6. Remove any scum which may rise
7. Then put in the oysters and let them cook for five minutes
8. Line a hot oyster dish with toasted, well-buttered bread
9. And pour over it the boiling oysters
10. Only rich juicy oysters will stew to advantage
11. Milk is always an improvement, but in this recipe it can be dispensed with
12. If, however, it is convenient pour in a half a pint just as the oysters are put in to boil
13. As earlier it might curdle

title: Stewed Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Medium-Sized Peaches
- 1 Pint of Cold Water
- 1/2 Pound of Granulated Sugar
- 1 Gill of Kirsch or Brandy, if desired

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches and cut them into quarters, carefully removing the stones.
2. Put them into a saucepan, with a pint of cold water and a half pound of granulated sugar.
3. Set on the stove, and, when they begin to boil, skim well.
4. Let them cook for six minutes longer, stirring slowly; avoid mashing the peaches.
5. Remove from the fire and add, if desired, a gill of Kirsch or Brandy.
6. Mix well and pour the peaches into a dessert dish to cool.
7. Serve cold, either plain or with cream.

title: Stewed Pigeons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- mace
- sweet herbs
- butter
- 1 quart of good gravy
- white wine
- picked mushrooms
- pepper corns
- 1 slice of onion
- pickled oysters
- yolk of 1 egg
- lemons

## Directions

1. Season the pigeons with salt, pepper, cloves, mace and sweet herbs
2. Wrap this seasoning up in a piece of butter and put in and stuff them
3. Tie up the neck and vent
4. Half roast them
5. Put them in the saucepan with 1 quart of good gravy, a little white wine, some picked mushrooms, pepper corns, a slice of onion, and some pickled oysters
6. Stew all together till sufficiently done
7. Thicken it with butter and the yolk of 1 egg
8. Garnish with lemons

title: Stewed Poules d'Eau
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Poules d'Eau
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and pick the Poules d'Eau nicely
2. Cut into joints or stew whole, as desired
3. Rub well with salt and pepper
4. Chop two onions very fine and put them into the stewpan with a tablespoonful of melted butter, and let them brown slightly
5. Add the well-seasoned ducks and let these brown well
6. Add the one square inch of finely minced ham
7. Add the clove of garlic and two sprigs each of thyme, parsley and one bay leaf, minced very fine
8. Let this brown with the Poules d'Eau, stirring frequently, and then pour over one good glass of claret
9. Let this simmer for ten minutes, stirring constantly so that it will not burn, and add one cup of boiling water
10. Season well to taste, and let them simmer well for about an hour
11. Serve hot with Croûtons for a garnish

title: Stewed Poules d'Eau With Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Poules d'Eau
- 6 Turnips
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Onions Chopped Fine
- 1 Square Inch of Ham, Minced Very Fine
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the Poules d'Eau and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the pot, and as it melts add the onions, chopped fine.
3. Let this brown and then add the pieces of Poules d'Eau.
4. Let them brown, and add the minced ham. (Omit the ham on fast days.)
5. Immediately after add the turnips, sliced or cut in quarters, and a tablespoonful of sifted flour.
6. Stir well, let it brown slightly, and add the minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf, and one clove of garlic, minced very fine.
7. Stir well again, and let it smother for about fifteen minutes, stirring frequently, so that it will not burn.
8. Then add water almost sufficient to cover the Poules d'Eau, and stir well.
9. Cover tight and let the mixture smother for a half hour longer.

title: Stewed Prunes for Sickness
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- water
- clear brown sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the prunes
2. put them in a stew pan
3. cover them with water
4. to each pound of prunes put a cupful of clear brown sugar
5. Cover the stew-pan and let them boil slowly, until the syrup is thick and rich

title: Stewed Pumpkin
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints of pumpkin or cashaw
- 4 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of ground mace
- 1 teaspoonful of ground cinnamon
- 1 teaspoonful of ground cloves
- 2 gills of good whisky or brandy
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin into halves and then into quarters, and pare.
2. Cut it into pieces of about one inch square.
3. Place them all in a deep pot, and add sufficient water to cover.
4. Let the pumpkin stew slowly for about an hour, stirring frequently to prevent burning.
5. Take out of the pot, press through a colander, and set back on the stove.
6. Add, for every pint of pumpkin, two large tablespoonfuls of butter, and a half teaspoonful of salt, one of ground mace, one of ground cinnamon and one of ground allspice.
7. Add a gill of good whiskey or brandy.
8. Mix all thoroughly, and add sugar to taste.
9. Let it simmer slowly for half an hour longer, and serve hot with daubes, Grillades a la Sauce, etc.

title: Stewed Rabbit
body:
## Ingredients

- A Pair of Rabbits
- 2 Onions
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Chopped Fine
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- Salt and Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the rabbit.
2. Wash well and cut into pieces at the joints, and rub well with salt and pepper.
3. Chop two onions very fine, and put them in the stewpan with a tablespoonful of melted butter.
4. Let them brown slightly; then add the rabbit.
5. Let it brown slightly, and then add one tablespoonful of flour, and let this brown a little.
6. Chop the square inch of ham very fine, mincing it, and add.
7. Then add the clove of garlic, and two sprigs each of thyme and parsley and a bay leaf, minced fine.
8. Let this brown nicely, and pour over one glass of good Claret.
9. Let this cook for ten minutes, stirring it constantly, so that it will not burn, and then add one cup of boiling water.
10. Stir well, season again to taste, and let it boil for thirty minutes, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Rabbit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Water or Consomme
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Rabbit
- Lard or Butter
- Two tablespoonfuls of Flour
- Ten or twelve Allspice
- Three sprigs each of chopped Thyme, Parsley, Bay Leaf and Sweet Marjoram
- One clove of Garlic
- One large Onion
- Six fresh large Tomatoes or one-half can of Tomatoes
- One glass of good Claret
- Juice of a Lemon

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, wash and cut the rabbit into pieces at the joints
2. Put the lard or butter into a deep stewpan or kettle
3. When hot, add gradually two tablespoonfuls of flour, stirring constantly to prevent burning
4. Throw in about ten or twelve well-mashed allspice, and three sprigs each of chopped thyme, parsley, bay leaf and sweet marjoram, one clove of garlic, and one large onion, chopped very fine
5. Add six fresh large tomatoes, chopped fine, or one-half can of tomatoes
6. Pour in one glass of good claret, add about one quart of water, and let it boil well
7. Then add salt and Cayenne to taste, and when this has boiled about five minutes, add the rabbit, putting in piece by piece
8. Add the Juice of a lemon, and let all boil about ten minutes
9. Serve with French Fried Potatoes, Mashed Potatoes, or Potato Croquettes

title: Stewed Rabbits or Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 rabbits
- 2 or 3 onions
- 1 bunch of parsley
- 6 spoonfuls of claret wine
- 3 spoonfuls of vinegar
- 2 or 3 anchovies
- 1 lb. of fresh butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Put 2 or 3 onions and 1 bunch of parsley with the rabbits
2. Half boil them and cut them in pieces as for a fricassee
3. Take the onions, parsley and the liver and cut it small
4. Take 6 spoonfuls of claret wine and 3 of vinegar
5. Dissolve 2 or 3 anchovies into it
6. Put all this in the stewpan with 1 lb. of fresh butter worked up with flour
7. Thicken it and serve it up

title: Stewed Shrimps
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Fine Lake Shrimp
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Can of Tomatoes or 12 Fresh Ones
- 4 Celery Stalks
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Get about 100 large Lake shrimp for this recipe
2. Boil the shrimp first according to the recipe given for Boiled Shrimps, and then pick off the shells, leaving the shrimp whole
3. Place them in a dish
4. Chop fine one large onion and brown it with a tablespoonful of butter
5. Add a can of tomatoes or twelve large, ripe tomatoes, chopped fine, in their own liquor
6. Stir well and brown lightly
7. Add three or four stalks of celery, a clove of garlic, a dash of Cayenne, a sprig of thyme, two bay leaves, all chopped finely and seasoned with salt to taste
8. After this has cooked ten minutes, add the shrimp
9. Let them cook ten minutes longer and serve

title: Stewed Smoked Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked beef
- fresh butter
- pepper
- 2 or 3 beaten yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Chip it very thin
2. Put it into a skillet with fresh butter, pepper, and 2 or 3 beaten yolks of eggs
3. Let it stew till the beef is crisp and curled up

title: Stewed Spinach
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- pepper
- salt
- fresh butter
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pick and boil the spinach nicely
2. Press it well in a cullender
3. Add some pepper and salt
4. Add a spoonful of fresh butter
5. Put it back in the skillet and let it stew gently a little longer
6. Add a small cup of sweet cream

title: Stewed Squirrel
body:
## Ingredients

- A Pair of Squirrels
- 2 Onions
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Chopped Fine
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the Squirrels; wash well and cut into pieces at the joints.
2. Chop two onions very fine and put them in a stewpan with a tablespoonful of melted butter.
3. Let them brown slightly; then add the Squirrel.
4. Let it brown slightly, and then add one tablespoonful of flour, and let this brown a little.
5. Chop the square inch of ham very fine, mincing it, and add.
6. Then add the clove of garlic, and two sprigs each of thyme and parsley and a bay leaf, minced fine.
7. Let this brown nicely, and pour over one glass of good Claret.
8. Let this cook for ten minutes, stirring it constantly, so that it will not burn, and then add one cup of boiling water.
9. Stir well, season again to taste and let it boil for thirty minutes.
10. Add a can of mushrooms immediately after adding the water if desired.
11. Serve very hot.

title: Stewed Summer Squash
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- summer squash
- butter
- gill of cream
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Gather them while young and tender
2. Peel, cut them up, take out the seeds
3. Put them on to boil
4. Let them cook rapidly until very tender
5. Drain them well in a colander
6. Mash with a wooden spoon
7. Put this pulp in a stew pan with a small piece of butter, a gill of cream, and a little pepper and salt
8. Cook this, and stir constantly until the squash is dry
9. Serve very hot

title: Stewed Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Tomatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sugar
- 1 Onion
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- 1 Inch of Ham
- Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf

## Directions

1. Scald the tomatoes, and let the water remain over them about five minutes.
2. Then peel the tomatoes, slipping the skins off easily, and cut them into small pieces.
3. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and as it melts add the onion, which you will have chopped very fine.
4. Let it brown, and add one inch of ham, chopped very fine.
5. Then add the tomatoes, and let them brown, slowly adding in the meantime a half cup of dry bread crumbs.
6. Let all simmer gently, and add one sprig each of thyme, parsley and bay leaf.
7. Stew gently for an hour longer, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoonful brown sugar
- grated bread or cracker
- butter
- mace

## Directions

1. Peel the tomatoes, cut them in half and squeeze out the seeds
2. Put them into a stewpan, without any water
3. Add to them salt and cayenne pepper to your taste, 1 teaspoonful of brown sugar, some grated bread, or cracker, butter and mace (powdered)
4. Stew them slowly, till perfectly done
5. Send them to table very hot

title: Stewed Tomatoes, with or without Sugar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight large, ripe tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- rolled cracker or toasted bread
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over six or eight large, ripe tomatoes
2. Let them remain in it a few minutes to scald the skins
3. Take them out and skin them
4. Chop them up and put them to stew with a little salt, pepper, and a small piece of butter
5. Add a spoonful of rolled cracker or toasted bread, and a tablespoonful of sugar, if liked; if not, omit the sugar
6. Let them stew gently for half an hour longer

title: Stewed Tripe
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Prepared Tripe
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- 1/2 Pint of Milk
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take the prepared tripe and cut into strips of about one finger length and a half inch in width.
2. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan; add a finely-sliced onion, a sprig of parsley and a bay leaf, minced.
3. Stir in the melted butter without letting it brown, then add a tablespoonful of flour.
4. Stir well and add a pint of milk.
5. Stir constantly till it comes to a boil, seasoning to the taste with salt and pepper.
6. Then add the well-seasoned tripe and let it cook over a moderate fire for about five minutes.

title: Stewed Tripe a la Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Prepared Tripe
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- The Juice of 1 Lemon, or a Teaspoonful of Vinegar
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- 1 Onion
- 1 Carrot
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Place a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan and add one chopped onion, one carrot finely sliced; a sprig each of thyme, parsley, bay leaf and salt and pepper.
2. Let it brown slightly and place the tripe on top, and adding a half clove of garlic, minced very fine.
3. Sprinkle with chopped parsley and add the juice of one lemon.
4. Let it all simmer for a few minutes and then add a half cup of broth or hot water.
5. Season to the taste, let it simmer for twenty minutes longer and serve.

title: Stewed Tripe, Plain
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- milk or milk and water
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- green onion

## Directions

1. Cut a pound of tripe in long narrow pieces, lay it in a stew-pan and add a cup of milk, or milk and water.
2. Add a piece of butter as large as a hen's egg and a tablespoonful of flour sifted in.
3. Add a bunch of parsley, and a green onion, if desired.
4. Cook slowly for nearly two hours.

title: Stewed Turtle
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Turtle Meat
- 1 Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter or Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Cup of Water
- A Wineglassful of Sherry or Madeira

## Directions

1. Cut the meat of the turtle about an inch in size.
2. Chop an onion and put all into a saucepan, with a tablespoonful of lard to brown.
3. As it begins to brown, add a tablespoonful of flour, one bay leaf, one clove of garlic, and a sprig of thyme, chopped very fine.
4. Mix this thoroughly with the turtle meat, then add a wineglassful of Sherry or Madeira, and a cup of water.
5. Cook for half an hour.

title: Stewed Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Brisket of Veal
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Pints of Boiling Water
- 1/2 Can of Tomatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Sweet Marjoram
- Salt and Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut the brisket of veal into pieces of about 1 1/2 inches square
2. Put a tablespoonful of lard in the stew pot, and when hot throw in the veal, which has been well seasoned with salt and black pepper
3. Let it brown, and then add the onions and carrots, which have been chopped fine, and one clove of garlic, minced very fine
4. Let this brown, and then add one tablespoonful of flour, sifted well, and let this brown nicely
5. Add two pints of boiling water and 1/2 can of tomatoes, and a bay leaf, chopped very fine, and salt and pepper again to taste, adding, if desired, a dash of Cayenne
6. Set the stew back on the stove and cover closely
7. Let it simmer slowly for an hour and a half, or until the meat is perfectly tender
8. Then serve hot

title: Stewed Veal With Mushrooms
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Brisket of Veal
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 2 Pints of Boiling Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Sweet Marjoram
- Salt and Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into pieces of about 1 1/2 inches square
2. Put a tablespoonful of lard in the stewpot, and when hot, throw in the veal, which has been well seasoned with salt and black pepper
3. Let it brown, and then add the onions and carrots, which have been chopped fine, and 1 clove of garlic, minced very fine
4. Let this brown, and then add 1 tablespoonful of flour, sifted well, and let this brown nicely
5. Add 2 pints of boiling water and 1 can of mushrooms whole, and a bay leaf, chopped very fine, and salt and pepper again to taste, adding, if desired, a dash of Cayenne
6. Set the stew back on the stove and cover closely
7. Let it simmer slowly for an hour and a half, or until the meat is perfectly tender
8. Then serve hot

title: Stewed Veal With Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Brisket or Knuckle of Veal
- 2 Small Potatoes
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Carrots
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Sweet Marjoram
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for Ragout de Veau a la Bourgeoise
2. Peel the potatoes and cut into quarters or halves, according to size
3. Just before adding the water, add the potatoes
4. Add the boiling water after they are in the stew about ten minutes, and set back, allowing it to simmer for an hour and a half, or until very tender

title: Stewed Venison
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of venison
- good butter
- pepper
- mustard
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- claret wine
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Have the chafing dish ready
2. Cut the slices of venison to proper thickness
3. Between the slices, as you place them in the dish, put bits of good butter, pepper, mustard, salt, a little cayenne pepper and claret wine
4. Let it stew 10 or 15 minutes
5. Put sufficient currant jelly to make it palatably sweet

title: Stewed Venison
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- venison slices
- butter
- pepper
- mustard
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- claret wine
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Have the chafing dish ready
2. Have the slices of venison nicely cut to proper thickness
3. Between the slices, as you place them in the dish, put bits of good butter, pepper, mustard, salt, a little cayenne pepper and claret wine
4. Let it stew 10 or 15 minutes
5. Put sufficient currant jelly to make it palatably sweet

title: Stewed Venison à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison Steaks, or Rougher Part of the Deer
- 2 Onions
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic, Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Herb Bouquet, Chopped Fine
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- 1 Can of Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the Venison into two-inch square pieces, and rub well with salt and pepper.
2. Chop two onions very fine, and put them in a stewpan with a tablespoonful of melted butter.
3. Let them brown slightly.
4. Add the Venison meat and let it brown slightly.
5. Add one tablespoonful of flour, and let this brown a little.
6. Chop the square inch of ham very fine, mincing it, and add.
7. Add the clove of garlic, and two sprigs each of thyme and parsley and a bay leaf, minced fine.
8. Let this brown nicely, and pour over one glass of good Claret.
9. Let this cook for ten minutes, stirring it constantly, so that it will not burn.
10. Add one cup of boiling water.
11. Stir well, season again to taste, and let it boil for thirty minutes.
12. If desired, add a can of mushrooms immediately after adding the water.
13. Serve very hot.

title: Stewed Venison, French Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 Pounds of Venison (the lower and lean part preferable)
- A Handful of Parsley
- 1 Onion
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 12 Whole Peppers
- A Half Glass of Vinegar
- 1.5 Glass of Claret
- 1 Pint of Veal Consommé
- 1 Ounce of Salt Pork
- 12 Small Onions
- 1 Dozen and a Half Mushrooms
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Cut the venison into small pieces of about two inches square.
2. Make a Marinade by placing the venison in an earthen jar with one large onion sliced, a handful of parsley, the chopped thyme and bay leaf, the whole peppers, a light seasoning of salt and black pepper, and the vinegar.
3. Let the venison marinate for twelve hours.
4. Then drain it from the juice and place it in a saucepan with one tablespoonful of the best butter, and let it brown over a moderate fire.
5. After ten minutes add three tablespoonfuls of flour and stir constantly.
6. Then moisten with the consommé and the claret.
7. Season again to taste with salt and pepper, and stir until it comes to a boil.
8. Then add the small onions which have been nicely peeled, and one ounce of salt pork and the herb bouquet.
9. Let all cook about forty minutes, and about five minutes before serving add the mushrooms.
10. Take the herb bouquet from the preparation; place the latter on a hot dish and decorate nicely with toasted Croutons, and serve hot.

title: Stewed Wild Ducks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pair of Ducks
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Herb Bouquet Chopped Very Fine
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 1 Cup of Water
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and pick the ducks nicely.
2. Cut into joints, or stew whole, as desired.
3. Rub well with salt and pepper.
4. Chop two onions very fine.
5. Put them into the stewpan with a tablespoonful of melted butter, and let them brown slightly.
6. Add the well-seasoned ducks.
7. Let these brown well and add the one square inch of finely minced ham.
8. Add the clove of garlic and two sprigs each of thyme, parsley and one bay leaf, minced very fine.
9. Let this brown with the ducks, stirring frequently, and then pour over one good glass of claret.
10. Let this simmer for ten minutes, stirring constantly, so that it will not burn, and add one cup of boiling water.
11. Season well to taste, and let the ducks simmer well for about an hour.

title: Stingaree au Beurre Noir
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Young Stingaree
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- A Handful of Chopped Parsley
- The Juice of 1 Lemon, or 1 Spoonful of French Vinegar
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take a fine young Stingaree and cut off the tail.
2. Cut the wings from the body and throw away the rest of the fish.
3. Throw the wings into boiling water and parboil them.
4. When boiled ten minutes, take them off the water and then take all the skin off.
5. Put one tablespoonful of butter in a frying pan and let it brown without burning.
6. When a nice coffee color, add a handful of chopped parsley and let it brown for a half minute.
7. Then add the juice of one lemon or a spoonful of French vinegar.
8. Pour this over the fish, salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.

title: Stingaree With Caper Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- A Fine Young Stingaree
- A Pint of French Vinegar
- A Handful of Parsley Minced Very Fine
- A Teaspoonful of Salt
- Parsley to Garnish
- Caper Sauce

## Directions

1. Cut off the tail of the Stingaree.
2. Cut off the wings, and throw the rest of the fish away.
3. Throw the wings into boiling water charged with salt.
4. Parboil them for fifteen or twenty minutes.
5. Take them out of the water and skin thoroughly.
6. Place in a heated dish and pour over a Caper Sauce.

title: Stone Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 spoonfuls lemon juice
- lemon peel grated
- apricot jam
- 1 pint cream
- 1/2 ounce isinglass
- sugar
- macaroons

## Directions

1. Put in the dish you intend sending to table, 3 spoonfuls of the lemon juice with a little of the peel grated, to apricot jam
2. Boil together 1 pint of cream, 1/2 ounce of isinglass, and some sugar
3. When nearly cold, pour it on the sweetmeat
4. Put a few macaroons at the bottom of the dish
5. Make a few hours before using

title: Strawberries Whole
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- equal weight of fruit and double refined sugar
- 1 pint of red currant juice to every 3 lbs. of strawberries
- largest scarlets, not dead ripe

## Directions

1. Lay fruit in a large dish and sprinkle 1/2 the sugar in fine powder
2. Give a gentle shake to the dish that the sugar may reach the under side of the fruit
3. The next day make a thin syrup with the remainder of the sugar
4. Allow 1 pint of red currant juice to every 3 lbs. of strawberries
5. Simmer them in this till sufficiently jellied

title: Strawberry Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Fresh, Stemmed Strawberries
- 1/2 Box of Gelatine, or 1/2 Pint
- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine, and prepare the whipped cream
2. Mash the strawberries and press them through a sieve
3. Add the white powdered sugar
4. Melt the gelatine by standing over boiling water, and then add it to the strawberry juice
5. Mix thoroughly
6. Turn it into a tin pan or mold, set the basin on a pan of cracked ice, stir constantly till it begins to thicken, and then add the whipped cream, stirring it gradually till all is thoroughly mixed
7. Pour into a mold and stand in an ice-cold place to harden

title: Strawberry Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Strawberries
- 1/2 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine strawberries, but not too ripe.
2. Wash them in cold water, and drain through a sieve.
3. Boil the sugar, and add the berries, and let them boil up once very gently.
4. Put them into a dish, pour the Jelly over, and serve.

title: Strawberry Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- flour
- crushed strawberries

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pint of milk, well sweetened
2. Moisten a dessertspoonful of flour with a little of the hot milk
3. Put the milk on the fire and stir with a wooden spoon
4. Pour in the juice of crushed strawberries till the cream becomes a pretty color
5. Strain the whole through a sieve

title: Strawberry Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- dessertspoonful of flour
- crushed strawberries

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pint of milk, well sweetened
2. Moisten a dessertspoonful of flour with a little of the hot milk
3. Put the milk on the fire and stir with a wooden spoon
4. Pour in the juice of crushed strawberries till the cream becomes a pretty color
5. Strain the whole through a sieve

title: Strawberry Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fine Large Strawberries
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of White Wine
- Grated Zest of ½ Lemon
- Fritter Batter a la Creole

## Directions

1. For this purpose have fine, large strawberries.
2. Make a Fritter Batter a la Creole, only just before adding the whites of the eggs add the grated zest of half a lemon and two tablespoonfuls of White Wine.
3. The batter must be of the consistency of thick cream.
4. Stem the strawberries, and drop them into the batter.
5. Proceed to fry as in recipe given for Apple Fritters, allowing two or three strawberries to each fritter.
6. Or make a marmalade of the strawberries, keeping them whole and proceed as in Orange Fritters.

title: Strawberry Granit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Strawberries
- 1 Tablespoonful of Strawberry Extract
- 1.5 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Mash the berries and cover with the sugar, and set aside to form a syrup.
2. Then drain and press through a sieve, extracting all the juice.
3. Add the extract.
4. Mix all with the water, and turn into a freezer and freeze till a light, snowy frosting appears.
5. Then serve in long, thin glasses.

title: Strawberry Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Cream
- 1 Quart of Strawberries or 2 Tablespoonfuls of Strawberry Extract
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 6 Eggs
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil; take from the fire, add the yolks of the eggs, well beaten to a cream, with half the sugar, and set aside to cool.
2. Then, when cool, add the remaining half of the sugar, which you will have blended with the strawberries, mashed well, and allowed to stand for several hours, and then press through a sieve.
3. Mix well with the milk, add the lemon juice and the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth; turn into the freezer, and freeze as above directed.
4. If the strawberries are too pale to impart a sufficiently rosy tinge to the cream add a few drops of Cochineal.
5. When not in season flavor with the Extract.

title: Strawberry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Strawberries
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe strawberries and weigh.
2. Allow equal weights of fruit and sugar.
3. Proceed to boil as directed.
4. When they begin to jelly, they are ready to be turned into the jelly glasses.

title: Strawberry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Berries
- 1 Pound of Sugar

## Directions

1. Select fine, ripe strawberries, and weigh, and allow equal weights of fruit and sugar.
2. Proceed to boil as directed.
3. When they begin to jelly, they are ready to be turned into the jelly glasses.

title: Strawberry Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of the juice of strawberries
- 2 lbs. of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil down

title: Strawberry Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- 1/2 pound of sugar to every pound of berries

## Directions

1. To every pound of strawberries allow a half of a pound of sugar.
2. Put the berries in a porcelain-lined kettle, and cover them with the sugar and let them stand for two hours.
3. Set over a moderate fire and bring the mixture to a boiling point.
4. Skim while boiling.
5. Pour immediately into cups and glasses, and cover tightly and place in a cool, dry place.

title: Strawberry Massepains
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sweet Almonds
- 8 Ounces of Sugar
- 6 Ounces of Crushed Strawberries

## Directions

1. Pound the almonds, and then proceed as in plain Massepains, adding the strawberries after you will have strained them through a sieve.

title: Strawberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Berries
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful Sweet Spices, Blended Equally

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for Raspberry Pie.

title: Strawberry Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Strawberries
- % Pound of Sugar
- 1 Gill of Sherry and Brandy, Mixed
- 1 Stale Small Sponge Cake
- 6 Eggs

## Directions

1. Stew the berries and proceed in the same manner as for Raspberry Pudding

title: Strawberry Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Quarts of Best Champagne or Claret
- 1 Quart of Strawberry Juice
- 2 Quarts of Seltzer Water
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Pint of Pineapple Juice
- 1½ Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- 3 Dozen Fine, Ripe Strawberries

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and water
2. Add the liquor, using either Claret or Champagne
3. Add the fruit juices and then the whole strawberries
4. Set to cool and serve in small punch glasses

title: Strawberry Ratafia
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Pounds of Strawberries
- 2 Gallons of Brandy
- 7 Pounds of Sugar
- 3 Quarts of Water

## Directions

1. Steep the berries, which you will have mashed well, for one month, or at least fifteen days, in the brandy
2. Add a syrup made of the sugar and water
3. Filter and proceed to bottle as directed

title: Strawberry Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Cream
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of fresh Mashed Strawberries

## Directions

1. Mash the strawberries, and press them through a sieve
2. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream; whip the cream and beat in the strawberries
3. Then add the berry juice and mix thoroughly
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of sherry, if desired, or brandy, and serve with strawberry pudding, strawberry shortcake, or strawberry pie
5. It may also be served cold with a plain bread pudding

title: Strawberry Sherbet
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen ounces of picked strawberries
- one quart of water
- one sliced lemon
- one teaspoonful of orange-flower water
- eighteen ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Crush strawberries in a mortar
2. Add to them a quart of water
3. Pour this into a basin with a sliced lemon and a teaspoonful of orange-flower water
4. Let it remain for two or three hours
5. Put eighteen ounces of sugar into another basin and cover it with a cloth
6. Pour the strawberry juice through the cloth
7. Gather up the cloth and squeeze out as much juice as possible from it
8. When the sugar is all dissolved, strain it again
9. Set the vessel containing it on ice until ready to serve

title: Strawberry Sherbet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Three lbs. of strawberries
- 8 ounces of red currants
- 1 pint of water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Crush the strawberries and currants in a sieve and let the juice run into a deep dish or bowl
2. Pour the water over the strawberries remaining in the sieve
3. Melt the sugar in a little water over the fire and add to the juice
4. Pour it into the mould
5. The taste must be the guide for the quantity of sugar

title: Strawberry Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Pints of Strawberry Juice or Pulp
- 1 1/2 Pints of Pickled Red Strawberries
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 3 Drops of Cochineal
- The Juice of 1/2 Lemon

## Directions

1. Stem the strawberries and add the sugar and lemon, and stand aside in their juice for an hour and a half.
2. Then strain through a fine sieve, and add the reserved juice.
3. Turn into the freezer and proceed to freeze according to directions given under the heading Ice Cream.

title: Strawberry Short Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 8 eggs
- 1 heaping cup of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sweet cream
- 2 teaspoonfuls of yeast powder
- nearly 1 quart of strawberries

## Directions

1. Rub butter into the sugar
2. Add eggs and flour
3. Add sweet cream and yeast powder
4. Bake in three jelly cake pans
5. When cold, lay between them nearly a quart of strawberries
6. Sprinkle sugar over each layer and over the top cake

title: Strawberry Short Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Sifted Flour
- 3/4 Pound of Sugar
- 2 Quarts of Strawberries
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Quart of Cream
- 3/4 Pound of Powdered Sugar
- Salt
- Grated zest of half a lemon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Stem the strawberries.
2. Sweeten to taste, and slightly mash.
3. Then set them aside.
4. Blend the sugar and butter together until very smooth, and add the baking powder, and blend well.
5. Add the salt.
6. Then add the eggs, breaking them in whole, one at a time, and beat all well for ten minutes.
7. Add the grated zest of half a lemon; by degrees add the flour, and mix all together very gently.
8. Divide into two parts; put one into well-buttered baking tins and bake in a moderate oven thirty minutes.
9. When done, remove the cakes and allow them to cool.
10. Detach the cakes from the tins by passing a knife gently around.
11. Have a dessert dish ready and lay one of the cakes upon it.
12. Spread two tablespoonfuls of whipped cream over, and then cover liberally with strawberries.
13. Cover with another cake and spread as before with cream and then with strawberries.
14. Lay the other cake on top and sprinkle with powdered sugar.
15. Serve with Whipped Cream a la Vanille or with rich cream or milk.

title: Strawberry Sponge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pint of Strawberries
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Boiling Water
- Gelatine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Stew the strawberries gently, and press them through a sieve.
2. Soak the gelatine in a cup of cold water for an hour, and then dissolve with a tablespoonful of sherry wine.
3. Pour it into the boiling water, and let it dissolve well.
4. Add the sugar, and let it thicken gently.
5. Then strain, and add the strawberries, and set it upon ice to cool.
6. Occasionally stir to prevent the fruit from settling at the bottom.
7. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and add them to the sponge, beating steadily till very smooth.
8. Set in a fancy bowl to harden, and serve with Vanilla Sauce poured over it.

title: Strawberry Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints of Strawberry Juice
- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 1 Ounce of Raspberry Essence

## Directions

1. Pick, stem and wash the strawberries, then pound and mash them well
2. Let them stand for thirty-six hours in a cool place
3. Strain through a bag
4. Cook the sugar and water till it forms the syrup called Le Petit Cassé, or crack, that is, till it instantly snaps asunder when placed between the fingers, after dipping them in cold water
5. Add the fruit syrups, and mix thoroughly
6. Let it boil for about five minutes
7. Take off the fire, let it get almost cold, and then put up in bottles

title: Strawberry Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Strawberries
- 1 Quart of Good French Vinegar
- White sugar in the proportion of one pound to every pint of the liquor

## Directions

1. Put one quart of strawberries into a jar or deep vessel, and pour over the vinegar
2. Let them stand for twenty-four hours, and then strain through a jelly bag, and add the other quart of strawberries, and let them stand for twenty-four hours longer
3. Strain them again, and again add the fruit, repeating this operation of straining and letting stand twenty-four hours three times
4. Strain through a muslin bag and add one pound of white sugar to every pint of the liquor
5. Boil the whole in a porcelain kettle for half an hour
6. When cold, bottle it and keep it in a cool place

title: Strawberry Water
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of strawberry juice
- 1 quart of water
- 8 ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Choose fine ripe strawberries, stem and bruise them in a little water, and let them stand for an hour or so
2. Squeeze them in a cloth and pour the juice off clear
3. To half a pint of the juice add one quart of water and eight ounces of sugar
4. Stir well, strain, and put it on ice, and serve as needed during the sultry summer days

title: Strawberry Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Quarts of Strawberry Juice
- 1.33 Quarts of Cold Water
- 3 Pounds of Granulated Sugar

## Directions

1. Follow in every respect the recipe given for Blackberry Wine

title: Strawberry, Raspberry, or Blackberry Cream Frozen
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sugar
- milk
- a quart of ripe fruit

## Directions

1. Make a quart of rich custard, with eggs, and sugar and milk
2. When cold, pour it on a quart of ripe fruit
3. Mash and pass it through a sieve
4. Add more sugar if required by the fruit
5. Freeze it

title: String Bean Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cold Boiled String Beans
- French Dressing (plain)

## Directions

1. Only very young and tender beans should be used for this salad.
2. Boil as directed under the heading Vegetables.
3. Put the beans in a salad bowl and allow to cool well.
4. Serve with a plain French dressing, or, better still, a simple dressing of vinegar and a dash of Cayenne.

title: String Beans & la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of String Beans
- A Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Boil the beans as in the recipe for String Beans with Drawn Butter Butter Sauce.
2. Drain and serve with a Sauce a la Poulette poured over.

title: String Beans a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of String Beans
- Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Boil the beans as in the recipe for String Beans with Butter Sauce.
2. Drain and serve with a Sauce a la Poulette poured over.

title: String Beans a la Vinaigrette
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of String Beans
- Sauce a la Vinaigrette
- Sliced lemon
- Chopped Parsley
- Small gherkins

## Directions

1. Boil the string beans according to the recipe for String Beans with Butter Sauce.
2. When done, drain and serve with a Vinaigrette Sauce.
3. Garnish the dish with sliced lemon dipped in parsley, which has been chopped very fine, and small gherkins cut in fan shapes.

title: String Beans Boiled With Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of String Beans
- 1 Pound of Lean Ham or Salt Pork
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed to prepare the beans in exactly the same manner as mentioned above.
2. Throw them into cold water, and let them stand for about ten minutes.
3. Have ready a large saucepan of boiling water in which you will have placed a pound of salt pork or ham.

title: String Beans Panaches
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of String Beans
- 1 Pint of Lima Beans
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the string beans and cut them into pieces of about three-fourths of an inch in length; mix them with an equal quantity of boiled Lima beans.
2. Put them in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter; let them saute for five minutes, and toss continually while cooking.
3. Season with salt and pepper to taste, place on a hot dish, sprinkle with finely-chopped parsley and serve hot.

title: String Beans With Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Fresh, Tender String Beans
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Always select tender beans.
2. Break the blossom end, and pull it backward, removing the string.
3. Then be careful to pare the thin strip from the other end of the bean pod.
4. Split the larger beans down the pod, and let the younger and more tender remain whole.
5. Wash them in clear, cold water, letting them stand about ten minutes.
6. Then drain off the water, put the beans into a saucepan, cover well with boiling water, and let them boil for forty minutes or an hour, according to the tenderness of the beans.
7. Just before serving drain off water, put a large tablespoonful of butter into the beans, mix well, salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.

title: String Beans With Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Fresh String Beans
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1/2 Cup of Fresh Milk or Cream
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. String and wash the beans according to the first recipe.
2. Place in a saucepan and cover with boiling water.
3. Let them boil for an hour.
4. Then drain off the water.
5. Take a tablespoonful of butter and one of flour, and blend well.
6. Add a half cup of fresh milk or cream, or one-half pint of the water in which the beans have been boiled.
7. Season nicely with salt and pepper.
8. Stir in the beans; set them back on the stove for several minutes; let them simmer gently.
9. Season again with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.

title: String Beans, Brittany Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Fresh String Beans
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Pint of Chicken Consomme or Water
- 1 Medium-Sized Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Take a medium-sized onion, peel and cut into small dice-shaped pieces.
2. Put the onion in a saucepan with the butter and let it saute to a golden brown.
3. Add the flour gradually, blending well, and moisten with the consomme or water.
4. Let the mixture come to a boil and skim the broth.
5. Then add the string beans, which have already been boiled and drained.
6. Let them simmer for ten minutes, adding in the meantime one finely-minced clove of garlic.
7. After ten minutes, place the beans in a hot dish, sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve hot.

title: Stuffed Apple Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Apples
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Orange or Apricot Marmalade
- 1/2 Cup of Water
- 1/4 Teaspoon of Ground Cinnamon
- 1/4 of a Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare fine, large apples, take out the core, and be careful to leave the apple entire
2. Fill the opening with orange or apricot marmalade
3. Put them together in a pan with a pound of clarified sugar
4. Add a half cup of water
5. Let them cook soft in the oven, sprinkling first with grated cinnamon
6. Add grated nutmeg when done, and serve either hot or cold

title: Stuffed Artichokes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Quarts of Boiled Artichokes
- 1 Onion, minced
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1/2 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1/4 Can of Mushrooms
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Bread crumbs
- Broth or consomme or water

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the artichokes according to recipe.
2. When the leaves begin to be tender and it is possible to pull out a leaf without difficulty, take the artichokes off the fire.
3. Pull off a few of the coarse leaves, and then scoop out the artichoke, without touching the fond, or bottom meat, and without breaking the outer scales or leaves from the sides and around.
4. Chop an onion, or, rather, mince it very fine, and mince two cloves of garlic and half a square inch of ham very, very fine.
5. Take a quarter of a can of mushrooms and mince them fine.
6. Mix all this together as a stuffing, and season well with salt, pepper and Cayenne.
7. Put a tablespoonful of butter in a frying pan, and fry the dressing in it for about five or ten minutes.
8. Take off the fire, and stuff each artichoke from the center, which you will have scooped, beginning just above the heart or fond of the artichoke.
9. Pour over each a spoonful of broth or consomme, or water; sprinkle lightly with bread crumbs; put a dot of butter on each, and set in the oven and bake for five minutes, till the crumbs are nicely browned.
10. Serve immediately.

title: Stuffed Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of fine white cabbage
- 1 dozen fine chaurice
- 2 onions
- 1 tomato
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 square inch of ham
- 1 spoonful of butter
- Thyme, parsley and bay leaf
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Select a fine head of cabbage
2. Take away the big green leaves and select about twelve of the finest and nicest of the large white leaves
3. Put them in cold water for about an hour
4. Parboil for about twenty minutes in boiling water
5. Prepare a stuffing with a dozen fine chaurice, one onion chopped fine, a tablespoonful of butter and a tomato
6. Mince the sausage meat very fine after taking out of the cases
7. Mince fine the onion and tomato
8. Mince fine one sprig each of thyme, parsley and bay leaf
9. Add one square inch of finely-minced ham
10. Put the butter in the frying pan and as it browns add the onions
11. Let these brown and add the sausage meat and the ham
12. Mince four or five of the tender white leaves of the cabbage very fine and add
13. Add the minced clove of garlic and onion
14. Let these brown for five minutes and then let all simmer for about ten minutes
15. Take out and take each leaf of the cabbage and drain dry
16. Lay open on the table and put in each leaf equal quantities of the stuffing
17. Fold over and close nicely
18. Take slender strips of bacon and lay at the bottom of a wide and deep frying pan
19. Place the stuffed leaves on top of these
20. Place other strips of bacon on top

title: Stuffed Cabbage with Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Fine Head Cabbage
- 1 Dozen Fine Chaurice
- 1 Onion
- 3 Sprigs each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf
- 1 Square inch of Ham
- 1 Inch of a Red Pepper Pod
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Select a fine tender head of cabbage. Pick off the outer leaves, and let it soak in cold fresh water for an hour.
2. Then throw the whole head into a pot of boiling water for about three-quarters of an hour.
3. In the meantime make a stuffing by taking one dozen fine Chaurice, or sausage-meat, one onion, and three sprigs each of thyme and parsley, and one bay leaf.
4. Mince the herbs and onion very fine, and add to them one square inch of finely minced ham.
5. Chop the sausage meat over, and mingle this with the ham and herbs, and then add the finely minced onion and one clove of finely minced garlic.
6. Mix these well together. Season to taste.
7. Take the cabbage out of the water, and open carefully to the very heart, and put in a teaspoonful of the dressing.
8. Fold over this two or three leaves, and then insert the mixture in between another layer of leaves, and so continue until each layer of leaves has been nicely stuffed.
9. Press all firmly together, and then tie in the large leaves, which you will have boiled with the cabbage head.
10. Put it into the kettle of boiling water, and add a little salt and two inches of a red pepper pod, thyme, parsley and bay leaf.
11. Let it boil for two hours.
12. When done carefully untie the larger leaves in which you have cooked the cabbage, and lay the head in a well-heated dish.
13. Pour over all a Cream Sauce, and serve hot.

title: Stuffed Crabs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen crabs
- yolks of two hard boiled eggs
- thyme
- parsley
- onion
- butter
- black pepper
- cayenne
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil two dozen crabs
2. Pick out all the meat
3. Chop the yolks of two hard boiled eggs, some thyme, parsley and onion, slightly fried in butter
4. Add a lump of butter size of a hen's egg, and mix well
5. Add a little black pepper and cayenne before putting back in shell
6. Sprinkle over with bread crumbs
7. Put a bit of butter on top
8. Bake them brown

title: Stuffed Crabs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen crabs
- grated crackers
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- four tablespoons butter
- one egg
- juice of one lemon
- tomato
- toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil for a few moments one dozen crabs
2. Crack and pick out all of the meat
3. To this meat, add one-third as much grated crackers, one onion chopped fine, a little parsley, pepper and salt, four tablespoons of butter
4. Mix well and add one egg
5. Mix again, then add the juice of one lemon, with a good sized tomato
6. Fill the shells with the mixture
7. Bake about half hour before serving
8. Grate over them some toasted bread crumbs

title: Stuffed Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Large Fine Crabs
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- Hot Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt to Taste
- 1 Cup of Wet Bread

## Directions

1. Boil the Crabs according to recipe.
2. Take off the fire and let them cool in their own water.
3. When cool crack the claws and pick out all the meat.
4. In like manner, after having removed the uneatable portions, pick out all the meat from the bodies.
5. Season well with salt and black pepper.
6. Chop the onions and herbs very fine.
7. Put a tablespoonful of butter heaping over into the frying pan.
8. As it melts, add the chopped onion, and when it begins to fry, add the crab meat, which has been thoroughly seasoned with the chopped thyme, bay leaf, parsley and a dash of Cayenne to taste.
9. Let this fry and add a small clove of garlic, chopped very fine, and finally add the bread, which has been wet and thoroughly squeezed of all water.
10. Mix this well with the ingredients in the frying pan and let it fry about five minutes longer.
11. Then take off the fire and let it get cool.
12. Take a dozen of the finest and largest crab shells, or as many as this mixture will fill, and wash and stuff with the mixture, forming it into a rolling lump.
13. Sprinkle this with grated bread crumbs and put a dot of butter on top, or, better still, sprinkle with the melted butter; place in the oven and bake about five minutes, or until a nice brown.
14. Place on a platter and garnish with sprigs of parsley or celery tops.

title: Stuffed Crabs No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Large Fine Crabs
- Grated Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- Thyme
- Parsley and Onion
- Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs according to recipe.
2. Clean and cut and pick out all the meat.
3. Chop an onion fine; chop the thyme, bay leaf, and parsley and hard-boiled eggs, and mix well with the crab meat.
4. Season highly with hot pepper and salt to taste.
5. Put one tablespoonful heaping over with butter into the frying pan.
6. As it melts, add the onion and fry, being careful not to burn.
7. Then add the crab meat, and, if desired, the very small clove of a garlic, chopped very fine.
8. Let this fry about five minutes, stirring constantly.
9. Mix thoroughly, fry three minutes longer, and then take off the stove.
10. Stuff the crab shells, forming a rolling lump in the middle.
11. Sprinkle lightly with grated bread crumbs, and put a dot of butter on top.
12. Place in a quick oven and let them bake about five minutes, or until a nice brown.

title: Stuffed Egg Plant
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- fried onions
- parsley
- egg
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- bread
- bread crumbs
- fresh tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cut in half and take out the centre
2. Boil the inside
3. When soft, chop fine
4. Season with fried onions, parsley, egg, salt, butter, pepper and bread
5. Stuff the outsides
6. Cover with bread crumbs
7. Add the insides of fresh tomatoes, if you choose

title: Stuffed Egg Plant
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- fried onions
- parsley
- egg
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- bread
- fresh tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cut in half, take out the centre
2. Boil the inside
3. When soft, chop fine, and season with fried onions, parsley, egg, salt, butter, pepper and bread
4. Stuff the outsides
5. Cover with bread crumbs, and add the insides of fresh tomatoes, if you choose

title: Stuffed Eggplant
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Eggplants of Good Size
- 1½ Cups of Bread, Wet and Squeezed
- 1 Tomato
- 1 Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Thyme, Parsley, Bay Leaf
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the eggplant in the middle, and put to boil in cold water for about half an hour, or until tender.
2. Take out of the water and set to cool.
3. When quite cool, take out the seeds and throw away.
4. Scoop out carefully the soft meat of the eggplant, and leave the skins unbroken.
5. Set these skins carefully aside.
6. Chop the soft eggplant fine, and then wet and squeeze one and a half cups of bread.
7. Chop the onion and tomato fine, and mince the herbs and garlic very fine.
8. Season the eggplant well with salt and pepper.
9. Put the butter in the frying pan and brown the onion in it slightly.
10. Add the chopped tomato and its juice, and let this fry for four or five minutes.
11. Add the minced herbs and the clove of garlic, and almost immediately the chopped eggplant.
12. Add the bread at once, and mix all well.
13. Season again to taste, and let all fry for about five minutes.
14. Fill the shells with the stuffing, sprinkle the top lightly with bread crumbs, dot with butter, and set in the oven to bake to a nice brown.

title: Stuffed Green Peppers
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen fresh green peppers
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1/2 onion, chopped very fine
- 1/2 inch of boiled ham
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1/2 cup of bread, wet and squeezed thoroughly
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Cayenne pepper
- Grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Parboil one dozen of the peppers.
2. Take off the skins and cut the topmost tip, and clean the inside of the peppers, throwing the seeds away.
3. Take a dozen or more raw peppers, cut off the stalk or stem, and clean the insides of all seeds, throwing the seeds away.
4. Chop the peppers very fine.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter on the fire.
6. Add one-half of an onion, minced fine, and let it brown slightly in the butter.
7. Add the peppers, minced very fine, almost mashed into a jelly, and the half inch of ham, minced very fine.
8. As these brown, add the clove of garlic, minced fine.
9. When the peppers are well-cooked add one-half a cup of bread that has been wet in water and squeezed thoroughly.
10. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and let it brown.
11. Add a dash of Cayenne pepper.
12. When the stuffing is well browned and well seasoned, take from the stove and stuff the peppers.
13. Sprinkle a little grated bread crumbs on top, and a dot of butter on each pepper top.
14. Put it in the stove, let it brown, and serve hot with meats, broiled or roasted, chicken, etc.

title: Stuffed Ham
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked ham
- spices
- sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Soak your ham all night, scrape it nicely, and boil it half an hour to make the skin tender.
2. Take it from the pot, gash it all over, introduce as much of the pounded spices as the incisions will hold, and then close the skin over the gashes and boil in the same manner, with vegetables thrown in, as in recipe for boiled ham.

title: Stuffed Macaroni
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- egg
- yolks of 2 eggs
- cinnamon
- pepper
- salt
- cupful of Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken until the meat separates from the bones
2. Pound the meat
3. Add 1 egg whole, and the yolks of 2 others, a little cinnamon, pepper, salt, and a cupful of Parmesan cheese, finely grated
4. Mix the ingredients well
5. Stuff the macaroni
6. Put it to boil in the broth or water in which the chicken was boiled

title: Stuffed Macaroni
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- 1 egg
- 2 egg yolks
- cinnamon
- pepper
- salt
- 1 cupful Parmesan cheese
- macaroni
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken until the meat separates from the bones
2. Pound the meat
3. Add 1 egg whole and the yolks of 2 others
4. Add a little cinnamon, pepper, salt, and a cupful of Parmesan cheese, finely grated
5. Mix the ingredients well
6. Stuff the macaroni
7. Put it to boil in the broth or water in which the chicken was boiled

title: Stuffed Mutton Feet
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Mutton Feet
- ½ Cup of Wet Bread
- 1 Hard-Boiled Egg
- 1 Spoon of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 3 Thin Slices of Veal
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 3 Carrots
- 2 Onions
- The Juice of 1 Lemon
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Clean the mutton feet well, according to directions given, and boil and take out all the bones.
2. Take a half cup of wet bread and squeeze well.
3. Season well with salt and pepper and fry in a little butter and add a chopped egg.
4. Stuff the feet with this, splitting down the length and sewing up to prevent the dressing escaping.
5. Take a saucepan and put in one tablespoonful of lard and lay over it thin slices of veal, well seasoned.
6. Add one bay leaf, one sprig of thyme and geranium (minced very fine), three chopped carrots, and two onions (chopped very fine).
7. Pour over this the juice of a lemon and let it simmer gently for about a half hour, turning the veal that it may cook well and be thoroughly penetrated by the juices.
8. Put the stuffed mutton feet on top, cover closely, and let all simmer for a half hour longer.
9. Unsew the mutton feet, lay them on the slices of veal, garnish nicely and serve with a Sauce à l'Espagnole, Sauce aux Tomates, a Sauce aux Oignons, or Sauce à la Provençale.

title: Stuffed Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Dozen Large Spanish Onions
- A Stuffing of Chaurice
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 Chaurice
- 1 Cup of Bread, wet and squeezed
- 1 Inch of Ham, finely chopped
- 1 Sprig each of Thyme and Parsley and Sweet Marjoram
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Powdered Bread Crumbs

## Directions

1. Take a half dozen fine, large Spanish onions, and put them in hot ashes to roast.
2. When they are sufficiently cooked, which will be in about half an hour, take them out of the ashes, dust off, and peel well.
3. Then open the interiors and fill with a stuffing made as follows: Take a tablespoonful of butter and put in a frying pan, and add three chaurice, which you will have taken out of the casings and chopped finely again.
4. Add a cup of bread, which has been wet and squeezed, and mix well.
5. Then add an inch of ham, finely chopped, and a sprig each of thyme and parsley and sweet marjoram, and one bay leaf, all finely minced.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Fry about fifteen minutes, and then stuff the onions as far down in the center as possible, and between the folds.
8. Sprinkle the top with powdered bread crumbs and put a little dot of butter on top of each.
9. Set in the oven and let them bake for about thirty or forty minutes.
10. Serve with roast beef.

title: Stuffed Peaches
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh white mustard seed
- vinegar
- scraped horseradish
- powdered ginger
- red pepper pods
- small onions or garlic clove
- chopped peach
- packthread
- salad oil
- leather

## Directions

1. Cut a piece from the stem end of peaches just sufficient to allow the seed to come out
2. Throw them into cold vinegar until you make the stuffing
3. Wet fresh white mustard seed with vinegar and allow to swell a few hours
4. Mix mustard seed, scraped horseradish, powdered ginger, red pepper pods, small onions or garlic clove with vinegar
5. Add half as much chopped peach to the mixture
6. Stuff the peaches hard with this mixture
7. Replace the piece cut out and tie it up tight with packthread
8. Boil a quart of vinegar for each dozen peaches and season it with the same spices as the stuffing
9. Boil the spices in a small bag, then put in the peaches and let them scald ten or fifteen minutes
10. Place the peaches in jars and pour scalding vinegar well spiced over them until they are covered
11. Add at the top a tablespoonful of salad oil
12. Cover the jar tight by tying leather over it

title: Stuffed Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- free-stone peaches
- salt and water
- garlic
- horse radish
- mustard seed
- cloves
- ginger root
- vinegar
- cinnamon
- fresh made mustard
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take free-stone peaches of the finest kind, let them be full grown, but not quite ripe
2. Lay them in salt and water for 2 days, covered with a board to keep them down
3. Wipe them dry
4. Cut them open, and extract the stones
5. Prepare the stuffing to the taste: minced garlic, scraped horse radish, bruised mustard seed, and cloves, a little ginger root soaked in water to soften it, and then sliced
6. Fill the peaches with the mixture
7. Tie them round, to secure the stuffing
8. Season the vinegar with broken cinnamon, fresh made mustard, a little ginger and nutmeg
9. Mix this all well together, and fill up the jar with the pickle

title: Stuffed Pig's Feet a la Perigueux
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pigs' Feet
- 2 Minced truffles
- 3/8 Glass of Madeira wine
- 1 Pound of boned turkey forcemeat
- 6 Pieces of crepinette
- 1 Egg
- 2 Ounces of butter
- 1/2 Pint of hot Perigueux sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the Pig's Feet, and then split them in two.
2. Take out the bones, lay the flesh on a dry, clean cloth and wipe well.
3. Make a forcemeat of boned turkey.
4. Add the truffles, which have been finely minced, and a half glass of Madeira or Sherry wine.
5. Mix this well together.
6. Take six pieces of crepinette, cut them the size of a man's hand and lay on a clean biscuit board.
7. Place on each piece of skin a portion of the forcemeat about the size of a hen's egg and flatten out well.
8. Place one-half of a pig's foot on top of this farcie, and cover with another layer of the stuffing.
9. On either side lay three thin slices of truffles.
10. Wrap the crepinettes up in some fanciful shape, such as an envelope or card case.
11. Dip them separately in a well-beaten egg, and then in grated bread-crumbs.
12. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter in a saucepan or deep frying pan, cover closely.
13. Let the feet cook on a slow fire for twenty-five minutes, allowing twelve minutes and a half to each side.
14. Serve with a pint of hot Perigueux sauce.
15. Place the sauce in a dish, lay the feet neatly over it and send to the table hot.

title: Stuffed Potatoes à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 potatoes
- Chaurice (sausage)
- 1 tablespoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Select fine large potatoes, and have ready several nice Chaurice.
2. Wash and peel the potatoes.
3. Potatoes may also be stuffed after washing, by peeling carefully and scooping out the insides nicely with a spoon or sharp-pointed knife.
4. Then make a sausage forcemeat and heap up lightly on top.
