5. Butter a baking dish nicely, place the potatoes upon it, and let them bake in a slow oven for a half hour or forty-five minutes, till nicely browned, and send to the table hot.

title: Stuffed Potatoes, Lenten Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- the yolk of 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- three-quarter cup of milk or cream
- three-quarter teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Bake the potatoes in the oven, and, when done, cut one end and scoop out the meat without breaking the skin.
2. Add the yolk of one egg, the butter, chopped parsley, salt and pepper to taste, and the grated nutmeg, and moisten all with the cream or milk.
3. Mix well and beat very light.
4. Then refill the skins and return to the oven for a few minutes, till very hot and slightly browned, and serve.

title: Stuffed Potatoes, Swiss Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- three-quarter cup of hot milk
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- pepper to taste
- the whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Bake the potatoes, and, when they are done, cut off the tops, and scoop out the meat into a hot bowl; mash very fine, and add the butter, the milk and salt and pepper.
2. Beat all till very light, and add the beaten whites of two eggs, and stir lightly.
3. Fill the potato skins with this preparation, heaping the flaky potatoes nicely on top.
4. Brush over lightly with the yolks of the eggs, set in the oven to brown and serve hot.

title: Stuffed Roast Shoulder or Breast of Veal
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Shoulder of Veal
- 1/4 Pound of Ham
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- 1 Hard-Boiled Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Spoon of Flour
- 1 Cup of stale bread
- Salt pork or ham, preferably ham
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- Hot pepper to taste
- 1/4 Glass of white wine or 2 Tablespoonfuls
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of beef broth
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- Salt and Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Prepared mustard (optional)

## Directions

1. If the shoulder of the veal is used, have the butcher remove the bone.
2. When ready to cook, wipe well with a damp towel, and then dredge with salt and pepper, seasoning highly.
3. Make a dressing by taking one cup of stale bread, wet and squeezed, one-quarter pound of sliced ham or salt pork, preferably the ham, and chop very fine.
4. Mix this with the bread and season highly with hot pepper, according to taste; add 1 carrot, 1 onion, 1 bay leaf, 1 hard-boiled egg, 1 sprig of thyme, 1 of parsley, all minced very fine.
5. Add one tablespoonful of butter, and place in a frying pan; let it fry for about ten minutes and then add, if you have it, a quarter of a glass of white wine or two tablespoonfuls, and two tablespoonfuls of beef broth; stir well and cook for five minutes longer.
6. Then stuff the shoulder well and skewer the filet to prevent the dressing from falling out in cooking. It is well to tie the veal at either end with a piece of twine.
7. Take one tablespoonful of lard and dredge roast according to preceding recipes.
8. If the breast of the veal is used, make long gashes between the ribs and fill with a dressing prepared as above, place in the baking pan and roast slowly, according to directions.
9. When finished cooking, remove the shoulder or breast, and stir the gravy well, adding a cup of broth or water and the juice of one lemon and a teaspoonful of butter, seasoning to taste.
10. Serve with the roast. Some like the addition of a teaspoonful of prepared mustard, but that is a matter of taste.

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Tomatoes
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Cup of Wet Bread, Squeezed Thoroughly
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Clove of Garlic (if desired)
- 1 Onion
- 1/4 Inch of Ham

## Directions

1. Wash the tomatoes, selecting fine, large, smooth ones for this purpose.
2. Either cut the tomato in two, or else cut one slice from the stem end.
3. Scoop out the inside of the tomato, and put it in a dish and save the skins.
4. Take one onion and one quarter of an inch of ham and chop very fine.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and add the onion, letting it brown nicely.
6. Then add the ham.
7. Let it brown.
8. Add the insides of the tomatoes, and then add, almost instantly, a cup of bread that has been wet and squeezed.
9. Beat all well together as it fries, and add salt and pepper to taste.
10. Let it cook well, and then take off, and stuff the tomatoes, cut in halves or whole.
11. Sprinkle the tops with bread crumbs, and dot with butter.
12. Place in the oven for fifteen or twenty minutes, till brown, and serve hot with filet of beef or chicken, etc.

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- crumbs of bread

## Directions

1. Take 12 large tomatoes and take out as much of the pulp and seed as you can with convenience scoop out, without injuring the form
2. Chop up the pulp, and add to it salt, pepper, thyme, to the taste, with 1 tablespoonful of butter
3. Form this into a stuffing with crumbs of bread, and put back into the skins
4. Bake them in a pan very nicely in a moderate oven

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- bread
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Scald and peel them
2. Stuff with bread, parsley, salt, pepper and butter, well mixed together
3. Place in pan and bake

title: Stuffed Tomatoes a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of the White Meat of a Chicken
- 2 Inches of Ham
- 1 Egg
- 1 Tablespoonful of Parsley
- 1 Onion, Minced Fine
- 1/2 Cup Bread Crumbs
- Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic (if desired)

## Directions

1. Chop the chicken meat very fine.
2. Cut the tomatoes into halves, and scoop out the insides without breaking the outer skins.
3. Take the inside meat and chop fine.
4. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the saucepan, and as it melts add the chopped onion, and let it brown.
5. After a few minutes, add the ham and chicken, and let these brown.
6. Then add the tomatoes, and, as they brown add the bread crumbs.
7. Add the minced herbs and a clove of garlic if desired.
8. Let all simmer gently.
9. Season to taste with salt and pepper and a dash of cayenne.
10. When well cooked, remove from the fire, when about the consistency of thick starch.
11. Let it cool slightly, and then stuff each tomato shell.
12. Sprinkle bread crumbs grated on top, and dot with butter.
13. Place in the oven for about half an hour, and serve as an entremet with chicken or veal.

title: Stuffing for Ducks
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Cup of Bread, Wet and Squeezed
- 1 Onion Chopped Very Fine
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1/8 Teaspoonful of Sage
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf, Minced Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. The Creoles generally stuff the domestic duck when roasted, using an oyster stuffing
2. The wild duck stuffed with oysters is a most delectable dish
3. The domestic duck is always roasted and stuffed
4. Serve with Currant Jelly

title: Stuffing for Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Mashed Potatoes
- 4 Apples
- 4 Onions
- 1/2 Teaspoonful Powdered Sage
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Thyme
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Water

## Directions

1. Place the apples and onions and herbs in a saucepan and add water sufficient to cover nicely
2. Let all cook together till soft
3. Mash well and rub through a sieve
4. Add the cup of mashed potatoes and mix well, seasoning with salt and pepper
5. Stuff the body and craw, sew up and truss the goose, and bake according to recipe

title: Stuffing of Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Can of Truffles
- 1 Cup of Bread, Wet and Squeezed
- 1 Onion, Chopped Very Fine
- 1/8 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 1/2 Teaspoonfuls of Sage
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme, Parsley and Bay Leaf, Minced Very Fine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for egg or oyster stuffing, using a quarter of a can of truffles chopped, instead of the oysters or egg

title: Succotash, or Corn and Beans Mixed
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pints of green shelled beans or string beans
- corn from two dozen ears
- salt
- pepper
- boiling water
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil for half an hour two pints of green shelled beans, or the same amount of string beans
2. Pour off the water
3. Cut the corn from two dozen ears, put it in the pot among the beans
4. Add salt and pepper, and cover them with boiling water
5. Let it boil for half an hour
6. Add a lump of butter as big as a hen's egg, rolled in flour
7. Let this boil up once and it is done

title: Suet Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cupful of chopped suet
- half a cup of molasses
- one cup of raisins chopped
- a teaspoonful of powdered cloves and cinnamon
- one-half cup of sugar
- two eggs well beaten
- half a cup of sweet milk
- a little salt
- two teaspoonfuls of yeastpowder
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir in flour until it is a thick batter.
2. Flour a cloth, and pour in the mixture, leaving room to swell.
3. Boil two hours.

title: Suffolk Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of flour
- 3 eggs
- cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a batter
2. Bake in cups for breakfast

title: Sugar Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of sifted flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 1/2 lbs. of powdered sugar
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brandy
- 1 small teaspoonful of soda dissolved in water
- 4 tablespoonfuls of carraway seed

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Sugar Boiling
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Put to boil on a brisk fire
2. Skim carefully and wash on the sides with a hair brush dipped in water, to prevent the sugar from graining

title: Sugar Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brown sugar to each egg
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lard
- 1 teaspoonful of soda dissolved in ½ wineglass of wine
- orange peel or cinnamon to the taste

## Directions

1. add eggs, brown sugar, butter, and lard to flour
2. dissolve soda in wine
3. season with orange peel or cinnamon to the taste
4. roll thin
5. bake in a quick oven

title: Sugar Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of dry sifted flour
- 1/4 lb. of fresh butter washed in rose water
- 1/4 lb. of sifted loaf sugar
- the yolk of an egg beaten with a tablespoonful of cream

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and sugar together
2. Rub in the butter
3. Add the egg yolk and cream mixture
4. Make it into a paste
5. Roll and cut it into small round cakes
6. Bake upon a floured tin

title: Sugar Candy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six cups of sugar
- one cup of vinegar
- one cup of water
- one spoonful of butter
- one teaspoonful of soda dissolved in a little hot water
- lemon or vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil all together without stirring, for half an hour
2. Flavor with lemon or vanilla
3. Pull like the old-fashioned molasses candy, or cool on a buttered plate

title: Sugar Candy Custard
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- vanilla
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 1 small tablespoonful of flour
- 3 ounces of white sugar

## Directions

1. Put on 1 quart of milk, with as much vanilla as will highly flavor it
2. Add the yolks of 8 eggs, beaten with a small tablespoonful of flour
3. Mix these together as for boiled custard
4. Put 3 ounces of white sugar into a clean frying pan
5. Let it boil up clear
6. As soon as it is brown, add a little of the custard to prevent the sugar from getting in lumps
7. Pour the whole into the custard, and sweeten it to the taste

title: Sugar Ginger Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 1/2 lbs. of brown sugar
- 1 1/2 lb. of butter
- 9 eggs
- 1 cup of powdered ginger
- 1 cup of wine

## Directions

1. Rub the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Beat the eggs and add them
3. Stir in the flour, ginger and wine
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Sugar Kisses
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- powdered white sugar
- two or three drops of essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of three eggs to a froth
2. Stir in powdered white sugar, a little at a time, till you have formed a very thick batter
3. Add two or three drops of essence of lemon
4. Wet a sheet of white paper, lay it on a tin and drop this mixture upon it in lumps about the size and shape of a walnut
5. Set them in a cool oven, and as soon as their surface is hardened, take them out and remove them from the paper with a broad-bladed knife
6. Let the oven cool still more, then place these little cakes, laying the flat part of two together, on a sieve and return them to the oven
7. Let them remain for fifteen minutes before they are done

title: Sugar Plums
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Best Confectionery Sugar
- Sufficient Water to Dissolve
- 1 Pound of Any Fruit

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of the best sugar, and add sufficient water to dissolve it or to start it boiling
2. Let it boil to a syrup
3. Place the fruit to be crystallized in a pan, and pour over the syrup
4. Turn the fruit lightly by shaking the pan till every side is coated, and set to cool
5. When cool, drain off the syrup and set the pans on their sides, so that remaining particles may be drained off, and every side be coated with the crystallized mixture
6. It is always best to pour the syrup over the fruit while it is warm, as it takes a firmer hold and makes a brighter crystal

title: Suississe
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a wineglass of absinthe
- half a tablespoon of orgeat syrup
- plenty of fine ice
- water

## Directions

1. add water
2. mix well
3. Serve in liquor glass

title: Sunshine Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 11 eggs
- 2 cups of sugar
- 1 cup of butter
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- 3 cups of flour
- extract of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix yolks of 11 eggs, sugar, butter, milk, cream of tartar, soda, and flour
2. Flavor with extract of vanilla
3. Bake

title: Superior Gold Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- one pound of flour
- yolks of ten eggs
- one teaspoonful of soda mixed with a little hot water

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar together
2. beat the eggs light, and add them to the butter and sugar
3. stir in the flour and soda

title: Superior Silver Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- three-quarters of a pound of flour
- whites of ten eggs beaten to a froth

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar together
2. add the eggs
3. stir in the flour

title: Superior Tomato Catsup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a bushel of ripe tomatoes
- a pint and a half of salt
- four tablespoonfuls of ground cloves
- four tablespoonfuls of cayenne pepper
- a quarter of a pound of allspice
- a tablespoonful of black pepper
- a head of garlic skinned and separated
- a half gallon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Get a bushel of ripe tomatoes, scald them until they are soft enough to squeeze through a sieve
2. When strained, add to the pulp a pint and a half of salt, four tablespoonfuls of ground cloves, same of cayenne pepper, a quarter of a pound of allspice and a tablespoonful of black pepper, a head of garlic skinned and separated, and a half gallon of vinegar
3. Boil until it is reduced one-half, then bottle

title: Superior Tomato Catsup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a bushel of ripe tomatoes
- a pint and a half of salt
- four tablespoonfuls of ground cloves
- four tablespoonfuls of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Get a bushel of ripe tomatoes
2. scald them until they are soft enough to squeeze through a sieve
3. When strained, add to the pulp a pint and a half of salt, four tablespoonfuls of ground cloves, same of cayenne pepper

title: Surprise Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Nice, Small Ripe Apples
- 1 Glass of Brandy
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- The White of 1 Egg
- ½ Teaspoonful of Ground Cinnamon
- ½ Teaspoon of Ground Allspice
- Apricots
- Fritter Batter
- Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Take nice, small, ripe apples, and peel them.
2. Cut off about an inch at the top end, and then scoop out the interior, leaving the apple frame whole.
3. Take out all the seeds and cores from these cuttings, and mince them very fine.
4. Then put them into a dish and put also the apple frames, and cover them with brandy.
5. Sprinkle well with the grated zest of a lemon and ground cinnamon and allspice, and add to the minced portion of the apples a few apricots, well minced.
6. After an hour, take the frames out of the marinade.
7. Then drain the apple stuffing of the brandy and add to it the white of an egg, well beaten to a stiff froth.
8. Mix this well, and then fill the interiors of the apple frames with it.
9. Cover with the pieces of apple that were taken from the tops, and which must be immersed in the marinade, like the frames, covering this opening well.
10. Take the fritter batter, and immerse each piece gently, just enough for the batter to cover, and place in a frying pan of boiling lard, and fry to a golden brown.
11. Place on a dish, sprinkle well with powdered sugar, and serve hot.
12. Or the apples may be simply placed in the oven, sprinkled with sugar, a little water added, and let them bake till a beautiful golden brown.

title: Sweet Almond Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Nice Almonds
- 1 Quart of Milk
- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 4 Ounces of Powdered White Sugar
- Orange Flower Water

## Directions

1. Take six nice almonds and shell them.
2. Blanch them by throwing into boiling water, and letting them stand on the back of the stove for five minutes.
3. Throw into cold water, and take out the inner skins by rubbing them together with the hands.
4. Whip in one quart of milk, the whites of two eggs, and four ounces of powdered sugar.
5. Let the milk boil slowly on the fire till it is reduced one-half, and then add the almonds, which you will have chopped fine, and add a tablespoonful of Orange Flower Water.
6. Let the mixture boil for three minutes longer.
7. Set it to cool.
8. Garnish with almonds that have been passed in Caramel or melted sugar and allowed to cool.

title: Sweet Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 1 quart of sugar
- 8 spoonfuls of milk
- butter sufficient to knead them
- the rind of 3 lemons and the juice of 1

## Directions

1. Mix the ingredients together
2. Knead and bake

title: Sweet Breads
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet breads
- cold water
- grated bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- lard
- hot water
- sherry
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil ten minutes to blanch
2. Throw them into cold water
3. Skim and lard them
4. Roll in grated bread crumbs, pepper and salt
5. Cook in boiling lard till quite tender
6. Lay them in a dish
7. Pour over the following gravy: a cup of hot water, a large wineglass of sherry, a little nutmeg and lemon juice, thickened with a tablespoonful of flour

title: Sweet Corn Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of cornmeal
- a pint of milk
- a pint of molasses
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of powdered ginger
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs light
2. Add the cornmeal, milk, molasses, butter, and powdered ginger—the meal last
3. Bake in a pan

title: Sweet Damsons
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 lbs. of fruit
- 3 lbs. of sugar
- 1 quart of vinegar
- mace
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. To 5 lbs. of fruit add 3 lbs. of sugar, 1 quart of vinegar
2. Boil the sugar and vinegar, with mace, cloves and cinnamon

title: Sweet Flannel Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pint soft-boiled rice
- 1 teacup cream
- 1 teacup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful yeast or 1 teaspoonful pearl ash

## Directions

1. Let the rice cool
2. Add the cream, sugar, eggs, and yeast or pearl ash
3. Rub them well together
4. Bake on a griddle

title: Sweet Journey Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large spoonfuls of brown sugar
- 2 large spoonfuls of good butter
- yolks of 3 eggs
- a nutmeg (grated), equal proportion of cornmeal and flour

## Directions

1. Stir together the brown sugar and butter
2. Beat the yolks of 3 eggs and add to the sugar and butter
3. Grate a nutmeg
4. Add an equal proportion of cornmeal and flour to knead it
5. Spread it on a board and glaze it with the white of an egg
6. Bake before the fire as other journey cake

title: Sweet Omelet Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- A Plain Omelet
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Brandy
- Creole Fritter Batter
- Powdered White Sugar

## Directions

1. Make an omelet, and add two tablespoonfuls of sugar and a tablespoonful of good brandy.
2. Cook it nicely and very soft, and then cut it into small slices.
3. Plunge these into the fritter batter, let them fry to a golden brown, and then drain in the mouth of the oven on brown paper.
4. Place in a heated dish, sprinkle well with fine white sugar, and serve hot.

title: Sweet Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of cling-stone peaches
- salt
- water
- 1 gallon of vinegar
- 4 lbs. of brown sugar
- allspice
- cloves
- nutmeg
- mace

## Directions

1. Cover peaches with salt and water
2. Let them remain all night
3. In the morning, take them out
4. To 1 gallon of vinegar add 4 lbs. of brown sugar, with allspice, cloves, nutmeg and mace to the taste
5. Boil it, and pour it over the peaches hot
6. Do the same 4 days in succession

title: Sweet Pickle of Figs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- brine
- alum water
- vinegar
- sugar
- cinnamon
- mace
- cloves

## Directions

1. Put the figs in brine at night
2. In the morning, or after being in brine about twelve hours, take them out
3. Wash off the salt
4. Put them in alum water for three hours
5. Take them out and scald them in hot water until heated through
6. Make a syrup of a quart of vinegar, a pint of sugar with a tablespoonful of cinnamon, mace, and cloves each
7. Boil half an hour
8. Pour on the figs boiling hot
9. Repeat the boiling next day
10. Bottle up and seal for future use

title: Sweet Pickle Peaches
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of peaches
- 3 lbs. of sugar
- 1 quart of vinegar
- allspice
- cloves
- mace

## Directions

1. In making the syrup add allspice, cloves and mace to the taste
2. Form the syrup
3. Throw in the fruit and cook it, but not as much as usual with preserves
4. If preferred with the skins on, rub the fuzz off with a coarse napkin

title: Sweet Pickle Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- 1 lb. of brown sugar to 2 lbs. of tomatoes
- 1 pint of vinegar to 1 lb. of tomatoes
- cinnamon
- allspice
- mace
- cloves
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Peel the tomatoes
2. Put 1 lb. of brown sugar to 2 lbs. of tomatoes
3. Sprinkle it on, and let it stand 2 hours
4. Boil them till the scum ceases to rise, skimming it off as fast as it rises
5. Add 1 pint of vinegar to 1 lb. of tomatoes
6. Add cinnamon, allspice, mace, cloves and black pepper
7. Boil them slowly until transparent
8. Be very careful to take off the scum

title: Sweet Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Small Cucumbers
- 3 Dozen Black Peppers
- 1 Dozen Allspice
- 1 Dozen Blades of Mace
- 3 Dozen Cloves
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Gallon of Boiling Vinegar
- Powdered alum
- Strong brine

## Directions

1. Wash the Cucumbers, selecting nice small ones, of about a finger in length.
2. Cover them with a strong brine, sufficiently strong to bear an egg.
3. Put a cover on the jar, and let them stand for about three days.
4. Then take them out and throw away the brine, rejecting all the pickles that have become soft under pressure.
5. Put into a kettle of fresh water, and add a little powdered alum allowing about a quarter of a teaspoonful, or a piece about half the size of a bird's egg, to one gallon of liquid.
6. Put a close lid over all the boiler, and steam, without letting them boil, for about four or five hours.
7. Boil five minutes with three dozen black peppers, one dozen blades of mace, three dozen cloves, one dozen allspice, all whole, and one cup of sugar.
8. Place the pickles in clean jars, and pour over the boiling vinegar.
9. These pickles must then be corked closely.
10. Three days after take or draw off this vinegar, boil it again, and again pour over the pickles.
11. Repeat this operation three times, at about intervals of three or four days.
12. Then cover with a stout cloth, cork with a wooden or cork stopper, and in about two months they will be ready for use.
13. Examine from time to time.

title: Sweet Plum Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of fruit
- four pounds of sugar
- two quarts of vinegar
- one ounce of cinnamon
- one ounce of cloves

## Directions

1. Boil the vinegar, sugar and spices together
2. Skim it carefully
3. Pour it boiling on the fruit
4. Pour it off, and skim and scald each day for three days
5. If for putting away, scald it the fourth time and cork up tightly

title: Sweet Plums
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of damsons, or prune plums
- 3½ pints of vinegar
- 7 lbs. of brown sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of cloves, mace and cinnamon powdered

## Directions

1. Stone the damsons
2. Put into a kettle the vinegar and brown sugar
3. Boil, and skim it well
4. Put in the fruit, with the cloves, mace and cinnamon powdered
5. Boil it 2 hours
6. Take out the fruit and let it boil some time longer
7. Remove it from the fire, and when cold mix all together

title: Sweet Potato Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 potatoes
- 4 tablespoonfuls butter or lard
- 1 pint milk
- 1 cake of compressed yeast
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful white sugar
- Sufficient flour to make a batter

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes, pare and mash them very fine through a sieve
2. Scald the milk and add the salt and sugar
3. Beat till perfectly smooth
4. Add these to the potatoes and mix well
5. Add about half a cup of flour, well-sifted several times, so that it will be very light
6. When the mixture grows tepid, add the yeast, which you will have dissolved in warm water, and beat well till you have a good batter
7. Cover the mixture with a thick cloth, and put to rise in a warm place
8. After three or four hours, when it is well-risen, add flour sufficient to make a nice, soft dough
9. Knead very lightly and quickly for about ten or fifteen minutes
10. Take up and knead lightly several times, and roll the dough out
11. Cut into little biscuits, place them in a slightly-buttered or greased pan, about two inches apart
12. Let them stand for half an hour in a warm place
13. Bake in a quick oven for twenty minutes

title: Sweet Potato Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Mashed Sweet Potatoes
- 1½ Pints of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk or Warm Water
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- ½ Teacup of Fresh Yeast, or Half a Cake of Compressed Yeast

## Directions

1. The potatoes must be baked, peeled and mashed.
2. Put the milk, or water, and the yeast and salt into a bowl or pan; add the butter, which must be melted.
3. Beat thoroughly, and set over night in a warm place.
4. In the morning add the potatoes, mashed and pressed through a sieve, and gradually add the flour, which must be sifted three times.
5. Beat to a light sponge; then cover, and set to rise in a warm place till it doubles its size.
6. Then turn out on a bread board and mold into loaves; put in a slightly greased pan and let rise till light.
7. Then bake in a moderate oven 45 minutes.

title: Sweet Potato Buns
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 nice potatoes
- flour
- nutmeg
- sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of good yeast
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Boil and mash 2 nice potatoes
2. Rub in as much flour as will make it like bread
3. Add nutmeg and sugar to your taste, with 1 tablespoonful of good yeast
4. When it has risen, work in 2 tablespoonfuls of butter, cut finely
5. Form it into small rolls, and bake on tins a nice brown
6. Serve hot; break open and butter

title: Sweet Potato Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Boiled Sweet Potatoes
- 3 Eggs
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1/2 Teaspoonful of Cinnamon
- The Grated Zest and Juice of an Orange
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy, if Liquor is Desired
- Pie crust

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well, and boil them until tender, but not overdone.
2. Thirty minutes will suffice.
3. When cold, peel and grate them, and then beat together the butter and sugar till light.
4. Add the yolks of the eggs, beaten till light, and then beat in the potatoes, stirring first gradually, and then beating lightly and steadily.
5. Add the spices and the orange, and brandy, if desired, and then line one deep pan or two small pie pans if preferred, with the pie crust.
6. Bake lightly, fill in lightly with the potatoes, and bake in a quick oven for a half hour.
7. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and while beating add two tablespoonfuls of white powdered sugar.
8. When the pies are baked, heap the meringue on the top, set back in the oven, and let them bake for two or three minutes only, to a light brown.

title: Sweet Potato Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Boiled Sweet Potatoes
- 1 Glass of Milk, White Wine or Brandy
- 1 Teaspoonful of Sugar
- Juice of Lemon
- Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the boiled sweet potatoes into slender round slices, and steep them for a half hour in a little White Wine or brandy or milk
2. Make a light batter, to which you will have added a tablespoonful of sugar
3. Add the juice of a lemon to the batter
4. Take each slice at a time, dip it in the batter, and let it drop from the spoon into the boiling lard
5. When fried to a nice golden brown place on a piece of brown paper, and drain in the oven
6. Place in the dish in which they are to be served
7. Sprinkle with powdered white sugar, and serve hot

title: Sweet Potato Pone
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large sweet potatoes
- 1 teacupful of brown sugar
- 1 teacupful of New Orleans molasses
- 1 teacupful of milk
- 1 teacupful of butter
- 4 eggs
- The zest of one lemon, grated
- A small bit of orange peel grated very fine
- 1/2 teaspoonful of nutmeg
- 3/4 teaspoonful of ground cloves
- 1/4 teaspoonful of ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate the potatoes and the zest of the lemon and orange.
2. Beat the eggs well; beat the butter and sugar till creamy, add the eggs, beat well, then add the grated potato and spices and milk; beat all well together.
3. Add the grated zest of the orange and lemon, put the mixture in a well-buttered pan and bake slowly for about an hour.
4. Serve hot or cold cut in slices.

title: Sweet Potato Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound or a pint of hot boiled sweet potato
- Six eggs well beaten
- Three-fourths of a pound of butter
- A pound of sugar
- Grated lemon rind
- A little brandy

## Directions

1. Pass the hot boiled sweet potato through a sieve—the finer the better
2. Add six eggs well beaten, three-fourths of a pound of butter, and a pound of sugar
3. Flavor with grated lemon rind, and a little brandy
4. Make a paste around the dish
5. Pour in the sweet potato mixture
6. Bake
7. Sprinkle finely pulverized sugar over the surface of the pudding

title: Sweet Potato Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sweet potatoes
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of fine sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- nutmeg
- lemon rind
- 1 wineglass of old brandy
- paste
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of sweet potatoes very tender, and press them while hot through a grater
2. add 6 eggs well beaten, 1/2 lb. of fine sugar, 1/2 lb. of butter, some grated nutmeg and lemon rind, a wineglass of old brandy
3. put a paste in the dish, and when the pudding is done, sprinkle the top with white sugar finely pulverized

title: Sweet Potato Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Mashed Sweet Potatoes
- % Pound of Sugar
- % Pound of Butter
- 8 Eggs
- The Juice and Rind of an Orange and Lemon
- 1/2 Spoon of Ground Mace
- The Same of Cinnamon and Allspice
- % Grated Nutmeg
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Brandy
- 1/4 Cup of Sherry

## Directions

1. Grate the potatoes and mash them through a sieve, if you use them raw
2. If you use the cold boiled potatoes, simply mash them
3. Beat the butter and the sugar to a rich cream
4. Add the well-beaten yolks of the eggs
5. Add the grated potatoes, and the spices
6. Add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
7. Mix all well
8. Add the liquors
9. Turn into a buttered baking pan
10. Let the pudding bake in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour
11. Serve hot or cold, with Cream Sauce

title: Sweet Potato Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Sweet Potatoes
- 3/4 Pound of Fine Sugar
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- The Zest of a Lemon, Grated Fine
- 1 Wineglass of Good Brandy
- 1/2 Grated Nutmeg
- 1/4 Ground Allspice
- 1 Piece of Ground Cinnamon
- 1 Pint of Milk or Cream
- Yolks of 6 eggs
- Whites of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil or bake the potatoes, peel and press them through a very fine sieve
2. Add to them the yolks of six eggs, and beat well
3. Add the butter, beating well and thoroughly, and add the sugar and milk
4. Beat all very light, and add the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Mix thoroughly, and add the grated nutmeg, ground spices, and blend well with the potatoes
6. Add finally, if desired, a wineglassful of good old brandy or whisky
7. Set the pan with the potatoes in an oven, and let it bake for an hour
8. Place a brown paper on top for the first three-quarters of an hour, and take it off at the last quarter for the top to brown nicely
9. Sprinkle with powdered sugar, and serve hot or cold

title: Sweet Potato Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls mashed sweet potato
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 pint milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls wheat flour

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Bake in a waffle iron

title: Sweet Potato Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Mashed Sweet Potatoes
- 1 Cup of Melted Butter
- ¼ Cup of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Flour

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes through a sieve, and add the eggs, beaten separately.
2. Mix well, and add the sugar, and beat till very light.
3. Then add the butter and beat again, and add the milk, mixing thoroughly.
4. Now add the flour, using sufficient to make a thin batter, that will bind the potatoes.
5. Grease the waffle irons well, and proceed to bake as above indicated.

title: Sweet Potato Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Left-Over Potatoes
- Butter
- Sugar
- Milk
- Flour
- Egg

## Directions

1. To every two tablespoonfuls of mashed potatoes add one of butter, one of sugar, one pint of milk, and four tablespoonfuls of flour
2. Beat all well together, and add one egg, well beaten
3. Bake in waffle iron

title: Sweet Potato Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of mashed potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1 pint of milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Beat all well together, and add one egg, well beaten.
2. Bake in waffle iron, and serve with butter spread over.

title: Sweet Potatoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Bake plain in their skins
2. Or boil, peel and slice, serve with butter
3. Or boil, and then slice in a pan, butter and sugar thrown over them, and bake in the stove
4. Or boil and mash with butter, and brown in the oven like Irish potatoes

title: Sweet Potatoes au Caramel
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Boiling Lard or Butter
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes and then cut them lengthwise in halves
2. Fry in boiling lard or butter, and while frying, sprinkle generously with sugar

title: Sweet Wafers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons of flour
- 1 egg
- 1 spoonful of sugar
- 1 spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Beat well
2. Cook in wafer irons
3. Roll up with a fork while warm

title: Sweet Wafers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 spoonfuls of white sugar
- 6 of flour
- 3 of butter
- 6 eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Make it into a thin batter

title: Sweetbreads a la Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- White pepper

## Directions

1. Remove all veins and nerves, and chop the meat into pieces of about an inch and a half.
2. Chop the mushrooms very fine indeed.
3. Put the butter in a saucepan, and when it melts, add the flour, being careful not to let it brown.
4. When perfectly smooth, add the milk and stir constantly until it boils.
5. Then add the chopped mushrooms and let them simmer for five minutes.
6. Season well to taste with salt and white pepper.
7. Then add the sweetbreads and cook for five minutes longer and serve hot.

title: Sweetbreads a la Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of sweetbreads
- 10 mushrooms
- 1.5 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1.5 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 pint of cream
- salt and white pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and parboil the sweetbreads for twenty minutes.
2. Then remove all veins and nerves, and chop the meat into pieces of about an inch and a half.
3. Chop the mushrooms very fine indeed.
4. Put the butter in a saucepan, and, when it melts, add the flour, being careful not to let it brown.
5. When perfectly smooth, add the cream and stir constantly until it boils.
6. Then add the chopped mushrooms and let them simmer for five minutes.
7. Season well to taste with salt and white pepper.
8. Then add the sweetbreads and cook for five minutes longer and serve hot.
9. At luncheons and dinners the sweetbreads a la Creme are served in small silver shells or fancy paper cases.

title: Sweetbreads a la Financiere
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 1/2 Pound of Butter
- 3 Carrots
- 1 Onion
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 1 Pint of Beef Consomme or Water
- 1 Pint of Rich Chicken Broth or Water
- 2 Truffles
- 12 Mushrooms
- 18 Stoned Olives
- 12 Godiveau Quenelles
- 2 Blanched Chicken Livers
- 1/2 Pint of Madeira or Sherry Wine
- A Dash of Cayenne
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons Fried in Butter to Garnish

## Directions

1. Select fine, fresh Sweetbreads and prepare as in the recipe for Sweetbreads Larded With Mushrooms.
2. Parboil for twenty minutes; then drain of all water; press them into shape, lay on a clean napkin and cover with a plank and place a weight upon them to press and make solid.
3. Take a piece of fine salt pork, and cut into little thin strips like matches and lard the Sweetbreads with this, using a very fine larding needle, larding each Sweetbread eight times.
4. Take a shallow saucepan and put within a half pound of butter.
5. When the butter melts, lay in the Sweetbreads, one by one.
6. Season with salt and pepper very lightly, and add the three carrots, sliced fine, and the onion, sliced very fine.
7. Add the finely minced thyme and bay leaves.
8. Butter a piece of brown paper and cover the saucepan; then set in the oven and let the Sweetbreads cook slowly till they are of a bright golden brown.
9. From time to time uncover the saucepan and turn the Sweetbreads, so that all portions of them may be evenly colored.
10. When they have reached this beautiful color add one pint of good beef broth and let them simmer for a half or three-quarters of an hour.
11. When nearly ready to serve, prepare a Sauce a la Financiere: Take two tablespoonfuls of butter, melt and remove from the fire, and add gradually a tablespoonful of flour.
12. Blend well with a wooden spoon till very smooth, and moisten with one pint of rich chicken broth and set on the fire.
13. Add the truffles, nicely sliced; a dozen and a half stoned olives; the blanched chicken livers, cut in pieces; the mushrooms, nicely chopped; a half pint of Madeira or Sherry Wine, salt and pepper to taste, and a dash of Cayenne or Tabasco.
14. Let the sauce cook for twenty minutes.
15. Place the sauce in a round dish, lay the Sweetbreads over it, garnish with the Godiveau Quenelles and Croutons fried in butter and send to the table hot.

title: Sweetbreads a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 4 Egg Yolks
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 1/4 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Chopped Parsley to Garnish
- Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Parboil the Sweetbreads for about twenty minutes.
2. Make a Sauce a la Poulette, adding the juice of one lemon and seasoning to taste, but do not add the eggs till the sauce has been taken from the fire or it will curdle.
3. When the sauce is made, place the sweetbreads in it, one by one, and let it come to the boiling point.
4. Then remove from the fire and stir in the yolks of four eggs that have been well beaten, and a half tablespoonful of butter.
5. Sprinkle with finely-chopped parsley, pour over the sweetbreads and serve.

title: Sweetbreads Crespinettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 1 Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1/2 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Teaspoonful of Prepared Mustard
- 1 Pint of Water
- Cream Sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and parboil the sweetbreads as already shown in recipe.
2. Chop an onion very fine and place it in a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter.
3. Let them simmer without browning.
4. Add one bay leaf, one sprig of thyme, one-half clove of garlic, a teaspoonful of prepared mustard, and mix well.
5. Then add a pint of water and stir well.
6. Add the sweetbreads which have been chopped very fine and formed into little fringed balls, by patting with the hand.
7. Let them simmer for about fifteen minutes longer.
8. Serve with a Cream Sauce.

title: Sweetbreads in Casseroles
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 1/4 Can of Mushrooms
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine
- Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Parboil the Sweetbreads in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for Sweetbreads a la Poulette.
2. Cut them into dice pieces after parboiling.
3. Add a quarter of a can of finely-chopped mushrooms to the sauce, and a glass of Sherry wine.
4. Take two dozen oysters and cut in pieces, taking off all the hard portions.
5. Add the chopped sweetbreads to the sauce, and after ten minutes, add the oysters.
6. Let them cook for five minutes.
7. Have ready a pan filled with a rich vol-au-vent crust, pour the mixture in and serve.
8. Alternatively, make the vol-au-vent crust into small shells; bake and fill with the sweetbreads.

title: Sweetbreads Larded with Mushroom Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 sweetbreads
- 1 carrot
- 4 thin slices of bacon
- 1 onion
- 1/4 can of mushrooms
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 whole sprig of thyme
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 wineglass of Madeira or Sherry
- 1 pint of consomme
- salt and pepper
- croutons

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in clear, cold water as soon as they come from market.
2. Wash well, to take off all the blood; wash again in a clear cold water, and parboil them for ten minutes.
3. Then drain them of all water, press them into shape and put them on a clean cloth on a table and cover with a plank and put a weight upon them to flatten.
4. When cold, clean with a knife, cutting off all the outside nerves, veins and fibers, without breaking the sweetbreads, however.
5. Cut fat lard into little strips like matches, and, with a larding needle, lard the sweetbreads, slipping the needle in on one side and bringing out on the other.
6. Lard each sweetbread eight times.
7. Then slice one onion and one carrot very fine; mince three bay leaves and a whole sprig of thyme.
8. Take a very thin slice of very fat bacon, cut it into thin strips and cover the bottom of the saucepan with these.
9. Lay the sweetbreads on top and put on top of these the sliced onion, carrot and finely minced herbs, omit and pepper by sprinkling nicely.
10. Cover this with a few fine strips of fat bacon.
11. Cover the whole with a brown paper which has been well greased with butter, and put the pan in a slow oven with the paper on top.
12. Let the sweetbreads bake for about twenty minutes, basting occasionally.
13. In the meantime make a Sauce a l'Espagnole as follows: Chop a few pieces of beef very fine, or else use good stock.
14. If meat is used boil in about two pints of water.
15. When it is reduced to about one pint, take off and strain.
16. Take a tablespoonful of butter or lard, and brown lightly with a tablespoonful of flour.
17. Then add the water and dissolve well, stirring constantly to prevent being lumpy.
18. Add to this a half can of mushrooms, and let it simmer a few minutes, and then add a glass of Sherry or Madeira Wine.
19. Let it cook rapidly, for about ten minutes.
20. Take the sweetbreads out of the pan and put one by one into the sauce, and let them cook ten minutes longer.
21. Serve with buttered croutons cut in dice shapes.

title: Sweetbreads Smothered
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Sweetbreads
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Onion
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1 Pint of Consomme or Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the Sweetbreads as in the recipe for "Sweetbreads Larded With Mushrooms."
2. Put them into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter, and let them brown slightly.
3. Add a finely-sliced carrot and onion and the minced herbs.
4. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
5. Cover the saucepan with a buttered paper, and then cover closely.
6. Occasionally uncover and turn the Sweetbreads till they are all browned evenly to a nice golden brown.
7. When they have reached this color, add the pint of Consomme or water and cover again and let them simmer for about twenty minutes.
8. Serve with any kind of sauce or garnish that may be desired, placing the sauce on the dish first with the dish very hot, laying the Sweetbreads over the sauce, and garnishing nicely with fried Croutons.

title: Sweetbreads with Green Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of sweetbreads
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- 4 slices of fat bacon
- 1 can of French Petit Pois, or 1 pint of fresh young green peas
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 sprig of thyme
- 3 cloves
- 1/2 pint of fresh milk
- 1 pint of consomme
- salt and pepper to taste
- croutons

## Directions

1. Prepare the sweetbreads in exactly the same manner as indicated in the above recipe, which is the very nicest way in which they can be served.
2. Make the sauce as indicated, letting it brown slightly, and, instead of the mushrooms, add a can of French Petit Pois or a pint of fresh young green peas that have already been boiled well and drained from all liquor.
3. Place the sweetbreads in one by one and let them cook for ten minutes longer and serve with the sweetbreads placed in the center of the dish, and the green peas around them as a garnish.

title: Sweetbreads with Truffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of sweetbreads
- 4 slices of fat bacon
- 3/4 can of truffles
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- 1 glass of Madeira or Sherry wine
- 1 pint of consomme or water
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 sprig of thyme
- salt and pepper
- croutons

## Directions

1. Prepare the sweetbreads in exactly the same manner as in the recipe Sweetbreads with Mushrooms.
2. When making the sauce, add a wineglassful of Madeira or Sherry, and one-half can of truffles cut in halves.
3. Serve with the truffles as a garnish about the sweetbreads.

title: Sweetmeat Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- preserves or jam or marmalade
- flour
- cloth

## Directions

1. Make the paste, roll it out in square pieces
2. Take the preserves, and put them in with a knife (any kind of jam or marmalade)
3. Have each layer different
4. Do this till you have a sufficient quantity
5. Roll it round
6. Sprinkle the cloth with flour and put the pudding on to boil

title: Sweetmeat Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- citron
- peaches
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 spoonful of flour
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- yolks of 10 eggs
- preserved lemon peel

## Directions

1. Make a layer of quinces, citron and peaches
2. cream 1/2 lb. of butter with a spoonful of flour
3. add 1/2 lb. of sugar, the yolks of 10 eggs
4. bake about 1/2 hour
5. preserved lemon peel is an improvement

title: Swiss Canapes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Anchovy Butter
- 8 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 6 Stuffed Olives
- 3 Minced Green Gherkins
- 6 Slices of Toast

## Directions

1. Prepare the French toast, and cut six delicate pieces into the shape of a triangle
2. Spread these with Anchovy butter
3. Decorate along one side with the whites of the eggs, finely minced
4. Decorate along the second triangular edge with the minced yolks of the eggs
5. Decorate on the third with the minced green gherkins
6. Place a stuffed olive in the center
7. Arrange nicely on a folded napkin on a dish and serve

title: Swiss Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 quart of milk
- white sugar
- extract of vanilla or nutmeg
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat together the yolks of 5 eggs
2. Add 1 quart of milk
3. Sweeten with white sugar to the taste
4. Flavor with extract of vanilla, or nutmeg
5. If baked in cups, pour cold water in a pan, and place the cups in that for the oven
6. If in a dish, cut bread very thin; butter it, and lay on the top

title: Syllabub
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- the juice of a large lemon
- the yellow rind of a lemon
- one glass of brandy
- two glasses of white wine
- a quarter of a pound of powdered sugar
- a pint of thick cream
- the whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Take the juice of a large lemon, and the yellow rind pared thin
2. Put these ingredients into a pan with one glass of brandy and two glasses of white wine, and let them remain one night
3. the next day add a pint of thick cream, and the whites of two eggs beaten together
4. beat them all together to a fine froth
5. serve in jelly glasses

title: Syllabub and Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large lemons
- 1 pint of wine
- 1 lb. loaf sugar
- 1 quart of cream
- preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Pare off the rinds of 4 large lemons and put it in the bottom of a deep dish
2. Press the juice into a bowl containing 1 pint of wine and 1 lb. loaf sugar
3. By degrees, mix in 1 quart of cream
4. Pour the whole into the dish with the lemon peel
5. Let the mixture remain untouched 3 hours
6. Beat it with rods to a stiff froth, first taking out the peel
7. Put in each of the glasses a tablespoonful of preserved fruit
8. Heap upon it the syllabub so as to stand high on the top of the glass

title: Tabasco a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Dozen Large Red Peppers
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1/2 Pint of Hot Spiced Vinegar

## Directions

1. Take three dozen large red Tabasco peppers or Chili peppers and one clove of garlic, chopped very fine
2. Scald them until very tender
3. Mash the peppers and garlic together
4. Press them through a sieve
5. Dilute the paste with spiced vinegar till it reaches the consistency of rich cream
6. Bottle and seal for use

title: Talmouses
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pound of Flour
- 1 Glass of Water
- A Pinch of Salt
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 6 Eggs
- 2 Cream Cheeses
- A Brioche or Plain Paste

## Directions

1. Put the butter in a saucepan, and add the water and the salt.
2. Let all come to a boil, stirring constantly.
3. Then take from the fire and add the eggs, beaten well, and the cream cheese.
4. Mix all well.
5. Then take small molds or tins and line with the Plain Paste, or Brioche Paste, rolled very thin.
6. Place within the preparation of cream cheese in quantity equal to an egg in size.
7. Then cover the cakes with a piece of rolled dough, and bake in a slow oven.

title: Tamales
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tender Young Chicken
- 1 Cup of Boiled Irish Potatoes
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 1 Spoon of Butter
- 1 Cup of Cracker Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper, to Taste
- A Good Dash of Cayenne
- Corn-shuck leaves

## Directions

1. Boil a young chicken until the meat can be picked off the bones
2. Chop up very fine
3. Mince the onion, clove of garlic, thyme, bay leaf, and parsley, very fine
4. Put the onion and butter into a saucepan and let brown slightly
5. Add the minced garlic, thyme, parsley and bay leaf
6. Add the chicken immediately and one cup of mashed potatoes and grated cracker crumbs
7. Stir well, and season to taste with salt and Chili pepper
8. Add a good dash of Cayenne
9. Mix all well by stirring and let cook for five minutes
10. Have ready a pot of boiling water and about a dozen nice, clean corn-shuck leaves
11. Cut the leaves into nice oblong shapes, and divide the chicken mixture into equal parts
12. Roll each of these parts into a corn leaf and tie, and immerse in boiling water for five minutes
13. Drain off the water, arrange the husks on a dish and send to the table hot

title: Tancredi Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of butter
- 6 ounces of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- a couple of eggs
- a teaspoonful of rose water
- spice

## Directions

1. Stir to a cream the butter and sugar
2. Add the eggs, flour and spice
3. Roll it out thin
4. Cut it into small cakes

title: Tapioca Cream
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two teaspoonfuls of tapioca
- cold water
- a quart of milk
- yolks of three eggs
- a cup and a half of sugar
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Soak two teaspoonfuls of tapioca for two hours in a little cold water
2. Boil a quart of milk, and to it add the tapioca, the yolks of three eggs, well beaten with a cup and a half of sugar
3. Give it one boil, and set it away to cool; do not boil it long, or the eggs will curdle
4. Beat the whites of the eggs, and put them on top, or boil them in a little of the milk and put it on the cream
5. Set it on ice until wanted

title: Tapioca Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Cup of Tapioca
- 3/4 Cup of Sugar
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Tumbler of Currant or Strawberry Jelly

## Directions

1. Wash the tapioca well several times, and then soak it well in the pint of water for two hours.
2. Add it to the Jelly, mixing thoroughly, and then add the sugar, and mix again until the tapioca is very clear.
3. Beat the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth, and add them to the tapioca, and turn it into a mold and set away to harden.
4. Make a Vanilla Cream Sauce with the yolks of the eggs, and serve cold with the custard.

title: Tapioca Milk
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a large tablespoonful of tapioca
- a quart of sweet milk
- a little salt
- sugar
- nutmeg or cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash and soak a large tablespoonful of tapioca
2. put it to a quart of sweet milk
3. add a little salt
4. cover it, and set it over a gentle fire for an hour
5. Take it up, add sugar and nutmeg, or cinnamon to taste

title: Tapioca Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a coffee-cup of tapioca
- a pint and a half of milk
- four eggs
- lemon and peach

## Directions

1. Put a coffee-cup of tapioca (soak it well first) into a pint and a half of milk
2. set it where it will get hot slowly
3. take it off when it boils
4. when cool add four well-beaten eggs
5. flavor with lemon and peach
6. sweeten it to taste
7. bake for an hour in a hot oven
8. If this is wanted for one person, take half the quantity of tapioca and milk

title: Tapioca Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacup of lump tapioca
- milk
- 1 pint of new milk
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- salt
- 2 large spoonfuls of sugar
- 1 wineglass of rose water
- nutmeg
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Wash over night 1 teacup of the best lump tapioca, and let it remain in milk to soak all night
2. the next morning boil 1 pint of new milk and stir in the soaked tapioca
3. add 1 large spoonful of butter and a little salt, 2 large spoonfuls of sugar, 1 wineglass of rose water, a little grated nutmeg
4. beat 4 eggs very light and stir in
5. mix all well together, and bake in a dish half an hour

title: Tapioca Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Cup of Tapioca
- % Cup of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the tapioca well
2. Add it to the milk and let it soak for at least two hours and a half
3. Beat the eggs separately until light
4. Add them in order to the sugar
5. Beat very light
6. Add the salt and the tapioca
7. Bake for forty-five minutes in a moderate oven
8. The pudding may be served either hot or cold

title: Tapioca Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces of Tapioca
- 3 Quarts of Broth

## Directions

1. Soak the tapioca a few hours
2. Add to three quarts of broth about forty minutes before serving
3. Stir frequently in the broth while boiling
4. Serve hot

title: Tarragon Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/8 pint of tarragon leaves
- 1 quart of best cider or white wine

## Directions

1. Gather the tarragon leaves and expose them to the heat of the sun for several days.
2. Throw them into the best cider or white wine and steep for about two weeks.
3. Carefully strain the liquor, using a flannel bag.

title: Tavern Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 4 glasses of wine
- 8 of sweet cream
- 1 lb. of butter
- 2 ounces of allspice, or 1 ounce of cinnamon, nutmeg or mace

## Directions

1. Cut out the biscuits and bake on tin sheets

title: Tea
body:
## Ingredients

- Tea
- Fresh boiling water
- Cream or milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. First see that the teapot is perfectly clean
2. Pour boiling water into it, and let it stand for five minutes, so that the metal or earthen urn may become thoroughly heated
3. Throw out this water, and drain the urn well
4. Allow a teaspoonful of tea for each cup of boiling water, or, if you desire stronger tea, allow two teaspoonfuls
5. Put this tea into the hot pot, and pour over one pint of boiling water, if you wish to make a quart, or, according to the quantity desired, one-half a cup of water to each cup
6. Let this solution draw for five minutes in front, not on top, of the stove or range
7. After five minutes add the other pint of water, or water in proportion to the number of cups desired, allowing a half cup more for each person
8. Serve at once, very hot
9. Put the cream or milk or sugar into the cups
10. Pour a little hot water into each cup to warm it, and then empty before putting in the milk and tea
11. Pour the tea through a strainer, so as to avoid the possibility of leaves or dregs passing into the cup

title: Tea Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1/2 pint milk
- 3 eggs
- 2 ounces butter or lard
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 1/4 cake of compressed yeast

## Directions

1. Scald the milk and add the butter
2. Set to cool, and then add the salt and the sugar and yeast
3. Add one-half of the flour
4. Mix well, and then beat rapidly for about five or ten minutes
5. Cover up the pan with a cloth, and set in a warm place for about two hours
6. Take up, add the well-beaten eggs and the remainder of the flour
7. Make a soft dough, and knead lightly but constantly for about twenty minutes
8. If the dough is then elastic, form into loaves and set to rise in a warm place
9. When it has increased to twice its bulk, which will be in about two hours, pinch off into small pieces about half the size of a hen's egg, and mold into a small ball
10. Place all in a pan about two inches apart, and cover again for half an hour
11. Bake in a moderate oven for about a quarter of an hour to a nice brown

title: Tea Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- mace
- salt to the taste

## Directions

1. Mix the cream of tartar and soda with the flour
2. Mix all ingredients together
3. Season with mace and salt
4. Bake

title: Tea Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 8 eggs
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 6 ounces of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- milk
- spice

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of 8 eggs and whites of 4 eggs very light
2. Add 1 lb. of brown sugar
3. Rub 2 lbs. of flour and 6 ounces of butter well together
4. Add a teaspoonful of soda dissolved in a little milk
5. Add any kind of spice you like
6. Pour the beaten eggs and sugar on the flour and butter
7. Knead well
8. Roll and bake in a quick oven

title: Tea Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints of flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 pint of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teacup of milk
- 1 large spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together

title: Tea Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 cup of butter
- 2 cups of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. roll out
2. bake in small cakes

title: Tea Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Flour
- 3/4 Pound of Butter
- 4 Eggs
- 6 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Sour Milk

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the well-beaten eggs, and incorporate thoroughly
3. Add the milk, and then gradually add the flour, making a light soft dough
4. Mix all well together, and roll the paste out very thin
5. Cut into rounds or squares, and put into a slightly buttered baking pan
6. Bake to a bright yellow, and serve as needed
7. Some brush the tops of the cakes with beaten eggs, and sprinkle the tops with raisins or currants, well seeded and washed and dried

title: Tea Cakes. Cheap and Nice. No Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter or a large spoonful of lard
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of sour milk
- one teaspoonful of soda
- grated orange peel or nutmeg
- flour enough to roll out
- a pinch of salt (if using lard)

## Directions

1. Roll very thin
2. cut with fancy cutters
3. bake in a quick oven

title: Tea Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Strong Tea
- French Brandy or Wine or Rum to Taste
- 6 Lemons
- ½ Pounds of Sugar

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from the lemons and grate the zest
2. Add this to one quart of boiling tea, and add the sugar
3. Dissolve well
4. Let it boil up three times
5. Take off the fire, and add rum, brandy or wine to taste

title: Tea Rolls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- 1 gill of water
- 1 gill of milk
- 1 gill of good yeast

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a pan, with salt
2. Warm together the water and milk with the yeast
3. Mix into the liquid enough flour to make a thin batter
4. Stir it till quite smooth and free from lumps
5. Put a handful of flour over the top, and set it in a warm place to rise for 2 hours or more
6. When quite light, make it into a dough, with some more milk and water
7. Knead it well for 10 minutes
8. Mould it into rolls, or round balls, and bake it

title: Tea Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Flour
- 1 Pint of Milk
- ½ Cake of Compressed Yeast
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Gill of Warm Water

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and salt together.
2. Scald the milk, and when tepid add the yeast, dissolved in a little warm water, to one-half of the milk.
3. Add one-half of the flour, and make a thin batter, stirring very smooth.
4. Set it to rise in a warm place, and at the end of three hours make it into a dough with the remainder of the milk and flour, and knead well till the dough no longer adheres to the board or to your hands.
5. Then shape it with your hands into small balls, set to rise for a half hour longer, or an hour, and bake in a moderate oven for a half hour.

title: Teacup Cake Without Eggs
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of sour cream or thick milk
- a teaspoonful of saleratus dissolved in hot water
- a gill of brandy
- half a grated nutmeg
- a teaspoonful of essence of lemon or the yellow rind of a grated lemon
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir in flour until the batter is as thick as pound cake
2. bake an inch deep in a buttered basin

title: Teal Duck Broiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Teal Ducks
- 6 Strips of Bacon
- Melted Butter
- Chopped Parsley
- Olives and Slices of Lemon to Garnish
- 6 Slices of Toast
- Currant Jelly

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the duck in exactly the same manner as for roasting, binding with the strip of bacon.
2. Place on a broiler, turning frequently, and let it broil for about thirty minutes, very slowly.
3. Serve with melted butter and chopped parsley spread over, and the juice of a lemon squeezed in.
4. Garnish the dish nicely with sprigs of parsley, slices of lemon and olives.
5. Serve with Currant Jelly.

title: Teal Duck à la Bigarade
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Teal Ducks
- 1 Bigarade or Sour Orange
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Ground Allspice and Cloves
- Lemon Zest
- A Strip of Bacon
- Buttered Toast
- Sauce à l'Espagnole

## Directions

1. Clean the ducks and take the livers and fry them in a little melted butter.
2. Season well with salt and pepper and a slight pinch of ground allspice and cloves and the zest of a bigarade, or sour orange.
3. If the orange is not available, take the zest of a lemon.
4. Place this in the interior of the ducks, and then rub the outside well with salt and pepper.
5. Bind with a strip of bacon and place on the broiler.
6. In about thirty minutes it will be done, cooking over a slow fire, turning frequently.
7. In the meantime prepare a Sauce à l'Espagnole.
8. As soon as the birds are done pour off from the broiler all the juice that has fallen, and put this into the sauce, with the juice of two sour oranges or citrons.
9. Let it warm without boiling, pour over the birds, which you have placed on buttered toast, and serve hot.

title: Teal Ducks Roasted
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pairs of Teal Ducks
- 6 Thin Strips of Bacon
- 6 Truffles
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Water
- 6 Slices of Toast
- Currant Jelly
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the duck nicely and put one truffle and a lump of butter about the size of a peanut, with salt and pepper, on the inside.
2. Rub well with salt and pepper and a little butter melted.
3. Take a thin strip of bacon and bind it around the body of the duck, fastening with a skewer.
4. Place in the oven for roasting (directions continue on next page).

title: Tea—Green and Black
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tea
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald your tea-pot always before putting in the tea
2. Throw out the scalding water and allow a teaspoonful of tea to each person expected to drink it
3. Turn on half a pint of boiling water at first and let it steep—green tea requires about five minutes, black tea ten minutes
4. After this, pour on more boiling water according to the number of persons

title: Temperance Iced Cabinet Pudding for Summer
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sponge cake
- A tablespoonful of any favorite extract
- Currants
- Seedless raisins
- Sliced citron
- Chopped almonds
- One pint of milk
- The yolks of two eggs
- A quarter of a pound of sugar
- Half a teaspoonful of extract of lemon, rose, or almonds

## Directions

1. Cut some sponge cake about half an inch thick
2. Shape it nearly to the mould
3. Dilute a tablespoonful of any favorite extract, and pour it on to the cake
4. Commence to fill up the mould in layers of currants, seedless raisins, sliced citron, and chopped almonds
5. Add a layer of cake
6. Continue until it is full
7. Make a custard of one pint of milk, the yolks of two eggs, a quarter of a pound of sugar, and half a teaspoonful of extract of lemon, rose, or almonds
8. Let it simmer a little, but not enough to curdle
9. When it simmers, take it off, and let it cool a little
10. When only lukewarm pour it over the cake mixture

title: Terrapin
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- boiling water
- salted water
- sherry wine
- hot buttered toast
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- mace
- butter

## Directions

1. Like crabs and lobsters, terrapins are thrown alive into boiling water and let boil till the outer shell and toe-nails can be removed.
2. Then wash and boil them in salted water till the fleshy part of the leg is tender.
3. Put them in a bowl or deep dish, take off the second shell, remove the sand bag and gall bladder, and cut off the spongy part.
4. Cut up the meat, season it with salt, pepper, cay, and mace, thicken with butter and flour, and cook.
5. Just before serving put in a gill of sherry wine for every terrapin, and pour all over hot buttered toast.

title: Terrapin or Gopher Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large fresh water terrapin
- water
- bacon
- cloves
- allspice
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper
- wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take a large fresh water terrapin and clean it
2. Place it in a digester with 2 quarts of water, a slice of bacon, 2 dozen cloves, 3 dozen allspice, salt, black and red pepper
3. Boil this for 3 or 4 hours
4. Thicken and brown it before serving
5. Throw in a glass of wine in which has been grated half a nutmeg

title: Terrapin Pies
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 4 small terrapins
- 1/2 pound of butter
- 6 eggs
- allspice
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- small quantity of eschalot
- 2 or 3 slices of bread

## Directions

1. Take 1 large or 4 small terrapins
2. After they have been opened, have the meat well chopped
3. Add to it half a pound of butter, 6 eggs, allspice, mace, pepper, salt, and a small quantity of eschalot
4. Add 2 or 3 slices of bread which have been soaked and mashed smooth
5. Stir these well together
6. Have the shells washed
7. Put the mixture into them
8. Bake a light brown

title: Terrapin Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Two-Pound Diamond-Back Terrapin
- 2 Fine Large Onions
- 6 Cloves
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 6 Allspice
- 1/2 of a Small Lemon
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Glass of Sherry Wine
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay Leaf
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- a grated nutmeg
- a glass of Sherry or Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Clean the terrapin as you would a turtle
2. Place in a kettle and boil till tender
3. Take out and cut into small pieces, saving the water
4. Proceed as for Turtle Soup
5. When it boils up take from the fire, add a grated nutmeg, a glass of Sherry or Madeira wine and serve
6. Make farci or forcemeat balls in exactly the same manner as for Turtle Soup
7. Serve with green pickle and delicate slices of fried toast

title: Terrapins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large, fat, thick terrapin bodies
- salt
- eggs
- fresh butter
- beaten yolks of eggs
- sherry or madeira
- powdered nutmeg
- mace
- lumps of butter
- liquorice

## Directions

1. Select large, fat, thick bodies (females are best)
2. Put them whole into water that is boiling hard
3. Add a little salt
4. Let them boil until done
5. Take off the shells
6. Extract the meat
7. Carefully remove the sand-bags, gall and entrails
8. Cut the meat into pieces
9. Put in stew pan with eggs, and sufficient fresh butter to stew well
10. Let it stew until quite hot, keeping the pan carefully covered and shaking it over the fire
11. Make a sauce of beaten yolks of eggs highly flavored with sherry or madeira, powdered nutmeg and mace, and a few lumps of butter
12. Stir this well over the fire until it comes almost to a boil
13. Serve separately if you prefer or add a small lump of liquorice about the size of a 5 cent piece
14. Serve very hot

title: The Blow
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Continue boiling, and after a few minutes dip the skimmer in the boiling sugar
2. Strike it on the edge of the boiler
3. Blow through the holes, and you will find that if the sugar is of the right degree, it will form into small globules or air bubbles on the other side

title: The Crack or Snap
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Continue boiling, and dip the finger after a few moments in iced water and then in the boiling sugar, and instantly in the water again
2. The proper degree is reached when the sugar is placed between the fingers, and will not stick, or when distended it snaps hard and dry

title: The Famous Bakewell Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Nice puff paste
- An inch of nice preserve or jam
- J lb. of butter
- J lb. of well boiled potatoes
- J lb. of sugar
- J ounce each of sweet and bitter almonds
- 5 eggs
- 1 tablespoon of brandy

## Directions

1. Lay a nice puff paste on plate, then an inch of nice preserve or jam
2. Mix, like pound cake, J lb. of butter, beaten to cream, J lb. of well boiled potatoes pressed through a sieve, J lb. of sugar, J ounce each of sweet and bitter almonds, both blanched and pounded
3. When beaten well together, add 5 eggs well frothed, 1 tablespoon of brandy
4. Pour over the layer of preserves and bake

title: The Feather
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Boil up the sugar a little longer
2. Dip the skimmer in it
3. On finding that it shivers into a thready fringe from the edge, it will have reached the degree called the Feather
4. If large and elevated, the degree is the Large Feather
5. In a few minutes more they become small and flaky, and the next degree, the Small Feather, is reached

title: The Gardener's Wife Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Carrot
- 3 Beets
- 3/4 Cup of Green Peas
- 1 Cup String Beans
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Take fine strips of vegetables of different colors, cooked and cold, with green peas and string beans, and dress nicely with oil and vinegar and serve.

title: The Hard Ball
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Continue the test, and if a firmer ball is formed, this degree is reached

title: The Herb Bouquet
body:
## Ingredients

- Spray of parsley
- Sprig of thyme
- Celery
- Parsley
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Tie together the spray of parsley, sprig of thyme, celery, parsley and bay leaf
2. This bouquet will flavor a gallon of soup if cooked for an hour

title: The Pearl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Let the sugar boil a little longer
2. If you find that on drawing it out to a thread it snaps quickly, and also that while boiling the sugar presents the form of large pearls or globules on its surface, it will then have reached the small pearl degree
3. When the pearls become closely connected on the surface of the sugar, it will have reached the large pearl degree

title: The Picayune Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of finely sifted flour
- 1 cup of fine white sugar
- whites of 8 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 1 teaspoonful of vanilla extract
- 1 teaspoonful lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, after which very little beating will be required.
2. Add slowly a heaping cupful of fine white sugar, and one cup of flour, both of which have been sifted four times to reach this measurement.
3. Then add one teaspoonful each of vanilla and lemon extract, and two teaspoonfuls of baking powder, just before putting into the oven.
4. Bake in a moderate oven for twenty-five minutes.

title: The Queen of All Puddings
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Bread Crumbs
- 1 Quart of Milk
- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 4 Eggs
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- The Juice of a Lemon
- % Pint of Seeded Currants
- % Pint of Raisins
- Fruit Jelly
- Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the bread crumbs in water for about an hour
2. Squeeze thoroughly
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light
4. Add the milk and the bread crumbs, beating all light
5. Add the melted butter and the seeded raisins and the currants
6. Add the juice of one lemon and the grated zest of half a lemon
7. Beat all thoroughly together
8. Set in a moderate oven for a half hour
9. Beat the whites to a very stiff froth
10. Add four tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, and beat thoroughly till very stiff
11. After half an hour take the pudding out of the oven, if it is nicely browned
12. Put over the top a layer of some fruit jelly, and then a layer of the whites, and then another layer of the jelly, and a last layer of the whites of the eggs
13. Set it back in the oven to brown for several minutes
14. Serve cold with Cream or Wine Sauce

title: The Soft Ball
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water

## Directions

1. Keep the sugar boiling
2. Dip the finger into iced water and then into the boiling sugar and immediately into the water again
3. If the sugar has enough consistency to form a small ball when rolled between the finger and the thumb, the degree is reached

title: Thin Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of butter
- skimmed milk
- 1 lb. of flour

## Directions

1. Warm 2 ounces of butter in as much skimmed milk as will make 1 lb. of flour into a stiff paste
2. Beat it with a rolling pin, and work it smooth
3. Roll it thin, and cut it into round biscuits
4. Prick them full of holes with a fork
5. Bake for about 6 minutes

title: Timbale of Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Pound of Macaroni or Spaghetti
- 1 Head of Edam's Cheese
- 3/4 Pound of Grated Parmesan Cheese
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil a little over one-quarter of a pound of macaroni, or sufficient to fill the head, according to the directions for Boiled Macaroni.
2. When boiled, take out of the water and set in a dish.
3. Take about half a pint of the liquor in which the macaroni was boiled, strain it and set it back on the stove.
4. Add the flour and butter blended, and three-quarters of the grated cheese, and let it boil five minutes.
5. Pour this sauce over the macaroni in the dish and mix well, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Fill the head with this mixture, and sprinkle on top the rest of the grated cheese.
7. Set in the stove to bake, and when nicely browned, serve hot from the shell of the cheese.

title: Tips of Asparagus Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Asparagus Tips
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. Boil the Asparagus tips (See recipe)
2. When cold, place on a dish and garnish nicely
3. Serve very cold, with French Dressing

title: Tipsy Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
