## Ingredients

- a sponge cake weighing about a pound
- half a pint of sherry and Madeira (mixed)
- six eggs
- a quart of milk
- half a pound of almonds

## Directions

1. Place a sponge cake weighing about a pound in a glass bowl
2. pour over it half a pint of sherry and Madeira (mixed)
3. Make a rich custard of six eggs and a quart of milk, sweeten to taste, flavor and let it cool
4. Blanch half a pound of almonds
5. stick them in the top of the sponge cake
6. pour over it the custard

title: Tipsy Squire
body:
## Ingredients

- Left Over Sponge Cake
- 1 Glass Sherry Wine
- Brandy or Sherry Wine
- A Boiled Custard
- Blanched Lemons

## Directions

1. Saturate left-over sponge cake with Brandy or Sherry Wine
2. Take a rich Boiled Custard
3. Place the cake in a deep glass dish
4. Pour the custard over it
5. Decorate with blanched lemons
6. Serve cold

title: To Bake Red Snapper
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- stale bread crumbs
- onions
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- water
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a large fish, clean it, cut off the head, and draw the entrails through that part, as it must not be cut open
2. Take the crumbs of stale bread, some onions and parsley (chopped), pepper and salt for seasoning
3. Stuff the fish with this seasoning
4. Put small lumps of butter all over the dish
5. Pour in water to the depth of 2 inches
6. Sprinkle over it a little flour
7. Put the pan in a well heated oven and bake an hour and a half

title: To Bake Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen or more fine, ripe tomatoes
- butter
- stale bread
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Scald and peel about a dozen or more fine, ripe tomatoes
2. Butter a shallow baking dish, and put in the finest, without breaking them, and not quite touching
3. Fill up the space between with stale bread, buttered
4. Mash the rest of the tomatoes, and strain out all the hard parts
5. Mix with a spoonful of butter, pepper and salt
6. Pour it over the dish
7. Strew bread crumbs on the top
8. Sprinkle sugar between each layer of tomatoes
9. Bake about ½ hour

title: To Batter Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 1 dessertspoonful of butter
- wheat flour
- pepper and salt
- 1 quart of oysters
- boiling lard

## Directions

1. Make a light batter of 8 eggs, a dessertspoonful of butter, a little wheat flour, pepper and salt to the taste
2. Drain the oysters from the liquor
3. Stir them into the batter
4. Drop the mixture from a ladle into boiling lard
5. Let the fritters cook until they are of a rich brown

title: To Boil a Cauliflower
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- water
- drawn butter

## Directions

1. Let the cauliflower lie a short time in salt and water
2. Put it into boiling water with a handful of salt
3. Keep the pot uncovered and skim the water well
4. A small cauliflower will require 15 minutes to boil
5. A large one will require 20 minutes to boil
6. Dress with drawn butter

title: To Boil a Ham
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- cold water
- carrots
- onions
- celery
- parsley
- mace
- cloves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Run a knife, or skewer, into the thickest part of the ham next the bone; if the knife comes out clean the ham is good, if it smells rank and smears the knife the ham is not good.
2. Select your ham, then, according to this rule, and when good lay it in cold water; scrape and wash it carefully, and let it remain in the water all night.
3. In the morning, when the water—enough to cover the ham—is nearly boiling lay the ham in, and keep the water in a simmer.
4. When it has boiled about an hour throw in two carrots, four onions, two heads of celery, a sprig of parsley, two or three blades of mace and four cloves.
5. If the ham is very salt, it is well to change the water before putting in the seasoning.
6. The ham must not be allowed to boil hard, only simmer.
7. When the ham is done set it off in its own water, let it cool in it; by this means it will retain its moisture.
8. When cool take it out, skin it, and dredge sugar over it, set it in the oven till it browns, or hold a hot shovel over it.

title: To Boil Artichokes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the artichokes
2. Parboil them in the water
3. Then boil in milk, salt to the taste
4. Boil 15 minutes in the water, and in the milk, until they are so soft that a straw may be passed through them

title: To Boil Hams
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoonful of brown sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak all night in water enough to cover the ham
2. Put the ham into a large vessel full of cold water and simmer 20 minutes to each lb. of meat
3. When done, take off the skin
4. Grate bread crumbs over the top
5. Sprinkle 1 tablespoonful of brown sugar over the bread crumbs
6. Pour over them 1 tablespoonful of wine vinegar
7. Bake for 1 hour
8. Serve cold

title: To Broil a Chicken
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean it as usual and split it down the back
2. Break the breast-bone with a stroke of the potato beetle
3. Spread it out flat and lay it on the gridiron over clear coals
4. Put the inside of the chicken to the fire first
5. Put a tin cover over it
6. Let it broil quickly until nearly done
7. Turn it and finish without the cover
8. When nicely browned take it on a dish
9. Season it with salt and pepper
10. Butter it freely
11. Turn it once or twice in the butter and serve it hot

title: To Candy Fruit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, quinces, plums, or citron, preserved
- A pound of loaf sugar
- A small cup of water

## Directions

1. Take preserved peaches, quinces, plums, or citron from the syrup, and drain them on a sieve
2. To a pound of loaf sugar, put a small cup of water
3. When it is dissolved, set it over a moderate fire, and let it boil
4. When it boils, put in the fruit to be candied, and stir continually until the sugar granulates over the fruit
5. Take it up, and dry it in a warm oven
6. If not sufficiently candied, repeat the operation

title: To Clarify Sugar
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of sugar
- white of 1 egg
- 1 1/2 pints of water

## Directions

1. To 3 lbs. of sugar allow the white of 1 egg, and 1 1/2 pints of water
2. Break the sugar into small lumps
3. Put it into a saucepan, and pour the water over it
4. Let it stand some time before it is put on the fire
5. Add the beaten white of the egg
6. Stir it until the sugar is entirely dissolved
7. When it boils up, pour in a pint of cold water
8. Let it boil up a second time
9. Take it off the fire
10. Let it settle for 15 minutes
11. Remove the scum
12. Put it on the fire again, and boil it till thick enough

title: To Clarify Sugar
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Pounds of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- The White of 1 Egg

## Directions

1. Take the whole white of an egg for brown sugar and half the white for refined sugar
2. Beat to a froth in a dish with about a cupful of water, adding it by degrees
3. Put about eight pounds of sugar into a kettle, and dilute it with the half of the beaten egg
4. Let it become very thick
5. Set it over the fire, and let it boil up twice before you skim it
6. Skim and continue to add the egg by degrees, until the scum is perfectly white
7. Take the skimmer from the sugar, add another cup of cold water, to carry off any remaining scum
8. Take it from the fire and strain
9. The sugar is then kept for use

title: To Cook Salsify
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Boil salsify until the skin comes off easily
2. When taken off, cut the roots in pieces about the size of an oyster
3. Put into a deep vegetable dish a layer of crumbs of bread or crackers, a little salt, pepper and nutmeg, and a covering of butter
4. Then a layer of salsify
5. Put these layers alternately until the dish is filled, having crumbs of bread on the top
6. Pour in as much water as the dish will hold
7. Bake brown

title: To Cook Spinach
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- soda
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Wash in two or three waters, as the grit adheres very closely to spinach
2. When well washed, boil it one half hour in clear water
3. Add a little soda, if it does not look a nice green
4. When soft, drain it well and chop very fine
5. Add butter, salt if needed, and pepper to taste
6. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut in fancy shapes
7. Or in early spring, poach two or three eggs, and lay on the freshly cooked spinach

title: To Dress a Goose
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cold water
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- sage
- cloves
- thyme
- lard
- butter
- goose
- fat bacon
- water

## Directions

1. Beat 2 eggs until very light
2. Add a pint of cold water and stir in flour until you have a stiff batter
3. When quite smooth add salt and pepper to taste
4. Add 2 good sized onions, sage, cloves, and 2 tablespoons of thyme, chopped fine
5. Put a large spoonful of lard and one of butter into pan
6. When boiling stir in the batter and continue to stir until it becomes dry and light
7. After stuffing, put the goose in oven with a slice of fat bacon and pint of water
8. Baste with flour as it cooks, adding some boiling water when necessary
9. If not a mere gosling, parboil it first, more or less as may be necessary

title: To Dress A Turtle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- head
- liver
- eggs
- meat from under-shell
- shell
- bacon
- onions
- water

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and let it bleed well
2. Separate the bottom shell from the top with care
3. For fear of breaking the gall bag
4. Throw the liver and eggs, if any, into a bowl of water
5. Slice off all the meat from the under-shell and put in water also
6. Break the shell in pieces, wash carefully and place it in a pot
7. Cover it with water, and add one pound of middling or flitch of bacon with four chopped onions
8. Set this on the fire to boil

title: To Dress Oysters in Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart oysters
- half pint sweet cream
- tablespoonful butter
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. After draining the oysters, add half a pint of sweet cream, a tablespoonful of butter, salt and black and red pepper
2. Place the whole in an a la braise dish
3. When nearly done, thicken it with wheat flour and serve it up hot

title: To Dress Sheephead
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- sheephead 18 or 20 inches long
- 1/2 pint of tomato catsup
- a large spoonful of butter
- 1 pint of water
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Take a sheephead 18 or 20 inches long and put it into a pan
2. Place that in a dutch oven
3. Add 1/2 pint of tomato catsup, a large spoonful of butter, 1 pint of water, salt, black and red pepper to suit the taste
4. Cook it over the fire and serve it up with the dressing

title: To Dress Shrimps
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- butter
- salt
- black pepper
- red pepper

## Directions

1. To every half pint of shrimps, put a tablespoonful of butter
2. Add a little salt, black and red pepper
3. Stew over a slow fire till perfectly done
4. When the gravy becomes of a pinkish hue, it is sufficiently cooked

title: To Dress Shrimps in Tomato Catsup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- catsup
- butter
- salt
- red pepper
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Boil your shrimps and pick them
2. Put them into an a la braise dish
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of catsup and one of butter to every half pint of shrimps
4. Season with salt, red and black pepper to your taste

title: To Dress Turtle Steaks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle steaks from the thick part of the turtle fins
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- flour
- butter
- lard
- water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Season the steaks with pepper, salt and mace
2. Flour them
3. Fry in butter and lard mixed together
4. When fried pour a little water over them
5. Let them simmer for a quarter of an hour
6. Just before serving squeeze a lemon over the steaks

title: To Finish the Pie Mixture
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- some tart juicy apples
- one-third as much of the prepared meat
- one pound of raisins
- a quarter of a citron
- a gill of brandy
- enough sweet cider to make the whole quite wet

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and chop, not very fine, some tart juicy apples
2. put to them one-third as much of the prepared meat
3. stone one pound of raisins, and cut a quarter of a citron in small bits
4. add a gill of brandy, and enough sweet cider to make the whole quite wet

title: To Fry a Steak to Taste as if Broiled
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- steak
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Get up a quick blaze in the stove with some kindlings.
2. Put in a pan, over the blaze, a little butter; when it is hot lay in your steak; let it fry quickly; while frying cover the pan.
3. Work some butter, salt and pepper together in a tin pan, and when the steak is done to taste, let it lie in this mixture a few minutes, and then serve.
4. Do not salt a steak until it is cooked as salt will toughen it and draw out its juices.

title: To Fry Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fat from bacon or salt pork
- flour

## Directions

1. The fat from bacon, or salt pork, is much nicer to fry fish in, than lard.
2. After the fish is cleaned, wash it and wipe it dry, and let it lie on a cloth till all the moisture is absorbed;
3. then roll it in flour.
4. No salt is required if fried in bacon or pork fat.
5. There must be fat enough to float the fish or they will not fry nicely, but instead soak fat and be soft to the touch.

title: To Fry Tripe Brown
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- bacon grease
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. It must be thoroughly boiled and tender, or no frying will make it good.
2. Let it be perfectly cold, cut it in pieces, roll each piece in salt, pepper and flour, and fry brown in bacon grease.
3. When nicely brown take it up, dredge a little flour in the gravy, and put in a half cup of vinegar.
4. Serve in a sauceboat, or pour over the tripe as preferred.

title: To Green Fruit for Pickling or Preserving
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- vine leaves
- a teaspoonful of beaten or ground alum
- water
- saleratus

## Directions

1. Put vine leaves under, between, and over the fruit in a brass kettle
2. over the leaves sprinkle a teaspoonful of beaten or ground alum
3. cover the fruit to be greened, with water
4. boil it gently with the leaves and alum
5. if not a fine green, take more leaves and dust a little saleratus over them
6. Spread them out to cool when green
7. proceed to preserve or pickle them as desired

title: To Keep Tomatoes the Whole Year
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- sugar or salt

## Directions

1. Take the tomatoes, when perfectly ripe, and scald them in hot water, in order to take off the skin easily
2. When skinned, boil them well in a little sugar or salt, but no water
3. Spread them in cakes about an inch thick, and place the cakes in the sun
4. They will be ready in 3 or 4 days to pack away in bags, which should be hung in a dry place

title: To Make a French Pilau
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair of fowls
- rice
- butter
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil a pair of fowls
2. When done, take them out and put the rice in the same water, first taking out some of the liquor
3. When the rice is done, butter it well
4. Cover the bottom of the dish with 1/2 of it
5. Put the fowls on it and add the remainder of the liquor
6. Cover the fowls with the other 1/2 of the rice
7. Make it smooth
8. Spread over it the yolks of 2 eggs, well beaten
9. Bake in a moderate oven

title: To Make A Loaf Of Good Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One large spoonful of hop yeast, or a yeast cake
- a pint of water
- two Irish potatoes
- flour
- a spoonful of lard

## Directions

1. put this to a pint of water, mash to this two Irish potatoes, and stir all together
2. Sift in flour until you have a stiff batter, and set it to rise
3. When it is very light, stir in a spoonful of lard, and enough flour to enable you to mould it into a loaf
4. When moulded, grease the top, and set it to rise again
5. If really light, you can now bake it; but if not, work it down again, and mould it over and let it rise again

title: To Make Any Kind of Fruit Jelly
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fruit
- Water
- White sugar
- Beaten white of an egg

## Directions

1. Wash and drain the fruit, put it in a stone jar, and put the jar into a kettle of water over the fire
2. Let it boil, but see that none of the water gets into the fruit
3. When the fruit is tender, it will begin to break; pour it now into a flannel bag, but do not squeeze it
4. To each pint of juice strained, add one pound, or one pound and a quarter of white sugar, and the half of the beaten white of an egg
5. Boil this rapidly, skim, but do not stir the syrup, as stirring breaks its continuity and prevents its jellying
6. Boil it twenty minutes, and try a little in some cold water, to find out if it jellies; if it does not, boil it a little longer

title: To Make Chocolate
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- best chocolate
- half a pint of milk or milk and water for each square or large spoonful of ground chocolate

## Directions

1. Scrape the best chocolate
2. Allow for each square or large spoonful of ground chocolate half a pint of milk or milk and water
3. Let it boil a few moments
4. Put it on the back part of the stove until wanted

title: To Make Chocolate Another Way
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate
- cold milk
- hot milk or milk and water

## Directions

1. Scrape or grate the chocolate
2. Take a heaping tablespoonful for each cup to be served
3. Allow half a pint of milk or milk and water to each heaping spoonful of chocolate
4. Make the milk hot, rub the chocolate to a smooth paste with the cold milk
5. Stir it in the boiling milk
6. Let it boil up once and cover it
7. Set it back in a place where it will keep warm
8. Serve with toasted biscuit or rolls
9. Sweeten the chocolate unless you use the prepared chocolate

title: To Make French Mustard
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- mustard
- salt
- garlic or tarragon leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put on a plate an ounce of the best mustard, add to it salt, a clove of garlic or a few tarragon leaves.
2. Mince the garlic, stir it in, and pour on vinegar till it is of the proper thickness for use.

title: To Make Good and Cheap Vinegar
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of molasses
- eight gallons of rain water
- good yeast, or two yeast cakes
- one sheet of common brown paper
- molasses

## Directions

1. Take three quarts of molasses, add to it eight gallons of rain water
2. Turn the mixture into a clean cask, shake it well two or three times
3. Throw in a few spoonfuls of good yeast, or two yeast cakes
4. Place the cask in a warm place, and in ten days throw in it a sheet of common brown paper, smeared with molasses; it should be torn into narrow strips

title: To Make Good Bread
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of meal
- two quarts of flour
- salt
- warm water, or milk
- a cup of yeast
- a little lard

## Directions

1. Make a pint of meal into mush, then pour it onto two quarts of flour
2. when cool, add a little salt and warm water, or milk, and a cup of yeast
3. work it with a spoon, and set it by to rise until morning
4. Knead it well; yes, very well, and make it into loaves
5. place in the pan, and when light, bake it
6. Add a little lard, if liked

title: To Make Good Vinegar for Pickles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of whisky
- four pounds of brown sugar
- one cup of yeast
- seven gallons of water

## Directions

1. To a gallon of whisky add four pounds of brown sugar, a cup of yeast, and seven gallons of water
2. Put it into a demijohn or keg
3. If you set the vinegar in April, it will be good in November to pickle with
4. Cover the mouth of the vessel with muslin, to keep out flies or insects
5. When sharp and clear, bottle it

title: To Make Good Vinegar; No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of molasses
- three gallons of rain water
- one pint of sharp yeast

## Directions

1. Mix a quart of molasses in three gallons of rain water
2. Add to this, one pint of sharp yeast
3. Let it ferment and stand four weeks; you will then have good vinegar

title: To Make Icing For Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of two eggs
- a quarter of a pound of white sugar, ground fine like flour
- extract of lemon or vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of two eggs to a froth
2. add to them a quarter of a pound of white sugar, ground fine like flour
3. flavor with extract of lemon or vanilla
4. beat it until it is light and very white
5. Beat the frosting until it may be spread smoothly on the cake

title: To Make Mince Pie Mixture
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of the chopped meat
- two pounds of suet free from strings or skin, and chopped fine
- two pounds of currants, picked, washed, and dried
- four pounds of peeled and chopped rich tart apples
- the juice of two lemons
- the chopped peel of one lemon
- a pint of sweet wine
- one large nutmeg grated, or teaspoonful of ground mace
- three pounds and a half of sugar
- quarter of an ounce of ground cloves, or allspice
- the same of cinnamon
- a large tablespoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Mix the whole well together
2. put it in a stone pot, or jar, cover it close, and set it in a cool place for use
3. Mix it well together again before using

title: To Make Preserves
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Weigh the fruit, and to each pound of fruit use a pound of sugar
2. make a syrup of the sugar with a half pint of water to each pound of fruit
3. Boil it clear
4. put in the fruit and cook it well
5. boil gently till the fruit is clear

title: To Make Rising With Yeast Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- good yeast cake
- warm water
- sugar
- sifted flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Take a heaping spoonful of good yeast cake pounded
2. Add one-half a cup of warm water and a lump of sugar
3. Add enough sifted flour to make a thick batter
4. Set this to rise in a cool place in summer and a warm place in winter
5. It will be light and ready to use in about three hours unless it is kept very cool
6. Use a heaping spoonful for one quart of flour
7. Add half a cup of lard to make the bread better and richer
8. Grease the bread on top before baking

title: To Make Rolls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 pint of yeast

## Directions

1. Rub butter into flour, beat up eggs and mix with it, mix in yeast, cover up to lighten
2. Then bake in rolls

title: To Make Slip
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 gallon of new milk
- 1 tablespoonful of wine with prepared rennet steeped for a week
- sugar
- 1 pint of cream
- wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Warm 1/2 gallon of new milk
2. Before the milk is heated, sweeten to the taste
3. Stir into it a tablespoonful of wine in which prepared rennet has been steeped for a week
4. While warm, stir the wine round several times in the bowl in which it is to be served
5. Let it stand for 3 hours on ice if the weather is very warm
6. Season a pint of cream with wine, sugar and nutmeg to make a rich syllabub
7. Serve the syllabub with the slip

title: To Make Sweet Breads
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of raw veal
- 1 lb. of marrow or beef suet
- pepper
- salt
- the yolk of an egg
- butter
- crumbs of bread

## Directions

1. Take 1 lb. of raw veal, and shred it very fine
2. Add 1 lb. of marrow or beef suet, and season it with pepper and salt
3. Beat it very fine in a marble mortar, and make it the shape of sweet breads
4. Beat well the yolk of an egg and dip these into it
5. Fry them in a good deal of butter, and lay crumbs of bread between them
6. Pour a little melted butter on them
7. They must not be fried of a dark color

title: To Make Watermelon Preserves
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon rind
- alum
- grape leaves
- sugar
- water
- ginger
- lemons
- lemon skins
- saffron

## Directions

1. Take the firm outside rind of the watermelon; scrape off the green and cut out the soft inside.
2. Cut the rind into any shapes you choose, stars, crescents, diamonds, etc.
3. After they have been boiled in alum and leaves to green and harden, weigh them and make a syrup of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, with a cup of water to each pound.
4. Boil the syrup clear, and put in the cut rinds, and boil them until transparent.
5. Flavor with ginger for green color, and lemons for the yellow.
6. If the rind is wanted yellow you must boil it with fresh lemon skins and a little saffron before preserving it.

title: To Pickle Cabbage. A Very Nice Yellow Pickle.
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four cabbage heads
- strong brine
- clear water
- strong vinegar
- powdered turmeric
- five pounds of sugar
- three ounces of turmeric
- two ounces of ginger
- four ounces of horseradish
- two ounces of white mustard seed
- one-half ounce of celery seed
- quarter of an ounce of mace
- two ounces of whole pepper
- four ounces of garlic

## Directions

1. Cut four cabbage heads into eighths, if large, or quarters, if small
2. Soak it in strong brine for three days
3. Scald it in clear water until you can pierce it with a straw
4. Take it out and dry it on large dishes for twenty-four hours
5. Put it into strong vinegar, with powdered turmeric, sufficient to color the cabbage yellow
6. Let it remain in this vinegar ten days
7. Take it out and drain on a sieve for several hours
8. Pack in a jar alternately one layer of cabbage and one of spices
9. For each gallon of vinegar allow five pounds of sugar, three ounces of turmeric, two of ginger, four of horseradish, two of white mustard seed, one-half ounce of celery seed, quarter of an ounce of mace, two ounces of whole pepper, white if you can get it, and four ounces of garlic
10. Scald the vinegar and sugar together
11. Pour hot on the cabbage and the spices
12. Cover tight

title: To Pickle Cucumbers Plain Without Spices
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one hundred small cucumbers
- salt
- boiling vinegar
- vine leaves

## Directions

1. Take one hundred small cucumbers, or more if you wish
2. Salt them freely and let them remain eight or ten hours
3. Drain them
4. Put them into boiling vinegar enough to cover them
5. Place vine leaves among and over them to green them
6. Let them scald a few minutes in the vinegar
7. Take them from the fire but place them near it to keep warm and become green
8. If the leaves turn yellow, put fresh ones among them
9. When green pack them away in jars
10. Season them at any time you may desire
11. Scald them thoroughly with boiling vinegar to keep well

title: To Pickle Onions
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- strong salt water
- vinegar
- whole pepper
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Peel the onions
2. Boil some strong salt and water
3. Put it over them
4. Cover
5. Let them stand twenty-four hours
6. Take them up with a skimmer
7. Make some vinegar boiling hot
8. Put to it whole pepper and mustard seed
9. Pour it over the onions to cover them
10. When cold cover close

title: To Pickle Onions
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- whole pepper
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Peel the onions, boil some strong salt and water and put it over them, cover, and let them stand twenty-four hours
2. Take them up with a skimmer
3. Make some vinegar boiling hot, put to it whole pepper and mustard seed, and pour it over the onions to cover them
4. When cold cover close

title: To Pickle Red Cabbage
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- vinegar
- mace
- ginger
- whole pepper
- cloves

## Directions

1. Slice the cabbage and sprinkle with salt
2. Let it remain three days
3. Drain
4. Pour over it boiling vinegar in which you have put mace, bruised ginger, whole pepper and cloves
5. Let it remain in this until next day
6. Then give one more scald
7. It is ready to put up for use

title: To Pickle Shrimps
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimps
- salt water
- strong vinegar
- water
- allspice
- sweet oil
- brandy

## Directions

1. Pick the shrimps and rinse them well in salt and water
2. Take two parts of strong vinegar and one of water, add a few allspice, and boil this pickle
3. Pour it hot over the shrimps
4. If the shrimps are to be sent to a distance, boil the pickle again, adding a little more vinegar with a few tablespoonfuls of sweet oil
5. Ensure they are entirely covered with the pickle
6. Place a paper wet with brandy over the top

title: To Pickle Tongues
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- a beefs tongue
- 1 pound of white salt
- 1 ounce of saltpetre
- 1 pound of coarse salt

## Directions

1. Take a beefs tongue, rub it with 1 pound of white salt, 1 ounce of saltpetre, 1 pound of coarse salt
2. Rub it well and turn it every day in the pickle for 2 weeks
3. This pickle will do several tongues, only adding a little more white salt

title: To Pickle Walnuts White
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- green walnuts
- salt
- spring water
- blades of mace
- nutmeg
- strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Pare green walnuts very thin till the white appears
2. Throw them into spring water with a handful of salt
3. Keep them under water 6 hours
4. Put them in a stewpan to simmer 5 minutes, but do not let them boil
5. Take them out and put them in cold water and salt, keeping them quite under the water with a board
6. Lay them on a cloth and cover them with another to dry
7. Carefully rub them with a soft cloth
8. Put them into the jar with blades of mace and nutmeg cut in slices
9. Pour strong vinegar over them and put away for use

title: To Pot Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 tablespoonful of black pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of allspice
- fish
- butter
- walnut pickle vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 tablespoonful of salt, 1 of black pepper, and 1 of allspice
2. Rub the fish after being well cleaned with this mixture
3. Put them in a deep dish of a size to suit the fish, as the bottom should be covered
4. Butter the dish before putting the fish in, with a bit of butter on the top of each fish
5. Mix walnut pickle vinegar, and if it is very sharp, add a little water after the dish is filled
6. Bake it in a slow oven

title: To Prepare and Boil Rice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint rice
- 3 pints water
- 1 tablespoonful salt

## Directions

1. Wash and pick 1 pint of rice
2. Add to it 3 pints of water, and 1 tablespoonful of salt
3. Boil over a quick fire for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally
4. Pour off all, or nearly all the water (as the rice will be soft or grainy, according to the quantity of water left on it when put to steam, and the length of time allowed in the steaming)
5. Cover the vessel for 15 minutes on a very slow fire, stirring it occasionally

title: To Preserve Crab Apples, Green
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- crab apples
- water
- vine leaves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the apples and boil them in a very little water, cover them with vine leaves, while on the fire simmering, and they will then be very yellow.
2. Take them out and spread them on a large dish to cool.
3. Pare and core them, put them back in the kettle, with fresh leaves to cover them.
4. Hang them over the fire, or on the stove in a preserving kettle until they are green.
5. Take them out of the pot, let them cool, weigh them, and allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit.
6. Put only water sufficient to dissolve the sugar, as the fruit, having been already boiled, will require very little water—a small cupful to each pound being quite enough.
7. Boil this syrup, skim it, and put in your green apples, and boil them until they are clear and tender.
8. Put the apples in jars, turn the juice on to them, and when cold tie them up, or rather seal them.

title: To Preserve Peaches
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- white clings
- one pound of sugar per pound of fruit
- half a pint of water per pound of fruit
- an egg

## Directions

1. Select white clings if you desire to preserve them whole
2. peel and weigh them
3. to each pound of fruit put one pound of sugar and half a pint of water
4. Put the syrup to boil, clarify it with an egg
5. as it boils remove the scum
6. Keep the peaches in cold water all the time the syrup is boiling
7. When the syrup has boiled clear, put in the peaches
8. let them boil gently for half an hour
9. then take them out on a dish for two hours
10. put them back in the syrup and boil again until they are clear
11. put them in jars and pour the syrup over them
12. cork and seal up for future use

title: To Preserve Pears
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- small rich pears
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Take small rich pears, and boil them gently in water until they will yield to the pressure of the finger.
2. They must not be soft, or they will not preserve well.
3. Take them out when a little boiled; let them cool, and pare them neatly, leaving a little of the stem on, as well as the blossom end.
4. Make a syrup of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, and when it is boiling hot, pour it on the pears.
5. Next day boil them in the syrup till clear, and bottle them for use.

title: To Preserve Strawberries
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. strawberries
- 1 lb. sugar

## Directions

1. To 1 lb. of strawberries add 1 lb. of sugar, strew it over them, and let them remain until they have made a syrup
2. Put them in a skillet, and let them simmer over a fire until they are half or two-thirds done; take them off and spread them in dishes; they must remain in the sun until they are sufficiently cooled, to keep the summer; be careful not to bruise the strawberries in stirring them

title: To Roast a Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- force meat or butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- marjoram
- water
- pounded and sifted crackers
- butter
- onion
- cloves
- red wine

## Directions

1. Clean very carefully, trim the tail and fins, but do not cut them off
2. stuff the fish with force meat, or only a piece of butter rolled in flour, with a little pepper, salt and marjoram
3. Tie a string around the fish
4. put some water in the pan, and raise the fish from it
5. put some pounded and sifted crackers over, salt it
6. baste it often with butter
7. add a very little onion to the stuffing
8. For gravy, take what falls in the pan, add cloves and red wine
9. If it does not mix, put a little boiling water with it
10. If not rich enough, make it so by browning some flour and butter together and adding it to the gravy

title: To Roast a Sucking Pig
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three onions
- a dozen leaves of sage
- pepper and salt
- half a pint of bread crumbs
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- the yolks of four eggs
- olive oil
- a little sugar
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- a cup of the gravy from the pig
- the juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Select a pig four weeks old
2. Let the pig be prepared in the usual way by the butcher, that is scalded, drawn, etc.
3. Stuff it with a mixture of two or three onions, say half a pint when sliced and chopped, and a dozen leaves of sage, pepper and salt
4. Set this to simmer on the fire
5. Throw in half a pint of bread crumbs if the pig is small—if a large one, put a pint of crumbs—a quarter of a pound of butter, and the yolks of four eggs
6. Cook this and stuff the pig with it
7. Sew the pig up and put it in the oven to roast
8. Baste it often with a brush or swab dipped in olive oil
9. Dust a little sugar over it, and brown it evenly
10. Take off the head before serving
11. Take out the brains, put them in a stew pan
12. Add to them some chopped parsley, pepper, and salt, a cup of the gravy from the pig, and the juice of a lemon
13. Stir this over the fire, and send it to the table hot in a separate boat

title: To Roast Beef in a Stove
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine roasting piece of beef
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- hot water
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat as if for roasting, season it well with salt, pepper, and a little onion if liked
2. Set the meat on muffin rings, or a trivet in a dripping pan, and pour into the pan a pint or so of hot water to baste the meat with
3. Keep the oven hot and well closed on the meat; when it begins to bake, baste it freely, using a long-handled spoon
4. Baste every fifteen minutes; add hot water to the pan as it wastes, that the gravy may not burn
5. Allow fifteen minutes to each pound of meat unless you wish it very rare
6. Half an hour before taking it up, dredge flour thickly over it, baste freely and let it brown
7. Take the meat from the pan, dredge in some flour and seasoning if needed; throw into the gravy a cup of water, let it boil up once, and strain into a sauce boat or gravy tureen

title: To Stew Apricots
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- grape vine leaves or grass
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Put your fruit on in bell metal with plenty of water to cover them; lay grape vine leaves or grass on top; let them boil till tender, by which time they will probably become green
2. Put your sugar in another skillet, allowing ¼ pint of water, or more (if you wish a great deal of syrup) and boil it till quite clear; then take the fruit from the water, and put them into the syrup to boil together till the syrup is as thick as you wish

title: To Stew Beef
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- salt
- water
- potatoes
- parsnips

## Directions

1. Wash the beef, rub salt over it, and put it in a pot, and just cover it with water
2. Set it over a slow fire, and after it has stewed an hour, put in some potatoes, pared and cut in half, and some parsnips, scraped and split
3. Let them stew with the beef till quite tender
4. Turn the meat several times in the pot
5. When all is done, serve up the meat and vegetables together, and the gravy in a boat, having first skimmed it

title: To Stew Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- onion
- sage
- mint
- sweet herbs
- beef gravy
- pepper
- salt
- green peas
- flour
- butter
- port wine

## Directions

1. Roast the ducks for 20 minutes
2. Place them in a stewpan with an onion cut in slices
3. Add a little sage and mint and sweet herbs chopped fine
4. Add about a pint of good beef gravy, seasoned with pepper and salt
5. Let it stew gently for 20 minutes
6. Take out the duck carefully and keep it warm
7. Strain the gravy
8. Pour it into a clean stewpan
9. Add to it, when well heated, the duck and a quart of green peas
10. Let it simmer for a half hour
11. Add a little flour and butter, a glass of port wine
12. Send to table, with the peas in the same dish as the duck

title: To Stew Ducks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- onion
- sage
- mint
- sweet herbs
- 1 pint good beef gravy
- pepper
- salt
- green peas
- flour
- butter
- port wine

## Directions

1. Roast the ducks for 20 minutes
2. Place them in a stewpan with an onion cut in slices, a little sage and mint and sweet herbs chopped fine
3. Add about a pint of good beef gravy, seasoned with pepper and salt
4. Let it stew gently for 20 minutes
5. Take out the duck carefully and keep it warm
6. Strain the gravy
7. Pour it into a clean stewpan, and when well heated, add the duck and a quart of green peas
8. Let it simmer for a half hour
9. Add a little flour and butter, a glass of port wine
10. Send to table with the peas in the same dish as the duck

title: To Stew Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- 1 pound of good butter
- 2 blades of mace
- 10 cloves
- cinnamon
- red pepper
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well
2. put it in a dish and pour vinegar over it; this will take off the slime, and the scales come off easily
3. put some butter into a frying pan and make it hot
4. put the fish into the pan and let it remain over the fire for 6 minutes more
5. take it out and put it into the kettle in which it is to be stewed
6. sprinkle some flour into the pan
7. put it over the fire for 3 minutes and pour it over the fish
8. take 1 pound of good butter, and roll it well in flour, and put it to the fish
9. add 2 blades of mace, 10 cloves, a little cinnamon, red pepper, and salt, with water sufficient to keep it from burning
10. put it over a slow fire to stew
11. when half done, add a little red wine

title: To Stew Oysters
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck oysters
- quarter pound butter
- wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put quarter pound of butter to 1 peck of oysters
2. Let the oysters boil until they look plump
3. Then add wine and thickening to your taste
4. When you put them in the tureen grate a little nutmeg on the top

title: To Stew Partridges
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- force meat
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- powdered mace
- fine flour
- gravy
- 2 spoonfuls of lemon pickle or anchovy
- 1 quarter of a lemon
- sweet marjoram

## Directions

1. Truss the partridges and stuff them with force meat
2. Rub a lump of butter in pepper, salt, and powdered mace, and put it into the bodies
3. Sew them up and after dredging them with fine flour, fry them in butter to a light brown
4. Put them in a stewpan with gravy sufficient to cover them
5. Add 2 spoonfuls of lemon pickle or anchovy, a quarter of a lemon sliced, and a sprig of sweet marjoram
6. Cover them closely and stew them for about ½ hour
7. Thicken the gravy if necessary

title: To Stew Red Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of mace
- 1 teaspoonful of allspice
- 1/2 spoonful of cloves
- 1 onion
- a handful of parsley
- 1 teacup of wine
- water
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- 3 spoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Have the scales well taken from the fish, well washed and wiped dry
2. put the fish into the pan and half fry it
3. chop mace, allspice, cloves, onion, and parsley very fine together
4. rub the butter and flour well together, then add warm water to thin it
5. add all the ingredients
6. put the fish on the strainer, and then in the kettle
7. pour the gravy over it
8. stew it half an hour
9. dish it for the table

title: To Stew Red Fish
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- red fish
- 1 teaspoonful of mace
- 1 teaspoonful of allspice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of cloves
- 1 onion
- parsley
- 1 teacup of wine
- water
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- 3 spoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Have the scales well taken from the fish, well washed and wiped dry
2. Put the fish into the pan and half fry it
3. Chop very fine together 1 teaspoonful of mace, 1 of allspice, 1/2 of a spoonful of cloves, 1 onion, and a handful of parsley
4. Add 1 teacup of wine and as much water as you think sufficient for the gravy
5. Mix 1 large spoonful of butter with 3 spoonfuls of flour, rub them well together, then add warm water to thin it, then add all the ingredients
6. Put the fish on the strainer, and then in the pan

title: To Stuff And Bake Fish
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- plain bread stuffing
- veal stuffing
- force-meat

## Directions

1. Choose any of the many dressings in this book.
2. Take either plain bread stuffing, veal stuffing, or force-meat;
3. fill the fish and sew it up;
4. put a teacup of water in the

title: Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Slices of Bread
- Butter to Taste

## Directions

1. Slice the bread, neither too thick nor too thin.
2. Cut off the rough edges of the crust.
3. Hold on a fork before burning coals till nicely toasted on one side.
4. Then turn on the other, and proceed as before.
5. If dry toast is desired, the bread is not buttered.
6. If buttered toast, butter nicely, set in the oven a second and send to the table hot.

title: Toast Water
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- slices from a loaf of wheat bread
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut two or three slices from a loaf of wheat bread
2. Toast them very brown
3. While hot, put them in a small pitcher, and pour over them a pint and a half of water
4. Sugar may be added if liked, but when the stomach is affected it is better without it

title: Toasted Cheese
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese
- grated bread crumbs
- fresh butter
- egg yolks
- cream
- mustard
- salt
- pepper
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Mix with 3 ounces of cheese, finely grated, 4 ounces of grated bread crumbs, 2 1/2 ounces of fresh butter, the yolks of 2 eggs well beaten, 1 tablespoonful of cream, 1 teaspoonful of mustard, and a little salt and pepper
2. Put it into a saucepan, and stir it over the fire till heated
3. Lay it thick upon toasted bread, and brown it, or put it covered with a dish into a Dutch oven, till thoroughly heated
4. Let the cheese be just brown

title: Toasted Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 Pound of Cheese
- 6 Slices of Bread
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Toast the bread nicely and butter it.
2. Cut the cheese into very thin slices and hold to the fire, letting it toast nicely, first on one side and then on the other.
3. Lay this upon the buttered toast and serve hot.

title: Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pecks of ripe tomatoes
- 2 quarts of vinegar
- 1/2 pound of salt
- 1/2 pound of sugar
- 3/8 ounce of black pepper
- 3/8 ounce of allspice
- 1 ounce of mustard
- 1 ounce of ginger, ground
- 1/3 ounce of cloves, ground
- 1/8 ounce of garlic, minced very fine
- a good dash of cayenne
- 3/8 pint of alcohol

## Directions

1. Set the tomatoes to boil in a porcelain-lined pot, and, after half an hour, press them through a sieve, to remove all the seeds and skin.
2. Then return the liquid obtained to the pot, and let it reduce to three-quarters.
3. Now add the vinegar, and let all reduce to about one and a quarter quarts.
4. Mix thoroughly, and add the spices, garlic, ground mustard, pepper, etc., and let all come to a boil, stirring continually.
5. Then take from the fire, and add the alcohol.
6. Bottle while the mixture is very hot, and seal well.

title: Tomato Catsup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes to fill a jar
- vinegar
- half an ounce of garlic sliced
- a quarter of an ounce of salt
- white pepper finely ground

## Directions

1. Take enough ripe tomatoes to fill a jar, put them in a moderate oven, and bake them until they are thoroughly soft
2. Strain them through a coarse cloth or sieve, and to every pint of juice put a pint of vinegar, half an ounce of garlic sliced, a quarter of an ounce of salt, and the same of white pepper finely ground
3. Boil it for one hour, then rub it through a sieve
4. Boil it again to the consistency of cream
5. When cold, bottle it, put a teaspoonful of sweet oil in each bottle; cork them tight, and keep in a dry place

title: Tomato Catsup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck of tomatoes
- salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of scraped horse radish
- 1 large onion
- cloves
- ground allspice
- red pepper
- 1 pint of strong vinegar
- 1 teacup of made mustard

## Directions

1. Slice and wash the tomatoes
2. Boil or stew them with a little salt, until perfectly soft
3. Strain them through a hair sieve, and press as much of the pulp through as possible
4. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of scraped horse radish, 1 large onion stuck full of cloves, ground allspice, red pepper and salt, to the taste
5. Put all on together, boiling, until the catsup looks rich and thick
6. When done, add 1 pint of strong vinegar, and 1 teacup of made mustard
7. Let the catsup be cold, before you bottle it
8. Let it be free of the onion and horse radish
9. Rosin the bottles tightly, and put away for use

title: Tomato Catsup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of skinned tomatoes
- 4 spoonfuls of salt
- 4 spoonfuls of black pepper
- 3 spoonfuls of mustard
- ½ a spoonful of allspice
- 8 pods of red pepper
- sharp vinegar
- 2 spoonfuls of the juice of garlic

## Directions

1. To one gallon of skinned tomatoes, put 4 spoonfuls of salt, 4 of black pepper, 3 of mustard, ½ a spoonful of allspice, 8 pods of red pepper
2. Make all the ingredients fine
3. Simmer slowly in sufficient sharp vinegar to have 2 quarts of catsup
4. After simmering 3 or 4 hours, strain it through a wire sieve
5. Those who like it may add 2 spoonfuls of the juice of garlic, after the simmering is somewhat over, and the ingredients are cool

title: Tomato Catsup (French)
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- 2 ounces cloves to every gallon of liquor
- 1 ounce of allspice to every gallon of liquor
- 1 ounce of mace to every gallon of liquor
- 1 bottle of port wine to every 3 quarts
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take a quantity of tomatoes, put them into a tub in layers with salt till all are in
2. Let them stand 24 hours
3. Strain off all the juice, squeezing the pulp well
4. Put the juice on to boil
5. Reduce it to 1/3, and to every gallon of liquor put 2 ounces cloves, 1 ounce of allspice, 1 ounce of mace, and boil it down well
6. To every 3 quarts add 1 bottle of port wine, and boil it a little longer
7. Put it away to cool and settle, then bottle it
8. If there is not enough spice, pound a fresh nutmeg and put it in

title: Tomato Catsup. Recipe for Making a Small Quantity
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a gallon of ripe tomatoes
- four tablespoonfuls of salt
- four tablespoonfuls of ground black pepper
- half a spoonful of ground allspice
- three spoonfuls of ground mustard
- eight pods of red pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a gallon of ripe tomatoes, skin them by pouring boiling water over them
2. Let them get cold and put them in a stew pan with four tablespoonfuls of salt, and the same of ground black pepper, half a spoonful of ground allspice, and three spoonfuls of ground mustard
3. Throw in eight pods of red pepper, and let all stew slowly until the tomatoes are soft and tender
4. Thin the mixture with enough vinegar to allow the catsup to be strained through a sieve
5. Cook it fifteen minutes, and bottle up when cold

title: Tomato Consomé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 shin of veal
- 3 pounds shin of beef
- 8 quarts water
- 1 cup tomatoes
- a handful of sorrel if tomatoes are not used
- salt and pepper
- 3 onions
- 3 leeks
- 10 allspice
- 6 cloves
- 3 large carrots
- 1 head of celery
- 1 bunch of parsley

## Directions

1. Put the meat into a large soup kettle and let it come slowly to a boil
2. Draw it forward and as it begins to boil more rapidly skim as the scum rises
3. After another hour add the pepper, salt and vegetables
4. The soup should boil incessantly but gently for about eight hours
5. Take it off and strain into a large bowl and set it away in the ice box until the next day if not for immediate use
6. Remove the fat from the surface and pour off all the clear part into a saucepan and boil again for one or two hours
7. Remove it from the fire

title: Tomato Jam
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe tomatoes
- Sugar
- Lemons

## Directions

1. Take nice ripe tomatoes, skin them, take out all their seeds, but save the juice to put with the sugar
2. Weigh the fruit, and to each pound, add three-fourths of a pound of sugar
3. Boil some lemons soft, take one for each pound of tomatoes, mash them fine, take out the pips, and put the lemons to the sugar and tomatoes
4. Boil slowly and mash the jam smooth with a silver spoon
5. When smooth and jelly-like, it is done
6. Put it away in glasses carefully

title: Tomato Jelly
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ripest and best tomatoes (slices)
- brown sugar (1 lb. per pint of juice, or 1/2 lb. for meat)
- eggs (whites of 8 per gallon of juice)
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fill a large jar with slices of the ripest and best tomatoes
2. Lay a cloth over the jar, and over that put a piece of dough, to keep in the heat
3. Place the jar in a large pot of water, and boil 4 or 5 hours constantly
4. Strain the juice through a coarse hair sieve
5. To every pint of juice add 1 lb. of the best brown sugar, if you wish the jelly very sweet, or half that quantity, if to eat with meat
6. Add the whites of 8 eggs to every gallon of juice
7. Skim it and boil till nearly half evaporated
8. Put it into glasses, and keep them in the sun till sufficiently thick
9. A very good jelly may be made to eat with meat, by putting salt, pepper, mace and nutmeg, instead of sugar

title: Tomato Omelet
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart fine, ripe tomatoes
- 2 onions
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- large spoonful of bread crumbs
- 5 eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Select 1 quart of fine, ripe tomatoes, pour over them boiling water to remove the skin, then chop them finely
2. Put them into a saucepan without any water
3. Chop 2 onions very finely, cover closely, and let them simmer slowly, an hour
4. Then add a little salt and cayenne pepper, a large spoonful of bread crumbs, and cover tightly
5. Beat up 5 eggs to a stiff froth
6. Have ready a heated pan, and a small piece of butter and grease it
7. Stir the eggs into the tomatoes
8. Mix all together, and pour it into the pan
9. Brown it on one side, fold it over, and serve up on a hot dish

title: Tomato Omelette
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- tomatoes to make 3 pints when cooked
- 1½ teaspoonfuls crumbs of bread
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1 heaping teaspoonful black pepper
- 4 tablespoonfuls butter
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Parboil 2 onions
2. While this is doing, peel a sufficient quantity of tomatoes to make 3 pints when cooked
3. Cut them up, and add the onions, also 1½ teaspoonfuls of crumbs of bread, 1 tablespoonful of salt, 1 heaping teaspoonful of black pepper, and 4 tablespoonfuls of butter
4. Beat these thoroughly together and set them over a slow fire, gradually to stew
5. They should cook never less than 3 hours, but the longer the better
6. About 15 minutes before they are to be served up, beat 6 eggs, and stir them in
7. Put them on a hot fire, and let them boil, stirring them all the time

title: Tomato Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Fresh Fine Tomatoes
- French Dressing

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes nicely and place on a salad dish.
2. Never peel or scald tomatoes intended for salad.
3. Serve nicely with a plain French dressing or any of the above dressings.

title: Tomato Salad, With or Without Shrimp
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen large tomatoes
- three or four sweet peppers
- pepper
- salt
- oil
- mustard
- vinegar
- a pint of shelled shrimp

## Directions

1. Slice a dozen large tomatoes, slice with them three or four sweet peppers
2. Pepper and salt the tomatoes
3. Lay slices of tomato and a little sweet pepper until the dish is full
4. Pour over all a dressing of oil, mustard and vinegar
5. A pint of shelled shrimp is a great improvement to this salad, but it is good without

title: Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dozen Tomatoes
- 1/2 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1 Sprig Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- 1 Pint of Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- Salt and Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- Sherry to Taste
- Chopped Parsley for Garnish

## Directions

1. Take one dozen large tomatoes, or one can, and put in a pot to boil, with one-half tablespoonful of butter
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, and one pint of water
3. Let it cook for about ten minutes and add minced thyme, parsley and bay leaf, very fine, and two cloves of garlic, minced fine
4. Let it boil, and, when well boiled, take from the fire and mash through a sieve, reducing to a pulp
5. Take a tablespoonful of flour and put in a saucepan, and add a half spoon of flour
6. When it blends and browns nicely, add the tomato juice, season nicely to taste
7. When ready to serve, add chopped parsley as a garnish
8. Serve with meat, fish or game

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- 1 spoonful of butter
- a little salt and pepper
- 2 eggs, well beaten
- a small handful of bread crumbs
- a teacup of milk
- 1 onion thinly sliced

## Directions

1. Scald the tomatoes and rub them through the sieve
2. to 1 pint of juice, add a spoonful of butter, a little salt and pepper, 2 eggs well beaten, a small handful of bread crumbs soaked in a teacup of milk, and 1 onion thinly sliced
3. stew over a slow fire for 1 or 2 hours

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tomatoes
- 3 pounded crackers
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and slice 12 tomatoes, picking out the seed
2. add 3 pounded crackers, and pepper and salt to your taste
3. stew twenty minutes

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- pint of juice
- spoonful of butter
- salt
- pepper
- 2 eggs
- small handful of bread crumbs
- teacup of milk
- 1 onion

## Directions

1. Scald the tomatoes and rub them through the sieve
2. To 1 pint of juice, add a spoonful of butter, a little salt and pepper, 2 eggs well beaten
3. Add a small handful of bread crumbs, soaked in a teacup of milk, and 1 onion thinly sliced
4. Stew over a slow fire for 1 or 2 hours

title: Tomato Sauce Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of tomatoes
- one gallon of vinegar
- salt
- onions
- cloves
- pepper
- ginger
- mustard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes (green ones are firmest), and sprinkle salt between each layer
2. Let them remain thus for twelve hours, then rinse them, and put them to drain on a sieve
3. Put your vinegar to boil with a dozen onions cut up in it, season high with cloves, pepper and ginger
4. When this boils throw in your tomatoes and let them boil five minutes
5. Finish by stirring in one-quarter of a pound of mustard and a pound of sugar
6. Add a quart of vinegar and bottle it

title: Tomato Sauce, Plain
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes (twelve)
- crackers (three pounded)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and slice twelve tomatoes, pick out the seeds;
2. add three pounded crackers, salt and pepper;
3. stir twenty minutes and serve.

title: Tomato Sauce, Rich And Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen large ripe tomatoes
- four ounces of raw ham
- a dozen small eschalots
- a bunch of thyme or parsley
- butter
- a cupful of good soup-stock
- salt

## Directions

1. Pick off the stalks from the tomatoes
2. Extract the seeds and watery juice by squeezing them in the hand
3. Place the pulp in a stewpan with four ounces of raw ham, cut into cubes
4. Add a dozen small eschalots and a bunch of thyme or parsley
5. Throw in a little butter, and fry all gently until the tomatoes soften sufficiently to be passed through a strainer
6. Mix this puree with a cupful of good soup-stock
7. Add the strained juice of the tomatoes
8. Let boil fifteen minutes, then set it by to clarify
9. Serve it hot

title: Tomato Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- 1 dozen fine tomatoes

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart milk
2. Thicken with 1 tablespoonful flour
3. Season with salt, pepper and a little butter
4. Stew 1 dozen fine tomatoes
5. Drain partially, strain through cullender
6. Pour the boiling milk to it
7. Boil up once, and serve

title: Tomato Soup With Vegetables. Very Fine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three carrots
- three heads of celery
- four onions
- two turnips
- a tablespoonful of butter
- a slice of ham
- a half cup of water
- two or three quarts of good soup stock
- ten or twelve ripe tomatoes
- a half-dozen whole peppers

## Directions

1. Cut small, three carrots, three heads of celery, four onions and two turnips; put them into a saucepan with a tablespoonful of butter, a slice of ham and a half cup of water; let them simmer gently for an hour; then if a very rich soup is desired add to the vegetables two or three quarts of good soup stock, made by boiling a beef bone in three quarts of water until the meat is tender. Let all boil together for half an hour, and then add ten or twelve ripe tomatoes and a half-dozen whole peppers. It should cook for another hour or so. It must then be strained through a sieve or coarse cloth. Serve with toasted or fried bread cut in bits in the tureen. This is an elegant family soup, particularly nice in summer when the vegetables are fresh.

title: Tomato Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Let the tomatoes be very ripe
2. Mash them well, let them stand twenty-four hours
3. Strain, and to every quart of the tomato juice, add a pound of white sugar
4. This will ferment and should be allowed to do so, only keep it carefully covered from the flies
5. Skim off the foam as it rises
6. When the liquor becomes clear, bottle it
7. This wine will be a pleasant acid, and should be served with sugar and water, in the tumbler with the wine

title: Tomato, Green Pepper and Onion Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Tomatoes
- 2 Green Peppers
- 1 Large Onion
- French Dressing, Plain

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes, onions and green peppers nicely and thin.
2. Arrange on a dish, placing a layer of tomatoes, an alternate layer of onion and green pepper, and tomatoes mixed.
3. Dress either before bringing to the table, or at the table, with French dressing.

title: Tomatoes au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Tomatoes
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Cup of Grated Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Scald and skin the tomatoes.
2. Then place a layer of them at the bottom of a baking dish, cover with a layer of bread crumbs, spread very thick, and season with salt and pepper and dots of butter at intervals.
3. Continue in this way till the last layer is reached, finishing with a layer of bread crumbs, sprinkle with salt and pepper, dot with butter, and put in an oven to bake for an hour and a quarter.
4. Canned tomatoes will require about half this time.

title: Tomatoes Stuffed
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- five large tomatoes
- parsley
- fat bacon
- ham
- bread crumbs
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- thyme
- two eggs
- brown sauce or gravy

## Directions

1. Take five large tomatoes, slice off that part which joins the stalk
2. Cut out a little of their pulp, take out the seeds, and strain them
3. Chop up the pulp with a handful of parsley, a slice of fat bacon, a slice of ham, and a cup of bread crumbs
4. Fry all these, and season with butter, pepper, salt, thyme, and the yolks of two eggs
5. Take it off the fire as soon as the eggs are beaten in
6. Stuff the tomatoes
7. Bake them for half an hour
8. Pour some brown sauce or gravy over them and serve

title: Transparent Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound of Pine White Sugar
- Half of the White of 1 Egg
- The Juice of Half a Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Any Fancy Liqueur

## Directions

1. Take a half pound of the finest white clarified sugar, and clarify again by boiling it with half of the white of an egg, beaten to a stiff froth
2. Clear off the scum as it rises, and continue skimming till the sugar is perfectly clear
3. Then add to the sugar half a gill of clarified sugar, boiled to a syrup, and to which you will have added the juice of a quarter or half a lemon
4. Strain, and add to the sugar enough Liqueur, Curaçoa, Noyau, Cedrati or Maraschino to flavor the icing to taste, and beat or whip it to the proper consistency
5. Then take a silver tablespoon and stir the sugar well together, rubbing up and against the sides of the pan till it acquires an opaline appearance
6. Then take a silver fork, lift one of the cakes intended to be iced, dip in the sugar, or else hold it on a fork and with a spoon pour the icing over
7. Take another fork, lift off the cake and place it on the baking sheet, and so on till all the small cakes are iced
8. Then dry in the sun or in the oven for about ten minutes

title: Transparent Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of sugar
- half a nutmeg
- delicate paste

## Directions

1. Beat eight eggs very light
2. Add them to half a pound of butter, and the same of sugar, which have been beaten to a cream together
3. Grate in half a nutmeg
4. Set it on the fire in a stew-pan, and stir it constantly until it is hot
5. Do not leave it more than five minutes on the fire, as you only wish to slightly cook the whites of the eggs to prevent their running when put on the paste
6. Line two pie pans with delicate paste, and pour in the mixture
7. Bake in a moderate oven
8. Do not allow the top to burn, as it will, if not covered when first put in the oven
9. Cover with a pan until the bottom is cooked, and then a few moments colors the top
10. Serve it with a tart pie

title: Transparent Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- ½ lb. of white sugar pounded fine
- ½ lb. of butter
- little nutmeg
- rich puff paste
- candied orange and citron

## Directions

1. Beat 8 eggs well
2. Put them in a stewpan with ½ lb. of white sugar pounded fine, the same quantity of butter, a little nutmeg
3. Set it on the fire, stirring it till it thickens
4. Put it in a basin to cool
5. Put a rich puff paste around the edges of the dish
6. Pour in the pudding and bake it in a moderate oven
7. It will cut light and clear, when you may add candied orange and citron

title: Trifle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint rich cream
- 4 teaspoonfuls white wine
- pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Add to 1 pint of rich cream 4 teaspoonfuls of white wine
2. Sweeten it with pounded loaf sugar
3. Whisk it well and as the froth rises, lay it upon a sieve placed over a deep dish
4. As it drains, pour the cream into the pan in which it is whisked, till it is done

title: Trifles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three fresh eggs
- a saltspoonful of salt
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Beat two or three fresh eggs a few minutes
2. add a saltspoonful of salt, and enough of sifted flour to make into a stiff paste
3. roll very thin
4. cut into small round cakes
5. fry in boiling lard, and sprinkle sugar over them

title: Trifles
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three fresh eggs
- a saltspoonful of salt
- sifted flour
- boiling lard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat two or three fresh eggs a few minutes
2. add a saltspoonful of salt, and enough of sifted flour to make into a stiff paste
3. roll very thin
4. cut into small round cakes
5. fry in boiling lard, and sprinkle sugar over them

title: Trifles. Delicious
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- lady fingers
- half dozen macaroons
- one cup of wine or diluted extract
- three tablespoonfuls of jelly or jam
- one pint of milk
- three eggs
- one cup of white sugar
- whites of two eggs
- one cup of white sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a glass bowl, or dish, with lady fingers
2. Break up, and put also half dozen macaroons
3. Pour over them a cup of wine, or diluted extract, to moisten them
4. Put in three tablespoonfuls of jelly or jam
5. Pour over this a boiled custard, made with a pint of milk, three eggs and a cup of white sugar
6. Whip up the whites of two eggs with a cup of white sugar and lemon juice to taste
7. When it will stand alone, put it on the custard, and serve

title: Tripe a la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Tripe
- 12 Tomatoes, or a 2-Pound Can
- 2 Onions
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Square Inch of Lean Ham
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A Dash of Cayenne

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe well, and boil till tender.
2. Cut it into slices of about two inches long and half an inch wide.
3. Take two onions and slice them fine, and put with a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan together and let them smother well.
4. Chop about one inch square of lean ham very fine, and add.
5. Take two cloves of garlic, chopped fine, with three sprigs each of thyme and bay leaf, minced very fine, put in the saucepan, and let all brown.
6. Then add about twelve large, fresh tomatoes, or the contents of a two-pound can.
7. Season all to taste with salt and Cayenne pepper.
8. Let it cook for ten minutes, and then add the tripe, and let all smother for twenty-five minutes.
9. Season to taste, and serve hot.

title: Tripe a la Mode de Caen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of tripe
- 3 onions
- 3 carrots
- 1 dozen whole bay leaves
- 1 dozen whole cloves
- 1 dozen whole allspice
- 3 cloves of garlic (whole)
- 1 ounce of thyme (whole)
- 2 dozen pieces of bacon 2 inches square
- 1/2 bottle of white wine
- 1 cup of broth or water
- butter
- salt, Cayenne and Chili pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Take three pounds of tripe and cut the tripe into pieces of about two inches square
2. Slice three onions and three large carrots very fine
3. Take one dozen whole bay leaves, one ounce of thyme whole, one dozen whole cloves, and the same number of allspice, three whole cloves of garlic, two dozen pieces of very thin bacon cut into pieces of two inches square
4. Have ready a two-gallon earthen jar that can stand baking in an oven
5. Put in the bottom of the jar a thin layer of butter
6. Place on top a thin layer of bacon, then a thin layer of onions, carrots, bay leaves, thyme, garlic, spices, dividing into two equal portions the whole amount
7. Sprinkle over the whole salt, Cayenne and Chili pepper
8. On top of this lay one-half of the tripe
9. Over the tripe place a layer of bacon; then vegetables, seasonings, etc
10. Over this place another layer of tripe, and remnants of thyme, bay leaf, vegetables, bacon, etc., as below, this being the last layer
11. Pour over all a half bottle of White Wine and one cup of broth or water
12. Cover the jar closely with a layer of Pie Paste
13. Set in a very moderate oven, and let it cook slowly for at least five hours of constant, steady cooking

title: Tripe a la Poulette
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Tripe
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Sprig of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- The Yolks of 2 Eggs
- 8 small white onions
- Sauce a la Poulette

## Directions

1. Prepare the tripe according to the directions given under the heading of "Tripe."
2. Make a rich Sauce a la Poulette, always omitting the eggs till later.
3. Take eight small white onions that have already been boiled in plain water until they are perfectly tender, and add to the sauce.
4. Let them stew for about five minutes.
5. Then add the tripe, which has been cut into pieces of three inches in length, and one-half inch in width, and stew the whole gently for about a half hour.
6. Take off the fire and add the beaten yolks of two eggs, stirring constantly and serve hot.

title: Tripe With Mushrooms
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- pepper
- salt
- flour or rolled cracker
- bacon fat
- vinegar
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Clean and parboil tripe before cooking.
2. When it is white and tender, cut it into pieces suitable to fry; pepper and salt it, and dip it in flour or rolled cracker, then drop it into hot bacon fat.
3. When browned on both sides, take up and make a gravy of some of the fat in which it was fried, a little flour, and a wineglass of good vinegar.
4. Pour this around the tripe and serve with mushrooms.

title: Truffle Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 truffles
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Make an omelet with truffles in the same manner as a Mushroom Omelet

title: Truffles And Chestnut Stuffing For A Pig
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- fat bacon
- livers of veal or fowl
- sweet herbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- chestnuts
- hulls
- parsley

## Directions

1. Many persons like truffles for stuffing for a roast pig; they should be mixed with fat bacon, livers of veal or fowl, sweet herbs, pepper, salt and butter.
2. Chestnut stuffing is prepared by roasting sixty chestnuts.
3. Remove their hulls while hot, and pound them fine,

title: Truffles and Chestnut Stuffing for a Pig
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- fat bacon
- livers of veal or fowl
- sweet herbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- chestnuts (sixty)
- green onions or chives
- sweet basil
- parsley
- thyme
- nutmeg
- eggs (three)

## Directions

1. Many persons like truffles for stuffing for a roast pig; they should be mixed with fat bacon, livers of veal or fowl, sweet herbs, pepper, salt and butter.
2. Chestnut stuffing is prepared by roasting sixty chestnuts.
3. Remove their hulls while hot, and pound them fine, add four ounces of butter, run this through a sieve, and add to it a few green onions, or chives, sweet basil, parsley and thyme;
4. grate in a nutmeg, put in pepper and salt, and bind it with three eggs.
5. Stuff the pig with it and serve with tomato sauce.

title: Truffles in Spanish Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Truffles
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Salad Oil or Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sliced Parsley
- 3 Finely Minced Shallots
- 3 Sprigs of Minced Parsley
- 1/2 Glass of White Wine
- Sauce Espagnole
- Salt and Pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Slice the Truffles very fine, and place in a saucepan on a slow fire, with a tablespoonful of butter or two of oil, salt and pepper to taste and the minced parsley and shallots.
2. After letting them smother for a few minutes, moisten well with the White Wine or Champagne and a Sauce Espagnole, using equal proportions of the wine and sauce.
3. Quicken the fire slightly, and let them cook slowly till done.
4. Skim off all grease, and serve hot with ragouts, etc.

title: Truffles on Minced Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Truffles
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 8 Croutons Fried In Butter
- Juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the Truffles as in the recipe 'a l'Espagnole,' or simply 'a la Provençale,' that is, slice them very fine
2. Let them stew or sauter them in butter
3. Season well with salt and pepper and the clove of garlic minced very fine
4. When done, add the juice of a lemon
5. Take some slices of bread, cut in fancy shapes, and fry in butter, or toast
6. Cover each piece with some of the Ragout of Truffles, and serve hot

title: Truffles With Champagne
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Truffles
- 1 Glass of Champagne
- Spices and vinegar for Marinade

## Directions

1. Make a Marinade, that is, a liquor with spices and vinegar; set to cook, and add a glass of champagne.
2. Add the Truffles, and season again to taste.
3. Let all cook for upwards of half an hour.
4. Take the Truffles from the sauce.
5. Let them cool, and serve on a folded napkin as an hors d'oeuvre.

title: Turbot, Halibut And Other Fish A La Creme
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- 1 quart of cream or milk
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 onion
- bunch of parsley
- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 pound clarified butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil fish with salt and separate from bones
2. Boil 1 quart of cream or milk with the yolks of 4 eggs beaten in
3. Add 1 onion and a bunch of parsley
4. When boiled, strain the cream and heat again
5. When boiling stir in 3 tablespoonfuls of flour, perfectly smooth
6. Add 1 pound clarified butter
7. Butter a deep dish and place first a layer of fish, then of sauce, until it is full, having a layer of sauce on top
8. Cover with bread crumbs and bake 1 hour

title: Turkey en Daube
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Large Turkey
- 1 Bunch Each of Parsley, Thyme and Small Celery Leaves
- Large Slice of Salt Pork
- 2 Onions and 2 Carrots, Sliced
- 10 Cloves
- ⅛ Calfs Foot
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- Bouquet of Sweet Herbs
- 1½ Pints of Broth or Boiling Water
- 2 Spoonfuls of Brandy
- 1 Pint of White Wine

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey, then stuff either with egg dressing or oyster stuffing.
2. Rub well with salt and pepper.
3. Place at the bottom of a deep pot slender strips of salt pork and half of a calf's foot, well prepared.
4. Place on top of this the slices of onions, carrots, fine herbs, minced nicely; garlic, minced, celery, parsley, etc., and lay the turkey on this bed.
5. Pour over it one pint of white wine and two tablespoonfuls of brandy, and one pint and a half of good broth or boiling water.
6. Season well to taste and cover tightly.
7. Set on the stove to simmer very slowly for at least five hours if the turkey is old.
8. Turn it once very carefully when half done cooking.
9. After five hours, lift the turkey out of the sauce, place on a hot dish.
10. Strain the sauce through a sieve, and if the turkey is served at once, serve hot in a separate dish.
11. If not, pour it over the turkey and set it away to cool.
12. It will become quite jellied and makes an excellent luncheon dish.

title: Turkey Gumbo
body:
## Ingredients

- The Remains of a Turkey
- 1/2 Pound of Lean Ham
- 2 Tablespoons of Butter or 1 of Lard
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 3 Sprigs of Parsley
- 3 Dozen Oysters
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Quarts of Oyster Water
- 1/2 Pod of Red Pepper, Without the Seeds
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste

## Directions

1. Strip the turkey meat, black and white, from the bones and carcass
2. Chop fine and add to the hot lard
3. Add the ham, cut fine into dice shapes
4. Proceed exactly as in the Chicken Gumbo recipe, adding the boiling water and throwing in the bones and carcass of the turkey
5. At the proper time remove the carcass and bones
6. Add the oysters
7. Remove the pot and File the gumbo
8. Serve with boiled rice

title: Turkey With Chestnuts or Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Turkey
- Chestnut or Oyster Dressing

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey in the manner indicated in Roast Turkey.
2. Stuff according to taste with either a Chestnut or Oyster Dressing.
3. Cook as in recipe for Roast Turkey.

title: Turkish Egg Plant
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- 2 lbs. cold beef, mutton or veal
- 1 onion
- 6 whole peppers
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon allspice
