- celery seed
- white pepper
- salt
- cold gravy or water

## Directions

1. Slice the egg plant and just brown it in a frying pan
2. Chop the cold beef, mutton or veal very fine
3. Season with one fine chopped onion, 6 whole peppers, 1 teaspoon cloves, 1 teaspoon allspice, celery seed, white pepper and salt
4. Put in baking dish a layer of egg plant, then of beef, and so on until it is filled
5. Have layer of egg plant on top
6. Pour cold gravy or water on the whole
7. Cover with another dish when set in oven
8. Remove it in time to let the top brown a little before done

title: Turnips Baked With Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Roast Mutton Drippings

## Directions

1. Boil the turnips until nearly tender.
2. Drain in a colander.
3. If very large, cut in quarters; if small, let them remain uncut.
4. Lay them on the pan, around the roasting leg of mutton, and let them bake about an hour, basting frequently with the drippings from the mutton.
5. When done, place around the leg of mutton as a garnish, and serve.

title: Turnips Fried in Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Turnips
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Boil the turnips according to directions, and, when very tender, drain in a colander.
2. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter into a frying pan, and when it is hot, add the turnips.
3. Season well with salt and pepper, and, when nicely browned, dish on a hot platter and serve with roast ducks.

title: Turnips, To Cook
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil or steam them after peeling
2. When they are quite tender, mash them like potatoes
3. Season with pepper, salt and butter

title: Turnpike Cakes, Commonly Called Hard Yeast
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- hops
- water
- corn-meal
- bakers' yeast
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a cup of hops into a pint of water
2. When boiling hot, strain it over a pint of corn-meal
3. Add a teacup of bakers' yeast
4. When cool roll the dough in flour and cut it out into cakes
5. Dry them for use

title: Turtle Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- the whole of a turtle
- a leg of beef
- water
- 2 quarts and a half of soup
- an ounce of mace
- a dessertspoonful of allspice
- a teaspoonful of cloves
- salt
- black pepper
- cayenne pepper
- parsley
- thyme
- onions
- two tablespoons of flour

## Directions

1. Take the whole of the turtle out of the shell; cut it in pieces, that it may be more easily scalded
2. Throw these pieces, with the fins, into the pot, and when scalded, take off the coarse skin of the fins and lay them aside to make another dish
3. Take off the thick skin of the stomach; under it lies the fat, or what is termed the citron
4. Cut up the turtle in small pieces, throw them into a pot
5. Before doing this, take a leg of beef and boil it to a gravy
6. Put in the pieces of turtle, adding as much water as will cover the whole, about 2 inches
7. Let it boil slowly for 3 hours
8. The seasoning and the citron should be put in when the soup is half done
9. To 2 quarts and a half of soup add an ounce of mace, a dessertspoonful of allspice, a teaspoonful of cloves, and salt, black and cayenne pepper to your taste
10. Use parsley, thyme, and onions in the soup while boiling
11. When nearly done thicken with 2 tablespoons of flour

title: Turtle Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of beef
- turtle
- water
- mace
- allspice
- cloves
- salt
- black pepper
- cayenne pepper
- parsley
- thyme
- onions
- flour
- brown sugar
- ham
- sweet herbs
- eggs
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Cut up the turtle in small pieces and throw them into a pot
2. Before doing this, take a leg of beef and boil it to a gravy
3. Put in the pieces of turtle, adding as much water as will cover the whole, about 2 inches
4. Let it boil slowly for 3 hours
5. Put in the seasoning and citron when the soup is half done
6. To 2 quarts and a half of soup add 1/2 an ounce of mace, a dessertspoonful of allspice, 1/2 teaspoonful of cloves, and salt, black and cayenne pepper to your taste
7. Use parsley, thyme, and onions in the soup while boiling
8. When nearly done thicken with 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
9. Take a tablespoonful of brown sugar and burn it to give it good color
10. When sufficiently burnt add a wineglass of water
11. Use 2 tablespoonfuls of this coloring in the soup
12. Make forcemeat balls with a bit of the turtle, a small piece of ham, and seasoned highly with sweet herbs
13. Add the yolk of one egg, roll it into balls, fry them and put them into the soup
14. Boil 1/2 a dozen eggs hard, slice them and put them in
15. Add 1/2 a pint of Madeira wine
16. After the wine cooks a little, the soup will be ready for table

title: Turtle Soup for a Large Company, No. 1
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of beef or veal
- one pound of ham or bacon
- eight onions
- pepper, salt, and sweet herbs to taste
- the liquor of a boiled turtle
- wine
- spice
- cayenne
- catsup
- the flesh of the turtle, prepared as in recipe No. 1
- sliced lemon
- whole pepper
- a bunch of parsley
- two leeks sliced
- a quarter of a pound of good sweet butter
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a lemon sliced thin

## Directions

1. Cut the head off the turtle the day before you dress it, and drain the blood thoroughly from the body
2. Then cut it up in the following manner: Divide the back, belly, head and fins from the intestines and lean parts
3. Be careful not to cut the gall bag
4. Scald in boiling water to remove the skin and shell
5. Cut up in neat pieces and throw into cold water
6. Boil the back and belly in a little water long enough to extract the bones easily
7. If for a large company a leg of veal will also be required, and a slice of ham, which must be stewed with the lean parts till well browned
8. Then add boiling water, and the liquor and bones of the boiled turtle
9. Season with sliced lemon, whole pepper, a bunch of parsley, two leeks sliced, and salt to taste
10. Let this all boil slowly for four hours then strain
11. Add the pieces of back, belly, head and fins (take the bones from the fins)
12. Pour in half a pint of Madeira wine and a quarter of a pound of good sweet butter, with a tablespoonful of flour worked in it
13. Also, a lemon sliced thin
14. Let it boil gently for two hours, then serve

title: Turtle Soup No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of beef or veal
- one pound of ham or bacon
- eight onions
- pepper, salt, and sweet herbs to taste
- the liquor of a boiled turtle
- wine
- spice
- cayenne
- catsup
- the flesh of the turtle, prepared as in recipe No. 1
- sliced lemon
- whole pepper
- a bunch of parsley
- two leeks sliced
- a quarter of a pound of good sweet butter
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a lemon sliced thin

## Directions

1. Put on, at an early hour in the morning, eight pounds of beef or veal, one pound of ham or bacon, eight onions, with pepper, salt, and sweet herbs to taste
2. Make a rich soup of this, and add to it the liquor of a boiled turtle
3. Season very high with wine, spice, cayenne, and catsup
4. Put in the flesh of the turtle, prepared as in recipe No. 1—do not use the eyes or tongue
5. Let this boil

title: Turtle Soup No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of beef or veal
- one pound of ham or bacon
- eight onions
- pepper
- salt
- sweet herbs to taste
- the liquor of a boiled turtle
- wine
- spice
- cayenne
- catsup
- the flesh of the turtle, prepared as in recipe No. 1
- force-meat balls

## Directions

1. Put on, at an early hour in the morning, eight pounds of beef or veal, one pound of ham or bacon, eight onions, with pepper, salt, and sweet herbs to taste.
2. Make a rich soup of this, and add to it the liquor of a boiled turtle;
3. Season very high with wine, spice, cayenne, and catsup.
4. Put in the flesh of the turtle, prepared as in recipe No. 1—do not use the eyes or tongue.
5. Let this boil up till tender, and serve with force-meat balls in tureen.

title: Turtle Soup No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Turtle Meat
- 3/4 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 1/4 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 2 Tomatoes
- 1 Large Onion
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Dozen Cloves Tied in Muslin
- 6 Allspice Mashed Fine
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs
- 1 Glass of Sherry or White Wine
- Salt and Cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the turtle and entrails by cutting open the latter and washing thoroughly in cold water.
2. Put the meat and entrails into a saucepan and parboil them for ten minutes. Carefully save this stock of water.
3. Chop the onion very fine, and cut the ham into very fine pieces.
4. Cut the turtle meat into one-inch pieces; mash the allspice very fine, and mince the parsley, thyme and bay leaf.
5. Brown the onions in the lard and butter mixed, and almost immediately add the turtle meat.
6. Brown together for ten minutes and add the finely chopped ham.
7. As this continues to brown, add the cloves of garlic minced fine, the thyme and bay leaf and the ground allspice.
8. Mix all together, stirring almost constantly to prevent burning.
9. Add the well-rubbed tablespoonfuls of flour, stirring constantly.
10. Scald and skin the tomatoes and chop them fine, and add to the turtle meat.
11. When well-browned, pour over three quarts of the water in which the turtle was parboiled, season with salt and pepper and Cayenne to taste, and let it boil slowly for fully an hour, stirring frequently.
12. After one hour, taste the soup, and if not sufficiently seasoned, add seasoning of salt, pepper and Cayenne again, according to taste.
13. Let it cook for an hour longer and then take off the stove if the turtle is thoroughly done. This may be ascertained by sticking it with a fork. If no blood exudes, the soup is ready to serve.
14. Take off the stove and strain through a colander into the tureen.
15. Add the whites and yolks of two hard-boiled eggs, chopped fine, and one good glass of Sherry or White Wine.
16. Slice a lemon fine and add to the soup and serve hot.

title: Tutti-Frutti Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup of Sugar
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 1 Teacup of Milk
- 2½ Teacups of Flour
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Pound of Raisins
- 1 Pound of Figs
- 1 Pound of Powdered Almonds
- The Whites of 7 Eggs

## Directions

1. Take one teacupful of sugar and one of butter and beat to a cream
2. Add a teacupful of milk and two and a half teaspoonfuls of flour sifted, with two heaping teaspoonfuls of baking powder
3. Add one pound of raisins, well-seeded, and one pound each of shredded figs and pounded almonds
4. After mixing all these well, add the whites of seven eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Bake in a slow oven
6. When done, make a light syrup and spread over the top, and garnish with pieces of finely-cut preserved citron, oranges, preserved, currants and other candies

title: Tutti-Frutti à la Creole
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Orange Sherbet
- ¾ Pint of Cherries, Seeded
- ¾ Pint of Stoned Apricots
- ¾ Pint of Stoned Peaches
- ¾ Pint of Figs
- ¼ Pint of Pineapple
- ¾ Pint of Watermelon

## Directions

1. Cut the watermelon and the pineapple into very small square lozenges, first seeding the watermelon.
2. Cut the rest of the fruits very fine.
3. Put a layer of the watermelon into the freezer, and sprinkle generously with white powdered sugar.
4. Put a layer of the mixed fruits, sugar abundantly, pour in a little of the sherbet, and proceed in this way until the whole form is nearly filled with the mixture in alternate layers.
5. Set in a double boiler just long enough for the sugar to melt.
6. Take out, pour over the orange sherbet, close tightly, and set to freeze.

title: Twist Loaves
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce of Compressed Yeast
- 2 Quarts of Water
- 1½ Ounces of Salt
- Flour Sufficient to Make a Smooth Dough

## Directions

1. Make the identical dough as in Bakers' Bread
2. After the first rising and throwing in of water and working the bread, cut the dough, and divide each portion into three pieces of dough
3. Dust this dough well with parched flour and roll the pieces into lengths of about two feet, or a foot and a half, and plait them
4. Lay them in a long box or on a board, and cover with a stiff cloth, as in French Loaves, and let them lie till they begin to rise
5. Place in the oven and bake to a nice brown

title: Vanilla Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Fresh Creole Eggs
- 1 Ounce of Vanilla Sugar
- 4 Ounces of Flour
- 10 Ounces of Pulverized Sugar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar to a cream.
2. Add the chocolate, and mix well.
3. Then add the flour, alternating with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Add one tablespoonful of Vanilla Extract.
5. Bake upon wafer sheets in small cakes to a light color.

title: Vanilla Ice
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pints of milk
- 8 ounces of cream
- 12 ounces of sugar
- 4 grains of vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat vanilla with a little sugar in a marble mortar till it becomes powdered
2. Put it in a stewpan or skillet with the milk, cream and sugar
3. Let them boil until the whole is sufficiently thick
4. Strain it through a cloth and pour it into a bowl to cool

title: Vanilla Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- 6 Eggs
- ¾ Pound of Sugar
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Any Extract

## Directions

1. Put the milk or cream on to boil.
2. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar very light, and then beat the whites to a stiff froth.
3. Take the milk off the fire, and pour it over the sugar and yolks, stirring all the while.
4. Then add the whites, mixing thoroughly, and two tablespoonfuls of extract of either Lemon or Vanilla.
5. Set to cool, and then turn into the freezer, pack, and stir around in the ice for about ten minutes, and finish as in recipe for making and freezing cream.

title: Vanilla Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Set the milk to boil, and beat the yolks of the eggs and sugar very light: add them to the boiling milk
2. Stir for two minutes over the fire
3. Take off and add the Vanilla and the whites of the eggs beaten to a froth; mix thoroughly, and serve either hot or cold.

title: Vanilla Sugar
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds of Loaf Sugar
- 3 Vanilla Beans

## Directions

1. Take three vanilla beans and cut them into shreds
2. Let them dry, and then pound them with about two pounds of fine loaf sugar
3. When reduced to a very fine powder, sift, put in a jar and cork tight

title: Vanilla Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Gallon of Plain Syrup
- 3 Ounces of Extract of Vanilla
- 1/4 Ounce of Caramel

## Directions

1. Boil the syrup and add the Caramel
2. Drain, and then add the essence of vanilla
3. Stir well and bottle

title: Variegated Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of Milk or Cream
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 6 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful of Essence of Strawberry
- 1 Teaspoonful of Essence of Vanilla
- 1 Teaspoonful of Chocolate
- 1 Teaspoonful of Cream of Violets
- 1 Teaspoonful of Spinach Greenery

## Directions

1. Prepare a Plain Ice Cream
2. Divide it into portions
3. Color one with Essence of Strawberry, another with Spinach Greenery, another with Vanilla to obtain yellow, another with Creme de Violettes, another with Chocolate, etc.
4. Freeze all these in separate molds that come specially prepared for this purpose
5. When frozen, take from the individual molds and pack in a large ice cream freezer in a beautiful order of arrangement, so that the colors may blend well
6. Freeze again, and then remove from the mold according to directions given for unmolding
7. Cut into slices and serve

title: Veal and Ham Raised Pie, or Timbale
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of lean veal
- strips of fat bacon
- two pounds of ham
- rich paste
- liver forcemeat
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- dried and pounded sweet herbs
- egg
- about a pint of good gravy or veal consomé

## Directions

1. Lard two pounds of lean veal well with strips of fat bacon, and add two pounds of ham.
2. Line a deep pan or mould with rich paste.
3. Lay in the bottom of this a layer of liver forcemeat, then the veal and ham, and so on in alternate layers, till the dish is full.
4. Season between each layer with thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, or any dried and pounded sweet herbs.
5. Fill up the hollow places, and cover the pan with paste.
6. Decorate the top of the pie with cut dough leaves.
7. Make a hole in the top to pour in the gravy, and let out the steam.
8. Egg the top of the pie and bake it for three hours.
9. Withdraw it from the oven, and place the point of a funnel in the hole in the top, and pour in about a pint of good gravy or veal consomé.
10. This should be eaten cold and will be jellied all through if cooked enough.

title: Veal Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 of a Pound of Cold Cooled Veal
- 1 Small Onion
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 4 Sprigs of Parsley
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Onion
- 1 Cup of Milk
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- Cayenne and Pepper to Taste
- Bread
- 2 Eggs
- Powdered Bread Crumbs
- Boiling Lard
- Fried Parsley

## Directions

1. Remove all the tough fibers and nerves
2. Hash the veal well and season with the minced vegetables and sweet herbs, mixing all thoroughly
3. Take a cup of the soft of the bread, wet it and squeeze, and soak in milk, in which you have beaten two eggs
4. Mix all this with the meat very thoroughly and season to taste
5. Form the meat into cylindrical shapes and brush with a little butter
6. Roll in a beaten egg and roll again in powdered bread crumbs
7. Fry in boiling lard and serve hot on a plate garnished with fried parsley

title: Veal Cutlets Breaded
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Veal Cutlets
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Lemon and Parsley to Garnish
- Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel

## Directions

1. Have the cutlets cut thin.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Beat an egg well and roll the cutlets in the egg, then roll in bread crumbs.
4. Drop in boiling lard and fry to a nice golden brown.
5. Take out, drain off the lard, place on a heated dish and serve with garnish of parsley and sliced lemon and a Sauce a la Maitre d'Hotel.
6. The addition of a little lemon juice adds to the flavor when eating the cutlets.

title: Veal Cutlets Breaded and Broiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Veal Cutlets
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup of Bread Crumbs
- Parsley Sprigs
- Melted Butter or Sauce a la Ravigotte

## Directions

1. Have the cutlets as thin as possible.
2. Season well, after having beat with the rolling-pin, and roll in a well-beaten egg and then in bread crumbs.
3. Pat lightly with the hands and brush with melted butter.
4. Place on a double broiler and broil on a very slow fire till no blood exudes.
5. Serve with drawn butter sauce poured over or a Sauce a la Ravigotte.
6. Garnish the dish nicely with parsley sprigs.

title: Veal Cutlets Smothered à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Veal Cutlets
- 6 Onions, Sliced Fine
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- Pepper and Salt to Taste
- Chopped Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay Leaf
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 1 Cup of Water or 1 Pint

## Directions

1. Cut off the outer skin of the cutlets, and season well with salt and pepper.
2. Dredge with flour.
3. Place the tablespoonful of lard in a deep frying pan and let it melt.
4. Lay in the sliced onions, and over these lay the cutlets.
5. Cover closely.
6. Let them simmer over a hot fire for a few minutes and then turn the cutlets on the other side.
7. After three minutes, add two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, chopped parsley, thyme and bay leaf and a clove of garlic.
8. Turn the veal, letting the flour brown well, and keep the pot closely covered.
9. When brown, pour over one cup of water, or a pint, which will be sufficient to cover the meat.
10. Bring this to a brisk boil and set the pot back, where it can simmer gently for about two hours.
11. Serve with a nice garnish of parsley or radishes.

title: Veal Daube à la Créole
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of Veal Rump
- 2 Pig's Feet
- ½ Pound of Salt Fat Meat
- 5 Large Onions
- 2 Turnips
- 5 Carrots
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 3 Bay Leaves
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard
- 1 Glass of Sherry
- 3 Sprigs of Thyme and Parsley
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 2 Pounds Veal Steak
- 4 Quarts of Water
- 2 Cloves
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- Madeira Wine

## Directions

1. Cut the salt meat into shreds, roll well in Cayenne and black pepper.
2. Chop finely several sprigs of thyme and three bay leaves, one clove of garlic, three sprigs of parsley, and mash well three cloves and six allspice.
3. Roll the strips of salt meat, which must be about three inches in length and one-half inch thick, in this.
4. Make incisions into the rump of meat and force in the strips of fat meat and the spices.
5. Rub the whole well with salt and pepper, judging according to taste, and proceed to cook according to the recipe for Beef à la Mode.
6. Let the daube cook about four hours when you intend to serve it cold.
7. In the meantime, in another pot, place a veal steak of about two pounds and two pigs' feet.
8. Season well with salt and pepper and Cayenne, and cover well with four quarts of water, and let them boil.
9. Add one bay leaf, one sprig of thyme, one-half clove of garlic and one onion, all minced very fine, and two cloves mashed into almost a jelly, and one glass of Sherry or Madeira Wine.
10. Let these boil well with the veal and pigs' feet.
11. When the veal and pigs' feet are cooked very tender, take them out of the pot and mince the meat of each very fine.
12. Return to the sauce, and again season highly, according to taste.
13. After the daube has cooked four or five hours, take off the stove and pour over the sauce and set all in a cool place.
14. Serve the next day—cold, cutting into thin slices.

title: Veal en Ratatouille
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of Brisket of Veal
- 1/2 Pound of Ham
- 2 Cloves of Garlic
- 1/2 Can of Tomatoes, or 6 Fresh
- 4 Dozen Fresh Okra
- 2 Large Onions
- 2 Sweet Potatoes
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Sprig of Thyme
- 2 Sprigs of Parsley
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter
- 1/2 Sweet Pepper Pod

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into pieces of about three inches in length and two in width
2. Cut the ham into dice shape, and the sweet potatoes, after peeling, into cubes of about one and a half inches
3. Add a half sweet pepper pod, if possible, being careful to extract all the seeds
4. Season the veal highly
5. Put a tablespoonful of lard into the stew pot, add the veal and let it brown nicely
6. Then add the ham, the sweet potatoes and the pepper pod, and let them simmer gently for about fifteen minutes
7. Place a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and when it melts add the chopped onion
8. As they brown nicely, add the minced herbs and garlic, and then the tomatoes sliced and chopped, in their own liquor
9. Let this stew for about fifteen minutes, and then add this to the stewing veal
10. Mix thoroughly and set back on the stove, covering tightly, and let it simmer slowly and constantly, with a regular fire, for about two hours
11. Then add the okras, which have been tipped and sliced very thinly
12. Let the mixture simmer for a half hour longer, and serve

title: Veal Gravy Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound veal cutlet
- three slices of ham
- two tablespoonfuls of lard
- three sliced onions
- two carrots
- two parsnips
- a head of celery
- a few cloves
- a pint and a half of boiling broth or water
- a cup of mushrooms

## Directions

1. Throw into a stewpan one pound veal cutlet, three slices of ham, two tablespoonfuls of lard, and let them fry gently; then, before browning, add three sliced onions, two carrots, two parsnips, a head of celery, and a few cloves. Let them cook slowly till lightly browned, then add a pint and a half of boiling broth or water; let this cook for an hour, and then put in a cup of mushrooms; skim and strain for use.

title: Veal Hash For Breakfast. Very Nice
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal
- flour
- butter
- water
- parsley
- sweet herbs
- milk
- bread

## Directions

1. Take a pint cup of cold veal cut small, dredge it with a spoonful of flour, and add a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg.
2. Put all in a stew-pan with half a pint of water; cover up and put it on the stove; let it simmer for an hour at least, stir it occasionally and add to it some parsley and sweet herbs.
3. Just before serving add a teacup of milk, and serve on toasted bread.

title: Veal Loaf
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of veal
- 2 slices of salt pork
- 2 eggs
- 2 Boston crackers
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls of pepper
- 1 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop veal and salt pork fine
2. Beat 2 eggs well
3. Roll 2 Boston crackers fine
4. Mix veal, salt pork, eggs, crackers, salt, pepper, and nutmeg well together
5. Bake from 2.5 to 3 hours
6. Bake well and serve cold sliced

title: Veal Loaf for Lunch or Tea
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast veal
- fat ham
- a cup of grated bread or cracker crumbs
- two well-beaten eggs
- salt
- pepper (black and red)
- yolk of egg
- fine cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Mince cold roast veal as fine as possible.
2. Add a fourth part as much fat ham, a cup of grated bread or cracker crumbs, and two well-beaten eggs to bind the crumbs together.
3. Season with salt, and pepper (black and red).
4. Mix and form it into a loaf.
5. Glaze the outside with yolk of egg, and sprinkle over it fine cracker crumbs.
6. Bake half an hour, and serve with gravy made from the bones of the veal.
7. Serve the gravy hot.

title: Veal Olives
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of veal
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- a little water
- butter
- pounded biscuit or bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Slice pieces of the fillet of veal 1/2 an inch thick and 8 or 10 inches wide and long
2. Put them in a dish
3. Sprinkle over them on 1 side, a little pepper, salt and mace, all finely pounded
4. Roll tightly and tie them up separately
5. Put them into a stewpan with a little water and butter, and simmer down to a brown gravy
6. Stew them 1 hour
7. When they are to be served, have ready some pounded biscuit or bread crumbs, which sprinkle over them after they are put into the dish

title: Veal Patties or Veal Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Veal Cutlets
- 3 Yolks of Eggs, beaten light
- 2 Crackers
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- Chopped Parsley, Thyme and Bay Leaf
- Salt, Pepper and Cayenne to Taste
- 1 Pound of Lean Ham
- The Juice of 1 Lemon

## Directions

1. Stew the veal, and then powder the crackers very fine
2. Mince the cutlets, the herbs and the ham very fine
3. Season well with salt and pepper, and mix with the bread crumbs and beaten eggs
4. Add a clove of garlic to the taste, if desired
5. Add juice of 1 lemon
6. With the whole form nice little loaves of pie shape, smooth over the top with butter
7. Brush with egg, beaten well, sprinkle with crumbs, and bake in a moderate oven, placing a buttered paper over the patés
8. The loaf may be formed whole and cut in thin slices and serve cold

title: Veal Pâté, to Serve Cold
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds of veal cutlets
- good slice of salt pork
- 2 eggs
- 6 crackers
- butter to size of a hen's egg
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- cayenne pepper
- sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Chop fine 8 pounds of veal cutlets with a good slice of salt pork
2. Break in 2 eggs after meat is well mixed
3. Add 6 crackers powdered fine
4. Add butter to size of a hen's egg
5. Rub in a tablespoonful of salt, cayenne pepper, and sweet herbs to your taste
6. Mix the whole thoroughly
7. Make up like a loaf of bread
8. Smooth over the top with butter melted in water
9. Bake in a moderate oven
10. When cold, cut in thin slices and serve

title: Veal Salad for Lunch
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of minced veal
- three heads of celery
- yolks of four hard-boiled eggs
- a tablespoonful of dry mustard
- a large spoonful of olive oil
- four tablespoonfuls of good wine vinegar
- cayenne
- salt
- parsley
- celery leaves

## Directions

1. To a pint of minced veal add three heads of celery.
2. Make a dressing of the yolks of four hard-boiled eggs, a tablespoonful of dry mustard, and a large spoonful of olive oil.
3. Beat this dressing till perfectly smooth.
4. Add to it slowly (to keep from curdling) four tablespoonfuls of good wine vinegar, a little cayenne and salt.
5. Garnish the dish with parsley and celery leaves.

title: Veal Sweetbread And Oyster Pie
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbread
- oysters
- cream
- butter
- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- flour
- paste

## Directions

1. The sweetbread of veal is the most delicate part of the animal
2. Boil it tender, season with pepper, salt and butter
3. Put in two dozen oysters
4. Thicken their juice with a cup of cream, a tablespoonful of butter, the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs, and a tablespoonful of flour
5. Pour all in a deep pan, and cover with paste and bake
6. If there is too much liquid, keep it to serve with the pie, if necessary, when baked
7. After baking, the pie is sometimes too dry

title: Veal Sweetbreads, With Tomatoes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of ripe tomatoes
- four or five sweetbreads
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- three spoonfuls of flour
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- beaten yolks of three eggs

## Directions

1. Set over the fire two quarts of ripe tomatoes and stew slowly.
2. Strain through a coarse sieve.
3. Add to them four or five sweetbreads, well trimmed and soaked in warm water.
4. Season with salt and cayenne pepper.
5. Thicken with three spoonfuls of flour and a quarter of a pound of butter, mixed.
6. Cook slowly till done.
7. Just before serving stir in the beaten yolks of three eggs.

title: Veal With Olives
body:
## Ingredients

- A Flank of Veal
- 1 Slice of Cold Boiled Ham
- 1 Grated Onion
- 1 Hard-Boiled Egg
- 1 Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Dozen Stoned Olives
- Chopped Herbs
- Bread Crumbs
- Soup Broth

## Directions

1. Get a flank of veal and cut it into strips of about four inches in length and four in width
2. Cut off sufficient to make a half cup, and chop this fine, with a slice of cold boiled ham
3. Make a mince meat, adding chopped herbs, according to taste, 1 grated onion, 1 hard-boiled egg, the juice of 1 lemon and a tablespoonful of butter, with a half cup of bread crumbs
4. Take the strips of veal, stuff them nicely with this mixture and roll over the ends, tying to prevent the farcie from escaping
5. Place a tablespoonful of butter in a frying pan, and, when it heats, add the rolls of veal
6. Let them fry for ten minutes, turning, and then add soup broth sufficient to cover them
7. Cover closely and set back on the stove, and let them simmer steadily but slowly for an hour longer
8. Place in a hot dish, pour the gravy over, seasoning highly; add about two dozen stoned olives, and pour over the rolls and serve

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow split peas
- dried white beans
- cold water
- salt
- cayenne
- celery
- celery seed
- water
- turnips
- carrots
- parsnips
- potatoes
- onions
- salsify
- asparagus tops
- fresh butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Soak all night in cold water either two quarts of yellow split peas or two quarts of dried white beans
2. In the morning drain them
3. Season them with a very little salt and cayenne and a head of minced celery, or else a heaped tablespoonful of celery seed
4. Put them into a soup pot with four quarts of water
5. Boil them slowly till they are all dissolved
6. Stir them frequently
7. Have ready a quantity of fresh vegetables, such as turnips, carrots, parsnips, potatoes, onions, also salsify, and asparagus tops
8. Put in first the vegetables that require the longest boiling
9. Cut all vegetables into small pieces
10. Enrich the whole with some bits of fresh butter rolled in flour
11. Boil these vegetables in the soup till they are quite tender
12. Put it in a tureen and serve it up

title: Vegetable Soup Without Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Sweet Potato
- 1 Bunch of Celery Leaves
- 1 Turnip
- 1 Parsnip
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 2 Onions
- Sprig of Parsley and Thyme
- 1 Irish Potato
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Flour
- 1 Large Tablespoonful of Butter
- 3 1/2 Quarts of Cold Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- A gill of cream or milk

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetables into dice and boil until thoroughly tender in about three and a half quarts of water, which will require about two hours
2. Then press the whole through a sieve
3. Add the remaining water and bring to a boil
4. Add the butter, rubbed smooth with the flour in a little rich cream, or a little of the hot soup
5. A gill of cream or milk added just before serving increases the flavor
6. Boil and stir about two or three minutes more and serve

title: Veloute Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Ounces Butter, or 1 Tablespoonful and a Half
- 1 Tablespoonful and a Half of Flour
- 2 Gills of Water
- The Well-Beaten Yolks of 2 Eggs
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Juice of a Lemon
- 1 Tablespoonful of Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Blend the flour and butter as in White Sauce, only letting it become slightly yellow
2. Add by degrees the boiling water, and season to taste
3. Add a tablespoonful of white wine if desired
4. Add the juice of half a lemon, and a tablespoonful of chopped parsley
5. Let it simmer for about ten minutes
6. Take from the fire and add the well-beaten yolks of two eggs
7. Serve immediately with any boiled fish or meats

title: Velvet Blanc Manger
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Cream
- 3/4 Cup of Powdered Sugar
- 3/4 Cup of Gelatine, Dissolved in Water (hot)
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- 1 Teaspoonful of Almond Extract

## Directions

1. Boil the cream, sugar and gelatine together, stirring until smooth.
2. Add a teaspoonful of Almond Extract.
3. Mix well, and pour into a mold, and set to cool and harden.

title: Velvet Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of yeast
- three eggs well beaten
- one quart of warm milk
- one quart of sifted flour
- salt
- a large spoonful of butter well beaten

## Directions

1. Combine yeast, eggs, milk, flour, salt, and butter
2. let it rise
3. Pour into greased muffin rings
4. bake

title: Velvet Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1 spoonful flour
- whites of 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoonfuls powdered sugar
- vanilla
- lemon

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of milk
2. When boiling, stir into it the yolks of 6 eggs, 6 tablespoonfuls of sugar, 1 spoonful of flour, which has been well beaten together
3. When boiled, turn into a dish, and pour over it the whites beaten to a stiff froth, and mixed with 6 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, and brown slightly
4. Flavor the top with vanilla and the bottom with lemon

title: Velvet Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 6 Ounces of Sugar
- Juice of 1 Lemon
- 3 Tablespoonfuls Milk
- 1/2 Pint of Gelatine

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine in the water, and let it dissolve well.
2. Set the milk to boil, and add the powdered sugar.
3. Then dissolve the gelatine in it.
4. Take off the fire, and add, as it cools, essence of vanilla or orange, using about two tablespoonfuls.
5. Mix well.
6. Then add the whipped cream, set over the fire one minute, pour into a mold to harden, and serve cold.

title: Velvet Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 Cups of Powdered Sugar
- 1/4 Cup Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Cornstarch
- 1 Gill of Boiling Water
- 1 Gill of White Wine or Maraschino

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and blend the cornstarch with a little cold water to form a paste
2. Boil the gill of water and add the cornstarch, stirring constantly as it thickens; continue stirring till transparent
3. Then add the butter and sugar, stirring well till perfectly blended, being careful to have the vessel containing the sauce in a bain-marie, or another vessel of boiling water
4. Add a gill of White Wine or Maraschino, and the Juice of a lemon or orange, or vanilla extract, according to taste
5. Serve hot with any pudding.

title: Venison Cutlets Broiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Venison Cutlets
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Melted Butter
- Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Watercress to Garnish

## Directions

1. Trim the cutlets nicely, rub well with salt and pepper, and brush with a little butter.
2. Broil over a quick clear fire, allowing about eight minutes, or less, according to size, to each cutlet.
3. Place in a very hot dish, pour over a little melted butter and chopped parsley.
4. Garnish with watercress, and serve with Currant Jelly.

title: Venison Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Left Over Venison
- 6 Potatoes
- 1 Herb Bouquet
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- 1 Tablespoonful of Lard or Butter

## Directions

1. After having taken off all the rough edges of the roast and cut out the gristle and hard membrane, hash the venison into pieces of about one-inch in size.
2. Take six left-over tomatoes, or freshly boiled, and cut into quarters.
3. Chop fine one herb bouquet.
4. Place a tablespoonful of butter or a half tablespoonful of lard into the stewpot, and as it melts add the venison, seasoned well, and a few minutes later the fine herbs.
5. Mince the clove of garlic if the flavor is liked and add.
6. Stir constantly without browning much, and add a tablespoonful of flour.
7. Let this brown very slightly, and then add the tomatoes.
8. Cover and let all simmer for about twenty minutes, and then pour over a pint of boiling water.
9. Season again to taste and set back on the stove and let it simmer gently for about three-quarters of an hour.
10. Cut some Croutons and fry them in butter; place on a dish and serve with the hash.

title: Venison In a Chafing Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 or 10 Slices of Venison
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Currant Jelly
- 1 Tablespoonful of Water
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Slice the venison very thin in pieces about two inches long and one inch wide, and about the thickness of a silver dollar.
2. Have the chafing dish or alcohol lamp on the dining table as you sit to eat.
3. The pan must be very hot.
4. Season the meat well with salt and pepper, and ready to put into the pan.
5. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the dish.
6. Let it get very hot, without burning.
7. Put the slices of Venison in the dish.
8. In one minute turn them over.
9. Take a tablespoonful of melted butter, and blend well with a tablespoonful of Currant Jelly and a tablespoonful of water.
10. Spread this over the cooking Venison.
11. Turn again.
12. Let it cook for five minutes only, and serve very hot.

title: Venison Pasty
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- bones, meat, etc., of venison
- pepper
- salt
- port wine
- butter
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut up and stew, or braise the parts of meat intended for the pie
2. Season with pepper, salt, port wine, butter, and if liked, mushrooms
3. Stew all until tender
4. Make a paste and finish like chicken pie
5. This is better to eat cold than hot and should be rich enough to be a solid jelly when cold

title: Venison Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- trimmings of venison (½ lb. of meat)
- ½ pint of water
- 1 tablespoonful of whole cloves
- 1 tumbler of currant jelly
- 1 tumbler of claret
- ½ lb. of butter and flour rubbed together
- 1 tablespoon of ground cinnamon or mace
- black pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the venison trimmings with ½ pint of water and a tablespoonful of whole cloves down to 1 pint
2. Strain the mixture
3. Add 1 tumbler of currant jelly, 1 tumbler of claret, ½ lb. of butter and flour rubbed together, 1 tablespoon of ground cinnamon or mace, a little black pepper, and salt if your taste requires

title: Venison Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- trimmings of venison, 1/2 lb. of meat
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1 tablespoonful of whole cloves
- 1 tumbler of currant jelly
- 1 tumbler of claret
- 1/2 lb. of butter and flour rubbed together
- 1 tablespoon of ground cinnamon or mace
- a little black pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. The trimmings of the venison (1/2 lb. of meat) to 1/2 pint of water; boil, with a tablespoonful of whole cloves, down to 1 pint
2. strain, and add 1 tumbler of currant jelly, ditto of claret, 1/2 lb. of butter and flour rubbed together, 1 tablespoon of ground cinnamon, or mace, a little black pepper, and salt, if your taste requires

title: Venison Steak
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison steak
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- wine
- flour

## Directions

1. Season with pepper, salt, and butter
2. Cook quickly on a hot gridiron if to be broiled
3. If the meat is not fat, make a gravy for it of wine, flour, and butter
4. Serve hot

title: Venison Steak
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- venison steak
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the venison steak nicely and drain the water from it
2. Butter it very thoroughly
3. Sprinkle with pepper and salt
4. Cook it in a hurry
5. Serve it very hot

title: Venison Steaks à la Poivrade
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Filets or as Many Filets as Desired
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Sauce Poivrade
- Butter
- Chopped Parsley

## Directions

1. Take the filets or Venison steaks from any part of the Venison. The best are from the haunch or leg, and cut three-quarters of an inch in thickness.
2. Rub them well with salt and pepper.
3. Fry in butter, allowing about five minutes to the steak.
4. Place in a heated dish, and garnish with melted butter and chopped parsley.
5. Serve with a Sauce Poivrade for Venison, pouring the sauce over the steaks.

title: Venison, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds of Venison Meat
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 1 Onion
- 1 Square Inch of Ham
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Clove of Garlic
- 2 Sprigs of Thyme
- 2 Bay Leaves
- 1/2 Box of Mushrooms
- The Zest of a Lemon
- 1 Glass of White Wine
- Salt and Pepper to Taste
- Croutons to Garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the venison into pieces of about two inches square.
2. Salt and pepper well.
3. Put two tablespoonfuls of butter into a saucepan with the venison and let it brown slowly.
4. When nearly brown, add an onion, chopped fine, and let this brown slightly.
5. Then add the ham, minced very fine, and the clove of garlic and bay leaves and thyme, minced very fine.
6. Stir in with the rabbit and let these brown for about two minutes.
7. Then add a tablespoonful of flour and brown for a few minutes more.
8. Add a half bottle of White Wine and let all simmer for five minutes.
9. Then add a quart of consommé or water and let all cook for about one hour.
10. Season again according to taste and add a half can of mushrooms chopped fine and the zest of a lemon and season again to taste.
11. Let all cook a half hour longer and serve on a hot dish with Croutons fried in butter.

title: Vermicelli Or Macaroni Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of vermicelli or macaroni
- a quart of warm water
- good stock or plain veal, chicken or beef soup
- a spoonful of butter
- half a pint of stewed tomatoes

## Directions

1. Swell a quarter of a pound of vermicelli or macaroni (whichever is preferred) in a quart of warm water for one hour; then add it to some good stock or plain veal, chicken or beef soup; add a spoonful of butter and half a pint of stewed tomatoes just before the soup is served. This is a very fine soup, and is especially nourishing for delicate stomachs.

title: Vermicelli Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- A Pint of Broth to Each Person
- 1 Ounce of Vermicelli to Each Person

## Directions

1. Prepare a good Bouillon, or Pot-au-Feu, or Consomme, according to the taste of the household
2. A half hour before serving add the vermicelli to the broth
3. Serve hot

title: Vermicelli Soup, No. 2
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of knuckle of veal
- one pound and a half of the scrag of mutton
- a few slices of ham
- one onion stuck with cloves
- four ounces of butter
- carrots
- mace
- bunch of sweet herbs
- one anchovy
- celery
- four quarts of water
- three pints of liquid
- gravy drawn from the meat
- one head of celery cut fine
- a little cayenne
- salt
- vermicelli

## Directions

1. Cut about four pounds of knuckle of veal, one pound and a half of the scrag of mutton, and a few slices of ham into small pieces; put them into a saucepan with one onion stuck with cloves, and four ounces of butter; then add the carrots, mace, bunch of sweet herbs, one anchovy, and the celery. Mix all together, cover it close, and set it over the fire till all the gravy has been extracted from the meat; pour the liquor into a bowl, let the meat brown in the pan, and add to it four quarts of water; boil it slowly till it is reduced to three pints, strain it, and stir in the gravy drawn from the meat. Set it over the fire, add the vermicelli, one head of celery cut fine, a little cayenne, and salt; boil it up for ten minutes. Lay a French roll in the tureen, pour the soup over it, and strew some vermicelli on the top.

title: Vermouth Frappé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of French vermouth
- 1 dash of orange bitters
- A dash of syrup
- A bit of lemon peel
- Finely crushed ice

## Directions

1. Mix all the above ingredients well together, stir well and strain into a small punch glass, and serve very cold.

title: Very Healthy Slaw
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- 1 pint best cider vinegar
- 1 lb. fresh butter
- flour
- salt
- cayenne
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Take a nice, fresh, white cabbage
2. Wash and drain it and cut off the stalk
3. Shave it in small and very even shreds with a cabbage cutter or a sharp knife
4. Put it into a deep china dish
5. Take 1 pint of best cider vinegar and mix it with 1 lb. of fresh butter divided into 4 bits and rolled in flour
6. Add a small saltspoon of salt and the same quantity of cayenne
7. Stir this all well together and boil it in a saucepan
8. Have ready the yolks of 4 eggs nicely beaten
9. As soon as the mixture has boiled, take it off the fire and stir in the beaten egg
10. Pour it boiling hot over the shred cabbage and mix it well all through with a spoon
11. Set it to cool on ice or in the open air
12. It must be perfectly cold before it is placed on the table

title: Very Light Paste
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 2½ lbs. of butter
- ½ lb. of lard
- 2 well beaten eggs
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. add 2 well beaten eggs to the flour
2. wash the butter nicely, and then salt the crust
3. Wet it with water enough to make it roll easily
4. roll it out once, divide the paste and put rather more butter in 1 part for the top
5. get it all in 3 times rolling it
6. bake with moderate heat

title: Very Rich Pudding
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- butter
- jam
- eggs for custard
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Line a deep pie dish with puff paste, having first buttered it thoroughly.
2. Place on this a layer of jam, then a layer of custard, then jam, then custard, until the dish is nearly full, leaving the custard layer at the top.
3. Bake for twenty minutes in a moderate oven.
4. Let the pudding cool.
5. Beat up the whites of the eggs that were used for the custard into a stiff whip with a little powdered sugar.
6. Pile the whip on as high as possible, and serve.

title: Victoria Buns
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 lbs. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1 glass of wine
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 pint of milk
- yeast

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients together with as much yeast as will rise them
2. Bake

title: Victoria Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of best almonds
- rose water
- 1 lb. of good butter
- 1/4 lb. of white sugar
- 1 wineglass of wine and brandy mixed
- 1 wineglass of rose water
- whites and yolks of 7 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/4 lb. of best almonds
2. Rub them in a mortar with rose water to prevent their oiling
3. Rub them together to a smooth paste
4. Cream 1 lb. of good butter and 1/4 lb. of white sugar
5. When smooth and light, stir in the almonds
6. Add 1 wineglass of wine and brandy mixed and 1 wineglass of rose water
7. Beat separately the whites from the yolks of 7 eggs
8. First add the yolks, then the whites
9. Beat all well together
10. Line the dish with puff paste, and bake as long as a custard

title: Vinaigrette Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Shallots
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 5 Tablespoonfuls of Oil
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Mix all together, as in a Sauce Ravigote, cold (see recipe), and add the oil and vinegar
2. Serve cold, with cold boiled meat, cold boiled fish, etc.

title: Vinaigrette Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Shallots
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Vinegar
- 5 Tablespoonfuls of Oil
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Chopped Parsley
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Mix all together as in a Sauce Ravigote, cold
2. Add the oil and vinegar
3. Serve cold with cold boiled meat, cold boiled fish, etc.

title: Violet Conserve
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Freshly-Gathered Violet Petals
- 1 1/2 Pounds of Sugar
- Distilled Water

## Directions

1. Pound two ounces of freshly-gathered violet petals, with great care, into a paste, after steeping in distilled water sufficient to make a paste.
2. Then add the sugar, in proportion of one and a half pounds and boil to a flake.

title: Virginia Buns
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1 pint of new milk
- 1 lb. of butter
- 8 eggs
- 1 1/2 grated nutmegs
- 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1 1/2 wineglassful of yeast
- 1 lb. of white sugar

## Directions

1. Sift into a pan 2 lbs. of flour
2. Warm 1 pint of new milk, 1 lb. of butter
3. Stir this, if not too warm, into the flour
4. Beat 8 eggs very light, separating the yolks from the whites
5. Add 1 1/2 grated nutmegs, 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
6. Beat these well together, adding the whites of the eggs lastly, 1 1/2 wineglassful of yeast
7. Set it to rise
8. When risen, stir in 1 lb. of white sugar, to be sifted in and mixed through
9. Set to rise in the pans in which they are to be baked

title: Virginia Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities flour and meal
- 4 eggs to a quart
- 1 teacup good yeast
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Sift flour and meal together well
2. Make up with water and salt

title: Virginia Corn Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 and a half pints of boiling milk
- 1 quart of Indian meal
- a pint of wheat flour
- a tablespoonful of sugar
- a little salt
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 tablespoonful of butter in 1 and a half pints of boiling milk
2. Into this scald 1 quart of Indian meal
3. When cool, add a pint of wheat flour, a tablespoonful of sugar, and a little salt
4. Add 2 eggs well beaten
5. Bake in 2 cakes

title: Virginia Corn Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1½ pints boiling milk
- 1 quart Indian meal
- ½ pint wheat flour
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- a little salt
- 2 eggs well beaten

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 tablespoonful of butter in 1½ pints of boiling milk
2. Scald 1 quart of Indian meal into this
3. When cool, add ½ pint of wheat flour, a tablespoonful of sugar, a little salt, and 2 eggs well beaten
4. Bake in 2 cakes

title: Virginia Egg Bread
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of meal
- a pint of wheat flour
- 1 and a half pints of milk
- 2 eggs
- a tablespoonful of butter or lard

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Bake either in cups or a tinpan

title: Virginia Rolls
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoonful of good yeast
- one egg
- one large spoonful of butter
- one pound or pint of flour
- a little salt
- enough milk

## Directions

1. Set it to rise in a warmed pan until it is light
2. sift a cup of flour into the bread-tray, and pour the light batter in
3. work it well and keep the dough very soft
4. set the dough to rise
5. when light, make into rolls, and lay them on a warmed and buttered pan
6. set them by the fire to rise again
7. baste the top over with butter
8. bake in a quick oven as soon as they are light

title: Virginia Toddy
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two lumps of sugar
- two sherry or wineglassfuls of water
- same of whiskey
- plenty of ice
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Shake well
2. strain into small bar glass, with grated nutmeg on top

title: Vol-au-Vent
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Flour
- 1 Pound of Butter, or Half Butter and Half Lard
- 1 Teaspoonful of Salt
- 1 Cup of Ice Water
- 1 Egg
- Chicken, pigeon or other meat, already fricasseed

## Directions

1. Make the Puff Paste as directed and let it stand in a cool place at least six hours.
2. Roll the paste out very thin, and then line the deep pan in which you intend to cook the chicken or meat.
3. Cut around the edge nicely with a knife.
4. Then take another small pan and cover it only half way with the paste.
5. Cut around the edges, trimming off all strings of dough.
6. Then brush the paste all around the top with a beaten egg.
7. Put it on a sheet iron or square tin, and set it on the ice till very cold.
8. Then set it in a very hot oven to bake for about half an hour.
9. Do not let it burn or scorch.
10. When done, fill the inside with chicken, pigeon or other meat, already fricasseed, with which you intend to make the Vol-au-Vent and then cover with the top crust.

title: Wafers
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of sugar
- ¾ lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 2 wineglasses of wine
- 1 grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. bake in wafer irons

title: Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1/2 pint sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift the flour very, very fine, and then add the salt, and mix well
2. Add the milk and make into a nice, rather stiff dough
3. Place on a biscuit board and beat for fully a half hour
4. Turn the dough over and beat for fifteen minutes more
5. Roll them out into cakes that are almost transparent in thinness, but which can also be handled without losing their shape
6. Lay them gently in the pan, or roll them out on a baking sheet which has been sprinkled with flour
7. Bake quickly in a hot oven

title: Waffles
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 egg
- 1 spoonful good yeast
- milk
- 2 ounces butter

## Directions

1. Make 1 quart of flour, 1 egg, and 1 spoonful of good yeast into a thin batter with milk
2. Put in 2 ounces of butter
3. In summer this should be done early in the morning
4. In winter, the night before

title: Waffles, Economical Way
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- a cup of sweet milk
- one cup of water
- three cups of flour
- two tablespoonfuls of yeast powder
- a tablespoonful of melted lard or butter
- a teaspoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix two tablespoonfuls of yeast powder with flour before sifting
2. Combine eggs, milk, water, sifted flour, melted lard or butter, and sugar
3. Mix all well
4. Bake in waffle irons

title: Walnut Pickle
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- ground pepper
- ginger
- mace
- cloves
- nutmegs
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Pick the walnuts about the Fourth of July when they should be so soft that a pin can be run through them
2. Lay them in salt and water ten days, change the water two or three times during the ten days
3. Rub off the outside with a coarse cloth
4. For one hundred nuts, make a pickle of two quarts of vinegar, one ounce of ground pepper, same of ginger, half an ounce of mace, cloves, nutmegs and mustard seed
5. Put these spices in a bag, lay it in the vinegar and boil all together a few minutes
6. Set the pickle away for use
7. If the vinegar is not very strong, add fresh vinegar to the last scalding of the pickles

title: Walterian Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ lb. of pippins
- ½ lb. of sugar
- ½ lb. of butter
- rind of a fresh lemon
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Grate ½ lb. of pippins
2. Stir to a cream ½ lb. of sugar and ½ lb. of butter, and add the apples
3. Grate the rind of a fresh lemon
4. Whip very light 5 eggs, and beat all well together
5. Bake it in puff paste ½ hour

title: Warm Slaw
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- large red cabbage
- water
- cider vinegar
- butter
- flour
- salt
- cayenne
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take a large red cabbage
2. Wash, drain, and shred it finely
3. Put it in a deep dish
4. Cover it closely and set it on the top of a stove or in a bake oven until it is warm all through
5. Make a dressing as for cold slaw
6. Pour it boiling over the cabbage
7. Cover the dish and send it to the table as warm as possible

title: Warsaw Breakfast Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoonful of good butter
- warm milk
- 1 1/2 lbs. of sifted flour
- 2 eggs well beaten
- a pinch of salt
- a large spoonful of good yeast

## Directions

1. Cut up butter in warm milk
2. When cool, stir in the sifted flour
3. Add eggs well beaten, salt, and yeast
4. Mix well together
5. Put it into buttered tins to rise
6. When risen, bake 1/2 hour

title: Washington Loaf
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tumblerful of good rice flour
- 1 teacupful of wheat flour
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- 1 egg well beaten
- a pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 cup of new milk

## Directions

1. Stir cream of tartar into the flours
2. Cut up the butter very finely
3. Mix all ingredients well together
4. Bake in a pan like pound cake
5. Serve hot for tea or breakfast

title: Water Gruel of Corn Meal or Oat Meal
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- sweet corn meal or oatmeal
- milk
- salt
- butter
- wine or brandy
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put a quart of water on to boil in a stew-pan
2. Take a tablespoonful of sweet corn meal, or oatmeal, make it into a batter with milk and salt
3. Stir it in the boiling water and let it boil gently for half an hour
4. When served it may be sweetened and nutmeg grated over it
5. If wanted for a strengthening nourishment, a bit of butter and a glass of wine or brandy may be added
6. Use very little salt

title: Water Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Sugar
- A Pinch of Cream of Tartar
- 1 Tablespoonful of Vanilla, Peach or Strawberry Extract

## Directions

1. Take two cups of sugar and a small pinch of Cream of Tartar
2. Stir with just enough water to make a thick paste
3. Flavor with Peach or Strawberry Extract
4. If Strawberry is used, add a teaspoonful of Cochineal
5. Beat well, and if not stiff enough, add more sugar
6. Proceed as in Plain Icing

title: Water Muffins
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of good yeast
- 1 pint of lukewarm water
- a little salt
- sifted flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Put the yeast into the water
2. Add salt
3. Stir in gradually as much flour as will make a thick batter
4. Cover the pan, and set it in a warm place to rise
5. When quite light and the middle is hot, grease and set muffin rings on it, having first buttered them round the inside
6. Put a ladleful of the batter for each ring
7. Bake them over a quick fire
8. When sent to table do not cut them; split them by pulling them open with the hands

title: Watercress Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Watercress
- Minced Potato, if desired
- A Plain French Dressing

## Directions

1. This salad is made of watercress simply, or watercress and minced potatoes, mixed in equal quantities, and served with a French dressing.
2. It is a most healthy, light and excellent salad, especially in summer.
3. The salad is delightful without the potatoes. They may be added if desired.

title: Watermelon Rind Preserves
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon rind
- fresh water
- alum
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the watermelon rind into thin slices
2. Throw into a bucket of fresh water, into which you have dissolved a teaspoonful of alum for every quart of water
3. Let it remain for several hours, until they grow crisp
4. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in the recipe for Citron preserves

title: Watermelon Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ripe Watermelon
- 1/2 Pound of Pulverized Sugar
- 6 Teaspoonfuls of Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Split a ripe watermelon in half.
2. Scoop out the center, rejecting the seeds.
3. Put the scooped fruit in a bowl.
4. Take a silver fork and pick it in small pieces.
5. Mix with a half pound of pulverized sugar.
6. Turn the mixture into a freezer and turn slowly for fifteen minutes.
7. Serve in glasses, adding a teaspoonful of Sherry to each glass.

title: Wellington Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of good brown sugar
- 2 1/2 large spoonfuls of good butter
- 1 well whipped egg
- a little grated nutmeg
- a large spoonful of orange flower water
- 1 pint of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- 1 cup of new milk
- 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Beat to a cream 1 cup of good brown sugar and 2 1/2 large spoonfuls of good butter
2. Stir in 1 well whipped egg, a little grated nutmeg, and a large spoonful of orange flower water
3. Sift into a pan 1 pint of flour, and stir in 2 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
4. Dissolve in a cup of new milk 1 teaspoonful of soda
5. Beat these well together
6. Add the milk and soda with the flour, eggs and spice
7. Butter a baking dish, and pour in the pudding
8. Bake 1/2 an hour
9. Serve with wine sauce

title: Welsh Rarebit
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Cups of Grated Cheese (Very Rich)
- 0.5 Cup of Milk
- The Yolks of Two Eggs
- Salt and Cayenne to Taste
- bread

## Directions

1. Toast the bread nicely in square slices and cut off the crusts.
2. Butter nicely while very hot and then plunge them into a bowl of boiling milk.
3. Place them on a heated dish and stand in the oven to keep hot while you proceed to make the Rarebit.
4. Have a porcelain-lined saucepan and set a half cup of milk in it over a moderate fire.
5. When it is boiling hot, add the cheese which has been finely grated.
6. Stir unceasingly till the cheese melts, and then add the salt, Cayenne and the yolks of the eggs and pour over the toasted bread.
7. Serve hot.

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of cheese
- butter
- two well-beaten eggs
- mustard
- pepper
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Cut a pound of cheese in slices a quarter of an inch thick.
2. Fry them together five minutes in butter.
3. Add two well-beaten eggs, a little mustard and pepper.
4. Stir it up and send it to table hot, on slices of buttered bread.

title: Whigs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ lb. butter
- ¾ lb. sugar
- 6 eggs
- 2 lbs. flour
- 1 pint new milk
- a little salt
- 1 gill good yeast

## Directions

1. Cream ¾ lb. of butter and ¾ lb. of sugar
2. When well mixed, stir in 6 well beaten eggs
3. Sift in 2 lbs. of flour, 1 pint of new milk, and a little salt
4. Mix this well together
5. Add 1 gill of good yeast
6. Bake in small tins or muffin rings

title: Whigs
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 4 tablespoonfuls of white sugar
- 6 eggs
- 1 teacup of yeast
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- mace or nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients in the morning
2. Season with mace or nutmeg
3. Set it to rise
4. If it does not rise sufficiently, add a small teaspoonful of yeast powder dissolved in a little milk just before it is baked
5. Bake it in a pan for tea
6. Eat it hot with fresh butter and pounded sugar sifted over it

title: Whip For A Trifle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cream
- 1 quart of water
- half lb. of powdered sugar
- juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Combine 1 pint of cream, 1 quart of water, half lb. of powdered sugar, and the juice of 4 lemons
2. Whisk it to a strong froth
3. Take the froth off as it rises with a skimmer and lay it on a sieve to drain
4. Continue doing so as long as you can obtain any
5. It must be made and kept in a cool place 4 or 6 hours before it is wanted

title: Whipped Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Sweet Cream
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar
- 6 Drops of Jamaica Rum

## Directions

1. Always set the cream on ice for several hours before using
2. Sweeten it to taste, and add a few drops of Jamaica rum
3. Have ready a second bowl, beat the cream, or, rather, whip it, as you would in beating eggs to a froth, with clear, steady strokes, using an egg beater, or, if you have a small crank churn, which is better, use it, pressing the dasher up and down very hard, and removing the cream as it rises around the dashboard
4. In the same way remove the cream as it rises, while beating with the egg beater, putting the whipped portion into the second bowl, and continuing the process of whipping until all the cream is drained
5. Set on ice, and it will keep cool, fresh and sweet and firm

title: Whipped Cream and Strawberries, Raspberries, Blackberries, etc.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Berries
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Stem the berries, sprinkle nicely with white sugar, and set aside for several hours in a cool place
2. Serve with the whipped cream, dishing the berries first in serving, and heaping the cream prettily on top

title: Whipped Cream With Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 3 Ounces of Extract of Coffee

## Directions

1. Make three ounces of Creole Café Noir.
2. Take three tablespoonfuls and add to the cream and sugar.
3. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in Whipped Cream with Vanilla.
4. When ready to serve, heap the cream in the center of a very cold dish, using artistic taste, and serve immediately.

title: Whipped Cream With Cognac
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Gill of Cognac

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in the directions for Whipped Cream with Rum, substituting instead a gill of good old Cognac
2. Serve in the same manner directed

title: Whipped Cream With Curacao
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Gill of Curacao

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as in the directions for Whipped Cream with Rum, adding, instead of the Rum, one gill of Curacao.

title: Whipped Cream With Kirsch
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Gill of Kirsch

## Directions

1. Prepare the cream exactly as in the Foamy Cream with Rum recipe, only, instead of the rum, substitute a gill of Kirsch

title: Whipped Cream With Liquors
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 Gill of Liqueur
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream

## Directions

1. Just before whipping the cream, add a few drops of the liqueur with which you wish to flavor the cream

title: Whipped Cream With Maraschino
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Gill of Maraschino

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as in the direction for Crème en Mousse with Rum, substituting a gill of Maraschino instead of the Rum

title: Whipped Cream With Peaches, Cherries, etc.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Fruit, Peaches, or Cherries, or Bananas, or Pears, etc.
- 1 Pint of Whipped Cream
- 1 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Stone the peaches, and slice or cut them nicely, adding sugar to taste
2. In like manner stone, but do not cut, the cherries if this fruit is used
3. If bananas or pears are preferred peel and slice nicely
4. Seed the pears
5. Sprinkle the fruit with sugar, and set it aside in a cool place for two hours
6. Serve with the whipped cream, heaping it in generous measure at the moment of serving

title: Whipped Cream With Vanilla, etc.
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Cream
- 2 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Put a pint of sweet cream into a basin, add the sugar and the essence
2. Always remember to add the essence before whipping the cream
3. Proceed to whip according to the directions given in Whipped Cream

title: Whipped Cream with Wine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- one pint of cream
- four tablespoonfuls of sweet wine
- four spoonfuls of sugar
- bright jelly or light-colored marmalade
- sponge cake

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of three eggs to a froth
2. Add one pint of cream, four tablespoonfuls of sweet wine, and four spoonfuls of sugar
3. Put bright jelly, or light-colored marmalade in spots among the cream and serve sponge cake with it

title: Whips
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cream
- 3 gills white wine
- juice and peel of 1 lemon
- whites of 6 or 8 eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Combine 1 pint of cream, 3 gills of white wine, the juice and peel of 1 lemon, the whites of 6 or 8 eggs, and sugar to the taste
2. Whisk it well, and put in on a sieve to drain

title: Whiskey Punch, Plain
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two wineglassfuls of lemon or lime juice
- half a tablespoonful of sugar
- one-half wine glass of whiskey
- plenty of ice

## Directions

1. Shake and strain into punch glasses

title: Whiskey, Brandy, or Gin Cocktails - New Orleans Style
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dashes of Boker's, Angostura or Peychaud bitters
- One lump of sugar
- one piece of lemon peel
- one tablespoonful of water
- one wineglassful of liquor
- plenty of ice

## Directions

1. Stir well
2. strain into a cocktail glass

title: White Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of White Beans
- 2 Quarts of Cold Water
- 1 Minced Onion
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Bunch of Parsley
- Sprig of Thyme
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the beans and soak them overnight in lukewarm water.
2. Drain and put them in a saucepan with the cold water.
3. Allow them to come gradually to a boil; then set them back and let them simmer gently for about two hours.
4. Melt the butter in a saucepan and fry in it the onion, parsley, thyme and bay leaf until brown.
5. Add these to the beans and boil about an hour and a half longer.
6. If the beans are perfectly tender at this point, press the whole through a colander.
7. Return to the fire and add the seasonings.
8. Let them boil up once and then serve with the Croqutons.
9. A beaten egg may be added when about to pour into the tureen by beating up the egg and pouring the boiling soup gradually over, stirring all the while.

title: White Beans à la Maître d'Hôtel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart of White Beans
- 2 Sprigs of Minced Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- Sauce à la Maître d'Hôtel

## Directions

1. Prepare the white beans and cook in exactly the same manner as red beans à la Maître d'Hôtel.
2. Before adding the butter, add a minced sprig of thyme, bay leaf and parsley, and salt and pepper to taste.
3. Let them simmer for a few minutes on the fire.
4. Take off and add the juice of a lemon.

title: White Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. powdered sugar
- ½ lb. butter
- ½ lb. of flour
- ¼ lb. corn starch
- whites of 16 eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the butter free of salt
2. Cream it well, and add the sugar, using a porcelain pie masher instead of a spoon
3. Add gradually the flour and cornstarch
4. Add lastly the well beaten whites
5. Flavor with almond to taste
6. Bake as pound cake

title: White Celery Sauce For Boiled Poultry
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- six heads of celery
- half a pint of water
- three teaspoonfuls of flour
- milk
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the green tops of celery, slice the remainder into small bits and boil in half a pint of water until it is tender
2. Mix three teaspoonfuls of flour smoothly, with a little milk
3. Add six spoonfuls more of milk, stir it in
4. Add a little salt and a small piece of butter
5. On boiling take off

title: White Coffee Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Ounces of Coffee
- 1 Pint of Milk
- 1/2 Pint of Cream
- 4 Ounces of Powdered Sugar
- The Yolks of 3 Eggs

## Directions

1. Parch two ounces of coffee and throw the grains, while hot, into the boiling milk.
2. Proceed exactly as in Creamed Coffee.

title: White Compote of Apples
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large apples
- 1 pint water
- juice of ½ a lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut 6 large apples in half, peel and take out the seeds; stew them in 1 pint of water, the juice of ½ a lemon and sugar
2. When the apples are sufficiently tender take them out and arrange them in the dessert dish; let the syrup keep on boiling till it is clear and rich; then pour it over the apples

title: White Compote of Pears
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- water
- syrup
- lemon

## Directions

1. Blanch as many pears as you intend to use, whole, with the skins on, in boiling water; take them out when soft, peel and put into cold water
2. Have your syrup ready in the stewpan; when it is boiling hot, put in the pears, with a slice of lemon, to keep them white; when thoroughly stewed take them out and serve in the syrup; they may be served hot or cold

title: White Consommé Of Fowl
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two fowls, old or young
- cold water
- one large carrot, or two small ones
- turnips
- one onion
- one head of celery
- two cloves
- a piece of mace
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Take one or two fowls, old or young. Let them lie half an hour in cold water to cleanse from the blood, then drain and put them in a pot; fill it with water, let it boil, then skim it. Add one large carrot, or two small ones, two turnips, one onion, one head of celery, two cloves, a piece of mace, a little salt. Let it boil gently for two hours if the chickens are young; if old, three hours. When they are tender, skim off the fat, and pass the consommé through a sieve. This consommé may be considered a basis for all white soups, as well as white sauces, and should be used instead of water for filling them up.

title: White Cream Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Cups of Flour
- 1 Cup of Butter
- 1 Cup of Sweet Cream
- 3 Cups of Sugar
- The Whites of 10 Eggs
- 2 Teaspoonfuls of Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of White Rose Extract

## Directions

1. Proceed in exactly the same manner as for White Mountain Cake, only adding the cream just before adding the flour and the whites of the eggs

title: White Cucumber Sauce For Meats
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five cucumbers
- three-quarters of a pint of veal stock
- yolks of three eggs
- cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and take out the seeds from the cucumbers, cut them into very small pieces and put them into a saucepan with the stock and seasoning
2. Simmer it slowly until they are tender
3. Stir in the yolks of the eggs well beaten
4. Make it very hot, but do not let it boil
5. Serve it up quickly

title: White Fricassee of Chicken
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair chickens
- salt
- boiling water
- blades of mace
- pepper corns
- celery
- onion
- milk
- 2 teaspoons flour
- 1 lb. fresh butter
- 1 pint boiling milk
- madeira or sherry
- nutmeg
- 4 tablespoons rich cream

## Directions

1. Cut a pair of chickens and wash the pieces through 2 or 3 waters
2. Lay them in a large pan and sprinkle slightly with salt
3. Fill the pan with boiling water, cover and let the chicken stand in it half an hour
4. Put them into a stewpan and add a few blades of mace and pepper corns (whole)
5. Add a handful of celery split thin and chopped fine and a small onion sliced
6. Pour on cold water and milk in equal quantities to cover the chicken
7. Cover the pan and let them stew until quite done and tender
8. Prepare gravy in a small saucepan by mixing 2 teaspoons of flour with enough cold water to make a batter, stir it quite smooth
9. Add gradually 1 pint boiling milk and 1 lb. fresh butter cut in pieces
10. Set on the fire until it comes to a boil and the butter is perfectly mixed
11. Take off and while hot, stir in a glass of madeira or sherry, a little nutmeg, 4 tablespoons of rich cream
12. Take chickens out of saucepan and pour off the liquor
13. Return the chickens and pour the gravy over them
14. Cover the pan closely and set it over a kettle of boiling water for 10 minutes
15. Serve very hot

title: White Fruit Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. white sugar
- 1 lb. flour
- 12 eggs beaten separately
- 0.5 lb. butter
- 2 lbs. citron cut small
- 2 lbs. almonds blanched and cut into strips
- 2 coconuts grated

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and stir with the sugar
2. Beat all a long time
3. Pour into a mould and bake
4. Ice either with almond paste, or icing into which you have stirred a grated coconut

title: White Fruit Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 0.5 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons yeast powder
- whites of 9 eggs

## Directions

1. Bake in jelly pans

title: White Fruit Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of white sugar
- 1 lb. of flour
- 12 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 2 lbs. of citron
- 2 lbs. almonds
- 2 cocoanuts
- almond paste or icing

## Directions

1. Beat eggs separately
2. Cream butter and stir with the sugar
3. Cut citron small
4. Blanch almonds and cut into strips
5. Grate cocoanuts
6. Beat all a long time
7. Pour into a mould
8. Bake
9. Ice either with almond paste or icing into which you have stirred a grated cocoanut

title: White Lady Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of eight eggs
- one pound of white sugar finely ground
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a tea-cupful of sweet milk
- a small teaspoonful of powdered volatile salts or saleratus
- sifted flour
- a teaspoonful of orange-flower water or lemon extract
- a quarter of a pound of shelled almonds blanched
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eight eggs to a high froth
2. add gradually the sugar
3. beat butter to a cream
4. add milk with dissolved salts to butter
5. put eggs to the butter and milk
6. add flour to make it as thick as pound-cake mixture
7. add flavoring
8. add almonds beaten to a paste with white of egg
9. beat whole together until light and white
10. line square tin pan with buttered paper
11. put in mixture an inch deep
12. bake half an hour in a quick oven
