13. turn upside down when cold and ice the exposed side
14. mark in slices the width of a finger and two inches and a half long

title: White Mountain Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound Sugar
- 1½ Pounds Butter
- 1 Pound of Flour
- The Whites of 10 Eggs
- ⅛ Teaspoonful of Almond Extract

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, add the sugar, and beat till very, very light
2. Add the flour alternately with the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth, and then the essence extract
3. Bake in a moderate oven one hour

title: White Onion Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- onions (six white)
- milk (a cup of hot)
- butter (a large spoonful)
- pepper
- salt
- flour (to thicken)

## Directions

1. Peel and boil six white onions, and when tender pour off the water;
2. chop the onions small, and add to them a cup of hot milk, a large spoonful of butter, and pepper and salt to taste;
3. thicken with a little flour if preferred.

title: White or Silver Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 20 eggs
- 1 1/2 lbs. of flour
- 1 1/2 lbs. of sugar
- 15 ounces of butter
- a little more than 1 teaspoon of almond extract
- 1 level teaspoon of soda
- 1 good teaspoon of cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly and cream butter
2. Roll sugar and add to it
3. Beat the whites of eggs very stiff and add to this
4. Sift in the flour and beat mixture well 10 or 15 minutes
5. Put in the extract
6. Last of all, pulverize soda and cream of tartar in a little flour and sift into the mixture
7. Beat as little as possible to mix well
8. Bake 1 1/2 hours, the first 15 minutes in a very moderate oven, increasing heat gradually
9. Cover the pan the first 1/2 or 3/4 of the hour
10. This makes a good large cake, or 2 small ones

title: White Oyster Sauce
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three dozen oysters
- half a pound of butter
- flour
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- a cup of milk or cream
- a cup of oyster juice

## Directions

1. Put three dozen oysters in a stewpan, without their juice, which save
2. Mix with the oysters, half a pound of butter, thickened with flour (work it well with a spoon)
3. Season with cayenne pepper and salt
4. Thin with a cup of milk or cream, and a cup of oyster juice
5. Boil altogether for ten minutes

title: White Pound Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pound of Flour
- 1 Pound of Butter
- The Whites of 16 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoon-ful Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Lemon Extract

## Directions

1. Wash the butter. Then beat it and the sugar to a fine cream.
2. Add one-quarter of the flour, well-sifted with the baking powder.
3. Then add one-quarter of the whites of the eggs, which you will have beaten to a stiff froth.
4. Continue alternating the eggs and flour till all are used.
5. Add the flavoring extract, and beat steadily for a half hour.
6. Bake in a moderate oven for one hour.

title: White Pralines
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound of Fine White Sugar (Granulated)
- 1 Freshly-Grated Cocoanut (Small Size)
- 4 Tablespoonfuls of Water

## Directions

1. Use a farina boiler or a porcelain-lined saucepan. Put the sugar in the saucepan with the water and let it boil well.
2. When it begins to form a syrup, take from the fire and stir in the freshly-grated cocoanut.
3. Mix thoroughly and return to the fire, and let it boil until you can draw it like a thread between your finger and thumb.
4. Be careful to stir constantly from the time you add the cocoanut.
5. When it begins to bubble, take from the stove, for it will have reached the above-mentioned state in two or three minutes.
6. Have ready a cleanly washed and somewhat wet marble slab or buttered dish.
7. Take a kitchen spoon and drop the mixture into cakes on the slab, spreading them out with the spoon and rounding with a fork till they form a neat round cake of about a quarter of an inch in thickness and four or five inches in diameter.
8. Let them dry; and then take a knife and gently raise them from the slab.

title: White Roux
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- good butter
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Melt some good butter slowly
2. stir into it sifted flour till it is like thin, firm paste
3. stir it well over a slow fire for a quarter of an hour, but do not let it brown
4. Pour into a jar and keep for use

title: White Roux
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Tablespoonful Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful Flour

## Directions

1. Put the butter and flour simultaneously into the saucepan
2. Do not allow to brown
3. Moisten with a little broth or boiling water
4. Allow to boil a few minutes till thick

title: White Sauce
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of milk
- a little flour
- a piece of butter
- a blade of mace
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take 1/2 pint of milk
2. thicken it with a little flour, a piece of butter, a blade of mace, and grated nutmeg

title: White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- A Tablespoonful and a Half of Butter
- A Tablespoonful and a Half of Flour
- Half a Cup of Water or White Broth
- Salt and Pepper to Taste

## Directions

1. Blend the flour and butter in the saucepan without browning in the least
2. Add by degrees the boiling water or White Consommé of veal or chicken, stirring until smooth
3. Boil three minutes
4. Salt and pepper to taste
5. Add the juice of half a lemon

title: White Sauce Allemande
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds of Raw Veal
- The Bones of a Chicken
- 1 Gallon of Water
- 1 Carrot
- 1 Turnip
- Celery Tops
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Spoonfuls of Lard
- 1 Herb Bouquet of Thyme, Parsley, Bay Leaf
- 1 Stalk of Celery
- 2 Long Carrots
- 1 Wineglassful of Madeira or Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Take the veal and the bones of the chicken and put into a pot with a gallon of water
2. Add the herb bouquet, tied together, and one chopped carrot, one turnip chopped, celery tops, and other ingredients of a good pot-au-feu
3. Let all boil slowly for three hours until it is reduced one-half
4. Salt and pepper to taste
5. Take off the fire and strain the broth into a jar
6. Take two tablespoonfuls of butter and three of flour, and put into a saucepan together, letting the butter and flour blend, without browning
7. Add all the broth to this, stirring slowly while on the fire
8. Add a good, strong bouquet of herbs, thyme, parsley and bay leaf, all tied whole together
9. Add two large carrots, and let it boil till reduced to one-half again
10. Season to taste
11. When it has reached the consistency of starch take off the fire and strain and let it get cool
12. This sauce can be used for all white meats and fish

title: White Sauce for Fowls
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacupful of milk
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- flour
- yolk of 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful of cream
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Melt in a teacupful of milk, 1 large spoonful of butter kneaded in flour
2. beat up the yolk of an egg with a teaspoonful of cream
3. stir it into the butter and heat it over the fire, stirring it constantly
4. Chopped parsley improves the sauce

title: White Sauce For Veal Or Lamb
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of milk
- flour
- small piece of butter
- blade of mace
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take 1/2 pint of milk and thicken it with flour
2. Add a small piece of butter, a blade of mace, and grated nutmeg

title: White Soup
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of butter
- a slice of ham
- a half cup of water
- a quart of stock
- any remains of cold veal, chicken, game or rabbit roasted dry

## Directions

1. Let them simmer gently for an hour
2. If a very rich soup is desired add to the vegetables two or three quarts of good soup stock, made by boiling a beef bone in three quarts of water until the meat is tender
3. Let all boil together for half an hour
4. Add ten or twelve ripe tomatoes and a half-dozen whole peppers
5. It should cook for another hour or so
6. It must then be strained through a sieve or coarse cloth
7. Serve with toasted or fried bread cut in bits in the tureen

title: White Sponge Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- the whites of ten eggs
- one tumbler of sifted flour
- one and a half tumblers of sifted white sugar
- half a teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of ten eggs to a froth
2. Stir the cream of tartar and salt well into the flour
3. Add the sugar to the whites first
4. Stir in the flour very lightly last of all
5. Flavor with any delicate extract
6. Bake immediately
7. No soda required

title: White Sponge Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 11 eggs
- 10 ounces of granulated sugar
- 5 ounces of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoonful of extract of vanilla

## Directions

1. Sift the mixture 4 times
2. Beat the eggs
3. Add the sugar and flour
4. Bake 40 minutes

title: White Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Pulverized White Sugar
- 1 Cup of Flour
- The Whites of 11 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoonful Baking Powder
- 1 Teaspoonful of Vanilla Extract

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of the eggs and the sugar to a thick froth
2. Mix the baking powder and the flour, adding the powder after the fourth sifting
3. Then add the flour gradually to the eggs, beating lightly and thoroughly
4. Add the essence, and bake for from thirty to forty-five minutes in a quick oven

title: White Wine Cup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint of Best Sauterne
- 3/4 of a Sliced Lemon
- 3/4 of a Sliced Orange
- A Dash of Old French Cognac
- A Dash of Maraschino
- 1 Cup of Pineapple Juice
- 1 Large Piece of Ice
- 1 1/2 Pint of Seltzer Water
- 1/2 Cup of Sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and the Seltzer water, add the wine and fruit juices and liquor
2. Add the cherries and sliced lemon and orange
3. Stir well
4. Let cool and serve in small, thin tumblers
5. Decorate the tops with sprigs of fresh green mint, with two or three cherries laid on top

title: White Wine Punch
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quarts of Best Sauternes
- 1 Pint of Old French Cognac
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- 1 Pint of Pineapple Juice
- 1 Quart of Seltzer Water
- ½ Grated Pineapple
- ¾ of a Sliced Pineapple
- 2 Dozen Cherries
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and water
2. Add the liquors and the fruit juices and the grated and sliced fruit
3. Stir well, set to cool and serve in small glass cups

title: White Wine Punch, Creole Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ Quarts of Sauterne
- 1 Pound of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Lemon Juice
- ¾ Pint of Brandy
- 1 Quart of Seltzer Water
- ¾ of a Grated Pineapple
- ¾ of a Sliced Pineapple
- 2 Dozen Cherries
- Sprigs of Fresh Young Mint
- 1 Large Piece of Ice

## Directions

1. Take a large punch bowl
2. Put one pound of sugar into it, with one pint of lemon juice, two and a half quarts of Sauterne (White Wine), half a pint of brandy, two quarts of Apollinaris or Seltzer, one-half of a grated pineapple
3. Decorate with sliced pineapple and whole cherries and sprigs of fresh young mint
4. Put in a large piece of ice, and let it cool, and serve in small glass cups

title: White Wine Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Cup of Butter
- 2 1/4 Cups Powdered Sugar
- 1/4 Cup of Boiling Water
- 2 Wineglasses of White Wine
- 1 Tablespoonful Grated Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a cream, and add the boiling water
2. Set the vessel containing the mixture into a bain-marie or saucepan of boiling water, and stir in gradually the white wine and the grated nutmeg
3. Let the sauce get hot, but do not let it boil
4. When it forms a thick cream take off, and serve either hot or cold
5. Stir all the time it is on the fire
6. Serve with any pudding.

title: White Wine Syllabub
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of milk
- sugar
- wine
- sweetened whipped cream
- extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Season a pint of milk with sugar and wine, but not enough wine to curdle the milk
2. Full your glasses nearly full, and crown them with sweetened whipped cream
3. Season the cream with extract of lemon

title: White, or Bride's Cake
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. and 4 ounces flour
- 1 lb. and 2 ounces butter
- 1 lb. sugar
- whites of 24 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- almond essence

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and flour to a cream
2. Beat the whites very lightly, then add the sugar, and again beat well
3. Mix all the ingredients together, and at the last add 1 teaspoonful of soda mixed with a little white of egg
4. Season with almond essence
5. Pour into a mould and bake
6. Ice the cake when cold

title: Wild Ducks
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- wild ducks
- small onion
- butter
- bunch of celery
- pepper
- salt
- lemon

## Directions

1. Truss wild ducks and lay them in a pan to bake with a small onion in the body
2. Put butter over them, with a bunch of celery, a little pepper and salt
3. Cook slowly and garnish with lemon
4. Wild ducks should be wiped dry after they are drawn, and rubbed on the inside with pepper and salt, except the canvas-back, which should be left to its own delicious flavor

title: Wild Ducks or Teal
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- wild ducks or teal
- butter
- lemon juice
- cayenne pepper
- sugar
- port wine

## Directions

1. Roast the birds; a duck should take about 15 minutes with a good fire; a teal will take less time
2. Baste them very frequently
3. Attend to the fire and motion of the spit
4. When you dish it, draw the knife four times down the breast
5. Have ready a little hot butter, the juice of a lemon, cayenne pepper, a little dust of sugar, and a glass of port wine
6. Pour it hot over the ducks

title: Wild Ducks With Olives
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Cups of Left-Over Duck
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Tablespoonful of Flour
- 1 Onion
- 2 Sprigs Each of Thyme and Parsley
- 1 Bay Leaf
- 1 Cup of Broth or Water
- 1 Glass of Claret
- 2 Dozen Spanish Olives
- Toasted Croutons

## Directions

1. Take all the remains of the Duck and select the good parts, and cut them into pieces of about an inch and a half square.
2. Put a tablespoonful of butter into the stewpan, and, as it browns, add one onion, chopped fine.
3. Let this brown and then add the Duck.
4. Stir well; add the tablespoonful of sifted flour, stir again, and in four or five minutes add two sprigs each of thyme and parsley and one bay leaf, minced very fine.
5. Let this brown well, and smother nicely for about ten minutes.
6. Add a pint of good broth if you have it; if not, a cup of boiling water.
7. Stir well, and season again according to taste.
8. Pour in a half glass of good Claret, and add about two dozen fine olives, stoned.
9. Let all boil for thirty minutes longer, and serve hot, with garnish of diamond-shaped toasted Croutons.

title: Wild Ducks, Hunters' Style
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Fine Canvas-Back Ducks
- 3/4 Pint of Veal Broth or Water
- 1 Tablespoonful of Butter
- 1 Dozen Mushrooms, Sliced
- 3 Fresh or Canned Tomatoes
- 1 Onion
- 3/4 Glass of Madeira Wine or Lemon Juice
- 3/4 Pint of Sauce Espagnole
- The Zest of 1 Lemon
- Croutons
- Salt and Pepper

## Directions

1. Pick the ducks; singe, draw, and, after rinsing clean within, wipe neatly within and without.
2. Cut off the wings, legs and breasts.
3. Take the two carcasses and sprinkle rightly with salt and place in the oven to bake about six minutes.
4. Remove the carcasses and hash them up.
5. Put them into the saucepan; add a pint of veal broth, consommé or water in lieu of either of these.
6. Add a herb bouquet tied together and let the preparation simmer for about a quarter of an hour over a moderate fire.
7. Put a tablespoonful of butter into a saucepan, and lay in the wings, breasts and legs of the Ducks.
8. Season lightly with salt and pepper, and set on a very brisk fire and let cook for a few minutes, on either side.
9. Add a half glassful of Madeira wine and a half pint of Sauce Espagnole and the grated zest of a lemon.
10. Take the gravy from the carcasses and strain over the Ducks, and allow all to cook about a quarter of an hour.
11. Place on a hot dish and decorate nicely.

title: Wild Geese
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- wild geese
- bread
- butter
- onion
- cayenne
- mustard

## Directions

1. Stuff wild geese with a dressing of bread, butter, and a small quantity of pungent seasoning, such as onion, cayenne, or mustard
2. Cook rare

title: Wild Turkey
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- oysters
- bread
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- button mushrooms
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. If the turkey is old, or tough, boil it one hour before being stuffed for baking.
2. Stuff it with oysters, bread and butter, and season with pepper and salt.
3. Baste with butter, and the juice of the turkey.
4. Make the gravy by putting in the pan a pint of oysters, or button mushrooms, throw in a cup of cream, or milk, salt and pepper.
5. Send to table hot, with the turkey.

title: Windsor Pudding
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 4 or 6 eggs
- A piece of butter
- 1 ounce of almonds (half bitter)
- A little lemon peel
- Juice of 1 lemon
- i lb. of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Thicken 1 pint of milk with 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
2. Boil and let it stand till cold
3. Add 4 or 6 eggs, a piece of butter, 1 ounce of almonds (half bitter), a little lemon peel, the juice of 1 lemon and i lb. of loaf sugar
4. Bake in cups

title: Wine Cakes
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound of butter
- one-half pound of sugar
- one egg
- a few drops of essence of lemon
- a good half pound of flour

## Directions

1. Mix, roll thin, and cut out in round cakes

title: Wine Cobblers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Wineglassful of Sherry or Any Other Wine
- A Small Piece of Lemon Peel
- 1 Tablespoonful of Sugar or Syrup

## Directions

1. Take a glass, put in a tablespoonful of sugar or syrup, a small piece of cut lemon peel, one wineglass of Sherry or any other of the above wines, and then fill the glass with crushed ice
2. Stir well
3. Decorate the mixture with sprigs of fresh mint or strawberries or sliced pineapple, and serve

title: Wine Cream
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cream
- 6 tablespoonfuls of white wine
- nutmeg
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Set 1 quart of cream on the fire
2. When it boils, stir in very slowly 6 tablespoonfuls of white wine, taking care that the cream does not curdle
3. Season with nutmeg and orange flower water
4. Sweeten to the taste
5. Let it stand until it is cold

title: Wine Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- The Yolks of 8 Eggs
- 1 Cup Sugar
- 1 Quart of Madeira or Sherry Wine

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and eggs well together, and add gradually one quart of Madeira or Sherry Wine, flavored with lemon or vanilla.
2. Set the vessel in which they were mixed in another containing boiling water.
3. Let the mixture simmer until the cream is perfectly formed, and then set to cool.
4. Serve with whipped cream.

title: Wine Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- The Whites of 2 Eggs
- 1 Pound of White Sugar
- 1 Gill of Sherry or Madeira Wine

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as in Lemon or Orange Icing, only adding instead one gill of good Sherry or Madeira wine
2. Then proceed as in Plain Icing

title: Wine Jelly for the Sick
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of Madeira wine
- one pint of water
- one ounce of isinglass
- a teacupful of water
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one pint of Madeira wine, one pint of water, and one ounce of isinglass dissolved in a teacupful of water
2. Let the wine and water be boiling hot, then stir into it the dissolved isinglass, and sugar to taste
3. make it quite sweet
4. let it come to a boil
5. try it by taking a little in a saucer, and if not a good jelly when cold, boil it until it is so
6. if lemon is allowed, use the juice of two to flavor this jelly

title: Wine Jelly from Sparkling Gelatine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce or one ounce and a half of gelatine
- one pint of cold water
- three-quarters of a pint of boiling water
- one and a half pounds of white sugar
- juice and grated rind of one lemon
- a spoonful of any essence
- whites of two eggs
- one pint of wine
- two tablespoonfuls of extract of lemon or vanilla

## Directions

1. Pour gelatine upon one pint of cold water, and let it remain to soften for an hour or so
2. Pour three-quarters of a pint of boiling water on to the gelatine, and stir until the gelatine is dissolved
3. Add to it one and a half pounds of white sugar, the juice and grated rind of one lemon, and a spoonful of any essence
4. Beat the whites of two eggs well, and stir briskly into the mixture
5. Put it on a gentle fire, let it simmer slowly, take it off as soon as it boils up
6. Add a pint of wine and two tablespoonfuls of extract of lemon or vanilla
7. Strain it through a jelly bag until it runs clear
8. After it is clear and still warm, pour in a pint of wine and set the jelly on ice in summer, or to cool in the winter

title: Wine Sangaree of Port or Madeira
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a glass of water
- a tablespoonful of white powdered sugar
- a gill of Madeira or Port
- nutmeg
- pounded ice
- lady-cake or pound-cake

## Directions

1. Take half a glass of water, sweeten it with a tablespoonful of white powdered sugar, and stir well until dissolved
2. add a gill of Madeira or Port
3. add some nutmeg and pounded ice
4. Serve with lady-cake or pound-cake, cut small

title: Wine Sauce For Venison Or Mutton
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of soup from the stock pot
- a dozen cloves
- a teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- a cup of wine or currant jelly

## Directions

1. Take from the stock pot a pint of the soup
2. Let it boil down to half a pint
3. Season with a dozen cloves, a teaspoon of salt, and a little pepper
4. Stir in a cup of wine, or of currant jelly

title: Winter Dish of Baked Beans and Pork
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- water
- salt pork

## Directions

1. Pick the beans, wash them, and put them to soak over night in plenty of water.
2. In the morning pour this water off and put the beans in a kettle of cold water.
3. Place them on the fire and let them simmer till quite tender.
4. Take them up and drain them.
5. When thoroughly drained, put them in a baking pan with a large piece of salt pork.
6. Score the pork and lay it deep in among the beans, not upon them.
7. Pour boiling water over them and bake till brown.
8. If in a range, leave them in all night.

title: Winter Ginger Cakes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 pint of sweet cream
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 2 1/2 ounces of ginger, powdered
- 2 lbs. of flour

## Directions

1. Break 3 eggs in a bowl and beat them well
2. Add 1 pint of sweet cream, which must be beaten with them, and the whole put into a saucepan over the fire
3. Stir till it gets warm
4. Add 1 lb. of butter and 1 lb. of loaf sugar and 2 1/2 ounces of ginger, both powdered
5. Stir the whole carefully over the fire, just to melt the butter
6. Pour it on to 2 lbs. of flour and form it all into a paste
7. Roll it or break it into pieces as you think proper and bake it

title: Winter Okra Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Can of Good New Orleans Okra
- 1 Can of Tomatoes
- 2 Onions Chopped Fine
- 2 Tablespoonfuls of Butter
- 2 Dozen Oysters
- 3 Tablespoonfuls of Rice
- A Red Pepper Pod, Without the Seed
- 3 Quarts of Water
- 1 Pint of Oyster Water

## Directions

1. Chop the onions fine and fry them in the butter.
2. Wash the rice well, then stew the onions, tomatoes and pepper together in about three quarts of water and one pint of oyster water for about three hours, stirring frequently.
3. Ten minutes before serving add the okra and let it come to a boil.
4. Then drop in the oysters.
5. Boil up once and serve.

title: Winter Tomatoes
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and cut all of the hard part of the tomatoes
2. Add a little salt and red pepper
3. Let them stew until thick enough to bottle
4. Put all the bottles in an oven with straw placed around them to prevent their breaking
5. Fill the oven with lukewarm water
6. Let it boil 10 minutes
7. Cork the bottles tightly, whilst in the oven, so as to make them air tight
8. Rosin them also

title: Wisconsin Fruit Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound of raw salt, fat pork
- a pint of boiling water
- one cup of sugar
- two cups of molasses
- two teaspoonfuls of cloves
- one teaspoonful of cinnamon
- one nutmeg
- two teaspoonfuls of saleratus
- one pound and a half of raisins
- one pound of citron and currants
- flour

## Directions

1. Take three quarters of a pound of raw salt, fat pork, chopped very fine
2. pour on a pint of boiling water, one cup of sugar, two cups of molasses, two teaspoonfuls of cloves, one of cinnamon, one nutmeg, two teaspoonfuls of saleratus, one pound and a half of raisins, also a pound of citron and currants if liked
3. add flour as stiff as can be stirred
4. bake very slowly an hour, or longer if necessary, as it will burn without great care
5. This will make three loaves, and will keep well

title: Yankee Biscuits
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar
- 1 quart of flour
- lard or butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- water or milk

## Directions

1. Mix 2 teaspoonfuls of cream of tartar into 1 quart of flour
2. Dissolve 1 teaspoonful of soda in water or milk
3. Mix all together, roll it out, cut it into cakes, and bake it as soon as you please

title: Yeast With Hops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- Irish potatoes
- hops
- water
- flour
- good yeast or yeast cake

## Directions

1. Peel and boil eight large Irish potatoes
2. Boil a handful of hops in a little water or in the water the potatoes were boiled in
3. Mash the potatoes fine and strain the water from the hops over them
4. Put in a cup of flour to the potatoes before the water is poured on
5. Mix all together and beat it
6. Put in half a cup of good yeast or a yeast cake

title: Yeast With Hops
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- eight large Irish potatoes
- a handful of hops
- water
- a cup of flour
- half a cup of good yeast, or a yeast cake

## Directions

1. Peel and boil eight large Irish potatoes
2. Boil a handful of hops in a little water, or in the water the potatoes were boiled in
3. mash the potatoes fine
4. strain the water from the hops over them
5. Put in a cup of flour to the potatoes before the water is poured on, as it mixes better when dry
6. mix all together and beat it
7. then put in half a cup of good yeast, or a yeast cake

title: Yellow (Saffron)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Ounces of Saffron
- 1 Ounce of Alum
- 20 Ounces of Sugar
- 1 Pint of Water
- alcohol

## Directions

1. Take three ounces of Saffron, one ounce of powdered Alum, twenty ounces of sugar, and one pint of water and boil all together
2. Strain and add a little alcohol, and bottle for use

title: Yellow Custard Jelly from Gelatine
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of gelatine
- one pint of water
- one quart of milk
- yolks of eight eggs
- vanilla or lemon for flavoring

## Directions

1. Soak one ounce of gelatine in one pint of water
2. Add a quart of milk
3. Set the milk and gelatine on to get hot, let it give one boil up
4. Sweeten it, and when a little cooled stir in the beaten yolks of eight eggs
5. Do not let the eggs boil up or you might curdle them
6. Flavor with vanilla or lemon, pour into moulds, and set in a cool place, or on ice to harden

title: Yellow Lady Cake
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine white sugar
- half a pound of butter
- yolks of eight eggs
- one cup of sweet milk
- a small teaspoonful of powdered volatile salts or saleratus dissolved in hot water
- half a nutmeg grated
- a teaspoonful of lemon extract or orange-flower water
- sifted flour
- a quarter of a pound of shelled almonds

## Directions

1. Beat butter to a cream
2. add sugar and beat
3. add beaten yolks
4. add milk with dissolved salts
5. add flour to make it as thick as pound-cake batter
6. add nutmeg and flavoring
7. beat until light and creamy
8. beat almonds to a paste and stir into cake
9. beat well
10. line buttered tin pans with white paper
11. put in mixture an inch deep
12. bake half an hour in a quick oven or forty minutes in a moderate oven

title: Yellow Pickle
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of ginger sliced and dried
- 1 lb. of horse radish scraped
- 1 lb. of white mustard seed washed and dried
- 1 lb. of garlic salted, sliced and dried
- 1 lb. of nutmegs
- 1 ounce of long peppers
- 1 ounce of turmeric
- 1.5 gallons of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the vegetables intended for the pickle into a kettle
2. Pour the vinegar and spices over them
3. Set them over a clear fire
4. Let them boil a few moments
5. Put them in jars and tie them up tightly

title: Young Cooks' Jelly Roll
author: Lafcadio Hearn
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of white sugar
- one cup of flour
- three eggs
- baking powder
- jelly

## Directions

1. Make the sponge by taking a cup of white sugar, one cup of flour, and three eggs
2. Mix and add baking powder with the flour
3. Bake in a stewpan with a quick fire
4. turn the cake out on a towel when done
5. spread the jelly while it is still warm and soft
6. roll it carefully
7. Cut it in slices when cold

title: Zouave Rusks
author: Christian Woman's Exchange
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint and a half of good flour
- 1/2 pint of white sugar
- a heaped tablespoon of butter
- a teacup of milk
- 2 teaspoonfuls of yeast
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix yeast with the dry flour
2. Rub the sugar, butter, and yolks of eggs as in pound cake
3. Beat the whites to a stiff froth
4. Add the milk just before you put in the beaten whites
5. The flour should be added last
6. Bake immediately
